0.translit:
vaazhi parakaalan vaazhi kalikanRi
vaazhi kuRaiyaloor vaazh vEndhan vaazhiyarO-
maayOnai vaaL valiyaal mandhiram koL mangaiyar-kOn
tooyOn sudar maana vEl.
0.meaning: Long live Parakalan, long live Kalikanri! Long live the king who reigns at Kuraiyalur, may he flourish! He is the lord of Mangai, the pure one with a radiant great spear, who by the might of his sword seized the sacred mantra from the dark-hued Lord (Mayon).

1.translit:
nenjukku iruL kadi dheepam adangaa nedum piRavi
nanjukku nalla amudham tamizha nan nool thuRaigaL
anjukku ilakkiyam aaraNa saaram parasamayap
panjukku analin poRi parakaalan panuvalgaLE.
1.meaning: The compositions of Parakalan are a lamp that dispels the darkness for the heart, a sweet nectar against the poison of unending birth, and a fine Tamil scripture comprising the disciplines of learning. They are the very essence and exemplar of the Vedanta, and like a spark of fire to the cotton of rival doctrines.

2.translit:
engaL gathiyE! iraamaanusa muniyE!
sangai keduttu aaNda thavaraajaa
pongu pugazh mangaiyar kOn eendha maRai aayiram anaiththum
thangu manam nee enakkut thaa.
2.meaning: O our refuge! O sage Ramanuja! O king of penance who removed all doubt and took us into your rule! Grant me a heart in which may abide all the thousand Vedic verses bestowed by the lord of Mangai of surging fame.

3.translit:
vaadinEn vaadi varundhinEn manaththaal perun thuyar idumbaiyil piRandhu,
koodinEn koodi iLaiyavar tham mOdu avar tharum kalaviyE karudhi
OdinEn Odi uyvadhu Or poruLaal uNarvu enum perum padham thirindhu
naadinEn naadi naan kaNdu koNdEn naaraayaNaa ennum naamam.
3.meaning: I withered, and withering I grieved in my mind, born into great sorrow and affliction. I joined with young women, joining them and craving only the union they gave; I ran about, and running I roamed, having strayed from that high state called true wisdom which alone could save me. Then I sought, and seeking I found at last the name that is 'Narayana.'

4.translit:
aaviyE amudhE ena ninaindhu urugi avaravar paNai mulai thuNaiyaa,
paaviyEn uNaraadhu eththanai pagalum pazhudhu pOy ozhindhana naatkaL,
thoovi sEr annam thuNaiyodum puNarum soozh punaR kudandhaiyE thozhudhu,
en naavinaal uyya naan kaNdu koNdEn naaraayaNaa ennum naamam.
4.meaning: Thinking 'O my life, O my nectar' and melting with longing, taking the full breasts of this woman and that as my companions, I, a sinner, lived in ignorance, and however many days passed, those days went to waste. Worshipping at Kudandhai surrounded by waters, where feathered swans unite with their mates, I found, that my tongue might be saved, the name that is 'Narayana.'

5.translit:
sEmamE vENdith theevinai perukkith therivaimaar uruvamE maruvi,
oomanaar kaNda kanavilum pazhudhaay ozhindhana kazhindha an naatkaL,
kaamanaar thaadhai nammudai adigaL tham adaindhaar manaththu iruppaar,
naamam naan uyya naan kaNdu koNdEn naaraayaNaa ennum naamam.
5.meaning: Seeking only my own security, I multiplied evil deeds and clung to the forms of women; those past days went to waste, more useless even than a dumb man's dream. Our Lord, who is the father of Kaman (Manmatha), dwells in the hearts of those who take refuge in him. That I might be saved, I found the name that is 'Narayana.'

6.translit:
venRiyE vENdi veezh poruTku irangi vER kaNaar kalaviyE karudhi,
ninRavaa nillaa nenjinai udaiyEn en seygEn nedu visumbu aNavum,
panRiyaay anRu paaragam keeNda paazhiyaan aazhiyaan aruLE,
nanRu naan uyya naan kaNdu koNdEn naaraayaNaa ennum naamam.
6.meaning: Desiring only victory, pining over base wealth, and craving the embrace of spear-eyed women, I have a heart that will not stay as it stands. What shall I do? By the grace of the strong one who bears the discus, who long ago as a boar lifted up the earth that reaches the vast sky, that I might truly be saved, I found the name that is 'Narayana.'

7.translit:
kaLvanEn aanEn padiRu seydhiruppEn kaNdavaa thiri thandhEnElum,
theLLiyEn aanEn sel gadhikku amaindhEn sikkenat thiruvaruL peRREn,
uLLelaam urugik kural thazhuththu ozhindhEn udambelaam kaNNa neer sOra,
naL iruL aLavum pagalum naan azhaippan naaraayaNaa ennum naamam.
7.meaning: Though I became a thief, lived in deceit, and wandered wherever my fancy led, yet I became clear-sighted, fit for the path of salvation, and firmly obtained the sacred grace. My whole inner being melted, my voice grew choked, and tears poured down all over my body. Through the dead of night and through the day I call out the name that is 'Narayana.'

8.translit:
embiraan, endhai, ennudaic chuRRam enakku arasu, ennudai vaazhnaaL
ambinaal arakkar verukkoLa nerukki avar uyir seguththa em aNNal,
vambu ulaam sOlai maa madhiL thanjai maa maNik kOyilE vaNangi,
nambigaaL! uNNa naan kaNdu koNdEn naaraayaNaa ennum naamam.
8.meaning: He is my Lord, my father, my kindred, my king, and my very lifespan, my noble one who with his arrows pressed the demons until they trembled and destroyed their lives. Worshipping at the great jewelled temple of Thanjai with its great ramparts and fragrance-laden groves, O devotees, that you may partake of it, I found the name that is 'Narayana.'

9.translit:
iR piRappu aRiyeer ivar avar enneer innadhu Or thanmai enRu uNareer
kaRpagam pulavar kaLai kaN enRu ulagil kaNdavaaru thoNdaraip paadum,
soR poruLaaLeer sollugEn vammin soozh punaR kudandhaiyE thozhumin,
naR poruL kaaNmin paadi neer uymmin naaraayaNaa ennum naamam.
9.meaning: You do not inquire about high birth, you do not say 'this one or that one,' you do not perceive any such distinctions of nature. O you masters of words who, calling Him the wish-granting tree and the refuge of the learned, sing in this world of devotees just as you see them—I will tell you, come! Worship at Kudandhai surrounded by waters. Behold the good treasure, and sing and save yourselves: the name that is 'Narayana.'

10.translit:
kaRRilEn kalaigaL aimpulan karudhum karuththuLE thiruththinEn manaththai,
peRRilEn adhanaal pEdhaiyEn nanmai peru nilaththaar uyirkku ellaam,
seRRamE vENdith thiri tharuvEn thavirndhEn sel gadhikku uyyum aaRu eNNi,
naR thuNaiyaagap paRRinEn adiyEn naaraayaNaa ennum naamam.
10.meaning: I did not learn the arts and sciences; I fixed my mind only on the thoughts that crave the five senses. Because of this, foolish one that I am, I gained no good. I who wandered about seeking only enmity toward the lives of all the people of this great earth have now ceased; reflecting on the way to be saved unto the high path, I, your servant, have grasped as my good support the name that is 'Narayana.'

11.translit:
kulam tharum selvam thandhidum adiyaar padu thuyar aayina ellaam,
nilam thaRanj cheyyum neeL visumbu aruLum aruLodu peru nilam aLikkum,
valam tharum maRRum thandhidum peRRa thaayinum aayina seyyum,
nalam tharum sollai naan kaNdu koNdEn naaraayaNaa ennum naamam.
11.meaning: It grants noble lineage and bestows wealth; it grinds to the ground all the sufferings that befall the devotees; it graciously grants the vast high heaven, and along with that grace gives the great earth. It gives strength and grants all else besides; it does for us even more than the mother who bore us. I found that word which gives all good: the name that is 'Narayana.'

12.translit:
manju ulaanj cholai vaNdu aRai maa neer mangaiyaar vaaL kalikanRi,
senj cholaal eduththa dheyva nan maalai ivai koNdu sikkenat thoNdeer
thunjum pOdhu azhaimin thuyar varil ninaimin thuyar ileer sollilum nanRaam,
nanju thaan kaNdeer nammudai vinaikku naaraayaNaa ennum naamam.
12.meaning: Kalikanri, the swordsman of Mangai, land of clouded groves, humming bees, and great waters, composed this fine divine garland of beautiful words. Taking these, O devotees, hold firmly: call out at the time of death, remember when sorrow comes, and even if you are free of sorrow it is good to recite it. Behold, it is the very poison that destroys our karma—the name that is 'Narayana.'

13.translit:
vaali maa valaththu oruvanadhu udal keda vari silai vaLaiviththu anRu
Ela naaRu thaN thadam pozhil idam peRa irundha nal imayaththuL,
aali maa mugil adhir thara aru varai agadu uRa mugadu ERi,
peeli maa mayil nadanj cheyum thadanj chunaip piridhi senRu adai nenjE.
13.meaning: Bending his beautiful bow that day so that the body of mighty-armed Vali might perish, He abides in the good Himalayas, where cool, vast, fragrant groves spread wide. There, in Piridhi with its broad springs, where the great dark clouds thunder and climb up the bellies and crests of the lofty mountains, and the splendid peacocks spread their plumes and dance—go there and reach it, O my heart.

14.translit:
kalanga maak kadal ari kulam paNi seyya aru varai aNai katti,
ilangai maa nagar podi seydha adigaL thaam irundha nal imayaththu,
vilangal pOl vana viRal irunj chinaththana vEzhangaL thuyar koora,
pilam koL vaaL eyiRRu ariyavai thiri tharu piridhi senRu adai nenjE.
14.meaning: Building a bridge of mighty mountains while the troop of monkeys served Him so that the great sea churned in turmoil, the Lord who ground the great city of Lanka to dust dwells in the good Himalayas. In Piridhi, where lions with cavern-like mouths and gleaming sword-like fangs roam, so that the forest elephants—mighty, fierce, and mountain-like—are stricken with anguish—go there and reach it, O my heart.

15.translit:
thudi koL nuN idaic churi kuzhal thuLangu eyiRRu iLam kodi thiRaththu aayar
idi koL vem kuralina vidai adarththavan irundha nal imayaththu,
kadi koL vEngaiyin naRu malar amaLiyin maNi aRai misai vEzham,
pidiyinOdu vaNdu isai solat thuyil koLum piridhi senRu adai nenjE.
15.meaning: For the sake of the cowherd girl—slender as a drum-waist, with curling locks and bright teeth, tender as a creeper—He subdued the bulls that bellowed with fierce, thunderous roars; that Lord dwells in the good Himalayas. In Piridhi, where upon a couch of the fragrant blossoms of the vengai tree, on jewelled rocks, the bull elephant sleeps with his mate while the bees hum their music—go there and reach it, O my heart.

16.translit:
maRam koL aaL ari uru ena veruvara oruvanadhu agal maarvam
thiRandhu vaanavar maNi mudi paNi thara irundha nal imayaththuL,
iRangi Enangal vaLai maruppu idandhida kkidandhu arugu eri veesum,
piRangu maa maNi aruviyodu izhi tharu piridhi senRu adai nenjE.
16.meaning: Taking the form of a fierce man-lion so that all trembled in terror, He tore open the broad chest of that one (Hiranya), and the celestials bowed their jewelled crowns before Him; that Lord dwells in the good Himalayas. In Piridhi, where boars descend and dig with their curved tusks, and from beside them streams descend with shining great gems that flash like darting fire—go there and reach it, O my heart.

17.translit:
karai sey maak kadal kidandhavan kanai kazhal amararkaL thozhudhu Eththa,
arai sey mEgalai alar magaL avaLodum amarndha nal imayaththu,
varai sey maak kaLiRu eeLa vedhir vaLar muLai aLai migu thEn thOyththu,
pirasa vaari than iLam pidikku aruL seyum piridhi senRu adai nenjE.
17.meaning: He who reclines upon the great sea that fashions shores, whose resounding anklets the immortals worship and praise, abides contentedly with the lotus-born Goddess of the jewelled waist in the good Himalayas. In Piridhi, where the mountain-like great bull elephant draws up the bamboo shoots, dips them in honey abundant in the hollows, and offers the honey-water lovingly to his young mate—go there and reach it, O my heart.

18.translit:
paNangaL aayiram udaiya nalla aravu aNaip paLLi koL paramaa enRu,
iNangi vaanavar maNi mudi paNi thara irundha nal imayaththu,
maNam koL maadhavi nedum kodi visumbu uRa nimirndhavai mugil paRRi,
piNangu poom pozhil nuzhaindhu vaNdu isai solum piridhi senRu adai nenjE!
18.meaning: Crying 'O Supreme One who rests in slumber upon the fine serpent couch that bears a thousand hoods,' the celestials draw near in love and bow their jewelled crowns; that Lord abides in the good Himalayas. In Piridhi, where the long fragrant madhavi creepers rise to reach the sky and catch hold of the clouds, and bees enter the tangled flowering groves and hum their music—go there and reach it, O my heart!

19.translit:
kaar koL vEngaigaL kana varai thazhuviya kaRi vaLar kodi thunni,
pOr koL vEngaigaL puna varai thazhuviya poom pozhil imayaththuL,
Er koL poonj chunaith thadam padindhu ina malar ettum ittu imaiyOrgaL,
pErgaL aayiram paravi ninRu adi thozhum piridhi senRu adai nenjE.
19.meaning: Where vengai trees dark as rain-clouds embrace the great mountain, thick-grown with pepper creepers, and warring tigers cling to the wooded slopes, in the flowering groves of the Himalayas, the celestials bathe in the broad and lovely flower-springs, offer the eight kinds of blossoms, and standing there extol His thousand names and worship His feet—to that Piridhi go and reach it, O my heart.

100.translit:
seeyan aNiyan ena sindhaiyuL ninRa maayan
maNi vaaL oLi veN daraLangkaL,
vey viNdu udhir vengkata maamalai meeya,
aayan adi alladhu maRRu aRaiyeenee.
100.meaning: The wondrous Lord who dwells in my mind, at once far away and near, the Maayan, abides on the great hill of Venkatam where the bamboos burst and scatter bright white pearls like shining sapphire-radiance. I will speak of nothing other than the feet of that cowherd Lord.

101.translit:
vandhaay en manam pugundhaay manni ninRaay,
nandhaadha kozhum sudaree engkaL nambee,
sindhaamaNiyee tiruvengkadam meeya endhaay!
iniyaan unnai enRum vidteenee.
101.meaning: You came, You entered my heart, and You stood there abiding forever, O our Lord, O never-fading radiant flame. O wish-granting gem, O my Father who dwells on holy Venkatam! From now on I will never let You go, at any time.

102.translit:
villaar mali vengkata maamalai meeya,
mallaar tiraL tooL maNi vaNNan ammaanai,
kallaar tiraL tooL kaliyan sonna maalai,
vallaar avar vaanavar aaguvar taamee.
102.meaning: Of the sapphire-hued Lord with rounded wrestler's shoulders, who abides on the great hill of Venkatam abounding with bows, Kaliyan of the rock-like rounded shoulders has sung this garland of verses. Those who can master it shall themselves become celestials.

103.translit:
vaanavar tangkaL sindhai poolen nenjamee, ini vandhu, maadhava
maanavar tangkaL sindhai amarndhu uRaiginRa vendhai,
kaanavar idu kaar agil pugai oongku vengkadam meevi, maaN kuRaL
aana andhaNaRku inRu adimait tozhil pooNdaayee.
103.meaning: O my heart, you were not even like the minds of the celestials! Now come and serve our Lord, the Father who dwells settled in the minds of those great souls devoted to penance and to Madhava, who abides on Venkatam where the dark agil-incense set alight by the hill-dwellers rises high. Today you have taken up the work of service to that holy one who became the noble dwarf brahmin.

104.translit:
uRavu suRRam enRu onRu ilaa oruvan ugandhavar tammai,
maN misai piRaviyee keduppaanadhu kaNdu en nenjam enbaay,
kuRavar maadhargaLoodu vaNdu kuRinji maruL isai paadum
vengkadattu, aRava naayagaRku inRu adimait tozhil pooNdaayee.
104.meaning: The peerless One who has no kin or relations of His own, who delights to destroy the earthly rebirth of those He loves, beholding this, O you my heart! Today you have taken up the work of service to that Lord of righteousness at Venkatam, where the bees, together with the hill-women, sing the bewildering kurinji melody.

105.translit:
iNdai aayina koNdu toNdargaL eettuvaar uRavoodum,
vaan idaik koNdu pooyidavum adhu kaNdu en nenjam enbaay,
vaNdu vaazh vada vengkada malai kooyil koNda adhanoodum,
mee misai aNdam aaNdiruppaaRku adimait tozhil pooNdaayee.
105.meaning: He bears His devotees who praise Him with garlands, and their kin too, away up into the sky; beholding this, O you my heart! Today you have taken up the work of service to Him who, taking the northern Venkatam hill where bees thrive as His temple, also rules the cosmos high above.

106.translit:
paaviyaadhu sey taay en nenjamee! paNdu toNdu sey taarai,
maN misai meevi aatkoNdu pooy visumbu eeRa vaikkum endhai,
koovi naayagan koNdal undhu uyar vengkada malai aaNdu,
vaanavar aaviyaay iruppaaRku adimait tozhil pooNdaayee.
106.meaning: What wrong have you done, O my heart? Yet our Father seeks out on this earth those who once rendered Him service, takes them as His own, and raises them up to ascend the sky. Today you have taken up the work of service to that cowherd Lord who rules the lofty Venkatam hill that the clouds push against, and who abides as the very life-breath of the celestials.

107.translit:
pongku poodhiyum piNdiyum udaip puttar noonbiyar paLLiyuL uRai,
tangkaL deevarum taangkaLumee aaga en nenjam enbaay!
engkum vaanavar daanavar niRaindhu eettum vengkadam meevi ninRaruL,
angkaN naayagaRku inRu adimait tozhil pooNdaayee.
107.meaning: Let the Buddhists with their swelling bodhi and asoka trees, and the Jain ascetics dwelling in their cloisters, keep to their own gods and to themselves, O you my heart! Today you have taken up the work of service to the lovely-eyed Lord who graciously stands abiding at Venkatam, where on every side celestials and titans throng and praise Him.

108.translit:
tuvari aadaiyar mattaiyar samaN toNdargaL maNdi uNdu pinnarum,
tamarum taangkaLumee tadikka en nenjam enbaay,
kavari maak kaNam seerum vengkadam kooyil koNda kaN aar visumbidai,
amara naayagaRku inRu adimait tozhil pooNdaayee.
108.meaning: Let the ochre-robed ones, the witless ones, and the Jain followers gorge themselves and grow fat, both their kin and themselves, O you my heart! Today you have taken up the work of service to the Lord of the immortals, who in the eye-filling sky took as His temple Venkatam, where herds of wild yak gather.

109.translit:
tarukkinaal samaN sey thu sooRu taN tayirinaal tiraLai,
midaRRidai nerukkuvaar alakkaN adhu kaNdu en nenjam enbaay,
maruL kaL vaNdugaL paadum vengkadam kooyil koNda adhanoodum,
vaan idai arukkan meevi niRpaaRku adimait tozhil pooNdaayee.
109.meaning: Out of arrogance the Jains practice their rite, stuffing balls of rice and cool curd down their throats - behold their wretchedness, O you my heart! Today you have taken up the work of service to Him who took Venkatam, where the intoxicated bees hum, as His temple, and who also abides as the sun set in the sky.

110.translit:
seeyan aNiyan siRiyan periyan enbadhu silarp peesak keettirundhee,
en nenjam enbaay enakku onRu sollaadhee,
veygaL ninRu veN muttamee sori vengkada malai kooyil meeviya,
aayar naayagaRku inRu adimait tozhil pooNdaayee.
110.meaning: Having listened to some who debate whether He is far or near, small or great, O you my heart, without saying a single word to me, today you have taken up the work of service to the Lord of the cowherds, who abides at the temple of Venkatam hill, where the standing bamboos shed white pearls.

111.translit:
koodi aadi uraittadhee uraittaay en nenjam enbaay tuNindhu keeL,
paadi aadip palarum paNindhu eettik kaaNgilaa,
aadu taamaraiyoonum eesanum amar koonum ninRu eettum,
vengkadattu aadu kootthanukku inRu adimait tozhil pooNdaayee.
111.meaning: You have only repeated again what gathered crowds dance and chant, O you my heart; now listen resolutely. Today you have taken up the work of service to the dancing Lord of Venkatam, whom even the One on the swaying lotus, and Siva, and the king of the immortals stand and praise, yet cannot fully behold, though many sing, dance, and bow in worship.

112.translit:
minnum maa mugil meevu taN tiru vengkada malai kooyil meeviya,
annamaay nigazhndha amarar perumaanai,
kanni maa madhiL mangkaiyark kali kanRi in tamizhaal uraittha,
im mannu paadal vallaarkku idam aagum vaan ulagee.
112.meaning: Of the Lord of the immortals who appeared in the form of a swan, who abides at the temple of the cool, holy Venkatam hill where the great lightning-bearing clouds gather - Kalikanri of Mangai with its grand maiden-like ramparts has spoken in sweet Tamil. For those who can master these enduring songs, the celestial world shall be their abode.

113.translit:
kaasai aadai moodi oodik kaadhal seydhaan avan oor,
naasam aaga nambavalla nambi nam perumaan,
veeyin anna tooL madavaar veNNey uNdaan ivan enRu
eesa ninRa em perumaan evvuL kidandhaanee.
113.meaning: Our Lord, the trustworthy One who could lay waste the city of him who, covering himself in dark robe, ran off in lust - our Lord whom women with bamboo-like arms reproached, saying, This is the one who ate the butter - our Lord reclines at Evvul.

114.translit:
taiyalaaL meel kaadhal seydha daanavan vaaL arakkan,
poyyilaadha pon mudigaL onbadhoodu onRum, anRu
seydha vem poor tannil angku oor sen saratthaal uruLa,
eydha endhai em perumaan evvuL kidandhaanee.
114.meaning: The titan, the sword-bearing demon who lusted after the lady - that day in fierce battle my Father loosed a single red arrow so that his unfailing nine and one golden crowns rolled to the ground. That Father, our Lord, reclines at Evvul.

115.translit:
munnoor toodhu vaanaratthin vaayil mozhindhu, arakkan
mannoor tannai vaaLiyinaal maaLa munindhu avanee
pinnoor toodhan aadhi mannarkku aagip peru nilatthaar,
innaar toodhan ena ninRaan evvuL kidandhaanee.
115.meaning: He who once sent His message through the mouth of a monkey, and in wrath destroyed with His arrows the demon's enduring city - that same Lord later became an envoy for the foremost kings, so that the people of the wide earth said, He is such a one as to be a messenger. That Lord reclines at Evvul.

116.translit:
pandhu aNaindha mel viralaaL paavai tan kaaraNatthaal,
vendhiRal eeR eezhum venRa veendhan viri pugazh seer,
nandhan maindhan aagavaagum nambi nam perumaan,
endhai tandhai tam perumaan evvuL kidandhaanee.
116.meaning: The king who, for the sake of the maiden of soft fingers used to the ball, vanquished the seven fierce bulls - the one of wide-spreading glory who came to be the son of Nanda, our Lord, the Lord of my fathers and forefathers, reclines at Evvul.

117.translit:
baalanaagi naalam eezhum uNdu paNdu aal ilai meel,
saala naaLum paLLikoLLum taamarai kaNNan eNNil,
neelam aar vaNdu uNdu vaazhum neydhal am taN kazhani,
eela naaRum paim puRavil evvuL kidandhaanee.
117.meaning: The lotus-eyed Lord who, becoming an infant, swallowed all the seven worlds and of old lay reposing for endless days upon a banyan leaf, beyond all counting, reclines at Evvul, with its cool fields fragrant with neydhal blossoms where dark bees feed and thrive, amid green meadows fragrant with cardamom.

118.translit:
soottha nambi enRu toNdar miNdit todarndhu azhaikkum,
aatthan nambi sengkaN nambi aagilum deevarkku ellaam,
mootttha nambi mukkaN nambi enRu munivar tozhudhu
eettum, nambi em perumaan evvuL kidandhaanee.
118.meaning: Our Lord whom the devotees thronging press and call out to, crying Hail, O Lord! - the trustworthy Lord, the red-eyed Lord, who though He is the eldest Lord over all the gods, the three-eyed Lord, whom even the sages worship and praise - that Lord, our Lord, reclines at Evvul.

119.translit:
tingkaL appu vaan eri kaal aagi, tisai muganaar
tangkaL appan saami appan baagatthu irundha, vaNdu uN
tongkal appu neeL mudiyaan soozh kazhal sooda ninRa,
engkaL appan em perumaan evvuL kidandhaanee.
119.meaning: He who became the moon, the waters, the sky, fire, and wind; the Father of the four-faced Brahma; the Father who is master, in whose form Siva abides - so that even he of the long crown bedecked with garlands where bees feed stands to don the encircling anklets of His feet - our Father, our Lord, reclines at Evvul.

120.translit:
munivan moorthi moovaraaki vedam viritthuraittha
punithan, poovai vaNNanaNNal puNNiyan viNNavarkoan,
thaniyan seyan thaanoruvan aakilum thannadiyaarkku
iniyan, enthai yemperumaan evvuL kidanthaane.
120.meaning: He is the sage, the Lord who became the three forms and expounded the spreading Vedas; the pure one, the noble Lord hued like the kaaya-flower, the virtuous one, the chief of the celestials. Though he is unique, far beyond, and stands alone as the One, he is sweet to his own devotees. My father, my Lord, has reclined at Evvul.

121.translit:
panthi rukkum melviralaaL paavai panimalaraaL,
vanthi rukkum maarvanneela meni maNivaNNan,
anthi ratthil vaazhum vaanor naayaka naayamaintha,
inthi rarkkum thamperumaa nevvuL kidanthaane.
121.meaning: On his chest abides the maiden of the cool lotus-flower, she of soft fingers that grip the ball, Lakshmi. He is dark-bodied, the sapphire-hued one. He is the Lord even of Indra, established as the chief of the celestials who dwell in the high heavens. Our Lord has reclined at Evvul.

122.translit:
iNdai koNdu thoNdarettha evvuL kidanthaanai,
vaNdu paadum paipuravil mangaiyar koankaliyan,
koNda seeraal thaNdamizhsey maalaiyee rainthumvallaar,
aNda maaLva thaaNaiyandre laaLva rarulake.
122.meaning: To the one who reclines at Evvul, whom devotees worship offering garlands of flowers, Kaliyan, the king of Mangai whose fields are wide and where bees hum, has made this garland of ten verses in cool Tamil with gathered excellence. Those who master them shall rule the celestial world; if not, by his command they shall rule even the grace-filled world.

123.translit:
virperu vizhavum kanjanum mallum vezhamum paakanum veezha,
setravan dranai, purameri seytha sivanuru thuyarkaLai thevai,
patralar veeyak kolkaiyil koNdu paartthandran thermunnin draanai,
sitravai paNiyaal mudithuran thaanaith thiruvallik keNikkaN dene.
123.meaning: He destroyed the great bow-festival, Kamsa, the wrestlers, the elephant and its mahout, felling them all; he is the God who removed the suffering that came upon Shiva who had burned the three cities. Taking the goad in hand so that foes perished, he stood before Arjuna's chariot; and at the bidding of his young stepmother he renounced the crown. Him I have seen at Tiruvallikkeni.

124.translit:
vedatthai vedath thinsuvaip payanai vizhumiya munivarkaL vizhungum,
kothilin kaniyai nanthanaar kaLitraik kuvalayath thortozhu dethum,
aathiyai yamuthai yennai yaaLudai appanai oppava rillaa maatharkaL vaazhum,
maadamaa mayilaith thiruvallik keNikkaN dene.
124.meaning: He is the Veda, the very fruit of the Veda's sweet taste, the flawless ripe fruit that the noble sages savor; he is the elephant-calf of Nanda, the primal one whom those of the world worship and praise, the nectar, the Father who owns and rules me, the matchless one. At mansion-filled great Mylai, at Tiruvallikkeni where peerless women dwell, I have seen him.

125.translit:
vanchanai seyyath thaayuru vaaki vanthape yalarimaN sera,
nanjamar mulaiyoo duyirseka vuNda naathanaith thaanavar koortrai,
vinjaivaa navarsaa raNarsitthar viyanthu thuthiseyyap peNNuru vaaki,
anjuvai yamutha mandraLith thaanaith thiruvallik keNikkaN dene.
125.meaning: When the demoness Putana came in a mother's form to work deceit, he sucked her poison-smeared breast so that her life was taken, and she fell screaming to the ground; he is the Lord, death itself to the demons. When the celestials, the kinnaras, the charanas and the siddhas wondered and praised, he took a woman's form and once gave out the nectar of five sweet tastes. Him I have seen at Tiruvallikkeni.

126.translit:
inthira nukken raayarka Leduttha ezhilvizha vilpazha nadaisey,
manthira vithiyil poosanai peraathu mazhaipozhin thidatthaLarnthu,
aayar anthamo dinavaa niraithaLa raamal emperu maanaru Lenna,
anthamil varaiyaal mazhaithaduth thaanaith thiruvallik keNikkaN dene.
126.meaning: When the cowherds raised the beautiful festival they used to keep for Indra, and he, denied the worship of mantric rite, poured down rain so that they grew weary, the cowherds with their kin cried 'Lord, grant your grace, that our herds of cattle not perish.' He held off the rain with the boundless mountain. Him I have seen at Tiruvallikkeni.

127.translit:
inthuNaip pathumath thalarmakaL thanakkum inpannar puvithanak kiraivan,
thanthuNai yaayar paavainap pinnai thanakkirai matraiyork kellaam vanthuNai,
panja paaNdavark kaaki vaayurai thoothusen riyangum enthuNai
enthai thanthaitham maanaith thiruvallik keNikkaN dene.
127.meaning: He is the joy of the lady of the blooming lotus, his close companion, and the Lord of the good earth; the lord of the cowherd-maiden Nappinnai with her matching grace, and the strong refuge of all others. He is my companion who, for the five Pandavas, went bearing their spoken message as envoy; my father, the Lord of my fathers. Him I have seen at Tiruvallikkeni.

128.translit:
anthakan siruva narasartha marasar kiLaiyava naNiyizhai yaichchendru,
enthamak kurimai sey enath thariyaathu emperu maanaruL enna,
santhamal kuzhalaaL alakkaNnootr uvartham peNdiru meythinoo lizhappa,
inthiran siruvan thermunnin draanaith thiruvallik keNikkaN dene.
128.meaning: When the younger brother of the king of kings went to Draupadi, the fair-jeweled lady, and said 'serve as our own,' she, unable to bear it, cried 'Lord, grant your grace.' Then the lady of fragrant abundant tresses suffered torment, and the wives of the hundred Kauravas in turn lost their honor; and he, Krishna, stood before the chariot of Arjuna, the son of Indra. Him I have seen at Tiruvallikkeni.

129.translit:
parathanum thampi satthuruk kanannum ilakkuma nodumai thiliyum
iravunan pakalum thuthiseyya nindra iraavaNaan thakanaiyem maanai,
kuravame kamazhum kuLirppozhi loodu kuyilodu mayilkaLnin draala,
iraviyin kathirkaL nuzhaithalsey thariyaath thiruvallik keNikkaN dene.
129.meaning: My Lord, the destroyer of Ravana, before whom Bharata, his brother Shatrughna, and Lakshmana together with Sita stood praising night and good day. Where in cool groves fragrant with kurava-blossoms the koels and peacocks stand and call, where the sun's rays do not know how to pierce through, at Tiruvallikkeni I have seen him.

130.translit:
paLLiyi lothi vanthathan siruvan vaayiloo raayira naamam,
oLLiya vaakip pothavaang kathanuk kondrumor poruppil naaki,
piLLaiyaich seeri veguNduthooN pudaippap piraiyeyitr analvizhip pezhvaay,
theLLiya singa maakiya devaith thiruvallik keNikkaN dene.
130.meaning: When from the mouth of his little son, who had learned and come back from school, the thousand bright names came forth, and he (Hiranya) could not bear even one of them; enraged at the child he struck the pillar in fury, and the Lord became the clear-eyed lion with crescent-fangs, flaming eyes and gaping mouth. Him I have seen at Tiruvallikkeni.

131.translit:
meenamar poykai naaNmalar koyvaan vetkaiyi nodusen rizhintha,
kaanamar vezham kaiyeduth thalarak karaavathan kaalinaik kathuva,
aanaiyin thuyaram theerappuL Loornthu sendrunin raazhithot thaanai,
thenamar solai maadamaa mayilaith thiruvallik keNikkaN dene.
131.meaning: When the forest elephant, descending in eagerness to pluck the fresh blossoms of the fish-filled pond, raised its trunk and trumpeted in pain as the crocodile seized its foot, he rode the bird (Garuda) to end the elephant's anguish, came, stood, and hurled the discus. At honey-laden grove and mansion-filled great Mylai, at Tiruvallikkeni I have seen him.

132.translit:
mannuthaN pozhilum vaaviyum mathiLum maadamaa Likaiyum maN dapamum,
thennanthoN daiyarkkon seythananmayilaith thiruvallik keNinin raanai,
kanninan maada mangaiyar thalaivan kaamaru seerkkali kandri,
sonnason maalai patthudan vallaar sukamini thaaLvarvaa nulake.
132.meaning: To the one who stands at Tiruvallikkeni in good Mylai with its abiding cool groves, ponds, ramparts, mansions, palaces and pavilions, made by the king of the southern Tondaiyar, Kalikanri, the lord of Mangai with its fine new mansions and of lovely renown, has sung this garland of words. Those who master all ten shall rule the celestial world in sweet happiness.

133.translit:
andraayarku lakkodi yodaNimaa malarmangaiyo danpaLavi,
avuNarkkendraanu mirakkami laathavanukkuk kuraiyumida maavathu,
irumpozhilsoozh nandraayapu nalnarai yoortthiruvaa likudanthai thadanthikazh kovalnakar,
nindraanirunth aankidan thaannadanthaar kidammaamalai yaavathu neermalaiye.
133.meaning: He once joined in love with the creeper of the cowherd clan and with the lady of the beautiful lotus, yet to the demons he is one without any mercy. The places that suffice for him are good Naraiyur surrounded by dark groves and fresh waters, Tiruvali, Kudanthai, and the city of Koval bright with broad tanks. And for him who stands, sits, reclines and walks, the great mountain that is his abode is Neermalai.

134.translit:
kaaNdaava nampathor kaadamarark karaiyanathu kaNdavan nirka,
mune mooNdaarazha luNNamu ninthathuvum athuvandriyum munnula kamporaitheerth thaaNdaan,
avuNanavan maarvakalam ukiraalvaki raakamu ninthu,
ariyaaynee Ndaankura Laakini mirnthavanuk kidammaamalai yaavathu neermalaiye.
134.meaning: The forest called Kandava, the woodland of the king of the celestials (Indra), while he looked on, the Lord made the kindled fire consume in his wrath; and besides that, of old he ruled and relieved the world of its burden. In wrath he split the broad chest of the demon (Hiranya) with his nails, becoming the towering man-lion; and he became the dwarf and then grew tall. For him the great mountain that is his abode is Neermalai.

135.translit:
alamannu madalsuri sangameduth thadalaazhiyi naalaNi yaaruruvil,
pulamannu vadampunai kongaiyinaaL poraitheeramu naaLadu vaaLamaril,
palamannar padachchuda raazhiyinaip pakalonmarai yappaNi koNdu aNiser
nilamannanu maayula kaaNdavanuk kidammaamalai yaavathu neermalaiye.
135.meaning: Taking up the beautiful right-whorled conch and the destroying discus, in his lovely-hued form, to relieve the burden of the earth-lady adorned with garlands upon her breasts, in the fierce sword-battle of old he made many kings fall by the radiant discus, causing the sun to set, and he became the comely king of the well-adorned land and ruled the world. For him the great mountain that is his abode is Neermalai.

136.translit:
thaangaathatho raaLari yaayavuNan ranaiveeda muninthava naalamarum,
poongothaiyar pongeri moozhkavaiLaith thathuvandriyum vendrikoL vaaLamaril,
paangaakamun aivaro danpaLavip pathitrainthirat tippadai ventharpada,
neengaaccheru vilnirai kaatthavanuk kidammaamalai yaavathu neermalaiye.
136.meaning: Becoming the man-lion that could not be withstood, he slew the demon (Hiranya) in his wrath, and brought it about that the flower-tressed women (the demon's people) sank into the surging fire; and besides that, joining of old in love with the five (Pandavas), in the victorious sword-battle he caused the kings of the twice-five-times-ten armies to fall and guarded the host in the unceasing war. For him the great mountain that is his abode is Neermalai.

137.translit:
maalungkada laaramalaikkuvadit aNaikatti varampuruva
mathiserkolamathi Laayavilangaikedap padaithottoru kaalama riladhira,
kaalamithu vendrayan vaaLiyinaal kathirneeNmudipatthu marutthamarum,
neelamukil vaNNane makkiraivar kidammaamalai yaavathu neermalaiye.
137.meaning: Setting the mountain-peaks as a causeway upon the great wave-tossing sea and building a bridge to the very bounds, he took up his weapons so that Lanka with its lovely moon-touching ramparts was destroyed; and when one time the battle thundered, saying 'this is the time,' with arrows from the bow he severed the ten radiant tall crowns (of Ravana) and fought. For the dark-cloud-hued one, our Lord, the great mountain that is his abode is Neermalai.

138.translit:
paaraarula kumpani maalvaraiyum kadalumsuda rumivai yuNdum,
enakkaaraa thena nindrava nemperumaan alaineerula kukkarasaakiya,
appeeraanaiminthamunikkaraiyan pirarillai nunakkenu mellaiyinaan,
neeraarppe raannedu maalavanuk kidammaamalai yaavathu neermalaiye.
138.meaning: Having swallowed the wide earth, the cool great mountains, the sea and the lights, my Lord who stood saying 'even this is not enough for me'; the king who became sovereign of the wave-tossing watery world. He is the great Lord whom the sage-king (Indra), who set bounds saying 'there is none other than you,' once opposed. For the water-abounding great one, the tall Tirumal, the great mountain that is his abode is Neermalai.

139.translit:
pukaraaruru vaakimuninthavanaip pukazhveeda muninthuyi ruNdu,
asuranna karaayina paazhpada naamamerinthathuvandriyum vendrikoL vaaLavuNan,
pakaraathava naayira naamamadip paNiyaathava naippaNi yaamaril,
nikaraayavan nenchidan thaanavanuk kidammaamalai yaavathu neermalaiye.
139.meaning: Becoming the spotless-hued (man-lion) form, in his wrath he took the life of the one (Hiranya) so that his fame perished, and shattered his name so that the demon's city became a waste; and besides that, the matchless one tore open the heart, in battle, of the sword-bearing demon who would not utter the thousand names, who would not bow at the feet, who would not worship. For him the great mountain that is his abode is Neermalai.

140.translit:
picchacchiRu peeli piDitthulagil piNan thinmaDavaaravar pool,
angngane achchamilar naaNila raathanmaiyaal avarseygai veRutthaNi maamalarthuy,
nacchinama naaraDai yaamainamak karuLsey enavuLkuzhain thaarvamoDu,
nichchamninai vaarkkaruL seyyumavaR kiDammaamalai yaavadhu neermalaiye.
140.meaning: Like the wretches who carry small peacock feathers, begging, and like the women who eat corpses in this world, the Jains are shameless and without modesty. Loathing their ways, I scatter beautiful pure flowers and, melting in my heart with longing, pray, 'Cherishing You, grant that we never reach the abode of Yama; bestow Your grace upon us.' For Him who grants grace to those who meditate on Him every day, the abode is the great hill that is Neermalai.

141.translit:
peesumaLa vanRidhu vamminnamar! piRarkkeeTpadhan munpaNi vaarvinaigaL,
naasamadhu seydhiDum aathanmaiyaal adhuvenama dhuyviDam naaNmalarmel
vaasamaNi vaNDaRai paimpuRavil manamaindhoDu naindhuzhal vaar,
madhiyil neesaravar senRaDai yaadhavanuk kiDammaamalai yaavadhu neermalaiye!
141.meaning: This is no time for mere talk - come, our kinsfolk! Before others hear, those who bow down have their evil deeds destroyed; for that reason that very place is our refuge of liberation. In the fresh groves where fragrant bright bees hum over fresh-blossomed flowers, the witless base ones, melting and wandering with their minds set astray, do not reach Him - His abode is the great hill that is Neermalai!

142.translit:
neDumaalavan meeviya neermalaimel nilavumpugazh mangaiyar kon,
amaril kaDamaagaLi yaanaivallaan kaliyan oliseydhamizh maalaival laarkku,
uDane viDumaalvinai veNDiDil meelulagum eLidhaayiDu manRiyi langoliser,
koDumaakaDal vaiyaga maaNDumadhik kuDaimannava raayaDi kooDuvare.
142.meaning: On Neermalai where Nedumal (the tall Tirumal) abides, Kalian, the king of the Mangai people of enduring fame, master of the rutting battle-elephant, composed this resounding Tamil garland of verses. For those who master it, their great karma at once departs; and if they wish, even the higher world becomes easy to attain. Else, here in this resounding world girt by the cruel great sea, they shall rule under the white parasol of righteous kings and reach His feet.

143.translit:
paaraaya thuNDumizhndha pavaLatthooNaip paaDukaDali lamudhatthaip parivaaykeeNDa seeraanai,
emmaanaith thoNDarthangaL sindhaiyuLLe muLaitthezhundha theengarumbinai,
pooraanaik komboseittha porerRRinaip puNarmarudha miRanaDandha poRkunRinai,
kaaraanai yiDarkkaDindha kaRpagatthaik kaNDadhunaan kaDalmallaith thalasayanatthe.
143.meaning: The coral pillar that swallowed the earth and spewed it forth, the nectar of the roaring sea, the glorious One who lovingly split open Hiranya; my Lord, the sweet sugarcane that sprouted up within the hearts of His devotees; the warring bull that broke the tusk of the battle-elephant, the golden hill that walked between and felled the twin marudam trees; the wish-granting kalpaka tree that ended the affliction of the dark elephant - Him I beheld at Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam.

144.translit:
pooNDavattham piRarkkaDaindhu thoNDupaTTup poynnoolai meynnoolen RenRumodhi maaNDu,
avattham pogaadhe vamminendhai envaNangap paDuvaanai,
kaNangaLetthum neeNDavatthaik karumugilai emmaanthannai ninRavoor nitthilatthaith thotthaarsolai,
kaaNDavatthaik kanalerivaaip peyvitthaanaik kaNDadhunaan kaDalmallaith thalasayanatthe.
144.meaning: Do not be ruined in vain, bound in servitude to others, ceaselessly reciting false scriptures as though they were true scriptures - come! He is my Father, the One who is worshipped, the lofty hill that hosts of gods praise, the dark cloud, my Lord, the pearl of the town where He stands, who in the cluster-laden groves made the Khandava forest be cast into blazing fire - Him I beheld at Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam.

145.translit:
uDamburuvil moonRoRaay moortthiveeRaay ulaguyya ninRaanai,
anRupeychchi viDamparugu vitthaganaik kanRumeytthu viLaiyaaDa vallaanai varaimeekaanil,
thaDamparugu karumugilaith thanjaikkoyil thavaneRikkor peruneRiyai vaiyangaakkum,
kaDumparimel kaRkiyainaankaNDukoNDen kaDipozhilsoozh kaDanmallaith thalasayanatthe.
145.meaning: He who stands as the three-in-one in bodily form, yet as distinct deities, for the world's salvation; the cunning One who that day made the demoness drink her own poison; He who could herd calves and play, the dark cloud that drinks the pools on the wooded heights; the temple of Thanjai, the great path for the path of penance; Kalki upon the swift horse who shall guard the world - Him I beheld at Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam girt by fragrant groves.

146.translit:
peytthaayai mulaiyuNDa piLLaithannaip piNaimaruppil karungaLiRRaip piNaimaannokkin,
aaytthaayar thayirveNNe yamarndhakovai andhaNarthamamudhatthaik kuravaimunne kotthaanai,
kuDamaaDu kootthanRannaik kogulangaL thaLaraamal kunRamendhik kaatthaanai,
emmaanaik kaNDukoNDen kaDipozhilsoozh kaDalmallaith thalasayanatthe.
146.meaning: The child who sucked the breast of the demoness-mother, the dark elephant with paired tusks, the cowherd lord who relished the curds and butter of the doe-eyed cowherd women; the nectar of the brahmins, who long ago strung the kuravai dance; the one who danced the pot-dance, who lifting up the hill protected the cattle-herds so they did not weaken; my Lord - Him I beheld at Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam girt by fragrant groves.

147.translit:
paayndhaanaith thirisagaDam paaRiveezhap paalaganaa yaalilaiyil paLLiyinbam eeyndhaanai,
ilangoLiser maNikkunRanna eeriraNDu maalvaraitthoo Lemmaanranai,
thoyndhaanai nilamagaLthoL thoodhiRsenRap poyyaRaivaaip pugappeydha mallarmangak kaaytthaanai,
emmaanaik kaNDukoNDen kaDipozhilsoozh kaDalmallaith thalasayanatthe.
147.meaning: He who sprang and made the three-wheeled demon shatter and fall, who as a babe enjoyed the bliss of resting on the banyan leaf; my Lord with four mountain-like arms resembling a radiant gem-hill; who embraced the shoulders of the Earth-goddess, who went as an envoy and, growing wrathful at the wrestlers thrust into the false hall, destroyed them; my Lord - Him I beheld at Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam girt by fragrant groves.

148.translit:
kiDandhaanaith thaDangaDaluL paNangaLmeevik kiLarppoRiya maRithiriya vadhaninpinne paDarndhaanaip,
paDumadhattha kaLiRRinkombu paRitthaanaip paariDatthai eyiRukeeRa iDandhaanai,
vaLaimaruppi nenamaagi irunilanum peruvisumbu meydhaavaNNam kaDandhaanai,
emmaanaik kaNDukoNDen kaDipozhilsoozh kaDalmallaith thalasayanatthe.
148.meaning: He who reclines in the vast sea upon the hoods of the serpent with its bright spots that coil and turn, and who spreads forth beyond it; who broke off the tusk of the rutting elephant; who, that the earth might split, tore it up; who, becoming a boar with curved tusk, lifted the great earth; who strode so that both the wide earth and the vast sky could not measure Him; my Lord - Him I beheld at Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam girt by fragrant groves.

149.translit:
peeNaadha valiyarakkar meliyavanRu peruvaraitthoo LiRaneritthan RavuNarkkonai,
pooNaagam piLaveDuttha porvallonaip porukaDaluL thuyilamarndha puLLoordhiyai
ooNaagap peymulainanj suNDaan thannai uLLuvaa ruLLatthe yuRaiginRaanai,
kaaNaadhu thiritharuven kaNDukoNDen kaDipozhilsoozh kaDanmallaith thalasayanatthe.
149.meaning: He who that day made the strong arrogant demons grow weak, crushing the mountain-like shoulders of the demon-king; the war-master who split open the bejewelled chest of the asura; who rides the bird and rests in slumber upon the raging sea; who as His food drank the poison of the demoness's breast; who dwells in the hearts of those who contemplate Him - I who wandered unable to see Him have now beheld Him at Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam girt by fragrant groves.

150.translit:
peNNaagi yinnamudham vanjitthaanaip piRaiyeyiRRan RaDalariyaaip peruginaanai,
thaNNaarndha vaarppunalsoozh meyyamennum thaDavaraimel kiDandhaanaip paNangaLmeevi,
ennaanai yeNNiRandha pugazhinaanai ilangoLise raravindham ponRuneeNDa
kaNNaanai, kaNNaarak kaNDukoNDen kaDipozhilsoozh kaDalmallaith thalasayanatthe.
150.meaning: He who, becoming a woman, beguiled away the sweet nectar; who that day grew into the mighty man-lion with crescent-like fangs; who reclines upon the hoods of the serpent on the great hill called Meyyam girt by cool flowing waters; my Lord of immeasurable fame; He whose long eyes resemble the radiant lotus - Him I beheld to my eyes' content at Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam girt by fragrant groves.

151.translit:
thoNDaayar thaamparavu maDiyinaanaip paDikaDandha thaaLaaLaR kaaLaayuydhal viNDaanai,
thennilangai yarakkarvendhai vilanguNNa valangaivaaych charangaLaaNDu,
paNDaaya vedhangaL naankumaindhu veLvigaLum keLviyoo DangamaaRum kaNDaanai,
thoNDanen kaNDukoNDen kaDipozhilsoozh kaDalmallaith thalasayanatthe.
151.meaning: He whose feet His devotees extol, who possesses the feet that strode across the earth - whoever refuses to be His servant forfeits salvation; who wielded the arrows of His right hand so that the king of the demons of southern Lanka was devoured by wild beasts; who revealed the four ancient Vedas, the five sacrifices, and the six limbs (angas) together with the hearing of sacred lore - Him I, His servant, beheld at Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam girt by fragrant groves.

152.translit:
paDanaagath thaNaikkiDandhan RavuNarkonaip paDavekuNDu marudhiDaippoyp pazhanaveli,
thaDamaarndha kaDalmallaith thalasayanatthuth thaamaraikkaN thuyilamarndha thalaivanthannai,
kaDamaarum karungaLiRu vallaanvelpoRk kaligenRi yoliseydha inbappaaDal,
thiDamaaga ivaiyaindhu maindhumvallaar theevinaiyai mudhalariya vallaarthaame.
152.meaning: He who reclines upon the couch of the hooded serpent, who grew wrathful so that the asura-king was slain, who passed between the marudam trees; the Lord who rests in lotus-eyed slumber at Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam, set amid water-fields with ponds; Kalikanri, master of the rutting dark elephant and victorious in war, composed these sweet songs. Those who can master these ten verses firmly shall be able to root out their evil karma at its very source.

153.translit:
naNNaadha vaaLavuNa riDaippukku, vaanavaraip
peNNaagi yamudhooTTum perumaanaar, maruviniya
thaNNaarndha kaDanmallaith thalasayanath thuRaivaarai,
eNNaadhe yiruppaarai yiRaippozhudhu meNNoome.
153.meaning: The great Lord who entered amid the hostile sword-bearing asuras and, becoming a woman, fed the nectar to the celestials; who dwells at the sweetly pleasant, cool Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam - as for those who do not think upon Him, we shall not think of them even for a moment.

154.translit:
paarvaNNa maDamangai paninanmaa malarkkizhatthi,
neervaNNan maarvagatthi lirukkaiyaimun ninaindhavanoor,
kaarvaNNa mudhumunneerk kaDalmallaith thalasayanam,
aareNNum nenjuDaiyaa ravaremmai yaaLvaare.
154.meaning: The earth-hued tender lady (Bhudevi), and the lady of the cool fine flower (Lakshmi) - their abode is on the chest of the ocean-hued Lord; the town of Him whom they first hold in mind is Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam beside the dark-hued ancient sea. Those who have a heart that contemplates this place - they shall rule over us.

155.translit:
enatthi nuruvaagi nilamangai yezhilkoNDaan,
vaanatthi lavarmuRaiyaal magizhndhetthi valangoLLa,
kaanatthin kaDalmallaith thalasayanath thuRaiginRa,
GNaanatthi noLiyuruvai ninaivaaren naayagare.
155.meaning: He who took the form of a boar and won the beauty of the Earth-goddess; while in the heavens the gods, joyfully praising Him in due order, circumambulate Him; the radiant form of pure wisdom who dwells at Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam amid the groves - those who contemplate Him are my lords.

156.translit:
viNDaarai venRaavi vilanguNNa, melliyalaar
koNDaaDum mallagalam azhalera venjamatthuk
kaNDaarai, kaDalmallaith thalasayanath thuRaivaarai,
koNDaaDum nenjuDaiyaa ravarengaL kuladheyvame.
156.meaning: He who conquered His foes so that their lives were devoured by wild beasts, whose broad wrestler's chest the tender-natured ladies extol, who in fierce battle made it blaze; who dwells at Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam - those who have a heart that exalts Him are our family deity.

157.translit:
picchach chiRupeelich chamaNkuNDar mudhalaayor,
vichchaik kiRaiyennu mavviRaiyaip paNiyaadhe,
kachchik kiDandhavanoor kaDanmallaith thalasayanam,
nacchith thozhuvaarai nachchenRan nannenje.
157.meaning: Without bowing to that supreme Lord who is the Master of all knowledge, the Jain wretches with their small peacock feathers and their begging and others like them reject Him; but as for those who lovingly worship the town of Him who reclines at Kachi - Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam - cherish them, O my good heart.

158.translit:
pulankoLnidhik kuvaiyoDu puzhaikkaimaa kaLiRRinamum
nalangoLnava maNikkuvaiyum sumandhekkum naanRosindhu,
kalangaLiyang kummallaik kaDalmallaith thalasayanam,
valangoLmanath thaaravarai valangoLLen maDanenje.
158.meaning: Where ships, laden and bending low everywhere, carry heaps of glittering treasure that captivate the senses, droves of trunk-handed great elephants, and heaps of fine nine gems, and move about - at flourishing Mallai, Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam - those who have a heart that circumambulates it, go circumambulate them, O my tender heart.

159.translit:
panjich chiRukoozhai yuruvaagi, maruvaadha
vanjappeN nanjuNDa aNNalmun naNNaadha,
kanjaik kaDandhavanoor kaDanmallaith thalasayanam,
nenjil thozhuvaaraith thozhuvaayen thooynenje.
159.meaning: The Lord who, in the form of a small one with cotton-soft hair, drank the poison of the deceitful woman (Putana) who came in enmity; whose town - of Him who overcame Kamsa who would not draw near - is Tirukkadalmallai's Thalasayanam; worship those who worship it in their hearts, O my pure heart.

160.translit:
sezhuneer malarkkamalam tiraiyunda vanpagaTTaal,
uzhuneer vayaluzhava ruzhappinmun pizhaittezhunda,
kazhuneer kadikamazhum kadanmallait talasayanam,
tozhuneer manattavarait tozhuvaayen tooyanenjE.
160.meaning: At Tirukkadalmallai (the shore-shrine of Talasayanam), where the rich-watered lotus blossoms are pushed about by the waves, and the lily-flowers spread their sweet fragrance, having escaped the trampling of the strong oxen and the ploughing of the farmers in the water-soaked fields. O my pure heart, worship those whose minds are devoted to worshipping this place.

161.translit:
piNangaLidu kaadadanuL nadamaadu piNYNYaganOdu,
iNangutiruc chakkarattem perumaanaark kidam, visumbil
kaNangaLiyang kummallaik kadanmallait talasayanam,
vaNangumanat taaravarai vaNangenran madanenjE.
161.meaning: The abode of our Lord, who bears the beautiful divine discus, together with Pinnyagan (Shiva) who dances in the cremation-ground where corpses are laid, is Tirukkadalmallai, the shore-shrine of Talasayanam, where hosts of celestials move about in the sky. O my tender heart, worship those whose minds bow down to that place.

162.translit:
kadikamazhu nedumaRukil kadalmallait talasayanattu,
adigaLadi yEninaiyu madiyavargaL tammadiyaan,
vadikoLnedu vElvalavan kalikanRi yolivallaar,
mudikoLnedu mannavartam mudalvarmuda laavaarE.
162.meaning: At Tirukkadalmallai (Talasayanam), the shore-shrine with long, fragrant streets, those who can recite the songs of Kalikanri (Tirumangai Alwar) — wielder of the long, sharp spear — who is the servant of the servants who ever meditate on the very feet of the Lord, they will become the foremost among the great crowned kings.

163.translit:
tivaLumveN matipOl tirumukat tarivai sezhungada lamudiniR piRanda avaLum,
ninnaagat tiruppadu maRindum aagilu maasaivi daaLaal,
kuvaLaiyang kaNNi kolliyam paavai sollunin taaLnayan tirunda ivaLai,
un manattaa lenninaind tirundaay idaventai yendai piraanE.
163.meaning: Even knowing that She (Lakshmi), born from the nectar of the rich ocean, whose holy face is like the glittering white moon, abides upon Your chest, this girl does not give up her longing. O my Lord at Idaventai, this maiden — lovely as the doll of Kolli, with eyes like the kuvalai-flower — speaks only of and yearns for Your feet. What have You been thinking of her in Your mind?

164.translit:
tuLampadu muRuval tOzhiyark karuLaaL tuNaimulai saandukoN daNiyaaL,
kuLampadu kuvaLaik kaNNiNai yezhutaaL kOlanan malarkkuzhaR kaNiyaaL,
vaLampadu munneer vaiyamun naLanda, maalennum maalina mozhiyaaL,
iLampadi yivaluk kenninaind tirundaay idaventai yendai piraanE.
164.meaning: She does not grace her friends with even a faint smile; she does not adorn her twin breasts with sandal-paste; she does not paint her pair of kuvalai-flower eyes; she does not deck her lovely flower-laden tresses. She speaks only the deluding words, calling out 'Tirumal' — He who long ago measured the world girdled by the bounteous ocean. O my Lord at Idaventai, what have You been thinking of this tender young girl?

165.translit:
saandamum pooNum sandanak kuzhambum tadamulaik kaNiyilum tazhalaam,
pOndaveN tingaL kadirsuda meliyum porukadal pulambilum pulambum,
maantaLir mEni vaNNamum ponnaam vaLaigaLum iRainillaa,
entan Endizhai yivaluk kenninaind tirundaay idaventai yendai piraanE.
165.meaning: Sandal, ornaments, and sandal-paste, even when adorning her broad breasts, turn to fire; she withers when the rays of the risen white moon scorch her, and she laments when the surging sea laments. Her mango-tender complexion has paled to the colour of gold, and her bangles will not stay upon her wrists. O my Lord at Idaventai, what have You been thinking of this jewel-decked girl of mine?

166.translit:
oozhiyil peridaal naazhigai. ennum oNsudar tuyinRadaal ennum,
aazhiyum pulambum. anRilu muRangaa tenRalum teeyiniR kodidaam,
tOzhiyO. ennum tuNaimulai yarakkum sollumi nenseykEn ennum,
Ezhaiyen ponnuk kenninaind tirundaay idaventai yendai piraanE.
166.meaning: She says 'A single moment is longer than an aeon'; she says 'The bright sun has gone to sleep'; the sea wails too, the anril-bird does not sleep, and the breeze is crueller than fire. She cries 'O my friend!', she wastes away her twin breasts, she says 'Tell me, what shall I do?'. O my Lord at Idaventai, what have You been thinking of this poor golden girl of mine?

167.translit:
Odilum unpE ranRimaR ROdaaL urugumnin tiruvuru ninaindu,
kaadanmai peridu kaiyaRa vudaiyaL kayalnedung kaNtuyil maRandaaL,
pEdaiyEn pEdai piLLaimai peridu teLLiyaL vaLLinuN marungul,
Edalar munnaa enninaind tirundaay idaventai yendai piraanE.
167.meaning: Even when she recites, she utters nothing but Your name; she melts away thinking of Your sacred form. Her love is great and she is utterly helpless; she has forgotten sleep in her long carp-like eyes. The innocence of this foolish girl of foolish me is great, yet she is clear-minded, with a slender creeper-like waist. O my Lord at Idaventai, in front of strangers, what have You been thinking of her?

168.translit:
tankudik kEdum takkavaa ninaiyaaL tadangadal nudangeyi lilangai,
vankudi madanga vaaLamar tolaitta vaarttaikET TinpuRum mayangum,
minkodi marungul surungamEl nerungi menmulai ponpayan tirunda,
enkodi yivaluk kenninaind tirundaay idaventai yendai piraanE.
168.meaning: She does not think at all of what is fitting for her own family's honour; she rejoices and swoons on hearing the tale of how, with the sword, You destroyed the mighty households of Lanka of the swaying ramparts, girt by the broad sea. Her waist, slender as a streak of lightning, grows thin, while her soft breasts above have turned pale as gold. O my Lord at Idaventai, what have You been thinking of this creeper-like girl of mine?

169.translit:
uLanganind tirukkum unnaiyE pitaRRum unakkanRi yenakkanbon RilaLaal,
vaLanganin pozhilsoozh maaliruNY chOlai maayanE! enRuvaay veruvum,
kaLanganin muRuval kaarigai peridu kavalaiyO davalamsErn tirunda,
iLanganin yivaluk kenninaind tirundaay idaventai yendai piraanE.
169.meaning: With her heart ripened in love she babbles only of You; she has no affection for anyone but You. Her mouth cries out in dread, 'O wondrous One of Maliruncholai, ringed by groves of ripe abundance!'. This maiden, with a smile like the ripe kalanga-fruit, is overcome, sunk in grief and sorrow. O my Lord at Idaventai, what have You been thinking of this tender unripe-fruit of a girl?

170.translit:
alangezhu tadakkai yaayanvaa yaampaR kazhiyumaa lennuLLam ennum,
pulangezhu poruneerp puTkuzhi paadum pOdumO neermalaik kennum,
kulangezhu kolli kOmaLa vallik kodiyidai nedumazhaik kaNNi,
ilangezhil tOLik kenninaind tirundaay idaventai yendai piraanE.
170.meaning: She says, 'My heart is lost to the flute at the mouth of the cowherd of the lovely full hands'; she sings of Putkuzhi by the rich, abundant waters, and says, 'Shall we go to Tiruneermalai?'. This girl of noble lineage, lovely as the doll of Kolli, with a creeper-slender waist, and long rain-cloud eyes. O my Lord at Idaventai, what have You been thinking of this maiden of radiant, beautiful shoulders?

171.translit:
ponkulaam payalai pootttana mendOL porukayal kaNtuyil maRandaaL,
anbinaa lunmE laadaram peridu ivva Nanginuk kuRRanO yaRiyEn,
minkulaa marungul surungamEl nerungi veengiya vanamulai yaaLukku,
enkolaam kuRippi lenninaind tirundaay idaventai yendai piraanE.
171.meaning: A golden pallor has blossomed on my girl's shoulders, and her fighting carp-like eyes have forgotten sleep. Through love her devotion to You is great; I do not know what ailment has seized this lovely girl. Her waist, bright as lightning, grows thin while her lovely swelling breasts above press close. O my Lord at Idaventai, what is Your intent — what have You been thinking of her?

172.translit:
annamum meenum aamaiyum ariyum aayaem maayanE! aruLaay,
ennumin toNdark kinnaruL puriyum idaventai yendai piraanai,
mannumaa maada mangaiyar talaivan maanavEl kaliyanvaa yoligaL,
panniya panuval paaduvaar naaLum pazhavinai paRRaRup paarE!
172.meaning: To the devotees who plead, 'O our wondrous One who became the swan, the fish, the tortoise, and the man-lion — grace us!', the Lord of Idaventai grants His sweet grace. Those who daily sing the elaborated verses, the utterances of Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar), chief of the people of Mangai with its enduring great mansions, wielder of the mighty spear, will cut away the hold of their ancient karma.

173.translit:
tiripura moonRerit taanummaRRai malarmisai mElaya numviyappa,
muritirai maakadal pOlmuzhangi moovula kummuRai yaalvaNanga,
eriyana kEsara vaaLeyiRROdu iraNiya naaga miraNdukooRaa,
ariyuru vaamivar aarkkolenna aTTa puyagarat tEnenRaarE.
173.meaning: While Shiva who burnt the three cities and Brahma above, seated on the lotus, looked on in wonder, roaring like the great sea of breaking waves, so that all three worlds bowed in due order — He took the man-lion form, with a flaming mane and sword-like fangs, and split the body of Hiranya in two. When (the maidens) asked, 'Who can this be, in the form of the Lion-Man?', He said, 'I am He of Ashtabhujakaram.'

174.translit:
vendiRal veerareril veeraroppaar vEda muraittimai yOrvaNangum,
sendamizh paaduvaar taamvaNangum tEva rivarkol terikkamaaTTEn,
vandu kuRaLaru vaaynimirndu maavali vELviyil maNNaLanda,
andaNar pOnRivar aarkkolenna aTTa puyagarat tEnenRaarE.
174.meaning: He resembles a hero among fierce, valorous heroes; He is worshipped by the celestials who recite the Vedas; even those who sing chaste Tamil bow to Him — is He some god? I cannot tell. Coming as a tiny dwarf and then rising tall, He measured the earth at the sacrifice of Mahabali, like a Brahmin. When (they) asked, 'Who can this be?', He said, 'I am He of Ashtabhujakaram.'

175.translit:
sempo nilangu valangaivaaLi tiNsilai taNdodu sangamoLvaaL,
umba rirusuda raazhiyOdu kEdaga moNmalar paRRiyeRRE,
vembu sinattadal vEzhamveezha veNmarup ponRu paRittu,
iruNda ambudam pOnRivar aarkkolenna aTTa puyagarat tEnenRaarE.
175.meaning: In His hands shine the red-gold arrow, the stout bow, the mace, the conch, the bright sword, the discus blazing like the two luminaries of the heavens, the shield, and the bright lotus — how is this? Like a dark rain-cloud He stands, He who plucked out one white tusk when He made the cruel, raging, mighty elephant fall. When (they) asked, 'Who can this be?', He said, 'I am He of Ashtabhujakaram.'

176.translit:
manjuyar maamaNik kunRamEndi maamazhai kaattoru maayavaanai yanja,
adanmarup ponRuvaangum aayarkol maaya maRiyamaaTTEn,
venjuda raazhiyum sangumEndi vEdamu nOduvar neetivaanattu,
anjudar pOnRivar aarkkolenna aTTa puyagarat tEnenRaarE.
176.meaning: Lifting up the great jewel-hill that towers into the clouds, He sheltered (the cows) from the great rain; and so that one wondrous elephant should fear, He wrenched out one of its tusks — is He the cowherd? I cannot fathom the wonder. Bearing the fierce blazing discus and the conch, He of old recited the Veda. Like the bright luminaries of the righteous heavens — when (they) asked, 'Who can this be?', He said, 'I am He of Ashtabhujakaram.'

177.translit:
kalaigaLum vEdamum neetinoolum kaRpamum soRporuL taanum,
maRRai nilaigaLum vaanavarkkum piRarkkum neermaiyi naalaruL seydu,
neeNda malaigaLum maamaNi yummalarmEl mangaiyum sangamum tangukinRa,
alaikadal pOnRivar aarkkolenna aTTa puyagarat tEnenRaarE.
177.meaning: The arts, the Vedas, the books of righteousness, the ritual-codes, the words and their meanings, and all the other states of being — out of His grace He bestowed these on the celestials and on others. Like the surging sea in which dwell the long mountains, the great gems, the Lady upon the lotus (Lakshmi), and the conch — when (they) asked, 'Who can this be?', He said, 'I am He of Ashtabhujakaram.'

178.translit:
engnganum naamivar vaNNameNNil Edu maRikilam, Endizhaiyaar
sangum manamum niRaivumellaam tammana vaagap pugundu,
taamum pongu karungadal poovaikaayaap pOtavizh neelam punaindamEgam,
angnganam pOnRivar aarkkolenna aTTa puyagarat tEnenRaarE.
178.meaning: However we reckon His hue, we cannot know it at all. He entered and took as His own the conches (bangles), the hearts, and all the composure of the jewel-decked women. He Himself is like the surging dark sea, the poovai-bird, the kaaya-blossom, the opening neelam-flower, and the cloud adorned (with all these dark hues). When (they) asked, 'Who can this be, so like all these?', He said, 'I am He of Ashtabhujakaram.'

179.translit:
muzhusivaN daadiya taNduzhaayin moymmalark kaNNiyum,
mEniyanjaan tizhisiya kOla mirundavaaRum engnganaNY chollugEn. Ovinallaar,
ezhudiya taamarai yannakaNNum Endezhi laagamum tOLumvaayum,
azhagiya taamivar aarkkolenna aTTa puyagarat tEnenRaarE.
179.meaning: The garland of dense, cool tulasi blossoms in which the bees plunge and revel, and the beautiful way His body is anointed with sandal-paste — how can I describe them? His eyes are like lotuses painted by master artists, and His radiant chest, His shoulders, and His mouth are all beautiful. When (they) asked, 'Who can this be?', He said, 'I am He of Ashtabhujakaram.'

180.translit:
meeviyep paalum viNNoor vaNanga veeda muraippar mun neer madandai deevi,
ap paaladhir sangam ippaal chakkaram maRRivar vaNNam eNNil,
kaaviyoppaar kadaleeyum oppaar kaNNum vadivum nediyaraay,
en aaviyoppaar ivar aarkkol enna atta puyagarat teen enRaarE.
180.meaning: He stands worshipped on every side by the celestials, who recite the Vedas; the goddess of the deep ocean is His consort. On one side resounds the conch, on the other side the discus — if one considers the hue of these lords, they resemble the blue waterlily and they resemble the ocean too; tall in eye and form. When I asked, 'Who can these be, who seem like my very life?' He answered, 'I am the Lord of Ashtabhujakaram.'

181.translit:
tanjam ivarkken vaLaiyum nillaa nenjamum tammadhE sindhitteRkku,
vanji marungul nerunga nokki vaay tiRandhonRu paNitthadhuNdu,
nanjam udaitthivar nokkum nokkam naan ivar tammai aRiya maatteen,
anjuvan maRRivar aarkkol enna atta puyagarat teen enRaarE.
181.meaning: My bangles will not stay, and even my heart is His; for me who pondered thus, He is the refuge. Gazing closely at my slender vine-like waist, He opened His mouth and said something to me. Poison is in the glance with which these eyes look; I cannot fathom Him, and I am afraid. When I asked, 'Who can these be?' He answered, 'I am the Lord of Ashtabhujakaram.'

182.translit:
mannavan toNdaiyar kOn vaNangum neeL mudi maalai vayiramEgan,
tan vali tan pugazh soozhndha kachchi atta puyagarat taadhi tannai,
kanni nan maa madhiL mangai vEndhan kaamaru seerk kali kanRi,
kunRaa innisaiyaal sonna senjol maalai EttavallaarkkidamvaikundhamE.
182.meaning: The king Tondaiyar-kon with his tall crown bows to Him — He, Vajramegha, is the primordial Lord of Ashtabhujakaram in Kanchi, encircled by His own might and His own glory. Kaliyan, lord of Mangai with its fine virgin ramparts, of cherished excellence, composed this garland of true words in unfailing sweet melody. For those able to praise it, Vaikuntha is their abode.

183.translit:
solluvan soRporuL taan avaiyaay suvai ooRu oli naaRRamum tORRamumaay,
nallaran naanmugan naaraNanukkidam taan tadam soozhndhazhagaaya kachchi,
pallavan villavan enRulagil palaraay pala vEndhar vaNangu kazhaR pallavan,
mallaiyar kOn paNindha paramEchchura viNNagaramadhuvE.
183.meaning: I shall speak: He is the words and their meaning, taste, touch, sound, smell, and form. The abode of the good Hara, of the four-faced Brahma, and of Narayana is beautiful Kanchi, surrounded by tanks. The Pallava king — called Pallava and Villava in the world, before whose anklet-feet many kings bow — the lord of Mallai bowed to it: that is Parameshvara-vinnagaram.

184.translit:
kaar mannu neeL visumbum kadalum sudarum nilanum malaiyum,
tan undhit taar mannu taamaraik kaNNanidam tada maa madhiL soozhndhazhagaaya kachchi,
tEr mannu tennavanai munaiyil seruvil tiRal vaattiya tiN silaiyOn,
paar mannu pallavar kOn paNindha paramEchchura viNNagaramadhuvE.
184.meaning: The cloud-dwelling vast sky, the ocean, the luminaries, the earth, and the mountains — all rest upon the navel-lotus of the lotus-eyed Lord; His abode is beautiful Kanchi, encircled by great broad ramparts. The Pallava king of strong bow, who on the battlefield crushed the chariot-borne Pandya's strength, and who reigns over the earth, bowed to it: that is Parameshvara-vinnagaram.

185.translit:
uran taru mel aNaip paLLi koNdaan oru kaal munnam maa uruvaay kadaluL,
varan tarum maa maNi vaNNanidam maNi maadangaL soozhndhazhagaaya kachchi,
nirandhavar maNNaiyil puN nugar vEl nedu vaayil ugach seruvil munanaaL,
parandhavan pallavar kOn paNindha paramEchchura viNNagaramadhuvE.
185.meaning: He who reclines upon the soft, strong serpent-couch, who once long ago took the form of a great horse (fish) in the ocean and granted boons — that gem-hued Lord's abode is beautiful Kanchi, encircled by jeweled mansions. The Pallava king who in olden days, his long wound-drinking spear breaking the foe at Mannai, spread wide in battle, bowed to it: that is Parameshvara-vinnagaram.

186.translit:
aNdamum eN disaiyum nilanum alai neerodu vaan eri kaal mudhalaa uNdavan,
endhai piraan adhidam oLi maadangaL soozhndhazhagaaya kachchi,
viNdavar iNdaik kuzhaamudanE viraindhaar iriyas seruvil munaindhu,
paNdoru kaal vaLaitthaan paNindha paramEchchura viNNagaramadhuvE.
186.meaning: He who swallowed the cosmic egg, the eight directions, the earth, the surging waters, sky, fire, and wind — my father and Lord — His abode is beautiful Kanchi, encircled by shining mansions. The Pallava king who once long ago, charging in battle so that the massed throngs of foes fled in haste, bent his bow, bowed to it: that is Parameshvara-vinnagaram.

187.translit:
toombudaith tiN kai van taaL kaLiRRin tuyar teerttu aravam veruva,
munanaaL poom punal poygai pukkaanavanukkidam taan tadam soozhndhazhagaaya kachchi,
tEm pozhil kunRu eyil tennavanait tisaippas seru mEl viyandhu anRu senRa,
paambudaip pallavar kOn paNindha paramEchchura viNNagaramadhuvE.
187.meaning: He who relieved the suffering of the elephant with its trunk-like strong legs, and once long ago entered the flowered pool to make the crocodile-serpent tremble — His abode is beautiful Kanchi, surrounded by tanks. The Pallava king of the serpent-banner, who in that day went exulting into battle to confound the Pandya of honey-groves and hill-forts, bowed to it: that is Parameshvara-vinnagaram.

188.translit:
tiN padaik kOL ariyin uruvaay tiRalOn agalam seruvil munanaaL,
puN padap pOzhndha piraanadhidam poru maadangaL soozhndhazhagaaya kachchi,
veN kudai neezhal seng kOl nadappa idai vel kodi vEl padai mun uyartthа,
paNbudaip pallavar kOn paNindha paramEchchura viNNagaramadhuvE.
188.meaning: He who took the form of the fierce lion-beast and long ago in battle clove open the chest of the mighty one (Hiranya), wounding him — that Lord's abode is beautiful Kanchi, encircled by lofty mansions. The virtuous Pallava king, beneath whose white parasol's shade the just scepter prevails, who raised aloft the victorious-bannered spear-army before him, bowed to it: that is Parameshvara-vinnagaram.

189.translit:
ilagiya neeN mudi maavali tan peru vELviyil maaN uruvaay munanaaL,
salamodu maa nilang koNdavanukkidam taan tadam soozhndhazhagaaya kachchi,
ulagudai mannavan tennavanaik kanni maa madhiL soozh karuvoor veruva,
pala padai saaya venRaan paNindha paramEchchura viNNagaramadhuvE.
189.meaning: He who long ago, as a dwarf-Brahmin at the great sacrifice of Mahabali of the shining tall crown, took the vast earth with water poured — His abode is beautiful Kanchi, surrounded by tanks. The Pallava king, lord of the earth, who conquered so that the many armies fell and Karuvur with its virgin great ramparts trembled before the Pandya, bowed to it: that is Parameshvara-vinnagaram.

190.translit:
kudaith tiRal mannavanaay oru kaal kurangaip padaiyaa,
malaiyaal kadalai adaitthavan endhai piraanadhidam maNi maadangaL soozhndhazhagaaya kachchi,
vidaith tiRal villavan nen meliyil veruvas seru vEl valang kaip pidittha,
padaith tiRal pallavar kOn paNindha paramEchchura viNNagaramadhuvE.
190.meaning: He who once, as a king of parasol-might, made an army of monkeys and dammed the ocean with mountains — my father and Lord — His abode is beautiful Kanchi, encircled by jeweled mansions. The Pallava king of army-might, who grasped the battle-spear in his right hand so that the bull-strong Chera (Villavan) weakened and trembled at Nen(mali), bowed to it: that is Parameshvara-vinnagaram.

191.translit:
piRaiyudai vaaL nudhal pinnai tiRattu mun oru kaal seruvil urumin,
maRaiyudai maal vidai Ezh adartthaaRkidam taan tadam soozhndhazhagaaya kachchi,
kaRaiyudai vaaL maRa mannarkkedak kadal pOla muzhangum kural kaduvaay,
paRaiyudaip pallavar kOn paNindha paramEchchura viNNagaramadhuvE.
191.meaning: He who, for the sake of Nappinnai of the crescent-bright forehead, once long ago in battle subdued the seven great bulls like a thunderbolt — His abode is beautiful Kanchi, surrounded by tanks. The Pallava king of the war-drum, whose harsh roaring voice thunders like the sea so that the stained-sworded warrior-kings perish, bowed to it: that is Parameshvara-vinnagaram.

192.translit:
paar mannu tol pugazhp pallavar kOn paNindha paramEchchura viNNagar mEl,
kaar mannu neeL vayal mangaiyar tan talaivan kali kanRi kunRaadhuraittha,
seer mannu sendhamizh maalaivallaart tiru maa magaL tan aruLaal,
ulagil tEr mannaraay oli maa kadal soozh sezhu neer ulagaaNdu tigazhvargaLE.
192.meaning: Upon Parameshvara-vinnagaram, to which the Pallava king of ancient enduring glory bowed, Kaliyan, lord of Mangai of cloud-girt long fields, composed this faultless garland of fine pure Tamil. Those able to master it, by the grace of Tiru, the great Goddess, shall reign in this world as chariot-mounted kings and shine, ruling the fertile-watered land girt by the resounding great sea.

193.translit:
manjaadu varaiyEzhum kadalgaL Ezhum vaanagamum maNNagamum maRRum ellaam,
enjaamal vayiRRadakki aalin mElOr iLan taLiril kaN vaLarndha eesan tannai,
tunjaa neer vaLanjsurakkum peNNait tenpaal tooya naan maRaiyaaLar sOmuch seyya,
senjaali viLai vayaluL tigazhndhu tOnRum tirukkOvaloor adhanuL kaNdEn naanE.
193.meaning: He who, without remainder, held within His belly the seven cloud-roaming mountains, the seven seas, the heavens, the earth, and all else, and slept as a child upon a tender banyan-leaf — that Lord, on the south bank of the Pennai whose unceasing waters yield abundance, where pure reciters of the four Vedas perform the Soma sacrifice, shining amid red-paddy fields — Him I beheld within Tirukkovalur.

194.translit:
kondhalarndha narun tuzhaay saandham toobam deepam koNdamarart tozhap paNang koL paambil,
sandhaNi men mulai malaraaL taraNi mangai taam iruvar adi varudum tanmaiyaanai,
vandhanai seydhu isai EzhaaRu angam aindhu vaLar vELvi naan maRaigaL moonRu teeyum,
sindhanai sey iru pozhudhum onRum selvat tirukkOvaloor adhanuL kaNdEn naanE.
194.meaning: He of the nature whose feet the two — the flower-dwelling Lakshmi of soft sandal-anointed breasts and the Earth-goddess — gently press, while the immortals worship Him with clustered fragrant tulasi, sandal, incense, and lamp, He resting on the hooded serpent. In wealthy Tirukkovalur, where in both twilights the worshippers offer reverence with the seven notes, the six limbs, the five (rites), the growing sacrifices, the four Vedas, the three fires — meditating as one — Him I beheld.

195.translit:
kozhundhalaru malarchchOlaik kuzhaang koL poygaik kOL mudhalai vaaL eyiRRuk koNdaRkeLgi,
azhundhiya maa kaLiRRinuk anRu aazhi Endhi andharamE varat tOnRi aruL seydhaanai,
ezhundha malark karu neelam irundhil kaatta irum punnai muttarumbis sempon kaatta,
sezhun tada neerk kamalam tee vigaipOl kaattum tirukkOvaloor adhanuL kaNdEn naanE.
195.meaning: He who, for the great elephant sinking, seized by the sword-tusked fierce crocodile of the grove-girt pool, that day appeared bearing the discus, coming through the very sky, and granted grace — in Tirukkovalur, where the risen blue lotuses show as darkness, the great punnai trees bud forth pearls and show as red gold, and the lotus in rich broad waters shows like a flaming torch — Him I beheld.

196.translit:
taangarum pOr maali padap paRavai oorndhu taraadalatthOr kuRai mudittha tanmaiyaanai,
aang arumbik kaNNeer sOrndhu anbu koorum adiyavargaTk aaramudham aanaan tannai,
kOng arumbu surapunnai kuravaar sOlaik kuzhaa vari vaNd isai paadum paadal kEttu,
teeng arumbu kaN vaLarum kazhani soozhndha tirukkOvaloor adhanuL kaNdEn naanE.
196.meaning: He of the nature who, riding the bird (Garuda), slew the unbearable warrior Mali in battle and fulfilled the wants of the people of the earth, who became sweet nectar to the devotees whose love overflows there with welling tears — in Tirukkovalur, girt by fields of sweet sugarcane that sleep, where the kongu buds, the surapunnai and kuravu groves resound with the song of swarming striped bees — Him I beheld.

197.translit:
kaRai vaLar vEl karan mudhalaak kavandhan vaali kaNai onRi naal madiya ilangai tannuL,
piRai eyiRRu vaaL arakkar sEnai ellaam perun tagaiyOd udan tuNittha pemmaan tannai,
maRai vaLarap pugazh vaLara maadan tORum maNdabamoN toLi anaitthum vaaramOdha,
siRai aNaindha pozhil aNaindha tenRal veesum tirukkOvaloor adhanuL kaNdEn naanE.
197.meaning: The Lord who, with His unfailing arrows, felled Khara of the blood-fed spear, Kabandha, and Vali, and in Lanka cut down with their great chief the whole army of crescent-tusked sword-wielding demons — in Tirukkovalur, where the Vedas flourish and glory grows, where in every mansion and bright pavilion the sacred hymns are chanted in chorus, and the breeze blows wafting through the groves it has reached — Him I beheld.

198.translit:
uriyaarndha naru veNNeyy oLiyaal senRangu uNdaanaik kaNdu aaychchi uralOd aarkka,
taRiyaarndha karung kaLiRE pOla ninRu tadang kaNgaL pani malgum tanmaiyaanai,
veRiyaarndha malar magaL naa mangaiyOdu, viyan kalai eN tOLinaaL viLangu,
selvach seRiyaarndha maNi maadam tigazhndhu tOnRum tirukkOvaloor adhanuL kaNdEn naanE.
198.meaning: He who slipped in stealthily and ate the fragrant white butter hung in the pot, and when the cowherd-woman bound Him to the mortar, stood like a dark elephant tethered to a post, His broad eyes welling with tears — He of that nature, with the fragrant flower-goddess Lakshmi, with Sarasvati the goddess of speech, and with the eight-armed one resplendent in vast learning — in Tirukkovalur, shining with wealthy close-set jeweled mansions — Him I beheld.

199.translit:
irung kaim maa karimunindhu pariyaik keeRi ina vidaigaL Ezh adartthu marudham saaytthu,
varum sagadam iRa udhaitthu mallai attu vanjaganjsey kanjanukku nanjaanaanai,
karung kamugu pasum paaLai veN mutt eenRu kaay ellaam maragadhamaayp pavaLam kaatta,
serundhi miga mottalartthum tEn koL sOlaith tirukkOvaloor adhanuL kaNdEn naanE.
199.meaning: He who in wrath slew the great elephant of the mighty trunk, tore apart the horse (Kesi), subdued the seven bulls, felled the marudam trees, kicked the oncoming cart to break it, slew the wrestlers, and became poison to the treacherous Kamsa — in Tirukkovalur, where the dark areca palms yield white pearls from their fresh sheaths, the unripe fruits all show as emeralds and coral, and the serunti trees burst abundantly into bud in honey-laden groves — Him I beheld.

20.translit:
iravu koorndhu iruL perukiya varai muzhai irum pasiyadhu koora,
aravam aavikkum agan pozhil thazhuviya aru varai imayaththu,
paraman aadhi em pani mugil vaNNan endru eNNi nindru imaiyOrgaL,
biramanOdu sendru adi thozhum perundhagai piridhi sendru adai nenjE.
20.meaning: In the deep night when darkness thickens, in the great mountain caves where dire hunger grows and serpents breathe out their hissing, around the lofty hard-to-reach Himalaya embraced by vast groves — there the celestials, deeming Him the Supreme One, the Primordial, our Lord dark like a cool rain-cloud, go together with Brahma and worship His feet. O my heart, go and reach that Pridhi (Pirudhi) of the great noble Lord.

21.translit:
Odhi aayira naamangaL uNarndhavarkku uru thuyar adaiyaamal,
Edham indri nindru aruLum nam perundhagai irundha nal imayaththu,
thaadhu malgiya piNdi viNdu alarginra thazhal purai ezhil nOkki,
pEdhai vaNdugaL eri ena veruvaru piridhi sendru adai nenjE.
21.meaning: To those who recite and realize His thousand names, our great noble Lord stands and graces them flawlessly so that no grievous sorrow befalls them; He abides in the good Himalaya. There, gazing at the beauty of the pollen-laden asoka blossoms bursting open like blazing fire, the foolish bees take fright as if at flame. O my heart, go and reach that Pridhi.

22.translit:
kariya maa mugiR padalangaL kidandhu avai muzhangida, kaLiru endru
periya maasuNam varai ena peyar tharu piridhi em perumaanai,
vari koL vaNdu arai pai pozhil mangaiyar kaliyanadhu oli maalai,
ariya in isai paadu nal adiyavarkku aru vinai adaiyaavE.
