asaivaru amarar adiyiNai vaNanga alaikadal thuyindra am maanai,
thisaimugan anaiyor naangainan naduvuL sembon sey koyilin uLLE,
uyarmaNi maguda soodi nindraanaik kaNdukoNdu uyndhozhindhEnE.
326.meaning: Him who, as the faultless dwarf, measured out the earth at Mahabali's sacrifice, that Lord who slept upon the surging ocean while the steadfast immortals bowed at His twin feet — within the temple wrought of pure gold in the fair midst of Nangur of Brahmins like the four-faced Brahma, I beheld Him standing crowned with a lofty jewelled diadem, and was saved.

327.translit:
thee manath tharakkar thiral azhiththavanE endru sendru adaindhavar thamakku,
thaay manath thirangi aruLinaik kodukkum thayaradhan madhalaiyai sayamE,
thEmalarp pozhil soozh naangainan naduvuL sembon sey koyilin uLLE,
kaamanaip payandhaan thannai naan adiyEn kaNdukoNdu uyndhozhindhEnE.
327.meaning: To those who come and take refuge crying 'O You who destroyed the might of the evil-minded demons!', He gives His grace, melting with a mother's tenderness — the son of Dasharatha, the very victory, who fathered Kama (Manmatha) — within the temple wrought of pure gold in the fair midst of Nangur ringed with groves of honeyed blossoms, I, His servant, beheld Him and was saved.

328.translit:
mallaimaa munneer adharpada malaiyaal aNaisey magizhndhavan thannai,
kallin meedhu iyandra kadimadhiL ilangai kalanga Or vaaLi thottaanai,
selva naanmaraiyor naangainan naduvuL sembon sey koyilin uLLE,
alli maamalaraaL thannodum adiyEn kaNdukoNdu allal theerndhEnE.
328.meaning: Him who joyfully built a causeway of mountains to make a path across the vast tumultuous ocean, who shot a single arrow to shatter Lanka with its strong walls raised upon rock — within the temple wrought of pure gold in the fair midst of Nangur of prosperous Brahmins of the four Vedas, together with the great lotus-seated Lakshmi, I His servant beheld Him and my sorrows were ended.

329.translit:
venjina kaLiRum villodu mallum veguNd iruththu adarththavan thannai,
kanjanaik kaayndha kaaLai am maanaik karumugil thirunirath thavanai,
senjol naanmaraiyor naangainan naduvuL sembon sey koyilin uLLE,
anjana kundram nindradhop paanaik kaNdukoNdu allal theerndhEnE.
329.meaning: Him who in wrath broke and crushed the furious elephant, the bow and the wrestlers, the youthful Lord who destroyed Kamsa, hued in the sacred color of a dark cloud — within the temple wrought of pure gold in the fair midst of Nangur of Brahmins of the four Vedas of true speech, I beheld Him standing like a mountain of collyrium, and my sorrows were ended.

330.translit:
andriya vaaNan aayiram thoLum thuNiya andru aazhi thottaanai,
min thigazh kudumi vEngadamalaimEl mEviya vEda nal viLakkai,
then thisaith thiladham anaiyavar naangaich sembon sey koyilin uLLE,
mandradhu poliya magizhndhu nindraanai vaNanginaan vaazhndhozhindhEnE.
330.meaning: Him who once hurled His discus to sever the thousand shoulders of the hostile Bana, the good lamp of the Vedas who abides upon the lightning-bright peak of Venkatam hill — within the temple wrought of pure gold in Nangur whose people are like the ornamental mark (tilaka) of the southern land, I beheld Him standing joyfully to grace the assembly hall, bowed to Him and lived, redeemed.

331.translit:
kaLangani vaNNaa. kaNNanE endran kaarmugilE ena ninaindhittu,
uLam kanindhu irukku madiyavar thangaL uLLaththuL ooriya thEnai,
theLindha naanmaraiyor naangai nannaduvuL sembon sey koyilin uLLE,
vaLangoL pErinbam manni nindraanai vaNanginaan vaazhndhozhindhEnE.
331.meaning: He is the honey that wells up within the hearts of His devotees who dwell with melting hearts, thinking 'O You of the dark velvet-fruit hue! O Kannan! O my dark cloud!' — within the temple wrought of pure gold in the fair midst of Nangur of clear-minded Brahmins of the four Vedas, I beheld Him standing firm as abundant great bliss, bowed to Him and lived, redeemed.

332.translit:
thEnamar solai naangainan naduvuL sembon sey koyilin uLLE,
vaanavar konaik kaNdamai sollum mangaiyaar vaaTkali kandri,
oonamil paadal ondrod ondrum ozhivindri katruvallaargaL,
maana veN kudaikkeezh vaiyaga maaNdu vaanavar aaguvar magizhndhE.
332.meaning: Kaliyan of Tirumangai, lord of the sword, who tells how he beheld the King of the celestials within the temple wrought of pure gold in the fair midst of Nangur with its honey-laden groves — those who can learn without omission all ten of these flawless songs will rule the earth beneath a great white umbrella, and then joyfully become celestials themselves.

333.translit:
maatrarasar maNimudiyum thiralum thEsum matravar tham kaadhalimaar kuzhaiyum, thandhai
kaal thaLaiyum udankazhala vandhu thondrik kadhanaagam kaaththaLiththa kaNNar kaNdeer,
nootridazh koL aravindham nuzhaindha paLLath iLangamugin mudhupaaLai paguvaay naNdin,
sEtraLaiyil veNmuththam sindhu naangoorth thiruththetri ambalaththen sengaN maalE.
333.meaning: Behold, He is Kannan who appeared so that the jewelled crowns, might and glory of rival kings, the ear-ornaments of their beloved queens, and the fetters on His father's feet all fell away together, and who protected the furious serpent (Kaliya). He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tiruttetriyambalam shrine at Nangur, where in the muddy holes the crab scatters white pearls from the old spathe of the young areca palm in the hollow where hundred-petalled lotuses sink.

334.translit:
pottodith thoL madamagaL than vadivukoNda pollaadha vanpEychchi kongai vaangi,
petreduththa thaay pola maduppa aarum pENaa nanjuNdu ugandha piLLai kaNdeer,
nel thoduththa malar neelam niraindha soozhal irunjiraiya vaNdoliyum nedungaNaar tham,
sittradimEl silamboliyum mizhattru naangoorth thiruththetri ambalaththen sengaN maalE.
334.meaning: Behold, He is the Child who gladly drank the poison none would touch, seizing the breast of the wicked, cruel demoness (Putana) who had taken the form of a tender maiden with golden-bangled arms, as she suckled Him like a doting mother. He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tiruttetriyambalam shrine at Nangur, where amid expanses thick with blue lilies strung among the paddy, the hum of broad-winged bees and the ringing of anklets on the little feet of wide-eyed women resound.

335.translit:
padaladaindha sirukurambai nuzhaindhu pukkup pasu veNNey padhamaarap paNNai muttrum,
adal adarththa vERkaNNaar thokkai patri alandhalaimai seydhuzhalu maiyan kaNdeer,
madaleduththa nedun thengin pazhangal veezha maanganigaL thiratturuttaa varuneerp ponni,
thidaleduththu malar sumandhang izhiyu naangoorth thiruththetri ambalaththen sengaN maalE.
335.meaning: Behold, He is the Lord who, slipping into the little thatched huts, ate fresh butter to His content throughout the cowherd settlement, and who roamed making mischief, grasping the garments of the sharp spear-eyed women. He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tiruttetriyambalam shrine at Nangur, where the river Ponni (Kaveri) rises over the sandbanks bearing flowers, as the ripe fruits drop from the tall coconut palms and the waters roll the mango fruits together.

336.translit:
vaaraarum mulaimadavaaL pinnaikk aagi vaLaimaruppin kadunjinaththu van thaaL aarndha,
kaaraar thiN vidaiyadarththu vadhuvai aaNda karumugilpol thiruniraththen kaNNar kaNdeer,
Eraarum malarp pozhilgaL thazhuvi engum ezhilmadhiyaik kaal thodaa viLangu sodhi,
seeraaru maNimaadam thigazhum naangoorth thiruththetri ambalaththen sengaN maalE.
336.meaning: Behold, He is my red-eyed Kannan of dark-cloud-like sacred hue, who for the sake of Nappinnai of the bodice-bound breasts subdued the dark, mighty, strong-legged bulls of curved horns and furious temper, and won her in marriage. He is my Tirumal of the Tiruttetriyambalam shrine at Nangur, where everywhere the radiant light shines so high it almost touches the lovely moon, embracing the beautiful flowering groves, with its splendid jewelled mansions gleaming.

337.translit:
kalai ilangu magalalgul kamalap paavai kadhir muththa veNNagaiyaaL karunga Naaychchi,
mulai ilangu oLimaNippooN vadamum thEyppa moovaadha varai nedunthoL moorththi kaNdeer,
malai ilangu niraichchandhi maada veedhi aadavarai madamozhiyaar mugaththu iraNdu
silai vilangi manam siraikoNd irukkum naangoorth thiruththetri ambalaththen sengaN maalE.
337.meaning: Behold, He is the Lord of ageless, mountain-like long shoulders, against which rubs the pearl-jewelled garland upon the breast of the dark-eyed cowherd-lady — the lotus-figured maiden of broad waist bright with raiment and of radiant pearl-white smile. He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tiruttetriyambalam shrine at Nangur, on whose mansion-lined streets at the cross-junctions the two bow-like brows on the faces of soft-spoken women capture and imprison the hearts of passing men.

338.translit:
thaanpolu mendrezhundhaan tharaNiyaaLan adhukaNdu thariththiruppaan arakkar thangaL,
konpolu mendrezhundhaan kundram anna irubadhuthoL udan thuNiththa oruvan kaNdeer,
maanpolu mennokkin seyya vaayaar maragatham pol madakkiLiyaik kaimEl koNdu,
thEnpolu menmazhalai payittrum naangoorth thiruththetri ambalaththen sengaN maalE.
338.meaning: Behold, He is the peerless One who at once severed the twenty mountain-like shoulders of the demon-king Ravana — who had risen up thinking himself a very ruler of the earth, that the watching demons might endure (under his rule). He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tiruttetriyambalam shrine at Nangur, where red-lipped women of gentle deer-like glances take the soft emerald-green parrots upon their hands and teach them honey-sweet baby-prattle.

339.translit:
pongilangu purinoolum tholum thaazhap pollaadha kuRaLuruvaay porundhaa vaaNan
mangalam sEr maraivELvi adhanuL pukku maNNagalam kuRaiyirandha maindhan kaNdeer,
kongalarndha malarkkuzhalaar kongai thoyndha kungumaththin kuzhambaLaindha kolan thannaal,
sengalangal veNmaNalmEl thavazhum naangoorth thirutetri ambalaththen sengaN maalE.
339.meaning: Behold, He is the mighty youth who, taking the form of an unsightly dwarf with the bright sacred thread and deerskin hanging low, entered the auspicious Vedic sacrifice of the unyielding Bana (Mahabali) and begged for the breadth of the earth. He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tiruttetriyambalam shrine at Nangur, who is smeared with the paste of saffron from the breasts of women with fragrant flower-decked tresses, and who creeps (as a child) upon the reddened white sand.

340.translit:
silambinidaich chiruparalpool periya meru tirukkuLambil kaNakaNappath tiruvaakaaram
kulunga, nilamadandai tanaiyidandu pulgik koottidaivaith taruLiyaveng koomaan kaNdeer,
ilangiyanaan maRaiyanaittu manga maaRum eezhisaiyum keeLvikaLu meNdik kengum,
silambiyanaR peruncelvam tikazhum naangoort tirutheRRi yambalatten cengaN maalee.
340.meaning: He who, with the great Mount Meru jingling in His sacred hoof like a tiny pebble within an anklet, His sacred form swaying, lifted up the Earth-maiden, embraced her and held her safe between His tusks — behold, He is our King. He is my lotus-eyed Tirumal of the Tirutherri Ambalam shrine at Nangur, where the resplendent four Vedas, the changing seven musical notes, and sacred learning spread everywhere amid flourishing great wealth.

341.translit:
eezhulagum taazhvaraiyu mengu moodi eNdisaiyu maNdalamum maNdi, aNdam
moozhaiyezhun taazhimigum oozhi veLLam munnagatti lodukkiyavem moorti kaNdeer,
oozhitoRu moozhitoRu muyarnda celvath toongiyanaan maRaiyanaittum taangu naavar,
seezhuyarnda maNimaadam tikazhum naangoort tirutheRRi yambalatten cengaN maalee.
341.meaning: He, our Lord, who at the great deluge when the flood-waters welled up bubbling and rose past the cosmic egg, covering the seven worlds, the low mountains, all eight directions and the spheres — He drew them all in and held them within His own belly; behold Him. He is my lotus-eyed Tirumal of the Tirutherri Ambalam at Nangur, gleaming with tall jewel-mansions, where dwell those who in age after age bear upon their tongues all the four Vedas that flourish with exalted, ever-abundant wealth.

342.translit:
seeraNinda maNimaadam tikazhum naangoort tirutheRRi yambalatten cengaN maalai,
kooraNinda veelvalavan aali naadan kodimaada mangaiyarkoon kuRaiya laaLi
paaraNinda tolpukazhaan kaliyan conna paamaalai yivaiyaindu maindum vallaar,
seeraNinda vulagattu manna raagich seeNvisumbil vaanavaraay tikazhvar taamee.
342.meaning: Upon my lotus-eyed Tirumal of the Tirutherri Ambalam at Nangur, gleaming with splendid jewel-mansions, Kaliyan — the wielder of the sharp victorious spear, lord of the Ali land, king of the Mangai folk of banner-crowned mansions, lord of Kuraiyalur, of ancient fame that adorns the whole earth — sang this garland of verses. Those who can master all these ten verses, five and five, will themselves become kings in this world adorned with glory and then shine as celestials in the far high heavens.

343.translit:
toombudaip panaikkai veezham tuyarkeduth taruLi, mannu
kaambudaik kunDra meendik kadumazhai kaatta endai,
poompunal ponni muRRum pukundupon varaNda, engum
teempozhil kamazhum naangoort tirumaNik koodath taanee.
343.meaning: My Father, who removed the suffering of the elephant whose hollow-trunked tube-like trunk was great, and who lifted up the bamboo-clad hill (Govardhana) and warded off the fierce rain — He dwells in the Tirumanikkudam shrine at Nangur, where the flower-strewn waters of the Ponni (Kaveri) flow in everywhere depositing gold, and where the honeyed groves spread their fragrance all around.

344.translit:
kavvaivaa Leyitru vanpeeyk katirmulai suvaittu, ilangai
vavviya idumbai teerak kadungaNai turanda endai,
kovvaivaay magaLir kongaik kungumam kazhuvip poonda,
theyvaneer kamazhum naangoort tirumaNik koodath taanee.
344.meaning: My Father, who sucked at the radiant breast of the cruel demoness (Putana) with her clamorous sword-like fangs, and who loosed His fierce arrows to end the misery of Lanka — He dwells in the Tirumanikkudam shrine at Nangur, fragrant with sacred waters that come having washed away the saffron paste from the breasts of women whose lips are red as the kovvai fruit.

345.translit:
maatozhil madangak chetru maRutiRa nadandu vantaaL
seetozhil sitaittup pinnai cevvitthooL puNarnda endai,
naattozhil maRaival laarkaL nayandaRam payanda vaNkait
theettozhil payilum naangoort tirumaNik koodath taanee.
345.meaning: My Father, who slew so that the great deed (of Kesi the horse-demon) ceased, who walked the other way, who destroyed the wild deed of the strong-legged bulls and so won and embraced the beautiful shoulders of Nappinnai — He dwells in the Tirumanikkudam shrine at Nangur, where those skilled in the Vedic recitation that is the work of the tongue, generous-handed and devoted to virtue, practise the fire-rituals.

346.translit:
taangarun chinattu vantaaL tadakkaimaa maruppu vaangi,
poonkurun dosittup puLvaay piLanderu tadarttha endai,
maanganee nugarnda mandi vanduvaN diriya
vaazhaitteenkani nugarum naangoort tirumaNikkoodath taanee.
346.meaning: My Father, who broke off the tusks of the great strong-legged elephant (Kuvalayapida) of unbearable rage, who snapped the kurunda tree in bloom, split open the beak of the bird-demon (Bakasura), and quelled the bulls — He dwells in the Tirumanikkudam shrine at Nangur, where the monkeys, having tasted the mango fruit, then taste the sweet banana fruit so that the bees scatter and fly away.

347.translit:
karumaga Lilangai yaatti pilangoLvaay tirandu tanmeel
varumavaL seviyum mookkum vaaLinaal tadinda endai,
perumagaL peedai mangai tannodum pirivi laata,
tirumagaL maruvum naangoort tirumaNik koodath taanee.
347.meaning: My Father, who cut off with His sword the ears and nose of the dark woman, that demoness who ruled Lanka (Surpanakha), as she came at Him opening her cave-like mouth — He dwells in the Tirumanikkudam shrine at Nangur, embraced by Tirumagal (Lakshmi), the great goddess, the gentle maiden who is never parted from Him.

348.translit:
keNdaiyum kuraLum puLLum keezhalu mariyum maavum,
aNdamum sudarum alla aatralu maaya endai,
oaNdiRal tenna nooda vadavara soottang kaNda,
tiNdiRa laaLar naangoort tirumaNik koodath tanee.
348.meaning: My Father, who became the fish, the dwarf (Vamana), the bird (Garuda/Hamsa), the boar, the man-lion, the horse, and the cosmos and the luminaries and all other powers besides — He dwells in the Tirumanikkudam shrine at Nangur, home of men of firm valour who saw the mighty Pandya of the south flee and the northern kings break and run.

349.translit:
kunDramum vaanum maNNum kuLirpunal tinga Loodu,
ninDravenj sudarum allaa nilaikaLu maaya endai,
manDramum vayalum kaavum maadamum maNangoNdu, engum
tenDralvan dulavum naangoort tirumaNik koodath taanee.
349.meaning: My Father, who became the hills, the sky, the earth, the cool waters, the moon, the standing fierce sun, and all other states of being — He dwells in the Tirumanikkudam shrine at Nangur, where the southern breeze comes wandering everywhere, taking up the fragrance of the meeting-halls, the fields, the groves and the mansions.

350.translit:
sangaiyum tuNivum poyyum meyyum it taraNi yoombum,
pongiya mukilum allaap poruLkaLu maaya vendai,
pangaya mugutta teeRal parugiya vaaLai paaya,
sengaya lugaLum naangoort tirumaNik koodath taanee.
350.meaning: My Father, who became doubt and certainty, falsehood and truth, the swelling clouds that nourish this earth, and all other things besides — He dwells in the Tirumanikkudam shrine at Nangur, where the scabbard-fish (vaalai), having drunk the nectar dripping from the lotuses, leap, and the red carp frolic.

351.translit:
paavamum aRamum veedum inbamun tunban taanum
koavamum aruLum allaak kuNangaLu maaya endai,
moovari lengaL moorti ivan, ena munivaroodu,
deevarvan diRainjum naangoort tirumaNik koodath tanee.
351.meaning: My Father, who became sin and virtue, liberation, pleasure and pain, wrath and grace, and all other qualities besides — saying 'Among the three (Brahma, Vishnu, Siva) this one is our true Lord,' the celestials come with the sages to worship Him at the Tirumanikkudam shrine at Nangur where He dwells.

352.translit:
tingaLtooy maada naangoort tirumaNik koodath taanai
mangaiyar talaivan vaNtaark kaliyanvaa yoligaL vallaar,
pongunee rulaga maaNdu ponnula kaaNdu, pinnum
vengatirp paridi vattath toodupooy viLangu vaaree.
352.meaning: Of the Lord who dwells in the Tirumanikkudam shrine at Nangur, where the mansions touch the moon, the leader of the Mangai folk, garland-decked Kaliyan, sang these utterances. Those who can master them will rule this world girdled by surging waters, then rule the golden world (of the heavens), and after that pass beyond the orb of the fierce-rayed sun and shine there.

353.translit:
taavaLan dulaga muRRum tadamalarp poygai pukku,
naavaLam navinDring ketta naagattin nadukkan teerttaay,
maavaLam perugi mannum maRaiyavar vaazhum naangaik,
kaavaLam paadi meeya kaNNanee kaLaikaNeeyee.
353.meaning: O You who strode and measured the whole world, who entered the broad lotus-pond and, when it sang Your praise eloquently with its tongue, ended the trembling of the serpent (saving Gajendra) — O Kannan who abides at Kavalampadi in Nangai, where Vedic brahmins dwell amid ever-growing abundance, You are my refuge and support.

354.translit:
maNNidan dheena maagi maavali valito laippaan,
viNNavar veeNdach senDru veeLviyil kuRayi randaay
tuNNena maatraar tammait tolaittavar naangai meeya,
kaNNanee kaava Landan paadiyaay kaLaika Neeyee.
354.meaning: O You who became the boar and dug up the earth, and who, when the celestials begged You, went to break the strength of Mahabali and asked for a boon (of land) at his sacrifice — O Kannan who abides at cool Kavalampadi in Nangai, where dwell those who swiftly destroyed their foes, You are my refuge and support.

355.translit:
uruttezhu vaali maarvil oarukaNai yuruva voottii,
karuttudaith tambik kinbak katimudi yarasa Littaay,
paruttezhu palavum maavum pazhamvizhun dozhugum naangaik
karuttanee kaava Landan paadiyaay kaLaika Neeyee.
355.meaning: O You who drove a single arrow piercing through the chest of the raging Vali, and gave the gladdening swift crown and kingship to his right-minded younger brother (Sugriva) — O You who are the very thought (Lord of hearts) of Nangai, where from the great thick-grown jackfruit and mango trees the ripe fruits fall and flow, You who abide at cool Kavalampadi, You are my refuge and support.

356.translit:
munaimagath tarakkan maaLa mudikaLpath taRuttu veezhttu, aang
ganaiyavaR kiLaiya vaRkee arasaLith taruLi naanee,
sunaikaLil kayalkaL paayach surumbuteen nugarum naangaik,
kanaikazhal kaava Landan paadiyaay kaLaika Neeyee.
356.meaning: O You who cut off and felled the ten heads of the demon (Ravana) of the battlefront, so that he perished, and then graciously gave the kingdom to his younger brother (Vibhishana) — O You of resounding warrior-anklets who abide at cool Kavalampadi in Nangai, where the carp leap in the springs and the bees sip honey, You are my refuge and support.

357.translit:
padavara vucci tanmeel paayndu pannadangaL seydu,
madavaral mangai tannai maarvagath tiruttii naanee,
tadavarai tangu maadath takupukazh naangai meeya,
kadavuLee kaava Landan paadiyaay kaLaika Neeyee.
357.meaning: O You who leapt upon the crest of the hooded serpent (Kaliya) and performed many dances, and who set the gentle young maiden (Lakshmi) upon Your chest — O God who abides at far-famed Nangai of mansions tall as great mountains, You who dwell at cool Kavalampadi, You are my refuge and support.

358.translit:
mallarai yattu maaLak kanjanai malaindu konDru,
pallara savindu veezhap paaratap poormu dittaay,
nallaraN kaavin neezhal naRaikamazh naangai meeya,
kallaraN kaava Landan paadiyaay kaLaika Neeyee.
358.meaning: O You who struck down the wrestlers so they perished, who fought and slew Kamsa, and who concluded the war of the Bharatas so that many kings withered and fell — O You who are a fortress of stone, abiding at fragrant Nangai in the shade of its goodly protecting groves, who dwell at cool Kavalampadi, You are my refuge and support.

359.translit:
moottavaR karasu veeNdi munbu toodezhun daruLi,
maattamar paagan veezha mathakari maruppo sittaay,
poottamar soolai yoongip punalparan dozhugum, naangaik
kaattavanee kaava Landan paadiyaay kaLaika Neeyee.
359.meaning: O You who long ago graciously went as an envoy seeking the kingdom for the elder (the Pandavas/Dharmaputra), and who broke off the tusk of the rutting elephant so that its skilled mahout fell — O Protector of Nangai, where the flowering groves rise high and the waters spread and flow, who abide at cool Kavalampadi, You are my refuge and support.

360.translit:
Eevizhang kannik kaaki imaiyavar koonaich cheRRu,
kaavaLam kadithiRuththuk kaRpakam kondu poonthaay,
poovaLam pozhilkaL soozhntha purantharan seytha naangaik,
kaavaLam paadi meeya kannanee kaLaika neeyee.
360.meaning: O Kannan, you who, for the sake of the radiant young maiden, conquered Indra the king of the celestials, broke down the lush grove and bore away the kaRpaka (wish-granting) tree! You who reside in Tirukkaavalampaadi in Naangai, a town girded by groves abounding in flowers, built by Purandara (Indra) — you alone are my refuge.

361.translit:
Santhamaay samaya maakich chamayavaim poota maaki,
anthamaa yaathi yaaki arumaRai yavaiyu maanaay,
manthamaar pozhilka doaRum madamayi laalum naangai,
kanthamaar kaava Lanthan paadiyaay kaLaika neeyee.
361.meaning: You became the metre of verse, became the religious paths, and became the five elements upon which those paths rest; you became the end and the beginning, and you became the rare sacred Vedas themselves. O you who reside in cool, fragrant Tirukkaavalampaadi of Naangai, where in every breeze-filled grove the tender peacocks dance — you alone are my refuge.

362.translit:
MaavaLam peruki mannum maRaiyavar vaazhum, naangaik
kaavaLam paadi meeya kannanaik kaliyan sonna,
paavaLam paththum vallaar paarmisai yarasa raaki,
koaviLa mannar thaazhak kudainizhal polivar thaamee.
362.meaning: Those who can master all ten of these rich verses, sung by Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) upon Kannan who resides in Tirukkaavalampaadi of Naangai — where prosperity ever abounds and the Vedic brahmins dwell — shall become kings upon this earth, and shall shine resplendent beneath the shade of the royal parasol, with crowned monarchs bowing low before them.

363.translit:
Kannaar kadalpool thirumeeni kariyaay,
nannaarmunai venRikoL vaarmannu naangoor,
thinnaar mathiLsoozh thiruveLLak kuLaththuL
annaa, adiyee nidaraik kaLaiyaayee.
363.meaning: O Lord whose sacred form is dark like the vast brimming ocean! O Father who dwells in Tiruvellakkulam, girded by strong ramparts, in Naangoor where abide those warriors who win victory over the battle-fronts of their foes — remove the sufferings of me, your servant.

364.translit:
Konthaar thuLavamalar kon dani vaanee,
nanthaatha perumpukazh veethiyar naangoor,
senthaa maraineerth thiruveLLak kuLaththuL
enthaay, adiyee nidaraik kaLaiyaayee.
364.meaning: O you who wear upon yourself the clustered blossoms of the tulasi! O my Father who dwells in Tiruvellakkulam of clear waters bearing red lotuses, in Naangoor of the Vedic brahmins whose great fame never fades — remove the sufferings of me, your servant.

365.translit:
KunRaal kuLirmaa rithaduth thukanthaanee,
nanRaa yaperum pukazhvee thiyarnaangoor
senRaar vanangum thiruveLLak kuLaththuL
ninRaay, nediyaay adiyee nidarneekkee.
365.meaning: O you who joyously held up the mountain (Govardhana) and stopped the great cool rain! O Tall One who stands in Tiruvellakkulam, worshipped by all who go there, in Naangoor of the Vedic brahmins of truly great renown — remove the suffering of me, your servant.

366.translit:
Kaanaar karikom pathosiththa kaLiRee,
naanaa vakainal lavarman niyanaangoor,
theenaar pozhilsoozh thiruveLLak kuLaththuL
aanaay, adiyeenuk karuLpuri yaayee.
366.meaning: O bull-elephant of a Lord who broke off the tusk of the forest elephant (Kuvalayapida)! O you who became (the abiding one) in Tiruvellakkulam encircled by honey-laden groves, in Naangoor where good people of many kinds abide — grant your grace to me, your servant.

367.translit:
Veedaar thiruveeng kadammeeya viLakkee,
naadaar pukazhvee thiyarman niyanaangoor,
seedaar pozhilsoozh thiruveLLak kuLaththaay,
paadaa varuveen vinaiyaa yinapaaRRee.
367.meaning: O Lamp who resides in sacred Venkatam thronged by hunters! O you of Tiruvellakkulam encircled by luxuriant groves, in Naangoor of the Vedic brahmins whose fame fills the land — I come singing your praise; drive away the karmic deeds that have become (my burden).

368.translit:
Kallaal kadalai yanaikatti yukanthaay,
nallaar palarvee thiyarman niyanaangoorch
chelvaa, thiruveLLak kuLaththuRai vaanee,
ellaa idarum kedumaa ruLaayee.
368.meaning: O you who joyously built a bridge across the ocean with stones! O Wealthy Lord who dwells in Tiruvellakkulam, in Naangoor where dwell the Vedic brahmins, many good ones — grant your grace so that all my sufferings may be destroyed.

369.translit:
Koalaal niraimeeyththa engoa valarkoavee,
naalaa kiyavee thiyarman niyanaangoor,
seelaar vayalsoozh thiruveLLak kuLaththuL
maalee, enaval vinaitheerth tharuLaayee.
369.meaning: O King of our cowherds who grazed the herds with your staff! O Tirumal who dwells in Tiruvellakkulam encircled by fields full of sael-fish, in Naangoor where dwell the brahmins of the four (Vedas) — be gracious and cut away my cruel karma.

370.translit:
Vaaraa kamathaaki yimmannai yidanthaay,
naaraa yananee nallavee thiyarnaangoor,
seeraar pozhilsoozh thiruveLLak kuLaththuL
aaraa vamuthee, adiyeeR karuLaayee.
370.meaning: O you who became the Boar (Varaha) and lifted up this earth! O Narayana, O insatiable Nectar of Tiruvellakkulam encircled by splendid groves, in Naangoor of the good Vedic brahmins — grant your grace to me, your servant.

371.translit:
Poovaar thirumaa makaLpul liyamaarpaa,
naavaar pukazhvee thiyarman niyanaangoorth
theevaa thiruveLLak kuLaththuRai vaanee,
aavaa adiyaa nivan, enR aruLaayee.
371.meaning: O you upon whose chest the great Lakshmi, born of the lotus, is embraced! O God who dwells in Tiruvellakkulam, in Naangoor where abide the Vedic brahmins whose praise fills every tongue — say with compassion, 'Ah, this one is my servant,' and grant your grace.

372.translit:
Nallan pudaivee thiyarman niyanaangoorch
chelvan thiruveL Lakkulath thuRaivaanai,
kallin malithoaL kaliyan sonnamaalai,
valla renaval lavarvaa navarthaamee.
372.meaning: This garland of verses sung by Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) of stone-like sturdy shoulders, upon the Wealthy Lord who dwells in Tiruvellakkulam in Naangoor where dwell the loving, good Vedic brahmins — those who are able to master it shall themselves become as the celestials (in heaven).

373.translit:
KavaLa yaanaik kombosiththa kanna nenRum, kaamaruseerk
kuvaLai meeka mannameeni konda koanen naanaiyenRum,
thavaLa maada needunaangaith thaama raiyaaL keeLvanenRum,
pavaLa vaayaa Lenmadanthai paarththan paLLi paaduvaaLee.
373.meaning: Saying 'He is Kannan who broke the tusk of the morsel-eating elephant,' and 'He is my Lord, my elephant, who bears a form like the lovely-hued dark cloud of cherished glory,' and 'He is the beloved of Lakshmi seated on the lotus, of Naangai with its tall white mansions' — my coral-lipped girl sings of Parthanpalli.

374.translit:
Kanjan vitta venjinaththa kaLiR adarththa kaaLaiyenRum,
vanja meevi vanthapeeyin uyirai yunda maayanenRum,
senjo laaLar needunaangaith theeva theeva nenRenRoathi,
panji yanna melladiyaaL paarththan paLLi paaduvaaLee.
374.meaning: Saying 'He is the youthful Lord who crushed the fiercely raging elephant sent by Kamsa,' and 'He is the wondrous one (Maayan) who consumed the life of the demoness (Putana) who came with deceit,' and chanting again and again 'He is the God of gods of long-streeted Naangai of those who speak sweet true words' — my girl with feet soft as cotton sings of Parthanpalli.

375.translit:
Andar koanen naanaiyenRum aayar maathar kongaipulku
senda nenRum, naanmaRaikaL theedi yoadum selvanenRum,
vandu lavupozhil koLnaangai mannu maaya nenRenRoathi,
pandu poolan Renmadanthai paarththan paLLi paaduvaaLee.
375.meaning: Saying 'He is the King of the celestials, my elephant,' and 'He is the gallant one who embraces the breasts of the cowherd women,' and 'He is the Wealthy one whom even the four Vedas run seeking,' and chanting 'He is the wondrous one (Maayan) who abides in Naangai of groves where bees rove' — my girl, no longer as she once was, sings of Parthanpalli.

376.translit:
Kollai yaanaaL parisazhinthaaL koalva Laiyaar thammukappee,
mallai munneer thattilangai katta zhiththa maayanenRum,
selvam malku maRaiyoarnaangai theeva theeva nenRenRoathi,
palva Laiyaa Lenmadanthai paarththan paLLi paaduvaaLee.
376.meaning: She has become unruly, she has lost her composure, in the very presence of the women adorned with rounded bangles. Saying 'He is the wondrous one (Maayan) who dammed the surging ocean and destroyed Lanka,' and chanting again and again 'He is the God of gods of Naangai of the brahmins where wealth abounds' — my girl of many bangles sings of Parthanpalli.

377.translit:
Arakka raavi maaLavanRu aazhkadalsoo zhilangaiseRRa,
kurakka rasanen Rumkoala villi yenRum, maamathiyai
nerukku maada needunaangai ninma lanthaa nenRenRoathi,
parakka zhinthaa Lenmadanthai paarththan paLLi paaduvaaLee.
377.meaning: Saying 'He is the king of the monkeys (their lord, Rama) who that day destroyed Lanka, girded by the deep sea, so that the lives of the demons perished,' and 'He is the wielder of the splendid bow,' and chanting again and again 'He is the flawless one of long-streeted Naangai whose mansions press against the great moon' — my girl, who has utterly lost herself, sings of Parthanpalli.

378.translit:
Njaala muRRu munduvmizhintha naatha nenRum, naani lanjsoozh
veelaiyanna koalameeni vanna nenRum, meelezhunthu
seelu kaLumvayal koLnaangaith theeva theeva nenRenRoathi,
paalin nalla menmozhiyaaL paarththan paLLi paaduvaaLee.
378.meaning: Saying 'He is the Lord who swallowed the whole world and spat it forth again,' and 'He is the one of beautiful hued form like the ocean that girds the four-quartered earth,' and chanting again and again 'He is the God of gods of Naangai with fields where the sael-fish leap up and frolic' — my girl of soft words sweet as milk sings of Parthanpalli.

379.translit:
Naadi yenRanuL Longkonda naatha nenRum, naanmaRaikaL
theedi yenRum kaanamaattaach chelva nenRum, siRai koLvandu
seedu lavupozhil koLnaangaith theeva theeva nenRenRoathi,
paada kamseer melladiyaaL paarththan paLLi paaduvaaLee.
379.meaning: Saying 'He is the Lord who sought me out and took his place within me,' and 'He is the Wealthy one whom even the four Vedas, ever searching, can never see,' and chanting again and again 'He is the God of gods of Naangai of luxuriant groves where winged bees rove' — my girl with soft feet bearing the paadakam anklet sings of Parthanpalli.

380.translit:
ulaga meettum oruvan enRum oN sudarodum perai-thaa,
nilavum aazhip padaiyan enRum neesan enRum, then-thisaikkuth
thilada manna maRaiyor naangaith theva thevan enRu enRu oodhi,
palarum eesa en madandhai paarthan paLLi paaduvaaLe
380.meaning: My slender maiden sings of Parthan Palli, declaring again and again that He is the One who raised up and protected the world, the One who bears the bright effulgence, the One who holds the ever-shining discus, the loving One, and the God of gods of Naangai whose Brahmins are like a tilaka adorning the southern land. She sings so that many may praise Him.

381.translit:
kaNNan enRum vaanavargaL kaadhaliththu malargaL thoovum,
eNNan enRum inban enRum eezh-ulagukku aadhi enRum,
thiNNa maada needu naangaith theva thevan enRu enRu oodhi,
paNNin anna men-mozhiyaaL paarthan paLLi paaduvaaLe
381.meaning: She of soft words sweet as melody sings of Parthan Palli, declaring again and again that He is Kannan, the One whom the celestials lovingly worship by scattering flowers, the One held in their thoughts, the blissful One, the origin of the seven worlds, and the God of gods of Naangai of lofty firm mansions.

382.translit:
paaruL nalla maRaiyor naangaip paarthan paLLi sengaN maalai,
vaar koL nalla mulai madavaaL paadal aindhaay mozhindha maaRRam,
koor koL nalla vel kaliyan kooRu thamizh paththum vallaar,
ever koL nalla vaikundhaththuL inbam naaLum eydhuvaare
382.meaning: This is the speech uttered as the song of a fair maiden with lovely full breasts bound in a band, about the red-eyed Tirumal of Parthan Palli in Naangai of good Brahmins on earth. Those who master these ten Tamil verses composed by Kaliyan of the good sharp spear will daily attain bliss in the beautiful, glorious Vaikuntha.

383.translit:
nummaith thozhudhom nundham paNi sey thirukkum num adiyom,
immaikku inbam peRRom endhaay indhaLooriire,
emmaik kadidhaak karumam aruLi aavaa enRu irangi,
nammai orukaal kaatti nadandhaal naangaL uyyome?
383.meaning: We worship You; we are Your servants who abide doing Your bidding. We have gained bliss even in this life, O our Father, O Lord of Indalur. If You graciously and swiftly grant us Your purpose, and taking pity say 'Alas!', and show Yourself to us but once and come forth, shall we not be redeemed?

384.translit:
sindhai thannuL neengaadhu irundha thiruve maruvu iniya
maindhaa, andhaN aali maale solai mazha-kaLiRe
nandhaa viLakkin sudare naRaiyoor ninRa nambee, en
endhaay, indhaLooraay, adiyeRku iRaiyum irangaaye
384.meaning: O Auspicious One who dwells unfailingly within my heart, O dear youthful Lord, O Tirumal of the cool beautiful Tiruvali, O young elephant of the grove, O flame of the never-dimming lamp, O perfect Lord who stands at Naraiyur, O my Father, O Lord of Indalur! Will You not take even a little pity on me, Your servant?

385.translit:
pesu kinRadhu idhuve vaiyam eer-adiyaal aLandha,
moosi vaNdu muralum kaNNi mudiyeer, ummaik kaaNum
aasai ennum kadalil veezhndhu ingu ayarndhom, ayalaarum
eesu kinRadhu idhuve kaaNum indhaLooriire
385.meaning: O Lord crowned with a garland upon which clustering bees swarm and hum, who measured the world in two strides! This alone is what people speak of: that we have fallen into the sea called the desire to behold You, and here we languish, while even strangers mock at this very thing. Behold, O Lord of Indalur.

386.translit:
aasai vazhuvaadhu eththu emakku ingu izhukku aayththu, adiyorkkuth
tesam aRiya umakke aaLaayth thiri kinRomukku,
kaasin oLiyil thigazhum vaNNam kaatteer, emperumaan
vaasi valleer indhaLooreer vaazhndhe pom neere
386.meaning: To us who praise You with unwavering longing this has become a reproach; for us, Your servants, who wander known to all the world as belonging to You alone. Show us Your form that shines bright as the lustre of refined gold, O our Lord! O Lord of Indalur, skilled and mighty One, may You fare well and prosper.

387.translit:
thee emperumaan neer emperumaan thisaiyum iru-nilanum
aay, emperumaan aagi ninRaal adiyom kaaNomaal,
thaay emperumaan thandhai thandhai aaveer, adiyemuk-
ke emperumaan alleeero neer indhaLooriire
387.meaning: Our Lord is fire, our Lord is water; if our Lord stands as the directions and the wide earth too, we Your servants cannot behold Him. You are our mother, our father, our father's father, O Lord. Are You not indeed our Lord, O Lord of Indalur?

388.translit:
sollaadhu ozhiya killen aRindha sollil, num adiyaar,
ellaarodum okka eNNi irundheer adiyenai,
nallar aRiveer theeyaar aRiveer namakku iv ulagaththil,
ellaam aRiveer eedhe aRiyeer indhaLooriire
388.meaning: I cannot keep from speaking the words I know: You have reckoned me, Your servant, as equal to all Your other devotees. You know the good and You know the wicked; for us, in this world You know everything, yet this one thing You do not know, O Lord of Indalur.

389.translit:
maatteer aaneer paNi neer koLLa emmaip paNi aRiyaa
vitteer, idhanai veRe sonnom indhaLooriire,
kaatteer aaneer nundham adikkaL kaattil umakku indha,
naatte vandhu thoNdar aana naangaL uyyome
389.meaning: You are unwilling, yet You take our service; You have left us not knowing how to serve. We have said this plainly to You, O Lord of Indalur. You do not show; if You will not show us Your sacred feet, shall we who have come to this land and become Your servants ever be redeemed?

390.translit:
munni vaNNam paalin vaNNam muzhudhum nilai ninRa
pinnai vaNNam koNdal vaNNam vaNNam eNNum-kaal
ponnin vaNNam maNiyin vaNNam puraiyum thirumeni
inna vaNNam enRu kaatteer indhaLooriire
390.meaning: The hue of old, the hue of milk; the hue that ever abides fully; the hue of the dark rain-cloud—when one counts the hues, Your sacred form resembles the hue of gold and the hue of the gem. Show us, saying 'such is its hue,' O Lord of Indalur.

391.translit:
endhai thandhai thammaan enRu enRu emar eer emar eezhavum,
vandhu ninRa thoNdaror kke vaasi valleeraal,
sindhai thannuL mundhi niRRir siRidhum thirumeni,
indha vaNNam enRu kaatteer indhaLooriire
391.meaning: Calling You 'my father, his father, his lord,' my kin through generation upon generation—You are skilled and mighty toward the devotees who have come and stood before You. You stand foremost within our hearts, yet You do not show even a little of Your sacred form, saying 'such is its hue,' O Lord of Indalur.

392.translit:
eraar pozhil soozh indhaLooril endhai perumaanai,
kaaraar puRavin mangai vendhan kaliyan oli seydha,
seeraar in-sol maalai kaRRuth thirivaar ulagaththu,
aaraar avare amararkku enRum amarar aavaare
392.meaning: Of our Father, the Lord at Indalur surrounded by beautiful groves, Kaliyan, king of Mangai with its rain-filled forest-lands, has made this sounding garland of sweet excellent words. Whoever they may be in this world who learn it and wander reciting it, they shall forever become immortals among the immortals.

393.translit:
aaychchiyar azhaippa veNNey uNdu orukaal
 aalilai vaLarndha emperumaan
peychchi azhalai uNdu iNai marudhu iRuththup
 peru nilam aLandhavan koyil
kaayththa neeL kazhugum kadhaliyum thengum
 engumaam pozhilgaL naduve
vaayththa neer paayum maNNiyin then-paal
 thiruveLLiyangudi adhuve
393.meaning: He is our Lord who once ate the butter while the cowherd women called out, and who reclined upon the banyan leaf; who consumed the life of the demoness, who broke the twin marudu trees, who measured the vast earth. His temple lies amid groves everywhere full of tall fruit-laden areca, plantain, and coconut—that is Tiruvelliyangudi, on the southern bank of the Manni where ample waters flow.

394.translit:
aa-nirai meyththu anRu alai kadal adaiththittu
 arakkar tham sirangaLai urutti,
kaar niRai megam kalandhor uruvak
 kaNNanaar karudhiya koyil,
poo neeraich cherundhi punnai muththu arumbip
 podhumbidai vari vaNdu miNdi,
then iraiththu uNdu angu in-isai muralum
 thiruveLLiyangudi adhuve
394.meaning: Of Kannan of the form dark as the gathered rain-laden cloud, who tended the cattle, who once dammed the surging sea, and who set rolling the heads of the demons—this is the temple He cherished. There the flowering serundhi and punnai trees bud forth like pearls, and in the groves striped bees throng, drinking up the honey in streams and humming sweet music—that is Tiruvelliyangudi.

395.translit:
kadu vidam udaiya kaaLiyan thadaththaik
 kalakki mun alakkazhiththu, avan than
padam iRap paayndhu pal maNi sindhap
 pal nadam payinRavan koyil,
pada-arav alkul paavai nallaargaL
 payiRRiya naadagath oli poy,
adai pudai thazhuvi aNda ninRu adhirum
 thiruveLLiyangudi adhuve
395.meaning: His temple is where, long ago, He churned and devastated the pool of the venomous serpent Kaliyan, leapt upon him so that his hoods burst and his many gems scattered, and danced his many dances upon him. There the sound of the dance-music performed by lovely women, whose loins curve like the spreading serpent's hood, goes forth and embraces all around, resounding even unto the heavens—that is Tiruvelliyangudi.

396.translit:
kaRavai mun kaaththuk kanjanaik kaayththa
 kaaLa megath thiru uruvan,
paRavai mun uyarththup paaR-kadal thuyinRa
 paramanaar paLLi koL koyil,
thuRai thuRai thoRum pon maNi sidhaRum
 thogu thirai maNNiyin then-paal,
seRi maNi maadak kodi kadhir aNavum
 thiruveLLiyangudi adhuve
396.meaning: He of the sacred form dark as a rain-cloud who once guarded the cattle and destroyed Kanja (Kamsa), who raised the bird (Garuda) as His banner, who reclined upon the milk-ocean—this is the temple where the Supreme One reposes. On the southern bank of the Manni, whose massed waves scatter gold and gems at every ford, the flags of jewel-studded mansions reach up to touch the sun's rays—that is Tiruvelliyangudi.

397.translit:
paarinai uNdu paarinai umizhndhu
 paaradham kai eRindhu, orukaal
therinai oorndhu therinaith thurandha
 sengaN maal senRu uRai koyil,
er nirai vayaluL vaaLaigaL maRugi
 emakku idam anRu idhu enRu eNNi,
seer mali poygai senRu aNai kinRa
 thiruveLLiyangudi adhuve
397.meaning: The red-eyed Tirumal who swallowed the earth and spat the earth forth, who marshalled the Bharata war with His hand, and once drove the chariot He had mounted—this is the temple where He went and dwells. There the vaalai fish in the beautiful rows of paddy fields, thinking 'this is no place for us,' turn back and go to take refuge in the splendid abundant ponds—that is Tiruvelliyangudi.

398.translit:
kaaRRidaip pooLai karandhana arandhai uRak
 kadal arakkar tham senai,
kooRRidaich chellak kodum kaNai thurandha
 kola vil iraaman than koyil,
ooRRidai ninRa vaazhaiyin kanigaL
 oozhththu veezhndhana uNdu maNdi,
seeRRidaik kayalgaL uL thigazh vayal soozh
 thiruveLLiyangudi adhuve
398.meaning: This is the temple of Rama of the beautiful bow, who loosed cruel arrows so that the army of the sea-island demons was destroyed in grievous ruin and passed into the jaws of death, like the pooLai cotton-flower scattered in the wind. There fallen ripened plantain fruits from trees standing by the springs are devoured greedily, and the temple is encircled by fields where carp gleam amid the mire—that is Tiruvelliyangudi.

399.translit:
oLLiya karumam seyvan enRu uNarndha
 maavali velviyil pukku,
teLLiya kuRaLaay moovadi koNdu
 thikku uRa vaLarndhavan koyil
aLLiyam pozhil vaay irundhu vaazh kuyilgaL
 ari ari enRavai azhaippa
veLLiyaar vaNanga viraindhu aruL seyvaan
 thiruveLLiyangudi adhuve
399.meaning: His temple is where He entered the sacrifice of Mahabali, who deemed he was performing a worthy deed, came as a clear-formed dwarf, took three strides, and grew till He filled all the quarters of space. There the koels that dwell happily in the lovely close groves call out 'Hari! Hari!', and He who swiftly grants grace as the radiant ones bow before Him—that is Tiruvelliyangudi.

40.translit:
maaN munindhu oru kaal vari silai vaLaiththa mannavan ponniRaththu uravoon,
oon munindha avanadhu udal iru piLavaa ugir nudhi maduththu, ayan aranaith
thaan munindhu itta vendhiRal saabam thavirththavan thavam purindhu uyarndha
maa muni koNarndha gangaiyin karai mel vadhariyaachchiraamaththuLLaane.
40.meaning: He who once bent His ornamented bow in anger at the deer (in Rama's avatara), and as the golden-hued, mighty Man-Lion (Narasimha) split open the body of Hiranya, driving His sharp claw-tips into him; He who removed the fierce, fiery curse that Brahma had angrily laid upon Shiva — that Lord dwells at Vadari-ashram, on the banks of the Ganga brought down by the great sage who had risen high through his austerities.

41.translit:
koNdal maarudhangaL kula varai thogu neerk kurai kadal ulagudan anaiththum,
uNda maa vayiRROn oN sudar eyndha umbarum oozhiyumaanaan,
aNdam oodu aRuththu anRu andharaththu izhindhu angu avani aaL alamara, perugu
maNdu maa maNi neerk gangaiyin karai mel vadhariyaachchiraamaththuLLaane.
41.meaning: He of the vast belly who swallowed the clouds, the winds, the principal mountains, the gathered waters of the roaring ocean, and all the world; He who became the bright-radiant celestials of the heavens and the very eons themselves; He who once cleaved through the cosmic egg and descended from the firmament, so that the Earth-goddess was distressed — that Lord dwells at Vadari-ashram, on the banks of the surging, gem-clear waters of the Ganga.

42.translit:
varum thirai maNi neerk gangaiyin karai mel vadhariyaachchiraamaththuLLaanai,
karum kadal munneer vaNNanai eNNik kaliyan vaay oli seydha panuval,
varam seydha aindhum aindhum vallaargaL vaanavar ulagudan maruvi,
irum kadal ulagam aaNdu veN kudaikkeezh imaiyavar aaguvar thaame.
42.meaning: Those who can master these blessed ten verses (five and five) — the song which Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) uttered aloud, meditating upon the Lord who dwells at Vadari-ashram on the banks of the Ganga of wave-rolling gem-clear waters, the Lord hued like the dark sea — they shall unite with the world of the celestials, and having ruled the world girdled by the great sea beneath a white parasol, they themselves shall become the unblinking immortals.

43.translit:
kalaiyum kariyum parimaavum thiriyum kaanam kadandhu poy,
silaiyum kaNaiyum thuNaiyaagach chenRaan venRich cheRukkaLaththu,
malai koNdu alai neer aNai katti madhiL neer ilangai vaaL arakkar thalaivan,
thalai paththu aRuththu ugandhaan saaLakkiraamam adai nenje.
43.meaning: He who crossed the forest where stags, elephants and steeds roam, and went forth (as Rama) with His bow and arrows as His companions; in the field of victorious battle He built a causeway over the surging waters with mountains, and joyfully struck off the ten heads of the sword-wielding demon king of moat-girt, water-encircled Lanka — reach Salagramam, O my heart.

44.translit:
kadam soozh kariyum parimaavum oli maan theerum kaalaaLum,
udan soozhndhu ezhundha kadi ilangai podiya vadivaay charam thurandhaan,
idam soozhndhu engum iru visumbil imaiyoor vaNanga maNam kamazhum,
thadam soozhndhu engum azhagaaya saaLakkiraamam adai nenje.
44.meaning: He who let fly His arrows so that fortified Lanka — risen up surrounded with rut-flowing elephants, steeds, resounding swift chariots and foot-soldiers — was reduced to dust; the place worshipped on every side by the immortals of the wide heavens, fragrant and beautiful everywhere, ringed about by ponds — reach Salagramam, O my heart.

45.translit:
ulavu thiraiyum kula varaiyum oozhi mudhalaa veN thikkum,
nilavum sudarum iruLumaay ninRaan venRi viRal aazhi valavan
vaanoor tham perumaan maruvaa arakkarkku ennaanRum
chalavan, salam soozhndhu azhagaaya saaLakkiraamam adai nenje.
45.meaning: He who stands as the rolling waves, the principal mountains, the eight directions beginning with the eons, as moonlight and sunlight and darkness; the wielder of the victorious, mighty discus; the Lord of the celestials, who is ever at war with the demons who do not draw near Him — reach Salagramam, beautiful and surrounded by water, O my heart.

46.translit:
oor aam kudandhai uththaman oru kaal iru kaal silai vaLaiya,
theeraa arakkar ther veLLam seRRaan vaRRaa varu punal soozh peraan,
per aayiram udaiyaan piRangu siRai vaNdu aReiginRa thaaraan
thaaraa vayal soozhndha saaLakkiraamam adai nenje.
46.meaning: The Supreme One whose town is Kudandhai (Kumbakonam), who once and again bent His bow and destroyed the flood-like chariots of the relentless demons; the Lord of Per (Tiruppernagar) girt with ever-flowing waters; He of a thousand names, wearing a garland on which winged bees hum — reach Salagramam, surrounded by fields where waterfowl dwell, O my heart.

47.translit:
aduththu aarththu ezhundhaaL pilavaay vittu alaRa avaL mookku ayil vaaLaal
viduththaan, viLangu sudar aazhi viNNoor perumaan naNNaar mun
kaduththu aarththu ezhundha peru mazhaiyaik kal onRu endhi ina niraik kaaththu
thaduththaan thadam soozhndhu azhagaaya saaLakkiraamam adai nenje.
47.meaning: He who, with His sharp sword, cut off the nose of her (Surpanakha) who rose up screaming with her cavernous mouth wide open; the Lord of the celestials bearing the shining radiant discus; He who, lifting up a single mountain (Govardhana), warded off and held back the great storm that swelled up roaring before His foes, thereby protecting the herds of cattle — reach Salagramam, beautiful and ringed with ponds, O my heart.

48.translit:
thaayaay vandha pey uyirum thayirum vizhudhum udan uNda vaayaan
thooya ari uruvil kuRaLaaych chenRu maavalaiyai eyaan irappa
moovadi maNNinRu thaa enRu ulagu ezhum thaayaan
kaayaa malar vaNNan saaLakkiraamam adai nenje.
48.meaning: He of the mouth that drank away the very life of the demoness who came in the guise of a mother (Putana), and that ate up curds and butter together; He who, in the pure and radiant form of a dwarf (Vamana), went and begged of Mahabali, saying 'Grant me three feet of earth,' and then strode across all the seven worlds; the Lord hued like the kaya blossom — reach Salagramam, O my heart.

49.translit:
enoor anja venj samaththuL ariyaayp pariya iraNiyanai,
oon aar agalam piLavu eduththa oruvan thaane iru sudaraay,
vaanaay theeyaay maarudhamaay malaiyaay alai neer ulagu anaiththum thaanaay
thaanumaanaan than saaLakkiraamam adai nenje.
49.meaning: He who, while all others trembled, became the Man-Lion (Narasimha) in fierce battle and tore open the fleshy chest of the huge Hiranya; the One who is Himself the two luminaries (sun and moon), the sky, fire, wind, the mountains, the surging waters, and all the worlds — and who is yet Himself, transcending all — reach His Salagramam, O my heart.

50.translit:
vendhaar enbum sudu neeRum meyyil poosik kai agaththu oor
sandhaar thalai koNdu ulagu ezhum thiriyum periyoon thaan senRu, en
endhaay! saabam theer enna ilangu amudha neerth thiru maarbil thandhaan,
sandhaarp pozhil soozhndha saaLakkiraamam adai nenje!
50.meaning: When the great one (Shiva), who smears burnt bones and hot ash upon his body and wanders through all seven worlds bearing a skull in his hand, came and said, 'O my Father, remove this curse!' — He gave forth the gleaming nectar-water from His sacred chest (to release Shiva from the cleaving skull) — reach Salagramam, surrounded by sandalwood groves, O my heart!

51.translit:
thoNdaam inamum imaiyoorum thuNai nool maarbin andhaNarum,
aNdaa emakke aruLaay enRu aNaiyum koyil arugellaam,
vaNdu aarp pozhilin pazhanaththu vayalin ayale kayal paaya,
thaN thaamaraigaL mugam malarththum saaLakkiraamam adai nenje.
51.meaning: The temple to which the host of devotees, the celestials, and the brahmins with the sacred thread upon their chests all draw near, crying 'O Boundless One, grace us alone!' — all around it, in the watered fields of the bee-humming groves, beside the paddy-fields carp leap, and cool lotuses open their faces — reach that Salagramam, O my heart.

52.translit:
thaaraa vaarum vayal soozhndha saaLakkiraamaththu adigaLai,
kaar aarp puRavin mangai vendhan kaliyan oli sey tamizh maalai,
aar aar ulagaththu aRivu udaiyaar amarar nannaattu arasu aaLa,
per aayiramum oodum ingaL anRi ivaye pidhaRRumine.
52.meaning: This Tamil garland of melodious verses, composed by Kaliyan, the king of Mangai with its cloud-wreathed groves, upon the Lord of Salagramam ringed by fields where waterfowl gather — let whoever in this world possesses wisdom recite His thousand names, or else continually utter just these very verses, so that they may rule as kings in the good land of the immortals.

53.translit:
vaaN nilaa muRuval siRu nudhal perun thoL maadharaar vana mulaip payane peNinen
adhanaip pizhai enak karudhip pedhaiyen piRavi noy aRuppaan,
eeN ilen irundhen eNNinen eNNi iLaiyavarkku alavi in thiRai naaNinen,
vandhu un thiruvadi adaindhen naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay.
53.meaning: I had craved only the enjoyment of the lovely breasts of women with bright moonlike smiles, small foreheads and broad shoulders. Now, deeming that a sin, I — a foolish, ignoble one — pondered well how to cut off the disease of my birth, and I grew ashamed of the sweet dalliance of dallying with young women. So I have come and reached Your sacred feet, O my Father at Naimisaranyam.

54.translit:
silambu adi uruvil karu nedung kaNNaar thiRaththanaay aRaththaiye maRandhu,
pulam padindhu uNNum bogame perukkip pokkinen pozhudhinai vaaLaa,
alam puri thadak kaiyaayane maayaa! vaanavarkku arasane!
vaanoor nalam purindhu iRainjun thiruvadi adaindhen naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay.
54.meaning: Devoted to women of dark, long eyes and anklet-adorned feet, I utterly forgot righteousness, and I increased only the pleasures of indulging in the senses, wasting away my days in vain. O Cowherd of the broad hands that wield the plough! O wondrous Maya! O King of the celestials! I have come and reached Your sacred feet that the heaven-dwellers seek and worship for their good, O my Father at Naimisaranyam.

55.translit:
soodhinaip perukkik kaLavinaith thuNindhu suri kuzhal madandhaiyar thiRaththu,
kaadhale miguththuk kaNda vaa thirindha thoNdanen naman thamar seyyum,
vedhanaikku odungi nadunginen velai veN thirai alamarak kadaindha, naadhane
vandhu un thiruvadi adaindhen naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay.
55.meaning: Increasing my gambling, taking boldly to theft, heaping up lust for women of curling tresses, I — a servant who wandered wherever my eyes led me — now cringe and tremble at the torment that Yama's servants inflict. O Lord who churned the ocean till its white waves tossed about! I have come and reached Your sacred feet, O my Father at Naimisaranyam.

56.translit:
vambu ulaang koondhal manaiviyaith thuRandhu piRar poruL thaaram enRu ivaRRai,
nambinaar iRandhaal naman thamarp paRRa eRRi vaiththu,
eri ezhuginRa sembinaal iyanRa paavaiyaip paavee! thazhuvu ena mozhivadhaRku anji,
nambane. vandhu un thiruvadi adaindhen naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay.
56.meaning: Dreading the word — that when those who, abandoning their own fragrant-tressed wives, set their trust upon others' wealth and others' women, die, Yama's servants seize them, fling them down, and say 'O sinner, embrace this!' pointing to an image fashioned of fire-blazing copper — O trustworthy Lord, I have come and reached Your sacred feet, O my Father at Naimisaranyam.

57.translit:
idumbaiyaal adarppuNdu idumino thuRRu ennu irandhavarkku illaiye enRu,
neduny solaal manuththa neesanen andho. ninaikkilen vinaip payan thannai,
kaduny solaark kadiyaark aalanaar thamaraal paduvadhu or kodu miRaikku anji,
nadungi naan vandhu un thiruvadi adaindhen naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay.
57.meaning: When those afflicted by hardship begged, 'Give me a morsel,' I — a base one — drove them off with harsh, cutting words, saying 'There is none.' Alas, I never reflected upon the fruit of my deeds. Now, terrified of the cruel torment to be suffered at the hands of harsh-tongued, pitiless Yama's servants, trembling I have come and reached Your sacred feet, O my Father at Naimisaranyam.

58.translit:
kodiya manaththaal sinath thozhil purindhu thirindhu naay inaththodun thiLaiththittu,
oodiyum uzhanRum uyirgaLe konRen uNarvu ilenaadhalaal,
namanaar paadiyaip peridhum parisu azhiththitten paramane! paaR kadal kidandhaay!,
naadi naan vandhu un thiruvadi adaindhen naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay!
58.meaning: With a cruel mind I worked deeds of wrath, and wandering about, consorting with packs of dogs, running and roaming, I killed living creatures, for I was wholly without understanding. Thus I have greatly enriched and swelled the township of Yama. O my Supreme One! O You who recline upon the Ocean of Milk! Seeking You, I have come and reached Your sacred feet, O my Father at Naimisaranyam!

59.translit:
nenjinaal, ninaindhum vaayinaal mozhindhum needhi allaadhana seydhum,
thuncinaar sellun thol neRi kette thuLanginen viLangani munindhaay!
vancanedi yen nenjiniR piriyaa vaanavaa! thaanavarkku enRum nancane!,
vandhu un thiruvadi adaindhen naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay.
59.meaning: Thinking unjust thoughts in my mind, speaking unjust words with my mouth, and doing unrighteous deeds, I trembled when I heard of the ancient path that those who so die must travel. O You who once destroyed the demon disguised in the wood-apple fruit! O Heavenly One who never parts from the heart of even a deceitful one like me! O ever-poison to the demons! I have come and reached Your sacred feet, O my Father at Naimisaranyam.

400.translit:
mudiyudai yamarark kidarseyu masurar
 tamperu maanai, an' rariyaay
madiyidai vaiththu maarvaimun keeNda
 maayanaar manniya kooyil,
padiyidai maadath thadiyidaith thooNil
 padhiththapan maNigaLi noLiyaal,
vidipaga liraven' rarivari thaaya
 thiruveLLi yangudi yathuvee.
400.meaning: The Lord of those crowned celestials, who once took the form of the Man-Lion (Narasimha) to bring distress to the Asuras who tormented the gods: that wondrous one placed the demon (Hiranya) upon His lap and tore open his chest. This is the temple where He abides. By the radiance of the many gems set into the pillars at the foot of the mansions of that town, day cannot be told from night — that very place is sacred Tiruvelliyangudi.

401.translit:
kudikudi yaagak koodinin' ramarar
 guNangaLee pidharrinin' reeththa
adiyavarkk aruLi yaravaNaith thuyinra
 aaziyaa namarnthurai kooyil,
kadiyudaik kamalam adiyidai malarak
 karumbodu peruncenne lasaiya,
vadivudai yannam pedaiyodum seerum
 vayalveLLi yangudi yathuvee!
401.meaning: The temple where the Lord who holds the discus abides — He who, blessing His devotees, reclines in sleep upon the serpent-couch, while the celestials gather generation after generation, ceaselessly babbling and praising His qualities. There fragrant lotuses bloom at one's feet, sugarcane and tall red paddy sway, and graceful swans dwell together with their mates — that very place of fields is Tiruvelliyangudi!

402.translit:
paNdumun eena maagiyan' rorukaal,
 paaridan theyirrinil koNdu,
theNdirai varudap paarkadal thuyinra
 thiruveLLi yangudi yaanai,
vaNdarai soolai mangaiyar thalaivan
 maanavel kaliyanvaa yoligaL,
koNdivai paadum thavamudaiyaarkaL
 aaLvarik kuraikada lulagee.
402.meaning: Long ago, becoming the Boar (Varaha) on a single occasion, He lifted up the earth upon His tusk; and He reclines in sleep upon the milk-ocean as its clear waves caress Him — He, the Lord of Tiruvelliyangudi. Those who have the merit to take up and sing these words sounded from the mouth of Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar), chieftain of Tirumangai with its groves where bees hum, wielding a mighty spear — they shall rule over this world girt by the roaring sea.

403.translit:
ariva thariyaa nanaiththulagum udaiyaa nennai yaaLudaiyaan
kuriya maaNi yuruvaaya kooththan manni yamarumidam,
nariya malarmel surumbaarkka ezhilaar manjnjai nadamaada,
porikoL siraivaN disaipaadum puLLam poothang kudithaanee!
403.meaning: He whom none can fully comprehend, the owner of all the worlds, He who has made me His own — the dancer who took the form of a short young brahmachari (Vamana): the place where He firmly abides. There bees hum upon fragrant flowers, beautiful peacocks dance, and dappled-winged bees sing their music — that is Pullambhutangudi!

404.translit:
kaLLak kuRaLaay maavaliyai vanjiththu ulagam kaippaduththu,
poLLaik karaththa poodhagaththin thunbam thavirththa punithanidam,
paLLac ceruvil kayalugaLap pazhanak kazhani yathanuLpooy,
puLLup piLLaik kiraithedum puLLam poothang kudithaanee!
404.meaning: As the deceitful dwarf He beguiled Mahabali and seized the worlds; the pure one who relieved the suffering of the elephant (Gajendra) with the wounded, hollow trunk: this is His place. There carp leap in the low watery furrows, and going amid the wet paddy fields the birds seek food for their young — that is Pullambhutangudi!

405.translit:
meevaa varakkar thennilangai veendhan veeyac caramthurandhu,
maavaay piLandhu malladarththu marudham saaiththa maaladhidam,
kaavaar thengin pazhamveezhak kayalgaL paayak kurugiriyum,
poovaar kazhani yezhilaarum puLLam poothang kudithaanee!
405.meaning: He sped His arrows so that the king of southern Lanka, lord of the hostile demons, perished; He split the mouth of the horse-demon (Kesi), crushed the wrestlers, and felled the marudu trees — the place of that Tirumal. There fruits fall from the grove-filling coconut palms, carp leap, herons cry, and flower-filled fields shine in beauty — that is Pullambhutangudi!

406.translit:
verpaal maari pazhudhaakki viralvaa larakkar thalaivanran,
varpaar thiraltho Lainnaangum thuNiththa valvil iraamanidam,
karpaar purisai seykunRam kavinaar koodam maaLigaigaL,
porpaar maada mezhilaarum puLLam poothang kudithaanee!
406.meaning: With a mountain He thwarted the rain (lifting Govardhana); and as Rama of the mighty bow He severed the twenty rounded, muscular shoulders of the valiant sword-wielding lord of the demons (Ravana) — this is His place. There stone-built ramparts rise like hills, beautiful halls and palaces, and golden mansions shine in beauty — that is Pullambhutangudi!

407.translit:
maiyaar thadangaN karungoondhal aaicci maRaiya vaiththadhayir,
neyyaar paaloo damudhuseydha neemi yangai maayanidam,
seyyaar aaral iraikarudhic cengaal naarai senraNaiyum,
poiyaa naavil maRaiyaaLar puLLam poothang kudithaanee!
407.meaning: The curd that the cowherdess with dark, kohl-lined wide eyes and black tresses had hidden away — the wondrous one with the discus in His lovely hand ate it up along with the ghee, milk and ambrosia: this is His place. There red-legged herons come and gather, seeking the eel-fish as prey in the fields, and brahmins of the unfailing (truthful) tongue who recite the Vedas dwell — that is Pullambhutangudi!

408.translit:
minni nanna nuNmarungul veeyeey thadanthoaL melliyarkaa,
mannu sinaththa mazhavidaigaL eezhan' radarththa maaladhidam,
mannu mudhunee aravindha malarmel varivaN disaipaada,
punnai ponney thaadhudhirkkum puLLam poothang kudithaanee.
408.meaning: For the sake of the tender-natured one (Nappinnai) with a slender waist like lightning and broad bamboo-like shoulders, He once subdued the seven enraged young bulls — the place of that Tirumal. There striped bees sing upon lotus blossoms in the ancient deep waters, and the punnai trees shed pollen like gold — that is Pullambhutangudi.

409.translit:
kudaiyaa vilangal koNdeendhi maari pazhudhaa niraikaaththu,
sadaiyaa nooda adalvaaNan thadanthoaL thuNiththa thalaivanidam,
kudiyaa vaNdu kaLLuNNak koola neelam mattugukkum,
pudaiyaar kazhani yezhilaarum puLLam poothang kudithaanee.
409.meaning: Lifting up a mountain as an umbrella He held it aloft, thwarted the rain and protected the cattle; and He severed the broad shoulders of the mighty Banasura, making Siva of the matted locks flee — the place of that Lord. There bees settle in to drink the nectar, the lovely blue water-lilies pour out their honey, and surrounding fields shine in beauty — that is Pullambhutangudi.

410.translit:
karaiyaar neduvel maramannar veeya visayan theerkadavi,
iraiyaan kaiyil niRaiyaadha muNdam niRaiththa vendhaiyidam,
maRaiyaal moottee yavaivaLarkkum mannu pugazhaal vaNmaiyaal,
poraiyaal mikka andhaNarvaazh puLLam poothang kudithaanee.
410.meaning: Driving Arjuna's chariot so that the warrior-kings with long stained spears perished, my Father filled up the begging-bowl (skull) in the hand of the Lord (Siva) that could never be filled: this is His place. There brahmins, abounding in lasting fame, in generosity and in forbearance, who kindle and tend the sacred Vedic fires, dwell — that is Pullambhutangudi.

411.translit:
thunni maNNum viNNaadum thoanRaa thiruLaay moodiyanaaL,
anna maagi yarumaRaigaL aruLic ceydha amalanidam,
minnu soothi navamaNiyum veeyin muththum saamaraiyum,
ponnum ponni koNarndhalaikkum puLLam poothang kudithaanee,
411.meaning: On the day when earth and the heaven-world, crowded together, appeared not but were enveloped in darkness, the stainless one became the Swan (Hamsa) and graciously imparted the precious Vedas: this is His place. There the river Ponni (Kaveri) brings down and tosses up glittering bright nine kinds of gems, pearls from the bamboo, chowries, and gold — that is Pullambhutangudi.

412.translit:
karraa maRiththu kaaLiyanRan senni nadunga nadampayinRa
porraa maraiyaaL thankeeLvan puLLam poothangkudithanmel
karraar paravum mangaiyarkkoan kaaraar puyarkaik kaliganRi,
soldhaaneerain dhivaipaadac coora nillaa thuyardhaamee!
412.meaning: The consort of her who dwells on the golden lotus (Lakshmi), He who turned back the calves and danced so that the head of Kaliya trembled — upon His Pullambhutangudi, the chieftain of Tirumangai praised by the learned, Kaliyan (Kalikanri) of the rain-cloud-like generous hand: if one sings these ten verses he composed, their sorrows will not stay but will slip away and vanish!

413.translit:
thaamtham perumai yariyaar, thoodhu
veendharkk aaya veendha roorpool,
kaandhaL viralmen kalainan madavaar,
koondhal kamazhum kooda looree.
413.meaning: Like the town of the King of kings, who became an envoy and yet did not reveal His own greatness (Krishna as messenger to the Kauravas) — this is Kudaloor, where the tresses of fair, gentle, well-adorned women with fingers slender as the kantal flower spread their fragrance.

414.translit:
serumthiN thimile rudaiya, pinnai
perumthaN koolam perraa roorppool,
narumthaN theemthe nuNda vaNdu,
kurinji paadum kooda looree!
414.meaning: Like the town of Him who, having subdued the strong fierce bulls, won the rich cool ornament that is Nappinnai — this is Kudaloor, where bees that have drunk fragrant cool sweet honey sing the kurinji melody!

415.translit:
piLLai yuruvaayth thayiruNdu, adiyeen
uLLam pugundha voruva roorpool,
kaLLa naarai vayaluL, kayalmeen
koLLai koLLum kooda looree!
415.meaning: Like the town of the One who, as a little child, ate the curd and then entered into the heart of me, His servant — this is Kudaloor, where the cunning heron plunders the carp-fish in plenty in the fields!

416.translit:
koorre ruruvin kuRaLaay, nilaneer
erraa nendhai perumaa noorpool,
seerre ruzhuvar koathaip poadhooN,
koaldhen muralum kooda looree!
416.meaning: Like the town of my Father, my great Lord, who as the dwarf (Vamana) of a form like Yama received the water (of Bali's gift) over the land — this is Kudaloor, where the ploughmen wading in the mire feed upon the blossoms of their garlands, and the honey-bees on stalks hum!

417.translit:
thoNdar paravac cudarsen' raNava,
aNdath thamarum adiga loorpool,
vaNda lalaiyuL keNdai miLira,
koNda lathirum kooda looree.
417.meaning: Like the town of the Lord who abides in the firmament, whose radiance goes forth and reaches afar as devotees praise Him — this is Kudaloor, where carp glisten amid the silt-laden waves and the rain-clouds thunder.

418.translit:
thakkan veeLvi thagarththa thalaivan,
thukkam thudaiththa thuNaiva roorpool,
ekka lidunuN maNalmel, engum
kokkin pazhamveezh kooda looree!
418.meaning: Like the town of the Lord, the Companion who wiped away sorrow, He who shattered Daksha's sacrifice — this is Kudaloor, where everywhere upon the fine sand heaped by the floods the fruits of the kokku (mangrove) trees fall!

419.translit:
karundhaN kadalum malaiyu mulagum,
arundhum adigaL amaru moorpool,
perundhaN mullaip piLLai yoodi,
kurundham thazhuvum kooda looree!
419.meaning: Like the town where the Lord who consumes the dark cool ocean, the mountains and the worlds abides — this is Kudaloor, where the tender shoot of the great cool mullai jasmine runs and embraces the kurundu tree!

420.translit:
kalaivaazh piNaiyoo daNaiyum, tiruneer
malaivaa zhendai maruvu moorpool,
ilaitaazh tengin meelninRu,
iLaneerk kulaitaazh kidangin koodaloorE!
420.meaning: Like the city of my Lord who abides at the sacred-watered mountain (Tirumalai), where the stag dwells embracing its doe — such is Koodaloor, where on the leaf-drooping coconut palm the cluster of tender coconuts hangs down low over the moat.

421.translit:
perugu kaada ladiyEn uLLam,
urugap pugunda voruva roorpool,
arugu kaidai malara, keNdai
kurugen Ranjum koodaloorE.
421.meaning: Like the city of that peerless One who entered the heart of this servant, so that my swelling love melted me — such is Koodaloor, where the screw-pine blossoms nearby, and the keNdai fish, mistaking the blossom for a crane (heron), takes fright.

422.translit:
kaavip peruneer vaNNan, kaNNan
meevit tigazhum koodaloormeel,
koovait tamizhaal kaliyan sonna,
paavaip paadap paavam poomE.
422.meaning: Kannan, hued like the great waters of the blue kaavi lily, dwells and shines in Koodaloor. If one sings this song-garland, this 'paavai', which Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) composed in flawless Tamil upon that place, all sin will depart.

423.translit:
venRi maamazhu vEndimun maNmisai mannarai moovezhukaal
konRa tEva,nin kuraikazhal tozhuvadoor vagaiyenak karuLpuriyE,
manRil maampozhil nuzhaidandu mallikai mauvalin pOdalarttI,
tenRal maamaNam kamazhdara varudiru veLLaRai ninRaanE!
423.meaning: O Lord who, wielding the victorious great axe, slew the kings upon the earth thrice-seven times (as Parasurama) — grant me grace, that I may have a way to worship Your resounding anklet-clad feet. O You who stand at Tiruvellarai, where the southern breeze, slipping into the mango-groves of the common-ground, makes the jasmine and the wild-jasmine burst into bloom and comes wafting their great fragrance!

424.translit:
vasaiyil naanmaRai keduttavam malarayaR karuLi,mun parimugamaay,
isaikoL vEdanoo lenRivai payandhava nE enak karuLpuriyE,
uyarkoL maadhavip pOdodu laaviya maarudam veedhiyinvaay,
tisaiyel laamkama zhumpozhil soozhdiru veLLaRai ninRaanE
424.meaning: O You who, when the faultless four Vedas were lost, graced the lotus-born Brahma, and taking the form of the horse-faced one (Hayagriva) brought forth these melodious Vedic scriptures — grant me grace! O You who stand at Tiruvellarai, girt with groves where, along the streets, the breeze playing with the blossoms of the tall maadhavi creeper spreads fragrance in all directions.

425.translit:
veyya naayula gEzhudan nalindavan udala gamirupiLavaa,
kaiyil neeLugirp padaiyadu vaaytthava nE enak karuLpuriyE,
maiyi naartaru varaalinam paayavaN tadattidaik kamalangaL,
teyva naaRumoN poygaigaL soozhdiru veLLaRai ninRaanE
425.meaning: O You who, when the fierce one (Hiranya) afflicted all the seven worlds, split his body in twain with the weapon of the long nails on Your hands (as Narasimha) — grant me grace! O You who stand at Tiruvellarai, encircled by lovely ponds breathing a divine fragrance, where dark schools of varaal fish leap in the rich pools amid the lotuses.

426.translit:
vaampa riyuga mannarta muyirsega aivarkkat karasaLitta,
kaampi naarttiru vEngadap poruppa.nin kaadalai yaruLenakku,
maampo zhiltaLir kOdhiya madakkuyil vaayadu tuvarppeyda,
teempa langanit tEnadu nugardiru veLLaRai ninRaanE
426.meaning: O Lord of the Tiruvenkatam hill bound with bamboo, who destroyed the lives of the leaping-horse-charioted kings and bestowed the kingdom upon the five (the Pandavas) — grant me Your love! O You who stand at Tiruvellarai, where the gentle female cuckoo, having pecked the mango-grove's tender shoots, finds its mouth turned bitter (astringent) and so drinks the honey of the sweet ripe jackfruit.

427.translit:
maana vEloNkaN madavaral maNmagaL azhungamun neerpparappil,
Ena maagiyan Rirunila midandhava nEenak karuLpuriyE,
kaana maamullai kazhaikkarum pERiveN muRuvalsey dalarginRa,
tEnin vaaymalar murugukuk kumtiru veLLaRai ninRaanE.
427.meaning: O You who, when the bright spear-eyed maiden, the Earth-goddess, grieved sunk in the expanse of the three waters, became the boar (Varaha) and lifted up the sacred earth — grant me grace! O You who stand at Tiruvellarai, where the wild great jasmine, climbing upon the tall sugarcane, smiles its white smile and blossoms, and its honeyed flower-mouths spill fragrant nectar.

428.translit:
pongu neeNmudi yamararkaL tozhudezha amudhinaik koduttaLippaan,
ango raamaiya thaagiya vaadhinin adimai yaiyaru Lenakku,
tangu pEdaiyo doodiya madhukaram taiyalaar kuzhala Naivaan,
tingaL tOysenni maadamsenRaNai tiruveLLaRai ninRaanE
428.meaning: O Primordial One who, that the crowned celestials with surging tall diadems might worship and rise, became the tortoise (Kurma) there and gave them the nectar to sustain them — grant me Your service! O You who stand at Tiruvellarai, where the honey-bee that has quarrelled with its mate goes to nestle in the tresses of lovely women, in mansions whose summits touch the moon.

429.translit:
aaRi nOdoru naankudai nedumudi arakkanRan siramellaam,
vERu vERuga villadu vaLaitthava nEenak karuLpuriyE,
maaRil sOdiya maradhagap paasadait taamarai malarvaarnta,
tERal maandhivaN dinnisai murala tiruveLLaRai ninRaanE
429.meaning: O You who bent the bow and struck off, one severed from another, all the heads of the tall-crowned demon (Ravana) of the ten (six-and-four) diadems — grant me grace! O You who stand at Tiruvellarai, where the bees, having drunk the dripping nectar of the lotuses set amid emerald-green leaves of peerless lustre, hum their sweet music.

430.translit:
munniv vEzhula guNarvinRi yiruLmiga umbarkaL tozhudEttha,
anna maagiyan RarumaRai payandhava nEenak karuLpuriyE,
mannu kEdhakai soodaka menRivai vanaththidaic surumbinangaL,
tenna vennavaN dinnisai muraltiru veLLaRai ninRaanE
430.meaning: O You who, when long ago these seven worlds were without knowledge and darkness prevailed, became the swan (Hamsa) so that the celestials worshipped and praised, and brought forth the rare Vedas — grant me grace! O You who stand at Tiruvellarai, where amid the groves of abiding screw-pine and mango the swarms of bees hum their sweet music, droning 'tenna-venna'.

431.translit:
aangu maavali vELviyi lirandhusen Ragalida muzhudinaiyum,
paangi naaRkoNda paramaniR paNindhezhu vEnenak karuLpuriyE,
oongu piNdiyin semma lERivaN duzhidhara, maavERit
teengu yilmizhaR Rumpadap paitthiru veLLaRai ninRaanE.
431.meaning: O Supreme One who there went and begged at Mahabali's sacrifice, and by Your skill took the whole of the wide earth (as Vamana) — grant me grace, that I may bow to You and rise! O You who stand at Tiruvellarai, in whose gardens the bees climb and wander over the red blossoms of the tall ashoka tree, while sweet cuckoos perch on the mango trees and warble.

432.translit:
manju laamaNi maadangaL soozhdiru veLLaRai yadanmEya,
anja nampurai yumtiru vuruvanai aadhiyai yamudhatthai,
nanju laaviya vElvala vankali kanRisol aiyiraNdum,
enja linRinin REtthaval laarimai yOrkra saavaarkkaLE.
432.meaning: Of the One whose sacred form resembles dark collyrium, the Primordial One, the very nectar, who dwells in Tiruvellarai girt with jewelled mansions amid which clouds wander — of Him, Kaliyan, master of the poison-smeared spear, has spoken these ten (twice-five) verses. Those who can praise Him standing firm by these without fail will become kings (lords) over the celestials.

433.translit:
undhi mElnaan muganaip padaiththaan ula guNdavan
endai pemmaan, imaiyOrgaL taadhaikkida menparaal,
sandhi nOdu maNiyum kozhikkumpunal kaaviri,
andhi pOlum niRattaar vayalsoozhden narangamE!
433.meaning: He who created the four-faced Brahma upon His navel, who swallowed the worlds, my Father, my Lord — they say the abode of Him, the Father of the celestials, is southern Srirangam, surrounded by fields whose folk are hued like the twilight, where the watery Kaveri washes down sandalwood and gems.

434.translit:
vaiyamuN daalilai mEvu maayanmaNi neeNmudi,
paikoL naagat taNaiyaan payilumida menparaal,
taiyal nallaar kuzhalmaa laiyummaRRavar tadamulai,
seyya saandhum kalandhizhi punalsoozhden narangamE!
434.meaning: The wondrous One who swallowed the world and reclines upon the banyan leaf, gem-crowned, who lies upon the hooded serpent-couch — they say the place where He abides is southern Srirangam, encircled by waters that flow bearing the flower-garlands from good women's tresses and the red sandal-paste from their full breasts.

435.translit:
paNdiv vaiya maLappaan senRumaavali kaiyilneer
koNda aazhit tadakkaik kuRaLanida menparaal,
vaNdu paadum madhuvaar punalvandhizhi kaaviri
aNda naaRum pozhilsoozhndhu azhagaarden narangamE.
435.meaning: He who long ago went to measure this world and received the water (of pledge) into His hands from Mahabali — they say the abode of that dwarf (Vamana) with the broad discus-bearing hands is beautiful southern Srirangam, surrounded by the Kaveri whose honeyed waters come flowing while bees sing, girt with groves fragrant to the very heavens.

436.translit:
viLaitta vempOr viRalvaa Larakkannagar paazhpada,
vaLaitta valvil tadakkai yavanukkida menparaal,
tuLaikkai yaanai maruppu magilumkoNarn dhundhi,mun
tiLaikkum selvap punalkaa virisoozhden narangamE,
436.meaning: He who bent the strong bow with His broad hands so that the city of the valiant sword-bearing demon (Ravana), who waged fierce war, was laid waste — they say His abode is southern Srirangam, surrounded by the bountiful waters of the Kaveri, which carry along and toss up the tusks of the hollow-trunked elephant and fragrant eagle-wood as they sport.

437.translit:
vambulaam koondhal maNdOdhari kaadhalan vaanpuga,
ambu tannaal munindha azhaganida menparaal,
umbar kOnu mulagEzhum vandheeNdi vaNangum,nal
sembo naarum madhiLsoozhndhu azhagaarden narangamE.
437.meaning: The beautiful One who, in wrath, sent the husband of fragrant-tressed Mandodari (Ravana) to the heavens by His arrow — they say His abode is beautiful southern Srirangam, surrounded by ramparts gleaming like fine red gold, where the king of the celestials (Indra) and all the seven worlds come thronging to worship.

438.translit:
kalaiyu duttha agalalgul vanpEymagaL taayena,
mulaiko dutthaa Luyirun davanvaazhumida menparaal,
kulaiye duttha kadhalip pozhiloodum vandhundhi, mun
alaiye dukkum punaRkaa virisoozhden narangamE
