438.meaning: He who took the life of the cruel demoness (Putana) — who, garment-girt about her broad loins, gave her breast feigning to be His mother — they say the place where He lives is southern Srirangam, surrounded by the watery Kaveri that comes flowing and tossing up its waves through the groves of cluster-laden plantain trees.

439.translit:
kanjan nenjum kadumal larumsagadamungaalinaal,
tunja venRa sudaraazhi yaanvaazhumida menparaal,
manju sErmaa Ligainee dagilpugaiyum, maRaiyOr
senjol vELvip pugaiyum kamazhumden narangamE,
439.meaning: The bright discus-bearing One who, with His foot, made the cart (Sakata) perish, and overcame Kamsa's heart and the fierce wrestlers — they say the place where He lives is southern Srirangam, fragrant with the long-rising eagle-wood smoke from cloud-touching mansions and the smoke of the right-worded sacrifices of the Vedic seers.

440.translit:
eena meenaa maiyoodu ariyum siru kuRaLumaay,
taanu maayat taraNit talaivanidam enparaal,
vaanum maNNum niRaiyap pugundeeNdi vaNangum, nal
teenum paalum kalandan navarseert ten arangamee,
440.meaning: They say His abode is the place of the Lord of the earth, who Himself became the boar, the fish, the man-lion, and the little dwarf (Vamana). It is southern Srirangam, where He dwells, worshipped by all who throng and crowd in from the heavens and the earth, and where the devotees mingle like sweet honey and milk.

441.translit:
seeyan endrum migapperiyan nuNNeermaiyinaaya, im
maayaiyai aarum aRiyaa vagaiyaanidam enparaal,
veeyin muttum maNiyum koNarndaarppunaR kaaviri,
aaya ponmaa madiLsoozhnd azhagaartten arangamee,
441.meaning: They say His abode is the place of Him who is utterly distant, exceedingly great, and yet of the subtlest finesse, whose maya no one can comprehend. It is beautiful southern Srirangam, encircled by great golden walls, where the roaring Kaveri brings pearls from the bamboo and precious gems.

442.translit:
alli maadar amarum tirumaarvan arangattai,
kallin mannu madiLmangaiyarkoon kalikandrisol,
nallisai maalaigaL naali raNdum iraN dumudan,
vallavar taamulagaaNdu pin vaanulagaaLvaree.
442.meaning: This is the song of Kaliyan, the chieftain of the Mangai people whose forts are built of stone, on the Lord of Srirangam upon whose sacred chest the lotus-maiden Lakshmi resides. Those who master these eight and these two sweet-melodied garlands of verse together shall rule this world and afterward rule the world of heaven.

443.translit:
veruvaadaaL vaayveruvi veengadamee veengadamee enginRaaLaal,
maruvaalaal en kudangaal vaaNedungaN tuyilmaRandaaL, vaNdaar koNdal
uruvaaLan vaanavartam uyiraaLan olitiraineerp pauvam koNda
tiruvaaLan en magaLai seydanagaL engnganam naan sindik keenee.
443.meaning: She who never used to babble now babbles in fear, crying 'Venkatam! Venkatam!' She no longer nestles at my lap; her long bright eyes have forgotten sleep. He whose form is like the bee-swarmed dark cloud, the life of the celestials, the Lord of fortune (Lakshmi) who took the ocean of resounding waves — how can I conceive of what He has done to my daughter?

444.translit:
kalaiyaalaa vagalalgul kanavaLaiyum kaiyaalaa enseykeen naan,
vilaiyaalaa vadiyeenai veeNdudiyoo veeNdaayoo? ennum, meyya
malaiyaaLan vaanavarttam talaiyaaLan maraamarameezh eyda vendRich
silaiyaaLan, en magaLai seydanagaL engnganam naan sindik keenee.
444.meaning: The garment will not stay on her broad hips, the firm bangles will not stay on her hands — what shall I do? She keeps saying, 'O priceless one, do You want this slave-girl or not?' He, the Lord of the true mountain (Tirumalai), the chief of the celestials, the master of the bow whose victorious arrow pierced through the seven maramara trees — how can I conceive of what He has done to my daughter?

445.translit:
maanaaya men nokki vaanedung kaNNeermalgum vaLaiyum soorum,
teenaaya narunduzhaay alangalin tiRambeesi yuRangaaL kaaNmin,
kaanaayan kadimanaiyil tayiruNdu neyparuga nandan petRa
aanaayan, en magaLai seydanagaL ammanai meeraRiki leenee.
445.meaning: See — her soft doe-like eyes brim with tears from those long eyes, her bangles slip off; she speaks confusedly of the honey-sweet fragrant tulasi garland and cannot sleep. The forest-cowherd, the cattle-herder born to Nanda, who ate curds and drank ghee in the protected house — O mothers, I cannot fathom what He has done to my daughter.

446.translit:
taayvaayil soRkeeLaaL tannaayat toodaNaiyaaL tadamen kongai
ye, aarac saandaNiyaaL, emberumaan tiruvaranga mengee? ennum,
peeymaaya mulaiyuNdiv vulaguNda peruvayiRRan peesil nangaay,
maamaayan en magaLai seydanagaL mangaimeer madikki leenee.
446.meaning: She heeds not her mother's words at the door, she does not join her band of companions, she does not anoint her broad soft breasts with full sandal-paste; she only asks, 'Where is my Lord's sacred Srirangam?' If one speaks of Him — the great-bellied one who drank the deceitful demoness's (Putana's) breast and swallowed this world — O good women, that great wondrous one: I cannot comprehend what He has done to my daughter.

447.translit:
pooNmulaimeel saandaNiyaaL porukayalkaN maiyezhudaaL poovai peeNaaL,
eeNaRiyaa LettanaiyumI emberumaan tiruvaranga mengee ennum,
naaNmalaraaL naayaganaay naamaRiya vaayppaadi vaLarnda nambi,
aaNmaganaay en magaLai seydanagaL ammanai meeraRiki leenee.
447.meaning: She anoints not her jeweled breasts with sandal, she paints not her fighting-carp-like eyes with collyrium, she tends not her myna bird, she has lost all sense — she only keeps asking, 'Where is my Lord's sacred Srirangam?' He, the consort of the lotus-dwelling lady (Lakshmi), the noble one who grew up in Ayppadi (Gokula) as we all know, born a virile man — O mothers, I cannot fathom what He has done to my daughter.

448.translit:
taadaadu vanamaalai taaraanoo vendRendRee taLarndaaL kaaNmin,
yaadaanu monduraikkil emberumaan tiruvarangam ennum, poomeel
maadaaLan kudamaadi madusoodan mannarkkaay munnam sendRa
toodaaLan, en magaLai seydanagaL engnganam naan sollukeenee.
448.meaning: See how she has grown faint, saying again and again, 'Will He not give me the wild-flower garland with pollen-laden petals?' If one says anything at all to her, she only says, 'My Lord's sacred Srirangam!' He, the lord of her who dwells on the lotus, the dancer of the pot-dance, Madhusudana, who once went as an envoy for the kings (Pandavas) — how can I tell of what He has done to my daughter?

449.translit:
vaaraaLu miLangongai vaNNamvee Raayinavaa Re eNNaaL, eNNil
peeraaLan peerallaal peesaaLip peNpetRee enseykeen naan,
taaraaLan taNkudandai nagaraaLan aivarkkaay amaril uytta teeraaLan,
en magaLai seydanagaL engnganam naan seppukeenee.
449.meaning: She gives no thought to how the color of her young, girdle-clad breasts has utterly changed; beyond the name of the boundlessly great-named Lord she speaks no name — having borne this girl, what am I to do? The garland-wearing Lord, the master of cool Kudandai (Tirukkudandai) town, the charioteer who drove the chariot in battle for the five (Pandavas) — how am I to utter what He has done to my daughter?

450.translit:
uRavaadu milaLendRen Rozhiyaadu palareesum alaraay iRRaal,
maRavaadee yeppozhudum maayavanee maadavanee enkin RaLaal,
piRavaada peeraaLan peNNaaLan maNNaaLan viNNoor tangaL
aRavaaLan, en magaLai seydanagaL ammanai meeraRiki leenee.
450.meaning: Saying she has no kinship with us, people without ceasing keep spreading scandalous gossip. Never forgetting, at every moment she keeps crying, 'O wondrous one! O Madhava!' He, the unborn one of great renown, the lord of the lady (Lakshmi), the lord of the earth, the righteous one of the celestials — O mothers, I cannot fathom what He has done to my daughter.

451.translit:
pandoodu kazhalmaruvaaL paingiLiyum paalooTTaaL paavai peeNaaL,
vandaanoo tiruvarangan vaaraanoo endRendRee vaLaiyum soorum,
sandoogan pauzhiyan ain tazhaloombu taittiriyan saama veedi,
andoo van denmagaLai seydanagaL ammanai meeraRiki leenee.
451.meaning: She no longer takes to her ball and dice, she does not feed her green parrot with milk, she does not tend her doll; her bangles slip off as she keeps saying, 'Has the Lord of Srirangam come? Will He not come?' He of the Chandogya (Sama-singer), the Paushya, the keeper of the five sacred fires, the Taittiriya, the Sama-Vedin — alas, having come, O mothers, I cannot fathom what He has done to my daughter.

452.translit:
seelugaLum vaylpudaisoozh tiruvarangat tammaanai sindai seyda,
neelamalark kaNmadavaaL niRaiyazhivait taaymozhinda vadanai, neeraar
kaalaveel parakaalan kalikandri olimaalai katRu vallaar,
maalaiseer veNkudaikkeezh mannavaraayp ponnulagil vaazhvar taamee.
452.meaning: This garland of resounding verse — composed by Parakalan, Kaliyan, of the death-dealing spear, telling in a mother's words how the dark-flower-eyed maiden lost her modesty meditating on the Lord of holy Srirangam, whose fields are surfounded by leaping carp — those who learn and master it shall live as kings beneath the white parasol adorned with garlands, and reign in the golden world (heaven).

453.translit:
kaimmaana mazhagaLitRai kadalkidanda karumaNiyai,
maimmaana maragadattai maRaiyuraitta tirumaalai,
emmaanai enakkendru miniyaanaip panikaatta
vammaanai, yaankaNda taNineert ten arangattee,
453.meaning: The graceful young elephant of strength, the dark gem that reclines upon the ocean, the dark-hued emerald, the Tirumal whom the Vedas proclaim, my Lord who is forever sweet to me, the great one who guarded against the cold (Govardhana) — Him I beheld in cool-watered southern Srirangam.

454.translit:
peeraanai kuRungudiyem perumaanai, tirutaNkaal
ooraanai karambanoor uttamanai, muttilangu
kaaraart tiN kadaleezhum malaiyeezhiv vulageezhuNdum,
araadendR irundaanai kaNdadu ten arangattee,
454.meaning: The Lord of Tiruppernagar, our Lord of Kurungudi, the dweller of Tirutankal, the supreme one of Karambanur, who though He swallowed the seven dark firm oceans gleaming with pearls, the seven mountains, and these seven worlds, yet remained as if unsated — Him I beheld in southern Srirangam.

455.translit:
eenaagi yulagidandan dirunilanum peruvisumbum,
taanaaya perumaanai tannadiyaar manattendrum
teenaagi yamudaagit tigazhndaanai, magizhndorukaal
aanaayan aanaanai kaNdadu ten arangattee,
455.meaning: The great Lord who, becoming the boar, lifted up the world long ago, who Himself is the sacred earth and the vast sky, who ever shines as honey and as nectar in the hearts of His own devotees, and who joyfully once became a cowherd — Him I beheld in southern Srirangam.

456.translit:
vaLarndavanai tadangadaluL valiyuruvil trisagadam,
taLarndudira vudaittavanai tariyaadan diraNiyanaip
piLandavanai, perunilamee radineeTTip paNdorunaaL
aLandavanai, yaankaNda taNineerttte n arangattee,
456.meaning: He who grew (as the child) and reclined upon the vast ocean, who kicked the mighty-formed cart-demon Sakata so that it crumbled apart, who could not bear it and split open Hiranya (Hiranyakashipu), and who long ago stretched out His two feet to measure the great earth — Him I beheld in cool-watered southern Srirangam.

457.translit:
neerazhalaay nedunilanaay nindRaanai, andrarakkan
oorazhalaa luNdaanai kaNdaarpin kaaNaamee,
peerazhalaayp peruvisumbaayp pinmaRaiyoor mandirattin,
aarazhalaa luNdaanai kaNdadu ten arangattee,
457.meaning: He who stands as water and fire and the vast earth, who once consumed the demon's city (Lanka) with fire so that those who saw it saw it no more, who is the great fire and the vast sky, and who is received through the abundant fire of the brahmins' Vedic mantras — Him I beheld in southern Srirangam.

458.translit:
tanjinattai tavirttadaindaar tavaneRiyai, tariyaadu
kanjanaikkon dandrulaga muNdumizhnda kaRpagattai,
venjinatta kodundozhiloon visaiyuruvai yasaivitta,
anjiRaipput paaganaiyaan kaNdadu ten arangattee,
458.meaning: He who is the path of penance for those who, casting off their anger, take refuge in Him; the wish-granting tree (Kalpaka) who, unable to bear it, slew Kamsa and once swallowed the world and spat it forth again; who shook the form of strength of the fierce-tempered cruel-deeded one — He, the rider of the beautiful-winged bird (Garuda), I beheld in southern Srirangam.

459.translit:
sindanaiyai tavaneRiyai tirumaalai, piriyaadu
vandenadu manattirunda vadamalaiyai, varivaNdaar
kondaNainda pozhilkoova lulagaLappaa nadinimirtta
andaNanai, yaankaNda taNineertten arangattee,
459.meaning: He who is my very thought and the path of penance, Tirumal, the northern mountain (Venkatam) who, never parting from me, came and dwelt in my heart, the pure one of Tirukkovalur whose groves are clustered with striped-bee-laden blossoms, who raised His foot to measure the worlds — Him I beheld in cool-watered southern Srirangam.

60.translit:
Eevinaarkkaliyaar naliga venḏṟu en mEl eṅṅaNE vaazhumaaṟu?
Aivar kOvinaar seyyuk koḍumaiyai maḍiṯṟEn kuṟuṅkuḍi neḍuṅkaḍal vaNNaa
Paavin aarin sol pal malark koNḍu un paadhamE paravi naan paNindhu,
en naavinaal vandhu un tiruvaḍi aḍaindhEn naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay.
60.meaning: When the cruel ones, sent by others, oppressed me, how was I to live? O Lord of the colour of the deep ocean who dwells in Kurungudi, I have crushed the cruelties wrought upon me. With sweet melodious words and many flowers I praised and bowed at Your feet alone, and with my tongue I came and reached Your sacred feet, O my Father who dwells in Naimisaranyam.

61.translit:
Ooniḍaic chuvar vaiththu enbu thooN naaṭṭi urOmam vEyndhu onbadhu vaasal,
thaan uḍaik kurambaip piriyum pOdhu unṟan saraNamE saraNam enṟirundhEn,
thEnuḍaik kamalath tiruvinukk arasE! tirai koL maaNḍuṅkaḍaR kiḍandhaay!,
naan uḍaith thavaththaal tiruvaḍi aḍaindhEn naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay.
61.meaning: This body is a hut walled with flesh, with bones for pillars, thatched with hair and pierced with nine doorways; when the soul departs from it, I have remained firm in knowing that Your feet alone are my refuge. O King of Lakshmi who dwells on the honeyed lotus! O Lord who reclines on the vast wave-tossed ocean! By the merit that is mine I have reached Your sacred feet, O my Father who dwells in Naimisaranyam.

62.translit:
Edham vandhu aNugaa vaNNam naam eNNi ezhuminO thozhudhum enṟu,
imaiyOr naadhan vandhu iraiñjum naimisaaraNiyath tendhaiyaic chindhaiyuL vaiththu,
kaadhalE miguththa kaliyan vaay oli sey maalai thaam kaṯṟu vallaargaL,
Odha neer vaiyagam aaNḍu veN kuḍaik keezh umbarum aaguvar thaamE.
62.meaning: "Let us think and rise up and worship, that no harm may come near us" - so saying, the Lord of the celestials comes and bows down at Naimisaranyam. Those who can learn this garland of verses, uttered with abounding love by Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar), placing in their hearts our Father of that shrine, shall rule this sea-girt earth beneath a white parasol, and shall themselves become dwellers of the celestial world.

63.translit:
Aṅ kaN ñaalam añja aṅgu Or aaL ariyaay avuNan
poṅga aagam vaḷ ugiraal pOzhndha punidhan iḍam,
paiṅ kaN aanaik kombu koNḍu paththimaiyaal, aḍikkeezhc
ceṅ kaN aaḷi iṭṭu iṟaiñjum siṅgavEL kunṟamE.
63.meaning: Where the holy Lord, taking the form of a man-lion to make the wide beautiful world tremble, tore open with His sharp claws the swelling chest of the demon - that is His abode: Singavel Kundram, where the red-eyed lions, out of devotion, bring the tusks of fresh-eyed elephants and lay them at His feet and worship Him.

64.translit:
Alaiththa pEzhvaay vaaL eyiṯṟu Or kOL ariyaay, avuNan
kolaik kaiyaaḷan neñju iḍandha koor ugiraaḷan iḍam,
malaiththa sel saaththu eṟindha poosal van thuḍi vaay kaḍuppa,
silaik kai vEḍar thezhippu aṟaadha siṅgavEL kunṟamE.
64.meaning: Taking the form of a fierce man-lion with a gaping mouth, raging tongue and sword-like teeth, He of the sharp claws who tore open the chest of the murderous-handed demon - that is His abode: Singavel Kundram, where the uproar of robbers attacking travelling caravans is like the beating of strong drums, and the shouting of the bow-handed hunters never ceases.

65.translit:
Eyndha pEzhvaay vaaL eyiṯṟu Or kOL ariyaay, avuNan
vaayndha aagam vaḷ ugiraal vagirndha am maan adhan iḍam,
Oyndha maavum uḍaindha kunṟum anṟiyum ninṟu azhalaal,
thEyndha vEyum alladhu illaac chiṅgavEL kunṟamE.
65.meaning: Taking the form of a fierce man-lion with a fitting gaping mouth and sword-like teeth, that great Lord who split with His sharp claws the strong chest of the demon - that is His abode: Singavel Kundram, where there is nothing but wearied wild beasts, shattered hills, and bamboo withered away by the standing flames.

66.translit:
Evvam vev vEl pon peyarOn Edhalin in uyirai
vavvi aagam vaḷ ugiraal vagirndha am maan adhu iḍam,
kavvu naayum kazhugum ucchi pOdhoḍu kaal suzhanṟu,
dheyvam allaal sella oNNaac chiṅgavEL kunṟamE.
66.meaning: On account of the enmity of Hiranya, the one named for gold who wielded the cruel grievous spear, that great Lord seized his sweet life and split open his chest with His sharp claws - that is His abode: Singavel Kundram, where snarling dogs and vultures roam, where whirlwinds spin in the noonday sun, and where none but the divine can pass.

67.translit:
Menṟa pEzhvaay vaaL eyiṯṟu Or kOL ariyaay, avuNan
ponṟa aagam vaḷ ugiraal pOzhndha punidhan iḍam,
ninṟa sen thee moNḍu sooṟai neeḷ visumb ooḍu iriya,
senṟu kaaNḍaṟku ariya kOyil siṅgavEL kunṟamE.
67.meaning: Taking the form of a fierce man-lion with a gaping crushing mouth and sword-like teeth, the holy Lord who tore open with His sharp claws the chest of the demon so that he perished - that is His abode: Singavel Kundram, a temple hard to reach and behold, where whirlwinds scoop up the standing red fire and scatter it through the vast sky.

68.translit:
Erindha paiṅ kaN ilaṅgu pEzhvaay eyiṯṟoḍu i dhev uru enṟu,
irindhu vaanOr kalaṅgi Oḍa irundha am maan adhu iḍam,
nerindha vEyin muzhaiyuL ninṟu neeL neṟi vaay uzhuvai,
thirindha aanaic chuvaḍu paarkkum siṅgavEL kunṟamE.
68.meaning: With blazing fresh eyes, gleaming gaping mouth and tusks - "what fearsome form is this!" - so that the celestials, terrified and bewildered, fled and scattered, that great Lord abode there - that is His abode: Singavel Kundram, where the tiger, standing within a hollow of crushed bamboo, watches along the long path for the tracks of roaming elephants.

69.translit:
Munaiththa seeṯṟam viN suḍap pOy moo ulagum piṟavum,
anaiththum añja vaaL ariyaay irundha am maan adhu iḍam,
kanaiththa theeyum kallum allaa vil uḍai vEḍarum aay,
thinaith thanaiyum sella oNNaac chiṅgavEL kunṟamE.
69.meaning: His sharpened wrath went forth to scorch the heavens, so that the three worlds and all else trembled in fear; as the fierce man-lion that great Lord abode there - that is His abode: Singavel Kundram, where, what with roaring fire and rocks and bow-bearing hunters, one cannot advance even a millet's measure.

70.translit:
Naa thazhumba naan muganum eesanum aay muṟaiyaal
Eththa aṅgu Or aaL ariyaay irundha am maan adhu iḍam,
kaayththa vaagai neṟṟu olippak kal adhar vEyṅ kazhai pOy,
thEyththa theeyaal viN sivakkum siṅgavEL kunṟamE.
70.meaning: While the four-faced Brahma and Shiva in due order praised Him till their tongues grew calloused, as a man-lion that great Lord abode there - that is His abode: Singavel Kundram, where the pods of the ripened vagai trees crackle, and the sky reddens with the fire kindled by bamboos on the rocky paths rubbing against one another.

71.translit:
Nallai neñjE! naam thozhudhum nammuḍai nam perumaan,
alli maadhar pulga ninṟa aayiram thOḷan iḍam,
nelli malgik kal uḍaippap pul ilai aarththu, adhar vaayc
silli sill enṟu ol aṟaadha siṅgavEL kunṟamE.
71.meaning: You are good, O my heart! Let us worship - the abode of our own Lord, He of the thousand shoulders embraced by Lakshmi born of the lotus - that is His place: Singavel Kundram, where amla fruits ripen abundantly and burst the rocks, where dry leaves rustle, and on the paths the crickets chirp "silli, sill" without ceasing.

72.translit:
Seṅ kaN aaḷ iṭṭu iṟaiñjum siṅgavEL kunṟu uḍaiya,
eṅgaḷ eesan em piraanai irun tamizh nool pulavan,
maṅgaiyaaḷan mannu thol seer vaNḍu aṟai thaark kaliyan,
seṅ kaiyaaḷan sen sol maalai vallavar theedhu ilarE.
72.meaning: Of our Lord and Master who owns Singavel Kundram - where red-eyed lions bow and worship - Kaliyan, the poet of the great Tamil works, lord of Tirumangai, of enduring ancient glory, wearing a garland on which bees hum, the generous-handed one, has made this garland of refined words; those who can master it shall be free from all evil.

73.translit:
Koṅgu alarndha malark kurundham mosiththa kOvalan em piraan,
saṅgu thaṅgu thaḍaṅ kaḍal thuyil koNḍa thaamaraik kaNNinan,
poṅgu puḷḷinai vaay piḷandha puraaNar tham iḍam, poṅgu neerc
seṅ kayal thiḷaikkum sunaith tiruvEṅgaḍam aḍai neñjamE.
73.meaning: The cowherd Lord, our master, who uprooted the fragrant blossoming kurundha tree, the lotus-eyed one who reclines in sleep on the vast conch-bearing ocean, the Ancient One who tore apart the beak of the raging bird (the demon Bakasura) - this is His place: reach Tiruvenkatam, O my heart, where red carp sport in the springs of surging water.

74.translit:
Palli aavadhu paaṟkaḍal araṅgam iraṅga van pEy mulai,
piḷḷai aay uyir uNḍa endhai piraan avan perugum iḍam,
veḷḷiyaan kariyaan maNi niṟa vaNNan enṟu eNNi, naaḍoṟum
theḷḷiyaar vaNaṅgum malai tiruvEṅgaḍam aḍai neñjamE.
74.meaning: His resting places are the Milk Ocean and Srirangam; as a child He sucked away the very life from the cruel demoness's breast in pity - our Father and Lord; this is the place where He flourishes: a mountain where the clear-minded, daily contemplating Him as the white-hued, the dark-hued, the sapphire-coloured one, come and worship - reach Tiruvenkatam, O my heart.

75.translit:
Ninṟa maa marudhu iṟṟu veezha naḍandha nin malan nEmiyaan,
enṟum vaanavarkku ai thozhum iNaith thaamarai aḍi em piraan,
kanṟi maari pozhindhiḍak kaḍidhu aa niraikku iḍar neekkuvaan,
senṟu kunṟam eḍuththavan tiruvEṅgaḍam aḍai neñjamE.
75.meaning: The flawless one who crawled so that the two great standing marudha trees snapped and fell, He who bears the discus, our Lord whose twin lotus feet the celestials ever clasp and worship; He who, when the rain poured down in fury, swiftly went and lifted up the hill to remove the distress of the cattle - reach His Tiruvenkatam, O my heart.

76.translit:
Paarthaṟku aay anṟu paaradham kai seydhiṭṭu venṟa parañ suḍar,
kOththu aṅgu aayar tham paaḍiyil kuravai piNaindha veṅ kOvalan,
Eththuvaar tham manaththuḷḷaan iḍavendhai mEviya em piraan,
theerththa neerth thaḍañ sOlai soozh tiruvEṅgaḍam aḍai neñjamE.
76.meaning: The supreme effulgence who, for Arjuna's sake, took the Bharata war into His own hands and won it; the ardent cowherd who there in the herdsmen's hamlet joined hands and danced the kuravai; our Lord who abides in the hearts of those who praise Him and who dwells at Idavendai - reach His Tiruvenkatam, encircled by groves with pools of holy water, O my heart.

77.translit:
VaN kaiyaan avuNarkku naayagan vELviyil senṟu maaNi aay,
maN kaiyaal irandhaan maraa maram Ezhum eydha valaththinaan,
eN kaiyaan imayaththuḷḷaan iruñ sOlai mEviya em piraan,
thiN kai maa thuyar adhu theerththavan tiruvEṅgaḍam aḍai neñjamE.
77.meaning: He who, going as a young brahmin to the sacrifice of the open-handed king of the demons (Mahabali), begged with His hand for land; He of the prowess who pierced the seven maramara trees with one arrow; He who dwells at Imayam (the Himalayas) and our Lord who abides at Irunsolai; He who removed the grievous distress of the strong-tusked great elephant (Gajendra) - reach His Tiruvenkatam, O my heart.

78.translit:
EN disaigaḷum Ezh ulagamum vaaṅgip pon vayiṯṟil peydhu,
paNḍu Or aal ilaip paḷḷi koNḍavan paal madhikku iḍar theerththavan,
oN thiṟal avuNan uraththu ugir vaiththavan oḷ eyiṯṟoḍu
thiN thiṟal ariyaay avan tiruvEṅgaḍam aḍai neñjamE.
78.meaning: He who drew in the eight directions and the seven worlds and placed them in His golden belly, who long ago reclined upon a banyan leaf, who removed the distress of the milk-white moon; He who set His claws into the chest of the bright-strong demon, as the man-lion of firm valour with gleaming teeth - reach His Tiruvenkatam, O my heart.

79.translit:
Paarum neer eri kaaṟṟinoḍu aagaasamum ivai aayinaan,
pErum aayiram pEsa ninṟa piṟappili perugum iḍam,
kaarum vaarp pani neeḷ visumbiḍaic sOrum maa mugil thOy thara,
sErum vaarp pozhil soozh ezhil tiruvEṅgaḍam aḍai neñjamE.
79.meaning: He who became these very elements - earth, water, fire, wind and sky; the birthless one whose names that men utter number a thousand; this is the place where He flourishes: reach beautiful Tiruvenkatam, encircled by spreading groves, where the great clouds of the rainy season, pouring down cool mist across the long sky, descend and touch the trees, O my heart.

80.translit:
amparam anal kaal nilam salam aagi ninRa amararkkOn,
vampu ulaa malar mEl mali mada mangai tan kozhunan avan,
kompin anna idai madak kuRamaadhar neel idhaN thoRum,
sempunam avai kaaval koL thiruvEngadam adai nenjamE.
80.meaning: He, the Lord of the celestials, who stands as the five elements — sky, fire, wind, earth and water; the beloved consort of the tender young Lakshmi who dwells on the fragrant blossom. O heart, take refuge in Tiruvengadam, where on every long platform the slender, creeper-waisted Kurava maidens guard their lush red millet fields.

81.translit:
pEsum int thirunaamam ettezhuthum solli ninRu, pinnarum,
pEsuvaar thammai uyya vaangip piRappu aRukkum piraan idam,
vaasa maa malar naaRu vaar pozhil soozh tharum ulagukku ellaam,
dhEsamaayth thigazhum malai thiruvEngadam adai nenjamE.
81.meaning: It is the abode of the Lord who, when devotees stand uttering this sacred name of eight letters and keep speaking it, lifts them up to be saved and severs their cycle of births. O heart, take refuge in Tiruvengadam, the hill that shines as a radiant beacon for all the worlds, encircled by long groves fragrant with great blossoms.

82.translit:
sengayal thiLaikkum sunaith thiruvEngadaththu uRai selvanai,
mangaiyar thalaivan kalikanRi vaN tamizhch chenchol maalaigaL,
sangaiyinRith thariththu uraikka vallaargaL thanjam adhaagavE,
vangamaa kadal vaiyam kaavalar aagi vaanulagu aaLvarE.
82.meaning: Those who can hold firm and recite without doubt these garlands of fine, generous Tamil words composed by Kalikanri, the lord of Tirumangai, upon the Lord of wealth who dwells in Tiruvengadam where red carp sport in the springs — they shall become protectors of this earth girt by the ship-bearing great ocean, and shall thereafter rule the celestial world, taking that as their refuge.

83.translit:
thaayE thanthai enRum thaaramE kiLai makkaL enRum,
nOyE pattu ozhinthEn unnaik kaaNpadhu Or aasaiyinaal,
vEy Ey poom pozhil soozh viraiyaar thiruvEngadavaa,
naayEn vanthu adainthEn nalgi aaL ennaik koNdaruLE.
83.meaning: Calling 'mother,' 'father,' 'wife,' 'kinsmen and children,' I wasted away afflicted as if by a disease. With a single longing to behold You, O Lord of fragrant Tiruvengadam encircled by flowering groves thick with bamboo, I, lowly as a dog, have come and taken refuge. Be gracious, accept me and make me Your servant.

84.translit:
maan Ey kaN madavaar mayakkil pattu maa nilaththu,
naanE naanaavidha naragam pugum paavam seydhEn,
dhEn Ey poom pozhil soozh thiruvEngada maa malai, en
aanaay vanthu adainthEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
84.meaning: Caught in the bewitchment of tender women with eyes like a doe, I myself committed on this great earth the sins that lead to hells of every kind. O great Tiruvengadam hill encircled by flowering groves filled with honey, my own one — I have come and taken refuge. Be gracious and accept me, Your servant, into Your service.

85.translit:
konREn pal uyirai kuRikkOL onRu ilaamaiyinaal,
enREnum irandhaarkku inidhaaga uraiththaRiyEn,
kunRu Ey mEgam adhir kuLir maa malai vEngadavaa,
anRE vanthu adainthEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
85.meaning: For want of any guiding principle I have slain many lives, and I have never known how to speak even one kind word to those who came begging of me. O Lord of cool, great Vengadam hill where the clouds resound upon the peaks, that very day I came and took refuge. Be gracious and accept me, Your servant, into Your service.

86.translit:
kulam thaan ettanaiyum piRanthE iRanthu eyththu ozhinthEn,
nalam thaan onRum ilEn nalladhu Or aRam seydhum ilEn,
nilam thOy neeL mugil sEr neRiyaar thiruvEngadavaa,
alanthEn vanthu adainthEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
86.meaning: Through every kind of birth-line I was born and died and grew weary and wasted away. I possess no goodness whatsoever, nor have I done any worthy act of virtue. O Lord of fair Tiruvengadam where long clouds touch the earth and gather, I have suffered, and now I have come and taken refuge. Be gracious and accept me, Your servant, into Your service.

87.translit:
ep paavam palavum ivaiyE seydhu iLaiththu ozhinthEn,
thuppaa! nin adiyE thodarnthu Eththavum kiRkinRilEn,
seppaar thiN varai soozh thiruvEngada maa malai, en
appaa! vanthu adainthEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
87.meaning: Doing only these manifold sins of every kind, I wore myself out and wasted away. O my strength, I am not even able to follow and praise Your feet alone. O great Tiruvengadam hill girt by firm mountains like upturned vessels, my Father — I have come and taken refuge. Be gracious and accept me, Your servant, into Your service.

88.translit:
maNNaay neer eri kaal manju ulaavum aagaasamum aam,
puN aaraakkai thannuL pulambith thaLarnthu eyththu ozhinthEn,
viNNaar neeL sigara viraiyaar thiruvEngadavaa,
aNNaa! vanthu adainthEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
88.meaning: Within this wound-ridden body, made of earth, water, fire, wind and the sky where clouds roam, I lamented, grew weak and wasted away. O Lord of fragrant Tiruvengadam whose long peaks reach the heavens, my master — I have come and taken refuge. Be gracious and accept me, Your servant, into Your service.

89.translit:
theri en baalaganaayp pala theemaigaL seydhum ittEn,
periyEn aayina pin piRarkkE uzhaiththu Ezhaiyaan En,
kari sErp poom pozhil soozh kana maa malai vEngadavaa!,
ariya! vanthu adainthEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
89.meaning: As a thoughtless child I committed and heaped up many evil deeds; and once grown into an adult, I toiled only for others and became destitute. O rare Lord of dense, great Vengadam hill encircled by flowering groves where elephants gather, I have come and taken refuge. Be gracious and accept me, Your servant, into Your service.

90.translit:
nORREn pal piRavi unnaik kaaNpadhu Or aasaiyinaal,
ERREn ip piRappE idar uRRanan em perumaan!,
kOl thEn paaynthu ozhugum kuLir sOlai soozh vEngadavaa!,
aaRREn vanthu adainthEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
90.meaning: With the single longing to behold You I performed penance through many births; yet I accepted this present birth and have come to grief, O my Lord. O Lord of Vengadam encircled by cool groves where honey streams and flows from the combs, I can bear it no longer — I have come and taken refuge. Be gracious and accept me, Your servant, into Your service.

91.translit:
paRRu El onRum ilEn paavamE seydhu paaviyaanEn,
maRRu El onRu aRiyEn maayanE! engaL maadhavanE!,
kal thEn paaynthu ozhugum kamalach chunai vEngadavaa!,
aRREn vanthu adainthEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
91.meaning: I have not a single support; I have done only sin and become a sinner. I know nothing else at all, O wondrous one, O our Madhava! O Lord of Vengadam with lotus-filled springs where honey streams down from the rocks, I am bereft of all — I have come and taken refuge. Be gracious and accept me, Your servant, into Your service.

92.translit:
kaNNaay Ezhulagukku uyiraaya em kaar vaNNanai,
viNNOr thaam paravum pozhil vEngada vEdhiyanai,
thiNNaar maadangaL soozh thirumangaiyarkkOn kaliyan,
paNNaar paadal patthum payilvaarkku illai paavangaLE.
92.meaning: Upon our dark-hued Lord, who is the eye and the very life of the seven worlds, the Vedic Lord of grove-clad Vengadam whom the celestials extol — Kaliyan, lord of Tirumangai which is encircled by sturdy mansions, composed these ten tuneful songs. For those who practice them there are no sins.

93.translit:
kaN aar kadal soozh ilangaikku iRaivan than,
thiN naagam piLakkach charam sela uyththaay,
viNNOr thozhum vEngada maa malai mEya,
aNNaa! adiyEn idaraik kaLaiyaayE.
93.meaning: You who shot Your arrow speeding so that it split open the firm chest of the lord of Lanka that is girt by the wide, spreading sea! O master who abides on the great Vengadam hill worshipped by the celestials, remove the suffering of me, Your servant.

94.translit:
ilangaip padhikku anRu iRaiyaaya, arakkar
kulam kettu avar maaLak kodip puL thiriththaay!,
vilangal kudumith thiruvEngadam mEya,
alangal thuLaba mudiyaay! aruLaayE!
94.meaning: You who once made the bird-bannered Garuda wheel about so that the race of demons was destroyed and their lord of the city of Lanka perished! O Lord who abides on Tiruvengadam with its mountain peaks, wearer of the swaying tulasi crown, grant Your grace!

95.translit:
neer aar kadalum nilanum muzhudhu uNdu,
Er aala iLam thaLir mEl thuyil enthaay!,
seer aar thiruvEngada maa malai mEya,
aaraa amudhE! adiyERku aruLaayE!
95.meaning: My Father, who swallowed up the whole water-filled ocean and the earth and then slept upon a tender, beautiful banyan leaf! O insatiable nectar who abides on the glorious great Vengadam hill, grant Your grace to me, Your servant!

96.translit:
uNdaay uRi mEl naRu ney amudhaaga,
koNdaay kuRaLaay nilam eer adiyaalE,
viN thOy sigarath thiruvEngadam mEya,
aNdaa! adiyEnukku aruL puriyaayE!
96.meaning: You who ate the fragrant butter from the hanging pot as if it were nectar; You who, as the dwarf, took the earth with two strides! O Lord of the cosmic egg who abides on Tiruvengadam whose peaks touch the sky, bestow Your grace upon me, Your servant!

97.translit:
thooN aaya adhanoodu ariyaay vanthu thOnRi,
pENaa avuNan udalam piLanthittaay,
sEN aar thiruvEngada maa malai mEya,
kON naaga aNaiyaay! kuRikkoL enai neeyE!
97.meaning: You who appeared as the man-lion, bursting forth from within the pillar, and tore apart the body of the hostile demon! O Lord who abides on the lofty great Vengadam hill, who reclines upon the coiled serpent-couch, take heed of me!

98.translit:
mannaa im manisap piRaviyai neekki,
thannaagith thanninaruL seyyum thalaivan,
minnaar mugil sEr thiruvEngadam mEya,
en aanai en appan en nenjil uLaanE!
98.meaning: The Lord who removes this impermanent human birth, makes one His own, and bestows His own grace — He who abides on Tiruvengadam where lightning-laden clouds gather; my elephant, my Father, dwells within my heart!

99.translit:
maan Ey mada nOkki thiRaththu edhir vantha,
aan Ezh vidai seRRa aNi varaith thOLaa!,
thEnE. thiruvEngada maa malai mEya,
kOnE. en manam kudikoNdu irunthaayE.
99.meaning: O You of the beautiful mountain-like shoulders, who slew the seven bulls that came charging at You, for the sake of the gentle, doe-eyed maiden! O honey, O King who abides on the great Vengadam hill, You have come and taken up dwelling within my heart!

1000.translit:
koTTaay pallikkuTTi, kuDamaaDi yulakaLanda,
maTTaar poongkuzhal maadava naivarak, koTTaay pallikkuTTi.
1000.meaning: Clap your hands, little house-bird! When Madhava — he who danced the pot-dance and who measured the worlds — comes, clap your hands, O little house-bird.

1001.translit:
sollaay paingkiLiye, suDaraazhi valanuyartta,
mallaar tooLvaDa veengkaDa vanvara, sollaay paingkiLiye.
1001.meaning: Speak, O green parrot! When the strong-shouldered Lord of northern Venkatam, who raised aloft the radiant discus in his right hand, comes, speak, O green parrot.

1002.translit:
koozhi koovennumaal, toozhi naanenseykeen,
aazhi vaNNar varumpozhu daayittu koozhi koovennumaal.
1002.meaning: The rooster keeps crowing! O friend, what shall I do? Just at the time when the ocean-hued Lord was to come, the rooster keeps crowing!

1003.translit:
kaamat kenkaDaveen, karumaamukil vaNNatkallaal,
poome laingkaNai koottup pukundeyyak, kaamat kenkaDaveen.
1003.meaning: What do I owe to Kama (the love-god), except to the dark great-cloud-hued Lord? Stringing his five flower-arrows on the bow he entered and struck me — what do I owe to Kama?

1004.translit:
ingkee pootungkoloo, inaveelneDung kaNgaLippa,
kongkaar soolaik kuDandaik kiDandamaal, ingkee pootungkoloo.
1004.meaning: Will he come here, perhaps, to delight my long spear-like eyes? The great Lord who reclines at Kudandai (Tirukkudandai/Kumbakonam) of honey-laden groves — will he come here, perhaps?

1005.translit:
innaa rentaRiyeen, anne. aazhiyoDum,
ponnaar saarnga muDaiya aDikaLai, innaa rentaRiyeen.
1005.meaning: I do not know who he is, O mother! With his discus, and bearing the golden Sharnga bow — those holy feet — I do not know who he is.

1006.translit:
toNDeer. paaDuminoo, surumbaarppozhil mangkaiyarkoon,
oNDaar veelkali yanoli maalaikaL, toNDeer. paaDuminoo.
1006.meaning: O devotees, sing! These resounding garland-verses of Kaliyan, lord of Mangai, who wields the bright-flowered spear, of the groves humming with bees — O devotees, sing them!

1007.translit:
kuNDra monteDut teendi, maamazhai
antu kaattavam maan, arakkarai
venta villiyaar veera mekoloo,
tental vandu tee veesu menseykeen.
1007.meaning: Is this the heroism of that great Lord, the archer, who once lifted up a single mountain and held it aloft to shield from the great rain, and who conquered the demons? The southern breeze comes and scatters fire upon me — what shall I do?

1008.translit:
kaarum vaarpanik kaDalum annavan,
taarum maarvamum kaNDa taNDamoo,
sooru maamukil tuLiyi nooDuvandu
eera vaaDaitaan eeru mennaiye.
1008.meaning: Is this the punishment for having seen the garland and the chest of him who is like the rain-cloud and the broad cool ocean? Coming through the droplets of the streaming great cloud, the chill north wind cuts through me.

1009.translit:
sangku maamaiyum taLaru meenimeel,
tingkaL vengkadir seeRu menseykeen,
pongku veNDiraip puNari vaNNanaar,
kongka laarndadaar koovu mennaiye.
1009.meaning: Upon my body, whose conch-bangles and complexion are wearing away, the moon's hot rays rage — what shall I do? The garland, fragrant with honey, of the Lord hued like the ocean of surging white waves, calls out to me.

1010.translit:
angko raaykkulat tuLva LarndusenDru,
angkor taayuru vaaki vandavaL,
kongkai nansuNDa kooyin maikoloo,
tingkaL vengkadir seeRu kinTradee.
1010.meaning: Is it the wickedness done when he, grown up there in a cowherd clan, went and sucked dry the breast of her (Putana) who came taking the form of a mother — that now the moon's hot rays rage at me?

1011.translit:
angko raaLari yaay, avuNanaip
pangka maaviru kooRu seydavan,
mangkul maamadi vaangka vekoloo
pongku maakaDal pulambu kinTradee.
1011.meaning: He who became the man-lion (Narasimha) and tore the demon (Hiranya) into two pieces in ruin — is it to draw forth the great moon among the clouds that the surging great ocean wails so?

1012.translit:
senDru vaarsilai vaLaittu ilangkaiyai,
venta villiyaar veera mekoloo,
muntil peNNaimeel muLarik kooTTakattu,
antri linkural aDaru mennaiye.
1012.meaning: Is this the heroism of the archer (Rama) who went and bent his long bow and conquered Lanka? From within the lotus-shaped nest atop the palmyra tree in the front yard, the cry of the anril bird torments me.

1013.translit:
poovai vaNNanaar puLLin meelvara,
meevi ninTrunaan kaNDa taNDamoo,
veevi laingkaNai villi yampukoottu,
aavi yeyilak kaaka eyvadee.
1013.meaning: Is this the punishment for having stood longing and beheld the Lord, hued like the kaya flower, as he came upon his bird (Garuda)? The archer of the unfailing five arrows strings his shaft and shoots, making my very life his target.

1014.translit:
maali nanduzhaay varumen nenjakam,
maalin anduzhaay vanden nuLpuka,
koola vaaDaiyum koNDu vandadu,
oor aali vandadaal aridu kaavale.
1014.meaning: The Lord's beautiful tulasi comes into my heart; that beautiful tulasi of the Lord came and entered within me. It brought with it the bright north wind too; a rain-cloud has come — guarding myself is hard.

1015.translit:
koNDai yoNkaNum tuyilum,
enniRam paNDu paNDupoo lokkum,
mikkaseert toNDa riTTapoon tuLavin vaasamee,
vaNDu koNDuvan dootu maakilee.
1015.meaning: My adorned bright eyes and my sleep, and my complexion, would become just as they were in days long past — if only the bees would carry and waft to me the fragrance of the flowering tulasi offered by the devotees of surpassing glory.

1016.translit:
antru paaratat taivar tootanaay,
sentra maayanaic sengkaN maalinai,
mantri laarppukazh mangkai vaaLkali
kantri, solvallaark kalla lillaiye.
1016.meaning: To the wondrous one (Krishna) who once went as messenger of the five (Pandavas) in the Mahabharata, the red-eyed Tirumal — Kaliyan of the sword, of Mangai whose praise resounds in the assembly, sang these words; for those able to recite them there will be no sorrow.

1017.translit:
kuNDra meDuttu mazhaita Duttu iLai yaaroDum
mantril kuravai yiNainda maalenne maalseydaan,
muntil tanininTra peNNai meelkiDan deerkinTra
antrilin kooTTaip pirikka kiRpava raarkoloo.
1017.meaning: The Lord who lifted the mountain and stayed the rain, and who joined the kuravai dance in the common ground with the young cowherd girls, has cast his enchantment upon me. Who is there able to part the nest of the anril birds that lie crying together atop the lone palmyra standing in the front yard?

1018.translit:
poongku rundosittu aanai kaayndari maaccekuttu,
aangku veezhattin kombu koNDuvan peeymulai
vaangki yuNDa, av vaayan niRkaiv vaayanvaay,
eengku veengkuzhal ennoo DaaDum iLamaiye.
1018.meaning: He broke the flowering kurunda tree, struck down the elephant, destroyed the great lion-beast, and there, taking the elephant's tusk, drained dry the breast of the cruel demoness (Putana) — while that cowherd stands apart, the plaintive flute on this cowherd's lips toys with my tender youth.

1019.translit:
mallooDu kanjanum tunja ventra maNivaNNan,
alli malarttaN Duzhaayni naindirun deenaiye,
elli yinmaa rutamvan daDumadu vantriyum,
kollaival leetrin maNiyum kooyinmai seyyumee.
1019.meaning: The sapphire-hued Lord who conquered, making the wrestlers and Kamsa perish — upon me who sit absorbed in thoughts of his cool tulasi of opening blossoms, the night's wind comes and torments; and not only that, the bell of the fierce bull of the field too does its cruel mischief to me.

1020.translit:
poruntu maamara meezhu seyta punithanaar
tiruntu seevaDi yenma nattu ninaitorum,
karuntaN maakaDal mangu laarkkum atuvanriyum,
varunta vaaDai varumi tarkini yenseykeen.
1020.meaning: The pure Lord who pierced the seven great sal trees in a row — whenever my heart contemplates his perfect sacred red feet, the dark cool great ocean roars at me; and besides that, the chill north wind comes to torment me. What more can I now do against this?

1021.translit:
annai munivatum anri lin kura leervatum,
mannu marikaDal aarppa tumvaLai soorvatum,
ponnang kalaiyalku lanna mennaDaip poonguzhal,
pinnai maNaaLar tiratta vaayina pinnaiye.
1021.meaning: My mother's scolding, the cry of the anril bird piercing me, the everlasting roaring ocean's clamour, and my bangles slipping loose — O you with golden-girdled waist, swan-soft gait and flower-decked tresses, all these came to be on account of Pinnai's bridegroom (Kannan).

1022.translit:
aazhiyum sangu muDaiya nangaL aDigaLtaam,
paazhimai yaana kanavil nammaip pakarvittaar,
toozhiyum naanu mozhiya vaiyam tuyinratu,
koozhiyum kookinra tillaik kooriru Laayitre.
1022.meaning: Our Lord, who bears the discus and the conch, revealed himself to me in a vast wondrous dream. Except for my friend and me, the whole world has fallen asleep; not even the rooster is crowing — it has become a thick, deep darkness.

1023.translit:
kaaman ranakku muraiyal leenkaDal vaNNanaar,
maamaNa vaaLa renakkut taanum makansollil,
yaamangaL toreri veesu menniLang kongaikaL,
maamaNi vaNNar tiratta vaayvaLar kinrave.
1023.meaning: I will not even speak to Kaman (the love-god); should anyone speak of the ocean-hued Lord as my great bridegroom, my young breasts scatter fire through every watch of the night. They have grown ripe on account of the Lord hued like a flawless sapphire.

1024.translit:
mansuru maalirun soolai ninra maNaaLanaar,
nenjam niraikoNDu pooyi naarninai kinrilar,
venjuDar pooyviDi yaamal evviDam pukkatoo,
nanju uDalam tuyinraal namakkini nallate.
1024.meaning: The bridegroom who stands at Maaliruncholai full of glory has carried off my heart and gone; he does not think of me at all. Where has the fierce sun gone, that day will not break? If this body, like poison to me, would only sleep, that now would be good for me.

1025.translit:
kaaman kaNaikko rilakka maaynalat tilmiku,
poomaru koolanam peNmai sintitti raatupooy
toomalar neerkoDu toozhi naamtozhu teettinaal
kaarmukil vaNNaraik kaNga laalkaaNa laangkoloo.
1025.meaning: Having become a target for Kaman's arrows, our beauty rich in loveliness, adorned with flowers, withers without his giving it a thought. O friend, if we go and worship him, offering pure flowers and water, and praise him — might we then behold with our own eyes the Lord hued like a dark rain-cloud?

1026.translit:
venri viDaiyuDa neezha Datta aDigaLai,
manril malipukazh mangai mankali kanrisol,
onru ninravon patumu raippavar tangaLmeel
enrum nillaavinai yonrum solli lulakile.
1026.meaning: Of the Lord who triumphantly subdued the seven bulls, Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) of Mangai, abounding in fame in its assembly hall, sang these. For those who recite these ten verses that stand firm as one, no abiding karmic affliction whatsoever will remain in this world, it is said.

1027.translit:
mannilangu paaratattut teeroorntu, maavaliyaip
ponnilangu tiNvilangil vaittup porukaDalsoozh
tennilangai yeeDazhitta teevark kitukaaNeer
ennilangu sangoo Dezhiltoor rirunteene.
1027.meaning: He who drove the chariot in the glorious Bharata (Mahabharata) war, who bound Mavali in firm shining golden fetters, who destroyed the might of southern Lanka girt by the surging ocean — behold, for that Lord I sit here, my radiance gone along with my conch-bangles.

1028.translit:
iruntaane nuLLat tiraivan,karaiseer
paruntaaL kaLitruk karuLseyta,
sengaN peruntooL neDumaalaip peerpaaDi yaaDa
varuntaaten kongai yoLimannum anne.
1028.meaning: The Lord, master, has come to dwell in my heart — he who showed grace to the elephant (Gajendra) with its broad stained legs. When I sing and dance, praising the names of the red-eyed, broad-shouldered Nedumal, the radiance of my breasts abides without fading, O mother.

1029.translit:
anne! ivarai yarivan, marainaankum
munne yuraitta muniva veevarvantu
ponney vaLaikavarntu pookaar manampukuntu
enne yivareNNum eNNam ariyoome.
1029.meaning: O mother! I know him — this sage who long ago uttered the four Vedas has come; having seized my golden bangles he will not depart, but has entered my mind. Alas, we do not know what scheme this one is plotting.

1030.translit:
ariyoome yenruraikka laame emakku,
veriyaar pozhilsoozh viyankuDantai meevi,
siriyaanoor piLLaiyaay meLLa naDanttiTTu uriyaar
naruveNNe yuNDukantaar tammaiye.
1030.meaning: Can we say 'we do not know' regarding him — he who, dwelling in spacious Kudandai (Kumbakonam) ringed by fragrant groves, came as a small child, toddled softly along, and joyfully ate the fragrant white butter from the hanging pot?

1031.translit:
tammaiye naaLum vaNangit tozhuvaarkku,
tammaiye yokka aruLseyva raatalaal,
tammaiye naaLum vaNangit tozhutiraingi,
tammaiye patraa manattenrum vaittoome.
1031.meaning: For those who daily bow and worship him alone, he grants grace to make them equal to himself. Therefore, daily bowing and worshipping and bending low before him alone, we have set him in our heart forever, clinging to him alone.

1032.translit:
vaittaa raDiyaar manattinil vaittu, inpam
uyttaa roLivisumpi looraDivaittu, ooraDikkum
eyttaatu maNNen rimaiyoor tozhutiraingi,
kaittaa maraikuvikkum kaNNanen kaNNanaiye.
1032.meaning: He has placed himself in the hearts of his devotees and bestowed bliss; he set one foot upon the luminous sky, and for the other foot the earth not sufficing, the celestials worship and bow and join their lotus hands — that Kannan, my own Kannan.

1033.translit:
kaNNan manattuLLe nirkavum, kaivaLaikaL
enno kazhanra? ivaiyenna maayangaL?
peNNaanoom peNmaiyoom nirka, avanmeeya,
aNNal malaiyum arangamum paaDoome.
1033.meaning: Even while Kannan stands within my heart, why have the bangles slipped from my hands? What sorcery is this? Though we have become women and our womanhood remains, let us sing of the noble Lord's mountain (Venkatam) and of Arangam (Srirangam) where he abides.

1034.translit:
paaDoome yentai perumaanai? paaDininru
aaDoome yaayiram peeraanai? peerninaintu
suuDoome suuDum tuzhaayalangal? suuDi,naam
kuuDoome kuuDak kurippaakil? nannenje.
1034.meaning: Shall we not sing of our Father, our Lord? Standing and singing, shall we not dance for him of the thousand names? Recalling his names, shall we not wear the tulasi garland that he wears? And wearing it, if it be his intent that we unite, shall we not unite with him? O good heart.

1035.translit:
nannenje. namperumaan naaLum initamarum,
annamseer kaanal aNiyaali kaitozhutu,
munnamseer valvinaikaL pooka mukilvaNNan,
ponnamseer seevaDimeel pootaNiyap petroome.
1035.meaning: O good heart! Worshipping the beautiful Aali (Tiruvali), with its swan-filled seaside grove where our Lord abides sweetly every day, our former grievous karma departs — and we have been blessed to lay flowers upon the golden-hued sacred red feet of the cloud-hued Lord.

1036.translit:
petraaraar aayiram peeraanaip, peerpaaDap
petraan kaliya nolisey tamizhmaalai,
katraaroo mutrula kaaLva rivaikeeTka
lutraarkku, urutuya rillai yulakatte.
1036.meaning: This garland of melodious Tamil that Kaliyan composed, being blessed to sing the names of the Lord of a thousand names — those who learn it will rule the whole world, and for those who come to hear these, no grievous affliction will befall them in this world.

1037.translit:
nilaiyiDa mengu minri neDuveLLam umpar vaLanaaDu mooDa imaiyoor
talaiyiDa matre makkor saraNillai enna araNaava nennu maruLaal
alaikaDal neerku zhampa akaDaaDa oDi yakalvaa nurinja, mutukil
malaikaLai meetu koNDu varumeenai maalai maravaa tiraingen manane.
1037.meaning: When there was no place to stand anywhere, and the vast flood engulfed even the fertile world of the celestials above, and the immortals cried 'there is no other refuge for us' — out of his grace, saying 'I shall be your protection,' the surging ocean-water churned, his belly grazing, he sped scraping the far heavens, bearing the mountains upon his back: that Fish (Matsya), the Lord — never forgetting him, bow down, O my heart.

1038.translit:
serumiku vaaLe yitra aravonru sutrit tisaimaNNum viNNu muDane
veruvara veLLai veLLam muzhutum kuzhampa imaiyoorgaL ninru kaDaiya,
paruvarai yonru ninru mutukir parantu suzhalak kiDantu tuyilum,
aruvarai yanna tanmai aDalaamai yaana tirumaal namakko raraNe.
1038.meaning: With a single battle-fierce, sharp-fanged serpent coiled around it, while the earth and sky of all directions trembled and the whole white ocean of milk was churned, the immortals standing and churning — a great mountain (Mandara) set upon his back spread and spun about as he lay still asleep: Tirumal who became that mighty tortoise (Kurma), like an awesome mountain, is our refuge.

1039.translit:
teetaru tingaL pongu suDarumpar umpa rulakeezhi nooDu muDane,
maatira maNsu mantu vaDakunru ninra malaiyaarum eezhu kaDalum
paatamar soozhku Lampi nakamaNDa latti norupaa loDunga vaLarseer,
aatimun eena maaki araNaaya muurtti atunammai yaaLu marase.
1039.meaning: The flawless moon, the surging-rayed sun, and the seven worlds of the celestials above all together, and the directions, the earth-bearing northern hill that stood firm, the six mountains and the seven seas — all shrinking into one corner of the inner region within the rim of his encircling hoof — that primordial Lord of glory who of old became the Boar (Varaha) and became our protection, that very form is the king who rules us.

1040.translit:
thaLaiyavizh koadhai maalai yirupaal thayanga erikaan'riraNdu tha'rukaN,
aLavezha vemmai mikka ariyaagi an'ru pariyoan sinanga Lavizha,
vaLaiyugi raaLi moymbil ma'ravoana thaagam madhiyaadhu sen'ro rugiraal
piLavezha vitta kutta madhuvaiya moodu peruneeril mummai peridhe.
1040.meaning: With the loosened garland of flowers swaying on both sides, He became Narasimha, the lion-man, with eyes spitting fire and two fierce fangs, His fury overflowing beyond measure on that day to undo the wrath of Hiranya. Heedless of the body of that warrior of mighty shoulders and curved claws, He went and with a single nail tore him open. That tearing of His is greater by three times than even the great waters that cover this earth.

1041.translit:
vendhi'ral vaaNan veaLvi yidameydhi angoar ku'raLaagi meymmai yuNara
sendhozhil veadha naavin muniyaagi vaiya mudimoon 'randhu pe'rinum,
mandhira meedhu poagi madhinin'ri'rainja malaroan vaNanga vaLarsear,
andhara meazhi noodu selavuyththa paadham adhunammai yaaLu marase.
1041.meaning: Reaching the sacrificial ground of Mahabali of fierce strength and renown, becoming a small dwarf so that truth might be known, becoming a sage with the Vedas of pure rites upon His tongue, though He begged for and received but three measured steps of the earth, He grew up beyond the chant, so that the moon-crowned ones bowed and Brahma on the lotus worshipped Him. The foot He set traveling through the seven skies is the very King who rules over us.

1042.translit:
irunila mannar thammai yirunaalum ettu morunaalu mon'ru mudane,
serunudha loodu poagi yavaraavi manga mazhuvaaLil ven'ra thi'raloan,
perunila mangai mannar malarmangai naadhar pulamangai keaLvar pugazhsear,
perunila muNdu mizhndha peruvaaya raagi yavarnammai yaaLvar peridhe.
1042.meaning: He of valour who, going into the battlefield, conquered with His axe-blade the kings of the wide earth - twice eight, eight and one and four together - so that their lives perished. He is the lord of the lady Earth, the master of the lotus-lady Lakshmi, the husband of the lady of learning, full of glory; with His great mouth He swallowed the wide earth and spat it forth, and He shall greatly rule over us.

1043.translit:
ilaimali paLLi yeydhi yidhumaayam enna inamaaya maanpin ezhilsear
alaimali vealka NaaLai yagalvippa dha'rko ruruvaaya maanai yaamaiyaa
kolaimali yeydhu viththa kodiyoan ilangai podiyaaga ven'ri yamaruL
silaimali senja rangaL selavuyththa nangaL thirumaal namakko rarane.
1043.meaning: Saying "this is illusion," He took a form to drive away from beside the deceptive deer of beauty the lady of wave-like swelling spear-eyes (Sita), reaching a leafy resting-place; that cruel one (Ravana) He made the target of slaughter, and crushing Lanka to dust He triumphed in war. Our Tirumal, who sent forth red arrows from His bow in that battle, is our sole refuge.

1044.translit:
munnula gangaL ezhum iruLmaNdiyuNNa mudhaloadu veedu ma'riyaadhu
ennidhu vandha thenna imaiyoar thigaippa ezhilveadha min'ri ma'raiya
pinnaiyum vaana varkkum munivarkkum nalgi yiruLdheerndhiv vaiya magizha
annama thaayi rundhang a'ranool uraiththa adhunammai yaaLu marase.
1044.meaning: When in former times all seven worlds were swallowed by thickening darkness, with neither origin nor end to be known, and the celestials were bewildered, asking "how has this come about?", and the beautiful Vedas vanished, He then, bestowing grace on the heavenly ones and the sages, dispelling the darkness so that this world rejoiced, became a swan and there expounded the scripture of dharma - that is the King who rules over us.

1045.translit:
thuNainilai ma'rre makko ruLadhen 'riraadhu thozhuminga thoNdar tholaiya
uNamulai mungoduththa vuravoaL thaaviyugavuNdu veNNey maruvi
paNamulai yaayar maadha ruraloadu katta adhanoadu moadi adalsear
iNaimaru thi'r'ru veezha nadaika'r'ra the'r'ral vinaipa'r'ra 'rukkum vidhiye.
1045.meaning: O devotees, worship without thinking "is there any other support for us" - that your bonds may end. He drank the very life of the mighty demoness (Putana) who gave her breast to slay Him; He delighted in the butter; when the cowherd women with full breasts bound Him with the mortar, He dragged it along and made the twin marudu trees fall - the warrior-child who learned to walk thus is the very ordinance that severs and breaks the grip of our karma.

1046.translit:
kolaikezhu semmu kaththa kaLi'ron'ru kon'ru kodiyoan ilangai podiyaa
silaikezhu senja rangaL selavuyththa nangaL thirumaalai, vealai pudaisoozh
kalikezhu maada veedhi vayalmangai mannu kalikan'ri sonna panuvil
oligezhu paadal paadi yuzhalgin'ra thoNdaravaraaLva rumba rulage.
1046.meaning: Of our Tirumal who slew that one red-faced murderous elephant, and who, reducing the wicked one's Lanka to dust, sent forth red arrows from His bow - the verses composed by Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar), abiding in Tirumangai of fields, with bustling mansion-streets surrounded on all sides by the sea: the devotees who wander singing this resounding song of praise shall rule over the world of the celestials above.

1047.translit:
maanamaru mennoakki vaidheavi yindhuNaiyaa,
kaanamarum kalladharpoaykkaadu'raindhaan kaaneadee
kaanamarum kalladharppoaykkaadu'raindha ponnadigaL,
vaanavardham senni malarkkaNdaay saazhale.
1047.meaning: With Vaidehi (Sita) of soft deer-like glances as His companion, He went along stony forest paths and dwelt in the wilderness - behold, O friend! Those golden feet that went along the stony forest paths and dwelt in the wilderness are, behold, the flower upon the crowns of the celestials - saazhale (refrain).

1048.translit:
thandhai thaLaikazhalth thoan'rippoay, aayppaadi
nandhan kulamadhalai yaayvaLarndhaan kaaneadee,
nandhan kulamadhalai yaayvaLarndhaan naanmuga'rkuth
thandhaikaaN, endhai perumaankaaN saazhale.
1048.meaning: He appeared as the father's fetters fell away, and going to Ayarpadi (Gokula) He grew up as the darling child of Nanda's line - behold, O friend! He who grew up as the darling child of Nanda's line is, behold, the father of the four-faced Brahma; behold, He is our Lord and master - saazhale (refrain).

1049.translit:
aazhkadalsoozh vaiyagaththaa reasappoay, aayppaadith
thaazhkuzhalaar vaiththa thayiruNdaan kaaneadee,
thaazhkuzhalaar vaiththa thayiruNda ponvayi'ru,iv
veazhulagu muNdum idamudaiththaal saazhale.
1049.meaning: So that the people of the world girded by the deep sea would mock, He went to Ayarpadi and ate the curds set aside by the women of long flowing hair - behold, O friend! That golden belly which ate the curds set aside by the women of long flowing hair, even after swallowing these seven worlds, still has room to spare - saazhale (refrain).

1050.translit:
a'riyaadhaark kaanaa yanaagippoay, aayppaadi
u'riyaar na'ruveNNe yuNdugandhaan kaaneadee
u'riyaar na'ruveNNe yuNdugandha ponvayi'rukku,
e'rinee rulaganaiththu meydhaadhaal saazhale.
1050.meaning: Becoming a cowherd to those who knew Him not, He went to Ayarpadi and gladly ate the fragrant white butter from the hanging pots - behold, O friend! For that golden belly which gladly ate the fragrant white butter from the hanging pots, all the worlds girded by the surging waters would not suffice - saazhale (refrain).

1051.translit:
vaNNak karunguzha laaychchiyaal moththuNdu,
kaNNik ku'rungayi'r'raal kattuNdaan kaaneadee,
kaNNik ku'rungayi'r'raal kattuNdaa naagilum,
eNNa'r kariyan imaiyoarkkum saazhale.
1051.meaning: He was struck by the cowherd-woman of beautiful dark tresses, and He was bound with a short knotted rope - behold, O friend! Though He was bound with a short knotted rope, He is hard to comprehend even for the celestials - saazhale (refrain).

1052.translit:
kan'rap pa'raika'rangak kaNdavardham kaNkaLippa,
man'ril marakkaalkooth thaadinaan kaaneadee,
man'ril marakkaalkooth thaadinaa naagilum,
en'rum ariyan imaiyoarkkum saazhale.
1052.meaning: As the drums sounded thunderously and the eyes of the onlookers delighted, He danced the stilt-dance in the public square - behold, O friend! Though He danced the stilt-dance in the public square, He is forever hard to attain even for the celestials - saazhale (refrain).

1053.translit:
koadhaiveal aivarkkaay maNNagalam koo'riduvaan,
thoodhanaay mannavanaal solluNdaan kaaneadee,
thoodhanaay mannavanaal solluNdaa naagilum,
oadhaneer vaiyagammun uNdumizhndhaan saazhale.
1053.meaning: For the five (Pandavas) of garlanded spears, to apportion to them the breadth of the land, He went as an envoy and was rebuked with words by the king (Duryodhana) - behold, O friend! Though as an envoy He was rebuked with words by the king, He is the one who once swallowed and spat forth the world girded by the surging waters - saazhale (refrain).

1054.translit:
paarmannar mangap padaithottu venjamaththu,
thearmannarkaay an'ru thearoorndhaan kaaneadee,
thearmannarkaay an'ru thearoorndhaa naagilum,
thaarmannar thangaL thalaimealaan saazhale.
1054.meaning: So that the kings of the earth perished, He took up arms in the fierce battle, and on that day He drove the chariot for the chariot-king (Arjuna) - behold, O friend! Though He drove the chariot for the chariot-king, He stands upon the heads of the garlanded kings - saazhale (refrain).

1055.translit:
kaNdaar irangak kazhiyak ku'raluruvaay,
vaNthaaraan veaLviyil maNNirandhaan kaaneadee,
vaNthaaraan veaLviyil maNNirandhaa naagilum
viNde zhulagukkum mikkaankaaN saazhale.
1055.meaning: So that those who saw Him pitied Him exceedingly, He took the form of a dwarf and begged for land at the sacrifice of the one (Mahabali) of generous garlands - behold, O friend! Though He begged for land at the sacrifice of the one of generous garlands, behold, He is greater than the sky and the seven worlds - saazhale (refrain).

1056.translit:
kaLLaththaal maavaliyai moovadimaN koNdaLandhaan,
veLLaththaan veangadaththaan enparaal kaaneadee,
veLLaththaan veangadaththaa nealum, kalikan'ri
uLLaththi nuLLea ulankaNdaay saazhale.
1056.meaning: By a stratagem He took three steps of land from Mahabali and measured out the worlds - behold, O friend! They say He is the one upon the waters and the one at Venkatam. Yet though He is the one upon the waters and the one at Venkatam, behold, He dwells within the heart of Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) - saazhale (refrain).

1057.translit:
mainnin'ra karungadalvaa yulagin'ri vaanavarum yaamumellaam,
neynnin'ra sakkaraththan thiruvayi'r'ril nedungaalam kidandhadhoareer,
ennan'ri seydhaaraa eadhiloar deyvaththai yeaththuginreer?
seynnan'ri kun'reanmin thoNdarkaaL aNdanaiyea eaththeerkaLe.
1057.meaning: When there was no world but only the dark collyrium-hued sea, the celestials and we and all of us lay for a long age within the sacred belly of Him who holds the gleaming discus - reflect on this! What good have they done for you, those strange gods you praise? Do not fail in gratitude, O devotees; praise only Him, the Lord of all.

1058.translit:
nillaadha peruveLLam neduvisumbin meedhoadi nimirndhakaalam,
mallaaNda thadakkaiyaal pagiraNda magappaduththa kaalaththu,
an'ru ellaarum a'riyaaroa emperumaan uNdumizhndha echchildheavar,
allaadhaar thaamuLare? avanaruLea ulagaava dha'riyeerkaLe.
1058.meaning: In the time when the unceasing great flood rose and stretched over the vast sky, when with His great wrestler-subduing arms He brought forth the cosmic spheres - do not all know it from of old? Are there any gods other than the leavings that my Lord swallowed and spat forth? Know that it is by His grace alone that the world exists.

1059.translit:
ne'r'rimeal kaNNaanum ni'raimozhivaay naanmuganum neeNdanaalvaay,
o'r'raikkai veNpagattin oruvanaiyum uLLitta amararoadum,
ve'r'rippoark kadalaraiyan vizhungaamal thaanvizhungi yuyyakkoNda,
ko'r'rappoa raazhiyaan guNamparavaach si'ruthoNdar kodiyavaa're.
1059.meaning: Together with the one who has an eye on his forehead (Shiva), and the four-faced one of full-spoken word (Brahma), and the one (Indra) upon the white elephant of long pendulous mouth and single trunk, and the rest of the celestials - the king of the warring ocean would have swallowed them all, but He Himself swallowed them and saved them. How wicked are the petty devotees who do not extol the virtues of Him who bears the victorious battle-discus!

1060.translit:
panipparavait tiraitatumppap paarellaam neDungkaDalE aanakaalam,
inikkaLaikaN ivarkkillai enRulagam EzhinaiyumUzhil vaangki
munittalaivan muzhangkoLisEr tiruvayiRRil vaittummai uyyakkoNDa
kanikaLavat tiruvuruvat toruvanaiyE kazhal tozhumaa kallIrkaLE.
1060.meaning: When in the age of deluge the cold waters surged in waves and the whole earth became one vast long ocean, the Lord — chief of sages whose body shines with resounding light — gathered up all seven worlds in their turn and held them within His sacred womb to save you. Will you not learn to worship at the feet of that One alone, He of the ripe-fruit-hued sacred form?

1061.translit:
paaraarum kaaNaamE paravaimaa neDungkaDalE aanakaalam,
aaraanum avanuDaiya tiruvayiRRil neDungkaalam kiDanthatu, uLLat
tOraata vuNarvilIr. uNarutirEl ulagaLantha vumparkOmaan,
pEraaLan pEraana pErkaLaa yirangkaLumE pEsIrkaLE.
1061.meaning: In the age when even the wide world could not be seen and all became one great vast ocean, every being lay for a long time within His sacred womb. O you of unknowing minds who do not perceive this in your hearts — if you would understand, then speak the thousand names of the great One, the Lord of the celestials, who measured the worlds.

1062.translit:
pEyirukku neDuveLLam peruvisumpin meethODip perugukaalam,
taayirukkum vaNNamE yummait tan vayiRRirutti uyyakkoNDaan,
pOyirukka maRRingkOr pututtheyvam koNDaaDum toNDIr, peRRa
taayirukka maNaivennIr aaTTutirO maaTTaata takavaRRIrE.
1062.meaning: In the age when the dreadful endless flood ran above and overflowed the great sky, He held you within His own womb like a mother and saved you. O devotees who go off and celebrate some other new-found deity here — you do as if to bathe a corpse in hot water while the very mother who bore you yet lives; you are helpless ones, devoid of worth.

1063.translit:
maNNaaDum viNNaaDum vaanavarum taanavarum maRRumellaam
uNNaata peruveLLam uNNaamal taanvizhungki uyyakkoNDa,
kaNNaaLan kaNNamangkai nakaraaLan kazhalsooDi, avanaiyuLLat
teNNaata maaniDattai yeNNaata pOthellaa miniyavaaRE.
1063.meaning: When the great flood that consumed not the earth-world, the sky-world, the celestials, the demons, nor anything else, He alone swallowed up without being swallowed and so saved all — that gracious Lord, ruler of Kannamangai town. Wear His holy feet; and every moment that you do not dwell upon those people who do not think of Him in their hearts is sweet.

1064.translit:
maRamkiLarnthu karungkaDalnee uramturanthu paranthERi aNDattappaal,
puRamkiLarntha kaalattup ponnulagam EzhinaiyumUzhil vaangki,
aRamkiLarntha tiruvayiRRin akampaDiyil vaittummai uyyakkoNDa,
niRamkiLarntha karunjOthi neDunthakaiyai ninaiyaathaar neesartaamE.
1064.meaning: When fiercely the dark sea-waters rose in fury and spread and surged beyond the cosmic egg, in that age of overflowing destruction He gathered up all seven golden worlds in their turn and held them within His sacred womb wherein righteousness abounds, and so saved you. Those who do not meditate upon that great noble One of radiant dark lustre are the lowest of the low.

1065.translit:
aNDattin mukaDazhuntha alaimunneert tiraitatumpa aavavenRu,
toNDarkkum amararkkum munivarkkum taanaruLi, ulagamEzhum
uNDotta tiruvayiRRin akampaDiyil vaittummai uyyakkoNDa,
koNDaRkai maNivaNNan taNkuDanthai nakarppaaDi yaaDIrkaLE.
1065.meaning: When the waves of the wide ocean welled up till the very summit of the cosmic egg was submerged, crying 'Ah, ah!' in compassion He bestowed His grace upon devotees, celestials and sages, and swallowing all seven worlds He held you within His sacred womb and so saved you. Sing and dance, praising that sapphire-hued Lord with hands like the rain-cloud, of the cool city of Kudanthai.

1066.translit:
thEvaraiyum asuraraiyum tisaikaLaiyum kaDalkaLaiyum maRRum muRRum,
yaavaraiyu mozhiyaamE yemperumaan uNDumizhntha taRinthusonna,
kaavaLarum pozhilmangaik kalikanRi olimaalai kaRRu vallaar,
poovaLarum tirumakaLaal aruLpeRRup ponnulagil polivar taamE.
1066.meaning: Our Lord swallowed and then spat forth again the gods and the demons, the directions, the seas and all else, leaving none out. Those who learn and master this resonant garland of verses by Kalikanri (Tirumangai) of Mangai, rich in groves and flourishing gardens, who told of this knowingly, will win the grace of Lakshmi who dwells on the lotus and shine in the golden world.

1067.translit:
neeLnaakam suRRi neDuvarainaTTu, aazhkaDalaip
pENaan kaDainthamutam koNDugantha pemmaanai,
pooNaara maarvanaip puLLoorum ponmalaiyai,
kaaNaathaar kaNNenRum kaNNalla kaNDaamE.
1067.meaning: He planted the great mountain (Mandara) and wound the long serpent about it, and churning the deep ocean He rejoiced in the nectar He obtained — that great Lord. He whose chest is adorned with garlands and jewels, the golden mountain who rides the bird (Garuda) — we have seen that the eyes of those who do not behold Him are no eyes at all.

1068.translit:
neeLvaan kuRaLuruvaay ninRiranthu maavalimaN,
taaLaal aLaviTTa takkaNaikku mikkaanai,
thOLaata maamaNiyait toNDarkk iniyaanai,
kELaac chevikaL seviyalla kETTaamE.
1068.meaning: As the dwarf of the tall heavens He stood and begged, and measured out Mahabali's land with His foot — He who is greater than any gift demanded. He is the great flawless gem unpierced, sweet to His devotees. We have seen that ears that do not hear of Him are no ears at all.

1069.translit:
tooyaanait tooya maRaiyaanai, tennaali
mEyaanai mEvaa LuyiruN DamutuNDa
vaayaanai, maalai vaNangki yavanperumai,
pEsaathaar pEcchenRum pEcchalla kETTaamE.
1069.meaning: He is the pure One, He of the pure Vedas, who abides in southern Tirunaraiyur (Naali), whose mouth devoured the life of the hostile demoness (Putana) and drank the nectar. We have seen that the speech of those who do not bow to Tirumal and speak of His greatness is no speech at all.

1070.translit:
kooDaa iraNiyanaik kooruhiraal maarviDantha,
ODaa aDalariyai umparaar kOmanai,
thODaar naRuntuzhaay maarvanai, aarvattaal
paaDaathaar paaTTenRum paaTTalla kETTaamE.
1070.meaning: With sharp claws He tore the chest of the hostile Hiranya — the unretreating fierce lion (Narasimha), the king of the celestials, whose chest is adorned with fresh fragrant tulasi. We have seen that the song of those who do not sing of Him with love is no song at all.

1071.translit:
maiyaar kaDalum maNivaraiyum maamuhilum,
koyyaar kuvaLaiyum kaayaavum pOnRiruNDa
meyyaanai, meyya malaiyaanaic changkEnthum
kaiyaanai, kaitozhaa kaiyalla kaNDaamE.
1071.meaning: His dark body is like the kohl-dark sea, the sapphire mountain, the great cloud, the freshly-plucked kuvalai blossom and the kaaya flower. He dwells on the true mountain (Tirumeyyam) and bears the conch in His hand. We have seen that hands that do not fold in worship of Him are no hands at all.

1072.translit:
kaLLaar tuzhaayum kaNavalarum kooviLaiyum,
muLLaar muLariyum aampalumun kaNDakkaal,
puLLaayOr Enamaayp pukkiDanthaan ponnaDikkenRu,
uLLaathaa ruLLattai uLLamaak koLLOmE.
1072.meaning: When you behold the honeyed tulasi, the kanaval, the bael (vilva), the thorny lotus and the white lily, if you do not offer them at the golden feet of Him who became a bird (Garuda's Lord) and who entered as a boar (Varaha) — the hearts of such people we will not count as hearts at all.

1073.translit:
kanaiyaar kaDalum karuviLaiyum kaayaavum
anaiyaanai, anpinaal aarvattaal, enRum
sunaiyaar malariTTut toNDaraay ninRu,
ninaiyaathaar nenjenRum nenjalla kaNDaamE.
1073.meaning: He is like the roaring sea, the karuvilai and the kaaya flower in hue. We have seen that the hearts of those who do not stand as devotees with love and longing, offering Him flowers from the springs and meditating ever upon Him, are no hearts at all.

1074.translit:
veRiyaar karungkoonthal aaycchiyar vaitta
uRiyaar naRuveNNey taanuhan thuNDa
siRiyaanai, sengka NeDiyaanaic chinthit
taRiyaathaar, enRum aRiyaathaar kaNDaamE.
1074.meaning: As the little child He gladly ate the fragrant white butter from the hanging pots set by the cowherd-women with fragrant dark tresses — yet He is the red-eyed lofty One. We have seen that those who do not contemplate and know Him are forever the ones who know nothing.

1075.translit:
thEnODu vaNDaalum tirumaa liruncOlai,
taaniDamaak koNDaan taDamalark kaNNikkaay,
aanviDaiyE zhanRaDarttaaR kaaLaanaa rallaathaar,
maaniDavar allarenR enmanattE vaittEnE.
1075.meaning: For the sake of her of large flower-like eyes (Nappinnai) He once subdued the seven bulls — that Lord who took Tirumaliruncholai, where bees hum with the honey, as His own abode. Those who do not become His servants — I have fixed it firmly in my mind that they are no human beings at all.

1076.translit:
meynninRa paavam akala, tirumaalaik
kainninRa aazhiyaan soozhum kazhalsooDi,
kainninRa vERkaik kaliya nolimaalai,
aiyonRu mainthum ivaipaaDi yaaDuminE.
1076.meaning: That the sin lodged in the body may depart, wear the encircling holy feet of Tirumal who bears the discus in His hand. Sing and dance to these resonant verses — five and five and one (these ten and one) — of Kaliyan (Tirumangai), the spear-bearer.

1077.translit:
maaRRamuLa vaakilum solluvan, makkaL
tORRak kuzhitORRu vippaaykko lenRinnam,
aaRRang karaivaazh marampOla anjuginREn,
naaRRanj suvaiyU Roliyaa kiyanambee.
1077.meaning: Though there be objections, still I will speak it out — fearing 'Will You yet cause me to be born again in the pit of human births?' I tremble like a tree growing on a river's bank. O perfect Lord who have become fragrance, taste, touch, sound and form!

1078.translit:
seeRRamuLa vaakilum seppuvan, makkaL
tORRak kuzhitORRu vippaaykko lenRanji,
kaaRRat tiDaippaTTa kalavar manampOla,
aaRRat tuLangkaa niRpanaa zhivalavaa.
1078.meaning: Though it bring Your wrath, still I will say it — fearing 'Will You make me to be born again in the pit of human births?', I tremble greatly like the minds of sailors caught in a storm-wind. O Lord skilled in wielding the discus!

1079.translit:
toongkaar piRavikka Linnam pukappeythu,
vaangkaayenRu sinthittu naanataR kanji,
paampO Dorukoo raiyilE payinRaaRpOl,
taangkaatuL Lamthametu LLam thaLLum en taamaraikkaNNaa.
1079.meaning: Thinking 'Will You thrust me again into these wretched births that give no rest, and not draw me out?' — fearing that, my heart cannot bear it and trembles, like one made to dwell in a single hut together with a serpent. O my lotus-eyed Lord!

1080.translit:
uruvaar piRavikkaL innam pukappeydhu,
thirivaay enRu sindhiththi enRadhaR kanji,
iru paadu erikoLLiyinuL eRumbE pOla,
urukaa niRkum en uLLam oozhi mudhalvaa.
1080.meaning: O Primordial One who is the first cause of the aeons! Thinking that You might yet cast me again into these embodied births to wander, and fearing that very thought, my heart melts and writhes like an ant caught upon a firebrand burning at both ends.

1081.translit:
koLLak kuRaiyaadha idumbaik kuzhiyil,
thaLLi pukappeydhi kol enRadhaR kanji,
veLLath thidaippatta nariyinam pOlE,
uLLam thuLangaa niRpan oozhi mudhalvaa.
1081.meaning: O Primordial One who is the first cause of the aeons! Fearing that You might shove me down and cast me into the pit of misery that never lessens however much it is taken, my heart trembles and shakes like a pack of jackals trapped amid a flood.

1082.translit:
padai ninRa paindhaa maraiyOdu aNi neelam
madai ninRu alarum vayalaali maNaaLaa,
idaiyan eRindha maramE oththu iraamE,
adaiya aruLaay enak kunRan aruLE.
1082.meaning: O Bridegroom of Vayalali (Tiruvali), where in the watered fields the fresh lotus stands like a host of weapons and the lovely blue lily blossoms at the sluice-gates! Do not let me be like the tree struck down by the cowherd; bestow Your grace upon me so that I may reach You.

1083.translit:
vEmbin puzhu vEmbu inRi uNNaadhu, adiyEn
naan pinnum un sEvadi anRi nayavEn,
thEmbal iLam thingaL siRai viduththu, aivaayp
paambin aNaippaLLi koNdaay paranjOdhee.
1083.meaning: As the worm born in the neem tree feeds on nothing but the neem, so I, Your servant, desire nothing but Your sacred feet. O Supreme Light, who released the slender young crescent moon from its captivity and who reposed upon the bed of the five-headed serpent!

1084.translit:
aNiyaar pozhil soozh aranga nakar appaa,
thuNiyEn ini nin aruL alladhu enakku,
maNiyE maNimaa NikkamE madhusoodhaa,
paNiyaay enak uyyum vakai, paranjOdhi.
1084.meaning: O Father who dwells in the city of Arangam (Srirangam) girt by beautiful groves! Henceforth I am resolved on nothing for myself but Your grace. O Gem, O precious ruby, O Slayer of Madhu, O Supreme Light, declare to me the way by which I may be saved.

1085.translit:
nandhaa narakath thazhundhaa vakai, naaLum endhaay
thoNdar aanavarkku in aruL seyvaay,
sandhOkaa thalaivanE thaamaraik kaNNaa,
andhO adiyERku aruLaay un aruLE.
1085.meaning: O my Father who daily grants sweet grace to those who become Your devotees, so that they sink not into the unceasing hell! O Lord of the Chandogya (the Sama-veda chanters), O Lotus-eyed One! Alas, bestow Your grace upon me, Your servant.

1086.translit:
kunRam eduththu aa nirai kaaththavan thannai,
manRil pukazh mangai man kali kanRi sol,
onRu ninRa onbadhum val lavar tham mEl,
enRum vinaiyaayina saara killaavE.
1086.meaning: These are the words of Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar), the king of Mangai praised in the public square, sung upon Him who lifted the hill and protected the herds of cattle. Upon those who can master these ten verses, the binding ones and the nine, no deeds of karma shall ever cling.

280.translit:
thuLaiyaar karumen kuzhalaaycchi yardham thugilvaari yumchitril sidhaitthum,
mutraa viLaiyaar viLaiyaat toduahaadhal veLLam viLaivittha ammaanidam,
veel nedunkaN muLaivaaLeyitru madavaar payitru mozhikeet tirundhu mudhiraadhavinsol,
vaLaivaaya kiLLai maRaipaadu naangoor maNimaadak koayil vaNangen mananee.
280.meaning: He is the wondrous Lord who, by stealing the garments and breaking the little sand-houses of the cowherd girls with dense soft tresses, and by sporting playfully with these not-yet-grown maidens, caused a flood of love to swell. He dwells where the immortals listen to the words taught by the lovely women with long spear-like eyes and sprouting bright teeth. O my heart, worship the Manimadakkoyil of Nangur, where green-beaked parrots, untiring in their sweet unripened speech, chant the Vedas.

281.translit:
vidaiyoda vendraaycchi mendhoaLnayandha vigirdhaa viLangu sudaraazhi yennum,
padaiyodu sangon rudaiyaay enanindru imaiyoar paravu midam,
paindhadatthup pedaiyoda sengaala vannam thugaippath thogaippuNda reegatthi daicchen kazhuneer,
madaiyoda nindru madhuvimmu naangoor maNimaadak koayil vaNangen mananee.
281.meaning: "O distinct One who, having conquered by routing the bulls, desired the soft shoulders of the cowherd-girl! O You who bear, as Your weapons, the conch and the gleaming radiant discus!" — thus the immortals stand and praise Him in His place. O my heart, worship the Manimadakkoyil of Nangur, where in the lush ponds red-legged swans tread with their mates, and from the clustered lotuses the red water-lilies brim with honey that flows along the channels.

282.translit:
