vaNdaar pozhilsoozhn dhazhagaaya naangoor maNimaadak koayil nedumaalukku,
endrum thoNdaaya tholseer vayalmang aiyarkkoan kaliya nolisey thamizhmaalai vallaar,
kaNdaar vaNangak kaLiyaanai meedhee kadalsoo zhulagukku orukaa valaraay,
viNdoay neduveN kudaineezha lingeezh virineer ulagaaN duvirum puvaree.
282.meaning: This Tamil garland of song was composed by Kaliyan, the king of fertile Mangai of ancient glory, ever a servant to the great Lord Nedumal of the beautiful Manimadakkoyil of Nangur, girt with groves humming with bees. Those who can master it shall ride upon a rutting elephant, all who behold them bowing, becoming the sole protector of the sea-girt world. They shall rule with joy over the wide watery earth beneath the shade of the tall white parasol that touches the sky.

283.translit:
salangoNda iraNiyana dhagalmaarvam keeNdu
 thadangadalaik kadaindhamudhang koNdugandha kaaLai,
 nalangoNda karumugilpoal thirumeeni yammaan
naadoaRum magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai koayil,
salangoNdu malarsoriyum mallihaiyoN serundhi
 saNbahangaL maNanaaRum vaNpozhili noodee,
valangoNdu kayaloadi viLaiyaadu naangoor
 vaihundha viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
283.meaning: He is the youthful Lord who tore open the broad chest of the deceitful Hiranya, and who rejoiced churning the wide ocean to take the nectar; His sacred form is like a dark beautiful cloud. This is the temple where He dwells, day after day, joyously and sweetly. O simple heart, worship Vaikuntha Vinnagaram of Nangur, where carp swim playing and circling within the fertile groves fragrant with bright jasmine, serunti, and champak that take in water and shower forth blossoms.

284.translit:
thiNNiyathoa rariyuruvaayth thisaiyanaitthum nadungath
 theevarodu thaanavargaL thisaippa,
iraNiyanai naNNiyavan maarvagalath thugirmadutha naadhan
 naadoaRum magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai koayil,
eNNilmigu peruncelvath thezhilviLangu maRaiyum
 eezhisaiyum keeLvigaLu miyandraperung guNatthoar,
maNNilmigu maRaiyavargaL maliveydhu naangoor
 vaihundha viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
284.meaning: Taking the form of a fierce, firm man-lion that made all directions tremble, while gods and demons alike stood bewildered, the Lord drew near Hiranya and plunged His claws into the broad expanse of his chest. This is the temple where He dwells, day after day, joyously and sweetly. O simple heart, worship Vaikuntha Vinnagaram of Nangur, abounding in earthly brahmins of great virtue, accomplished in the radiant Vedas of immeasurable spiritual wealth, the seven-toned music, and sacred learning.

285.translit:
aNdamumiv valaikadalu mavanigaLu mellaam
 amudhuseydha thiruvayitran arankoNdu thiriyum,
muNdamadhu niRaitthavankaN saabamadhu neekkum
 mudhalvanavan magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai koayil,
eNdisaiyum perunchenna LiLandhengu kadhali
 ilaikkodiyoN kulaikkamugo dihalivaLam soriya,
vaNdupala visaipaadamayilaalu naangoor
 vaihundha viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
285.meaning: His sacred belly once held as nectar the cosmos, this surging sea, and all the worlds; He is the primal One who removed the curse upon Siva, who roamed bearing the skull-bowl, by filling it. This is the temple where He dwells, day after day, joyously and sweetly. O simple heart, worship Vaikuntha Vinnagaram of Nangur, where in all eight directions fine red paddy, young coconut palms, plantains, leafy creepers, and bright clustered areca vie to yield abundance, where many bees sing tunefully and peacocks dance.

286.translit:
kalaiyilangu mahalalgul arakkarkkulak kodiyaik
 kaadhodumook kudanariyak kadhaRiyava Loadi,
thalaiyilangai vaitthumalai yilangaipugach cheydha
 thadandhoaLan magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai koayil,
silaiyilangu maNimaadath thucchimisaicchoolam
 sezhungoNda lahadiriyacchorindhasezhu mutham,
malaiyilangu maaLihaimeel maliveydhu naangoor
 vaihundha viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
286.meaning: He is the broad-shouldered Lord who, when the creeper of the demon clan — she with the garment-draped wide loins — had her ears and nose sliced off so that she fled wailing, made Lanka topple, hill and city alike. This is the temple where He dwells, day after day, joyously and sweetly. O simple heart, worship Vaikuntha Vinnagaram of Nangur, where atop the bow-bright jeweled mansions the fertile rain-clouds graze their bellies, and the rich pearls they scatter abound upon the mountain-like palaces.

287.translit:
minnanaiya Nmarungul melliyaRkaa
 yilangai veendhanmudi yorupadhumthoa Lirubadhumpoa yudhira,
thanniharil silaivaLaitthan rilangaipodi seydha
 thadandhoaLan magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai koayil,
sennelodu sengamalam seelkayalgaL vaaLai
 sengazhunee rodumidaindhu kazhanidhihazhn dhengum,
mannupugazh veediyargaL maliveydhu naangoor
 vaihundha viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
287.meaning: For the sake of the tender-natured one with a waist slender as lightning, He bent His matchless bow so that the ten crowns and twenty shoulders of Lanka's king fell scattered, and ground Lanka to dust; He is the broad-shouldered Lord. This is the temple where He dwells, day after day, joyously and sweetly. O simple heart, worship Vaikuntha Vinnagaram of Nangur, abounding in brahmins of enduring fame, where everywhere the fields gleam with red paddy, red lotus, sel and kayal fish, vaalai fish, and red water-lilies all intermingled.

288.translit:
peNmaimigu vadivukodu vandhavaLaip
 periyapeeyinadhu uruvukodu maaLavuyi ruNdu
thiNmaimigu marudhodunaR chagadamiRuth tharuLum
 theevanavan magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai koayil,
uNmaimigu maRaiyodunaR kalaigaLniRai poRaigaL
 udhavukodai yendrivatri nozhivillaa, periya
vaNmaimigu maRaiyavargaL maliveydhu naangoor
 vaihundha viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
288.meaning: He is the divine Lord who consumed the very life of the great demoness Putana who came in the abundant form of womanhood, killing her, and who graciously broke the strong marudu trees and the good cart. This is the temple where He dwells, day after day, joyously and sweetly. O simple heart, worship Vaikuntha Vinnagaram of Nangur, abounding in great, supremely generous brahmins unfailing in true Vedic lore, fine learning, fullness, forbearance, and the giving of charity.

289.translit:
viLanganiyai yiLangandru koNdudhira veRindhu
 veelnedunga Naaycchiyargal vaitthadhayir veNNey
uLanguLira amudhuseydhiv vulaguNda kaaLai
 ugandhinidhu naadoaRum maruviyuRai koayil,
iLampadinaR kamuguhulaith thengukodicchennel
 eenkarumbu kaNvaLarak kaaltadavum punalaal,
vaLangoNda perunchelvam vaLarumaNi naangoor
 vaihundha viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
289.meaning: He is the youthful Lord who flung the young calf to bring down the wood-apple fruits, who, to gladden His heart, ate as nectar the curd and butter set aside by the spear-eyed cowherd women, and who once swallowed this world. He dwells here joyously and sweetly day after day. O simple heart, worship the beautiful Vaikuntha Vinnagaram of Nangur, where great abundant wealth grows, for the waters lap at the roots and nurture the young areca clusters, coconut palms, creepers, red paddy, and sweet-yielding sugarcane.

290.translit:
aaRaadha sinatthinmigu naraganura mazhittha
 adalaazhith thadakkaiyan alarmagatkum araRkum,
kooRaagak koduttharuLum thiruvudambana imaiyoar
 kulamudhalvan magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai koayil,
maaRaadha malarkkamalam sengazhuneer thadhumbi
 madhuveLLa mozhugavaya luzhavarmadai yadaippa,
maaRaadha perunchelvam vaLarumaNi naangoor
 vaihundha viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
290.meaning: With the mighty discus in His broad hand He destroyed the strength of Naraka of unquenchable wrath; He is the primal One of the immortals' clan, whose sacred body He graciously gave as a half to the flower-born goddess and to Siva. This is the temple where He dwells, day after day, joyously and sweetly. O simple heart, worship the beautiful Vaikuntha Vinnagaram of Nangur, where unfading lotus and red water-lily brim over so that floods of honey flow, and the field-farmers dam the channels, where unfailing great wealth ever grows.

291.translit:
vangamali thadangadaluL vaanavarga Loadu
 maamunivar palarkoodi maamalargaL thoovi,
engaLthani naayaganee emakkaruLaay ennum
 eesanavan magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai koayil,
sengayalum vaaLaihaLum sennelidaik kudhippach
 cheelugaLum sezhumpaNaisoozh veedhidhoaRum midaindhu,
mangulmadhi yahaduriñju maNimaada naangoor
 vaihundha viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
291.meaning: In the wide ship-laden ocean, the celestials together with many great sages gathered and scattered great flowers, crying, "O our peerless Lord, grant us grace!" — He is that Isvara. This is the temple where He dwells, day after day, joyously and sweetly. O simple heart, worship Vaikuntha Vinnagaram of Nangur, where red carp and vaalai fish leap among the red paddy, sel fish frolic, and throughout the streets girt with fertile fields the jeweled mansions rise so high that the misty moon grazes their bellies.

292.translit:
sangumali thaNdumudhal sakkaramuneendhum
 thaamaraikkaN nediyapiraan thaanamarum koayil,
vangamali kadalulagil maliveydhu naangoor
 vaihundha viNNagarmeel vaNdaRaiyum pozhilsoozh,
mangaiyartham thalaivanmaru valarthamudal thuNiya
 vaaLveesum paragaalan kaligandri sonna,
sangamali thamizhmaalai patthivaivallaargaL
 tharaNiyodu visumbaaLum thanmaipeRu vaaree.
292.meaning: This Tamil garland was sung by Kaliyan, the Parakala who brandishes his sword to sever the bodies of foes — the chief of Mangai — upon Vaikuntha Vinnagaram of Nangur, abounding in the ship-laden sea-world and girt with bee-humming groves, the temple where the lotus-eyed exalted Lord, bearing the conch, club, and discus, gladly resides. Those who can hold these ten conch-resounding verses in devotion shall attain the state of ruling both earth and sky.

293.translit:
thirumadandhai maNmadandhai yirubaalum thigazhath
 theevinaigaL poayagala adiyavargat kendrum
aruLnadandhu,iv veezhulagath thavarppaNiya
 vaanoar amarndheettha irundhavidam
perumpugazhvee dhiyarvaazh tharumidangaL malargaLmigu kaidhaigaLsengazhuneer
 thaamaraigaL thadangadoaRu midangadoaRum thigazha,
aruvidangaL pozhilthazhuvi yezhilthigazhu naangoor
 arimeeya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
293.meaning: With the goddess Lakshmi and the Earth-goddess shining on either side, ever extending grace to His devotees so that their cruel deeds depart, He resides here, worshipped by the people of these seven worlds and praised by the celestials who abide there. O simple heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram of beautiful Nangur, the dwelling of brahmins of great renown, where in pond after pond and place after place gleam abundant blossoms — screw-pine, red water-lily, and lotus — and the rare expanses embraced by groves shine in beauty.

294.translit:
vendrimigu naraganura madhuvazhiya visiRum
 viRalaazhith thadakkaiyan viNNavargatku, andru
kundrukodu kuraikadalaik kadaindhamudha maLikkum
 kurumaNiyen naaramudham kulaviyuRai koayil,
endrumigu perunchelvath thezhilviLangu maRaiyoar
 eezhisaiyum keeLvigaLu miyandraperung guNatthoar,
andrulagam padaitthavanee yanaiyavargaL naangoor
 arimeeya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
294.meaning: In His broad hand He wields the valorous discus that He hurled to destroy the strength of victorious Naraka; long ago, with a mountain He churned the roaring sea and bestowed the nectar — He is the lustrous gem, my rare ambrosia. This is the temple where He delightedly dwells. O simple heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram of Nangur, where the radiant brahmins of ever-abounding spiritual wealth and great virtue, accomplished in the seven-toned music and sacred learning, are like the very One who created the worlds of old.

295.translit:
umparumiv veezhulagu meezhkadalu mellaam
 uNdapiraaNandargaLmun kaNdumagizha veydha,
kumbamigu madhayaanai maruppositthuk
 kanjan kunjipidith thaditthapiraan koayil,
marung kengum paimponodu veNmutham palapunnai kaattap
 palanganigaL theenkaattap padavaravee ralgul,
ampanaiya kaNmadavaar magizhveydhu naangoor
 arimeeya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
295.meaning: He is the Lord who once swallowed the heavens, these seven worlds, and the seven seas — and again, to the joy of the celestials beholding it, He broke off the tusk of the rutting must-elephant, and seized Kamsa by the hair and struck him down. This is His temple. O simple heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram of Nangur, where, all around, women with hooded-snake-like loins and arrow-like eyes rejoice, as the punnai trees display fresh gold and white pearls, and ripe fruits offer up their honey.

296.translit:
oadaadha vaaLariyi nuruvamadhu koNdu
 andrulappilmigu peruvaratha viraNiyanaip patri,
vaadaadha vaLLugiraal piLandhavandran maganuk
 karuLseydhaan vaazhumidam mallihaiseng kazhuneer,
seedeeRu malarccherundhi sezhungamugam paaLai
 seNbahangaL maNanaaRum vaNpozhili noodee,
aadeeRu vayalaalaip puhaikamazhu naangoor
 arimeeya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
296.meaning: Taking the form of a never-retreating fierce man-lion, He seized Hiranya of boundless, immense boons, and with His unwithering strong claws split him open, then bestowed His grace upon that one's son. This is His dwelling. O simple heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram of Nangur, fragrant with the smoke of the sugar-mills set amid the fertile fields, where within the lush groves jasmine, red water-lily, blossoming serunti, fine areca spathes, and champak spread their fragrance.

297.translit:
kaNdavartham manammagizha maavalithan veeLvik
 kaLavilmigu siRukuRaLaay moovadiyen riranthittu,
aNdamumiv valaikadalu mavanigaLumellaam
 aLandhapiraa namarumidam vaLangoLpozhi layalee,
aNdamuRu muzhavoliyum vaNdinanga Loliyum
 arumaRaiyi noliyummada vaarsilambi noliyum,
aNdamuRu malaikadali nolithigazhu naangoor
 arimeeya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
297.meaning: As a tiny dwarf, beyond measure, He went to Mahabali's sacrifice — to the joy of all who beheld Him — and begging for three paces of land, then measured out the cosmos, this surging sea, and all the worlds. This is His abode. O simple heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram of Nangur, where, beside the wealth-laden groves, the sky-reaching boom of drums, the hum of bee-swarms, the sound of the rare Vedas, the jingle of women's anklets, and the roar of mountain and sea all resound together.

298.translit:
vaanedunkaN malarkkoondhal maidhilikkaa
 ilangai mannanmudi yorupadhumthoa Lirubadhumpoa yudhira,
thaaNedundhin silaivaLaittha dhayaradhanseey
 endhan thaniccharaNvaa navarkkarasu karudhumidam, thadamaar
seeNidangoL malarkkamalam seelkayalgaL vaaLai
 sennelodu madutthariya vudhirndhasezhu mutham,
vaaNedunkaN kadaisiyargaL vaarumaNi naangoor
 arimeeya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
298.meaning: For the sake of Mythili (Sita) of long bright eyes and flower-decked hair, He bent His tall firm bow so that the ten crowns and twenty shoulders of Lanka's king fell scattered; He is the son of Dasaratha, my unique refuge, deemed king of the celestials. This is His abode. O simple heart, worship the beautiful Arimeya Vinnagaram of Nangur, where in the wide expansive ponds blossom lotus, sel and kayal fish, vaalai fish, and red paddy grow together, and the rich pearls that spill forth are gathered by the bright-long-eyed harvesting women.

299.translit:
theemanatthaan kanjanadhu vanchanaiyil thiriyum
 theenuganum poodhanaitha naaruyirum sehutthaan,
kaamanaitthaan payandhagaru meeniyudai yammaan
 karudhumidam porudhupunal thuRaithuRaimuth thundhi,
naamanatthaal mandhirangaL naalveedham aindhu
 veeLviyoa daarangam navindrukalai payindru,ang
kaamanatthu maRaiyavargaL payilumaNi naangoor
 arimeeya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjee.
299.meaning: He destroyed the very lives of Dhenuka, who roamed in the deceitful schemes of evil-minded Kamsa, and of Putana; He is the dark-bodied Lord who begot Kama (Pradyumna). This is His chosen abode. O simple heart, worship the beautiful Arimeya Vinnagaram of Nangur, where the contending waters push pearls onto bank after bank, and where the willing-hearted brahmins, with mind set on the Name, recite the mantras, the four Vedas, the five sacrifices, and the six auxiliary disciplines, ever practicing sacred learning.

460.translit:
thuvaritha vudaiyaarkkum thoonmaiyillaach chamanarkkum,
avarkatkang karuLillaa aruLaanai, thannadaintha
emarkatkum adiyeerkkum emmaarkkum emmanaikkum,
amararkkum piraanaaraik kandathuthen narangaththee.
460.meaning: He gave no grace to the saffron-robed Buddhists nor to the impure Jains; that very Lord of grace, the one who is the Lord of my kinsmen who have taken refuge in Him, of me His servant, of my master, of my household, and of the immortals (devas) as well — I beheld Him here in Srirangam.

461.translit:
poyvannam manaththakatrip pulanainthum chelavaiththu,
meyvannam ninainthavarkku meynNindra viththakanai,
maivannam karumukilpool thikazhvanna marathakaththin,
avvanna vannanaiyaan kandathuthen narangaththee.
461.meaning: He is the wondrous one who stands as truth for those who, casting falsehood from the mind and controlling the five senses, meditate upon His true form. He whose hue is dark like a black rain-cloud, who shines with the colour of emerald — that very-hued Lord I beheld here in Srirangam.

462.translit:
aamaruvi niraimeyththa aniyarangath thammaanai,
kaamarucheerk kalikandri yolicheytha malipukazhseer
naamaruvu thamizhmaalai naalirandoo dirandinaiyum,
naamaruvi vallaarmeel chaaraathee vinaithaamee.
462.meaning: Upon the Lord of beautiful Srirangam, who tended the herds of cows, Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) of desirable fame composed this melodious Tamil garland filled with abounding glory — these eight verses with two added. Upon those who can recite them dwelling on the words, no karma-deeds shall ever cling.

463.translit:
pandainaal maraiyum veeLviyum keeLvip pathangaLum pathangaLin poruLum,
pindamaay viriththa pirangoLi yanalum perukiya punalodu nilanum,
kondalmaa ruthamum kuraikada leezhum eezhumaa malaikaLum visumbum,
andamum thaanaay nindravem perumaan arangamaa nakaramarn thaanee.
463.meaning: He is the ancient four Vedas, the sacrifices, the sacred words heard (Vedanta) and the meaning of those words; He is the brilliant blazing fire that He spread as a single mass, the surging waters and the earth, the clouds and the wind, the seven roaring oceans, the seven great mountains, the sky, and the cosmic egg — being all these Himself, our Lord has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

464.translit:
indhiran piraman eechanen drivarkaL ennilpal kunangaLee yiyatra,
thanthaiyum thaayum makkaLum mikka chutramum chutrinin drakalaap
panthamum, pantha maruppathoor marunthum paanmaiyum palluyirk kellaam,
anthamum vaazhvu maayavem perumaan arangamaa nakaramarn thaanee.
464.meaning: As Indra, Brahma and Shiva He works through countless manifold qualities; He is the bond of father, mother, children and abundant kin that surround one and do not leave, and also the medicine that severs that bond; He is the nature and the end and the life of all the many souls — our Lord who is all this has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

465.translit:
mannumaa nilanum malaikaLum kadalum vaanamum thaanava rulakum,
thunnumaa yiruLaayth thulangoLi churungith thollainaan maraikaLum maraiya,
pinnumvaanavarkkum munivarkkum nalkip pirangiruL nirangeda, orunaaL
annamaay andrang karumarai payanthaan arangamaa nakaramarn thaanee.
465.meaning: When the enduring great earth, the mountains, the sea, the sky and the world of the asuras became dense darkness, when shining light dwindled and the ancient four Vedas were hidden away, He, on a certain day, becoming a swan, gave grace to the gods and the sages and, dispelling the spreading darkness, brought forth the precious hidden Vedas — He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

466.translit:
maayirung kundra mondrumath thaaka maasuna mathanodum aLavi,
paayirum pauvam pakaduvin dalarap paduthirai visumbidaip padara,
cheyiru visumbum thingaLum chudarum theevarum thaamudan thisaippa,
aayiran thoolaa lalaikadal kadainthaan arangamaa nakaramarn thaanee.
466.meaning: Making the one great dark mountain (Mandara) the churning-staff, binding it with the serpent (Vasuki), so that the spreading vast ocean roared like a bellowing bull and its breaking waves spread up into the sky, while the far high heavens, the moon, the sun, the gods and all were bewildered together — with His thousand arms He churned the billowing sea; He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

467.translit:
engngnanee yuyvar thaanavar ninainthaal iraniyan ilangupoo nakalam,
ponguveng kuruthi ponmalai pilanthu pozhitharu maruviyoth thizhiya,
vengkanvaa Leyitroor veLLimaa vilangal vinnurak kanalvizhith thezhunthathu,
angnganee yokka ariyuru vaanaan arangamaa nakaramarn thaanee.
467.meaning: How can the asuras escape, if one but thinks of it? When He tore open Hiranya's chest adorned with shining ornaments, the boiling fierce blood gushed forth like a cascading torrent that splits a golden mountain. With fierce eyes and sword-like fangs He rose up like a great silver mountain reaching the sky, blazing with fiery eyes — in just that form He became the man-lion (Narasimha); He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

468.translit:
aayiram kundram chendruthok kanaiya adalpurai yezhilthikazh thiradool,
aayiran thuniya adalmazhup patri matravan akalvisum panaiya,
aayiram peyraal amarchen drirainja arithuyi lalaikadal naduvee,
aayiram chudarvaa yaravanaith thuyindraan arangamaa nakaramarn thaanee.
468.meaning: With round shoulders gathered like a thousand mountains come together, shining with valorous beauty, He seized the mighty axe to cut off the thousand arms (of Kartavirya) so that he was flung into the wide sky. He, whom the gods came worshipping with a thousand names, rests in rare slumber in the midst of the billowing sea upon the serpent of a thousand radiant mouths (Adisesha) — He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

469.translit:
churikuzhal kanivaayth thiruvinaip piriththa kodumaiyir kaduvisai yarakkan,
erivizhith thilangu manimudi podichey thilangaipaazh paduppathar kenni,
varisilai vaLaiya adicharam thuranthu marikadal neripada, malaiyaal
arikulam panikon dalaikada ladaiththaan arangamaa nakaramarn thaanee.
469.meaning: Resolving to lay waste Lanka and to grind to dust the bejeweled crown of the swift fierce demon (Ravana) who in his cruelty had separated her of curly tresses and fruit-red lips, Sri (Sita), He bent His striated bow and let fly His arrows from His foot; taking the labour of the monkey hosts, He dammed the billowing ocean with mountains so that the heaving sea gave way — He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

470.translit:
oozhiyaay oomath thuchchiyaay orukaal udaiyathee roruvanaay ulakil
choozhimaal yaanaith thuyarkeduth thilangai malangavan daducharan thuranthu
paazhiyaal mikka paarththanuk karuLip pakalava noLikeda, pakalee
aazhiyaa landrang kaazhiyai maraiththaan arangamaa nakaramarn thaanee.
470.meaning: He is the aeon and the summit of the sacrificial fire; once becoming the one with a single chariot (the sun) in the world, He removed the sorrow of the great rutting elephant (Gajendra); driving His mighty arrows so that Lanka was thrown into confusion, and showing grace to the powerful Arjuna, with His discus He on that day veiled the sea (some read: the sun) in broad daylight so that the sun's light was lost — He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

471.translit:
peyinaar mulaiyoon piLLaiyaay orukaal perunilam vizhungiyathumizhntha
vaayanaay maalaay aalilai vaLarnthu manimudi vaanavar thamakkuch
cheeyanaay, adiyeer kaniyanaay vanthen chinthaiyuL venthuya rarukkum,
aayanaay andru kundramon dreduththaan arangamaa nakaramarn thaanee.
471.meaning: As an infant He fed on the demoness's (Putana's) breast; once, with the mouth that swallowed and spat forth the vast earth, He grew upon the banyan leaf; He who is far beyond the bejeweled-crowned celestials, yet near to me His servant, came and severs the burning sorrow within my heart — He, the cowherd who once lifted up a mountain (Govardhana), has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

472.translit:
ponnumaa maniyum muththamum chumanthu poruthirai maanathi pudaichoozhnthu,
annamaa dulavum alaipunal choozhntha arangamaa nakaramarn thaanai
mannumaa maada mangaiyar thalaivan maanaveer kaliyanvaa yoligaL
panniya panuval paaduvaar naaLum pazhavinai patrarup paaree.
472.meaning: Upon the Lord who dwells in the great city of Srirangam — encircled by the great river whose battling waves bear gold, great gems and pearls, surrounded by rippling waters where swans roam — Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) of the deer-like spear, chief of Tirumangai with its enduring great mansions, composed these elaborated verses from his mouth. Those who sing them shall, day by day, have their old karma utterly cut away at the root.

473.translit:
eezhai eethalan keezhmakan ennaa thirangi matravar kinnaruL surantha
maazhai maanmada nookkiyun thoozhi, umbi yembi yen drozhinthilai, ukanthu
thoozha neeyenak kingozhi endra chorkaL vanthadi yeenmanath thirunthida,
aazhi vannanin nadiyinai yadaintheen anipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanee.
473.meaning: Not regarding him as a pitiable one, a stranger of low birth, but with compassion You poured out Your sweet grace upon him (Guha/Sugriva); You did not hold back saying 'your companion of the tender fawn-eyed maiden, your younger brother, my younger brother,' but gladly said 'You are my friend, stay here with me.' Those words come and dwell in the heart of me Your servant — O Lord of the hue of the ocean, I have reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful-groved Srirangam!

474.translit:
vaatha maamakan markkadam vilangu matroor chaathiyen drozhinthilai, ukanthu
kaathal aatharam kadalinum perukach cheytha thakavinuk killaikaim maarendru
koothil vaaymaiyi naayodu mudanee unpan naan endra onporuL enakkum
aathal veendumen dradiyinai yadaintheen anipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanee.
474.meaning: You did not set him aside as a creature of another kind, a monkey, the son of the Wind (Hanuman/Sugriva), but gladly let love and affection swell greater than the ocean. 'For the kindness you have done there is no recompense' — and 'with the faultlessly truthful one (Guha the hunter) I myself will eat together' — that noble truth, I prayed, should be mine too, and so I have reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful-groved Srirangam!

475.translit:
kadikoL poompozhil kaamaru poykai vaiku thaamarai vaangiya veezham,
mudiyum vannamoor muzhuvali muthalai patra matrathu nincharan ninaippa
kodiya vaayvilang kinnuyirmalangak kondacheetramon drundula tharinthu, un
adiya neenumvan dadiyinai yadaintheen anipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanee.
475.meaning: When the elephant (Gajendra) that had plucked a lotus in the fragrant flower-grove pond was seized by a single full-strength crocodile so as to perish, and it thought of Your refuge, You showed such wrath that the cruel-mouthed beast's very life was thrown into confusion — knowing of that anger which You held, I too, even I Your servant, have come and reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful-groved Srirangam!

476.translit:
nanju choorvathoor venchina aravam veruvi vanthunin charanenach charanaa
nenjir kondunin nanchiraip paravaik kadaikka lamkoduth tharuLcheytha tharinthu
venchollaaLarkaL namandramar kadiyar kodiya cheyvana vuLaathar kadiyeen
anji vanthunin nadiyinai yadaintheen anipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanee.
476.meaning: Knowing how, when the fierce-natured serpent (whose venom dripped) came in terror saying 'You are my refuge,' holding You in its heart as refuge, You granted it shelter through Your beautiful-winged bird (Garuda) and showed grace — and knowing that Yama's harsh messengers of cruel words do cruel deeds, fearing that, I Your servant have come and reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful-groved Srirangam!

477.translit:
maaka maanilam muzhuvathumvan dhiraijum malara dikanda maamarai yaaLan,
thookai maamayi lannava rinpam thutri laamai yilaththaving kozhinthu
pookam neeyeythip pinnumnam midaikkee poathu vaay, endra ponnaruL, enakkum
aaka veendumen dradiyinai yadaintheen anipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanee.
477.meaning: To the great Vedic sage (Markandeya) who beheld the flower-feet that the high heavens and the whole great earth come and worship — 'Do not remain here losing the joys of women like fine great peacocks; you go and attain enjoyment, and then come again into our midst' — that golden grace, I prayed, should be mine too, and so I have reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful-groved Srirangam!

478.translit:
mannu naanmarai maamuni petra maintha naimathi yaathaveng kootran
thannai yanjinin charanenach charanaayth thakavil kaalanai yukamunin dhozhiyaa
pinnai yendrumnin thiruvadi piriyaa vanna menniya peraruL, enakkum
anna thaakumen dradiyinai yadaintheen anipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanee.
478.meaning: When the youth (Markandeya), the son born to the great sage steadfast in the four Vedas, in fear of fierce Death who heeds none, said 'You are my refuge,' You took him as refuge and angrily destroyed the worthless Kala (Yama), and granted that great grace, intending that thereafter he should never be parted from Your sacred feet — I prayed that such would be mine too, and so I have reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful-groved Srirangam!

479.translit:
oothu vaaymaiyum uvaniyap pirappum unakku munthantha anthana noruvan,
kaatha lenmakan pukalidang kaaneen, kandu neetharu vaayenak kendru,
koothil vaaymaiyi naanunai veendiya kuraimu diththavan chiruvanaik koduththaay,
aatha laalvanthun adiyinai yadaintheen anipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanee.
479.meaning: To the brahmin (Sandipani) who before had given You the recited learning and the upanayana investiture — when he said 'I see no refuge for my beloved son; find him and give him back to me' — You, of faultless truth, fulfilled the want of him who besought You and gave him back his son. Therefore I have come and reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful-groved Srirangam!

480.translit:
veda vaaymozhi yandhaNan oruvan endhai nin saraN ennudai manaivi,
kaadhal makkaLaip payaththalum kaaNaaL kadiyadhoor deyvam koNdu oLikkum, endru azhaippa
eedhalaar munnee in aruL avarkkuch cheydhu un makkaL mattru ivar endru koduththaay,
aadhalaal vandhu un adi-iNai adaindheen aNi pozhil tiru-arangaththammaanee.
480.meaning: O Lord of beautiful, grove-girt Srirangam! When a certain Brahmin learned in the truth of the Vedas cried out, 'O my Father, by Your feet — my wife cannot even behold the beloved children she has borne, for a cruel deity seizes and hides them,' You, before his very enemies, showed Your sweet grace to them and gave the children back, saying, 'These are your children.' Therefore I too have come and taken refuge at Your twin feet.

481.translit:
thuLangu neeN mudi arasar tham kurisil thoNdai mannavan thiN thiRal oruvarkku
uLam koL anbinoodu in aruL surandhu angu oodu naazhigai eezhudan iruppa,
vaLam koL mandhiram mattru avarkku aruLich cheydha aaRu adiyeen aRindhu, ulagam
aLandha pon adiyee adaindhu uyndheen aNi pozhil tiru-arangaththammaanee.
481.meaning: O Lord of beautiful, grove-girt Srirangam! Knowing how, with whole-hearted love, You poured out Your sweet grace upon Thondaiman, the mighty sovereign and lord of crowned kings whose tall diadems shimmer — abiding there with him through seven naazhigai and graciously imparting to him the fruitful sacred mantra — I, Your servant, have taken refuge at and been saved by those very golden feet that measured the worlds.

482.translit:
maada maaLigai soozh tiru-mangai mannan onnalar thangaLai vellum,
aadal maa val kali-kandri aNi pozhil tiru-arangaththammaanai,
needu thol pugazh aazhi vallaanai endhaiyai nedu maalai ninaindha,
paadal paththu ivai paadumin thoNdeer paada num idaip paavam nillaavee.
482.meaning: This decad of songs was composed by Kalikanri (Tirumangai), the king of Tirumangai girt with mansions and palaces, who conquers his foes and is mighty with battle-steeds, contemplating the Lord of beautiful, grove-girt Srirangam — my Father, the great Tirumal of enduring ancient glory who wields the discus. O devotees, sing these ten songs! As you sing, no sin will cling to you.

483.translit:
kai ilangu aazhi sankan karu mugil tiru niRaththan,
poyyilan meyyan thandhaaL adaivareel adimai aakkum,
seyyalar kamalam oongu seRi pozhil then tiruppeer
pai aravu aNaiyaan naamam paravi naan uyndha aaRee.
483.meaning: He is the one of dark-cloud hue with the radiant form, who bears the discus and conch gleaming in His hands; He is free of falsehood, the very Truth — and if they reach His given feet, He makes them His own servants. Behold how I have been saved by uttering the name of Him who reclines upon the hooded serpent at southern Tiruppeer, dense with groves where red lotuses rise high!

484.translit:
vangam aar kadalgaL eezhum malaiyum vaanagamum mattrum,
am kaN maa gnaalam ellaam amudhu seydhu umizhndha endhai,
thingaL maa mugil aNavu seRi pozhil then tiruppeer,
engaL maal iRaivan naamam eeththi naan uyndha aaRee.
484.meaning: My Father swallowed and then spewed forth again the seven ship-laden seas, the mountains, the heavens and all else — this whole vast and beautiful earth. Behold how I have been saved by praising the name of our Lord Tirumal at southern Tiruppeer, whose dense groves the great clouds and the moon draw near to touch!

485.translit:
oruvanai undhip poo meel oonguviththu aagam thannaal,
oruvanaich chaabam neekki umbar aaL, endru vittaan,
peru varai madhiLgaL soozhndha peru nagar arava aNai meel
karu varai vaNNan then peer karudhi naan uyndha aaRee.
485.meaning: He raised up one (Brahma) high upon the lotus from His navel; and by His own body He removed the curse of another (Shiva), sending him forth saying, 'Rule the heavens.' Behold how I have been saved by setting my mind on Tiruppeer — the great city girt with mountain-like walls — and on its Lord, dark as a mountain, who reclines upon the serpent couch!

486.translit:
oon amar thalai ondru eendhi ulagu elaam thiriyum eesan
eenam amar saabam neekkaay, enna oN punalai eendhaan,
theen amar pozhilgaL soozhndha seRi vayal then tiruppeer,
vaanavar thalaivan naamam vaazhththi naan uyndha aaRee.
486.meaning: When Shiva — who wandered through all the worlds bearing a fleshy skull — pleaded, 'Remove this base, clinging curse,' He gave forth the bright water (to fill it). Behold how I have been saved by extolling the name of that Lord of the celestials at southern Tiruppeer, girt with honey-laden groves and dense paddy fields!

487.translit:
vakkaran vaay mun keeNda maayavanee endru vaaneer
pukku, araN thandhu aruLvaay, ennap pon aagaththaanai,
nak ari uruvam aagi nagam kiLarndhu idandhu ugandha,
chakkarach chelvan then peerth thalaivan thaaL adaindhu uyndheenee.
487.meaning: When the celestials entered and prayed, 'O wondrous one who once tore open the mouth of Vakkara (Keshi), grant us refuge and grace,' He of the golden-hued form — who took the shape of the man-lion roaring with bared teeth and joyously clove (Hiranya) open with His rising claws — the wealthy Lord of the discus: I have taken refuge at and been saved by the feet of that Lord of southern Tiruppeer.

488.translit:
vilangalaal kadal adaiththu viLangu izhai poruttu, villaal,
ilangai maa nagarkku iRaivan irupadhu puyam thuNiththaan,
nalam koL naan maRai vallaargaL oththu oli eeththak keettu
malangu paay vayal tiruppeer maruvi naan vaazhndha aaRee.
488.meaning: For the sake of her of resplendent jewels (Sita), He dammed the sea with mountains, and with His bow severed the twenty shoulders of the king of great Lanka. Behold how I have flourished, drawing near to Tiruppeer — whose paddy fields the eels leap and dart through — hearing the harmonious chanted praises of those skilled in the good four Vedas!

489.translit:
veNNey thaan amudhu seyya veguNdu maththu aaychchi oochchi,
kaNNiyar kuRum kayittraal kattu vettu endru irundhaan,
thiNNa maa madhiLgaL soozhndha then tiruppeer uL, veelai
vaNNanaar naamam naaLum vaay mozhindhu uyndha aaRee.
489.meaning: When He ate the butter as if it were nectar, the cowherd-mother in anger raised the churning-rod, and when the gracious one bound Him fast with a short rope He sat there abashed. Behold how I have been saved by uttering daily with my mouth the name of that ocean-hued Lord within southern Tiruppeer, girt with firm great walls!

490.translit:
am pon aar ulagam eezhum aRiya aayppaadi thannuL,
kombu anaar pinnai koolam koodudaRku eeRu kondraan,
sem pon aar madhiLgaL soozhndha then tiruppeer uL meevum,
em piraan naamam naaLum eeththi naan uyndha aaRee.
490.meaning: That the seven worlds rich with fine gold might know it, within Ayarpadi (the cowherd village) He slew the bulls to win union with the beauty of Pinnai, slender as a flowering creeper. Behold how I have been saved by daily praising the name of our Lord who dwells within southern Tiruppeer, girt with walls bright with red gold!

491.translit:
naal vagai veedam aindhu veeLvi aaRu angam vallaar,
meelai vaanavarin mikka veediyar aadhi kaalam,
seel ugaL vayal tiruppeerch chem kaN maaloodum vaazhvaar,
seelam maa thavaththar sindhai aaLi en sindhaiyaanee.
491.meaning: From the beginning of time, those Brahmins — masters of the four kinds of Veda, the five sacrifices and the six limbs (of the Vedas), surpassing even the celestials above — dwell together with the red-eyed Tirumal at Tiruppeer, where the carp-fish leap in the paddy fields. He who rules the hearts of those virtuous men of great austerity dwells also in my heart.

492.translit:
vaNdu aRai pozhil tiruppeer vari aravu aNaiyil paLLi
koNdu uRaiginra maalaik kodi madhiL maada mangai,
thiN thiRal thoaL kaliyan chen solaal mozhindha maalai,
koNdu ivai paadi aada kooduvaar neeL visumbee.
492.meaning: This garland of fair words was sung by Kaliyan of mighty, strong shoulders — lord of Tirumangai with its banner-decked walls and mansions — upon Tirumal who reclines and abides on the striped-serpent couch at Tiruppeer, where bees hum in the groves. Those who take up these verses, singing and dancing them, shall attain the vast high heavens.

493.translit:
theedhu aRu nilaththodu eri kaalinodu neerkku ezhu visumbum avaiyaay,
maasu aRu manaththinodu uRakkamodu iRakkai avaiyaaya perumaan,
taay seRa uLaindhu thayir uNdu kudam aadu thada maarvar thagai seer,
naadhan uRaiginra nagar nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamee.
493.meaning: He became the flawless earth, the fire, the wind, the water and the soaring sky; that great Lord became the spotless mind, and sleep, and dying. When His mother chid Him He fretted, He ate the curds, He danced with the pots — He of the broad and lovely chest. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram, the city where that excellent Lord dwells!

494.translit:
uyyum vagai uNdu sona seyyil ulagu eezhum ozhiyaamai muna naaL,
meyyin aLavee amudhu seyya vala aiyan avan meevu nagar thaan,
mai vari vaNdu madhu uNdu kiLaiyoodu malar kiNdi adhan meel,
nai vaLam naviltru pozhil nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamee.
494.meaning: There is a way to be saved if we do as He has told; in days of old that capable Lord swallowed up the seven worlds within His very body so that none should perish — this is His city. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram, with its groves where dark-streaked bees drink the nectar, probe the blossoms with their swarms, and hum the sweet naivaLam strain upon them!

495.translit:
umbar ulagu eezhu kadal eezhu malai eezhum ozhiyaamai muna naaL,
tham pon vayiRu aar aLavum uNdu avai umizhndha thada maarvar thagai seer,
vambu malarginra pozhil paim pon varu thumbi maNi kangul vayal soozh,
namban uRaiginra nagar nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamee.
495.meaning: In days of old He swallowed up — so that none should perish — the seven upper worlds, the seven seas and the seven mountains, filling His own golden belly, and then spewed them forth again: He of the broad and lovely chest. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram, the city where that trustworthy Lord dwells, girt with groves where fresh flowers bloom, with green-gold bees, and with paddy fields dark as gems in the night!

496.translit:
piRaiyin oLi eyiRu ilaga muRugi edhir porudhum ena vandha asurar
iRaigaL avai neRu neRu ena veRiya avar vayiRu azhala nindra perumaan,
siRai koL mayil kuyil payila malargaL uga aLi mural adi koL nedu maa,
naRai sey pozhil mazhai thavazhum nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamee.
496.meaning: The Lord stood firm so that the joints of the asuras — who came on, their teeth gleaming with the brightness of the crescent, swelling to do battle, crying 'We shall fight!' — were crushed to splinters and their bellies burned. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram of the great and lofty Lord — where winged peacocks and cuckoos abound, blossoms shed, bees hum, and clouds creep low over the fragrant groves!

497.translit:
mooLa eri sindhi muni eydhi amar seydhum ena vandha asurar,
thoaLum avar thaaLum mudiyoodu podi aaga nodi aam aLavu eydhaan,
vaaLum vari villum vaLai aazhi gadhai sangam ivai am kai udaiyaan,
naaLum uRaiginra nagar nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamee.
497.meaning: In the span of an instant He smote the asuras — who came in blazing fury, kindling fire and crying 'We shall make war!' — so that their shoulders and feet and crowns were ground to dust. He bears in His lovely hands the sword, the striped bow, the curved discus, the mace and the conch. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram, the city where He dwells forever!

498.translit:
thambiyodu thaam oruvar than thuNaivi kaadhal thuNai aaga muna naaL,
vembi eri kaanagam ulaavum avar thaam inidhu meevu nagar thaan,
kombu kudhi koNdu kuyil koova mayil aalum ezhil aar puRavu seer,
nambi uRaiginra nagar nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamee.
498.meaning: In days of old He, alone with His younger brother and His loving consort as His companions, wandered through the burning, blazing forest — this is the city where He now sweetly dwells. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram, the city where that perfect Lord (Nambi) dwells, with its beautiful surrounding woodlands where cuckoos leap upon the branches and call, and peacocks dance!

499.translit:
thandhai manam undhu thuyar nanna iruL vandha viRal nandhan madhalai,
endhai ivan endru amarar kandha malar koNdu thozha nindra nagar thaan,
mandha muzhava oosai mazhai aaga ezhu kaar mayilgaL aadu pozhil soozh,
nandhi paNi seydha nagar nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamee.
499.meaning: The mighty son of Nanda, born to drive away the deep sorrow that pressed upon his father's heart — He is the one before whom the celestials stand and worship with fragrant blossoms, saying 'This is our Father.' O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram — the city built by Nandi's service, girt with groves where the gentle drum-like sound (of thunder) is as rain, and dark peacocks rise and dance!

500.translit:
eNNil ninaiveydhi iniyillai iRai eNRu muniyaaLar tiruvaar,
paNNil mali geethamodu paadiyavar aadalodu kooda ezhilaar,
maNNil idhu pola nagar illai ena vaanavargaL taam malargaL tooy
naNNi uRaiginRa nagar nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamE.
500.meaning: The sages, having meditated countlessly, say there is now nothing more to attain and praise the Lord as the highest. They sing melodious songs rich in tune and join in dance, and the celestials, scattering pure flowers, say there is no city on earth like this beautiful one. O my heart, reach for Nandhipura Vinnagaram, the city where the Lord lovingly dwells.

501.translit:
vangam mali pauvamadhu maamugadin uchchi puga mikka peruneer,
angu amazhiyaa avanadhu aaNai talai sooDum aDiyaar aRidhiyEl,
pongu punal undhu maNi kangul iruL seeRum oLi engumuLadhaal,
nangaL perumaan uRaiyum nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamE.
501.meaning: Where the great ocean filled with ships rises to the very peak of the mountain with abundant waters, know that the devotees who bear upon their heads the command of that Lord whose glory never fades. The surging waters carry gems whose radiant light dispels the darkness of night everywhere. O my heart, reach for Nandhipura Vinnagaram, the city where our Lord dwells.

502.translit:
naRaisey pozhil mazhai tavazhum nandhipura viNNagaram naNNi uRaiyum,
uRaikoL pugar aazhi suri sangam avai angai uDaiyaanai, oLisEr
kaRai vaLarum vEl valla kaliyan oli maalai ivai aindhum aindhum,
muRaiyil avai payila valla aDiyavargaL koduvinaigaL muzhudhu agalumE.
502.meaning: Upon the Lord who dwells in Nandhipura Vinnagaram, where clouds creep over honey-laden groves, the Lord who holds in his fair hand the gleaming sheathed discus and the curved conch, Kaliyan, master of the radiant blood-stained spear, sang these ten garland-verses of melody. For the devotees who can recite them duly, all their cruel sins will wholly depart.

503.translit:
vaNDuNu naRumalar iNDai koNDu paNDai nam vinai keDa eNRu, aDimEl
toNDarum amarum paNiya ninRu angu aNDamoDu agalidam aLandhavanE
aaNDaay unai kaaNbadhu Or aruL enakku aruLudhiyEl,
vENDEn manai vaazhkkaiyai viNNagar mEyavanE.
503.meaning: You who stood and measured the wide earth together with the heavens, so that even your devotees bow down with garlands of fragrant flowers that bees feed upon, saying may our ancient sins be destroyed at your feet. You have ruled me; if you grant me the grace to behold you, I shall not desire the life of household existence, O Lord who abides in Vinnagar.

504.translit:
aNNal sey talaikaDal kaDaindhadhu adhanuL kaNNudhal nanju uNa kaNDavanE
viNNavar amudhu uNa amudhil varum peNNamudhu uNDa em perumaanE
aaNDaay unai kaaNbadhu Or aruL enakku aruLudhiyEl,
vENDEn manai vaazhkkaiyai viNNagar mEyavanE.
504.meaning: O noble one who, when the mighty ocean was churned, looked on as the forehead-eyed Shiva consumed its poison. O my Lord who, so that the celestials might drink nectar, partook of the maiden-nectar (Lakshmi) who arose with the ambrosia. You have ruled me; if you grant me the grace to behold you, I shall not desire the life of household existence, O Lord who abides in Vinnagar.

505.translit:
kuzhal niRa vaNNa nin kooRu koNDa tazhal niRa vaNNan naNNaar nagaram
vizha nani malai silai vaLaivu sey angu azhal niRa ambadhu vaanavanE
aaNDaay unnai kaaNbadhu Or aruL enakku aruLudhiyEl,
vENDEn manai vaazhkkaiyai viNNagar mEyavanE.
505.meaning: O Lord of the celestials, whose form is the hue of dark cloud, who, with the fire-hued one (Shiva) who shares half your body, bent the great mountain as a bow and shot the fire-colored arrow so that the cities of your foes were destroyed. You have ruled me; if you grant me the grace to behold you, I shall not desire the life of household existence, O Lord who abides in Vinnagar.

506.translit:
nilavoDu veyil nila irusuDarum ulagamum uyirgaLum uNDu orukaal,
kalaitaru kuzhaviyin uruvinaiyaay alaikaDal aalilai vaLarndhavanE
aaNDaay unai kaaNbadhu Or aruL enakku aruLudhiyEl
vENDEn manai vaazhkkaiyai viNNagar mEyavanE.
506.meaning: O Lord who once swallowed both luminaries, the moon and the sun, and the world and all its living beings, and who, taking the form of an infant, grew (reclining) upon a banyan leaf on the surging ocean. You have ruled me; if you grant me the grace to behold you, I shall not desire the life of household existence, O Lord who abides in Vinnagar.

507.translit:
paar ezhu kaDal ezhu malai ezhumaay seer kezhu ivvulagu Ezhum ellaam,
aar kezhu vayiTRinil aDakki ninRu angu Or ezhuttu Or uruvaanavanE
aaNDaay unai kaaNbadhu Or aruL enakku aruLudhiyEl,
vENDEn manai vaazhkkaiyai viNNagar mEyavanE.
507.meaning: O Lord who became the seven worlds, the seven seas, and the seven mountains, and held all these glorious seven worlds within your full belly, standing as the one syllable (Om) and the one form. You have ruled me; if you grant me the grace to behold you, I shall not desire the life of household existence, O Lord who abides in Vinnagar.

508.translit:
kaar kezhu kaDalgaLum malaigaLumaay Er kezhu ulagamumaagi, mudhal
aargaLum aRivaru nilaiyinaiyaay seer kezhu naanmaRaiyaanavanE
aaNDaay unai kaaNbadhu Or aruL enakku aruLudhiyEl,
vENDEn manai vaazhkkaiyai viNNagar mEyavanE.
508.meaning: O Lord who became the dark seas and the mountains and the beautiful world, being of a nature that even the foremost ones cannot comprehend, and who became the glorious four Vedas. You have ruled me; if you grant me the grace to behold you, I shall not desire the life of household existence, O Lord who abides in Vinnagar.

509.translit:
urukkuRu naruney koNDu aarazhalil iRukkuRum andhaNar sandhiyin vaay,
perukkamoDu amarargaL amara nalgum irukkinil innisaiyaanavanE
aaNDaay unai kaaNbadhu Or aruL enakku aruLudhiyEl,
vENDEn manai vaazhkkaiyai viNNagar mEyavanE.
509.meaning: O Lord who became the sweet music of the Rig Veda, by which the brahmins, pouring melted fragrant ghee into the blazing sacrificial fire at the junctures of the day, abundantly bestow blessings so that the celestials are gratified. You have ruled me; if you grant me the grace to behold you, I shall not desire the life of household existence, O Lord who abides in Vinnagar.

510.translit:
kaadhal sey iLaiyavar kalavi tarum vEdhanai vinaiyadhu veruvudhalaam,
aadhalin unadhu aDi aNuguvan naan podhal aar neDumuDi puNNiyanE
aaNDaay unai kaaNbadhu Or aruL enakku aruLudhiyEl,
vENDEn manai vaazhkkaiyai viNNagar mEyavanE.
510.meaning: Dreading the suffering and the karma that arises from union with amorous young women, I therefore draw near to your feet, O righteous one of the tall crown adorned with blossoms. You have ruled me; if you grant me the grace to behold you, I shall not desire the life of household existence, O Lord who abides in Vinnagar.

511.translit:
saadhalum piRattalum eNRu ivaTRai kaadhal seyyaadhu una kazhal aDaindhEn,
oodhal sey naanmaRaiyaagi umbar aadhal sey moovuruvaanavanE,
aaNDaay unai kaaNbadhu Or aruL enakku aruLudhiyEl
vENDEn manai vaazhkkaiyai viNNagar mEyavanE.
511.meaning: Not desiring such things as death and birth, I have reached your feet, O Lord who became the four chanted Vedas and who became the three forms (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva) of the celestials. You have ruled me; if you grant me the grace to behold you, I shall not desire the life of household existence, O Lord who abides in Vinnagar.

512.translit:
poo maru pozhil aNi viNNagar mEl,
kaamaru seer kalikaNRi soNNa,
paa maru tamizh ivai paaDavallaar,
vaamanan aDiyiNai maruvuvarE.
512.meaning: Those who can sing these melodious Tamil verses composed by the renowned Kalikanri (Tirumangai Alwar) upon Vinnagar adorned with flower-filled groves, will attain and abide at the twin feet of Vamana.

513.translit:
poRuttEn pun sol nenjil poruL inbam ena iraNDum
iRuttEn, aimpulan kaTku aDanaayina vaayil oTTi aRuttEn,
aarva seTRam avai tannai manattu agaTRi veRuttEn,
ninnaDaindhEn tiru viNNagar mEyavanE.
513.meaning: I have endured base words in my heart; I have demolished both wealth and pleasure as ends; I have closed the doorways that serve the five senses and severed them; I have driven from my mind desire and hatred and abhorred them. I have come to you, O Lord who abides in holy Vinnagar.

514.translit:
maRandhEn unnai munnam maRandha madhi in manattaal,
iRandhEn ettanaiyum adhanaal iDumbai kuzhiyil
piRandhE eyttu ozhindhEn perumaanE tirumaarbaa
siRandhEn nin aDikkE tiru viNNagar mEyavanE.
514.meaning: I had formerly forgotten you, and with a mind that had lost its wisdom I died many times over, and thereby, born into the pit of misery, I grew weary and was undone. O my Lord, O one with Lakshmi on your chest, now I have become exalted at your very feet, O Lord who abides in holy Vinnagar.

515.translit:
maaney nOkkiyar tam vayiTRu kuzhiyil uzhaikkum,
oon Eraakkai tannai udhavaamai uNarndhu uNarndhu,
vaanE maanilamE vandhu vandhu en manattu irundha tEnE,
ninnaDaindhEn tiru viNNagar mEyavanE.
515.meaning: Realizing again and again that this fleshly body, which toils in the womb-pit of doe-eyed women, is of no avail, I have come to you, O honey who came again and again into my heart, O Lord of the heavens and the great earth. I have reached you, O Lord who abides in holy Vinnagar.

516.translit:
piRindhEn peTRa makkaL peNDir eNRu ivar pinnu udhavaadhu
aRindhEn nee paNittavaruL ennum oL vaaL uruvi
eRindhEn aimpulangaL iDar teera eRindhu vandhu
seRindhEn nin aDikkE tiru viNNagar mEyavanE.
516.meaning: I have parted from the children and wife I begot, knowing that they are of no help in the hereafter; drawing the bright sword that is the grace you bestowed, I struck down the five senses so that my affliction might end, and coming, I have clung fast to your very feet, O Lord who abides in holy Vinnagar.

517.translit:
paaN tEn vaNDu aRaiyum kuzhalaargaL pallaaNDu isaippa,
aaNDaar vaiyam ellaam arasaagi, mun aaNDavarE
maaNDaar eNRu vandhaar andhO manai vaazhkkai tannai vENDEn,
ninnaDaindhEn tiru viNNagar mEyavanE.
517.meaning: While women with tresses where bees hum tunes sang long-life praises, those who ruled all the world as kings, those very ones who once reigned, have come to perish, alas. I do not desire this life of household existence; I have reached you, O Lord who abides in holy Vinnagar.

518.translit:
kallaa aimpulan kaLavai kaNDavaa seyya killEn,
mallaa, mallamaruL mallar maaLa mall aDarttha mallaa,
mallalam seer madhiL neer ilangai azhitta
villaa, ninnaDaindhEn tiru viNNagar mEyavanE.
518.meaning: I am unable to act as the untaught five senses, that thieve, would have me act. O wrestler who, in the wrestling combat, crushed the wrestlers so that they perished; O archer who destroyed water-girt Lanka with its prosperous, glorious ramparts. I have reached you, O Lord who abides in holy Vinnagar.

519.translit:
vERaay aan irandhEn veguLaadhu manakkoLandhaay,
aaRaa vennaragattu aDiyEnai iDak karudhi,
kooRaa aivar vandhu kumaikka kuDi viTTavarai,
tERaadhu unnaDaindhEn tiru viNNagar mEyavanE.
519.meaning: Set apart, I have beseeched you; without growing angry, take me into your heart. When the five senses, that cannot be reasoned with, came and tormented me, intending to cast this servant into the unquenchable cruel hell, I, without trusting those who had ruined my house, have reached you, O Lord who abides in holy Vinnagar.

520.translit:
theevaay valvinai yaarudan inRu siRanthavarpool,
mevaa vennarakaththida uRRu viraindhuvanthaar,
moovaa vaanavar tham mudhalvaa madhi kooLvidhuththa
theevaa, ninnadain theentiru viNNagar meeyavane.
520.meaning: Like one who has flourished while living among cruel, fiery, hard karma, I had come hastening only to fall into the hateful, burning hell. O God who are the first cause of the ageless celestials, who freed the moon from the grip that seized it! I have taken refuge in You, O Lord who dwell in sacred Tiruvinnagar.

521.translit:
poodhaar thaamarai yaaL pulavik kula vaanavar tham
koodhaa, koodhil sengkool kudai manna ridainadandha
thoodhaa, thoomozhi yaay, sudar pool en manaththirundha
veedhaa, ninnadain theentiru viNNagar meeyavane.
521.meaning: O blossoming Lotus-Lady's Lord, O glory of the learned race of celestials, O messenger who walked among the kings of faultless just scepter and parasol, O pure-worded one, O Veda-revealing One who dwelt in my mind like a radiant light! I have taken refuge in You, O Lord who dwell in sacred Tiruvinnagar.

522.translit:
thenaar poompuRavil thiru viNNagar meeyavanai,
vaanaa rum madhil soozh vayal mangaiyar koon, maruvaar
oonaar veelkali yan oli seydhamizh maalaivallaar,
koonaay vaanavar tham kodi maanagar kooduvare.
522.meaning: Whoever masters this melodious garland of Tamil composed by Kaliyan, lord of Mangai of fields girt by sky-high walls, wielder of the flesh-piercing spear against foes — on Him who dwells in sacred Tiruvinnagar of honey-rich flowering groves — they shall reach as kings the great banner-decked city of the celestials.

523.translit:
thuRappeen alleen inbam thuRavaadhu, ninnuruvam
maRappeen alleen enRum maRavaadhu, yaan ulagil
piRappeen aagaveN NeN piRavaamai peRRadhu, nin
thiRaththeen aadhanmaiyaal thiruviN Nagaraane.
523.meaning: I shall not renounce the bliss of You, not renouncing it; I shall not forget Your form, never forgetting it. I do not intend to be born again in this world; that I have attained birthlessness is because I belong wholly to You, O Lord of sacred Tiruvinnagar.

524.translit:
thuRandheen aarva seRRach chuRRam thuRandhamaiyaal,
siRandheen ninnadikke yadimai thirumaale,
aRandhaa naaytthiri vaay, unnai en manaththagaththe,
thiRambaa mal koNdeen thiruviN Nagaraane.
524.meaning: Because I have renounced desire, hatred and kin, I have become exalted as a servant only at Your feet, O Tirumal. O You who wander about as Dharma itself, I have grasped You unswervingly within my mind, O Lord of sacred Tiruvinnagar.

525.translit:
maaneey nookkunallaar madhipool mugaththulavum,
ooneey kaNvaaLik kudainthoot tanthun nadaintheen,
koone kuRunkudiyuL kuzhagaa thirunaRaiyoort
thene, varupunalsoozh thiruviN Nagaraane.
525.meaning: Pierced and routed by the flesh-cutting arrows that are the eyes of fair doe-eyed women playing upon their moon-like faces, I fled and reached You. O King, O charming one of Kurungudi, O honey of Tirunaraiyur, O Lord of sacred Tiruvinnagar girt by flowing waters!

526.translit:
saanthendhu menmulai yaar thadanthool puNarinba veLLath
thaazhndheen, arunagarath thazhundhum payanpadaiththeen,
poondheen, puNNiyane, unaiyeydhi en theevinaigaL
theerndheen, ninnadaintheen thiruviN Nagaraane.
526.meaning: I sank into the flood of pleasure of embracing the broad shoulders of women with soft sandal-smeared breasts, and so earned the fruit of drowning in the dreadful city of hell. But I have come away. O Righteous One, having reached You my cruel deeds are ended; I have taken refuge in You, O Lord of sacred Tiruvinnagar.

527.translit:
maRRoor theyvameNNen unnaiyen manaththuvaiththup
peRReen, peRRadhuvum piRavaamai emperumaan,
vaRRaa neeLkadalsoo zhilangaiyi raavaNanaich
seRRaay, koRRavane, thiruviN Nagaraane.
527.meaning: I think of no other deity; having set You in my mind I have gained — and what I have gained is birthlessness, O my Lord. O victor who destroyed Ravana of Lanka, girt by the vast unfailing ocean, O triumphant Lord of sacred Tiruvinnagar!

528.translit:
maiyoN karunkadalum nilanu maNivaraiyum,
seyya sudarirandum ivaiyaaya ninnai, nenjil
uyyum vagaiyuNarndhe uNmaiyaal ini yaadhu maRRoor
theyvam piRidhaRiyeen thiruviN Nagaraane.
528.meaning: Having truly realized in my heart, in a way that redeems me, that You are the dark blue ocean, the earth, the jeweled mountains, and the two bright luminaries — the sun and moon — I henceforth know no other deity whatsoever, O Lord of sacred Tiruvinnagar.

529.translit:
veeRe kooRuvadhuN dadiyeen viriththuraikkum
aaRe, neepaNiyaa thadainin thirumanaththu,
kooReen nenjuthannaal kuNangkoNdu maR Roortheyvam
theeReen unnaiyallaal thiruviN Nagaraane.
529.meaning: There is yet another thing this servant has to declare in detail — bid me, O You who abide in Your own sacred mind without command. I shall not speak; through my heart I take only Your qualities, and I trust no other deity but You, O Lord of sacred Tiruvinnagar.

530.translit:
muLitheentha vengkadaththu moorippe rungkaLiRRaal,
viLitheentha maamarampool veezhndhaarai ninaiyaadhe
aLindhoornadha sindhaininpaa, ladiyeeRkku, vaanulagam
theLindheyen Reyduvadhu? thiruviN Nagaraane.
530.meaning: Without thinking of those who have fallen like a great tree felled by a mighty rutting elephant in the parched, scorched forest, when shall I, Your servant whose melting, single-minded thought is toward You, clearly attain the heavenly world? O Lord of sacred Tiruvinnagar.

531.translit:
sollaay thirumaarvaa unakkaagith thoNdupatta
nalle nai vinaigaL naliyaamai nambunambee,
mallaagudamaadi, madhusootha ne ulagil sellaa
nallisaiyaay thiruviN Nagaraane.
531.meaning: Speak, O You with the sacred chest! Trust me, O trustworthy One, so that karma does not torment me, the good one who has become a servant for Your sake. O wrestler who danced the pot-dance, O Madhusudana, O One of good renown spreading through the world, O Lord of sacred Tiruvinnagar!

532.translit:
thaaraar malarkkamalath thadanjoozhndha thaNpuRavil,
seeraar nedumaRugil thiruviN Nagaraanai
kaaraar puyalthadakkaik kaliya nolimaalai,
aaraa rivaivallaar avarkkallal nillaave.
532.meaning: Whoever is able in these melodious garland-verses by Kaliyan of cloud-like generous hands, on the Lord of sacred Tiruvinnagar — set in the cool grove ringed by broad lotus-flower ponds, with its splendid long streets — for them no sorrow shall remain.

533.translit:
kaNNum suzhanRu peeLaiyoo deeLaivan thengkinaal,
paNNin mozhiyaar paiya nadamin en naadhamun,
viNNum malaiyum vedhamum veeLviyu maayinaan,
naNNu naRaiyoor naam thozhudhum ezhu nenjame.
533.meaning: Before the eyes grow dim and whirl and, with rheum and phlegm, one gasps — and women of musical speech say 'walk gently, will you not!' and so scorn us — rise, O my heart, let us go and worship at Naraiyur where He who became the sky, the mountains, the Vedas and the sacrifices abides.

534.translit:
kongkuN kuzhalaar koodi yirundhu siriththu, neer
ingkennirumi yempaal vandhadhen Rigazhaadhamun,
thinga Lerikaal senjuda raayavan thesudai
nangaL naRaiyoor naam thozhudhum ezhu nenjame.
534.meaning: Before the women with fragrant tresses gather, sit, laugh and scorn us saying 'why have you come coughing to us here!' — rise, O my heart, let us go and worship at our radiant Naraiyur of Him who became the moon, the fire, the wind, and the bright sun.

535.translit:
kongkaar kuzhalaar koodi yirundhu, siriththu, emmai
engkolam aiyaa enninik kaaNbadhen naadhamun
sengkool valavan thaanpaNin theththith thigazhumoor,
nangkoon naRaiyoor naam thozhudhum ezhu nenjame.
535.meaning: Before the fragrant-tressed women gather, sit, laugh and scorn us saying 'Sir, what beauty have you — what is there to look at in you now!' — rise, O my heart, let us go and worship at Naraiyur, the city that shines where the wielder of the just scepter himself bowed and praised, the city of our King.

536.translit:
kombum aravamum valliyum venRanuN Neridai,
vambuN kuzhalaar vaasa ladaiththiga zhaadhamun,
sembon kamuginan thaankani yum sezhunj solaisoozh
namban naRaiyoor naam thozhudhum ezhu nenjame.
536.meaning: Before the women with fragrance-filled tresses and waists slender, surpassing the creeper, the serpent and the flowering branch, shut their doors and scorn us — rise, O my heart, let us go and worship at Naraiyur of the trustworthy Lord, girt by lush groves where the reddish-gold areca clusters ripen into fruit.

537.translit:
vilangkum kayalum veelumoN kaaviyum venRakaN
salamkoNda sollaar thaangaL siriththiga zhaadhamun,
malangkum varaalum vaaLaiyum paayvayal soozhtharu,
nalangkoL naRaiyoor naam thozhudhum ezhu nenjame.
537.meaning: Before women with eyes outshining the leaping carp, the spear and the bright water-lily, and with deceitful words, laugh and scorn us — rise, O my heart, let us go and worship at lovely Naraiyur, ringed by fields where the murrel and the scabbard-fish leap and writhe.

538.translit:
minne ridaiyaar veetkaiyai maaRRi yirundhu,
enneer irumiyem paalvandha thenRiga zhaadhamun,
thonneer ilangkai malanga ilangkeri yoottinaan,
nanneer naRaiyoor naam thozhudhum ezhu nenjame.
538.meaning: Before women with lightning-like waists, having turned away their desire, sit and scorn us saying 'why have you come coughing to us!' — rise, O my heart, let us go and worship at Naraiyur of fine waters, of Him who set blazing fire to confound ancient sea-girt Lanka.

539.translit:
villeer nudhalaar veetkaiyai maaRRich siriththu, ivan
pollaan thiraindhaan ennum puRanurai keetpadhanmun,
sollaar maRainaan koodhi yulagil nilaayavar,
nallaar naRaiyoor naam thozhudhum ezhu nenjame.
539.meaning: Before women with bow-like brows turn away their desire, laugh, and we hear the slighting talk behind our backs — 'this one is ugly, he has grown wrinkled' — rise, O my heart, let us go and worship at Naraiyur of the good, where dwell in this world those who recite the four spoken Vedas.

540.translit:
vaaL oN kaN nallaar taangaL madhanan endraar thammai,
keeLumin kaL leelaiyoodu eengu kizhavan ennaadha mun,
veeLvum vizhavum veedhiyil endrum aRaadha oor,
naaLum naRaiyoor naam thozhuthum ezhu nenjamee.
540.meaning: Before the days come when bright sword-eyed beautiful women call you their Manmatha (god of love) and then, hearing your faltering ways, sigh and call you an old man, let us worship Naraiyur—the town where sacrifices, festivals, and revelry never cease in its streets day after day. O my heart, rise, let us go and worship it.

541.translit:
kani seerndhu ilangu nal vaayavar kaadhanmai vittida,
kuni seerndhu udalam koalil thaLarndhu iLaiyaadha mun,
pani seer visumbil paal madhi koaL viduththaan idam,
nani seer naRaiyoor naam thozhuthum ezhu nenjamee.
541.meaning: Before the day comes when women of lips bright like ripe fruit cast off their love for you, and your body, stooping and leaning on a staff, grows feeble and worn, let us worship the abode of Him who released the milk-white moon from its eclipse in the cool dewy sky—the rich town of Naraiyur. O my heart, rise, let us go and worship it.

542.translit:
piRai seer nudhalaar peenudhal nammai ilaadha mun,
naRai seer pozhil soozh naRaiyoor thozhu nenjamee endra,
kaRai aar nedu veel mangaiyar koan kalikandRi sol,
maRavaadhu uraippavar vaanavarkku innarasaavaaree.
542.meaning: "Before women whose foreheads are like the crescent moon cease to cherish us, O my heart, worship Naraiyur encircled by groves fragrant with honey"—so sang Kalikanri, lord of the Mangai people, wielder of the long blood-stained spear. Those who recite his words without forgetting them shall become sweet rulers among the celestials.

543.translit:
kalanga mun neer kadaindhu amudham koNdu, imaiyoar
thulangal theera nalgu soadhi sudaraaya,
valangai aazhi idangai sangam udaiyaan oor,
nalam koL vaaymai andhaNar vaazhum naRaiyooree.
543.meaning: When the ancient ocean was churned into turmoil, He took the nectar and graciously gave it so the suffering of the celestials would end—the radiant flaming Lord who bears the discus in His right hand and the conch in His left. His town is Naraiyur, where virtuous, truthful Brahmins dwell.

544.translit:
munai aar seeyam aagi avuNan muraN maarvam,
punai vaaL ugiraal poazh pada eerndha punidhan oor,
sinai aar thee maam sem thaLir koadhi kuyil koovum,
nanai aar soalai soozhndhu azhagaaya naRaiyooree.
544.meaning: Becoming a fierce Lion-man (Narasimha), the pure Lord tore apart the mighty chest of the demon (Hiranya) with His sharp gleaming claws so that it split open. His town is the lovely Naraiyur, surrounded by dew-laden groves where the cuckoo pecks at the red tender shoots of branching sweet-mango trees and calls out.

545.translit:
aanai puravi theerodu kaaLaaL aNi koNda,
seenai thogaiyai saadi ilangai sendRaan oor,
meenai thazhuvi veezhndhu ezhum maLLarku alamandhu,
naanam pudhalil aamai oLikkum naRaiyooree.
545.meaning: He destroyed the massed armies arrayed with elephants, horses, chariots, and foot-soldiers, and laid waste to Lanka—that is His town. It is Naraiyur, where the turtle, alarmed by the strong men who plunge in embracing the fish and rise again, hides itself within the fragrant musk-thickets.

546.translit:
uRi aar veNNey uNdu ural oodum kattuNdu,
veRi aar koondhal pinnai poruttu aan vendRaan oor,
poRi aar maññai poom pozhil thoaRum nadamaada,
naRu naaL malar meel vaNdu isai paadum naRaiyooree.
546.meaning: He ate the butter from the hanging pots, was bound to the mortar, and conquered the bulls for the sake of Pinnai of fragrant tresses—that is His town. It is Naraiyur, where spotted peacocks dance through the flowering groves and bees sing their melody upon the fresh fragrant blossoms.

547.translit:
vidai eezh vendRu men thoaL aaychchikku anbanaay,
nadaiyaal nindRa marudham saaiththa naadhan oor,
pedaiyodu annam pey vaLaiyaar tham pin sendRu
nadaiyodu iyali naaNi oLikkum naRaiyooree.
547.meaning: He conquered the seven bulls and, as the beloved of the soft-shouldered cowherd-girl (Pinnai), He felled the marudam trees that stood in His path—that Lord's town. It is Naraiyur, where the swan with its mate, following behind the bangled women, matches their gait and then, ashamed, hides itself away.

548.translit:
pagu vaay van peey kongai suvaiththu aar uyir uNdu,
pugu vaay nindRa poadhagam veezha porudhaan oor,
negu vaay neydhal poo madhu maandhi kamalaththin
nagu vaay malar meel annam uRangum naRaiyooree.
548.meaning: He sucked the breast of the gaping-mouthed cruel demoness (Putana) and drank away her life, and fought and felled the elephant (Kuvalayapida) that stood blocking the entrance—that is His town. It is Naraiyur, where the swan, having drunk the honey from the opening waterlily blossoms, sleeps upon the smiling petals of the lotus.

549.translit:
mundhu noolum muppuri noolum mun eendha,
andhaNaaLan piLLaiyai annaandRu aLiththaan oor,
pondhil vaazhum piLLaikku aagi puL oadi,
nandhu vaarum paim punal vaavi naRaiyooree.
549.meaning: He restored, on that day long ago, the children of the Brahmin who had earlier given Him the ancient scriptures and the triple-stranded sacred thread—that is His town. It is Naraiyur, where, just as the bird flew for the sake of the young ones living in its hollow, the conch-shells drift and gather in the fresh-watered ponds.

550.translit:
veLLai puravai theer vijayaRkaay viRal viyoogam
viLLa, sindhu koan vizha oorndha vimalan oor,
koLLai kozhu meen uN kurugu oadi pedaiyodum,
naLLa kamalaththu eeRal ugukkum naRaiyooree.
550.meaning: For the sake of Arjuna of the white-horsed chariot, the spotless Lord drove the chariot so that the mighty battle-formation broke apart and the king of Sindhu (Jayadratha) fell—that is His town. It is Naraiyur, where the heron that has feasted on plentiful fat fish runs with its mate, and as it nestles among the lotuses, the nectar spills from the blossoms.

551.translit:
paarai oorum paaram theera paarththan than
theerai oorum deeva deevan seerum oor,
taarai oorum thaN thaLir veeli pudai soozha,
naarai oorum nal vayal soozhndha naRaiyooree.
551.meaning: To remove the burden borne by the earth, the God of gods drove Arjuna's chariot—that is the town where He dwells. It is Naraiyur, surrounded on all sides by hedges of cool tender shoots upon which dew descends, and encircled by fine fields where herons gather.

552.translit:
thaamath thuLapa neeL mudi maayan thaan nindRa
naamath thiraL maa maaLigai soozhndha naRaiyoor meel,
kaama kadhir veel vallaan kaliyan oli maalai,
seemath thuNaiyaam seppum avarkku thirumaalee.
552.meaning: This garland of resonant verses on Naraiyur—surrounded by great mansions and bearing the sacred name—where the wondrous Lord (Mayan) stands with His long crown of garlanded tulasi, was sung by Kaliyan, master of the gleaming desire-spear. For those who recite it, Tirumal Himself becomes their protecting refuge.

553.translit:
ambaramum peru nilanum disaigaL ettum alai kadalum kula varaiyum uNda kaNdan,
kombu amarum vada maraththin ilai meel paLLi koodinaan thiruvadiyee koodi kiRpeer,
vamba vizhum seNbagaththin vaasam uNdu maNi vaNdu vaguLaththin malar meel vaigu,
sembiyan koach sengaNaan seerndha koayil thirunaRaiyoor maNi maadam seerminkaLee.
553.meaning: He who swallowed the sky, the vast earth, the eight directions, the surging seas, and the great mountains, and who reclined in slumber upon a leaf of the branching banyan tree—if you would attain His holy feet, then come and join the jewelled mansions of Tirunaraiyur, the temple where the red-eyed Sembiyan Chola king (Kochengannan) dwelt, where the jewel-like bee, having drunk the fragrance of the fresh-blooming shenbaga flower, rests upon the bakula blossom.

554.translit:
kozhung kayalaay nedu veLLam koNda kaalam kula varaiyin meedhu oadi aNdaththu appaal,
ezhundhu inidhu viLaiyaadum eesan endhai iNai adi keezh inidhu iruppeer ina vaNdu aalum
uzhum seRuvil maNi koNarndhu karai meel sindhi ulagellaam sandhanamum agilum koLLa,
sezhum ponni vaLam kodukkum soazhan seerndha thirunaRaiyoor maNi maadam seerminkaLee.
554.meaning: At the time the great flood rose, the Lord became a great fish and, swimming over the lofty mountains and beyond the cosmic egg, rose up and sported joyfully—if you would dwell sweetly beneath the twin feet of that Lord, my Father, then come to the jewelled mansions of Tirunaraiyur. There, where swarms of bees hum, the bountiful Ponni (Kaveri) river carries gems into the ploughed fields and scatters them on the banks, and gives the whole world sandalwood and aghil-wood—the river that the Chola king's land enjoyed.

555.translit:
pavva neer udai aadaiyaaga sutRi paar agalam thiruvadiyaa pavanam meyyaa
sevvi maa diram ettum thoaLaa aNdam thiru mudiyaa nindRaan paal sella kiRpeer
kavvai maa kaLiRu undhi veNNi eetRa kazhal mannar maNi mudi meel kaagam eeRa
deyva vaaL valam koNda soazhan seerndha thirunaRaiyoor maNi maadam seerminkaLee.
555.meaning: He who stands wearing the ocean-waters as His wrapped garment, the breadth of the earth as His feet, the wind as His body, the eight great directions as His shoulders, and the cosmic egg as His sacred crown—if you would draw near to Him, then come to the jewelled mansions of Tirunaraiyur, the town of the Chola who won victory with his divine sword, who at Vennikkalam drove off the great roaring elephants and made crows perch upon the jewelled crowns of the anklet-wearing kings.

556.translit:
paing kaN aaL ari uruvaay veruva noakki paru varai thoaL iraNiyanai patRi vaangi
angai vaaL ugir nudhiyaal avanadhu aagam angu urudhi pongu viththaan adi keezh niRpeer
veng kaN maa kaLiRu undhi veNNi eetRa viRal mannar thiRal azhiya vem maa uyththa
sengaNaan koach soazhan seerndha koayil thirunaRaiyoor maNi maadam seerminkaLee.
556.meaning: Taking the form of a tawny-eyed lion that struck terror with its gaze, He seized and dragged the mountain-shouldered Hiranya, and with the sharp points of the gleaming claws of His palm He made the blood gush forth from that demon's chest—if you would stand beneath the feet of that Lord, then come to the jewelled mansions of Tirunaraiyur, the temple of the red-eyed Kochenganan Chola, who at Vennikkalam drove off the fierce-eyed great elephants and, destroying the strength of the mighty kings, drove his fierce horses upon them.

557.translit:
andRu ulagam moondRinaiyum aLandhu veeRu oar ari uruvaay iraNiyanadhu aagam keeNdu
vendRu avanai viN ulagil sela uyththaaRku virundhaaveer meel ezhundhu vilangal paayndhu
pon sidhaRi maNi koNarndhu karai meel sindhi pulam parandhu nilam parakkum ponni naadan,
then tamizhin, vada pulak koan soazhan seerndha thirunaRaiyoor maNi maadam seerminkaLee.
557.meaning: On that day He measured the three worlds, and in a unique lion-form He tore open Hiranya's chest, vanquished him, and sent him on to the world of the heavens—if you would be guests of that Lord, then come to the jewelled mansions of Tirunaraiyur. It is the town the Ponni-river country enjoys, whose river leaps over the mountains, scatters gold and carries gems to spill upon its banks, spreading over the fields and the land—the land of the Chola, king of the northern realm of sweet Tamil.

558.translit:
thannaalee than uruvam payandha thaanaay thayang oLi seer moovulagum thaanaay vaanaay,
thannaalee thaan uruvil moorththi moondRaay thaan aayanaayinaan saraN endRu uyveer
minnaadu veel eendhu viLaindha veeLai viN eeRa thani veel uyththu ulagam aaNda
then naadan kuda kongan soazhan seerndha thirunaRaiyoor maNi maadam seerminkaLee.
558.meaning: He who by His own self brought forth His own form and became all, who became the three radiant glorious worlds and the heavens, who by His own self became the three forms (the trinity) and yet became Krishna the cowherd—take refuge in Him and be saved; come to the jewelled mansions of Tirunaraiyur, the town of the Chola, lord of the southern land and of Kudakongu, who hurled his solitary spear to send the rising enemy host up to the heavens and ruled the world.

559.translit:
mulai thadaththa nañju uNdu thuñja peychchi mudhu thuvaraik kula padhiyaa kaali pinnee
ilai thadaththa kuzhal oodhi aayar maadhar ina vaLai koNdaan adi keezh eydha kiRpeer
malai thadaththa maNi koNarndhu vaiyam uyya vaLam kodukkum varu punalam ponni naadan
silai thadak kai kula soazhan seerndha koayil thirunaRaiyoor maNi maadam seerminkaLee.
559.meaning: He drank the poison from the demoness's breast so that she perished, became the clan-lord of ancient Dvaraka, and behind the cattle played the leaf-shaped flute, taking away the rows of bangles of the cowherd women—if you would reach beneath the feet of that Lord, then come to the jewelled mansions of Tirunaraiyur, the temple of the bow-armed Kula Chola of the flowing Ponni-river land, whose river brings gems down from the mountains and gives bounty for the world to flourish.

560.translit:
murukkilangu kanith-thuvar-vaayp pinnai keeLvan mannellaam munnaviyac chenru, venric
cherukkaLaththuth thiral-azhiyac chetra veendhan sirandhuNindhaan thiruvadi num senni vaippeer
irukkilangu thirumozhi-vaay eNtooL eesarkku ezhil-maada mezhupadhu seydhu ulagam aaNda
thirukkulaththu vaLac-choozhan seerndha kooyil thirunaRaiyoor maNimaadam seermingaLee.
560.meaning: He who is the beloved of Pinnai of red coral-like lips bright as the murukku blossom, who went forth and made all the assembled kings perish before Him, who in the victorious battlefield struck them down so their valour was destroyed and severed their heads -- place His sacred feet upon your heads. Resort to the jewelled palace town of Tirunaraiyur, the temple reached by the bountiful Chola of the sacred lineage who ruled the world and built seventy beautiful mansions for the eight-shouldered Lord of the radiant sacred Vedic word.

561.translit:
thaaraaLan thaN-aranga vaaLan poo-meel thaniyaaLan muniyaaLar eeththa ninra
peeraaLan aayiram-peer udaiya vaaLan pinnaikku maNavaaLan perumai keetpeer,
paaraaLar avar-ivar enru azhundhai eetra padai-mannar udal thuNiyap parimaa uyththa
theeraaLan koc-choozhan seerndha kooyil thirunaRaiyoor maNimaadam seermingaLee.
561.meaning: Hear the greatness of the generous Lord, the master of cool Srirangam, the matchless one upon the lotus, the great one whom the sages praise as they stand, the Lord bearing a thousand names, the bridegroom of Pinnai. Resort to the jewelled palace town of Tirunaraiyur, the temple reached by the great charioteer Kochozhan, who -- saying 'these and those rule the earth' -- drove his horses and chariot so the bodies of the warring kings who came against Azhundai were cut asunder.

562.translit:
chemmozhi-vaay naal-veedha vaaNar vaazhum thirunaRaiyoor maNimaadac chengaN-maalai
poym-mozhi onru illaadha meymmaiyaaLan pula-mangaik kula-veendhan pulamai-aarndha
ammozhi-vaayk kalikanri inbap paadal paaduvaar viyan-ulagil namanaar-paadi
vemmozhi keettu anjaadhee meymmai chollil viNNavarkku virundhaagum perun-thakkooree.
562.meaning: This is the sweet song of the red-eyed Lord of the jewelled palace town of Tirunaraiyur, where dwell scholars of the four Vedas of refined speech -- composed by Kalikanri, the truthful one without a single false word, the chief of the line of Pulamangai, rich in learning and noble speech. The great and worthy ones who sing these joyous songs in this wide world, if they speak the truth and do not fear hearing the harsh words of Yama's town, shall become honoured guests of the celestials.

563.translit:
aaLum paNiyum adiyeenaik koNdaan viNda nisaasararai
tooLum thalaiyum thuNiveydhac chuduveny chilai-vaayc charandhurandhaan
veeLum seeyum anaiyaarum veeRkaNaarum payil-veedhi
naaLum vizhavin oli-oovaa naRaiyoor ninra nambiyee.
