563.meaning: He took me, His servant, into service and bondage; against the rebellious night-roving demons He sped arrows from His fierce blazing bow so that their shoulders and heads were severed. He is the Nampi who stands in Naraiyur, whose streets throng with those lovely as Kama and Murugan, and with spear-eyed women, where the sound of festivals never ceases day after day.

564.translit:
muniyaay vandhu moo-ezhu-kaal mudi-seer mannar udal-thuNiya
thani-vaay mazhuvin padai-aaNda thaaraar tooLaan, vaar-puRavil
pani-seer mullai pal-arumbap paanal oru-paal kaN-kaatta
nani-seer kamalam mugam-kaattum naRaiyoor ninra nambiyee.
564.meaning: He came as the sage (Parasurama) and wielded the weapon of the single-bladed axe so that the bodies of the crowned kings were cut asunder thrice-seven times -- the Lord of garlanded shoulders. He is the Nampi who stands in Naraiyur, where in the wide meadows the dew-laden jasmine sprouts many buds, the blue water-lily on one side seems to show its eyes, and the abundant lotus shows its face.

565.translit:
theLLaar kadal-vaay vida-vaaya sina-vaaL aravil thuyil-amarndhu
thuLLaa varum-aan vizha-vaaLi thurandhaan irandhaan maavali-maN
puLLaar puRavil poong-kaavi pulang-koL maadhar kaN-kaatta
naLLaar kamalam mugam-kaattum naRaiyoor ninra nambiyee.
565.meaning: He reposes in slumber upon the venom-mouthed, wrathful, bright serpent in the clear sea; He sped an arrow to fell the leaping deer, and He begged for the land from Mahabali. He is the Nampi who stands in Naraiyur, where in the bird-filled meadows the beautiful blue lily seems to show the eyes of bewitching women and the dense lotus shows its face.

566.translit:
oLiyaa veNNey uNdaan enru uraloodu aaycchi oN-kayitraal
viLiyaa aarkka aappuNdu vimmi azhudhaan men-malar-meel
kaLiyaa vaNdu kaLLuNNak kaamar thenral alar-thootra
naLir-vaay mullai muRuvalikkum naRaiyoor ninra nambiyee.
566.meaning: Saying 'He ate the butter secretly,' the cowherd woman bound Him with a fine rope to the mortar, and tied and called out -- and He sobbed and wept. He is the Nampi who stands in Naraiyur, where the joyous bees drink nectar upon soft flowers, the lovely southern breeze scatters the blossoms, and the cool-mouthed jasmine smiles.

567.translit:
villaar vizhavil vada-madhurai virumbi virumbaa mal-adarththu
kallaar thiral-tooL kanjanaik kaayndhaan paayndhaan kaaLiyan-meel
chollaar surudhi muRai-oodhic choomuc cheyyum thozhilinoor
nallaar maRaiyoor palar-vaazhum naRaiyoor ninra nambiyee.
567.meaning: Delighting in the bow-festival at northern Mathura, He crushed the unwilling wrestlers, raged against the stout stone-like shouldered Kamsa, and leapt upon Kaliya. He is the Nampi who stands in Naraiyur, where dwell many good brahmins versed in the Vedas, who chant the scriptures in due order of words and perform the Soma sacrifices.

568.translit:
vaLLi kozhunan mudhalaaya makkaLoodu muk-kaNNaan
veLgi-ooda viRal-vaaNan viyan-tooL vanaththaith thuNiththu-ugandhaan
paLLi kamalaththu idaippatta paguvaay alavan mugam-noogi
naLLi-yoodum vayal-soozhndha naRaiyoor ninra nambiyee.
568.meaning: While the three-eyed one (Shiva), together with his sons led by the consort of Valli (Murugan), fled in shame, He rejoiced in severing the forest of the mighty Banasura's broad shoulders. He is the Nampi who stands in Naraiyur, surrounded by fields where the wide-mouthed crab, lying amid the lotus bed, looks upon the female crab's face and dotes upon it.

569.translit:
midaiyaa vandha veel-mannar veeya visayan theer-kadavi,
kudaiyaa varai-onru eduththu-aayar koovaay ninraan kooraazhip
padaiyaan veedham naangu-aindhu veeLvi angam aaru-isai-eezh
nadaiyaa valla andhaNar-vaazh naRaiyoor ninra nambiyee.
569.meaning: He drove Arjuna's chariot so the spear-bearing kings who came thronging perished, and He lifted a mountain as an umbrella and stood as the chief of the cowherds; He bears the sharp discus. He is the Nampi who stands in Naraiyur, where dwell brahmins skilled in the four Vedas, the five (great sacrifices), the sacrifices, the six limbs (of the Vedas), the seven musical notes -- masters of all observances.

570.translit:
pandhaar viralaaL paanjaali koondhal mudikkap paaradhaththu
kandhaar kaLitruk kazhal-mannar kalangac changam vaay-vaiththaan
chen-thaamarai-meel ayanoodu sivanum anaiya perumaiyoor
nandhaa vaN-kai maRaiyoor vaazh naRaiyoor ninra nambiyee.
570.meaning: That Panchali of ball-playing fingers might bind up her hair, He set the conch to His lips in the Bharata (war) so that the anklet-wearing kings with their fragrant rutting elephants were thrown into confusion. He is the Nampi who stands in Naraiyur, where dwell brahmins of unfailing generous hands, great as Brahma upon the red lotus and as Shiva.

571.translit:
aaRum piRaiyum aravamum adambum sadai-meel aNindhu,
udalam neeRum poosi eer-oorum iraiyoon senru kuRai-irappa
maaru onru illaa vaasa-neer varai-maar agalaththu aLiththu-ugandhaan naaRum
pozhil-soozhndhu azhagaaya naRaiyoor ninra nambiyee.
571.meaning: When the Lord (Shiva) -- who wears the river, the crescent, the serpent and the adampu flower upon his matted locks, who smears his body with ash and rides his bull -- came and begged in his need, He gladly granted from His mountain-like broad chest the peerless fragrant water. He is the Nampi who stands in beautiful Naraiyur, surrounded by fragrant groves.

572.translit:
nanmai-udaiya maRaiyoor-vaazh naRaiyoor ninra nambiyai
kanni madhil-soozh vayal-mangaik kaliyan oli-sey thamizh-maalai
panni ulagil paaduvaar paadu saara pazh-vinaigaL
manni ulagam aaNdu-pooy vaanoor vaNanga vaazhvaaree.
572.meaning: Upon the Nampi who stands in Naraiyur, where good brahmins dwell, Kaliyan of Vayalmangai girded by maiden-like ramparts composed this melodious Tamil garland. Those who recite and sing it in the world -- their old karmas drawing near to ruin -- shall rule the earth and depart to live where the celestials bow to them.

573.translit:
maan-koNda tool-maarvil maaNiyaay, maavali-maN
thaan-koNdu thaaLaal aLandha perumaanai
theen-koNda saaral thiruveeng-kadaththaanai
naan senru naadi naRaiyooril kaNdeenee.
573.meaning: The great Lord who, as the brahmin youth with a deerskin upon His chest, took Mahabali's land and measured it with His feet -- the Lord of Tiruvenkatam with its honey-laden slopes -- Him I went and sought, and beheld in Naraiyur.

574.translit:
munneerai munnaaL kadaindhaanai moozhththa-naaL
an-neerai meenaay amaiththa perumaanai
then-naali meeya thirumaalai emmaanai
nan-neer vayal-soozh naRaiyooril kaNdeenee.
574.meaning: He who churned the ocean of old, the great Lord who in the time of the deluge took the form of a fish in those waters, Tirumal who abides in lovely Tennali, my Lord -- Him I beheld in Naraiyur, ringed by fields of good water.

575.translit:
thoo-vaaya puLL-oorndhu, vandhu thuRai-veezham
moovaamai nalgi mudhalai thuNiththaanai
theevaadhi deevanaic chengamalak kaNNaanai
naavaayuLaanai naRaiyooril kaNdeenee.
575.meaning: He who, riding the pure-mouthed bird (Garuda), came and saved the elephant at the ghat from decay and severed the crocodile, the God of gods, the one with red lotus eyes, He who abides in Naavaay -- Him I beheld in Naraiyur.

576.translit:
oodaa ariyaay iraNiyanai oonidandha
seedaar pozhil-soozh thiruneer malaiyaanai
vaadaa malar-thuzhaay maalai mudiyaanai
naadooRum naadi naRaiyooril kaNdeenee.
576.meaning: He who, as the unretreating man-lion, tore open the flesh of Hiranya, the Lord of Tiruneermalai surrounded by lush groves, He who wears upon His crown a garland of never-fading tulasi blossoms -- Him I sought day after day and beheld in Naraiyur.

577.translit:
kallaar madhil-soozh kadi-ilangaik kaar-arakkan
vallaagam keeLa vari-veny charam-thurandha
villaanai, selva-vibeedaNaRku veeRaaga
nallanai naadi naRaiyooril kaNdeenee.
577.meaning: He of the bow, who sped fierce striped arrows to split open the mighty body of the dark demon of fortified Lanka girt by stone ramparts, and who was especially gracious to the noble Vibhishana -- Him I sought and beheld in Naraiyur.

578.translit:
umbar-ulagoodu uyir-ellaam undhiyil
vambu malar-meel padaiththaanai maayoonai
am-banna kaNNaaL asoodhai-than singaththai
nambanai naadi naRaiyooril kaNdeenee.
578.meaning: He who created all the worlds above and every living being upon the fresh lotus flower of His navel, the wondrous one, the lion-cub of Yashoda of arrow-like eyes, the trustworthy Lord -- Him I sought and beheld in Naraiyur.

579.translit:
kattu-eeRu neeL-soolaik kaaNdavaththaith thee-moottri
vittaanai meyyam amarndha perumaanai
mattu-eeRu kaRpagaththai maadharkkaay vaN-thuvarai
nattaanai naadi naRaiyooril kaNdeenee.
579.meaning: He who set fire to the dense long forest of Khandava, the great Lord who abides at Meyyam, the honey-laden wish-granting Kalpaka tree, He who founded bountiful Dvaraka for the sake of women -- Him I sought and beheld in Naraiyur.

580.translit:
maNNinmee paarang keduppaan maRamannar
paNNinmeel vandha padaiyellaam bhaaratattu
viNNinmee theeRa visayan thee roornthaanai
naNNi naan naadi naRaiyooril kaNdEnE.
580.meaning: To remove the burden of the earth, He who drove Arjuna's chariot in the great Bharata war, so that all the armies of warrior-kings that came mounted in array ascended to the high heavens. Seeking and searching for Him, I beheld Him at Tirunaraiyur.

581.translit:
pongERu neeLsOdhip ponnaazhi thannOdum
sangERu kOlath thadakkaip perumaanai
kongERu sOlaik kudandhaik kidandhaanai
nangkOnai naadi naRaiyooril kaNdEnE.
581.meaning: The Lord with the golden discus of surging, vast radiance, and with the conch in His handsome, broad hand, who reclines at Kudandai amid groves laden with pollen. Seeking our King, I beheld Him at Tirunaraiyur.

582.translit:
mannu madhurai vasudhEvar vaazhmudhalai
nannaRaiyoor ninRa nambiyai vambavizhthaar
kannavilum thOLaan kaliyan olivallaar
ponnulagil vaanavarkkup puttheeLi raaguvarE.
582.meaning: He who is the source of life of Vasudeva of enduring Mathura, the perfect Lord standing at good Naraiyur. Those able in the resounding songs of Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) of strong shoulders garlanded with fragrant, blossom-opening wreaths shall become gods among the celestials in the golden world.

583.translit:
pedaiyadarttha madavannam piriyaadhu, malarkkamalam
madaleduttu madhunugarum vayaluduttha thirunaRaiyoor
mudaiyadarttha siramEndhi moovulagum balithirivOn
idarkeduttha thiruvaaLan iNaiyadiyE adai nenjE.
583.meaning: Tirunaraiyur, girt with fields where graceful swans cling close to their mates and lift the petals of the blooming lotus to drink its honey. He who bore the reeking skull and wandered the three worlds for alms, the gracious Lord who removes all distress — O heart, take refuge at His twin feet.

584.translit:
kazhiyaarum kanasangam kalandhengum niRaindhERi
vazhiyaara mutteenRu vaLangkodukkum thirunaRaiyoor
pazhiyaarum viRalarakkan parumudiga LavaisidhaRa
azhalaaRum sarandhurandhaan adiyiNaiyE adai nenjE.
584.meaning: Tirunaraiyur, where dense conches abounding in the backwaters mingle and fill everywhere, bringing forth pearls along the paths and giving abundance. He who let fly the fire-spewing arrows so that the great crowns of the mighty, blameworthy demon (Ravana) were shattered — O heart, take refuge at His twin feet.

585.translit:
suLaikoNda palangkanigaL thEnpaaya kadhaligaLin
thiLaikoNda pazhamkezhumu thigazhsOlaith thirunaRaiyoor
vaLaikoNda vaNNattan pinthOnRal moovulagOdu
aLaiveNNe yuNdaanthan adiyiNaiyE adai nenjE.
585.meaning: Tirunaraiyur of shining groves abounding in ripe segmented jackfruits dripping honey and in clustered plantain fruits. The younger brother of Him of conch-bright hue (Balarama), who along with the three worlds ate the churned butter — O heart, take refuge at His twin feet.

586.translit:
thunROLith thugilpadalam thunniyengum maaLigaimEl
ninRaara vaanmoodum neeLselvath thirunaRaiyoor
manRaarak kudamaadi varaiyeduttu mazhaithaduttha
kunRaarum thiradOLan kuraikazhalE adai nenjE.
586.meaning: Tirunaraiyur of vast wealth, where masses of bright banners cling everywhere upon the mansions, standing and covering the very sky. He who danced the pot-dance fully in the public square, who lifted the mountain and held back the rain — O heart, take refuge at the resounding anklets of Him of rounded shoulders like a hill.

587.translit:
agiRkuRadunjandhanamum amponnum maNimuttum
migakkoNarndhu thiraiyundhum viyanponnith thirunaRaiyoor
pagaRkarandha sudaraazhip padaiyaan ivvulagEzhum
pugakkarandha thiruvayiRRan ponnadiyE adai nenjE.
587.meaning: Tirunaraiyur on the wondrous Ponni (Kaveri), whose waves push along blocks of eaglewood, sandalwood, fine gold, and gem-pearls in plenty. He who bears the radiant discus that hid the day (over the sun), in whose sacred belly all these seven worlds were hidden — O heart, take refuge at His golden feet.

588.translit:
ponmuttum ariyugirum puzhaikkaimmaa karikkOdum
minnaththaN thiraiyundhum viyanponnith thirunaRaiyoor
minnottha nuNmarungkul melliyalai thirumaarvil
mannaththaan vaitthugandhaan malaradiyE adai nenjE.
588.meaning: Tirunaraiyur on the wondrous Ponni, whose cool waves push along golden pearls, lion's claws, and the tusks of trunk-handed elephants, glittering bright. He who joyfully and enduringly placed upon His sacred chest the tender lady (Lakshmi) of slender, lightning-like waist — O heart, take refuge at His blossom feet.

589.translit:
seerthazhaittha kadhirsennel sengamalath thidaiyidaiyin
paarthazhaitthuk karumpOngkip payanviLaikkum thirunaRaiyoor
kaarthazhaittha thiruvuruvan kaNNapiraan viNNavarkOn
thaarthazhaittha thuzhaaymudiyan thaLiradiyE adai nenjE.
589.meaning: Tirunaraiyur where rich ears of red paddy flourish, and amid the red lotuses the sugarcane rises and the land thrives, yielding harvest. Lord Krishna of sacred form lush like the rain-cloud, King of the celestials, who wears the tulsi crown thick with flowering garlands — O heart, take refuge at His tender feet.

590.translit:
kulaiyaarndha pazhukkaayum pasungkaayum paaLaimuttum
thalaiyaarndha viLangkamugin thadanjOlaith thirunaRaiyoor
malaiyaarndha kOlanjEr maNimaadam migamanni
nilaiyaara ninRaanRan neeLkazhalE adai nenjE.
590.meaning: Tirunaraiyur of vast groves of bright areca palms crowned high with clustered ripe nuts, green nuts, and pearl-like spathes. He who stands firmly and enduringly in the jewelled mansions adorned in mountain-like splendour — O heart, take refuge at His long anklet-feet.

591.translit:
maRaiyaarum peruvELvik kozhumpugaipOy vaLarndhu, engkum
niRaiyaara vaanmoodum neeLselvath thirunaRaiyoor
piRaiyaarum sadaiyaanum biramanumun thozhudhEttha
iRaiyaagi ninRaanRan iNaiyadiyE adai nenjE.
591.meaning: Tirunaraiyur of vast wealth, where the thick smoke of the great Vedic sacrifices rises and spreads, filling everywhere and covering the very sky. He who stands as the supreme Lord, worshipped and praised in His presence by Shiva of the crescent-bearing matted locks and by Brahma — O heart, take refuge at His twin feet.

592.translit:
thiNkanaka madhiLpudaisoozh thirunaRaiyoor ninRaanai
vaNkalaga nilaveRikkum vayalmangkai nagaraaLan
paNkalagam payinRaseerp paadalivai patthumvallaar
viNkalagath thimaiyavaraay veeRRirundhu vaazhvaarE.
592.meaning: He who stands at Tirunaraiyur encircled on its sides by strong golden ramparts — about Him the lord of Vayal Mangai town, where the radiant moonlight glistens, sang these ten songs of excellence practised in melody. Those able in them shall dwell in glory as deathless ones in the firmament of heaven.

593.translit:
kidandha nambi kudandhai mEvik kEzha laayulagai
idandha nambi, engaL nambi eRinjar araNazhiya
kadandha nambi kadiyaa rilangkai ulagai yeeradiyaal
nadandha nambi naamam sollil namOnaaraayaNamE!
593.meaning: The perfect Lord reclining at Kudandai; the perfect Lord who, becoming the boar, lifted up the earth; our perfect Lord who, so that the fortresses of foes were destroyed, conquered fenced Lanka; the perfect Lord who measured the world in two strides — if you speak His name, it is 'Namo Narayanaya'!

594.translit:
vidandhaa nudaiya aravam veruvac cheruvil munanaaL, mun
thadandhaa maraineerp poygai pukku mikka thaadaaLan
idandhaan vaiyam kEzha laagi ulagai yeeradiyaal
nadandhaa nudaiya naamam sollil namOnaaraayaNamE!
594.meaning: In olden days He, the mighty one, entered the pool of broad lotuses and water in battle so that the venom-bearing serpent (the elephant's foe) trembled. He who lifted the earth becoming the boar, who walked across the world in two strides — if you speak His name, it is 'Namo Narayanaya'!

595.translit:
pooNaa thanalum thaRukaN vEzham maRuga vaLaimaruppaip
pENaan vaangi yamudham koNda perumaan thirumaarvan
paaNaa vaNdu muralum koondhal aaycchi thayirveNNey
naaNaa thuNdaan naamam sollil namOnaaraayaNamE!
595.meaning: The Lord who, so that the fierce, raging elephant (Kuvalayapida) writhed, snatched off without a care its curved tusk and took it as His relish, He on whose chest is Lakshmi. He who, without shame, ate the curds and butter of the cowherdess whose tresses hummed with droning bees — if you speak His name, it is 'Namo Narayanaya'!

596.translit:
kallaar madhiLsoozh kacchi nagaruL nacchip paadagattuL,
ellaa vulagum vaNangka virundha ammaan, ilangkaikkOn
vallaa Laagam villaal munindha endhai, vibheedaNaRku
nallaa nudaiya naamam sollil namOnaaraayaNamE!
596.meaning: The Lord who, with desire, abides at Paadagam within the city of Kanchi encircled by stone ramparts, that all the worlds may worship Him. My Father who in anger pierced with His bow the body of the mighty king of Lanka, He who is good to Vibhishana — if you speak His name, it is 'Namo Narayanaya'!

597.translit:
kudaiyaa varaiyaal niraimun kaattha perumaan maruvaadha
vidaithaa nEzhum venRaan kOval ninRaan thennilangkai
adaiyaa arakkar veeyap porudhu mEvi vengkooRRam
nadaiyaa vuNNak kaNdaan naamam namOnaaraayaNamE!
597.meaning: The Lord who guarded the cattle-herds long ago by making the mountain a parasol, who vanquished the seven untamed bulls, who stands at Goval (Tirukkovalur). He who fought so that the unsubmissive demons of southern Lanka perished, and watched as fierce Death devoured them as its prey — His name is 'Namo Narayanaya'!

598.translit:
kaana eNkum kurangkum musuvum padaiyaa adalarakkar
maana mazhittu ninRa venRi ammaan enakkenRum
thEnum paalum amudhu maaya thirumaal thirunaamam
naanum sonnEn namaru muraimin namOnaaraayaNamE!
598.meaning: The victorious Lord who stood having destroyed the pride of the warring demons, taking forest bears, monkeys, and apes as His army. The sacred name of Tirumal, who is ever like honey, milk, and nectar to me — I too have spoken it; O my kinsmen, do you also recite: 'Namo Narayanaya'!

599.translit:
ninRa varaiyum kidandha kadalum thisaiyu mirunilanum
onRu mozhiyaa vaNNa meNNi ninRa ammaanaar
kunRu kudaiyaa veduttha adiga Ludaiya thirunaamam
nanRu kaaNmin thoNdeer sonnEn namOnaaraayaNamE!
599.meaning: The great Lord who stands, having conceived all — the standing mountains, the spread-out seas, the directions, and the wide earth — so that not one is left out. The sacred name of the Lord who lifted the hill as a parasol — see that it is good, O devotees; I have spoken it: 'Namo Narayanaya'!

600.translit:
kaDungkaal maari kallE poziya allE yemakkenRu
paDungkaal neeyE saraNenRaayar anjcha anjchaamun
neDungkaal kunRam kuDaiyonREnthi niraiyaich chiramaththaal
naDungkaa vaNNam kaaththaan naamam namOnaa raayaNamE.
600.meaning: When the fierce wind and rain poured down stones upon the cowherds, before they could even take fright they cried, 'You alone are our refuge!' Hearing this, He lifted up a single tall hill as an umbrella and, sparing them all hardship, protected the herds from trembling. The name of that One is Namo Narayana.

601.translit:
pongku puNarik kaDalsoo zhaaDai nilamaa magaLmalarmaa
mangai praman sivanin thiranvaa navarnaa yagaraay
engaL aDiga limaiyOr talaiva ruDaiya tirunaamam
nangaL vinaigaL tavira vuraimin namOnaa raayaNamE.
601.meaning: He who is Lord of the Earth-goddess robed by the surging sea, of the great Lakshmi born on the flower, of Brahma, Siva, Indra and all the celestials, our Master, the chief of the deathless ones. Utter His holy name so that our deeds (karma) may be removed: Namo Narayana.

602.translit:
vaavith taDanjchoozh maNimuth thaaRRu naRaiyoor neDumaalai
naavil paravi nenjchil koNDu nambi naamaththai
kaavith taDangkaN maDavaar kELvan kaliya nolimaalai
mEvich cholla vallaar paavam nillaa veeyumE.
602.meaning: Tirumal of Naraiyur, encircled by tanks and the pearl-bearing Manimuttaru river — praise Him with the tongue and hold Him in the heart, the perfect Lord (Nambi). This garland of resounding verses by Kaliyan, beloved of the lily-eyed maidens — for those able to recite it with devotion, their sins will not remain but shall perish.

603.translit:
kaRavaa maDanaaguthan kanRuLLi naaRpOl,
maRavaa thaDiyE nunnaiyE yazhaikkinREn,
naRavaar pozhilsoozh naRaiyoor ninRa nambi,
piRavaamai yenaippaNi yenthai piraanE!
603.meaning: Like a young, gentle, un-milked cow yearning in love for its calf, I your servant call upon You alone, never forgetting. O perfect Lord standing in Naraiyur ringed by honey-laden groves, grant me the boon of no more birth, O my Father, my God!

604.translit:
vaRRaa muthuneerODu maalvarai yEzhum,
thuRRaa munthuRRiya tholpuga zhOnE,
aRRE naDiyE nunnaiyE yazhaikkinREn,
peRRE naruLthanthiDu en enthai piraanE.
604.meaning: O ancient one of glory who once swallowed and held within the never-drying deep waters and the seven great mountains. I your servant, cut off from all else, call upon You alone. I have found refuge — grant me Your grace, O my Father, my God.

605.translit:
thaarEn piRarkkun naruLen niDaivaiththaay,
aarE nathuvE parugik kaLikkinREn,
kaarEy kaDalE malaiyE tirukkOTTi
yoorE, ugaNthaa yaiyugaN thaDiyEnE.
605.meaning: I will not give Your grace to others — You have placed it within me; satisfied, I drink of it alone and rejoice. O cloud, O sea, O mountain, O Lord delighting in the town of Tirukkottiyur — delighting in You, I am Your servant.

606.translit:
puLvaay piLantha punithaa en Razhaikka,
uLLEninR ennuLLang kuLiru moruvaa,
kaLvaa kaDanmallaik kiDantha karumbE,
vaLLaal unnai yenGnganamnaan maRakkEnE.
606.meaning: When I call, 'O pure one who split open the beak of the bird (the demon)!', You, the matchless One, stand within and cool my heart. O cunning one, O sugarcane reclining at Kadalmallai (Mamallapuram), O bountiful Lord — how can I ever forget You?

607.translit:
villEr nuthalvEl neDungkaN Niyum neeyum,
kallaar kaDungkaanam thirintha kaLiRE,
nallaay naranaa raNanE engaLnambi,
chollaa yunnaiyaan vaNangkith thozhumaaRE.
607.meaning: O elephant-like Lord who wandered with her — she of bow-like brow and long spear-like eyes — through the harsh stony wilderness. O good one, O Nara-Narayana, our perfect Lord (Nambi), tell me the way that I may bow down and worship You.

608.translit:
paNiyEy parangkunRin pavaLath thiraLE,
muniyE tirumoozhik kaLaththu viLakkE,
iniyaay thoNDarOm parugin namuthaaya
kaniyE unnaikkaNDu koNDuyn thozhinthEnE!
608.meaning: O coral-cluster of Parangunram crowned with serpents, O sage, O lamp of Tirumoozhikkalam. O sweet one, O fruit become the ambrosia for us Your servants to drink — having beheld You, I am redeemed and saved!

609.translit:
kathiyE lillaiNin naruLal lathenakku,
nithiyE tiruneer malaiNith thiloththE,
pathiyE paravith thozhumthoN Dartamakkuk
kathiyE unaikkaNDu koNDuyn thozhinthEnE.
609.meaning: There is no refuge for me other than Your grace. O treasure, O cluster of pearls of Tiruneermalai, O dwelling-place, O refuge of the servants who praise and worship You — having beheld You, I am redeemed and saved.

610.translit:
aththaa ariyE enRun naiyazhaikka,
piththaa venRu pEsuginRaar piRaren nai,
muththE maNimaa NikkamE muLaikkinRa
viththE unnaiang nganamnaan viDukEnE.
610.meaning: When I call out, 'O Father! O Hari!', others speak of me, saying, 'He is mad!' O pearl, O sapphire and great ruby, O sprouting seed (of all) — how can I ever let go of You?

611.translit:
thooyaay. suDarmaa mathipO luyirkkellaam,
thaayaay aLikkinRa thaNDaa maraikkaNNaa,
aayaa alaiNee rulagEzhum munnuNDa
vaayaa unaiyeng nganamnaan maRakkEnE!
611.meaning: O pure one, like the bright great moon, being a mother to all living beings — O lotus-eyed one with cool lotus eyes who grant Your grace. O cowherd, O mouth that once swallowed the seven worlds in the tossing flood-waters — how can I ever forget You?

612.translit:
vaNDaar pozhilsoozh naRaiyoornam pikku,
enRum thoNDaayk kaliya nolisey thamizhmaalai,
thoNDeer ivaipaaDu minpaaDi ninRaaDa,
uNDE visumbu unthamakkil laithuyarE.
612.meaning: This garland of resounding Tamil verses, made by Kaliyan who is ever a servant to the perfect Lord (Nambi) of Naraiyur ringed by bee-filled groves — O devotees, sing these! For those who sing and stand dancing, heaven is yours, and for you there shall be no sorrow.

613.translit:
puLLaay Enamumaayp pugunthu, ennai yuLLangkoNDa
kaLvaa enRalum en kaNgaLneer sOrtarumaal,
uLLE ninRurugi nenjchamunnai yuLLiyakkaal,
naLLE nunnaiyallaal naRaiyoorninRa nambeeyO.
613.meaning: When I but say, 'O thief who came as the bird and as the boar and stole my heart!', tears stream from my eyes. When my heart, melting within, thinks of You, I cling to none but You, O perfect Lord standing in Naraiyur!

614.translit:
ODaavaaLariyi n uruvaay maruvi enRan
maaDE vanthaDiyEn manangkoLLa vallamainthaa,
paaDEn thoNDartammaik kavithaip panuval koNDu,
naaDE nunnaiyallaal naRaiyoorninRa nambeeyO.
614.meaning: O mighty youth who, taking the form of the unyielding fierce man-lion, came near me and won the heart of me Your servant. I will not sing of (mere) devotees with poems and verse; I seek none but You, O perfect Lord standing in Naraiyur!

615.translit:
emmaanu memmanaiyum enaippeR Rozhinthatharpin,
ammaanu mammanaiyum aDiyEnuk kaagininRa,
nanmaana voNsuDarE naRaiyoorninRa nambee un
maimmaana vaNNamallaal magizhntheththa maaTTEnE.
615.meaning: After my father and mother who bore me passed away, You became father and mother for me Your servant. O fine, noble, bright radiance, O perfect Lord standing in Naraiyur — save for Your dark beautiful (cloud-like) form, I cannot joyfully praise any other.

616.translit:
siRiyaayOr piLLaiyumaa yulaguNDOr aalilaimEl
uRaivaay, ennenjchinuL uRaivaay uRainthathuthaan
aRiyaa thirunthaRiyE naDiyEn aNi vaNDukiNDum
naRaivaa rumpozhilsoozh naRaiyoorninRa nambeeyO.
616.meaning: O small one, as an infant You swallowed the worlds and rested upon a banyan leaf; You dwell within my heart. Yet I Your servant remained unknowing and did not know that You had taken up dwelling there — O perfect Lord standing in Naraiyur, ringed by honey-flowing groves where lovely bees burrow!

617.translit:
neeNDaayai vaanavargaL ninainthEththik kaaNparithaal,
aaNDaayen RU aatharikkap paDuvaaykku naanaDimai,
pooNDEn en nenjchinuLLE pugunthaayaip pOgaloTTEn,
naaNthaa nunakkozhinthEn naRaiyoorninRa nambeeyO.
617.meaning: You who grew tall (as Trivikrama) — even the celestials cannot behold You though they meditate and praise. To You, cherished as 'our ruler,' I have taken up servitude. You who entered within my heart, I will not let go; I have cast off all shame for Your sake, O perfect Lord standing in Naraiyur!

618.translit:
enthaathai thaathaiyappaal ezhuvar pazhavaDimai
vanthaar en nenjchinuLLE vanthaayaip pOgaloTTEn,
anthO en naaruyirE arasE aruLenakku nanthaamal
thanthaventhaay naRaiyoorninRa nambeeyO.
618.meaning: For seven generations beyond my father and his father, my ancestors came into ancient servitude to You. You who came within my heart, I will not let go. Ah, O my precious life, O my king, O my Father who gave grace to me unfailingly — O perfect Lord standing in Naraiyur!

619.translit:
mannanjcha aayiranthOL mazhuviltu Niththamainthaa,
ennenjchath thuLLirunthing inippOyp piRaroruvar,
vannenjcham pukkirukka voTTEn vaLaiththuvaiththEn,
nannenjcha annammannum naRaiyoorninRa nambeeyO.
619.meaning: O mighty youth who, while kings trembled, severed the thousand arms (of Kartavirya) with Your axe. Dwelling within my heart, I will not let You go off now to enter and stay in any other one's hard heart — I have encircled and held You fast, O perfect Lord standing in Naraiyur, where the swan abides upon the good heart (of the lotus)!

620.translit:
eppootum ponmalarit timaiyoor thozhuthu thangaL,
kaippootu konDiRainjik kazhalmeel vaNanga ninRaay,
ippootu en nenjinuLLee pukunthaayaip pookaloTTeen
naRpootu vaNDukiNDum naRaiyoor ninRa nampiiyoo!
620.meaning: O Lord standing at Naraiyur, where bees burrow into fresh blossoms! Always the immortals worship You, casting golden flowers and offering the blooms in their hands, bowing low at Your feet. Now You have entered into my heart, and I will not let You go!

621.translit:
oonee raakkai thannai uzhanthoompi vaiththamaiyaal,
yaanaay enRanakkaay aDiyeen manampukuntha
teenee tiingkarumpin teLivee en sinthai thannaal,
naanee eythappeRReen naRaiyoor ninRa nampiiyoo.
621.meaning: O Lord standing at Naraiyur! Because I had toiled to nourish and keep this fleshly body, regarding it as 'I' and as mine, You—the honey, the clear sweetness of sweet sugarcane—entered the heart of this servant. By my own thought I have at last attained You.

622.translit:
nanniir vayalpuDaisuuzh naRaiyoor ninRa nampiyai
kanniira maalvaraiththooL kalikanRi mangaiyarkoon,
sonniira solmaalai solvaarkaL, suuzhvisumpil
nanniirmaiyaal makizhnthu neDungkaalam vaazhvaaree.
622.meaning: Those who recite this garland of well-formed words—composed by Kalikanri, lord of the Mangai people, whose dark mountain-like shoulders are firm—about the Lord standing at Naraiyur, which is surrounded by fields of good water, shall live for a long age, rejoicing in good nature in the encircling heavens.

623.translit:
sinavil sengkaN arakkar uyirmaaLa
  seTRa villi enRu kaTRavar thanththam manavuT koNDu,
enRum eppootum ninReettum maamuniyai
  marameezh eytha mainthanai,
nanavil senRaarkkum naNNaR kariyaanai
  naan aDiyeen naRaiyoor ninRa nampiyai,
kanavil kaNDeen inRu kaNDamaiyaal en
  kaNNiNaikaL kaLippak kaLiththeenee.
623.meaning: The learned ones, holding in their minds that He is the archer who destroyed the lives of the red-eyed, wrathful demons, stand and praise Him always at every moment as the great sage. He is the youth who pierced the seven trees, hard to reach even for those who go to Him in waking life. I, His servant, beheld in a dream the Lord standing at Naraiyur; and because I have seen Him this day, my two eyes overflowed with joy and I rejoiced.

624.translit:
taay ninainthu akanRee okka
  ennaiyum thanniyee ninaikkach cheythu,
taan enakkaay ninainthu aruL seyyum appanai
  anRu iv vaiyakam uNDumizhnthiTTa vaayanai,
makarakkuzhaik kaathanai
  mainthanai mathiL koovaliDaikazhi
aayanai, amararkku ariyeeTRai
  en anpanai anRi yaathariyeenee.
624.meaning: Like a child longing for its mother as it grows apart, He made me too think of Him alone, and as my own Father He thought of me and bestowed grace. He is the One whose mouth once swallowed this world and spat it out, who wears the makara-shaped earrings, the youthful one, the cowherd of Kovalur of the ramparts, the rare bull-lord of the gods. Apart from Him, my beloved, I shall cherish nothing.

625.translit:
vantha naaL vanthu en nenjiDangkoNDaan
  maTRoor nenjaRiyaan,
aDiyeenuDaich chinthaiyaay vanthu
  tenpulark kennaich cheerkoDaan ithu sikkenap peTReen,
konthulaam pozhil suuzh kuDanthaiththalaik koovinaik
  kuDamaaDiya kuuththanai,
enthaiyai enthai thanthai thammaanai
  empiraanai eththaal maRakkeenee?
625.meaning: On the day He came, He came and took His place in my heart, and He knows no other heart. Becoming this servant's very thought He came and would not let me go to the southern realm (of death); this I have surely obtained. He is the chief of Kudanthai, encircled by groves where blossom-clusters sway, the dancer who danced the pot-dance, my Father, the Lord of my father and forefathers—by what means could I ever forget my Lord?

626.translit:
urangkaLaal iyanRa mannar maaLap
  paarathaththoru theer aivarkkaaych chenRu,
irangki uurnthavarkku innaruL seyyum empiraanai
  vampaar punal kaaviri,
arangkam aaLi en aaLi viN aaLi
  aazhi suuzh ilangkai malangkach chenRu,
sarangkaL aaNDa thaNDaamaraik kaNNanukku anRi
  en manam thaazhnthu nillaathee!
626.meaning: He is my Lord who, when the kings strong in might were destroyed in the Bharata war, went and drove a single chariot for the five (Pandavas) and, taking pity, drove it and bestowed sweet grace upon them. He rules Arangam (Srirangam) of the fragrant fresh waters of the Kaveri, rules me, rules the heavens; He went and threw into turmoil sea-girt Lanka, the cool-lotus-eyed One who wielded arrows. Apart from Him, my heart shall not bow down and abide elsewhere!

627.translit:
aangku vennarakaththu azhunthumpootu
  anjeel enRu aDiyeenai angkee vanthu thaangku,
taamarai annaponnaar aDi empiraanai
  umparkku aNiyaay ninRa,
veengkaDaththu ariyaip parikiiRiyai
  veNNey uNDu uraliniDai yaappuNDa tiingkarumpinai,
teenai nan paalinai anRi
  en manam sinthai seyyaathee.
627.meaning: When I am sinking there in fierce hell, He will come and uphold me, this servant, saying 'Fear not!' He is my Lord with golden, lotus-like feet, who stands as an ornament to the celestials, the lion who stands on Venkatam, who tore apart the horse-demon, the sweet sugarcane that ate the butter and was bound to the mortar. Apart from Him—the honey, the good milk—my heart shall not meditate on anything.

628.translit:
eTTanaippozhuthaakilum enRum en manaththu akalaathirukkum pukazh,
taTTalarttha ponnai alar koonginthaazh pozhil tirumaalirunj choolai angkaTTiyai,
karum piinRa in saaTRaik
  kaathalaal maRai naankum un nootiya paTTanai,
paravaith thuyileeTRai en
  paNpanai anRip paaDal seyyeenee!
628.meaning: His glory never departs from my mind even for an instant. He is the gold whose petals spread wide, the sweet lump (of jaggery) of beautiful Tirumalirunjolai with its low groves of blossoming kongu trees, the sweet juice yielded by the sugarcane, the priest who of old chanted the four Vedas. Other than Him—the bull-lord who reclines on the ocean, my gracious one—I shall make no song!

629.translit:
paNNin in mozhiyaam narampil peTRa
  paalaiyaaki ingkee pukunthu, en
kaNNum nenjum vaayum iDangkoNDaan
  koNDa pin maRaiyoor manam thannuL,
viNNuLaar perumaanai emmaanai
  viingku niir makaram tiLaikkum kaDal vaNNan
maamaNi vaNNan em aNNal
  vaNNamee anRi vaayurai yaathee!
629.meaning: Becoming the Pālai melody born of the strings, whose tune is sweeter than music, He entered here and took His place in my eyes, my heart, and my mouth. After He took hold, He is the Lord of those in the heavens, within the minds of the Veda-knowers, my Master, of the hue of the ocean where makara-fish frolic in swelling waters, the great-gem-hued One, my chief. Of nothing but His beauty shall my mouth speak!

630.translit:
ini eppaavam vanthu eythum solliir
  emakku immaiyee aruL peTRamaiyaal, aDum
thuniyaith tiirthu inpamee tarukinRathoor
  thooTRath thonneRiyai, vaiyam thozhappaDum
muniyai vaanavaraal vaNangkappaDum
  muttinaip pattar taam nukarkinRathoor
kaniyai, kaathal seythu ennuLLang koNDa
  kaLvanai inRu kaNDukoNDeenee.
630.meaning: Tell me—what sin can now come and reach us? For in this very life we have obtained His grace. He is the ancient Way of that manifestation which, removing the searing sorrow, grants only bliss; the sage whom the world worships; the pearl reverenced by the celestials; the ripe fruit that the devotees savor; the thief who, making love to me, has taken my heart. This day I have beheld and won Him.

631.translit:
en seykeen aDiyeen uraiyiir itaR
  kenRum en manaththee irukkum pukazh,
thanjai aaLiyaip ponpeyaroon than
  nenjam anRu iDantha vanaith thazhalee purai
minjey vaaL arakkan nakar paazhpaDach
  chuuzh kaDal siRaivaiththu imaiyoor thozhum,
ponsey maalvaraiyai maNik kunRinai
  anRi en manam pooTRi ennaathee.
631.meaning: What shall I do? Tell me. His glory abides in my mind forever. He is the ruler of Tanjai, the One who that day tore open the breast of the golden-named one (Hiranyakasipu); who laid waste the city of the sword-wielding demon fierce as flame, binding up the encircling sea; whom the immortals worship; the golden great mountain, the gem-hill. Apart from Him my heart shall utter no praise to any other.

632.translit:
tooDu viNDalar poompozhil mangaiyar
  toonRal vaaL kaliyan tiruvaali
naaDan, nannaRaiyoor ninRa nampithan
  nalla maamalar seevaDi, senniyil
suuDiyum thozhuthum ezhunthaaDiyum
  thoNDarkaTku avan sonna solmaalai,
paaDal pathth ivai paaDumin thoNDiir
  paaDa num iDaip paavam nillaavee.
632.meaning: This garland of words spoken for the devotees by Kaliyan, the wielder of the sword, the chief of the Mangai people, lord of the Tiruvaali land of blossoming flower-groves where buds burst open—about the good, great-flowered red feet of the Lord standing at fair Naraiyur, which devotees wear upon their heads, worship, and rise up and dance for. O devotees, sing these ten songs as a poem; if you sing them, no sin shall remain within you.

633.translit:
kaNsoora vengkuruthi vanthizhiya venthazhalpool kuunthalaaLai,
maN seera mulaiyuNDa maamathalaay vaanavartham koovee enRu,
viN seerum iLanthingkaL akaDurinju maNimaaDa malku, selvath thaN seeRai
yemperumaan taaL thozhuvaar kaaNmin en talaimeelaaree.
633.meaning: Crying 'O great child who, until she sank into the earth, sucked the breast of the demoness whose hair blazed like fierce fire, her eyes draining and hot blood flowing down—O King of the celestials!'—behold, those who worship the feet of our Lord of wealthy, cool Sherai, abounding in gem-set mansions whose roofs brush the young crescent moon that reaches the sky: they are upon my head.

634.translit:
amburuva varineDungkaN, alarmakaLai varaiyakalath tamarnthu, mallal
komburuva viLanginameel iLangkanRu koNDeRintha kuuththar poolaam,
vampalarum thaN soolai vaN seeRai vaanunthu kooyil meeya,
emperumaan taaL thozhuvaar eppozhuthum en manaththee irukkinRaaree.
634.meaning: He set the flower-maiden (Lakshmi), with her long arrow-like striped eyes, firmly upon His broad chest. He is like the dancer who flung a young calf to bring down the wood-apples (vilam) from a flourishing branching tree. Those who worship the feet of our Lord, who dwells in the temple soaring to the sky at bountiful Sherai with its cool groves where fresh flowers bloom, abide in my mind at every moment.

635.translit:
miithooDi vaaLeyiRu minnilakam mun vilakum uruvinaaLai
kaathoDu koDimuukku anRu uDanaRuttha kaiththalaththaa enRu ninRu,
taathoDu vaNDalampum thaN seeRai emperumaan taaLai yeetthi,
poothoDu punalthuuvum puNNiyaree viNNavaril polikinRaaree.
635.meaning: Standing and praising, saying 'O One whose hand that day cut off together the ears and the creeper-like nose of her (Surpanakha) whose form had a gleaming sword-tusk flashing like lightning before us'—those righteous ones who scatter blossoms and water upon the feet of our Lord of cool Sherai, where bees hum amid the pollen, shine bright among the celestials.

636.translit:
theeraaLum vaaL arakkan tennilangkai venjamaththup ponRi viizha,
pooraaLum silaiyathanaal porukaNaikaL pookkuviththaay enRu, naaLum
taaraaLum varaimaarpan thaN seeRai emperumaan umparaaLum,
peeraaLan peerootum periyoorai orukaalum pirikileenee.
636.meaning: Saying 'O You who, with the bow that commands battle, sent forth warring arrows so that the sword-wielding demon who ruled chariots fell and perished in the fierce battle of southern Lanka'—I shall never for a moment part from the great ones who daily chant the names of the great-famed One, our Lord of cool Sherai who rules the heavens, whose mountain-like chest is adorned with a garland.

637.translit:
vanthikkum maTRavarkkum maasuDampin vallamaNar tamakkum alleen,
munthi senRu ari uruvaay iraNiyanai muraNazhittha muthalvarkku allaal,
santhappuu malarch choolaith thaN seeRai emperumaan taaLai, naaLum
sinthippaarkku ennuLLam teenuuRi eppozhuthum tiththikkummee.
637.meaning: I belong neither to those who worship others, nor to the strong Jains of foul body. Except for the Primordial One who went forth as the Lion-form and destroyed the strength of Hiranya—for those who daily meditate upon the feet of our Lord of cool Sherai with its flower-groves of fragrant blossoms, my heart, oozing honey, grows ever sweet.

638.translit:
paNDeena maay ulakai anRu iDantha paNpaaLaa enRu ninRu,
thoNDaaneen tiruvaDiyee thuNaiyallaal thuNaiyilleen solluginReen,
vaNDeenthum malarppuRavil vaN seeRai emperumaan aDiyaar tammai,
kaNDeenukku ithu kaaNiir en nenjam kaNNiNaiyum kaLikkumaaRee.
638.meaning: Standing and saying 'O gracious One who that day, becoming the great boar, lifted up the earth!'—I have become a devotee; Your sacred feet alone are my refuge, I have no other refuge, I declare. Behold this: for me who have seen the devotees of our Lord of bountiful Sherai, where in the flowering meadows bees hold sway, see how my heart and my two eyes rejoice!

639.translit:
paiviriyum variyaravil paDukaDaluL thuyilamarntha paNpaa enRum,
maiviriyum maNivaraipool maayavanee enRenRum, vaNDaar niilam
seyviriyum thaN seeRai yemperumaan tiruvaDiyai sinthiththeeRku, en
aiyaRivum koNDaanukku aaLaanaarkku aaLaam en anpu thaanee!
639.meaning: To Him I cry ever 'O gracious One who reposes upon the striped serpent with spreading hood in the surging sea!' and ever 'O dark wondrous One like a gem-mountain veiled in dark mist!' For me who meditate upon the sacred feet of our Lord of cool Sherai, where bee-thronged blue lilies bloom in the fields—He has taken my five senses; and my love itself becomes a servant to those who have become His servants!

640.translit:
uNNaadhu vengkootRam Ovaadha paavangkaL Seraa, meelai
viNNOrum maNNOrum vandhiRainjum mendhaLirpoo ladiyi naanai,
paNNaara vaNdiyambum paimpozhilsoozh thaNSeeRai yammaan RaNNai,
kaNNaarak kaNduarukik kaiyaarath thozhuvaaraik karudhung kaalee!
640.meaning: The cruel god of death will not devour, and ceaseless sins will not cling to those who meditate upon Him - upon the Lord whose feet are soft as tender shoots, before whom the celestials above and the people of the earth come and worship; the Lord of cool Cheerai, surrounded by fresh groves where bees hum melodiously full of tune. Such is the lot of those who behold Him until their eyes are sated, melt with love, and worship Him with hands filled in adoration.

641.translit:
kaLLaththeen poyyakaththee naadhalaal poadhorukaal kavalai yennum,
veLLaththeeR kenkoloo viLaivayaluL karuneelam kaLainjar thaaLaal
thaLLaththeen maNanaaRum thaNSeeRai emperumaan thaaLai, naaLum
uLLaththee vaippaaruk kidhukaaNeer ennuLLa muruku maaRe!
641.meaning: Because I am a deceitful and false-hearted one, when even for a moment I am caught in the flood called worry - behold how my heart melts for those who daily place within their hearts the feet of my Lord of cool Cheerai, where, when the weeders push aside the dark blue lilies in the ripening fields with their feet, honey wells up and the air is fragrant with its scent!

642.translit:
poomaaNSeer karungkuzhalaar poolnadandhu vayalninRa pedaiyOdu, annam
theemaavin innizhalil kaNduyilum thaNSeeRai yammaan RaNNai,
vaamaandheerp parakaalan kalikanRi olimaalai koNdu thoNdeer,
thoomaaNSeer ponnadimeel soottumin num thuNaikkaiyaal thozhudhu ninRe.
642.meaning: The swan, walking like dark-tressed women adorned with flowers, sleeps in the sweet shade of the mango tree beside its mate that stands in the field - such is the Lord of cool Cheerai. O devotees, take this garland of resounding verses by Kalikanri, the Parakala who rides a chariot drawn by swift horses, and place it upon His pure and glorious golden feet, worshipping with your folded hands as you stand.

643.translit:
thandhai kaalil peruvi langku thaaLavizha, naLLirutkaN vandha
endhai perumaanaar maruvi ninRa voorpoolum,
mundhi vaanam mazhaipozhiyum moovaa vuruvil maRaiyaaLar
andhi moonRu manaloombum aNiyaar veedhi azhundhoore.
643.meaning: It seems to be the town where my Lord, my Father, dwells - He who, when the great fetters fell away from His father's feet, came in the dead of midnight darkness. This is Azhundhur of beautiful streets, where the rain pours from the sky above, and the brahmins of unaging form tend the three sacred fires at the three twilights.

644.translit:
paarith thezhundha padaimannar thammai yaaLa,
paaradhaththuth theeril paaka naayoorndha theeva theeva noorpoolum,
neeril paNaiththa neduvaaLaik kanjip poona kuruginangkaL,
aaral kavuLO darukaNaiyum aNiyaar vayalsoozh azhundhoore!
644.meaning: It seems to be the town of the God of gods, who, to bring under His sway the army-kings who rose up arrayed for battle, served as charioteer riding the chariot in the Bharata war. This is Azhundhur surrounded by beautiful fields, where flocks of herons, frightened away by the long scabbard-fish that thrive in the water, draw near with small fish in their beaks.

645.translit:
Sempon madhiLsoozh thennilangkaik kiRaivan Sirangka L aiyiraNdum,
umbar vaaLik kilakkaaka udhirththa vuravO noorpoolum,
kombi laarndha maadhavimeel koodhi meeyndha vaNdinangkaL,
ambaraavum kaNmadavaar aimpaalaNaiyum azhundhoore.
645.meaning: It seems to be the town of the mighty one who felled the ten heads of the king of southern Lanka, girt by walls of pure gold, making them the target of His heaven-bound arrows. This is Azhundhur, where the swarms of bees that have grazed combing over the kurukkatti creeper laden on its branches settle upon the five-braided tresses of the beautiful-eyed, tender women who long for the sky.

646.translit:
veLLath thuLLO raalilaimeel meevi yadiyeen manampukundhu, en
uLLath thuLLum kaNNuLLum ninRaar ninRa voorpoolum,
puLLup piLLaik kiraitheedip poona kaadhal pedaiyOdum,
aLLal SeRuvil kayalnaadum aNiyaar vayalsoozh azhundhoore.
646.meaning: It seems to be the town where He who reclines upon a banyan leaf amid the flood waters has entered the heart of me, His servant, and stands within my heart and within my eyes. This is Azhundhur surrounded by beautiful fields, where, along with its loving mate, the bird seeks carp in the muddy field, having gone in search of food for its young.

647.translit:
pakalu miravum thaaneeyaayp paarum viNNum thaaneeyaay,
nikaril Sudaraa yiruLaaki ninRaar ninRa voorpoolum,
thukilin kodiyum theerthukaLum thunni maadhar koondhalvaay,
akilin pukaiyaal mukileeykkum aNiyaar veedhi azhundhoore.
647.meaning: It seems to be the town where He stands who is Himself the day and the night, Himself the earth and the heavens, the peerless radiance and also the darkness. This is Azhundhur of beautiful streets, where the cloth banners and the dust of the chariots mingle, and the smoke of agil incense from the women's tresses imitates the clouds.

648.translit:
Edi langku thaamaraipool Sevvaay muRuval SeydharuLi,
maadu vandhen manampukundhu ninRaar ninRaa voorpoolum,
needu maadath thaniccoolam poazhak koNdal thuLidhoova,
aada laravath thaarppOvaa aNiyaar veedhi azhundhoore.
648.meaning: It seems to be the town where He stands who, smiling graciously with His red lips like a petal-bright lotus, came near and entered my heart and stood there. This is Azhundhur of beautiful streets, where the solitary trident atop the tall mansion pierces the clouds so they shower their drops, and the sound of the dancing throng never ceases.

649.translit:
maalaip pukundhu malaraNaimeel vaiki yadiyeen manampukundhu, en
neelak kaNkaL panimalka ninRaar ninRa voorpoolum,
veelaik kadalpool neduveedhi viNthOy Sudhaiven maNimaadaththu,
aalaip pukaiyaal azhalkadhirai maRaikkum veedhi azhundhoore.
649.meaning: It seems to be the town where He came at evening, rested upon the flower-bed, entered the heart of me His servant, and stood there as my dark eyes brimmed with cool tears. This is Azhundhur of streets long as the surging sea, where, from the sky-touching white-plastered gem-set mansions, the smoke of the sugarcane mills hides the fiery rays of the sun along the streets.

650.translit:
vanji marungku lidainoova maNandhu ninRa kanavakaththu, en
nenju niRaiyak kaikoopppi ninRaar ninRa voorpoolum,
panji yanna melladhinaR paavai maarkaL, aadakaththin
anji lambi naarppOvaa aNiyaar veedhi azhundhoore.
650.meaning: It seems to be the town where, within a dream in which He embraced me until my willow-slender waist ached, He stood while I folded my hands with my heart filled full. This is Azhundhur of beautiful streets, where the sound from the lovely golden anklets of the fair maidens with feet soft as cotton never ceases.

651.translit:
ennaim pulanu mezhilungkoN dingkee nerunda lezhundharuLi
ponnang kalaikaL melivaeydhap poona punidha roorpoolum,
mannu mudhunee raravindha malarmeel varivaN diSaipaada
annam pedaiyO dudanaadum aNiyaar vayalsoozh azhundhoore!
651.meaning: It seems to be the town of the pure one who, having taken my five senses and my beauty, graced His presence here only yesterday and then departed, leaving my golden ornaments to grow loose upon me. This is Azhundhur surrounded by beautiful fields, where, as the striped bees sing upon the lotus blossoms in the abiding ancient waters, the swan dances together with its mate.

652.translit:
nellil kuvaLai kaNkaatta ril kumudham vaaykaatta,
allik kamalam mukangkaattum kazhani yazhundhoor ninRaanai,
vallip podhumbil kuyilkoovum mangkai veendhan parakaalan,
Sollil polindha thamizhmaalai Sollap paavam nillaavee.
652.meaning: He stands in Azhundhur of fields where, amid the paddy, the blue lily shows its eyes, the red water-lily shows its mouth, and the petalled lotus shows its face. If one recites this radiant Tamil garland of words by Parakala, lord of Mangai, where the cuckoo calls in the creeper-bowers, sins will not abide.

653.translit:
Singkama thaayavuNan thiRalaakammun keeNdukandha,
Sangkami daththaanaith thazhalaazhi valaththaanai,
Sengkamalath thayananaiyaar thennazhundhaiyil mannininRa,
angkama lakkaNNanai adiyeenkaNdu koNdeene.
653.meaning: He who, becoming a lion, joyfully tore open of old the mighty chest of the demon Hiranya; who bears the conch on His left and the blazing discus on His right; the lotus-eyed beautiful one who abides firmly in southern Azhundhai full of brahmins resembling the red-lotus-born Brahma - Him have I, His servant, beheld and gained.

654.translit:
kOvaa naarmadiyak kolaiyaarmazhuk koNdaruLum,
moovaa vaanavanai muzhuneervaN Nanai, adiyaarkku,
aavaa enRirangkith thennazhundhaiyil mannininRa,
theevaadhi theevanaiyaan kaNdukoNdu thiLaiththeene.
654.meaning: The unaging celestial who graciously wielded the deadly axe so that kings should perish; the one whose hue is like the brimming flood-waters; the God of all gods who, taking pity on His servants crying 'Alas, Alas,' abides firmly in southern Azhundhai - Him have I beheld and reveled in.

655.translit:
udaiyaa naiyolinee rulakangkaL padaiththaanai,
vidaiyaa nOdavanRu viRalaazhi viSaiththaanai,
adaiyaar thennilangkai yazhiththaanai aNiyazhundhoor
udaiyaanai, adiyee nadaindhuuyndhu pooneene.
655.meaning: The Lord, who created the worlds of resounding waters; who that day, when His bull-mount Nandi was struck, hurled the mighty discus; who destroyed the hostile southern Lanka; the master of beautiful Azhundhur - I, His servant, have reached Him and gone forth redeemed.

656.translit:
kunRaal maaridhaduth thavanaikkula veezhamanRu
ponRaa mai, adhanuk karuLSeydha pooreeRRai,
anRaa vinnaRuney yamarndhuuNNa aNiyazhundhoor
ninRaanai, adiyeen kaNdukoNdu niRaindheene.
656.meaning: He who held up a mountain to ward off the rain; the bull-like warrior who showed grace to the noble elephant of old so that it would not perish; who once delighted to eat the fragrant ghee of the cows - He who stands in beautiful Azhundhur, Him have I, His servant, beheld and become fulfilled.

657.translit:
kanjanaik kaayndhaanaik kaNNamangkaiyuL ninRaanai,
vanjanap peeymulaiyoo duyirvaaymaduth thuNdaanai,
SenjSol naanmaRaiyOr thennazhundhaiyil mannininRa
anjanak kunRandhannai yadiyeenkaNdu koNdeene.
657.meaning: He who destroyed Kamsa in wrath; who stands in Kannamangai; who sucked out the very life through the breast of the deceitful demoness Putana; the mountain dark as collyrium who abides firmly in southern Azhundhai of the brahmins of the four Vedas and sweet words - Him have I, His servant, beheld and gained.

658.translit:
periyaanai yamarar thalaivaRkum piramanukkum,
uriyaanai yukandhaa navanukku muNarvadhanuk
kariyaanai, azhundhoor maRaiyOrka LadipaNiyum
kariyaanai, adiyeen kaNdukoNdu kaLiththeene!
658.meaning: The great one; He who belongs to the lord of the immortals and to Brahma; He who is hard to comprehend even for one who loves Him; the dark one at whose feet the brahmins of Azhundhur bow down - Him have I, His servant, beheld and rejoiced!

659.translit:
thiruvaazh maarvanRannaith thiSaimaNNee rerimudhalaa,
uruvaay ninRavanai yoliSeerum maaruthaththai,
aruvaay ninRavanaith thennazhundhaiyil mannininRa
karuvaar kaRpakaththaik kaNdukoNdu kaLiththeene!
659.meaning: He on whose chest Lakshmi dwells; He who stands as the very forms of the directions, earth, water, fire and the rest; He who is the resounding wind; He who stands formless; the rain-bearing wish-granting Kalpaka tree who abides firmly in southern Azhundhai - Him have I beheld and rejoiced!

660.translit:
nilai aaLaaga ennai ugandaanai, nila magaL tan
mulaiyaaL vittaganai mudu naanmaRai veedhi toRum,
alai aarum kadal pool muzhangu azhundaiyil manni ninRa
kalaiyaar soR poruLaik kandu koNdu kaLitthEnE.
660.meaning: He who graciously took me to be His servant, the wise lord of her who is the breasted Earth-maiden, who abides firm in Azhundai where, through every street, the ancient four Vedas resound like the wave-filled roaring sea. Having beheld Him who is the very meaning of the words of the sacred arts, I have rejoiced.

661.translit:
pEraanaik kudandaip perumaanai, ilangu oLi sEr
vaar aar vana mulaiyaaL malar mangai naayaganai,
aaraa innamudait ten azhundaiyil manni ninRa,
kaar aar karu mugilaik kandu koNdu kaLitthEnE.
661.meaning: The great Lord, the Perumal of Kudandai, the consort of the lotus-born Lakshmi of beautiful breasts bound in a shining bodice, the never-cloying nectar who abides firm in southern Azhundai. Having beheld Him, the dark cloud-hued one full of rain-clouds, I have rejoiced.

662.translit:
thiRal murugan anaiyaar ten azhundaiyil manni ninRa
aRa mudalvan avanai aNi aaliyar kOn, maruvaar
kaRai nedu vEl valavan kalikanRi sol aiyiraNdum,
muRai vazhuvaamai vallaar muzhudu aaLvar vaan ulagE.
662.meaning: On Him who is the source of dharma, abiding firm in southern Azhundai where dwell men valorous as Murugan, the king of beautiful Aali, master of the long stain-smeared spear that pierces foes, Kalikanri sang these ten verses. Those who can recite them without breaking the proper order shall rule entirely over the heavenly world.

663.translit:
thiruvukkum thiru aagiya selvaa
   deyvatthukku arasE seyya kaNNaa,
uruvac chenj sudar aazhi vallaanE
   ulagu uNda oruvaa, thiru maarbaa,
oruvaRku aaRRi uyyum vagai enRaal
  udan ninRu aivar en uL pugundu, ozhiyaadu
aruvitthu inRida anji nin adaindEn
  azhundoor mEl thisai ninRa ammaanE.
663.meaning: O treasure who are the wealth even of Lakshmi, O king of the gods, O red-eyed one, O wielder of the shapely red-radiant discus, O unique one who swallowed the worlds, O one with Lakshmi on Your chest. If there is a way for a man to endure and be saved, since the five senses stand together and have entered my heart and ceaselessly tear and devour me, in fear I have come to take refuge in You, O Lord who stands in the western quarter of Azhundur.

664.translit:
pandu aar mel viral nal vaLait thOLi
  paavai poo magaL tannodum udanE
vandaai, en manatthE manni ninRaai
  maal vaNNaa mazhai pol oLi vaNNaa,
sandhOgaa pauzhiyaa taittiriyaa
  saama vEdhiyanE nedu maalE,
andhO nin adi anRi maRRu aRiyEn
  azhundoor mEl thisai ninRa ammaanE.
664.meaning: You came together with the doll-like lotus-maiden Lakshmi, she of soft fingers that play with the ball and shoulders bright with fine bangles, and You abide firm in my heart, O dark-hued one, O one whose radiance is like the rain-cloud. O reciter of the Sama, Chandogya, Paushya, and Taittiriya, O great Vishnu, alas, I know no refuge other than Your feet, O Lord who stands in the western quarter of Azhundur.

665.translit:
ney aar aazhiyum sangamum Endum
  neeNda thOL udaiyaai, adiyEnaic
seyyaadha ulagatthidaic seydaai
  siRumaikkum perumaikkum uL pugundu,
poyyaal aivar en mei kudi ERip
  pORRi vaazhvadaRku anji nin adaindEn
aiyaa nin adi anRi maRRu aRiyEn
  azhundoor mEl thisai ninRa ammaanE.
665.meaning: O one with long arms bearing the gleaming discus and the conch, You created me, Your servant, in this world though it had not been made. Entering into both my littleness and my greatness, the five senses have through falsehood taken up residence in my body, and in fear of having to live praising them I have come to take refuge in You. O lord, I know no refuge other than Your feet, O Lord who stands in the western quarter of Azhundur.

666.translit:
paranE panchavan pauzhiyan sOzhan
 paar mannar mannar taam paNindu Etthum
varanE, maadhavanE madhu soodhaa
 maRRu Or nal tuNai nin alaal ilEn kaaN
naranE naaraNanE thiru naRaiyoor nambee
 em perumaan umbar aaLum
aranE aadhi varaagam mun aanaai
 azhundoor mEl thisai ninRa ammaanE.
666.meaning: O Supreme one, O boon-granting Lord whom the Pandya, the Pauzhiya, the Chola and the kings of kings of the earth bow to and worship, O Madhava, O slayer of Madhu, I have no other good helper but You. O man-form, O Narayana, O Nambi of holy Naraiyur, my Lord, O ruler over the celestials, O one who became the primordial Boar of old, O Lord who stands in the western quarter of Azhundur.

667.translit:
viNdaan viN puga venj samatthu ariyaaip
 pariyOn maarvagam paRRip piLandu,
paNdaan uyya Or maal varai Endum
 paNbaaLaa paranE pavittiranE,
kandEn naan kali ugattadan tanmai
 karumam aavadum enRanakku aRindEn,
aNdaa, nin adi anRi maRRu aRiyEn
 azhundoor mEl thisai ninRa ammaanE.
667.meaning: As a lion in fierce battle You seized the chest of the mighty one (Hiranya) and tore it so that he departed to the heavens; of gracious nature, You lifted up a great mountain so that the cowherds of old might be saved, O Supreme one, O pure one. I have seen the nature of this Kali age and have understood for myself what my duty must be. O Lord of the cosmos, I know no refuge other than Your feet, O Lord who stands in the western quarter of Azhundur.

668.translit:
thOyaa in tayir nei amudu uNNac
 sonnaar solli nagum parisE, peRRa
taayaal aappuNdu irundu azhudu Engum
 taadaaLaa tharaiyOrkkum viNNOrkkum
sEyaai, kirEta tirEta dvaapara
 kali yugam ivai naangu mun aanaai,
aayaa nin adi anRi maRRu aRiyEn
 azhundoor mEl thisai ninRa ammaanE.
668.meaning: O generous-handed one who, for eating the sweet undisturbed curds and the nectar-like ghee, was bound by Your own mother as others spoke and laughed, and sat there weeping and sobbing; O one far beyond the reach of both earthlings and the celestials, You became these four ages of old, Krita, Treta, Dvapara and Kali. O cowherd, I know no refuge other than Your feet, O Lord who stands in the western quarter of Azhundur.

669.translit:
kaRutthuk kanjanai anja munindaai
 kaar vaNNaa kadal pol oLi vaNNaa
iRutthittaan vidai Ezhum mun venRaai
 endaai andaram Ezhum mun aanaai,
poRutthuk koNdirundaal poRukkoNaap
 pOgamE nugarvaan pugundu, aivar
aRutthut tinRida anji nin adaindEn
 azhundoor mEl thisai ninRa ammaanE.
669.meaning: In wrath You raged so that Kamsa trembled, O dark-hued one, O one whose radiance is like the sea; You once vanquished and broke the seven bulls, my Father, You who of old became the seven worlds. If I keep enduring it, the five senses enter to enjoy pleasures unbearable, and in fear that they cut and devour me, I have come to take refuge in You, O Lord who stands in the western quarter of Azhundur.

670.translit:
nediyaanE kadi aar kali nambee
    ninnaiyE ninaindu ingu iruppEnai
kadi aar kaaLaiyar aivar pugundu
    kaaval seydha avak kaavalaip pizhaitthu
kudi pOndu un adik keezh vandu pugundEn
    kooRai sOR ivai tandu enakku aruLi
adiyEnaip paNi aaNdu koL endaai
    azhundoor mEl thisai ninRa ammaanE.
670.meaning: O tall one, O Nambi of the fierce-thronging age, I who remain here thinking only of You escaped the guard that the five fierce youthful senses had entered and set; leaving their fold I came and entered beneath Your feet. Grant me these, garment and food, and accept me as Your servant in service, my Father, O Lord who stands in the western quarter of Azhundur.

671.translit:
kOvaai aivar en mei kudi ERik
 kooRai sOR ivai taa enRu kumaitthup
pOgaar, naan avaraip poRukka kilEn
 punidhaa put kodiyaai nedu maalE,
thee vaai naagaNaiyil tuyilvaanE
 thirumaalE ini seyvadu onRu aRiyEn,
aavaa enRu adiyERku iRai irangaai
 azhundoor mEl thisai ninRa ammaanE.
671.meaning: As masters the five senses have mounted into my body as their dwelling and torment me, demanding 'give garment, give food,' and will not depart; I cannot endure them. O pure one, O one with the bird-banner, O great Vishnu, O one who sleeps upon the serpent-bed of fiery mouth, O Tirumal, I know not one thing now to do. Saying 'alas,' have a little mercy on me, Your servant, O Lord who stands in the western quarter of Azhundur.

672.translit:
annam mannu paim poom pozhil soozhnda
 azhundoor mEl thisai ninRa ammaanai,
kanni mannu thiN tOL kalikanRi
 aali naadan mangaik kula vEndan,
sonna in tamizh nan maNik kOvai
 thooya maalai ivai patthum vallaar,
manni mannavar aai ulagu aaNdu
 maana veN kudaik keezh magizhvaarE.
672.meaning: On the Lord who stands in the western quarter of Azhundur, surrounded by fresh blooming groves where swans abide, Kalikanri of strong ageless shoulders, lord of the Aali land, royal chief of the Mangai clan, sang these ten verses, a fine gem-garland of sweet Tamil, a pure garland. Those who master them shall abide as enduring kings, ruling the world and rejoicing beneath the proud white parasol.

673.translit:
sem kamalat tiru magaLum puviyum sem pon
  thiruvadiyin iNai varuda munivar Etta
vangam mali tadam kadaluL ananthan ennum
  vari aravin aNait tuyilnRa maayOn kaaNmin
engum mali niRai pugazh naal vEdam aindu
  vELvigaLum kELvigaLum iyanRa tanmai
am kamalat ayan anaiyaar payilum selvat
  taNi azhundoor ninRu ugandha amarar kOvE.
673.meaning: Behold the wondrous one upon whom the red-lotus Lakshmi and the Earth-goddess stroke His golden sacred feet while sages praise Him, who reclines in sleep upon the bed of the striped serpent called Ananta in the vast ship-laden sea. He is the king of the immortals who delights to stand in cool, prosperous Azhundur, where dwell men like Brahma born of the lovely lotus, full everywhere of abounding fame, accomplished in the four Vedas, the five sacrifices, and sacred learning.

674.translit:
mun iv ulagu Ezhum iruL maNdi uNNa
  munivarodu daanavargaL thigaippa, vandu
pannu kalai naal vEdap poruLai ellaam
  pari mugamaai aruLiya em paraman kaaNmin
sem nel mali kadhirk kavari veesac sangam
  avai muralac sem kamala malaraiyERi
annam mali pedaiyOdum amarum selvat
  taNi azhundoor ninRu ugandha amarar kOvE.
674.meaning: When of old darkness gathered to swallow all the seven worlds and sages and demons were bewildered, behold our Supreme One who came in horse-faced form (Hayagriva) and graciously restored the whole meaning of the manifold arts and the four Vedas. He is the king of the immortals who delights to stand in cool, prosperous Azhundur, where the ears of rich red paddy wave like fly-whisks, the conches resound, and the swan with its mate settles, mounting the red lotus blossom.

675.translit:
kulat talaiya madha vEzham poigai pukkuk
  kOL mudalai pidikka adaRku anungi ninRu
nilat tigazhum malarc cudar Ey sOdhee enna
  nenju idar teertthu aruLiya en nimalan kaaNmin
malait tigazh sandu agil kanaga maNiyum koNdu
  vandu undhi vayalgaL toRum madaigaL paaya
alaitthu varum ponni vaLam perugum
  selvat taNi azhundoor ninRu ugandha amarar kOvE.
675.meaning: When the lordly rutting elephant entered the pond and the fierce crocodile seized it, and it stood in anguish crying out 'O radiance like a lotus shining on the earth, O light,' behold my flawless one who relieved the distress of its heart and showed grace. He is the king of the immortals who delights to stand in prosperous, cool Azhundur, where the wealth of the Kaveri swells, surging and bringing down sandalwood, eagle-wood, gold and gems from the mountains, and pours through the sluices into every field.

676.translit:
silambu mudal kalan aNindu Or sem kaN kunRam
  tigazhndadu ena thiru uruvam panRi aagi
ilangu puvi madandai tanai idandu pulgi
  eyiRRidai vaitthu aruLiya em meesan kaaNmin
pulambu siRai vaNdu olippap poogam tokka
  pozhil kadoRum kuyil koova mayilgaL aala
alambu thiraip punal pudai soozhndu azhagu aar selvat
  taNi azhundoor ninRu ugandha amarar kOvE.
676.meaning: Taking the sacred form of a boar, as though a red-eyed hill adorned with anklets and other ornaments shone forth, behold our Lord who graciously dug up the shining Earth-maiden, embraced her and held her safe upon His tusk. He is the king of the immortals who delights to stand in beautiful, prosperous, cool Azhundur, where in every grove thick with areca palms the winged bees hum, the cuckoos call and the peacocks dance, and the surging-waved waters surround it on every side.

677.translit:
sinam mEvum adal ariyin uruvam aagit
  thiRal mEvum iraNiyan aagam keeNdu
manam mEvu vanjanaiyaal vandha pEicchi
  maaLa uyir vavviya em maayOn kaaNmin
inam mEvu vari vaLaik kai Endum kOvai
  Ey vaaya maragatham pol kiLiyin in sol
anam mEvu nadai madavaar payilum selvat
  taNi azhundoor ninRu ugandha amarar kOvE.
677.meaning: Taking the form of a wrathful, mighty man-lion, He split open the chest of the powerful Hiranya; behold our wondrous one who seized the life of the demoness (Putana) who came through deceitful intent so that she perished. He is the king of the immortals who delights to stand in prosperous, cool Azhundur, where women of swan-like gait dwell, bearing in their hands strings of striped bangles, whose sweet words are like the matched green emerald-hued parrot.

678.translit:
vaanavar tam tuyar teera vandu thOnRi
  maaN uruvaai moovadi maa valiyai vENdi
taan amara Ezh ulagum aLanda venRit
  thani mudal sakkarap padai en talaivan kaaNmin
tEn amarum pozhil tazhuvum ezhil koL veedhic
  sezhu maada maaLigaigaL koodam tORum
aana tol seer maRaiyaaLar payilum selvat
  taNi azhundoor ninRu ugandha amarar kOvE.
678.meaning: To end the suffering of the celestials He came and appeared as a dwarf of noble form, begged three feet of ground from great Bali, and victoriously measured all the seven worlds; behold my Lord, the unique primal one with the discus weapon. He is the king of the immortals who delights to stand in prosperous, cool Azhundur, where in every hall of the rich storeyed mansions along the lovely groves embraced by honey-laden gardens dwell brahmins of enduring ancient excellence.

679.translit:
pandu aNainda mel viralaaL seethaikku aagip
  pagalavan meedhu iyangaadha ilangai vEndan
andam il thiN karam sirangaL puraNdu veezha
  adu kaNaiyaal eydhu ugandha ammaan kaaNmin
sem tamizhum vada kalaiyum tigazhnda naavar
  thisai muganE anaiyavargaL semmai mikka
andaNar tam aaguthiyin pugai aar selvat
  taNi azhundoor ninRu ugandha amarar kOvE.
679.meaning: For the sake of Sita of soft fingers that played with the ball, behold the Lord who joyfully shot with deadly arrows the king of Lanka, over which the sun could not pass, so that his countless strong arms and heads rolled and fell. He is the king of the immortals who delights to stand in prosperous, cool Azhundur, full of the smoke of the sacrifices of upright brahmins, men like Brahma the four-faced one, whose tongues shine with both pure Tamil and the northern arts.

680.translit:
kumba mihu mada vEzham kulaiyak kombu
  paRiththu mazha vidai adarththuk kuravai kOththu
vamba vizhum malarkkuzhalAL Aychchi vaiththa
  thayir veNNey uNDuhandha mAyOn kANmin
sem pavaLa marakata nan muththam kATTath
  thihazh pUham kadali pala vaLam mikku engum
am pon madhiL pozhil pudai sUzhndhu azhahAr selvath
  thaNi azhundhUr ninRuhandha amarar kOvE.
680.meaning: Behold Mayon, the wondrous Lord who broke the tusks of the proud rutting elephant, subdued the young bulls, danced the kuravai, and joyfully ate the white curds and butter set aside by the cowherdess with flower-decked fragrant tresses. He is the King of the immortals who stands in joy at beautiful, prosperous Tiruvazhundur, ringed by groves and golden ramparts, where red coral, emerald, and fine pearls gleam and where areca and plantain abound in rich plenty everywhere.

681.translit:
UDERu kanjanodu mallum villum
  oN kariyum uruL sakaDum udaiyach cheRRa
nIDERu peruvalith thOL udaiya venRi
  nilavu puhazh nEmi am kai nediyOn kANmin
sEDERu pozhil thazhuvum ezhil koL veedhith
  thiruvizhavil maNi aNindha thiNNai thORum
ADERu malarkkuzhalAr payilum selvath
  thaNi azhundhUr ninRuhandha amarar kOvE.
681.meaning: Behold the tall Lord with the discus in His handsome hand, of abiding fame and victory, who has mighty long arms of surpassing strength, who destroyed the hostile Kamsa, the wrestlers, the bow, the splendid elephant, and the rolling cart. He is the King of the immortals who stands in joy at prosperous Tiruvazhundur, where women with flower-decked dancing tresses frequent the gem-adorned porches along the lovely festival streets embraced by lofty groves.

682.translit:
panRiyAy meenAhi ariyAyp pAraip
  padaiththuk kAththu uNDu umizhndha paraman thannai
anRu amararkku adhipadhiyum ayanum sEyum
  adi paNiya aNi azhundhUr ninRa kOvai
kanRi nedu vEl valavan Ali nADan
  kalikanRi oli seydha inbap pADal,
onRinodu nAnkum Or aindhum vallAr
  oli kadal sUzh ulahALum umbar thAmE.
682.meaning: Kaliyan, lord of the Ali land who wields the long fierce spear, has composed these sweet melodious songs on the Supreme One who became the Boar, the Fish, and the Man-Lion, and who created, protected, swallowed and spewed forth the earth, the King who stands at beautiful Tiruvazhundur, where the chief of the immortals, Brahma, and Skanda bowed at His feet. Those who can master all these ten verses, the one with the four and the five, shall themselves become gods who rule the world girt by the resounding sea.

683.translit:
kaLLam manam viLLum vahai karudhik kazhal thozhuveer
veLLam mudhu paravaith thirai viriya, karai engum
teLLum maNi thihazhum siRu puliyUrch chala sayanaththu
uLLum, enadhu uLLaththuLum uRaivAraiyuL leerE.
683.meaning: O you who worship His feet, resolving that your deceitful minds shall be cleared: He who dwells in the ocean-couch (Jalashayana) of Chiruppuliyur, where the waves of the ancient swelling flood spread out and clear gleaming gems shine along every shore, dwells also within my own heart. Bear Him within you.

684.translit:
theruvil thiri siRu nOnbiyar senjOTToDu kanji maruvi,
pirindhavar vAymozhi madhiyAdhu vandhu adaiveer,
thiruvil poli maRaiyOrch chiRu puliyUrch chala sayanaththu,
uruvak kuRaL adihaL adi uNarmin uNarveerE.
684.meaning: O you of understanding, do not heed the words of those fallen ones, the petty ascetics who roam the streets fond of red rice and gruel, who stand apart from the true path; come and take refuge. Realize the feet of the Lord who took the dwarf form, who reclines in the ocean-couch of Chiruppuliyur, home of the Vedic brahmins resplendent with sacred wealth.

685.translit:
paRaiyum vinai thozhudhu uymin neer paNiyum siRu thoNDeer.
aRaiyum punal oru pAl vayal oru pAl pozhil oru pAl
siRai vaNDinam maRaiyum siRu puliyUrch chala sayanath uRaiyum,
iRai adi alladhu onRu iRaiyum aRiyEnE.
685.meaning: O you humble devotees who serve, worship and be saved, for your karma will flee. He dwells in the ocean-couch of Chiruppuliyur, where on one side roar the waters, on another lie the fields, and on another the groves where swarms of winged bees nestle hidden. Apart from the feet of this Lord, I know nothing else, not even a little.

686.translit:
vAnAr madhi podhiyum sadai mazhuvALiyodu oru pAl,
thAnAhiya thalaivan avan amararkku adhipadhiyAm
thEnAr pozhil thazhuvum siRu puliyUrch chala sayanath
thAnAyanadhu, adi alladhu onRu aRiyEn adiyEnE.
686.meaning: He who Himself became, on one side, the chief Lord together with the axe-wielding one (Shiva) whose matted locks hold the sky-filling moon, is the chief of the immortals. He is the Lord who reclines in the ocean-couch of Chiruppuliyur, embraced by honey-rich groves. I, His servant, know nothing other than His feet.

687.translit:
nandhA nedu narahaththiDai naNuhA vahai, nALum
endhAy ena imaiyOr thozhudhu Eththum idam,
eRi neerch chendhAmarai malarum siRu puliyUrch chala sayanaththu
an thAmarai adiyAy. una adiyERku aruL puriyE.
687.meaning: It is the place where the unwinking gods worship and praise Him daily, crying "O our Father," so that they may not draw near the long, unceasing hell. O Lord with feet like the lovely lotus, who reclines in the ocean-couch of Chiruppuliyur where red lotuses bloom in the dashing waters: grant Your grace to me, Your servant.

688.translit:
muzhu neelamum alarAmbalum aravindhamum viravi,
kazhu neerodu madavAr avar kaN vAy muham malarum,
sezhu neer vayal thazhuvum siRu puliyUrch chala sayanam,
thozhu neermaiyadhu udaiyAr adi thozhuvAr thuyar ilarE.
688.meaning: In the rich watered fields of the ocean-couch of Chiruppuliyur, the deep blue lily, the night-lotus, the red lotus, and the water-lily all bloom mingled together, resembling the eyes, lips and faces of fair maidens. Those who possess the disposition to worship Him there, those who bow at His feet, shall be free from sorrow.

689.translit:
sEy Ongu thaN thirumAliruny sOlai malai uRaiyum
mAyA enakku uraiyAy idhu maRai nAnkinuL AyO,
thee Ombu kai maRaiyOrch chiRu puliyUrch chala sayanath
thAyO una adiyAr manaththAyO aRiyEnE.
689.meaning: O wondrous Lord who dwells on the lofty, cool Tirumalirunjolai mountain, tell me this: are You within the four Vedas, or are You in the ocean-couch of Chiruppuliyur of the brahmins who tend the sacred fire, or are You in the hearts of Your devotees? I do not know.

690.translit:
maiyAr vari neelam malark kaNNAr manam viTTiTTu,
uyvAn una kazhalE thozhudhu ezhuvEn, kiLi madavAr
sevvAy mozhi payilum siRu puliyUrch chala sayanaththu,
aivAy aravu aNai mEl uRai amalA. aruLAyE.
690.meaning: Casting away the minds of the women whose flower-like eyes are streaked dark with collyrium, I rise worshipping only Your feet, that I may be saved. O stainless Lord who reclines upon the five-headed serpent couch in the ocean-couch of Chiruppuliyur, where graceful women practice red-lipped speech like parrots: grant Your grace.

691.translit:
karu mA muhil uruvA! kanal uruvA! punal uruvA,
peru mAl varai uruvA! piRa uruvA! ninadhu uruvA,
thiru mA mahaL maruvum siRu puliyUrch chala sayanaththu,
aru mA kadal amudhE! una adiyE saraN AmE.
691.meaning: O You whose form is the great dark cloud! Whose form is fire! Whose form is water! Whose form is the great mighty mountain! Whose form is every other thing! All forms are Your own. O rare nectar of the great ocean, who reclines in the ocean-couch of Chiruppuliyur where the great sacred Lakshmi abides: Your feet alone are my refuge.

692.translit:
seerAr nedu maRuhil siRu puliyUrch chala sayanaththu,
ErAr muhil vaNNan thanai imaiyOr perumAnai,
kArAr vayal mangaikku iRai kaliyan oli mAlai,
pArAr ivai paravith thozhap pAvam payilAvE.
692.meaning: Kaliyan, lord of Mangai with its rain-fed fields, has made this resounding garland of song upon the beautiful cloud-hued Lord, the master of the immortals, who reclines in the ocean-couch of Chiruppuliyur of the long, splendid streets. For the people of the earth who recite and worship with these verses, sin shall not cling.

693.translit:
perum puRak kadalai adal ERRinaip
  peNNai ANai, eNNil munivarkku aruL
tarum thavaththai muththin thiraL kOvaiyaip
  paththar Aviyai niththilath thoththinai,
arumbinai alarai adiyEn manath
  Asaiyai amudham podhi in suvaik
karumbinaik, kaniyaich chenRu nADik
  kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNDu koNDEnE.
693.meaning: The vast outer ocean, the mighty fighting bull, the female and the male, the very penance that grants grace to countless sages, the string of clustered pearls, the very life of devotees, the cluster of pearls, the bud and the blossom, the desire within this servant's heart, the sugarcane laden with nectar-sweet juice, the ripe fruit — Him I went seeking and found and beheld within Kannamangai.

694.translit:
meynnalath thavaththaith thivaththaith tharum
  meyyaip poyyinaik kaiyil Or sangu udai,
mainniRak kadalaik kadal vaNNanai
  mAlai Al ilaip paLLi koL mAyanai,
nennalaip pahalai iRRai nALinai
  nALaiyAy varum thingaLai ANDinai,
kannalaik karumbinidaith thERalaik
  kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNDu koNDEnE.
694.meaning: The true penance of genuine goodness, He who grants heaven, the truth and the falsehood, the dark-hued one of the sea who holds a conch in His hand, the wondrous Lord who reclines upon the banyan leaf, yesterday and today's daylight and this very day, the month and the year that come as tomorrow, the sweet candy, the syrup within the sugarcane — Him I went and found and beheld within Kannamangai.

695.translit:
engaLukku aruL seyhinRa eesanai
  vAsa vAr kuzhalAL malai mangai than
panganai, pangil vaiththu uhandhAn thannaip
  pAnmaiyaip pani mA madhiyam thavazh
mangulaich, chudarai vada mA malai
  uchchiyai, nachchi nAm vaNangap padum
kangulai, pahalaich chenRu nADik
  kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNDu koNDEnE.
695.meaning: The Lord who grants grace to us, He who shares His side with the mountain-daughter of fragrant flowing tresses, who joyfully placed her at His side, His very nature, the cloud over which the cool great moon glides, the radiance, the summit of the great northern mountain (Venkatam), the night and the day that we long for and worship — Him I went seeking and found and beheld within Kannamangai.

696.translit:
pEy mulaith thalai nanju uNDa piLLaiyath
  theLLiyAr vaNangap padum dhEvanai,
mAyanai madhiL kOvali daikazhi
  maindhanai anRi andhaNar sindhaiyuL eesanai,
ilangum sudarch chOdhiyai endhai
  yai enakku eyppinil vaippinai
kAsinai maNiyaich chenRu nADik
  kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNDu koNDEnE.
696.meaning: The Child who drank the poison from the demoness's breast, the God whom the clear-minded worship, the wondrous one, the youthful Lord of Tirukkovalur within its ramparts, the Lord within the hearts of the brahmins, the shining radiant flame, my Father, my treasure laid up for my time of weariness, the gold coin, the gem — Him I went seeking and found and beheld within Kannamangai.

697.translit:
ERRinai imayaththuL em eesanai
  immaiyai maRumaikkum marundhinai,
ARRalai aNdath appuRath thuyththidum
  aiyanaik kaiyil Azhi onRu Endhiya
kUTRinai, guru mA maNik kunRinai
  ninRa Ur ninRa niththilath thoththinai,
kARRinaip punalaich chenRu nADik
  kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNDu koNDEnE.
697.meaning: The bull, our Lord on the Himalayas, this life and the medicine for the next, the might, the master who carries us beyond the cosmos, the destroyer who holds a single discus in His hand, the great precious gem-hill, the cluster of pearls who stood at Ninravur, the wind and the water — Him I went seeking and found and beheld within Kannamangai.

698.translit:
thuppanaith thurangam paDach cheeRiya
  thOnRalaich chudar vAn kalan peydhadhOr
seppinai, thiru mangai maNALanaith
