978.meaning: Clad in ochre-colored cloth, twirling a staff, your hair tied up in branching curls, a fine girdle bound on, you have come and stood outside the wall's door. Who is this? What is this, what is this, what indeed?

979.translit:
karuLak kodi onRu udaiyeer. thanippaageer,
uruLach chakadam adhu uRakkil nimirththeer,
maruLaik kodu paadi vandhu illam pugundheer,
iruLaththu idhu enn idhu enn idhu ennO.
979.meaning: You who bear the single banner of the eagle (Garuda), you peerless charioteer, you who set straight the cart as it lay rolling -- you have come singing a bewildering tune and entered the house. In the dark, what is this? What is this, what is this, what indeed?

980.translit:
naamam palavu mudainaa raNanambee,
thaamath thuLavam mikanaa Ridukindreer,
kaaman enappaadi venthillam pukunththeer,
emath thidu ven nithuven nithuvennoo.
980.meaning: O Narayana, my noble Lord who bears many names! You come fragrant with the cool tulasi garland upon you. Singing of you as Kaaman (the god of love), you have entered my burning house. What is this? What is this protection? What is this, alas, what is this?

981.translit:
sutrum kuzhalthaazhach churikai yaNaiththu,
matrum palamaamaNi ponko daNinthu,
mutram pukunthu muRuvalseythu nindreer,
etruk kithuven nithuven nithuvennoo.
981.meaning: With your curling locks hanging all around, a sword girded at your side, and adorned with many great gems and gold, you have entered the front yard and stand there smiling. Why is this? What is this, what is this, alas, what is this?

982.translit:
aanaa yarum aa niraiyumang kozhiyak,
koonaaya thoorkotra villondru kaiyenthip,
poonaa rirunthaa raiyumpaarththup pukutheer,
enoorkaL munnen nithuven nithuvennoo.
982.meaning: Leaving aside there the rare herd of cows that you tended, holding in your hand a single curved victorious bow, you enter, looking at both those who have gone and those who remain. Before other people, why is this? What is this, what is this, alas, what is this?

983.translit:
malle porutha thiraLthool maNavaaLeer,
alle yarinthoom_m manaththin karuththai,
sollaa thozhiyeer sonnapoo thinaalvaareer
elle yithuven nithuven nithuvennoo.
983.meaning: O bridegroom with rounded shoulders that wrestled the wrestlers! We have indeed known the thought in your mind. Do not leave it unspoken; when it is spoken, come. Lo! What is this? What is this, what is this, alas, what is this?

984.translit:
pukkaa daravam pidiththaattum puniteer,
ikkaalangaL yaamumak keethondru malloom,
thakkaar palarththeevi maarsaala vudaiyeer,
ekkee ithuven nithuven nithuvennoo.
984.meaning: O pure one who entered and seized the serpent and made it dance! In these times we are nothing at all to you. You have many worthy ones and a great many divine consorts. How, then? What is this, what is this, alas, what is this?

985.translit:
aadi yasainthaay madavaa roduneepooyk
koodik kuravai piNaikoo maLappiLLaay,
theedith thirumaa makaLmaN makaLnirpa,
eedi ithuven nithuven nithuvennoo.
985.meaning: You danced and swayed, and going off with the young women you joined together and clasped them in the kuravai round-dance, O tender youth! While Tiru, the great Lakshmi, and the Earth-goddess stand seeking you, O girl-friend, what is this? What is this, what is this, alas, what is this?

986.translit:
allik kamalak kaNNanai angoraaychchi
ellip pozhuthoo diyavoodal thiRaththai,
kallin malithooL kaliyan sonna maalai,
sollith thuthippaa ravarthukka milaree.
986.meaning: This garland of verses, composed by Kaliyan of the strong rock-like shoulders, tells of the manner of the love-quarrel that a certain cowherd girl had at nightfall with the lotus-eyed Kannan. Those who recite and praise with these words shall be free from sorrow.

987.translit:
puLLuru vaaki naLLiruL vantha poothanai maaLa, ilangai
oLLeri maNdi yuNNap paNiththa ookka mathanai ninanthoo,
kaLLavizh koothai kaathalu mengaL kaarikai maathar karuththum,
piLLaithan kaiyil kiNNamee yokkap peesuva thenthai piraanee.
987.meaning: Recalling, perhaps, your might in causing Putana, who came in the dead of night in disguise, to perish, and in ordaining that bright fire spread and consumed Lanka — my Lord, my father, you speak of the love of this honey-dripping-garlanded one, the heart of our beautiful lady, as though it were a cup in a child's hand (to be taken at will).

988.translit:
mandril malinthu kooththuvan thaadi maalvidai yeezhuma darththu, aayar
andru nadunga aaniraikaaththa aaNmaiko looaRi yeen naan,
nindra piraanee neeLkadal vaNNaa. neeyivaL thannai nin kooyil,
mundri lezhuntha murungaiyil theenaa munkai vaLaikavarn thaayee.
988.meaning: Whether it was the manliness with which you came to the common-ground and danced abounding in dance, subdued the seven fierce bulls, and guarded the cattle-herd while the cowherds trembled that day, I do not know. O Lord who stands firm, O hued like the vast ocean! Like honey on the murungai tree that grew in the front-yard of your temple, you have seized away the bangles from this girl's forearm.

989.translit:
aarmali yaazhi sangodu patri aatralai yaatral mikuththuk,
kaarmukil vaNNaa. kansanai munnam kadanthanin kadunthiRal thaanoo,
neerizhai maathai niththilath thoththai nedungadal amuthanai yaaLai,
aarezhil vaNNaa angaiyil vattaam ivaLenak karuthukin Raayee.
989.meaning: Grasping the resounding discus together with the conch and increasing strength upon strength — O cloud-hued one! — is it that very fierce valor of yours, by which you destroyed Kamsa of old, (that you show now)? O one of supreme beauteous hue! This lady of fine ornaments, like a cluster of pearls, like the nectar of the vast sea, you reckon to be a mere round-toy (vattu) in your palm.

990.translit:
malkiya thooLum maanuri yathaLum udaiyavar thamakkumoor paakam,
nalkiya nalamoo? narakanai tholaiththa karathalath thamaithiyin karuththoo?
alliyang koothai yaNiniRam koNdu vanthumun neenindru pookaay,
solliyen nambee ivaLainee yungaL thoNdarkaith thaNdendra vaaree.
990.meaning: Is it the goodness with which you granted a share even to him (Shiva) of swelling shoulders who wears the deerskin garment? Or is it the disposition of the calm of that hand which destroyed Naraka? Taking on the lovely hue of this one with the lily-garland, come and stand before me and then go. What is there to say, my Lord? How is it that you have made this girl into a staff in the hand of your devotees?

991.translit:
seruvazhi yaatha mannarkaL maaLath theervalang koNdavar sellum,
aruvazhi vaanam atharpadak kaNda aaNmaiko looaRi yeennaan,
thirumozhi yengaL theemalark koothai seermaiyai ninanthilai yanthoo,
peruvazhi naaval kaniyinu meLiyaL ivaLenap peesukin Raayee.
991.meaning: Whether it was the manliness by which you saw the kings who knew not the way of war perish, drove your chariot in triumph, and made the high path of the heavens a roadway for them — I do not know. O of sacred speech! You have not considered the excellence of our girl of the honeyed-flower garland — alas! You speak as though this girl were more easily had than a jambu fruit on the great roadside.

992.translit:
arakkiya raakam pullena villaal aNimathi LilangaiyarkoNai,
serukkazhith thamarar paNiyamun nindra seevaka moocheytha thindru
murukkithazh vaaychchi munkaiveN sangam koNdumun neenindru pookaay,
erukkilaik kaaka eRimazhu voochchal enseyva thenthai piraanee.
992.meaning: Was it heroism, the thing you did — standing forth so that the bodies of the demons withered and the king of beautiful-walled Lanka was humbled and the celestials bowed in homage? Take the white bangle-conch from the forearm of this girl of murukku-blossom lips, and stand before me and go. To brandish a hurled axe at an erukku leaf — what good does it do, my father, my Lord?

993.translit:
aazhiyan thiNdeer arasarvan thiRainja alaikadal ulakammun aaNda,
paazhiyan thooloo raayiram veezhap padaimazhup patriya valiyoo?,
maazhaimen nookki maNiniRang koNdu vanthumun neenindru pookaay,
koozhiveN muttaik kenseyva thenthaay kuRunthadi nedungadal vaNNaa.
993.meaning: Is it the strength with which you grasped the battle-axe weapon so that a thousand mighty shoulders fell — those of him who once ruled the world girt by the surging sea while kings came in their strong wheeled chariots to pay tribute? Taking on the gem-like hue of this tender, soft-eyed girl, come and stand before me and go. O my father of stout limbs, O hued like the vast ocean, what is to be done over a hen's white egg (so small a matter)?

994.translit:
porunthalan aakam puLLuvan theeRa vaLLuki raalpiLanthu, andru
perunthakaik kirangi vaaliyai muninTha perumaiko loocheytha thindru,
perunthadang kaNNi surumpuRu koothai perumaiyai ninanthilai peesil,
karungadal vaNNaa kavuLkoNda neeraam ivaLenak karuthukin Raayee.
994.meaning: You rent the chest of the enemy (Hiranya) with sharp claws as the man-lion mounted upon him; and that day, taking pity on the noble one (Sugriva), you raged against Vali — was it that greatness which was the deed you did? O dark-ocean-hued one! You have not considered the greatness of this large-wide-eyed girl of the bee-swarming garland. To speak of it, you reckon her to be like water held in your cheek (to be spat out at will).

995.translit:
neerazhal vaanaay nedunilang kaalaay nindranin neermaiyai ninanthoo?,
seerkkezhu koothai yennala thilaLen Randanoor theetranmai thaanoo?,
paarkkezhu pavvath thaaramu thanaiya paavaiyaip paavamsey theenukku,
aarazha loompum anthaNan thootta maakanin manaththuvaith thaayee.
995.meaning: Is it remembering your nature as you stood — being water, fire, sky, the vast earth, and wind? Or is it some sureness of yours, namely that this girl rich in excellence has no recourse but you? This maiden, like the nectar that rose from the sea that surrounds the earth, you have set in your mind as if she were a brahmin's grove tended with raging fire (a thing fenced off and forbidden) — and this to me, a sinner.

996.translit:
veettaththaik karuthaa thadiyiNai vaNangi meymmainin Rempperu maanai,
vaattiRal thaanai mangaiyar thalaivan maanavel kaliyanvaa yoligaL,
thoottalar painthaarch chudarmudi yaanaip pazhamozhi yaalpaNin thuraiththa,
paattivai paadap paththimai perukich chiththamum thiruvoodu mikumee veNduRai.
996.meaning: These are the songs from the mouth of Kaliyan, lord of the Mangai people, of the destructive army and the great spear, who — not minding the hunt (of worldly aims) — worshipped at the twin feet and bowed in truth to our great Lord, addressing in the manner of olden proverbs the radiant-crowned One of fresh blossoming garland. Of Vendurai. As these songs are sung, devotion will swell and the heart will increase together with prosperity (Tiru).

997.translit:
thiruththaay sempooththee, thirumaamakaL thankaNavan,
maruththaartholpukazh maathava naivarath thiruththaay sempooththee.
997.meaning: Set things right, O red sempothu bird! That he may come — the husband of the great Lakshmi, Madhava of ancient fragrant glory — set things right, O red sempothu bird.

998.translit:
karaiyaay kaakkaippiLLaay, karumaamukil poolniRaththan,
uraiyaar tholpuka zhuththama naivarak, karaiyaay kaakkaippiLLaay.
998.meaning: Call out, O young crow! That he may come — the supreme one of dark great-cloud hue, of ancient renowned glory beyond words — call out, O young crow.

999.translit:
koovaay poonguyilee, kuLirmaari thaduththukantha,
maavaay keeNda maNivaNNa naivarak, koovaay poonguyilee.
999.meaning: Sing out, O lovely cuckoo! That he may come — the gem-hued one who joyfully held back the cold rains and who split open the horse-demon (Kesi) — sing out, O lovely cuckoo.

0.translit:
nidhiyinaip pavalath thooNai neRimaiyaal ninaiya vallaar,
kadhiyinaik kanjan maaLak kaNdumun aaNda maaLum,
madhiyinai maalai vaazhththi vaNangiyen manaththu vandha,
vidhiyinaik kaNdu koNda thoNdanEn vidugi lEnE!
0.meaning: He is the treasure, the pillar of coral; to those who can meditate on him rightly he is the way and the goal. He destroyed Kamsa and of old ruled as my king. Having praised and worshipped Tirumal, the very moon of wisdom, and beholding him who came into my heart as my destiny, I, his servant, will never let him go.

1.translit:
kaaRRinaip punalaith theeyaik kadimadhi Lilangkai seRRa
ERRinai, imayam mEya ezhilmaNith thiraLai, inba
aaRRinai amudhan thannai avuNanaa ruyirai yuNda
kooRRinai, guNangoN duLLam kooRunee kooRu maaRE.
1.meaning: He is the wind, the water, the fire; he is the bull who crushed Lanka of strong fortress-walls. He is the lovely heap of gems abiding on the Himalaya, the river of bliss, the very nectar, and Death itself that devoured the life of the demon. O my heart, take his qualities and sing them, sing of him as best you can.

2.translit:
paayirum paravai thannuL paruvarai thiriththu, vaanORk
kaayirun thamudhang koNda appanai empi raanai,
vEyirunjOlai soozhndhu virigadhi ririya ninRa,
maayirunjOlai mEya maindhanai vaNangi nEnE.
2.meaning: He churned the great mountain within the wide spreading ocean and obtained the nectar for the celestials, my Father and Lord. I have worshipped the young hero who abides at Maaliruncholai, surrounded by tall groves of bamboo where the spreading rays of the sun are driven away.

3.translit:
kEtkayaa nuRRa thuNdu kEzhalaa yulagang koNda,
pookkezhu vaNNa naaraip pOdharak kanavil kaNdu,
vaakkinaal karuman thannaal manaththinaal siraththai thannaal,
vEtkaimee thoora vaangi vizhungineRk kiniya laaRE!
3.meaning: I have something to tell you that I have heard. Seeing in a dream the One of flower-hued beauty who became the boar and lifted up the earth, with word, with deed, with mind and with devotion, my longing overflowed and I drew him in and swallowed him. How sweet it is for me!

4.translit:
irumbananR uNda neerpOl emperu maanukku, enRan
arumbeRa lanbu pukkittadimaipooN duyndhu pOnEn,
varumbuyal vaNNa naarai maruviyen manaththu vaiththu,
karumbinin saaRu pOlap parugineRk kiniya vaaRE.
4.meaning: Like water once drunk by red-hot iron, my rare-won love entered into my Lord, and taking up his service I was saved. Embracing him who is hued like the gathering rain-cloud and setting him in my heart, I drank of him as of the sweet juice of sugarcane. How sweet it is for me!

5.translit:
moovaril mudhalva naaya oruvanai yulagang koNda,
kOvinaik kudandhai mEya gurumaNith thiraLai, inbap
paavinaip pachchaith thEnaip paimponnai yamarar sennip
poovinai, pugazhum thoNdar enjollip pugazhvar thaamE?
5.meaning: He is the foremost one among the three, the king who measured the worlds, the heap of precious gems abiding at Kudandhai. He is the song of bliss, the fresh honey, the pure gold, the flower upon the heads of the immortals. With what words can his devotees who praise him utter his praise?

6.translit:
immaiyai maRumai thannai emakkuvee daaki ninRa,
meymmaiyai virindha sOlai viyandhiru varangam mEya,
semmaiyaik karumai thannaith thirumalai orumai yaanai,
thanmaiyai ninaivaa renRan thalaimisai mannu vaarE.
6.meaning: He is this life and the life beyond, the Truth who stands as our deliverance. He abides at the wondrous, holy Arangam set amid spreading groves; he is the red and the dark, the unique Lord of Tirumalai. Those who meditate upon his nature shall ever abide upon my head.

7.translit:
vaanidaip puyalai maalai varaiyidaip prasam eenRa,
thEnidaik karumbin saaRRaith thiruvinai maruvi vaazhaar,
maanidap piRavi yandhO madhikkilar koLka, thandham
oonidaik kurambai vaazhkkaik kuRudhiyE vENdi naarE.
7.meaning: They do not embrace and live with Tirumal, who is the rain-cloud in the sky, the honey born among the mountains, the juice of sugarcane in that honey, the consort of Lakshmi. Alas, they do not even value their human birth, but seek instead the gain of this life lived in their hut of flesh.

8.translit:
uLLamO onRil nillaadhu OsaiyiL nerinin RuNNum
koLLimE leRumbu pOlak kuzhaiyumaal enRa nuLLam,
theLLiyeer dhEvark kellaam dhEvaraa yulagam koNda
oLLiyeer, ummai yallaal ezhumaiyum thuNaiyi lOmE.
8.meaning: My heart will not rest in any one thing; it melts away like an ant upon a firebrand that burns and is consumed in its crackling. O Clear One, O Radiant One who became God of all the gods and measured the worlds, except for you I have no refuge in any of the seven births.

9.translit:
siththamum sevvai nillaa denj seykEn theevi naiyEn,
paththimaik kanbu daiyEnaavadhE paNiyaa yandhaay,
muththoLi maradha gammE muzhangoLi mugilvaN Naa,en
aththa ninnadimai yallaal yaadhumon RaRiki lEnE.
9.meaning: My mind too will not stay upright; what shall I do, I of evil deeds? Command me, O Father, that I may become one who has love and devotion for you. O you of pearl-like radiance, O emerald, O cloud-hued one of resounding glory, my Lord, apart from service to you I know nothing at all.

10.translit:
thoNdellaam paravi ninnaith thozhudhadi paNiyu maaRu
kaNdu, thaan kavalai theerppaan aavadhE paNiyaa yendhaay,
aNdamaa yeNdi saikkum aadhiyaay needhi yaana,
paNdamaam parama sOdhi ninnaiyE paravu vEnE.
10.meaning: Seeing how all your devotees praise you and worship and bow at your feet, command me, O Father, that I too may become one who has his sorrows ended. O Supreme Light who are the cosmos, the source of the eight directions, the very substance of justice, it is you alone that I will praise.

11.translit:
aaviyai aranga maalai azhukkuram pechchil vaayaal,
thooymaiyil thoNda nEnnaan sollinEn thollai naamam,
paaviyEn pizhaththa vaaREn RanjineRk kanjal enRu
kaavipOl vaNNar vandhen kaNNuLE thOnRinaarE.
11.meaning: With a mouth defiled by the impure body, I, an impure servant, uttered the ancient name of Tirumal of Arangam, the soul of all. When I, a sinner, feared, asking 'what wrong have I done?', he of the hue of the kaavi flower came and said 'Fear not', and appeared within my eyes.

12.translit:
irumbananR uNda neerum pOdharum koLka, enRan
arumbiNi paava mellaam akanRana ennai vittu,
surumbamar sOlai soozhndha arangamaa kOyil koNda,
karumbinaik kaNdu koNden kaNNiNai kaLikku maaRE.
12.meaning: Even water drunk by red-hot iron may come forth again, take it so; for all my grievous diseases and sins have departed and left me. Beholding the sweet sugarcane who has taken as his temple great Arangam, surrounded by groves where bees abide, how my two eyes rejoice!

13.translit:
kaaviyai venRa kaNNaar kalaviyE karudhi, naaLum
paaviyE naaga veNNi adhanuLLE pazhuththo zhindhEn,
thoovisEr annam mannum soozhpunal kudandhai yaanai,
paaviyen paavi yaadhu paaviyE naayi nEnE.
13.meaning: Daily desiring only union with women whose eyes vanquish the kaavi flower, I thought of myself as a sinner and rotted away within that desire. Without contemplating the Lord of Kudandhai surrounded by waters where graceful swans abide, I, a sinner, have become a wretched sinner indeed.

14.translit:
munbolaa iraava NanRan mudhumadhi Lilangkai vEviththu,
anbinaal anuman vandhaang kadiyiNai paNiya ninRaarkku,
enbelaa murugi yukkit tennudai nenja mennum,
anbinaal njaana neerkoN daattuva nadiya nEnE.
14.meaning: To him who burned the old-walled Lanka of Ravana who had never bowed before, and stood while Hanuman came in love and worshipped at his feet, I, his servant, with my bones all melting and dissolving, will bathe him with the water of wisdom drawn through the love that is my very heart.

15.translit:
maayamaan maayach cheRRu marudhiRa nadandhu, vaiyam
thaayamaa paravai pongath thadavarai thiriththu, vaanORk
keeyumaal empi raanaark kennudaich choRka Lennum,
thooyamaa maalai koNdu soottuvan thoNda nEnE!
15.meaning: He slew the illusory deer, walked between the marudu trees and broke them, and measured the earth; he churned the great mountain so the wide ocean surged, and gave the nectar to the celestials, my Lord. To him I, his servant, will offer my words as a pure great garland and crown him with it.

16.translit:
pEsinaar piRavi neeththaar pEruLaan perumai pEsi,
Esinaar uyndhu pOnaar enbadhiv vulagin vaNNam,
pEsinEn Esa maattEn pEdhaiyEn piRavi neeththaRku,
aasaiyO peridhu koLka alaikadal vaNNar paalE.
16.meaning: Those who spoke of him crossed beyond birth; even those who reviled him, having uttered the greatness of him whose name endures, were saved and went on, this is the way of this world. I have spoken of him; I cannot revile him. Foolish as I am, take it that my longing to be freed from birth is very great toward him of the surging-ocean hue.

17.translit:
iLaippinai yiyakkam neekki yirundhumun nimaiyaik kootti,
aLappilaim pulana dakki anbavar kaNNE vaiththu,
thuLakkamil sindhai seydhu thOnRalum sudarvittu, aangE
viLakkinai vidhiyil kaaNbaar meymmaiyE kaaNgiR paarE!
17.meaning: Removing weariness and the body's restless motion, sitting still and joining the eyelids together, subduing the boundless five senses, fixing love upon him alone, and making the mind unwavering, when he appears shining with radiance there, those who behold that Lamp by the right way are the ones who truly see the Truth.

18.translit:
piNdiyaar maNdai Endhip piRarmanai thiridhan thuNNum,
uNdiyaan saabam theerththa oruvanoor, ulaga mEththum
kaNdiyoor arangam meyyam kachchipEr mallai enRu
maNdinaar, uyyal allaal maRRaiyaark kuyya laamE?
18.meaning: He alone is the Lord who removed the curse of the one who, holding a skull-bowl, wandered through others' houses begging his food. Those who throng to his towns that the world praises, saying 'Kandiyur, Arangam, Meyyam, Kachchi, Per, Mallai,' shall surely be saved; can salvation be possible for any others?

19.translit:
vaanavar thangaL kOnum malarmisai ayanum, naaLum
thEmalar thoovi Eththum sEvadich chengaN maalai,
maanavEl kaliyan sonna vaNdamizh maalai naalaindhum,
oonamadhin Ri vallaar oLivisum paaLvar thaamE.
19.meaning: Tirumal of red eyes, at whose lovely red feet the king of the celestials and Brahma upon the lotus daily scatter honeyed flowers and worship, is praised by Kaliyan of the great spear. Those who can master without fault these twenty verses of the rich Tamil garland he sang shall rule the realm of radiant heaven.

0.translit:
minnuruvaay munnuruvil vEdam naankaay
 viLakkoLiyaay muLaitthezhuntha thingaL thaanaay,
pinnuruvaay munnuruvil piNimoop pillaap
 pirappiliyaay irappatharkE eNNaadhu, eNNum
ponnuruvaay maNiyuruvil pootham ainthaayp
 punaluruvaay analuruvil thigazhum sOdhi,
thannuruvaay ennuruvil ninRa enthai
 thaLirppuraiyum thiruvadi yenthalai mElavE.
0.meaning: He who is the form of lightning, who in His earlier form became the four Vedas, who is the radiance of the lamp and the moon that sprouts and rises; who in His final and former forms is birthless, free of disease and old age, never thinking of death; He of golden form and of sapphire form, who is the five elements, the form of water and the brilliant light shining as fire — He, my Father, stood in my own form. His sacred feet, soft as tender shoots, are upon my head.

1.translit:
paaruruvil neererikaal visumbu maagip
 palvERu samayamumaayp paranthu ninRa,
Eruruvil moovarumE yenna ninRa,
 imaiyavarthan thiruvuruvE ReNNum pOdhu,
Oruruvam ponnuruvam onRu sentheee
 onRumaa kadaluruvam oththu ninRa,
moovuruvum kaNdapO thonRaam sOdhi
 mugiluruvam emmadigaL uruvan thaanE.
1.meaning: He who became the earth, water, fire, wind and sky, and spread out as the many different faiths; who stood as the beautiful three (the Trinity) — when one examines the divine forms of these celestials, one form is of gold, one of red fire, and one is like the great ocean. When the three forms are seen, they become one radiance — and that cloud-hued form is the very form of our Lord.

2.translit:
thiruvadivil karunedumaal sEyan enRum
 thirEthaikkaN vaLaiyuruvaayth thigazhnthaa nenRum,
poruvadivil kadalamutham koNda kaalam
 perumaanaik karuneela vaNNan Ranai,
oruvadivath thOruruven RuNara laagaa
 oozhithO Roozhinin REtthal allaal,
karuvadivil sengaNNa vaNNan Ranaik
 katturaiyE yaaroruvar kaaNkiR paarE?
2.meaning: They say the dark, lofty Tirumal of beautiful form was distant; they say in the Treta age He shone as one bearing the conch-form (white hue). At the time He took the nectar of the churned ocean, that Lord of dark-blue color — He cannot be grasped as one single fixed form. Apart from praising Him standing through age after age, who indeed can fully describe and behold that One of dark form with red eyes?

3.translit:
indhiraRkum piramaRkum mudhalvan Ranai
 irunilamkaal theeneerviN pootham ainthaay,
senthiRattha thamizhOsai vadasol laagith
 thisainaangu maaytthing kaL njaayi Raagi,
antharatthil thEvarkkum aRiya laagaa
 anthaNanai anthaNarmaat tanthi vaittha
manthiratthai, manthiratthaal maRavaa thenRum
 vaazhuthiyEl vaazhalaam madanenjamE.
3.meaning: He who is the source for both Indra and Brahma, who became the five elements — the wide earth, wind, fire, water and sky; who became the sweet-toned Tamil and the northern (Sanskrit) speech, the four directions, the moon and the sun; the pure One (Brahmin) whom even the gods in the heavens cannot know, the sacred mantra that the Brahmins placed in the keeping of Brahmins. O foolish heart, if you live ever remembering Him through that mantra, never forgetting, then you may truly live.

4.translit:
oNmidhiyil punaluruvi orukaal niRpa
 orukaalung kaamarusee r avuNan uLLatthu,
eNmadhiyung kadandaNda meedhu pOgi
 iruvisumbi noodupO yezhunthu mElaith
 thaNmadhiyum kadhiravanum thavira Odith
 thaaragaiyin puRanthadavi appaal mikku,
maNmuzhuthum agappaduththu ninRa enthai
 malarpuraiyum thiruvadiyE vaNanginEnE.
4.meaning: He set one bright foot piercing the waters (of Mahabali's gift) and stood, while the other foot, beyond the thought of the mighty Asura's heart, transcended all the eight directions and passed above the cosmic egg; it rose up through both the heavens, ran past the cool moon and the sun, grazed beyond the stars and surpassed even that — my Father who stood encompassing the whole earth. To His sacred feet, resembling flowers, I have bowed down.

5.translit:
alampurintha nedunthadakkai amarar vEnthan
 anjiRaippuL thanippaagan avuNark kenRum,
salampurinthang karuLillaath thanmai yaaLan
 thaanugantha voorellaam thanthaaL paadi,
nilamparanthu varumkaluzhip peNNai yeertha
 neduvEygaL padumuttha mundha vunthi,
pulamparantha ponviLaikkum poygai vElip
 poongOva loorthozhuthum pOdhu nenjE.
5.meaning: He of the long, broad hands that bestow grace, the king of the celestials, the unique rider of the beautiful-winged bird (Garuda), who is ever without mercy and full of wrath toward the Asuras — singing of His feet and of all the towns He cherishes, let us go, O heart, worshipping at beautiful Tirukkovalur, hedged by ponds that yield gold spread over the fields, where the swelling Pennai river that flows over the spreading land carries forth the pearls born in the tall bamboos.

6.translit:
vaRpudaiya varainedunthOL mannar maaLa
 vadivaaya mazhuvEnthi yulaga maaNdu,
veRpudaiya nedungadaluL thanivE luytha
 vELmudhalaa venRaanoor vintham mEya,
kaRpudaiya madakkanni kaaval pooNda
 kadipozhilsoozh nedumaRugil kamala vEli,
poRpudaiya malaiyaraiyan paNiya ninRa
 poongOva loorthozhuthum pOdhu nenjE.
6.meaning: Wielding the well-shaped axe so that the kings with strong mountain-like long shoulders perished, He ruled the world; and where He who conquered Murugan and others — the one who hurled the unique spear into the great mountain-bearing ocean — dwells at Vindam: there, guarded by the chaste, gentle maiden, surrounded by fragrant groves and lotus hedges along long streets, where the splendid lord of the mountains bows before Him — let us go, O heart, worshipping at beautiful Tirukkovalur.

7.translit:
neeragatthaay neduvaraiyi nucchi mElaay
 nilaaththingaL thuNdagatthaay! niRaintha kacchi
ooragatthaay, oNthurainee r vehkaa vuLLaay
 uLLuvaa ruLLatthaay, ulaga mEtthum
kaaragatthaay kaarvaanath thuLLaay kaLvaa
 kaamarupoong kaaviriyin thenpaal mannu
pEragatthaay, pEraadhen nenji nuLLaay
 perumaanun thiruvadiyE pENinEnE.
7.meaning: You who are in Tiruneeragam, You who are atop the tall hill (Tiruvenkatam), You in Tirunilaattingaltundam! You in Tiru-Ooragam in full Kanchi, You within the bright-watered Tiruvehka, You in the hearts of those who contemplate You, You in Tirukkaragam praised by the world, You within the dark sky, O thief! You who abide in Tipper (Tirupper) on the southern bank of the lovely Kaveri, You who are within my heart never to depart — O Lord, it is Your sacred feet alone that I have cherished.

8.translit:
vangatthaal maamaNivan thunthu munneer
 mallaiyaaymadhiLkacchi yooraay! pEraay,
kongatthaar vaLangonRai yalangal maarvan
 kulavaraiyan madappaavai yidappaal koNdaan,
pangatthaay paaRkadalaay paarin mElaay
 panivaraiyi nucchiyaay pavaLa vaNNaa,
enguRRaay emperumaan unnai naadi
 EzhaiyEn inganamE oozhitharugEnE.
8.meaning: You who are at Tirukkadalmallai where the ships bring and heap great gems on the seashore, You of Kanchi with its walls, You of Tirupper! You who shared Your form with Him on whose chest hangs the garland of honeyed Konrai blossoms and who took the gentle maiden, daughter of the mountain-king, on His left — You who are upon the milk-ocean, You above the earth, You atop the snowy mountain, O coral-hued One! Where are You? O my Lord, seeking You, wretched I pass age after age here in this very manner.

9.translit:
ponnaanaay. pozhilEzhum kaaval pooNda
 pugazhaanaay igazhvaaya thoNda nEnnaan,
ennaanaay? ennaanaay? ennal allaal
 ennaRiva nEzhaiyEn, ulaga mEtthum
thennaanaay vadavaanaay kudapaa laanaay
 kuNapaala thaayinaay imaiyOrk kenRum
munnaanaay pinnaanaar vaNangum sOdhi
 thirumoozhik kaLatthaanaay mudhalaanaayE.
9.meaning: You who became gold, You of renown who took upon Yourself the guarding of the seven worlds — lowly devotee that I am, fit to be scorned, what can I do but cry 'What has become of You? What has become of You?' What do I, a wretch, know? You who are in the south and in the north, in the west and in the east, praised by the world; You who are ever the first before the celestials, the radiance worshipped by all who came after — You who are in Tirumoozhikkalam, You who are the Primordial One.

10.translit:
pattudukkum ayarndhirangum paavai pENaaL
 paninedungaN Neertthathumbap paLLi koLLaaL,
ettanaippO thengudangaal irukka killaaL
 emperumaan thiruvaranga mengE? ennum
mattuvikki maNivaNdu muralum koonthal
 madamaanai ithusaithaar thammai, meyyE
kattuvicchi sol, ennach sonnaaL nangaay
 kadalvaNNa rithusaithaar kaappaa raarE?
10.meaning: She wears the silk and dons it (distractedly), she grows weary and grieves, she cares not for her dolls; tears brimming in her long cool eyes, she does not sleep. Not for the briefest moment will she stay seated on my lap; 'Where is my Lord's Srirangam?' she asks. This doe-like girl with tresses where honey-drunk gem-like bees hum — 'O lady, tell me truly,' I said to the soothsayer, 'who did this to her?' She replied: 'The ocean-hued One has done this — who then can protect her?'

11.translit:
nenjurugik kaNpanippa niRkum sOrum
 nedithuyirkkum uNdaRiyaaL uRakkam pENaaL,
nanjaravil thuyilamarntha nambee ennum
 vambaarpoom vayalaali mainthaa ennum,
anjiRaiya putkodiyE yaadum paadum
 aNiyaranga maaduthumO thOzhee ennum,
enjiRa gin keezhadangaap peNNaip peRREn
 irunilatthu OrpazhipadaiththEn E paavamE.
11.meaning: Her heart melting, eyes brimming, she stands and swoons and sighs long; she knows not food and cares not for sleep. 'O perfect One who reclines on the venom-spitting serpent!' she says; 'O youth of fragrant, flowering Vayalali (Tirumangai)!' she says; 'O You whose banner bears the bird of beautiful wings!' — she dances and sings, 'Shall we go bathe in lovely Srirangam, O friend?' she says. I have got a daughter who will not stay contained beneath my wing; upon this wide earth I have earned reproach — alas, what a sin!

12.translit:
kalleduththuk kalmaari kaaththaay enRum
 kaamarupoong kacchiyoo ragatthaay! enRum,
villiRuththu melliyal thOL thOynthaay enRum
 vehkaavil thuyilama rndha vEndhE. enRum,
alladarththu mallaraiyan Rat taay enRum,
 maakeeNda katthalatthen mainthaa enRum,
solleduththuth thangiLiyaich sollE enRu
 thuNaimulaimEl thuLisOrach sOrkkin RaaLE.
12.meaning: 'O You who lifted the hill and warded off the rain of stones (Govardhana)!' she says; 'O You of beautiful Tiru-Ooragam in lovely Kanchi!' she says; 'O You who broke the bow and embraced the shoulders of the tender-natured one (Sita)!' she says; 'O King who slumbers at Tiruvehka!' she says; 'O You who crushing the darkness slew the king of the wrestlers (Kamsa)!' she says; 'O my youth whose palm tore open the demon (Hiranya)!' she says. Bidding her parrot, 'Speak, say it!' she swoons, while drops fall upon her twin breasts.

13.translit:
muLaikkadhiraik kuRunguduyuL mugilai moovaa
 moovulagum kadandhappaal mudhalaay ninRa,
aLapparriya aaramu thai arangam mEya
 anthaNanai anthaNartham sinthai yaanai,
viLakkoLiyai maragathatthaith thiruththaN kaavil
 vehkaavil thirumaalaip paadak kEttu
vaLarththathanaal payanpeRREn varuga enRu
 madakkiLiyaik kaikooppi vangi naaLE.
13.meaning: The sprouting ray of light, the cloud in Tirukkurungudi, the unaging First Cause who stood beyond the three transcendent worlds, the immeasurable rare nectar, the pure One who dwells at Srirangam, He who is in the minds of the Brahmins, the radiance of the lamp, the emerald in Tiruttankaa — hearing (her parrot) sing of Tirumal in Tiruvehka, she said, 'I have reaped the fruit of having raised you — come!' and folding her hands she beckoned the gentle parrot.

14.translit:
kalluyarntha nedumadhiLsoozh kacchi mEya
 kaLiRenRum kadalkidantha kaniyE enRum,
alliyampoo malarppoygaip pazhana vEli
 aNiyazhunthoor ninRugantha ammaan enRum,
solluyarntha neduveeNai mulaimEl thaangith
 thoomuRuval nagaiyiRaiyE thOnRa nakku,
melviralgaL sivappeythath thadavi yaangE
 menkiLipOl migamizhaRRum enpEthaiyE.
14.meaning: 'O elephant who dwells in Kanchi girt by tall stone walls!' she says; 'O fruit that reclines upon the sea!' she says; 'O Lord who gladly stood in lovely Tiruvazhundur, hedged by fields and lotus-blossomed ponds!' she says — holding the lofty-toned, long veena upon her breast, with a faint pure smile just dawning on her lips, she laughs; and strumming until her soft fingers turn red, my innocent girl warbles on and on like a tender parrot.

15.translit:
kanRumEyth thinithugantha kaaLaay enRum,
 kadipozhilsoozh kaNapuratthen kaniyE enRum,
manRamarak kooththaadi magizhnthaay enRum,
 vadathiruvEng kadammEya mainthaa enRum,
venRasurar kulangaLaintha vEnthE enRum,
 viripozhilsoozh thirunaRaiyoor ninRaay enRum,
thunRukuzhal karuniRatthen thuNaiyE enRum
 thuNaimulaimEl thuLisOrach sOrkkin RaaLE.
15.meaning: 'O youth who grazed the calves and rejoiced!' she says; 'O my fruit of Tirukkannapuram girt with fragrant groves!' she says; 'O You who danced the pot-dance in the public square and were glad!' she says; 'O youth who dwells at northern Tiruvenkatam!' she says; 'O King who destroyed the clans of the conquered Asuras!' she says; 'O You who stand at Tirunaraiyur encircled by spreading groves!' she says; 'O dark-hued companion of mine with thick clustering locks!' she says — and she swoons, while drops fall upon her twin breasts.

16.translit:
pongaarmel liLangongai ponnE poopap
 porukayalkaN Neerarumbap pOnthu ninRu
sengaala madappuRavam pedaikkup pEsum
 siRukuraluk kudalurugich sinthiththu, aangE
thaNkaalum thaNkudanthai nagarum paadith
 thaNkOva loorppaadi yaadak kEttu,
nangaay nang kudikkithuvO nanmai? enna
 naRaiyoorum paaduvaaL navilkin RaaLE.
16.meaning: Her swelling soft young breasts turning pale as gold, tears budding in her fighting-carp eyes, she comes forth and stands; her insides melting at the little cooing call that the red-legged gentle dove makes to its mate, she broods. And there, hearing (someone) sing of Tankaal and cool Tirukkudandai town and dance singing of cool Tirukkovalur — when I asked, 'O girl, is this what befits our family's honor?' — she goes on, now singing also of Tirunaraiyur.

17.translit:
kaarvaNNam thirumEni kaNNum vaayum
 kaiththalamum adiyiNaiyum kamala vaNNam,
paarvaNNa madamangai piththar piththar
 panimalarmEl paavaikkup paavam saithEn,
ErvaNNa enpEthai enjol kELaaL
 emperumaan thiruvaranga mengE? ennum,
neervaNNan neermalaikkE pOvEn ennum
 ithuvanRO niRaiyazhinthaar niRku maaRE?
17.meaning: His sacred body is of cloud-color; His eyes, mouth, palms and pair of feet are lotus-hued. He is mad, mad with love for the gentle earth-hued maiden (Bhudevi) and for the Lady upon the cool blossom (Lakshmi) — and I have sinned against my own daughter. My lovely-formed innocent girl heeds none of my words; 'Where is my Lord's Srirangam?' she asks; 'I will go to Tiruneermalai of the water-hued One,' she says. Is this not how those who have lost their modesty behave?

18.translit:
muRRaaraa vanamulaiyaaL paavai maayan
 moyyagalath thuLLiruppaaL ahthum kaNdum
aRRaaL,than niRaiyazhinthaaL aavik kinRaaL
 aNiyaranga maaduthumO thOzhee ennum,
peRREnvaaych solliRaiyum pEsak kELaaL
 pErppaadith thaNkudanthai nagaram paadi,
poRRaama raikkayamnee raadap pOnaaL
 poruvaRRaa Lenmagalum ponnum ahthE.
18.meaning: Though she sees that the Maiden (Lakshmi) of full, lovely young breasts dwells within the broad chest of the wondrous Lord, my doll-like girl is undone, her modesty lost, her very life withering away; 'Shall we go bathe in lovely Srirangam, O friend?' she says. Though I, who bore her, plead, she heeds not a word; singing of Tirupper and cool Tirukkudandai town, she has gone off to bathe in the golden-lotus pool. Beyond compare is my daughter — and beyond compare too is that Golden One (the Lord) of hers.

19.translit:
thOraaLum vaaLarakkan selvam maaLath
 thennilangai munmalangach senthee olgi,
pEraaLa naayiram vaaNan maaLap
 porukadalai yaraNkadanthu pukku mikka
paaraaLan, paaridanthu paarai yuNdu
 paarumizhnthu paaraLanthu paarai yaaNda
pEraaLan, pErOthum peNNai maNmEl
 perunthavatthaL enRallaal pEsa laamE?
19.meaning: So that the wealth of the sword-wielding demon perished and southern Lanka was first thrown into disarray, He flung the red fire; that the thousand-armed Bana of great might might perish, He crossed the fortress of the battling sea, entered and prevailed — Ruler of the earth, who dug up the earth, swallowed the earth, spat out the earth, measured the earth and ruled the earth — that great Ruler. The girl who chants His name — can one speak of her except as one of great penance upon this earth?

20.translit:
maivaNNa naRunkuNYchi kuzhalpin thaazha
 makaramsEr kuzhaiyirupaa dilangki yaada,
eyvaNNa veNYchilaiyE thuNaiyaa ingkE
 iruvaraay vandhaaren munnE ninRaar
kaivaNNam thaamaraivaay kamalam pOlum
 kaNNiNaiyum aravindham adiyum aqdhE,
avvaNNath thavarnilaimai kaNdum thOzhee
 avarainaam dhEvaren RaNYchi nOmE.
20.meaning: With his dark, fragrant lock of hair hanging down his back, the makara-shaped earrings on his two sides glittering and swaying, with only a fierce beautiful bow as his companion, two of them came and stood here before me. The hue of his hands was like the red lotus, his mouth too like a lotus, his pair of eyes like the lotus, and his feet the same. O friend, even seeing the manner of one of such beauty, we, taking them to be gods, were afraid.

21.translit:
naivaLamon Raaraayaa nammai nOkkaa
 naaNinaar pOliRaiyE nayangkaL pinnum,
seyvaLavi lenmanamum kaNNu mOdi
 emperumaan thiruvadikkeezh aNaiya, ippaal
kaivaLaiyum mEkalaiyum kaaNEn kaNdEn
 kanamakarak kuzhaiyiraNdum naanku thOLum,
evvaLavuN demperumaan kOyil? enRERku
 idhuvanRO ezhilaali? enRaar thaamE.
21.meaning: Without examining at all the melody of my fainting, without even looking at me, as though abashed for a moment, with his charming graces, while my mind and eyes ran and clung beneath the sacred feet of my Lord, on this side I no longer found my bangles or my girdle. I beheld the two heavy makara earrings and his four shoulders. When I asked, 'How far away is my Lord's temple?' he himself said, 'Is this not beautiful Aali?'

22.translit:
uLLoorum sindhainOy enakkE thandhen
 oLivaLaiyum maaniRamum koNdaa ringkE,
theLLooru miLandhengkin thERal maandhich
 chElukaLum thiruvarangkam nammoo rennak
kaLLoorum paindhuzhaay maalai yaanaik
 kanavidaththil yaankaaNban kaNda pOdhu,
puLLoorum kaLvaanee pOkEl, enban
 enRaalu midhunamakkOr pulavi thaanE?
22.meaning: Giving to me alone the love-sickness that festers within, here they took away my bright bangles and my fine complexion, saying that Srirangam, where the carp leap drinking the nectar of the clear young coconut, is our town. That Lord with his fresh tulasi garland dripping with honey I shall see in my dream; and when I see him I shall say, 'O thief who rides the bird, do not go!' Yet even so, is this not a love-quarrel for us?

23.translit:
irukaiyilsang kivainillaa ellE paavam
 ilangkolineer perumpeLavam maNdi yuNda,
peruvayiRRa karumukilE yoppar vaNNam
 perundhavaththar arundhavaththu munivar soozha
orukaiyilsang korukaimaR Raazhi yEndhi
 ulakuNda peruvaaya ringkE vandhu,en
porukayalkaN Neerarumbap pulavi thandhu
 punalarangka moorenRu pOyinaarE.
23.meaning: The conches on my two hands will not stay on — alas, what a sin! His hue is like the dark cloud of vast belly that pressed in and swallowed up the great resounding bright-watered ocean. Surrounded by great ascetics and sages of rare penance, holding the conch in one hand and the discus in the other, the Lord of the great mouth who swallowed the worlds came here, gave me a love-quarrel so that tears welled in my fighting carp-like eyes, and saying his town is Srirangam of the waters, he went away.

24.translit:
minnilangku thiruvuruvum periya thOLum
 karimunindha kaiththalamum kaNNum vaayum,
thannalarndha naRundhuzhaay malarin keezhE
 thaazhndhilangkum makaramsEr kuzhaiyum kaatti
ennalanum enniRaiyum eNYchin dhaiyum
 envaLaiyum koNdennai yaaLung koNdu,
ponnalarndha naRuNYcherundhip pozhili noodE
 punalarangka moorenRu pOyinaarE.
24.meaning: Showing his sacred form that shone like lightning, his great shoulders, the palm of his hand that destroyed the elephant, his eyes and mouth, and the makara earrings hanging and gleaming beneath his fragrant blossomed tulasi flowers, he took away my well-being, my dignity, my remaining mind and my bangles, and taking me also as his own, through the grove of fragrant golden-blossomed serunthi trees, saying his town is Srirangam of the waters, he went away.

25.translit:
thEmaruvu pozhilidaththu malarndha pOdhaith
 thEnadhanai vaaymaduththun pedaiyum neeyum,
poomaruvi yini dhamarndhu poRiyi laarndha
 aRukaala siRuvaNdE thozhudhEn unnai,
aamaruvi niraimEyththa amarar kOmaan
 aNiyazhundhoor ninRaanuk kinRE senRu,
neemaruvi yaNYchaadhE ninROr maadhu
 ninnayandhaaL enRiRaiyE iyambik kaaNE.
25.meaning: O little six-legged bee covered with spots, drinking the honey of the flowers blossomed in the grove fragrant with sweetness, you and your mate dwelling happily upon the flowers — I worship you. Go this very day to the Lord of the celestials who tended the cows joined with the calves, who stands at beautiful Azhunthoor; draw near to him without fear and say to him just this: 'A certain maiden stood and longed for you.'

26.translit:
sengkaala madanaaraa yinRe senRu
 thirukkaNNapuram pukken sengkaNmaalukku
enkaadhalen thuNaivarkkuraiththi yaakil
 idhuvoppa demakkinba millai naaLum
paingkaana meedhellaa munadhEyaakap
 pazhanameen kavarndhuNNath tharuvan thandhaal
ingkE vandhini dhirundhun pedaiyum neeyum
 irunilaththilinidhenbameydhalaamE.
26.meaning: O tender red-legged crane, if you go this very day and enter sacred Kannapuram and tell of my love to my red-eyed Lord Tirumal, my companion, there will be no joy equal to this for us ever. I shall grant that the whole of the green expanse be yours alone, to seize and eat the fish of the fields; and if I grant it, you and your mate, coming and dwelling here happily, may attain sweet joy upon this wide earth.

27.translit:
thennilangkai yaraNsidhaRi avuNan maaLach
 chenRulaka moonRinaiyum thirindhOr thEraal,
mannilangku paaradhaththai maaLa voorndha
 varaiyuruvin maakaLiRRaith thOzhee, enRan
ponnilangku mulaikkuvattil poottik koNdu
 pOkaamai vallEnaayp pulavi yeydhi,
ennilangka mellaamvandh inba meydha
 eppozhudhum ninaindhuruki yiruppan naanE.
27.meaning: O friend! That great elephant in mountain form, who shattered the fortress of southern Lanka so that the demon perished, who went and ranged over all three worlds, who drove the chariot that destroyed the renowned Bharata battle — having fastened him upon the peaks of my golden gleaming breasts so that I am able to keep him from going away, and engaging in a love-quarrel, so that joy may come to all my limbs, I shall ever remain melting, thinking of him, I myself.

28.translit:
anRaayar kulamakaLuk karaiyan Rannai
 alaikadalaik kadaindhadaiththa ammaan Rannai,
kunRaadha valiyarakkar kOnai maaLak
 koduNYchilaivaaych charandhurandhu kulangka Laindhu
venRaanai, kunReduththa thOLi naanai
 viridhiraineer viNNakaram maruvi naaLum
ninRaanai, thaNkudandhaik kidandha maalai
 nediyaanai adinaayEn ninaindhit dEnE.
28.meaning: That Lord who long ago became the king to the cowherd-clan's daughter, who churned the surging ocean and dammed it; who, shooting arrows from his cruel bow so that the unfailingly strong demon-king perished, destroyed and conquered his clan; he of the shoulders that lifted the mountain; who dwells forever at Vinnagaram of the spreading-waved waters; Tirumal who reclines at cool Kudanthai, the tall one — I, his dog-like servant at his feet, have thought of him.

29.translit:
minnumaa mazhaidhavazhum mEka vaNNaa
 viNNavartham perumaanE aruLaay, enRu,
annamaay munivarO damara rEththa
 arumaRaiyai veLippaduththa ammaan Rannai,
mannumaa maNimaada vEndhan
 maanavEl parakaalan kaliyan sonna panniyanool
thamizhmaalai vallaar thollaip
 pazhavi naiyai mudhalariya vallar thaamE.
29.meaning: 'O cloud-hued one over whom the lightning and great rain creep, O Lord of the celestials, grant your grace!' — thus, when the sages and gods praised, the Lord who, becoming a swan, brought forth the rare Vedas; about him, Kaliyan, Parakalan of the spear, the king of the enduring great jewelled mansions, sang this elaborated work, a Tamil garland. Those who master it are themselves able to root out, from its very beginning, ancient old karma.

0.translit:
kaithaichEr poompozhil soozh kachchi nakar vandhudhittha
poikaippiraan kavinjar pOrERu vaiyaththu
adiyavarkaL vaazha arundhamizh nooRRandhaadhip
padiviLangach cheidhaan parindhu.
0.meaning: Poigai Pirān, the bull-like champion among poets, was born in the city of Kanchi which is surrounded by fragrant flower-groves where the fragrant screw-pine (kaithai) grows. So that the devotees of the world might flourish, he lovingly composed this Andhādhi of a hundred verses of rare Tamil, making it shine brightly upon the earth.

1.translit:
vaiyam thakaLiyaa vaarkadalE neyyaaka,
veyya kadhirOn viLakkaaka, - cheyya
sudaraazhi yaanadikkE soottinEnchol maalai,
idaraazhi neengukavE enRu.
1.meaning: Taking the earth as the lamp-vessel, the wide ocean as the ghee, and the hot-rayed sun as the flame, I have offered this garland of words at the feet of Him who bears the radiant red discus, so that the ocean of my sorrows may depart.

2.translit:
enRu kadalkadaindha thevvulakam neerERRadhu,
onRu madhanai yuNarEn naan, - anRu
thadaiththudaiththuk kaNpaduththa aazhi, idhunee
padaiththidan thuNdumizhndha paar.
2.meaning: When the ocean was churned, the time it (or that world) received water, that one matter I do not comprehend. This earth which you on that day created, scooped up, swallowed, and spat forth, is the very same ocean that you once dammed, broke open, and reclined upon.

3.translit:
paaraLavu mOradivaith thOradiyum paaruduththa,
neeraLavum chella nimirndhadhE - sooruruvil
pEyaLavu kaNda perumaan aRikilEn,
neeyaLavu kaNda neRi.
3.meaning: Placing one foot to measure the whole earth, the other foot stretched upward to the very limit of the waters that gird the earth. O Lord who measured out the demoness (Pūtanā) who came in a frightful form, I cannot fathom the path by which you measured all things.

4.translit:
neRivaasal thaanEyaai ninRaanai, aindhu
poRivaasal pOrkkadhavam saarththi, - aRivaanaam
aalamara neezhal aRamnaalvark kanRuraiththa,
aalamamar kaNdath tharan.
4.meaning: He who stands as the very gateway of the righteous path - closing the battling-doors of the five gateways of the senses, one comes to know Him. He is also the source of Aran (Shiva) of the poison-stained throat, who long ago, seated beneath the banyan tree's shade, expounded dharma to the four sages.

5.translit:
arannaa raNannaamam aanvidaipuLLoorththi,
urainool maRaiyuRaiyum kOyil, - varainee
karumam azhippaLippuk kaiyadhuvEl nEmi,
uruvameri kaarmEni onRu.
5.meaning: Their names are Aran (Shiva) and Nāraṇaṇ (Vishnu); their mounts are the bull and the bird (Garuda); their abode is the Vedas declared in spoken scripture; their realms are the mountain and the sea; their tasks are destruction and protection; in their hands are the trident and the discus; their forms are fire-hued and dark cloud-hued - yet they are one.

6.translit:
onRum maRandhaRiyEn Odhaneer vaNNanainaan,
inRu maRappanO Ezhaikaal - anRu
karuvarangath thutkidandhu kaithozhudhEn kaNdEn
thiruvaranga mEyaan thisai.
6.meaning: I have never once forgotten Him of the surging-water hue. O ignorant ones, would I forget Him today? On that day, lying within the womb's enclosure, I worshipped with folded hands and beheld the direction of Him who dwells in Srirangam.

7.translit:
thisaiyum thisaiyuRu theyvamum, theyvath
thisaiyungk karumanga LellaaM - asaivilseerk
kaNNan nedumaal kadalkadaindha,
kaarOdha vaNNan padaiththa mayakku.
7.meaning: The directions, the deities presiding over the directions, the realms of those deities and all their works - all these are the bewildering creation of Kannan (Krishna), the great Tirumāl of unswerving glory, the dark ocean-hued one who churned the sea.

8.translit:
mayanga valampuri vaaivaiththu, vaanath
thiyangum eRikadhirOn Rannai, - muyangamaruL
thOraazhi yaal maRaiththa thennee thirumaalE,
pOraazhik kaiyaal porudhu?
8.meaning: O Tirumāl, blowing the conch (valampuri) to set them in confusion, why did you, with the battle-discus in your hand, fight and conceal the blazing-rayed sun that moves through the sky, behind a single discus?

9.translit:
porukOttOr Enamaaip pukkidandhaaikku, anRun
orukOttin mElkidandha thanRe, - viridhOtta
sEvadiyai neettith thisainadunga viNthuLanga,
maavadivin neeyaLandha maN?
9.meaning: For you who entered as the boar with battling tusks and lay there, did not this earth - that you measured by stretching forth your great red feet so that the directions trembled and the heavens shook - rest upon a single one of your tusks long ago?

10.translit:
maNNum malaiyum maRikadalum maarudhamum,
viNNum vizhungiyadhu meyyenbar, - eNNil
alakaLavu kaNdasee raazhiyaaikku, anRiv
vulakaLavu muNdOvun vaai?
10.meaning: They say it is true that you swallowed the earth, the mountains, the rolling sea, the wind and the sky. O bearer of the glorious discus whose measure cannot be counted in number, was the measure of this world ever too much for your mouth?

11.translit:
vaayavanai yalladhu vaazhththaadhu kaiyulakam
thaayavanai yalladhu thaandhozhaa - pEymulainanj
chooNaaka vuNdaan uruvOdu pErallaal
kaaNaakaN kELaa sevi.
11.meaning: The mouth praises none but Him; the hands of the world worship none but Him who once strode across the world. Except for the form and the name of Him who drank as nectar the poison of the demoness's breast (Pūtanā), the eye sees nothing and the ear hears nothing.

12.translit:
sevivaai kaNmook kudalen Raimpulanum
sendheepuvikaal neervaN poodhamaindhum - aviyaadha
njaanamum vELviyum nallaRamumenbarE
Enamaai ninRaaR kiyalvu.
12.meaning: The five senses - ear, mouth, eye, nose and body; the five elements - red fire, earth, wind, water and bountiful ether; the unquenchable knowledge, sacrifice and good dharma - all these, they say, are the very nature of Him who stood as the boar.

13.translit:
iyalvaaka eendhuzha yaanadikkE chella,
muyalvaar iyalamarar munnam, - iyalvaaka
needhiyaa lOdhi niyamanga LaalparavA,
aadhiyaai ninRaar avar.
13.meaning: Those who strive fittingly to reach the feet of Him who wears the sweet tulasi, the celestials of true nature, recite according to right manner and praise Him through their ordained observances - and so He stands as the Primordial One, He alone.

14.translit:
avaravar thaandham aRindhavaa REththi, ivariva
remperumaa nenRu, - suvarmisaich
chaarththiyum vaiththum thozhuvar, ulakaLandha
moorththi yuruvE mudhal.
14.meaning: Each person, praising Him according to the manner each has understood, saying 'this one is my Lord' and 'that one is my Lord,' worships images set upon walls and placed about. Yet the source of all is the very form of the Lord who measured the worlds.

15.translit:
mudhalaavaar moovarE ammoova ruLLum
mudhalaavaan moorineer vaNNan, - mudhalaaya
nallaan aruLallaal naamaneer vaiyakaththu,
pallaar aruLum pazhudhu.
15.meaning: The primordial ones are the three (Brahmā, Vishnu, Shiva); and among those three the foremost is He of the vast ocean's hue (Vishnu). In this water-girt world that bears a name, save for the grace of that supremely good Primordial One, the grace of all the many others is in vain.

16.translit:
pazhudhE palapakalum pOyinavenRu, anji
azhudhEn aravaNaimEl kaNdu - thozhudhEn,
kadalOdham kaalalaippak kaNvaLarum, sengaN
adalOdha vaNNar adi.
16.meaning: Fearing that many days had passed away uselessly, I wept; and beholding Him upon the serpent-couch, I worshipped the feet of the red-eyed Lord of fierce ocean-hue, who sleeps as the waves of the sea wash against His feet.

17.translit:
adiyum padikadappath thOLthisaimEl chella,
mudiyum visumbaLan thadhenbar, - vadiyukiraal
eerndhaan iraNiyana thaakam, irunjchiRaippuL
oorndhaa nulakaLandha naanRu.
17.meaning: They say that when He measured the worlds, His feet strode across the earth, His shoulders reached over the directions, and His crown measured the sky. He who rode the great-winged bird (Garuda) is the same who tore open the body of Hiranya(kashipu) with His sharp claws.

18.translit:
naanRa mulaiththalai nanjuNdu, uRiveNNey
thOnRavuN Daanvenri soozhkaLiRRai - oonRi,
porudha daivu kaNdaanum puLLinvaai keeNdaanum,
marudhidaipOi maNNaLandha maal.
18.meaning: He who drank the poison from the drooping breast (of Pūtanā), who ate the butter set out in the hanging pots, who pressed down and destroyed in battle the encircling elephant (Kuvalayāpīda), who split open the beak of the bird (Bakāsura), who passed between the marudu trees - He is the Tirumāl who measured the earth.

19.translit:
maalung karungadalE ennORRaai, vaiyakamuN
Daalin ilaiththuyinRa aazhiyaan, - kOlak
karumEnich chengaNmaal kaNpadaiyuL, enRum
thirumEni neetheeNdap peRRu.
19.meaning: O great dark ocean, what penance did you perform? For the discus-bearing Lord who swallowed the earth and slept upon the banyan leaf - the beautiful dark-bodied, red-eyed Tirumāl - rests ever in your embrace, and you are blessed forever to touch His sacred form during His sleep.

100.translit:
oradiyum saadudaitta oNmalarch chevadiyum,
Iradiyum kaaNalaa mennenje. - oradiyil
taayavanaik kesavanait taNduzhaay maalaiser,
maayavanai yemanattu vai.
100.meaning: O my heart! With one foot He kicked and shattered the demon Sakata, and that beautiful flower-like red foot, together with His other foot, can both be beheld. In that single measuring stride He spanned all the worlds. Set firmly in your mind that wondrous Lord Kesava, who wears the cool tulasi garland.

20.translit:
pettraar thaLaikazhalap poarnthoar kuRaLuruvaay,
settraar padikadantha sengkaNmaal, - nattraa
maraimalarc seevadiyai vaanavarkai kooppi,
niraimalarkoNdu eaththuvaraal ninRu.
20.meaning: As the dwarf (Vamana) who took a small form so that the fetters of his parents (Devaki and Vasudeva, or the bound earth) might fall loose, the red-eyed Tirumal strode across and conquered the realm of the enemies. To his lovely lotus-flower red feet the celestials join their hands in worship, and standing they extol him, offering rows of flowers.

21.translit:
ninRu nilamangkai neerEttru moovadiyaal,
senRu thisaiyaLantha sengkaNmaaRku, - enRum
padaiyaazhi puLLoorththi paampaNaiyaan paatham,
adaiyaazhi nenjE aRi.
21.meaning: To the red-eyed lord who, receiving water as the pledge over the Earth-maiden, stood and went and measured out all the directions in his three strides, who bears the discus as his weapon, rides the bird (Garuda), and reclines on the serpent-couch — to his feet take refuge, O heart, and know him.

22.translit:
aRiyu mulakellaam yaaneyu mallEn,
poRikoL siRaiyuvaNa moornthaay, - veRikamazhum
kaampEymen thoaLi kadaiveNNe yuNdaayai,
thaampEkoN daarththa thazhumbu.
22.meaning: The whole world knows it, and I am not the only one who knows — O you who ride the bright-winged bird (Garuda) marked with spots! On you who stole and ate the churned butter, in the home of the fragrant lady with soft bamboo-like shoulders (Yashoda), there remains the welt left by the rope with which she bound you.

23.translit:
thazhumbiruntha saarngkanaaN thoayntha mangkai,
thazhumbiruntha thaaLsakadam saadi, - thazhumbiruntha
poongkoathai yaaLveruvap ponpeyaroan maarppidantha,
veengkoatha vaNNar viral.
23.meaning: On those fingers there is the mark left by the string of the Saaranga bow which has touched them; the mark left from kicking and destroying the cart (Sakatasura); and the mark from tearing open the chest of the one named gold (Hiranyakashipu) while the flower-tressed lady (Lakshmi) trembled — such are the fingers of the lord whose hue is like the swelling ocean.

24.translit:
viraloadu vaaythoayntha veNNeykaNdu, aaycchi
uralo duRappiNiththa njaanRu - kuraloavaa
thoangki ninainthayalaar kaaNa irunthilaiyE?
oangkoatha vaNNaa urai.
24.meaning: When the cowherd-woman (Yashoda), seeing the butter that smeared your fingers and mouth, bound you tightly to the mortar — did you not sit there crying out without ceasing, sobbing and shrinking, for the neighbors to see? O you whose hue is like the towering ocean, speak!

25.translit:
uraimeaRkoN dennuLLam Oovaathu eppoathum
varaimeal marakathamE poala, - thiraimeal
kidanthaanaik keeNdaanai, keazhalaayp poomi
idanthaanai yeaththi yezhum.
25.meaning: Taking up his praise, may my heart rise up unceasingly at all times, extolling him who reclines upon the waves, like an emerald set upon a mountain — him who split open (the asuras), and who, becoming a boar, dug up and lifted the Earth.

26.translit:
ezhuvaar vidaikoLvaar eenthuzhaa yaanai,
vazhuvaa vakaininainthu vaikal - thozhuvaar,
vinaicchudarai nanthuvikkum veengkadamE, vaanoar
manacchudaraith thooNdum malai.
26.meaning: Those who rise up and those who take leave think without fail upon the lord wearing sweet tulasi, and worship him daily — for them Venkatam is the hill that quenches the blaze of their (evil) deeds, even as it kindles the light in the minds of the celestials.

27.translit:
malaiyaal kudaikaviththu maavaay piLanthu,
silaiyaal maraamaramEzh settru, - kolaiyaanaip
poarkkoa dosiththanavum poongkuruntham saayththanavum
kaakkoadu pattriyaan kai.
27.meaning: Holding up the mountain (Govardhana) as an umbrella, splitting open the mouth of the horse-demon (Kesi), piercing through the seven maramara trees with the bow, breaking off the tusk of the murderous elephant (Kuvalayapida) in battle, and felling the flowering kurunda tree — these were done by the hands of him who grasps the discus.

28.translit:
kaiya valampuriyum neamiyum, kaarvaNNath
thaiya malarmakaLnin naakaththaaL, - seyya
maRaiyaannin unthiyaan maamadhiLmoon Reytha
iRaiyaannin aakath thiRai.
28.meaning: In your hands are the right-coiled conch and the discus; O dark-hued lord, the maiden of the flower (Lakshmi) abides upon your chest; the lord of the holy Vedas (Brahma) dwells on your navel; and the lord (Shiva) who burnt the three great-walled cities — that very Lord himself rests upon your body.

29.translit:
iRaiyum nilanum iruvisumbum kaattrum,
aRaipunalum senthee yumaavaan, - piRaimaruppin
paingkaNmaal yaanai paduthuyaram kaaththaLiththa,
sengkaNmaal kaNdaay theLi.
29.meaning: He who became the Lord himself, and the earth, the two skies, the wind, the roaring waters, and the red fire — he it is who guarded and saved the great green-eyed elephant (Gajendra) with crescent-like tusks from the suffering that befell it. Behold him, the red-eyed Tirumal, and be assured.

30.translit:
theLithaaka vuLLaththaic chennireeI, njaanath
theLithaaka nankuNarvaar sinthai, - eLithaakath
thaaynaadu kanREpoal thaNduzhaa yanadikkE,
poaynaadik koLLum purinthu.
30.meaning: Setting their hearts firmly in clarity, those who rightly understand with the clarity of true knowledge — their minds, easily, like a calf seeking its mother, go seeking and lovingly attain the feet of him who wears the cool tulasi.

31.translit:
puriyorukai pattriyoar ponnaazhi yeanthi,
ariyuruvum aaLuruvumaaki, - eriyuruva
vaNNaththaan maarppidantha maaladiyai allaal,mat
ReNNaththaa naamoa imai?
31.meaning: Holding the conch in one hand and bearing a golden discus, becoming both the lion-form and the man-form (Narasimha), he tore open the chest of the one of fiery hue (Hiranya) — except for the feet of that Tirumal, is there any other worth contemplating, even for the blink of an eye?

32.translit:
imaiyaatha kaNNaal iruLakala noakki,
amaiyaap poRipulanka Lainthum - namaiyaamal,
aakath thaNaippaa raNaivarE, aayiravaay
naakath thaNaiyaan nakar.
32.meaning: Gazing with unblinking eyes until the darkness departs, and without letting the five restless senses afflict them, those who clasp him to their hearts — they alone shall reach the city of him who reclines upon the thousand-mouthed serpent (Adisesha).

33.translit:
nakara maruLpurinthu naanmukaRku, poomeal
pakara maRaipayantha paNpan, - peyarinaiyE
punthiyaal sinthiyaa thoathi uruveNNum,
anthiyaa laampanang ken?
33.meaning: He who graciously bestowed the cities (of the worlds), and the gracious one who brought forth the Vedas to four-faced Brahma upon the lotus — of what use is the twilight ritual to one who only recites his name and counts the syllables by form, without contemplating it in the mind?

34.translit:
ennoruvar meyyenbar EzhulakuNdu aalilaiyil
munnoruva naaya mukilvaNNaa, - ninnurukip
pEyththaay mulaithanthaaL pErnthilaLaal, pEramarkkaN
aayththaay mulaithantha aaRu?
34.meaning: How can anyone call it true? O cloud-hued one who, of old, swallowed the seven worlds and lay alone upon the banyan leaf — the demoness (Putana) who in feigned-mother form gave you her breast melted away (perished and could not depart), yet the cowherd mother (Yashoda) with wide lovely eyes who gave you her breast remained safe — how is this?

35.translit:
aaRiya anbil adiyaartham aarvaththaal,
kooRiya kuttramaak koLLalnee - thERi,
nediyoay adiyadaithaR kanRE,ee rainthu
mudiyaan padaiththa muraN?
35.meaning: O tall lord (Trivikrama)! Do not reckon as a fault whatever is uttered out of the fervor of your devotees whose love has matured and grown calm. Was not the might of the ten-crowned one (Ravana) created so that it might be reached and ended — was it not for the sake of reaching your feet?

36.translit:
muraNai valitholaithaR kaamanRE, munnam
tharaNi thanathaakath thaanE - iraNiyanaip
puNNirantha vaLLukiraal ponnaazhik kaiyaal,nee
maNNiranthu koNda vakai?
36.meaning: Was it not to destroy strength by strength that, of old, you tore Hiranya with claws that wounded deep, while bearing the golden discus in your hand — and the manner in which you begged for the earth (as Vamana) and took it, so that the whole world might become your own?

37.translit:
vakaiyaRu nuNkeaLvi vaayvaarkaL, naaLum
pukaiviLakkum poompunalum eanthi, - thisaithisaiyin
veathiyarkaL senRiRainjum veengkadamE, veNsangkam
oothiyavaay maalukantha voor.
37.meaning: Where the brahmins endowed with subtle and faultless sacred learning, bearing incense and lamp and flowers and water, come from every direction to worship — that Venkatam is the place dear to Tirumal of the mouth that blew the white conch.

38.translit:
oorum variyaravam oNkuRavar maalyaanai,
pEra eRintha perumaNiyai, - kaarudaiya
minnenRu puttradaiyum veengkadamE, melasurar
ennenRa maala thidam.
38.meaning: Where the bright hunters fling the great gem (snatched) from the lordly elephant, and the moving striped serpent, mistaking it for the cloud's lightning, retreats into its anthill — that Venkatam is the abode of the Tirumal at whom the asuras above cried out (in dismay).

39.translit:
idanthathu poomi eduththathu kunRam,
kadanthathu kanjanaimun anja, - kidanthathuvum
neeroatha maakadalE ninRathuvum veengkadamE,
pEroatha vaNNar perithu.
39.meaning: What he dug up was the Earth; what he lifted was the hill (Govardhana); what he strode across made Kamsa (and the demons) tremble of old; where he reclines is the wave-tossed great ocean of waters; and where he stands is Venkatam — vast indeed is the lord whose hue is like the great resounding ocean.

40.translit:
peruvil pagazhik kuRavar kaich chenthii
veruvip punam thuRandha vEzham, - iruvisumbil
miin viizhak kaNDanjum vEngkaDamE, mElasurar
kOn viizhak kaNDukandhaan kunRu.
40.meaning: The Venkatam hills are the place where the elephant, terrified by the red fire in the hands of the hunters with their great bows and arrows, fled its forest-field, and where it grew frightened seeing a meteor fall from the vast sky. This is the hill of Him who rejoiced to see the chief of the lofty asuras fall.

41.translit:
kunRanaiya kuRRanj cheyinum guNangkoLLum
inRu mudhalaaga ennenjE, - enRum
puRanuraiyE aayinum ponnaazhik kaiyaan
thiRanuraiyE sindhith thiru.
41.meaning: O my heart, from this day onward He accepts as virtue even faults as great as a mountain. Therefore, even though all else be idle outer talk, ever meditate and dwell only upon the glory of Him who bears the golden discus in His hand.

42.translit:
thirumagaLum maNmagaLum aaymagaLum sErndhaal
thirumagaTkE thiirndhavaa Renkol, - thirumagaL mEl
paalOdham sindhap paDanaagaNaik kiDandha,
maalOdha vaNNar manam?
42.meaning: When Lakshmi, the Earth-goddess, and the cowherd-maiden all come together, why is it that the heart of the dark ocean-hued Lord, who reclines upon the hooded serpent-couch while the milky waves break, is given over wholly to Lakshmi alone?

43.translit:
manamaasu thiiru maRuvinaiyum saara,
dhanamaaya thaanE kai kuuDum, - punamEya
puundhuzhaa yaNaDikkE pOdhoDu niirEndhi,
thaam thozhaa niRpaar thamar.
43.meaning: For those devotees who, holding flowers and water, stand worshipping the feet of Him who wears the fragrant tulasi grown in the field, the mind's impurity is cleansed, evil karma departs and does not cling, and true wealth itself comes of its own accord into their hands.

44.translit:
thamarugandha thevvuruvam avvuruvam thaanE,
thamarugandha theppErmaR RappEr, - thamarugandhu
evvaNNam sindhith thimaiyaa thirupparE,
avvaNNam azhiyaa naam.
44.meaning: Whatever form His devotees delight in, that form He Himself takes; whatever name His devotees delight in, that name becomes His name. In whatever manner His devotees lovingly meditate upon Him without wavering, in that very manner He becomes, the imperishable One.

45.translit:
aamE yamararkku aRiya? adhuniRka,
naamE yaRikiRpOm nannenjE, - puumEya
maadhavaththOn thaaLpaNindha vaaLarakkan niiNmuDiyai,
paadhamaththaal eNNinaan paNbu.
45.meaning: Is He able to be known even by the immortals? Let that be. O good heart, can we ourselves know the nature of Him who, with His foot, counted off the long crowns of the sword-bearing demon (Ravana) who bowed at the feet of the great ascetic Brahma seated on the lotus?

46.translit:
paNbarindha naanmaRaiyOn chennip paliyERRa,
veNpurinuul maarban vinaidhiira, - puNpurindha
aagaththaan thaaLpaNivaar kaNDiir, amarardham
pOgaththaal puumiyaaL vaar.
46.meaning: Behold, those who bow at the feet of the Lord whose chest bears the white sacred thread, who took alms in the skull of the four-Veda-knowing Brahma to free him from sin, the Lord whose body bears the scar of wounds, those devotees shall rule over the earth, possessing even the enjoyments of the immortals.

47.translit:
vaari surukki madhakkaLi Raindhinaiyum,
sEri thiriyaamal sennaRiiஇ, - kuuriya
meijnjaanath thaaluNarvaar kaaNbarE, mEloru naaL
kainnaagam kaaththaan kazhal.
47.meaning: Those who, drawing in and restraining the five rutting elephants of the senses so they do not stray and wander among objects, and who through sharp true wisdom perceive Him, they alone shall behold the feet of Him who once long ago protected the elephant (Gajendra) seized in the crocodile's grip.

48.translit:
kazhalon Reduththorukai suRRiyOr kaimEl,
suzhalum suraasurargaL anja, - azhalum
seruvaazhi yEndhinaan sEvaDikkE sella,
maruvaazhi nenjE magizh.
48.meaning: O fond and beloved heart, rejoice, and go to the sacred red feet of Him who, raising one foot, whirling it about with one hand, while gods and demons spun around in terror, held aloft the blazing battle-discus in His other hand.

49.translit:
magizhalagu onREpOl maaRumbal yaakkai,
negizha muyalkiRpaark kallaal, - mugil virindha
sOdhipOl thOnRum suDarppon neDumuDi, em
aadhi kaaNbaarkku maridhu.
49.meaning: Except for those who strive to loosen and shed the many ever-changing bodies that drop away like petals, it is hard indeed to behold our primordial Lord, whose radiant golden tall crown appears like effulgence spreading through the clouds.

50.translit:
ariyapulan aindhaDakki aaymalarkoNDu, aarvam
pariyap parisinaal pulgil, - periyanaay
maaRRaadhu viiRRirundha maavalipaal, vaNkainiir
ERRaanaik kaaNba dheLidhu.
50.meaning: If one subdues the five hard-to-control senses, takes choice flowers, and embraces Him with overflowing love in the proper manner, then it is easy to behold Him who, with His bounteous hand receiving the water-offering, took the gift from Mahabali who sat unmoving in his greatness.

51.translit:
eLidhil iraNDaiyum kaaNbadhaRku, ennuLLam
theLiyath theLindhozhiyum sevvE, - kaLiyil
porundhaa dhavanaip poraluRRu, ariyaay
irundhaan thirunaamam eN.
51.meaning: To behold both with ease, my heart grows wholly and rightly clear. Meditate upon the holy name of Him who, taking the form of the Man-Lion (Narasimha), rose up to do battle with the one (Hiranya) who in his arrogance would not submit.

52.translit:
eNmar padhinoruvar iiraRuvar Oririvar,
vaNNa malarEndhi vaikalum, - naNNi
orumaalai yaalparavi Ovaadhu, ep pOdhum
thirumaalaik kaithozhuvar senRu.
52.meaning: The eight (Vasus), the eleven (Rudras), the twice-six (Adityas), and the two (Asvins), bearing lovely flowers each day, draw near and praise Him with a single garland of song unceasingly; at all times they go and worship Tirumal with folded hands.

53.translit:
senRaal kuDaiyaam irundhaal singaasanamaam,
ninRaal maravaDiyaam niiLkaDaluL, - enRum
puNaiyaam maNiviLak kaam puumpaTTaam pulgum
aNaiyaam, thirumaaR karavu.
53.meaning: To Tirumal the serpent Adisesha is everything: when He walks it is His parasol, when He sits it is His throne, when He stands it is His sandals; and in the vast ocean it is ever His raft, His jewelled lamp, His silken flowery robe, and the embracing couch on which He rests.

54.translit:
aravam adalvEzham aankurundham puLvaay
kuravai kuDam mulaimal kunRam, - karavinRi
viTTiRuththu mEyththosiththuk kiiNDukOth thaaDi, uN
daTTeduththa sengaN avan.
54.meaning: He of the red eyes did all these without holding back: He cast out and crushed the serpent (Kaliya), the fierce elephant (Kuvalayapida), broke the kurundha trees, tore open the bird's beak (Bakasura), danced the kuravai, lifted the hill, drank the poisoned breast (Putana), and raised up the mountain (Govardhana) as cattle grazed.

55.translit:
avan thamar evvinaiya raagilum, engkOn
avandhamarE enRozhiva dhallaal, - namandhamaraal
aaraayap paT DaRiyaar kaNDiir, aravaNaimEl
pEraayaR kaaTpaTTaar pEr.
55.meaning: Whatever deeds His devotees may have done, it is enough that they are called the devotees of our King. Behold, those who have surrendered to the great cowherd Lord who reclines upon the serpent-couch, never come to be examined or judged by the servants of Yama (Death).

56.translit:
pErE varappidhaRRal allaalem pemmaanai,
aarE aRivaar? adhuniRka, - nErE
kaDikkamalath thuLLirundhum kaaNgilaan, kaNNan
aDikkamalan thannai ayan.
56.meaning: Other than rambling on, calling out His many names, who can truly know our Lord? Let that be: though Brahma sits directly within the fragrant lotus that springs from Kannan's navel, even he cannot behold the lotus feet of Kannan himself.

57.translit:
ayalninRa valvinaiyai anjinE nanji,
uyanin thiruvaDiyE sErvaan, - nayaninRa
nanmaalai koNDu namOnaaraNaa ennum,
sonmaalai kaRREn thozhudhu.
57.meaning: Fearing the powerful evil karma that stands close beside me, and that I might be saved, to reach Your sacred feet I have taken up a fine garland full of devotion and, worshipping, have learned the garland of words that says 'Namo Narayana.'

58.translit:
thozhudhu malarkkoNDu thuubamkai yEndhi,
ezhudhum ezhuvaazhi nenjE, - pazhudhinRi
mandhirangkaL kaRpanavum maalaDiyE kaithozhuvaan,
andharamon Rillai aDai.
58.meaning: O dear heart, let us rise and offer worship, taking flowers and holding incense in our hands. There is no hindrance at all; reach and attain Him, for the learning of mantras too is meant only for the worship of Tirumal's feet with folded hands.

59.translit:
aDaindha aruvinaiyO DallalnOy paavam,
miDaindhavai miiNDozhiya vENDil, - nuDangkiDaiyai
munnilangkai vaiththaan muraNazhiya, munnoru naaL
thanvilangkai vaiththaan saraN.
59.meaning: If you wish that the cruel karma, sorrow, sickness, and sin that have densely gathered upon you should turn back and depart, take refuge in the feet of Him who, to destroy the strength of the one who once kept the slender-waisted Sita captive in Lanka, long ago set Himself there as His own fortress.

60.translit:
saraNaa maRaipayandha thaamaraiyaa nOodu,
maraNaaya mannuyirkat kellaam, - araNaaya
pEraazhi koNda piraananRi maRRaRiyaadhu,
Ooraazhi soozhndha vulagu.
60.meaning: The world encircled by a single great ocean knows no refuge but the Lord who, as a shelter for all the enduring lives that are subject to death, holds the great discus, and who became the support of the lotus-born Brahma from whom the Vedas sprang. It recognizes no other than Him.

61.translit:
ulagum ulagiRandha voozhiyum, oNkEzh
vilagu karungadalum veRpum, - ulaginil
sendheeyum maaruthamum vaanum, thirumaalthan
pundhiyi laaya puNarppu.
61.meaning: The world, and the aeon that transcends the world, the dark sea of bright lustre that spreads wide, and the mountains; the red fire upon the earth, the wind, and the sky too - all these are the creation that arose within the mind of Tirumal.

62.translit:
puNarmaruthi noodu pOyp poonkurundham saaiththu,
maNamaruva maal vidaiyEzh seRRu, - kaNamveruva
Ezhulagum thaayinavum eNdisaiyum pOyinavum,
soozharavap pongaNaiyaan thOL.
62.meaning: The shoulders of Him who reclines on the spreading hooded serpent went between the twin marudu trees and crept through, felled the beautiful kurundha tree, and destroyed the seven rampant bulls to win the bride; those shoulders strode and measured the seven worlds, and they reached out across the eight directions while the hosts of beings trembled in awe.

63.translit:
thOLavanai yallaal thozhaa, en seviyiraNdum,
kELavana dhinmozhiyE kEttirukkum, - naanaaLum
kONaa kaNaiyaan koorai kazhalE kooRuvadhE,
naaNaamai naLLEn nayam.
63.meaning: My two arms will not worship any but Him of the strong shoulders; my two ears stay only to hear His sweet words. My tongue, day after day, will speak only of the feet of Him who reclines on the unbending serpent. Shamelessly I will not draw near to any other delight.

64.translit:
nayavEn piRarppoRuLai naLLEnkee zhaarOdu,
uyavEn uyarndhavarO dallaal, - viyavEn
thirumaalai yalladhu dheyvamen REththEn,
varumaaRen nammEl vinai?
64.meaning: I will not covet another's wealth, I will not consort with base men, I will not seek to live except with the exalted ones. I will not wonder at, nor praise as god, any but Tirumal. How then shall any sin come upon us?

65.translit:
vinaiyaa ladarppadaar vennaragil sEraar,
thinaiyEnum theekkadhikkat sellaar, - ninaidhaR
kariyaanaic chEyaanai, aayirampErc chengat
kariyaanaik kaithozhudhak kaal.
65.meaning: When men fold their hands and worship the dark-hued one of the red eyes, who bears a thousand names, who is hard to conceive of and dwells afar, they will not be oppressed by their deeds, they will not enter the fierce hell, nor will they go even a grain's measure toward the evil paths.

66.translit:
kaalai yezhundhulagam kaRpanavum, kaRRuNarndha
mElaith thalaimaRaiyOr vEtpanavum, - vElaikkaN
Oraazhi yaanadiyE Odhuvadhum Orppanavum,
pEraazhi koNdaan peyar.
66.meaning: That which the world rises at dawn to learn, that which the foremost learned Brahmins, who have mastered and understood, long to perform, that which men chant and meditate upon as the very feet of the one who holds the discus beside the sea - all of it is the name of Him who took up the great discus.

67.translit:
peyarum karung kadalE nOkkumaaRu, oNpoo
uyarum kadhiravanE nOkkum, -uyirum
tharumanaiyE nOkkumoN daamaraiyaaL kELvan,
oruvanaiyE nOkkum uNarvu.
67.meaning: As the moving waters look only toward the dark sea, as the bright lotus that rises looks only toward the sun, and as the soul looks toward Yama, the lord of justice - so true wisdom looks only toward the One, the consort of the lady seated on the bright lotus.

68.translit:
uNarvaaraa runperumai? yoozhidhO Roozhi,
uNarvaaraa runnuruvan thannai?, uNarvaaraar
viNNagaththaay maNNagaththaay vEngadaththaay naalvEdhap
paNNagaththaay neekidandha paal?
68.meaning: Who can comprehend your greatness, aeon after aeon? Who can comprehend your form? O you who are in the heavens, who are upon the earth, who are at Venkatam, who are within the melody of the four Vedas - who can comprehend the milk-ocean on which you lie reclining?

69.translit:
paalan Ranadhuruvaay EzhulaguNdu, aalilaiyin
mElanRu neevaLarndha meyyenbar, - aalanRu
vElainee ruLLadhO viNNadhO maNNadhO?
sOlaisoozh kunReduththaay sollu.
69.meaning: They say it is truth that, in the form of an infant, you swallowed the seven worlds and lay grown upon a banyan leaf that day. O you who lifted up the hill girt with groves, tell me - that banyan leaf that day, was it within the waters of the sea, or in the sky, or upon the earth?

70.translit:
sollun thanaiyum thozhumin vizhumu dambu,
sollun thanaiyum thirumaalai, - nallidhazhth
thaamaththaal vELviyaal thandhiraththaal mandhiraththaal,
naamaththaal EththidhirEl nanRu.
70.meaning: As long as you can speak, worship Tirumal with this precious body; as long as you can speak, if you praise Him with garlands of fair petals, with sacrifice, with rite, with sacred chant, and with His names - that is good.

71.translit:
nanRupi Nimooppuk kaiyagaRRi naankoozhi,
ninRu nilamuzhudhum aaNdaalum,- enRum
vidalaazhi nenjamE vENdinEn kaNdaay,
adalaazhi koNdaanmaat tanbu.
71.meaning: Even if one were to drive away sickness and old age, and stand and rule the whole earth for four aeons - that would not match it. O heart, the love I have sought, never to be let go, is love toward Him who holds the destroying discus; behold it.

72.translit:
anbaazhi yaanai yaNugennum, naaavanRan
paNbaazhith thOLparavi yEththennum, - munboozhi
kaaNaanaik kaaNennum kaNsevi kELennum
pooNaaram pooNdaan pugazh.
