72.meaning: My love bids me draw near to Him who holds the discus; my tongue bids me extol and praise His gracious, discus-bearing shoulders; it bids me behold Him who beheld the aeon of old; it bids my eyes to see and my ears to hear the glory of Him who wears the strung garland of jewels.

73.translit:
pugazhvaay pazhippaaynee poondhuzhaa yaanai,
igazhvaay karudhuvaay nenjE, - thigazhneerk
kadalum malaiyum iruvisumbum kaaRRum,
udalum uyirumERRaan.
73.meaning: O heart, whether you praise or blame, whether you scorn or revere - meditate on Him who wears the fresh tulasi, who has taken upon Himself the shining-watered sea, the mountains, the two skies, the wind, and bodies and souls alike.

74.translit:
ERRaan puLLoorththaan eyileriththaan maarvidandhaan
neeRRaan nizhalmaNi vaNNaththaan, - kooRRorupaal
mangaiyaan poomagaLaan vaarsadaiyaan, neeNmudiyaan
gangaiyaan neeLkazhalaan kaappu.
74.meaning: He who rode the bull, who rode the bird, who burned the citadels, who clove the chest; who is smeared with ash, and who is of the lustrous sapphire hue; who has the lady on one side, who has the lotus-lady, who has long matted locks and who has the lofty crown, who bears the Ganga and whose long feet are protection - He is our refuge.

75.translit:
kaappunnai yunnak kazhiyum aruvinaigaL,
aappunnai yunna avizhndhozhiyum - mooppunnaic
chindhippaark killai thirumaalE, ninnadiyai
vandhippaar kaaNbar vazhi.
75.meaning: To meditate on you is protection; the cruel deeds depart when you are remembered; the bonds loosen and fall away when you are remembered. O Tirumal, there is no old age for those who think of you; those who reverence your feet shall behold the way.

76.translit:
vazhininRu ninnaith thozhuvaar, vazhuvaa
mozhininRa moorththiyarE yaavar, - pazhudhonRum
vaaraadha vaNNamE viNkodukkum, maNNaLandha
seeraan thiruvEng kadam.
76.meaning: Those who stand in the right path and worship you become themselves enshrined deities whose words never fail. Tiruvenkatam, abode of the glorious one who measured the earth, grants heaven in such a way that no flaw whatsoever can come.

77.translit:
vEngadamum viNNagarum vehkaavum, ahkaadha
poongidangil neeLkOval ponnagarum, - naankidaththum
ninRaa nirundhaan kidandhaan nadandhaanE,
enRaal kedumaam idar.
77.meaning: At Venkatam, at Vinnagar, at Vehka, and at the golden city of long Koval with its unfailing, flower-filled moat - in these four places, when one says 'He stood, He sat, He reclined, He walked,' all affliction is destroyed.

78.translit:
idaraar paduvaar? ezhunenjE, vEzham
thodarvaan kodumudhalai soozhndha, - padamudai
painnaagap paLLiyaan paadhamE kaithozhudhum,
koinnaagap poompOdhu koNdu.
78.meaning: Who shall suffer affliction? Arise, O heart! Let us, with freshly plucked punnai flowers, fold our hands and worship the feet of Him who reclines on the broad-hooded fresh serpent - He who came when the cruel crocodile seized and entwined the elephant that pursued Him.

79.translit:
koNdaanai yallaal koduththaarai yaarpazhippaar,
maNthaa enavirandhu maavaliyai, oNthaarai
neerangai thOya nimirndhilaiyE, neeLvisumbil
aarangai thOya aduththu?
79.meaning: Who would blame the giver rather than the taker? Begging Mahabali, saying 'Give me earth,' so that the bright stream of water poured upon your palm - did you not then rise and stretch up, until your reaching hand touched the boundary of the vast high heavens?

80.translit:
aDutta kaDum pakaiñark kaaTREn enRu ooDi,
paDutta porum paazhi soozhntha - viDattaravai,
vallaaLan kaikkoDutta maamEni maayavanukku,
allaadum aavarO aaL?
80.meaning: When the elephant Gajendra, unable to withstand its fierce and cruel foe, cried out 'I cannot bear it!' and came running, the Lord of the dark, magnificent form, the wondrous one, slew with His discus the venomous crocodile lurking in the deep pool. To that mighty Lord who came to His devotee's aid, can there be any servants other than ourselves?

81.translit:
aaLamar venRi yaDukaLattuL aññaanRu,
vaaLamar vENDi varainaTTu, - neeLaravaic
chuTRik kaDaintaan peyaranRE, ton narakaip
paTRik kaDattum paDai?
81.meaning: On that ancient day, desiring the strength to win the battle on the field where mighty warriors clashed, He planted the mountain Mandara, wound the long serpent around it, and churned the ocean. Is not that very army the name and power of Him, which seizes and ferries us across the ancient hell?

82.translit:
paDaiyaarum vaaTkaNNaar paarasi naaL, paimpoon
toDaiyalOD Endiya thoopam, - iDaiyiDaiyin
meenmaaya maasooNum vEngkaDamE, mEloru naaL
maanmaaya eythaan varai.
82.meaning: Venkatam is the hill of Him who, on a former day, shot His arrow so that the demon-deer Maricha perished. There women with sword-sharp eyes, on festival days, carry fresh flower-garlands and incense, whose smoke rises so thickly between the slopes that it dims and hides the very stars in the sky.

83.translit:
varaikuDaithOl kaampaaka aanirai kaattu, aayar
niraiviDaiyEzh cheTRavaa REnnE, - uravuDaiya
neeraazhi yuLkiDantu nEraa nisaasarar mEl,
pEraazhi koNDa piraan?
83.meaning: Making the mountain his umbrella and his arm its handle, He guarded the herds of cattle and the cowherds; how wondrous was the way He subdued the seven fierce bulls! He is the Lord who reclines within the mighty ocean of waters and hurled His great discus upon the hostile night-roving demons.

84.translit:
piraan un perumai piRar aar aRivaar?,
uraa ay ulakaLanta ñaanRu, - varaakat
teyiTRaLavu pOtaavaa REnkolO, entai
aDikkaLavu pOnta paDi?
84.meaning: O Lord, who else can know Your greatness? On the day You strode forth and measured the worlds, what could it be, my Father, that the span of Your feet that day did not even reach the measure of the boar's single tusk with which You once lifted the earth?

85.translit:
paDi kaND aRitiyE paampaNaiyi naan, puT
koDi kaND aRitiyE? kooRaay, - vaDivil
poRiyaintu muLLaDakkip pOtoDu nee rEnti,
neRininRa neñchamE nee.
85.meaning: O heart standing firm in the righteous path, drawing inward all five senses within your form, bearing flowers and water in worship, tell me: do you truly know Him who reclines on the serpent-couch? Do you truly know Him whose banner bears the bird Garuda? Speak!

86.translit:
neeyum thirumakaLum ninRaayaal, kunReDuttup
paayum panimaRaitta paNpaaLaa, - vaayil
kaDaikazhiyaa vuLpukaak kaamarpoong kOval
iDaikazhiyE paTRi yini.
86.meaning: O gracious Lord who lifted the mountain and held it as a shield against the rushing cold rain, You and the Goddess Lakshmi stand together now, holding fast to the threshold-hall of beautiful, flowering Tirukkovalur, neither passing out through the gate nor entering wholly within.

87.translit:
iniyaar pukuvaar ezhunaraka vaasal?
muniyaadu moorittaaL kOmin, - kanisaayak
kanReRinta thOLaan kanaikazhalE kaaNpataRku,
nankaRinta naavalam soozh naaDu.
87.meaning: Who now will enter the gateways of the seven hells? Without anger, take up and worship His mighty feet! The land girt by the rose-apple, the Jambu continent, has come to know well how to behold the resounding anklets of Him whose arms hurled the calf to bring down the fruits.

88.translit:
naaDilum ninnaDiyE naaDuvan, naaDORum
paaDilum nin pukazhE paaDuvan, sooDilum
ponnaazhi yEntinaan ponnaDiyE sooDuvERku,
ennaaki lennE enakku?
88.meaning: If I seek anything, I shall seek only Your feet; day after day, if I sing anything, I shall sing only Your praise; if I adorn my head with anything, I shall adorn it only with the golden feet of Him who holds the golden discus. For me who am thus, what can ever harm me, whatever befall?

89.translit:
enakkaa vaaraar oruvarE, emperumaan
tanakkaavaan thaanEmaT RallaaL, - punakkaayaam
poomEni kaaNap potiyavizhum poovaippoo,
maamEni kaaTTum varam.
89.meaning: Is there anyone who is solely for me? My Lord is for none but His own self alone. Yet the kaaya blossom of the field and the poovai flower, bursting open their buds, are granted the boon of revealing to me the dark, beautiful divine form so that I may behold it.

90.translit:
varattaal valininaintu maatava nin paatam,
chirattaal vaNangkaanaa menRE, - urattinaal
eerariyaay nErvaliyO naaya iraNiyanai,
Orariyaay nee yiDanta thoon?
90.meaning: O Madhava, was it not because Hiranya, swollen with strength through his boon and refusing to bow his head at Your feet, deemed himself a match for You, that You took the form of a single man-lion, twofold lion in fury, and tore open his body with Your mighty chest and claws?

91.translit:
oonak kurampaiyi nuLpuk kiruLneekki,
ñaanac chuDarkoLeei naaDORum, - Enat
turuvaa yulakiDanta voozhiyaan paatam,
maruvaadaark kuNDaamO vaan.
91.meaning: Entering within this hut of flesh, dispelling the darkness, and kindling day after day the lamp of wisdom within it — for those who do not cleave to the feet of the Lord of the aeons, who took the form of the boar and lifted up the earth, can the high heaven ever be attained?

92.translit:
vaanaakit theeyaay maRikaDalaay maarutamaay
tEnaakip paalaam thirumaalE, - aanaayicchi
veNNey vizhungka niRaiyumE, munnoru naaL
maNNai umizhnta vayiRu.
92.meaning: O Tirumal, who became the sky, the fire, the surging sea, the wind, and who are as sweet as honey and milk — could the belly that once spat forth the whole earth You had swallowed ever be filled by gulping down the cowherd-woman's butter?

93.translit:
vayiRazhala vaaLuruvi vantaanai yañcha
eyiRilaka vaaymaDutta then nee, - poRiyukiraal
poovaDiyai yeeDazhitta ponnaazhik kaiyaa, nin
chEvaDimE leeDazhinach cheTRu.
93.meaning: O You of the hand that bears the golden discus, who with sparkling claws crushed and tore Hiranya — was it not You who, baring Your gleaming fangs and opening Your mouth, made him who came drawing his sword tremble with his belly aflame, destroying him as he assailed the worship offered at Your sacred red feet?

94.translit:
cheTRezhuntu theevizhittuc chenRavinta Ezhulakum,
maTRivaiyaa venRuvaa yangkaantu, muTRum
maRaiyavaRkuk kaaTTiya maayavanai yallaal,
iRaiyEnum Ettaaden naa.
94.meaning: My tongue will not praise even a little anyone save the wondrous Lord who, rising in wrath with fire-flashing eyes, opened wide His mouth and showed to the brahmin boy all these seven worlds and more, asking, 'Are these here too?' — revealing all within Himself.

95.translit:
naavaayi luNDE namO naaraNaa enRu,
oovaa duraikku muraiyuNDE, - moovaata
maakkatikkaN chellum vakaiyuNDE, ennoruvar
theekkatikkaT chellum thiRam.
95.meaning: There is a tongue in the mouth; there are the words 'Namo Narayana' to be uttered ceaselessly without end; there is the way that leads to the great, unfading, supreme goal. Then how could anyone ever have the means to travel the evil path to hell?

96.translit:
thiRampaaden neñchamE. chengkaNmaal kaNDaay,
aRampaava menRiraNDu maavaan, - puRantaanim
maNDaan maRikaDaltaan maarutantaan, vaantaanE,
kaNDaay kaDaikkaT piDi.
96.meaning: O my heart that does not waver, behold — the lotus-eyed Lord is Himself the two, both virtue and sin; behold, He is the outer being too — this earth, the surging sea, the wind, and the sky itself are all He. Knowing this, hold fast to Him at the very last.

97.translit:
piDisEr kaLiRaLitta pEraaLaa, unRan
aDisEr ntaruLpeTRaaL anRE, - poDisEr
analkangkai yERRaan avirsaDaimEl paaynta,
punalkangkai yennumpErp pon.
97.meaning: O great Lord who graced the bull-elephant that sought its mate — was it not that the precious water-river called Ganga, which fell upon the gleaming matted locks of Him who bears the ash-smeared fire and the Ganga, attained grace by drawing near and reaching Your feet?

98.translit:
pontikazha mEnip purisaDaiyam puNNiyanum,
ninRulakam thaaya neDumaalum, - enRum
iruvarangkat taaltirivar Elum, oruvan
oruvanangkat tenRu muLan.
98.meaning: Though the holy one of golden-glowing form with twisted matted locks, and the great Lord who stood and measured the worlds, ever move about as two in their forms, yet one of them dwells always within the body of the other.

99.translit:
uLankaNDaay nanneñchE. uttaman enRum
uLankaNDaay, uLLoovaa ruLLat - tuLankaNDaay,
veLLatti nuLLaanum vEngkaDattu mEyaanum,
uLLatti nuLLanen ROr.
99.meaning: He exists, behold, O my good heart! The Supreme One exists forever, behold; He exists within the hearts of those who unceasingly think of Him, behold. Know that He who reclines within the milk-ocean and He who dwells at Venkatam is the very one who abides within your own heart.

0.translit:
enpiravi teera iRainjineen innamudaa
anbee thagaLi aLiththaanai nanpugazh seer
seedaththaar muththukkaL seerum kadanmallai
bhoodhaththaar ponnangazhal.
0.meaning: That my births may end, I have worshipped the golden, beautiful feet of Bhoodathalvar of Kadalmallai (Tirukkadalmallai), rich in good fame, where cool, lustrous pearls gather. He it was who, as sweet nectar, offered up love itself as the lamp.

1.translit:
anbee thaLiyaa aarvamee neyyaaga,
inburugu sindhai yiduthiriyaa, - nanburugi
gnaanach chudarviLak keeRRineen naaraNaRku
gnaanath thamizhburindha naan.
1.meaning: With love itself as the lamp-vessel, with fervent devotion as the ghee, and a heart melting in bliss as the wick, melting in goodness I lit the bright lamp of wisdom for Narayana - I who have rendered the Tamil of wisdom.

2.translit:
gnaanaththaal nankuNarndhu naaraNanRan naamangaL,
thaanaththaal maRRavan peer saaRRinaal, - vaanath
thaNiyamara raakkuvikku maḥdhanRee, naangaL
paNiyamarar koomaan parisu.
2.meaning: If one knows him well through wisdom, and with proper place and order proclaims the names of Narayana, that very deed will make him one of the serene immortals in heaven. Such is the grace of the Lord of the immortals whom we serve.

3.translit:
parisu naRumalaraal paaRkadalaan paadham,
purivaar pugazhpeRuvar poolaam, - purivaargaL
tholamarar keeLvith thulangoLiseer thooRRaththu
nallamarar koomaan nagar.
3.meaning: Those who, with fragrant flowers, devotedly worship the feet of the Lord of the Ocean of Milk shall, it seems, attain glory. Those devotees reach the city of the Lord of the good immortals, of resplendent radiant form, renowned in the sacred learning of the ancient celestials.

4.translit:
nagarizhaiththu niththilaththu naaNmalar koNdu, aangee
thigazhum aNivayiram seerththu, - nigarillaap
paingamala meendhip paNindheen panimalaraaL,
angamvalam koNdaan adi.
4.meaning: Building a shrine, with fresh-blossomed flowers of pearls and adding therein gleaming, beautiful diamonds, holding up peerless fresh lotuses, I bowed at the feet of the Lord who holds on the right side of his body Lakshmi of the cool flower (lotus).

5.translit:
adimoonRi livvulagam anRaLandhaay poolum
adimoon Rirandhavani koNdaay, - padininRa
neerootha meeni nedumaalee ninnadiyai
aaarootha vallaar aRindhu.
5.meaning: You measured this world long ago in three steps, it seems; begging for three feet of land you took the whole earth. O Nedumal of ocean-dark form with surging waves, who can ever know and fully extol your feet?

6.translit:
aRindhaindhu muLLadakki aaymalarkoNdu, aarvam
seRindha manaththaraaych chevvee, - aRindhavanRan
peeroothi yeeththum perundhavaththoor kaaNparee,
kaaarootha vaNNan kazhal.
6.meaning: Restraining the five senses within through knowledge, taking choice flowers, with hearts filled with fervent devotion, rightly knowing him and reciting and praising his name - such ones of great penance shall behold the feet of the Lord whose hue is like the dark sea.

7.translit:
kazheleduththu vaaymadiththuk kaNsuzhanRu, maaRRaar
azheleduththa sindhaiyaraay anja, thazheleduththa
pooraazhi eeththinaan ponmalarch cheevadiyai
ooraazhi nenjee uganadhu.
7.meaning: O my one heart, rejoice and meditate upon the golden-blossom-like sacred red feet of him who wields the battle-discus blazing with fire - at the sight of which his enemies, raising their legs to flee, biting their lips, eyes rolling, trembled with hearts set aflame.

8.translit:
ugandhunnai vaangi oLiniRangoL kongai
agamkuLira vuNNenRaaL aavi, - ugandhu
mulaiyuNbaay poolee munindhuNdaay, neeyum
alaipaNbaa laanamaiyaal anRu.
8.meaning: Rejoicing she took you up and said, 'drink that my bright-hued breast and my inner self may grow cool,' (giving) her very life. As if delighting to suckle the breast, you suckled it in wrath - because at that time even you became of a turbulent (destroying) nature.

9.translit:
anRadhukaN danjaadha aaychchi yunakkirangi,
ninRu mulaidhandha inneermaikku, anRu
varanmuRaiyaal neeyaLandha maakadalsoozh gnaalam,
perumuRaiyaa leydhumoo peerththu.
9.meaning: For the sweet tenderness of that cowherd-woman (Yashoda) who, seeing it then was unafraid and, pitying you, stood and gave her breast - can even the great world girt by the vast ocean, which you measured in due order long ago, ever match it, however greatly weighed again?

10.translit:
peerththanai maasagadam piLLaiyaay, maNNirandhu
kaaththanai pulluyirum kaavalanee, eeththiya
naavudaiyeen poovudaiyeen ninnuLLi ninRamaiyaal
kaavadiyeen patta kadai.
10.meaning: As an infant you kicked over and destroyed the great unclean cart; begging for the earth you protected even the lowliest lives, O Guardian. Having a tongue that praises you, having flowers (to offer), since I stood meditating upon you, I your servant shall be saved from the misery (of birth).

11.translit:
kadainin Ramarar kazhaltozhudhu, naaLum
idaininRa inbaththa raavar, pudaininRa
neerootha meeni nedumaalee, ninnadiyai
aaarootha vallaar avar.
11.meaning: O Nedumal of ocean-dark form with surging waves on every side - those who are able to fully extol your feet, those very ones, standing even at the threshold and worshipping the feet of the immortals, become ones of unbroken, ceaseless bliss day after day.

12.translit:
avarivaren Rillai aravaNaiyaan paadham,
evarvaNangi yeeththaadhaa reNNil, palarum
sezhungadhiroo neNmalaroon kaNNudhaloon anRee
thozhundhagaiyaar naaLum thodarndhu.
12.meaning: There is no distinction of 'these' or 'those'; if you count who all worship and praise the feet of the Lord who reclines on the serpent, are not the bright-rayed Sun, the eight-petalled-lotus-born Brahma, and the forehead-eyed Shiva among the many who are worthy to worship him, following him day after day?

13.translit:
thodareduththa maalyaanai soozhgayam pukkanjip
padareduththa paingamalam koNdu,an - Ridaradukka
aazhiyaan paadham paNindhanRee vaanavarkoon
paazhidhaa neydhiRRup paNdu.
13.meaning: When the great elephant (Gajendra), tugging at its chain, entered the surrounding pool and, terrified as calamity heaped upon it, took a stretching fresh lotus and bowed at the feet of the Lord who holds the discus - was it not thereby of old that even the King of the celestials (Indra) attained his great abode?

14.translit:
paNdip perumpadhiyai yaakki pazhipaavam
koNduingu vaazhvaaraik kooRaadhee, - eNdisaiyum
peerththakaram naangudaiyaan peeroothip peedhaigaaL,
theerththakarar aamin thirindhu.
14.meaning: Without praising those who, building this great city as their dwelling, live here amassing blame and sin, O foolish ones, reciting the name of the four-armed Lord who pervades all eight directions, go about and become purifiers (worthy of sacred waters).

15.translit:
thirindhadhu venjamaththuth theerkadavi, anRu
pirindhadhu seedhaiyaiman pinpooy, - purindhadhuvum
kaNpaLLi koLLa azhagiyadhee, naagaththin
thaNpaLLi koLvaan Ranakku.
15.meaning: He roamed driving the chariot in the fierce battle; once he was parted from Sita, going after the (golden) deer. And yet what is most fitting for him is to take his repose in sleep - for him who takes his cool rest upon the (serpent) Naga.

16.translit:
thanakkadimai pattadhu thaanaRiyaa neelum
manaththadaiya vaippadhaam maalai, - vanaththidarai
eeriyaam vaNNam iyaRRu midhuvallaal,
maariyaar peygiRpaar maRRu.
16.meaning: Even if Tirumal does not (seem to) know that one has become his servant, to fix him firmly in one's heart is the way; this alone can transform the calamity of the wilderness (of worldly existence) into a fertile lake - who else among the rain-clouds can pour down (such grace)?

17.translit:
maRRaa riyalaavar vaanavarkoon maamalaroon,
suRRum vaNangum thozhilaanai, - oRRaip
piRaiyirundha senjadaiyaan pinjenRu, maalaik
kuRaiyirandhu thaanmudiththaan koNdu.
17.meaning: Who else can equal him - him whom the King of the celestials (Indra) and the great-lotus-born (Brahma) all around bow to in worship? The red-matted-haired one (Shiva), bearing the single crescent moon, going as a humble suppliant and begging Tirumal to relieve his lack, took (the boon) and accomplished his purpose.

18.translit:
koNda dhulagam kuRaLuruvaayk kooLariyaay,
oNdiRa loon maarvath thugirvaiththadhu - uNdadhuvum
thaankadandha eezhulagee thaamaraikkaN maalorunaaL,
vaankadandhaan seydha vazhakku.
18.meaning: He took the worlds in the form of a dwarf; as the mighty man-lion he set his claws on the chest of the powerful one (Hiranyakashipu); and what he swallowed (at deluge) was the seven worlds that he himself had spanned - such are the deeds done by the lotus-eyed Tirumal who one day strode beyond the heavens.

19.translit:
vazhakkanRu kaNdaay valisagadam seRRaay,
vazhakkonRu neemadhikka veeNdaa, - kuzhakkanRu
theeviLavin kaaykkeRindha theemai thirumaalee,
paarviLangach cheydhaay pazhi.
19.meaning: It is not justice, see - O you who destroyed the strong cart (the asura Sakata)! You need not reckon any (such) justice. As the young calf (the asura Vatsasura) you hurled (against the wood-apple tree) striking down its fruit - that cruel deed, O Tirumal, you have done as a blame for all the world to see.

100.translit:
maale! nediyoone! kaNNanee, viNNavarkku
meelaa! viyanthuzhaayk kaNNiyanee, - meelaal
viLavinkaay kanrinaal veezhththavanee, enran
aLavanraal yaanudaiya anbu.
100.meaning: O Tirumal! O lofty one of immeasurable greatness! O Kannan! O supreme one exalted above the celestials! O wearer of the wondrous fragrant tulasi garland! O you who once hurled the calf to bring down the wood-apple fruit! The love that I bear for you is beyond all measure.

20.translit:
pazhipaavam kaiyakaRRip palkaalum ninnai,
vazhivaazhvaar vaazhvaraam maadhoo, - vazhuvinRi
naaraNanRan naamangaL nankuNarndhu nankeeththum,
kaaraNangaL thaamudaiyaar thaam.
20.meaning: Those who cast away sin and evil from their hands and follow your path through all ages shall truly live a blessed life. They are the ones who possess the very causes that lead them to know well and praise well, without fault, the holy names of Narayana.

21.translit:
thaamuLaree thammuLLam uLLuLadhee, thaamaraiyin
poovuLadhee yeeththum pozhudhuNdee, - vaaman
thirumaruvu thaaLmaroovu senniyaree, sevvee
arunaragam seerva dharidhu.
21.meaning: If they themselves exist, and their heart exists within, and the lotus flower exists, and there is time to offer praise, and they bow their heads to the feet that the Lord Vamana's beauty graces, then truly it is hard for such people to enter the dreadful hell.

22.translit:
ariya theLidhaagum aaRRalaal maaRRi,
peruga muyalvaaraip peRRaal, - kariyadhoor
veNkoottu maalyaanai venRumudith thanRee,
thaNkoottu maamalaraal thaazhndhu.
22.meaning: What is difficult becomes clear through strength. If one obtains those who strive to flourish by transforming themselves, it is like the dark, great elephant Gajendra with white tusks triumphed and accomplished its purpose by bowing low with a great cool blossom from the pond.

23.translit:
thaazhndhuvarang koNdu thakka vagaigaLaal
vaazhndhu kazhivaarai vaazhvikkum, - thaazhndha
viLanganikkuk kanReRindhu veeRRuruvaay, njaalam
aLandhadikkeezhk koNda avan.
23.meaning: He grants life to those who, bowing low and receiving boons, live and pass their days in fitting ways. He is the one who, in another form, flung the calf at the low-hanging wood-apple fruit, and who measured the world and held it beneath his feet.

24.translit:
avankaNdaay nannenjee aararuLum keedum,
avankaNdaa yaimpulanaay ninRaan, - avankaNdaay
kaaRRuththee neervaan karuva raimaN kaaroodha,
seeRRaththee yaavaanum senRu
24.meaning: Behold him, O good heart, who is the source of rare grace and of ruin. Behold him who stands as the five senses. Behold him who became the wind, fire, water, sky, the heavy mountains, the earth, the dark ocean, and even the fire of wrath that goes forth.

25.translit:
senRa dhilangaimeel sevveedhan seeRRaththaal,
konRa dhiraavaNanaik kooRungaal, - ninRadhuvum
veeyoongu thaNsaaral veengadamee, viNNavartham
vaayoongu tholpugazhaan vandhu.
25.meaning: When one tells how he marched upon Lanka and in his wrath slew Ravana, the place where he came and stood is Venkatam with its cool slopes where bamboo flourishes, he whose ancient fame is exalted in the mouths of the celestials.

26.translit:
vandhith thavanai vazhininRa aimboodham
aindhum agaththadakki yaarvamaay, - undhip
padiyamarar veelaiyaan paNdamararak keendha,
padiyamarar vaazhum padhi.
26.meaning: Worshipping him, drawing in the five elements that stood in the way and confining them within with devotion, one reaches the abode where the gods dwell in equal measure, the place of him who churned the ocean and gave nectar to the gods of old.

27.translit:
padhiyamaindhu naadip paruththezhundha sindhai,
madhiyurinjsi vaanmugadu nokki - kadhimiguththang
koolteedi yaadum kozhundhadhee poonRadhee,
maalteedi yoodum manam.
27.meaning: The mind that runs seeking Tirumal is like a creeping tendril that, having settled and grown thick and risen, brushes the moon and looks up to the vault of heaven, and with great speed seeks a support and sways about.

28.translit:
manaththuLLaan veengadhaththaan maagadalaan, maRRum
ninaippariya neeLarangath thuLLaan, - enaippalarum
dheevaadhi dheeva nenappaduvaan, munnorunaaL
maavaay piLandha magan.
28.meaning: He dwells within the heart, he dwells on Venkatam, he dwells in the great ocean, and also in the vast Srirangam that is hard to conceive. He is called by countless people the God of gods, he who in days of old, as a child, tore apart the mouth of the horse-demon.

29.translit:
maganaagak koNdeduththaaL maaNpaaya kongai,
akanaara vuNpanen RuNdu, - maganaiththaay
theeRaadha vaNNam thiruththinaay, thennilangai
neeRaaga eydhazhiththaay nee.
29.meaning: She took you up as her son and offered her glorious breast; you sucked it saying you would drink it until your inner self was full, and so you set it right that the mother could not recognize you as her child. You it was who shot down and destroyed southern Lanka, reducing it to ashes.

30.translit:
neeyan RulagaLandhaay neeNda thirumaalee,
neeyan Rulagidandhaa yenparaal, - neeyanRu
kaaroodham munkadaindhu pinnadaiththaay maagadalai,
peeroodha meenip piraan.
30.meaning: You it was who once measured the worlds, O exalted Tirumal; you it was, they say, who once raised the earth up; you it was who once churned the dark ocean of old and afterward dammed the great sea, O Lord whose form is like the vast surging waves.

31.translit:
piraanenRu naaLum perumbulari yenRum,
kuraanal sezhumboodhu koNdu, - varaagath
thaNiyuruvan paadham paNiyumavar kaNdeer,
maNiyuruvam kaaNbaar magizhndhu.
31.meaning: Those who, calling him Lord, daily at the great dawn take fine, fresh kura blossoms and bow at the feet of him who took the cool form of the Boar, they, behold, are the ones who shall joyfully behold his sapphire-hued form.

32.translit:
magizhndhadhu sindhai thirumaalee, maRRum
magizhndhadhun paadhamee poRRi, - magizhndha
dhazhalaazhi sanga mavaipaadi yaadum,
thozhilaagam soozhndhu thuNindhu.
32.meaning: My heart rejoiced, O Tirumal; further, it rejoiced in praising your feet alone. With resolve and embracing the work, it rejoiced surrounding and singing and dancing of those things, the blazing discus and the conch.

33.translit:
thuNindhadhu sindhai thuzhaayalangal, angam
aNindhavanpee ruLLaththup pal kaal, - paNindhadhuvum
veeypiRangu saaral viRalveeng kadavanaiyee,
vaaythiRangaL sollum vagai.
33.meaning: My heart resolved upon him who is adorned with the tulasi garland on his frame; many times within my heart it bowed to his name, and the way my mouth speaks bowed too, to the mighty Lord of Venkatam whose slopes gleam with bamboo.

34.translit:
vagaiyaa lavani yirandhaLandhaay paadham,
pugaiyaal naRumalaaraal munnee, - migavaayndha
anbaakki yeeththi yadimaippatteenunakku,
enbaakki yaththaal ini.
34.meaning: O you who by stratagem begged the earth and measured it, I have praised your feet of old with incense and fragrant flowers, making my love abundant, and have become your servant. By that fortune of mine I am now wholly yours.

35.translit:
inidhenbar kaamam adhanilum aaRRa,
inidhenbar thaNNeerum endhaay, - inidhenRu
kaamaneer veeLaadhu ninperu mai veetpareel,
seemanee raagum siRidhu.
35.meaning: They say desire is sweet, and they say cool water is even sweeter, O my Lord. But if, without longing for the waters of desire, people should yearn for your greatness, that yearning becomes, even a little, the water of true welfare and salvation.

36.translit:
siRiyaar perumai siRidhinka Neydhum,
aRiyaarum thaamaRiyaa raavar, - aRiyaamai
maNkoNdu maNNuNdu maNNumizhndha maayanenRu,
eNkoNdeen nenjee. iru.
36.meaning: The greatness of small ones is attained in a small measure; even those who do not know come to know not their own ignorance. O my heart, I have set my mind firmly on him, the wondrous one who took the earth, swallowed the earth, and spat out the earth. Abide thus.

37.translit:
irundhaN kamalath thirumalari nuLLee,
thirundhu thisaimuganaith thandhaay, - porundhiyanin
paadhanga LeeththipaNiyaaveel, palpiRappum
eedhanga Lellaa memakku.
37.meaning: From within the great cool lotus, the sacred flower, you brought forth the well-formed four-faced Brahma. If we do not praise and bow at your fitting feet, all the births that befall us will be nothing but afflictions for us.

38.translit:
emakken Rirunidhiyam eemaandhi raadhee,
thamakkenRum saarva maRindhu, - namakkenRum
maadhavanee yennum manampadaiththu maRRavanpeer
oodhuvadhee naavinaa looththu.
38.meaning: Do not be deluded thinking great treasures are ours; knowing what is forever a refuge to oneself, cultivate a mind that ever says "Madhava is ours," and let the reciting of his name with the tongue be your true recitation.

39.translit:
ooththin poruLmudivum iththanaiyee,
uththamanpeer eeththum thiRamaRimi neezhaigaaL,- ooththanai
valleereel nanRadhanai maatteereel, maadhavanpeer
solluvadhee ooththin surukku.
39.meaning: The final meaning of the scriptures is only this much, O ignorant ones: learn the way to praise the name of the Supreme One. If you are able in the scriptures, well and good; and if you are not able, then the very reciting of Madhava's name is the essence and summary of the scriptures.

40.translit:
surukkaaga vaangich sulaavininRu aiyaar
nerukkaamun neerninaimin kaNdeer, - tiruppolinda
aagattaan paadam aRindum, aRiyaada
bhoogattaal illai poruL.
40.meaning: Before the messengers of death surround and snatch you away abruptly, reflect and realize this, you people: there is no real worth in pleasures enjoyed in ignorance, even when one has come to know the feet of the Lord on whose chest the radiant Lakshmi resides. Turn instead to that knowledge.

41.translit:
poruLaal amarulagam pukkiyal laagaadu
aruLaal aRamaruLu manRE, - aruLaalE
maamaRaiyoorkku eenda maNivaNNan paadamE,
neemaRavEl nenjE ninai.
41.meaning: One cannot enter the world of the immortals by wealth; it is only by grace that righteousness yields its fruit, is it not? O my heart, do not forget the very feet of the sapphire-hued Lord who, out of grace, gave Himself to the great Vedic seers; meditate upon them.

42.translit:
ninaippan tirumaalai neeNdadoaL kaaNa,
ninaippaar piRappondrum nEraar, - manaippaal
piRandaar piRandeydum pErinba mellaam,
tuRandaar tozhudaar at tooL.
42.meaning: I meditate upon Tirumal so that I may behold His long arms; those who so meditate will face no further birth. All the great pleasures that householders born into worldly life are born to attain — those have been renounced by the ones who worship those arms.

43.translit:
tooLiraN deTTEzhum moondru mudiyanaittum,
taaLiraNdum veezha sarandurandaan, - taaLiraNdum,
aartozhuvaar paadam avaitozhuva tandrE en
seerkezhutooL seyyum siRappu?
43.meaning: He loosed His arrows so that all the heads upon the twice-eight-and-seven necks (the twenty crowns of Ravana) and the two feet fell to the ground. Is not the worship of His two feet truly the worship of the feet of those who themselves worship them? What glory then can my fine, well-formed shoulders accomplish, except this?

44.translit:
siRandaarkku ezhutuNaiyaam sengaNmaal naamam,
maRandaarai maanidamaa vaiyEn, aRamtaangum
maadhavanE ennum manampadaittu, maRRavanpEr
oaduvadE naavinaal uLLu.
44.meaning: The name of the red-eyed Tirumal is the unfailing support for those who are exalted. I will not count as human those who have forgotten Him. Cultivate a mind that calls out 'O Madhava who upholds righteousness,' and contemplate within that the very chanting of His name with the tongue is the goal.

45.translit:
uLaden RiRumaavaar uNdillai yendru,
taLardal adanarugum saaraar, - aLavariya
vEdattaan vEngadattaan viNNoar mudidhoayum,
paadattaan paadam payindru.
45.meaning: Those who practice and dwell upon the feet of Him — the Lord of the immeasurable Vedas, the Lord of Venkatam, whose feet the crowns of the celestials touch — neither swell with pride saying 'It exists,' nor come anywhere near despondency saying 'It is gone.' They remain steady through devotion to His feet.

46.translit:
payinRa tarangam tirukkoaTTi, pannaaL
payinRaduvum vEngadamE pannaaL, - payinRa
daNidigazhum soalai aNineer malaiyE
maNidigazhum vaNdadakkai maal.
46.meaning: The Lord of the lustrous broad bountiful hands, dark as a gem — the place He has long frequented is Thirukkottiyur, and over many days too Venkatam, and likewise the cool, beautiful Malirunjolai with its lovely waters and gleaming groves, the hill where He long abides.

47.translit:
maalai ariyuruvan paada malaraNindu,
kaalai tozhudezhumin kaikoali, - njaalam
aLandidan duNdumizhnda aNNalai maR Rallaal
uLangidanda vaaRRaal uNarndu.
47.meaning: Adorning yourselves with the lotus feet of Tirumal who took the man-lion form, rise at dawn with joined hands and worship Him. Realize within your hearts that there is none other than the Lord who measured the world, who dug it up, who swallowed it and spat it forth — worship none but Him.

48.translit:
uNarndaay maRainaangum oadinaay needhi
maNandaay malarmagaltooL maalE. - maNandaaypoay
vEyirum saaral viyalirun njaalamsoozh,
maayirun soalai malai.
48.meaning: O Tirumal, You comprehended all four Vedas; You proclaimed righteousness; You embraced the shoulders of the lotus-born Lakshmi. And You went and made Your abode upon Maalirunjolai hill, encircled by the vast wide world, with its slopes thick with bamboo.

49.translit:
malaiyEzhum maanilanga LEzhum adira,
kulaisoozh kuraikadalka LEzhum, - mulaisoozhnda
nanjurattup peNNai navindruNda naavanendru,
anjaaden nenjE azhai.
49.meaning: He of the tongue that, while the seven mountains and the seven great lands trembled and the seven roaring tossing seas shook, suckled and drained the demoness whose breasts oozed poison — O my heart, call upon Him without fear, naming Him thus.

50.translit:
azhaippan tirumaalai aangavargaL sonna,
pizhaippil perumpeyarE pEsi, - izhaippariya
aayavanE yaadhavanE endravanai aarmugappum,
maayavanE endru madhittu.
50.meaning: I call upon Tirumal, uttering the flawless great names that those (sages and devotees) have spoken — 'O Cowherd, O Yadava' — and before all faces I esteem Him, holding Him to be the wondrous Lord, the inconceivable one.

51.translit:
madhikkaNdaay nenjE maNivaNNan paadam,
madhikkaNdaay maRRavan pEr tannai, - madhikkaNdaay
pEraazhi nindru peyarndu kadalkadainda
neeraazhi vaNNan niRam.
51.meaning: See, O my heart, esteem the feet of the sapphire-hued Lord; see, esteem His name; see, esteem the hue of the Lord, colored like the watery ocean, who stirred up the great sea and churned it. Hold all these in high regard.

52.translit:
niRangariyan seyya nedumalaraaL maarvan,
aRamperiyan aara daRivaar? – maRampurinda
vaaLarakkan poalvaanai vaanavarkoan taanattu,
neeLirukkaikku uyttaan neRi.
52.meaning: Dark of hue is He, on whose chest dwells the red lotus-seated Lakshmi, and vast is His righteousness — who can fully know it? By His way He raised the demon-natured one (Vibhishana, brother of the sword-wielding Ravana bent on war) to the lasting high seat in the realm of Indra, king of the celestials.

53.translit:
neRiyaar kuzhaRkaTRai munnindru pindaazhndu,
aRiyaa diLangiriyendru eNNi, - piRiyaadu
poongodigaL vaigum porupunal kundrendrum,
vEngadamE yaamvirumbum veRpu.
53.meaning: Standing in front and trailing behind are the thick tresses of dense, beautiful curls — mistaking them in ignorance for a young dark hillock, the flowering creepers cling without parting, dwelling there as on a hill where battling waters flow. Such is Venkatam, the peak that we cherish.

54.translit:
veRpen Rirunjoalai vEngadamen RivviraNdum
niRpendru neemadhikkum neermaipoal, - niRpen
RuLangoayil uLLamvait tuLLinEn, veLLat
tiLangoayil kaividEl endru.
54.meaning: Just as You graciously deem the two hills — Maalirunjolai and Venkatam — to be Your standing abodes, so too I have set my heart as Your inner shrine and meditated, fixing it there as Your dwelling, praying: 'O Lord of the youthful shrine upon the flood, do not forsake it.'

55.translit:
endrum maRandaRiyEn EzhpiRappum eppozhudum,
nindru ninaippozhiyaa neermaiyaal, - vendri
adalaazhi koNda aRivanE, inbak
kadalaazhi neearuLik kaaN.
55.meaning: Never have I forgotten, through all seven births, and at every moment, by my nature of unceasing meditation upon You. O wise Lord who wields the victorious battle-discus, O ocean of bliss, deep as the sea, look upon me and grant Your grace.

56.translit:
kaaNak kazhikaadhal kaimikkuk kaaTTinaal,
naaNap padumendraal naaNumE? - pENik
karumaalaip ponmEni kaaTTaamun kaaTTum,
tirumaalai naangaL tiru.
56.meaning: If, longing greatly to behold Him, my overflowing love be displayed openly, will it be put to shame if one says it should feel shame? No — for before the dark Tirumal even shows His golden form, our Lakshmi, our wealth, lovingly reveals Him to us.

57.translit:
tirumangai nindraruLum deyvamnaa vaazhttum,
karumam kadaippidimin kaNdeer, - urimaiyaal
Ettinoam paadam irundadakkai endaipEr,
naaRRisaiyum kETTeerE naam?
57.meaning: Take firmly to heart, you people, the duty of the tongue's praising of the divine Lord on whom Lakshmi abides bestowing grace. By our rightful claim we have praised His feet; have we (made it so that) the names of my Father of the broad gathering hands are heard throughout the four directions?

58.translit:
naampeTRa nanmaiyum naamangai nannenjattu
oambi yirundemmai oaduvittu, - vEmbin
poruLneermai yaayinum ponnaazhi paadendru,
aruLneermai tanda aruL.
58.meaning: The good that we have received — that Lakshmi (Naamangai), cherishing us within our good hearts and dwelling there, made us recite His praise, saying 'Even if your nature be bitter as the neem, sing of the Lord of the golden discus' — such is the grace of His gracious nature, bestowed upon us.

59.translit:
aruLpurinda sindai adiyaarmEl vaittu,
poruLterindu kaaNkuTRa appoadu, - iruLtirindu
noakkinEn noakki ninaindEna toNkamalam,
oakkinEn ennaiyumang koarndu.
59.meaning: When He set His grace-bestowing mind upon His devotees, and I came at that moment, discerning the truth, to behold Him — the darkness dispelled, I gazed, and gazing I meditated upon His cool lotus feet, and offering even my own self there, I considered and surrendered.

60.translit:
Oruruvan allai oLiyuruvam ninnuruvam,
eeruruvan enbar irunilattoor, oruruvam
aadiyaam vaNNam aRindaar avarkaNDeer,
neediyaal maNkaappaar ninRu.
60.meaning: You are not of one form alone; your form is a form of light. Those of this wide earth say you are of two forms (Hari and Hara). But behold, those who have truly known you as the one primal form, as you were at the beginning, are the ones who, standing firm, righteously guard the earth.

61.translit:
ninRadoor paadam nilampuDaippa, neeNDatooL
senRaLanda tenbar disaiyellaam, - anRu
karumaaNi yaayiranda kaLvanee, unnaip
piramaaNit taarperRa peeRu.
61.meaning: One foot stood and struck the earth, while your long arm reached out and measured all the directions, so they say. O cunning one who once came begging disguised as a small dark Brahmin boy! Great is the reward gained by those who have taken you as their authority and measure.

62.translit:
peeRonRu munnaRiyeen peRRaRiyeen peedaiyaal,
maaRenRu solliviNangineen, - eeRin
perutterut tam kooDosiyap peNnasaiyin pinpooy,
eruttirunda nallaayar eeRu.
62.meaning: Out of folly I knew no boon before, nor had I gained one. Yet I bowed before you, calling you my refuge against all adversity. O bull-like lord of the good cowherds, who out of love for the maiden (Nappinnai) went after the bulls and broke the great humped necks and horns of those raging bulls!

63.translit:
eeReezhum venRaDartta endai, eriyuruvattu
eeReeRip paTTa iDusaapam - paaReeRi uNDatalai
vaayniRaiyak kooTTangai oNkurudi,
kaNDaporuL sollin kadai.
63.meaning: My Father conquered and subdued the seven bulls. As for Shiva, who rides the bull and bears a fiery form, the curse laid upon him made the skull cling to his hand; and that hand was filled to the brim with bright blood (until Vishnu's grace released it). If one were to tell the true matter as it is seen, this is the story.

64.translit:
kadaiyum perumporuLum kaNNaanin peeree,
idaya miruntavaiyee eettil, - kadaiyum
tirumozhiyaay ninRa tirumaalee unnaip,
parumozhiyaal kaaNap paNi.
64.meaning: O Kanna, your names are both the great epic narrative and the supreme essence! If one praises you, those names dwell within the heart. O Tirumal, who stand as the very sacred utterance that is the story itself, grant that I may behold you through fitting, weighty words of praise.

65.translit:
paNindeen tirumeeni paingamalam kaiyaal
aNindeenun seevaDimee lanbaay, - tuNindeen
purindeetti yunnaip pugaliDampaarttu, aangee
irundeetti vaazhum idu.
65.meaning: I bowed to your sacred form; with my hands I lovingly adorned your reddened feet with fresh lotus flowers. Seeking you as my refuge, I have resolved upon this: to praise you with devotion, and to live forever dwelling there, extolling you.

66.translit:
idukaNDaay nannenjee! ippiRavi yaavadu,
idukaNDaa yellaamnaa muRRadu, - idukaNDaay
naaraNanpee roodi nagarat tarukaNaiyaa,
kaaraNamum vallaiyeel kaaN.
66.meaning: Behold this, O good heart! This is what this birth is for. Behold, for us all has been fulfilled in this. Behold, draw near to the holy city, chanting the name of Narayana. And if you are able, perceive too the very cause of all things.

67.translit:
kaNDeen tirumeeni yaankanavil, aangavankaik
kaNDeen kanaluny suDaraazhi, - kaNDeen
uRunooy vinaiyiraNDum ooTTuvittu, pinnum
maRunooy seRuvaan vali.
67.meaning: I beheld his sacred form in my dream; there I beheld the blazing, radiant discus in his hand. And I beheld too the power by which he drives away both kinds of afflicting karma (good and bad deeds), and thereafter destroys the recurring affliction (of rebirth).

68.translit:
valimikka vaaLeyiRRu vaaLavuNar maaLa
valimikka vaaLvaraimat taaga, - valimikka
vaaNaagam suRRi maRugak kaDalkaDaindaan,
kooNaagam komposhitta koo.
68.meaning: He churned the ocean, making the mighty bright mountain (Mandara) the churning rod and coiling the mighty radiant serpent (Vasuki) around it so that it whirled, so that the powerful demons with sword-like fangs might perish. He is the Lord who broke the tusk of the crooked-bodied elephant (Kuvalayapida).

69.translit:
koovaagi maanilamkaattu, nangaNmugappee
maaveegis selginRa mannavarum - pooveegum
sengamala naabiyaan seevaDikkee yeezhpiRappum,
taNkamala meeyndaar tamar.
69.meaning: Even kings who rule as sovereigns guarding the great earth, who ride upon horses before our very eyes, are but the servants of those devotees who, through all seven births, have cleaved fast to the reddened feet of the Lord, from whose navel blossoms the lotus; they have attained the cool lotus feet.

70.translit:
tamaruLLam tanjai talaiyarangam taNkaal,
tamaruLLum taNporuppu veelai, - tamaruLLum
maamallai kooval madiTkuDandai yenbaree,
eevalla endaik kiDam.
70.meaning: The hearts of his devotees, Tanjai (Tanjaimamanikkoyil), supreme Arangam (Srirangam), Tankal; and among his devotees' beloved places, the cool hill (Tirumalai/Venkatam) and the sea (Tiruppparkadal); also Mamallai (Tirukkadalmallai), Koval (Tirukkovalur), and walled Kudandai (Tirukkudandai) - these, they say, are the abodes of my capable Father.

71.translit:
iDangai valampuriNin Raarppa, erikaan
RaDangaa roDunguvitta taazhi, - viDangaalum
teevaay aravaNaimeel toonRal disaiyaLappaan,
poovaa raDinimirnda poodu.
71.meaning: When the conch in his left hand resounds, and the discus that spews fire subdues and crushes the foes who would not submit - he who appears upon the serpent couch whose poison-spitting mouths blaze with fire - in the moment when he raised his flower-soft feet to measure all the directions (such is the Lord).

72.translit:
poodaRindu vaanarangaL poonjunaipukku, aangalarnda
poodarindu koNDeettum poodu, uLLam - poodu
maNiveeng kaDavan malaraDikkee sella,
aNiveeng kaDavanpee raayndu.
72.meaning: At the time when the monkeys, knowing the hour, enter the flowering springs and gather the blossoms that have opened there to offer worship - at that time, O heart, contemplating the names of the radiant Lord of Venkatam, go forth to the flower-soft feet of the jewel-hued Lord of Venkatam.

73.translit:
aaynduraippa naayirampeer aaynaDu vandivaay,
vaaynda malartoovi vaikalum, - eeynda
piRaikkooTTus sengaN kariviDutta pemmaan
iRaikkaaT paDattuNinda yaan.
73.meaning: I shall contemplate and recite his thousand names; choosing fitting flowers, with steadfast speech I shall scatter them in worship day after day. I have resolved to enter the service of the Lord who saved the red-eyed elephant (Gajendra) with its crescent-curved tusks.

74.translit:
yaanee tavam seydeen eezhpiRappum eppozhudum,
yaanee tavamuDaiyeen emperumaan, - yaanee
irundatamizhnan maalai iNaiyaDikkee sonneen,
perundamizhan nalleen peridu.
74.meaning: It is I who have performed penance, through all seven births and at every moment; it is I, O my Lord, who am rich in penance. For it is I who have uttered, at your twin feet, this fine garland of enduring Tamil verses. I am one good in great Tamil - exceedingly so.

75.translit:
perugu madaveezham maappiDikki munninRu,
iruka NiLamoongil vaangi, - arugirunda
teenkalandu neeTTum tiruveeng kaDamkaNDeer,
vaankalanda vaNNan varai.
75.meaning: Behold Tiruvenkatam, where the great rutting elephant, standing before its splendid mate, pulls down with both eyes-worth of care the tender bamboo shoots, mixes them with the honey nearby, and offers them to her - this is the hill of the Lord whose hue is mingled with the sky.

76.translit:
varaichanda nakkuzhmbum vaankalanum paTTum,
viraip polinda veNmal likaiyum - niraittukkoNDu
aadikkaN ninRa aRivan aDiyiNaiyee
oodip paNiva tooRum.
76.meaning: Gathering together mountain sandal paste, fine ornaments, silken garments, and fragrant white jasmine flowers, to chant the praises of and bow before the twin feet of the all-knowing One who stood at the very beginning of all things - this is the proper, befitting way.

77.translit:
uRungkaNDaay nannenjee! uttamannaR paadam,
uRungkaNDaay oNkamalan tannaal, - uRungkaNDaay
eettip paNindavan peer eerainjnjaa Reppozhudum,
saaRRi yuraittal tavam.
77.meaning: Draw near, O good heart - draw near to the good feet of the Supreme One! Draw near with the bright lotus (of devotion). Behold, the true penance is this: at all times to praise and bow, and to proclaim and recite his one thousand names.

78.translit:
tavamseydu naanmuganee peRRaan, taraNi
nivarndaLappa neeTTiyapoR paadam, - sivandadan
kaiyanaittu maarak kazhuvinaan, gangainneer
peydanaittup peermozhindu pin.
78.meaning: Having performed penance, it was Brahma (Naanmukan) who obtained it: the golden foot that the Lord stretched out to measure the whole earth as it rose. With both his reddened hands fully, Brahma washed that foot, pouring the waters of the Ganga upon it, and thereafter uttered the Lord's names.

79.translit:
pinninRu taayirappak keeLaan, perumpaNaittooL
munninRu taanirappaaL moymmalaraaL - sol ninRa
tooLnalandaan neerillaat toonRal, avanaLanda
neeNilandaan attanaikkum neer.
79.meaning: Even when the mother (the soul or the world) stands behind and pleads, he does not heed; yet Lakshmi (she of the swarming lotus) stands before him and herself intercedes. He is the peerless Lord whose great shoulders' renown stands celebrated in word, beyond compare. The vast earth that he measured is fit and equal to all of this (his glory).

80.translit:
neerndheen adimai ninaindheen thoNkamalam,
aarndheenun seevadimeel anbaay, - aarndha
adikkOlam kaNDavark kenkolO, munnaip
padikkOlam kaNDa pagal?
80.meaning: I have offered myself to You in servitude, and have meditated, bearing the lotus of devotion. With abounding love I have clung fast to Your sacred red feet. To those who have beheld the splendid adornment of those feet, what then is the day when they beheld Your earlier form that measured the world?

81.translit:
pagaRkaNDeen naaraNanaik kaNDeen, - kanavil
migakkaNDeen meeNDavanai meyyee - migakkaNDeen
oondhigazhum neemi olidhigazhum seevadiyaan,
vaandhigazhum soadhi vadivu.
81.meaning: By day I beheld Him, I beheld Narayana; in a dream too I beheld Him abundantly, Him who returns, truly I beheld Him in abundance. I beheld the One whose flesh-cleaving discus gleams, whose red feet shine with radiance, whose form is the effulgent light that glows in the heavens.

82.translit:
vadikkOla vaaLnedungaN maamalaraaL, sevvip
padikkOlam kaNDagalaaL pannaaL, - adikkOli
GnaalaththaaL pinnum nalampurindha thenkolO,
kOlaththaa lillai kuRai.
82.meaning: The great lotus-dwelling Lakshmi, with long sword-like beautiful eyes, beholding the fresh beauty of His form, would not part from Him for many days. Yet why, having sought refuge at His feet, did Earth-goddess too render Him loving service besides? In His beauty there is no lack whatsoever.

83.translit:
kuRaiyaaga venjoRkaL kooRineen kooRi,
maRaiyaang kenavuraiththa maalai, - iRaiyeenum
eeyungol enRee irundheen enaippagalum,
maayankaN senRa varam.
83.meaning: I have spoken many harsh and faulty words, and having spoken them, I beheld Him who is declared by the Vedas. Thinking 'Will He grant me even a little?' I waited through all my days for the boon that has gone toward the wondrous Lord, the Maayan.

84.translit:
varamgaru thiththannai vaNangaadha vanmai,
uramgarudhi moorkka thavanai, - naramgalandha
singamaayk keeNDa thiruvan adiyiNaiyee,
angaNmaa Gnaalath thamudhu.
84.meaning: The pair of feet of the glorious Lord who, taking the form of a man-lion (Narasimha), tore apart that fierce one (Hiranya) who, trusting in his strength and presuming on the boon, would not bow to Him - those very feet are the nectar of this vast and beautiful world.

85.translit:
amudhenRum theenenRum aazhiyaan enRum,
amudhanRu koNDugandhaan enRum, - amudhanna
sonmaalai yeeththith thozhudheen solappaTTa,
nanmaalai yeeththi navinRu.
85.meaning: Calling Him nectar, calling Him honey, calling Him the wielder of the discus, calling Him the One who once delightedly took up the nectar (at the churning) - with a garland of words sweet as nectar I have praised and worshipped Him, reciting and praising that good Lord who is sung of by all.

86.translit:
navinRu raiththa naavalarkaL naaNmalarkoNDu, aangee
payinRadhanaal peRRapayan enkol, - payinRaartham
meyththavaththaal kaaNpariya meegamaNi vaNNanai,yaan
eththavaththaal kaaNpankol inRu?
86.meaning: What is the fruit gained by the eloquent poets who, taking fresh blossoms, recited and worshipped there? Him, of the hue of a jewel-laden cloud, whom even those steeped in true penance can scarcely behold by their austerities - by what penance shall I behold Him this day?

87.translit:
inRaa vaRiginRee nalleen irunilaththaich
chenRaang kaLandha thiruvadiyai, - anRu
karukkOTTi yuLkidandhu kaithozhudheen kaNDeen,
thirukkOTTi endhai thiRam.
87.meaning: It is not only today that I have come to know the sacred feet that went forth and measured the wide earth there. Long ago, even while lying within the confines of the womb, I worshipped with folded hands and beheld the glory of my Father at Tirukkottiyur.

88.translit:
thiRambiR Riniyarindheen thennarangath thendhai,
thiRambaa varusenRaark kallaal, - thiRambaach
chedinaragai neekkiththaan selvadhanmun, vaanoor
kadinagara vaasaR kadavu.
88.meaning: Now I have understood the truth without fail: my Father at southern Srirangam, before He goes to remove the dense hell unfailingly, opens the door at the gateway of the fortified city of the celestials only for those who come to Him steadfast and unswerving.

89.translit:
kadhavik kadhanjiRandha kanjanai munkaayndhu,
adhavippOr yaanai osiththu, - padhaviyaayp
paaNiyaal neereeRRup paNDorukaal maavaliyai,
maaNiyaayk koNDilaiyee maN.
89.meaning: In olden days did You not, in wrath striking down Kanchan (Kamsa) whose rage had grown beyond bounds, and breaking the warring rutted elephant, and graciously, taking water poured upon Your hand, once take the earth from Mahabali by becoming a dwarf-brahmachari?

90.translit:
maNNulaga maaLeenee vaanavarkkum vaanavanaay,
viNNulagam thannagaththu meeveenee, - naNNith
thirumaalai senga nediyaanai, engaL
perumaanaik kaithozhudha pin.
90.meaning: After clasping and worshipping with my hands Tirumal, the red-eyed tall One, our great Lord - shall I not rule this world of earth? And being made even the god of the gods, shall I not dwell within the world of the heavens?

91.translit:
pinnaal arunaragam seeraamal peeduRuveer,
munnaal vaNanga muyalminoo, - pannool
aLandhaanaik kaarkkadalsoozh Gnaalaththai, ellaam
aLandhaa navanjee vadi.
91.meaning: O you who are troubled, lest you reach the cruel hell hereafter, strive to worship Him beforehand - Him who measured out (the truths of) the many scriptures, the One who measured all the earth girt by the dark ocean: worship His pure red feet.

92.translit:
adiyaalmun kanjanaich cheRRu,amara reeththum
padiyaan kodimeelpuL koNDaan, - nediyaanRan
naamamee eeththuminga Leeththinaal,thaamveeNDum
kaamamee kaaTTum kadidhu.
92.meaning: He who long ago slew Kanchan (Kamsa), He whose nature is praised by the immortals, He who bears the bird (Garuda) upon His banner, the tall One - praise only His name! If you praise it, it swiftly grants the very desire that you seek.

93.translit:
kadidhu kodunaragam piRkaalum seigai,
kodidhen RadhukooDaa munnam, - vadisangam
koNDaanaik koondhalvaay keeNDaanai, kongainanj
chuNDaanai eeththuminoo uRRu.
93.meaning: Cruel hell awaits hereafter; the deeds (that lead there) are evil - before that befalls you, worship with devotion Him who took up the well-shaped conch, who tore apart Kesi by the mouth seizing his mane, who sucked the poison from the breast (of Putana).

94.translit:
uRRu vaNangith thozhumin, ulageezhum
muRRum vizhungum mugilvaNNam, - paRRip
porundhaadhaan maarbidandhu poompaa dagaththuL
irundhaanai, eeththumen nenju.
94.meaning: Draw near, bow, and worship Him of the cloud-dark hue who swallows all the seven worlds entirely; who tore open the breast of the one (Hiranya) that seized Him in enmity and would not submit; and who is seated within Poompaadagam. O my heart, praise Him!

95.translit:
ennenja meeyaanen senniyaan, thaanavanai
vannenjang keeNDa maNivaNNan, - munnamseey
oozhiyaa noozhi peyarththaan, ulageeththum
aazhiyaan aththiyoo raan.
95.meaning: He who dwells in my heart, who is upon my head; the sapphire-hued One who tore apart the hard heart of the demon (Hiranya); He who of old, being the Lord of the aeons, turned the aeons about; the discus-bearing One whom the world praises - He is the Lord of Attiyur (Tiruvattiyur / Kanchi Atthigiri).

96.translit:
aththiyoo raanpuLLai yoorvaan, aNimaNiyin
thuththiseer naagaththin meelthuyilvaan, - mooththee
maRaiyaavaan maakadalnanj chuNDaan RanakkumiRaiyaavaan engaL piraan.
96.meaning: The Lord of Attiyur, who rides upon the bird (Garuda); who reposes upon the serpent (Adisesha) bright with hooded jewel-spots; who is the ancient sacrificial fire and the Vedas; who is Lord even to Siva who drank the poison of the great ocean - He is our Lord.

97.translit:
engaL perumaan imaiyoor thalaimagannee,
senga nedumaal thirumaarbaa, - pongu
padamookki naayiravaayp paambaNaimeel seerndhaay,
kudamookkil kOyilaak koNDu.
97.meaning: You are our great Lord, the chief of the celestials; the red-eyed Tirumal, bearer of Lakshmi upon Your sacred chest. You recline upon the serpent-couch of a thousand mouths with swelling raised hoods, having taken Kudandhai (Tirukkudandhai / Kumbakonam) as Your temple.

98.translit:
koNDu vaLarkkak kuzhaviyaayth thaanvaLarndhadhu,
uNDa thulageezhu muLLodunga, - koNDu
kudamaadik kOvalanaay meevi,en nenjam
idamaagak koNDa iRai.
98.meaning: As an infant taken to be reared He grew of His own accord; He ate, drawing the seven worlds within Himself; and taking up pots He danced (the kudakkoothu) as a cowherd - the Lord who has made my heart His abode.

99.translit:
iRaiyem perumaan aruLenRu, imaiyoor
muRaininRu moymmalarkaL thoova, - aRaikazhala
seevadiyaan senga nediyaan, kuRaLuruvaay
maavadivil maNkoNDaan maal.
99.meaning: While the celestials, standing in due order, scatter dense flowers crying 'Grace us, O Lord, our great One!' - He of the red feet with sounding anklets, the red-eyed tall One, Tirumal, took the earth in the form of a great dwarf.

0.translit:
seeraarum maadath tirukkOvalUr adhanuL
kaaraar karumugilaik kaaNappukku Oraa
tirukkaNdEn ena uraitha seeraan kazhalE
uraik kaNdaay nenjE ugandhu.
0.meaning: O heart, in renowned Tirukkovalur of stately mansions, the one who went forth to behold the dark, cloud-hued Lord, and uttered "I have seen the auspicious form!" -- speak you with joy of the feet of that glorious one. Praise his anklet-adorned feet, my heart, with delight.

1.translit:
tirukkaNdEn ponmEni kaNdEn thigazhum
arukkan aNiniRamum kaNdEn serukkiLarum
ponnaazhi kaNdEn purisangam kaikkaNdEn
ennaazhi vaNNanpaal inRu.
1.meaning: This day, in my ocean-hued Lord I have beheld Lakshmi (Tiru); I have seen his golden form; I have seen the radiant complexion fair as the sun. I have seen the war-rousing golden discus; I have seen the coiled conch in his hand.

2.translit:
inRE kazhal kaNdEn Ezh piRappum yaan aRuthEn,
ponthOy varai maarvil poonthuzhaay, - anRu
tirukkaNdu koNda tirumaalE, unnai
marukkaNdu koNdEn manam.
2.meaning: This very day I have seen your feet, and I have severed my seven births. O Tirumal who wears the flowering tulasi upon your mountain-like chest gleaming with gold, who long ago received Lakshmi (Tiru) -- I have grasped you within my mind's fragrance and held you fast in my heart.

3.translit:
manathuLLaan maagadal neeruLLaan, malaraaL
tanathuLLaan thaNduzhaay maarban, - sinathuch
serunar ugach seRRu ugandha thEngOdha vaNNan,
varunaragam theerkkum marundhu.
3.meaning: He dwells within the mind, he dwells within the waters of the great sea, he dwells in the bosom of Lakshmi who was born on the lotus, he who wears the cool tulasi upon his chest. The Lord, hued like the sweet-rolling ocean waves, who in wrath joyfully destroyed his foes so that they perished, is the very medicine that cures the hell that comes to us.

4.translit:
marundhum poruLum amudhamum thaanE,
tirundhiya sengaN maal aangE, - porundhiyum
ninRu ulagam uNdu umizhndhum neer ERRum moovadiyaal,
anRu ulagam thaayOn adi.
4.meaning: He himself is the medicine, the very substance, and the nectar -- the perfect red-eyed Tirumal is all these. The feet of him who long ago measured the worlds with three strides, having stood firm, swallowed and spat forth the worlds, and received the water of donation -- these are his feet.

5.translit:
adivaNNam thaamarai anRu ulagam thaayOn,
padivaNNam paarkadal neer vaNNam, - mudivaNNam
Oraazhi veyyOn oLiyum aqdhanRE
aaraazhi koNdaaRku azhagu.
5.meaning: The hue of the feet of him who long ago measured the worlds is the lotus; the hue of his form is the hue of the water of the milky sea; the hue of his crown is the very radiance of the single-wheeled fierce sun. Is not such beauty fitting for him who holds the deep ocean-like discus?

6.translit:
azhagu anRE aazhiyaaRku aazhineer vaNNam,
azhagu anRE aNdam kadaththal, - azhagu anRE
angai neer ERRaaRku alarmElOn kaal kazhuva,
gangai neer kaanRa kazhal.
6.meaning: Is it not beautiful, the sea-hued complexion belonging to him who bears the discus? Is it not beautiful, the spanning beyond the cosmic egg? Is it not beautiful, the anklet-adorned foot of him who received the water in his open palm -- the foot which, when the lotus-born Brahma washed it, gushed forth the waters of the Ganga?

7.translit:
kazhal thozhudhum vaa nenjE. kaarkadal neer vElai,
pozhil aLandha puLLooridhich selvan, - ezhil aLandhu
angu eNNaRku ariyaanai epporutkum sEyaanai,
naNNaRku ariyaanai naam.
7.meaning: Come, O heart, let us worship his feet. The Lord who rides the bird (Garuda), who measured the groves girt by the dark sea-waters -- he who is hard to fathom in his measureless splendor, who is far beyond all things, who is hard for us to approach. Let us worship him.

8.translit:
naamam pala solli naaraayaNaa enRu,
naa mangaiyaal thozhudhum nannenjE. - vaa, maruvi
maNNulagam uNdu umizhndha vaNdaRaiyum thaNduzhaay,
kaNNanaiyE kaaNga nam kaN.
8.meaning: Uttering his many names, and saying "Narayana!", let us worship him with the tongue that is the goddess of speech, O good heart. Come, draw near -- may our eyes behold none but Kannan, who wears the cool tulasi where bees hum, who swallowed and spat forth the world of earth.

9.translit:
kaNNum kamalam kamalamE kaiththalamum,
maN aLandha paadhamum maRRavaiyE, - eNNil
karumaa mugil vaNNan kaarkadal neer vaNNan,
thirumaa maNi vaNNan thEsu.
9.meaning: If one reflects, the radiance of the great precious sapphire-hued Lord -- dark like a great cloud, hued like the waters of the dark sea: his eyes are the lotus, his palms too are very lotuses, and the feet that measured the earth are likewise lotuses.

10.translit:
thEsum thiRalum thiruvum uruvamum,
maasil kudippiRappum maRRavaiyum - pEsil
valam purindha vaaJsangam koNdaan pEr Odha,
nalam purindhu senRu adaiyum nanRu.
10.meaning: If one would speak of radiance, valor, fortune, form, blameless noble birth and all else -- by chanting the names of the Lord who holds the desirable right-coiled conch, all good will be inclined toward us and come and abide with us, surely.

11.translit:
nanku Odhu naalvEdhath uLLaan naRu viriyum
pongu Odhu aruvip punal vaNNan, - sangu Odhap
paaRkadalaan paambaNaiyin mElaan, payinRu uraippaar
nooRkadalaan nuNNaRivinaan.
11.meaning: He dwells within the four Vedas that are nobly chanted; he is hued like the water of the surging cascade whose fragrance spreads. He of the milk-ocean where the conch resounds, who reclines upon the serpent-couch, is the ocean of scriptures of those who study and recite, and he is of subtle, fine wisdom.

12.translit:
aRivennum thaaL koLuvi aimpulanum thammil,
seRivennum thiNkadhavam semmi, - maRaiyenRum
nanku Odhi nanku uNarvaar kaaNbarE, naadORum
paingOdha vaNNan padi.
12.meaning: Fixing the bolt that is wisdom, and shutting firm the strong door that is the restraint of the five senses within themselves, those who ever chant the Vedas well and comprehend them well -- they indeed will behold, day after day, the very nature of the Lord hued like the fresh green ocean wave.

13.translit:
padivattath thaamarai paNdu ulagam neer ERRu,
adivattaththaal aLappa neeNda - mudivattam,
aagaaya moodaRuththu aNdam pOy neeNdadhE,
maagaayamaay ninRa maaRku.
13.meaning: For Tirumal who stood as the vast cosmic body -- when long ago, having received the water (of donation) over the lotus-shaped world, he measured it with his disc-like feet -- his crown's circle stretched, piercing through the sky and reaching far beyond the cosmic egg.

14.translit:
maaRpaal manam suzhippa mangaiyar thOL kaividdu,
nooRpaal manam vaikka noyvidhaam, - naaRpaala
vEdhaththaan vEngadaththaan viNNOr mudi thOyum,
paadhaththaan paadham paNindhu.
14.meaning: For the mind to whirl toward Tirumal and let go of the shoulders of women, and to set itself upon the path of the scriptures, becomes easy -- by bowing at the feet of him who is in the four-fold Vedas, who is in Venkatam, whose feet the crowns of the celestials touch.

15.translit:
paNindhu uyarndha pauvap padu thiraigaL mOdha,
paNindha paNi maNigaLaalE - aNindhu, ang
anandhan aNaikkidakkum ammaan, adiyEn
mananthan aNaikkidakkum vandhu.
15.meaning: As the swelling waves of the deep sea, rising and bending, beat upon him, the Lord who reclines, adorned, upon the couch of Ananta the serpent with its bowed, gem-bearing hoods -- that Lord comes and reclines upon the couch that is this servant's heart.

16.translit:
vandhu udhaiththa veNdiraigaL sempavaLa veNmuththam
andhi viLakkum aNi viLakkaam, - endhai
oruvalli thaamaraiyaaL onRiya seer maarvan,
tiruvallikkENiyaan senRu.
16.meaning: The white waves that come surging up serve as a fine lamp at dusk, kindling red coral and white pearls. My father, on whose glorious chest abides the lotus-dwelling Lakshmi the one creeper -- he is the Lord of Tiruvallikkeni, to whom let us go.

17.translit:
senRa naaL sellaadha sengaN maal engaL maal,
enRa naaL ennaaLum naaLaagum, - enRum
iRavaadha endhai iNaiyadikkE aaLaay,
maRavaadhu vaazhththuga en vaay.
17.meaning: The red-eyed Tirumal, our beloved Lord, over whom the passing days hold no sway -- on whatever day one says his name, that day becomes a true day, every day. Becoming a servant to the paired feet of my Father who never perishes, may my mouth praise him unforgettingly.

18.translit:
vaay mozhindhu vaamananaay maavalipaal, moovadi maN
nee aLandhu koNda nedumaalE, - thaaviya nin eJsaa
iNaiyadikkE Ezh piRappum aaLaagi,
aJsaadhirukka aruL.
18.meaning: O great Tirumal who, as Vamana, speaking the word to Mahabali, measured and took three feet of earth -- grant your grace that, becoming a servant through all seven births to your unfailing paired feet that strode across the worlds, I may abide without fear.

19.translit:
aruLaadhu ozhiyumE aalilai mEl, anRu
theruLaadha piLLaiyaaych chErndhaan, iruLaadha
sindhaiyaraaych chEvadikkE semmalar thooyk kaithozhudhu,
mundhaiyaraay niRparkku mun.
19.meaning: Will he indeed withhold his grace -- he who long ago, as an unknowing infant, lay upon the banyan leaf? To those who, with minds free of darkness, scatter fresh flowers at his red feet, worship with folded hands, and stand foremost as devotees, he will surely not deny his grace.

100.translit:
saarvu namakkenRum chakkaraththaan, thaNthuzhaayth
taarvaazh varaimaarpan thaanmuyangum, - kaaraarntha
vaanamaru minnimaikkum vaNdaamarai neduNkaN,
thEnamarum poomEl thiru.
100.meaning: She who dwells upon the lotus flower abounding in honey, with long eyes like the broad lotus, eyes that gleam like the lightning that lingers in the dark rain-laden sky — She, Lakshmi, is the one whom the Lord with the discus embraces, He whose mountain-like chest bears the garland of cool tulasi where she resides. He is forever our sole refuge.

20.translit:
munnulaga muNDumizhndaaykku, avvulaga
meeraDiyaal pinnaLandu kooDal peritonRee? - ennee
tirumaalee senga NeDiyaanee, engaL
perumaanee neeyidanaip peesu.
20.meaning: O Tirumal who once swallowed and brought forth the worlds again, is it so great a feat for You to then measure those worlds with two strides? What is this? O Tirumal, O red-eyed tall one, O our Lord, You Yourself speak and tell us this.

21.translit:
peesuvaa revvaLavu peesuvar, avvaLavee
vaasa malartuzhaay maalaiyaan, - teesuDaiya
sakkarattaan sanginaan saarngattaan, pongarava
vakkaranaik konRaan vaDivu.
21.meaning: However much those who praise may speak, only to that extent can the form be told of Him who wears the fragrant blossoming tulasi garland. He bears the radiant discus, the conch, and the Saarnga bow, and He is the one who slew the demon Vakkaran (Sambara) of the writhing-serpent rage.

22.translit:
vaDivaar muDikooTTi vaanavarkaL, naaLum
kaDiyaar malartoovik kaaNum - paDiyaanai,
semmaiyaal uLLurugic chevvanee nenjamee,
meymmaiyee kaaNa virumbu.
22.meaning: O heart! Long to truly behold Him whom the celestials, bowing their well-formed crowns, daily worship by scattering fragrant flowers to obtain His vision. Melting within in uprightness and with sincerity, desire to see Him as He truly is.

23.translit:
virumbiviN maNNaLanda anjiRaiya vaNDaar
surumbu toLaiyilsen Rooda, arumbum
punanduzhaay maalaiyaan ponnang kazhaRkee,
manamtuzhaay maalaay varum.
23.meaning: He who, in His desire, measured the heavens and the earth wears a garland of fresh wild tulasi, where beautiful-winged bees and droning beetles enter its hollows and hum. O let my mind, turning churned and love-maddened, come to His beautiful golden anklet-adorned feet.

24.translit:
varungkaal irunilanum maalvisumbum kaaTRum,
nerungkutee neeruruvu maanaan, - porundum suDaraazhi
yonRuDaiyaan soozhkazhalee, naaLum
toDaraazhi nenjee tozhudu.
24.meaning: He became the wide earth, the great sky, the wind, the close-pressing fire, and the form of water. He who holds the one fitting radiant discus - O heart, daily worship and cling to His encircling feet, bowing low.

25.translit:
tozhudaal pazhuduNDee tooneeruleagam,
muzhuduNDu moyguzhaaL aaycchi, - vizhuduNDa
vaayaanai maalviDaiyeezh seTRaanai, vaanavarkkum
seeyaanai nenjee siRandu.
25.meaning: Is there any fault if we worship Him, O heart, with eagerness - Him who swallowed whole the pure-watered world, who as the dense-tressed cowherdess Yashoda's beloved had His mouth smeared with the butter He ate, who subdued the seven great bulls, and who is far beyond even the celestials?

26.translit:
siRandaven sindaiyum sengaN aravum,
niRaindaseer neeLkacchi yuLLum, - uRaindaduvum,
veengkaDamum veqkaavum veeLukkaip paaDiyumee,
taamgaDavaar taNDuzhaa yaar.
26.meaning: He of the cool tulasi garland abides and never departs from my devoted heart, from the red-eyed serpent (Adisesha), from the vast Kanchi full of glory, from Venkatam, from Vehka, and from Velukkai-padi. These are the places He dwells in.

27.translit:
aaree tuyaruzhandaar tunbuTRaar aaNDaiyaar,
kaaree malinda karung kaDalai,- neeree
kaDaindaanaik kaaraNanai, neeraNaimeel paLLi
aDaindaanai naaLum aDaindu.
27.meaning: Who has ever suffered grief or fallen into misery among those who daily take refuge in Him - in Him who churned the dark cloud-laden ocean, the primal cause, who reclines upon His couch upon the waters? None at all.

28.translit:
aDainda taravaNaimeel aivarkkaay, anRu
miDaindadu paarata vemboor, - uDaindaduvum
aaycchipaal mattukkee ammanee, vaaLeyiTRup
peeycchipaa luNDa piraan.
28.meaning: Reclining upon the serpent couch, for the sake of the five (Pandavas) the fierce Bharata war was once joined and waged. Yet, O wonder! - that very Lord, who drank the milk of the fanged demoness (Putana), was made to break down and yield before the cowherdess's (Yashoda's) churning rod.

29.translit:
peeycchipaa luNDa perumaanaip peerndeDuttu,
aaycchi mulaikoDuttaaL anjaadee, vaaytta
iruLaar tirumeeni inbavaLac chevvaay,
teruLaa mozhiyaanaic cheerndu.
29.meaning: The cowherdess (Yashoda), unafraid, took up and embraced the great Lord who had drunk the milk of the demoness, and gave Him her breast. Drawing close to Him whose divine body is dark like fitting darkness, whose lovely lips are like sweet coral, and whose speech is clear and gracious.

30.translit:
seernda tirumaal kaDalkuDandai veengkaDam
neerndaven sindai niRaivisumbu, - vaaynda
maRaiyaa Dagamananatan vaNDuzhaayk kaNNi,
iRaipaaDi yaaya ivai.
30.meaning: Tirumal who has united with me - these are His places: the milk-ocean, Kudandai (Kumbakonam), Venkatam, my fitting heart, the full heavens, the well-suited Tirunaraiyur (Tiruvadigam), Anantapuram, and He of the bee-swarmed tulasi crown - all sung as the Lord's abodes.

31.translit:
ivaiyavan kooyil iraNiyana taagam,
avaisey tariyuruva maanaan, - sevideriyaa
naagattaan naalveedat tuLLaan, naRaveeTRaan
paagattaan paaRkaDalu Laan.
31.meaning: These are His temples, and He took the man-lion form, tearing apart the body of Hiranya. He rests upon the serpent (Adisesha) whose ears cannot be reckoned, He dwells within the four Vedas, He has on one side the one fragrant with toddy (or Shiva), and He reclines in the milk-ocean.

32.translit:
paaRkaDalum veengkaDamum paambum panivisumbum,
nooRkaDalum nuNNula taamaraimeel, - paaRpaT
Dirundaar manamum iDamaagak koNDaan,
kurundosittha koopaa lagan.
32.meaning: The milk-ocean, Venkatam, the serpent, the cool sky, the ocean of scriptures, the subtle lotus (of Brahma), and the hearts of those who have become devoted to Him - all these He took as His dwelling places: He, the cowherd Gopala who broke the kurundu tree.

33.translit:
paalanaay aalilaimeel paiya, ulagellaam
meelorunaa LuNDavanee meymmaiyee, - maalava
mantirattaal maaneerk kaDalkaDaindu, vaanamudam
andarattaark keendaaynee anRu.
33.meaning: O You who, as an infant gently resting upon a banyan leaf, once long ago swallowed up all the worlds - this is the truth! By a great deceptive scheme (or by the mighty Mandara mantra) You churned the great-watered ocean and gave the celestial nectar to the dwellers of the heavens that day.

34.translit:
anRiv vulagam aLanda asaiveekol,
ninRirundu veeLukkai neeNagarvaay, - anRu
kiDandaanaik keeDilsee raanai,mun kanjaik
kaDandaanai nenjamee kaaN.
34.meaning: Is it from the weariness of measuring this world that day that He stands and rests in the great city of Velukkai? O heart, behold Him who once reclined (upon the waters), Him of imperishable glory, who long ago overcame Kamsa.

35.translit:
kaaNkaaN enavirumbum kaNgaL, kadirilagu
pooNDaar agalattaan ponmeeni, - paaNkaN
tozhilpaaDi vaNDaRaiyum tongalaan, semboR
kazhalpaaDi yaamtozhudum kai.
35.meaning: My eyes long, saying 'let us see, let us see,' for His golden form, His broad chest adorned with radiant gleaming jewels. He of the garland upon which bees hum as if singing in melodious craft - let us worship His pure-golden anklet feet, singing with folded hands.

36.translit:
kaiya kanalaazhi kaarkkaDalvaay veNsangam,
veyya kadaisaarngam venjuDarvaaL, seyya
paDaiparava paazhi pani nee rulagam,
aDiyaLanda maayan avaRku.
36.meaning: In His hands are the fiery discus, the white conch from the dark ocean, the fierce mace, the Saarnga bow, and the fierce-radiant sword - these red weapons spread in glory around Him, the Maayan (wondrous Lord) who measured with His foot the spacious cool-watered world. To Him (I belong).

37.translit:
avaRkaDimaip paTTeen agattaan puRattaan,
uvakkum karungkaDalnee ruLLaan, tuvarkkum
pavaLavaayp poomagaLum panmaNippoo Naaram,
tigazhum tirumaarvan taan.
37.meaning: I have become the servant of Him who is within and without, who dwells in the joyful dark ocean's waters. He bears on His divine chest the lotus-born Lady (Lakshmi) of coral lips, along with the many-jeweled flower-garland that shines there.

38.translit:
taanee tanakkuvaman tannuruvee evvuruvum,
taanee tavavuruvum taaragaiyum, - taanee
erisuDarum maalvaraiyum eNDisaiyum, aNDat
tirusuDaru maaya iRai.
38.meaning: He alone is His own likeness; His own form is every form. He Himself is the form of penance and the stars; He Himself is the blazing flame, the great mountains, the eight directions, and the two luminaries (sun and moon) of the cosmos - the Lord who became all.

39.translit:
iRaiyaay nilanaagi eNDisaiyum taanaay,
maRaiyaay maRaipporuLaay vaanaay - piRaivaaynda
veLLat taruvi viLangkolineer veengkaDattaan,
uLLatti nuLLee uLan.
39.meaning: As the Lord, He became the earth, the eight directions, the Vedas, the inner meaning of the Vedas, and the heavens. He of Venkatam, with its crescent-touching flooding waterfalls of bright-sounding waters, dwells within my very heart.

40.translit:
uLan kaNDaay nan nenjee, uttaman enRum
uLan kaNDaay, uLLuvaar uLLattu uLan kaNDaay,
viN neDungak koDu uyarum veeng karuvi veengkaDattaan,
maN neDungat taan aLanda man.
40.meaning: He exists, behold, O my good heart; the Supreme One ever exists, behold. He dwells, behold, in the hearts of those who meditate on Him. He of Venkatam, with its swelling cascades and peaks that rise high toward the vast sky, is the King who once measured out the whole wide earth.

41.translit:
mannu maNi muDi neeNDu aNDam poy eN disaiyum,
tunnu pozhil anaittum suuzh kazhalee, - minnai
uDaiyaakak koNDu anRu ulagu aLandaan, kunRum
kuDaiyaaga aagaattha koo.
41.meaning: His enduring jeweled crown stretched up and reached the cosmos, and His sacred feet encompassed all the dense forests of the eight directions. Long ago He wore the lightning as His garment and measured the worlds. He is the King who once held up a mountain as an umbrella to shelter the cattle.

42.translit:
koovalanaay aanirisaikaL meyttu kuzhal uudi,
maavalanaayk keeNDa maNi vaNNan,
meevi ari uruvam aagi iraNiyanadu aagam,
teri ugiraal keeNDaan sinam.
42.meaning: As the cowherd He grazed the herds of cattle and played the flute, and as the mighty one the sapphire-hued Lord tore apart the demon. Taking on the form of the man-lion in His wrath, He split open the breast of Hiranya with His sharp claws.

43.translit:
sinamaa mada kaLiRRin tiN maruppaic saayttu,
punam meya boomi adanai, - tanamaagap peer agalattu
uLLoDukkum peer aaram maarvanaar,
oor agalattu uLLadu ulagu.
43.meaning: He broke down the strong tusks of the raging, rutting elephant. The Lord whose broad chest, adorned with the great necklace, draws within itself this earth covered with fertile fields holds the entire world within His single embrace.

44.translit:
ulagamum uuzhiyum aazhiyum, oN keezh
alar kadirum sen teeyum aavaan, pala kadirkaL
paarittha paim pon muDiyaan aDi iNaikkee,
puurittu en nenjee puri.
44.meaning: He becomes the worlds, the aeons, the ocean, the bright-rayed blossoming sun, and the red fire. O my heart, swell with devotion and turn wholly toward the twin feet of Him whose fresh-golden crown radiates many beams of light.

45.translit:
purindu mada veezham maappiDiyoDu uuDi,
tirindu sinattaal porudu, virinda seer veN koTTu
muttu udirkkum veengkaDamee, meel oru naaL
maN koTTuk koNDaan malai.
45.meaning: At Venkatam the rutting tusker, sulking with its great mate and roaming about, fights in fury so that pearls scatter from its broad, splendid white tusks. This is the hill of Him who once, taking the form of a boar, lifted the earth upon His tusk.

46.translit:
malai mugaDu meel vaittu vaasukiyaic suRRi,
talai mugaDu taan oru kai paRRi, alai mugaD
aNDam poy neer teRippa anRu kaDal kaDaindaan,
piNDamaay ninRa piraan.
46.meaning: Setting the mountain peak above, winding the serpent Vasuki around it, and grasping its crest with one hand, He once churned the ocean so that crests of waves rose up and the water splashed toward the cosmos. He is the Lord who stood as the very substance of all.

47.translit:
ninRa perumaanee neer eeRRu, ulagu ellaam
senRa perumaanee sengkaNNaa, - anRu
turaga vaay keeNDa tuzhaay muDiyaay, naangaL
naraga vaay keeNDaayum nee.
47.meaning: O great Lord who stood firm, who received the water and pervaded all the worlds, O red-eyed one! O tulasi-crowned one who once tore apart the mouth of the horse-demon! It is You who tore open the jaws of hell for us as well.

48.translit:
nee anRee neer eeRRu ulagam aDi aLandaay,
