nee anRee ninRu nirai meyttaay - nee anRee
maa vaay uram piLandu maa marudin uuDu poy,
deevaasuram porudaay seRRu?
48.meaning: Was it not You who received the water and measured the worlds with Your feet? Was it not You who stood and grazed the herds? Was it not You who split open the strong mouth of the horse-demon, passed between the great marudu trees, and waged and won the war of the gods and demons, destroying the foes?

49.translit:
seRRaduvum seeraa iraNiyanaic senRu eeRRup
peRRaduvum maa nilam, pinnaikkaay - muRRal
muri eeRRin mun ninRu moymbu ozhittaay,
muuric suri eeRu sangkinaay suuzhndu.
49.meaning: You went forth and confronted the unyielding Hiranya and destroyed him, and You won the great earth. For Pinnai's sake You stood before the fierce, full-grown bulls and quelled their strength. O Lord who holds the great spiralling conch!

50.translit:
suuzhnda tuzhaay alangal sooti maNi muDi maal,
taazhnda aruvit taDa varai vaay, - aazhnda
maNi neerc sunai vaLarnda maa mudalai konRaan,
aNi neela vaNNattavan.
50.meaning: Tirumal of the radiant jeweled crown, garlanded with encircling tulasi, He of beautiful dark-blue hue, slew the great crocodile that had grown in the deep, gem-clear pool by the broad mountain where cascades pour down.

51.translit:
avanee aru varaiyaal aanirisaikaL kaattaan,
avanee aNi marudam saayttaan, - avanee
kalangaap poru nagaram kaaTTuvaan kaNDeer,
ilangaa puram erittaan eydu.
51.meaning: It was He who guarded the herds of cattle with the steep mountain; it was He who felled the lovely marudu tree; it is He, behold, who will show us the unshakable city of liberation. It was He who, with His arrows, burned the city of Lanka.

52.translit:
eydaan maraamaram eezhum iraamanaay,
eydaan am maan maRiyai eendizhaikkaay, - eydaduvum
ten ilangaik koon veezhac senRu kuRaL uruvaay
mun nilam kaik koNDaan muyanRu.
52.meaning: As Rama He shot through the seven sal trees; for the lovely-ornamented one He shot at the deer-fawn. He shot too so that the king of southern Lanka fell, and earlier, taking the dwarf's form, He earnestly took the earth into His hand.

53.translit:
muyanRu tozhu nenjee, muuri neer veelai,
iyanRa marattu aali ilaiyin meelaal, - payinRu angu oor
maN nalam koL veLLattu maayak kuzhaviyaay,
taN nalangal maalaiyaan taaL.
53.meaning: Strive and worship, O my heart, the feet of the Lord garlanded with cool, fragrant blossoms, who as the wondrous infant lay upon a banyan leaf afloat on the surging flood that swallowed the goodly earth, in the great waters of the boundless ocean.

54.translit:
taaLaal sakaDam udaittup pagaDu undi, keeLaa
marudiDai poyk keezhalaay, - meeLaadu
maN agalam keeNDu angu oor maadu uganda maarvaRku,
peN agalam kaadal peridu.
54.meaning: With His foot He kicked apart the cart and shoved aside the demon-bull, passed unbreaking between the marudu trees, and as the boar tore up and lifted the wide earth. For Him, on whose chest one beloved lady delights, great is His love for the bosom of the Goddess.

55.translit:
periya varai maarvil peer aaram puuNDu,
kariya mugil iDai min pola, - teriyungkaal
paaN oDunga vaNDu aRaiyum pangkayamee, maRRu avan tan
neeN neDung kaN kaaTTum niRam.
55.meaning: On His broad mountain-like chest He wears the great necklace, like lightning amid a dark cloud. When one looks closely, it is the lotus where bees hum, their music subsiding, that shows the hue of His long, large eyes.

56.translit:
niRam veLidu seydu pasidu karidu enRu,
iRai uruvam yaam aRiyoom eNNil, - niRaivuDaiya
naamangkai taanum nalam pugazha vallaLee,
poo mangkai keeLvan polivu.
56.meaning: Whether His complexion is white, or green, or dark — if we consider it, we do not know the Lord's true form. Can even the all-accomplished Goddess of Speech fully praise the splendor of the consort of the lotus-dwelling Lakshmi?

57.translit:
polindu irun_da kaar vaanil minnee pol toonRi,
malindu tiru irunda maarvan, - polindu
karuDan meel koNDa kariyaan kazhalee,
teruDan meel kaNDaay teLi.
57.meaning: On whose chest Lakshmi abides in abundance, appearing like lightning in the dark, resplendent sky — the dark-hued Lord who rides gloriously upon Garuda. Behold and grow clear in spirit at His sacred feet.

58.translit:
teLinda silaa talattin meel irunda mandi,
aLinda kaDuvanaiyee nookki, - viLangiya
veN madiyam taa ennum veengkaDamee, meel oru naaL
maN madiyil koNDu ugandaan vaazhvu.
58.meaning: At Venkatam the she-monkey, seated upon the smooth rock-slab, gazes at her loving mate and bids him fetch the bright white moon. This is the abode of the Lord who once gladly took the earth by His cleverness.

59.translit:
vaazhum vagai aRindeen mai pol neDu varai vaay,
taazhum aruvi pol taar kiDappa, - suuzhum
tiru maa maNi vaNNan sengkaN maal, engaL
perumaan aDi seerap peRRu.
59.meaning: I have come to know the way to truly live, having attained the feet of our Lord — the great sapphire-hued one whom Lakshmi surrounds, the red-eyed Tirumal, on whose collyrium-dark, mountain-like form His garland lies like a cascading waterfall.

60.translit:
peRRam piNaimarutham peeymulai maacchakaḍam,
muRRakkaath thooḍupoo yuNḍuthaiththu, - kaTRuk
kuNilai viLaṅkaNikkuk koNḍeRindhaan, veTRip
paNilamvaay vaiththugandhaan paNḍu.
60.meaning: In days of old He guarded the cattle, broke through the twin marudam trees, sucked the life from the demoness's breast, kicked apart the great cart-demon, and devoured it all. Taking a calf as a staff He flung it to bring down the wood-apple fruit. And He joyfully set the victorious conch to His mouth and sounded it.

61.translit:
paNḍellaam veeṅkaḍam paaRkaḍal vaikundham,
koNḍaṅ kuRaivaarkkuk koayilpoal, - vaNḍu
vaLaṅkiLarum neeḷchoalai vaNpooṅ kaḍikai,
iLaṅkumaran RanviN Nakar.
61.meaning: For those who in old times took as their abode Venkatam, the Milk-Ocean, and Vaikuntha and dwelt there as in a temple, there is now Kadigai with its long groves where bees swarm in abundance and beautiful flowers bloom. This is the heavenly city of the youthful Lord.

62.translit:
viNNakaram veḥkaa virithiraineer veeṅkaḍam,
maNNakaram maamaaḍa veeLukkai, maNNakaththa
thenkuḍandhai theenaar thiruvaraṅkam thenkoaṭṭi,
thankuḍaṅkai neereeTRaan thaazhvu.
62.meaning: Vinnagar, Vehka, Venkatam of the wide rolling waters, Mannagar, Velukkai of great mansions, southern Kudandhai upon the earth, honey-filled Srirangam, and southern Tirukkottiyur. These are the humble dwellings of Him who received the water of pledge in the hollow of His palm.

63.translit:
thaazhchaḍaiyum neeNmuḍiyum oNmazhuvum chakkaramum,
choozharavum ponnaaNum thoaNRumaal,- soozhum
thiraNḍaruvi paayum thirumalaimeel endhaikku,
iraNḍuruvu monRaay ichaindhu.
63.meaning: The flowing matted locks and the tall crown, the bright battle-axe and the discus, the encircling serpent and the golden sacred thread all appear together. For my Father upon the sacred hill, where thick streaming waterfalls cascade all around, the two forms blend and join harmoniously as one.

64.translit:
ichaindha aravamum veRpum kaḍalum,
pachaindhaṅ kamudhu paḍuppa, - achaindhu
kaḍaindha varuththamoa kachchiveḥ kaavil,
kiḍandhirundhu ninRadhuvum aṅku.
64.meaning: The fitting serpent, the mountain, and the ocean were joined together, and there the nectar was churned out. Was it the weariness of that swaying churning that made Him lie down, sit, and stand there at Tiruvehka in Kanchi?

65.translit:
aṅkaR kiḍarinRi andhip pozhudhaththu,
maṅka iraNiyana thaakaththai,- poṅki
ariyuruva maayppiLandha ammaa navanee,
kariyuruvam komposiththaan kaayndhu.
65.meaning: So that no harm might come to that child Prahlada, in the twilight hour He swelled up, took the form of the man-lion, and tore apart the body of Hiranya so that it perished. That very Lord, in anger, broke off the tusk of the dark elephant.

66.translit:
kaayndhiruḷai maTRik kadhirilaku maamaNikaḷ,
eeyndha paNakkadhirmeel vevvuyirppa, - vaayndha
madhukai ḍavarum vayiruruki maaNḍaar,
adhukee ḍavarkkiRudhi aaṅkee.
66.meaning: Dispelling the darkness, when the rays of the great gleaming gems set upon the hoods of the radiant serpent breathed forth their heat, even the strong Madhu and Kaitabha melted within and perished. That destruction was the end for those who do harm, right there.

67.translit:
aaṅku malarum kuviyumaa lundhivaay,
oaṅku kamalaththi noNpoadhu, - aaṅkaith
thikiri suḍarenRum veNchaṅkam, vaanil
pakaru madhiyenRum paarththu.
67.meaning: There the bright bud of the lofty lotus upon His navel opens and closes, mistaking the discus in His hand for the blazing sun, and the white conch for the moon that shines in the sky.

68.translit:
paarththa kaḍuvan sunaineer nizhaRkaNḍu, peerththoar
kaḍuvanenap peerndhu, - kaarththa
kaḷaṅkaNikkuk kaineeṭṭum veeṅkaḍamee, meenaaḷ
viḷaṅkaNikkuk kanReRindhaan veRpu.
68.meaning: At Venkatam the male monkey, seeing its own reflection in the spring water and taking it for another monkey, recoils, then reaches out its hand toward the dark-ripened kalanga fruit. This is the hill of Him who long ago flung the calf to bring down the wood-apple fruit.

69.translit:
veRpenRu veeṅkaḍam paaḍum, viyanthuzhaayk
kaRpenRu sooḍum karuṅkuzhal meel, maRponRa
neeNḍadhoaḷ maalkiḍandha neeḷkaḍalnee raaḍuvaan,
pooNḍanaa ḷellaam pukum.
69.meaning: Praising Venkatam as the hill, and wearing the wondrous tulasi upon their dark tresses as a sign of their chaste vow, the women, all the days they have undertaken it, go to bathe in the vast ocean where reclined Tirumal of the long arms that crush wrestlers.

70.translit:
pukumadhaththaal vaaypoosik keezhthaazhndhu, aruvi
ukumadhaththaal kaalkazhuvik kaiyaal, mikumadhaththeen
viNḍamalar koNḍu viRalveeṅ kaḍavanaiyee,
kaNḍu vaNaṅkum kaḷiRu.
70.meaning: The elephant rinses its mouth with the gushing rut-fluid, bows low, washes its feet with the rut that flows down like a waterfall, and with its trunk takes the flowers opened with honey rich and abundant. Then it beholds and worships the mighty Lord of Venkatam.

71.translit:
kaḷiRu mukilkuththak kaiyeḍuth thoaḍi,
oḷiRu maruppoçikai yaaḷi, - piḷiRi
vizha,konRu ninRadhirum veeṅkaḍamee, meenaaḷ
kuzhakkanRu koNḍeRindhaan kunRu.
71.meaning: At Venkatam, the lion-beast (yali) that has broken off the gleaming tusks of an elephant which raised its trunk and ran to gore the clouds, makes it trumpet and fall, slays it, and stands roaring. This is the hill of Him who long ago flung the young calf as a weapon.

72.translit:
kunRonRi naaya kuRamakaḷir koalva Laikkai,
chenRu viḷaiyaaḍum theeṅkazhaipoay, - venRu
viḷaṅkumadhi koaḷviḍukkum veeṅkaḍamee,
meelai iḷaṅkumarar koamaan iḍam.
72.meaning: At Venkatam the bangled-armed hunter-women, born of one of the hills, go and play, and the sweet bamboo grows tall, conquering and releasing the shining moon from its eclipse. This is the abode of the Lord of the ancient youthful celestials.

73.translit:
iḍamvalam eezh pooNḍa iraviththee roaṭṭi,
vaḍamuka veeṅkaḍaththu mannum, - kuḍamnayandha
kooththanaay ninRaan kuraikazhalee kooRuvadhee,
naaththan naaluḷḷa nalam.
73.meaning: He drives the sun's chariot yoked to the seven horses ranged left and right, and abides firmly at northern Venkatam. To extol with the tongue the resounding anklets of Him who stood as the dancer that delighted in the pot-dance, this is the highest good that the tongue can yield.

74.translit:
nalamee validhukol nañchooṭṭu vanpeey,
nilamee puraNḍu poay veezha , - chalameedhaan
veṅkoṅkai yuNḍaanai meeṭṭaaycchi yooṭṭuvaan,
thankoṅkai vaayvaiththaaḷ saarndhu.
74.meaning: Was His benign nature stronger, or her power? The cruel demoness Putana, who came to feed Him poison, rolled upon the ground and fell dead. Yet by His grace the cowherd mother Yashoda, to suckle Him whom that one had tried to feed, drew near and set her own breast to His mouth.

75.translit:
saarndhakaḍu theeyppath thaḍaaviyakoaṭ ṭucchivaay
oorndhiyaṅkum veNmadhiyi noNmuyalai, - seerndhu
sinaveeṅkai paarkkum thirumalaiyee, aayan
punaveeṅkai naaRum poruppu.
75.meaning: It is the sacred hill where the angry vengai tree, rubbing against the nearby clouds, gazes intently at the bright hare seen moving within the white moon that rides over its spreading high branches. It is the mountain that breathes the fragrance of the wild vengai blossom of the Cowherd.

76.translit:
poruppiḍaiyee ninRum punalkuḷiththum, aindhu
neruppiḍaiyee niRkavumneer veeNḍaa - viruppuḍaiya
veḥkaavee seerndhaanai meymmalarthooyk kaithozhudhaal,
aḥkaavee theevinaikaḷ aayndhu.
76.meaning: You need not stand upon mountains, plunge into waters, or stand amid the five fires. If with sincere flowers you offer worship and bow your hands to Him who has settled at the beloved Tiruvehka, your evil deeds will surely wither away, examined and undone.

77.translit:
aayndha arumaRaiyoan naanmukaththoan
nankuRaṅkil vaayndha kuzhaviyaay vaaḷarakkan, - eeyndha
muḍippoadhu moonReezhan ReNNinaan, aarndha
aḍippoadhu naṅkaṭ karaN.
77.meaning: As an infant resting fittingly upon the good lap of the four-faced Brahma, lord of the deep, well-pondered Vedas, He counted out the ten times three plus seven blossom-like crowns of the sword-bearing demon (Ravana). His abundant lotus-feet are our refuge.

78.translit:
araNaam namakkenRum aazhi valavan,
muranaaḷ valamsuzhin tha moympan, - charaNaameel
eedhukadhi eedhunilai eedhupiRap pennaadhee,
oadhukadhi maayanaiyee oarththu.
78.meaning: The discus-wielder of the strong right hand is forever our fortress; the mighty one who on the day of Mura's slaying whirled His discus rightward. Since He is our refuge, do not ask what fate, what state, what birth will come; meditate upon the wondrous Lord, the way of salvation.

79.translit:
oarththa manaththaraay aindhaḍakki yaaraaayndhu,
peerththaal piRappeezhum peerkkalaam, - kaarththa
viraiyaar naRundhuzhaay veeṅkoadha meeni,
niraiyaara maarvanaiyee ninRu.
79.meaning: With a contemplative mind, subduing the five senses and inquiring fully, one can drive away all the seven births. Standing firm upon Him whose body is dark like the swelling sea, garlanded with fragrant fresh tulasi, who bears rows of ornaments upon His chest, this can be done.

80.translit:
nindrethi raaya niraimaNitthEr vaaNanthOL,
ondriyaveerai gnaaru udanthuNiya - vendrilangum
aarpaduvaan nEmi aravaNaiyaan sEvadikkE,
nErpaduvaan thaanmuyalum nenju.
80.meaning: The mind labours to attain the sacred feet of the Lord who reclines on the serpent and wields the resounding, victorious discus—the same Lord who severed in a single stroke the thousand arms of Banasura, who came against him with his rows of jewel-studded chariots. To that Lord's red feet my heart strives to draw near.

81.translit:
nenjaal ninaippariya nElum nilaipetrEn
nenjamE pEsaay ninaikkungkaal, nenjatthup
pEraathu nirkum perumaanai enkolO,
oraathu nirpa thuNarvu.
81.meaning: Though He is hard to grasp even by the mind, I have gained a firm hold. O my heart, speak—when one truly contemplates Him, the great Lord stands unmoving within the heart. Why then does my understanding stand without realizing Him?

82.translit:
uNaril uNarvariyan uLLam pugundhu
puNarilum kaaNpariya nuNmai, - iNaraNaiyak
konganaindhu vaNdaraiyum thaNthuzhaayk kOmaanai,
engaNaindhu kaaNdum ini.
82.meaning: Even if one strives to know Him, He is hard to know; though He enters and unites with the soul, His true nature is hard to behold. Where now shall we go to draw near and see that Lord adorned with the cool tulasi garland, upon whose clustered blossoms bees gather for the fragrance and hum?

83.translit:
iniyavan maayan enavuraippa rElum,
iniyavan kaaNpariya nElum, - iniyavan
kaLatthaal maNkoNdu viNkadandha paingazhalaan,
uLLatthi nuLLE yuLan.
83.meaning: Though they declare that He, the sweet one, is the wondrous Maya, and though that sweet one is hard to behold, still that sweet one—He of the fresh, beautiful anklets who by guile took the earth and strode across the heavens—dwells within, in the very depths of the heart.

84.translit:
uLanaaya naanmaraiyin utporuLai, uLLath
thuLanaagath thErndhuNarva rElum, - uLanaaya
vaNdaa maraineduNkaN maayavanai yaavarE,
kaNdaa rugappar kavi.
84.meaning: Though men discern and realize in their hearts the inner meaning of the four Vedas, in which He abides, who indeed can compose poetry pleasing enough, having truly seen that wondrous Lord with long eyes like the fresh lotus?

85.translit:
kaviyinaar kaipunaindhu kaNNaar kazhalpOy,
seviyinaar kELviyaraays sErndhaar, - puviyinaar
pOtri yuraikkap poliyumE, - pinnaikkaay
Etruyirai attaan ezhil.
85.meaning: The beauty of the Lord who, for the sake of Nappinnai, slew the life of the bulls by mastering them—when poets fashion verses with their hands, when men's eyes feast on His feet and their ears are filled with hearing of Him, and when the people of the earth praise Him in song, that beauty shines forth all the more glorious.

86.translit:
ezhilkoNdu minnuk kodiyeduthu, vEgath
thozhilkoNdu thaanmuzhangith thOndrum, - ezhil koNda
neermEga manna nedumaal nirampOla,
kaarvaanam kaattum kalandhu.
86.meaning: Taking on splendour, raising the lightning as its banner, thundering with swift and forceful action, the rain-cloud appears. Like the hue of the great Lord Tirumal who resembles that beautiful water-laden cloud, the dark monsoon sky reveals Him, blending all together.

87.translit:
kalandhu maNiyimaikkum kaNNaa, ninmEni
malarndhu maragathamE kaattum, - nalandhigazhum
kondhinvaay vaNdaraiyum thaNthuzhaayk kOmaanai,
andhivaan kaattum athu.
87.meaning: O Kanna, whose eyes glimmer mingled like gems, Your form blossoming forth reveals the very emerald. That Lord adorned with the cool tulasi garland—upon whose clusters, bright with goodness, bees hum—the evening sky reveals as it spreads its glow.

88.translit:
athunan ridhutheethen raiyap padaadhE,
madhunindra thaNthuzhaay maarvan, - podhunindra
ponnan kazhalE thozhumin, muzhuvinaigaL
munnan kazhalum mudindhu.
88.meaning: Do not fall into doubt over 'this is good' and 'that is evil'; worship the golden, lovely feet—shared by all—of the Lord whose chest bears the cool tulasi dripping with honey. Then all your accumulated sins, fulfilling their course, will slip away beforehand.

89.translit:
mudindha pozhudhil kuRavaaNar, Enam
padindhuzhusaal painthinaigaL vittha, - thadindhezhundha
vEynkazhaipOy viNthiRakkum vEngadamE, mEloru naaL
theenguzhalvaay vaitthaan silambu.
89.meaning: Venkatam—where the hunter folk, in the furrows that the wild boars wallowed and ploughed, sow fresh millet, and the tall bamboo shoots, springing up cleared of growth, rise to pierce the sky—is the mountain of Him who once long ago set the sweet flute to His lips.

90.translit:
silambum seRikazhalum sendrisaippa, viNNaa
ralambiya sEvadipOy, aNdam - pulambiyathOL
eNdisaiyum soozha idampOdhaa thenkolO,
vaNduzhaay maalaLandha maN.
90.meaning: When His anklets and close-fitting warrior rings rang out as He went, His red feet that stirred the river of the heavens reached the cosmic egg, and His outstretched arms encircled and resounded through the eight directions—why then was the earth that the tulasi-garlanded Tirumal measured not space enough?

91.translit:
maNNuNdum pEychchi mulaiyuNdu maatraadhaay,
veNNey vizhunga veguNdu, aaychchi - kaNNik
kayitrinaal kattatthaan kattuN dirundhaan,
vayitrinO daatraa magan.
91.meaning: Having swallowed the earth, and drunk the breast of the demoness Putana unaltered, when He gobbled the butter and the cowherd mother in anger bound Him with a knotted rope, He stood there bound—the child whose belly was never sated.

92.translit:
maganoruvark kallaadha maamEni maayan,
maganaa mavanmagandran kaadhal - maganai
siRaiseydha vaaNanthOL setraan kazhalE
niRaiseydhen nenjE ninai.
92.meaning: The wondrous Lord of the great form who is the son of none in particular, whose son was Pradyumna, and whose grandson's beloved son Aniruddha was imprisoned—He shattered the arms of Banasura who held him captive. O my heart, contemplate fully His sacred feet.

93.translit:
ninaitthulagil aartheLivaar neeNda thirumaal,
anaitthulagum uLLodukki aalmEl, - kanaitthulavu
veLLatthOr piLLaiyaay meLLath thuyindraanai,
uLLatthE vainenjE uyttu.
93.meaning: Who in the world, by mere thinking, can clearly comprehend the vast Tirumal who drew all the worlds within Himself and, as a small child upon a banyan leaf, gently slept upon the roaring, surging waters of the deluge? O my heart, take Him in and set Him within you.

94.translit:
uyttuNar vennum oLikoL viLakkEtri,
vaitthavanai naadi valaippaduthEn, - metthenavE
nindraa nirundhaan kidandhaanen nenjatthu,
pondraamai maayan pugundhu.
94.meaning: Kindling the radiant lamp called true realization, I sought the Lord set therein and caught Him in my net. Softly the wondrous Maya entered, and that He might never perish, He stood, He sat, He reclined within my heart.

95.translit:
pugundhilangum andhip pozhudhagatthu, ariyaay
igazhndha iraNiyana thaagam, sugirndhengum
sindhap piLandha thirumaal thiruvadiyE
vandhitthen nenjamE vaazhtthu.
95.meaning: In the glowing twilight hour, taking the form of the man-lion, He clawed and split the body of the scornful Hiranyan so that it was torn and scattered everywhere. O my heart, salute and praise the sacred feet of that very Tirumal.

96.translit:
vaazhtthiya vaayaraay vaanOr maNimagudam
thaazhtthi vaNangath thazhumbaamE, - kEzhtha
aditthaa maraimalarmEl mangai maNaaLan,
aditthaa maraiyaam alar.
96.meaning: When the celestials, with praising mouths, bow down their jewelled crowns so that their crowns grow worn from worship—the lotus feet of the consort of the Lady seated upon the radiant lotus blossom become the very blooming lotus flowers (of refuge).

97.translit:
alareduttha vundhiyaan aangezhi laaya,
malareduttha maamEni maayan, - alareduttha
vaNNatthaan maamalaraan vaarsadaiyaa nendrivargat
keNNatthaa naamO imai.
97.meaning: He from whose navel the lotus sprang and who is there resplendent, the wondrous Lord of the great form who took on a blossoming radiance—can He ever be encompassed by the thought of Brahma of the lotus-form, of like brilliance, or of the long-matted Shiva and such ones, even for the blink of an eye?

98.translit:
imanjoozh malaiyum iruvisumbum kaatrum,
amanjoozhn dharaviLangith thOndrum, - namanjoozh
naragatthu thammai naNugaamal kaappaan,
thuragatthai vaaypiLandhaan thottu.
98.meaning: The snow-girt mountains, the vast two skies, and the wind—within Him they appear, encompassed and shining wholly. He who tore open the mouth of the horse-demon (Kesi) protects those who hold to Him from drawing near the hell girded by Yama.

99.translit:
thotta padaiyettum thOlaadha vendriyaan,
atta puyagaratthaan agnaandru, - kuttatthuk
kOLmudhalai thunjak kuRittheRindha sakkaratthaan
thaaLmudhalE nangatkuch saarvu.
99.meaning: He of unconquered victory who wields the eight weapons that He takes up, the Lord of Ashtabhuja(karam), who long ago in the pool hurled His discus with sure aim so that the mighty crocodile met its death—His feet alone are our refuge.

0.translit:
naaraayaNan paDaiththaan naanmuganai
naanmugaNukku eraar sivanpiRanthaan ennum sol
seeraar mozhi seppi vaazhalaam nenjamE
moyppoomazhisaip paranaDiyE vaazhththu.
0.meaning: Narayana created the four-faced Brahma, and from Brahma was born the radiant Siva — this is the truth. O heart, let us live by uttering these glorious words. Worship the feet of the supreme Lord of Mazhisai, surrounded by swarming flowers.

1.translit:
naanmuganai naaraayaNan paDaiththaan, naanmuganum
thaanmugamaayc cankaranaith thaanpaDaiththaan, - yaanmugamaay
anthaathi mEliTTu aRiviththEn aazhporuLai,
sinthaamal koNmineer thErnthu.
1.meaning: Narayana created the four-faced Brahma, and Brahma in turn created Sankara (Siva) from himself. Taking it upon myself, through this anthaathi (linked verse) I have made known the deep truth. Grasp it without losing it, after careful discernment.

2.translit:
thErunkaal thEvan oruvanE, enRuraippar
aarumaRiyaar avanperu mai, - Orum
poruLmuDivu miththanaiyE eththavamsey thaarkkum
aruLmuDiva thaazhiyaan paal.
2.meaning: When one inquires, the wise declare that God is but One. None can know His greatness. The final truth, when considered, is just this much: for all who perform any penance, the ultimate grace lies with the Lord who bears the discus.

3.translit:
paaliR kiDanthathuvum paNDarakam mEyathuvum,
aaliR Ruyinrathuvum aaraRivaar, - njaalath	horuporuLai vaanavartham meypporuLai, appil
aruporuLai yaanaRintha vaaRu?
3.meaning: Who can fully know Him who reclined upon the milk-ocean, who resides in Srirangam, and who slept upon the banyan leaf? He is the one Reality of the world, the true essence of the celestials, the rare essence amid the waters — how then did I come to know Him?

4.translit:
aaRu saDaikkaranthaan aNDarkOn RannODum,
kooRuDaiyan enpathuvum koLkaiththE, - vERoruvar
illaamai ninRaanai emmaanai, epporuTkum
sollaanaic connEn thoguththu.
4.meaning: He who hid the river (Ganga) in his matted hair (Siva) shares a part of his body with the Lord of the celestials — this too is established. I have spoken, gathering it together, of my Lord who stands without any other equal to Him, who is the very word (meaning) of all things.

5.translit:
thoguththa varaththanaayth thOlaathaan maarvam,
vagirththa vaLaiyugirththOL maalE, - ugaththil
oruNaanRu neeyuyarththi yuLvaangi neeyE,
aruNaanku maanaay aRi.
5.meaning: O Tirumal of the shoulders with curved nails that tore open the chest of Hiranya, the undefeated one armed with boons! In each age, You raise up creation and draw it back into Yourself. Know that You alone became the rare four (the Vedas / the fourfold creation).

6.translit:
aRiyaar samaNar ayarththaar pavuththar,
siriyaar sivappaTTaar seppil, veRiyaaya
maayavanai maalavanai maathavanai Eththaar
eenavarE yaathalaal inRu.
6.meaning: The Jains know nothing, the Buddhists are deluded, and the lowly ones, if one must speak, have fallen for Siva. Those who do not praise the fragrant wondrous one, Tirumal, the consort of Lakshmi (Madhava), are indeed base people, even today.

7.translit:
inRaaga naaLaiyE yaaga, inicchiRithum
ninRaaga ninnaruLen paalathE, - nanRaaga
naanunnai yanRi yilEnkaNDaay, naaraNanE
neeyennai yanRi yilai.
7.meaning: Whether it be today, or tomorrow, or any little while hence, let Your grace remain upon me. For surely, O Narayana, I have no existence apart from You — and You too are not apart from me.

8.translit:
ilaithuNaimaR Rennenjee eesanai venRa
silaikoNDa sengaNmaal sEraa - kulaikoNDa
eeraintha laiyaan ilangaiyai yeeDazhiththa
kooramban allaal kuRai.
8.meaning: There is no other refuge, O my heart! There is no remedy except the red-eyed Tirumal who wields the bow that conquered Siva — He of the sharp arrows who destroyed Lanka of the ten-headed Ravana whose heads were severed in clusters.

9.translit:
kuRaikoNDu naanmugan kuNDigainee peythu
maRaikoNDa manthiraththaal vaazhththi, - kaRaikoNDa
kaNDaththaan sennimEl ERak kazhuvinaan
aNDaththaan sEvaDiyai aangu.
9.meaning: Taking it as a need, Brahma poured water from his vessel and praised with mantras drawn from the Vedas, and there washed the sacred red feet of the Lord of the universe — so that the water rose upon the head of Siva, he of the stained (dark) throat.

10.translit:
aangaara vaaram athu kETTu, azhalumizhum
poongaar aravaNaiyaan ponmEni, - yaangaaNa
vallamE yallamE? maamalaraan vaarsaDaiyaan
vallarE yallarE vaazhththu.
10.meaning: Hearing that proud uproar, can we behold the golden form of Him who reclines on the dark serpent that spews fire? Are we not able? And the lotus-born Brahma and the long-matted-haired Siva — are they not able, or are they able, to praise Him fully?

11.translit:
vaazhththugavaay kaaNkagaN kETka sevimaguDam
thaazhththu vaNanguminkaL thaNmalaraal, - soozhththa
thuzhaaymannum neeNmuDiyen thollaimaal thannai
vazhaavaNkai kooppi mathiththu.
11.meaning: Let the mouth praise Him, let the eyes behold Him, let the ears hear Him. Bow your crowned heads low and worship with cool flowers, with hands faultlessly cupped in reverence, my ancient Tirumal who wears the tall crown ever wreathed with surrounding tulasi.

12.translit:
mathiththaaypOy naankin mathiyaarpOy veezha
mathiththaay mathikOL viDuththaay, - mathith thaay
maDukiDantha maamuthalai kOLviDuppaan, aazhi
viDaRkiraNDum pOyiraNDin veeDu.
12.meaning: You resolved and went forth: to make those who do not heed the Vedas (or the four) fall, You resolved; You released the moon from the grip (of Rahu); and You resolved to free the great elephant Gajendra seized by the crocodile lying in the pool. To hurl the discus on both occasions — both went forth, and from both came release.

13.translit:
veeDaakkum peRRi yaRiyaathu meyvaruththik
kooDaakku ninRuNDu koNDuzhalveer, - veeDaakkum
meypporuLthaan vEtha mutharpporuLthaan, viNNavarkku
naRporuLthaan naaraa yaNan.
13.meaning: O you who, not knowing the way that grants liberation, torment your bodies, build up this fleshly cage, eat, and wander about — know this: the true essence that grants liberation, the primal essence of the Vedas, and the good essence for the celestials, is Narayana.

14.translit:
naaraa yaNanennai yaaLi, naragaththuc
chEraamal kaakkum thirumaalthan - pEraana
pEsap peRaatha piNacchamayar pEsakkETTu
aasaippaT Taazhvaar palar.
14.meaning: Narayana who rules over me, Tirumal who protects from falling into hell — there are many who, hearing the speech of the corpse-like sectarians who are not fit to utter His names, become enamored of them and sink down.

15.translit:
palarththEva rEththap paDikaDanthaan paatham
malarERa viTTiRainji vaazhththa - valaraagil
maarkkaNDan kaNDa vagaiyE varunkaNDeer
neerkkaNDan kaNDa nilai.
15.meaning: He who strode across the worlds while many gods praised Him — if you are able to cast flowers upon His feet, bow, and praise Him, then, behold, there will come to you the very state that Markandeya beheld, the state that the throat-stained Siva (who holds water in his hair) attained.

16.translit:
nilaimannum ennenjam annaanRu, thEvar
thalaimannar thaamEmaaR Raaga, - palarmannar
pOrmaaLa vengathirOn maayap pozhilmaRaiya
thEraazhi yaalmaRaiththaa raal.
16.meaning: My heart stands ever firm, recalling that day when, with the chief kings of the gods (the great warriors) ranged as His foes, He made the hot-rayed sun vanish, screening it with the wheel of His chariot so that the many kings perished in the war — He concealed it as the grove darkened.

17.translit:
aala nizhaRkeezh aRaneRiyai naalvarkku
mElai yugaththuraiththaan meyththavaththOn, - njaalam
aLanthaanai yaazhik kiDanthaanai, aalmEl
vaLarnthaanaith thaanvaNangu maaRu.
17.meaning: He of true penance who, in a former age, taught the path of dharma to the four sages beneath the shade of the banyan tree — that very One measured the world, reclined upon the ocean, and grew (lay) upon the banyan leaf. This is the way to worship Him.

18.translit:
maaRaaya thaanavanai vaLLugiraal maarviraNDu
kooraagak keeriya kOLariyai, - vERaaga
Eththi yiruppaarai vellumE, maRRavaraic
chaarththi yiruppaar thavam.
18.meaning: The penance of those who cling to other gods can never surpass the penance of those who, distinctly praising, abide upon the mighty man-lion (Narasimha) who tore the chest of the hostile demon Hiranya into two parts with His pointed claws.

19.translit:
thavamseythu naanmuganaal peRRa varaththai
avamseytha aazhiyaa yanRE, - uvanthemmaik
kaappaaynee kaappathanai yaavaaynee, vaikuntham
eeppaayu mevvuyirkkum nee.
19.meaning: O Lord of the discus, it was You who rendered void the boon that Hiranya gained from Brahma through penance, was it not? Gladly You protect us, and You are also that which is the protection itself. You are the one who bestows Vaikunta upon every living soul.

20.translit:
neeyee yulagelaam ninnaruLee niRpanavum
neeyee thavaththeeva theevanum - neeyee
eorisudarum maalvaraiyum eNdisaiyum, aNdath
thirusudaru maaya ivai,
20.meaning: You yourself are all the worlds, and by your grace alone do they stand firm. You yourself are the God of gods attained through penance. You yourself are the blazing fire, the great mountains, the eight directions, and these two luminaries (the sun and the moon) shining within the cosmic egg.

21.translit:
ivaiyaa pilavaay thiRandheri kaanRa
ivaiyaa erivaTTak kaNgaL - ivaiyaa
eoripoNgik kaaTTu mimaiyoor perumaan,
aripoNgik kaaTTum azhagu.
21.meaning: Are these the cavern-like mouth opened wide that spewed forth fire? Are these the round fiery eyes? This is the Lord of the celestials who reveals himself blazing with fire, this is the beauty of Hari that surges forth and shows itself (as the Man-Lion, Narasimha).

22.translit:
azhagiyaan thaanee ariyuruvan thaanee
pazhagiyaan thaaLee paNimin - kuzhaviyaayth
thaanee zhulagukkum thankaikkum thanmaiyanee
meenaa yuyiraLikkum viththu.
22.meaning: He himself is the beautiful one, he himself bears the form of Hari (the Man-Lion). Worship the feet of him who is intimately familiar to us. As an infant he himself held all the seven worlds within his own hand, and became the seed (cause) who, taking the form of a fish, preserved all lives.

23.translit:
viththu midalveeNduN kolloo, viDaiyaDarththa
paththi yuzhavan pazhampunaththu, - moiththezhundha
kaarmeega manna karumaal thirumeeni,
neervaanam kaaTTum nigazhndhu.
23.meaning: Does the bull-subduing devotee-farmer need to sow seed afresh in his ancient field? The dark sacred form of the dark Tirumal, like the dense dark rain-cloud thickly gathered, the watery sky reveals manifestly (as if reflecting his hue).

24.translit:
nigazhndhaay paalpon pasuppuk kaarvaNNam
naangum igazhndhaa yiruvaraiyum veeyap - pugazhndhaay
sinappoorch chuveedhanaich cheenaa padhiyaay
manappoor muDikkum vagai.
24.meaning: You manifested in the four colours of milk-white, gold, green, and dark (across the four ages). You spurned the two (demons), causing them to perish. As the commander of the army you praised Svetana of the wrathful battle, in the manner that fulfills the resolve in the heart.

25.translit:
vagaiyaal madhiyaadhu maNkoNdaay, maRRum
vagaiyaal varuvadhon RuNdee, vagaiyaal
vayiram kuzhaiththuNNum maavalidhaa nennum
vayira vazhakkozhiththaay maRRu.
25.meaning: By a clever device, without his realizing it, you took the earth (from Mahabali). Is there yet any other thing that can come by such means? You put an end to the obstinate claim of the one called Mahabali, who would have crushed and consumed (his enemies) in his hardness.

26.translit:
maRRuth thozhuvaa roruva raiyum yaaninmai,
kaRRaich chaDaiyaan karigaNdaay, eRRaikkum
kaNdukoL kaNdaay kaDalvaNNaa, yaanunnaik
kaNdukoL kiRku maaRu.
26.meaning: That I worship no other being whatsoever - the one with matted locks (Shiva) is witness to this, behold. O ocean-hued one, for all time grant me grace to behold you, that I may behold you forever.

27.translit:
maaldhaan pugundha maDanenjan maRRadhuvum
peeRaagak koLvanoo peedhaigaaL - neeRaaDi
thaankaaNa maaTTaadha thaaragalach cheevaDiyai
yaankaaNa valleeR kidhu.
27.meaning: My innocent heart into which Tirumal has entered - shall I take even that as a great gain, O foolish ones? When I am able to behold the broad, expansive sacred red feet which the ash-smeared one (Shiva) himself could not behold, this (heart's devotion) is but a small matter to me.

28.translit:
idhuvilangai yeeDazhiyak kaTTiya seedhu,
idhuvilangu vaaliyai veezhththadhu, - idhuvilangai
thaanoDungka vilnuDangath thaNdaa riraavaNanai,
ooNoDungka eydhaan ugappu.
28.meaning: This is the causeway he built so that Lanka was destroyed. This is (the deed by which) he felled the shining Vali. This is the joy of him who, making the bow tremble until Lanka itself was subdued, shot down the cool-garlanded Ravana so that his flesh perished.

29.translit:
ugappuruvan thaanee oLiyuruvan thaanee,
magappuruvan thaanee madhikkil, - migappuruvam
onRukkon Roosanaiyaan veezha, orukaNaiyaal
anRikkoN Deydhaan avan.
29.meaning: If you reflect, he himself is the form of delight, he himself is the form of radiance, he himself is the form of (the eternal). With a single arrow, that one shot down (Ravana), whose two eyebrows were a yojana apart from each other, bearing enmity against him.

30.translit:
avanennai yaaLi arangaththu, arangil
avanennai eydhaamal kaappaan, avanenna
thuLLaththu ninRaa nirundhaan kidakkumee,
veLLath thravaNaiyin meel.
30.meaning: He is the one who rules me, at Srirangam; in the arena (of life) he guards me from harm. He who stood and sat (took residence) within my heart - does he not also recline upon the serpent-bed amid the flood of waters (the ocean of milk)?

31.translit:
meelnaan muganaranai yiTTaviDu saabam
thaannaa raNanozhiththaan thaaragaiyuL, vaanoor
perumaanai yeeththaadha peeygaaL, piRakkum
karumaayam peesil kadhai.
31.meaning: The curse that the four-faced Brahma laid upon Hara (Shiva) - it was Narayana himself who removed it, within this starry world. O fiends who do not praise the Lord of the celestials! To speak of the dark illusion (maya) of why you are born is a long tale.

32.translit:
kadhaipporuLdhaan kaNNan thiruvayiRRi nuLLa
udhaippaLavu poadhupoak kinRi, - vadhaip
poruLdhaan vaayndha guNaththup paDaadha thaDaiminoo
aayndha guNaththaan aDi.
32.meaning: The substance of the story is Kannan; without spending the time idly to the measure of a kick within his sacred belly (those who do not meditate so will face destruction). Renounce those who do not partake of his excellent qualities; (hold fast to) the feet of him of well-discerned virtues.

33.translit:
aDichchakaDam saaDi yaravaaTTi, aanai
piDiththosiththup peeymulai nanjuNDu - vaDippavaLa
vaayppinnai thoaLukkaa valleeR ReruththiRuththu,
koappinnu maanaan kuRippu.
33.meaning: Kicking and shattering the cart with his foot, making the serpent (Kaliya) dance, seizing and breaking the elephant (Kuvalayapida), drinking the poison of the demoness's (Putana's) breast, and for the sake of the shoulders of Nappinnai of coral-red lips, breaking the necks of the strong bulls - such is the intent (the playful deeds) of him who yet became the cowherd.

34.translit:
kuRippenakkuk koaTTiyoor meeyaanai yeeththa,
kuRippenakku nanmai payakka, - veRuppanoo
veengkaDaththu meeyaanai meyvinaino yeydhaamal,
thaankaDaththum thanmaiyaan thaaL.
34.meaning: My intent is to praise the Lord who dwells at Kottiyur, so that my intent may yield me good. Shall I ever spurn the feet of him who dwells at Venkatam, who by his very nature carries us across (the ocean of samsara) so that the disease of karmic deeds born of the body does not reach us?

35.translit:
thaaLaal ulagam aLandha asaiveekol,
vaaLaa kidandharuLum vaaydhiRavaan, - neeLoadham
vandhalaikkum maamayilai maavallik keeNiyaan,
aindhalaivaay naagath thaNai.
35.meaning: Is it from the weariness of measuring the worlds with his foot that he reclines silently, gracing us, not opening his mouth - the Lord of Mavallikkeni in great Mylapore, where the long ocean waves come surging - resting upon the serpent of five mouths?

36.translit:
naagath thaNaikkudandhai veohkaa thiruvevvuL,
naagath thaNaiyarangam peeranbil, - naagath
thaNaippaaR kadalkidakku maadhi neDumaal,
aNaippaar karuth thanaa vaan.
36.meaning: Upon the serpent-bed at Kudandhai (Kumbakonam), at Thiruvehka, at Thiruvevvul, upon the serpent-bed at Srirangam, at Thirupperanbil, and upon the serpent-bed he reclines on the ocean of milk - the primordial all-pervading Tirumal becomes the very thought of those who embrace him in devotion.

37.translit:
vaanulavu theevaLi maagadal maaboruppu,
thaanulavu vengkadhirum thaNmadhiyum, - meenilavu
koNdal peyarum thisaiyeTTum soozhchchiyum,
aNdan thirumaal agaippu.
37.meaning: The sky, the moving fire, the wind, the great ocean, the great mountains, the moving hot-rayed sun and the cool moon, the high-roaming clouds, the eight directions and (all) that encompasses them, and the cosmic egg - all these are the creation (expansion) of Tirumal.

38.translit:
agaippu il manisarai aaRu samayam pugaiththaan,
poru kadal neer vaNNan,
ugaikku meel, eth theevar vaalaaTTum, evvaaRu seykaiyum,
appoadhu ozhiyum azhaippu.
38.meaning: The one of the hue of the surging ocean's waters drove the steadfast, undiminished people into confusion through the six (alien) doctrines. If he but wills it, whatever tail-wagging fawning any god may do, and whatever deeds they perform, at that very moment their summoning (claim to authority) ceases.

39.translit:
azhaippan thiruveeng kadaththaanaik kaaNa,
izhaippan thirukkooDal kooDa, - mazhaippee
raruvi maNivaranRi vandhizhiya, yaanai
veruvi yaravoDungkum veRpu.
39.meaning: I call out to behold the Lord of sacred Venkatam; I yearn to join (and worship) at sacred Tirukkudal. It is the hill where the great rain-fed waterfalls come pouring down tumbling gems, and the elephants in fear, and the serpents shrink and hide.

40.translit:
veRpenRu veengkaDam paaDineen, veeDaakki
niRkinReen ninRu ninaikkinReen, - kaRkinRa
noolvalaiyil paTTirundha noolaaTTi keeLvanaar,
kaalvalaiyil paTTirundheen kaaN.
40.meaning: I sang of Venkatam as merely a hill, and now I stand having made it my refuge, standing and meditating upon it. Behold, I have fallen into the snare of the feet of the Lord, the consort of Lakshmi who dwells upon the lotus, who is caught in the net of the scriptures that the learned study.

41.translit:
kaaNa luRukinReen kallaruvi muththudhira,
ooNa vizhavil oliyadhira - peeNi
varuveeng kaDavaa en nuLLam pukundhaay,
tiruveeng kaDamadhanaic chenRu.
41.meaning: I long to behold you, O Lord of holy Venkatam, where rocky cascades scatter pearls and the festival of Onam resounds with its din. Cherishing me, you came and entered my heart, having gone to that sacred Venkatam.

42.translit:
senRu vaNangkuminoo seeNuyar veengkaDaththai,
ninRu vinaikeDukkum neermaiyaal, enRum
kaDikkamala naanmukanum kaNmoonRath thaanum,
aDikkamalam iTTeeththu mangku.
42.meaning: Go and worship the towering, lofty Venkatam, which by its very nature stands ever destroying the deeds (karma) of those who approach. There the four-faced Brahma, born of the fragrant lotus, and the three-eyed Shiva offer lotuses at His feet and praise Him.

43.translit:
mangkulthooy chenni vaDaveeng kaDaththaanai
kangkul pukundhaarkaL kaappaNivaan, - tingkaL
saDaiyeeRa vaiththaanum taamaraimee laanum
kuDaiyeeRath thaamkuviththuk koNDu.
43.meaning: To the Lord of northern Venkatam whose summit touches the cloud-laden sky, they came at night to offer protective worship. The one who set the crescent moon upon his matted locks (Shiva) and the one seated upon the lotus (Brahma) gathered themselves together, raising up their cupped hands as a canopy.

44.translit:
koNDu kuDangkaalmeel vaiththa kuzhaviyaay,
taNDa arakkan talaithaLaal- paNDeNNi,
poomkumaran niRkum pozhilveeng kaDamalaikkee,
poomkumara ruLLeer purindhu.
44.meaning: As the divine child set upon the lap (of Devaki/Yashoda), and as the youthful one who long ago counted and struck down the heads of the punishing demon (Ravana) - to that grove-clad Venkatam hill where that flower-fresh youth abides, O fortunate young ones, go with devotion.

45.translit:
purindhu malariTTup puNDarikap paadham,
parindhu paDukaaDu niRpa - terindhengkum
taanoongki niRkinRaan taNNaruvi veengkaDamee
vaanoorkkum maNNoorkkum vaippu.
45.meaning: With devotion casting flowers upon His lotus feet, while crowds throng in loving worship, He stands manifest and supreme, perceived everywhere, exalting Himself. Venkatam of cool cascades is the treasure-store for the dwellers of heaven and the dwellers of earth alike.

46.translit:
vaippan maNiviLakkaa maamadhiyai, maalukken
Reppozhudhum kaineeTTum yaanaiyai, - eppaaDum
veeDuvaLaik kakkuRavar villeDukkum veengkaDamee,
naaDuvaLaith thaaDumeel nanRu.
46.meaning: At Venkatam, where the elephant raises its trunk reaching out ever as if for Tirumal, taking the great moon to be its jewel-lamp, and where on every side hunters encircle the prey and the hill-dwellers take up their bows - if I could roam encircling that whole land, it would be good.

47.translit:
nanmaNi vaNNanoor aaLiyum kooLariyum,
ponmaNiyum muththamum poomaramum, - panmaNinee
rooDu poruthuruLum kaanamum vaanaramum
veeDu muDaiveeng kaDam.
47.meaning: This is Venkatam, abode of the Lord hued like a fine gem - filled with yaali-lions and fierce lions, with gold, gems and pearls and flowering trees, with forests where many-gemmed waters tumble and clash as they roll, with monkeys and with hunters' settlements.

48.translit:
veengkaDamee viNNoor tozhuvadhuvum meymmaiyaal
veengkaDamee meyvinainooy teerppadhuvum, - veengkaDamee
taanavarai veezhaththan naazhip paDaitoTTu
vaanavaraik kaappaan malai.
48.meaning: It is Venkatam that the celestials worship; in truth it is Venkatam that cures the diseases born of the body's deeds. Venkatam is the hill of Him who, taking up His own discus to make the demons fall, protects the heavenly ones.

49.translit:
malaiyaamai meelvaiththu vaasukiyaic chuRRi,
talaiyaamai taanorukai paRRi, - alaiyaamal
peeRak kaDaindha perumaan tirunaamam,
kooRuvadhee yaavarkkum kooRRu.
49.meaning: To utter the holy name of the Lord who, placing the tortoise (Kurma) beneath the mountain, winding the serpent Vasuki around it, and gripping the tortoise's head firmly with one hand, churned the ocean steadily until it split apart - that uttering is itself the death-stroke against death (the destroyer of fate) for everyone.

50.translit:
kooRamum saaraa koDuvinaiyum saaraa,tee
maaRRamum saaraa vakaiyaRindheen, - aaRRang
karaikkiDakkum kaNNan kaDalkiDakkum, maayan
uraikkiDakku muLLath thenakku.
50.meaning: I have learned the way by which no slander shall reach me, no cruel karma shall reach me, and no evil utterance shall reach me - for in my heart abides the word of Kannan who reclines on the riverbank, the wondrous one who reclines upon the ocean.

51.translit:
enakkaavaa raaroru vareeem perumaan
tanakkaavaan taaneemaR Rallaala - punakkaayaa
vaNNanee. unnaip piRaraRiyaar, enmadhikku
viNNellaa muNDoo vilai.
51.meaning: Who is there that can be a refuge for me, except my Lord who is His own support and no other's? O Lord hued like the wild kaaya blossom! Others know you not. For my mind (that has known you), is even the whole heaven a worthy price?

52.translit:
vilaikkaaT paDuvar visaadhiyeeR RuNbar,
talaikkaaT palithirivar takkoor - mulaikkaal
viDamuNDa veendhanaiyee veeRaaeeth thaadhaar,
kaDamuNDaar kallaa tavar.
52.meaning: Those who do not praise as supreme the Lord who drank the poison from the breast (of Pootana) will be sold into bondage, will eat by hiring out for sickness, and will wander begging alms - such unlearned ones, those worthy ones (so called), are but debtors weighed down by debt.

53.translit:
kallaa tavarilangkai kaTTazhiththa, kaakuththan
allaa lorudheyvam yaanileen, - pollaadha
teevarai teevaral laarai, tiruvillaath
teevaraith theeRalmin teevu.
53.meaning: I acknowledge no deity other than the descendant of Kakutstha (Rama) who destroyed the fortress-citadel of Lanka of the ignorant ones. Do not trust as God the wicked gods, the non-gods, the gods bereft of Lakshmi (Sri).

54.translit:
teevaraay niRkumath theevum,ath teevaril
moovaraay niRkum mudhupuNarppum, - yaavaraay
niRkinRa tellaam neDumaalen Rooraadhaar,
kaRkinRa tellaam kaDai.
54.meaning: That divinity that stands as the gods, and the ancient ordained framework that stands as the three (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva) among those gods, and all that stands as every being - whoever does not realize that all this is Nedumaal (the great Vishnu), all that they learn is base and worthless.

55.translit:
kaDainin Ramarar kazhaltozhudhu naaLum
iDaininRa inbaththa raavar, - puDaininRa
neeroodha meeni neDumaalee, ninnaDiyai
yaaroodha vallaa ravar.
55.meaning: Standing at the threshold (gate of liberation), the celestials worship His ankleted feet and become those abiding in unending bliss. O Nedumaal whose form is like the surging ocean of waters that stands all around - those who are able to chant of your feet are such ones (who attain that bliss).

56.translit:
avarivaren Rillai anangkaveeL taadhaikku,
evaru medhirillai kaNDeer, - uvarik
kaDalnanja muNDaan kaDanenRu, vaaNaR
kuDaninRu tooRRaa norungku.
56.meaning: For the father of Kama (Manmatha) - that is, the Lord - there is no distinction of this one or that one; behold, no one stands as His equal. When the matter concerned Bana (Vaana), He who stood by Shiva, the one who drank the poison of the salty ocean, fought and showed his defeat all at once (Vishnu prevailed over them together).

57.translit:
orung kirundha nalvinaiyum teevinaiyu maavaan,
perungkurun tam saaiththavanee peesil, - marungkirundha
vaanavartaam taanavartaam taarakaitaan, ennenjam
aanavartaa mallaaka ten.
57.meaning: If one speaks of it, the One who became together both good deeds and evil deeds, who toppled the great kurundha tree (Krishna) - the celestials standing on every side, the demons, the stars themselves, and all that has filled my heart - what are they all if not He alone?

58.translit:
ennenja meeyaan iruLneekki yempiraan,
mannanja munnorunaaL maNNaLandhaan, - ennenja
meeyaanai yillaa viDaiyeeRRaan, vevvinaiteerth
taayanuk kaakkineen anbu.
58.meaning: He who pervades my heart, dispelling darkness, my Lord, who long ago measured the earth so that kings trembled - to that herdsman (Krishna) who pervades my heart, who cures cruel karma, the one whom the bull-rider (Shiva) does not (truly) possess, I have rendered up my love.

59.translit:
anbaavaay aaramudham aavaay, aDiyeenuk
kinbaavaay ellaamum neeyaavaay, - ponpaavai
keeLvaa kiLaroLiyena keesavanee, keeDinRi
aaLvaayk kaDiyeennaan aaL.
59.meaning: You become love itself, you become the rare nectar, you become bliss for me your servant, you become all things for me. O consort of the golden goddess (Lakshmi), O Kesava of radiant splendor - I, this servant, am the bondsman of you who rule me without ruin forever.

60.translit:
aaTpaarth thuzhitharuvaay kaNDukoL enRu, nin
taaTpaarth thuzhitharuvEn tanmaiya - kETpaarkk
arumporuLaay ninRa aranganE, unnai
virumbuvadhE viLLEn manam.
60.meaning: You wander about seeking those who will serve You, saying 'Behold and accept me'; and I, of such a nature, wander seeking Your sacred feet to behold them. O Lord of Srirangam, who stand as the rare, deep truth to those who would listen! My mind shall never cease longing for You.

61.translit:
manakkEdham saaraa madhusoodhan Ranai,
tanakkEdhaan thanjamaak koLLil, - enakkEdhaan
inRonRi ninRulagai yEzhaaNai yOTTinaan,
senRonRi ninRa thiru.
61.meaning: No distress of mind comes near to one who takes Madhusudana himself as his sole refuge. For me, the sacred grace (Lakshmi) abides united, the same one who, joined with the Lord, governs the seven worlds by His sole command.

62.translit:
thiruninRa pakkam thiRavidhen ROraar,
karuninRa kallaarkk uraippar, - thiruvirundha
maarban sireedharanRan vaNDulavu thaNduzhaay,
thaarthannaich choodith thariththu.
62.meaning: Those who do not realize that the side where Lakshmi dwells is the way of liberation, expound their teaching to the stone-hearted ignorant ones. But I, wearing and bearing the cool tulasi garland over which bees hover, of Sridhara on whose breast Lakshmi resides, am saved.

63.translit:
thariththirundhE naagavE thaaraa kaNappOr,
viriththuraiththa vennaagath thunnai, - theriththezhuthi
vaasiththum kETTum vaNangi vazhipaTTum,
poosiththum pOkkinEn pOdhu.
63.meaning: Having held You firm within me, I spent my time well on You who rest upon the fierce serpent (Adisesha), of whom the hosts of stars expound widely. I passed my days clearly inscribing Your praise, reciting it, hearing it, bowing, worshipping and offering worship to You.

64.translit:
pOdhaana iTTiRainji EththuminO, ponmagarak
kaadhaanai aadhip perumaanai, - naadhaanai
nallaanai naaraNanai nammEzh piRappaRukkum
sollaanai, solluvadhE soodhu.
64.meaning: Offer flowers, bow down and praise Him who wears golden makara earrings, the primordial great Lord, the Master, the good One, Narayana, He who as the sacred Word cuts off our seven-fold cycle of births. To speak His name is the only sure way.

65.translit:
soodhaava thennenjath theNNinEn, solmaalai
maadhaaya maalavanai maadhavanai - yaadhaanum
vallavaa sindhith thiruppERkku, vaikundhath
thillaiyO sollee riDam.
65.meaning: In my heart I have reckoned what is the sure way: it is to praise with garlands of words the great dark Lord, the Consort of Lakshmi (Madhava). For me who keep meditating on Him by whatever means I can, tell me — is there no place for me in Vaikuntha?

66.translit:
iDamaava thennenjam inRellaam, paNDu
paDanaa kaNainediya maaRku, - thiDamaaga
vaiyyEn madhisoodi thannODu, ayanainaan
vaiyEnaaT cheyyEn valam.
66.meaning: From this day on, my heart is wholly the dwelling place of the tall Lord of long ago who reclines on the hooded serpent. Firmly I declare: I will not set Him on a level with Shiva who wears the moon, nor with Brahma; I will perform no circumambulation in their honor.

67.translit:
valamaaga maaTTaamai thaanaaga, vaigal
kulamaaga kuRRamthaa naaga, - nalamaaga naaraNanai
nampadhiyai njaanap perumaanai,
seeraNanai yEththum thiRam.
67.meaning: Whether it be that I have no strength, or that day by day my faults pile up as my heritage — yet the way of good is to praise Narayana, our Lord, the great One of wisdom, the holy refuge. That is the right path.

68.translit:
thiRambEnmin kaNDeer thiruvaDithan naamam
maRandhum puRandhozhaa maandhar - iRainjiyum
saadhuvaraayp pOdhuminkaL, enRaan, namanumthan
thoodhuvaraik koovich chevikku.
68.meaning: Yama himself called his messengers and said into their ears: 'See that you do not waver! As for people who, even by forgetting, never worship any other than the sacred feet of the Lord — bow to them and go your way as gentle ones.'

69.translit:
sevikkinbam aavadhuvum sengaNmaal naamam,
puvikkum puviyadhuvE kaNDeer, - kavikku
niRaiporuLaay ninRaanai nErpaTTEn, paarkkil
maRaipporuLum aththanaiyE thaan.
69.meaning: That which is sweet to the ear is the name of the red-eyed Tirumal; and behold, that very name is heaven even for this earth. I have met face to face Him who stands as the full meaning of all poetry; and if one looks, the meaning of the Vedas too is just that much — Him alone.

70.translit:
thaanoruva naagith tharaNi yiDandheduththu,
Enoruva naayeyiTRil thaangiyadhum - yaanoruvan
inRaa vaRiginRE nallEn, irunilaththaich
senRaang kaDippaduththa sEy.
70.meaning: That He, being the One, dug up and lifted the earth, becoming the unique Boar and bearing it upon His tusk — I do not come to know this only today. He is the One who long ago strode forth and held the vast earth as His own.

71.translit:
sEyan aNiyan siRiyan migapperiyan,
aayan thuvaraikkO naayninRa - maayan, an
ROdhiya vaakkadhanaik kallaar, ulagaththil
Edhilaraay meynjnjaana mil.
71.meaning: He is far and He is near, He is small and exceedingly great, the cowherd, the One who became the king of Dwaraka — the wondrous Maya. Those who do not learn the word He spoke of old wander as strangers in this world, devoid of true knowledge.

72.translit:
illaRam illEl thuRavaRamil ennum,
sollaRa mallanavum sollalla - nallaRam
aavanavum naalvEdha maaththavamum, naaraNanE
yaavadhee thanRenbaa raar.
72.meaning: The household dharma, the renunciate's dharma if one lacks that, and whatever words are not these are no words at all (worth speaking). The good dharmas, the four Vedas and great austerity — who is there that would say all this is not Narayana Himself?

73.translit:
aarE yaRivaar anaiththulagu muNDumizhndha,
pEraazhi yaanRan perumaiyai, - kaarseRindha
kaNDaththaan eNkaNNaan kaaNaan, avanvaiththa
paNDaiththaa naththin padhi.
73.meaning: Who indeed can know the greatness of Him of the great discus, who swallowed all the worlds and brought them forth again? Even the One with the dark-stained throat (Shiva) and the eight-eyed One (Brahma) cannot see the abode of the ancient seat that He established.

74.translit:
padhippagainjark kaaTRaadhu paydhiraineerp paazhi,
madhiththaDaindha vaaLaravan thannai, - maththivanRan
vallaagath thERRiya maamEni maayavanai,
aladhonR EththaadhEn naa.
74.meaning: My tongue praises nothing other than that wondrous Lord of the great form, who set upon His mighty body the shining serpent that, unable to withstand its enemies of the realm, took refuge in the surging, spreading waters of the ocean.

75.translit:
naakkoNDu maanidam paaDEn, nalamaagath
theekkoNDa senjaDaiyaan senRu, enRum - pookkoNDu
vallavaa REththa magizhaadha, vaikundhach
chelvanaar sEvaDimEl paaTTu.
75.meaning: With my tongue I will not sing of mere men. My song is set only upon the sacred red feet of the wealthy Lord of Vaikuntha — He who is not satisfied even though Shiva of the fire-bearing red matted locks goes and worships Him as best he can, offering flowers.

76.translit:
paaTTum muRaiyum paDukadhaiyum palporuLum
eeTTiya theeyum iruvisumbum, - kETTa
manuvum surudhi maRainaankum maayan
Ranamaayai yiRpaTTadhaRpu.
76.meaning: Song and ordinance, the unfolding epics and the many sciences, the gathered sacrificial fire and the twofold sky, the Manu codes that are heard, the Sruti and the four Vedas — all this is caught within the wondrous Maya of the Lord; such is the truth of it.

77.translit:
thaRpennaith thaanaRiyaa nElum, thaDangadalaik
kaRkoNDu thoorththa kaDalvaNNan, - eRkoNDa
vevvinaiyum neenga vilangaa manamvaiththaan,
evvinaiyum maayumaal kaNDu.
77.meaning: Though He may not regard me as anything, the ocean-hued Lord who dammed the wide sea with stones has set my mind unwavering, so that the fierce karma that gripped me departs. Behold — seeing Him, every karma melts away.

78.translit:
kaNDu vaNanginaark kennaangol, kaamanuDal
koNDa thavaththaaRku umaiyuNarththa, - vaNDalambum
thaaralangal neeNmuDiyaan Ranpeya rE kETTirundhu, ang
kaaralanga laanamaiyaa laaynthu.
78.meaning: When Uma made it known to the ascetic (Shiva) who had burned the body of Kama, he listened to and pondered the very name of the Lord who wears a long crown and a garland over which bees hum, becoming radiant by it. If such was its effect on him, what then shall come to those who behold and worship the Lord directly!

79.translit:
aaynthukoNDa Daadhip perumaanai, anbinaal
vaayntha manadhiruththa vallaargaL, - Eynthadham
meykundha maaga virumbuvarE, thaamumtham
vaikundham kaaNpaar viraindhu.
79.meaning: Those who, having discerned and grasped Him, are able with love to firmly seat the primordial great Lord in their fitting hearts — they themselves shall hold their own bodies to be Vaikuntha, and shall swiftly come to behold their Vaikuntha.

80.translit:
viraindhadaimin meelorunaaL veLLam parakka,
karanthulagam kaaththaLiththa kaNNan - paranthulagam
paadina aadina keettu, padunaragam
veedina vaasaR kadhavu.
80.meaning: Hasten and take refuge in Kannan, who long ago when the great flood spread, hid and protected and sustained the world. As all the world spread out singing and dancing in praise of Him and He heard it, even the doorway-gate of the cruel hell was sealed shut. So hurry and seek Him.

81.translit:
kadhavu manamenRum kaaNalaa menRum, kudhaiyum
vinaiyaavi theerntheen, - vidhaiyaaga naRRamizhai
viththiyen uLLaththai neeviLaiththaay,
kaRRamozhi yaagik kalanthu.
81.meaning: Saying that the gate of the closed mind may yet be seen open, the soul tormented by deeds was freed of its suffering. Sowing good Tamil as seed into my heart, You made it flourish, mingling within me as learned words.

82.translit:
kalanthaanen nuLLaththuk kaamaveeL thaadhai
nalanthaanu meedhoppa thuNdee - alarnthalargaL
iddeeththu meesanum naanmuganum, enRivargaL
vitteeththa maattaadha veendhu.
82.meaning: He, the father of Kama (the god of love), has mingled within my heart. Is there any good that equals this? He is the Sovereign whom even Shiva and the four-faced Brahma, who worship Him scattering blossoming flowers, can never cease to praise.

83.translit:
veendhraay viNNavaraay viNNaagith thaNNaLiyaay
maantharaay maadhaaymaR Rellaamaay, - saarnthavarkkuth
thannaaRRaan neemiyaan maalvaNNan thaankodukkum,
pinnaalthaan seyyum pidhir.
83.meaning: Becoming kings, becoming the celestials, becoming the very heavens, becoming cool grace, becoming men, becoming mother, and becoming all else - the dark-hued Lord who bears the discus gives Himself by His own will to those who take refuge in Him; afterward He completes whatever remains to be done.

84.translit:
pidhirum manamileen pinjnjagan RannOdu,
edhirvan avanenakku neeraan, - adhirum
kazhaRkaala mannanaiyee kaNNanaiyee, naaLum
thozhakkaadhal pooNdeen thozhil.
84.meaning: I have no mind that wavers toward Shiva; he is not equal to me. I have taken up as my daily work the loving worship - day after day - of Kannan alone, the King whose anklet-clad feet resound.

85.translit:
thozhilenakkuth thollaimaal thannaama meeththa,
pozhudhenakku maRRadhuvee poadhum, - kazhisinaththa
vallaaLan vaanarakkOn vaali madhanazhiththa,
villaaLan nenjnjath thuLan.
85.meaning: My one task is to extol the names of the ancient Tirumal; for me that alone is enough for all my time. The great archer who destroyed the pride of the fierce and mighty Vali, lord of the monkeys, dwells within my heart.

86.translit:
uLankaNdaay nannenjnjee. uththama nenRum
uLankaNdaay, uLLuvaa ruLLath, - thuLankaNdaay
thannoppaan thaanaa yuLankaaN thamiyeeRkum,
ennoppaark keesa nimai.
86.meaning: He exists, see, O good heart! As the Supreme One He ever exists, see. He exists in the hearts of those who meditate on Him, see. There is none like Him - He alone is His own equal, see. And the Lord shows grace even to a lonely one like me, and to those like me.

87.translit:
imaiyap perumalaipoo linthiranaark kitta,
samaya virundhuNdaar kaappaar - samayangaL
kaNdaan avaikaappaan kaarkkaNdan naanmuganOdu
uNdaa nulagO duyir.
87.meaning: He who at the proper time partook of the feast offered to Indra, like the great Himalaya mountain, will protect us. He beheld all the systems of faith and guards them; with the dark-throated Shiva and the four-faced Brahma, He swallowed the world and its lives.

88.translit:
uyirkoN dudalozhiya Odumpoo thOdi,
ayarvenRa theerppaanpeer paadi, - seyaltheerach
sinthiththu vaazhvaaree vaazhvaar, sirusamayap
pandhanaiyaar vaazhveel pazhudhu.
88.meaning: When life departs leaving the body and runs away, those who run singing the name of Him who removes all weariness, who meditate on Him until all worldly action ceases - they alone truly live. The life of those bound to petty creeds is wasted.

89.translit:
pazhudhaagaa donRaRintheen paaRkadalaan paadham,
vazhuvaa vagaininaindhu vaigal - thozhuvaarai,
kaNdiRainjnji vaazhvaar kalanutha vinaikeduththu
viNthiRanthu veeRRiruppaar mikku.
89.meaning: I have learned one thing that never fails: those who, without lapse, daily worship the feet of the Lord who reclines on the ocean of milk - those who behold such devotees and bow and live - will have their accumulated deeds destroyed, and the heavens opening, they will abide there in abundant glory.

90.translit:
veeRRirunthu viNNaaLa veeNduvaar, veengadaththaan
paaltirundha vaiththaaree panmalargaL, - meeltirundha
vaazhvaar varumadhipaarth thanpinaraay, maRRavarkkee
thaazhaa yiruppaar thamar.
90.meaning: Those who desire to abide and rule in the celestial world place many flowers fittingly at the feet of the Lord of Venkatam. Living rightly on high, with love and awaiting His grace to come, the devotees remain ever humble and devoted to Him alone.

91.translit:
thamaraavar yaavarukkum thaamaraimee laaRkum
amararkkum aadaravaarth thaaRkum - amarargaL
thaaLthaa maraimalarga LittiRainjnji, maalvaNNan
thaaLthaa maraiyadaivoo menRu.
91.meaning: They become kin to all - to the one seated on the lotus (Brahma), to the immortals, and to Him who girds the dancing serpent (Shiva) - those who, like the celestials, offer lotus blossoms at His feet and bow, resolving to reach the lotus feet of the dark-hued Tirumal.

92.translit:
enRum maRandhaRiyeen ennenjnjath theevaiththu
ninRu mirundhum nedumaalai - enRum
thiruvirundha maarpan sireedharanuk kaaLaay,
karuvirundha naaLmudhalaak kaappu.
92.meaning: I have never once forgotten Him; placing the great Tirumal in my heart, whether standing or sitting, I remember Him always. Being a servant to Sridhara, on whose chest Lakshmi ever dwells, He has been my protection from the very day I lay in the womb.

93.translit:
kaappu maRandhaRiyeen kaNNanee yenRiruppan
aappang kozhiyavum palluyirkkum, - aakkai
koduththaLiththa kOnee guNapparanee, unnai
vidaththuNiyaar meytheLinthaar thaam.
93.meaning: I have never forgotten that protection; I abide saying 'Kannan, Kannan.' O King who, removing all bonds, gave bodies and sustained the many lives! O Lord supreme in all good qualities! Those who have realized the truth will never dare to forsake You.

94.translit:
meytheLinthaa renjeyyaar? veeRaanaar neeRaaga
kaitheLinthu kaattik kaLappaduththu, paitheLintha
paampin aanaiyaay. aruLaay adiyeeRku
veembum kariyaagum eenRu.
94.meaning: What will those who have realized the truth not do? You who, mounted upon the hooded serpent, struck down and reduced Your foes to ashes, showing Your might in battle - grant Your grace to me, Your servant. For when You accept it, even the bitter neem becomes sweet curry.

95.translit:
eenReen adimai izhin theen piRappidumbai
aanReen amarark kamaraamai, - aanReen
kadannaadum maNNaadum kaivittu, meelai
idanaadu kaaNa ini.
95.meaning: I have accepted servitude; I have descended from the misery of birth. I have attained the deathless state of the immortals. Casting aside the world of the seas and the world of the earth, I am now fit to behold the supreme realm on high.

96.translit:
iniyaRinthee neesaRkum naanmugaRkum theyvam
iniyaRintheen emperumaan. unnai - iniyaRintheen
kaaraNannee kaRRavainee kaRpavainee, naRkirisai
naaraNannee nankaRintheen naan.
96.meaning: Now I have understood the God even of Shiva and of the four-faced Brahma; now I have understood You, my Lord. Now I have understood: You are the cause, You are all that has been learned, You are all that will be learned, You are Narayana of holy deeds - this I have well understood.

0.translit:
kaasiniyoor taamvaazhak kaliyukatthee vandhuditthu,
aasiriyap paavadhanaal arumaRaikaL viritthaanai,
dheesikanaip paraankusanaith thikazhvakuLath thaaraanai,
maasadaiyaa manatthuvaitthu maRavaamal vaazhtthudhumee.
0.meaning: For the welfare of the people of this earth, he was born into the age of Kali; through the aasiriyam metre he expounded the rare Vedas. He is the teacher (Desika), he is Parankusa (Nammalwar), he who wears the shining magizham garland. Holding him in an unblemished mind, without ever forgetting, let us praise him.

1.translit:
sekkar maa mukil uduthtu mikka sen sudarp
paridhi soodi am sudar madhiyam poondu
pala sudar punaindha pavaLach sevvaay
thikazh pasun soodhi marakathak kunRam
kadaloon kaimmisaik kaNvaLarvadhu pool
peedhaka aadai mudi pooN mudhalaa
meedhaku pal kalan aNindhu soodhi
vaayavum kaNNavum sivappa meedhittup
pachchai meeni mikap pakaippa
nachchu vinaik kavartalai aravin amaLi eeRi
eRi kadal naduvuL aRiduyil amarndhu
sivan ayan indhiran ivar mudhal anaitthoor
theyvak kuzhaangaL kaithozha kidandha
thaamarai undhith thanip peru naayaka
moovulaku aLandha seevadiyooyee
1.meaning: Clothed in the crimson great cloud at twilight, crowned with the abundant red-rayed sun, adorned with the lovely bright moon, with a coral-red mouth set with many radiances, like a shining green emerald hill resting upon the hand of the ocean-lord — wearing the golden garment, the crown, ornaments and many fitting jewels, your radiance, your mouth and your eyes glowing red, your green-hued body shining in vivid contrast — you reclined upon the bed of the venom-bearing, hungry-mouthed serpent in the midst of the surging sea, immersed in mystic slumber, while the hosts of gods led by Shiva, Brahma and Indra worship you with folded hands. O unique great Lord from whose lotus-navel all arose, O you of the sacred red feet that measured the three worlds!

2.translit:
ulaku padaitthu uNda endhai aRai kazhal
sudarp poon thaamarai soodudhaRku avaavu
aar uyir uruki ukka neeriya kaadhal
anbil inbu een theeRal amudha
veLLatthaan aam siRappu vittu oru poruTku
asaivoor asaika thiruvodu maruviya
iyaRkai maayaap peru viRal ulakam
moonRinodu nal veedu peRinum
koLvadhu eNNumoo theLLiyoor kuRippee?
2.meaning: My Father who created the worlds and swallowed them — to crown myself with the radiant lotus-flowers of his resounding anklet-clad feet, my whole melting soul has dissolved in fine longing. Letting go of the glory of being immersed in the nectar-flood of the bliss born of loving devotion, those who waver after some other object may waver. But would the intent of the clear-minded ever think of accepting it, even were they granted the three worlds together with good Liberation — those worlds ruled by the One of natural, undiminishing, great prowess who is wedded to Lakshmi?

3.translit:
kuRippil koNdu neRippada ulakam
moonRu udan vaNangu thoonRu pukazh aaNai
mey peRa nadaaya theyvam moovaril
mudhalvan aaki sudar viLangu akalatthu
varai purai thirai pora peru varai veruvar
urum mural oli mali naLir kadal pada aravu
arasu udal thada varai suzhaRRiya thani maath
theyvatthu adiyavarkku ini naam aaLaakavee
isaiyungol oozhidhooRu oozhi oovaadhee?
3.meaning: He who, holding it in his purpose, made the three worlds tread the right path — of manifest fame and command, who duly carried out his sovereign rule with true effect — being the first among the three gods, with the radiant Lakshmi resting on his broad chest. When he churned with the great mountain-king's body as the churning-rod, so that the cool ocean abounding in thundering roar was stirred and the serpent (Vasuki) coiled round it as he whirled the wave-beaten, mountain-like deep — to the devotees of that peerless great God, shall it now be granted that we become servants, age after age, unceasingly?

4.translit:
oozhidhooRu oozhi oovaadhu vaazhiya
enRu yaam thozha isaiyungolloo
yaavakai ulakamum yaavarum illaa
meel varum perumpaazhk kaalatthu irum poruTku
ellaam arum peRal thani viththu oru thaan
aaki theyva naanmukak kozhu muLai
eenRu mukkaN eesanodu theevu pala nudhali
moovulakam viLaitthа undhi
maayak kadavuL maa mudhal adiyee?
4.meaning: "May you live, unceasing age after age!" — that we may worship him thus, will it be granted to us? In the great void-time that comes when there is no world of any kind and no being whatsoever, he alone became the single rare seed of all great substances. From his navel he brought forth the divine four-faced one (Brahma) as a swelling sprout, and ordaining many gods together with the three-eyed Lord (Shiva), he caused the three worlds to grow forth — the supreme primal feet of that wondrous God!

5.translit:
maa mudhal adip poodhu onRu kavizhtthu alartthi
maN muzhudhum akappadutthu oN sudar adip poodhu
onRu viN seleei naanmukap putheeL
naadu viyandhu uvappa vaanavar muRaimuRai
vazhipada neReei thaamaraik kaadu
malark kaNNodu kani vaay udaiyadhum aay
iru naayiRu aayiram malarndhanna
kaRpakak kaavu paRpala anna
mudi thooL aayiram thazhaittha
nediyooykku alladhu adiyadhoo ulakee?
5.meaning: With one of his supreme primal flower-feet turned down and spread out he encompassed the whole earth; with the other bright radiant flower-foot he reached up to the sky, so that the four-faced god's realm marveled in wonder and the celestials worshipped him rank by rank, showing the way. Possessing a forest of lotuses (as feet), eyes like blossoms and a fruit-red mouth, with a crown shining like a thousand suns risen together, and a thousand arms flourishing like many groves of wish-granting Kalpaka trees — to whom does the world belong, if not to that vast One?

6.translit:
oooo ulakinadhu iyalvee! eenRooL irukka
maNai neeraatti padaitthu idandhu uNdu umizhndhu
aLandhu theeerndhu ulaku aLikkum mudhal perung
kadavuL niRpa pudaip pala thaan aRi
theyvam peeNudhal thanaadhu
pullaRivaaNmai porundhak kaatti
kolvana mudhalaa allana muyalum
inaiya seykai inbu thunbu aLi
thol maa maayap piRaviyuL neengaap
pal maa maayatthu azhundhumaam naLirndhee
6.meaning: Oh, oh, the way of this world! While the very Mother who bore them — the great primal God who creates, lifts up (the earth), eats and spits it out, measures and judges, and protects the world — stands present like one who bathes the bridal couch, men instead resort to worshipping many petty deities of their own, openly displaying their own dull-witted ignorance. Engaging in such deeds as killing and other unworthy acts, with their attendant pleasure, pain and bias, they fail to escape the ancient great delusory cycle of birth and sink deep, shivering, into manifold great delusion.

7.translit:
naLir madhich chadaiyanum naanmukak kadavuLum
thaLir oLi imaiyavar thalaivanum mudhalaa
yaavakai ulakamum yaavarum akappada
nilam neer thee kaal sudar iru visumbum
malar sudar piRavum siRidhu udan mayanga
oru poruL puRappaadu inRi muzhuvadhum
akappadak karandhu oor aal ilaich seerndha em
peru maa maayanai alladhu
oru maa theyvam maRRu udaiyamoo yaamee?
7.meaning: Including the one with the cool moon on his matted locks (Shiva), the four-faced God (Brahma), and the lord of the tender-radiant immortals (Indra) as foremost — every kind of world and every being, earth, water, fire, wind, the radiant sun, the two skies, the blossoming luminaries and all else, mingling together in a tiny instant, with not one thing left outside — all wholly concealed within him, he who reclined upon a single banyan leaf, our great wondrous Mystic One: other than him, do we have any other great God at all?

0.translit:
munduttRa nenjee. muyatRRi tharitthuraitthu
vandhitthu vaayaara vaazhtthiyee,-chandha
murukoorum sOlasoozh moyppoom porunal
kurukooran maaRan peer kooRu.
0.meaning: O heart that has gone ahead of me! Fix yourself in effort and recite, bowing low and praising him to your mouth's fill. Speak the name of Maaran of Kurukoor (Nammalwar), the town surrounded by fragrant flowering groves on the banks of the Porunal river where bees swarm with sweet melody.

1.translit:
muyatRRi sumandhezhundhu munduttRa nenjee,
iyatRRuvaay emmodunee koodi,-nayappudaiya
naaveen thodaikkiLavi yuLpodhivOm, naRpoovaip
pooveenRa vaNNan pukazh.
1.meaning: O heart that has risen up bearing effort and gone ahead! Join with me and accomplish this. Within our pleasing string of words born of the tongue let us enclose the glory of the Lord whose hue is like the blossom of the good poovai flower.

2.translit:
pukazhvOm pazhippOm pukazhOm pazhiyOm
ikazhvOm madhippOm madhiyOm-ikazhOm
matRRengaL maal. sengaNmaal. seeRalnee, theevinaiyOm
engaLmaal kaNdaay ivai.
2.meaning: We will praise, we will blame, we will not praise, we will not blame; we will scorn, we will revere, we will not revere, we will not scorn -- whatever we do, you are our Tirumal, our red-eyed Lord. Do not be angry: behold, these are but the deeds of us who are full of evil karma, and you are still our Lord.

3.translit:
ivaiyanRee nalla ivaiyanRee theeya,
ivaiyen RivaiyaRiva neelum,-ivaiyellaam
ennaal adaippuneek koNNaa thiRaiyavanee,
ennaal seyaRpaala then?
3.meaning: These indeed are good, these indeed are bad -- though I know that these are this and these are that, O Lord, all of these can neither be brought about nor removed by me. What then is there that can be done by my own effort?

4.translit:
ennin mikupukazhaar yaavaree, pinnaiyummatR
ReNNil mikupukazheen yaanallaal,-enna
karunjOdhik kaNNan kadalpuraiyum, seelap
perunjOdhik kennenjaaT petRRu.
4.meaning: Who are there of greater glory than I? And again, if one reckons, there is none of greater glory than I -- for I have obtained my heart's service to the dark-radiant Kannan, the great light of gracious nature vast as the sea.

5.translit:
petRRathaay neeyee piRappittha thandhainee
matRRaiyaa raavaarum neepesil,- etRReeyO!
maaya! maa maayavaLai maayamulai vaaivaittha
neeyammaa! kaattum neRi.
5.meaning: You yourself are the mother who bore me, you are the father who begot me; if one speaks of it, you are all the others too. O wondrous one! O Lord who set to your mouth the deceitful breast of the great deluding demoness (Putana) -- alas, what indeed is the path that you reveal to me!

6.translit:
neRikaatti neekkudhiyO? ninpaal karumaa
muRimeeni kaattudhiyO? meenaaL-aRiyOmai
enjeyvaa neNNinaay kaNNanee! eedhuraiyaay
enjeydhaa lenpadOm yaam?
6.meaning: Will you show the path and push me away from you? Or will you show your great dark tender-hued form? O Kannan, what have you resolved to do with us who knew you not in former days? Tell me this: whatever you do, what shall become of us?

7.translit:
yaamee aruvinaiyOm seeyOm, en nenjinaar
thaamee yaNukkaraays saarndhozhindhaar,-poomeeya
semmaadhai nin maarvil seervitthu, paaridandha
ammaa!nin paadhath tharuku.
7.meaning: We ourselves are of cruel karma and are far away, yet my heart has gone and become intimate, drawing close to your feet. O Lord who settled the red Lady (Lakshmi) who dwells in the lotus upon your chest and who lifted up the earth -- it has reached near your feet.

8.translit:
arukum suvadum therivuNarOm, anbee
perukum mikavidhuven? peseer,-parukalaam
paNpudaiyeer! paaraLandheer! paaviyeem kaNkaaNpariya
nuNpudaiyeer nummai numakku.
8.meaning: We cannot discern your nearness or your trace, yet love wells up greatly -- what is this? Tell us. O you of nature sweet enough to drink in! O you who measured the earth! O you of such subtlety that you cannot be seen by the eyes of us sinners -- you belong to your own self alone.

9.translit:
numakkadiyOm enRenRu nondhuduraitthen?, maalaar
thamakkavartthaam saarvariya raanaal - emakkini
yaadhaanu maakidukaaN nenjee! avartthiratthee
yaadhaanum sindhith thiru.
9.meaning: What use is it to grieve and say again and again, 'We are your servants'? If even to themselves those who love Tirumal are hard to attain, then for us, O heart, let it be whatever it may be! Only keep thinking ever of him in some way or other.

10.translit:
irunaalvar eeraindhin meloruvar, ettO
dorunaalvar Oriruvar allaal,- thirumaaRku
yaamaar vaNakkamaar eepaavam nannenjee
naamaa mikavudaiyOm naazh.
10.meaning: Apart from the great ones -- the eight (Vasus), the eleven (Rudras), the one above the ten, the twelve (Adityas), the four-and-eight, the two -- what are we to Tirumal, what is our worship worth? Alas, O good heart, we possess only abundant fault and unworthiness.

11.translit:
naazhaal amarmuyanRa vallarakkan, innuyirai,
vaazhaa vakaivalidhal ninvaliyee,-aazhaadha
paarumnee vaanumnee kaalumnee theeyumnee,
neerumnee yaayninRa nee.
11.meaning: To crush the very life of the mighty demon (Ravana) who strove in battle through deceit, so that he could live no more -- that was your strength alone. You are the earth that does not sink, you are the sky, you are the wind, you are the fire, you are the water -- you who stand as all these.

12.translit:
neeyanRee aazhthuyaril veezhvippaan ninRuzhanRaay?
pOyonRu solliyen? pOnenjee,-neeyenRum
kaazhtthupadhee sandharinum kaikoLLaay, kaNNan thaaL
vaazhtthuvadhee kaNdaay vazhakku.
12.meaning: Are you not the very one who has stood and toiled to cast me into deep sorrow? What use is it to go and say something? Go, O heart -- even if I instruct you firmly and again, you will not take it to heart; yet know that to praise the feet of Kannan is the proper way.

13.translit:
vazhakkodu maaRukoL anRadiyaar veeNda,
izhakkavum kaaNdum iRaiva.- izhapuNdee,
emmaaT koNdaakilum yaanveeNda, enkaNgaL
thammaalkaaT tunmeenis saay.
13.meaning: In times past, when your devotees prayed against custom and contradiction, we have even seen you suffer loss, O Lord. Even taking my service, since there is such loss, I pray: show the beauty of your form to my eyes, by my own eyes.
