Having brought forth the lotus-born Brahma and the eye-on-the-forehead Shiva, and having created, along with the pure gods, the world itself, He abides upon my forehead.

101.translit:
netriyuL ninru ennai aaLum nirai malar-paadangaL soodi,
kattrai-thuzhaay-mudi-koala kaNNa-piraanai thozhuvaar,
ottrai-pirai aNindhaanum naanmuganum indhiranum,
mattrai amararum ellaam vandhu enadhu ucchiyuLaane.
101.meaning: Wearing upon my head the close-clustered blossoming feet of Him who stands on my forehead and rules me, those who worship Krishna the Lord, beautiful with His crown of dense tulasi - Shiva who wears the single crescent moon, the four-faced Brahma, Indra, and all the other celestials too - have come and taken their place upon the crown of my head.

102.translit:
ucchiyuLLe nirkum deva-devarku kaNNa-piraarku,
icchaiyuL sella uNarthi vaN-kurugoor-Satakopan,
icchonna aayirattuL ivaiyum oar-pattu empiraarku,
nicchalum viNNappam seyya neeL-kazhal senni perume.
102.meaning: To the God of gods who stands upon the crown of my head, to Krishna my Lord, Satakopan of bountiful Kurugur, having made his longing known so that it reached Him, sang these. These too are ten among the thousand he uttered, offered to my Lord. For those who daily offer them as a prayer, His long feet shall grow great upon their heads.

103.translit:
poru maa neeL padai aazhi sangaththodu,
thiru maa neeL kazhal ezh-ulagum thozha,
oru maaNi kuraLaagi nimirndha, ak
karu-maaNikkam en kaNNuLadhaagume.
103.meaning: With His great long discus that battles and His conch, so that all seven worlds worship His sacred great long feet, He who became a single dwarf-bachelor and then rose up - that dark sapphire-gem Lord abides within my eye.

104.translit:
kaNNuLLe nirkum kaadhanmaiyaal thozhil,
eNNilum varum enin ini veNduvam?
maNNum neerum eriyum nal-vaayuvum,
viNNumaay viriyum em-piraanaiye.
104.meaning: Out of His love He stands within my eye; if, when I reckon them, His deeds come too in countless number, then what more do we need? He is my Lord who, becoming earth and water and fire and the good wind and the sky, spreads forth as all.

105.translit:
em-piraanai endhai thandhai thandhaikkum
tham-piraanai, thaN-thaamarai-kaNNanai,
kombu araavu nuN-ner-idai maarvanai,
em-piraanai thozhaay mada-nenjame.
105.meaning: He who is my Lord, the Lord even of my father and forefathers, the one with cool lotus eyes, who bears upon His chest the lady of slender waist like a creeper-tendril - worship that Lord of mine, O my tender heart.

106.translit:
nenjame nallai nallai, unnai petraal
en seyyoam, ini enna kuraivinam?
maindhanai malaraaL maNavaaLanai,
thunjum poadhum vidaadhu thodar kaNdaay.
106.meaning: O heart, you are good, you are good! Having gained you, what shall we not accomplish, and what want shall we now have? See that you pursue without ceasing, even in the hour of death, the youthful Lord, the bridegroom of the lotus-born Lakshmi.

107.translit:
kaNdaaye nenje karumangaL vaaykkinru, oar
eNdaanum indriye vandhu iyalumaaru,
uNdaanai ulagu-ezhum oar-moovadi
koNdaanai, kaNdu koNdanai neeyume.
107.meaning: You have seen, O heart, how all our purposes come to fruition, coming to pass of themselves without even a single thought of ours - He who swallowed the seven worlds, who took them all in one set of three strides; Him you yourself have beheld and grasped.

108.translit:
neeyum naanum in ner nirkil, mel mattroar,
noayum saark kodaan nenjame, sonnen,
thaayum thandhaiyumaay iv-vulaginil,
vaayum eesan maNi-vaNNan endhaiye.
108.meaning: If you and I stand together in this way, then He will not let any other affliction draw near us hereafter, O heart - I have told you. He is the Lord who, being mother and father in this world, comes to us readily, the gem-hued one, my Father.

109.translit:
endhaiye endrum em-perumaan endrum,
sindhaiyuL vaippan solluvan paaviyen,
endhai em-perumaan endru vaanavar,
sindhaiyuL vaiththu sollum selvanaiye.
109.meaning: Sinful though I am, I keep Him within my thought and utter 'My Father' always and 'My great Lord' always - He, the wealthy one, whom the celestials too place within their thought and call 'Our Father, our great Lord.'

110.translit:
selva naaraNan endra sol kettalum,
malgum kaN-pani naaduvan maayame,
allum nan-pagalum idai-veedu indri,
nalgi ennai vidaan nambi nambiye.
110.meaning: The very moment I hear the words 'the wealthy Narayana,' my eyes brim and overflow with tears, and I seek out His wondrous nature. Night and good day without any interval, showering grace, the perfect Lord will not let me go, the perfect one indeed.

111.translit:
nambiyai then-kuRungudi-ninra, ach
sem-pone thigazhum thiru-moorthiyai,
umbar vaanavar aadhi am-soadhiyai,
em-piraanai en solli maRappanoa.
111.meaning: The perfect Lord who stands in southern Kurungudi, the sacred form that shines like pure red gold, the beautiful radiance who is the source of the celestials above - my Lord; saying what could I ever forget Him?

112.translit:
maRappum gnaanamum naan onru uNarndhilan,
maRakkum endru sen-thaamarai-kaNNodu,
maRappu aRa ennuLLe manninaan thannai,
maRappanoa ini yaan en maNiyaiye.
112.meaning: Of forgetting and of knowing I myself understood nothing at all; lest I should forget, He with His red-lotus eyes settled firmly within me so that no forgetting could be - shall I now ever forget Him, my precious jewel?

113.translit:
maNiyai, vaanavar kaNNanai, thannadhu oar
aNiyai, then-kurugoor Satakopan sol
paNi sey aayirattuL ivai paththudan
thaNivilar kaRparel, kalvi vaayume.
113.meaning: The jewel, the Krishna of the celestials, the ornament that is His very own - Satakopan of southern Kurugur sang in service these among the thousand. If, without slackening, men learn these ten as well, their learning shall bear fruit.

114.translit:
vaayum thirai ugaLum kaanal mada-naaraay,
aayum amar-ulagum thunjilum nee thunjaayaal,
noayum payalaimaiyum meedhoora emme-poal,
neeyum thirumaalaal nenjam koat-pattaaye.
114.meaning: O tender heron of the seashore grove where the foaming waves leap and dance - even when the whole assembled world of the gods sleeps, you do not sleep! With sickness and pallor overwhelming you, like me you too have had your heart seized away by Tirumal.

115.translit:
koat-patta sindhaiyaay koor-vaaya andrile,
seat-patta yaamangaL seraadhu irangudhiyaal,
aat-patta emme-poal neeyum aravu-aNaiyaan,
thaat-patta thaN-thuzhaay-thaamam kaamutraaye.
115.meaning: O sharp-beaked andril bird, your mind too is captured; through the long-drawn watches of the night you do not rest but cry out in grief. Like me who have become His bondservant, you too have come to crave the cool tulasi garland that lies at the feet of Him who rests upon the serpent-couch.

116.translit:
kaamutra kai-aRavoadu elle iraap-pagal,
nee mutra kaN-thuyilaay nenju-urugi engudhiyaal,
thee mutra then-ilangai oottinaan thaaL nayandha,
yaam-utradhu utraayoa vaazhi kanai-kadale.
116.meaning: With the helplessness of one who longs, alas, by night and by day you never close your eyes in sleep, but your heart melts and you pine. Have you too come to suffer what we suffer, who yearn for the feet of Him who set southern Lanka wholly to the fire - may you flourish, O roaring sea!

117.translit:
kadalum malaiyum visumbum thuzhaay em-poal,
sudar-koL iraap-pagal thunjaayaal thaN-vaadaay,
adal-koL padai-aazhi am-maanai kaaNbaan nee,
udalam noay-utraayoa oozhi-thoar-oozhiye.
117.meaning: Ranging over sea and mountain and sky like me, O cool north wind, through the bright nights and days you never sleep! To behold the Lord with His battling discus-weapon, have you too fallen sick in body, age after endless age?

118.translit:
oozhi-thoar-oozhi ulagukku neer koNdu,
thoazhiyarum yaamum-poal neeraay negizhginra,
vaazhiya vaaname, neeyum madhu-soodan,
paazhimaiyil patta avan kaN-paasaththaal naivaaye.
118.meaning: Age after endless age you bring water for the world - may you flourish, O sky, who melt away dissolving into rain, like my companions and me! You too have grown faint, caught fast in the snare of the eyes of Madhusudana, fallen into His mighty splendour.

119.translit:
naivaaya emme-poal naaL-madhiye nee in-naaL,
mai-vaan iruL agatraay maazhaandhu thembudhiyaal,
ai-vaay aravu-aNai-mel aazhi-perumaanaar,
mey-vaasagam kettu un meyn-neermai thoatraaye.
119.meaning: O moon of the day, pining away like me, on this day you do not dispel the darkness of the black sky but grow bewildered and waste away. Having heard the true word of the discus-bearing Lord who reclines upon the five-headed serpent-couch, you too have lost your true nature.

1000.translit:
toNDeer! vammin nam sudaroLi yoru tani mudalvan
aNDam moovula kaLandavan aNi tiru mOhoor
eNdisaiyum meen karumbodu poruñ chennel viLaiya
koNDa kOyilai valañ cheydiŋ kaaDudum kootthE.
1000.meaning: O devotees, come! He is our radiant light, the one unique primal Lord who measured the three worlds and all the cosmos. Let us circumambulate the temple He has taken as His abode in beautiful Tirumohur, where in all eight directions fish, sugarcane and ripening red paddy flourish, and let us dance the sacred dance there.

1001.translit:
kootthan kOvalan kudatRRu val asurargaL kootRRam
Etthum naŋgaTkum amararkkum munivarkkum inban
vaaittha taN paNai vaLa vayal soozh tiru mOhoor
aatthan taamarai yaDi yandRi matRRilam araNE.
1001.meaning: He is the dancer, the cowherd, the death of the cruel demons who would harm; He is the joy of us who praise Him, of the gods, and of the sages. He is the trusted one of Tirumohur, ringed by fertile fields and cool meadows. We have no refuge other than His lotus feet.

1002.translit:
matRRilam araN vaan perum paazh tani mudalaa
sutRRu neer padaitthadan vazhit ton muni mudalaa
mutRRum dEvarOD ulagu seyvaan tiru mOhoor
sutRRi naam valañ cheyya nam tuyar keDum kaDidE.
1002.meaning: We have no other refuge. He who, as the sole primal cause out of the vast great void, created the encircling waters, and then through them the ancient first sage Brahma, and so made the gods and all the worlds, dwells in Tirumohur. If we go round and circumambulate Him, our sorrows will perish swiftly.

1003.translit:
tuyar keDum kaDidaDaindu van daDiyavar tozhumin
uyar koL sOlai oN taDa maNi oLi tiru mOhoor
peyargaL aayiram uDaiya val arakkar puk kazhunda
tayaradan petRRa maradaka maNit taDatthinaiyE.
1003.meaning: Our sorrow will quickly perish; come quickly, devotees, and worship Him. In Tirumohur of lofty groves, bright ponds and gleaming jewels, He is the emerald-gemmed pool that Dasaratha begot as a son, who made the mighty demons of a thousand names sink and drown. Worship Him.

1004.translit:
maNit taDattaDi malar kaNgaL pavaLac chevvaay
aNi koL naal taDam tOL deyvam asuraraiy endRum
tuNikkum val araTTan uRai pozhil tiru mOhoor
naNitthu nammuDai nal araN naam aDaindanamE.
1004.meaning: That gem-pool has lotus eyes, a coral-red mouth, and four broad ornamented shoulders; He is the divine Lord, the mighty hero who forever cuts down the demons. He dwells in the groves of Tirumohur, near at hand. We have reached Him, our good refuge.

1005.translit:
naam aDaindu nal araN tamak kendRu nal amarar
teemai seyyum val asuraraiy añjic chendRaDaindaal
kaama roopam koNDezhundaLip paan tiru mOhoor
naama mE navindReNNumin Etthumin namar gaaL.
1005.meaning: When the good immortals, fearing the powerful demons who do evil, came and took refuge in Him as their good fortress, He of Tirumohur rose up taking a beautiful form at will and protected them. O our people, recite, ponder and praise only His holy name.

1006.translit:
Etthumin namar gaaL endRu taan kuDam aaDu
kootthanai kurugoorc chaTagOpan kutRREval
vaaittha aayiratt uLLivai vaN tiru mOhoorkku
eettha pattivai Ettha vallaarkk iDar keDumE.
1006.meaning: Saying 'praise Him, O our people,' Satakopa of Kurugur sang in loyal service these ten of the well-wrought thousand verses on the Lord who dances the pot-dance, offering them to bountiful Tirumohur. For those able to praise with these ten, all distress will perish.

1007.translit:
keDum iDaraaya vellaam kEsavaa veNNa naaLum
koDu vinai seyyum kootRRin tamargaLum kuRugakillaar
viDamuDai yaravil paLLi virumbinaan surumbalatRRum
taDamuDai vayal anandapura nagarip puhuduminRE.
1007.meaning: All distress will perish if one says 'Kesava' each day; even the agents of cruel-acting Death will not come near. He who loves to recline on the venom-bearing serpent dwells where bees hum—enter this day the city of Anantapuram with its pond-filled fields.

1008.translit:
indRu pOyp puhudiraagi lezhumaiyum Edam saara
kundRu nEr maaDam aaDE kurundu sEr seruntipunnai
mandRalar pozhil anandapura nagar maayan naamam
ondRumOr aayiramaam uLLuvaark kumbaroorE.
1008.meaning: If you go and enter this day, no calamity will befall you for all seven births. Anantapuram is a city of mansions tall as hills, of groves where kurundu, serunti and punnai trees blossom and shed fragrance. To those who meditate on the names of the wondrous Lord—each one is as a thousand—heaven becomes their town.

1009.translit:
oorum puT koDiyum ahdE ulagollaam uNDumizhndaan
sErum taN ananadapuram sikkenap puhudiraagil
teerum nOy vinaigaLellaam tiNNam naam aRiyac chondROm
pErum Or aayiratt uL ondRu neer pEsuminE.
1009.meaning: He who swallowed all the worlds and spat them out again rides the bird and bears the bird as His banner. If you firmly enter the cool Anantapuram where He resides, all your diseases and karmas will end—surely; we tell you that you may know it. Speak even one of His thousand names.

1010.translit:
pEsumin koosaminRip periya neer vElai soozhndu
vaasamE kazhumuñ chOlai vayalaNi anandapuram
nEsam sey duRaiginRaanai neRimaiyaal malargaL toovi
poosanai seyginDRaargaL puNNiyam seydavaaRE.
1010.meaning: Speak His names without hesitation. Anantapuram, ringed by the great sea, adorned with fields and fragrant groves, is where He dwells with love. Those who scatter flowers upon Him in the proper way and perform worship—how blessed they are, how much merit they have done!

1011.translit:
puNNiyam seydu nalla punalODu malargaL toovi
eNNumin endai naamam ip piRapp aRukkum appaal
tiNNam naam aRiyac chondROm seRi pozhil
anandapuratt aNNalaar kamala paadam aNuguvaar amararaavaar.
1011.meaning: Doing meritorious deeds, offering pure water and flowers, meditate on our Father's name; it will cut off this birth and what lies beyond—firmly we tell you, that you may know. Those who approach the lotus feet of the Lord of Anantapuram with its dense groves will become immortals.

1012.translit:
amararaayt tiriginDRaarkaTk aadi sEra anandapuratt
amarar kOn arccikkinDRaŋ kahappaNi seyvar viNNOr
namargaLO! sollak kENmin naamum pOy naNugavENDum
kumaranaar taadai tunbam tuDaittha gOvindanaarE.
1012.meaning: To reach the source of those who roam as immortals, Indra the king of the gods worships at Anantapuram, and the celestials do inner service there. O our people, listen as I tell you—we too must go and draw near to Govinda, who wiped away the sorrow of Kumara's father (Brahma).

1013.translit:
tuDaittha gOvindanaarE ulaguyir dEvum matRRum
paDaittha em parama moortti paambaNaip paLLi koNDaan
maDaittalai vaaLai paayum vayalaNi anandapuram
kaDaittalai seeykkap petRRaal kaDu vinai kaLaiyalaamE.
1013.meaning: Govinda who wiped away that sorrow is our supreme Lord who created the worlds, their lives, the gods and all else; He reclines upon the serpent-couch. In field-adorned Anantapuram, where scabbard-fish leap at the sluice-gates, if we are granted even to sweep the temple threshold, our fierce karma can be cleared away.

1014.translit:
kaDu vinai kaLaiyalaagum kaamanaip payanda kaaLai
iDavakai koNDaden bar ezhilaNi anandapuram
paDamuDai yaravil paLLi payinDRavan paadam kaaNa
naDaminO namargaL uLLeer! naam umak kaRiyac chondROm.
1014.meaning: Fierce karma can be cleared. They say that the young Lord who fathered Manmatha took Anantapuram, adorned with beauty, as His chosen abode. To behold the feet of Him who reclines upon the hooded serpent, walk forth, O you among our people! We have told you so that you may know.

1015.translit:
naam umak kaRiyac chonna naaLgaLum naNiyavaana
sEmam nam guDaitthuk kaNDeer seRi pozhil anandapuram
tooma nal virai malargaL tuvaLaRa aaynduk koNDu
vaamanan aDikk endRetta maayndaRum vinaigaL taamE.
1015.meaning: The days we told you of, that you might know, have drawn near; behold, our well-being is assured. Anantapuram has dense groves. Choose fragrant, unfading flowers free of blemish, and offer them at the feet of Vamana—and your karmas will themselves wither and vanish.

1016.translit:
maayndaRum vinaigaL taamE maadavaa enna naaLum
Eyntha pon madiL anandapura nagar endaikk endRu
chaandoDu viLakkam toobam taamarai malargaL nalla
aayndu koNDEttha vallaar antamil puhazhinaarE.
1016.meaning: Karmas of themselves will wither and vanish if one daily says 'Madhava.' For our Father in the city of Anantapuram, girt with fitting golden ramparts, those who can choose sandal-paste, lamps, incense and fine lotus flowers and praise Him—theirs is endless glory.

1017.translit:
antamil puhazh anandapura nagar aadi tannai
kondalar pozhil kurugoor maaRan sol aayiratthuL
aindinODaindum vallaar aNaivar pOy amar ulagil
paintoDi maDandaiyar tam vEymaru tOL iNaiyE.
1017.meaning: Of the thousand verses of Maran of Kurugur with its blossom-clustered groves, on the primal Lord of endlessly glorious Anantapuram city, those who master these ten will go and reach, in the world of the immortals, the bamboo-like twin shoulders of the gold-bangled celestial maidens.

1018.translit:
vEymaru tOL iNai meliyumaalO!
 melivum en tanimaiyum yaadum nOkkaa
kaamaru kuyilgaLum koovumaalO!
 kaNamayil avai kalandaalumaalO
aamaruvina nirai mEykka nee pOkku
 oru pahalaayira moozhiyaalO taamaraik
kaNgaL koNDeerdiyaalO!
 tagavilai tagavilaiyE nee kaNNaa.
1018.meaning: My bamboo-like twin shoulders waste away! Heeding neither my wasting nor my loneliness, the lovely cuckoos keep calling, and the flocks of peacocks mingle and dance. The single day you go off to graze the herds of cattle stretches like a thousand aeons. With Your lotus eyes You draw away my life—You are without grace, without grace, O Kanna!

1019.translit:
tagavilai tagavilai yE nee kaNNaa!
 taDamulai puNar toRum puNarcchik kaaraa
suga veLLam visumb iRandu aRivai moozhkkac
 soozhndadu kanavena neeŋgi yaaŋgE
akavuyir agamadan dORum uL pukku
 aaviyin paramalla vETkai andO
miga miga iniyunnaip pirivai yaamaal
 veeva nin pasu nirai mEykkap pOkkE.
1019.meaning: You are without grace, without grace, O Kanna! Each time You embrace my full breasts, the embrace never sates; a flood of bliss rising past the heavens overwhelms my consciousness, then, like a dream, it withdraws and is gone. Entering deep into the inmost soul, this longing is beyond what my life can bear—alas! Now more and more we must endure Your parting, as You go off to graze Your herds of cattle, that I should perish.

1020.translit:
veevan nin pasunirai meeykkap pookku
vevvuyir koNden thaavi veemaal
yaavarum thuNaiyillai yaan irundhu
un anjana meeniyai aaTTam kaaNeen
poovadhu andru oru pakal nee akandraal
poru kayal kaNNiNai neerum nillaa
saavadhu iv aaykkulattu aaychchiyoomaayp pirandha
vit thozhuthaiyoom thanimai thaanee.
1020.meaning: You go out to graze your fresh herds of cattle, and I shall perish; my breath grows hot and my soul burns. I have no one to help me; sitting here I cannot bear to gaze upon your dark collyrium-hued form. If you depart even for a single day, the tears will not cease from this pair of fighting-fish-like eyes. This loneliness of ours, born as the lowly slave-women of this cowherd clan, is itself a thing fit to die of.

1021.translit:
thozhuthaiyoom thanimaiyum thuNai pirindhaar
thuyaramum ninaikilai koovindhaa nin
thozhuthanil pasukkaLai yee virumbith
thurandhu emmai iTTu avai meeykkap poodhi
pazhutha nal amudhin in saatru veLLam
paaviyeen manam akam thoorum uL puk
kazhutha nin sengani vaayin kaLvap paNimozhi
ninai thorum aaviveemaal.
1021.meaning: Govinda, you do not consider the loneliness of us slave-women, nor the grief of those parted from their beloved. Driving forth the cattle from your cowshed, longing for them, you forsake us and go out to graze them. O sweet-throated one, whose words are like a flood of nectar-juice from ripe good fruit that has flowed and entered deep into the heart of sinful me, each time I recall the deceitful gentle words of your red fruit-like mouth, my soul burns.

1022.translit:
paNimozhi ninai thorum aavi veemaal pakal nirai
meeykkiya pooya kaNNaa!
piNi avizh mallikai vaadai thoovap
peru madha maalaiyum vandhu indru aaloo
maNi miku maarvinil mullaip poodhu
ev vana mulai kamazhvittu un vaay amudham thandhu
aNi miku thaamaraik kaiyai andhoo
adichchiyoom thalai misai nee aNiyaay!
1022.meaning: O Kannan who went by day to graze the herds, each time I recall your gentle words my soul burns. The north wind scatters the jasmine whose buds have burst open, and the great maddening dusk too has come this day. O let the jasmine blossoms upon my jewel-rich bosom make my forest-fragrant breasts breathe sweetness, and giving me the nectar of your mouth, alas, place your beauty-rich lotus hands upon the head of us slave-women!

1023.translit:
adichchiyoom thalai misai nee aNiyaay
aazhi am kaNNaa! un koolap paadham
pidiththadhu naduvu unakku arivaimaarum palar
adhu nirka em peNmai aatroom
vadi thadam kaNNiNai neerum nillaa
manamum nillaa emakku adhu thannaalee
vedippu nin pasunirai meeykkap pookku
veem emadhu uyir azhal mezhukil uLukkee.
1023.meaning: O Kannan with the discus, place upon the head of us slave-women your beautiful feet that we have grasped. Though there are many other women between us, leave that aside, our womanhood cannot endure. Because of it the tears will not cease from this pair of long sharp eyes, nor will my mind be still. Your going out to graze your fresh herds is a thing that bursts us apart; our souls melt and burn like wax in fire.

1024.translit:
veem emadhu uyir azhal mezhukil uLukku
veL vaLai meekalai kazhandru veezha
thoo malark kaNNiNai muttham soorath
thuNai mulai payandhu enadhu thoLkaL vaada
maa maNi vaNNaa un sem kamala vaNNa
men malar adi noova nee pooy
aa makizhndhu ukandhavai meeykkindru unnoodu
asurarkaL thalaippeyyil evankol aangee?
1024.meaning: Our souls burn and melt like wax in fire, my white bangles and waist-girdle slip off and fall, the pearls of tears pour from this pair of pure flower-like eyes, my twin breasts grow pale and my shoulders wither. O sapphire-hued one, while your soft lotus-flower feet of red-lotus hue suffer pain, you go out delighting and graze the cattle; if the demons should come to grips with you there, what then would happen?

1025.translit:
asurarkaL thalaippeyyil evankol aangu endru
aazhum en aaruyir aan pin pookeel
kasikaiyum veeTkaiyum uL kalandhu
kalaviyum naliyum en kai kazhiyeel
vasi seyyum un thaamaraik kaNNum vaayum
kaikaLum peethaka udaiyum kaaTTi
osi sey nuN idai iLa aaychchiyar
nee ukakkum nallavarodum uzhitharaayee.
1025.meaning: "What would happen there if the demons should come to grips with you?" thus my precious life sinks in fear; do not go behind the cattle. Tenderness and longing mingle within, and love itself torments me; do not leave my hand. Showing your captivating lotus eyes, your mouth, your hands and your golden garment, do not go roaming about with those good women, the young cowherd-girls of slender swaying waists whom you delight in.

1026.translit:
ukakkum nallavarodum uzhi thandhu
un than thiru uLLam idar kedum thorum naangaL
viyakka inburudhum em peNmai aatroorum
em perumaan pasu meeykkap pookeel
mikap pala akararkaL veeNdu uruvam koNdu
nindru uzhi tharuvar kanjan eeva
akappadil avarodum ninnodu aangee
avaththangaL viLaiyum en sol koL andhoo!
1026.meaning: As you roam with those good women you delight in, whenever your sacred heart's distress is relieved, we marvel and rejoice, though our womanhood can scarcely bear it. O my Lord, do not go out to graze the cattle. Very many demons, taking on whatever forms they wish, go roaming about at Kamsa's command; should you be caught, calamity will befall both them and you there. Alas, heed my word!

1027.translit:
avaththangaL viLaiyum en sol koL andhoo
asurarkaL vankaiyar kanjan eevath
thavaththavar maruka nindru uzhi tharuvar
thanimaiyum peridhu unakku iraamanaiyum
uvar thalai udan thiri kilaiyum endrendru
ooduru en eDai aavi veemaal
thivaththilum pasunirai meeyppu uvaththi
sengani vaay engaL aayar deevee.
1027.meaning: Calamity will befall; heed my word, alas! The cruel-handed demons, at Kamsa's command, go roaming about so that even the austere ones tremble; and your loneliness is great. "You will not even roam together with Balarama," thinking this again and again, in my sulking my whole frame and soul burn. O red fruit-mouthed god of our cowherds, you delight in grazing the fresh herds more than in heaven itself.

1028.translit:
sengani vaay engaL aayar deevu
at thiruvadi thiruvadi meel porunal
sangu aNi thuraivan vaN then kurukoor
vaN sadakoopan sol aayiraththuL
mangaiyar aaychchiyar aayndha maalai
avanodum pirivadharku irangi thaiyal
angu avan pasunirai meeppozhippaan
uraiththana ivaiyum patthu avatrin saarvee.
1028.meaning: Upon the sacred feet of that Lord, the red fruit-mouthed god of our cowherds, are these — of the thousand verses of bounteous Satakopan of fertile southern Kurukur, lord of the shore of the Porunal river adorned with conches, this garland of ten composed as the words of the cowherd-maidens. These ten, in which a tender girl, grieving at parting from him, spoke to stop him there from going to graze the cattle, are part of those thousand.

1029.translit:
saarvee thava nerikku Daamoodharan thaaL taL,
kaar meeka vaNNan kamala nayanaththan,
neer vaanam maN er kaal aay nindra neemiyaan,
peer vaanavarkaL pidhatrum perumaiyanee.
1029.meaning: The feet of Damodara are the refuge for the path of austerity — he of the dark cloud's hue, with lotus eyes, the discus-bearer who stands as water, sky, earth, fire and wind, the great one whose glory the celestials with their many names babble forth in praise.

1030.translit:
perumaiyanee vaanaththu imaiyoorkkum kaaNdarku
arumaiyanee aakaththu aNaiyaadhaarkku endrum
thirumey uraikindra sengaN maal naaLum
irumai vinai kadindhu ingu ennai aaL kindraanee.
1030.meaning: He is the glorious one, hard even for the unblinking gods of heaven to behold, ever unattainable to those who do not embrace him in their hearts; the red-eyed Tirumal in whose sacred body Lakshmi dwells. Daily destroying the twofold karma, he rules over me here.

1031.translit:
aaLkindraan aazhiyaan aaraal kuraivudaiyam?
meeLkindradhu illai piravith thuyar kadindhoom,
vaaL keNdai oN kaN madappinnai than keeLvan,
thaaL kaNdu koNdu en thalai meel punaindheenee.
1031.meaning: The discus-bearer rules over me; by whom then are we lacking in anything? There is no returning; we have cut off the sorrow of birth. The beloved of the gentle Nappinnai of bright bream-like sword-eyes — I have beheld his feet and placed them upon my head.

1032.translit:
thalai meel punaindheen saraNangaL aalin
ilai meel thuyindraan imaiyoor vaNanga
malai meel thaan nindru en manaththuL irundhaanai
nilai peerkka laakaamai nichchiththirundheenee.
1032.meaning: I have placed upon my head the refuge-giving feet of him who slept upon the banyan leaf, who, while the unblinking gods bow to him, stands upon the hill and yet dwells within my mind. I have become certain that he can never be dislodged from his place there.

1033.translit:
nichchiththirundheen en nenjam kazhiyaamai
kaichchakkaraththaNNal kaLvam peridhudaiyan
mecchappadaan pirarkku mey poolum poy vallan
nacchappadum namakku naakath thaNaiyaanee.
1033.meaning: I have become certain that he will not leave my heart. The Lord with the discus in his hand has great cunning; he is not praised by others, and is skilled at falsehood that seems like truth. Yet he who reclines upon the serpent is the one to be longed for by us.

1034.translit:
naakath thaNaiyaanai naaL thorum njaanaththaal
aakath thaNaippaarkku aruL seyyum ammaanai
maakaththu iLa madhiyam seerum sadaiyaanai
paakaththu vaiththaan than paadham paNindheenee.
1034.meaning: To him who reclines upon the serpent, to the Lord who grants grace to those who daily embrace him in their hearts through wisdom — I have bowed at the feet of the one who placed in one half of his body the one with matted locks upon which the young crescent moon of the sky rests.

1035.translit:
paNi nenjee! naaLum parama paramparanai
piNi ondrum saaraa piravi keduththu aaLum
maNi nindra soodhi madhusoodhan en ammaan
aNi nindra sempon adal aazhiyaanee.
1035.meaning: Bow, O heart, daily to the supreme one beyond all that is supreme — Madhusudana, my Lord, the radiant light shining like a gem, who, untouched by any affliction, destroys births and rules over us; he who bears the fierce discus of beauteous red gold.

1036.translit:
aazhiyaan aazhi amararkkum appaalaan oozhiyaan
oozhi padaiththaan nirai meeyththaan
paazhi am thoLaal varai eduththaan paadhangaL
vaazhi en nenjee! maravaadhu vaazh kaNdaay.
1036.meaning: The discus-bearer, the ocean-coloured one, who is beyond even the immortals, the lord of the aeons, who created the aeons, who grazed the herds, who lifted the mountain with his mighty beautiful shoulders — long live his feet! O my heart, live without forgetting them, behold.

1037.translit:
kaNdeen kamala malarp paadham, kaaNdadhumee,
viNdee ozhindha vinaiyaayin ellaam,
thoNdee seydhu, endrum
thozhudhu vazhi ozhuka,
paNdee paraman paNiththa paNivakaiyee.
1037.meaning: I have beheld the lotus-flower feet, and the very moment I beheld them all my karmas burst apart and vanished. To do service forever, to worship and live in his way — this is the manner of service that the Supreme One ordained of old.

1038.translit:
vakaiyaal manam ondri maadhavanai naaLum
pukaiyaal viLakkaal pudhu malaraal neeraal
thikai thorum amararkaL sendru iraindja nindra
thakaiyaan saraNam thamarkaT koor patree.
1038.meaning: With the mind made one in every way, worshipping Madhava daily with incense, with lamp, with fresh flowers, with water — he who stands as the celestials come from every direction to bow before him: his refuge-giving feet are the sole support for his devoted servants.

1039.translit:
patru endru patrip parama paramparanai
matru iNdoL maalai vazhudhi vaLanaadan
sol todai andhaadhi oor aayiraththuL ip patthum
katraarkku oor patraakum kaNNan kazhaliNaiyee.
1039.meaning: Holding fast, saying "this is the support," to the supreme one beyond all that is supreme — these ten of the thousand word-woven antati verses of the lord of fertile Vazhudi land, garlanded with strong shoulders. To those who have learned them, the twin ankleted feet of Kannan become the sole refuge.

1040.translit:
kaNNan kazhal iNai naNNum manam udaiyeer
veNNum tirunaamam thiNNam naaraNamee.
1040.meaning: O you who possess the heart that draws near to the twin feet of Kannan! The sacred name to meditate upon is surely Narayana.

1041.translit:
naaraNan emmaan paaraNangaaLan
vaaraNam tolaiththa kaaraNan thaanee.
1041.meaning: Narayana, my Lord, is the master who is the beloved adornment of the earth. He himself is the cause who destroyed the elephant.

1042.translit:
thaanee ulagelaam thaanee padaiththidanthu
thaanee uNdumizhnthu thaanee aaLvaanee.
1042.meaning: He himself is all the worlds; he himself created them and lifted up the earth; he himself swallowed and spat them forth; he himself rules over all.

1043.translit:
aaLvaan aazhineer kOLvaaya aravaNaiyaan
thaaLvaay malaridtu naaLvaay naadeeree.
1043.meaning: He who rules reclines upon the serpent whose mouth seizes, upon the ocean of deep waters. Offering flowers at his feet, seek him each and every day.

1044.translit:
naadeer naaLthOrum vaadaa malarkoNdu
paadeer avannaamam veedee peRalaamee.
1044.meaning: Seek him each day with unfading flowers; sing his name. Thereby you may attain liberation.

1045.translit:
meeyaan veengadam kaayaa malarvaNNan
peeyaar mulaiyuNda vaayaan maadhavanee.
1045.meaning: He who dwells on Venkatam, of the hue of the kaaya blossom, with the mouth that drank from the breast of the demoness (Putana) - he is Madhava.

1046.translit:
maadhavan endrendru Odha valleereel
theedhondru madaiyaa eedham saaraavee.
1046.meaning: If you are able to recite, again and again, 'Madhava,' no single evil shall draw near, and no harm shall reach you.

1047.translit:
saaraa eedhangaL neeraar mugilvaNNan
peer aar Odhuvaar aaraar amararee.
1047.meaning: Harms shall not reach those who recite the names full of him who is of the hue of the water-laden cloud. Whoever recites them - they indeed become immortals (devas).

1048.translit:
amararkku ariyaanai tamarkadku eLiyaanai
amarath thozhuvaarkadku amaraa vinaigaLee.
1048.meaning: To him who is hard to attain even for the immortals, yet easy for his own devotees - for those who worship him steadfastly, deeds (karma) shall not cling.

1049.translit:
vinaival iruLennum munaigaL veruvippOm
sunai nan malaridtu ninaimin nediyaanee.
1049.meaning: The battlefronts called the darkness of powerful karma flee in terror. Offering the fine flowers of the spring-pool, meditate upon the tall (eminent) Lord.

1050.translit:
nediyaan aruL soodum padiyaan sadagOpan
nodi aayiraththippaththu adiyaarkku aruL pERee.
1050.meaning: Of the thousand utterances of Satakopa, who is one fitted to wear the grace of the tall Lord, this decad is, for the devotees, the gift of grace.

1051.translit:
aruLpeRuvaar adiyaartham adiyaneeRku aazhiyaan
aruLtharuvaan amaikindraan adhunamadhu vidhivagaiyee
iruLtharumaa nyaalaththuL inippiRavi yaanveeNdeen
maruLozhinee madanenjee! vaattaaRRaan adivaNangee.
1051.meaning: To me, the servant of the servants of those who receive grace, the Lord who holds the discus is ready to grant his grace - and that is our destined fortune. In this great world that breeds darkness, I desire no further birth. Cast off your delusion, O foolish heart, and bow at the feet of the Lord of Vaattaaru.

1052.translit:
vaattaaRRaa nadivaNangi maanyaalap piRappaRuppaan
keettaayee madanenjee! keesavanem perumaanai
paattaaya palapaadip pazhavinaigaL paRRaRuththu
naattaarO diyalvozhinthu naaraNanai naNNinamee.
1052.meaning: Bowing at the feet of the Lord of Vaattaaru, to cut off birth in this great world - have you heard, O foolish heart? Singing many songs of our Lord Kesava, severing the grip of old karmas, ceasing to follow the way of worldly folk, we have drawn near to Narayana.

1053.translit:
naNNinam naaraNanai naamangaL palasolli
maNNulagil vaLammik ka vaattaaRRaan vandhindru
viNNulagam tharuvaanaay viraigindraan vidhivagaiyee
eNNinavaa Raagaavik karumanga LennenjEe.
1053.meaning: We have drawn near to Narayana, uttering his many names. The Lord of Vaattaaru, abundant in richness in this earthly world, has come today and hastens to grant the world of heaven - and that is our destined fortune. O my heart, these deeds shall not turn out as we had planned (by our own reckoning)!

1054.translit:
ennenjath thuLLirundhing kirundhamizhnoolivaimozhinthu
vannenjath thiraNiyanai maarvidantha vaattaaRRaan
mannanjap paaradhaththup paaNdavarkkaap padaithottaan
nannenjee! namperumaan namakkaruLthaan seyvaanee.
1054.meaning: Dwelling within my heart and prompting me here to utter these sweet Tamil verses, the Lord of Vaattaaru who tore open the chest of hard-hearted Hiranya - who took up arms for the Pandavas in the Bharata war so that kings trembled - O good heart! Our Lord will surely bestow his grace upon us.

1055.translit:
vaaneeRa vazhithantha vaattaaRRaan paNivagaiyee
naaneeRap peRu kindren naragaththai nakunenjee
theeneeRu malarththuLavam thigazhpaadhan sezhumpaRavai
thaaneeRith thirivaana thaaLiNaiyen thalaimeelee.
1055.meaning: By serving the Lord of Vaattaaru who granted the path to ascend to heaven, I am able to rise. O heart that mocks at hell! He whose feet shine with honey-laden tulasi blossoms, who roves mounted upon the lovely bird (Garuda) - his twin feet are upon my head.

1056.translit:
thalaimeela thaaLiNaigaL thaamaraikkaN ennammaan
nilaipeeraan ennenjath theppozhudhum emperumaan
malaimaadath tharavaNaimeel vaattaaRRaan madhamikka
kolaiyaanai marupposith thaan kuraikazhalthaaL kuruginamee.
1056.meaning: His twin feet are upon my head; my Lord of the lotus eyes never departs from my heart at any time - my Lord. He who reclines upon the serpent in the mountain-like mansion at Vaattaaru, who broke the tusk of the murderous, frenzied elephant - we have drawn near to his resounding, anklet-adorned feet.

1057.translit:
kuraikazhalgaL kuruginam nam gOvindhan kudikoNdaan
thiraikuzhuvu kadalpudaisoozh thennaadtuth thiladhamanna
varaikuzhuvum maNimaada vaattaaRRaan malaradimeel
viraikuzhuvum naRunthuLavam meynnindru kazhumumee.
1057.meaning: We have drawn near to his resounding feet. Our Govinda has taken up his abode in Vaattaaru, which is like the sacred mark (tilaka) of the southern land, girt all round by the wave-thronged sea, with gem-set mansions thronging like mountains. Upon the flower-feet of the Lord of Vaattaaru, fragrant tulasi clusters cling and waft their scent truly.

1058.translit:
meynnindru kazhumthuLava viraiyeeRu thirumudiyan
kainnindra sakkaraththan karudhumidam porudhupunal
mainnindra varaipOlum thiruvuruva vaattaaRRaaRku
ennandri seydheenaa ennenjil thigazhavadhuvee?
1058.meaning: He whose sacred crown is heaped with the rising fragrance of clinging tulasi, who bears the discus poised in his hand, whose chosen abode is Vaattaaru of the surging waters, of a holy form like a dark-hued mountain - what good deed have I done that he should shine within my heart?

1059.translit:
thigazhgindra thirumaarbil thirumangai thannOdum
thigazhgindra thirumaalaar seervidamthaN vaattaaRu
pugazhgindra puLLoo rdhi pOrarakkar kulamkeduththaan
igazhvindri ennenjath theppozhudhum piriyaanee.
1059.meaning: The abode where Tirumal shines together with Lakshmi who shines upon his radiant sacred chest is the cool Vaattaaru. He who rides the praised bird (Garuda), who destroyed the clan of the warring demons, never departs from my heart at any time, without scorn.

1060.translit:
piriyaadhaaT cheyyendru pirappaRuththaaL aRakkoNdaan
aRiyaaki iraNiyanai aakangkeeN daanandru
periyaarkkaaT paTTakkaal peRaadhabayan peRumaaRu
varivaaLvaay aravaNaimEl vaaTTaaRRaan kaaTTinanE.
1060.meaning: Saying 'serve me without parting,' he severed the cycle of births and graciously accepted her. As Hari of old, he tore open the chest of Hiranya. So that those who serve the great ones may obtain the boon they could not otherwise gain, the Lord of Vaattaaru, reclining upon the striped-mouthed sword-fanged serpent-couch, has shown the way.

1061.translit:
kaaTTiththan kanaikazhalkaL kadunarakam pukalozhiththa
vaaTTaaRRem perumaanai vaLangkurukoorch chadakOpan
paaTTaaya tamizhmaalai yaayiraththuL ippaththum
kETTu aaraar vaanavarkaL chevikkiniya chenjchollE.
1061.meaning: Showing his resounding anklet-clad feet, our Lord of Vaattaaru who removes the path to cruel hell — of the thousand verses, a garland in Tamil song, sung by Satakopan of fertile Kurukoor — these ten with their sweet and noble words are pleasing to the ears of the celestials, who never tire of hearing them.

1062.translit:
chenchoR kavikaaL. uyirkaaththaaT cheymin tirumaa lirunjchOlai
vanjchak kaLvan maamaayan maayak kaviyaay vandhu en
nenjchu muyiru muL kalandhu nindraar aRiyaa vaNNam en
nenjchu muyirum avaiyuNdu thaanE yaaki niRaindhaanE
1062.meaning: O poets of noble words! Guard your souls and render service to Tirumaalirunjcholai. That deceptive thief, the great enchanter, came as a mysterious poem and, mingling within my heart and soul, stood in a way none could perceive; consuming my very heart and soul, becoming himself, he filled them entirely.

1063.translit:
thaanE yaaki niRaindhellaa ulakum uyirum thaanEyaay
thaanE yaanen paanaakith thannaith thaanE thudhiththu enakkuth
thEnE paalE kannalE amudhE tirumaalirunjchOlai
kOnE yaaki nindrozhindhaan ennai muRRum uyiruNdE
1063.meaning: Becoming himself and filling all, being himself all the worlds and all souls, becoming the very 'I' that says 'I,' praising himself through himself, he became for me honey, milk, sugarcane, nectar — the Lord-King of Tirumaalirunjcholai he stood as, having wholly consumed my soul.

1064.translit:
ennai muRRum uyiruNden maaya aakkai yidhanuLpukku
ennai muRRum thaanEyaay nindra maaya ammaan chEr
thennan tirumaa lirunjchOlaith thichaikai kooppich thErndhayaan
innam pOvE nEkolO. engol ammaan tiruvaruLE?
1064.meaning: Having wholly consumed my soul, entering into this illusory body of mine, the wondrous Lord who stood as entirely myself — toward his abode, the southern Tirumaalirunjcholai, I joined my hands and reflected. Shall I yet have to wander on? How great is my Lord's grace!

1065.translit:
enkol ammaan tiruvaruLkaL? ulakum uyirum thaanEyaay
nankenN nudalam kaividaan njaalath thoodE nadandhuzhakki
thenkoL thichaikkuth thiladhamaay nindra tirumaalirunjchOlai
nangaL kundram kaividaan naNNaa achurar naliyavE
1065.meaning: How great are my Lord's gracious deeds! Being himself all the worlds and all souls, he does not abandon this body of mine. Walking and trampling through the earth, standing as the sacred mark upon the southern quarter — that Tirumaalirunjcholai, our hill, he does not forsake, while the hostile asuras are made to perish.

1066.translit:
naNNaa achurar nalivaeydha nalla amarar polivaeydha
eNNaa thanakaL eNNumnan muniva rinbam thalaichiRappa
paNNaar paadal inkavikaL yaanaayth thannaith thaanpaadi
thennaa vennum ennammaan tirumaalirunjchOlaiyaanE
1066.meaning: That the hostile asuras be brought low and the good immortals flourish, that the joy of the noble sages who ponder the unponderable be made supreme — through me he sang of himself in tuneful songs and sweet verses, my Lord who utters 'thennaa,' the one of Tirumaalirunjcholai.

1067.translit:
tirumaalirunjchOlaiyaanE aakich chezhumoo vulakum than
orumaa vayiRRi nuLLEvaiththu oozhi yoozhi thalaiyaLikkum
tirumaalennai yaaLumaal chivanum piramanumkaaNaadhu
arumaa leydhi yadiparava aruLai yeendha ammaanE
1067.meaning: Being the one of Tirumaalirunjcholai, placing the lush three worlds within his single great belly, cherishing them age after age — Tirumal rules over me. He is the Lord who, when Shiva and Brahma could not behold him and, falling into deep bewilderment, bowed at his feet, bestowed his grace upon them.

1068.translit:
aruLai eeyen ammaanE. ennum mukkaN ammaanum
theruL kaaL piraman ammaanum dhEvar kOnum dhEvarum
iruLkaL kadiyum munivarum Eththum ammaan tirumalai
maruLkaL kadiyum maNimalai tirumaalirunjchOlaimalaiyE
1068.meaning: 'Grant me your grace, O my Lord!' — so prays even the three-eyed Lord (Shiva); the clear-sighted Lord Brahma, the king of the gods (Indra), the gods, and the sages who dispel darkness all extol him. The sacred hill of that Lord, the jewel-hill that dispels delusion, is the hill of Tirumaalirunjcholai.

1069.translit:
tirumaalirunjchOlaimalaiyE tiruppaaR kadalE enthalaiyE
tirumaalvaikundhamE thaN tiruvEngkadamE enaudalE
arumaa maayath tenadhuyirE manamE vaakkE karumamE
orumaa nodiyum piriyaan en oozhi mudhalvan oruvanE.
1069.meaning: The hill of Tirumaalirunjcholai, the Ocean of Milk, my head; Tirumal's Vaikuntha, the cool Tiruvenkatam, my body; through rare great maaya my soul, my mind, my speech, my action — not for a single instant does he part from these, my one primal Lord of all the ages, the matchless one.

1070.translit:
oozhi mudhalvan oruvanE ennum oruvan ulakellaam
oozhi thORum thannuLLE padaiththuk kaaththuk keduththuzhalum
aazhi vaNNan ennammaan andhaN tirumaalirunjchOlai
vaazhi manamE kaividEl udalum uyirum mangavoTTE
1070.meaning: He is the one primal Lord of the ages, the matchless one — who in every aeon creates within himself all the worlds, protects and dissolves them, ever moving. The ocean-hued one, my Lord of the lovely cool Tirumaalirunjcholai — long may you live, O my mind! Do not let go of him; do not let body and soul perish.

1071.translit:
manga voTTun maamaayai tirumaalirunjchOlaimEya
nangaL kOnE. yaanEnee yaaki yennai yaLiththaanE
pongaim pulanum poRiyaindhum karumEndhiriyum aimbhoodham
ingu ivvuyirEy pirakirudhi maanaangkaara manangaLE
1071.meaning: You let your great maaya perish — O our Lord enshrined at Tirumaalirunjcholai! Becoming yourself me and me yourself, you graced me. The five surging senses, the five sense-organs, the organs of action, the five elements, this embodied soul, primordial nature (prakriti), ego, and mind — all these are yours.

1072.translit:
maanaangkaara manamkeda aivar vankai yarmanga
thaanaangkaara maayppukkuth thaanE thaanE yaanaanai
thEnaang kaarap pozhilkurukoorch chadakOpanchollaayiraththuL
maanaangkaaraththivaipaththum tirumaalirunjchOlaimalaikkE
1072.meaning: That ego and mind be destroyed and the five strong-handed ones (senses) perish — entering as the self-conceiving 'I,' he became himself, he alone, all that is. Of the thousand verses of Satakopan of Kurukoor of honey-fragrant groves, these ten on the dissolution of ego are dedicated to the hill of Tirumaalirunjcholai.

1073.translit:
tirumaalirunjchOlai malaiyendrEn enna
tirumaalvandhu en nenjchu niRaiyap pukundhaan
kurumaa maNiyundhu punalponniith thenbaal
tirumaalchendru chErvidam then tiruppErE
1073.meaning: No sooner had I said 'the hill of Tirumaalirunjcholai' than Tirumal came and entered, filling my heart to overflowing. South of the Ponni river that rolls great glowing gems, the place where Tirumal went and abides is the lovely TiruppEr.

1074.translit:
pErE yuRaikindra pira[aa]n indru vandhu
pErEnen drennenjchu niRaiyap pukundhaan
kaarEzh kadalEzh malaiyE zhulakuNdum
aaraava yiRRaanai yadangkap pidiththEnE
1074.meaning: The Lord who dwells in TiruppEr came this day, and saying 'I will not depart,' he entered and filled my heart. Him of the unsated belly who swallowed the seven clouds, the seven seas, the seven mountains, and the worlds — I have grasped wholly and held fast.

1075.translit:
pidiththEn piRavi keduththEn piNichaarEn
madiththEn manaivaazhkkai yuLniRpadhOr maayaiyai
kodikkO puramaadangkaL choozhthirup pEraan
adichchErvadhenenakkeLi thaayina vaaRE
1075.meaning: I have grasped him, destroyed rebirth, and will be touched by affliction no more; I have cast off that illusion which abides within household life. The Lord of TiruppEr girt by mansions and towers with banners — how easy it has become for me to reach his feet!

1076.translit:
eLidhaa yinavaaRen dRengkaNkaL kaLippa
kaLidhaa kiyachin thaiyanaayk kaLikkindrEn
kiLidhaa viyachOlaikaL choozhthirup pEraan
theLidhaa kiyachEN vichumbutharu vaanE
1076.meaning: Saying 'how easy it has become!' my eyes brimming with joy, and being one of a heart filled with delight, I rejoice. The Lord of TiruppEr girt by groves where parrots leap is the one who grants the clear, far-stretching high heaven.

1077.translit:
vaanE tharuvaa nenakkaayen nOdoTTi
oonEy kurambai yidhanuL pukundhu indru
thaanE thadumaaRRa vinaikaL thavirththaan
thEnE pozhilthen tiruppEr nakaraanE
1077.meaning: He who grants me heaven, making a pact with me for my sake, entered this fleshly hut this very day and of his own accord put an end to the deeds that cause my faltering. He is the Lord of the city of southern TiruppEr of honey-laden groves.

1078.translit:
tiruppEr nakaraan tirumaalirunjchOlaip
poruppE yuRaikin Rabira[aa]nindruvandhu
iruppEn endR ennenjchu niRaiyap pukundhaan
viruppE peRRamu dha muNdu kaLiththEnE
1078.meaning: The Lord of the city of TiruppEr, the Lord who dwells on the mountain of Tirumaalirunjcholai, came this day and, saying 'I will abide here,' entered and filled my heart. Gaining the nectar I longed for, I drank of it and rejoiced.

1079.translit:
uNdu kaLiththaRku [a]mbaren kuRai mElaith
thoNdu kaLiththandhi thozhumchollup peRREn
vaNdu kaLikkum pozhilchoozh tiruppEraan
kaNdu kaLippak kaNNuLnin RakalaanE
1079.meaning: Having drunk and rejoiced, what want have I of even the celestials above? I have received the gift of words that delight in service and worship him at twilight. The Lord of TiruppEr girt by groves where bees revel, that I might behold him and rejoice, stands within my eyes and never departs.

1080.translit:
kaNNuL ninRu agalaan karuttinkaN periyan
eNNil nuN poruL ezhisaiyin suvaidaane
vaNNa nan maNi maaDangaL soozh tiruppEraan
tiNNam en manattu pugundaan seRindu inRE
1080.meaning: He does not depart from within my eye, yet in subtle thought He is the vast one; He is the boundless minute essence and the very sweetness of the seven musical notes. The Lord of TiruppEr, surrounded by fine mansions adorned with bright gems, has surely entered firmly into my heart this very day.

1081.translit:
inRu ennai poruL aakki tannai en uL vaittaan
anRu ennai puRambu okka puNarttadu en seyvaan?
kunRu enna tigazh maaDangaL soozh tiruppEraan
onRu enakku aruL seyya uNarttal uRREnE
1081.meaning: Today He has made me of worth and placed Himself within me; then why, formerly, did He set me to wander outside? The Lord of TiruppEr, ringed by mansions gleaming tall like hills, granting me one grace, has caused me to understand.

1082.translit:
uRREn ugandu paNi seydu una paadam
peRREn eedE innam vENDuvadu endaay
kaTRaar maRai vaaNarkaL vaazh tiruppEraaRku
aTRaar aDiyaar tamakku allal nillaavE
1082.meaning: I have attained Him, joyfully done service, and gained Your feet; this alone is still what I desire, O my Father. For those devotees who have given themselves wholly to the Lord of TiruppEr, where learned masters of the Vedas dwell, no sorrow can remain.

1083.translit:
nillaa allal neeL vayal soozh tiruppEr mEl
nallaar palar vaazh kurugoor saDagOpan
sol aar tamizh aayirattuL ivai pattum
vallaar toNDar aaLvadu soozh pon visumbE
1083.meaning: Of TiruppEr ringed by long fields, where sorrow cannot stand, these ten among the thousand eloquent Tamil verses of SaDagOpan of Kurugoor, where many noble ones dwell - those who master them, the devotees, shall rule the encircling golden sky.

1084.translit:
soozh visumbu aNi mugil tooriyam muzhakkina
aazh kaDal alai tirai kai eDuttu aaDina
Ezh pozhilum vaLam Endiya en appan
vaazh pugazh naaraNan tamarai kaNDu ugandE
1084.meaning: The drums of the surrounding sky's beautiful clouds thundered; the waves of the deep ocean raised their hands and danced - rejoicing to behold the devotees of glory-living Narayana, my Father who upholds the bounty of all seven worlds.

1085.translit:
naaraNan tamarai kaNDu ugandu nan neer mugil
pooraNa poR kuDam poorittadu uyar viNNil
neer aNi kaDalgaL ninRu aarttana neDu varai
tOraNam niraittu engum tozhudanar ulagarE
1085.meaning: Rejoicing to behold Narayana's devotees, the fine watery clouds poured forth like full golden pots high in the sky; the water-laden seas stood and roared; the people, ranging festoons everywhere upon the tall mountains, worshipped.

1086.translit:
tozhudanar ulagargaL toopa nal malar mazhai
pozhivanar poozhi anRu aLandavan tamar munnE
ezhumin enRu imarungu isaittanar munivargaL
vazhi idu vaikundaRku enRu vandu edirE
1086.meaning: The people worshipped, raining down incense and fine flowers before the devotees of Him who long ago measured the dust of the worlds; coming to meet them, the sages proclaimed on both sides, 'Arise! This is the path to Vaikuntha.'

1087.translit:
edir edir imaiyavar iruppiDam vaguttanar
kadiravar avaravar kai nirai kaaTTinar
adiri kural murasangaL alai kaDal muzhakku otta
madu viri tuzhaay muDi maadhavan tamarkkE
1087.meaning: Facing them, the celestials prepared dwelling places; the sun-gods showed each their arrayed hands; the resounding drums roared like the surging ocean - all for the devotees of Madhava, crowned with honey-spreading tulasi.

1088.translit:
maadhavan tamar enRu vaasalil vaanavar
pOdumin emadu iDam puguduga enRalum
geetangaL paaDinar kinnarar geruDargaL
vEda nal vaay avar veLLiyuL maDuttE
1088.meaning: When the celestials at the gateway said, 'These are Madhava's devotees - come, enter our abode,' the Kinnaras and Garudas sang songs, and those of fine Vedic speech offered them into the sacrifice.

1089.translit:
vELviyuL maDuttalum virai kamazh naRum pugai
kaaLangaL valampuri kalandu angum isaittanar
aaLuminggaL vaanagam aazhiyaan tamar enRu
vaaL oN kaN maDandaiyar vaazhttinar magizhndE
1089.meaning: As they offered into the sacrifice, fragrant sweet smoke arose; trumpets and conches sounded blended together; 'Rule the heavens, O devotees of the discus-bearer!' - so the bright sword-eyed women blessed them, rejoicing.

1090.translit:
maDandaiyar vaazhttalum marudarum vasukkaLum
toDarndu engum tOttiram sollinar toDu kaDal
kiDanda en kEsavan kiLar oLi maNi muDi
kuDandai en kOvalan kuDi aDiyaarkkE
1090.meaning: As the women blessed them, the Maruts and Vasus too followed everywhere uttering praises - for the hereditary devotees of my Kesava who reclines on the deep ocean, my Govinda of KuDandai with the radiant gem-crown.

1091.translit:
kuDi aDiyaar ivar gOvindan tanakku enRu
muDi uDai vaanavar muRai muRai edir koLLa
koDi aNi neDu madiL gOpuram kuruginar
vaDivu uDai maadhavan vaikundam pugavE
1091.meaning: 'These are the hereditary devotees of Govinda' - so the crowned celestials received them in due order; and they drew near the towered gateway of the long banner-decked ramparts, to enter the Vaikuntha of comely Madhava.

1092.translit:
vaikundam pugudalum vaasalil vaanavar
vaikundan tamar emar emadu iDam puguga enRu
vaikunda tamararum munivarum viyandanar
vaikundam puguvadu maNNavar vidhiyE
1092.meaning: As they entered Vaikuntha, the celestials at the gateway said, 'The devotees of the Lord of Vaikuntha are our own - enter our abode'; the immortals of Vaikuntha and the sages marvelled: that earth-dwellers should enter Vaikuntha is the rare destiny.

1093.translit:
vidhi vagai pugundanar enRu nal vEdiyar
padiyinil paanginil paadangaL kazhuvinar
nidhiyum naR suNNamum niRai kuDa viLakkamum
madi muga maDandaiyar Endinar vandE
1093.meaning: 'They have entered by the way of destiny' - so the good Vedic ones, fittingly in that abode, washed their feet; and the moon-faced women came bearing treasures, fine fragrant powder, full pots, and lamps.

1094.translit:
vandavar edir koLLa maa maNi maNDapattu
andam il pErinba tu aDiyarODu irundamai
kondu alar pozhil kurugoor saDagOban sol
sandangaL aayirattu ivai vallaar munivarE
1094.meaning: How those who came were received, and how he dwelt amid endless great bliss together with the devotees in the great gem-hall - those who master these verses of metre among the thousand songs of SaDagOpan of Kurugoor with its cluster-blossoming groves, they themselves become sages.

1095.translit:
muniyE naanmuganE mukkaN appaa en pollaa
kani vaay taamarai kaN karu maaNikkamE en kaLvaa
taniyEn aar uyirE en talai misaiyaay vandiTTu
ini naan pOgal oTTEn onRum maayam seyyEl ennaiyE
1095.meaning: O Sage, O four-faced one, O Father with three eyes, my flawless dark sapphire with fruit-like lips and lotus eyes, O my thief, O very life of me who am alone! Having come to rest upon my head, now I will not let You go - work no illusion upon me at all.

1096.translit:
maayam seyyEl ennai un tiru maarvattu maalai nangai
vaasam sey poonguzhalaaL tiru aaNai nin aaNai kaNDaay
nEsam seydu unnODu ennai uyir vERu inRi onRaagavE
koosam seyyaadu koNDaay ennai koovi koLLaay vandandO
1096.meaning: Work no illusion upon me - by the oath of the Lady garlanded upon Your sacred chest, the fragrant flower-tressed Lakshmi, and by Your own oath, behold! Having shown love and taken me as one with You without my life being separate, do not hesitate - alas, come and call me to Yourself.

1097.translit:
koovi koLLaay vandandO en pollaa karu maaNikkamE
aavikku Or paTRu kombu ninnalaal aRiginRilEn yaan
mEvi tozhum biraman sivan indiran aadikku ellaam
naavi kamala mudaR kizhangE umbar andaduvE
1097.meaning: Alas, come and call me to Yourself, my flawless dark sapphire! For my soul I know no supporting bough but You. O root-source of the navel-lotus from which Brahma, Siva, Indra and all who resort to worship arose - You are the very end beyond the highest realms.

1098.translit:
umbar an taN paazhEyO adan uL misai neeyEyO
ambara naR sOdi adan uL biraman aran nee
umbarum yaadavarum paDaitta munivan avan nee
em param saadikka uTRu ennai pOra viTTiTTaayE
1098.meaning: You are the lofty cool primal expanse, and You are within and above it; You are the fine radiance of the firmament, and within it You are Brahma and Hara. You are that sage who created the celestials and the Yadavas; yet, intent on having me accomplish my own state, You have cast me away into the strife.

1099.translit:
pOra viTTiTTu ennai nee puRam pOkkal uTRaal pinnai yaan
aarai koNDu ettai andO enadu enbadu en yaan enbadu en
teera irumbu uNDa neer adu pOla en aar uyirai
aara paruga enakku aaraa amudaanaayE
1099.meaning: Having cast me into the strife, if You set me outside, then afterwards whom and what shall I hold to - alas! What is 'mine' and what is 'I'? Like water utterly drunk up by hot iron, You have become for me the nectar never-sating, to drink my very soul to the full.

1100.translit:
enakkaa raavamu thaayena thaaviyai innuyi rai
manakkaa raamaimanni yundittaa yiniyun dozhiyaay
punakkaa yaaniRatta pundaree kakkaN sengkanivaay
unakkERkum kola malarippaavaik kanbaa..en anbeyo
1100.meaning: O You who became inexhaustible ambrosia for me, who entered and became my very life-breath! Though I could not contain You in my mind, You graciously came, dwelt within and consumed me; now do not depart from me anymore. O Beloved of the lotus-seated Lady (Lakshmi), whose form is dark-hued like the wild kaaya blossom, with red lotus eyes and a red, ripe-fruit-like mouth that befits You! O my own love!

1101.translit:
kola malarippaavaik kanbaa giyaven anbeyo
neela varaiyirandu pi Raikavvi nimirndha thoppa
kola varaagamon Raaynilang kottidaik kondendhaay
neelak kadalkadain thaayunnaip peTRinip pokkuvano?
1101.meaning: O my love who became the beloved of the lotus-seated Lady! In a beautiful form, resembling two dark mountains as if they had bitten and lifted up two crescent moons, You became the matchless Boar and bore up the earth, holding it upon Your tusks. You churned the dark ocean. Having now obtained You, will I ever let You go?

1102.translit:
peTRinip pokkuvano unnai en thanip
peruyirai uTra iruvinaiyaay uyiraayp payanaay avaiyaay
muTRavim moovulagum perun thooRaayt thooTRilpukku
muTRak karandhozhit thaay.en mudhalthani nittheyo
1102.meaning: Having obtained You, my matchless great life-breath, will I let You go? You became the two kinds of karma that cling to souls, the soul itself, the fruit of action, and those very objects; You became the great subtle ground of all the three worlds, and entering into that which You pervaded, You wholly concealed and hid Yourself within. O my primal, matchless, eternal One!

1103.translit:
mudhalthani vitheyo. muzhumoo vulagaadhik kellaam
mudhalthani yunnaiunnai enainaal vandhu kooduvannaan
mudhalthani angumingum muzhumuT RuRuvaazh paazhaay
mudhalthani soozhndh agan Raazhndhuyarndhamudiviieeyo
1103.meaning: O primal, matchless Seed! You who are the sole first cause of all the entire three worlds and their origins, You, You alone - on what day shall I come and be united with You? O primal, matchless One, who as the all-pervading, fully-present, living, unmanifest substratum dwell everywhere, here and there; O primal, matchless, endless One, who surrounds, extends far, plunges low and rises high without limit!

1104.translit:
soozhndhagan Razhndhuyarndhamudivilperumbaa zheyo
soozhndhatha nilperiya parananmalarc cho theeyo
soozhndhatha nilperiya sudarnjaana vinba meyo
soozhndhatha nilperiya ennavaa aRacchoozhndh thaaye
1104.meaning: O boundless great unmanifest Expanse, that surrounds, extends far, plunges low and rises high! O supreme, excellent, blossoming Radiance, greater than even that which it surrounds! O luminous bliss of wisdom, greater than even that which it surrounds! Greater than even that, You surrounded and utterly fulfilled my longing, encompassing me so that my craving was wholly satisfied.

1105.translit:
avaavaRac choozhariyai ayanai aranai alaTRi
avaavaTRu veedupeTRa kurugoorc cadakopan sonna
avaavilan thaadhigalaal ivaiyaa yiramum mudindha
avaavilan thaadhiyip pattaRindh thaarpiRandh thaaruyarndhe
1105.meaning: Crying out in longing to Hari (Vishnu), to Brahma and to Siva, and having his own craving wholly fulfilled and thereby attaining liberation, Satakopan of Kurugur uttered these verses linked in antaadi-chains born of longing; with this antaadi of longing these thousand verses are completed. Those who have learned these ten verses of the antaadi of longing are born exalted and high.

120.translit:
thootroom madanenjcham emperumaan naaraNarkku
em aatraamai solli azhuvOmai nee naduvE,
vEttROr vakaiyil koditaay enai oozhi,
maatraaNmai nitriyO vaazhi kanai iruLE.
120.meaning: O foolish heart, we are defeated; we who weep, telling our helplessness to our Lord Narayana. O dense black night, you come between us in a strange and cruel way, behaving in an opposing manner for ages on end. May you live long, dark night!

121.translit:
iruLin thiNivaNNam maaneerk kazhiyE, pOy,
maruLutru iraappakal thunjcilum nee thunjcaayaal,
uruLum sakaTam utaitta perumaanaar,
aruLin perunasaiyaal aazhaandhu nondhaayE.
121.meaning: O great backwater of vast waters, dark in color like the dense darkness, go away! Even when others sleep, bewildered, through night and day, you do not sleep. You suffer, sunk deep in great longing for the grace of the Lord who kicked apart the rolling cart-wheel demon.

122.translit:
nondhaaraak kaadhal nOy mel laaviyuL ularththa,
nandhaa viLakkamE neeyum aLiyaththaay,
sendhaamarait thaTangkaN sengkani vaay emperumaan,
andhaamath thaNthuzhaay aasaiyaal vEvaayE.
122.meaning: O ever-burning unfading lamp, you too are pitiable, as the disease of love that does not abate withers my tender soul within. You burn with longing for the cool fragrant tulasi-garland of my Lord, whose eyes are wide red lotuses and whose lips are like ripe red fruit.

123.translit:
vEvaaraa vEtkai nOy mel laaviyuL ularththa,
Ovaadhu iraappakal unpaalE veezhththu ozhindhaay,
maavaay piLandhu marudhiTai pOy maNNaLandha,
moovaa mudhalvaa ini emmaic sOrElE.
123.meaning: The disease of unquenchable burning desire withers my tender soul within. Without ceasing, night and day, you have cast me down at your feet. O primal one who never ages, who split the horse-demon's mouth, who passed between the marudu trees, who measured the earth, do not now let me slip away from you.

124.translit:
sOraadha epporuTku aadhiyaam sOdhikkE,
aaraadha kaadhal kurukoorc caTakOpan,
Oraayiram sonna avatruL ivai paththum,
sOraar viTaark kaNTeer vaikundham thiNNenavE.
124.meaning: Of the thousand verses sung by Sadagopan of Kurugur, with unsated love, to the radiant Light who is the unfailing source of all things, those who do not slacken and do not let go of these ten verses will assuredly attain Vaikuntha, for certain.

125.translit:
thiNNan veeTu mudhal muzhudhumaay,
eNNin meedhiyan emperumaan,
maNNum viNNum ellaam uTan uNTa,
nam kaNNan kaN alladhu illai Or kaNNE.
125.meaning: Firm liberation and the source of all and the whole of all is he; my Lord who is beyond all counting. There is no eye, no guide, other than the eye of our Kannan who swallowed up the earth and the heavens and everything together.

126.translit:
E paavam, paramE, Ezhulakum,
ee paavam seydhu aruLaal aLippaar aar,
maa paavam viTa araRkup pichchai pey,
kOpaala kOLari yERu andRiyE.
126.meaning: Alas, is it within anyone's power? Who else can create the seven worlds and protect them by grace through such effort, except Gopala, the lion-like mighty bull of a Lord, who gave alms to Siva to free him from his great sin?

127.translit:
yERanaip poovanaip poomakaL thannai,
vEtRu indRi viN thozhat thannuL vaiththu,
mEl thannai meediTa nimirndhu maN koNDa,
maal thanil mikkumOr dhEvum uLadhE.
127.meaning: He who holds within himself, without difference, Siva of the bull, Brahma of the lotus, and Lakshmi the lotus-lady, so that the heavens worship him, who rose up surpassing himself and seized the earth above. Is there any god greater than this Tirumal?

128.translit:
dhEvum epporuLum paTaikka,
poovil naanmukanaip paTaiththa,
dhEvan em perumaanuk kallaal,
poovum poosanaiyum thakumE.
128.meaning: Except for the God, our Lord, who created the four-faced Brahma on the lotus, so that he might create the gods and all things, is it fitting to offer flowers and worship to any other?

129.translit:
thakum seerth than thani mudhalin uLLE,
mikum dhEvum epporuLum paTaikka,
thakum kOlath thaamaraik kaNNan emmaan,
mikum sOdhi mEl aRivaar yavarE.
129.meaning: Within his own sole primal self of fitting glory, he creates the abundant gods and all things. Who can know anything beyond the surpassing radiance of our Lord, the lotus-eyed one of fitting beauty?

130.translit:
yavarum yaavaiyum ellaap poruLum,
kavarvu indRith than uLoTungka nindRa,
pavark koL njaana veLLac cuTar moorththi,
avar em aazhiyam paLLiyaarE.
130.meaning: He who stood firm, drawing all beings and all things and everything wholly into himself without loss, the radiant form of a flooding ever-growing wisdom. He is our Lord who reclines on the beautiful ocean.

131.translit:
paLLi aalilai Ezhulakum koLLum,
vaLLal val vayitRup perumaan,
uL uLaar aRivaar avan than,
kaLLa maaya manak karuththE.
131.meaning: The generous Lord of the mighty stomach that holds in the seven worlds, reclining on the banyan leaf. Only those who dwell deep within can know the hidden, mysterious intent of his mind.

132.translit:
karuththil dhEvum ellaap poruLum,
varuththith thamar ayap piraanai andRi,
aarE thiruththith thiN nilai moovulakum
thammuL iruththik kaakkum iyalvinarE.
132.meaning: Except for the Lord who, by his will, created with effort all the gods and all things and the gods themselves, who else has the nature to set right and hold the three worlds in firm stability within himself and protect them?

133.translit:
kaakkum iyalvinan kaNNa perumaan,
sErkkai seydhu than undhi uLLE,
vaayththa thisaimukan indhiran vaanavar,
aakkinaan deyva ulakukaLE.
133.meaning: Kannan, the Lord whose nature it is to protect, after bringing forth creation, made within his own navel the four-directioned Brahma, Indra, the celestials, and the divine worlds.

134.translit:
kaLvaa emmaiyum Ezhulakum,
nin uLLE thOtriya iRaivaa endRu,
veL ERan naanmukan indhiran vaanavar,
puL oordhi kazhal paNindhu EththuvarE.
134.meaning: "O deceiver, O Lord who brought forth both us and the seven worlds within yourself!" - so saying, Siva of the white bull, the four-faced Brahma, Indra, and the celestials bow to and praise the feet of him who rides the bird Garuda.

135.translit:
Eththa Ezhulakum koNTa kOlak
kooththanai, kurukoorc caTakOpan sol,
vaayththa vaayiraththuL ivai paththuTan,
Eththa vallavarkku illai Or oonamE.
135.meaning: Of the perfect thousand verses composed by Sadagopan of Kurugur in praise of the beautiful dancer who took the seven worlds, those who are able to praise with these ten as well shall have no defect whatsoever.

136.translit:
oonil vaazh uyirE nallai pO unnaip petru,
vaanuLaar perumaan madhusoodhan en ammaan,
thaanum yaanum ellaam than uLLE kalandhu ozhindhOm,
thEnum paalum neyyum kannalum amudhum oththE.
136.meaning: O soul living in the body, you are good; go on! Having attained you, my Lord the Lord of those in heaven, Madhusudana my master, both he and I and all have merged completely within him, like honey and milk and ghee and sugarcane juice and nectar blended into one.

137.translit:
oththaar mikkaarai ilaiyaaya maamaaya,
oththaay epporuTkum uyiraay, ennaip petra
aththaayaayth thandhaiyaay aRiyaadhana aRiviththa,
aththaa, nee seydhana aTiyEn aRiyEnE.
137.meaning: O great wondrous one who has none equal or superior, you have become the soul of every being, being like all yet beyond. O father who became the mother that bore me, the father, who taught me things I did not know - O Lord, I, your servant, cannot comprehend the things you have done.

138.translit:
aRiyaak kaalaththuLLE aTimaikkaN anpu seyviththu,
aRiyaa maamaayath thaTiyEnai vaiththaayaal,
aRiyaamai kuRaLaay nilam maavali moovaTi endRu,
aRiyaamai vanjciththaay enadhaaviyuL kalandhE.
138.meaning: Even in the time when I knew nothing, you made me feel love toward your service, and you placed me, your servant, within your unknowable great wondrous play. As the dwarf who, asking ignorant Mahabali for three feet of land, deceived him through his ignorance, so you have deceived me, mingling within my very soul.

139.translit:
enadhaaviyuL kalandha peru nal udhavik kaimmaaru,
enadhaavi thandhu ozhindhEn ini meeLvadhu enbadhu uNTE,
enadhaavi aaviyum nee pozhil Ezhum uNTa endhaay,
enadhaavi aar yaan aar thandha nee koNTaakkinaiyE.
139.meaning: In return for the great good help of mingling within my soul, I have given over my soul itself; is there any taking it back now? You are the very life of my life, O my father who swallowed the seven worlds. What is my soul, what am I? You, who gave them, have taken and made them your own.

140.translit:
iniyaar njaanangaLaal edukkal ezhaada vendaay,
kanivaar veettinbamee en kadal padaa amudee,
taniyeen vaazh mudalee, pozhil eezhum eenam ondraay,
nuniyaar koottil vaittaay un paadam seerndeenee.
140.meaning: O my Lord, who cannot be grasped by any knowledge whatever, who are the ripened bliss of the eternal abode, the nectar that the ocean of milk never yielded! You are the very source of life for me who am alone; as the one Boar you lifted the seven worlds upon the tip of your sharp tusk. I have now reached and taken refuge at your feet.

141.translit:
seerndaar tee-vinaikatku arunanjaiyait tiNmadiyai,
teerndaar tam manattu piriyaada avar uyirai,
soorndee poogal kodaa sudarai arakkiyai mook-
keerndaayai adiyeen adaindeen mudal munnamee.
141.meaning: You are the rare poison that destroys the cruel sins of those who draw near you, and the firm wisdom they hold; you are the very life, never parting from the hearts of those who have renounced, the radiance that does not let them slip and fall away; you are the one who slit the nose of the demoness Surpanakha. To you I, your servant, have come, as the very first and foremost refuge.

142.translit:
mun nal yaazh payil nool narambin mudir suvaiyee,
pannalaar payilum paranee, pavittiranee,
kannalee, amudee, kaarmugilee, en kaNNaa,
ninnalaal ileen kaaN, ennai nee kurikkoLLee.
142.meaning: O ripened sweetness of the strings of the fine lute that has long been played according to the scriptures! O Supreme One whom the wise constantly recite, O utterly pure One, O sweet sugar-cane, O nectar, O dark rain-cloud, O my Kannan! See, I have none but you; do take me as your own and hold me in your care.

143.translit:
kurikkoL njaanangaLaal enai oozhi sey tavamum,
kirikkoNdu ip-pirappee sila naaLil eydinan yaan,
urikkoNda veNNey paal oLittu uNNum ammaan pin,
nerikkoNda nenjanaay piravit tuyar kadindee.
143.meaning: Through focused knowledge and the penances I had performed over countless aeons, by your grace I have in this very birth attained, within a few days, what I sought. With my heart set firmly on the path that follows after the Lord who steals and secretly eats the butter and milk kept hanging in the loops, I have cut away the misery of birth.

144.translit:
kadivaar taNNan tuzhaay kaNNan viNNavar perumaan,
padi vaan miranda paraman pavittiran seer,
sediyaar noygaL keda padindu kudaindu aadi,
adiyeen vaay maduttu parugi kaLitteenee.
144.meaning: Kannan, who wears the cool fragrant tulasi, the Lord of the celestials, the Supreme One who transcends all earthly and heavenly nature, the utterly pure One -- into His glory I, His servant, plunged deep and bathed, so that my thick, overgrown maladies might be destroyed. Pressing it to my lips I drank of it and rejoiced.

145.translit:
kaLippum kavarvum atru pirappu piNi mooppu irappu atru,
oLi koNda jootiyumaay udan kooduvadu endru koloo,
tuLikkindra vaan in-nilam sudar aazhi sangu eendi,
aLikkindra maayap piraan adiyaargaL kuzhaangaLaiyee.
145.meaning: Freed from joy and grief, freed from birth, disease, old age and death, becoming one with the radiant Effulgence -- when, O when, shall I be united with the throngs of devotees of the wondrous Lord, who graciously protects this raining sky and this earth, bearing the blazing discus and the conch?

146.translit:
kuzhaang koL peer arakkan kulam veeya munindavanai,
kuzhaang koL ten kurugoor sadagoopan terindu uraitta,
kuzhaang koL aayirattuL ivai pattum udan paadi,
kuzhaangaLaay adiyeer udan koodi nindru aaduminee.
146.meaning: Of the thousand verses that Satakopan of beautiful southern Kurugur, gathered in assembly, discerned and uttered about the Lord who in wrath destroyed the whole clan of the great demon and his hordes -- sing these ten together. O servants, gather into groups, unite, and stand and dance together.

147.translit:
aadi aadi agam karaindu, isai
paadi paadi kaNNeer malgi, engum
naadi naadi narasingaa endru,
vaadi vaadum iv-vaaL nudalee.
147.meaning: Dancing and dancing till her inner self melts away, singing and singing in melody till her eyes brim with tears, searching everywhere and everywhere, crying out "O Narasimha!" -- this bright-browed maiden withers and withers away.

148.translit:
vaaL nudal im-madavaral, ummai
kaaNum aasaiyuL naiginraaL, viral
vaaNan aayiram tooL tuNitteer, ummai
kaaNa neer irakkam ileeree.
148.meaning: This bright-browed, tender maiden grows faint within the longing to behold you. O you who severed the thousand mighty arms of the valiant Bana -- you have no compassion to let her behold you.

149.translit:
irakkam manattoodu eri aNai,
arakkum ezhugum okkum ivaL,
irakkam ezhil idarku en seygeen,
arakkan ilangai setreerukkee.
149.meaning: With a pitying heart she is like lac and wax laid upon fire -- melting away. What can I do for this her grief, to you who destroyed the demon's Lanka?

150.translit:
ilangai setravanee! ennum, pinnum
valam koL puL uyarttaay! ennum, uLLam
malanga ev-vuyirkkum, kaNNeer miga
kalangi kai tozhum nindru ivaLee.
150.meaning: "O you who destroyed Lanka!" she cries; and again, "O you who raised the mighty bird upon your banner!" she cries; her heart reeling, she sighs deeply, her eyes brimming with tears, and trembling she stands and worships with folded hands.

151.translit:
ivaL iraap-pagal vaay vereei, tana
kuvaLai oN kaNNa neer koNdaaL, vaNdu
tivaLum taNNan tuzhaay kodeer, ena
tavaLa vaNNar tagavugaLee.
151.meaning: Night and day this maiden babbles, her mouth uttering wildly, and her bright lotus-like eyes have filled with tears. You do not give her the cool fragrant tulasi about which the bees hum and glisten -- such, O fair-hued Lord, is the measure of your compassion!

152.translit:
tagavu udaiyavanee ennum, pinnum
miga virumbum piraan ennum, ena
taga uyirkku amudee, ennum, uLLam
uga urugi nindru uLLuLee.
152.meaning: "O you who are full of grace!" she cries; and again, "O Lord whom I love beyond measure!" she cries; "O nectar to my life!" she cries -- her heart melting and dissolving within, deep inside her she stands thus.

153.translit:
uLLuL aavi ularndu ularndu, ena
vaLLalee kaNNanee ennum, pinnum
veLLa neer kidandaay, ennum, en
kaLvi taan patta vanjanaiyee.
153.meaning: Her very inner soul drying up and drying up, she cries "O my generous benefactor! O Kannan!"; and again, "O you who reclined upon the flood of waters!" she cries -- such is the deceit into which this secretive girl of mine has fallen.

154.translit:
vanjanee, ennum kai tozhum, tan
nenjam vee va nedidu uyirkkum, viral
kanjanai vanjanai seydeer, ummai
tanjam endru ivaL pattanavee.
154.meaning: "O deceiver!" she cries, yet she worships with folded hands; her heart burning, she sighs long and deep. O you who worked deceit upon the mighty Kamsa -- such are the things this girl has suffered, having taken you as her refuge.

155.translit:
patta poodu ezhu poodu ariyaaL, virai
mattu alar taN tuzhaay ennum, sudar
vatta vaay nudi neemiyeer, numadu
ittam en kol iv-veezhaikkee.
155.meaning: She knows neither the time she lay down nor the time she rose; she only cries "the cool tulasi that blossoms with fragrant honey!" O you who bear the radiant disc with its round, sharp-pointed edge -- what is your intention toward this hapless girl?

156.translit:
eezhai peedai iraap-pagal, tana
keezh il oN kaNNa neer koNdaaL, kiLar
vaazhvai vee va ilangai setreer, ivaL
maazhai nookku ondrum vaatteenminee.
156.meaning: This helpless, simple girl, night and day, has filled her peerless bright eyes with tears. O you who burned away flourishing Lanka with its thriving life -- do not in any way let her tender, fawn-like gaze wither away.

157.translit:
vaattam il pugazh vaamananai, isai
kootti vaN sadagoopan sol, amai
paattu oor aayirattu ip-pattaal, adi
kuttal aagum andaamamee.
157.meaning: By this set of ten among the thousand well-wrought verses, set to melody, sung of Vamana of unfading glory by generous Satakopan -- one may serve at and embrace those beautiful lotus feet.

158.translit:
andaamattu anbu sey ten naavi seer ammaanukku,
andaama vaazh mudi sangu aazhi nool aaram uLa,
sen-taamarai tadang kaN sen-kani vaay sengamalam,
sen-taamarai adigaL sem-pon tiru udambee.
158.meaning: For the Lord who dwells in my life with love, abiding in the supreme realm -- there is the everlasting flourishing crown, the conch, the discus, the sacred thread and the garland; His are the wide red-lotus eyes, the red ripe-fruit lips, the lotus hands, the red-lotus feet, and the body of reddish gold divine.

159.translit:
tiru udambu vaan sudar sen-taamarai kaN kai kamalam,
tiru idamee maarvam ayan idamee koppoozh,
oru idamum endai perumaarku aranee yoo,
oru idam ondru indri en uL kalandaanukkee.
159.meaning: His divine body is heavenly radiance, His eyes the red lotus, His hands the lotus; His chest is the very abode of Lakshmi, His navel the very abode of Brahma. For my Lord and Master, and for Siva too, there is a place in Him -- yet without leaving out even a single spot, He has wholly mingled within me.

160.translit:
ennuL kalandhavan sengkanivaay sengkamalam,
minnum sudar malaikkuk kaN paadham kai kamalam,
mannu muzhu ezh ulagum vayiRRin uLa,
thannuL kalavaadhu thep poruLum thaan ilaiyE
160.meaning: He who has mingled within me has a mouth red like a ripe fruit and lotus-red hue; for that shining radiant mountain-like Lord, His eyes, feet and hands are like lotuses. All the seven enduring worlds rest within His belly, yet there is no single substance into which He has not entered and become.

161.translit:
ep poruLum thaanaay maradhakak kunRam okkum,
appozhudhaith thaamaraippoo kaN paadham kai kamalam,
ep pozhudhum naaL thingaL aaNdu oozhi oozhi thoRum,
appozhudhaikku appozhudhu en aaraa amudhamE.
161.meaning: Becoming Himself every substance, He resembles an emerald hill; His eyes, feet and hands are like lotus flowers freshly blossomed at that very moment. At every time -- through days, months, years and aeon upon aeon -- moment after moment He is to me unfailing, insatiable nectar.

162.translit:
aaraa amudhu amarndhu en aaviyuL kalandha,
kaaraar karu mugil pol en ammaan kaNNanukku,
neeraa vaay sem pavaLam kaN paadham kai kamalam,
peraara neeN mudi naaN pinnum izhai palavE.
