162.meaning: To my Lord Kannan, who as insatiable nectar lovingly mingled into my soul -- dark like a rain-laden cloud -- His matching mouth is red coral, His eyes, feet and hands are lotuses; His tall crown gleams with strands and many ornaments besides.

163.translit:
pala palavE aabaraNam perum pala palavE,
pala palavE soadhi vadivu paN eNNil,
pala pala kaNdu uNdu kEttu uRRu moandhu inbam,
pala palavE Gnaanamum paambu aNai mElaaRkE yO.
163.meaning: Many, many are His ornaments, and many, many His names too; many, many are His radiant forms, and beyond counting His qualities. Many, many are the joys of seeing, tasting, hearing, touching and smelling Him, and many, many forms of knowledge -- all these belong to Him who reclines upon the serpent-couch.

164.translit:
paambu aNai mEl paaR kadaluL paLLi amarndhadhuvum,
kaambu aNai thoaL pinnaikkaa Eru udan Ezh seRRadhuvum,
thEm paNaiya soalai maraamaram Ezh eydhadhuvum,
poom piNaiya thaN thuzhaay pon mudiya poarERE.
164.meaning: He rested upon the serpent-couch in the milk-ocean; for Pinnai of bamboo-like shoulders He subdued the seven bulls together; He pierced through the seven maramara trees in the honey-rich grove. He wears cool tulasi entwined with flowers upon His golden crown -- He, the bull among warriors.

165.translit:
pon mudiya poarERRai emmaanai naal thadan thoaL,
than mudivu onRu illaadha thaN thuzhaay maalaiyanai,
en mudivu kaaNaadhE ennuL kalandhaanai,
sol mudivu kaaNEn naan solluvadhu en solleerE.
165.meaning: The bull among warriors with the golden crown, my Lord of four broad shoulders, who wears the cool tulasi garland and whose own end none can find -- He mingled within me without seeing my downfall. I can find no end to words for Him; what shall I say -- you tell me!

166.translit:
solleer en ammaanai en aavi aaviyathanai,
ellai il seer en karu maaNikka sudarai,
nalla amudham peRRaRku ariya veedumaay,
alli malar virai oththaan allan peN allanE.
166.meaning: Tell me of my Lord, the very life of my life, the radiance of my dark sapphire, of boundless glory! He is the sweet nectar and the liberation hard to attain; fragrant like the lily-flower's scent, He is not male, He is not female.

167.translit:
aaN allan peN allan allaa aliyum allan,
kaaNalum aagaan uLan allan illai allan,
pENungaal pENum uru aagum allanumaam,
koaNai peridhu udaiththu emmaanaik kooRudhalE.
167.meaning: He is not male, not female, nor is He neuter; He cannot be seen, He is not existent, yet He is not non-existent. When one cherishes Him He becomes whatever form is cherished, and He becomes that too -- to define my Lord holds great difficulty and crookedness.

168.translit:
kooRudhal onRu aaraak kudak kooththa ammaanai,
kooRudhalE mEvi kurugoor chchatakoapan,
kooRina andhaadhi oar aayiraththuL ip paththum,
kooRudhal vallaar uLarEl kooduvar vaigundhamE.
168.meaning: The Lord who danced the pot-dance, whom no single definition can fully describe -- of the thousand antaadi verses devoted to praising Him by Satakopan of Kurugur, those who can recite these ten will surely reach Vaikuntha.

169.translit:
vaigundhaa maNi vaNNanE, en pollaa thirukkuRaLaa,
ennuL manni, vaigum vaigal thoRum amudhaaya vaanERE,
seyguindhaa varum theemai un adiyaarkku theerththu surarkku theemaigaL
seyguindhaa, unnai naan pidiththEn koL sikkenavE.
169.meaning: O Lord of Vaikuntha, of gem-hued form, my lovely sacred dwarf (Vamana), abiding within me, becoming nectar day after day, O celestial bull! You who remove the coming evils of Your devotees, and who bring evils upon the asuras -- I have seized You firmly; hold You me fast too!

170.translit:
sikkena siRu thor idamum puRappadaa thannuLLE, ulagugaL
okkavE vizhungi pugundhaan pugundhadhaR pin,
mikka Gnaana veLLa sudar viLakkaay thuLakku aRRu amudhamaay, engum
pakka noakku aRiyaan en paindhaamarai kaNNanE.
170.meaning: Swallowing all the worlds together so that not even the smallest space remained outside Himself, He entered within me; and after entering, He shone as the radiant lamp of the flood of supreme knowledge, unwavering and nectar-like. Knowing no sideways glance anywhere, He is my lotus-eyed Kannan.

171.translit:
thaamarai kaNNanai viNNoar paravum thalaimaganai, thuzhaay viraip
poo maruvu kaNNi em piraanai pon malaiyai,
naa maruvi nan kEththi uLLi vaNangi naam magizhndhu aada, naavalar
paa maruvi niRka thandha paanmaiyE vaLLalE.
171.meaning: The lotus-eyed One, the foremost whom the celestials worship, my Lord wearing a garland of fragrant tulasi blossoms, the golden hill -- that we might well praise Him with our tongues, meditate upon Him, bow and dance in joy, the generous one granted that poets' verses should ever abide. O bountiful Lord!

172.translit:
vaLLalE, madhusoodhanaa, en maradhaka malaiyE, unai ninaindhu,
theL kal thandha endhaay unnai engnganam vidugEn,
veLLamE purai nin pugazh kudaindhu aadi paadi kaLiththu ugandhu ugandhu
uLLa noaygaL ellaam thurandhu uyndhu poandhirundhE.
172.meaning: O generous one, Madhusudana, my emerald hill! O my Father who granted clear knowledge as I meditated on You -- how can I ever let You go? Diving into Your glory that swells like a flood, dancing, singing, rejoicing and ever delighting, I have driven away all my inner ills and survived, abiding so.

173.translit:
uyndhu poandhu en ulappilaadha vem theevinaigaLai naasam seydhu un
thandhamil adimai adaindhEn viduvEnO,
aindhu pain thalai aadu aravu aNai mEvi paaR kadal yoga niththirai,
sindhai seydha vendhaay unnai sindhai seydhu seydhE.
173.meaning: Having survived and come through, destroying my endless fierce evil deeds, I have attained Your own intimate service -- how could I let go? O my Lord who rest in yogic sleep upon the milk-ocean, on the couch of the dancing serpent with five swaying hoods -- I attained You meditating upon You again and again.

174.translit:
unnai sindhai seydhu seydhu un nedu maa mozhi isai paadi aadi en
munnai theevinaigaL muzhu vEr arindhanan yaan,
unnai sindhaiyinaal igazhndha iraNiyan agal maarvam keeNda, en
munnai koaL ariyE, mudiyaadhadhu en enakkE?
174.meaning: Meditating upon You again and again, singing and dancing to the music of Your great glorious word, I have cut away the very root of my former evil deeds. O my Lord of the man-lion form of old, who tore open the broad chest of Hiranya who insulted You in his mind -- what is there that I cannot achieve?

175.translit:
mudiyaadhadhu en enakku ini muzhu Ezh ulagum uNdaan ugandhu vandhu
adiyEnuL pugundhaan agal vaanum allan ini,
sedi aar noaygaL ellaam thurandhu emar keezh mEl ezhu piRappum,
vidiyaa vennaragaththu enRum sErndhal maaRinarE.
175.meaning: What is there now that I cannot achieve? He who swallowed all the seven worlds came gladly and entered into me, His servant, and now He will not depart even to the high heavens. Driving away all my dense-clustering ills, my kin of the seven generations below and above have forever ceased ever to enter the fierce dawnless hell.

176.translit:
maari maari pala piRappum piRandhu adiyai adaindhu uLLam thERi
eeRu il inba thiru veLLam yaan moozhginan,
paari paariya asurar tham pal kuzhaangaL neeRu ezha, paay paRavai onRu
ERi veeRRirundhaay unnai en uL neekkEl endhaay.
176.meaning: Born again and again in many births, I reached Your feet, my heart grew clear, and I have plunged into the sacred flood of endless bliss. O Lord who sit enthroned, mounted upon the swooping bird, so that the many scattered hordes of asuras are reduced to dust -- do not depart from within me, my Father!

177.translit:
endhaay thaN thiruvEngadaththuL ninRaay! ilangai seRRaay, maraamaram
pain thaaL Ezh uruva oru vaali koaththa villaa!
kondhaar thaNNam thuzhaayinaay amudhE! unnai en uLLE kuzhaiththa vem
maindhaa! vaanERE, ini engu poaginRadhE?
177.meaning: My Father who stand in cool Tiruvenkatam! You who destroyed Lanka, O bowman who strung a single arrow to pierce through the seven fresh-trunked maramara trees! O nectar wearing the cool clustered tulasi garland! O my mighty one who melted Yourself into me, O celestial bull -- now where can You go?

178.translit:
poaginRa kaalangaL poaya kaalangaL poagu kaalangaL, thaay thandhai uyir
aaginRaay unnai naan adaindhEn viduvEnO,
paaginRa thol pugazh moo ulagukkum naadhanE. paramaa, thaN vEngada
mEginRaay thaN thuzhaay virai naaRu kaNNiyanE.
178.meaning: The times that pass, the times that are gone, the times yet to come -- You become my mother, father and very life; I have attained You, how could I let You go? O Lord of the three worlds with ancient spreading glory, O Supreme One, who dwell on cool Venkatam, wearing the garland fragrant with cool tulasi!

179.translit:
kaNNi thaNNam thuzhaay mudi kamala thadam perung kaNNanai, pugazh
naNNi then kurugoor chchatakoapan maaRan sonna,
eNNil soarvu il andhaadhi aayiraththuL ivaiyum oar paththu isaiyodum,
paNNil paada vallaar avar kEsavan thamarE.
179.meaning: Of the Kannan with broad lotus-eyes and a crown of cool fragrant tulasi garland, whom Satakopan, Maaran of southern Kurugur, drew near in praise and sang -- of the flawless thousand antaadi verses, those who can sing these ten too with melody and music shall be the very people of Kesava.

180.translit:
kesavan tamarkkeezh-mel emar-eezh-ezhu-piRappum,
maa sadhir idhu peRRu nammudai vaazhvu vaaykkinRavaa,
eesan en karu-maaNikkam en sengkOlak-kaNNan
viNNOr naayakan, embiraan emmaan naaraayaNanaalE
180.meaning: All my seven generations of kinsfolk, above and below, have become the servants of Kesava; that we have attained this great fortune and that our life has come to fruition is by the grace of Narayana — the Lord, my dark sapphire, my beautiful-eyed Kannan, the chief of the celestials, my benefactor, my master.

181.translit:
naaraNan muzhu-vezhulagukkum naadhan vEdha-mayan,
kaaraNam kirisai karumam ivai mudhalvan endhai,
seer aNangu amarar piRar palarum thozhudhu-EththaninRu,
vaaraNaththai maruppu-osiththa piraan en maadhavanE.
181.meaning: Narayana is the Lord of all the entire seven worlds, He who is the very essence of the Vedas; my father is the source of cause, action and effect (deed). He is my Madhava, the benefactor who broke the tusk of the elephant, standing while the glorious goddess, the immortals and many others worship and praise Him.

182.translit:
maadhavan enRadhE koNdu ennai iniyippaal-pattadhu,
yaadhu avangaLum sErkkodEn enRu ennuL pugundhirundhu,
theedhu avam kedukkum amudham sendhaamaraikkaN kunRam,
kOdhu avam il en kannaRkatti emmaan en gOvindhanE
182.meaning: Having merely accepted me as one who said 'Madhava,' He resolved 'I will not let any sin henceforth cling to him,' and entered and dwelt within me — He is the nectar that destroys evil and sin, the mountain with red-lotus eyes, my flawless sweet sugar-candy, my master, my Govinda.

183.translit:
gOvindhan kudak-kooththan gOvalan enRu enRE kuniththu
dhEvum thannaiyum paadi aada thiruththi, ennaik koNdu en
paavam thannaiyum paaRak-kaiththu emar eezh-ezhu piRappum,
mEvum thanmaiya maakkinaan vallan embiraan vittuvE.
183.meaning: Making me dance and bow, singing of the gods and of Himself as 'Govinda, the pot-dancer, the cowherd,' He reformed me; taking hold of me, He scattered and drove away my sin and made my seven generations of kinsfolk to be of a nature fit to attain Him — such is my mighty benefactor, Vishnu.

184.translit:
vittilangu senchOdhith thaamarai paadham kaigaL kaNgaL,
vittilangu karunchudar malaiyE thiru-udambu,
vittilangu madhiyam seer sangu chakkaram paridhi,
vittilangu mudiyammaan madhusoodhananRanakkE.
184.meaning: His feet, hands and eyes are radiant red-glowing lotuses; His sacred body is a mountain of shining dark lustre; His glorious conch is like the bright moon, His discus like the blazing sun; the crowned Lord, my master, is Madhusudana to whom all this belongs.

185.translit:
madhusoodhanai anRi maRRilEn enRu eththaalum karumam inRi,
thudhi soozhndha paadalgaL paadi aada ninRu oozhi-oozhi-thoRum,
edhir soozhal pukku enaiththu-Or piRappum enakkE aruLgaL seyya,
vidhi soozhndhadhaal enakkEl ammaan thirivikkiramanaiyE.
185.meaning: Saying 'I have none other than Madhusudana,' standing free of any other concern, singing and dancing songs woven with praise age after age — that the Lord Trivikrama should come to meet me through every birth and bestow His graces on me alone, this fate has surrounded and been granted to me.

186.translit:
thirivikkiraman sendhaamaraik-kaN emmaan en sengkanivaay
uruvil polindha veLLaip-paLingku-niRaththanan enRu enRu,
uLLip paravip paNindhu pal-oozhi-oozhi nin paadha-pangkayamE,
maruvith thozhum manamE thandhaay vallaikaaN en vaamananE.
186.meaning: Trivikrama, my master with red-lotus eyes and a red-fruit (bimba) mouth, of a form gleaming like white crystal — meditating, praising and bowing thus, age after age You have given me a heart that clings to and worships only Your lotus feet. You are indeed capable, my Vamana!

187.translit:
vaamanan en maragadha-vaNNan thaamaraik-kaNNinan
kaamanaip payandhaay, enRu enRu un kazhal paadiyE paNindhu,
thoo-manaththananaay piRavith thuzhadhi neengka, ennaith
thee-manam keduththaay unakku en seygEn en sireedharanE.
187.meaning: Singing of Your anklets and bowing, saying again and again 'Vamana, my emerald-hued one, the lotus-eyed who fathered Kama (Manmatha),' You made me pure-minded and removed the affliction of birth, and destroyed my evil-mindedness. What can I do for You, my Sridhara?

188.translit:
sireedharan seyya-thaamaraik-kaNNan enRu enRu iraap-pagal vaay
vereei, alamandhu kaNgaL neer malgi vev-uyirththu-uyirththu
mareeiya thee-vinai maaLa inbam vaLara vaigal vaigal
ireei, unnai ennuL vaiththanai en irudeekEsanE.
188.meaning: Crying out day and night with my mouth 'Sridhara, the red-lotus-eyed one,' distressed, with eyes brimming with tears and breathing hard sigh upon sigh — so that the clinging evil deeds perish and bliss may grow, day after day You have settled and placed Yourself within me, my Hrishikesha!

189.translit:
irudeekEsan embiraan ilangkai arakkar-kulam,
murudu theerththa piraan emmaan amarar pemmaan enRu enRu,
therudiyaagil nenjE, vaNangku thiNNam aRi-aRindhu,
marudiyElum vidEl kaNdaay nambi paRpa-naabanaiyE
189.meaning: O heart, if you can understand, worship Him saying again and again 'Hrishikesha my benefactor, the gracious one who rooted out the demon-clan of Lanka, my master, Lord of the immortals' — knowing this firmly for certain; and even if you grow confused, do not let go, behold, of the perfect One, Padmanabha.

190.translit:
paRpa-naaban uyarvu-aRa uyarum perun-thiRalOn,
eRparan ennai aakkik-koNdu enakkE thannaith thandha
kaRpagam, en amudham kaar-mugil pOlum vEngkada nal
veRpan, visumbOr piraan endhai dhaamOdharanE.
190.meaning: Padmanabha is the one of great might who rises so high that none rises beyond Him; He is my Lord who made me His own and gave Himself to me alone — the wish-granting tree, my nectar, the good Lord of Venkatam hill who is like a dark rain-cloud, the benefactor of the celestials, my father Damodara.

191.translit:
dhaamOdharanaith thani-mudhalvanai gnaalam uNdavanai,
aamOdharam aRiya oruvarkku enRu thozhumavargaL,
dhaamOdharan uruvaagiya sivaRkkum thisai-mugaRkkum,
aamOdharam aRiya emmaanai en aazhi-vaNNanaiyE.
191.meaning: Damodara, the sole primal cause, who swallowed the earth — to know Him in His fullness is possible for no single one of those who worship; even for Shiva, who has Damodara as the substance of his form, and for the four-faced Brahma, He cannot be fully known — my master, my ocean-hued Lord.

192.translit:
vaNNa maa-maNich-chOdhiyai amarar-thalai-maganai,
kaNNanai nedumaalaith then-kurugoor-ch-chadagOpan,
paNNiya thamizh-maalai aayiraththuL ivai panniraNdum,
paNNil panniru naamap paattu aNNal thaaL aNaivikkumE.
192.meaning: Of the radiant gem of beauteous colour, the chief of the immortals, Kannan, the great Tirumal — these twelve verses out of the thousand crafted as a Tamil garland by Satakopa of southern Kurugur, the twelve-named song set to melody, will bring one to the feet of the Lord.

193.translit:
aNaivadhu aravaNai mEl poom-paavai aagam
puNarvadhu, iruvar avar mudhalum thaanE,
iNaivan aam epporutkum veedu mudhalaam,
puNaivan piRavik-kadal neendhuvaarkkE.
193.meaning: He who reclines upon the serpent-couch, who embraces the bosom of the flower-like Lady (Lakshmi), is Himself the origin of those two (Brahma and Shiva); He pervades and unites with every being, and is the source of release (liberation) — He is the raft for those who would swim across the ocean of birth.

194.translit:
neendhum thuyarp-piRavi yutpada maRRu-evvevaiyum,
neendhum thuyar illaa veedu mudhalaam,
poon-thaN punal poygai aanai idar-kadindha,
poon-thaN thuzhaay en thani-naayakan puNarppE.
194.meaning: Including the sorrowful birth that must be swum across, and all other things whatsoever — He is the source of the liberation that is free of the sorrow to be crossed; He of the cool flowery tulasi, my matchless Lord who in the flower-cool pond removed the elephant's distress — such is His union (purpose).

195.translit:
puNarkkum ayanaam azhikkum aranaam,
puNarththa than undhiyOdu aagaththu manni,
puNarndha thiru-aagith than maarvil thaan sEr,
puNarppan perum-puNarppu engkum pulanE.
195.meaning: He is Brahma who creates (joins together) and He is Hara (Shiva) who destroys; abiding with both His navel and His body that He fashioned, and with Lakshmi who, having united, He keeps joined upon His own chest — He is the joiner whose great act of joining is manifest everywhere.

196.translit:
pulan-aindhum mEyum poRi-aindhu neekki,
nalam-antham illadhu-Or naadu puguveer,
alamandhu veeya asurarai cheRRaan,
palam undhu seeril padiminO vaadhE.
196.meaning: O you who would cast off the five senses that graze upon the five sense-objects and enter the land of boundless bliss — immerse yourselves without ceasing in the fruitful, abounding glory of Him who, so that the asuras perished in distress, struck them down.

197.translit:
Ovaath-thuyarp-piRavi yutpada maRRu-evvevaiyum,
moovaath-thani-mudhalaay moo-ulagum kaavalOn,
maavaagi aamaiyaay meenaagi maanidamaam,
dhEvaadhi-dhEva perumaan en theerththanE.
197.meaning: Including the unceasing sorrowful birth and all else whatsoever, He is the unaging sole primal cause and the protector of the three worlds; becoming horse, becoming tortoise, becoming fish, becoming man — He is the supreme Lord, God of gods, my purifying sacred one.

198.translit:
theerththan ulagu-aLandha sEvadi mEl poon-thaamam,
sErththi avaiyE sivan mudi mEl thaan kaNdu,
paarththan theLindhu-ozhindha pain-thuzhaayaan perumai,
pErththum oruvaraal pEsak-kidandhadhE?
198.meaning: The flowery garland that was placed upon the sacred feet which measured the worlds — seeing those very flowers upon Shiva's own crown, Arjuna (Partha) attained clarity. Could the greatness of Him of the fresh tulasi ever again be told by any single one?

199.translit:
kidandhu irundhu ninRu aLandhu kEzhalaayk-keezh-puk-
kidandhidum, thannuL karakkum umizhum,
thadam perum-thOL aarath-thazhuvum paar ennum
madandhaiyai, maal seyginRa maalaarkaaNbaarE.
199.meaning: Lying down, sitting, standing, measuring the worlds, becoming the boar and plunging below, He hides the worlds within Himself and spits them forth; He who embraces with His broad mighty shoulders to the full the maiden called Earth — who can comprehend the wondrous one and the love that the Lord performs?

20.translit:
paRRila neesanum muRRavum ninRanan
paRRilai yaay avan muRRi ladangkE.
20.meaning: Even the lowly one who has no other attachment stands wholly established in Him. You too, becoming one without any other attachment, surrender entirely and abide wholly within Him.

21.translit:
adangkezhil sambattu adangkakkaNdu eesan
adangkezhi laqdenRu--adangkuga vuLLE.
21.meaning: Seeing that all this abundant beauty and wealth belongs entirely to the Lord, the Master, knowing that this beauty too is wholly His, let your mind subside and abide within.

22.translit:
uLLa muraiseyal uLLavim moonRaiyum
uLLik keduttu iRai yuLLilo dungkE.
22.meaning: These three that exist—thought, word, and deed—reflect upon them and root them out, and submit, abiding within, into the Lord.

23.translit:
odungka avankaN odungkalu mellaam
vidumpinnu maakkai vidumpozhu deNNE.
23.meaning: When you subside, subsiding into Him, you let go of all; and afterward when the body too is cast off, reflect upon that moment of release.

24.translit:
eNperuk kannalattu oNporu Leerila
vaNpukazh naaraNan thiNkazhal sErE.
24.meaning: Attain the firm feet of Narayana of generous fame—the radiant Reality whose boundless bliss is beyond all count and without end.

25.translit:
sErttadat thenkuru koorchcha dakOpansol
seerttodai yaayirattu Ortthavip patthE.
25.meaning: This decade of ten verses, considered well, is from the thousand finely-woven verses of Satakopan of beautiful southern Kurukur set amid abundant ponds.

26.translit:
patthudai yadiyavark keLiyavan, piRarkaluk kariya
vitthagan malarmagaL virumbumnam arumpeRa ladigaL
matthuRu kadai veNNey kaLavinil uravidai yaappuNdu
etthiRam uralinO diNaindhirun dEngkiya eLivE.
26.meaning: He is easy of access to His devotees who possess the ten, yet hard for others to attain—the wondrous one whom the lotus-born Lakshmi loves, our Lord so rare to obtain. What humility, that He was bound tightly at the waist for stealing the butter churned by the churning-staff, and tied to the mortar stood lamenting!

27.translit:
eLivaru miyalvinan nilaivaram pilapala piRappaay,
oLivaru muzhu nalam mudalila kEdila veedaam,
theLidarum nilaimaiya thozhivilan muzhuvadum, miRaiyOn,
aLivaru maruLinO dagatthanan, puRatthan namaindhE.
27.meaning: He is one of an easily-attainable nature, taking countless births without bounds of station; He is radiant, the whole goodness, without beginning, without decay, the very liberation. Of a clearly-known nature, never failing, wholly present, the supreme Lord; with gracious compassion He dwells within and is also without, all-pervading.

28.translit:
amaivudai yaRaneRi muzhuvadu muyarva Rauyarndha,
amaivudai mudalkedal odividai yaRanila madhuvaam,
amaivudai yamararum yaavaiyum yaavarum thaanaama,
amaivudai naaraNan maayaiyai yaRibavar yaarE?
28.meaning: He is the perfect righteous path entire, exalted beyond all that is high; the perfect one, without origin or destruction, the abode of righteousness in between. Being Himself the perfect immortals and all things and all beings—who are they that can comprehend the maaya of the perfect Narayana?

29.translit:
yaarumOr nilaimaiya nenavaRi variya vemperumaan,
yaarumOr nilaimaiya nenavaRi veLiyavem perumaan,
pErumO raayiram piRapala vudaiyavem perumaan,
pErumO ruruvamu muLadhillai yiladhillai piNakkE.
29.meaning: Our Lord, hard for anyone to know as being of any one fixed state; our Lord, yet easy for anyone to know as being of one such state. Our Lord who has a thousand names and many others besides—there is no name or form that He does not have, none that He has not; there is no dispute in this.

30.translit:
piNakkaRa aRuvagaich chamayamum neRiyuLLi yuraittha,
kaNakkaRu nalatthanan andhami laadhiyam bagavan,
vaNakkudaith thavaneRi vazhininRu puRaneRi kaLaikattu,
uNakkumin pasaiyaRa avanudai, yuNarvukoN duNarndhE.
30.meaning: He is the one of incalculable goodness who, removing dispute, declared the paths of the six systems; the endless, beginningless, glorious Bhagavan. Standing in the path of humble austerity and uprooting the outer paths, dry out attachment utterly—grasping and realizing knowledge of Him.

31.translit:
uNarndhuNarn dhizhindhagan RuyarndhuRu viyandhavinnilaimai,
uNarndhuNarn dhuNarilum iRainilaiyuNarva rithuyirkaaL,
uNarndhuNarn dhuraitthurait thariyaya naranennumivarai,
uNarndhuNarn dhuraitthurait thiRaignjumin manappattadhonRE.
31.meaning: O souls! This state—lower than the lowest yet exalted, wondrous and beyond grasp—even when realized and realized again, the Lord's nature is hard to comprehend. Realizing and realizing again, speaking and speaking again of these called Brahma, Hari, and Hara, realize and worship the One that has settled in your mind.

32.translit:
onRenap palavena aRivarum vadivinuL ninRa,
nanRezhil naaraNan naanmugan aranennu mivarai,
onRanum manatthu vaith thuLLinum irubasai yaRutthu,
nanRena nalanjeyva thavanidai nammudai naaLE.
32.meaning: Within the form hard to know as one or as many stood the good and beautiful Narayana. Holding as one these called Narayana, the four-faced Brahma, and Hara, and meditating in your mind, cut off both attachments. Doing good well, our days are those spent in Him.

33.translit:
naaLu ninR adu namapazha maiyang koduvinaiyudanE
maaLum, Or kuRaivillai mananaka malamaRak kazhuvi,
naaLoonan thiruvudai yadigaLtham nalang kazhal vaNangki,
maaLumO ridatthilum vaNakkodu maaLvadhu valamE.
33.meaning: Our ancient sins that day after day stand and torment us shall perish along with our grievous karma; there is no want at all. Washing away the impurity within the mind, daily worship the gracious feet of our Lord who possesses Lakshmi; and even at the place of dying, to die in worship is the rightful course.

34.translit:
valatthanan thiripura meritthava nidambeRath thundhith
thalatthu, ezhudhisaimugan padaitthanal lulagamum thaanum
pulappada pinnumthan ulagatthi lagatthanan thaanE
solappugil ivaipinnum vayiRRuLa ivaiyavan thuyakkE.
34.meaning: He is the mighty one; granting a place to Him who burned the three cities, from His navel-lotus the seven-directioned four-faced Brahma created the good worlds, and both they and He are revealed; and again He holds them within His own world. If one is to tell, these too are within His belly—and these are but His bewildering sport.

35.translit:
thuyakkaRu madhiyilnal gnaanatthuL amararaith thuyakkum,
mayakkudai maayaigaL vaanilum periyana vallan,
puyaRkaru niRatthanan peru nilang kadandhanal ladippOdhu,
ayarppila nalaRRuvan thazhuvuvan vaNangkuva namarndhE.
35.meaning: He is master of the bewildering maayas, greater even than the heavens, which delude even the immortals of clear, faultless mind in good wisdom. He is of the dark hue of the rain-cloud, with blessed lotus-feet that strode across the vast earth. Without forgetting Him I will cry aloud, embrace Him, and worship, abiding in Him.

36.translit:
amararkaL thozhudhezha alaikadal kadaindhavan Rannai,
amarpozhil vaLangkuru koorchchadakOpan kuRREvalkaL,
amarsuvai yaayirat thavaRRinu Livaipatthum vallaar
amararO duyarviRchen RaRuvartham piRavaiyanj siRaiyE.
36.meaning: Those who can master these ten among the thousand of abiding sweetness—the humble services of Satakopan of fertile Kurukur with its lovely groves, sung to Him who churned the wave-tossed ocean while the immortals bowed and rose—shall go to the heights along with the immortals and sever the beautiful prison-cage of their births.

37.translit:
anjiRaiya madanaaraay. aLiyatthaay neeyumnin
anjiRaiya sEvalumaay aavaaven RenakkaruLi
venjiRaippuL Luyartthaarken vidudhoodaaych chenRakkaal
vansiRaiyil avanvaikkil vaippuNdaa lenseyyumO?
37.meaning: O tender-natured heron with beautiful wings, you who are full of compassion! If you and your beautiful-winged mate, taking pity on me and crying 'Alas, alas,' go as my dispatched messenger to Him who bears the fierce-winged bird (Garuda) on his banner—even if He should place you in a harsh prison, what harm would befall you?

38.translit:
enseyya thaamaraikkaN perumaanaark kenthoodaay
enseyyum uraitthakkaal inakkuyilkaaL. neeralirE?
munseydha muzhuvinaiyaal thiruvadikkeezhk kuRREval
munseyya muyalaadhEn agalvadhuvO vidhiyinamE?
38.meaning: O flocks of cuckoos! What harm would come if you spoke as my messenger to the Lord of the lovely red-lotus eyes? Are you not the right ones? Because of the grievous deeds I did before, I who did not strive to do humble service at His holy feet—is it my fated lot that I must be separated from Him?

39.translit:
vidhiyinaal pedaimaNakkum mennadaiya annangkaaL.
madhiyinaal kuRaLmaaNaay ulagirandha kaLvaRku
madhiyilEn valvi naiyE maaLaadhO venRu , orutthi
madhiyellaam muLkalangki mayangkumaal enneerE.
39.meaning: O swans of soft gait, who by destiny dwell mated with your partners! To the cunning one who as a dwarf brahmachari begged the worlds—say to Him: 'Will not the cruel karma of me, the witless one, ever perish?'—for this lone woman's whole mind is utterly confounded and bewildered. Tell Him so.

200.translit:
kaaNpaaraar emmeesan kaNNanai en kaaNumaaRu,
oonpeesil ellaa ulagumoor thuRRaaRRaa,
seeNpaala veedoo uyiroo maRRep poruTkum,
eeNpaalum sooraan paranthuLanaam engumee.
200.meaning: Who can see our Lord Kannan, and by what means can I behold Him? If one were to speak of His sustenance, all the worlds together would not be a single mouthful to satisfy Him. Whether the far-off realm of liberation, or the soul, or any other thing whatsoever, He does not slacken in any part; He is pervasively present everywhere.

201.translit:
engummuLan kaNNan enRa maganaik kaaynthu,
ingillaiyaal enRu iraNiyan thooNpuDaippa,
angapozhuthee avan veeyath thoonRiya, en
singappiraan perumai yaaraayum seermaiththee.
201.meaning: When Hiranya struck the pillar, scorning his son who said 'Kannan is present everywhere,' and declared 'He is not here,' my Lion-Lord appeared that very instant so that Hiranya perished. The greatness of my Man-Lion is of such excellence that it bears endless contemplation.

202.translit:
seermai koLveedu suvarkka naragu eeRaa,
eermai koLthevar naDuvaam maRRep poruTkum,
veermuthalaay viththaayp paranthu thanininRa,
kaarmugil poolvaNNan en kaNNanai naan kaNDenee.
202.meaning: Excellent liberation, heaven, hell as its limits, the gracious gods in their midst, and for every other thing too—He stands alone, pervading all as their root and their seed. That Lord, dark-hued like a rain cloud, my Kannan, I have beheld.

203.translit:
kaNDalangaL seyya karumeeni ammaanai,
vaNDalambum soolai vazhuthi vaLanaaDan,
paNDalaiyil sonna thamizh aayiraththu ippaththum vallaar,
viNDalaiyil veeRRirunthu aaLvar em maaveeDee.
203.meaning: Of the dark-bodied Lord with red lotus eyes, Satakopan of the fertile land of Vazhuthi, with groves humming with bees, sang in fine Tamil a thousand verses; those who master these ten will reign, seated high in the head of the heavens, attaining our great liberation.

204.translit:
em maaveeTTuth thiRamum seppam, nin
semmaa paatha paRputh thalaiseerthu ollai,
kaimmaa thunbam kaDintha piraanee,
ammaa aDiyen veeNDuvathu eethee.
204.meaning: I declare even the manner of our great liberation: O Lord who swiftly removed the suffering of the great elephant, let me place my head at Your beautiful great lotus feet without delay. O my Lord, this is what I, Your servant, desire.

205.translit:
itheeyaan unnaik koLvathu egngnaanRum, en
maithooy soothi maNivaNNa venthaay,
eythaa nin kazhal yaan eytha, gnaanak
kaithaa kaalak kazhivu seyyelee.
205.meaning: This is what I seek of You for all time: O my Father, radiant with a dark-tinged splendor, hued like a sapphire gem. So that I may reach Your feet that are hard to attain, grant me the hand of knowledge; do not cause delay by letting time pass.

206.translit:
seyyel theevinai enRu aruL seyyum, en
kaiyaar chakkarak kaNNapiraanee,
aiyaark kaNDam aDaikkilum nin kazhal
eyyaathu eeththa, aruL sey enakkee.
206.meaning: O Lord Kannan, with the discus filling Your hand, who graciously commands 'Do no evil deeds!' Even should my throat be choked at my final breath, grant me the grace to praise Your feet without wearying.

207.translit:
enakkee aaTchey ekkaalaththum enRu, en
manakkee vanthu iDai veeDinRi manni,
thanakkee aaga enaik koLLum eethee,
enakkee kaNNanai yaan koL siRappee.
207.meaning: Saying 'Serve me alone for all time,' He came into my very heart and abode there without interruption, taking me to be wholly His own. This is the glory by which I obtain Kannan for myself alone.

208.translit:
siRappil veedu suvarkkam naragam,
iRappil eythuga eythaRka, yaanum
piRappil palpiRavip perumaanai,
maRappu onRu inRi enRum magizhveenee.
208.meaning: Whether glorious liberation, heaven, or hell be attained at death or not attained—it matters not. I, free of even a trace of forgetfulness, shall ever rejoice in the Lord of many births who Himself is birthless.

209.translit:
magizhkoL theyvam ulogam alogam,
magizhkoL soothi malarntha ammaanee,
magizhkoL sinthai sol seykai koNDu, enRum
magizhvuRRu unnai vaNanga vaaraayee.
209.meaning: O Lord whose joyful splendor has blossomed forth over the rejoicing gods, the worlds, and what is beyond the worlds. With a joyful mind, joyful words, and joyful deeds, ever rejoicing, may You come so that I may worship You.

210.translit:
vaaraay un thiruppaatha malarkkeezh,
peeraathee yaan vanthaDaiyumpaDi
thaaraathaay, unnai ennuL vaippil enRum
aaraathaay, enak enRum ekkaalee.
210.meaning: Come! O You who have not granted that I might come and reach beneath the flower of Your sacred feet and remain there without straying. O You who, when I hold You within me, are never enough however much—for me, ever, at all times, come.

211.translit:
ekkaalaththu enthaiyaay ennuL mannil, maRRu
ekkaalaththilum yaathonRum veeNDeen,
mikkaar veetha vimalar vizhungum, en
akkaarak kaniyee, unnai yaanee.
211.meaning: If at any time You abide within me as my Father, then at no other time do I desire anything else whatsoever. O my sweet sugar-fruit, whom the supreme pure ones, masters of the Vedas, savor and consume—You alone do I seek, I myself.

212.translit:
yaanee ennai aRiyagilaathee,
yaanee enthanathee enRirunthen,
yaanee nee en uDaimaiyum neeyee,
vaanee eeththum em vaanavar eeRee.
212.meaning: I, not knowing my own self, lived thinking 'I am my own, and all that is mine is mine.' But I am Yours, and what I call my possessions are Yours too. O Chief of our celestials, whom the very heavens praise!

213.translit:
eeReel eezhum venRu eerkkoL ilangaiyai,
neeRee seytha neDunjuDarch soothi,
theeReel ennai un ponnaDich seerthu ollai,
veeRee pooga egngnaanRum viDalee.
213.meaning: O great effulgent splendor who conquered the seven mighty bulls, and reduced beautiful Lanka to ashes. Do not stay distant from me; swiftly join me to Your golden feet, and never let me go apart, at any time.

214.translit:
viDalil chakkarath thaNNalai, meeval
viDalil vaNkurugoorch chaDagoopan sol,
keDal il aayiraththuL ivaipaththum,
keDal il veeDu seyyum kiLarvaarkkee.
214.meaning: Of the Lord with the never-failing discus, whose love never fails, Satakopan of bountiful Kurugur sang; among the thousand imperishable verses, these ten will grant imperishable liberation to those who rise up devoted to them.

215.translit:
kiLaroLi iLamai keDuvathan munnam,
vaLaroLi maayoon maruviya kooyil,
vaLariLam pozhil soozh maaliruncoolai,
thaLarvilaraagil saarvathu sathiree.
215.meaning: Before the bright radiance of youth fades away, it is wise to take refuge—if one is not slack—at the temple where the Lord of growing splendor, the wondrous one, abides: Maliruncholai, surrounded by lush young groves.

216.translit:
sathir iLamaDavaar thaazhchiyai mathiyaathu,
athirkkural sangath thazhagar tham kooyil,
mathi thavazh kuDumi maaliruncoolai,
pathi athu eeththi ezhuvathu payanee.
216.meaning: Heeding not the lowly enticement of clever young women, to praise and rise toward that abode—Maliruncholai with peaks the moon glides over, the temple of the handsome Lord whose conch resounds with a roaring sound—this is the true gain.

217.translit:
payan alla seythu payan illai nenjee
puyal mazhai vaNNar purinthuRai kooyil
mayal migu pozhil soozh maaliruncoolai
ayan malai aDaivathu athu karumamee.
217.meaning: O heart, there is no profit in doing profitless deeds. To reach the hill nearby Maliruncholai, surrounded by bewilderingly beautiful groves, the temple where the Lord hued like a rain-bearing cloud dwells lovingly—that is the worthy task.

218.translit:
karuma van paasam kazhiththu uNarnthu uyyavee,
perumalai eDuththaan peeDu uRai kooyil
varu mazhai thavazhum maaliruncoolaith
thirumalai athuvee, aDaivathu thiRamee.
218.meaning: To cut away the strong bondage of karma, to gain true understanding and be saved—the proper way is to reach that sacred hill, Maliruncholai over which the coming rains glide, the glorious temple where He who lifted the great mountain dwells.

219.translit:
thiRamuDai valaththaal theevinai perukkaathu
aRamuyal aazhip paDaiyavan kooyil
maRuvil vaNsunai soozh maaliruncoolai
puRamalai saarap poovathu kiRiyee.
219.meaning: Not multiplying evil deeds through able strength, but striving in righteousness—to go and take refuge by the outer hill of Maliruncholai, surrounded by flawless bountiful springs, the temple of the Lord who bears the discus weapon, is the clever course.

220.translit:
kiRiyena ninaimin keezhmai seyyaadhE
uRiyamar veNNey uNdavan kOyil
maRiyodu piNaichEr maaliruNchOlai
neRipada adhuvE ninaivadhu nalamE.
220.meaning: Think of it as a clever aim and do not commit base, lowly deeds; it is the temple of Him who ate the butter set in the hanging pot. To meditate rightly upon Maliruncholai, where young deer and their mates gather together, is itself the good.

221.translit:
nalamena ninaimin naraku azhundhaadhE
nilamunam idaththaan needuRai kOyil
malamaRu madhichEr maaliruNchOlai
valamuRai eydhi maruvudhal valamE.
221.meaning: Think of it as good, and do not sink into hell; it is the temple where He who measured the earth of old dwells forever. To attain and cling in proper, rightful manner to Maliruncholai, where the spotless moon draws near, is true strength.

222.translit:
valaNcheydhu, vaikal valam kazhiyaadhE,
valaNcheyyum aaya - maayavan kOyil,
valaNcheyyum vaanOr maaliruNchOlai,
valaNcheydhu naaLum maruvudhal vazhakkE.
222.meaning: Circumambulate in reverence, and do not let your days pass without such worship; it is the temple of the wondrous one who acts for good. To circumambulate and cling each day to Maliruncholai, which the celestials too circle in worship, is the right way.

223.translit:
vazhakkena ninaimin valvinai moozhkaadhu
azhakkodi attaan amarperum kOyil
mazhakkaLiRRinam chEr maalirum chOlai
thozhak karudhuvadhu thuNivadhu choodhE.
223.meaning: Think of it as the proper way, so as not to drown in cruel karma; it is the great temple where He who slew the demoness Putana dwells. To resolve to worship Maliruncholai, where herds of young elephants gather, is the sure and wise plan.

224.translit:
choodhu enRu kaLavum choodhum seyyaadhE,
vEdham mun viriththaan virumbiya kOyil,
maadhu uRu mayil chEr maaliruNchOlaip
pOdhu avizh malaiyE, puguvadhu poruLE.
224.meaning: Do not, calling it strategy, commit theft and gambling; it is the temple beloved of Him who of old expounded the Vedas. To enter the blossom-laden hill of Maliruncholai, where lovely peahens gather, is the true substance and gain.

225.translit:
poruL enRu iv ulagam padaiththavan pugazhmEl
maruL il vaN kurugoor vaN chadakOpan
theruL koLLach chonna Or aayiraththuL ip paththu
aruLudaiyavan - thaaL aNaivikkum mudiththE.
225.meaning: These ten verses, out of the one thousand that the bounteous Satakopa of rich Kurukur, free of delusion, sang with clear understanding upon the glory of Him who created this world as its true purpose - these ten, when mastered, will surely bring one to the feet of the Lord who possesses grace.

226.translit:
mudichchOdhi yaayunadhu mugachchOdhi malandhadhuvO,
adichchOdhi neeninRa thaamaraiyaay alarndhadhuvO,
padichchOdhi yaadaiyodum palkalanaay, ninpaimpon
kadichchOdhi kalandhadhuvO? thirumaalE katturaiyE.
226.meaning: O Tirumal, tell me truly: has the radiance of the crown upon your head blossomed forth as the radiance of your face? Has the radiance of your feet bloomed as the lotus on which you stand? Has the radiance of your form blended with your bright garments, your many ornaments, and the radiance of your fragrant golden waistband?

227.translit:
katturaikkil thaamarainin kaNpaadham kaiyovvaa,
katturaiththa nan ponnuL thirumEni oLi ovvaadhu,
otturaiththiv vulagunnaip pugazhvellaam perumbaalum,
patturaiyaayp puRkenRE kaattumaal paraNchOdhee.
227.meaning: O supreme radiance, if I must speak truly, the lotus does not match your eyes, feet, and hands; even refined good gold does not match the lustre of your sacred form. All the praises that this world heaps upon you in agreement appear, for the most part, as mere empty, faded words that fall far short.

228.translit:
paraNchOdhi. neeparamaay ninnigazhndhu pin, maRROr
paraNchOdhi yinmaiyil padiyOvi nigazhkinRa,
paraNchOdhi ninnuLLE padarulagam padaiththa,em
paraNchOdhi kOvindhaa! paNpuraikka maattEnE.
228.meaning: O supreme radiance, you stand transcendent as the highest; and since there exists no other supreme light beyond you, you shine incomparable, without equal. O supreme radiance who created the spreading worlds within yourself, our supreme radiance, Govinda - I am unable to describe your nature.

229.translit:
maattaadhE yaagilumim malarthalaimaa Gnaalam,
nin maattaaya malarppuraiyum thiruvuruvum manamvaikka
maattaadha palasamaya madhikoduththaay, malarththuzhaay
maattEnee manamvaiththaay maaGnaalam varundhaadhE.
229.meaning: Even though it cannot, you granted to this vast, broad, flowering world the diverse-creed minds that are unable to fix their thought upon your blossom-like sacred form. Yet you, O Lord garlanded with blossoming tulasi, set your mind upon it so that the great world would not suffer.

230.translit:
varundhaadha arundhavaththa malarkkadhirin chuda rudambaay,
varundhaadha Gnaanamaay varambinRi muzhudhiyanRaay,
varungaalam nigazhkaalam kazhikaala maay,ulagai
orungaaga aLippaaycheer enkulakka OdhuvanE?
230.meaning: You whose body is radiant like the blossoming sun's rays, born of effortless rare penance; you who are effortless, unfailing knowledge, who pervade all without limit; you who are the time to come, the present time, and the time past, and who protect the whole world as one - how can I, whose stock is lowly, ever fully recount your glory?

231.translit:
Odhuvaar Oththellaam evvulagath thevvevaiyum,
chaadhuvaay ninpugazhin thagaiyallaal piRidhillai,
pOdhuvaazh punandhuzhaay mudiyinaay, poovinmEl
maadhuvaazh maarppinaay. encholliyaan vaazhththuvanE?
231.meaning: All the scriptures recited by those who recite, in every world and of every kind, are good only as expressions of your glory; there is nothing else they truly speak of. O Lord whose crown bears the wild tulasi alive with blossoms, on whose chest dwells Lakshmi born of the flower - with what words can I praise you?

232.translit:
vaazhththuvaar palaraaga ninnuLLE naanmuganai,
moozhththanee rulagellaam padaiyenRu mudhalpadaiththaay
kEzhththasee raranmudhalaak kiLardheyva maaykkiLarndhu,
choozhththamarar thudhiththaalun tholpugazhmaa chooNaadhE?
232.meaning: So that many might praise you, you created the four-faced Brahma from within yourself and commanded him, 'Create all the flood-engulfed worlds,' thus creating at the beginning. When the surrounding immortals, rising up as the resplendent gods beginning with the renowned Hara, extol you, will not your ancient glory be magnified?

233.translit:
maachooNaach chuda rudambaay malaraadhu kuviyaadhu,
maachooNaa Gnaanamaay muzhudhumaay muzhudhiyanRaay,
maachooNaa vaankOlath thamararkkOn vazhipattaal,
maachooNaa unpaadha malarchOdhi mazhungaadhE?
233.meaning: You whose body is radiant and untainted; you who are flawless knowledge that neither blooms nor closes, who are all and pervade all; when the king of the spotless, splendrous celestials worships you, will not the radiance of your stainless lotus feet only grow brighter and never dim?

234.translit:
mazhungaadha vainnudhiya chakkaranal valaththaiyaay,
thozhungaadhal kaLiRaLippaan puLLoorndhu thOnRinaiyE,
mazhungaadha GnaanamE padaiyaaga, malarulagil
thozhumbaayaark kaLiththaalun chudarchchOdhi maRaiyaadhE?
234.meaning: You who hold in your good right hand the sharp-pointed, never-blunting discus; you appeared riding the bird Garuda to save the elephant Gajendra who longed to worship you. If, wielding your never-dimming knowledge as your weapon, you grant grace to those who serve you in this flowering world, will your blazing radiance ever fade?

235.translit:
maRaiyaaya naalvEdhath thuLninRa malarchudarE,
muRaiyaaliv vulagellaam padaiththidandhuN dumizhndhaLandhaay,
piRaiyERu chadaiyaanum naanmuganum indhiranum
iRaiyaadhal aRindhEththa veeRiruththal idhuviyappE?
235.meaning: O blossoming radiance who stand within the four Vedas which are the sacred mystery; in proper order you created all these worlds, raised them up, ate them, spat them out, and measured them. Is it any wonder that, while crescent-crowned Shiva, four-faced Brahma, and Indra recognize you as the Lord and praise you, you should sit enthroned in majesty?

236.translit:
viyappaaya viyappillaa meyGnaana vEdhiyanai,
chayappugazhaar palarvaazhum thadanggurugoor chadakOpan,
thuyakkinRith thozhudhuraiththa aayiraththu Lippaththum,
uyakkoNdu piRappaRukkum olimunneer GnaalaththE.
236.meaning: Of the thousand verses that Satakopa of vast Kurukur, where many of victorious fame dwell, worshipped and uttered without confusion upon the true-knowledge Lord of the Vedas who is a wonder beyond all wonders - these ten will redeem and cut off rebirth for those in this world girt by the resounding sea.

237.translit:
munneer Gnaalam padaiththavem mugilvaNNanE,
annaaLnee thandha aakkaiyin vazhiyuzhalvEn,
vennaaLnOy veeya vi naikaLaivEr aRappaayndhu,
ennaaLyaan unnai inivandhu kooduvanE?
237.meaning: O our cloud-hued Lord who created the world girt by the threefold waters; I wander helplessly along the way of this body that you gave me in that day of old. Cutting away to the very root my karma so that the disease of these grievous days may perish, on what day shall I come at last and be united with you?

238.translit:
vanmaa vaiyam aLandha em vaamanaa,nin
panmaa maayap palpiRaviyil padikinRayaan,
thonmaa valvinaith thodarkaLai mudhalarindhu,
ninmaathaaL chErndhu niRpadheG GnaanRukolO?
238.meaning: O our Vamana who measured the strong, great earth; I who keep sinking through the many births caused by your manifold great maya - when will I sever at the source the chains of my ancient, mighty karma and reach and abide at your great feet?

239.translit:
kollaa maakkOl kolaiseydhu paaradhappOr,
ellaach chEnaiyum irunilath thaviththavendhaay,
pollaa aakkaiyin puNarvinai aRukkalaRaa,
chollaayyaa nunnaich chaarvadhOr choozhchchiyE.
239.meaning: O my Lord who, taking up the goad that does not kill yet making it kill, destroyed in the Bharata war all the armies upon the broad earth; since I am unable to sever the bondage of this evil body, tell me of some means, some device, by which I may reach you.

240.translit:
soozhcchi njaanach chudaroLi yaaki, enRum
yeezhcchikkae Dinri eNGkaNum niRaindha vendhaay,
taazhcchi maR ReNGkum tavirndhu nin taaLiNakkeezh
vaazhcchi, yaan saerum vakaiyaruLaay vandhae!
240.meaning: O my Lord, you are the radiant light of all-encompassing wisdom, ever without rise or decline, filling everywhere completely. Grant me the grace, having come to me, that I may attain the life of dwelling beneath your united feet, with all lowliness everywhere removed.

241.translit:
vandhaay poa laevandhum yenmanat tinaineee,
sindhaamal seyyaay iduvae yiduvaakil,
kondhaark kaayaavin kozhumalart tiruniRatta
yendhaay, yaanunnai eNGkuvan daNukiRpanae?
241.meaning: Even when you seem to have come to me, you do not keep my mind from wavering. O my Father, whose sacred hue is like the rich blossoms of the clustered kaaya flower, if this is how things remain, how and where shall I come to draw near to you?

242.translit:
kiRpan killaen yenrilan munanaaLaal,
aRpasaa raNGkaL avaisuvait takanRozhindhaen,
paRpal laayiram uyirseydha paramaa, nin
naRpoRchoa dittaaL naNukuva deNGNGaanRae?
242.meaning: In days past I never said whether I was able or unable; I tasted the trifling, worthless pleasures and was ruined and estranged. O Supreme One who created the many thousands upon thousands of lives, when shall I draw near to your good, golden, radiant feet?

243.translit:
yeNGNGaanRu naamirun dirundhiraNGki nenjsae
meyjnjaana minRi vinaiyiyal piRappazhundhi,
yeNGNGaanRum eNGkum ozhivaRa niRaindhuninRa,
meyjnjaanach choadik kaNNanai maevudhumae?
243.meaning: O my heart, how long, sitting and grieving, lacking true knowledge and sunk in births that are the nature of our deeds, shall we attain Kannan, the radiance of true wisdom, who stands everywhere at all times, filling completely without any gap?

244.translit:
maevu dhunba vinaikaLai vidutthumilaen,
oavudhalinri unkazhal vaNaNGgiRRilaen,
paavudhol seerkkaNNaa. en paranjsudarae,
koovukinraen kaaNbaan eNGkoydhak koovuvanae?
244.meaning: I have not cast off the sorrow-bringing deeds that cling to me, nor have I worshipped your feet without ceasing. O Kannan of ancient, far-spread glory, my supreme effulgence, I am calling out to behold you; where shall I go and from where shall I call?

245.translit:
koovikkoovik koduvinait tooRRuL ninru
paaviyaen palakaalam vazhidhikait talamarkkinraen,
maeviyan RaaNirai kaattava nulakamellaam,
taaviya ammaanai eNGkinit talaippeyvanae?
245.meaning: Calling out again and again, standing in the thicket of cruel deeds, I, a sinner, for a long time bewildered and losing my way, wander in distress. The Lord who once lovingly guarded the herds of cattle, who strode across all the worlds, where now shall I reach and join him?

246.translit:
talaippey kaalam namandhamar paasamvittaal,
alaippoo NuNNumav vallalel laamakala,
kalaippal njaanatten kaNNanaik kaNdukoNdu,
nilaippeRRen nenjsam peRRadu needuyirae.
246.meaning: When the time of joining comes and the messengers of Yama release their noose, all that affliction which is borne and endured as suffering departs. Beholding my Kannan of the manifold knowledge of the arts, my heart has gained steadfastness and has attained everlasting life.

247.translit:
uyirka Lellaa ulakamu mudaiyavanai,
kuyilkoL soalait tenkurukoorch chadakoapan,
seyiril sol isaimaalai aayiratt Lippattum,
uyirinmae laakkai ooNidai ozhivikkumae.
247.meaning: Of the thousand flawless-worded musical garland verses by Satakopan of southern Kurukur, whose groves hold koel birds, about the Lord who owns all lives and all worlds, these ten will remove the body, made of flesh, that rests upon the soul.

248.translit:
ozhivil kaalamellaam udanaay manni,
vazhuvilaa adimai seyya vaeNdumnaam,
tezhiku ral aru vittiru vaeNGgadattu,
ezhilkoL soadhi endhaidhandhai tandhaikkae.
248.meaning: For all unceasing time we must, being ever joined together and steadfast, perform flawless service to the radiant, beautiful light who is the Father of my father's fathers, dwelling at sacred Venkatam of the loud-roaring cascading waterfalls.

249.translit:
yendhai dhandhai tandhai tandhaikkum
mundhai, vaanavar vaanavar koanodum,
sindhupoo makizhum tiruvaeNG kadattu,
andha milpukazhk kaarezhil aNNalae.
249.meaning: He is the foremost one, even before the father of my father's fathers; the Lord of boundless glory and dark, cloud-like beauty, at sacred Venkatam where the celestials, together with the king of the celestials, scatter flowers and rejoice.

250.translit:
aNNal maayan aNikoLsen taamaraik
kaNNan, seNGkani vaaykkaru maaNikkam,
theNNi Raicchunai neertt tiruvaeNG kadattu,
eNNil tolpukazh vaanava reesanae.
250.meaning: The noble wondrous one, Kannan whose eyes are like lovely red lotuses, whose lips are like ripe red fruit, dark as a sapphire gem, at sacred Venkatam with its clear, brimming spring-water pools, he is the Lord of the celestials, of countless ancient glory.

251.translit:
eesan vaanavark kenpanen Raal, adu
taesa moadhiru vaeNGkadat taanukku?,
neesa nen niRai voNRumi laen, enkaN
paasam vaitta paranjsudarch choadhikkae.
251.meaning: If I were to say that he is the Lord of the celestials, would that be any glory to the Lord of Venkatam? I am lowly and possess no fullness whatsoever, before the supreme effulgent radiance who has set his love upon me.

252.translit:
soadhi yaakiyel laavula kumthozhum,
aadhi moortthiyen RaaLa vakumoa?,
vaedhi yarmuzhu vaedhat tamudhatthai,
theedhil seerttiru vaeNGkadat taanaiyae.
252.meaning: If one were to call him the radiance, the primal Lord whom all worlds worship, would even that have any measure? He is the nectar of the entire Veda of the Vedic seers, the Lord of sacred Venkatam of faultless excellence.

253.translit:
vaeNGka daNGkaLmeym maelvinai muRRavum,
taaNGgaL taNGgaTku nallana vaeseyvaar,
vaeNGka datthuRai vaarkku namavenna
laaNGga damai, adusumandh aarkkadkae.
253.meaning: Those who say 'namaha' (obeisance) to the Lord who dwells at Venkatam do only what is truly good for themselves, for all the deeds upon their bodies are wholly removed. That alone is the duty they bear, those who have taken it upon themselves.

254.translit:
sumandhu maamalar neersudar deepamkoNdu,
amarndhu vaanavar vaanavar koanodum,
namanRe zhumtiru vaeNGkadam naNGgaTku,
samankoL veedu tarumthadaNG kunramae.
254.meaning: Sacred Venkatam, where the celestials together with the king of the celestials, bearing great flowers, water, and radiant lamps, gather and rise up to bow in worship, that vast hill bestows upon us the liberation of equality.

255.translit:
kunram aendhik kuLirmazhai kaattavan,
anru njaalam aLandha piraan, paran
senru saertiru vaeNGkada maamalai,
onru maedhozha namvinai oayumae.
255.meaning: He who lifted the mountain and shielded from the cold rain, the Lord who once measured the world, the Supreme One who has come and settled there, the great hill of sacred Venkatam, if we but worship it once, our deeds will cease.

256.translit:
oayu mooppup piRappiRap puppiNi,
veeyu maaRusey vaantiru vaeNGkadat
taayan, naaNmala raamadit taamarai,
vaayuL Lummanat tuLLumvaip paarkadkae.
256.meaning: The cowherd Lord of sacred Venkatam brings about the destruction of waning, old age, birth, death, and disease, for those who place his lotus feet, fresh as the day's blossom, within their mouths and within their hearts.

257.translit:
vaittha naaLvarai ellai kuRukicchenru,
eytti Laippadan munnam adaiminoa,
paittha paampaNai yaantiru vaeNGkadam,
moyttha soalaimoy poondhadam taazhvarae.
257.meaning: Before the appointed span of life draws near its limit and you grow weary and faint, reach there: sacred Venkatam of the Lord who rests on the hooded serpent-couch, with its dense groves and the lovely flowering pools of its slopes.

258.translit:
taaLpa rappimaN vadaaviya eesanai,
neeLpo zhilkuru koorchchada koapansol,
kaezhil aayirat tippattum vallavar,
vaazhvar vaazhveydhi njaalam pukazhavae.
258.meaning: Of the thousand peerless verses of Satakopan of Kurukur with its vast groves, about the Lord who spread out his feet and measured the earth, those who can master these ten shall live, attaining true life, while the world praises them.

259.translit:
pukazhunalloruvanenkoa poruvilseerppoomiyenkoa
tikazhuntaNparavaiyenkoa teeyenkoavaayuvenkoa
nikazhumaakasamenkoa neeLsudariraNdu menkoa
ikazhvilivvanaittumenkae kaNNanaikkoovumaaRae.
259.meaning: Shall I call him the one good being whom all praise, or the earth of matchless excellence, or the cool shining sea, or fire, or wind, or the pervading sky, or the two great luminaries? Calling out to Kannan, how shall I name him as all these things together that are beyond contempt?

260.translit:
koovumaa Rariya maaTTeen kunRangaL anaiththum enkoo,
meevuseer maari enkoo viLanguthaa ragaigaL enkoo,
naaviyal kalaigaL enkoo njaananal laavi enkoo,
paavuseerk kaNNan emmaan pangayak kaNNa naiyee.
260.meaning: I do not know how to call out to Him. Shall I call Him all the mountains, or the abundant gracious rain, or the shining stars? Shall I call Him the arts that dwell on the tongue, or the good breath of wisdom? My Lord Kannan of widespread glory, the lotus-eyed one.

261.translit:
pangayak kaNNan enkoo pavaLacchev vaayan enkoo,
angadhir aDiyan enkoo anjana vaNNan enkoo,
sengadhir muDiyan enkoo thirumaRu maarvan enkoo,
sanguchak karaththan enkoo saadhimaa NikkaththaiyEE.
261.meaning: Shall I call Him the lotus-eyed one, or the one with coral-red lips? Shall I call Him the one with radiant feet, or the one of dark collyrium hue? Shall I call Him the one with a crown of bright rays, or the one bearing the sacred mole (Sri) on His chest? Shall I call Him the bearer of conch and discus, this flawless ruby gem?

262.translit:
saadhimaa Nikkam enkoo savikooLpon muththam enkoo,
saadhinal vayiram enkoo, thavivilseer viLakkam enkoo,
aadhiyanj soodhi enkoo aadhiyam puruDan enkoo,
aadhumil kaalath thendhai acchudhan amala naiyee.
262.meaning: Shall I call Him a pure ruby, or radiant gold, or a pearl? Shall I call Him a fine diamond, or a lamp of unceasing glory? Shall I call Him the primal beautiful light, or the primal Person? My Father from beginningless time, the imperishable, the spotless one.

263.translit:
acchudhan amalan enkoo, aDiyavar vinaigeDukkum,
nacchumaa marundham enkoo nalangaDal amudham enkoo,
acchuvaik kaTTi enkoo aRusuvai aDisil enkoo,
neicchuvaith theeRal enkoo kaniyenkoo paalen keenoo.
263.meaning: Shall I call Him the imperishable, the spotless one, or the great curative medicine that destroys the karma of His devotees? Shall I call Him the nectar of the good ocean, or a sweet lump of sugar, or a feast of the six tastes? Shall I call Him the ghee-flavored honey-drink, or ripe fruit, or shall I call Him milk?

264.translit:
paalenkoo naanguveedhappayanenkoo samayaneedhi
noolenkoo nuDangugeeLviyisai yenkoo ivaRRuLnalla
meelenkoo vinaiyinmikka payanenkoo kaNNanenkoo
maalenkoomaayanenkoo vaanavaraadhiyaiyee.
264.meaning: Shall I call Him milk, or the fruit of the four Vedas, or the scripture of religious law? Shall I call Him the swelling music of sacred learning, or that which is best and highest among these, or the great fruit beyond all action? Shall I call Him Kannan, or Tirumal, or the wondrous one, the source of the celestials?

265.translit:
vaanavaraadhiyenkoo vaanavartheyvamenkoo
vaanavarboogamenkoo vaanavarmuRRumenkoo
oonamilselvamenkoo oonamilsuvarkkamenkoo
oonamilmookkamenkoo oLimaNivaNNanaiyee.
265.meaning: Shall I call Him the source of the celestials, or the god of the celestials? Shall I call Him the enjoyment of the celestials, or the all-in-all of the celestials? Shall I call Him flawless wealth, or flawless heaven, or flawless liberation, this radiant gem-hued one?

266.translit:
oLimaNi vaNNan enkoo. oruvanen Reeththa ninRa
naLirmadhic chaDaiyan enkoo. naanmugak kaDavuL enkoo,
aLimagizhn dhulagamellaam paDaiththavai eeththa ninRa,
kaLimalarth thuLava nemmaan kaNNanai maaya naiyee.
266.meaning: Shall I call Him the radiant gem-hued one, or Him who stands praised by Siva of the cool-mooned matted locks as the One? Shall I call Him the four-faced god (Brahma)? Standing while all the worlds He graciously and joyfully created praise Him, my Lord adorned with the fragrant joyful tulasi, Kannan the wondrous one.

267.translit:
kaNNanai maayan Rannaik kaDalkaDain dhamudhang koNDa,
aNNalai acchu thannai ananthanai ananthan thanmeel,
naNNinan kuRaigin Raanai njaalamuN Dumizhndha maalai,
eNNumaa Rariya maaTTeen, yaavaiyum yavarum thaanee.
267.meaning: Kannan the wondrous one, the Lord who churned the ocean and took the nectar, the imperishable one, the infinite Ananta, who reclines well upon Ananta (the serpent), the great Tirumal who swallowed the world and spat it out again. I do not know how to comprehend Him, for He Himself is all things and all beings.

268.translit:
yaavaiyum yavarum thaanaay avaravar samayan dhooRum,
thooyvilan pula nain dhukkum solappaDaan uNarvin moorththi,
aaviseer uyirin uLLaal adhumoor paRRi laadha,
paavanai adhanaik kooDil avanaiyum kooDa laamee.
268.meaning: Being Himself all things and all beings, He is untouched by the various sects of differing people, beyond the reach of the five senses, not utterable in words, the form of pure consciousness. Within the life-soul joined to breath, if one attains that contemplation which clings to nothing else, then one can attain Him too.

269.translit:
kooDivaN DaRaiyum thaNthaarkkoNDalpool vaNNan Rannai
maaDalar pozhilgu rugoor vaNshaDa koopan sonna,
paaDaloor aayi raththuL ivaiyumoor paththum vallaar,
veeDila booga meydhi virumbuvar amarar moyththee.
269.meaning: On Him of cloud-dark hue, wearing a cool garland where gathered bees hum, the bounteous Satakopan of Kurugur, town of blossoming groves, composed these songs. Those who can master these ten too among the thousand songs will attain unceasing bliss, and the immortals will throng around and cherish them.

270.translit:
moymmaam poompozhil poygai mudhalaic chiRaippaTTu ninRa,
kaimmaa vukkaruL seydha kaarmugil poolvaNNan kaNNan,
emmaa naicchollip paaDi ezhundhum paRandhumthuL LaadhaaR,
thammaal karumamen solleer thaNkaDal vaTTaththuL Leeree.
270.meaning: Kannan, of hue like a dark rain-cloud, who granted grace to the great elephant that stood captive in the grip of the crocodile in the pond amid dense mango-blossom groves -- those who do not rise up, soar and dance singing of my Lord, what use, tell me, is there in them, O you who dwell within the circle of the cool ocean?

271.translit:
thaNkaDal vaTTaththuL Laaraith thamakkirai yaaththaDin dhuNNum,
thiNkazhaR kaalasu rarkkuth theengizhaik kumthiru maalai,
paNgaL thalaikkoLLap paaDip paRandhum kuniththuzha laadhaaR,
maNkoL ulagil piRappaar valvi nai mooda malaindhee.
271.meaning: Tirumal who works ruin upon the strong-anklet-legged demons that slay and devour the people within the cool ocean's circle as their prey -- those who do not soar and dance whirling, singing so that the melodies take hold, will be born again in this earthen world, beaten down and overwhelmed by cruel karma.

272.translit:
malaiyai yeDuththukkal maari kaaththup pasuniRai thannai,
tholaivu thavirththa piraanaic chollicchol liniRRep poodhum,
thalaiyinoo Daadhanam thaTTath thaDuguTTa maayppaRa vaadhaar,
alaikoL naragath thazhundhik kiDandhuzhaik kinRa vambaree.
272.meaning: The Lord who lifted up the mountain, warded off the hailstorm of rain, and saved the herd of cows from destruction -- those who do not speak of Him again and again and soar at all times, with head and seat striking together, become the worthless ones who lie sunk and suffering in the surging hell.

273.translit:
vambavizh koodhai poruTTaa maalviDai yeezhum aDarththa,
sembava LaththiraL vaayan sireedharan tholpugazh paaDi,
kumbiDu naTTamiT TaaDik koogukaT TuNDuzha laadhaaR,
thambiRap paalbaya nennee saadhu sananga LiDaiyee?
273.meaning: Sridhara of the lips like a cluster of red coral, who for the sake of the fragrance-spreading maiden (Nappinnai) subdued the seven mighty bulls -- those who do not sing His ancient glory, dance prostrating and whirling overcome with rapture, what is the fruit of their birth, amid the company of the good?

274.translit:
saadhu sanaththai naliyum kanjanaic chaadhippa thaRku,
aadhiyanj soodhi yuruvai anguvaith thinguppiRandha,
veedha mudhalva naip paaDi veedhigaL dhooRumthuL Laadhaar,
oodhi yuNarndhavar munnaa enchavip paarma nisaree?
274.meaning: To overcome Kamsa who tormented the company of the good, the Lord of the primal beautiful light-form left that form there and was born here -- the source of the Vedas. Those who do not sing of Him and dance through every street, what fitness have those men to stand before those who have learned and understood?

275.translit:
manisarum maRRum muRRumaay maayap piRavi piRandha,
thaniyan piRappili thannaith thaDangaDal seerndha piraanai,
kaniyaik karumbinin saaRRaik kaTTiyaith theenai amudhai,
munivinRi eeththik kunippaar muzhudhuNar neermaiyi naaree.
275.meaning: The unique one, the birthless one who yet took a wondrous birth, becoming men and all other things and all -- the Lord who reclines on the vast ocean. Those who praise without weariness and dance to Him -- who is fruit, the sweet juice of sugarcane, a lump of jaggery, honey, nectar -- they are of the nature that comprehends everything fully.

276.translit:
neermai nooRRuvar veeya aivark karuLseydhu ninRu,
paarmalgu seenai aviththa paranjuDa rainainaindh aaDi.
neermalgu kaNNina raagi nenjam kuzhaindhunai yaadhee,
oormalgi mooDu paruppaar uththamarkkaT kenchey vaaree?
276.meaning: The supreme effulgence who, granting grace to the five (Pandavas) while the hundred (Kauravas) perished, stood and destroyed the earth-filling armies -- for those who do not meditate and dance upon Him, becoming filled with tears in their eyes and melting in heart, but grow fat thronging the town, what can be done for them by the noble ones?

277.translit:
vaarppunal andhaN Naruvi vaDathiru veengaDath thendhai,
peerppala sollip pidhaRRip piththaren Reepira r kooRa,
oorppala pukkum pukaadhum uloogar sirikkanin Raadi,
aarvam perugik kunippaar amarar thozhappaDu vaaree.
277.meaning: My Father of the northern sacred Venkatam with its cool beautiful cascading streams -- those who, uttering His many names and babbling so that others call them madmen, entering many towns or not, dance while worldly folk laugh, whirling as their love overflows -- they become those whom even the immortals worship.

278.translit:
amarar thozhappaDu vaanai anaiththula gukkum piraanai,
amarar manaththinuL yoogu puNarndhavan thannoDon Raaga,
amarath thuNiyaval laargaL ozhiyaal laadhava rellaam,
amara ninaindhezhun thaaDi alaRRuva theekaru mamee.
278.meaning: He who is worshipped by the immortals, the Lord of all the worlds -- except for those who can firmly resolve to become one with Him through yoga joined within the minds of the immortals, for all others the very task is this: to think of Him steadfastly, rise up, dance and cry out.

279.translit:
karumamum karuma palanum aagiya kaaraNan thannai,
thirumaNi vaNNanaic chengaN maalinaith theeva piraanai,
orumai manaththinuL vaiththu uLLang kuzhaindhezhun thaaDi,
perumaiyum naaNum thavirndhu pidhaRRumin peedhamai theerndhee.
279.meaning: The Cause who is both the action and the fruit of action, the one of sapphire-gem hue, the red-eyed Tirumal, the Lord of the gods -- holding Him within a single-pointed mind, with melting heart rise up and dance, and casting aside both pride and shame, babble of Him, your ignorance ended.

280.translit:
theerntha adiyavar thammaith thiruth thip paNikoLLa valla,
aarntha pugazh achuthanai amarar piraanaiyem maanai,
vaayntha vaLavayal soozhthaN vaLanguru koorch chaTakOpan,
nErnthavO raayiratth ippath aruvinai neeRu seyyumE.
280.meaning: He is able to take into His sacred service those steadfast devotees who have fully surrendered; He is Achyuta of abundant fame, the Lord of the immortals, our Master. These ten verses, among the apt thousand composed by Satakopa of cool, prosperous Kurugur encircled by rich, fertile fields, will reduce one's karma to ashes.

281.translit:
seyya thaamaraik kaNNa naayula kEzhu muNDa avankaNDeer,
vaiyam vaanam manisar deyvam maTRum maTRum maTRum muTRumaay,
seyya soozhsu dar nyaana maayveLip paTTi vaipaDaith thaanpinnum,
moykoL sOthiyo daayi naanoru moova raagiya moorthiyE.
281.meaning: Behold, He is the lotus-red-eyed one who swallowed the seven worlds. Being the earth, the sky, men, gods, and everything else and more and more and all in all, He is the red effulgence of all-encompassing wisdom that manifested itself and created this world; and again, joined with dense radiance, He became the one Lord who is the threefold deity.

282.translit:
moova raagiya moorthi yaimudal moovark kumudal vandrannai,
saava muLLana neekku vaanaith thaTanga Dalkidan thaanthannaith,
dEva dEvanaith thenni langai eriye zhachchaTRa villiyai,
paava naasanaip panga yaththaDan kaNNa naipparа vuminO.
282.meaning: Worship Him who is the threefold deity, the first cause even of the foremost three; who removes all that brings death; who reclined upon the vast ocean; the God of gods; the Archer who destroyed Southern Lanka so that it burst into flames; the destroyer of sin; the one with broad lotus eyes.

283.translit:
paravi vaanava rEttha nindra parama naipparanj sOthiyai,
kuravai kOththa kuzhaga naimaNi vaNNa naikkuDak kooththanai,
arava mERi yalaika Dalama rumthu yilkoNDa aNNalai,
iravum nanpaka lumvi daadendrum Eththu thalmanam vaimminO.
283.meaning: Set your mind upon praising, both night and bright day, without ever ceasing, the Supreme One who stands while the celestials extol Him, the Supreme Light; the youthful one who wove the kuravai dance; the sapphire-hued one; the dancer of the pot-dance; the Lord who took His rest amid the surging ocean, mounted upon the serpent.

284.translit:
vaimmin nummanath thendru yaanuraik kindra maayavan seermaiyai
emma nOrka Luraippa thendru? adu niRka naaDoRum,
vaanavar thammai yaaLum avanum naanmuga numsa Daimudi aNNalum,
semmai yaalavan paatha pangayam sinthith thEththi thirivarE.
284.meaning: How can ones like me describe the greatness of the wondrous Lord whom I urge you to enshrine in your minds? Let that be. Day after day, the one who rules the celestials, and the four-faced Brahma, and the Lord with matted crown, with uprightness meditate upon His lotus feet, praise them, and go about.

285.translit:
thiriyum kaTRo dagalvi sumbu thiNintha maNkidan thakaDal,
eriyum theeyo dirusu darteyvam, maTRum maTRum muTRumaay,
kariya mEniyan seyya thaamaraik kaNNan kaNNanviN NOriRai,
suriyum palkarung kunji yengaL suDarmu DiyaNNal thOTRamE.
285.meaning: Being the moving wind, the wide expanse of the sky, the dense lying-down earth, the ocean, the blazing fire, the two luminaries, the gods, and everything else and all in all - He of the dark frame, the red-lotus-eyed Krishna, the Lord of the celestials, with abundant curling dark tresses, our radiant-crowned Master - such is His manifestation.

286.translit:
thOTRak kEDavai yilla vanuDai yaana vanoru moorthiyaay,
seeTRath thOdaruL peTRa vanaDik keezhppu ganindra senkaNmaal,
naaTRath thOTRach chuvaiyo liooRal aagi nindra, em vaanavar
ERRai yEyandri maTRo ruvarai yaani lEnezhu maikkumE.
286.meaning: He is without origin or decay, and He is the possessor of all; taking a single form, the red-eyed Tirumal beneath whose feet those favored with both His wrath and His grace come to take shelter; He who stands as smell, sight, taste, sound, and touch - that bull-Lord of our celestials, and no other, will I know for all seven births.

287.translit:
ezhumaik kumena thaavik kinnamu thatthi naiena thaaruyir,
kezhumi yakathirch chOthi yaimaNi vaNNa naikkuDak kooththanai,
vizhumi yavama rarmu nivarvi zhungum kannal kaniyinai,
thozhumin thooyama natha raayiRai yumnil laaduyarangaLE.
287.meaning: He is the sweet nectar to my soul for all seven births, my precious life-breath; the unified radiant effulgence; the sapphire-hued one; the dancer of the pot-dance; the sugarcane-sweet fruit that the exalted immortals and sages relish - worship Him with pure minds, and your sorrows will not remain even for a moment.

288.translit:
thuyara mEtharu thunba inba vinaika Laay a vai allanaay,
uyara nindrathOr sOthi yaayula kEzhu muNDumizhn thaanthannai,
ayara vaangu naman thamarkkaru nanji naiyachu thanthannai,
thayara thaRkuma kanaRan naiyandri maTRi lEnthanja maagavE.
288.meaning: He is the deeds of sorrow-bringing pain and pleasure, and yet He is not those; the supreme Light standing on high; He who swallowed and spat out the seven worlds; the bitter poison to the servants of Yama, making them waver; Achyuta; the son of Dasaratha (Rama) - none other than Him do I have as my refuge.

289.translit:
thanja maagiya thanthai thaayodu thaanu maayavai allanaay,
enja lilama rarku lamudal moovar thammuLLu maathiyai,
anji neerula kaththuL Leergal avani vanendru koozhEnmin,
nenji naalninaip paanya vanavan aagum neeLkadal vaNNanE.
289.meaning: He is the refuge-giving father and mother, and He Himself, and yet He is not these; the source of the flawless host of immortals, the first among the three; O you of this world, do not waver in fear, saying 'is it this one or that one' - whomever one thinks of in the heart, that He becomes, the one hued like the vast ocean.

290.translit:
kadalvaN NankaNNan viNNa varkkaru maaNik kamena thaaruyir
padava ravina Naikki dantha paranju darppaNDu nooTRuvar,
adava rumpaDai manga aivarkkaT kaagi venjamaththu, andruthEr
kadavi yaperu maankanaikazhal kaaNba thendrukol kaNgaLE?
290.meaning: The ocean-hued Krishna, the rare ruby of the celestials, my precious life; the supreme Light reclining upon the hooded serpent's couch; the Lord who, of old, for the sake of the five Pandavas drove the chariot in the fierce battle so that the army of the hundred Kauravas perished - when, oh when, will my eyes behold the sounding anklets of His feet?

291.translit:
kaNgaL kaaNDaR kariya naaykkarutth thukku nandrume Liyanaay,
maNkoL nyaalathu yirkkel laamaruL seyyum vaanava reesanai,
paNkoL sOlai vazhuthi naadan kurugaik kOnsaDa kOpansol,
paNkoL aayiratth ippath thaalpaththa raagak koodum payaluminE.
291.meaning: He is hard for the eyes to behold yet very easy to the heart, the Lord of celestials who bestows grace upon all the lives of the earth-bearing world. Learn well these ten of the melodious thousand verses of Satakopa, king of Kurugur in the Pandya land of song-filled groves, so that you may join in becoming His devotees.

292.translit:
payilum sudaroLi moorthiyaip pangayak kaNNanai,
payila iniyanam paaTkadal sErntha paramanai,
payilum thiruvuDai yaaryava rElum avarkkaNDeer,
payilum piRappidai thOTRemmai yaaLum paramarE.
292.meaning: He is the form of dense radiant effulgence, the lotus-eyed one, the Supreme One who reposes in our Milk-Ocean, sweet to dwell upon constantly. Behold, whoever they may be that constantly possess devotion to Him - they themselves are the supreme masters who rule over us through every birth we pass.

293.translit:
aaLum paramanaik kaNNanai aazhip piraandrannai,
thOLumOr naankudaith thoomaNi vaNNanem maandrannai
thaaLum thaDakkaiyum kooppip paNiyum avarkkaNDeer,
naaLum piRappidai thORu emmai yaaLudai naatharE.
293.meaning: The ruling Supreme One, Krishna, the Lord of the discus; our Master of pure-gem hue with four shoulders - behold, those who fold His feet and broad hands in worship and bow are themselves the masters who own and rule us in every birth, day after day.

294.translit:
naathanai nyaalamum vaanamum Eththum naRunthuzhaayppO
thanai, ponnenduny chakkaratth enthai piraandrannai
paatham paNiyaval laaraip paNiyum avarkkaNDeer,
Othum piRappi Dai thORemmai yaaLudai yaarkaLE.
294.meaning: The Lord whom both earth and heaven praise; the fragrant tulasi blossom; our Father-Lord with the great golden discus - behold, those who bow to the ones able to bow at His feet are themselves the masters who own and rule us in every birth that is spoken of.

295.translit:
uDaiyaarntha vaaDaiyan kaNDikai yanu Dai naaNinanpu
Daiyaarppon noolinan ponmuDi yanmaTRumpal kalan,
naDaiyaa vuDaiththiru naaraNan thoNDarthoN DarkkaNDeer,
iDaiyaar piRappi DaithORemak kemperu makkaLE.
295.meaning: He wears a fitting garment, a string of beads, a waist-girdle; about His waist a golden sacred thread, a golden crown, and many other ornaments - behold, the servants of the servants of holy Narayana whose sway ever endures are our great masters through every birth between.

296.translit:
perumakka LuLLavar thamperu maanai, amararkaTka
rumai yozhiya andraaramu thooTTiya appanai,
perumai pithaTRaval laaraip pithaTRum avarkkaNDeer,
varumaiyu mimmaiyum nammai yaLikkum piraakkaLE.
296.meaning: The Lord of the great ones; the Father who, removing the rarity for the immortals, fed them the nectar of old - behold, those who babble in praise of those who can babble of His greatness are themselves the lords who grace us both in the life to come and in this present life.

297.translit:
aLikkum paramanai kaNNanai aazhip piraandrannai,
thuLikkum naRungaNNith thoomaNi vaNNanem maandrannai,
oLikkoNDa sOthiyai uLLaththuk koLLum avarkkaNDeer,
salippindri yaaNDemmaich sanmasan maantharang kaapparE.
297.meaning: The grace-bestowing Supreme One, Krishna, the Lord of the discus; our Master of pure-gem hue with a fragrant dripping garland; the effulgence radiant with light - behold, those who take Him into their hearts will, without weariness, rule and protect us through birth after birth.

298.translit:
sanmasan maantharang kaaththaDi yaarkaLaik koNDupOy,
thanmai poRuththiththan thaaLiNaik keezhkkoLLumappanai,
thonmai pithaTRaval laaRaip pithaTRum avarkaNDeer,
nammai peRuththemmai naaLuyyak koLkindra namparE.
298.meaning: The Father who guards His devotees through birth after birth, bears with their nature, and takes them up beneath His twin feet - behold, those who babble in praise of those who can babble of His ancient glory are themselves our Lords who obtain us and day by day take us up to be saved.

299.translit:
nambanai nyaalam padaiththava naithiru maarbanai,
umbar ulaginil yaarkkum uNarvari yaandrannaik,
kumbinarakargaL Eththuva rElum avarkaNDeer,
empal piRappidai thORem thozhukulam thaangaLE.
299.meaning: The trustworthy one; He who created the world; the one upon whose chest Lakshmi dwells; He who is hard for any in the higher worlds to comprehend - behold, even were they the dwellers of the Kumbhi hell, should they praise Him, they themselves are the clan we worship in every one of our many births.

300.translit:
kuvalandaangu jaadhigaL naalilum keezhizhindhu, etthanai
nalandhaan ilaadha chaNDaaLa chaNDaaLargaL aagilum,
valandhaangu chakkaratthu aNNal maNivaNNaRku aaL endruL
kalandhaar, adiyaar tham adiyaar em adigaLe.
300.meaning: Even if they have fallen lower than the four castes that bear the earth, even if they are the lowest of the low without a trace of any goodness — yet if they have wholeheartedly joined as servants of the Lord, the gem-hued one who bears the discus of victory in his hand, then the servants of those devotees, their servants, are our masters.

301.translit:
adiyaarndha vaiyam uNDu aalilai annaJ cheyyum,
padi yaadhum il kuzhavippadi endhai piraan thanakku,
adiyaar adiyaar tham adiyaar adiyaar thamakku
adiyaar adiyaar tham, adiyaar adiyOngaLe.
301.meaning: To my Father, my Lord, who swallowed the vast earth and lay as an infant upon a banyan leaf, a form beyond all comparison — we are the servants of the servants of the servants of His servants, the servants of the servants of His devotees' devotees.

302.translit:
adiyOngu nooRRavar veeya andru aivarukku aruL cheydha
nediyOnai, then kurugoorch chadagOpan kuRREvalgaL,
adiyaarndha aayiratthuL ivai patthu avan thoNDarmEl
mudivu aara kaRkil, sanmam cheyyaamai mudiyumE.
302.meaning: These ten among the thousand humble verses of Satakopan of beautiful southern Kurugur — verses of service to the towering Lord who destroyed the hundred Kauravas and showed grace to the five Pandavas in that battle — if learned thoroughly and dedicated to His devotees, will bring an end to the cycle of births.

303.translit:
mudiyaane moovulagum thozhudhEtthum cheer
adiyaane, aazh kadalai kadaindhaay puL oor
kodiyaane, koNDal vaNNaa aNDatthu umbaril
nediyaane, endru kidakkum en nenjamE.
303.meaning: O crowned one! O glorious-footed one whom the three worlds bow and praise! O you who churned the deep ocean! O you who bear the bird (Garuda) on your banner! O cloud-hued one! O the tall one who is beyond, above the cosmos! — thus my heart lies pining, calling out to you.

304.translit:
nenjamE neeL nagar aaga irundha en
thanjanE, thaN ilangaikku iRaiyai cheRRa
nanjanE, gnaalam koLvaan kuRaL aagiya vanjanE,
ennum eppOdhum en vaasagamE.
304.meaning: O my heart that has become my dwelling, my great refuge! O poison to the lord of cool Lanka, whom you destroyed! O cunning one who became a dwarf to seize the whole earth! — thus my speech at all times keeps uttering.

305.translit:
vaasagamE Etthа aruL cheyyum vaanavar tham
