naayaganE, naaL iLandhingaLai kOL vidutthu,
vEy agam paal veNNey thoduvuNDa aanaayar
thaayavanE, endru thadavum en kaigaLE.
305.meaning: O Lord of the celestials, who graciously lets my speech praise you! O young crescent moon of the day, who freed the moon from affliction! O cowherd, dear as a mother, who scooped and ate butter within the bamboo huts! — thus my hands grope, reaching out for you.

306.translit:
kaigaLaal aara thozhudhu thozhudhu unnai,
vaigalum maatthiraippOdhum Or veedu indri,
pai koL paambu ERi uRai paranE, unnai
mey koLLa kaaNa virumbum en kaNgaLE.
306.meaning: O Supreme One who reposes mounted upon the hooded serpent! Worshipping you again and again with folded hands, every day without a moment's respite — my eyes long to behold you, to grasp your very form in truth.

307.translit:
kaNgaLaal kaaNa varungol endru aasaiyaal,
maN koNDa vaamanan ERa magizhndhu chel,
paN koNDa puLLin chiRagoli paaviththu,
thiN koLLa Orkkum kidandhu en chevigaLE.
307.meaning: Longing whether he might come to be seen with the eyes — the Vamana who took the earth, the one upon whom Garuda joyfully rides and goes — my ears lie firmly attentive, imagining and straining to catch the melodious sound of the wings of that bird.

308.translit:
chevigaLaal aara nin keertthi kani ennum
kavigaLE kaalappaN thEn uRaippa thuRRu,
puviyinmEl ponnedun chakkaratthu unnaiyE
avivindri aadharikkum enadhu aaviyE.
308.meaning: Filling my ears to the full, the very poems that are the ripe fruit of your fame, dripping with the honey of seasoned melody — my soul cleaves to them. O you of the golden, mighty discus, my soul cherishes you alone, without cease, here upon this earth.

309.translit:
aaviyE, aaramudhE ennai aaLudai,
thooviyam puLLudaiyaay! sudar nEmiyaay!
paaviyEn nenjam pulamba pala kaalum,
kooviyum kaaNa peREn una kOlamE.
309.meaning: O my life-breath! O sweet nectar who possesses me as your servant! O you who own the beautiful-feathered bird! O you of the blazing discus! Though my sinful heart laments and cries out to you again and again over long ages, I have not been able to behold your divine form.

310.translit:
kOlamE thaamaraik kaNNadhu Or anjana
neelamE, nindru enadhu aaviyai eerginRa cheelamE,
chendru chollaadhana mun nilaam kaalamE,
unnai en naaL kaNDu koLvanE?
310.meaning: O beauty with lotus eyes! O dark blue radiance like collyrium! O nature that abides and rends apart my very soul! O time of those things that stand before me unable to be uttered in words! — on what day shall I behold and attain you?

311.translit:
koLvan naan maavali moovadi thaa endra
kaLvanE, kanjanai vanjitthu vaaNanai
uL vanmai theera, Or aayiram thOL thuNittha
puL vallaay, unnai en gnaandru porundhuvanE?
311.meaning: O deceiver who said to Mahabali "Give me three feet of land — I will take it"! O you who outwitted Kamsa, and who, to break the inner strength of Banasura, severed his thousand arms — O master of the bird (Garuda)! — on what day shall I be united with you?

312.translit:
porundhiya maamarudhin idai pOya vem
perundhakaay, un kazhal kaaNiya pEdhuRRu,
varundhi naan vaasaga maalai koNDu unnaiyE
irundhirundhu etthanai kaalam pulambuvanE.
312.meaning: O great one who passed between the twin marudu trees that stood joined together! Bewildered with longing to behold your feet, I suffer, and with garlands of words I cry out to you alone — for how long must I sit and sit and lament?

313.translit:
pulambu cheer poomi aLandha perumaanai,
nalam koL cheer nan kurugoorch chadagOpan, chol
valam koNDa aayiratthuL ivaiyum Or patthu,
ilanguvaan yaavarum ERuvar chonnaalE.
313.meaning: Of the Lord of resounding glory who measured the earth, Satakopan of good Kurugur, rich in auspicious excellence — these too are ten among his thousand verses of eloquent power; whoever recites them will all ascend to the shining heaven.

314.translit:
chonnaal virOdham idhu aagilum cholluvEn kENminO,
en naavil in kavi yaan oruvarkkum kodukkilEn,
thennaa thenaa endru vaNDu mural thiruvEngadatthu,
en aanai en appan emperumaan uLanaagavE.
314.meaning: Though it may bring opposition if I say it, still I will say it — listen! The sweet poetry on my tongue I will not give to any other person, so long as my Lord exists — my elephant, my Father, our Master who dwells at Tiruvenkatam where the bees hum 'thennaa thenaa.'

315.translit:
uLanaagavE eNNi thannai ondraaga than
chelvatthai vaLanaa madhikkum im maanidatthai kavi paadi en,
kuLanaar kazhani choozh kaNNan kuRunguidi meymmaiyE,
uLanaaya endhaiyai endhai pemmaanai ozhiyavE?
315.meaning: What use is it to compose poetry praising mere mortals — who think themselves to be something, who reckon their wealth as substance — abandoning my Father, the Lord of my Father, Kannan who truly dwells at Kurungudi surrounded by fields and ponds full of water?

316.translit:
ozhivu indri illaadha pal oozhi dhORoozhi nilaava, pOm
vazhiyai tharum nangaL vaanavar eesanai niRkappOy,
kazhiya miga nalla vaan kavi koNDu pulaveergaaL,
izhiya karudhi Or maanidam paadal ennaavadhE.
316.meaning: O poets! Leaving aside the Lord of the celestials, our God, who grants the path by which to abide forever through endless eons without cease — what comes of debasing yourselves by deliberately singing of some mere mortal, using exceedingly fine and lofty poetry?

317.translit:
ennaavadhu etthanai naaLaikku pOdhum pulaveergaaL,
mannaa manisarai paadi padaikkum perumporuL?,
minnaar maNimudi viNNavar thaadhaiyai paadinaal,
thannaagavE koNDu sanmam cheyyaamaiyum koLLumE.
317.meaning: O poets, what becomes of it — for how many days will the great wealth suffice that you gain by singing of impermanent men? But if you sing of the Father of the celestials, who wears a gem-crown flashing like lightning, He will accept it as offered to Himself and will grant freedom from being born again.

318.translit:
koLLum payanillai kuppai kiLartthanna chelvatthai,
vaLLal pugazhndhu num vaaymai izhakkum pulaveergaaL,
koLLa kuRaivilan vENDiRRellaam tharum kOdhil, en
vaLLal maNivaNNan thannai kavi cholla vamminO.
318.meaning: O poets who lose your truthfulness by praising as bountiful the wealth that is heaped up like a dunghill, with no benefit to be gained! Come, compose poetry on my flawless, generous, gem-hued Lord, who never runs short when one receives from him and grants all that is desired.

319.translit:
vammin pulaveer! num mey varutthi kai cheydhu uymminO,
im man nulagil chelvar ippOdhu illai nOkkinOm,
nummin kavi koNDu num numittaa dheyvam Etthinaal,
chemmin chudarmudi en thirumaalukku chErumE.
319.meaning: Come, O poets! Toil your bodies, do your work, and save yourselves! We have looked — there are no wealthy patrons in this enduring world now. If with your own poetry you praise some god you yourselves have set up, that praise will only reach my Tirumal of the red, gleaming, radiant crown.

320.translit:
seerum koDai pukazh ellaiyilaanai, oraayiram
paerum uDaiya piraanaiyallaal maTTRu yaankilaen,
maariyanaiya kai maalvaraiyokkum thiN thoaLenRu,
paaril oar paTTRaiyaip pacchaip pasumpoaykaL veyavae.
320.meaning: Save for the Lord whose generosity and fame are boundless, and who possesses a thousand names, I will praise no other. To say of some petty man here on earth that his hand is like the rain-cloud and his firm shoulders like a great mountain is nothing but raw, green falsehood heaped upon falsehood.

321.translit:
veyin malipurai thoaLi pinnaikku maNaaLanai,
aaya perumpukazh ellaiyilaadana paaDip poay,
kaayam kazhitthu avan thaaLiNaik keezhp pukum kaadalan,
maaya manisarai en solla vallaen en vaaykoNDae?
321.meaning: I yearn to sing the boundless great fame of Him who is the bridegroom of Pinnai whose arms are like full lustrous bamboo, and so to cast off this body and enter beneath His two feet. With this mouth of mine, what could I possibly say in praise of deceptive mortal men?

322.translit:
vaaykoNDu maaniDam paaDa vandha kaviyaen allaen,
aaykoNDa seer vaLLal aazhippiraan enakkae uLan,
saaykoNDa immaiyum saadhitthu vaanavar naaTTaiyum,
nee kaNDu koL enRu veeDum tharum ninRu ninRae.
322.meaning: I am not a poet who came with a mouth to sing of men. The generous Lord of choicest qualities, who bears the discus, belongs to me alone. He grants me this present world made firm, and saying "Behold and take for yourself the realm of the celestials," He bestows liberation too, again and again.

323.translit:
ninRu ninRu pala naaL uykkum ivvuDal neengip poay,
senRu senRaakilum kaNDu sanmam kazhippaan eNNi,
onRi onRi ulakam paDaitthu aangu kaviyaayinaeRku,
enRum enRum ini maTTRoruvarkku kaviyaeRkumae?
323.meaning: Determined to leave this body that lingers and drags on through many days, and however far I must go to reach and behold Him and so end the cycle of births, I have become wholly the poet of Him who, uniting all, created the world. Will I ever, ever again become a poet for anyone else?

324.translit:
aeRkum perumpukazh vaanavar eesan kaNNan thanakku,
aeRkum perumpukazh vaN kurukoorch chaDakoapan sol,
aeRkum perumpukazh aayiratthuL ivaiyum oarppatthu
aeRkum perumpukazh solla vallaarkku illai sanmamae.
324.meaning: To Kannan, Lord of the celestials, to whom great fame is fitting; the words of Satakopan of bounteous Kurukur, to whom great fame is fitting; these too among the thousand verses of fitting great fame, this set of ten of fitting great fame. For those able to recite them, there is no rebirth.

325.translit:
sanmam pala pala seydhu veLippaTTuch changoDu chakkaram vil,
oNmai uDaiya ulakkai oL vaaL thaNDu koNDu puL oornthu, ulakil
vanmai uDaiya arakkar asurarai maaLap paDaiporudha,
nanmai uDaiyavan seerp parava peTTRa naan oar kuRaivilanae.
325.meaning: Taking many births and appearing forth, bearing the conch, discus, bow, the radiant mace-club, the bright sword and staff, riding upon the bird (Garuda), He waged war with His weapons to destroy the mighty demons and asuras of the world. Having been blessed to extol the glory of that gracious Lord, I lack for nothing at all.

326.translit:
kuRaivil thaDangaDal koaLaravu aeRith than koalach chendhaamaraik kaN,
uRaibavan poala oar yoaku puNarndha oLimaNi vaNNan kaNNan,
kaRaiyaNi mookkuDaip puLLik kaDaavi asuraraik kaayndha ammaan,
niRaipukazh aetthiyum paaDiyum aaDiyum yaan oru muTTilanae.
326.meaning: Mounting the mighty serpent (Adisesha) upon the boundless wide ocean, with His lovely red-lotus eyes He abides as though in deep yogic union, the radiant gem-hued Kannan. He is the Lord who drove the stain-marked-beaked bird (Garuda) and destroyed the asuras. Praising, singing, and dancing in His full glory, I want for nothing.

327.translit:
muTTil pal poagatthu oru thani naayakan moovulakukku uriya,
kaTTiyaith thaenai amudhai nanpaalaik kaniyaik karumpu thannai,
maTTavizh thaNNandhuzhaaymuDiyaanai vaNangi avan thiRatthup
paTTa pinnai, iRaiyaakilum yaan en manatthup parivilanae.
327.meaning: He is the unique sovereign of unceasing and manifold bliss, the Lord of the three worlds, sweet as jaggery, honey, nectar, good milk, ripe fruit and sugarcane, crowned with cool fragrant tulasi from which nectar drips. Having bowed to Him and fallen wholly into His service, I have not the slightest sorrow in my heart, not even for a moment.

328.translit:
parivinRi vaaNanaik kaatthum enRu anRu paDaiyoDum vandhedhirndha thiripuram
seTTRavanum makanum pinnum angiyum poar tholaiya,
porusiRaip puLLaik kaDaaviya maayanai aayanaip poRchakkaratth
ariyinai, achchudhanaip paTTRi yaan iRaiyaenum iDarilanae.
328.meaning: On that day, to protect Bana saying he would shield him, even Rudra who burned the three cities came with his army to oppose Krishna, along with his son and Agni too, and their battle was undone. He is the wondrous one (Maya), the cowherd (Ayan), who drove the bird with battling wings, who bears the golden discus, the lion among men, Achyuta. Having clung to Him, I have not the least trouble.

329.translit:
iDarinRiyae oru naaL oru poazhdhil ellaa ulakum kazhiya,
paDarppukazhp paartthanum vaidhikanum uDan aeRath thiN thaerk kaDavi,
suDaroLiyaay ninRa thannuDaich choadhiyil vaidhikan piLLaikaLai,
uDaloDum koNDu koDutthavanaip paTTRi onRum thuyarilanae.
329.meaning: Without any difficulty, in a single moment of a single day, going beyond all the worlds, He drove the strong chariot bearing both the renowned Arjuna and the brahmin together. From within His own radiant effulgence that stood as luminous splendour, He brought back the brahmin's children with their very bodies and restored them. Having clung to Him, I have not a single sorrow.

330.translit:
thuyaril suDaroLi thannuDaich choadhi ninRa vaNNam niRkavae,
thuyaril maliyum manisar piRaviyil thoanRik kaN kaaNa vandhu,
thuyarangaL seydhu nan theyva nilai ulakil puka uykkum ammaan,
thuyaramil seerk kaNNan maayan pukazh thuTTRa yaan oar thunbamilanae.
330.meaning: While His own untroubled, brilliant, effulgent splendour stands as it is, the Lord appears taking birth among sorrow-laden mortals, coming so as to be seen by the eye. Performing wondrous deeds, He leads souls to enter the good divine realm in His world. Immersed in the glory of sorrowless, gracious Kannan the wondrous one, I have not a single grief.

331.translit:
thunbamum inbamum aakiya seyvinaiyaay ulakangaLumaay,
inbamil vennarakaaki iniya nal vaan suvarkkangaLumaay,
manpal uyirkaLumaaki palapala maaya mayakkukaLaal,
inburum ivviLaiyaaTTuDaiyaanaip peTTRu aedhum allalilanae.
331.meaning: He becomes the deeds that yield sorrow and pleasure, and the worlds; He becomes the joyless burning hell and the sweet good heavens; He becomes the many lasting souls. Through many and varied wondrous illusions He delights in this sport of His. Having attained Him, I have no distress whatsoever.

332.translit:
allalil inbam aLaviRandhu engum azhakamar soozhoLiyan,
alli malarmakaL poaka mayakkukaL aakiyum niRkum ammaan,
ellaiyil njaanatthan njaanam aqdhae koNDu ellaak karumangaLum sey,
ellaiyil maayanaik kaNNanaith thaaL paTTRi yaan oar thukkamilanae.
332.meaning: His is sorrowless bliss beyond all measure, surrounded everywhere by encompassing beauteous radiance. He is the Lord who stands as the very raptures of union with the lotus-born Lakshmi. He is of boundless knowledge, and with that knowledge alone He performs all deeds. Having grasped the feet of that boundless wondrous one, Kannan, I have not a single grief.

333.translit:
thukkamil njaanach chuDaroLi moortthi thuzhaayalangkal perumaan,
mikkapan maayangaLaal vikirtham seydhu vaeNDum uruvu koNDu,
nakkapiraanoaDu ayan mudhalaaka ellaarum evaiyum, thannuL
okka oDunga vizhungavallaanaip peTTRu onRum thaLarvilanae.
333.meaning: He is the form of sorrowless knowledge and effulgent radiance, the great Lord garlanded with tulasi. By many surpassing wonders He works transformations and takes on whatever form He wills. He is able to swallow into Himself, all together and equally, all beings and all things, including the naked Lord (Siva) and Brahma and the rest. Having attained Him, I have not the least weakness.

334.translit:
thaLarvinRiyae enRum engum parandha thanimudhal njaanam onRaay,
aLavuDai aimpulankaL aRiyaavakaiyaal aruvaaki niRkum,
vaLaroLi eesanai moortthiyaip boodhangaL aindhai iru suDarai,
kiLaroLi maayanaik kaNNanaith thaaL paTTRi yaan enRum kaeDilanae.
334.meaning: He is the sole primal cause, unfailing, ever spread everywhere, single undivided knowledge; He stands formless in a way the limited five senses cannot grasp. He is the Lord of growing radiance, the embodied one, the five elements, the two luminaries (sun and moon). Having grasped the feet of that brilliantly radiant wondrous one, Kannan, I shall never perish.

335.translit:
kaeDil vizhuppukazhk kaesavanaik kurukoorch chaDakoapan sonna,
paaDal oar aayiratthuL ivai patthum payiTTRa vallaarkaTku, avan
naaDum nakaramum nankuDan kaaNa nalaniDai yoorthi paNNi,
veeDum peRutthith than moovulakukkum tharum oru naayakamae.
335.meaning: For those able to practise these ten among the thousand songs sung by Satakopan of Kurukur upon Kesava of imperishable, exalted fame, He grants that they behold well His realm and city, makes their journey pass amid bliss, and bestows liberation, granting them sole sovereignty over His three worlds.

336.translit:
oru naayakamaay oaDa, ulakuDan aaNDavar,
karunaay kavarndha kaalar sidhaikiya paanaiyar,
perunaaDu kaaNa immaiyilae picchai thaam koLvar,
thirunaaraNan thaaL kaalam peRach chindhitthu uymminoa.
336.meaning: Those who once ruled the whole world as sole sovereigns, in time fall to having their legs seized and torn by black dogs and to bearing broken begging-bowls; in this very life, for the great country to see, they take to begging. So meditate in good time upon the feet of holy Narayana and be saved.

337.translit:
uymmin thiRai koNarndhu enRu ulakaaNDavar, immaiyae
thammin suvai maDavaaraip piRar koLLath thaam viTTu
vemmin oLiveyil kaanakam poayk kumai thinbarkaL,
semmin muDith thirumaalai viraindhu aDi saerminoa.
337.meaning: Those who ruled the world commanding "Bring tribute," in this very life leave behind their sweeter-than-themselves delicate women for others to take, and going off into the forest under the harsh blazing sun they suffer torment. So make haste and reach the feet of Tirumal of the radiant crown.

338.translit:
aDisaer muDiyinaraaki arasarkaL thaam thozha,
iDisaer murasangaL muTTRatthu iyamba irundhavar,
poDisaer thukaLaayp poavarkaL aadhalin nokkena
kaDisaer thuzhaaymuDik kaNNan kazhalkaL ninaiminoa.
338.meaning: Those who sat as kings, their crowned heads touched by other kings bowing to their feet, with thunder-like drums sounding in their courtyards, will all turn into mingling dust and pass away. Therefore, swiftly think upon the feet of Kannan whose crown bears fragrant tulasi.

339.translit:
ninaippaan pukil kaDal ekkalin nuNmaNalil palar,
enaitthu oar ukangaLum ivvulakaaNDu kazhindhavar,
manaippaal marungaRa maaydhal allaal maTTRuk kaNDilam,
panaitthaaL madhakaLiRu aTTavan paadham paNiminoa.
339.meaning: If one tries to count them, those who through countless ages ruled this world and then passed away are more numerous than the fine grains of sand cast up by the sea. Yet we see nothing of them but their perishing utterly, their homes and lineage gone. So bow at the feet of Him who slew the rutting elephant with pillar-like legs (Kuvalayapida).

340.translit:
paNimin tiruvaruL ennum-an seetap paimpoom-paLLi,
aNimen kuzhalaar inpak-kalavi amutuNDaar,
tuNimunpu naalap-pal-leezhaiyar taamizhip-pacchelvar,
maNiminnu meeninam maayavan peersolli vaazhminoo.
340.meaning: Bow down and seek His grace, says the Lord whose lovely cool resting place is fresh and flower-laden. Those wealthy ones who tasted the nectar of blissful union with soft-tressed beautiful women, and the many wretched fools who chase such pleasures, will all be cast down in due course. Live well by uttering the names of our wondrous Lord (Maayavan) whose body gleams like a jewel.

341.translit:
vaazhntaarkaL vaazhntatu maamazhai mokkuLin maayntumaayntu,
aazhntaaren rallaal anru mutalin raruti-yaa,
vaazhntaarkaL vaazhntee nirparen patillai nirkuril,
aazhntaar kaDal-paLLi aNNal aDiyavar aaminoo.
341.meaning: Those who seemed to flourish flourished only like bubbles in heavy rain, perishing again and again and sinking away, with neither lasting beginning nor end. There is no truth in saying that those who lived will remain living forever. If you would truly endure, become the devotees of the Lord who reclines upon the deep ocean.

342.translit:
aamin suvaiyavai aaroDaDisil-uND aarntapin,
toomen mozhi-maDavaar-irakkap-pinnum turruvaar,
eemin emakkoru tur renriDaruva raatalin,
koomin tuzhaay-muDi aatiyan-cooti kuNangaLee.
342.meaning: Having eaten their fill of rice with the six delicious tastes, and though pure-spoken tender women plead with them, they go on gorging more; in the end they will stagger about begging, 'Give us even one mouthful!' Therefore take up instead the qualities of the primordial radiant Lord who wears the tulasi crown.

343.translit:
guNangoL niraipukazh mannar-koDaikkaDan pooNDiruntu,
iNangi yulaku-uDan aakkilum aangkavan-aiyillaar,
maNangoNDa koopattu manniyum-meeLvarkaL meeLvillai,
paNangoL aravaNai yaan-tiru naamam paDiminoo.
343.meaning: Even kings endowed with virtue and full renown, who take up the duty of generous giving and unite the whole world in harmony, if they do not possess that Lord, they remain bound in the fragrant fury of pride and must return again; there is no release. Learn well the holy name of Him who reclines upon the hooded serpent.

344.translit:
paDimannu palkalan parroDaruttu-aim pulanvenru,
ceDimannu kaayam-cerraarkaLu maangkavan-aiyillaar,
kuDimannu min-suvarkkam eytiyum-meeLvarkaL meeLvillai,
koDimannu puLLuDai aNNal kazhalkaL kurukuminoo.
344.meaning: Even those who have cut away with their roots all the many ornaments fixed in this world, conquered the five senses, and destroyed the body that is a thicket of attachment, if they do not possess that Lord, though they attain the shining heaven of settled abode they must return; there is no release. Draw near to the feet of the Lord who bears the bird on His banner.

345.translit:
kuruka mikavuNar vattoDu nookkiyel laamviTTa,
iruka lirappennum njaanikkum appaya nillaiyeel,
ciruka ninaivatoor paacam-uNDaam-pinnum veeDillai,
marupakalil eesanaip parri viDaaviDil veeDahtee.
345.meaning: Even the wise one who, gazing with heightened awareness drawn near, has renounced all and calls it the cessation of birth-bondage, if he does not gain that fruit, there yet remains a subtle bond of thought and so no liberation. But if, on the very next occasion, he grasps the Lord and does not let go, that itself is liberation.

346.translit:
ahtee uyyap pukumaarenru kaNNan kazhalkaLmeel,
koypoom pozhilsoozh kurukoorc-caDakoopan kurreeval,
ceykoolat taayiram seerttoDaip-paaDal ivaipattum,
ahkaamar karpavar aazhtuyar pooyuyyar paalaree.
346.meaning: Saying 'That alone is the way to be saved,' Satakopan of Kurugur, ringed by groves of pluckable flowers, rendered loving service upon the feet of Kannan and fashioned a beautiful garland of a thousand glorious verses. Those who learn these ten without diminishing are fit to be saved, their deep sorrow gone.

347.translit:
paalanaa yeezhulaku uNDu parivinri,
aalilai yannava sancceyyum aNNalaar,
taaLiNai meelaNi taNNan tuzhaayenree
maalumaal, valvinai yeenmaDa valliyee.
347.meaning: My delicate creeper-like girl, sunk in grievous karma, swoons crying only 'the cool beautiful tulasi that adorns the twin feet' of the Lord who, as an infant, swallowed the seven worlds and effortlessly performed His slumbering rest upon a banyan leaf.

348.translit:
vallisheer nuNNiDai yaaycciyar tammoDum,
kollaimai ceytu kuravai piNaintavar,
nallaDi meelaNi naaru tuzhaayenree
sollumaal, soozhvinai yaaTTiyeen paavaiyee.
348.meaning: My doll-like daughter, beset by encircling karma, speaks only of 'the fragrant tulasi that adorns the good feet' of Him who played wild and joined hands in the kuravai dance with the slender-waisted, creeper-like cowherd women.

349.translit:
paaviyal veedanan maalai palakoNDu,
teevarkaL maamuni varirainj-caninra
seevaDi meelaNi semporr-tuzhaayenree
koovumaal, kooLvinai yaaTTiyeen kootaiyee.
349.meaning: My garland-like girl, caught in seizing karma, cries out only of 'the reddish-gold tulasi that adorns the sacred red feet' before which the gods and great sages stand bowing, taking up many fine garlands of metrical Veda.

350.translit:
kootila vaNpukazh koNDu samayikaL,
peetangaL sollip pitarrum piraan-paran,
paatangaL meelaNi paimporr-tuzhaayenree
ootumaal, oozhvinai yeentaDan tooLiyee.
350.meaning: My broad-shouldered girl, bound by destined karma, recites only of 'the fresh-gold tulasi that adorns the feet' of the supreme Benefactor about whom adherents of various creeds, taking up His faultless bounteous fame, babble forth their differing doctrines.

351.translit:
tooLisheer pinnai poruTTerutu eezh-tazheei-k
kooLiyaar koovala naarkkuDak koottanaar,
taaLiNai meelaNi taNNan-tu zhaayenree
naaLunaaL, naikinra taal-en-tan maataree.
351.meaning: For the sake of Nappinnai of fair shoulders He embraced and subdued the seven fierce bulls; the lord of the cowherds, the dancer of the pot-dance, day after day my daughter wastes away, crying only of 'the cool beautiful tulasi that adorns His twin feet.'

352.translit:
maatarmaa maNmaDan taiporuT-Teenamaay,
aatiyang kaalat takaliDam keeNDavar,
paatangaL meelaNi paimporr-tuzhaayenree
ootum-maal, eytinaL en-tan maDantaiyee.
352.meaning: For the sake of the great lady Earth-goddess He became a boar and in the primordial time clove up the wide world; my young daughter has come to recite only of 'the fresh-gold tulasi that adorns His feet.'

353.translit:
maDantaiyai vaNkama lattiru maatinai,
taDangoLtaar maarpinil vaittavar taaLin-meel,
vaDangoLpoon taNNan tuzhaaymalark-keeyivaL
maDangumaal, vaaNuta leer!-en maDakkompee.
353.meaning: He set upon His garland-bearing broad chest the lady Lakshmi, the divine consort born of the bounteous lotus; for the strung, cool, beautiful flowering tulasi upon His feet this girl pines and droops, O bright-browed friends, this tender bough of mine!

354.translit:
kompupool seetai poruTTilang kainakar
amperi yuyttavar taaLiNai meelaNi,
vampavizh taNNan-tu zhaaymalark-keeyivaL
nampumaal, naanitar kenceykeen nangaimeer.
354.meaning: For the sake of Sita, slender like a bough, He sent fiery arrows to burn the city of Lanka; for the fragrance-bursting cool beautiful flowering tulasi upon His twin feet this girl yearns; what shall I do for this, O ladies?

355.translit:
nangaimeer. neerum-oor peNperru nalkineer,
engngnanee sollukeen yaanperra eezhaiyai,
sangennum cakkara mennum tuzhaayennum,
ingngnanee sollum iraappakal enceykeen?
355.meaning: O ladies, you too have borne a daughter and raised her, so how shall I tell you of the helpless girl I bore? 'The conch,' she says, 'the discus,' she says, 'the tulasi,' she says, thus she speaks night and day; what shall I do?

356.translit:
enceykeen ennuDaip peetaiyen koomaLam,
ensollum envasa mumallaL nangaimeer,
mincey-pooN maarpinan kaNNan kazhal-tuzhaay,
poncey-pooN menmulaik kenru meliyumee.
356.meaning: What shall I do for my innocent girl, my tender one? Whatever she may say, she is no longer in my control, O ladies. For the tulasi upon the feet of Kannan, whose chest bears glittering ornaments, my daughter with gold-adorned soft breasts wastes ever away.

357.translit:
meliyunooy teerkkum-nang kaNNan kazhalkaLmeel,
malipukazh vaNkuru koorc-caDa koopan-sol,
olipukazh aayirat tippattum vallavar,
malipukazh vaanavark kaavar-nar koovaiyee.
357.meaning: Upon the feet of our Kannan, who cures the wasting malady, Satakopan of bounteous Kurugur of abounding fame uttered this; those who master these ten of the resonant thousand of glory will become a fine string of jewels for the celestials of abounding fame.

358.translit:
koovai vaayaaL poruTTeerrin eruttam iruttaay, matiL-ilangkaik
koovai veeyac cilaikunittaay! kulanal yaanai maruppocittaay,
poovai veeyaa neertoovip pootaal vaNangee neelum,nin
poovai veeyaam meenikkup poosum saanten nencamee.
358.meaning: For the sake of her whose lips are like the kovai fruit You broke the necks of the bulls; You bent Your bow so that the chiefs of walled Lanka perished; You snapped the tusk of the noble proud elephant. Though I do not sprinkle pure water nor worship with flowers, my heart is the sandal-paste smeared upon Your dark kaya-flower-hued body.

359.translit:
poosum saanten nencamee punaiyum kaNNi enatuDaiya,
vaasa kamcey maalaiyee vaanpaT-Taatai yum-ahtee,
teesa maana aNikalanum enkai kooppuc ceykaiyee,
eesan njaala muNDumizhnta entai yeeka moorttikkee.
359.meaning: For the Lord, my Father, the singular Form who swallowed and gave back the world: my heart is the sandal-paste I smear on Him, my heart the wreath I deck Him with; my eloquent words are the garland for Him, and that too His fine silk robe; the radiant adorning jewel and the bright raiment are nothing but the cupping of my hands in worship.

360.translit:
Eeka moorththi irumoorththi moondru moorththi pala moorththi
Aaki, aindhu boodhamaay irandu sudaraay aruvaaki,
Naagam eeri naduk-kadaluL thuyindra Naaraayanaane, un
Aakam mutrum agath-thadakki aavi yallal maayththadhe.
360.meaning: You who became the single Form, the twofold Form, the threefold Form and the manifold Form, who became the five elements, who became the two luminaries (sun and moon), and who became the formless one — O Narayana who reclines in the midst of the ocean upon the serpent! You drew my entire being inward and destroyed the suffering of my soul.

361.translit:
Maayththal enni vaay-mulai thandha maayap-pey uyir
Maayththa, aaya maayane! Vaamanane! Maadhavaa,
Pooththan maalai kondunnaip podhaal vananggeen-eelum, nin
Pooththan maalai nedumudikkup punaiyum kanni enadhuyire.
361.meaning: O wondrous Mayan who destroyed the life of the deceitful demoness who, intending to kill you, gave you her poisoned breast! O Vamana! O Madhava! Even though I do not worship you with flowers, offering cool fresh blossoms, you are the garland and you are my very soul, fit to be strung upon your tall crown adorned with cool blossoming garlands.

362.translit:
Kanni yenadhuyir kaadhal kanagach-chodhi mudi-mudhalaa,
Ennil pal-kalan-galum eelum aadai yum-aqdhe,
Nanni moovulagum naviltrum keerththi yum-aqdhe,
Kannan em-piraan emmaan kaala-chakkarath-thaanukke.
362.meaning: My soul, my love, and that garland are all for him — for Kannan, my Lord, my master, who holds the wheel of Time. From the golden radiant crown down, the countless ornaments and the befitting raiment, all that is for him; and the fame that the three worlds draw near and proclaim, that too is all for him.

363.translit:
Kaala-chakkaraththodu ven-sang-gam kai yendhinaay,
Nyaala mutrum undumiindha Naaraayanane endrendru,
Olam ittu naan-aiaiththaal ondrum vaaraay-aagilum,
Kola maamen sennikkun kamalam anna kurai-kaiale.
363.meaning: O you who hold in your hands the wheel of Time and the white conch! Even though, when I cry out aloud calling again and again 'O Narayana who swallowed the entire earth and brought it forth again!', you come not at all — your lotus-like sounding ankleted feet are still the splendid adornment for my head.

364.translit:
Kurai kaialgaL neettiman konda kola Vaamanaa,
Kurai kaialkai kooppuvaargaL koodanindra maayane,
Virai-koL poovum neerum-kondeththa maatteen-eelum, un
Urai-koL sodhith thiruvuruvam ennadhaavi meladhe.
364.meaning: O splendid Vamana who stretched out your sounding ankleted feet and took the earth! O Mayan who stand united with those who join their hands at your sounding ankleted feet! Even though I cannot worship you with fragrant flowers and water, your radiant sacred form, celebrated in praise, rests upon my soul.

365.translit:
Ennadhaavi meelaiyaay eerkoL eelu lagamum,
Thunni mutrum aaginindra sodhi nyaana moorththiyaay,
Unnadhennadhaaviyum ennadhunnadhaaviyum
Inna vannameenindraay endruraikka valleene?
365.meaning: You who are above my soul, who pervade and have become wholly the beautiful seven worlds, standing as the radiant Form of knowledge — your soul is mine and my soul is yours. How am I able to declare that you stand in just this manner?

366.translit:
Uraikka valleen alleenun ulappil keerththi veLLaththin
Karaikkan endru selvan-naan? Kaadhal mayyal eerineen,
Puraippil-aadha param-parane! Poyyil-aadha paranjudare,
Iraiththu nalla meen-makkaL eeththa yaanum eeththineen.
366.meaning: I am not able to speak; when shall I reach the shore of the flood of your endless fame? I have risen into the madness of love. O flawless Supreme of the supreme! O imperishable Supreme Light free of all falsehood! As the good and noble ones swell forth in praise, I too have praised you.

367.translit:
Yaanum eeththi eeulagum mutrum eeththi, pinnaiyum
Thaanum eeththilum-thannai eeththa eeththa engeydhum,
Theenum paalum kannalum amudhumaagith thiththippa,
Yaanum em-piraanaiye eeththineen yaan uyvaane.
367.meaning: Though I praise him, and all the seven worlds praise him, and again he himself praise himself, however much he is praised, where does the praise reach (its limit)? Growing sweet as honey, milk, sugar-cane and nectar, I too have praised my Lord, so that I myself may be redeemed.

368.translit:
Uyvu paayam matrinmai theerik Kannan on-kaialgaLmel
Seyya thaamarai-p-paialnath thennan kurugoor sadakopan,
Poyyil paadal aayiraththuL ivaiyum paththum vallaargaL,
Vaiyam manni veetrirundhu vinnum aaLvar mannoode.
368.meaning: Knowing for certain that there is no other means of redemption, Satakopan of Kurugur in the southern land of cool red-lotus fields, sang these upon the bright ankleted feet of Kannan. Those who can master these ten among the thousand songs free of falsehood will dwell firmly and exalted upon the earth, and even while on earth shall rule the heavens too.

369.translit:
Mannai irundhu thuiaavi, Vaamanan man idhu ennum,
Vinnaith thoiudhu, avan mevu vaiguntham endru kai kaattum,
Kannai uL-neer malka nindru, kadal-vannan ennum, anne! En
Pennaip peru-mayal seydhaarkku en seygen, pey vaLaiyeere?
369.meaning: She sits and gropes the earth, saying 'This is Vamana's earth.' She worships the sky and points with her hand, saying 'That is the Vaikunta where he abides.' With tears welling in her eyes she stands and says 'Ocean-hued one!' O mother! O you maidens wearing bangles, what shall I do for the one who has cast this great love-madness upon my daughter?

370.translit:
Pey-vaLaik kaigaLaik kooppip piraan-kidak kum-kadal ennum,
Seyyadhor nyaayitraik kaattich Sireedharan moorththiyee dhennum,
Naiyum-kan neer-malka nindru Naaranan ennum-anne, en
Dheyva vuruvil sirumaan seyginra thondrari yene.
370.meaning: Joining her bangled hands in worship, she says 'This is the ocean where the Lord reclines.' Pointing to a single red sun she says 'This is the form of Sridhara.' Languishing, with tears welling in her eyes, she stands and says 'Narayana!' O mother! I do not know one thing of what this little doe of divine form is doing.

371.translit:
Ariyum-sen theeyaith thaivi Achchudhan ennum-mey vevaaL,
Eriyum-than kaatraith thaivi ennudaik Govindan ennum,
Veri-koL thuiaay-malar naarum vinaiyudai yaattiyen petra
Seri-vaLai mun-kai sirumaan seyginra dhen-kannukk ondre?
371.meaning: Embracing the blazing red fire she says 'Achyuta!' though her body burns; embracing the cool blowing wind she says 'My Govinda!' She is fragrant with the scent of the fragrant tulasi flower. What this little doe with close-set bangles on her forearm, born to me the sinful one, is doing — is it but one thing to my eyes?

372.translit:
Ondriya thinggaLaik kaatti oLi-mani vannane ennum
Nindra-kundraththinai nokki nedumaale! Vaa endru koovum,
Nandru-pey yum-maiai kaanil Naaranan vandhaan endraalum,
Endrina mayalgaL seydhaar ennudaik komaLath thaiye.
372.meaning: Pointing to the full moon she says 'O one hued like a bright gem!' Looking at a standing hill she calls out 'O Nedumal, come!' If she sees rain pouring down well she says 'Narayana has come!' What kind of love-madness have they wrought upon my tender girl?

373.translit:
KomaLa vaan-kandraip pulgik Govindan meyththana ennum,
Pom-iLa naagaththin pin-poy avan-kidak kaiyee dhennum,
AamaLa vondrum ariyen aruvinai yaattiyen petra,
KomaLa valliyai maayon maal seydhu seyginra koothe.
373.meaning: Embracing a fine young calf she says 'Govinda grazed these.' Going behind a moving young serpent she says 'This is the place where he reclines.' I do not know one bit of how it will turn out. The dance that Mayon performs, casting love upon the tender creeper-like girl born to me the grievously sinful one!

374.translit:
Kooththar kudameduth thaadil Govindan aam enaa odum,
Vaaththa kuial-osai keetkil maayavan endrumai yaakkum,
Aaychchiyar venneygaL kaanil avan-unda venney-ee dhennum,
Peychchi mulai-suvaith thaarkken pen-kodi yeeriya piththe.
374.meaning: If dancers lift the pot and dance, she runs saying 'It is Govinda.' If she hears the sweet sound of the flute she swoons saying 'Mayavan!' If she sees the butter of the cowherd-women she says 'This is the butter he ate.' What is this madness that has mounted upon my tender vine of a girl, over him who sucked the breast of the demoness?

375.translit:
Eeriya piththino dellaa vulagum-kan nan-padaip pennum,
Neeru-sev veyidak kaanil nedumaal adiyaar en drodum,
Naaru thuiaay-malar kaanil Naaranan kanniyee dhennum,
Theriyum theraadhu maayon thiraththana Leyith thiruve.
375.meaning: With the madness that has mounted her, she says 'All the worlds are Kannan's creation.' If she sees sacred ash applied properly she runs saying 'These are the devotees of Nedumal.' If she sees the fragrant tulasi flower she says 'This is Narayana's garland.' Knowing and yet not knowing, this lovely girl is wholly devoted to the way of Mayon.

376.translit:
Thiruvudai mannaraik kaanil Thirumaalaik kandene ennum,
Uruvudai vannanggaL kaanil ulagaLan dhaan-endru thuLLum,
Karuvudaith thevilga Lellaam kadal-vannan koyile ennum
Veruvilum veeivilum ovaak Kannan kaialgaL virumbume.
376.meaning: If she sees kings possessing fortune she says 'I have seen Tirumal.' If she sees forms of beautiful hue she leaps up saying 'It is he who measured the worlds.' All the temples that hold an inner sanctum she calls 'the shrine of the ocean-hued one.' Whether in fear or in falling, ceaselessly she longs for the ankleted feet of Kannan.

377.translit:
Virumbip pagaivaraik kaanil viyal-idam undaane ennum,
Karum-peru meganggaL kaanil Kannan en dreerap parakkum,
Perum-pula aanirai kaanil piraan-uLan endru-pin sellum,
Arum-peral penninai maayon alatri ayarppik kindrane.
377.meaning: If she lovingly sees enemies she says 'O you who swallowed the vast world!' If she sees the great dark clouds she flies up saying 'Kannan!' If she sees a herd of cattle on the great pastureland she goes behind it saying 'The Lord is there!' Mayon makes my rare and precious girl wail and swoon.

378.translit:
Ayarkkum-sutr rum-patri nokkum agalave neeL nokkuk koLLum,
Viyarkkum maiaik-kan thuLumba vevvuyirk koLLum-mey sorum,
Peyarththum kannaa endru pesum, perumaane vaa-endru koovum,
Mayal-perung kaadhalen pedhaikk en-seygen val-vinai yene.
378.meaning: She swoons; she grasps all around and gazes; she takes a long, far-reaching gaze; she sweats, her rain-cloud eyes brimming over; she draws deep hot sighs; her body grows faint. Again she speaks, saying 'O Kanna!'; she calls out 'O great Lord, come!' For my naive girl of great love-madness, what shall I do, I of grievous sins?

379.translit:
Val-vinai theerkkum Kannanai van-kuru koorch-chada kopan,
Sol-vinai yaal-sonna paadal aayirath thuLLivai paththum,
Nal-vinai yendru-kar paargaL nalan-idai vaiguntham nanni,
Thol-vinai theerave laarum thoiudheia veetrirup paare.
379.meaning: These ten among the thousand songs that Satakopan of bounteous Kurugur composed by the craft of words upon Kannan who removes grievous deeds — those who learn them as a good deed will reach blessed Vaikunta, and with their ancient karma destroyed, all shall worship and rise and dwell there exalted.

380.translit:
veetrirun theezhulagum thanikkool sella, veevilseer,
aatralmik kaaLum ammaanaivemmaa piLanthaanthannai,
pootri yendrE kaigaLaarath thozhuthu solmaalaigaL,
Etra nootrEr kiniyennakuRai yezhumaiyumE?
380.meaning: He who sits enthroned, ruling all the seven worlds with His sole unequalled scepter, of flawless glory, the Lord of mighty power, who split open the cruel horse-demon — I worship Him to my heart's content with my hands, saying 'Praise!' and offering garlands of words. Having taken up this blessed vow of praising Him, what want is left for me, through all the seven births?

381.translit:
maiya kaNNaaL malarmEluRaivaa LuRaimaarbinan,
seyya kolath thadangkaNNan viNNOr perumaanthannai
moyya sollaa lisaimaalaigaLEththi yuLLap petrEn,
veyya nOygaL muzhuthum viyanjnaalaththu veeyavE.
381.meaning: On His chest dwells she of the dark, kohl-lined eyes who abides upon the lotus flower; He of the large, beautiful red eyes, the Lord of the celestials — I have attained to praise Him within my heart with garlands of melody woven from dense, fitting words, so that all my cruel maladies might perish from this vast world.

382.translit:
veevi linba migaellai nigazhntham achchuthan,
veevil seeran malarkkaNNan viNNOr perumaanthannai,
veevil kaalam isaimaalaigaL Eththi mEvappetrEn,
veevi linbamiga ellai nigazhnthanan mEviyE.
382.meaning: Our Achyuta, in whom unfailing bliss has reached its very limit; He of unceasing glory, the lotus-eyed one, the Lord of the celestials — through endless time I have attained to draw near Him, praising Him with garlands of melody. Drawing near Him, I too have come to dwell in bliss that has reached its very limit, never to fail.

383.translit:
mEvi nindru thozhuvaar vinaipOga mEvumbiraan,
thooviyam puLLu daiyaan adalaazhiyam maandrannai,
naaviya laalisai maalaiga LEththinaN NappetrEn,
aaviyen naaviyai yaanaRiyEnseytha vaatraiyE.
383.meaning: The gracious Lord who draws near so that the deeds of those who steadfastly worship Him may depart; He who rides the beautiful-winged bird, the Lord wielding the fierce, beautiful discus — I have attained to reach Him, praising Him with garlands of melody fashioned by my tongue. He is the very breath of my breath; I do not know the manner of what He has done for me.

384.translit:
aatra nalla vagaikaattum ammaanai, amarartham
Etrai yellaap poruLum viriththaanai emmaanthannai,
maatra maalai punainthEththi naaLum magizhveythinEn,
kaatrin munnam kadugi vinainOygaL kariyavE.
384.meaning: The Lord who shows the most excellent way in abundance, the chief of the immortals, who has expounded all the truths — my own Lord — I have praised, weaving Him garlands of words, and day after day attained joy, so that the maladies of my deeds, swifter than the wind, are burned away.

385.translit:
kariya mEnimisai veliya neeRusiRi thEyidum,
periya kOlath thadangkaNNan viNNOr perumaanthannai,
uriya sollaa lisaimaalaigaL Eththiyippap petrERku,
ariya thuNdO enakkindru thottumini yendrumE?
385.meaning: He who places a little white sacred ash upon His dark body, He of the large, splendid, broad eyes, the Lord of the celestials — for me who have attained to praise Him within my heart with garlands of melody in fitting words, is there anything hard to obtain, from this day onward and forever more?

386.translit:
endrum ondraagi yoththaarummik kaargaLum, thandranak
kindri nindraanai yellaavulagum udaiyaan thannai,
kundra mondraal mazhaikaaththapiraanaichchon maalaigaL,
nandru soottum vidhiyey thinam enna kuRainamakkE?
386.meaning: He who stands forever as the One, having none equal to Him nor any greater than Him; He who owns all the worlds; the gracious Lord who warded off the rain with a single hill — we have attained the blessed dispensation of adorning Him well with garlands of words. What want, then, is there for us?

387.translit:
namakkum poovinmisai nangaikkum inbanai, njaalaththaar
thamakkum vaanath thavarkkum perumaanai, thaNdaamarai
sumakkum paadhap perumaanaich chonmaalaigaL, sollumaa
Ramaikka vallERku iniyaavar nigaragal vaanaththE?
387.meaning: He who is bliss both to us and to the Lady upon the flower, the Lord of the people of the earth and of the dwellers in heaven, the Lord whose feet the cool lotus bears up — for me who am able to compose and recite garlands of words to Him, who now is my equal in the wide heaven?

388.translit:
vaanaththum vaanaththuL Lumbarum maNNuLLum maNNinkeezhth
thaanaththum, eNdisai yumthavi raathunin Raanthannai,
koonaRchang gaththadak kaiyavanaik kudamaadiyai vaanak
kOnaik, kavisolla vallERku inimaa RuNdO?
388.meaning: He who stands present without fail in the heaven, and in the regions beyond the heaven, and upon the earth, and in the realm beneath the earth, and in all the eight directions; He of the large hand bearing the curved conch, the dancer with the pots, the King of heaven — for me who am able to compose poems to Him, is there now any equal?

389.translit:
uNdum umizhnthum kadanthum idanthum kidanthumnindrum,
koNda kOlath thoduveet rirunthum maNangkoodiyum,
kaNda vaatraal thanakkE yulagena nindraanthannai,
vaNthamizh noorka nootrEn adiyaark kinbamaariyE.
389.meaning: He who swallowed the worlds and spat them forth, who strode across them, who scooped them up, who reclined and who stood, who sat enthroned in beautiful adornment, and who joined in wedlock — He who, by all the ways one beholds, stands as the world that belongs to Him alone — for the sake of a work in rich Tamil I have undertaken my vow, He who is a rain of bliss to His devotees.

390.translit:
maari maaraatha thaNNammalai vEngadath thaNNalai,
vaari vaaraatha paimpoom pozhilsoozh kurukoornagar,
kaari maaRan sadagOpan sollaayiraththippaththaal,
vEri maaraatha poomE liruppaaL vinaitheerkkumE.
390.meaning: On the Lord of cool, beautiful Venkatam hill where the rains never cease, sang Satakopan — Kari's son Maran — of Kurukur city girdled by fresh, lush groves where the floods never fail; through this ten of his thousand verses, She who abides upon the flower whose fragrance never fades will cut away one's deeds.

391.translit:
theerppaarai yaamini yenghanam naaduthum annaimeer,
Orppaalivv oNNuthal utranan nOyithu theRinOm,
pOrppaagu thaanseythan Raivarai velviththa, maayappOrth
theerppaaga naarkkivaL sinthai thuzhaaiththisaik kindrathE.
391.meaning: O mothers, how shall we now seek out those who could cure her? On reflection, we have discerned this — that this fair-browed girl has been seized by a goodly malady. Her mind is bewildered, wandering toward Him who, that day, made the strategy of war and caused the five to triumph, the charioteer of the wondrous battle.

392.translit:
thisaikkindra thEyivaL nOyithu mikka peruntheyvam,
isaippindri neeraNang kaadum iLantheyvam andrithu,
thisaippindri yEsangu sakkara mendrivaL kEtka,neer
isaikkitri raagilnan REyil peRumithu kaaNminE.
392.meaning: This her malady is indeed a bewilderment caused by the supreme, great Deity. It is not some petty deity for which you dance the dance of possession in disorder, without true song. If, without confusion, you will but sound to her ears the words 'conch and discus,' it will be well — see, she will be cured.

393.translit:
ithukaaNmin annaimeer. ikkattu vichchisoR koNdu,neer
ethuvaanum seythangOr kaLLum iRaichchiyum thoovElmin,
mathuvaar thuzhaaimudi maayap piraankazhal vaazhththinaal,
athuvE yivaLutra nOykkum arumarunthaagumE.
393.meaning: See this, O mothers. Heeding the words of this binding sorceress, do not go about doing whatever and scattering liquor and meat. If you but praise the feet of the wondrous Lord crowned with honey-dripping tulasi, that alone shall be the rare medicine for the malady that has befallen her.

394.translit:
marunthaagum endrangOr maaya valavaisoR koNdu,neer
karunjOrum matraich chenjOrum kaLanizhaith thenbayan?
orungaaga vEyula Ezhum vizhungi umizhnthitta,
perunthevan pErsolla kiRkil ivaLaip peRuthirE.
394.meaning: Heeding the words of some deceitful, cunning woman who says 'this will be the cure,' what is the use of your laying out a sacrificial ground with black rice and red rice as well? If you can but utter the name of the great Lord who, all at once, swallowed the seven worlds and spat them forth, you shall recover her.

395.translit:
ivaLaip peRumpari sivvaNang kaaduthal andranthO,
kuvaLaith thadangkaNNum kOvaichchevvaayum payanthanaL,
kavaLak kadaakkaLi RattabiraanThiru naamaththaal,
thavaLap podikkoNdu neerittidumin thaNiyumE.
395.meaning: The way to recover her is not, alas, this dancing of possession! Her broad lily-eyes and her red kovai-fruit lips have grown pale. By the holy name of the gracious Lord who slew the rutting tusker that ate its fodder by the mouthful, take the white sacred ash and apply it upon her — and she will be soothed.

396.translit:
thaNiyum pozhuthillai neeraNangaaduthir annaimeer,
piNiyum ozhiginra thillai perugu mithuvallaal,
maNiyin aNiniRa maayan thamaradi neeRukoNdu,
aNiya muyalinmat rillaikaNdeeriv vaNangukkE.
396.meaning: There is no moment of relief, O mothers, while you dance the dance of possession; nor does her sickness cease — rather it only grows the more. Save by taking the dust of the feet of the devotees of the Wondrous One whose beautiful hue is like a sapphire and striving to adorn her with it, there is no other way, behold, for this malady.

397.translit:
aNanguk karumarun thendrang kOr aadumkaL Lumbaraay
suNangai yeRinthunun thOLkulaikkappadum annaimeer,
uNangal kedakkazhu thaiyuthadaattamkaN denbayan?
vaNangeergaL maayap piraanThamar vEdhamval laaraiyE.
397.meaning: O mothers, saying 'this is the rare cure for the malady,' you dance and scatter liquor, hurling about the turmeric powder, and let your shoulders be shaken in possession. What use is it to watch, like the braying lip-shaking of a donkey, that spoils the drying grain? Rather, bow down to those versed in the Veda, the devotees of the wondrous Lord.

398.translit:
vEdhamval laargaLaik koNduviNNOrperu maanThirup
paadham paNinthu,ivaL nOyithu theerththukkoL LaathupOy
Etham paRainthalla seythukaLLoodu kalaaiththooy,
keetha muzhavittu neeraNang aaduthal keezhmaiyE.
398.meaning: Instead of taking those versed in the Veda and bowing at the holy feet of the Lord of the celestials, and so curing her of this malady — to go on uttering faults and doing improper things, mingling in liquor and scattering it, sounding song and drum, and dancing the dance of possession — that is base.

399.translit:
keezhmaiyinaa langOr keezhmaganitta muzhavinkeezh,
naazhmai palasolli neeraNangaadumpoy kaaNkilEn,
Ezhmaip piRappukkum sEmamin nOykkumee thEmarunthu,
oozhmaiyil kaNNabi raankazhal vaazhththumin unniththE.
399.meaning: I cannot bear to see this falsehood — your dancing the dance of possession to the drum set up basely by some base fellow, uttering many idle words. This is the safeguard for the births through all seven generations and this is the medicine for this malady: meditating in due order upon Krishna, the gracious Lord, praise His feet.

40.translit:
en-neermai kaNDirangi yidhuthagaa dhennaadha
en-neela mugilvaNNaR kenjoliyaan jollugeenoo
nan-neermai yiniyavar kaN thangaadhendru oruvaaych chol
nan-neela magandrilgaaL. nalgudhiroo nalgeeroo?
40.meaning: What shall I, helpless, say to my Lord whose hue is like the dark blue cloud, who will not look upon my plight, feel pity, and say 'this is unjust'? O lovely dark blue magandril (sheldrake) birds, will you not carry one word to him, that her tender nature can no longer endure your staying away? Will you grant me this favor, or will you not?

41.translit:
nalgiththaan kaaththaLikkum pozhilEzhum vinaiyeeRkE,
nalgaththaa naagaadhoo? naaraNanaik kaNDakkaal
malguneerp punalpaDappai iraidheervaN siRukurugE.
malguneerk kaNNeeRkOr vaasagangoN DaruLaayE.
41.meaning: He who graciously protects and sustains the seven worlds — can he not be gracious to me, a sinner? O bountiful little heron who seek your prey in the water-rich groves of brimming waters, when you see Narayana, bring back one word of grace for me, whose eyes brim with welling tears.

42.translit:
aruLaadha neeraruLi yavaraavi thuvaaraamun
aruLaazhip puTkaDaveer avarveedhi yorunaaL endru
aruLaazhi yammaanaik kaNDakkaa lidhusolli
yaruL aazhi varivaNDE. yaamumen pizhaith thoomE?
42.meaning: O gracious striped bee who roams the deep waters, fly upon his graceful discus-bird (Garuda) and grant the grace not yet granted, before her life dries away, saying 'come down his street one day.' When you see the Lord of the gracious discus, tell him this — and ask, what wrong have I committed?

43.translit:
enpizhaikOp padhupOlap panivaaDai yeergindradhu
enpizhaiyE ninaindharuLi yaruLaadha thirumaalaarkku
enpizhaiththaaL thiruvaDiyin thagavinuk kendru oruthavaaych chol
enpizhaikkum iLangiLiyE yaanvaLarththa neeyalaiyE?
43.meaning: The cold north wind cuts through me as if counting up my faults. O young parrot who survives by my faults, were you not the one I raised? Carry one word that endures to Tirumal, who dwells on my errors and withholds his grace — ask what wrong she did against the grace of his sacred feet.

44.translit:
neeyalaiyE siRupoovaay. neDumaalaark kendhoodhaay
nOyenadhu nuvalenna, nuvalaadhE yirundhozhindhaay
saayalodu maNimaamai thaLarndhEn naan iniyunadhu
vaayalagil innaDisil vaippaarai naDaayE.
44.meaning: Are you not the one, O little flowering bird, I sent as my messenger to the great Lord, telling you to speak my malady? Yet you stayed silent without telling. My grace and lovely golden complexion have wasted away; now go and seek out one who would place sweet food in your beak.

45.translit:
naaDaadha malarnaaDi naaLthOdrum naaraNandhan,
vaaDaadha malaraDikkeezh vaikkavE vaguggindru,
veeDaaDi veedrirudhal vinaiyatra thenseyvadhO?
ooDaaDu panivaaDaay. uraiththeeraay enaduDalE.
45.meaning: Seeking the rare unsought blossoms each day, he is ordaining to place me beneath the never-fading lotus feet of Narayana. What does liberation and dwelling exalted, freed of all karma, avail? O cold wind that pierces through, speak to my very body.

46.translit:
uDalaaDip piRappuvee Duyirmudhalaa mutrumaay,
kaDalaazhi neerthOtri yadhanuLLE kaNvaLarum
aDalaazhi yammaanaik kaNDakkaa lidhusolli
viDalaazhi maDanenjE. vinaiyOmondru aamaLavE.
46.meaning: He is all — the embodied births and liberation, the soul and its origin; he created the deep ocean waters and reclines asleep within them. O foolish heart, do not abandon (the path) — when you see the Lord of the fierce discus, tell him this, so long as we sinners remain one with him.

47.translit:
aLaviyandra Ezhulagath thavarperumaan kaNNanai
vaLavayalsoozh vaNkurugoorch chaDagOpan vaayndhuraiththa
aLaviyandra andhaadhi yaayiraththuL ippaththin
vaLavuraiyaal peRalaagum vaanOngu peruvaLamE.
47.meaning: Of the thousand antaadi verses, measureless, that Satakopan of bountiful Kurukur girded by fertile fields fittingly composed upon Kannan, the Lord of the beings of the boundless seven worlds — by the abundant recitation of these ten, one may attain the great heaven-soaring prosperity.

48.translit:
vaLavE zhulagin mudhalaay vaanO riRaiyai aruvinaiyEn
kaLavEzh veNNey thoDuvuNDa kaLvaa enban, pinnaiyum,
thaLavEzh muRuval pinnaikkaay vallaa naayar thalaivanaay,
iLavE REzhum thazhuviya endhaay enban ninaindhunaindhE.
48.meaning: I, of cruel karma, call him 'O thief who stole and ate the butter by stealth,' him who is the origin of the rich seven worlds and the Lord of the celestials. And again I call, melting and pining as I think: 'O my Father who, for the sake of jasmine-smiling Pinnai, became the chief of the cowherds and embraced the seven young bulls.'

49.translit:
ninaindhu naindhuL karaindhurugi, imaiyOr palarum munivarum,
punaindha kaNNi neersaandham pugaiyO dEndhi vaNangninaal,
ninaindha ellaap poruLgaTkum viththaay, mudhalil sidhaiyaamE,
mananjey nyaanath thunperumai maasoo NaadhO maayOnE.
49.meaning: O wondrous Lord, you are the seed of all things conceived. When the many celestials and sages, thinking and pining, melting within and dissolving, worship you bearing woven garlands, water, sandal-paste and incense — will your greatness, grasped by the mind through knowledge, ever be sullied, you who remain unchanged from the beginning?

50.translit:
maayO nigaLaay naDaikatra vaanOr palarum munivarum,
neeyO nigaLaip paDai endru niRainaan muganaip paDaiththavan
sEyO nellaa aRivukkum, thisaiga lellaam thiruvaDiyaal
thaayOn ellaa vevvuyirkkum thaayOn thaanO ruruvanE.
50.meaning: By his wondrous powers the many celestials and sages learned their ways; he created the full-faced four-headed Brahma, commanding 'create the wombs of beings.' He is far beyond all knowing; he measured all the directions with his sacred feet; he is the mother to all and every living being — he, the One of a unique form.

51.translit:
thaanO ruruvE thaniviththaayth thannil moovar mudhalaaya
vaanOr palarum munivarum matrum matrum mutrumaay
thaanOr peruneer thannuLLE thOtri adhanuL kaNvaLarum
vaanOr perumaan maamaayan vaigun thanem perumaanE.
51.meaning: He, of unique form, the singular seed, became the three foremost (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva) and the many celestials, the sages, and all else besides, and everything. He brought forth a great expanse of water and reclines asleep within it — the Lord of the celestials, the great wondrous one, our Lord of Vaikuntha.

52.translit:
maanEy nOkki maDavaaLai maarvil koNDaay maadhavaa
koonE sidhaiya vuNDaivil niRaththil theriththaay gOvindhaa!
vaanaar sOdhi maNivaNNaa. madhusoo dhaanee yaruLaay un
thEnE malarum thiruppaadham sEru maaRu vinaiyEnE.
52.meaning: O Madhava, you who bear upon your chest the tender lady (Lakshmi) of the doe-like glance! O Govinda, who broke the hump (of the hunchback woman) and shattered (the demons) with the strength of your pellet-bow! O sapphire-hued one of radiance that fills the heavens, O Madhusudana — grant your grace that I, the sinner, may reach your sacred feet that blossom like honeyed flowers.

53.translit:
vinaiyEn vinaitheer marundhaanaay viNNOr thalaivaa kEsavaa!
manaisE raayar kulamudhalE maamaa yannE maadhavaa!
sinaiyEy thazhaiya maraamarangaL Ezhum eydhaay sireedharaa!
inaiyaa yinaiya peyarinaay endru naivan aDiyEnE.
53.meaning: O Kesava, leader of the celestials, you became the medicine that cures the karma of me, the sinner! O Madhava, the origin of the household-dwelling cowherd clan, O great wondrous one! O Sridhara, who pierced the seven sturdy maramara trees with their flourishing branches! Calling 'you are such a one, you of such names,' I, your servant, melt away.

54.translit:
aDiyEn siRiya nyaanaththan, aRidha laarku mariyaanai
kaDisEr thaNNan thuzhaaykkaNNi punaindhaan thannaik kaNNanai
seDiyaar aakkai yaDiyaaraich chErdhal theerkkum thirumaalai
aDiyEn kaaNbaan alatruvan, idhanil mikkOr ayarvuNDE?
54.meaning: I, your servant of small knowledge, cry out longing to behold him who is hard for any to know — Kannan who wears the wreath of cool fragrant tulasi, Tirumal who severs his devotees' bondage to the body thick with the weeds (of karma). Is there any greater bewilderment than this?

55.translit:
uNDaa yulagEzh munnamE, umizhndhu maayai yaalpukku
uNDaay veNNey siRumanisar uvalai yaakkai nilaieydhi
maNDaan sOrndha dhuNDElum manisarkkaagum peer siRidhum
aNDaa vaNNam maNkaraiya neyyooN marundhO? maayOnE
55.meaning: O wondrous Lord, you swallowed the seven worlds of old, then spat them out; and through your maya you entered (the cowherd house) and ate the butter. Even if some clay was sucked in (when you ate earth as a child), having taken on a despised human-like body — is the eating of ghee a medicine so that no blemish whatsoever, such as comes to mortals, should come near you, melting away the clay?

56.translit:
maayOm theeya alava laip perumaa vanjap pEyveeya
thooyaguzhavi yaayviDappaal amudhaa amudhu seydhiTTa
maayan vaanOr thaniththalaivan malaraaL maindha nevvuyirkkum
thaayOn thammaa nennammaan ammaamoorththi yaichchaarndhE.
56.meaning: We shall not perish, having taken refuge in the great sacred Lord — the wondrous one who, as a pure infant, drank the poisoned milk as nectar so that the cruel deceitful demoness (Putana) perished; the sole leader of the celestials, the consort of the lotus-lady (Lakshmi), the mother to every living being, the Lord of lords, my Lord, the great holy form.

57.translit:
saarndha iruval vinaigaLum sariththu maayap patratruththu
theerndhu thanpaal manamvaikkath thiruththi veeDu thiruththuvaan,
aarndha nyaanach chuDaraagi agalam keezhmEl aLaviRandhu,
nErndha vuruvaay aruvaagum ivatri nuyiraam neDumaalE.
57.meaning: Driving away the two clinging karmas (good and bad), severing the bonds of maya, he reforms (the devotee) so the mind is set wholly upon him, and he prepares the path to liberation. He is the radiance of perfect knowledge, boundless above and below in his vastness; the great Lord who, becoming both the fitting embodied form and the formless, is the very soul of all these.

58.translit:
maalE maayap perumaanE maamaa yanE endrendru
maalE yERi maalaruLaal mannu kurugoorch chaDagOpan
paalEy thamizha risaikaarar paththar paravum aayiraththin
paalE paTTa ivaipaththum vallaark killai parivadhE.
58.meaning: Crying again and again 'O Tirumal, O wondrous Lord, O great enchanter,' overcome with rapturous love and by his grace, Satakopan of enduring Kurukur (composed these). Of the thousand verses praised by the milk-sweet Tamil scholars, the musicians, and the devotees, those who master these ten — for them there shall be no sorrow.

59.translit:
parivadhi leesanaip paaDi virivadhu mEva luRuveer
pirivagai yindrinan neerthooy purivadhu vumpugai poovE.
59.meaning: O you who wish to attain expansiveness by singing of the Lord who is free of all flaw — without any manner of separation, offer pure water, and let what you perform be incense and flowers.

400.translit:
unniththu maRRoru deyvam tozhaaL avanaiyallaal,
nummichchai solli num tooL kulaikkappadum annaimeer,
mannappadum maRai vaaNanai vaN tuvaraapati
mannanai, eettumin eettudalum tozhutu aadume.
400.meaning: O mothers, you reproach her and shake your shoulders speaking your own willful words, but she worships no other deity than Him. Praise instead the Lord of the Vedas who is firmly established, the King of bountiful Dwaraka; the moment you praise Him, she worships and dances in joy.

401.translit:
tozhutu aadi toomaNi vaNNanukku aatcheydu nooy teernda
vazhuvaada tol pukazh vaN kurukoorch chatakoopan, sol
vazhuvaada aayiratt uL ivai pattu veRikaLum,
tozhutu aadip paada vallaar tukka seelam ilarkale.
401.meaning: Those who can worship, dance and sing these ten frenzied verses, out of the flawless thousand composed by Satakopan of fertile Kurukur, whose ancient unfailing glory was cleansed of disease by serving and worshipping the Lord pure as a flawless gem, shall be free of all sorrow and grief.

402.translit:
seelam illaach chiRiyaneelum seyvinaiyoo peridaal,
ñaalam uNdaay ñaanamoorti naaraayaNaa enRenRu,
kaalan tooRum yaan irundu kaitalai poosalittaal
koola meeni kaaNa vaaraay kooviyum koLLaaye.
402.meaning: Though I am lowly and without virtue, my evil deeds are vast. O You who swallowed the earth, embodiment of knowledge, Narayana! Crying out thus at every hour, I sit and wail with my hand on my head, yet You do not come for me to behold Your beautiful form, nor do You even call and accept me.

403.translit:
koLLa maaLaa inba veLLam kotil tandidum, en
vaLLaleeyoo vaiyam koNda vaamanaavoo enRenRu,
naLLiraavum naN pakalum naan irundu oolamittaal,
kaLLa maayaa unnai en kaN kaaNa vandu eeyaaye.
403.meaning: O my generous Lord who freely grants a flood of bliss that never diminishes however much one draws from it! O Vamana who seized the world! Crying out thus through the dead of night and the broad daylight, when I sit and lament, O deceptive enchanter, You do not come and grant that my eyes may behold You.

404.translit:
eevu ilaada teevinaikaL ettanai seydanan kol?
taavi vaiyam koNda endaay daamodaraa enRenRu
koovik koovi neñjurukik kaN pani soora ninRaal,
paavi nee enRu onRu sollaay paaviyeen kaaNa vande.
404.meaning: What countless unending evil deeds have I committed? O my Father who strode and seized the world, O Damodara! Calling and calling thus, my heart melting and tears streaming from my eyes, I stand; yet You do not even come and say one word to me, the sinner, calling me a wicked one, that I might behold You.

405.translit:
kaaNa vandu ena kaN mukappee taamaraik kaN piRazha,
aaNi sempon meeniyendaay ninRaruLaay enRenRu,
naaNam illaach chiRu takaiyeen naan ing alaRRuvaden,
peeNi vaanoor kaaNamaattaap peetudai appanaiye?
405.meaning: "Come and stand graciously before my eyes, my Father with body of pure refined gold, Your lotus eyes glancing," thus I cry. Why do I, a shameless, paltry creature, keep babbling here for that majestic Father whom even the celestials, for all their longing, cannot behold?

406.translit:
appanee adal aazhiyaanee, aazh katalaik katainda
tuppanee, un tooLkaL naankum kaNdida kooDung kol enRu,
eppozhudum kaNNa neer koNd aavi tuvarndu tuvarndu,
ippozhudee vandidaay enRu eezhaiyeen nookkuvane.
406.meaning: O my Father, O wielder of the fierce discus, O mighty One who churned the deep ocean! "Will it ever come to pass that I behold all four of Your shoulders?" Always with tears welling in my eyes, my spirit withering and withering, I, helpless one, keep gazing and saying, "Come to me this very moment!"

407.translit:
nookki nookki unnaik kaaNbaan yaan enadu aaviyuLLee,
naakku neeLvan ñaanam illai naadooRum ennudaiya,
aakkai uLLoom aavi yuLLum alla puRattin uLLum,
neekkam inRi engum ninRaay ninnai aRindu aRinde.
407.meaning: Gazing and gazing to behold You within my own soul, day after day I stretch out my tongue (in longing), having no true knowledge; yet You stand everywhere without separation - within my body, within my soul, and beyond as well - though I know You again and again.

408.translit:
aRindu aRindu teeRit teeRi yaan enadu aaviyuLLee,
niRainda ñaana moorti yaayai ninmalam aaka vaittu,
piRandum settum ninRu idaRum peetaimai teerndu ozhindeen
naRun tuzhaayin kaNNi ammaa. naan unnaik kaNdu koNde.
408.meaning: Knowing You again and again, growing ever clearer and clearer, and enshrining You, the embodiment of full knowledge, immaculately within my own soul, I have ended and cast off the ignorance of being born and dying and stumbling on. O Lord adorned with the garland of fragrant tulasi, I have beheld and grasped You!

409.translit:
kaNdu koNd en kaikaL aara nin tirup paadangaL meel,
eN disaiyum uLLa pook koNd eetti ukandu ukandu,
toNdar oongaL paadi aadach choozh katal ñaalattuLLee,
vaN tuzhaayin kaNNi veende! vandida killaaye.
409.meaning: That, beholding and grasping You, my hands might be filled offering flowers from all eight directions upon Your sacred feet, praising You and rejoicing again and again, and that we Your servants might sing and dance - O King wearing the garland of bountiful tulasi, yet within this sea-girt earth You will not come!

410.translit:
idaki leenu onRu atta killeen aimpulan vella killeen,
kadavanaakik kaalan tooRum poop paRittu eetta killeen,
madavan neñjam kaadal koora valvinaiyeen ayarppaay,
tadavukinReen engu kaaNban chakkarat taNNalaiye?
410.meaning: I cannot give, nor can I cook (offerings), nor can I conquer the five senses; bound by duty I cannot pluck flowers and worship at every appointed hour. My foolish heart overflows with love, yet I of cruel deeds grow bewildered and grope about - where shall I behold the gracious Lord of the discus?

411.translit:
chakkarat taNNalee enRu taazhndu kaNNeer tatumba,
pakkam nookki ninRu alandeen paaviyeen kaaNkinRileen,
mikka ñaana moorti yaaya veeda viLakkinai, en
takka ñaanak kaNkaLaale kaNdu tazhuvuvane.
411.meaning: Crying "O gracious Lord of the discus!" I bowed low with tears brimming, looking all around as I stood and lamented; yet I, the sinner, cannot behold You. Him who is the embodiment of supreme knowledge, the very lamp of the Vedas - with the fitting eyes of true knowledge I shall behold and embrace You!

412.translit:
tazhuvi ninRa kaadal tannaal taamaraik kaNNan tannai,
kuzhuvu maadatt en kuru koor maaRan chatakoopan, sol
vazhuvilaada voN tamizhkaL aayiratt uL ippattum,
tazhuvap paadi aada vallaar vaikuntam eeRuvare.
412.meaning: Of the flawless, lovely Tamil thousand composed by Maran Satakopan of Kurukur with its clustered mansions, who sang of the lotus-eyed Lord with embracing, abiding love - those who can sing and dance these ten, embracing them, shall ascend to Vaikunta.

413.translit:
eeRaaLum iRaiyoonum disaimukanum tirumakaLum,
kooRaaLum taniyudamban kulangkulamaa asurarkaLai,
neeRaakum padiyaaka nirumittup padaitotta,
maaRaaLan kavaraada maNi maamai kuRaivilame.
413.meaning: The peerless-bodied One in whose form share Shiva who rides the bull, Brahma of the (four) faces, and Lakshmi; the matchless Lord who took up His weapon and ordained that the demon hordes, clan upon clan, be reduced to ashes - unless He claims it, I shall not lack my jewel-like fair complexion.

414.translit:
maNi maamai kuRaivillaa malar maadar uRai maarvan,
aNi maanat tadavaraittooL adal aazhit takkaiyan,
paNi maanam pizhaiyaamee adiyeenaip paNi koNda,
maNi maayan kavaraada mada neñjaal kuRaivilame.
414.meaning: He on whose chest dwells the lotus-born Lady, the One of beautiful, noble mountain-like shoulders and broad hand bearing the fierce discus, the sapphire-hued enchanter who took me, His servant, into service without failing in dignity of command - unless He claims it, I shall not lack my tender heart.

415.translit:
mada neñjaal kuRaivillaa makaL taay sey tu oru peycchi,
vida nañja mulai suvaitta miku ñaanach chiRu kuzhavi,
padanaakatt aNaik kidanda paru varaittooL paramburudan,
nedu maayan kavaraada niRaiyinaal kuRaivilame.
415.meaning: The little child of abounding wisdom who sucked the poisoned breast of the demoness who, playing the mother's part, came (to kill Him); the Supreme Person of broad mountain-like shoulders who reclines upon the hooded serpent couch, the great enchanter - unless He claims it, I shall not lack my womanly modesty.

416.translit:
niRaiyinaaR kuRaivillaa nedum paNaittooL madappinnai,
poRaiyinaal mulai aNaivaan poru vidai eezh adarttu ukanda,
kaRaiyinaar tuvar udukkai kadaiyaavin kazhi kool kai,
saRaiyinaar kavaraada taLir niRattaal kuRaivilame.
416.meaning: To embrace the breasts of gentle Nappinnai of long bamboo-like shoulders through endurance, He joyfully subdued the seven fighting bulls; He of the ochre garment, herdsman with cattle-driving staff in hand and anklets jingling - unless He claims it, I shall not lack my tender sprout-like hue.

417.translit:
taLir niRattaal kuRaivillaat tanich chiRaiyil viLappuRRa,
kiLi mozhiyaaL kaaraNamaak kiLar arakkan nakar eritta,
kaLi malart tuzhaay alangkal kamazh mudiyan kadal ñaalattu,
aLi mikkaan kavaraada aRivinaal kuRaivilame.
417.meaning: For the sake of the parrot-tongued lady held shining in solitary captivity, He burned the city of the surging demon (Ravana); He of fragrant crown adorned with a garland of joyous tulasi blossoms, abounding in grace over the sea-girt earth - unless He claims it, I shall not lack my (sound) understanding.

418.translit:
aRivinaal kuRaivillaa akal ñaalatt avar aRiya,
neRi ella meduttu uraitta niRai ñaanatt oru moorti,
kuRiya maaN uruvaakik kodung kooLaal nilang koNda,
kiRiyammaan kavaraada kiLar oLiyaal kuRaivilame.
418.meaning: The peerless embodiment of perfect knowledge who set forth and declared all the paths so that the people of the wide world might know them; the cunning Lord who, taking the form of the dwarf Brahmin, seized the earth by a stern stratagem - unless He claims it, I shall not lack my radiant lustre.

419.translit:
kiLar oLiyaal kuRaivillaa ari uruvaayk kiLarndu ezhundu,
kiLar oLiya iraNiyanada akal maarbam kizhindu ukanda,
vaLar oLiya kanal aazhi valampuriyan maNi neela,
vaLar oLiyaan kavaraada vari vaLaiyaal kuRaivilame.
419.meaning: He who surged up arising as the man-lion form of radiant lustre and joyfully tore open the broad chest of the resplendent Hiranya(kashipu); He who bears the blazing fiery discus and the right-whorled conch, of growing radiance and sapphire-blue hue - unless He claims it, I shall not lack my striped bangles.

420.translit:
varivaLaiyaal kuRaivillaap perumuzhakkaal adangaarai,
yeriyazhalam pugavoodhi yirunila mun thuyarthavirttha,
therivariya sivanpiraman amarar koan paNindhEththum,
viripugazhaan kavaraadha mEgalaiyaal kuRaivilamE.
420.meaning: With our striped bangles and our great loud roar we have no want; He who blew His enemies into the blazing fire and earlier removed the great earth's suffering, the One whom Siva and Brahma — hard to comprehend — and Indra, king of the immortals, bow down and praise, He of wide-spreading fame: by the girdle He does not take from us we suffer no lack.

421.translit:
mEgalaiyaal kuRaivillaa melivuRRa agalalgul,
pOgamagaL pugazhththandhai viRalvaaNan puyamthuNiththu,
naagamisaith thuyilvaanpOl ulagellaam nankodunga,
yOgaNaivaan kavaraadha vudambinaal kuRaivilamE.
421.meaning: By our girdle we have no want, though our broad slender waist has grown frail. He severed the arms of the valorous Bana, the praised father of the goddess of enjoyment; like one who sleeps upon the serpent so that all the worlds bow well, He who unites in yoga — by the body He does not take from us we suffer no lack.

422.translit:
udambinaal kuRaivillaa uyirpirindha malaiththuNdam,
kidandhanapOl thuNipalavaa asurar kuzhaam thuNiththuganda,
thadampunala sadaimudiyan thaniyorukoo RamarndhuRaiyum,
udambudaiyaan kavaraadha uyirinaal kuRaivilamE.
422.meaning: By our body we have no want. He rejoiced cutting the throng of demons into many pieces, so that they lay like sundered mountain-fragments from which life has departed; He in whose one body abides as a single half the matted-locked one with the broad waters (Siva) — by the life He does not take from us we suffer no lack.

423.translit:
uyirinaal kuRaivillaa ulagEzhthan uLLodukki,
thayirveNNe yuNdaanaith, thadanggurugoorch chadagOpan,
seyirilsol lisaimaalai yaayiraththu Lippaththaal
vayiramsEr piRappaRuththu vaigundham naNNuvarE.
423.meaning: By life we have no want. Of Him who drew the seven worlds within Himself and ate curds and butter, Satakopan of vast Kurugur sang this garland of flawless, melodious words. By this ten of the thousand, men shall sever their stubborn birth and reach Vaikuntha.

424.translit:
naNNaadhaar muRuvalippa nalluRRaar karaindhEngga,
eNNaaraath thuyarviLaikkum ivaiyenna ulagiyaRkai?,
kaNNaaLaa kadalkadaindhaay unagazhaRkE varumparisu,
thaNNaavaa thadiyEnaip paNigaNdaay saamaaRE.
424.meaning: While those who are estranged smile and well-wishers melt in grief, what is this nature of the world that breeds sorrows beyond counting? O my Lord, You who churned the ocean! Without delay command this servant on the way to die, so that I may come to Your holy feet.

425.translit:
saamaaRum kedumaaRum thamaruRRaar thalaiththalaippeydhu,
EmaaRik kidandhalaRRum ivaiyenna ulagiyaRkai?,
aamaaRon RaRiyEnnaan aravaNaiyaay. ammaanE,
koomaaRE viraigaNdaay adiyEnaik kuRikkoNdE.
425.meaning: Kinsmen and dear ones, falling one upon another over how men die and perish, lie deceived and cry aloud — what is this nature of the world? I know not a single way out. O You who rest on the serpent, O my Master, mark this servant and make haste to call me to You.

426.translit:
koNdaattum kulampunaivum thamaruRRaar vizhunidhiyum,
vaNdaarpoong kuzhalaaLum manaiyozhiya vuyirmaaydhal,
kaNdaaRREn ulagiyaRkai kadalvaNNaa adiyEnaip
paNdEpOl karudhaadhun adikkEkooyp paNikoLLE.
426.meaning: Celebrations, exalting of lineage, dear kinsmen, great wealth, the woman with bee-swarmed flowery tresses, and the home — all left behind, life perishes. I cannot bear to see this nature of the world, O ocean-hued One! Do not regard this servant as before; call me to Your feet and take me into Your service.

427.translit:
koLLenRu kiLarndhezhundha peruñselvam neruppaaga,
koLLenRu thamammoodum ivaiyenna ulagiyaRkai?
vaLLalE maNivaNNaa unagazhaRkE varumparisu,
vaLLalsey thadiyEnai unadharuLaal vaanggaayE.
427.meaning: The great wealth that rises up saying 'take, take' becomes fire, and darkness covers it saying 'take' — what is this nature of the world? O bountiful One, O sapphire-hued One! Be generous and, by Your grace, lift up this servant so that he may come to Your holy feet.

428.translit:
vaangguneer maluragil niRpanavum eedhi rivanavum,
aangguyirgaL piRappiRappup piNimoopaal thagarppuNNum,
eenggidhanmEl vennaragam ivaiyenna ulagiyaRkai?
vaanggenainee maNivaNNaa. adiyEnai maRukkElE.
428.meaning: In this flowering world of ebbing waters, things that stand fixed and things that move — there the living are crushed by birth, death, disease and old age, and beyond this lies cruel hell. What is this nature of the world? O sapphire-hued One, lift me out; do not refuse this servant.

429.translit:
maRukkival valaippaduththik kumaiththittuk konRuNbar,
aRapporuLai yaRindhOraar ivaiyenna ulagiyaRkai?
veRiththuLava mudiyaanE vinaiyEnai yunakkadimai
aRakkoNdaay, iniyennaa ramudhE.kooy aruLaayE.
429.meaning: They catch creatures in cruel snares, torment them, kill and eat them, and do not understand the law of righteousness — what is this nature of the world? O You crowned with fragrant tulasi! You have wholly taken me, a sinner, as Your servant. O my sweet nectar, now call me and grant Your grace.

430.translit:
aayEyiv vulagaththu niRpanavum thirivanavum
neeyEmaR RoruporuLum inRinee ninRamaiyaal,
nOyEmoop piRappiRappup piNiyEyen Rivaiyozhiyak,
kooyEkoL adiyEnaik koduvulagam kaattElE.
430.meaning: Since the things that stand and move in this world are all Yourself, and You alone stand with no other thing existing besides You — so that disease, old age, birth, death and sickness may all cease, call this servant and take him; do not show me this cruel world.

431.translit:
kaattinee karandhumizhum nilaneerthee visumbukaal,
eettteenee vaiththamaiththa imaiyOrvaazh thanimuttaik,
kOttaiyinil kazhiththenaiyun kozhuñsOdhi yuyaraththu,
koottariya thiruvadikka LeññaanRu koottudhiyE?
431.meaning: You who reveal, conceal and emit earth, water, fire, sky and wind — having gathered, set and shaped the unique egg-fortress wherein the immortals dwell, when will You free me from that fortress and unite me up in the height of Your rich radiance, at Your holy feet that are so hard to attain?

432.translit:
koottudhinin kuraikazhalgaL imaiyOrum thozhaavagai seydhu,
aattudhinee yaravaNaiyaay. adiyEnum aḥdhaRivan,
vEtkaiyellaam viduththenaiyun thiruvadiyE sumandhuzhala,
koottariya thiruvadikkaL koottinainaan kaNdEnE.
432.meaning: You make even the immortals worship Your resounding anklets and You drive them — O You who rest on the serpent! This servant too knows it. Having made me cast away all craving so that I bear and wander with Your holy feet alone, You have joined me to those holy feet so hard to attain — I have seen it!

433.translit:
kaNdukEt tuRRumOndhuNduzhalum ainggaruvi
kaNdavinbam, theivariya aLavillaach chiRRinbam,
oNdodiyaaL thirumagaLum neeyumE nilaaniRpak,
kaNdasadhir kaNdozhindhEn adaindhEnun thiruvadiyE.
433.meaning: The petty pleasures known through the five senses that see, hear, touch, smell and taste — boundless, hard to perceive, fleeting trifles. While Lakshmi of the bright bangles, the divine lady, and You alone shine forth, I have seen and renounced the cleverness of seeking those pleasures, and I have reached Your holy feet.

434.translit:
thiruvadiyai naaraNanaikkEsavanaip parañsudarai,
thiruvadisEr vadhukarudhich chezhungurugoorch chadagOpan,
thiruvadimE luraiththathamizh aayiraththu Lippaththum,
thiruvadiyE adaivikkum thiruvadisErn donRuminE.
434.meaning: Of the holy feet — of Narayana, of Kesava, of the supreme effulgence — desiring to reach those holy feet, Satakopan of fertile Kurugur uttered in Tamil. These ten too of the thousand spoken upon the holy feet will lead you to those very holy feet; reach the holy feet and become one with them.

435.translit:
onRun dhEvu mulagum uyirum maRRum yaadhumillaa
anRu, naanmugan thannodu thEva rulagO duyirpadaiththaan,
kunRam pOlmaNi maadam needu thirukkuru kooradhanuL,
ninRa aadhippi raanniRka maRRaith theivam naadudhirE.
435.meaning: On that day when not a single god, nor world, nor life, nor anything else existed, He created Brahma and with him the gods, the worlds and life. While that primordial Lord stands within Tirukkurugur of tall jewelled mansions like mountains, do you go seeking other gods?
