436.translit:
naadi neerva Nangum theivamum ummaiyu munpadaiththaan,
veedil seerppuga zhaadhippi raanavan mEvi yuRaikOyil,
maada maaLigai soozhndhazha gaaya thirukkuru kooradhanaip,
paadi yaadip paravich chelmingaL pallula geerparandhE.
436.meaning: The gods you seek out and worship, and you yourselves — He created them all; the Lord, the primordial One of unfading auspicious fame. His chosen abiding temple is fair Tirukkurugur, ringed with mansions and palaces. O dwellers of the many worlds, spread out, sing, dance, extol Him and go forth!

437.translit:
parandha theivamum pallula kumpadaith thanRuda nEvizhungik,
karandhu mizhndhu kadandhi dandhadhu kaNdum theLiyagilleer,
sirangga Lala maravaNangum thirukkuru kooradhanuL,
parandhiRa manRip pallulageer theivam maRRillai pEsuminE.
437.meaning: He created the manifold gods and all the worlds, and on that day swallowed them, then concealed and emitted them, spanned and lifted them up — though you have seen this, you do not become clear. Within Tirukkurugur where the immortals bow with their heads, O dwellers of the many worlds, apart from the Supreme One there is no other god — declare it!

438.translit:
pEsaninRasivanukkumpiramanthanakkum piRarkkum
naayaganavanE kapaalanan mOkkaththukkaNdukoNmin
thEsamaamadhiLsoorndhazhagaaya thirukkurugooradhanuL
eesanpaalOravambaRaidhal ennaavadhilinggiyarkkE?
438.meaning: He alone is the Lord of Siva of whom men speak, and of Brahma, and of all others; behold, He granted release in the matter of the skull. Within fair Tirukkurugur ringed with splendid great ramparts He abides as the Supreme. What use is it for the Linga-worshippers to spread empty babble against the Lord?

439.translit:
ilinggaththittapuraaNaththeerum samaNarumsaakkiyarum
valindhuvaadhuseyveergaLum maRRunundheivamumaagininRaan
malindhusennelkavariveesum thirukkurugooradhanuL
полindhuninRapiraankaNdeer onRum poyyillaipORRuminE.
439.meaning: You followers of the Puranas that set up the Linga, the Jains and the Buddhists, and you who force disputes — He has become your other gods too. Behold the Lord standing resplendent within Tirukkurugur where rich paddy waves like fly-whisks: there is no falsehood in this at all — praise Him!

440.translit:
pootri matroor deyvam peeNap purattiTTu ummai innee
teetri vaittadel leerum veeDu petraal ulagillai endree,
seetril sennel kamalam oongu tirukkuru kooradanuL,
aatra vallavan maayam kaNDeeradu arin darindu ooduminee.
440.meaning: You who praise and exalt other deities, casting Him aside, while saying that even were you all to attain release there would be no world left to honor them — behold the wondrous power of the Lord who is supremely capable, abiding in Tirukkurugur where amid the mire red paddy and lotuses rise tall. Knowing and knowing it again, run to Him.

441.translit:
oodi yoodip palpirappum pirandu matroor deyvam,
paaDi yaaDip paNindu palpaDikaal vazhi eerik kaNDeer,
kooDi vaanava reetta nindra tirukkuru kooradanuL,
aaDu puT koDi yaadi moorttik kaDimai puguvaduvee.
441.meaning: Running on and on through many births, singing and dancing in homage to other deities, you have climbed up by their paths step after step and seen — now behold! The true thing is to enter into the service of the primal Lord whose banner bears the dancing bird, who stands in Tirukkurugur where the celestials gather and praise Him.

442.translit:
pukku aDi maiyinaal tannaik kaNDa maarkkaNDeeyan avanai
nakkapiraanum andru uyyak koNDadu naaraayaNan aruLee
kokkalar taDan taazhai veelit tirukkuru kooradanuL
mikka aadippiraan nirka matrai deyvam viLambudiree.
442.meaning: Markandeya, who entered in and through service beheld Him — and that the Lord Shiva (the naked one) once saved that devotee was but the grace of Narayana. While the supreme primal Lord stands present in Tirukkurugur, hedged by broad screwpine where cranes settle among the blossoms, will you yet go on extolling other deities?

443.translit:
viLambum aaru samaya mum avai aagiyum matrum tanpaal,
aLandu kaaNDarku ariya naagiya aadippi raan amarum,
vaLangoL taN paNai soozhndu azhagaaya tirukkuru koorudanai,
uLangoL njaanattu vaimmin ummai uyyak koNDu poguRilee.
443.meaning: He is the primal Lord who, being Himself the six religions that are proclaimed, and all else besides, is yet impossible to measure or fully comprehend. If you would be saved and carried across, set in the knowledge held within your heart that beautiful Tirukkurugur, encircled by cool fertile fields, where He abides.

444.translit:
uruva daava dettevum evvulaka kaLum matrum tanpaal,
maruvil moortti yoDu ottit tanaiyum nindra vaNNam nirkavee,
seRuvil sennel karumboDu oongu tirukkuru kooradanuL
kuriya maaNu ruvaagiya neeL kuDak koottanuk kaaTcheyvadee.
444.meaning: The thing worth attaining is to render service to the dancer of the pot — He who, while every god and every world and all else stand each in their own measure together with His own flawless form, took on the small form of the dwarf (Vamana) and grew tall — abiding in Tirukkurugur where in the fields red paddy rises with the sugarcane.

445.translit:
aaTcheyda taazhippi raanai seerndavan vaN kuru koor nagaraan
naaT kamazh magizh maalai maarbinan maaRan saThakoopan,
veeTkai yaal sonna paaDal aayirat tuLip pattum vallaar,
meeTchi yindri vaikunda maa nagar matradu kaiyaduvee.
445.meaning: Maran Satakopan of bountiful Kurugur city, on whose chest is a magizh garland fresh-fragrant by day, drew near and clung to the Lord of the discus whom he served. Those who can master these ten among his thousand songs uttered out of longing will, without return, hold in their hand that great city of Vaikuntha.

446.translit:
kaiyaar chakkarat tem karu maaNikkamee endrendru,
poyyee kaimmai solli puramee pura mee yaaDi,
meyyee petr ozhindeen, vidi vaaykkindru kaappaa raar,
aiyoo kaNNapiraan araiyoo inip poonaalee.
446.meaning: Saying again and again, 'O our dark sapphire-gem with the discus filling His hand!' — yet uttering only false, empty words and wandering ever on the outside — I have nevertheless truly attained Him. When destiny comes to pass, who can hold Him back? Alas, O Lord Kannan — I declare it: if He goes now, will it not be over?

447.translit:
poonaay maamarudin naDuvee en pol laamaNiyee!
teenee innamudee! endrendree sila kootru solla,
taanee em perumaan avan ennaa giyozhindaan,
vaanee maanilamee matrum utrum en uLLanavee.
447.meaning: When I had spoken only a few stray words — 'O my flawless gem who passed between the two great marudu trees! O honey! O sweet nectar!' — He, of His own accord, our Lord, became wholly one with me. The heavens, the great earth, and all else besides — all became mine, dwelling within me.

448.translit:
uLLana matruLavaap puramee sila maayanj solli,
vaLLal maNivaNNanee. endrendree unai yum vanjikkum,
kaLLa manam tavirndee unaik kaNDu koNDu uy tozhindeen,
veLLat taNaik kiDandaay ini unnai viTTu eng koLvanee!
448.meaning: Treating outward things as though they were the real ones, speaking some illusory words, calling again and again 'O munificent Lord of sapphire hue!' — even You I would deceive. But now, leaving aside that deceitful mind, I have seen You, taken hold of You, and been saved. O You who recline upon the flood — having left You, where now could I ever find You again?

449.translit:
en koLvan unnai viTTu ennum vaasagang kaL solliyum,
van kaLva neen manattai valittuk kaNNa neer karandu,
nin kaN nerunga vaittee en taaviyai neekkagilleen,
en kaN malina maruttu ennaik koovi yaruLaay kaNNanee.
449.meaning: Though I speak the words 'Having left You, where shall I find You?', I, a hard-hearted thief, force my mind and hold back my tears, and having set myself close to You I cannot tear away my soul from You. O Kannan, cleanse the impurity of my eyes and call me to You and grant Your grace.

450.translit:
kaNNapi raanai viNNoor karu maaNikkat tai amudai,
naNNiyum naNNagilleen naDuvee oor uDambil iTTu,
tiNNa mazhundak kaTTip pala sey vinai van kayitraal,
puNNai maraiya varin denaip poora vaittaay puramee.
450.meaning: Lord Kannan, the dark sapphire-gem of the celestials, the nectar — even drawing near Him I cannot truly reach Him. Casting me into a body in between, binding me hard and fast with the strong rope of many a deed, You have, as it were, bandaged over my wound and set me wandering on the outside.

451.translit:
puramaRak kaTTik koNDu iru valvinai yaar kumaikkum,
muRaimuRai yaakkai pugal ozhiyak kaNDu koNDu ozhindeen,
niRamuDai naal taDan tooL seyyavaay seyya taamaraik kaN,
aRamuya laazhi yangaik karu meeni am maan tannaiyee.
451.meaning: I have beheld and laid hold of Him and so done — the dark-bodied Lord with four broad shapely shoulders bright in hue, red mouth, red lotus eyes, and the righteousness-driving discus in His lovely hand — so that the entering into body after body, by which my two fierce deeds bound me wholly and tormented me, has ceased.

452.translit:
am maa naazhippiraan avan ev viDat taan yaan aar,
em maa paaviyarkkum vidi vaaykkindru vaaykkum kaNDeer,
kaimmaa tunbu ozhittaay endru kaitalai poosaliTTee,
meymmaa laay ozhindeen em piraanum en meelaanee.
452.meaning: That great Lord of the discus — where is He, and who am I? Yet behold, when destiny ripens it comes to pass even for the most grievous sinners! Crying out with hand on head, 'O You who ended the elephant's distress!', I was wholly overcome with true devotion — and my Lord too inclined toward me, over me.

453.translit:
meelaat tevargaLum nilat tevarum meevit tozhum,
maalaar vandina naaL aDiyeen manattee manninaar,
seeleey kaNNiyarum perunj selvamum nan makkaLum,
meelaat taay tandaiyum avaree ini yaavaaree.
453.meaning: The great Tirumal, whom both the gods on high and the gods on earth come and worship adoringly, has on this day come and abided firmly in the heart of me His servant. Henceforth my wives with eyes like sael-fish, my great wealth, my good children, and my father and mother above — all these are He alone.

454.translit:
aavaa raar tuNai endru alai neerk kaDa luL azhundum
naavaay pool, piravik kaDal uL nindru naan tuLanga,
deevaar koolat toDum tiruchchakkaram sanginoDum,
aavaa vendru aruL seydu aDiyeenoDum aananee.
454.meaning: As I trembled, standing within the ocean of births like a ship sinking in the surging sea with none to ask 'Who is there to help?' — He, in divine splendid form with His sacred discus and conch, said 'Ah, ah!' in compassion, granted His grace, and became one with me His servant.

455.translit:
aanaan aaLuDaiyaan endru aqdee koNDu uganduvandu,
taanee in aruL sey dennai muttravum taan aanaan,
meenaay aamaiyumaay narasingamu maay kuRaLaay,
kaanaar enaamumaay kaRkiyaaminnam kaar vaNNanee.
455.meaning: Taking simply this — 'He has become mine, He owns me as His servant' — and delighting, He came of His own accord, granted His sweet grace, and became my very all. He of the dark cloud hue, who was Fish and Tortoise and Man-Lion and Dwarf, who was Rama of the forests, and who is yet to be Kalki.

456.translit:
kaarvaNNan kaNNapiraan kamalat taDang kaNNan tannai,
eervaLa voN kazhanik kurukoorch chaTa koopan sonna,
seervaNNa voN tamizhgaL ivai aayirat tuLip pattum
aarvaNNat taal uraippaar aDik keezh puguvaar polindee.
456.meaning: Of the cloud-hued Lord Kannan, the lotus-broad-eyed one, Satakopan of Kurugur with its bright beautiful fields sang these — these glorious bright Tamil verses, a thousand of them; whoever recites these ten among them with fervor will enter, resplendent, beneath His feet.

457.translit:
polika polika polika poyitru valluyirch chaabam,
naliyum naragamum nainda namanuk king yaadondru millai,
kaliyum keDum kaNDu koLmin kaDal vaNNan poodangaL maNmeel,
maliyap pugundisai paaDi yaaDi yuzhitarak kaNDoom.
457.meaning: Hail, hail, hail! Gone is the harsh curse upon souls; ruined too is the tormenting hell, and for Yama there is now nothing left here to do. The Kali age too shall perish — behold and know it! For we have seen the devotees of the ocean-hued Lord throng in great numbers upon the earth, entering and roaming about, singing and dancing.

458.translit:
kaNDoom kaNDoom kaNDoom kaNNuk kiniyana kaNDoom,
toNDeer elleerum vaareer tozhudu tozhudu nindraarttum,
vaNDaar taNNan tuzhaayaan maadavan poodangaL maNmeel,
paNDaan paaDi nindraaDip parandu tirigindranavee.
458.meaning: We have seen, we have seen, we have seen — we have seen what is sweet to the eyes! Come, all you devotees, come; let us worship and worship and stand and cry aloud. The devotees of Madhava, who wears the cool fragrant tulasi swarming with bees, are spread abroad over the earth, singing as of old, standing and dancing, and roaming everywhere.

459.translit:
tiriyum kaliyugam neengit tevargaL taamum pugundu,
periya kida yugam patrip peerinba veLLam peruga,
kariya mugilvaNNan emmaan kaDal vaNNan poodangaL maNmeel,
iriyap pugundisai paaDi engum iDangoN Danavee.
459.meaning: The whirling Kali age has departed, and the celestials themselves have entered in; the great Krita age has taken hold, and the flood of supreme bliss swells. The devotees of our dark-cloud-hued Lord, the ocean-hued One, have come surging upon the earth, singing so that all flees before them, and have taken their place everywhere.

460.translit:
iDangoL samayattai yellaam eDuttuk kaLaivana pola,
taDangaDal paLLip perumaan tannuDaip poothanga Leyaay
kiDandhum irundhum ezhundhum geetham palapala paaDi,
naDandhum paRandhum kunittum naaDagam seygin Ranave.
460.meaning: As if to uproot and cast away all the contending creeds that have spread everywhere, the devotees who belong to the Lord who reclines upon the vast ocean have become His very own people. Lying down, sitting up, and rising, they sing songs of many kinds. Walking, leaping, and dancing, they enact this drama of devotion.

461.translit:
seyginRa tenkaNNuk konRe okkinRa tivvula gattu,
vaikundhan poothanga Leyaay maayatthi naalengum manni,
aiyamon Rillai yarakkar asurar piRandheeruL Leerel,
uyyum vagaiyillai toNDeer! oozhi peyartthiDum konRe.
461.meaning: To my eyes everything that is done in this world appears as one and the same. Having become the very people of the Lord of Vaikuntha, they abide everywhere through His wondrous power. There is no doubt about it: O devotees, if you are born among the rakshasas and asuras, there is no way of being saved, for He will overturn and destroy the very aeons.

462.translit:
konRuyi ruNNum visaadhi pagaipasi teeyana vellaam,
ninRiv vulagil kaDivaan nemippi raantamar pondhaar,
nanRisai paaDiyum tuLLi yaaDiyum njaalam parandhaar,
senRu tozhuduymmin toNDeer sindhaiyaic chenni Ruttiye.
462.meaning: To drive away from this world all the evils that endure here, killing and devouring life, disease, enmity, hunger and the rest, the servants of the Lord who bears the discus have come forth. Singing fine melodies, leaping and dancing, they have spread over the whole earth. O devotees, go, worship them and be saved, fixing your minds firmly in righteousness.

463.translit:
niRutthinum uLLattuk koLLum deyvangaL ummaiyuy yakkoL
maRutthu mavanoDe kaNDeer maarkkaN Deyanum kariye
kaRutta manamonRum veNDaa kaNNanal laaldeyva millai,
iRuppadel laamavan moortthi yaayavark keyi Ruminе.
463.meaning: The deities you set up and hold in your hearts can save you only by being refused and turning to Him alone, see; even Markandeya is witness to this. Harbor no resentful or contrary mind: there is no god but Kannan. Whatever tribute you offer, offer it to those who are His own embodiments.

464.translit:
iRukku miRaiyiRutthuNNa evvula gukkumtan moortthi,
niRutthinaan deyvangaL aaga atteyva naayagan Raane
maRutthiru maarvan avanRan poothangaL geethangaL paaDi,
veRuppinRi njaalattu mikkaar mevit tozhuduymmi neere.
464.meaning: To receive the tribute that is due, He set up His own embodiments as the deities for every world, and He alone is the Lord of those deities. He bears Lakshmi on His glorious chest; without any aversion, the great ones throughout the earth, His people, sing songs of Him. Cleave to Him, worship Him, and be saved.

465.translit:
mevit tozhuduymmi neergaL vedhap punitha irukkai,
naaviRkoN Dacchudhan Rannai njaana vidhipizhai yaame,
poovil pugaiyum viLakkum saandhamum neerum malindhu
mevit tozhumaDi yaarum bhagavarum mikka tulage.
465.meaning: Cleave to Him, worship Him, and be saved, taking upon your tongue the sacred Vedic hymns of Achyuta without erring from the path of true knowledge. The world abounds where, with flowers, incense, lamps, sandal-paste and water in plenty, the devotees who cleave and worship and the holy ones flourish abundantly.

466.translit:
mikka vulagugaL toRum mevikkaN NanthiRu moortthi,
nakkabi raano Dayanum indhira nummudhalaaga,
tokka amarar kuzhaangaL engum parandhana toNDeer,
okkat tozhugiRRi raagil kaliyuga monRumil laye.
466.meaning: Throughout the many worlds the holy form of Kannan abides, and along with the naked Lord Shiva, Brahma, Indra and the rest, the gathered hosts of the gods have spread everywhere. O devotees, if you will worship Him together as one, there will be no Kali age at all.

467.translit:
kaliyuga monRumin RikkE tannaDi yaarkkaruL seyyum,
maliyum suDaroLi moortthi maayappi raankaNNan Rannai,
kalivayal tennan kurukoork kaarimaa RansaDa kopan,
olipuga zhaayirat tippattu uLLattai maasaRuk kumme.
467.meaning: Of Him who grants His grace to His own devotees so that there is no Kali age at all, the Lord of abounding radiant light, the wondrous one, Kannan, Satakopan, Karimaran of Kurukur in the southern land of fertile fields, sang. This decad of the renowned thousand cleanses the heart of all its stains.

468.translit:
maasaRu sodhiyen seyya vaaymaNik kunRattai
aasaRu seelanai aadhi moortthiyai naaDiye,
paasaRa veydhi yaRivizhan denaina Laiyam?,
esaRu mooravar kavvai tozhee. enseyyume?
468.meaning: Seeking the flawless radiance, my red-mouthed jewel-hill, the one of faultless character, the primordial Lord, I have lost my love-bonds and lost my senses too. For how many days must I be in this anguish? O friend, what can the gossip of these reviling townsfolk do to me?

469.translit:
enseyyu mooravar kavvai tozhee ininammai,
enseyya taamaraik kaNNa nennai niRaikoNDaan,
munseyya maamai yizhandhu meni meliveydhi,
enseyya vaayum karungaN Numpayap poorndhave.
469.meaning: What can the gossip of the townsfolk do to us now, O friend? The lotus-red-eyed Lord has carried off my modesty. Having lost my former rosy radiance and grown wan of body, even my red mouth and dark eyes have turned pallid and faded.

470.translit:
oorndha sagaDam udhaitthapaadhatthan, peymulai
saarndhu suvaittha sevvaayan ennai niRaikoNDaan,
perndhum peyarndhum avanoDanRiyor sollilen,
teerndhaven tozhee enseyyu mooravar kavvaiye?
470.meaning: He whose foot kicked the rolling cart, the one of the red mouth that drew near and sucked the demoness's breast, He has carried off my modesty. Turn and turn again, I can speak of no one but Him. O my devoted friend, what then can the gossip of the townsfolk do?

471.translit:
ooravar kavvai yeruviTTannaisol neermaDutthu,
eeranel vitthi muLaittha nenjap perunjeyyuL,
peramar kaadhal kaDalpuraiya viLaivittha,
kaaramar meninang kaNNan tozhee! kaDiyane.
471.meaning: Spreading the townsfolk's gossip as manure, pouring in the slander against me as water, sowing the moist seed and making it sprout in the great field of my heart, He has raised up a vast love deep as the ocean. Our Kannan of the dark cloud-like form, O friend, is a cruel one indeed.

472.translit:
kaDiyan koDiyan neDiyamaalula gangoNDa
aDiyan, aRivaru menimaayatthan, aagilum
koDiyaven nenjam avanenRe kiDakku melle,
tuDiko LiDaimaDat tozhee! annaiyen seyyume?
472.meaning: He is harsh, He is cruel, He is the great Tirumal whose feet measured the worlds, of a form past knowing, of wondrous magic. And yet, alas, my wretched heart lies fixed on Him alone. O tender friend of waist slender as a drum, what then can my mother do?

473.translit:
ennaiyen seyyilen oorenن sollilen tozhimeer,
ennai yiniyumak kaasai yillai yagappaTTen,
munnai yamarar mudhalvan vaNduva raapadhi
mannan, maNivaN NanvaasU dhevan valaiyuLe.
473.meaning: What does it matter what is done to me, what the town may say, what is spoken, O my friends? You need have no more longing for me; I have been caught fast. I am within the snare of the ancient first one of the gods, the king of bountiful Dvaraka, the sapphire-hued Vasudeva.

474.translit:
valaiyuL agappaTTut tennainan nenjam koovikkoNDu,
alaikaDal paLLi yammaanai aazhip piraantannai
kalaikoL agalalgul tozhee. namkaNga laalkaNDu
talaiyil vaNanga maangkolo taiyalaar munpe?
474.meaning: Caught in the snare, having called and carried off my good heart, the Lord who reclines on the surging sea, the Lord who bears the discus, O friend of broad robe-clad hips, shall we ever be able to behold Him with our own eyes and bow our heads before Him, in the presence of all the womenfolk?

475.translit:
peymulai yuNDu sagaDam paayndhu marudhiDaip
poymudhal saaytthu, puLvaay piLandhu kaLiRaTTa,
toomuRu valtoNDai, vaayppiraanaiyen naaLkolo,
yaamuRu kinRadhu tozhee! annaiyar naaNave?
475.meaning: He who drank the demoness's breast, who leapt upon the cart, who passing between the twin marudu trees felled them at the root, who tore open the bird-demon's beak and destroyed the elephant, the Lord of the pure smile and coral-red mouth, on what day, O friend, shall we attain Him, that we so yearn for, while our mothers stand abashed?

476.translit:
naaNum niRaiyum kavarndhennai nannenjam koovikkoNDu,
seNuyar vaanat tirukkum deva piraantannai,
aaNaiyen? tozhee! ulagu toRalar tooRRi,aam
koNaigaL seydhu kudhiriyaay maDaloordhume.
476.meaning: Having seized my modesty and my reserve, and called and carried off my good heart, the Lord of the gods who dwells in the lofty far-off heaven, what oath can hold me, O friend? Throughout the world they will spread their slander, but, doing wild reckless deeds, becoming unbridled, I will ride the maddal-palmyra in love's open declaration.

477.translit:
yaamaDa loorndhum emmaazhiyangkaip piraanuDai,
toomaDal taNNam tuzhaaymalar koNDu sooDuvom,
yaamaDa minRit teruvu toRayal taiyalaar,
naamaDang kappazhi tooRRi naaDum iraikkave.
477.meaning: We will ride the maddal-palmyra, and wear the cool fragrant tulasi blossoms of our Lord who bears the discus in His beautiful hand. Casting away all bashfulness, in every street, while the womenfolk fall silent and the whole town spreads its slander and clamors aloud, this we will do.

478.translit:
iraikkum karungaDal vaNNan kaNNapiraantannai,
viraikkoL pozhilguru koorcchaDa kopan sonna,
niraikkoLan dhaadhi yoraa yirattu Lippattum,
uraikkaval laarkku vaikundha maagumtam moorellaam.
478.meaning: Of Kannan, the Lord of the hue of the roaring dark sea, Satakopan of Kurukur with its fragrant groves has sung. This decad among the thousand antadi verses linked in due order, for those who can recite it, their every town shall become Vaikuntha.

479.translit:
oorellaam tunji yulagellaam naLLiruLaay,
neerellaam teRiyor neeLiravaay neeNdadhaal,
paarellaa muNDanam paambaNaiyaan vaaraanaal,
aarelle! valvi naiyen aavikaap paarinaiye?
479.meaning: The whole town has fallen asleep, the whole world has become deep darkness, all the waters have grown still, and this has stretched into one long endless night. He who rests on the serpent, who swallowed all the earth, does not come. Alas! who is there to guard the life of one as cruelly fated as I?

480.translit:
aavikaap paariniyaar? aazhkadalmaN viNmoodi,
maavikaara maayor valliravaay neeNdadaal,
kaavisaer vaNNanen kaNNanum vaaraanaal,
paaviyaen nenjamae! neeyumpaang kallaiyae?
480.meaning: Who is there now to guard my life? The deep sea, the earth and the sky are all covered over, and this dark night, like the great cosmic dissolution wrought by the wondrous Lord, has stretched out endlessly. My Kannan, dark-hued like the blue kaavi flower, still does not come. O heart of sinful me! Will you too not stand by me?

481.translit:
neeyumpaang kallaikaaN nenjamae! neeLiravum,
oayum pozhudindri yoozhiyaay neeNdadaal,
kaayum kadunjsilaiyen kaakuttan vaaraanaal,
maayum vakaiyariyaen valvi naiyaen peNpirandhae.
481.meaning: You too will not stand by me, see, O heart! And this long night, without any moment of ceasing, has stretched out like an aeon. My Kakutstha (Rama), who bears the fierce blazing bow, still does not come. Born a woman, I of cruel deeds do not even know the way to perish.

482.translit:
peNpirandhaar eydhum perundhuyarkaaN kilaenendru,
oNsudaroan vaaraa doLitthaan, im
maNNaLandha kaNperiya sevvaayeng kaaraeru vaaraanaal,
eNperiya sindhainoay teerppaaraar ennaiyae?
482.meaning: As if unable to bear the sight of the great sorrow that befalls women born, the bright-rayed sun has hidden himself and does not come. My large-eyed, red-mouthed dark bull of a Lord, who measured this earth, still does not come. Who is there to cure the boundless sickness of my mind?

483.translit:
aarennai yaaraayvaar? annaiyarum thoazhiyarum,
neerennae? ennaadhae neeLiravum thunjsuvaraal,
kaaranna maeninang kaNNanum vaaraanaal,
paerennai maayaadhaal valvinaiyaen pinnindrae.
483.meaning: Who is there to inquire after me? Both my mothers and my friends, without asking 'what ails you?', sleep through the long night. Our Kannan, dark-bodied like the rain-cloud, still does not come. For me of cruel deeds, even my very name does not fade away, but stays clinging behind me.

484.translit:
pinnindra kaadhalnoay nenjam peridhadumaal,
munnin draavoozhi kaNpudhaiya moodittaal,
manninra sakkarattem maayavanum vaaraanaal,
innindra neeLaavi kaappaaraar ivvidattae?
484.meaning: The love-sickness that clings behind me torments my heart greatly. The aeon-long night standing before me has covered over and hidden my eyes. Our wondrous Lord (Mayavan), bearing the ever-abiding discus, still does not come. Who in this place will guard this long-drawn life that yet remains in me?

485.translit:
kaappaaraar ivvidattu? kangiruLin nuNthuLiyaay,
saetpaala thoozhiyaayc selkindra kanguvaay,
thoorppaala veNsangu sakkarattan thoanraanaal,
teeppaala valvi naiyaen theyvangaaL. enseykaen?
485.meaning: Who in this place will guard me? In this passing night that, like fine drizzling drops of pitch-black darkness, stretches far away like an aeon, He who bears the pure white conch and the discus does not appear. O gods! For me of cruel, fiery deeds, what shall I do?

486.translit:
theyvangaaL. enseykaen?oarira vaezh oozhiyaay,
meyvandhu nindrena daavi melivikkum
kaivandha sakkaraden kaNNanum vaaraanaal,
thaivandha thaNthendral venjsudaril thaanadumae.
486.meaning: O gods! What shall I do? This single night, stretched out like seven aeons, comes upon me in body and weakens my very life. My Kannan, in whose hand the discus rests, still does not come. The cool gentle southern breeze that wafts upon me scorches more than the cruel fire.

487.translit:
venjsudaril thaanadumaal veengiruLin nuNthuLiyaay,
anjsudara veyyoan aNinedundhaer thoanraadhaal,
senjsudart thaamaraikkaN selvanum vaaraanaal,
nenjidartheerp paariniyaar? nindrurugu kindraenae.
487.meaning: It scorches more than the cruel fire, falling as fine drizzling drops in the swelling darkness, while the radiant sun's beautiful tall chariot does not appear. The wealthy Lord with red-rayed lotus eyes still does not come. Who now will cure the anguish of my heart? I stand here melting away.

488.translit:
nindrurugu kindraenae poala neduvaanam,
sendrurugi nuNthuLiyaayc selkindra kanguvaay,
androrukaal vaiyam aLandhapiraan vaaraanendru,
ondrorukaal sollaadhulagoa urangumae.
488.meaning: In the passing night when even the wide sky, just as I stand melting, goes on melting away as fine drizzling drops, the world sleeps on, not even once saying a single word that the Lord who once measured the worlds will not come.

489.translit:
uranguvaan poalyoagu seydha perumaanai,
sirandhapozhil soozhkuru koorcsada koapansol,
niranggiLarndha andhaadhi yaayirattu Lippattaal,
irandhupoay vaikundham saeraavaa rengngaenaeyoa?
489.meaning: Of the great Lord who performed yoga as though sleeping, this is a decade out of the thousand brilliant antaadi verses sung by Satakopan of Kurukur, girt with splendid groves. How could one who masters it not pass on and reach Vaikuntha?

490.translit:
engngaenaeyoa annai meerkaaL! ennai munivadhu neer?,
nangaLkoalat thiruk kuRungudi nampiyai naankaNdapin,
sanginoadum naemi yoadum thaamaraik kaNkaLodum,
sengganivaa yondri nodum selkindra thennenjamae.
490.meaning: O mothers! In what way do you reproach me? Ever since I beheld the beautiful Nambi of Tirukkurungudi who is our adornment, my heart goes forth after him, with his conch and his discus, his lotus eyes, and his single red ripe-fruit mouth.

491.translit:
ennenji naalnoakkik kaaNeer ennai muniyaadhae,
thennan soalait thirukkuRungudi nampiyai naankaNdapin
minnum noolum kuNdalamum maarvil thirumaruvum,
mannum pooNum naankuthoaLum vandhengum nindridumae.
491.meaning: Look with my heart and see, do not reproach me! Ever since I beheld the Nambi of Tirukkurungudi with its southern groves, his shining sacred thread, his earrings, the auspicious mole upon his chest, his enduring ornaments, and his four shoulders come and stand everywhere before me.

492.translit:
nindridum thisaikkum naiyumendru annaiya rummunidhir,
kundra maadat thirukkuRungudi nampiyai naankaNdapin,
vendri villum thaNdum vaaLum sakkaramumsangamum,
nindru thoandrik kaNNuLneengaa nenjuLLum neengaavae.
492.meaning: You mothers reproach me, saying 'she stands frozen, she grows bewildered, she pines away.' Ever since I beheld the Nambi of Tirukkurungudi with its mountain-like mansions, his victorious bow, his mace, his sword, his discus and his conch stand revealed, never leaving my eyes, never leaving my heart.

493.translit:
neenganillaak kaNNa neerkaLendru annaiyarum munidhir,
thaenkoL soalait thirukkuRungudi nampiyai naankaNdapin,
poondhaN maalait thaNduzhaayum ponmudi yumvadivum,
paangu thoanrum pattumnaaNum paaviyaen pakkattavae.
493.meaning: You mothers reproach me, saying 'her tears never cease to flow.' Ever since I beheld the Nambi of Tirukkurungudi with its honey-laden groves, his cool tulasi garland of fresh flowers, his golden crown, his form, his silken raiment and waist-cord all appear close beside sinful me.

494.translit:
pakkamnoakki nirkum naiyumendru annaiya rummunidhir,
thakkakeertthik thirukku Rungudi nampiyai naankaNdapin
thokkasoadhit thoNdai vaayum neeNda puruvangaLum,
thakkathaamaraik kaNNum paaviyae naaviyin maelanavae.
494.meaning: You mothers reproach me, saying 'she stands gazing to her side, she pines away.' Ever since I beheld the Nambi of Tirukkurungudi of fitting fame, his coral-red kovai mouth gathered with radiance, his long brows, and his apt lotus eyes hover over the very life of sinful me.

495.translit:
maelum vanpazhi nangudikkival endrannai kaaNakkodaaL
soalaisoozh thaNthiruk kuRungudi nampiyai naankaNdapin,
koalaneeL kodimookkum thaamaraik kaNNum kanivaayum,
neelamaeniyum naanku thoaLumen nenjam niraindhanavae.
495.meaning: My mother, saying 'this girl brings still more grievous disgrace upon our family,' will not let me be seen. Ever since I beheld the Nambi of cool Tirukkurungudi girt with groves, his beautiful long creeper-like nose, his lotus eyes, his ripe-fruit mouth, his dark-blue body, and his four shoulders have filled my heart.

496.translit:
niraindha vanpazhi nangudikkival endrannai kaaNakkodaaL
siranthakeertthit thirukku Rungudi nampiyai naankaNdapin,
niraindhasoadhi veLLanj soozhndha neeNdapon maeniyodum
niraindhen nuLLae nindrozhindhaan naemiyang kaiyuLadhae.
496.meaning: My mother, saying 'this girl has brought brimming disgrace upon our family,' will not let me be seen. Ever since I beheld the Nambi of glorious Tirukkurungudi, with his tall golden body surrounded by a flood of abundant radiance, he has come and abided, filling my inmost being, the beautiful discus resting in his hand.

497.translit:
kaiyuLnan mukamvaikkum naiyumendru annaiya rummunidhir,
maikoL maadat thirukkuRungudi nampiyai naankaNdapin,
seyyathaamaraik kaNNu malkulum sittridai yumvadivum,
moyyaneeLkuzhal thaazhndha thoaLkaLum paaviyaen munnirkumae.
497.meaning: You mothers reproach me, saying 'she rests her fair face upon her hand, she pines away.' Ever since I beheld the Nambi of Tirukkurungudi with its cloud-touching mansions, his red lotus eyes, his hips, his slender waist, his form, and his shoulders over which his thick long locks hang down all stand before sinful me.

498.translit:
munnin raayendru thoazhimaarkaLum annaiya rummunidhir,
mannu maadat thirukkuRungudi nampiyai naankaNdapin,
senni neeNmudi yaadhiyaaya ulappi laNikalattan,
kannal paalamu thaakivandhen nenjam kazhiyaanae.
498.meaning: Both my friends and my mothers reproach me, saying 'you stand staring vacantly before us.' Ever since I beheld the Nambi of Tirukkurungudi with its enduring mansions, he who is adorned with countless ornaments, beginning with the tall crown upon his head, has come like cane-sugar, milk and nectar, and will not depart from my heart.

499.translit:
kazheeyamikkadhoar kaadhala LivaLen drannai kaaNakkodaaL,
vazhuvil keertthit thirukkuRungudi nampiyai naankaNdapin,
kuzhumit thaevar kuzhaangaLthozhac soadhiveL Lattinussae,
ezhuvadhoa ruruven nenjuLLezhum aarkku marivaridhae.
499.meaning: My mother, saying 'this girl has become one of excessive love,' will not let me be seen. Ever since I beheld the Nambi of flawless fame at Tirukkurungudi, a certain form that rises within the flood of radiance, while the throngs of gods gather and worship, rises up within my heart, hard for anyone to comprehend.

500.translit:
aRivariya piraanai yaazhiyangaiyanai yEyalaTRi,
naRiyanan malarnaadi nankurukoorccaTakOpan sonna,
kuRikoLaa yiratthuL Livaipatthum tirukkuRung kudiya tanmEl
aRiyak kaTRuvallaar vaiTTaNavaraazhkadal nyaalatthuLLE.
500.meaning: Crying out to the Lord who is hard to comprehend, who bears the beautiful discus in his hand, Satakopan of fair Kurugur, seeking out fragrant fine flowers, sang these ten among the thousand meaningful verses upon Tirukkurungudi. Those able to learn and master them with understanding will live as Vaishnavas in this world encircled by the deep sea.

501.translit:
kadalnyaalam seydEnum yaanE ennum
kadalnyaalam aavEnum yaanE ennum,
kadalnyaalam koNdEnum yaanE ennum
kadalnyaalam keeNdEnum yaanE ennum,
kadalnyaalam muNdEnum yaanE ennum
kadalnyaalat theesanvan dERak kolO?,
kadalnyaa latteerk kivaiyen sollukEn
kadalnyaa latten makaLkaR kinRanavE?
501.meaning: She says, 'It is I myself who created the sea-girt world'; she says, 'It is I myself who became the sea-girt world'; she says, 'It is I myself who took (measured) the sea-girt world'; she says, 'It is I myself who scooped up the sea-girt world (from the deluge)'; she says, 'It is I myself who swallowed the sea-girt world.' Has the Lord of the sea-girt world come and possessed her? O people of this sea-girt world, what shall I say to you of these things my daughter is learning to utter?

502.translit:
kaRkumkal vikkellai yilanE ennum
kaRkumkalvi yaavEnum yaanE ennum,
kaRkumkalvi seyvEnum yaanE ennum
kaRkumkalvi teerppEnum yaanE ennum,
kaRkumkalvic caaramum yaanE ennum
kaRkumkalvi naadanvan dERak kolO?,
kaRkum kalviyeerk kivaiyen sollukEn
kaRkum kalviyen makaLkaaN kinRanavE?
502.meaning: She says, 'There is no limit to the learning I can learn'; she says, 'It is I myself who am the learning that is learned'; she says, 'It is I myself who create the learning that is learned'; she says, 'It is I myself who remove (end) the learning that is learned'; she says, 'It is I myself who am the very essence of all learning.' Has the Lord of all learning come and possessed her? O you who are versed in learning, what shall I say to you of these visions my daughter is beholding?

503.translit:
kaaNkinRa nilamellaam yaanE ennum
kaaNkinRa visumbellaam yaanE ennum,
kaaNkinRa vendeeyellaam yaanE ennum
kaaNkinRa ikkaaTRellaam yaanE ennum,
kaaNkinRa kadalellaam yaanE ennum
kaaNkinRa kadalvaNNa nERak kolO?
kaaNkinRa vulagat teerkken sollukEn
kaaNkinRa venkaa rigaisey kinRanavE.
503.meaning: She says, 'All this earth that is seen is I myself'; she says, 'All this sky that is seen is I myself'; she says, 'All this scorching fire that is seen is I myself'; she says, 'All this wind that is seen is I myself'; she says, 'All this sea that is seen is I myself.' Has the ocean-hued Lord whom one beholds come and possessed her? O people of this visible world, what shall I say of these things my fair girl is doing before our eyes?

504.translit:
seykinRa kidiyellaam yaanE ennum
seyvaanin RanagaLum yaanE ennum,
seydumun niRandanavum yaanE ennum
seykaippaya nuNbEnum yaanE ennum,
seyvaarkaLaic ceyvEnum yaanE ennum
seyyakama lakkaNNa nERak kolO?
seyyavula gatteerk kivaiyen sollukEn
seyya kanivaa yiLamaan tiratthE.
504.meaning: She says, 'All deeds that are being done are I myself'; she says, 'The deeds yet to be done are I myself'; she says, 'The deeds done and passed before are also I myself'; she says, 'It is I myself who enjoy the fruit of deeds'; she says, 'It is I myself who make the doers act.' Has the red lotus-eyed Lord come and possessed her? O people of this bright world, what shall I say of these things concerning my young doe with the red ripe-fruit mouth?

505.translit:
tiRambaamal maN kaakkinREn yaanE ennum
tiRambaamal malaiyedut thEnE ennum,
tiRambaamal asuraraikkon REnE ennum
tiRangaatti yanRaivaraik kaatthEnE ennum,
tiRambaamal kadalkadain thEnE ennum
tiRambaada kadalvaNNa nERak kolO?
tiRambaada vulagat teerkken sollukEn
tiRambaa dentiru makaLey dinavE?
505.meaning: She says, 'It is I myself who guard the earth without fail'; she says, 'It is I who lifted up the mountain unfailingly'; she says, 'It is I who slew the asuras without fail'; she says, 'Showing my power, it was I who guarded the five (Pandavas) that day'; she says, 'It is I who churned the ocean unfailingly.' Has the unfailing ocean-hued Lord come and possessed her? O people of this unchanging world, what shall I say of the state my Lakshmi-like daughter has reached without fail?

506.translit:
inavEymalai yEndinEn yaanE ennum
inavERukaL seTREnum yaanE ennum,
inavaankanRu mEytthEnum yaanE ennum
inavaanirai kaatthEnum yaanE ennum,
inavaayar talaivanum yaanE ennum
inatthEvar talaivanvan dERak kolO?,
inavERkaN nalleerk kivaiyen sollukEn
inavER kaNNi yenmaka luTRanavE?
506.meaning: She says, 'It is I myself who lifted up the bamboo-clustered mountain'; she says, 'It is I who destroyed the herd of fierce bulls'; she says, 'It is I who herded the herds of fine calves'; she says, 'It is I who guarded the herds of cattle'; she says, 'It is I who am the chief of the herding cowherds.' Has the chief of the host of gods come and possessed her? O fair women with spear-like eyes, what shall I say of what has befallen my daughter with spear-like eyes?

507.translit:
uTRaarka Lenakkillai yaarum ennum
uTRaarka Lenakkingel laarum ennum,
uTRaarkaLaic ceyvEnum yaanE ennum
uTRaarkaLai azhippEnum yaanE ennum,
uTRaarkaLuk kuTREnum yaanE ennum
uTRaarili maayan vandERak kolO?,
uTReerkat kensollic collu kEnyaan
uTRen nudaippE taiyuraik kinRana vE.
507.meaning: She says, 'I have no kin at all'; she says, 'All here are my kin'; she says, 'It is I myself who create kinsfolk'; she says, 'It is I myself who destroy kinsfolk'; she says, 'It is I myself who am the kin of all kinsfolk.' Has the wondrous Lord who has no kin come and possessed her? O you my kinsfolk, what words shall I use to tell you what my innocent girl is uttering?

508.translit:
uraikkinRa mukkatpiraan yaanE ennum
uraikkinRa tisaimukan yaanE ennum,
uraikkinRa amararum yaanE ennum
uraikkinRa amarar kOn yaanE ennum,
uraikkinRa munivarum yaanE ennum
uraikkinRa mukilvaNNa nERak kolO?,
uraikkinRa ulagat teerkken sollukEn
uraikkinRa venkO maLavoN kodikkE?
508.meaning: She says, 'The three-eyed Lord (Siva) of whom men speak is I myself'; she says, 'The four-faced one (Brahma) of whom men speak is I myself'; she says, 'The celestials of whom men speak are I myself'; she says, 'The king of the celestials (Indra) of whom men speak is I myself'; she says, 'The sages of whom men speak are I myself.' Has the cloud-hued Lord of whom men speak come and possessed her? O people of this world that speaks, what shall I say of my tender, bright creeper-like girl?

509.translit:
kodiya vinaiyaadu milanE ennum
kodiyavinai yaavEnum yaanE ennum,
kodiyavinai seyvEnum yaanE ennum
kodiyavinai teerppEnum yaanE ennum,
kodiyaa nilangaicheT REnE ennum
kodiyapuL Ludaiyava nERak kolO?,
kodiya vulagatteerk kivaiyen sollukEn
kodiyEn kodiyen makaLkO langaLE?
509.meaning: She says, 'I have no cruel karma at all'; she says, 'It is I myself who am the cruel karma'; she says, 'It is I myself who do cruel deeds'; she says, 'It is I myself who remove cruel deeds'; she says, 'It is I who destroyed the cruel one's Lanka.' Has the Lord who bears the bird upon his banner come and possessed her? O cruel people of this world, what shall I say of the strange manners of my daughter, wretched and cruel that I am?

510.translit:
kOlangoL suvarkkamum yaanE ennum
kOlamil naragamum yaanE ennum,
kOlamtigazh mOkkamum yaanE ennum
kOlango Luyirkalum yaanE ennum,
kOlangoL tanimudal yaanE ennum
kOlangoL mukilvaNNa nERak kolO?
kOlango Lulagat teerkken sollukEn
kOlan tigazhkO daiyenkoon talukkE.
510.meaning: She says, 'The beautiful heaven is I myself'; she says, 'The unlovely hell is I myself'; she says, 'The radiant liberation (moksha) is I myself'; she says, 'The beautiful living souls are I myself'; she says, 'The beautiful sole First Cause is I myself.' Has the beautiful cloud-hued Lord come and possessed her? O people of this beautiful world, what shall I say concerning the hair of my daughter, lovely as a radiant garland?

511.translit:
koondalmalar mangaikkum maNmadan tikkum
kulavaayar kozhundukkum kELvan tannai
vaayntha vazhudi naadan mannu
kurukoorc cadakOpan kuTRE valseydu,
aayntha tamizhmaalai aayi ratthuL
ivaiyumOr patthumval laar,ulagil
Endu perunchelvan taaraayt tirumaal
adiyaar kaLaippoo sikkanOR RaarkaLE.
511.meaning: To the Beloved Lord of the flower-tressed Lady (Lakshmi), of the Earth-maiden, and of the tender shoot of the noble cowherd clan (Nappinnai), Satakopan of Kurugur, abiding in the fertile Pandya land, rendered devoted service. Those able to master these ten too among his thousand polished garlands of Tamil verse, holding great wealth in the world, have, as Tirumal's devotees, performed the penance fit to be worshipped.

512.translit:
nORRa nOnbilEn nuNNaRi vilEnaagilum ini yunnaivittu,onRum
aaTRa kinRilEn aravinaNai yammaanE,
sETRut taamarai sennel oodumalar sireevara mangalanagar,
veeTRirundha endaay. unakku migaiyallE nangE.
512.meaning: Though I have performed no penance and have no subtle wisdom, yet now, parted from You, I can endure nothing at all, O my Lord reclining on the serpent-couch. O my Father seated in glory at Srivaramangala-nagar, where in the muddy pools lotuses blossom amid the red paddy, I am no burden too great for You there.

513.translit:
anguTRE nallE ninguTRE nallEn unnaik kaaNum avaavil
veezhndu,naan enguT REnumal lEnilangaicheTRa ammaanE,
tingaL sErmaNi maadam needu sireevara mangala nagaruRai,
sangu sakkarat thaay! tamiyEnuk karuLaayE.
513.meaning: I am not fully there, I am not fully here; fallen into the longing to behold You, I am nowhere at all, O my Lord who destroyed Lanka. O You who bear the conch and discus, dwelling in Srivaramangala-nagar with its tall jewelled mansions that touch the moon, grant Your grace to me, all alone.

514.translit:
karuLa putkodi sakka rappadai vaana naada! eng kaarmukil vaNNaa,
poruLallaada ennaip poruLaakki adimai koNdaay,
teruLkoL naanmaRai vallavar palarvaazh sireevara mangalanagarkku,
aruLseydangirun thaayaRi yEnoru kaimmaaRE.
514.meaning: O Lord of the celestial realm, who bears the Garuda-eagle on Your banner and the discus as Your weapon, O my dark-cloud-hued one! You made me, who was nothing of worth, into something worthy, and took me as Your servant. Having graced Srivaramangala-nagar, where dwell many masters versed with clarity in the four Vedas, You abide there; I know not a single way to repay You.

515.translit:
maaRu sErpadai nooTRuvar manga vOraivarkku aayanRu maayappOr paNNi,
neeRu seyda endaay. nilangeeNda ammaanE,
tERu nyaanatthar vEda vELviyaRaac sireevara mangalanagar,
ERiveeT Rirundaay. unnai engeydak koovuvanE?
515.meaning: For the sake of the five (Pandavas), so that the hundred (Kauravas) with their hostile, massed armies might perish, You that day waged a wondrous war and ground them to dust, O my Father; O Lord who split open the earth (to rescue it). You sit enthroned in glory at Srivaramangala-nagar, where men of clear wisdom never cease their Vedic sacrifices. How and where shall I call out to reach You?

516.translit:
eydak koovudal aavadE enakku? evvadey vatthu Laayumaay ninRu,
kaidha vangaLsey yumkaru mEniyam maanE,
seydha vELviyar vaiyat thEvaraRaac sireevara mangalanagar,
kaitthozha irundaay adunaanum kaNdEnE.
516.meaning: Is it even possible for me to call out and reach You, who stand pervading within every deity, O my dark-bodied Lord who performs deeds of wondrous play? You abide to be worshipped with folded hands at Srivaramangala-nagar, where dwell the earthly gods (Brahmins) who unceasingly perform sacrifices, and that I too have seen.

517.translit:
Ena maaynilang keeNdaven appanE! kaNNaa! enRu mennai yaaludai,
vaananaa yaganE! maNimaa Nikkac cudarE,
tEna maampozhil taNsiree varamangalat thavarkkai tozhavuRai
vaana maamalai yE!adi yEntozha vandaruLE.
517.meaning: O my Father who, becoming a boar, split open the earth and lifted it! O Kannan! O Lord of the celestial realm who eternally rules me! O radiance like a precious ruby gem! O great heavenly mountain who dwells in cool Srivaramangalam of honeyed mango groves, to be worshipped with folded hands by its people, graciously come that I, Your servant, may worship You.

518.translit:
vandaruLi yennenj sidangoNda vaanavar kozhundE! ulagukkOr
mundait taaydandaiyE! muzhuEzhulagu muNdaay,
sendo zhilavar vEda vELviyaRaac sireevara mangalanagar,
andamil pugazhaay! adiyEnai akaTRElE.
518.meaning: O tender shoot of the celestials, who graciously came and took my heart as Your dwelling! O primordial Mother and Father to the world! You who swallowed all the seven worlds. O Lord of endless glory abiding at Srivaramangala-nagar, where men of pure deeds never cease their Vedic sacrifices, do not push me, Your servant, away from You.

519.translit:
akaTRa neevaitta maayaval laimpulangaLaam avai nankaRindanan,
akaTRi ennaiyum nee arunj sETRil veezhtti kaNdaay,
pagaRkadir maNimaadam needu sireevara mangai vaaNanE! enRum
pugaRkariya endaay!puLLinvaay piLandaanE!
519.meaning: I have come to know well those mighty five senses of illusion that You set up to draw me away. Yet, drawing me too away, You have cast me into this hard mire (of worldly bondage), see! O Lord of Srivaramangai with its jewelled mansions tall as the day's sun-rays! O my Father, ever hard to attain even by praise! O You who tore apart the beak of the (crane-demon) bird!

520.translit:
puLLin vaay piLandhaay, marudhidai pooyinaay, erudhEzh adarttha, en
kaLLa maayavanE! karu maaNikkach chudarE!
theLLiyaar thiru naan maRaigaL vallaar mali thaN siriivara mangai,
uLLirundha endhaay! aruLaay uyyumaaR enakkE.
520.meaning: O You who split open the beak of the crane-demon, who passed between the twin marudha trees, who subdued the seven bulls, my deceptive Lord of wondrous play! O radiance like a dark sapphire gem! O my Father who dwell within cool, abundant Sirivaramangai, where reside those of clear wisdom skilled in the sacred four Vedas! Grant me your grace, that I may be saved.

521.translit:
aaR enakku nin paadhamE saraN aaga thandhozhindhaay, unakku Or kaim
maaRu naan onR ilEn enadhu aaviyum unadhE,
sEru koL karumbum perum chennellum mali thaN siriivara mangai
naaRu poon thaN thuzhaay mudiyaay! deyva naayaganE!
521.meaning: You have given me your feet alone as my refuge, the way of release; I have nothing whatever to offer you in return, and even my soul is your own. O You who wear the crown of fragrant cool tulasi blossoms, dwelling in cool, abundant Sirivaramangai rich with growing sugarcane and tall red paddy! O Lord of the gods!

522.translit:
deyva naayagan naaraNan thirivikkiraman adi iNai misai,
koy koL poom pozhil soozh kurugoor sadagOpan
seydha aayiratth uL ivai thaN siriivara mangai mEya patthudan,
vaigal paada vallaar vaanOrkku aaraa amudhE.
522.meaning: Upon the twin feet of the divine Lord, Narayana, Trivikrama, Satakopan of Kurugur, encircled by blossoming groves whose flowers are plucked, composed a thousand verses; these ten among them are set upon cool Sirivaramangai. Those able to sing them daily become insatiable nectar to the celestials.

523.translit:
aaraa amudhE! adiyEn udalam ninpaal anbaayE,
niiraay alaindhu karaiya urukkuginRa nedumaalE,
siiraar chennel kavari viisum chezhu niir thirukkudandhai,
Eraar koolam thigazha kidandhaay kaNdEn emmaanE!
523.meaning: O insatiable nectar! O great Lord Nedumal, who melt this servant's body into love for you, making it dissolve and flow like water! In Tirukkudandai of fertile waters, where the lovely red paddy waves like fans, I beheld you reclining in resplendent beauty, O my Master!

524.translit:
emmaanE! en veLLai moortthi! ennai aaLvaanE,
em maa uruvum vENdumaaRRaal aavaay ezhil ERE,
sem maa kamalam chezhu niir misai kaN malarum thirukkudandhai,
am maa malar kaN vaLarginRaanE, en naan seygEnE.
524.meaning: O my Master! O my fair-hued form! O You who rule me! O majestic bull, who take whatever great forms are needful! In Tirukkudandai, where the lovely red lotuses blossom upon the fertile waters, You recline with great flower-like eyes. What am I to do?

525.translit:
en naan seygEn? yaarE kaLaikaN? ennai en seyginRaay?
unnaal allaal yaavaraalum onRum kuRaivENdEn,
kann aar madhiL soozh kudandhai kidandhaay! adiyEn aru vaazh naaL,
sen naaL en naaL an naaL un thaaL pidittE selakkaaNE.
525.meaning: What shall I do? Who else is my refuge? What are you doing with me? From none but you do I seek anything to fill my lack. O You who recline in Kudandai girt by walls of stone! May this servant's precious remaining days, whatever and whichever they be, pass while I cling to your feet.

526.translit:
selakkaaN kiRpaar kaaNum aLavum sellum kiirtthiyaay!
ulapp ilaanE ellaa ulagum udaiya oru moortthi!
nalatthaal mikkaar kudandhai kidandhaay! unnai kaaNbaan naan
alappaay, aagaasatthai nOkki azhuvan thozhuvanE.
526.meaning: O You whose glory extends as far as the seers who strive to behold can see! O endless One! O sole Form who own all the worlds! O You who recline in Kudandai, abode of those abounding in goodness! Longing to see you, I am distressed; gazing up at the sky, I weep and worship.

527.translit:
azhuvan thozhuvan aadi kaaNban paadi alaRRuvan,
thazhuval vinaiyaal pakkam nOkki naaNi kavizhndhiruppan,
sezhu oN pazhana kudandhai kidandhaay! sen thaamarai kaNNaa!
thozhuvanEnai un thaaL sErum vagaiyE soozh kaNdaay.
527.meaning: I weep, I worship, I dance and look, I sing and cry aloud; oppressed by clinging karma, I look about, grow ashamed, and bow my head down. O You who recline in Kudandai of rich, fair fields! O lotus-red-eyed One! Devise, I pray, the way by which I, your worshipper, may reach your feet.

528.translit:
soozh kaNdaay en thollai vinaiyai aRutth un adi sErum
oozh kaNd irundhE, thooraa kuzhi thoortthu enai naaL agan iruppan?
vaazh thol pugazhaar kudandhai kidandhaay! vaanOr kOmaanE!
yaazhin isaiyE! amudhE! aRivin payanE! ariyERE!
528.meaning: Devise, I pray, to cut away my ancient karma; though the destined hour to reach your feet has been seen, how long shall I remain apart, filling up the unfillable pit? O You who recline in Kudandai of those who live with ancient glory! O King of the celestials! O music of the lute! O nectar! O fruit of wisdom! O lordly lion!

529.translit:
ariyERE! en am poR chudarE! sengaN karu mugilE,
eriyE pavaLa kunRE! naal thOL endhaay! unadhu aruLE,
piriyaa adimai ennai koNdaay kudandhai thirumaalE,
thariyEn ini un saraNam thandh en sanmam kaLaiyaayE.
529.meaning: O lordly lion! O my beautiful golden radiance! O red-eyed dark cloud! O flame, O coral hill! O four-shouldered Father! By your grace alone you have taken me into undeparting servitude, O Tirumal of Kudandai. I can bear no longer; grant me your refuge and remove my births.

530.translit:
kaLaivaay thunbam kaLaiyaadh ozhivaay kaLaikaN maRR ilEn,
vaLai vaay nEmi padaiyaay! kudandhai kidandha maa maayaa!
thaLaraa udalam ennadhu aavi sarindhu pOm pOdhu,
iLaiyaadh unadhaaL orunga pidittu pOdha isai niiyE.
530.meaning: Whether you remove my sorrow or leave it unremoved, I have no other refuge. O You who bear the curved-mouthed discus! O great wonder-Lord reclining in Kudandai! When my body fails and my soul slips away and departs, grant that I may go grasping your feet together without faltering.

531.translit:
isaivitt ennai un thaaL iNai kiizh iruttum ammaanE!
asaivil amarar thalaivar! thalaivaa aadhi peru moortthi!
thisai vil viisum chezhu maa maNigaL sErum thirukkudandhai,
asaivil ulagam parava kidandhaay, kaaNa vaaraayE.
531.meaning: O my Master who, having gained my consent, settle me beneath your twin feet! O Lord of the immortals who are without wavering! O foremost One, ancient great Form! In Tirukkudandai, where great fine gems gather casting rainbow light across the directions, you recline while the steadfast world extols you. Come, that I may behold you.

532.translit:
vaaraa varuvaay varum en maayaa! maayaa moortthiyaay!
aaraa amudhaay adiyEn aavi agamE thittippaay,
thiiraa vinaigaL thiira ennai aaNdaay! thirukkudandhai
ooraay! unakku aatpattum adiyEn innam uzhalvEnO?
532.meaning: O my marvelous One who come though seeming not to come, yet ever coming! O Form that never decays! As insatiable nectar you grow sweet within this servant's very soul. You ruled me, so that my unending karma might end. O Lord of Tirukkudandai! Having become your servant, must I, your servant, still wander on?

533.translit:
uzhalai enbin pEychchi mulai oodu avaLai uyir uNdaan,
kazhalgaL avaiyE saraN aa koNda kurugoor sadagOpan,
kuzhalil maliya sonna Or aayiratt uL ip patthum
mazhalai thiira vallaar kaamar maanEy nOkkiyarkkE.
533.meaning: He who drank the life of the ogress through her breast, taking her very soul; Satakopan of Kurugur, who took those anklet-feet alone as his refuge. Of the thousand verses he sang, surpassing the flute in sweetness, these ten - those able to master them, free of childish faults, become beloved to women of fawn-like eyes.

534.translit:
maanEy nOkku nalliir! vaigalum vinaiyEn meliya,
vaan aar vaN kamugum madhu malligaiyum kamazhum,
thEn aar sOlaigaL soozh thiruvallavaazh uRaiyum
kOnaarai adiyEn adi kooduvadhu enRu kolO?
534.meaning: O good women of fawn-like glances! Day after day I, the karma-laden one, grow weak. When shall I, his servant, reach the feet of the Lord who dwells in Tiruvallavazh, encircled by honey-filled groves where lofty rich areca palms and sweet jasmine spread their fragrance?

535.translit:
enRu kol thOzhimiirgaaL emmai niir nalindh en seydhiirO?
pon thigazh punnai magizh pudhu maadhavi miidh aNavi,
thenRal maNam kamazhum thiruvallavaazh nagaruL
ninRa piraan, adi niiR adiyOm koNdu sooduvadhE?
535.meaning: O friends, when will it be? By harassing me, what do you achieve? It is this: that we, his servants, may take and wear upon our heads the dust of the feet of the Lord who stands within the city of Tiruvallavazh, where the southern breeze blows fragrant, brushing over the golden-gleaming punnai, the magizh, and the fresh madhavi blossoms.

536.translit:
soodum malar kuzhaliir! thuyar aattiyEnai meliya,
paadu nal vEdha oli paravai thirai pOl muzhanga,
maad uyarndh Oma pugai kamazhum thaN thiruvallavaazh
niidu uRaiginRa piraan kazhal kaNdum kol nichchalumE?
536.meaning: O women with flower-decked tresses! While I, tormented by sorrow, grow weak - shall I daily behold the anklet-feet of the Lord who dwells long in cool Tiruvallavazh, where the chanting of the good Vedas resounds like the waves of the sea, and the smoke of sacrifice rises fragrant above the tall mansions?

537.translit:
nichchalum thOzhimiirgaaL! emmai niir nalindh en seydhiirO?
pachchilai niiL kamugum palavum thengum vaazhaigaLum,
machch aNi maadangaL miidh aNavum thaN thiruvallavaazh
nachch ara aNai mEl nam piraanadhu nal nalamE.
537.meaning: O friends, daily by harassing me, what do you achieve? It is the great goodness of our Lord who reclines upon the venomous serpent-couch in cool Tiruvallavazh, where the tall green-leaved areca palms, jackfruit, coconut, and plantain trees rise above the turret-adorned mansions.

538.translit:
nan nala thOzhimiirgaaL, nalla andhaNar vELvi pugai,
mai nalam koNd uyar viN maRaikkum thaN thiruvallavaazh,
kannal am katti thannai kaniyai in amudham thannai,
en nalam koL sudarai enRu kol kaNgaL kaaNbadhuvE?
538.meaning: O dear good friends! In cool Tiruvallavazh, where the sacrificial smoke of the good brahmins, taking on the dark beauty of clouds, hides the high heavens - the Lord who is like a block of sweet sugar-candy, like ripe fruit, like sweet nectar, the radiance that takes my well-being: when, oh when, shall my eyes behold him?

539.translit:
kaaNbadh ennaanRu kolo vinaiyEn kani vaay madaviir,
paaN kural vandhinOdu pasum thenRalum aagi engum,
sEN sinai Ongu mara chezhum kaanal thiruvallavaazh,
maaN kuRaL kOla piraan malar thaamarai paadhangaLE?
539.meaning: O tender women with fruit-red lips, when shall I, the karma-laden one, behold them - the lotus-blossom feet of the gracious Lord who took the noble dwarf form, in Tiruvallavazh of fertile groves where high-branched trees rise, where everywhere the bees hum like minstrels and the fresh southern breeze blows?

540.translit:
paadhangaL meelaNi poot thozhak kooDungkol paavai nalleer,
oodha neDun thaDatthuL uyar thaamarai sengkazhuneer,
maadhargaL vaaNmugamum kaNNum eendhum thiruvallavaazh,
naadhan in nyaalam uNDa nampiraan thannai naaDoorume?
540.meaning: O lovely maidens! Will it ever be possible for us, day after day, to offer flowers upon and worship the feet of our Lord, the master of Tiruvallavazh, where in the vast deep ponds the tall lotus and the red water-lily bloom, holding up the radiant faces and eyes of women, He who is the Lord that swallowed this whole world?

541.translit:
naaL thoorum veeDinriye thozhak kooDungkol nannudhaleer,
aaDuru theeng karumbum viLai sennelum aagi engkum,
maaDuru poon thaDam seer vayal soozh thaN thiruvallavaazh,
neeDurai kinra piraan nilan thaaviya neeL kazhale?
541.meaning: O fair-browed maidens! Will it be granted to us to worship without ceasing, day after day, the long feet that measured the earth, the feet of the Lord who abides forever in cool Tiruvallavazh, surrounded everywhere by fields where sweet swaying sugarcane and ripening red paddy flourish beside flower-filled ponds?

542.translit:
kazhal vaLai poorippa yaam kaNDu kaithozhak kooDungkolo,
kuzhal enna yaazhum enna kuLir soolaiyuL theen arundhi,
mazhalai vari vaNDugaL isai paaDum thiruvallavaazh,
suzhalin mali chakkarap perumaanadhu thaal aruLe?
542.meaning: Will it ever be possible for us, our bangles growing full with joy, to behold and worship with folded hands the gracious feet of the Lord who bears the ever-whirling discus, He of Tiruvallavazh, where in the cool groves the lisping striped bees sip honey and sing music that sounds like the flute and like the lute?

543.translit:
thol aruL nal vinaiyaal sollak kooDungkol thoozhimeergaaL,
thol aruL maNNum viNNum thozha ninra thirunagaram,
nal aruL aayiravar nalan eendhum thiruvallavaazh,
nal aruL nam perumaan naaraayaNan naamangkaLe?
543.meaning: O my friends! Will it be granted to us, through ancient grace and good deeds, to utter the names of Narayana our Lord of abundant grace, of the holy city Tiruvallavazh to which earth and heaven bow, the sacred place where countless gracious ones hold up their goodness in devotion?

544.translit:
naamangkaL aayiram uDaiya nam perumaan aDimeel,
seemangkoL then kurugoor chaDagoopan therindhuraittha,
naamangkaL aayiratthuL ivai patthum thiruvallavaazh,
seemangkoL then nagar meel seppuvaar siranthaar piranthe?
544.meaning: These ten verses, out of the thousand discerning songs that Satakopan of beautiful southern Kurugur composed in praise of the feet of our Lord who bears a thousand names, are upon Tiruvallavazh, the guarded southern city. Those who recite them have been born to excellence.

545.translit:
pirandha aarum vaLarndha aarum periya bhaaratham kaisaidhu, aivarkku
thirangkaL chaaTTi iTTu seidhu poona maayangkaLum,
niranthan ooDu pukku enadhu aaviyai ninru ninru urukki uNginra, ich
sirandha vaan suDare! unnai enrukol seervadhuve?
545.meaning: The way You were born, the way You grew, the way You waged the great Bharata war and, assigning their roles to the five (Pandavas), wrought Your wondrous deeds and departed - these things, penetrating my very being, stand again and again melting and consuming my soul. O excellent heavenly radiance! When shall I come to attain You?

546.translit:
vadhuvai vaartthaiyuL eeru paayndhadhum maaya maavinai vaai piLandhadhum
madhuvai vaar kuzhalaar kuravai piNaindha kuzhagum, adhu idhu
udhu ennalaavana alla ennai un saigai naivikkum,
mudhu vaiya mudhalvaa! unnai enru thalaip peivane?
546.meaning: At the time of the marriage contest You leapt upon the bulls; You split open the mouth of the deceitful demon-horse; and the charm of Your dancing the kuravai entwined with the women of flowing tresses - these deeds of Yours, which cannot be told as 'this' or 'that' or 'the other', waste me away. O primal Lord of this ancient world! When shall I come to reach You?

547.translit:
peiyum poon kuzhal peei mulai uNDa piLLai theerramum peerndhu oor saaDu irach
seyya paadham onraal seidha nin siru seevagamum,
neyy uN vaartthaiyuL, annai kol koLLa nee un thaamarai kaNgaL neer malga,
paiyave nilaiyum vandhu en nenjai urukkungkaLe.
547.meaning: The composure of the child who, with flowing flower-decked hair, sucked the breast of the demoness Putana; and Your little heroic act of breaking the cart with one red foot; and, in the matter of stealing and eating ghee, the gentle way You stood when mother took up the rod, Your lotus eyes brimming with tears - these come and melt my heart.

548.translit:
kaLLa veeDatthai koNDu poi purambu pukka aarum,
kalandha surarai uLLam beedham seidhiTTu uyir uNDa upaayangkaLum,
veLLa neer chaDaiyaanum ninniDai veeralaamai
viLanga ninradhum, uLLamuL kuDaindhu en uyirai urukki uNNume.
548.meaning: The way You went forth in a false guise (as Mohini) and slipped to the outside; and the strategies by which You confounded the minds of the demons who had mingled in and took away their lives; and the way it stood revealed that even He of the flood-bearing matted locks (Shiva) is not separate from You - these dig deep within my heart, melting and consuming my soul.

549.translit:
uLLam vaanavar koonukku aayar oruppaDutta aDisil uNDadhum,
vaNNa maal varaiyai eDutthu mazhai kaatthadhum,
maNNai mun paDaitthu uNDu mizhndhu kaDandhu iDandhu maNandha maayangkaL,
eNNun thoorum en nenju eri vaai mezhugu okkum ninre.
549.meaning: The way You, set on it in Your heart, ate the food the cowherds had prepared for Indra king of the celestials; the way You lifted the great beautiful mountain (Govardhana) and warded off the rain; and the wonders of creating the earth of old, swallowing it, spitting it out, striding over it, digging it up (as boar) and wedding it - whenever I think of these, my heart stands like wax in the mouth of fire.

550.translit:
ninra aarum irundha aarum kiDandha aarum ninaipariyana
onralaa uruvaai aruvaaya nin maayangkaL,
ninru ninru ninaikkinrenu unnai engngnanam ninai kirpan, paaviyerku
onru nan kuraiyaai ulagam uNDa oN suDare.
550.meaning: The way You stand, the way You sit, the way You recline - hard to conceive - Your wonders that take form not of one kind, being both with-form and formless: standing and standing I keep thinking of You, yet how can I think You? O radiant light that swallowed the world, tell me, the sinner, one thing well.

551.translit:
oN suDarooDu iruLumaai ninra aarum uNmaiyooDu inmaiyaai vandhu, en
kaN koLaavagai nee karandhu ennai seiginrana,
eN koL sindhaiyuL naiginren en kariya maaNikkame, en kaNgaTku
thiN koLLa orunaaL aruLaai un thiruvuruve.
551.meaning: The way You stand being both radiant light and darkness, and coming as both being and non-being, You hide Yourself so that my eyes cannot grasp You - by what You do, I waste away in my pondering mind, O my dark sapphire-gem! Grant me one day, that my eyes may firmly hold it, Your divine form.

552.translit:
thiru uruvu kiDandha aarum koppoozh sen thaamarai meel, thisaimugan
karu uL veerru irundhu paDaitthiTTa karumangkaLum,
poru vil un thani naayagam avai keTkun thoorum en nenjam ninru nekku,
aruvi soorum kaNNeer en seigeen aDiyeene.
552.meaning: The way Your divine form reclines, and the deeds of creation that Brahma of the (four) directions, seated within the womb-calyx upon the red lotus of Your navel, performed; and Your unmatched sole sovereignty - whenever I hear of these, my heart stands and softens, and tears stream down like a waterfall. What shall I, Your servant, do?

553.translit:
aDiyai moonrai irandha aarum angke ninru aazh kaDalum maNNum viNNum
muDiya, eer aDiyaal muDitthu koNDa mukkiyamum,
noDiyu maaru avai keTkun thoorum en nenjam nin thanakke karaindhugum,
koDiya val vinaiyen unnai enrukol kooDuvadhe?
553.meaning: The way You begged for three feet of ground, and the supreme act by which, standing there, You finished off the deep sea, the earth and the sky with two strides - whenever I hear these recounted, my heart melts away for You alone. I of cruel, mighty karma - when shall I unite with You?

554.translit:
kooDi neerai kaDaindha aarum amudham theevar uNNa, asurarai
veeDum vaNNangkaLe seidhu poona vitthagamum,
ooDu pukku enadhu aaviyai urukki uNDiDuginra, nin thannai
naaDum vaNNam sollaai nachchu naagaNaiyaane.
554.meaning: The way You joined together and churned the ocean, and the cleverness by which - so the celestials might drink the nectar - You contrived only the ruin of the demons and departed: these penetrate within and melt and consume my soul. O Lord who reclines on the venomous serpent (Adisesha), tell me the way to seek You.

555.translit:
naagaNai misai nampiraan saraNe saraN namakku enru, naaDorum
eeka sindhaiyanaai kurugoor chaDagoopan maaran,
aaga noorra andhaadhi aayiratthuL ivaiyum oor patthum vallaar,
maaga vaikundhatthu magizhvu eidhuvar vaikalume.
555.meaning: Satakopan, Maran of Kurugur, single-minded day after day in holding 'the feet alone of our Lord who reclines on the serpent are our refuge,' composed a thousand antadi verses. Those who can master these ten among them will attain joy forever in lofty Vaikunta.

556.translit:
vaigal poong kazhi vaai vandhu meeyum kuruginangkaaL,
seykoL sennel uyar thiruvaNvaNDoor uraiyum,
kaikoL chakkaratthu en kani vaai perumaanai kaNDu,
kaigaL kooppi solleer vinaiyaaTTiyen kaadhanmaiye.
556.meaning: O flock of herons that come daily to feed at the lovely backwaters! When you behold my Lord of the coral-red lips who bears the discus in His hand, who dwells in Tiruvanvandur where tall red paddy flourishes in the fields, fold your hands and tell Him of the longing of me, the woman of (sorrowful) deeds.

557.translit:
kaadhal men peDaiyooDu uDan meeyum karunaaraai!
veedha veeLvi oli muzhangkum thaN thiruvaNvaNDoor,
naadhan nyaalam ellaam uNDa nam perumaanai kaNDu,
paadham kaithozhudhu paNiyeer aDiyeen thirame.
557.meaning: O dark crane that feeds together with your beloved tender mate! When you behold our Lord and master of cool Tiruvanvandur, where the sound of the Vedic sacrifices resounds, He who swallowed all the world, worship His feet with folded hands and tell Him of the plight of me, His servant.

558.translit:
thirangkaL aagi engkum sey kaLooDu uzhal puLLinangkaaL,
sirandha selvam malgu thiruvaNvaNDoor uraiyum,
karanggu chakkarak kai kani vaai perumaanai kaNDu,
iranggi neer thozhudhu paNiyeer aDiyeen iDare.
558.meaning: O flocks of birds that wander everywhere among the fields in your various ways! When you behold the Lord of the coral-red lips, whose hand bears the whirring discus, who dwells in Tiruvanvandur abounding in surpassing wealth, take pity, worship Him and tell Him of the distress of me, His servant.

559.translit:
iDaril boogam moozhgi iNaindhu aaDum maDa annangkaaL,
viDal il veedha oli muzhangkum thaN thiruvaNvaNDoor,
kaDalil meeni piraan kaNNanai neDu maalai kaNDu,
uDalam naindhu orutthi urugum enru uNartthumine.
559.meaning: O graceful swans that, immersed in unbroken delight, sport joined together in pairs! When you behold Kannan, the great Lord (Tirumal) of ocean-hued body who dwells in cool Tiruvanvandur where the unceasing sound of the Vedas resounds, make known to Him that a certain woman is wasting away in body and melting (with love).

560.translit:
uNartthal oodal uNarndhudan meeyum madavannangaaL,
thiNartththa vaNdalgaL meel sangu seerum thiruvaNvaNdoor,
puNarttha poondhaN thuzhaaymudi nampperumaanaik kaNdu,
puNarttha kaiyinaraay adiyeenukkum poorruminee.
560.meaning: O gentle swans who, even as you sense and quarrel, graze together! In Tiruvanvandur, where conches gather upon the heaped fertile sandbanks, go and behold our Lord who wears upon His crown the fragrant, cool tulasi garland. With your hands joined in worship, offer praise on behalf of me, His servant, too.

561.translit:
poorri yaan irandheen punnai meelurai poonguyilgaaL,
seerril vaaLai thuLLum thiruvaNvaNdooruraiyum,
aarral aazhiyang kaiyamarar perumaanaik kaNdu,
maarrang koNdaruLeer maiyal theervadhu oruvaNNamee.
561.meaning: O lovely cuckoos who dwell upon the punnai tree, I beseech you with praise! Behold the Lord of the immortals, who holds the mighty discus in His beautiful hand, dwelling in Tiruvanvandur where the vaaLai fish leap in the mire. Bring back His word and grace me, that in some way this lovesickness of mine may be cured.

562.translit:
oruvaNNam senru pukkenakku onrurai oNkiLiyee,
seruvoN poompozhil soozh sekkar veelai thiruvaNvaNdoor,
karuvaNNam seyyavaay seyyakaN seyyakai seyyakaal,
seruvoN chakkaram sangu adaiyaaLam thirundhak kaNdee.
562.meaning: O bright parrot, go in some manner, enter, and bring back to me a word! In Tiruvanvandur, surrounded by lush flowering groves and the red-hued sea, behold clearly His marks: the dark-cloud complexion, the red mouth, the red eyes, the red hands, the red feet, and the emblems of the fierce bright discus and the conch.

563.translit:
thirundhak kaNdenakku onrurai aay oN siruboovaay,
serundhi njaazhal magizh punnai soozh thaN thiruvaNvaNdoor,
perundhaN thaamaraik kaN peru neeNmudi naaL thadandhooL,
karundhiN maamugil poolthiru meeni adigaLaiyee.
563.meaning: O bright little mynah, behold Him clearly and bring back to me a word! In cool Tiruvanvandur, encircled by serundhi, njaazhal, magizh and punnai trees, behold the Lord with the great cool lotus eyes, the tall long crown, the broad mighty shoulders, and the sacred form like a dark, dense great cloud.

564.translit:
adigaL kaithozhudhu alarmeel asaiyum annangaaL,
vidivai sangolikkum thiruvaNvaNdooruraiyum,
kadiya maayandhannaik kaNNanai nedumaalaik kaNdu,
kodiya valvinaiyeen thiram kooruminveeru koNdee.
564.meaning: O swans who sway upon the flowers, joining your hands in worship of the Lord's feet! Behold the swift mysterious Lord, Kannan, the great Tirumal, who dwells in Tiruvanvandur where conches resound at daybreak. On behalf of me, who am laden with cruel, mighty sins, speak my plight to Him, setting it apart.

565.translit:
veeru koNdummai yaan irandheen veri vaNdinangaaL,
theeru neerp pambai vadabaalai thiruvaNvaNdoor,
maaril poraraikkan madhiL neerezhach serrugandha,
eeru seevakanaarkku ennaiyum uLaL enminkaLee.
565.meaning: O swarms of fragrant bees, I beseech you, setting this matter apart! In Tiruvanvandur, on the northern bank of the clear-watered Pampai river, dwells the bull-like valiant Lord who rejoiced to destroy the matchless warring demon's ramparts to ashes. Tell Him that I too exist for Him.

566.translit:
minkoL seerpuri nool kuraLaay agal njaalam koNda,
vankaLvan adimeel kurugoorch chadagoopan sonna,
paNkoL aayiratthuL ivai patthum thiruvaNvaNdoorkku,
inkoL paadal vallaar madhanar minnidaiyavarkkee.
566.meaning: These ten verses of the thousand set to music, sung by Satakopan of Kurugur upon the feet of the cunning thief who, taking the form of the dwarf wearing the lustrous sacred thread, seized the wide earth. Those able to sing these sweet songs in praise of Tiruvanvandur will become loved ones to the slender-waisted women.

567.translit:
minnidai madavaargaL ninnaruL sooduvaar munbu naan adhanjuvan,
mannudai ilangai araNkaayndha maayavanee!
unnudaiya suNdaayam naan arivan iniyadhu koNdu seyvadhen,
ennudaiya pandhum suzhalum thandhu poogu nambee!
567.meaning: O mysterious Lord who burnt down the fortress of enduring Lanka! I am afraid that, in front of me, the lightning-waisted maidens will receive your grace. I know your trickery well; now what is the use of carrying on with it? Give back my ball and my spinning-top and be gone, O Nambi!

568.translit:
poogu nambee! un thaamarai puraikaN iNaiyum sevvaay muruvalum,
aagulangaL seyya azhidharkkee noorroomee yaam?,
thoogai maamayilaargaL ninnaruL sooduvaar seviyoosai vaitthezha,
aagaL poogavittup kuzhaloodhu poyirundhee.
568.meaning: Be gone, O Nambi! Did we undertake penance only to be ruined by your lotus-like eyes, your red mouth, and your smile that cause such distress? The women lovely as great peacocks would receive your grace, setting their ears to listen; so set the cows free to graze, go off somewhere, and play your flute there.

569.translit:
poyirundhum nin puLLuvam ariyaadhavarkkurai nambi! nin seyya
vaay irungkaniyum kaNgaLum vipareedham innaaL,
veey irundhadanthooLinaar ithth iruvaruL peruvaar yavar kol
maayirung kadalaik kadaindha perumaanaalee?
569.meaning: Go off somewhere and tell your deceit to those who do not know it, O Nambi! These days your red mouth like a great ripe fruit and your eyes are deceitful. Who indeed will be the women of bamboo-like broad shoulders to win this grace from the Lord who churned the vast dark ocean?

570.translit:
aalin neeLilai eezhulagam uNdanru nee kidandhaay, un maayangaL
meelai vaanavarum ariyaar iniyem paramee?,
veelin neertthadang kaNNinaar viLaiyaadu soozhalaich soozhavee ninru
kaali meeykka vallaay! emmai nee kazharreelee.
570.meaning: On that day you lay upon the long leaf of the banyan, having swallowed the seven worlds; even the high celestials do not know your wonders, so are they within our measure? O you who are able to graze the cattle while hovering about the playground where the women with cool spear-like eyes play, do not scold us!

571.translit:
kazharreel nambee un kaidhavam maNNum viNNum nankariyum, thiN chakkara
neezharu tholpadaiyaay. unakku onrunartthuvan naan,
mazharu theenmozhiyaargaL ninnaruL sooduvaar manam vaadi nirka, em
kuzharu poovaiyoodum kiLiyoodum kuzhageelee.
571.meaning: Do not scold, O Nambi! Both earth and heaven know well your trickery, O wielder of the ancient weapon, the radiant firm discus. I shall tell you one thing: while the women of sweet, honeyed lisping speech who would receive your grace stand with wilting hearts, do not consort intimately with our babbling mynah and parrot.

572.translit:
kuzhagi engaL kuzhamaNan koNdu koyinmai seydhu kanmam onrillai,
pazhagi yaamiruppoom paramee ithth iruvaruLgaL?,
azhagi yaar ivvulagu moonrukkum theevi thamai thaguvaar palaruLar,
kazhaga meereel nambee! unakkum iLaidhe kanmamee.
572.meaning: Sporting with us and snatching away our little sand-dolls, playing thievish tricks, is no deed worth doing. Is this grace of yours something we can grow used to and abide? There are many lovely women fit to be consorts for all these three worlds. Do not climb into our circle, O Nambi! Such deeds are beneath even you.

573.translit:
kanmam anru engaL kaiyil paavai parippadhu kadalnjaala muNditta,
ninmalaa nediyaay. unakkeelum pizhai pizhaiyee,
vanmamee solli emmai nee viLaiyaaduthi adhukeerkil
ennaimaar, thanma paavam ennaar oru naanru thadipiNakkee.
573.meaning: It is no proper deed to snatch the dolls from our hands, O flawless, lofty One who swallowed the sea-girt earth! Even for you, a fault is still a fault. You torment us with cruel words and toy with us; if my brothers hear of it, one day they will not consider right and wrong but will come to blows in a quarrel.

574.translit:
piNakki yaavaiyum yaavarum pizhaiyaamal peedhitthum peedhi yaadhadhu, oor
kaNakkil keertthi veLLak kadhirnjaana moortthiyinaay,
iNakki emmai en thoozhimaar viLaiyaadap poodhumin ena poandhoomai,
uNakki nee vaLaitthaal en sollaar uga vaadhavaree?
574.meaning: O Lord of radiant wisdom, of fame like a boundless flood, who without fault distinguish and yet remain undistinguished from all beings and all things! When my companions coaxed us saying 'come, let us play,' and we came, if you parch us and surround us, what will those who do not approve not say?

575.translit:
ugavaiyaal nenjam uLLurugi un thaamaraith thadangaN vizhigaLin,
agavalaip paduppaan azhitthaay un thiruvadiyaal,
thagavu seydhilai engaL sirrilum yaam adu siru sooruN kaNdu, nin
mugavoLi thigazha muruval seydhu ninrilaiyee.
575.meaning: To ensnare us in the net of the glances of your large lotus eyes, so that our hearts melt within with joy, you destroyed our play with your sacred feet. You showed no grace; seeing our little sandcastle and the little rice we cook, you did not stand smiling with your face aglow.

576.translit:
ninrilangu mudiyinaay. irubatthoor kaal arasu kaLai katta,
venri neeNmazhuvaa viyanjnaalam munpadaitthaay,
inriv vaayar kulatthai veeduyyath thoonriya karumaaNikkach chudar,
nintannaal nalivee paduvoom enrum aaychchiyoomee.
576.meaning: O you with the shining crown! O victorious one of the long battle-axe who twenty-one times rooted out the kingly race, who long ago created the vast world! O dark-sapphire radiance who appeared so that this clan of cowherds might attain salvation! We are forever cowherd women who suffer torment at your hands.

577.translit:
aaychchi yaagiya annaiyaal anru veNNey vaartthaiyuL,
seerra muNdazhu kootthа appan thannaik kurugoorch chadagoopan,
eetthiya thamizhmaalai aayiratthuL ivaiyum oorpath thisaiyoodum,
naatthannaal navila uraippaarkku illai nalkuravee.
577.meaning: On the cowherd-mother Yashoda, in the matter of the butter, the Father who, when scolded, ate and wept and danced—of the Tamil garland of a thousand verses praising Him, sung by Satakopan of Kurugur, these ten too, set to melody. For those who recite them aloud with their tongue, there will be no poverty.

578.translit:
nalkuravum selvum naragum suvarkkamum aay,
velpagaiyum natpum vidamum amudhamum aay,
palvagaiyum parandha perumaan ennai aaLvaanai,
selvam malgu kudith thiru viNNagark kaNdeenee.
578.meaning: Being poverty and wealth, hell and heaven, the conquering foe and the friend, poison and ambrosia—the great Lord who pervades all manifold things, who rules me—I beheld in Tiruvinnagar, the town teeming with wealthy households.

579.translit:
kaNda vinbam thunbam kalakkangaLum theerra mum aay,
thaNdamum thaNmaiyum thazhalum nizhalum aay,
kaNdukoo darkariya perumaan ennai aaLvaanoor,
theNdiraip punalsoozh thiruviNNagar nannagaree.
