579.meaning: Being the joy and sorrow that are seen, both confusion and clarity, being punishment and gentleness, flame and shade—the great Lord, hard to behold, who rules me, dwells in the goodly city of Tiruvinnagar, surrounded by clear-waved waters.

580.translit:
nagaramum naadugaLumu gnaanamum moodamum aay,
nigar il soozh, sudar aay, iruL aay, nilan aay, visumbu aay,
sigara maadangaL soozh tiruvaNNagar seerntha piraan
pugar koL keerthi allaal, illai- yaavarkkum puNNiyamE
580.meaning: He becomes the cities and the lands, wisdom and ignorance; He is the matchless all-pervading radiance and darkness, the earth and the sky. Save for the resplendent glory of the Lord who has settled in Tiruvinnagar girt by tower-crowned mansions, there is no other source of merit for anyone.

581.translit:
puNNiyam paavam, puNarchchi pirivu enRu ivai aay,
eNNa aay maRappu aay uNmai aay inmai aay, allan aay,
tiNNa maadangaL soozh tiruviNNagar seerntha piraan.
kaNNan in aruLE kaNdu koLminkaL – kaithavamE?
581.meaning: He becomes these: merit and sin, union and separation; He is remembrance and forgetting, existence and non-existence, and yet He is none of these. Behold and grasp the sweet grace of Kannan, the Lord who has settled in Tiruvinnagar girt by sturdy mansions - is this any deception?

582.translit:
kaithavam semmai karumai veLumaiyumaay,
meypoy yiLamai muthumaiputhumai pazhamaiyumaay,
seyyathiN mathiLsoozh tiruviNNagarch seernthapiraan,
peythakaavu kaNdeer perunthEvudai moovulagE.
582.meaning: He becomes deceit and uprightness, blackness and whiteness; He becomes truth and falsehood, youth and age, newness and oldness. Behold - the three worlds, possessed of the great Lord who has settled in Tiruvinnagar girt by fair, strong ramparts, are like a grove He has planted.

583.translit:
moovulagang kaLumaay allanaayugap paaymunivaay,
poovilvaazh magaLaayth thavvaiyaayppu zhaaypazhiyaay,
dEvarmE viththezhum tiruviNNagarch seernthapiraan,
paaviyEn manaththE yuRaiginRa paranjsudarE.
583.meaning: He becomes the three worlds and yet is not they; He is delight and wrath, the Lady who dwells in the lotus (Lakshmi) and her elder sister (Jyeshtha), worth and blame. He is the supreme radiance, the Lord who has settled in Tiruvinnagar where the gods gather and rise in worship, who dwells in the heart of this sinner.

584.translit:
paranjsudar udambaay azhukkupathiththa vudambaay,
karanthumthOn Riyumninnum kaithavangaL seyyum,viNNOr
sirangaLaal vaNangum tiruviNNagarch seernthapiraan,
varangoLpaatha mallaalillai yaavarkkum vansaraNE.
584.meaning: He becomes the body of supreme radiance and the body soiled with impurity; hidden, appearing, and standing forth He performs His wondrous deeds. Save for the boon-granting feet of the Lord who has settled in Tiruvinnagar - which the celestials worship with bowed heads - there is no strong refuge for anyone.

585.translit:
vansaraN surarkkaay asurarkkuveng kooRRamumaay,
thansaraN nizhaRkee zhulagamvaiththum vaiyaathum,
thensaraN thisaikkuth tiruviNNagarch seernthapiraan,
ensaraNen kaNNan ennaiyaaludai ennappanE.
585.meaning: He is the strong refuge for the gods and the fierce death-god for the demons; placing the worlds beneath the shade of His feet as refuge, and not placing them so. He is the Lord who has settled in Tiruvinnagar of the southern quarter; He is my refuge, my Kannan, my Father who has made me His own.

586.translit:
ennappan enakkaayiguLaay ennaip peRRavaLaay,
ponnappan maNiyappan muththappanen appanumaay,
minnappon mathiLsoozhtiru viNNagarch seernthavappan,
thannoppaa rillappan thanthanan thana thaaLnizhalE.
586.meaning: He is my Father, being for my sake both companion and the mother who bore me; He is my golden Father, my jewel Father, my pearl Father, my Father indeed. The Father who has settled in Tiruvinnagar girt by gold ramparts that gleam like lightning, the Father who has no equal, has granted me the shade of His feet.

587.translit:
nizhalveyil siRumaiperumai kuRumai nedumaiyumaay,
suzhalvananiR panamaRRu maayavai allanumaay,
mazhalaivaazh vaNduvaazh tiruviNNagar mannupiraan,
kazhalkaLanRi maRROr kaLaikaNilam kaaNminkaLE.
587.meaning: He becomes shade and sunlight, smallness and greatness, shortness and length; He becomes the things that move and the things that stand still, and yet He is none of them. Behold - except for the feet of the Lord enthroned in Tiruvinnagar, where bees that lisp sweet murmurs dwell, we have no other recourse.

588.translit:
kaaNminka Lulageer enRu kaNmugap pEnimirntha,
thaaLiNaiyan thannaik kurugoorch sadakOpan sonna,
aaNaiyaa yiraththuththiru viNNagarppath thumvallar,
kONaiyinRi viNNOrk konRumaavarkuravarkaLE.
588.meaning: Of Him whose pair of feet stretched up before the very eyes of all, saying 'Behold, O people of the world!', Satakopan of Kurugur sang. Those who master these ten verses on Tiruvinnagar, among the commanding thousand, will without crookedness become honored teachers, indeed one with the celestials.

589.translit:
kuravai yaaychchiya rOdu kOththathum kunRamon REnthiyathum
uravuneerp poygai naagam kaaynthathum udpada maRRumpala,
aravil paLLip piraanthan maaya vinaikaLai yEyalaRRi,
iravum nanpaka lumtha virkilam enna kuRaivenakkE?
589.meaning: Joining the cowherd women in the kuravai dance, lifting up a single hill, scorching the serpent in the deep-watered pond, and many other deeds besides - crying out continually upon these wondrous deeds of the Lord who reclines on the serpent, I will not cease either by night or in the good day. What lack then is there for me?

590.translit:
kEyath theenguzha loothiRRum niraimEyth thathum,keNdai yoNkaN
vaasap poonguzhal pinnai thOLgaL maNanthathum maRRumpala,
maayak kOlap piraanthan seykai ninainthu manamkuzhainthu,
nEyath thOdu kazhintha pOthenak kevvula kamnikarE?
590.meaning: Piping on the sweet melodious flute, grazing the cattle, embracing the shoulders of Pinnai of carp-like fair eyes and fragrant flowery tresses, and many other deeds besides - when, with my heart melting in love as I muse on the deeds of the Lord of wondrous beauty, my time passes, what world can equal mine?

591.translit:
nigarilmalla raichcheRRa thumnirai mEyththathum neeNedungaich,
sikaramaagaLi RattathumivaipOlvanavum piRavum,
pugarkoL sOthip piraanthan seykai ninainthu pulambi, enRum
nugar vaikal vaikappeR REn enakku eniniyininozhuvavu.
591.meaning: Destroying the matchless wrestlers, grazing the cattle, slaying the elephant of mountain-like trunk and tusks, and deeds like these and others besides - musing and lamenting upon the deeds of the radiant, lustrous Lord, I have been granted days that I pass in such savoring forever. What is there now to grieve me?

592.translit:
nOva aaychchi yuralO daarkka irangiRRum vanjappeNNaich,
saavap paaluN dathumoor sakadam iRachchaa diyathum,
dhEvak kOla piraanthan seykai ninainthu manamkuzhainthu,
mEvak kaalangaL koodi nEnenakku eninivENduvathE?
592.meaning: Yielding meekly when the cowherd-mother bound Him painfully to the mortar, drinking the milk of the treacherous demoness (Putana) so that she died, kicking and shattering the cart that came against the village, and such deeds - musing on the deeds of the Lord of divine beauty, my heart melting, I have attained the times for union with Him. What more do I now need?

593.translit:
vEndi dhEva rirakka vanthu piRanthathum veengiruLvaay,
pooNdan Rannai pulambap pOyangOr aaykkulam pukkathum,
kaaNda linRi vaLarnthu kanjanaith thunjavanj samseythathum,
eeNdu naanalaR Rappper Renenakku enna igaluLathE?
593.meaning: Coming to be born when the gods prayed and besought, going off in the swelling darkness while His mother lamented and entering a certain cowherd clan there, growing up unseen and treacherously bringing about the death of Kamsa - I have been granted to cry these out abundantly. What enmity then is there against me?

594.translit:
igalkoL puLLai piLantha thumi mil ERugaL seRRathuvum,
uyarkoL sOlaik kurunho siththathum udpada maRRumpala,
agalkoL vaiyam aLantha maayanen appanRan maayangaLE,
pagaliraap paravap peRREn enakkenna manappa rippE?
594.meaning: Splitting open the hostile bird (Bakasura), subduing the humped bulls, breaking the kurunda tree of the lofty grove, and many deeds besides - the wondrous works of my Father, the marvel who measured the wide earth, I have been granted to extol day and night. What anxiety of mind then is there for me?

595.translit:
manappari pOda zhukku maanida saathiyil thaanpiRanthu,
thanakku vENduruk koNdu thaanRana seeRRaththinai mudikkum,
punaththu zhaaymudi maalai maarbanen appanthan maayangaLE,
ninaikkum nenjudai yEnne nakkiniyaar nigar neeNilaththE?
595.meaning: Being born of His own will into the human race that is full of mental affliction and defilement, taking on whatever form He needs and fulfilling His wrath - the wondrous works of my Father whose chest bears the garland of fresh tulasi from the field, these my heart muses upon. Who now on this wide earth is equal to me?

596.translit:
neeNilath thoduvaan viyappa niRaiperum pOrgaL seythu,
vaaNa naayiram thOLthu Niththathum udpada maRRumpala,
maaNi yaaynilam koNda maayanen appanRan maayangaLE,
kaaNum nenjudai yEnenak kiniyena kalakka muNdE?
596.meaning: Waging great teeming wars so that the wide earth and the heavens marvelled, severing the thousand shoulders of Bana, and many deeds besides - the wondrous works of my Father, the marvel who took the earth in the guise of a young brahmin (Vamana), these my heart beholds. What confusion can there now be for me?

597.translit:
kalakka vEzhkada lEzhmalai yulagE zhumkazhi yakkadaay,
ulakkath thEr kodu senRa maayamum udpada maRRumpala,
valakkai yaazhi yidakkai sangam ivaiyudai maalvaNNanai,
malakkunaa vudaiyERku maaRuLa thOvim maNNin misaiyE?
597.meaning: Churning the seven seas, seven mountains and seven worlds in turmoil, the wonder of driving forth with a chariot to destruction, and many deeds besides - for me who have a tongue that stirs in praise of the dark-hued Lord who bears the discus in His right hand and the conch in His left, is there any rival here upon this earth?

598.translit:
maNmisaip perumbaaram neengavOrbaaratha maape rumpOr,
paNNi, maayangaL seythu, sEnaiyaip paazhpada nooRRittuppOy,
viNmi saiththana thaama mEpuka mEviya sOthithanthaaL,
naNNi naanvaNang appeR Renenak kaarpiRar naayagarE?
598.meaning: Waging the great Bharata war so that the great burden upon the earth was removed, working His wonders, grinding the armies to ruin, and then going to enter His own abode high in the heavens - I have been granted to approach and worship the feet of that Radiant One. Who else then can be my Lord?

599.translit:
naaya kanmuzhu vEzhula kukkumaay muzhuvE zhulagum,than
vaaya kampuka vaiththumizhn thavaiyaay avaiyal lanumaam,
kEsavan adiyi Naimisaikkuru koorchchada kOpan sonna
thooya vaayiraththippath thaal paththaraavar thuvaLinRiyE.
599.meaning: He is the Lord of all the seven worlds entire, and placing all the seven worlds within His mouth He held them and spat them forth, becoming them and yet not being them. Of the pure thousand that Satakopan of Kurugur sang upon the pair of feet of Kesava, by this decad people will become devotees without flinching.

60.translit:
madhuvaar thaNNan thuzhaayaan muudhuvee tha mudhal vanukku
edhuvee thenbaNi ennaadhuvee yaaTcheyyum eeDee.
60.meaning: To the primordial First One adorned with the cool, fresh, honey-laden tulasi garland, He who is the source of the ancient Vedas, what indeed is the fitting service? Without questioning thus, simply to render service is itself the highest goal.

61.translit:
eeDum eDuppum ileesan maaDu viDaa en mananee
paaDum en naavalan paaDal aaDum en angam aNangee.
61.meaning: The Lord who has neither rising nor falling, neither equal nor diminishing -- my mind will never leave His side. My eloquent tongue sings His song, and my body dances, possessed as if by a divine spirit.

62.translit:
aNangena aaDum en angam vaNangi vazhipaDum eesan
piNangi amarar pidhaRRum guNangezhu koLkaiyinaanee.
62.meaning: My body dances as though seized by a deity, bowing and worshipping the Lord. He is of such a nature, rich in noble qualities, that even the immortals, vying with one another, babble forth His praise.

63.translit:
koLkai koLaamai ilaadhaan eLkal iraagam ilaadhaan
viLkai viLLaamai virumbi uLkalandhaarkku oor amudhee.
63.meaning: He who is free of accepting or rejecting, free of contempt or attachment -- to those who, longing for an unbroken, never-failing union, have mingled with Him within, He is the one nectar.

64.translit:
amudham amararkku eendha nimirsuDar aazhi neDumaal
amudhilum aaRRa iniyan nimirthirai neeLkaDalaanee.
64.meaning: The great Tirumal who gave the immortals the nectar, who bears the upward-blazing discus -- He is far sweeter than that very nectar, He who reclines upon the vast ocean of rising waves.

65.translit:
neeLkaDal soozh ilangaikkoon thoaLgaL thalaithuNi seydhaan
thaaLgaL thalaiyil vaNangi naaLgaL thalaik kazhiminee.
65.meaning: He severed the shoulders and heads of the king of Lanka, which was girdled by the vast ocean. Bowing your heads at His feet, pass your days letting them go forward in His service.

66.translit:
kazhimin thoNDeergaL kazhiththuth thozhumeen avanaith thozhudhaal
vazhininRa valvinai maaLviththu azhivinRi aakkam tharumee.
66.meaning: O devotees, cast away (worldly attachments) and worship; if you worship Him, He will destroy the cruel karma that stands in your path, and grant you imperishable, ever-increasing flourishing.

67.translit:
tharuma arumbayanaaya thirumagaLaar thanik keeLvan,
perumai uDaiya piraanaar, irumai vinaikaDi vaaree.
67.meaning: The peerless consort of Lakshmi, who is the rare fruit of righteousness -- the Lord possessed of greatness -- He will sever the twofold karma (of good and evil deeds).

68.translit:
kaDivaar theeya vinaigaL noDiyaarum aLavaikkaN
koDiyaa aDupuL uyarththa vaDivaar maadhavanaaree.
68.meaning: He will sever away the evil deeds in the mere measure of an instant -- Madhava of beautiful form, who has raised aloft on His banner the killing bird (Garuda) as His emblem.

69.translit:
maadhavanpaal saDakoapan theedhavam inRi uraiththa
eedham ilaayiraththu ippaththu oadhavallaarpiRavaaree.
69.meaning: This decad among the flawless thousand verses, which Sathakopan uttered upon Madhava without fault or blemish -- those able to recite it shall not be born again.

70.translit:
piRaviththuyaraRa gnaanaththuL ninRu,
thuRaviccuDar viLakkam thalaippeyvaar,
aRavanai aazhippaDai andhaNanai,
maRaviyai inRi manaththu vaippaaree.
70.meaning: Those who, standing firm in wisdom so that the sorrow of birth is ended, attain the radiant lamp of renunciation, will keep in their minds, without forgetting, the One of righteousness, the pure Lord who bears the discus as His weapon.

71.translit:
vaippaam marundhaam aDiyarai, valvinaith
thuppaam pulanaindhum thunjaka koaDaanavan,
eppaal evarkkum nalaththaal uyarndhu uyarndhu,
appaalavan engaL aayar kozhundhee.
71.meaning: He is the treasure and the remedy for His devotees; He does not let the five senses, which are the strength of cruel karma, perish (overcome them). Rising higher and higher in excellence beyond all and everyone everywhere, He who is beyond the beyond is the tender sprout (chief) of us cowherds.

72.translit:
aayar kozhundhaay avaraal puDaiyuNNum,
maayap piraanai en maaNikkac soadhiyai,
thooya amudhaip parugip parugi, en
maayap piRavi mayaravuRuththeenee.
72.meaning: The tender sprout of the cowherds, the wondrous Lord who let Himself be beaten by them, my radiance of the ruby gem -- drinking and drinking that pure nectar, I have rid myself of the bewilderment of my illusory birth.

73.translit:
mayaravu en manaththee manninaan thannai,
uyarvinaiyee tharum oNsuDark kaRRaiyai,
ayarvil amarargaL aadhik kozhundhai, en
isaivinai en solli yaan viDuveenee.
73.meaning: He who, removing my confusion, took firm abode within my heart; the cluster of bright radiance that grants only exaltation; the primordial sprout (chief) of the unwearying immortals -- saying what could I, who am set on Him, ever let Him go?

74.translit:
viDuveenoa en viLakkai en aaviyai,
naDuvee vandhu uyyak koLkinRa naadhanai,
thoDuvee seydhu iLa aaycchiyar kaNNinuL,
viDavee seydhu vizhikkum piraanaiyee.
74.meaning: Would I ever let Him go -- my lamp, my very life-breath, the Lord who came in the midst (of my helplessness) and redeems me? He who, touching (the butter to taste it), and casting glances into the eyes of the young cowherd women, looks back at them.

75.translit:
piraan perunilang keeNDavan, pinnum
viraaymalarth thuzhaay veyndhamuDiyan,
maraamaram eydha maayavan, ennuL
iraanenil pinnai yaan oTTuveenoa.
75.meaning: The Lord who clove the great earth (lifting it as the Boar); moreover He whose crown is wreathed with fragrant tulasi blossoms; the wondrous one who shot through the maraa trees -- if He will not dwell within me, then would I consent (to let it be)?

76.translit:
yaan oTTi ennuL iruththuvan enRilan,
thaan oTTi vandhu en thaninenjai vanjiththu,
oon oTTi ninRu en uyiril kalandhu, iyal
vaan oTTumoa ini ennai negizhkkavee.
76.meaning: I did not say, 'I will draw Him in and seat Him within me.' He of His own accord came, captivated my lonely heart, clung even to my flesh, and mingled into my very life. Can it be possible now for Him ever to loosen His hold and slip away from me?

77.translit:
ennai negizhkkilum ennuDai nannenjam
thannai, agalvikkath thaanum killaan ini,
pinnai neDumpaNaith thoaL magizh peeDuDai,
munnai amarar muzhumudhalaanee.
77.meaning: Even were He to loosen His hold on me, He cannot now make my good heart depart from Him -- He who delights in the long bamboo-like shoulders of Nappinnai, the majestic One, the absolute origin of the ancient immortals.

78.translit:
amarar muzhumudhalaagiya aadhiyai,
amararkku amudhu eendha aayar kozhundhai,
amara azhumbath thuzhaavi en aavi,
amararth thazhuviRRu iniya kalumoa.
78.meaning: The primal One who is the absolute origin of the immortals, the tender sprout of the cowherds who gave the immortals nectar -- my soul, having searched and clung to Him so as to abide and sink deep, having embraced Him among the immortals, how could it now part from Him?

79.translit:
agalil agalum aNugil aNugum,
pugalum ariyan poruvallan emmaan,
nigaril avan pugazh paaDi iLaippilam,
pagalum iravum paDindhu kuDaindhee.
79.meaning: If you withdraw, He withdraws; if you draw near, He draws near; hard to attain, our Lord who has no equal. Singing His matchless glory, we do not grow weary, immersing and steeping ourselves in it day and night.

600.translit:
tuvaLil maamaNi maada moongu tolaivilli mangalam tozhum
ivaLai, neer iniyannai meer! umakkaasai illai vidumino,
tavaLa voNshangu chakkaram enrum taamarai-t-tadang kaN enrum,
kuvaLai voNmalar-k kaNgaL neer-malga ninru ninru kumurume.
600.meaning: O mothers! This girl worships flawless Tolaivillimangalam with its tall mansions set with great gems; you have no claim on her any longer, so let her go. Saying 'the spotless bright conch and discus' and 'the eyes wide as the lotus pool,' her eyes lovely as the kuvalai blossom brim with tears as she stands again and again, sobbing aloud.

601.translit:
kumiru mosai vizhavolittolai villi mangalam koNdu pukku,
amuda menmozhiyaaLai neer umakkaasai inri akarrineer,
timirk-koNdaal ottu nirkkum marrivaL deva deva piraan enre,
nimiyum vaayodu kaNgaL neer-malga nekkosindu karaiyume.
601.meaning: You drove away this girl of sweet, gentle, nectar-like words, taking her into Tolaivillimangalam that resounds with the swelling din of festivals, claiming you had no need of her. Now she stands as if dazed and frozen, saying only 'He is the Lord, God of gods'; with parting lips and eyes brimming with tears she softens, melts and dissolves away.

602.translit:
karaikoL paimpozhil taNpaNai-t-tolai villi mangalam koNdu pukku,
uraikoL inmozhiyaaLai neer umakkaasai inri akarrineer,
tiraikoL pauvattu serndatum-tisai NYaalam taavi yaLandatum,
niraigaL meyttatume bitarri nedungaN neer-malga nirkkume.
602.meaning: You took away this girl of sweet, worthy words into Tolaivillimangalam with its cool fields and bank-lined verdant groves, claiming you had no need of her. Now babbling of how He reclined upon the wave-tossed ocean, how He strode and measured the worlds in every direction, and how He grazed the herds of cattle, she stands with her long eyes brimming with tears.

603.translit:
nirkkum naanmarai vaaNar vaazh-tolai villi mangalam kaNda pin,
arkam onrum arivuraaL malindaaL kaNdeer ivaL annaimeer,
karkkum kalvi ellaam karungadal vaNNan kaNNa piraan enre,
orkam onrum ilaL ugandu-ugandu uL-magizhndu kuzhaiyume.
603.meaning: O mothers! Ever since she beheld Tolaivillimangalam where the masters of the four Vedas abide and flourish, behold, she has lost all sense of her own self and overflows with love. All the learning she would learn is only 'He is the dark-sea-hued Lord, Kannan'; without the least slackening she rejoices and rejoices, glad within, and melts away.

604.translit:
kuzhaiyum vaaLmugattu ezhaiyai-t-tolai villi mangalam koNdu pukku,
izhaikoL sodi-c-cendaamarai-k kaN piraan irundamai kaattineer,
mazhaipey-daal okkum kaNNa neerinodu anru tottu mai-yaandu, ivaL
nuzhaiyum sindaiyaL annaimeer, tozhum at-tisai yurru nokkiye.
604.meaning: You took this tender girl of radiant, melting face into Tolaivillimangalam and showed her where the Lord dwells, He of the red lotus eyes glowing with jeweled splendour. O mothers! From that day her eyes pour tears like falling rain, her kohl smeared away; her mind has entered into Him, and she gazes fixed toward that quarter, worshipping it.

605.translit:
nokkum pakkam ellaam karumbodu sennel oongu sendaamarai,
vaaykkum taNporunal vadakarai vandu tolaivilli mangalam,
nokkumel at-tisai allaal maru nokkilaL vaigal naaL-dorum,
vaaykkoL vaasakamum maNivaNNan naame ivaL annaimeer.
605.meaning: On every side one looks, sugarcane, tall red paddy and red lotuses flourish at Tolaivillimangalam, come to the northern bank of the cool Porunal river. O mothers! Day after day, if she looks at all she looks toward that quarter and no other, and the words upon her lips are only the name of the sapphire-hued Lord.

606.translit:
annaimeer! aNimaamayil sirumaan ivaL nammai-k kaivalindu,
enna vaarttaiyum keekkuraaL tolaivilli mangalam enr-allaal,
munnam nora vidhi-kolo mugil vaNNan maayam-kolo, avan
sinnamum tirunaama mum ivaL vaayanagaL tirundave.
606.meaning: O mothers! This girl, lovely as a fine peacock, like a little fawn, has slipped from our hands and will heed no word of ours except 'Tolaivillimangalam.' Is it some merit she earned by penance long ago, or is it the magic of the cloud-hued Lord? His emblems and His holy names alone come perfectly upon her lips.

607.translit:
tirundu vedamum veLviyum tiru maamagaLirum taam, malindu,
tirundu vaazhporunal vadakarai vaN-tolaivilli mangalam,
karundadang kaNNi kaitozhuda annaaL todangi innaaL-dorum,
irundirundu aravinda-lochana! enrenre naindirangume.
607.meaning: At bounteous Tolaivillimangalam on the northern bank of the flourishing Porunal river, where the pure Vedas, the sacrifices, and the noble Sri (great auspicious women) abound, this dark-wide-eyed girl folded her hands in worship. From that day to this, sitting and sitting she pines, crying again and again, 'O lotus-eyed One!' and grieving.

608.translit:
irangi naaL-dorum vaay-vereei ivaL kaNNa neergaL alamara,
marangaL um-irang uvagai maNivaNNavo! enru koovumaal,
turangam vaay-piLandaan urai-tolai villi mangalam enru, tan
karangaL koop-pi-t tozhum av-voor-t-tiru naamam karradar pinnaiye.
608.meaning: Grieving day after day, with faltering mouth and tears welling and whirling in her eyes, she cries 'O sapphire-hued One!' in a way that would melt even the trees to pity. Ever since she learned the holy name of that town, Tolaivillimangalam, where dwells He who split open the horse-demon's mouth, she folds her hands and worships it.

609.translit:
pinnai-kol nilamaamagaL-kol? tirumagaL-kol? pirandittaaL,
enna maayam-kolo? ivaL nedu maal enre ninru koovumaal,
munni vandavan ninru irundurai yum-tolaivilli mangalam
senniyaal vaNangum av-voor-t-tiru naamam keeppadu sindaiye.
609.meaning: Was she born as Nappinnai, or as the great Earth-goddess, or as Lakshmi? What magic is this? She stands forever crying 'Nedumal!' She bows her head to Tolaivillimangalam, where He came of His own will and abides and dwells, and her whole mind longs only to hear the holy name of that town.

610.translit:
sindaiyaalum sollaalum seygaiyinaalum deva piraanaiye,
tandai taay enradainda vaN-kurukoor avar Satakopan-sol,
mundai aayirattuL ivai tolaivilli mangalattai-c-conna,
sendamizh pattum vallaar adimai seyvaar tirumaalukke.
610.meaning: These are the words of Satakopan of bounteous Kurukoor, who in thought, in word and in deed took the Lord, God of gods, as his father and mother. Those who master these ten verses of pure Tamil, which among his foremost thousand speak of Tolaivillimangalam, shall render loving service to Tirumal forever.

611.translit:
maalukku vaiyam aLanda maNaaLarkku,
neela-k karuniramega niyaayarkku,
kolac-cendaamarai-k kaNNarkku, en kongalar
elak-kuzhali izhandadu shange.
611.meaning: For Tirumal, the bridegroom who measured the world; for Him whose nature is like a dark blue-black cloud; for Him of the beautiful red lotus eyes - my daughter with fragrant blossoming tresses and aromatic locks has lost her bangles.

612.translit:
shanguvil vaaL taNdu chakkara-k kaiyarkku,
senkani vaay-c-ceyya taamarai kaNNarkku,
kongalar taNNan tuzhaay-mudiyaanukku, en
mangai izhandadu maamai nirame.
612.meaning: For Him who bears in His hands the conch, the bow, the sword, the mace and the discus; for Him of the ripe-red coral mouth and the red lotus eyes; for Him who wears upon His crown the cool, fragrant, blossoming tulasi - my girl has lost the golden lustre of her complexion.

613.translit:
nirangari-yaanukku needu-lagu uNda,
tiram-kiLar vaay-c-ciru kaLLan avarkku,
karangiya chakkara-k kaiya-vanukku, en
pirang-irung koondal izhandadu peede.
613.meaning: For Him of the dark hue, for Him who swallowed the wide worlds; for that little thief with His clever mouth full of guile; for Him who bears the whirling discus in His hand - my daughter of lustrous, abundant hair has lost her pride and dignity.

614.translit:
peeduDai naanmuganai-p padaittaanukku,
maaDuDai vaiyam aLanda maNaaLarkku,
naaDuDai mannarkku-t tudusel nambikku, en
paaDuDai algul izhandadu paNbe.
614.meaning: For Him who created the glorious four-faced Brahma; for the bridegroom who measured the wealth-laden world; for the perfect Lord who went as envoy to the kings who held the land - my broad-hipped girl has lost her gentle grace.

615.translit:
paNbuDai vedam payanda paranukku,
maNpurai vaiyam idanda varaaganukku,
teNpunal paLLi-yen tevap piraanukku, en
kaNpunai kodai izhandadu karpe.
615.meaning: For the Supreme One who brought forth the gracious Vedas; for the Boar (Varaha) who dug up the earth-filled world; for my Lord, God of gods, who reclines upon the clear waters - my garland-eyed daughter has lost her chaste resolve.

616.translit:
karpaka-k kaava-na narpala toLarkku,
porcudar-k kunrann-a poondaN mudiyarkku,
narpala taamarai naaNmalar-k kaiyarkku, en
virpuruva-k kodi torradu meyye.
616.meaning: For Him of many fine shoulders like a grove of wish-granting kalpaka trees; for Him whose cool flowered crown is like a hill of golden radiance; for Him whose hands are like many fine fresh-blown lotuses - my bow-browed slender vine of a girl has lost her very body.

617.translit:
meyyamar palkalan nan-kaNindaanukku,
paiyaravin-aNai-p paLLiyin-aanukku,
kaiyodu kaal-seyya kaNNa piraanukku, en
taiyal izhandadu tannudai-c-chaaye.
617.meaning: For Him who has well adorned His body with many ornaments; for Him who reclines upon the couch of the hooded serpent; for Kannan, the Lord whose hands and feet are red - my dear girl has lost her own radiant glow.

618.translit:
saaya-k kurundam osittha tamiyarkku,
maaya-c chakaDam udaittha maNaaLarkku,
peyai-p piNampaDa-p paaluN piraanukku, en
vaasa-k kuzhali izhandadu maaNbe.
618.meaning: For the matchless One who broke down the kurunda tree to topple it; for the bridegroom who kicked apart the demon-cart; for the Lord who sucked the breast till the demoness fell down a corpse - my daughter of fragrant tresses has lost her dignity.

619.translit:
maaNbamai kolattem maaya-k kuRaLarkku,
seNcudar-k kunrann-a senchudar moorttikku,
kaaNberun torrattem kaakutta nambikku, en
pooNpunai menmulai torradu porpe.
619.meaning: For our wondrous Dwarf (Vamana) of perfect, splendid form; for Him of red-radiant form, like a far-shining hill of light; for our perfect Lord Kakuttha (Rama) of vast, glorious aspect to behold - my soft-breasted, ornament-decked girl has lost her beauty.

620.translit:
poRpamai neeNmuDip poondhaN duzhaiyaRku,
maRporu thoaLuDai maaiyap piraanukku,
niRpana palluru vaainiRku maaiyaRku, en
kaRpuDai yaaTTi yizhandhadhu kaTTea.
620.meaning: To Him who wears the cool fragrant tulasi garland upon His beautiful tall crown, to the wondrous Lord whose shoulders are mighty in wrestling, to the marvellous one who stands as many forms while remaining the abiding one — my chaste maiden (daughter) has lost her bond and gone to Him.

621.translit:
kaTTezhil soalainal veangaDa vaaNanai,
kaTTezhil thenkuru koorccaDa koapansol,
kaTTezhil aayirath ippaththum vallavar,
kaTTezhil vaanavar poagamuN paarea.
621.meaning: Of the Lord of Venkatam dwelling amid groves of structured beauty, these ten verses among the thousand of richly beautiful crafting, composed by Satakopan of beautiful southern Kurukur — those who can master them shall feast upon the abundant bliss of the celestials of glorious splendour.

622.translit:
uNNuny soaRu paruguneer thinnumveR Rilaiyumellaam
kaNNan, emperumaan nenRen Reakangkal neermalgi,
maNNinuL avanseer vaLammik kavanoor vinavi,
thiNNa menniLa maanpugu moorthiruk koaLoorea.
622.meaning: The rice she eats, the water she drinks, the betel-leaf she chews — all of it is 'Kannan, my Lord!' she keeps saying, her eyes brimming with tears. Asking after His town rich with the abundance of His glory upon the earth, surely the place my tender doe-like daughter will enter is Tirukkolur.

623.translit:
oorum naaDum ulagamum thannaippoal avanuDaiya
pearum thaarkaLumea pidhaRRa, kaRpu vaan iDaRi,
searum nal vaLam sear pazhanath thirukkoaloorkkea
poarumkol, uraiyeer, koDiyeankoDi-poovaikaLea?
623.meaning: Town, country and world all babble His name and His garlands just as she does; her chastity tripped over and broke. Tell me, O myna-birds of this cruel woman — will she manage to reach Tirukkolur set amid good fertile fields with their fine bounty?

624.translit:
poovaipaingkiLigaL pandhudhoodhaipoompaTTilkaL
yaavaiyumthirumaal thirunaamangaLea kooviyezhum en
paavaipoayinith thaNpazhanaththirukkoaloorkkea
koavaivaaithuDippa mazhaikkaNNoDen seyyungkoloa?
624.meaning: Her myna, her green parrots, ball, doll, fine silken garments — to all of them she would call out only the holy names of Tirumal as she rose. My girl has gone now to cool, field-rich Tirukkolur; with her coral-red lips quivering and her rain-cloud eyes, what will she do there?

625.translit:
kollai yenparko loaguNam mikkanaL enparkoloa,
sillai vaaippeN DukaLayaR seariyuL Laarumellea,
selvam malgi yavan kiDandha thirukkoa loorkkea,
mellisai nuDanga iLamaan sella meavinaLea.
625.meaning: Will they call her wild? Will they say she had excessive (wayward) nature? The chattering women of the neighbouring quarter — gently now! My doe-like daughter has set her heart on going to Tirukkolur, where He reclines amid swelling wealth, her slender waist swaying as she goes.

626.translit:
meavi naindhu naindhuviLai yaaDaluRaa Lensiruth
theavipoay, iniththan thirumaal thirukkoa loooril,
pooviyal pozhilum thaDamum avankoa yilungkaNDu,
aaviyuL kuLira engnganea yugakkungkol inRea?
626.meaning: My little goddess does not play around; she has wasted away pining and pining. Going now to her Tirumal's Tirukkolur, beholding its blossom-filled groves and ponds and His temple — how, with her soul cooled, will she rejoice today?

627.translit:
inRenak kudhavaa dhaganRa iLamaan inippoay,
thendhisaith thila tha manaiya thirukkoa loorkkea senRu,
than thirumaal thirukkaNNum sevvaaiyum kaNDu,
ninRu ninRu naiyum neDungaNkaL panimalgavea.
627.meaning: My doe who, of no help to me today, has departed will now go to Tirukkolur, which is like the auspicious mark (tilaka) of the southern direction; reaching there and beholding the sacred eyes and the red mouth of her own Tirumal, she will stand and stand and melt away, her long eyes brimming with tears.

628.translit:
malguneer kaNNoaDu maiyaluRRa manaththanaLaay,
allunan pagalum neDumaalenRazhaith thinippoay,
selvam malgi avankiDandha thirukkoa loorkkea,
olgi yolgi naDandhengnganea pugungko loasindhea?
628.meaning: With eyes brimming with water and a mind seized with bewildered love, calling 'Nedumal!' through both the night and the bright day, she now goes; tottering and tottering as she walks toward Tirukkolur where He reclines amid swelling wealth — how, O mind, will she enter there?

629.translit:
osindha nuNNiDai mealkaiyai vaiththu nondhunondhu,
kasindha nenjina LaaykkaNNa neerththuLumbac cellungkol,
osindha voNmala raaLkozhunan thirukkoa loorkkea,
kasindha nenjina Laayemmai neeththae m kaarigaiyea?
629.meaning: Placing her hand upon her slender swaying waist, aching and aching, with a melting heart and tears welling in her eyes — will she go on toward Tirukkolur of the consort of Her of the bright swaying lotus (Lakshmi)? Our lovely girl, her heart melted, has abandoned us.

630.translit:
kaariyam nallana kaLavai kaaNilen kaNNanukkenRu,
eeriyaa yiruppaaLi dhellaam kiDakka inippoay,
seari palpazhi thooyiraippath thirukkoa loorkkea,
nearizhai naDandhaa Lemmai yonRum ninaiththilaLea.
630.meaning: Whatever good things she saw, she would say 'these are for my Kannan' and remain tender-hearted toward Him — letting all that be, she now goes; while the quarter raises up many a scandal, my finely-jeweled girl has walked off to Tirukkolur, not thinking of us at all.

631.translit:
ninaikkilean theyvangaaL neDungaN iLamaan inippoay,
anaiththu lagumu Daiya aravindha loasananai,
dhinaiththanai yumviDaa Lavansear thirukkoa loorkkea,
manaikku vaanpazhiyum ninaiyaaL sella vaiththanaLea.
631.meaning: I cannot fathom it, O gods! My long-eyed doe now goes; she will not leave even a grain's measure the lotus-eyed Lord who possesses all the worlds. Not even thinking of the great scandal it brings upon the house, she has set herself to go to Tirukkolur where He abides.

632.translit:
vaiththa maanidhi yaammadhu soodhanai yealaRRi,
koththa larpozhil soozhkuru koorccaDa koapansonna,
paththu nooru Lippath thavansear thirukkoaloorkkea,
siththam vaiththu raippaar thigazhpon nulagaaLvaarea.
632.meaning: Crying out to Madhusudana who is the great laid-up treasure, of the thousand verses composed by Satakopan of Kurukur girt with clustered-blossom groves, those who fix their minds and recite this decad about Tirukkolur where He abides shall rule the shining golden world (of Vaikunta).

633.translit:
ponnula gaaLeeroa? puvanimuzhu dhaaLeeroa?,
nannalap puLLinangaaL. vinaiyaaTTiyeannaanirandhean,
munnula gangaLellaam paDaiththamugilvaNNankaNNan,
ennalang koNDapiraan thanakken nilaimaiyuraiththea?
633.meaning: Will you rule the golden world? Will you rule the whole earth? O flocks of fine, good birds! I, a woman of ill fate, beseech you — to Kannan of cloud-hue who created all the worlds of old, the Lord who took away my well-being, tell Him of my state.

634.translit:
maiyamar vaaLneDungaN mangaimaarmunpen kaiyirundhu,
neyyama rinnaDisil niccalpaaloaDu meeveeroa,
kaiyamar sakkaraththen kanivaaipperu maanaikkaNDu
meyyamar kaadhalsollik kiLigaaL!viraindh oaDivandhea?
634.meaning: Before the maidens of dark-collyrium-laden, bright long eyes, will you, staying in my hand, daily feed on ghee-rich sweet rice along with milk — O parrots — when you have seen my fruit-mouthed Lord who bears the discus in His hand, and told Him of the true love seated in my heart, having run swiftly and come back?

635.translit:
oaDivan thenkuzhalmeal oLimaamala roodheeroa,
kooDiya vaNDinangaaL! kurunaaDuDai aivarkaTkaay
aaDiya maaneDundheap paDaineeRezha seRRapiraan,
sooDiya thaNDuLava muNDadhoomadhu vaaikaLkoNDea?
635.meaning: Having run and come, will you breathe upon the bright great blossom on my tresses, O gathered swarms of bees — with your pure-honey mouths that have tasted the cool tulasi worn by the Lord who, for the five (Pandavas) who held the Kuru-land, drove the great tall chariot and crushed the armies to dust?

636.translit:
thoomadhu vaaikaLkoNDu vandhenmullaikaL mealthumbi kaaL,
poomadhu vuNNaccellil vinaiyeanaip poyseydhaganRa,
maamadhu vaarthaNDuzhaay muDivaanavar koanaikkaNDu,
yaamidhu voadhakkavaa Rennaveandumkan deernungkaTkea.
636.meaning: With your pure-honey mouths, O bees come upon my jasmine flowers — if you go to drink the flower-nectar, you must, having seen the King of the celestials whose crown bears the great honey-dripping cool tulasi, the one who deceived me of ill fate and departed, tell Him: 'Is this a fitting way?' — see, this is required of you.

637.translit:
nungkaTki yaanuraikkean vamminyaanvaLarth thakiLigaaL,
vengkaTpuL LoorndhuvandhuvinaiyeanainenY samkavarndha,
sengkaT karumugilaic ceyyavaaiccezhung kaRpagaththai,
engukken Raagilumkan didhuvoadhakka vaaRenminea.
637.meaning: I shall tell you — come, O parrots that I have raised! That dark cloud of red eyes, the red-mouthed lush wish-tree, who came riding the fierce-eyed bird (Garuda) and stole away the heart of me of ill fate — wherever He may have gone, seek Him out and tell Him, 'Is this a fitting way?'

638.translit:
enminnu noolmaarva nenkarumberu maanenkaNNan,
thanmannu neeLkazhalmeal thaNDuzhaaynamak kanRinalgaan,
kanminka LenRummaiyaan kaRpiyaavaiththa maaRRamsolli,
senminkaL theevinaiyean vaLarththasiru poovaikaLea.
638.meaning: My Lord with the lustrous sacred thread on His chest, my dark Lord, my Kannan — He will not grant us the cool tulasi from His abiding long feet otherwise. Go, telling the message I have taught you with 'Learn this!', O little mynas that I of cruel fate have raised.

639.translit:
poovaikaL poalniRaththan puNDareekangaL poalumkaNNan,
yaavaiyum yaavarumaay ninRamaaiyanen aazhipiraan,
maavaival vaaipiLandha madhusoodhaRken maaRRamsolli,
paavaikaL theerkkiRRirea vinaiyaaTTiyean paasaRavea.
639.meaning: He whose hue is like the myna-flower, Kannan whose eyes are like lotuses, the wondrous one who stands as all things and all beings, my Lord of the discus — telling my message to Madhusudana who split the strong mouth of the horse-demon, O dolls (maidens), can you relieve the love-longing of me, a woman of ill fate?

640.translit:
paasaRa veytiyinnee vinaiyEnenai yoozhinaivEn?,
aasaRu thoovi veLLaik kurukE! aruL sey orunaaL,
maasaRu neelach chudar mudivaanavar kOnaik kaNdu,
EsaRum nummaiyallaal maRunOkkilaL pErththumaRRE.
640.meaning: Severed from union, how long, O sinful one that I am, must I waste away through the ages? O spotless white-feathered heron, do me one grace! Beholding the Lord of the celestials who wears a flawless dark radiant crown, she has no other look to turn to but you — speak to him on my behalf again.

641.translit:
pErththumaR ROrkaLaikaN vinaiyaattiyEn naanonRilEn,
neerththirai mEluxlavi yiraitErum puthaavinangaaL,
kaarththiraL maamukilpOl kaNNan viNNavar kOnaik kaNdu,
vaarththaikaL koNdaruLi yuraiyeer vaikal vandhirundhE.
641.meaning: I, a woman of evil deeds, have no other refuge at all. O flocks of birds that hover over the waves of the water seeking your prey! Behold Kannan, the Lord of the celestials, dark like a great mass of rain-clouds, take up my words and graciously convey them to him — come and stay here through the day and tell me his reply.

642.translit:
vandhirun thummudaiya maNichchEvalum neerumellaam,
andhara monRuminRi yalarmEl asaiyum annangaaL,
endhiru maarvaRkennai yinnaavaaRivaL kaaNminenRu,
mandhiraththon nuNarththi yuraiyeer vaikal maRumaaRRangaLE.
642.meaning: O swans that, with your jewel-like cocks and yourselves and all, sway upon the blossoms without the least difference between you! Coming and staying, point out to my Lord who bears Tiru (Lakshmi) on his chest — 'See her, in what a state she is!' — whisper it to him as a secret, and through the day bring back to me his replies.

643.translit:
maaRRangaL aaynthukoNdu mathusootha piraanadimEl,
naaRRangoL poompozhilsoozh kurukoorch chada kOpansonna,
thORRangaL aayiraththuL ivaiyumoru paththumvallaar,
ooRRingaN nuNmaNalpOl urukaaniRpar neeraayE.
643.meaning: Selecting these words, upon the feet of the Lord Madhusudana, Satakopan of Kurukur, girt with fragrant flowering groves, has sung. Those who can master this ten among the thousand revelatory verses will stand melting like fine sand at the mouth of a spring, dissolving into water (with love).

644.translit:
neeraay nilanaay theeyaayk kaalaay neduvaanaay,
seeraar sudarkkaL iraNdaaych chivanaay ayanaay,
kooraar aazhi veNsang Endhik kodiyEnpaal
vaaraay, orunaaL maNNum viNNum makizhavE.
644.meaning: As water, as earth, as fire, as wind, as the vast sky, as the two glorious luminaries (sun and moon), as Shiva and as Brahma — bearing the sharp discus and the white conch, come to me, the cruel-fated one, one day, that earth and heaven may rejoice.

645.translit:
maNNum viNNum makizhak kuRaLaay valangkaatti,
maNNum viNNum koNda maaya ammaanE,
naNNi yunainaan kaNdu kandhu kooththaada,
naNNi yorunaaL njaalath thoodE nadavaayE.
645.meaning: O wondrous Lord who, that earth and heaven might rejoice, came as the dwarf, showed your prowess, and seized both earth and heaven! That I may draw near, behold you, exult and dance for joy — draw near and one day walk here through this world.

646.translit:
njaalath thoodE nadanthum ninRum kidanthirunthum,
saalap palanaaL ukanthO Ruyirkal kaappaanE,
kOlath thirumaa makaLO dunnaik koodaathE,
saalap palanaaL adiyEn innam thaLarvEnO?
646.meaning: O you who, walking and standing and lying and sitting in this world through many ages, protect the lives (of all)! Without uniting with you, who are with the beautiful and great Tiru (Lakshmi), must I, your servant, go on languishing yet for very many days more?

647.translit:
thaLarndhum muRindhum sakada vasurar udalvERaa,
piLandhu veeyath thirukkaa laaNda perumaanE,
kiLarndhu piraman sivanin thiran viNNavarsoozha,
viLanga vorunaaL kaaNa vaaraay viNmeethE.
647.meaning: O great Lord who, with your sacred foot, ruled it (the world), splitting apart the body of the demon in the cart so that it slackened, broke and perished! While Brahma, Shiva, Indra and the celestials rise up and surround you, come one day in the sky for me to behold you in your splendor.

648.translit:
viNmeethiruppaay malaimEl niRpaay. kadalsErppaay!
maNmeethuzhalvaay ivaRRuLengum maRaindhuRaivaay!
eNmeethiyanRa puRavaNdaththaay enathaavi,
uNmeethaadi urukkaattaathE yoLippaayO?
648.meaning: O you who dwell above in heaven, who stand upon the hill, who recline in the sea! O you who roam upon the earth, who dwell hidden everywhere within all these! O you who are the outer cosmos that lies beyond all reckoning! Playing within my very soul, will you hide and not show your form?

649.translit:
paayOr adivaith thathankeezhp paravai nilamellaam
thaay, Or adiyaay ellaa vulakum thadavandha
maayOn, unnaik kaaNpaan varundhi yenainaaLum,
theeyOdudansEr mezhukaay ulakil thirivEnO?
649.meaning: O wondrous one who, setting down one striding foot, with another foot measured all the vast spread of land and every world! To behold you, must I grieve and wander in this world for endless days, like wax that has come close beside the fire (melting away)?

650.translit:
ulakil thiriyum karuma kathiyaay ulakamaay,
ulakukkEkEyO ruyiru maanaay! puRavaNdaththu,
alakil pelindha thisaipath thaaya aruvEyO!
alakil polindha aRivi lEnuk karuLaayE.
650.meaning: O you who are the law of karma that operates in the world, and the world itself, and the one life of the world! O formless one who, in the outer cosmos, are the ten directions spread out without measure! Grant your grace to me, the witless one, devoid of all measureless illumination.

651.translit:
aRivi lEnuk karuLaay aRivaa ruyiraanaay!
veRikoL sOthi moorththi! adiyEn nedumaalE!
kiRisey thennaip puRathit tinnam keduppaayO,
piRithon RaRiyaavadiyE naavi thikaikkavE?
651.meaning: Grant your grace to me, the witless one, O you who are the very life of the knowing! O fragrant radiant Form! O Nedumal (great Lord) of your servant! Will you, working your wiles, cast me out and ruin me still further, so that the soul of your servant — who knows nothing else but you — is bewildered?

652.translit:
aavi thikaikka aivar kumaikkum siRRinbam,
paavi yEnaip palanee kaattip paduppaayO,
thaavi vaiyam koNda thadanthaa maraikatkE,
koovik koLLum kaala minnam kuRukaathO?
652.meaning: Will you keep showing me, the sinful one, the many petty pleasures by which the five (senses) torment so that my soul is bewildered, and so destroy me? Will the time not yet draw near when you, by those broad lotus feet that strode and seized the world, will call and take me to yourself?

653.translit:
kuRukaa neeLaa iRuthi koodaa enaiyoozhi,
siRukaa perukaa aLavilinbam sErndhaalum,
maRukaa linRi maayO nunakkE yaaLaakum,
siRukaa laththai yuRumO andhO theriyilE?
653.meaning: O wondrous one! Even if I attained boundless bliss that has no shrinking nor swelling — bliss that comes not near (any limit), stretches not, reaches no end through all the ages — could it ever equal, alas, even the brief span of becoming wholly your servant without turning back, if one truly discerns it?

654.translit:
theridhal ninaithal eNNal aakaath thirumaalukku,
uriya thoNdar thoNdar thoNdan sadakOpan,
theriyach chonna Oraa yiraththu Lippaththum
uriya thoNda raakkum ulakam uNdaaRkE.
654.meaning: To Tirumal, who cannot be perceived, conceived or thought out (by the mind), Satakopan, the servant of the servants of his servants, has clearly sung. This ten among the thousand verses will make (those who master it) fitting servants of the one who swallowed the world(s).

655.translit:
ulakam uNda peruvaayaa! ulappil keerththi yammaanE!
nilavum sudarsoo zhoLimoorththi. nediyaay adiyE naaruyirE!
thilatham ulakuk kaayninRa thiruvEng kadaththem perumaanE!
kulathol ladiyEn unpaadham koodu maaRu kooRaayE.
655.meaning: O great-mouthed one who swallowed the world! O Lord of endless fame! O radiant Form encircled by abiding light, O Nedu-mal (great Lord), the very life of your servant! O our Lord of Tiruvenkatam who stands as the tilaka (sacred mark) of the world! To me, your servant of an ancient line, tell the way that I may reach your feet.

656.translit:
kooRaay neeRaay nilanaakik koduval lasurar kulamellaam
seeRaa eRiyum thirunEmi valavaa. theyvak kOmaanE!
sEraar sunaiththaa maraisendhee malarum thiruvEng kadaththaanE!
aaRaa anbil adi yEnun adisEr vaNNam aruLaayE.
656.meaning: O wielder of the sacred discus that, blazing, hurls the whole clan of the cruel mighty demons to become fragments, ashes and dust! O divine sovereign! O Lord of Tiruvenkatam where the lotuses in the slushy springs bloom like red fire! Grant to me, your servant of unceasing love, the way to reach your feet.

657.translit:
vaNNa maruLkoL aNimEka vaNNaa! maaya ammaanE!
eNNam pukundhu thiththikkum amudhE imaiyOr athipathiyE!
theNNal aruvi maNiponmuth thalaikkum thiruvEng kadaththaanE!
aNNa lE!un adisEra adiyER kaavaa vennaayE.
657.meaning: O you of the hue of a gracious lovely cloud! O wondrous Lord! O nectar that enters my thought and grows sweet, O sovereign of the celestials! O Lord of Tiruvenkatam where the clear cool cascades wash down jewels, gold and pearls! O noble one! Say 'Aha, alas!' (in compassion) for your servant, that I may reach your feet.

658.translit:
aavaa vennaa thulakaththai alaikkum asurar vaaNaaLmEl,
theevaay vaaLi mazhaipozhindha silaiyaa thirumaa makaLkELvaa,
dhEvaa! surarkaL munik kaNangaL virumbum thiruvEng kadaththaanE!
poovaar kazhalkaL aruvinaiyEn porundhu maaRu puNaraayE.
658.meaning: O wielder of the bow that, without saying 'Aha, alas!' (in pity), rained down fire-mouthed arrows upon the lifespan of the demons who harassed the world! O consort of the great Tiru (Lakshmi)! O God! O Lord of Tiruvenkatam, desired by the gods and the throngs of sages! Grant that I, of cruel deeds, may unite with your flower-decked feet.

659.translit:
puNaraa ninRa maramEzhan Reytha voruvil valavaavO,
puNarEy ninRa maramiraNdin naduvE pOna mudhalvaavO,
thiNaraar mEkam enakkaLiRu sErum thiruvEng kadaththaanE,
thiNaraar saarngath thunpaadham sErva thadiyE nennaaLE?
659.meaning: O peerless archer who once with a single bow pierced the seven sturdy trees standing close together! O primal Lord who passed between the two trees that stood joined! O Lord of Tiruvenkatam, where dense clouds and elephants gather! When shall I, your servant, reach your feet that bear the mighty Sharnga bow?

660.translit:
en-naaLE naam maNNaLanda iNait-taamaraigaL kaaNbadarken-Tru,
en-naaLum ninRu imaiyOrgaL Etti iRaignji inam-inamaay,
mey-nnaa manattaal vazhipaadu seyyum tiruvEngadattaanE,
mey naan eydi en-naaL un adikkaN adiyEn mEvuvadE?
660.meaning: On what day shall we behold the pair of lotus feet that measured the earth? Standing forever and worshipping in great throngs, bowing in reverence, the celestials adore you in truth with tongue and mind, O Lord of Tiruvengadam who receives their worship. When, attaining you truly, shall I your servant cleave to your feet?

661.translit:
adiyEn mEvi amarginRa amudE, imaiyOr adipadiyE,
kodiyaa adupuL udaiyaanE, kOlak-kanivaayp perumaanE,
sediyaar vinaigaL teer-marundE, tiruvEngadattu em perumaanE,
nodiyaar pozhudum un-paadam kaaNa nOlaadu aaRREnE.
661.meaning: O nectar in whom I your servant abide and delight, O Lord of the celestials! O you who bear the fierce striking bird as your banner, O Lord of the lovely fruit-red mouth! O healing medicine that ends the dense thicket of my deeds, O our Lord of Tiruvengadam! Without performing austerity I cannot bear even an instant's while without beholding your feet.

662.translit:
nOlaadu aaRREn nun-paadam kaaNa enRu nuN-uNarvil,
neelaar kaNdatt-ammaanum niRai naanmuganum indiranum,
sElEy kaNNaar palar-soozha virumbum tiruvEngadattaanE,
maalaay mayakki adiyEn-paal vandaay pOla vaaraayE.
662.meaning: "Without austerity I cannot bear to behold your feet" — so even the dark-throated Lord (Shiva), the four-faced Brahma full of fullness, and Indra, with subtle awareness, surround and long for you, O Lord of Tiruvengadam thronged by many fish-eyed women. As once you came bewildering with love, so come now to me your servant — come as though you would come.

663.translit:
vandaay pOlE vaaraadaay, vaaraadaay pOl varuvaanE,
sen-taamaraik-kaN sen-kanivaay naal-tOL amudE, enadu uyirE,
sindaamaNigaL pagar allaip pagal sey tiruvEngadattaanE,
andO adiyEn un-paadam agalagillEn iRaiyumE.
663.meaning: You who seem to have come yet have not come, who though not coming yet do come! O nectar of red-lotus eyes, red fruit-mouth, and four shoulders, O my very life! O Lord of Tiruvengadam where wish-granting gems turn night into day! Alas, I your servant cannot part from your feet even for a moment.

664.translit:
agalagillEn iRaiyum enRu alarmEl mangai uRaimaarbaa,
nigaril pugazhaay, ulagam moonRu udaiyaay, ennai aaLvaanE,
nigaril amarar muni-kaNangaL virumbum tiruvEngadattaanE,
pugal onRu illaa adiyEn un adikkeezh amarndu pugundEnE.
664.meaning: "I cannot part from you even for an instant" — so says she, the maiden upon the lotus, dwelling on your chest. O you of peerless renown, owner of the three worlds, you who rule me! O Lord of Tiruvengadam, longed for by the peerless immortals and the hosts of sages! Having no other refuge, I your servant have settled and entered beneath your feet.

665.translit:
adikkeezh amarndu pugundu adiyeer, vaazhmin enRu enRu aruL kodukkum
padikkEzh illaap perumaanaip pazhanak kurugoorch chadagOpan,
mudippaan sonna aayirattut tiruvEngadattukku ivai pattum,
pidittaar pidittaar veeRRirundu periya vaanuL nilaavuvarE.
665.meaning: "Settle and enter beneath my feet, O servants, and live" — so again and again the incomparable Lord grants his grace. These ten on Tiruvengadam, of the thousand verses composed to completion by Satakopan of fertile Kurugur, whoever holds them firmly, holding them, shall abide enthroned and dwell radiantly in the great heaven.

666.translit:
uN nilaaviya aivaraal kumai-teeRRi ennai un-paada pangayam,
naNNilaa vagaiyE nalivaan innam eNNuginRaay,
eNNilaap peru-maayanE imaiyOrgaL Ettum ulagam moonRu udai,
aNNalE amudE appanE ennai aaLvaanE!
666.meaning: Through the five (senses) that dwell within, you torment me and grind me, and you still plan to crush me so that I may never reach your lotus feet. O great wondrous one beyond all reckoning, O Lord of the three worlds praised by the celestials, O master, O nectar, O father, O you who rule me!

667.translit:
ennai aaLum vangO Or-aindu ivai peydu iraap-pagal mOduvittittu,
unnai naan aNugaa-vagai seydu pOdi kaNdaay,
kannalE amudE kaar-mugil-vaNNanE kadal-gnaalam kaakkinRa,
minnu nEmiyinaay vinaiyEn udai vEdiyanE!
667.meaning: Will you rule me, yet set these five upon me and make them batter me night and day, contriving that I cannot draw near you, and then depart? See this! O sugar, O nectar, O dark-cloud-hued one, O bearer of the flashing discus who guards the sea-girt earth, O sacred Lord of me the sinner!

668.translit:
vEdiyaa niRkum aivaraal vinaiyEnai mOduvittu,
un tiruvadich chaadiyaa-vagai nee taduttu en peRudi andO,
aadiyaagi agal-idam padaittu uNdu umizhndu kadandu idandu itta,
sOdi neeL-mudiyaay toNdanEn madusoodananE.
668.meaning: By the five that ever stand contending, you make me the sinner be battered, and you yourself hold me back from reaching your sacred feet — alas, what do you gain by it? O you of the long radiant crown who, being the primal cause, created the wide earth, ate it and spat it forth, strode it and dug it up, O Madhusudana of me your servant!

669.translit:
soodu naan aRiyaa-vagai suzhaRRi Or-aivaraik kaatti, un adip-
pOdu naan aNugaa-vagai seydu pOdi kaNdaay,
yaadum yaavarum inRi nin agambaal odukki Or-aalin neeL-ilai
meedu sEr kuzhavi! vinaiyEn vinai-teer marundE!
669.meaning: Whirling me about in ways I cannot fathom, you set the five upon me and contrive that I cannot reach the blossom of your feet — and then you depart? See this! O infant who, with nothing and no one remaining, drew all into yourself and lay upon the long leaf of a banyan, O healing medicine that cures the deeds of me the sinner!

670.translit:
teer-marundu inRi aindu nOy adum sekkil ittut tirikkum aivarai,
nEr-marungu udaittaa adaittu negizhippaan okkinRaay,
aar-marundu ini aaguvar? adal-aazhi Endi asurar van-kulam,
vEr-marungu aRuttaay viNNuLaar perumaanEyO.
670.meaning: The five that, with no curing remedy, afflict me with the five maladies and crush and turn me as in an oil-press — you seem about to shut them up close by and then loosen them upon me again. Who then will be the remedy? O you who, bearing the warring discus, cut away root and stock the fierce race of demons, O Lord of those in heaven!

671.translit:
viNNuLaar perumaaRku adimai seyvaaraiyum seRum aim-pulan ivai,
maNNuL ennaip peRRaal en sey yaa? maRRu neeyum vittaal?
paNNuLaay kavi-tannuLaay pattiyinuLLaay param-eesanE! vandu en
kaNNuLaay nenjuLaay! sol-uLaay onRu sollaayE.
671.meaning: These five senses oppress even those who serve the Lord of the celestials — what will they not do to me, found here on the earth, if you too let go of me? O you who dwell in melody, dwell in the song, dwell within devotion, supreme Lord! Come and dwell in my eyes, in my heart, in my words — say to me but one word.

672.translit:
onRu solli oruttinil niRkilaada Or-aivar van-kayavarai,
enRu yaan vel-kiRpan un tiruvaruL illaiyEl?,
anRu dEvar asurar vaanga alai-kadal aravam aLaavi, Or
kunRam vaitta endaay! kodiyEn paruginnamudE!
672.meaning: These five fierce cruel ones, who say one thing yet will not stand firm in one course — when shall I conquer them if your sacred grace be lacking? O my Lord who once, when gods and demons pulled, set a mountain amid the wave-tossed sea entwined with the serpent, O nectar that I the wretched one drink!

673.translit:
in-amudu enat tOnRi Or-aivar yaavaraiyum mayakka, nee vaitta
munna-maaya ellaam muzhu-vEr arindu, ennai un
sinnamum tiru-moortiyum sindittu Ettik kai-tozhavE aruL enakku,
en-ammaa! en kaNNaa! imaiyOr-tam kula-mudalE!
673.meaning: Cutting out root and all the ancient illusion you set up — the five that appear as sweet nectar to bewilder everyone — grant me grace, that I may meditate on your emblems and your sacred form, praise them, and worship with joined hands. O my Lord! O my Kannan! O first source of the race of the celestials!

674.translit:
kula-mudal idum tee-vinaikku odu van-kuzhiyinil veezhkkum aivarai
vala-mudal kedukkum varamE tandaruL kaNdaay,
nila-mudalin ivvulagukkum niRpana selvana ena, poruL
pala-mudal padaittaay! en kaNNaa! en paranjsudarE!
674.meaning: Grant me the boon, as your grace, that destroys at the root the strength of the five who hurl me into the cruel pit beside the dire deeds that cast down my lineage. O you who, beginning with the earth, created all the many primal things — the standing and the moving — for this world! O my Kannan! O my supreme radiance!

675.translit:
en paranjsudarE! enRu unnai alaRRi un iNait-taamaraigatku,
anbu urugi niRkum adu niRkach chumadu tandaay,
van-parangaL eduttu aivar disai-disai valittu eRRuginRanar
mun-paravai kadaindu amudam koNda moortiyO!
675.meaning: "O my supreme radiance!" — crying out to you, melting with love and standing devoted to your pair of lotus feet, leaving even that aside, you have given me a burden. The five, taking up heavy loads, drag and dash me in every direction. O Lord who of old churned the ocean and took the nectar!

676.translit:
koNda moorti Or-moovaraayk kuNangaL padaittu aLittuk kedukkum, ap
puNdareeka kop-poozhp punaR-paLLi appanukkE,
toNdar toNdar toNdar toNdan chadagOpan sollaayirattu ip-pattum,
kaNdu paadaval-laar vinai pOm kangulum pagalE.
676.meaning: To that father who, taking on three forms with the three qualities, creates, sustains, and dissolves — he of the lotus navel who reclines upon the waters — Satakopan, servant of the servants of the servants of his servants, composed this ten among the thousand songs. Those who can see and sing them, their deeds depart, night and day.

677.translit:
kangulum pagalum kaN tuyil aRiyaaL kaNNa-neer kaigaLaal iRaikkum,
sangu-chakkarangaL enRu kai-koobum taamaraik-kaN enRu taLarum,
engnganE tarikkEn unnai vittu ennum iru-nilam kai-tuzhaa irukkum,
sen-kayal paay-neert tiruvarangattaay, ivaL-tiRattu en sey-kinRaayE?
677.meaning: Night and day she knows no sleep; with her hands she bails away her flowing tears. "The conch and the discus!" she cries and joins her hands; "the lotus eyes!" she says and grows faint. "How shall I hold up, parted from you?" she says, and gropes the wide earth with her hands. O Lord of Srirangam of waters where red carp leap — what are you doing about this girl's plight?

678.translit:
en sey-kinRaay en taamaraik-kaNNaa ennum kaN-neer malga irukkum,
en sey-gEn neRi-neert tiruvarangattaay? ennum vev-vuyirttu uyirttu urugum,
mun seyda vinaiyE mugappadaay ennum mugil-vaNNaa taguvadO? ennum,
mun-seydu iv-ulagam uNdu umizhndu aLandaay engolO mudiginRadu ivatkE?
678.meaning: "What are you doing, O my lotus-eyed one?" she says, and sits with eyes brimming with tears. "What shall I do, O Lord of Srirangam of flowing waters?" she says, and sighing hot sighs again and again she melts. "O deed I did before, come show your face!" she says; "O cloud-hued one, is this fitting?" she says. "O you who of old created, ate, spat forth, and measured this world" — where, I wonder, will it end for her?

679.translit:
vatkilaL iRaiyum maNi-vaNNaa! ennum vaanamE nOkkum mai-aakkum,
ut-kudai asurar uyir ellaam uNda oruvanE ennum uL urugum,
katkilee unnaik kaaNumaaRu aruLaay kaaguttaa! kaNNanE! ennum,
tit-kodi madiL-soozh tiruvarangattaay! ivaL-tiRattu en seydittaayE?
679.meaning: She does not shrink at all; "O sapphire-hued one!" she says, and gazes at the sky and grows dazed. "O unique one who consumed the very lives of the inwardly fierce demons!" she says, and melts within. "O you unseen by the eye, grant me the way to behold you! O Kakutstha (Rama)! O Kannan!" she says. O Lord of Srirangam girt with ramparts and firm banners — what have you done to this girl's plight?

680.translit:
iTTakaal iTTa kaiyaLaay irukkum ezhunthulaay mayangkumkai kooppum,
kaTTamee kaadhal enrumoorch chikkum kaTalvaNNaa kaTiyaikaaN ennum,
vaTTavaay neemi valangkaiyaa ennum vanthiTaay enrenree mayangkum,
chiTTanee chezhuneerth thiruvarang kaththaay ivaLthiRath thenjchinthith thaayee
680.meaning: She sits with feet planted and hands held just as they were placed, then rises and wanders and faints, and folds her hands in worship. She moans, 'This love is a torment,' and swoons; she cries, 'O ocean-hued Lord, see how cruel you are!' She calls, 'O you who bear the round-mouthed discus in your right hand!' and 'Will you not come?' and so faints again and again. O righteous Lord of Srirangam of abundant waters, have you ever once thought in your heart about this girl's plight?

681.translit:
chinthikkum thichaikkum theeRumkai kooppum thiruvarang kaththuLLaay ennum
vanthikkum, aangkee mazhaikkaNNeer malka vanthiTaay enrenree mayangkum,
anthippoo thavuNan uTaliTan thaanee alaikaTal kaTainthavaa ramuthee,
chanthiththun charaNam chaarvathee valiththa thaiyalai maiyalchey thaanee
681.meaning: She broods, she grows bewildered, she revives, she folds her hands, she cries 'O you who dwell in Srirangam!' and bows in worship. There, as rain-cloud tears swell in her eyes, she pleads 'Will you not come?' and so swoons over and over. O you who at twilight tore open the demon's body, O nectar churned from the surging ocean—you have driven mad this delicate girl who longed only to meet you and take refuge at your feet.

682.translit:
maiyalchey thennai manamkavarnthaanee ennum maa maayanee! ennum,
cheyyavaay maNiyee ennum thaN punalchoozh thiruvarang kaththuLLaay! ennum,
veyyavaaL thaNTu changkuchak karamvil eenthumviN Noormuthal ennum,
paikoLpaam paNaiyaay! ivaLthiRath tharuLaay paaviyeen cheyyaRpaa lathuvee
682.meaning: She cries, 'O you who maddened me and stole away my heart! O great wondrous Lord!' She cries, 'O ruby of the red mouth!' and 'O you who dwell in Srirangam ringed by cool waters!' She cries, 'O first of the celestials, bearing fierce sword, mace, conch, discus, and bow!' O you who recline on the hooded serpent's coils—show grace toward this girl, for what is left for sinful me to do?

683.translit:
paalathun pangkaL inpangkaL paTaiththaay! paRRilaar paRRanin raanee,
kaalachak karaththaay! kaTaliTang koNTa kaTalvaNNaa kaNNaNee! ennum,
cheelkoLthaN punalchoozh thiruvarang kaththaay enum en theerththanee! ennum,
koolamaa mazhaikkaN panimalka irukkum ennuTaik koomaLak kozhunthee!
683.meaning: She cries, 'O you who created sorrows and joys! O you who became the refuge for those who have no other refuge! O you who hold the wheel of time! O ocean-hued one who took the ocean as your abode, O Krishna!' She cries, 'O Lord of Srirangam ringed by cool waters where carp leap! O my sacred ford of purity!' My tender, delicate sprout sits there with dew brimming in her beautiful cloud-like rain-eyes.

684.translit:
kozhunthuvaa navarkaTku ennum kunReenthik koonirai kaaththavan! ennum,
azhunthozhum aavi analavev vuyirkkum anjchana vaNNanee! ennum,
ezhunthumeel nookki yimaippilaL irukkum engngnanee nookkukeen? ennum,
chezhunthaTam punalchoozh thiruvarang kaththaay encheykeen enthiru makaTkee?
684.meaning: She cries, 'O tender sprout to the celestials!' and 'O you who lifted the hill and guarded the herds of cattle!' Her sinking life sighs hot like fire as she cries, 'O dark-collyrium-hued one!' She rises and gazes upward without blinking and cries, 'How shall I behold him?' O Lord of Srirangam ringed by rich expanses of water, what shall I do for my precious girl?

685.translit:
enthiru makaLcheer maarvanee! ennum ennuTai yaaviyee ennum,
ninthiru eyiRRaal iTanthunee koNTa nilamakaL keeLva nee! ennum,
anRuru veezhum thazhuvinee koNTa aaymakaL anpa nee! ennum,
thenthiru varangkam kooyilkoN Taanee! theLikileen muTivivaL thanakkee
685.meaning: She cries, 'O you on whose breast my Lakshmi dwells! O my very life!' She cries, 'O beloved of the Earth-goddess whom you lifted up and bore upon your sacred tusk!' She cries, 'O loved one of the cowherd-maid whom you won that day by subduing the seven bulls!' O you who have taken the temple at lovely southern Srirangam as your shrine—I cannot fathom what the end will be for this girl!

686.translit:
muTivivaL thanakkon RaRikileen ennum moovula kaaLiyee ennum,
kaTikamazh konRaich chaTaiyanee ennum naanmukak kaTavuLee! ennum,
vaTivuTai vaanoor thalaiva nee!ennum vaNthiru varangkanee! ennum,
aTiyaTai yaathaaL poolivaL aNuki aTainthanaL mukilvaNNan aTiyee!
686.meaning: She cries, 'I cannot know any end for this girl!' and 'O ruler of the three worlds!' She cries, 'O you who are the one with the matted locks fragrant with konrai blossoms! O four-faced god!' She cries, 'O chief of the comely celestials! O bounteous Lord of Srirangam!' Though she seemed one who would not reach his feet, this girl has drawn near and attained the feet of the cloud-hued Lord.

687.translit:
mukilvaNNan aTiyai aTaintharuL choodi uynthavan moypunal porunal,
thukilvaNNath thooneerch cheerppanvaNpozhilchoozh vaNkuru koorchchaTa koopan,
mukilvaNNan aTimeel chonnachonmaalai aayirath thippaththum vallaar,
mukilvaNNa vaanath thimaiyavar choozha irupparpee rinpaveL Laththee
687.meaning: Of these ten verses—a garland of words composed upon the feet of the cloud-hued Lord by Satakopan of fertile Kurukur, ringed by lush groves, lord of the Porunal river whose pure cloth-white waters abound—Satakopan who attained the feet of the cloud-hued Lord, wore them as his crown, and was saved: those who can master these ten of the thousand will abide, encircled by the unblinking celestials of the cloud-hued heaven, in the flood of supreme bliss.

688.translit:
veLLaich churichangko Taazhi yeenthith thaamaraik kaNNanen nenjchi noodee,
puLLaik kaTaakinra vaaRRaik kaaNeer enchollich chollukeen annai meerkaaL,
veLLach chukamavan veerri runtha veetha voliyum vizhaa voliyum,
piLLaik kuzhaaviLai yaaTToli yum aRaath thiruppee raiyil cheervan naanee
688.meaning: Bearing the white spiralled conch and the discus, the lotus-eyed one drives his bird-mount right through the midst of my heart—behold this, O mothers! What words can I speak to tell of it? He dwells there in a flood of bliss; I shall reach Tiruppereyil, where the sound of Vedic chanting, the sound of festivals, and the sound of children's bands at play never cease.

689.translit:
naanak karungkuzhal thoozhi meerkaaL! annai yarkaaL! ayal cheeriyeerkaaL,
naanith thaninenjcham kaakka maaTTeen envacham anrithi raappa kalpooy,
theenmoyththa poompozhil thaNpa Naichoozh thenthirup peeraiyil veerri runtha,
vaanappi raanmaNi vaNNan kaNNan chengkani vaayin thiraththa thuvee
689.meaning: O friends with musk-scented dark tresses! O mothers! O you of the neighboring streets! I can no longer guard this lonely heart of mine—it is not in my control; through night and day it has gone, drawn toward the red-fruit lips of Krishna, the sapphire-hued one, the Lord of heaven, who dwells enthroned in southern Tiruppereyil ringed by cool fields and honey-thronged flower groves.

690.translit:
chengkani vaayin thiraththa thaayum chenchuTar neeNmuTith thaazhntha thaayum,
changkoTu chakkaram kaNTu kanthum thaamaraik kaNkaLuk kaRRuth theernthum,
thingkaLum naaLum vizhaava Raatha thenthirup peeraiyil veerri runtha,
nangkaL piraanukken nenjcham thoozhee! naaNum niraiyu mizhantha thuvee!
690.meaning: Drawn toward his red-fruit lips, and bowed down before his long crown of red radiance, exulting at the sight of his conch and discus, utterly given over and undone by his lotus eyes—O friend, my heart has lost its modesty and its decorum to our Lord who dwells enthroned in southern Tiruppereyil, where month after month and day after day the festivals never cease.

691.translit:
izhanthavem maamai thiraththup poona ennenjchi naarumang kee ozhinthaar,
uzhanthini yaaraikkoN Tenu chaakoo? oothak kaTaloli poola engkum,
ezhunthanal veethath tholinin Roongku thenthirup peeraiyil veerri runtha,
muzhangkuchang kakkaiyan maayath thaazhntheen annaiyar kaaL!ennai yenmu ninthee?
691.meaning: Even those of my heart who went after my lost golden hue have stayed behind there with him. Wearied now, with whom shall I survive? I have sunk into the spell of the Lord whose hand sounds the roaring conch, who dwells enthroned in southern Tiruppereyil, where the sound of the noble Vedas rises and resounds everywhere like the roar of the surging sea. O mothers, why do you reproach me?

692.translit:
muninthu chakaTam uthaiththu maayap peeymulai yuNTu maruthi Taippooy,
kanintha viLavukkuk kanRe Rintha kaNNa piraanukken peNmai thoorReen,
muninthini yencheytheer annai meerkaaL muniya vanvanthu veerri runtha,
kanintha pozhilthirup peerai yiRkee kaalam peRavennaik kaaTTu minee
692.meaning: To Krishna my Lord—who in anger kicked and broke the cart, who suckled the wicked demoness's breast, who passed between the marutha trees, who hurled the calf at the ripe wood-apple—I have lost my womanly virtue. O mothers, what use is your anger now? Take me, while there is yet time, to Tiruppereyil of ripened groves where he himself came of old and dwells enthroned.

693.translit:
kaalam peRavennaik kaaTTu mingkaL kaathal kaTalin mikap perithaal,
neela mukilvaN Naththemperumaan niRkumun neevanthen kaikkum eythaan,
njaalath thavanvanthu veerri runtha naanmaRai yaaLarum veelvi yoovaa,
koolach chenneRkaL kavari veechum kooTu punalthirup peerai yiRkee
693.meaning: Take me there while there is yet time, for my love is greater far than the sea! Our Lord of the dark blue-cloud hue does not stand still before me, nor does he come within reach of my hand. Take me to Tiruppereyil of joined waters, where in this world he came and dwells enthroned, where masters of the four Vedas never cease their sacrifices and the lovely red paddy waves like ceremonial fans.

694.translit:
peereyil choozhkaTal thenni langkai cheRRa piraanvanthu veerri runtha,
peeraiyiR keepukken nenjcham naaTip peerththu varavengkum kaaNa maaTTeen,
aarai yiningku TaiyamthoozhI enenjcham koova vallaaru millai,
aarai yinikkoNTen chaathik kinrathu? ennenjcham kaNTathu veekaN Teenee
694.meaning: Having entered Tiruppereyil, where the Lord who destroyed southern Lanka of sea-girt mighty ramparts came and dwells enthroned, my heart went seeking him—and turning to bring it back, I cannot find it anywhere. Whom have we left here now, O friend? There is no one able to call my heart back. With whom and by what shall I now accomplish anything? I have seen only what my heart has seen.

695.translit:
kaNTathu veekoNTel laarung kooTik kaarkkaTal vaNNano Tenthi Raththuk
koNTu,alar thoorRiR rathumutha laak koNTaven kaatha luraikkil thoozhI,
maNTiNi njaala mumeezh kaTalum neeLvi chumpum kazhiyap perithaal,
theNthirai choozhnthavan veerri runtha thenthirup peeraiyil cheervan chenree
695.meaning: Beholding only what they saw, everyone gathered and began to spread slander about me and my dealings with the dark-sea-hued one—and if I were to tell of the love within me that began thereby, O friend, it is greater far than the dense earth, the seven seas, and the vast sky. I shall go and reach southern Tiruppereyil, ringed by clear waves, where he dwells enthroned.

696.translit:
cheervanjchen rennuTaith thoozhi meerkaaL! annaiyar kaaL! ennaith theerra veeNTaa,
neerkaL uraikkinra thennitha Rku? nenjchum niraivum enakking killai,
kaarvaNNan kaarkkaTal njaala muNTa kaNNa piraanvanthu veerri runtha,
eervaLa voNkazha nippazha naththuth thenthirup peerai yinmaa nakaree
696.meaning: I shall go and reach there, O my friends! O mothers! Do not try to console me. What is it you keep telling me? I have neither heart nor composure left here. He has come and dwells enthroned—the cloud-hued one, Krishna my Lord who swallowed the dark sea and the earth—in the great city of southern Tiruppereyil amid beautiful fertile bright fields and watered tracts.

697.translit:
nakaramum naaTum piRavum theervveen naaNenak killaiyen thoozhi meerkaaL!
chikaram aNineTu maaTam neeTu thenthirup peeraiyil veerri runtha,
makara neTungkuzhaik kaathan maayan noorruva raiyanru mangka noorra,
nikaril mukilvaNNan neemi yaanen nenjcham kavarnthenai yoozhi yaanee?
697.meaning: I shall search out the city, the land, and all else; I have no shame, O friends! The wondrous one wearing long crocodile-shaped earrings, who dwells enthroned in southern Tiruppereyil with its tall mansions adorned with lofty spires—the peerless cloud-hued one bearing the discus, who that day ground down the hundred [Kauravas] to destruction—has he stolen my heart and become mine for all the ages?

698.translit:
oozhithoo Roozhi yuruvam peerum cheykaiyum veeRavan vaiyang kaakkum,
aazhineer vaNNanai yachchu thannai aNikuru koorchchaTa koopan chonna,
keezhilan thaathiyoo raayi raththuL ivaithirup peeraiyil meeya paththum,
aazhiyang kaiyanai yeeththa vallaar avaraTi maiththiRath thaazhi yaaree
698.meaning: Of the matchless thousand antadi verses sung by Satakopan of beautiful Kurukur upon the ocean-water-hued, imperishable Lord—who age after age has a different form, name, and deed, and who protects the world—these are the ten set in Tiruppereyil. Those who can praise the Lord with the discus-bearing beautiful hand—who can fathom the depth of the servitude of such devotees?

699.translit:
aazhi yezhachchangkum villu mezha,thichai
vaazhi yezhaththaNTum vaaLu mezha,aNTam
moozhai yezhamuTi paatha mezha,appan
oozhi yezhavula kangkoNTa vaaRee
699.meaning: As the discus rose and the conch and the bow rose, as the blessing-cry rose throughout the quarters, as the mace and the sword rose, as the cosmos welled up, as crown and feet rose—behold how the Father strode forth, the aeon itself arising, and took possession of all the worlds!

700.translit:
aaRu malaikkethirn thOdu moli, ara
vooRu sulaaymalai theeykku moli, kadal
maaRu suzhanRazhaik kinRa voli, appan
saaRu padavamu thangoNda naanRE.
700.meaning: When the Lord, my Father, churned the ocean of milk to obtain the nectar in abundance, there was the roar of rivers rushing down against the mountain, the sound of the serpent (Vasuki) writhing and grinding away at the churning mountain, and the noise of the sea swirling and crying out in turmoil. All this happened on the day He took the nectar.

701.translit:
naanRila vEzhmaNNum thaanaththa, vE, pinnum
naanRila vEzhmalai thaanaththa vE, pinnum
naanRila vEzhkadal thaanaththa vE, appan
oonRi yidantheyiR Ril koNda naaLE.
701.meaning: On the day when the Lord, my Father, planted Himself firm and lifted up the earth, holding it on His tusk (as the Boar), the seven worlds of earth stood suspended each in its proper place, the seven mountains likewise stood suspended each in its place, and the seven seas too stood suspended each in its place.

702.translit:
naaLu mezhanila neeru mezhaviNNum
kOLu mezhEri kaalu mezha, malai
thaaLu mezhachchudar thaanu mezha, appan
oozhi yezhavula gamuNda vooNE.
702.meaning: When the Lord, my Father, swallowed the worlds at the time of the great deluge, the days arose, the earth and the waters arose, the sky arose, the planets arose, fire and wind arose, the foundations of the mountains arose, and the luminaries themselves arose — all came forth as the aeon dawned, by the very act of His devouring the worlds.

703.translit:
ooNudai mallar thatharntha voli, mannar
aaNudai sEnai nadungu moli, viNNuL
ENudaith thEvar veLippatta voli, appan
kaaNudaip paaratham kaiyaraip pOzhthE.
703.meaning: At the time when the Lord, my Father, joined in the great Mahabharata war that all could behold, there was the sound of the mighty wrestlers who feed for strength being shattered, the sound of the kings' valiant armies trembling in fear, and the sound of the dignified gods in the heavens openly rejoicing as they appeared.

704.translit:
pOzhthu melinthapun sekkaril, vaanthisai
soozhu mezhunthuthi rappuna laa, malai
keezhthu piLanthasing gamoththa thaal, appan
aazhthuya rseythasu raraikkollu maaRE.
704.meaning: When the Lord, my Father, slew the demons who had wrought deep sorrow, He resembled a lion that had split open a mountain from below — for in the faint red glow of the fading twilight hour, the quarters of the sky all around rose up brimming as if flooded with blood. Such was the manner in which He destroyed the asuras.

705.translit:
maaRu niraiththiraik kum saaran gaL, ina
nooRu piNammalai pOlpura La, kadal
aaRu maduththuthi rappuna laa, appan
neeRu padavilan gaisseTRa nErE.
705.meaning: When the Lord, my Father, justly destroyed Lanka, reducing it to dust, His arrows surged forth in opposing ranks like waves; corpses by the hundreds rolled about heaped up like mountains, and the sea was dammed up and turned into a flowing river of blood.

706.translit:
nErsarin thaangodik kOzhikoN daan, pinnum
nErsarin thaaneri yumana lOn, pinnum
nErsarin thaanmukkaN moorththikaN deer, appan
nErsari vaaNanthiN dOLkoNda anRE.
706.meaning: Behold — on the day when the Lord, my Father, took (cut off) the mighty arms of Banasura, the one with the rooster banner (Subrahmanya) drew aside and submitted before Him; likewise the blazing fire-god drew aside, and likewise the three-eyed deity (Siva) too drew aside and yielded before Him.

707.translit:
anRu maNneereri kaalviN malaimuthal,
anRu sudariraN dumpira vum, pinnum
anRu mazhaiyuyir thEvummaT Rum, appan
anRu muthalula gamsey thathumE.
707.meaning: On that day at the beginning, the Lord, my Father, created the whole world: in that beginning earth, water, fire, wind, sky, the mountains and the rest; in that beginning the two luminaries (sun and moon) and all else; and again in that beginning the rain, all living beings, the gods, and everything besides.

708.translit:
mEynirai keezhpuga maapura La, sunai
vaayniRai neerpiLi RichchoRi ya, ina
aanirai paadiyan gEyodun ga, appan
theemazhai kaaththuk kunRa meduth thaanE.
708.meaning: When the grazing herds of cattle stampeded and the great beasts rolled about (in fear), when the springs overflowed their brims and the waters gushed roaring down, and when the herds of cattle and the cowherds' settlement sought to huddle together, the Lord, my Father, lifted up the mountain (Govardhana) and warded off the destructive rain.

709.translit:
kunRa meduththa piraanadi yaarodum,
onRinin Rasada kOpa nuraiseyal,
nanRi punaintha OraayiraththuLLivai
venRi tharumpaththum mEvikkaR paarkkE.
709.meaning: Of the thousand verses composed in goodness by Satakopan (Nammalwar), who stood united with the devotees of the Lord that lifted up the mountain, these ten will grant victory to those who lovingly learn them.

710.translit:
kaRpaar iraama piraanaiyallaalmaTRum kaRparO?,
puRpaa muthalaap pulleRum paathiyon Rin Riye,
naRpaal ayOththiyil vaazhum saraasaram muTRavum,
naRpaaluk kuyththanan naanmuga naarpeTRa naattuLE.
710.meaning: Of those who learn (the scriptures), will they learn of any other than Rama, the Lord?
