For He led the whole of creation, moving and unmoving, that dwelt in righteous Ayodhya — not leaving out even a single blade of grass or an ant — to the good and high state, within the realm bestowed upon Brahma the four-faced.

711.translit:
naattil piRanthavar naaraNaR kaalanRi yaavarO,
naattil piRanthu padaathana pattu manisarkkaa,
naattai naliyum arakkarai naadith thadinthittu,
naattai yaLiththuyyach cheythu nadanthamai kEttumE?
711.meaning: Having heard how Rama, taking birth on earth, endured for the sake of mankind what no one should ever endure, then sought out and destroyed the demons who afflicted the land, and proceeded to protect and uplift the world — of those born in this world, who can be servants of any but Narayana?

712.translit:
kEtpaarkaL kEsavan keerththiyal laalmaTRoom kEtparO,
kEtpaar sevisudu keezhmai vasavuga lEvaiyum,
sEtpaal pazhampagaivan sisu paalan, thiruvadi
thaatpaal adaintha thanmai yaRivaarai yaRinthumE?
712.meaning: When we know of those who understand the way in which even Sisupala — a long-standing bitter foe who hurled base and ear-scorching abuses from afar — finally attained the sacred feet of the Lord, will those who listen ever listen to the fame of any other than Kesava?

713.translit:
thanmai yaRibavar thaamavaR kaalanRi yaavarO,
panmaip padarporuL aathumil paazhnedung kaalaththu,
nanmaip punalpaNNi naanmuganaippaNNi, thannuLLE
thonmai mayakkiya thOTRiya soozhalgaL sinthiththE?
713.meaning: If we ponder the wondrous schemes by which, in that long desolate age when no manifold expanded matter existed at all, the Lord created the auspicious primal waters, created the four-faced Brahma, and brought forth from within Himself the worlds long absorbed in subtle latency — of those who understand this nature, who can be servants of any but Him?

714.translit:
soozhalgaL sinthikkil maayan kazhalanRich choozhvarO,
aazhap perumpunal thannuL azhunthiya njaalaththai,
thaazhap padaamalthan paaloru kOttidaith thaankoNda,
kEzhal thiruvuru vaayiTRuk kEttum uNarnthumE?
714.meaning: Having heard and understood how the Lord took on the sacred form of a Boar to keep the earth — which had sunk and drowned in the deep vast waters — from going down, lifting it close to Himself upon a single tusk, who, if they reflect on His schemes, would seek refuge at any feet but the wondrous Lord's anklet-adorned feet?

715.translit:
kEttum uNarnthavar kEsavaR kaalanRi yaavarO,
vaattami laavaNkai maavali vaathikka vaathippuNdu,
eettangoL thEvargaL senRiran thaarkkidar neekkiya,
kOttangai vaamana naayachcheytha kooththugaL kaNdumE?
715.meaning: Having seen the sports the Lord enacted when, becoming Vamana with the bent (begging) hand, He removed the distress of the assembled gods who, oppressed by the unfailingly generous-handed Mahabali, had gone and begged Him — of those who have heard and understood, who can be servants of any but Kesava?

716.translit:
kaNdum theLinthumkaR RaarkkaNNaR kaalanRi yaavarO,
vaNdun malarththongal maarkkaNdEyanukku vaazhunaaL
iNdaich chadaimudi yeesanudankoNdu saachcholla,
koNdanguth thannodum koNdudansenRathuNarnthumE?
716.meaning: When the lifespan of Markandeya, wearer of bee-swarming flower garlands, was being decided, and Death came along with Isan (Siva) of the matted-lock crown wreathed in flowers — having understood how the Lord then took Markandeya unto Himself and bore him along together with Himself — of those who have seen, become clear, and learned, who can be servants of any but Kannan (the Lord)?

717.translit:
sella vuNarnthavar selvanRan seeranRik kaRparO,
ellai yilaatha perunthavath thaalpala seymiRai,
allal amararaich cheyyum iraNiya naagaththai,
mallal ariyuru vaaychcheytha maayam aRinthumE?
717.meaning: Having understood the wondrous deed by which the Lord, taking the mighty form of the Man-Lion, dealt with the body of Hiranya — who through boundless great austerity worked many outrages and inflicted grief upon the immortals — will those who have understood deeply learn of anything but the glory of the prosperous Lord?

718.translit:
maayam aRibavar maayavaR kaalanRi yaavarO,
thaayam seRumoru nooTRuvar mangavO raivarkkaay,
thEsa maRiyavOr saarathi yaaychchenRu sEnaiyai
naasamsey thittu, nadanthanal vaarththai yaRinthumE?
718.meaning: Having understood the noble account of how, for the sake of the five (Pandavas) and so that the hundred (Kauravas) who quarrelled over the inheritance might perish, the Lord went as a charioteer known to the whole land and brought about the destruction of the (enemy) army — of those who know His wonders, who can be servants of any but the wondrous Lord?

719.translit:
vaarththai yaRibavar maayavaR kaalanRi yaavarO,
pOrththa piRappodu nOyOdu moopodu iRappivai
pErththu, perunthunbam vEraRa neekkiththan thaalingeezhch
sErththu, avan seyyum sEmaththaiyeNNith theLivuTRE?
719.meaning: Having attained clarity by contemplating the protection the Lord grants — removing the enveloping cycle of birth, disease, old age and death, uprooting great suffering at its very root, and joining (the soul) beneath His sacred feet — of those who know His tidings, who can be servants of any but the wondrous Lord?

720.translit:
teLivuRRu veevanRi ninRavark kinpak katiseyyum,
teLivuRRa kaNNanai tenkuru kuurcchaTa koopanchol,
teLivuRRa aayirat tuLLivai pattumval laaravar,
teLivuRRa cintaiyar paamaru muuvula kattuLLee.
720.meaning: He grants blissful liberation to those who, having attained clarity, stand firm and do not perish. These ten among the thousand clear verses sung by Satakopan of beautiful Kurukur are about that Kannan attained in clarity. Those who can master these ten will become ones of clear mind throughout the three worlds known to the learned.

721.translit:
paamaru muuvulakum paTaitta paRpa naapaavoo,
paamaru muuvulakum aLanta paRpa paataavoo,
taamaraik kaNNaavoo. taniyeen taniyaa Laavoo,
taamaraik kaiyaavoo. unnai yenRukol saarvatuvee?
721.meaning: O Lotus-naveled one who created the three worlds known to the learned! O one with lotus-like feet who measured the three worlds! O lotus-eyed one! O lord of me, the solitary one, in my solitude! O one with lotus-like hands! When indeed shall I reach and take refuge in You?

722.translit:
enRukol seervatan tooaran naanmuka neettum,
seyya nintirup paatattai yaannilam neereri kaal,
viNNuyir enRivai taammuta laamuRRu maayninRa entaayoo,
kunReTut taanirai meyttavai kaattaveng kuuttaavoo
722.meaning: When, alas, shall I reach Your red sacred feet, praised by Shiva and the four-faced Brahma? O my Father who stand as all things from their very origin - earth, water, fire, wind, sky, and life! O my wondrous dancer who lifted up the mountain and protected the herds of cattle!

723.translit:
kaattaveng kuuttaavoo! malaiyeentik kanmaari tannai,
puuttaN TuzhaaymuTi yaay! punaikonRaiyany senychaTaiyaay,
vaayttaven naanmukanee! vanten naaruyir niiyaanaal,
eettarung kiirttiyinaay! unnai yenkut talaippeyvanee?
723.meaning: O my wondrous dancer who protected by holding up the mountain against the rain of stones! O one with a crown of blossomed cool tulasi! O one who, as Shiva, wears the konrai flowers on red matted locks! O my own excellent four-faced one! When You come and become my very life, O one of fame too great to praise, where shall I meet and unite with You?

724.translit:
enkut talaippeyvan naan?ezhil muuvula kumniiyee,
ankuyar mukkaT piraan piramanperu maanavannii,
venkatir vacchirak kaiyin tiranmuta laatteyvamnii,
konkalar taNNan tuzhaaymuTi yennuTaik koovalanee.
724.meaning: Where shall I meet and unite with You? You alone are the three beautiful worlds. You are the three-eyed lord (Shiva) on high; You are Brahma, and the great lord above him. You are the gods beginning with Indra of the fierce-rayed thunderbolt in his hand. O my cowherd (Govalan) with a crown of cool, fragrant, blooming tulasi!

725.translit:
ennuTaik koovalanee! en pollaakkaru maaNikkamee,
unnuTai yunti malarulakam avaimuun Rumparantu,
unnuTaicchootiveL Lattakam paalunnaik kaNTukoNTiTTu,
ennuTai yaaruyiraar enGanee kolvan teytuvaree?
725.meaning: O my cowherd! O my flawless dark sapphire gem! The three worlds, spreading forth from the lotus of Your navel, lie within the flood of Your radiance. Having beheld You there, how indeed shall my precious life come and reach You?

726.translit:
venteytu maaRaRi yeenmalku niilach suTartazhaippa,
senychuTarch chootikaL puuttoru maaNikkam seervatupool,
antara meelsempaT TooTaTi unti kai maarvukaNvaay,
senychuTarch chooti viTavuRai entiru maarpanaiyee.
726.meaning: I do not know the way to come and reach Him - my Lord with the sacred chest, on whom radiant red lustres bloom as upon a sapphire growing rich with abundant blue brilliance. Above, in the sky, with red silk garment, His feet, navel, hands, chest, eyes, and mouth shine forth, casting red radiant light.

727.translit:
entiru maarpan tannaiyen malaimakaL kuuRantannai,
enRumen naamaka Laiyakam paalkoNTa naanmukanai,
ninRa sasipati yainilang kiiNTeyil muunReritta,
venRu pulamturan tavisum paaLiyaik kaaNeenoo.
727.meaning: Shall I not behold Him - my Lord with the sacred chest, who shares His form with the daughter of the mountain (Parvati); the four-faced Brahma who eternally holds my Goddess of Speech within him; the lord of Sachi (Indra); the one who split open the earth, burnt the three forts, and who, victorious, drove away the enemies and rules over the sky?

728.translit:
aaLiyaik kaaNpari yaayari kaaNnari yaay,arakkar
uuLaiyiT TanRilankaikaTan tupilam pukkoLippa,
miiLiyam puLLaik kaTaayviRal maaliyaik konRu,pinnum
aaLuyar kunRankaL seytaTart taanaiyum kaaNTunkoloo?
728.meaning: Shall we behold Him who, hard to face, became the man-lion (Narasimha); the one who, when the demons howled and crossed beyond Lanka and hid in caves, drove on His fierce bird (Garuda), slew the mighty Mali, and further heaped up towering mountains of bodies to crush them?

729.translit:
kaaNTunko looneynychamee! kaTiyavinai yeemuyalum,
aaNTiRal miiLimoym pilarak kankulat taittaTintu,
miiNTumavan tampikkee viriniiri lankaiyaruLi,
aaNTutan chootipuk kavama rarari yeeRRinaiyee?
729.meaning: O heart! Shall we behold Him - the lion among the gods who entered His own radiance after destroying the clan of the demon (Ravana) of valiant, fierce strength, ever bent on cruel deeds, then graciously gave the sea-girt Lanka to that one's younger brother (Vibhishana) and ruled?

730.translit:
eeRRarum vaikuntat taiyaruLum namakku, aayarkulattu
iiRRiLam piLLaiyon Raayppukku maayanka LeeyiyaRRi,
kuuRRiyal kanychanaik konRuaivark kaayakkoTuny seenaitaTintu,
aaRRalmik kaanperi yaparany chootipuk kaariyee.
730.meaning: He grants us the Vaikunta that is hard to attain - He who entered the cowherd clan as a newborn infant, performed wondrous deeds, slew Kamsa of the nature of Death, destroyed the cruel armies for the sake of the five (Pandavas), the one mighty in power, the lion who entered the great supreme radiance.

731.translit:
pukka ariyuru vaayavuNanuTal kiiNTukanta,
sakkarach selvantannaik kurukuurcchaTa koopanchonna,
mikkavoo raayirat tuLivaipattumval laaravarai,
tokkuppal laaNTisait tukkavari seyva reezhaiyaree.
731.meaning: Those who can master these ten among the surpassing thousand verses sung by Satakopan of Kurukur about the wealthy Lord of the discus, who rejoiced in tearing apart the body of the demon (Hiranya) having taken the man-lion form - lovely women will gather, sing 'pallandu' (long life) to them, and fan them with the fly-whisk.

732.translit:
eezhaiyar aaviyuN NumiNaik kuuRRanko loovaRiyeen,
aazhiyung kaNNa piraantiruk kaNkaLko loovaRiyeen,
suuzhavum taamarai naaNmalar poolvantu toonRumkaNTiir,
toozhiyar kaaL! annai miir! enysey keentuya raaTTiyeenee?
732.meaning: I know not whether they are a pair of death-gods that devour the lives of helpless women, or whether they are the sacred eyes of Lord Kannan who bears the discus. See, all around they appear like fresh-blooming lotus flowers! O my friends! O mothers! What shall I, afflicted with sorrow, do?

733.translit:
aaTTiyum tuuRRiyum ninRannai miirennai niirnalintenN?
maaTTuyar kaRpakat tinvalli yookozhun too?aRiyeen,
iiTTiya veNNeyuN Daantiru muukkenA taaviyuLLee,
maaTTiya valviLakkin suTaaryniRkum vaaliyatee.
733.meaning: O mothers, what do you gain by harassing and reviling me as you stand over me? I know not whether it is a creeper or a tender shoot of the lofty kalpaka tree on high. That radiant sacred nose of His - of Him who ate the gathered butter - stands like the flame of a strong lamp lit within my very soul.

734.translit:
vaaliya toorkani kolvinai yaaTTiyeen valvi naikol,
koolam tiraLpava Lakkozhun tuNTanko loovaRiyeen,
niila neTumukil pooltiru meeniyam maantoNTaivaay,
eelum ticaiyuLel laamvantu toonRumen ninnuyirkkee.
734.meaning: Is it some fine ripe fruit, or is it the dire karma of sinful me - I know not whether it is a beautiful piece of rounded, tender coral. The coral-red mouth of my Lord, whose sacred body is like a great dark cloud, comes and appears in every fitting direction before my tender life.

735.translit:
innuyirk keezhaiyar meelvaLaiyum iNai niilaviRkol,
Manniya siirmata nankaruppucch silai kol,matanan
tannuyirt taataikaN Naperumaan puru vamavaiyee,
ennuyir meelana vaay aTukinRana enRu ninRee.
735.meaning: Are they a pair of bent blue bows that aim at the lives of helpless women, or the sugarcane bow of Manmatha (the god of love) of enduring glory? They are the very eyebrows of Lord Kannan, the father who gave Manmatha his life. They stand over my life and torment it ceaselessly.

736.translit:
enRu ninReetika zhumseyya viinsuTar veNminnukkol,
anRiyen naavi yaTumaNi muttanko loovaRiyeen,
kunRam eTuttapi raan muRuvalenataaviyaTum,
onRum aRikinRi leenannai miir! enak kuyviTamee!
736.meaning: Is it the white lightning of a sweet red radiance that ever shines forth, or is it a precious pearl that slays my life - I know not. The smile of the Lord who lifted up the mountain slays my soul. I know nothing at all. O mothers, where is my place of refuge and deliverance?

737.translit:
uyviTam eezhaiyark kum asurarkkum arakkarkaTkum
evviTam? enRilang kimakaram tazhaik kumtaLirkol,
paiviTap paampaNai yaan tirukkuNTalak kaatukaLee?
kaiviTa lonRumin Riy aTukinRana kaaNminkaLee.
737.meaning: Where is the place of refuge for helpless women, and for the asuras, and for the rakshasas too? Are they tender shoots on which makara (sea-monster) emblems shine and flourish - the sacred ear-ornamented ears of Him who reclines on the hooded venomous serpent (Adisesha)? Behold, without ceasing for a moment they keep slaying me!

738.translit:
kaaNminkaL annaiyar kaaL! enRu kaaTTum vakaiyaRiyeen,
naaNmannu veNtinkaL kol! nayantaarkaTku nacchilaikol,
seeNmannu naaltaTan tooL perumaantan tirunutalee?,
kooLmanni yaavi yaTumkoTiyeen uyir kooLizhaittee.
738.meaning: O mothers, I know not in what way to show you, saying 'Behold!' Is it the enduring white moon of the day, or a poison-arrow to those who desire Him? It is the sacred forehead of my Lord of four broad shoulders that reach to the heavens. Firmly seizing hold, it slays the life of cruel-fated me, working destruction upon my soul.

739.translit:
kooLizhait taamaraiyum koTiyum pavaLamum villum ,
kooLizhaittaN muttamum taLirum kuLirvaan piRaiyum,
kooLizhaiyaavuTaiya kozhuny chooti vaTTankol, kaNNan,
kooLizhaivaaL mukamaayk koTiyeen uyir koLkinRatee.
739.meaning: Is it the radiant orb of rich light, brilliantly set with the lotus, the creeper, the coral, the bow, the cool gleaming pearls, the tender shoot, and the cool crescent moon of the sky - all radiant and seizing? It becomes the bright resplendent face of Kannan and takes the life of cruel-fated me.

740.translit:
koLkindra kOLiruL aich chukirndhittha kozhunj churuLin,
uLkoNda neela nan nool thazhai kol? andru maayang kuzhal,
viLkindra poondhaN thuzhaay virai naara vandhu en uyirai,
kaLkindra aaRu aRiyeer annaimeer! kazhaRaa niRRirE.
740.meaning: Is her dark curling tresses a fold of darkness, peeled off and rolled up thick, or a leaf woven of fine dark-blue thread? On that wondrous day He came with the cool fragrant tulasi blossoms spreading their scent and stole away my life. O mothers, you do not understand how He robs me, yet you keep on scolding me!

741.translit:
niRRi muRRaththuL endru neriththa kaiyar aay ennai neer
suRRiyum soozhndhum vaidhir sudar sOdhi maNi niRamaay,
muRRa im moovulagum virigindra sudar mudikkE,
oRRumai koNdadhu uLLam annaimeer! nasai en nungatkE?
741.meaning: You stand in the courtyard, wringing your hands, surrounding and encircling me and reviling me. But my heart has set itself in oneness upon Him of radiant luster and gem-like hue, whose shining crown spreads over all these three worlds. O mothers, what desire is there in this for you?

742.translit:
kaT kariya biraman sivan indhiran endru ivarkkum,
kaT kariya kaNNanaik kurugoor chatakOpan sonna,
uTku udai aayiraththu UL ivaiyum oru paththum vallaar,
uTku udai vaanavarOdu udanaay endrum maayaarE.
742.meaning: Kannan is hard to see even for the eyes of Brahma, Siva and Indra. Those who can master these ten verses among the awe-inspiring thousand spoken by Satakopan of Kurugur will dwell forever in the company of the venerable celestials and never perish.

743.translit:
maayaa! vaamananE! madhusoodhaa! nee aruLaay,
theeyaay neeraay nilanaay visumbaay kaalaay,
thaayaay thandhaiyaay makkaLaay maRRum aay muRRum aay,
neeyaay nee nindravaaRu ivai enna niyaayangaLE.
743.meaning: O wondrous one! O Vamana! O Madhusudana! Grant me Your grace. You being fire, water, earth, sky and wind; being mother, father, children, and all else, and being the whole; standing as Yourself within all these, what manner of justice is this?

744.translit:
angaN malar thaN thuzhaay mudi achchudhanE! aruLaay,
thingaLum njaayiRum aay sezhum pal sudar aay iruLaay,
pongu pozhi mazhaiyaay pugazhaay pazhiyaay pinnum nee,
vengaN vengoorRamum aay ivai enna visiththiramE.
744.meaning: O Achyuta, crowned with cool fragrant tulasi of lovely-eyed blossoms! Grant me grace. You being the moon and the sun, the many bright lights and the darkness; being the swelling pouring rain, being praise and being blame, and yet again being fierce-eyed cruel Death itself, what manner of wonder is this?

745.translit:
chiththira thEr valavaa! thiru chakkaraththaay! aruLaay,
eththanaiyOr ugamum avai aaya avaRRuLLiyalum,
oththa oN pal poruLgaL ulappu illana aay viyavaay,
viththagaththaay niRRi nee avai enna vidamangaLE.
745.meaning: O skilled driver of the wondrous chariot! O wielder of the sacred discus! Grant me grace. Being all the countless eons and what moves within them; being the many fitting bright things without end and being marvellous, You stand as the wondrous one. What manner of expanse is this?

746.translit:
kaLLavizh thaamarai kaN kaNNanE! enakku ondru aruLaay,
uLLadhum illadhum aay ulappu illana aay viyavaay,
veLLa thadang kadaluL vida naagaNai mEl maruvi,
uLLa pal yOgu seydhi ivai enna ubaayangaLE.
746.meaning: O Kannan with eyes like a honey-laden lotus! Grant me one boon. Being all that exists and all that does not, being endless and being marvellous, You recline upon the venom-spewing serpent-couch in the vast flood-tide ocean and perform Your manifold yogic repose. What manner of devices are these?

747.translit:
paasangaL neekki ennai unakkE uRak koNdittu,
nee vaasa malar thaN thuzhaay mudi maayavanE! aruLaay,
kaayamum seevanum aay kazhivaay piRappaay pinnum nee,
maayangaL¦ seydhu vaiththi ivai enna mayakkugaLE.
747.meaning: Cutting away my bonds and making me wholly Yours, O wondrous one crowned with cool fragrant tulasi of perfumed blossoms, grant me grace. Being both body and soul, being decay and being birth, and yet again You work Your magic upon them. What manner of bewilderments are these?

748.translit:
mayakkaa! vaamananE! madhi aam vaNNam ondru aruLaay,
ayarppaay thERRamum aay azhalaay kuLiraay viyavaay,
viyappaay vendrigaL aay vinaiyaay payan aay pinnum nee,
thuyakkaay nee nindravaaRu ivai enna thuyarangaLE.
748.meaning: O bewilderer! O Vamana! Grant me one boon so that I may understand. Being forgetfulness and clarity, being flame and coolness, and being marvel; being wonder and victories, being deed and being fruit, and yet again being confusion, You stand within all these. What manner of sufferings are these?

749.translit:
thuyarangaL seyyung kaNNaa! sudar neeL mudiyaay aruLaay,
thuyaram sey maanangaL aay madhan aagi ugavaigaL aay,
thuyaram sey kaamangaL aay thulaiyaay nilaiyaay nadaiyaay,
thuyarangaL seydhu vaiththi ivai enna chuNdaayangaLE.
749.meaning: O Kannan who causes sorrows, crowned with a long radiant crown, grant me grace. Being the pride that causes grief, becoming desire and being joys; being the lusts that cause sorrow, being ruin, being stability and being the moving course; You inflict these sorrows. What manner of subtleties are these?

750.translit:
enna chuNdaayangaLaal nindrittaay ennai aaLum kaNNaa,
innadhu Or thanmaiyai endru unnai yaavarkkum thERRu ariyai,
munniya moovulagum avai aaya avaRRaip padaiththu,
pinnum uLLaay! puRaththaay! ivai enna iyaRkaigaLE.
750.meaning: O Kannan who rules me, with what subtleties do You stand! None can grasp that Your nature is of such a particular kind. Having created the three primordial worlds and become those very worlds, You are still within them and also without them. What manner of natures are these?

751.translit:
enna iyaRkaigaLaal enganE nindrittaay en kaNNaa,
thunnu kara saraNam mudhal aaga ellaa uRuppum
unnu suvai oLi ooRu oli naaRRam muRRum neeyE,
unnai uNara uRil ulappu illai nuNukkangaLE.
751.meaning: O my Kannan, by what natures do You stand, and how! Beginning with the closely-fitting hands and feet, You are all the limbs; You are the whole of taste, light, touch, sound and smell. If one seeks to comprehend You, Your subtleties are without end.

752.translit:
illai nuNukkangaLE idhanil piRidhu ennum vaNNam
thollai nan noolil sonna uruvum aruvum neeyE
allith thuzhaay alangal aNi maarba! en achchudhanE,
valladhu Or vaNNam sonnaal adhuvE unakku aam vaNNamE.
752.meaning: There are no subtleties such that anything else exists beyond this. You are both the form and the formless spoken of in the ancient sacred scriptures, O Achyuta of mine, whose chest is adorned with a garland of petalled tulasi! Whatever manner one is able to declare of You, that itself becomes the manner that is fitting to You.

753.translit:
aam vaNNam innadhu ondru endru aRivadhu ariya ariyai,
aam vaNNaththaal kurugoor chatakOpan aRindhuraiththa
aam vaNNa oN tamizhgaL ivai aayiraththuL ip paththum,
aam vaNNaththaal uraippaar amaindhaar thamakku endraikkumE.
753.meaning: He is the hard-to-grasp Lord, whose fitting nature cannot be known as being just this one thing. These ten verses among the thousand fine fitting Tamil songs, which Satakopan of Kurugur knew and uttered in their fitting manner - those who recite them in that fitting manner are made complete for themselves forever.

754.translit:
endraikkum ennai uyyak koNdu pOgiya,
andraikku andru ennai than aakki ennaal thannai,
in tamizh paadiya eesanai aadhiyaay
nindra en sOdhiyai, en solli niRpanO?
754.meaning: He who took me up to be saved for all time; on that very day He made me His own and through me sang sweet Tamil of Himself - that Lord, the primordial cause, my radiant light who stands firm - with what words shall I describe Him?

755.translit:
en solli niRpan en innuyir indru ondru aay,
en sollaal yaan sonna in kavi enbiththu,
than sollaal thaan thannaik keerththiththa maayan,
en mun sollum moovuru aam mudhalvanE.
755.meaning: With what words shall I describe Him - He who today became one with my sweet life, and made the sweet verse which I uttered with my words known as such, the wondrous one who through His own words praised Himself? He is the primal Lord, of the three forms, who speaks before me.

756.translit:
aam mudhalvan ivan endru thaR thERRi, en
naa mudhal vandhu pugundhu nal in kavi,
thoo mudhal paththarkku thaan thannaich chonna, en
vaay mudhal appanai endru maRappanO?
756.meaning: Convincing me that He is the fitting primal Lord, He came and entered upon my tongue and gave the good sweet verse, He who declared Himself to His pure foremost devotees - the Father who is the source of my speech - when shall I ever forget Him?

757.translit:
appanai endru maRappan en aagiyE,
thappudhal indri thanaik kavi thaan solli,
oppilaa theevinaiyEnai uyyak koNdu
seppamE seydhu thirigindra seer kaNdE?
757.meaning: When shall I forget the Father - seeing His glory as He goes about, having become myself and without fail uttering verse of Himself, taking up to salvation me of incomparable evil deeds, and setting all right?

758.translit:
seer kaNdu koNdu thirundhu nal in kavi,
nER pada yaan sollum neermai ilaamaiyil,
Er vilaa ennaith than aakki ennaal thannai,
paar paravu in kavi paadum paramarE.
758.meaning: Since I do not have the fitness to behold His glory and properly utter polished, good, sweet verse, the Supreme One made worthless me His own and through me sings sweet verse that the whole earth extols of Himself.

759.translit:
in kavi paadum parama kavigaLaal,
than kavi thaan thannaip paaduviyaadhu, indru
nangu vandhu ennudan aakki ennaal thannai,
van kavi paadum en vaigundha naadhanE.
759.meaning: Though there are supreme poets who sing sweet verse, He did not have His own verse sung of Himself by them; today He came graciously and joined with me, and through me my Lord of Vaikuntha sings sturdy verse of Himself.

760.translit:
vaikuntha naathanen valvinai maayndhara,
seykuNdran ranaiyen naakkiyen naalthannai,
vaikuntha naagap pugazhvaN theengkavi,
seykuNdran thannaiyen naaLsindhith thaarvano.
760.meaning: The Lord of Vaikuntha, who destroyed my cruel karma utterly, took me as his own and made me, through myself, compose rich, sweet verses extolling him who grants Vaikuntha. On what day will those who think upon him who fashioned me into a poet attain that grace?

761.translit:
aarvano aazhiyang kaiyembi raanpugazh,
paarviNNeer mutrum kalandhu parugilum,
ervilaa ennaiththan naakkiyen naalthannai,
seerpera inkavi sonna thiraththukke?
761.meaning: Even were one to drink in together all the earth, sky and waters, would one ever have one's fill of the glory of my Lord who holds the beautiful discus in his hand? He made graceless me into his own and, through myself, had sweet verses spoken so that his fame might prosper.

762.translit:
thiraththukke thuppura vaamthiru maalinseer,
irappethir kaalam parugilum aarvano,
marappilaa vennaith than naakkiyen naalthannai,
urappala inkavi sonna vudhavikke?
762.meaning: The glory of Tirumal is the very sustenance, the refuge for me. Even were one to drink it in through all past and future time, would one ever have one's fill of it? He made me, who cannot forget him, his own, and through myself had many sweet verses firmly spoken — what return can repay that help?

763.translit:
udhavikkaim maaren nuyirenna vutreNNil,
adhuvummar raangavan rannathen naalthannai,
padhaviya inkavi paadiya appanukku,
edhuvumon rumillai seyvathing kumange.
763.meaning: If, seeking what return I might give for his help, I reckon my very life to be the recompense, even that, too, is his alone. To the Father who, through myself, sang gracious sweet verses, there is nothing whatever that I can do, here or there, in this world or the other.

764.translit:
ingumang kumthiru maalandri inmaikaNdu,
angngane vaNkuru kuurchchada gopan sol,
ingngane sonnavo raayiraththth ippaththum,
engngane sollinum inbam payakkume.
764.meaning: Seeing that there is nothing here or there except Tirumal, Satakopan of bounteous Kurukur thus spoke. These ten verses among the thousand he composed in this manner, in whatever way they are uttered, will yield joy.

765.translit:
inbam payakka ezhilmalar maadharum thaanumiv vezhulagai,
inbam payakka inidhudan veetrirun thaaLkindra engkaLpiraan,
anbut ramarndhurai kindraaNi pozhil suuzhthiru vaaranviLai,
anbut ramarndhu valamseydhu kaithozhum naaLgaLum aagungkolo.
765.meaning: Our Lord, who together with the lady of the lovely lotus dwells gloriously, granting them joy, to bring joy to these seven worlds, abides lovingly in Tiruvaranvilai girt by beautiful groves. Will there come days when I too, abiding lovingly, circumambulate it and worship with folded hands?

766.translit:
aagungkol aiyamon rindri agalidam mutravum, eeradiye
aagum parisu nimirndha thirukkuraL appan amarndhuraiyum,
maagam thigazhkodi maadangkaL needum madhiLthiru vaaranviLai,
maagandha neerkoNdu thuuvi valanjeydhu kaithozhung kuudungkolo.
766.meaning: Without any doubt, will it come to be? The Father who, as the divine Dwarf, stretched up so that all the wide earth became but two steps, abides in Tiruvaranvilai with its ramparts and towering mansions whose banners gleam against the sky. Will it be granted that I sprinkle fragrant water, circumambulate it, and worship with folded hands?

767.translit:
kuudungkol vaikalum govindha naimadhu suudhanaik koLariyai,
aadum paravai misaikkaNdu kaithozhu thandri yavanuraiyum,
paadum perumpugazh naanmarai veLviyain thaarangkam panninarvaazh,
needu pozhilthiru vaaran viLaithozha vaaykkungkol nichchalume.
767.meaning: Will it come to be, every day, that I behold Govinda, Madhusudana, the man-lion, mounted upon the dancing bird, and worship him with folded hands? And not only that — will it be granted me daily to worship Tiruvaranvilai of long groves, where dwell those who recite the great praiseworthy four Vedas, the five sacrifices and the six limbs of the Vedas?

768.translit:
vaaykkungkol nichchalum eppozhu thummanaththu eengku ninaikkapper
vaaykkum karumbum perunjen nelumvayal suuzhthiru vaaranviLai,
vaaykkum perumpugazh muuvula geesan vadamadhu raippirandha,
vaaykkum maNinirak kaNNabi raanran malaradip podhugaLe.
768.meaning: Will it be granted me, every day and at all times, here to be able to think within my heart upon the lotus-bud feet of Kannan, the gem-hued Lord, who is the great-famed master of the three worlds, born in northern Mathura, and who graces Tiruvaranvilai girt by fields of sugarcane and tall red paddy?

769.translit:
malaradip podhugaL ennenjath theppozhu thumiruth thivaNangka,
paaradi yaarmun paruLiya paambaNai yappan amarndhuraiyum,
malaril maNinedu maadangkaL needu madhiLthiru vaaranviLai,
ulagam malipugazh paadanam melvinai ondrumnil laagedume.
769.meaning: To enshrine his lotus-bud feet within my heart at all times and worship them — the Father who reclines on the serpent, who showed grace before his many devotees, abides in Tiruvaranvilai with its ramparts and towering gem-set mansions tall as the sky. When we sing its world-filling fame, every karma resting upon us will not stand but will perish.

770.translit:
ondrumnil laagedum mutravum theevinai yuLLith thozhumindhoNdeer,
andrang amarven ruruppi Ninangkai yaNinedun thoaLpuNarndhaan,
endrumep podhumen nenjam thudhippa vuLLeyiruk kindrapiraan,
nindra aNithiru vaaran viLai yennum neenaga ramadhuve.
770.meaning: O devotees, worship with this thought, and all your evil karma will not stand but wholly perish! The Lord who that day won the battle and embraced the adorned long shoulders of lady Rukmini, who dwells within my heart so that always and at all times my heart praises him — he abides in the great beautiful city called Tiruvaranvilai.

771.translit:
neenaga ramadhu vemalarch chOlaigaL suuzhthiru vaaranviLai,
neenaga raththurai kindrapi raannedu maalkaNNan viNNavarkoan
vaaNa purambukku muk katpi raanaith tholaiyavem porgaLseydhu,
vaaNanai yaayiran thoaLthuNith thaanjaraN andrimar rondrilame.
771.meaning: The Lord who dwells in that great city, Tiruvaranvilai girt with flower-gardens — Nedumal, Kannan, king of the celestials — entered Bana's city, waged fierce battles to subdue the three-eyed Lord (Shiva), and severed the thousand shoulders of Bana. Other than his feet, we have no other refuge.

772.translit:
andrimar rondrilam ninsara Ne!en ragalirum poykai yinvaay,
nindruthan neeLkazha lethiya aanaiyin nenjidar theerththa piraan,
sendrang inidhurai kindra sezhumpozhil suuzhthiru vaaranviLai,
ondri valanjeyya ondrumo? theevinai yuLLaththin saarvallave.
772.meaning: "We have no other refuge than your feet!" — so crying, the elephant stood at the mouth of the vast deep pool and extolled his long anklated feet; the Lord who dispelled the elephant's heart-anguish has gone and dwells sweetly there in Tiruvaranvilai girt by lush groves. Will anything compare to circumambulating it with devotion? Evil karma will not cling to the heart.

773.translit:
theevinai yuLLaththin saarvalla vaagith theLivisum perralutraal,
naavinuL LumuLLath thuLLum amaindha thozhilinuL Lumnavindru,
yaavarum vandhu vaNangkum pozhilthiru vaaran viLaiyathanai,
mevi valanjeydhu kaithozhak kuudungkol ennumen sindhanaiye.
773.meaning: Once evil karma ceases to cling to the heart and one attains the clear high heaven — through the tongue, through the heart within, and through fitting service practiced — will it be granted that I draw near Tiruvaranvilai of groves, where all come and worship, circumambulate it, and worship with folded hands? So my mind keeps asking.

774.translit:
sindhaimar rondrin thiraththathal laaththanmai thevapi raanariyum,
sindhaiyi naalseyva thaanari yaadhana maayangkaL ondrumillai,
sindhaiyi naalsolli naalseykai yaalnilath thevar kuzhuvaNangkum,
sindhai magizhthiru vaaran viLaiyurai theerththanuk katrapinne.
774.meaning: After I have become wholly the Lord's — the pure One who dwells in heart-gladdening Tiruvaranvilai, where the assembly of earthly gods (brahmins) worship by thought, by word and by deed — the Lord of the celestials knows that my mind cannot turn toward anything else. There is no wondrous deed done by my mind that he does not know.

775.translit:
theerththanuk katrapin matroar saraNillai yendreNNi,
theerththanukke theertha manaththana naagich chezhungkuru kuurchchada gopansonna,
theerthangka Laayiran thuLLivai paththumval laargaLai, devarvaikal
theerthangka Leyendru puusiththu nalgi yuraippaarthham deviyarkke.
775.meaning: Thinking, after having become the pure Lord's, that there is no other refuge, Satakopan of fertile Kurukur, with a heart made pure unto the pure One alone, spoke these sacred songs. Those who can master these ten among the thousand purifying songs — the celestials, daily revering them as very sacred waters, will grant grace and speak of them; and they shall become the goddesses of the Lord.

776.translit:
devimaa raavaar thirumagaL puumi yevamar ramararaat seyvaar,
meviya vulagam muundravai yaatchi veNduveN duruvamnin nuruvam,
paaviyen thannai yadukindra kamalak kaNNathoar pavaLavaay maNiye,
aaviye! amudhe! alaikadal kadaindha appane! kaaNumaa raruLaay.
776.meaning: Their goddesses shall be Lakshmi and Bhumi; the others shall serve as attendants; rule over the three worlds is theirs. Whatever form is desired, that form is your form. O lotus-eyed, coral-mouthed gem who torments sinful me! O my life, O nectar, O Father who churned the surging ocean! Grant that I may behold you.

777.translit:
kaaNumaa raruLaay endrendre kalangkik kaNNaneer alamara vinaiyen
peNumaa rellaam peNinin peyare pidhatrumaa raruLenak kandho,
kaaNumaa raruLaay kaaguththaa. kaNNaa! thoNdanen karpagak kaniye,
peNuvaar amudhe. periyathaN punalsuuzh perunilam eduththape raaLaa!
777.meaning: "Grant that I may behold you!" — so crying again and again, distraught, with tears welling in my eyes, I, the karma-laden one, cherish all that I cherish; grant me, alas, that I may keep babbling your very name. Grant that I may behold you, O scion of the Kakutsthas, O Kannan, O wish-fulfilling fruit to me your servant, O nectar to those who cherish you, O great One who lifted up the vast earth girt by great cool waters!

778.translit:
eduththape raaLan nandhago pandran innuyirch chiruvane, asodhaik
kaduththape rinbak kulaviLang kaLire! adiyanen periyavam maane,
kaduththapor avuNan udaliru piLavaak kaiyugi raaNdaveng kadale,
aduththathor uruvaay indrunee vaaraay engnganam theruvar umare?
778.meaning: O great One who lifted the earth, dear little life-son of Nandagopa, O young elephant of surging great joy to Yasoda! O my great Lord to me your servant, O fierce sea who with the nails of your hand tore the body of the fierce battling demon (Hiranya) into two halves! Come to me today in some fitting form — else how shall those who are yours gain clarity?

779.translit:
umarugan dhugandha vuruvamnin nuruvamaagi un thanakkanba raanaar avar,ugan
dhamarndha seykaiyun maayai arivondrum sangkippan vi naiyen,
amaradhu paNNi agalidam pudaisuuzh adupadai aviththaam maane,
amarartham amudhe! asurargaL nanjse! ennudai aaruyi reyo.
779.meaning: Whatever form your devotees delight in, that becomes your form, and those who became your loving ones, the deeds in which they joyfully abide — all this is your maya, and I, the karma-laden one, doubt and cannot grasp one bit of it. O Lord who, making war, quelled the destroying armies that surrounded the wide earth! O nectar to the celestials, O poison to the demons, O my dear precious life!

780.translit:
aaruyi reeyoo! akalidam muzhuthum padaiththidan thuNdumizhn thaLantha,
peeruyi reeyoo. periyaneer padaiththang kuRainthathu kadainthadaith thudaiththa,
seeriya reeyoo. manisarkkuth thevar poolaththe varkkumthe vaavoo,
oruyi reeyoo. ulagangat kellaam unnainaan engkuvan thuRukoo?
780.meaning: O my dear life-breath! You who created this whole wide earth, lifted it, swallowed it and spat it out, and measured it. O great life! You who created the vast waters and, when they receded, churned them, dammed them and dried them. O glorious one, who are to gods what gods are to men, O God even of the gods! O one Life of all the worlds! Where shall I go to reach You?

781.translit:
engkuvan thuRukoo ennaiyaaL vaane! eezhula gangkaLum neeye,
angkavark kamaiththa theyvamum neeye avaRRavai karumamum neeye,
pongkiya puRambaal poruLuLa veelum avaiyumoo neeyinne yaanaal
mangkiya aruvaam neerppamum neeye vaanpulam iRanthathum neeye.
781.meaning: Where shall I go to reach You, O Lord who rules me? You are the seven worlds; You are the gods fashioned for them, and You are their deeds and acts too. If even all the things that exist beyond, swelling forth, are also You, then You are the faded, subtle, fine essence as well, and You are also that which lies beyond the realm of the sky.

782.translit:
iRanthathum neeye ethirnthathum neeye nigazhvathoo neeyinne yaanaal,
siRanthanin thanmai yathuvithu vuthuven RaRivonRum sangkippan vinaiyeen,
kaRanthapaal neyye! neyyin suvaiye. kadalinuL amuthame, amuthil
piRanthavin suvaiye! suvaiyathu payaneee! pinnaithooL maNanthapee raayaa!
782.meaning: You are the past, You are the future, and the present too is You. If all is thus You alone, then I, the sinner, am confused, unable to grasp with sure knowledge that Your excellent nature is this, or that, or yonder. O ghee from milk that is drawn! O taste of that ghee! O nectar within the sea! O sweet taste born in that nectar! O fruit-essence of that taste! O great Lord who embraced the shoulders of Nappinnai!

783.translit:
maNanthapee raayaa! maayaththaal muzhuthum valvi nai yeenaiyeer kinRa,
guNangkaLai yudaiyaay. asurarvan kaiyar kooRRame. kodiyapuL Luyarththaay,
paNangkaLaa yiramum udaiyapain naagap paLLiyaay! paaRkadal seerppaa,
vaNangkumaa RaaRiyeen. manamumvaa sakamum seykaiyum yaanumnee thaane.
783.meaning: O great Lord who embraced her! You have qualities that, through Your wondrous power, utterly cut apart me of cruel deeds. O death itself to the strong-handed demons! O You who raised the fierce bird (Garuda) on Your banner! O You who recline upon the lush serpent of a thousand hoods! O Lord of the Ocean of Milk! I no longer know the way to worship You, for my mind, my speech, my deeds, and I myself are all You alone.

784.translit:
yaanumnee thaane yaavathoo meyye arunara kavaiyum neeyaanaal,
vaanuya rinbam eythilen maRRai naragame yeythilen? enilum,
yaanumnee thaanaayth theLithoRum nanRum anjuvan naragamnaa nadaithal,
vaanuya rinbam manniveeR Rirunthaay aruLunin thaaLkaLai yenakkee!
784.meaning: Since I too am truly You, and even the terrible hell is You, what does it matter whether I attain the heavenly bliss above or the other place, hell? Even so, the more I clearly realize that I too am You, the more I greatly dread my reaching hell. O You who abide enthroned in the high heavenly bliss, grant me Your feet, for me alone!

785.translit:
thaaLkaLai yenakkee thalaiththalai siRappath thanthapee ruthavikkaim maaRaa,
tooLkaLai yaarath thazhuvithen nuyirai aRavilai seythanan sothee,
tooLkaLaa yiraththaay. mudikaLaa yiraththaay! thuNaimalark kaNkaLaa yiraththaay,
thaaLkaLaa yiraththaay! peerkaLaa yiraththaay! thamiyaneen periya appane.
785.meaning: In return for the great favor of giving Your feet to me alone, each gift surpassing the last, I have, embracing Your shoulders to my heart's content, given away my soul as a gift utterly free, O Radiant One! O You of a thousand shoulders! O You of a thousand crowns! O You of a thousand paired flower-like eyes! O You of a thousand feet! O You of a thousand names! O great Father of me, the lonely one!

786.translit:
periya appanaip piraman appanai uruththiran appanai, munivark
kuriya appanai amarar appanai ulagukkoor thaniyappan thannai,
periyavaN kurukoor vaNsada koopan peeNina aayiraththuLLum,
uriyasol maalai ivaiyumpath thivaRRaal uyyalaam thoNdeer nangkatkee.
786.meaning: The great Father, the Father of Brahma, the Father of Rudra, the Father proper to the sages, the Father of the immortals, the one peerless Father of all the world — of the thousand verses lovingly sung by Satakopan of bountiful, great Kurugur, these ten too form a fitting garland of words. By them, O devotees, we may be saved.

787.translit:
nangkaL varivaLai yaayang kaaLoo! nammudai eethalar munbu naaNi,
nungkat kiyaanonRu raikkum maaRRam nookkukin Reenengkum kaaNa maatteen,
sangkam sarinthana saayi zhantheen thadamulai ponniRa maaytha Larntheen,
vengkaN paRavaiyin paaga nengkoon veengkada vaaNaNai veeNdich senRe.
787.meaning: O my companions of lovely-streaked bangles! Ashamed before our strangers and foes, I look for some word to say to you, but nowhere can I find it. My bangles have slipped off, I have lost my beauty, my full breasts have turned pale, and I have grown faint — all from going forth longing for the rider of the fierce-eyed bird, our King, the Lord who dwells at Venkatam.

788.translit:
veeNdichchen RonRu peRukiR paaril ennudaith thoozhiyar nungkat keelum,
eeNdithu raikkum padiyai yantho! kaaNkinRi leenida raatti yeennaan,
kaaNthagu thaamaraik kaNNan kaLvan viNNavar koonnangkaL koonaik kaNdaal,
eeNdiya sangkum niRaivum koLvan eththanai kaalam iLaikkin Reene.
788.meaning: Among those who go forth and obtain what they long for, O my companions, even for you — alas! — I cannot find the way to speak this out fully here, I, the suffering woman. The lotus-eyed one, fair to behold, the thief, the King of the celestials, our King — if I see Him, I shall recover my slipped bangles and my modesty; how long must I keep wasting away?

789.translit:
kaalam iLaikkil laalvi naiyeen naaniLaik kinRilan kaNdu koNmin,
NYaalam aRiyap pazhisu mantheen nannutha leer. ini naaNith thaanen,
neela malarnedunj soothi soozhntha neeNda mukilvaNNan kaNNan koNda,
koola vaLaiyodu maamai koLvaan eththanai kaalamkoo dachchenRe.
789.meaning: Time may waste away, but I, the woman of deeds, do not waste — see and understand this. I have borne reproach for all the world to know, O fair-browed ones; what use is shame now? To recover the beautiful bangles and the bloom of complexion that Kannan took — He of the cloud hue, vast, ringed with the long blue-flower radiance — for how long must I keep going to join Him?

790.translit:
koodachchen Reenini enko dukkeen? koolvaLai nenjath thodakka mellaam,
paadaR Rozhiya izhanthu vaikal palvaLai yaarmun parisa zhintheen,
maadak kodimathiL thenku Lanthai vaNkuda paalninRa maayak kooththan,
aadal paRavai uyarththa velpoor aazhi valavi nai yaatharithee.
790.meaning: I have gone to join Him; what more have I now to give? My round bangles, the bonds of my heart, all of them — I have lost utterly, leaving nothing, and day by day I have lost my dignity before women of many bangles. Longing for Him who wields the conquering battle-discus — the wondrous dancer who stands on the bountiful west side of southern Kulanthai with its mansions, banners and ramparts, who raised the dancing bird on His banner.

791.translit:
aazhi valavinai aatharippum aangkavannammil varavum ellaam,
thoozhiyar kaaL nammudaiya meethaan? solluva thooving kariyathu thaan,
oozhithoo Roozhi oruva naaga nankuNar vaarkkum uNara laagaa,
soozha ludaiya sudarko Laathith thollaiyanj soothi ninaikkung kaale.
791.meaning: My longing for the right-handed discus-Lord, and His coming to us there — O companions, is all this our own doing? And to speak of it is itself a hard thing, when one considers that ancient, radiant, primal Light, He of mysterious power who, age after age, remains the One unknowable even to those who deeply know.

792.translit:
thollaiyanj soothi ninaikkung kaalen sollaLa vanRimai yoortha makkum,
ellaiyi laathana koozhppuch seyyum aththiRam niRkavem maamai koNdaan,
alli malarththaN duzhaayum thaaraan aarkkidu koovinip poosal solleer,
valli vaLavayal soozhku danthai maamalark kaNvaLar kinRa maale.
792.meaning: When one thinks of that ancient, radiant Light, He is beyond the measure of my words, and even for the celestials He creates endless confusion. Setting that aside — He took away my bloom of complexion, yet He will not give me even the cool tulasi of pollen-laden flowers. Against whom shall I raise my outcry now? Tell me — He, Tirumal, who sleeps with lotus-like eyes in Kudanthai ringed by rich fields with creepers.

793.translit:
maalari kesavan naara Nanjsee maathavan koovinthan vaikunthan enRenRu,
ola midavennaip paNNi vittit tonRu muruvum suvadum kaattaan,
eela malarkuzhal annai meerkaaL. ennudaith thoozhiyar kaaL! enseykeen?
kaalam palasenRum kaaNba thaaNai ungkaLoo dengka Lidaiyil laiye.
793.meaning: "Tirumal, Hari, Kesava, Narayana, Sri's lord Madhava, Govinda, Vaikunthan" — crying out thus again and again He has set me lamenting, yet He shows neither form nor trace of Himself. O mothers with flower-decked fragrant locks! O my companions! What shall I do? Even though much time has passed, there is no seeing Him — I swear there is nothing left between you and me.

794.translit:
idaiyil laiyaan vaLarththa kiLikaaL. poovaikaL kaaL.kuyil kaaL!ma yilkaaL,
udaiyanam maamaiyum sangkum nenjum onRum ozhiyavot taathu koNdaan,
adaiyum vaikuntha mumpaaR kadalum anjana veRpum avai naNiya,
kadaiyaRap paasangkaL vitta pinnai anRi yavanavai kaaNko taane.
794.meaning: O parrots that I, ceaselessly, have reared! O mynahs! O cuckoos! O peacocks! He has taken away, leaving nothing, my bloom of complexion, my bangles, my very heart — all of it. Though His abode Vaikuntha, the Ocean of Milk, and the dark mountain (Venkatam) be near, He will not let them be seen unless one has utterly cut off, to the very end, all bonds of attachment.

795.translit:
kaaNkodup paanalla naarkkum thannaik kaiseyap paalathoor maayan thannaal,
maaNkuRal koola vadivu kaatti maNNum viNNum niRaiya malarntha,
seeNsudarth thooLkaL palatha zhaiththa theva piraaRken niraivinodu, naaNkoduth
theniniy enkodukkeen ennudai nannuthal nangkai meerkaaL.
795.meaning: He does not show Himself to any who would lay hold of Him; through a wondrous power that is His to command, He showed the lovely form of the noble dwarf, and grew vast to fill earth and sky, flourishing with many far-shining radiant shoulders. To that divine Lord I have given away, along with my array of beauty, even my modesty — what more have I to give Him now, O my fair-browed maidens?

796.translit:
ennudai nannuthal nangkai meerkaaL! yaaninach seyvathen? ennenj sennai,
ninnidai yeenalleen enRu neengki neemiyum sangkum irukaik koNdu,
panneduinj soozhsudar NYaayiR Roodu paanmathi eenthiyor koola neela,
nanneduing kunRam varuva thoppaan naaNma larppaa thamadain thathuve.
796.meaning: O my fair-browed maidens! What can I do about this? My very heart, saying "I no longer belong with you," has departed from me, and bearing in its two hands the discus and the conch, holding the far-spreading radiant sun and the milk-white moon, like a beautiful blue lofty mountain coming near, it has reached and attained His fresh-blossomed flower-feet.

797.translit:
paatham adaivathan paasath thaale maRRavan paasangkaL muRRa vittu,
koothil pukazhkkaN Nanthan nadimeel vaNkuru koorchchada koopan sonna,
theethilanthaathiyoo raayi raththuL ivaiyumoor paththisai yoodum vallaar,
aathumoor theethila raaki yingkum angkumel laamamai vaarkaL thaame.
797.meaning: Through the bond of longing to reach His feet, casting off completely all His other bonds — upon the feet of Kannan of flawless fame, Satakopan of bountiful Kurugur sang this faultless antati of a thousand verses. Those who can master these ten too, along with their melody, will become wholly free of any fault, and will be at ease everywhere, both here and yonder.

798.translit:
angku mingkum vaanavar thaanavar yaavarum,
engkum inaiyaiyen Runnai aRiyakilaa thalaRRi,
angkam serum poomakaL maNmakaL aaymakaL,
sangku sakkarak kaiyava nenpar saraName.
798.meaning: There and here, the celestials, the demons, and all beings everywhere, unable to know You and say "such are You," cry out in bewilderment. The flower-maiden (Lakshmi), the earth-maiden, and the cowherd-maiden (Nappinnai) cling to Your form; they declare that He who holds the conch and discus in His hands is the refuge.

799.translit:
saraNa maakiya naanmaRai nuulkaLum saaraathe,
maraNam thooRRam vaanpiNi mooppen RivaimaayththooM,
karaNap palpadai paRRaRa voodum kanalaazhi,
araNath thiNpadai yeenthiya eesaR kaaLaaye.
799.meaning: Without even resorting to the four Vedas that are themselves a refuge, we have destroyed these — death, birth, dire disease, and old age. By becoming the servant of the Lord who wields the blazing discus, the firm protecting weapon, all the many instruments of sense flee away and fall from their grasp.

80.translit:
kuDainthu vaN DuNNum thuzhaaymuDi yaanai,
aDaintha then kurukoorch chaDa kOpan,
miDaintha sol thoDai aayiraththip paththu,
uDainthu nOykaLai yODu vikkumE.
80.meaning: These ten verses, out of the thousand of dense, woven words by Satakopan of beautiful southern Kurukur, who took refuge in the Lord crowned with the tulasi into which bees dive deep to drink its nectar — these will shatter our diseases and drive them away.

81.translit:
ODum puLLeRi, sooDu maN thuzhaay,
neeDu ninRavai, aaDum ammaanE.
81.meaning: He is my Lord who rides upon the swift-flying bird (Garuda), who wears the cool fresh tulasi, who stands eternal, and who dances (the dances of His sport).

82.translit:
ammaanaayp pinnum, emmaaNa pumaanaana,
vemmaa vaaykeeNDa, semmaa kaNNanE.
82.meaning: Being our Lord, and beyond that becoming for us the great one of wondrous glory, He is the red-eyed Krishna who tore open the mouth of the fierce horse-demon (Kesi).

83.translit:
kaNNaa vaan enRum, maNNOr viNNOrkku,
thaNNaar vEngka, viNNOr veRpanE.
83.meaning: Being for both the people of earth and the dwellers of heaven their very eyes (their guide and refuge) ever, He is the Lord of cool, abundant Venkatam, the hill of the celestials.

84.translit:
veRpai onRu eDuththu, oRkam inRiyE,
niRkum ammaan seer, kaRpan vaikalE.
84.meaning: He lifted up a single mountain (Govardhana) and stood firm without any weariness — I shall learn and contemplate the glories of that Lord every single day.

85.translit:
vaikalum veNNey, kaikalanthu uNDaan,
poykalavaathu, en - meykalanthaanE.
85.meaning: He who daily took fresh butter into His hands and ate it has, without any falsehood, united Himself with my very body (entered into me truly).

86.translit:
kalanthu en aavi, nalangkoL naathan,
pulangkoL maaN aay, nilamkoN DaanE.
86.meaning: Mingling with my soul, He is the Lord who takes my good (who is my well-being). Taking the form of a wondrous dwarf-Brahmin who captivated the senses, He measured and seized the whole earth.

87.translit:
koNDaan Ezh viDai, uNDaan Ezh vaiyam,
thaNDaam anjeythu, en eN thaan aanaanE.
87.meaning: He subdued the seven bulls; He swallowed the seven worlds; performing deeds beyond reproach, He has become the very object of my thought.

88.translit:
aanaan aanaayan, meenODu Enamum,
thaan aanaan ennil, thaan aaya sankE.
88.meaning: He became the cowherd (Krishna); He became the Fish and the Boar as well. If one says He Himself became all these — yes, He indeed truly became them, without doubt.

89.translit:
sanku sakkaram, angkaiyil koNDaan,
engkum thaanaaya, nangkaL naathanE.
89.meaning: He who holds the conch and the discus in His beautiful hands, He who pervades and is everywhere, is our Lord.

90.translit:
naathan njaalam koL paathan, en ammaan,
Otham pOl kiLar, vEtha neeranE.
90.meaning: The Lord whose feet measured and seized the whole world, my master, is of the nature of the Vedas that surge and swell like the ocean's tide.

91.translit:
neer purai vaNNan, seerch chaDakOpan,
nerthal aayiraththu, Ortha livaiyE.
91.meaning: These are the well-considered verses among the thousand of the glorious Satakopan, sung in praise of the Lord whose hue resembles the water (the dark ocean).

92.translit:
ivaiyum avaiyum uvaiyum ivarum avarum uvarum
evaiyum yavarum thannuLLE aagiyum aakkiyung kaakkum
avaiyuL thanimuthal emmaan kaNNapiraan en amutham
suvaiyan thiruvin maNaaLan ennuDaich choozhaluLaanE.
92.meaning: These things here, those things there, and those in between; these persons, those persons, and those in between; all things and all beings — holding them all within Himself, bringing them forth, and protecting them, He is among them the sole primal cause, my Lord, Krishna the gracious giver, my ambrosia of sweet savor, the bridegroom of Lakshmi — and He dwells within my embrace.

93.translit:
soozhal palapala vallaan thollai am kaalaththu ulakai
kEzhal onRu aagi iDantha kEsavan ennuDai ammaan,
vEzha maruppai osiththaan viNNavarkku eNNal ariyaan
aazha neDungkaDal sErnthaan avan en arukaliyaanE.
93.meaning: He is able in many, many divine sports; in the ancient beautiful age He became a single Boar and lifted up the earth — that Kesava is my Lord. He snapped the tusk of the elephant (Kuvalayapida); He is beyond the reckoning of the celestials; He reclined upon the deep, vast ocean — and yet that very one is here, never apart from me, close beside me.

94.translit:
arukal ilaaya peruncheer amararkaL aathi muthalvan,
karukiya neela nan mEni vaNNan senthaamaraik kaNNan,
poru siRaip puL uvanthu ERum poomakaLaar thanik kELvan,
oru kathiyin suvai thanthiTTu ozhivilan ennODu uDanE.
94.meaning: The first primal cause of the celestials of flawless, abundant glory, the Lord whose fine body is the hue of dark blue-black, the one with red-lotus eyes, who gladly rides the bird (Garuda) with battling wings, the peerless consort of Lakshmi who dwells on the lotus — He has granted me the savor of the one supreme goal and never leaves me, but stays together with me.

95.translit:
uDan amark kaathal makaLir thirumakaL maNmakaL aayar
maDamakaL, enRu ivar moovar aaLum ulakam moonRE,
uDan avai okka vizhungki aalilaich chErnthavan emmaan,
kaDal mali maayap perumaan kaNNan en okkalaiyaanE.
95.meaning: The loving consorts who abide together with Him — Lakshmi (the lady of fortune), Bhudevi (the earth lady), and the tender cowherd maiden Nappinnai — these three, and the three worlds they rule, He swallowed them all together alike and reclined upon the banyan leaf — He is our Lord, the wondrous one rich as the ocean, Krishna who rests upon my hip (as my own child).

96.translit:
okkalai vaiththu mulaippaal uN enRu thanthiDa vaangki,
sekkanj cheka anRu avaL paal uyir sega uNDa perumaan,
nakkapiraanODu ayanum inthiranum muthalaaka,
okkavum thORRiya eesan maayan en nenjinuLaanE.
96.meaning: When she (Putana) set Him on her hip and gave Him her breast saying 'drink the milk,' He seized it and, that day, drank her very life away till she crimsoned and perished — that great Lord, the supreme one who brought forth alike Siva the naked Lord, Brahma, Indra and all the rest, the wondrous one, dwells within my heart.

97.translit:
maayan en nenjinuLLan maRRum yavarkkum aḥthE,
kaayamum seevanum thaanE kaalum eriyum avanE,
sEyan aNiyan yavarkkum sinthaikkum kOsaram allan,
thooyan thuyakkan mayakkan ennuDaith thOLiNaiyaanE.
97.meaning: The wondrous one is within my heart, and so it is for all others too. He Himself is both body and soul; He Himself is the wind and the fire. He is far and He is near; to no one and to no mind is He an object of grasping. He is the pure one, the one who deludes and bewilders (the unworthy), and He rests upon my pair of shoulders.

98.translit:
thOLiNai mElum nan maarbin mElum suDarmuDi mElum,
thaaLiNai mElum punaintha thaNNanthuzhaay uDai ammaan
kEL iNai onRum ilaathaan kiLarum suDaroLi moorththi,
naaL aNainthu onRum akalaan ennuDai naavinuLaanE.
98.meaning: My Lord who wears the cool, beautiful tulasi adorning His pair of shoulders, His noble chest, His radiant crown, and His pair of feet; He who has no equal nor any peer, the form of surging brilliant light — day by day He draws near and never departs at all, and He abides within my tongue.

99.translit:
naavinuL ninRu malarum njaanak kalaikaLukku ellaam,
aaviyum aakkaiyum thaanE azhippODu aLippavan thaanE,
pooviyal naal thaDanthOLan poru paDai aazhi sangku Enthum,
kaavi nan mEnik kamalak kaNNan en kaNNinuLaanE.
99.meaning: For all the sciences of wisdom that stand and blossom upon the tongue, He Himself is both their soul and their body; He Himself is the one who destroys and the one who protects. He of the four broad blossom-like shoulders, who bears the battling weapons the discus and the conch, the lotus-eyed one with a fine body the hue of the blue water-lily — He dwells within my eyes.

800.translit:
aaLum aaLaar aazhiyum sangum sumappaartaam,
vaaLum villung koNdupin selvaar maaRRillai,
taaLum tooLum kaikaLai yaarat tozhakkaaNeen,
naaLum naaLum naaduvan adiyeen NYaalattee.
800.meaning: He has no servants to command, yet He Himself bears the discus and the conch; there is none to match those who follow behind Him bearing sword and bow. I do not get to behold and worship to my heart's content His feet, His shoulders and His hands. Day after day I, His servant, keep searching for Him here in this world.

801.translit:
NYaalam poonakam paRRiyoor muRRaa vuruvaagi,
aalam peerilai yannava sanjeyyum ammaanee,
kaalam peervatoor kaariru Luzhiyot tuLataal, un
koolang kaareezhil kaaNaluR Raazhum kodiyeeRkee.
801.meaning: O my Lord who, taking the whole world as Your food, became an unripe infant and lay upon the banyan leaf doing such wondrous deeds! To one of cruel fate like me, longing to behold Your dark beautiful form, every passing moment feels like an age of deep dark gloom.

802.translit:
kodiyaar maadak kooLu ragattum puLingudiyum,
madiyaa tinnee neetuyil meevi magizhndatutaan,
adiyaar allal tavirtta asaivoo? anReel,
ippaditaan neeNdu taaviya asaivoo? paNiyaayee.
802.meaning: In Tirukkozhicheri with its mansions flying banners, and in Tiruppuliyangudi, You sleep restfully and rejoice without ceasing. Is this rest because You have removed the sorrows of Your devotees? Or is it the rest of the One who, in this very manner, stretched and measured the worlds? Please tell me.

803.translit:
paNiyaa amarar paNivum paNpum taameeyaam,
aNiyaar aazhiyum sangamu meentum avarkaaNmin,
taNiyaa vennoo yulagil tavirppaan, tiruneela
maNiyaar meeniyo denmanam soozha varuvaaree.
803.meaning: The very homage and grace of the gods who would not bow before others belong to Him alone; behold Him who bears the beautiful discus and the conch! To remove the unquenchable fierce afflictions of this world, He comes with His body radiant like a fine blue sapphire to fill and pervade my mind.

804.translit:
varuvaar selvaar vaNpari saarat tirunda, en
tiruvaazh maarvaR kentiRam sollaar seyvateen,
uruvaar sakkaram sangu sumanding ummoodu,
orupaa duzhalvaanoradi yaanu muLanenRee.
804.meaning: Those who come and go in the precincts of His noble attendants do not say a word about my state to my Lord, on whose chest dwells Lakshmi. What can I do? Bearing the well-formed discus and the conch, wandering about one corner here with you all, there is also this servant of His who exists for Him.

805.translit:
enRee yennaiyun eeraar koolat tiruntadikkeezh,
ninRee yaatseyya neekoN daruLa ninaippatutaan,
kunRezh paareezh soozhkadal NYaalam muzhuveezhum,
ninRee taaviya neeLkazhal aazhit tirumaalee.
805.meaning: When indeed will You think to grant that I may stand and render service forever beneath Your beautiful radiant sacred feet? O Tirumal who bears the discus and the long anklets, who once stood and measured the seven worlds rising like mountains and all the seven seas encircling the earth!

806.translit:
tirumaal! naanmugan senjadai yaanen RivargaL, em
perumaan tanmaiyai yaaraRi kiRpaar peesiyen,
orumaa mutalvaa! oozhip piraanen naiyaaludai,
karumaa meeniyan enpanen kaatal kalakkavee.
806.meaning: Tirumal, the four-faced Brahma, the one with red matted locks — who can truly know the nature of my Lord beyond these, and what use is speaking of it? O peerless great First Cause, Lord of the aeons, You who own me as Your servant! When my love overwhelms me, I can only call Him the one with the great dark beautiful body.

807.translit:
kalakka millaa naltava munivar karaikaNdoor,
tuLakka millaa vaanava rellaam tozhuvaarkaL,
malakka meyta maakadal tannaik kadaindaanai,
ulakka naampugazh kiRpaten seyva turaiyeeree.
807.meaning: The undisturbed sages of fine austerities who have reached the far shore, and all the unwavering celestials, worship Him. He who churned the great ocean until it was thrown into turmoil — how can we ever praise Him to the full? Tell me, what is there to do?

808.translit:
uraiyaa vennoy tavira aruLneeN mudiyaanai,
varaiyaar maadam mannu kurugoorch chadagoopan,
uraiyeey soltodai ooraa yirattu Lippattum,
niraiyee vallaar needula kattup piRavaaree.
808.meaning: Of the thousand verses of well-fashioned poetry by Satakopan of Kurugur, the town with mansions tall as mountains, composed on the One with the long crown who graciously removes our unspeakable fierce afflictions — those who can recite these ten in order will not be born again in the enduring world.

809.translit:
vaarkadaa aruvi yaanaimaa malaiyin maruppi Naik kuvadiRut turutti,
oorkoLtiN paagan uyirsegut tarangin mallaraik konRusoozh paraNmeel,
poorkadaa varasar puRakkida maada meemasaik kanjanait tagartta,
seerkoLsiR Raayan tiruchengun Roorin tiruchiRRaa Rengalsel saarvee.
809.meaning: He broke off and rolled away the twin tusks of the great elephant streaming with rut like a mountain with cascading waterfalls, slew its strong mahout who held it for the town, killed the wrestlers in the arena, and from atop the high platform smashed Kamsa, driving away the kings who pressed the battle. That glorious cowherd-child of Tiruchengunrur — Tiruchittraru is our refuge.

810.translit:
engalsel saarvu yaamudai amutam imaiyavar appanen appan,
pongumoo vulagum padaittaLit tazhikkum poruntumoo vuruvanem aruvan,
sengayal ugaLum teempaNai pudaisoozh tiruchengun Roorttiruch chiRRaaRu
angamar kinRa, aatiyaan allaal yaavarmaR Renamar tuNaiyee?
810.meaning: Our refuge, the nectar we possess, the father of the celestials, my own father; the One of three fitting forms who creates, sustains and destroys the three surging worlds, our formless One. He, the primordial Lord, abides at Tiruchittraru in Tiruchengunrur, surrounded by sweet groves and tanks where red carp leap. Besides Him, who else is my companion?

811.translit:
ennamar perumaan imaiyavar perumaan irunilam idandavem perumaan,
munnaival vinaigaL muzhutudan maaLa ennaiyaaL kinReem perumaan,
tentisaik kaNikoL tiruchengun Rooril tiruchiRRaa Rangarai meepaal
ninRavem perumaan, adiyallaal saraNam ninaippilum piRitillai enakkee.
811.meaning: My Lord who is dear to me, Lord of the celestials, my Lord who lifted up the vast earth; my Lord who took me into His service so that all my old fierce karmas were utterly destroyed. My Lord who stands upon the bank of Tiruchittraru in Tiruchengunrur, the jewel of the southern quarter — for me there is no other refuge, even in thought, but His feet.

812.translit:
piRitillai yenakkup periyamoo vulagum niRaiyappee ruruvamaay nimirnda,
kuRiya maaN emmaan kuraikadal kadainda koolamaa Nikkamen emmaan,
seRikulai vaazhai kamugutheng kanisoozh tiruchengun Roorttiruch chiRRaaRu
aRiya, meymmaiyee ninRavem perumaan adiyiNai yallatoor araNee.
812.meaning: There is no other for me — my Lord, the noble dwarf who grew filling the three great worlds with His vast form, my Lord like a lovely ruby churned from the roaring ocean. He, my Lord, stands truly and manifest at Tiruchittraru in Tiruchengunrur, encircled by clustered plantains, areca palms and coconut groves, that all may know Him. No fortress is there for me but the pair of His feet.

813.translit:
allatoor araNum avanilvee Rillai atuporu Laagilum, avanai
allaten aavi amarndaNai killaa taatalaal avanu Rai kinRa,
nallanaan maRaiyoor veeLviyuL maduttta naRumpugai visumboLi maRaikkum,
nallaneeL maadat tiruchengun Rooril tiruchiRRaa Renakkunal laraNee.
813.meaning: There is no other fortress apart from Him, and though that be the truth, my soul cannot rest content and find repose in anything but Him. Therefore the place where He dwells — Tiruchittraru in Tiruchengunrur of fine tall mansions, where the fragrant smoke offered in the sacrifices of the good knowers of the four Vedas veils the light of the sky — that is my good fortress.

814.translit:
enakkunal laraNai enataa ruyirai imaiyavar tandaitaay tannai,
tanakkuntan tanmai aRivari yaanait tadangadal paLLiyam maanai,
manakkoLseer moovaa yiravarvaN sivanum ayanum taanumop paarvaazh,
kanakkoLtiN maadat tiruchengun Rooril tiruchiRRaa Ratanulkan deenee.
814.meaning: My good fortress, my very life-breath, the father and mother of the celestials, the One whose own nature is hard for even Himself to know, the Lord who reclines on the vast ocean — Him I have beheld at Tiruchittraru in Tiruchengunrur of dense strong mansions, where the three thousand of glorious renown dwell, and where the bountiful Siva, Brahma and He Himself are held as equals.

815.translit:
tiruchengun Rooril tiruchiRRaa Ratanul kaNdavat tiruvadi yenRum,
tiruchceyya kamalak kaNNoomsev vaayum sevvadi yumseyya kaiyum,
tiruchceyya kamala vuntiyum seyya kamalamaar pumseyya vudaiyum,
tiruchceyya mudiyum aaramum padaiyum tigazhaven sindaiyu Laanee.
815.meaning: That sacred Lord whom I beheld at Tiruchittraru in Tiruchengunrur — forever, His sacred red lotus eyes, red mouth, red feet and red hands, His sacred red lotus navel, His chest adorned with the red lotus, His red garment, His sacred red crown, His garland and His weapons — all shining radiantly, He dwells within my mind.

816.translit:
tigazhaven sindai yuLLirun taanaich chezhunilat teevarnaan maRaiyoor,
tisaikaikooppi yeettum tiruchengun Rooril tiruchiRRaa Rangarai yaanai,
pugarkoLvaa navarkaL pugalidan tannai asurarvan kaiyarveng kooRRai,
pugazhumaa RaRiyeen poruntumoo vulagum padaippodu keduppukkaap pavanee.
816.meaning: He who dwells shining within my mind, He on the bank of Tiruchittraru in Tiruchengunrur whom the gods of the rich earth and the knowers of the four Vedas worship with hands folded toward all directions; the refuge of the radiant celestials, the fierce death to the strong-handed demons — I know not how to praise Him, the One who creates, destroys and protects the three fitting worlds.

817.translit:
padaippodu keduppuk kaappavan birama paramparan sivaperumaan avanee,
idaippukkoo ruruvum ozhivillai yavanee pugazhvillai yaavaiyum taanee,
kodaipperum pugazhaar inaiyartan naanaar kooriya vicchaiyoo dozhukkam,
nadaippali yiyaRkait tiruchengun Rooril tiruchiRRaa Ramarnda naatanee.
817.meaning: He who creates, destroys and protects is Brahma, the supreme beyond all, and Siva the great Lord — He is all. There is no form in between that is not His, no praise that is not His; He Himself is all things. The Lord abiding at Tiruchittraru in Tiruchengunrur, whose people of great bountiful renown are likened to Him, following their declared learning and conduct and the way of offering oblations.

818.translit:
amarnda naatanai yavarava raagi avarkkaruL aruLumam maanai
amarndataN pazhanat tiruchengun Rooril tiruchiRRaaR Rangarai yaanai,
amarndaseer moovaa yiravarvee tiyarkaL tampati yavanitee varvaazhvu,
amarndamaa yoonai mukkaNam maanai naanmuga naiyamarn deenee.
818.meaning: The Lord who abides there, who becomes each and every being and bestows grace upon them, the Lord on the bank of Tiruchittraru in Tiruchengunrur of cool fields, the town of the three thousand Vedic ones of settled glory, the life of the gods upon earth — that wondrous dark One who is also Siva of the three eyes and the four-faced Brahma, in Him have I found my rest.

819.translit:
teenainan paalaik kannalai yamutait tiruntula guNdavam maanai,
vaananaan muganai malarndtaN koppoozh malarmisaip padaittamaa yoonai,
koonaivaN kurugoorch vaNchada goopan sonnavaa yirattuLip pattum,
vaaninmee teeRRi yaruLseytu mudikkum piRavimaa maayakkoot tinaiyee.
819.meaning: Of the thousand verses sung by the bountiful Satakopan of rich Kurugur upon the Lord — honey, fine milk, sugar and nectar, who graciously swallowed the well-ordered worlds, the wondrous One who created the four-faced Brahma upon the blossom of His cool navel-lotus — these ten will raise one above to the heavens and, by His grace, bring to an end the great delusive dance of birth.

820.translit:
maayak kooththaa! vaamanaa! vinaiyEn kaNNaa! kaNkaikaal
thooya seyya malarkaLaach chOdhich chevvaay mukizhadhaa,
saayal saamath thirumEni thaNpaa sadaiyaa, thaamarai neeL
vaasath thadampOl varuvaanE! orunaaL kaaNa vaaraayE!
820.meaning: O wondrous dancer! O Vamana! O Kannan of me the sinner! Your eyes, hands and feet are like pure red flowers, your radiant red mouth like a bud, your gentle dark sacred form cool and lovely, O lord with matted locks who comes like a vast fragrant lotus pool — come one day so that I may behold You!

821.translit:
kaaNa vaaraay enRenRu kaNNum vaayum thuvarndhu, adiyEn
naaNi nannaat talamandhaal irangi yorunaaL nee andhO,
kaaNa vaaraay karunaayi RudhikkUm karumaa maaNikka,
naaNal malaipOl sudarch chOdhi mudisEr senni yammaanE.
821.meaning: Saying again and again "Come that I may see You," my eyes and mouth grow parched; when I, Your servant, ashamed, suffer through the long good days, do You take pity and come one day, alas, that I may behold You — O great dark-sapphire Lord whose head bears a crown of radiant light rising like a black sun, like a mountain of reed-bright flame.

822.translit:
mudisEr senni yammaa! nin moypoon thaamath thaNthuzhaay,
kadisEr kaNNip perumaanE! enRenR Engi yazhudhakkaal,
padisEr makarak kuzhaikaLum pavaLa vaayum naal thOLum,
thudisE ridaiyum amaindhadhOr thooneer mukilpOl thOnRaayE.
822.meaning: O Lord whose head wears a crown! O great one wearing the cool tulasi garland thick with bees and fragrant blossoms! When I cry and yearn calling thus again and again, appear to me like a pure-watered cloud, formed complete with the makara earrings hanging by Your cheeks, Your coral mouth, Your four shoulders and Your slender waist like a small drum.

823.translit:
thooneer mukilpOl thOnRum nin sudarkkoL vadivum kanivaayum,
thEneerk kamalak kaNkaLum vandhen sindhai niRaindhavaa,
maaneer veLLee malaidhanmEl vaNkaar neela mukilpOl,
thooneerk kadaluL thuyilvaanE! endhaay! solla maattEnE!
823.meaning: How Your radiant form that appears like a pure-watered cloud, Your fruit-sweet mouth, and Your honey-watered lotus eyes have come and filled my mind — like a generous dark blue rain-cloud upon a great silver mountain of vast waters! O my Father who sleeps upon the pure-watered ocean! I cannot find words to tell it!

824.translit:
solla maattEn adiyEn un thuLangu sOdhith thiruppaadham,
ellai yilsee riLanjaayi RiraNdu pOlen nuLLavaa!,
allal ennum iruLsErdhaR kupaayam ennE?, aazhisoozh
mallal njaala muzhudhuNda maaneerk koNdal vaNNanE!
824.meaning: I, Your servant, cannot tell how Your glittering radiant sacred feet, like two young suns of limitless glory, have entered within me! What recourse is there to keep from sinking into the darkness called sorrow? O cloud-hued Lord of vast waters who swallowed the whole fertile world girdled by the sea!

825.translit:
koNdal vaNNaa! kudakkooththaa! vinaiyEn kaNNaa. kaNNaa, en
aNda vaaNaa! enRennai aaLak kooppit tazhaiththakkaal,
viNdan mEldhaan maNmEldhaan virineerk kadaldhaan maRRuththaan,
thoNda nEnuL kazhal kaaNa orunaaL vandhu thOnRaayE.
825.meaning: O cloud-hued one! O dancer with the pots! O Kannan of me the sinner! O Kannan, O Dweller in my world! When I cry out and call to be taken into Your service, whether it be in the heavens above, or upon the earth, or in the wide-watered ocean, or anywhere else — come and appear one day that I Your devotee may behold Your anklet-adorned feet.

826.translit:
vandhu thOnRaa yanREl un vaiyam thaaya malaradikkeezh,
mundhi vandhu yaan niRpa mukappE koovip paNikoLLaay,
sendhaN kamalak kaNkaikaal sivandha vaayOr karunaayiRu,
andha millaak kadhirparappi alarndha dhokkum ammaanE.
826.meaning: If You will not come and appear, then beneath the blossom-feet that measured the world — where I come and stand foremost before You — call me to Your face and take me into Your service. O Lord whose red-cool lotus eyes, hands and feet and red mouth resemble a black sun spreading endless rays and blossoming forth.

827.translit:
okkum ammaa nuruvamen RuLLam kuzhaindhu naaNaaLum,
thokka mEkap palkuzhaangaL kaaNun thORum tholaivan naan,
thakka aivar thamakkaayanRu Reraim padhinmar thaaLsaaya,
pukka naltherth thanippaakaa. vaaraay idhuvO poruththamE?
827.meaning: Thinking "this resembles my Lord's form," my heart melts, and day after day, whenever I see the gathered masses of many clustered clouds, I waste away. O peerless charioteer of the fine chariot who drove in that day, so that the feet (lives) of the twice-fifty (hundred Kauravas) fell down for the sake of the worthy five (Pandavas)! Come — is this fitting of You?

828.translit:
idhuvO poruththam minnaazhip padaiyaay ERum irunjchiRaip puL,
adhuvE kodiyaa vuyarththaanE! enRenR Engi yazhudhakkaal,
edhuvE yaakak karudhumkol immaa njaalam poRaitheerppaan,
madhuvaar sOlai yuththara madhuraip piRandha maayanE?
828.meaning: "Is this fitting?" — O You who bear the lightning-bright discus weapon, O You who raised high as Your banner that great strong-winged bird (Garuda)! When I cry out and yearn calling thus, what does He intend it to be — He the wondrous one born in Northern Mathura of honey-dripping groves, who came to relieve this great world's burden?

829.translit:
piRandha maayaa! paaradham porudha maayaa! nee innE,
siRandha kaaldhee neervaanmaN piRavu maaya perumaanE,
kaRandha paaluL neyyEpOl ivaRRu Lengum kaNdukoL,
iRandhu ninRa perumaayaa! unnai engE kaaNkEnE?
829.meaning: O wondrous one who was born! O wondrous one who fought the Bharata war! O great Lord who even now has become the excelling wind, fire, water, sky, earth and all else! Like the ghee within the milked milk, You are to be seen pervading everywhere within these; O great wondrous one who stands transcending all — where shall I behold You?

830.translit:
engE kaaNkEn een thuzhay ammaan Rannai yaan? enRenRu
angE thaazhndha soRkaLaal andhaN kurukoorch chadakOpan,
sengEzh sonna vaayiraththuL ivaiyum paththum vallaarkaL,
ingE kaaNa ippiRappE makizhvar elliyum kaalaiyE.
830.meaning: "Where shall I behold that Lord wearing the sweet tulasi?" — saying thus, Satakopan of beautiful cool Kurukur, in words that bowed low there, spoke a thousand of fine quality; those who master these ten among them shall, in this very birth, rejoice to behold Him here, both at evening and at morning.

831.translit:
elliyum kaalaiyum thannai ninaindhezha,
nalla aruLkaL namakkE thandh aruLseyvaan,
alliyan thaNNan thuzhaaymudi yappanoor,
selvarkaL vaazhum thirukkadith thaanamE.
831.meaning: That we may rise remembering Him both evening and morning, He grants and bestows good grace upon us alone. The town of the Father whose crown wears the cool, beautiful, blossom-laden tulasi is Tirukkadittanam, where the prosperous ones dwell.

832.translit:
thirukkadith thaanamum ennudaich chindhaiyum,
orukkaduth thuLLE uRaiyum piraankaNdeer,
serukkaduth thanRu thikaiththa arakkarai,
urukkeda vaaLi pozhindha oruvanE.
832.meaning: Behold — He is the Lord who, joining Tirukkadittanam and my mind together as one, dwells within. He is the peerless one who, that day, poured forth arrows so that the bewildered demons, swollen with the pride of battle, melted away in form.

833.translit:
oruva riruvarOr moova renaninRu,
uruvu karandhuL LundhORum thiththippaan,
thiruvamar maarvan thirukkadith thaanaththai,
maruvi yuraikinRa maayap piraanE.
833.meaning: Standing as one, as two, as three, He hides His form and grows ever sweeter each time within. He is the wondrous Lord on whose chest Lakshmi resides, who is intimately said to dwell in Tirukkadittanam.

834.translit:
maayap piraanena valvinai maayndhaRa,
nEyaththi naalnenjam naadu kudikoNdaan,
thEsath thamarar thirukkadith thaanaththai,
vaasap pozhilmannu kOyilkoN daanE.
834.meaning: When I called Him "the wondrous Lord" so that my fierce karma was destroyed and ended, with love He took up dwelling in the land of my heart. He is the one who, in Tirukkadittanam of the celestials of the radiant realm, has taken as His temple the place set amid fragrant groves.

835.translit:
kOyilkoN daanthan thirukkadith thaanaththai,
kOyilkoN dadhan Edumen nenjakam,
kOyilkoL theyvamel laamthozha, vaikundham
kOyilkoN dakudak kooththavam maanE.
835.meaning: He took Tirukkadittanam as His temple; and along with that He took my heart too as His temple — He the Lord, the dancer with the pots, who took Vaikuntha as His temple so that all the enshrined gods might worship Him.

836.translit:
kooththavam maankodi yEnidar muRRavum,
maayththavam maanmadhu soodhavam maanuRai,
pooththa pozhilthaN thirukkadith thaanaththai,
Eththanil laakuRik koNdamin idarE.
836.meaning: The Lord who is the dancer, the Lord Madhusudana who utterly destroyed the sorrows of me the wretched one, dwells in cool Tirukkadittanam of blossoming groves. Resolve to praise it without ceasing, and thereby take away your sorrows.

837.translit:
koNdamin idarkeda vuLLaththuk kOvindhan,
maNviN muzhudhum aLandhavoN daamarai,
maNNavar thaamthozha vaanavar thaamvandhu,
naNNu thirukkadith thaana nakarE.
837.meaning: Take it up within your heart, that your sorrows may perish — Govinda's town is Tirukkadittanam, the bright-lotus(-footed) one who measured all of earth and sky, where the people of earth worship Him and the celestials come and draw near.

838.translit:
thaana nakarkaL thalaisiRan thengengum,
vaanin nilamkadal muRRumem maayaRkE,
aana vidaththumen nenjum thirukkadith
thaana nakarum, thanadhaayap padhiyE.
838.meaning: Though all His sacred towns excel above the rest everywhere, and the sky, this earth and the sea and all things belong to our wondrous Lord, both my heart and the town of Tirukkadittanam are His very own inherited abode.

839.translit:
thaayap padhikaLthalaisiRan thengengum,
maayaththi naalmanni veeRRirun thaanuRai,
thEyath thamarar thirukkadith thaanaththuL,
aayark kadhipadhi aRpudhan thaanE.
