839.meaning: Though His inherited abodes excel above the rest everywhere, where through His wondrous power He abides enthroned in majesty, within Tirukkadittanam of the celestials of that realm dwells the wondrous one Himself, the lord of the cowherds.

840.translit:
aRputhan naaraayaNan ari vaamanan,
niRpathu meevi yiruppathu en nenjagam,
naR pugazh veethiyar naanmaRai ninRu athir,
kaRpagac choolaith thirukkadith thaanamee.
840.meaning: The wondrous one, Narayana, Hari, Vamana, has come and taken his standing and dwelling in my heart. He is also at Tirukkadittanam, where the good and renowned Vedic brahmins stand and resound the four Vedas, the place of groves of wish-granting kalpaka trees.

841.translit:
choolai thirukkadith thaanath thuRai thiru
maalai, mathiL kurugoor chadagoopan chol,
paalo damuthu anna aayiraththu ippaththum,
meelai vaigunthath thiruththum viyanthey.
841.meaning: These ten among the thousand verses, sweet as milk mingled with nectar, are the words of Satakopan of walled Kurukur about Tirumal who abides at Tirukkadittanam of the groves. Those who master them will be wondrously settled in the lofty Vaikuntha above.

842.translit:
vianthu, ennaith than paadhaamalark keezh, enRu
aruththiththu, enaiththu oor pala naaL arththeeRkku,
poruththam udai vaamanan thaan pugunthu, en-than
karuththai uRa, veeRRirunthaan kaNdukoNdee.
842.meaning: Wondering, when shall He set me beneath His lotus feet, I had longed and pleaded for so many countless days. The fitting Vamana himself entered and, attaining my very thought, has seated himself gloriously within me, and I beheld it.

843.translit:
irunthaan kaNdukoN denathu eezhai nenju aaLum,
thirunthaatha oor aivaraith theeynthu aRamanni,
perunthaaL kaLiRRukku aruLseytha perumaan,
tharunthaan aruLthaan iniyaan aRiyeenee.
843.meaning: He has seated himself, beholding and ruling my poor heart, wearing away the unreformed five senses and abiding in righteousness. The great Lord who showed grace to the elephant of mighty feet (Gajendra) bestows His own grace; what He will give henceforth I do not know.

844.translit:
aruLthaan iniyaan aRiyeen avan ennuL,
iruLthaan aRa veeRRirunthaan ithuvallaal,
poruLthaan enil moovulagum poruLalla,
maruLthaan eethoo? maaya mayakku mayakkee.
844.meaning: What grace He will give henceforth I do not know; He has seated Himself within me so that all darkness is dispelled. Apart from this, if we speak of what is real, the three worlds are not truly real. Is this then delusion? It is the bewildering enchantment of His maya.

845.translit:
maaya mayakku mayakkaan ennai vanjiththu,
aayan amararkku ariyeeRu enathu ammaan,
thooya chudarc choothi thanathu ennuL vaiththaan,
theeyam thigazhum than thiruvaruL seythee.
845.meaning: Without bewildering me with the enchantment of maya or deceiving me, the cowherd, the lion among the immortals, my Lord, has placed His own pure radiant light within me. He did so by granting His sacred grace that shines forth over all regions.

846.translit:
thigazhum than thiruvaruL seythu ulagaththaar,
pugazhum pugazhthaan athu kaattith thanthu, ennuL
thigazhum maNikkunRam onRee oththu ninRaan,
pugazhum pugazh maRRu enakkum oor poruLee?
846.meaning: Granting His shining sacred grace, He showed and gave me that glory which the people of the world extol, and stood within me like a single gleaming hill of gems. Is there any other glory worth praising left for me?

847.translit:
poruL maRRu enakkum oor poruL thannil cheerkkath
tharumeel, pin yaarkku avan thannaik kodukkum?,
karu maaNikkak kunRaththuth thaamaraipool,
thirumaarbu kaal kaN kai chevvaay unthiyaanee.
847.meaning: If He grants Himself, the most excellent substance, as my one true treasure, then to whom else would He give Himself? He is the one whose sacred chest, feet, eyes, hands, red mouth and navel are like lotuses upon a dark-emerald hill.

848.translit:
chevvaay unthi veNpal sudarkkuzhai, thannoodu
evvaayc chudarum thammil mun vaLaaykkoLLa,
chevvaay muRuvaloodu enathu uLLaththu iruntha,
avvaayan anRi yaan aRiyeen maRRu aruLee.
848.meaning: With His red mouth, navel, white teeth and radiant ear-ornaments, so that every kind of brilliance gathers and encircles before them, with a smile on His red lips He dwelt within my heart. Apart from that cowherd I know of no other grace.

849.translit:
aRiyeen maRRu aruL ennai yaaLum piraanaar,
veRitheey aruLseyvar seyvaarkatku uganthu,
chiRiyeen udaic chinthai uL moovulagum, tham
neRiyaa vayiRRiR koNdu ninRu ozhinthaaree.
849.meaning: I know of no other grace; my Lord who rules me bestows grace freely, delighting in those to whom He grants it. Within the heart of lowly me, holding the three worlds in His belly as His own way, He has come and stood.

850.translit:
vayiRRiR koNdu ninRu ozhinthaarum yavarum,
vayiRRiR koNdu ninRu oru moovulagum, tham
vayiRRiR koNdu ninRa vaNNam ninRa maalai,
vayiRRiR koNdu manna vaiththeen mathiyaalee.
850.meaning: He who holds all beings within His belly, and they too; He who holds the unique three worlds within His belly - that Tirumal who stands holding all within His belly, Him I have held and lastingly placed within my own being through wisdom.

851.translit:
vaiththeen mathiyaal enathu uLLaththu agaththee,
eyththee ozhiveen alleen enRum eppoothum,
moyththeey thirai moothu thaN paaR kadaluLaal,
paiththeey chudarp paambu aNai nam paranaiyee.
851.meaning: Through wisdom I have placed within my heart our Supreme One who reclines upon the radiant hooded serpent in the cool ocean of milk where dense waves surge. I shall never grow weary and lose Him, ever and at all times.

852.translit:
chudarp paambu aNai nam paranaith thirumaalai,
adicheer vagai vaN kurugoorc chadagoopan,
mudippaan chonna aayiraththu ippaththum janmam
vida, theeynthu aRa nookkum than kaNgaL chivanthee.
852.meaning: These ten among the thousand verses that Satakopan of bountiful Kurukur composed, to reach the feet of our Supreme One, Tirumal who reclines on the radiant serpent couch, will cause one's births to depart. He who finished them gazes with His reddened eyes so that they wholly wear away.

853.translit:
kaNgaL chivanthu periyavaay vaayum chivanthu kaninthu, uLLee
veNpal ilagu chudar ilagu vilagu magara kuNdalaththan,
koNdal vaNNan chudar mudiyan naangu thooLan kuni saarngan,
oN changu gathai vaaL aazhiyaan oruvan adiyeen uLLaanee.
853.meaning: His eyes are red and large, His mouth too red and tender, within shining white teeth gleaming with light, wearing flashing makara ear-rings. Cloud-hued, with a radiant crown, four-shouldered, bearing the bent Sharnga bow, holding the bright conch, mace, sword and discus - that peerless One dwells within me, His servant.

854.translit:
adiyeen uLLaan udaluLLaan aNdaththu agaththaan puRaththuLLaan,
padiyee ithu enRu uraikkalaam, padiyan allan paramparan,
kadiseer naaRRaththu uLaalai inbath thunbak kazhi neermai,
odiyaa inbap perumaiyoon uNarvil umbar oruvanee.
854.meaning: He is within me His servant, within my body, within the cosmos and outside it; He cannot be described saying His nature is just like this, for He is beyond all measure, the Supreme of supremes. He who is beyond the bounds of fragrant pleasure and pain, of unbreaking blissful greatness, is the one beyond all comprehension, above all.

855.translit:
uNarvil umbar oruvanai avanathu aruLaal uRal poruttu, en
uNarvin uLLee iruththineen athuvum avanathu innaruLee,
uNarvum uyirum udambum maRRu ulappilanavum pazhutheeyaam,
uNarvaip peRa oornthu iRa eeRi yaanum thaanaay ozhinthaanee.
855.meaning: That One who is beyond comprehension, above all, I have seated within my consciousness so that I might attain Him by His own grace - and even that is His sweet grace alone. Consciousness, life, body and all the rest without limit would be in vain; mounting and entering so that I gain true awareness, He made me one with Himself.

856.translit:
yaanum thaanaay ozhinthaanai yaathum yavarkkum munnoonai,
thaanum chivanum piramanum aagip paNaiththa thanimuthalai,
theenum paalum kannalum amuthum aagith thiththiththu, en
ooni luyiri luNarvinil ninRa onRai uNarntheenee.
856.meaning: Him who made me one with Himself, the primal One before all things and all beings, the sole source who expanded as Himself, as Shiva and as Brahma; who, sweetening like honey and milk and cane-sugar and nectar, stands within my flesh, my life and my awareness - that One I have come to realize.

857.translit:
ninRa onRai uNarntheenukku athanuL neermai athu ithu enRu,
onRum oruvarkku uNaralaagaathu uNarnthum meelum kaaNparithu,
chenRu chenRu paramparamaay yaathum inRith theeynthaRRu,
nanRu theethu enRu aRivarithaay nanRaay nyaanam kadanthathee.
857.meaning: For me who realized the One that abides, its inner nature cannot be told by anyone as this or that; even when realized, it is hard to fully see beyond. Going on and on, ever more transcendent, fading into utter nothingness, hard to discern as good or evil, it is wholly good, and lies beyond all knowledge.

858.translit:
nanRaay nyaanam kadanthu pooy nallinthiriya mellaam eerththu,
onRaayk kidantha arumperumpaazh ulappil athanai uNarnthu uNarnthu,
chenRaangu inba thunbangaL cheRRuk kaLainthu pasaiyaRRaal,
anRee appoothee veedu athuvee veedu veedaamee.
858.meaning: Passing wholly beyond knowledge, drawing in all the good senses, realizing again and again that rare, vast, limitless void that lies as one - going thither and destroying and casting off pleasures and pains until all attachment ceases, then and there is release; that very thing is release, the true liberation.

859.translit:
athuvee veedu veedu peeRRu inbanthaanum athu theeRi,
ethuvee thaanum paRRinRi yaathum iligaL aagiRkil,
athuvee veedu veedu peeRRu inbanthaanum athu theeRaathu,
ethuvee veedee thinbam? enRu eyththaar eyththaar eyththaaree.
859.meaning: That is release, and the bliss of attaining liberation too - realizing this, if one becomes wholly devoid, grasping at nothing whatsoever, that itself is release and the bliss of liberation. But failing to realize this, asking which is release and what is bliss, men have wearied and wearied and grown weary.

860.translit:
eyttaar eyttaar eyttaar enRu illaaththaarum puRaththaarum
moyththu, aang kalaRi muyangkaththaam poogum poodhu, unmaththar pool
piththee yeeRi yanuraagam pozhiyum poodhem pemmaanoodu
oththee senRu, angkuLamkuudak kuudiRRaakil nalluRaippee.
860.meaning: When even those who are not kin and outsiders crowd around crying 'they are weary, they are weary, they are exhausted,' embracing me as I depart, if at that time, like a person possessed, mounting upon frenzy and pouring out love, my heart goes forth in unison with my Lord and unites with Him there, then that is a good fortune of attainment.

861.translit:
kuudiRRaakil nalluRaippuk kuudaamaiyaik kuudinaal,
aadal paRavai yuyarkodiyem aayanaava thuvathuvee,
viidaip paNNi yoruparisee edhirvum nigazhvum kazhivumaay,
oodith thiriyum yoogikaLum uLaru millai yallaree.
861.meaning: If union is attained, that is the good attainment; and if instead what unites is the unattainable, then that too is none other than our cowherd Lord whose lofty banner bears the dancing bird (Garuda). Making this clear in a single manner, He is the future, the present, and the past; and the yogis who run about seeking Him are neither truly existent nor wholly non-existent.

862.translit:
uLaru millai yallaraay uLaraay illai yaagiyee,
uLarem moruvar avarvandhen uLLath thuLLee yuRaiginRaar
vaLarum piRaiyum theeypiRaiyum pool asaivum aakkamum,
vaLarum sudarum iruLumpool theruLum maruLum maayththoomee.
862.meaning: He who is and is not, who being existent yet is not graspable as merely existent — that One of ours came and dwells within my heart. Like the waxing moon and the waning moon are decline and growth; like the growing flame and the darkness, He has destroyed in us both clear knowing and bewilderment.

863.translit:
theruLum maruLum maayththuththan thirundhu sempoR kazhaladikkiizh
aruLi yiruththum ammaanaam ayanaam sivanaam, thirumaalaal
aruLap patta sadagoopan oraa yiraththu Lippaththaal,
aruLi yadikkiizh iruththumnam aNNal karumaa Nikkamee.
863.meaning: Destroying both clear knowing and delusion, He is the Lord who graciously seats us beneath His perfect red-golden anklet-feet — He who is Brahma, who is Shiva. Through these ten verses out of the thousand of Satakopan, who was graced by Tirumal, our Chief, the dark sapphire-hued one, will graciously seat us beneath His feet.

864.translit:
karumaa Nikka malaimeelmaNith thadandhaamaraik kaadukaLpool,
thirumaarvu vaaykaNkai yundhikaaludai yaadaikaL seyyapiraan
thirumaa lemmaan sezhuneervayal kuttanaattuth thiruppuliyuur,
arumaayan peeranRip peeccilaL annaimiiridhaR kenseykeenaa.
864.meaning: Like great groves of jewelled lotuses in broad pools upon a mountain of dark sapphire are the sacred chest, mouth, eyes, hands, navel, feet and red garments of the gracious Lord. O mothers, she will not speak of anything but the name of that rare wondrous one, Tirumal my Lord of Tiruppuliyur in Kuttanadu of rich watered fields — what shall I do for this?

865.translit:
annaimii ridhaRken seykeen? aNimeeruvin miidhulavum,
thunnu suuzhsudar gnaayiRum anRiyumpal sudarkaLumpool,
minnu niiNmudi yaarampalkalan thaanudai yemperumaan,
punnai yampozhil suuzhthirup puliyuur pugazhumivaLee.
865.meaning: O mothers, what shall I do for this? Like the dense radiant sun that moves above the lovely Mount Meru, and like the many other lights besides, our Lord wears a long glittering crown, a garland and many ornaments. She praises Tiruppuliyur encircled by beautiful groves of punnai trees.

866.translit:
pugazhu mivaLninR iraappagal poruniirkkadal thiippattu, engkum
thigazhu meriyodu selvadhoppac sezhungkadhi raazhimudhal
pugazhum porupadai yeendhippoor pukkasurarip ponRuviththaan
thigazhu maNinedu maadamniidu thiruppuuli yuurvaLamee.
866.meaning: She praises night and day, as if the churning watery sea had caught fire and were everywhere ablaze with spreading flames — He who, bearing the bright-rayed discus and other warring weapons, entered battle and destroyed the asuras. She praises the abundance of Tiruppuliyur where tall jewelled mansions gleam.

867.translit:
uurva LamkiLar soolaiyum karumbumperunj sennelumsuuzhndhu
eervaLam kiLar thaNpaNaik kuttanaattuth thiruppuuliyuur,
siirvaLam kiLar muuvula kuNdumizh deevapiraan,
peervaLam kiLarndh anRip peeccilaLinRip punaiyizhaiyee.
867.meaning: In Tiruppuliyur of Kuttanadu, whose cool fields swell with beauty, surrounded by groves, sugarcane and great red paddy, dwells the divine Lord who swallowed the three worlds and gave them forth again, His glory abounding. The maiden with fine ornaments will speak of nothing but the swelling abundance of His name.

868.translit:
punaiyizhaikaL aNivum aadaiyudaiyum pudhukka Nippum,
ninaiyum niirmaiya thanRivadkidhu ninRu ninaikkappukkaal,
sunuyi nuLthadan thaamarai malarumthaN thiruppuliyuur,
munaivan muuvula kaaLiyappan thiruvaruL muuzh kinaLee.
868.meaning: Adorning herself with fine ornaments, with garments and dress, and fresh decoration — these are no longer of a nature she can even think of, for when we come to reflect upon it, she has plunged into the grace of the First Lord, the Father who rules the three worlds, of cool Tiruppuliyur where broad lotuses bloom in the springs.

869.translit:
thiruvaruL muuzhki vaikaLum sezhuniirniRak kaNNapiraan,
thiruvaruL kaLumseern dhamaikkadai yaaLam thirundhavuLa,
aruLaal thiruvaruL avan senRu seerthaN thiruppuuliyuur,
thiruvaruL kamukoN pazhuththadhu melliyal sevvidhazhee.
869.meaning: Plunged in His grace, and bearing the very marks that show His grace has joined her — that of Kannan the dark-watered-hued Lord — through His grace she has reached, by His grace, cool Tiruppuliyur where He has come and dwells. The tender-natured one's red lips have ripened like the fruit of the graceful areca palm.

870.translit:
mellilaic selvaN kodipulka viingkiLan thaaLkamukin,
mallilai madalvaazhai yiingkani suuzhndhu maNamkamazhndhu,
pullilaith thengki nuudukaal ulavumthaN thiruppuuliyuur,
mallalam selvak kaNNandhaaL adaindhaaL im madavaralee.
870.meaning: In cool Tiruppuliyur, where soft-leaved rich vines embrace the swelling young-trunked areca palms, where strong-leaved plantains bear sweet fruit, fragrance spreads, and the breeze wanders among the slender-leaved coconut trees — this gentle maiden has reached the feet of Kannan of flourishing abundant wealth.

871.translit:
madavaral annaimiirkat kenjsollic sollukeen? mallaicselva
vadamozhi maRaivaaNar veeLviyuL neyyazhal vaanpugaipooy
thidavisum bilamarar naattai maRaikkumthaN thiruppuuliyuur,
padavara vaNaiyaan RannaamamallaaL paravaa LivaLee.
871.meaning: O mothers of this gentle maiden, what shall I tell you and how? In cool Tiruppuliyur, where the ghee-fed sacrificial flames of the masters of the rich northern-tongued Vedas send up great smoke that, in the firm sky, veils the land of the immortals — she will utter nothing but the name of Him who reclines on the hooded serpent.

872.translit:
paravaa LivaLninR iraappagal paniniirniRak kaNNapiraan,
viravaa risaimaRai veedhiyaroli veelaiyin ninRolippa,
karavaar thadandhoRum thaamaraik kayandhiivigai ninRalarum,
puravaar kazhanikaL suuzhthirup puliyuurppuga zhanRimaRRee.
872.meaning: She praises night and day Kannan of cool-water hue; in Tiruppuliyur, where the harmonious chant of the Veda-reciting brahmins resounds ceaselessly like the sea, where lotuses bloom abundantly in every brimming pool and pond, surrounded by sheltering fields — she will say nothing but His praise.

873.translit:
anRimaR Roorubaaya menniva LandhaNdu zhaaykamazhdhal,
kunRa maamaNi maadamaaLikaik koolakku zhaangkaLmalki,
thendhi saiththila thampuraik kuttanaattuth thiruppuuliyuur,
ninRa maayappi raandhiru varuLaamivaL neerppattadhee.
873.meaning: What other means is there but this, that she should be fragrant with the cool fresh tulasi? In Tiruppuliyur of Kuttanadu — which is like a tilaka adorning the southern direction, abounding with lovely throngs of mountain-like jewelled mansions and palaces — this has befallen her as the grace of the wondrous Lord who stands there.

874.translit:
neerppatta niRaimuu vulagukkum naayakan Rannadimai,
neerppatta thoNdarthoNdar thoNdarthoNdan sadagoopan, sol
neerppatta thamizhmaalai yaayiraththuL ivaiyu moorpaththum
neerppat taaravar, neerppattaar nedumaaRkadi maiseyyavee.
874.meaning: Of Satakopan — the servant of the servants of the servants of the devotees who have fittingly become the servants of the Lord, master of all the three full worlds — these ten among the thousand fitting Tamil garland-verses: those who fittingly attain them have attained to render service to the great Tirumal.

875.translit:
nedumaaR kadimai seyveenpool avanai karuda vanjiththu,
thadumaaR RaRRa thiikkadigaL muRRum thavirndha sadhirni naindhaal,
kodumaa vinaiyeen avanadiyaar adiyee kuudum idhuvallaal,
vidumaa Renba dhennandhoo! viyanmuu vulagu peRinumee?
875.meaning: As one who would render service to the great Tirumal, I deceitfully cherished Him — yet when I reflect on the cleverness by which all the evil paths that bring confusion were wholly removed, then for me of grievous deeds, what release could there be other than this, to join the very feet of His devotees' feet — alas, even were I to gain the vast three worlds?

876.translit:
viyanmuu vulagu peRinumpooyth thaanee thaanee yaanaalum,
puyalmee kampool thirumeeni ammaan punaipuung kazhaladikkiizh,
sayamee yadimai thalaininRaar thiruththaaL vaNangki, immaiyee
payanee yinbam yaanpeRRa dhuRumoo paavi yeenukkee?
876.meaning: Even were I to gain the vast three worlds, and even were I to become utterly self-sufficient and independent, I bow before the sacred feet of those who, by their own victory, stand foremost in service beneath the adorned blossom-anklet-feet of the Lord whose sacred form is like a rain-cloud. Could this fruit, this bliss that I have gained even in this very life, truly befit me, sinner that I am?

877.translit:
uRumoo paavi yeenukkiv vulagam muunRum udanniRaiya,
siRumaa meeni nimirththaven sendhaa maraikkaN thirukkuRaLan
naRumaa virainaaN malaradikkiizhp pugudhal anRi avanadiyaar,
siRumaa manisa raayennai aaNdaa ringkee thiriyavee.
877.meaning: Could it befit me, sinner that I am — not only to enter beneath the fragrant fresh-blossom feet of my Lord, the lotus-red-eyed dwarf (Vamana) who, to fill all the three worlds at once, stretched up His small yet mighty form — but also that His devotees, becoming small-and-great humans, ruled over me as they wander here?

878.translit:
ingkee thirindheeR kizhakkuRRen. irumaa nilamun nuNdumizhndha,
sengkoo laththa pavaLavaayc sendhaa maraikka Rennammaan
pongkeezh pugazhkaL vaayavaayp pulankoL vadiven manaththaay
angkeey malarkaL kaiyavaay vazhipat toada aruLilee?
878.meaning: What did I lack, wandering here? My Lord of coral red lips and red-lotus eyes, who once swallowed and brought forth again the great wide earth — His abundant rising glories upon my mouth, His captivating form within my mind, lovely blossoms in my hands — has He not granted that I follow and run in His path of worship?

879.translit:
vazhipat toada aruLpeRRu maayan koola malaradikkiizh,
suzhipat toodum sudarccoodhi veLLath thinbuRR irundhaalum,
izhipat toodum udaliniR piRandhu thansiir yaankaRRu,
mozhipat toodum kaviyamudha nugarcci yuRumoo muzhudhumee?
879.meaning: Even though, having received the grace to follow and run in His path, I were to abide in bliss within the swirling, rushing flood of radiant light beneath the wondrous Lord's adorned blossom-feet — could even that wholly equal the savour of the nectar-poems that, having learned His glory while born in this degraded perishing body, pour forth as flowing speech?

880.translit:
nugarchi yurumoo moovulagin veedu peeRu thankeezhil, pugarchchem
mugatha kaLiRatta ponnaa zhikkai yennammaan,
nigarchchem pangi yerivizhigaL neeNda asurar uyirellaam,
thagarththuN duzhalum putpaakan periya thanimaap pugazhee?
880.meaning: Can the liberation and attainment of bliss in the three worlds ever equal the great, peerless glory of my Lord, who holds the golden discus in His hand, who slew the elephant with its bright red face, and who rides the bird (Garuda) that crushes, devours and roams destroying the lives of all the demons with their red manes and blazing eyes?

881.translit:
thanimaap pugazhee egngnaanRum niRkum padiyaath thaanthoonRi,
munimaap birama mudalviththaay ulagam moonRum muLaippiththa,
thanimaath theyvath thaLiradik keezhp pugudal anRi avanadiyaar,
nanimaak kalavi yinbamee naaLum vaaykka nangatkee.
881.meaning: He whose unique great glory stands forever, who appeared of His own accord, who as the supreme primal seed of the great silent Brahman caused the three worlds to sprout. May it be granted to us each day not only to take refuge beneath the tender lotus feet of that unique great Lord, but also to enjoy the abundant great bliss of union with His devotees.

882.translit:
naaLum vaaykka nangatku naLirneerk kadalaip padaiththu, than
thaaLum thooLum mudigaLum samani laatha palaparappi,
neeLum padarpoong kaRpagak kaavum niRaipan naayiRRin,
kooLu mudaiya maNimalaipool kidanthaan thamargaL koottamee.
882.meaning: May it be granted to us each day. He who created the cool watery ocean, spreading wide His feet, His shoulders and His many incomparable crowns, lay there like a jewel-mountain resembling a vast spreading fragrant kalpaka grove and bearing the radiance of many full suns. May we belong to the assembly of His kinsmen-devotees.

883.translit:
thamargaL koottam valvi naiyai naasanj cheyyum sathirmoorththi,
amarkoL aazhi sanguvaaL vilthaN daathi palpadaiyan,
kumaran koola aingaNaiveeL thaadhai koodhil adiyaartham!
thamargaL thamargaL thamargaLaam sathiree vaaykka thamiyeeRkee!
883.meaning: The skillful Lord who destroys utterly the powerful karma of the assembly of His devotees, who bears many weapons - the battle-eager discus, the conch, the sword, the bow, the mace and more, the father of the youthful comely Manmatha of the five flower-arrows. May this good fortune be granted to lonely me - to become the devotee of the devotees of the devotees of His faultless devotees.

884.translit:
vaaykka thamiyeeR koozhitho Roozhi yoozhi maakaayaam
pookkoL meeni naanguthooL ponnaa zhikkai yennammaan,
neekka millaa adiyaartham adiyaar adiyaar adiyaareng
kookkaL, avarkkee kudigaLaaych chellum nalla kootpaadee!
884.meaning: May it be granted to lonely me through aeon upon aeon, age after age. My Lord of four shoulders, whose body has the hue of the dark kaaya blossom and who holds the golden discus in His hand - may that good ordinance be mine, whereby the devotees of the devotees of the devotees of His unfailing devotees are my kings, and I go forth as one belonging to their household.

885.translit:
nalla kootpaat tulagangaL moonRi nuLLum thaanniRaintha,
allik kamalak kaNNanai anthaN kurugoorch chadagoopan,
sollap patta aayiraththuL ivaiyum paththum vallaargaL,
nalla padaththaal manaivaazhvar koNda peNdir makkaLee.
885.meaning: Of the thousand verses sung by Satakopa of the cool beautiful Kurugur about the lotus-eyed Kannan whose eyes are like the petalled lotus and who pervades all the three worlds under good ordinance - those who can master these ten verses too shall live a household life in good estate, with the wives they wed and their children.

886.translit:
koNda peNdir makkaLuRRaar suRRaththavar piRarum
kaNdadhoodu pattadhallaal kaadhal maRRiyadhumillai
eNdisaiyung keezhum meelum muRRavamuNda piraan
thoNdaroomaay uyyalallaal illai kaNdeer thuNaiyee.
886.meaning: The wives we wed, our children, our relations, kinsmen and others - their love for us lasts only so long as they see profit; beyond that there is no other affection. There is no refuge, see, except by becoming the servants of the Lord who swallowed up the eight directions, the regions below and above - all entirely.

887.translit:
thuNaiyum saarvum aaguvaarpool suRRaththavar piRarum
aNaiya vandha aakkamuNdeel attaigaLpool suvaippar
kaNaiyonRuleeyeezh maramum eytha eng kaarmugilee
puNaiyenRu uyyap pookilallaal illai kaNdeer poruLee.
887.meaning: Kinsmen and others who seem as though they would be our help and support - if there be wealth coming to draw them near, they suck at it like leeches. There is no true wealth, see, except taking as our raft my dark cloud-hued Lord who pierced the seven trees with a single arrow, and so being saved.

888.translit:
poruLkai yuNdaaych chellak kaaNil pooRRinaR Reezhuvar
iruLkoL thunbath thimaikaaNil ennee enbaarum illai
maruL koLcheygai asurar manga vadamadhuraip piRandhaaRku
aruL koLaaLaay uyyavallaal illai kaNdeer araNee.
888.meaning: If they see wealth in hand as we go along, seven will rise up praising us; but if they see the dark moment of grief, there is none even to say 'what ails you.' There is no protection, see, except by becoming the servant who receives the grace of Him who was born in northern Mathura so that the deluded-deeded demons might perish, and so being saved.

889.translit:
araNamaa varaRRakaalaikku enRenRu amaikkappattaar
iraNangkoNda dhepparaavar inRiyittalu nahdhee
varuNiththennee vadamadhuraip piRandha vanvaN pugazhee
saraNenRu uyyap pookilallaal illai kaNdeer sathiree.
889.meaning: Those who are gathered together saying again and again 'they will be a protection in time of want' - what use are they who have taken the wound (the debt)? It is the same even if they were not there. Why describe it? There is no wise course, see, except taking as refuge the mighty bountiful glory of Him who was born in northern Mathura, and so being saved.

890.translit:
sathiramenRu nammaith thaamee sammathiththin mozhiyaar
madhurabhoogam thuRRavaree vaigimaR RonRuRuvar
adhikoLcheygai asurar manga vadamadhuraip piRandhaaRku
edhirkoLLaay uyyalallaal illai kaNdeer inbamee.
890.meaning: Those who praise us as clever and themselves speak sweet honeyed words to us, who feast on sweet pleasures - they will tarry awhile and then attain some other thing. There is no bliss, see, except by going forth to receive Him who was born in northern Mathura so that the fierce-deeded demons might perish, and so being saved.

891.translit:
illai kaNdeer inbamandhoo uLLudhu ninaiyaadhee
thollaiyaargaL eththanaivar thoonRik kazhindhozhindhaar
mallai moodhoor vadamadhuraip piRandhavan vaN pugazhee
solli uyyap pookilallaal maRRonRillai surukkee.
891.meaning: There is no bliss, see - alas, without inwardly contemplating this! How many men of olden times appeared and then passed away and were gone! In brief, there is nothing else except telling forth the bountiful glory of Him who was born in the prosperous ancient city of northern Mathura, and so being saved.

892.translit:
maRRonRillai surungach chonnoom maanilath thevvuyirukkum
siRRaveeNdaa sindhippee yamaiyum kaNdeer kaLandhoo
kuRRamanR engaL peRRaththaayan vadamadhuraip piRandhaan
kuRRimil seerkaRRu vaidhal vaazhdhal kaNdeer guNamee.
892.meaning: In brief we have said it - for every living being on this great earth, there is nothing else. No effort is needed; merely to meditate suffices, see, alas! It is no fault. Learning and uttering the faultless glory of our cowherd Lord of the cattle, who was born in northern Mathura - that, see, is the way of life and of true virtue.

893.translit:
vaazhdhal kaNdeer guNamidhandhoo maayavaN dhibarali
poozhdhupooga uLLuniRkum punmaiyilaadhavarkku
vaazhthuNaiyaa vadamadhuraip piRandhavan vaN pugazhee
veezhthuNaiyaap poomidhanil yaadhumillai mikkadhee.
893.meaning: This is the way of life, see, this is virtue, alas! For those who are without baseness and who keep Him within their hearts as time passes, the bountiful glory of Him who was born in northern Mathura is a living support. Take it as your sustaining support and go forth - there is nothing greater than this.

894.translit:
yaadhumillai mikkadhanil enRenRadhu karudhi
kaadhuseyvaan koodhaiseydhu kadaimuRai vaazhkkai yumpoom
maadhugilin kodik koLmaada vadamadhuraip piRandha
thaadhuseerthooL kaNnanallaal illai kaNdeer saraNee.
894.meaning: Pondering again and again that 'there is nothing greater than this,' even the worthless lowly life passes away as one heaps up trifles. There is no refuge, see, except Kannan of the pollen-laden shoulders, who was born in northern Mathura of mansions bearing banners of fine silk.

895.translit:
kaNnanallaal illai kaNdeer saraN adhuniRkavandhu
maNNin paaram neekkudhaRkee vadamadhuraip piRandhaan
thiNNamaa nummudaimaiyuNdeel avanadiseerththu uymino
eNNaveeNdaa nummadhaadhum avananRi maRRillaiyee.
895.meaning: There is no refuge, see, except Kannan; and beyond that He came and was born in northern Mathura solely to remove the burden of the earth. If you truly possess anything as your own, join His feet and be saved. You need not reckon - nothing whatever is yours; there is none other than He.

896.translit:
aadhumillai maRRavanil enRadhuvee thuNindhu
thaadhuseerthooL kaNnanaik kurugoorch chadagoopan sonna
theedhilaadha voNdamizhgaL ivaiyaayiraththuLippaththum
oodhavalla biraakkaL nammaiyaaLudaiyaargaL paNdee.
896.meaning: Resolving firmly that 'there is nothing apart from Him,' Satakopa of Kurugur sang of Kannan of the pollen-laden shoulders. Of these faultless beautiful Tamil thousand verses, those great ones who can chant these ten have long since been our masters who own and rule us.

897.translit:
paNdainaaLaalee nin thiruvaruLum pangayaththaaL thiruvaruLum
koNdu nin kooyilseeyththup palladigaal kudikudi vazhivandha naatcheyyum
thoNdaroorkkaruLich choodhivaaythiRandhu un thaamaraik kaNgaLaal nookkaay
thoNdiraipporunal thaNpaNai soozhndha thiruppuLinguditkidaa thaanee.
897.meaning: From the days of old, taking up both Your divine grace and the divine grace of her who dwells on the lotus (Lakshmi), look upon the servants who through generation after generation, family after family, have served by sweeping Your temple and performing daily service - open Your radiant mouth and gaze upon us with Your lotus eyes, O You who recline at Tiruppulingudi girt about with cool fields and the river Porunal whose waves break upon the shore.

898.translit:
kudikkidandhaakkanj cheydhu nin theerththapadimaikkuRReeval seydhu un pon
nadikkudavaadhee vazhivaruginRa adiyaroorkkaruLi neeyorunaaL
padikkaLavaaga nimirththa nin paadhapangayamee thalaikkaNiyaay
kodikkoLponmadhiL soozhkuLirvayaRchoolaith thiruppuLinguditkidandhaanee.
898.meaning: Showing grace to the servants who, belonging to Your household and doing service in worship, come down the generations never failing in service to Your golden feet - one day make the lotus of Your foot, which You once stretched out to measure the whole earth, an ornament for our heads, O You who recline at Tiruppulingudi of cool fields and groves girt by golden ramparts that bear banners.

899.translit:
kidandhanaaL kidandhaayeththanai kaalang kidaththi un thiruvu dambasaiya
thodarndhu kuRReevel seydhu tholladimai vazhi varundhoNda roorkaruLi
thadangkoL thaamaraik kaNvizhiththu neeyeezhundhun thaamarai mangaiyum neeyum
idangkoL moovulagun thozhavirun dharuLaay thiruppuLinguditkidandhaanee.
899.meaning: How long have You lain reclining through all these days! Showing grace to the servants who follow on and serve, coming down the path of ancient servitude, until Your sacred body grows weary - open Your wide lotus eyes, rise up, O You, and together with You and Your lotus lady (Lakshmi) be seated in grace, that the three encompassing worlds may worship, O You who recline at Tiruppulingudi.

900.translit:
puLingudik kidandhu vaaguNamangai irundhu vaikundhaththuL ninRu
theLindha en sindhai agam kazhiyaadhE ennai aaLvaay enakku aruLi
naLirndha seer ulagam moonRudan viyappa naangaL kooththaadi ninRu aarppa
puLingu neer mugilin pavaLam pOR kanivaay sivappa rik kaaNa vaaraayE.
900.meaning: O Lord who reclines at Tiruppulingudi, who is enthroned at Vaguna­mangai, and who stands in Vaikuntha! Having ruled me so that you never depart from my clear and discerning heart, and having graced me, come so that I may behold you — while the three cool and glorious worlds marvel together, while we dance and cry out in joy, and while your fruit-like lips, red as coral set against the rain-cloud-colored waters, glow.

901.translit:
pavaLam pOl kanivaay sivappa nee kaaNa vandhu nin pal nilaa muththam
thavazh kadhir muRuval seydhu nin thirukkaN thaamarai thayanga inRu aruLaay
pavaLa nan padark keezhch changu uRai porunal thaN thiruppuLingudik kidandhaay
kavaLa maa kaLiRRin idar keda thadaththuk kaay sinap paRai oorndhaanE.
901.meaning: Come so that I may behold your coral-red fruit-like lips; making your row of teeth shine forth like the spreading moonlight of pearls, and letting your lotus eyes glow, grace me this day. O Lord who reclines at cool Tiruppulingudi by the Porunal river, where conches dwell beneath fine coral spreads — you who rode the fierce-tempered bird (Garuda) to the pond to destroy the distress of the great rutting elephant (saving Gajendra)!

902.translit:
kaay sinap paRavai oorndhu pon malaiyin meemisaik kaarar mugil pOl
maa sinam maali maali maan enRu angu avar pada kananRu mun ninRa
kaay sina vEndhE kadhir mudiyaanE kali vayal thiruppuLingudiyaay
kaay sina vaazhi sangu vaaL vil thaNdu Endhi em idar kadivaanE.
902.meaning: Riding the fierce-tempered bird, you stood forth blazing in wrath so that the dark-cloud-like, mightily wrathful demons Mali, Sumali and Malyavan were slain there. O King of fierce anger! O Lord of the radiant crown! O Lord of Tiruppulingudi amid roaring fields! You who bear the fierce discus, the conch, the sword, the bow and the mace, and who drive away our distress!

903.translit:
em idar kadindhu ingu ennai aaLvaanE imaiyavar thamakkum aangaLaiyaay
sem madal maru thaamaraip pazhanath thaN thiruppuLingudik kidandhaay
nammudaiya adiyar kavvai kaNdu ugandhu naangaL ittu uLam ugoora
i madavulagar kaaNa nee oru naaL irundhidaay engaL kaN mugappE.
903.meaning: O Lord who, having driven away our distress, rules me here, and who is the support of the celestials too! O Lord who reclines at cool Tiruppulingudi with its fields of red-petalled fragrant lotuses! Rejoicing to see the longing of us your servants, so that our hearts overflow, do you abide one day before our very eyes, for these simple folk of the world to behold.

904.translit:
engaL kaN mugappE ulagargaL ellaam iNai adi thozhudhu Ezhndhu iRainjchi
thangaL anbu aara thamadhu sol valaththaal thalaiththalaich chiRandhu poosippa
thingaL sEr maadath thiruppuLingudiyaay thiruvaikundhaththuLLaay dhEvaa
ingaN maa njaalaththu idhanuLum oru naaL irundhidaay veeRRu idangoNdE.
904.meaning: So that all the people of the world before our eyes worship your paired feet, rise, bow down, and — their love brimming over — adore you with the eloquence of their speech, each surpassing the other. O Lord of Tiruppulingudi with its mansions that reach the moon! O Lord who dwells in sacred Vaikuntha! O God! Do you abide one day even here in this great wide world, taking your exalted seat.

905.translit:
veeRRu idangoNdu viyan koL maa njaalaththu idhanuLum irundhidaay adiyOm
pORRi OvaadhE kaN iNai kuLira poodhu malar aagaththaip paruga
sERRu iLa vaaLai sennel oodu ugaLum sezhum paNaith thiruppuLingudiyaay
kooRRam aaya asurar kula mudhal arindha kodu vinaip padaigaL vallanE.
905.meaning: Taking your exalted seat, do you abide even here in this vast wide world, so that we your servants may praise you unceasingly, so that our paired eyes are cooled, and so that we may drink in your flower-fresh blossoming form. O Lord of fertile Tiruppulingudi with its rich fields, where young scabbard-fish leap amid the red paddy in the mire! O wielder of the dreadful weapons that, becoming death itself, cut off the very root of the demon clan!

906.translit:
kodu vinaip padaigaL vallaiyaay amararkku idar keda asurarkatku idar sey
kadu vinai nanjE en udai amudhE kali vayal thiruppuLingudiyaay
vadivu iNai illaa malar magaL maRRai nila magaL pidikkum mel adiyai
kodu vinaiyEnum pidikka inRu oru naaL koovudhal varudhal seyyaayE.
906.meaning: O wielder of the dreadful weapons, who are like a fierce deadly poison to do harm to the demons so that the distress of the celestials is ended, yet who are my own nectar! O Lord of Tiruppulingudi amid roaring fields! Your tender feet, which the peerless flower-born Lakshmi and the Earth-goddess press — that even I of cruel deeds may press them this very day, either call me to you or come to me.

907.translit:
koovudhal varudhal seydhidaay enRu kurai kadal kadaindhavan thannai
mEli nan kamarndha viyan punaR porunal vazhudhi naadan sadagOpan
naa iyal paadal aayiraththuLLum ivaiyum Or paththum vallaargaL
Ovudhal inRi ulagam moonRu aLandhaan adi iNai uLLaththu OrvaarE.
907.meaning: Saying "either call me or come to me," Sadagopan of the Vazhudhi land, by the vast watered Porunal river richly endowed above, sang of Him who churned the roaring ocean. Those who can master these ten verses too, among the thousand tongue-fitting songs, will, without ceasing, meditate in their hearts upon the paired feet of Him who measured the three worlds.

908.translit:
Or aayiramaay ulagu Ezh aLikkum
pEr aayiram koNdadhu Or peedu udaiyan
kaaraayina kaaLa nal mEniyinan
naaraayaNan nangaL piraan avanE.
908.meaning: He is endowed with the greatness of bearing a thousand names, the one supreme name that, becoming a thousand, protects the seven worlds. He of the fair dark body, dark as the rain-cloud — He is Narayana, our Lord.

909.translit:
avanE agal njaalam padaiththu idandhaan
avanE ahdhu uNdu umizhndhaan aLandhaan
avanE avanum avanum avanum
avanE maRRu ellaamum aRindhanamE.
909.meaning: It is He alone who created the wide earth and lifted it up; it is He alone who swallowed it, spat it out, and measured it. He alone is this one and that one and the other; He alone is everything else besides — this we have come to know.

910.translit:
aRindhana vEdha varum poruL noolgaL
aRindhana koLga arum poruL aadhal
aRindhanar ellaam ariyai vaNangi
aRindhanar nOygaL aRukkum marundhE.
910.meaning: The Vedas and the scriptures of profound meaning have known Him; take it that He is their rare essence which they have known. All who have known have worshipped Hari and known Him to be the medicine that cuts away their afflictions.

911.translit:
marundhE nangaL bOga magizhchchikku enRu
perun dhEvar kuzhaangaL pidhaRRum piraan
karun dhEvan emmaan kaNNan viNNulagam
tharun dhEvanaich chOrEl kaNdaay manamE.
911.meaning: He is the Lord whom the throngs of great gods babble of, saying "He is the medicine for our enjoyment and joy." O my heart, do not let go of that dark Lord, our Master Kannan, the God who grants the heavenly world!

912.translit:
manamE annai val vinaiyEn iRandhu
ganamE sollinEn idhu sOrEl kaNdaay
punam mEliya poon thaN thuzhaay alangal
inam Edhum ilaanai adaivadhumE.
912.meaning: O my heart, my mother! I of grievous deeds have spoken this with grave weight — do not let it slip, behold: that we must reach Him who has no equal whatsoever, who wears the cool fragrant tulasi garland that flourishes on the fields.

913.translit:
adaivadhum aNi aar malar mangai thOL
midaivadhum asurarkku vem pOrgaLE
kadaivadhum kadaluL amudham en mana
mudaivadhum avaRkkE orungaagavE.
913.meaning: What we are to reach is the shoulders of the beauteous flower-maiden (Lakshmi); what He presses upon the demons is fierce battles; what He churned was the nectar within the ocean; and my heart's resolve too is for Him alone, wholly and together.

914.translit:
aagam sEr narasingam adhu aagi Or
aagam vaL ugiraal piLandhaan uRai
maaga vaikundham kaaNbadhaRku en manam
Egam eNNum iraap pagal inRiyE.
914.meaning: Becoming the Man-Lion that joined the bodies (of god and beast), He who tore open a body with His sharp claws — to behold the lofty Vaikuntha where He dwells, my heart longs single-mindedly, without distinction of night or day.

915.translit:
inRip pOga iru vinaiyum keduththu
onRi yaakkai pugaamai uyyak koLvaan
ninRa vEngadam neeL nilaththuLLadhu
senRu dhEvargaL kai thozhuvaargaLE.
915.meaning: To destroy both kinds of karma so that they perish away, and to save us so that we enter no body again by uniting (with Him), the Lord stands at Venkatam, which is here on the vast earth. Going there, the very gods worship Him with folded hands.

916.translit:
thozhudhu maa malar neer sudar dhoopam koNdu
ezhudhum en naam idhu migai aadhalil
pazhudhu il thol pugazhp paambu aNaip paLLiyaay
thazhuvum aaRu aRiyEn unadhu aaLgaLE.
916.meaning: "Let us worship, taking choice flowers, water, lamps, and incense" — let us call this our (path), since even this is excess. O Lord who reclines upon the serpent-bed, of faultless ancient glory! I know not the way to embrace your sacred feet.

917.translit:
thaaLaa thaamaraiyaan unadhu un madhiyaan
vaal koL neeL mazhu vaaLi un aagaththaan
aaLaraayth thozhuvaagum amarargaL
naaLum mEl pugazhgO una seelamE.
917.meaning: The lotus-seated one (Brahma) is at your feet; the moon-crested one is in your thought; the wielder of the long bright axe (Shiva) is upon your body. The celestials, becoming your servants, worship you — day after day shall I extol your noble character above all?

918.translit:
seelam ellai ilaan adi mEl aNi
kOla neeL kurugoorch chadagOpan sol
maalai aayiraththuL ivai paththin in
paalar vaikundham ERudhal paanmaiyE.
918.meaning: Of the garland of a thousand songs by Sadagopan of fair, tall Kurukur, set upon the feet of Him whose noble character has no bound — those who are sweetly devoted to these ten verses, for them it is the natural way to ascend to Vaikuntha.

919.translit:
maiyaar karung kaNNi kamala malar mEl
seyyaaL thiru maarbinil sEr thirumaalE
seyyaar sudar aazhi suri sangam Endhum
kaiyaa unnaik kaaNak karudhum en kaNNE.
919.meaning: O Tirumal, on whose sacred chest dwells the lovely lady (Lakshmi) of the lotus blossom, the dark-eyed one with collyrium-darkened eyes! O Lord whose hands bear the radiant red discus and the coiled conch! My eyes yearn to behold you.

920.translit:
kaNNeey unnaik kaaNak karudhi en nenjam
eNNee koNda sindhaiyanaay ninRiyambum
viNNoor munivarkku enRum kaaNbariyaayai
naNNaadhozhiyeen enRu naan azhaippanee.
920.meaning: O my Lord, my treasured one! Longing to behold You, my heart, with mind fixed only on this thought, stands and cries out. You who are ever hard to behold even for the celestials and the sages — I will not cease drawing near to You; thus I call out to You.

921.translit:
azhaikkinRa adi naayeen naay koozhai vaalaal
kuzhaikkinRadhu poola en uLLam kuzhaiyum
mazhaikku anRu kunRam eduththu aa nirai kaaththaay
pizhaikkinRadhu aruL enRu peedhuRuvanee.
921.meaning: As I call out, I, the lowly dog at Your feet — my heart melts the way a dog softly wags its drooping tail. O You who once lifted the mountain against the rain and protected the herds of cattle! Crying ‘grant Your grace that I may be saved,’ I grow bewildered with yearning.

922.translit:
uRuvadhu idhu enRu unakku aatpattu nin kaN
peRuvadhu edhu kol enRu pedhaiyeen nenjam
maru nal seyyum vaanavar daanavarkku enRum
aRivadhu ariya ariyaaya ammaanee.
922.meaning: Resolving ‘this is what is fitting,’ I have become Your servant; and my foolish heart wonders what it may obtain at Your side. O my Lord Hari, ever hard to be known even by the heaven-dwelling gods who do good deeds and by the demons!

923.translit:
ariyaaya ammaanai amarar piraanai
periyanaip piramanai mun padaiththaanai
vari vaaL aravin aNaip paLLi koLkinRa
kariyaana kazhal kaaNak karudhum karuththee.
923.meaning: My heart resolves to behold the feet of the dark-hued Lord — Hari my Master, chief of the immortals, the Great One who in the beginning created Brahma, and who reclines in sleep upon the couch of the striped, bright serpent.

924.translit:
karuththee unnaik kaaNak karudhi en nenjath
thiruththaaka iruththineen devargatku ellaam
viruththaa viLangum sudar sodhi uyaraththu
oruththaa unnai uLLum en uLLam ugandhee.
924.meaning: O my heart's intent! Longing to behold You, I have set You firmly within my heart, fixing You there. O ancient Source of all the gods, radiant gleaming light, You who are uniquely supreme on high — my heart, rejoicing, ever contemplates You.

925.translit:
ugandhee unnai uLLum en uLLaththu
agam paal akam thaan amarndhee idam koNda amalaa
migundhaanavan maarvu agalam iru kooRaa
nagandhaay narasingam adhaaya uruvee.
925.meaning: O Spotless One who, rejoicing, abide within my heart, taking Your place deep within its inner core! In the form of the man-lion You clawed apart the broad chest of that overweening one (Hiranya) into two halves.

926.translit:
uruvaagiya aaRu samayangatku ellaam
poruvaagi ninRaanavan ellaap porutkum
aruvaagiya aadhiyaith devargatku ellaam
karuvaagiya kaNNanaik kaNdu koNdeenee.
926.meaning: He who stands as the very meaning and goal of all the six established systems of doctrine; the formless First Cause of all things; the inner source and origin of all the gods — that very Kannan I have beheld and attained.

927.translit:
kaNdu koNdu en kaNNiNai aarak kaLiththu
paNdai vinaiyaayina paRRodu aRuththu
thoNdarkku amudhu uNNa sol maalaigaL sonneen
aNdaththu amarar perumaan adiyeenee.
927.meaning: Having beheld Him and feasted my pair of eyes to their fill with delight, having cut away my ancient deeds together with their root, I have uttered these garlands of words for His devotees to drink as nectar — I, the servant of the Lord of the immortals in the celestial sphere.

928.translit:
adiyaan ivan enRu enakku aar aruL seyyum
nediyaanai nirai pugazh am siRaip puLLin
kodiyaanai kunRaamal ulagam aLandha
adiyaanai adaindhu adiyeen aayndhavaaRee.
928.meaning: Saying ‘this one is my servant,’ He bestows full grace on me — the supreme Lord who bears the banner of the beautiful-winged bird amid endless praise, whose foot measured out the world without fail. Having reached that One of holy feet, I, His servant, have found my discernment.

929.translit:
aaRaa madha yaanai adarththavan thannai
seeRaar vayal then kurugoorch chadagoopan
nooree sonna oor aayiraththuL ip paththum
eeRee tharum vaanavar tham in uyirkkee.
929.meaning: Of the thousand verses sung by Satakopan of southern Kurugur, rich in mire-filled fields — he who composed a hundred about the Lord who subdued the rutting elephant whose ichor never ceases — these ten will grant the highest goal to the sweet life of the celestials.

930.translit:
in uyirch chevalum neerum koovik koNdu ingu eththanai
en uyir noova mizhaRReenmin kuyilp peedaigaaL
en uyirk kaNNapiraanai neer vara koovagileer
en uyir koolik koduppaarkkum iththanai veNdumoo.
930.meaning: You and your beloved cocks keep calling out here — how long? O hen-koels, do not warble so as to torment my very life! You will not call so that my life, my Lord Kanna, comes to me. Need it cost so much even for those who would pay back my life with hire?

931.translit:
iththanai veNduvadhu anRu andoo anRiR peedaigaaL
eththanai neerum num sevalum karaindhu eengudhir
viththaga kaNGovindhan meyyan allan oruvarkkum
aththanaiyaam ini en uyir avan kaiyanee.
931.meaning: It is not that so much should be required — alas, O hen-anril birds! How much do you and your cocks waste away crying and pining! That wondrous Govinda is not true to anyone. So be it now — my life is already in His hand.

932.translit:
avan kaiyadhee enadhu aar uyir anRiR peedaigaaL
evan solli neer kudaindhu aaduthir pudai suzhavee
thavam seydhillaa vinaiyaattiyeen uyir ingu uNdoo
evan solli niRRum num eengu kookkural keettumee.
932.meaning: My precious life is already in His hand, O hen-anril birds! For what do you bathe and play, circling all about? Does the life of sinful me, who has done no penance, still remain here? What can I say, hearing your pining cries here?

933.translit:
kookkural keettum nam kaNNan maayan veLippadaan
meR kiLai koLLeenmin neerum sevalum koazhigaaL
vaakkum manamum karumamum namakku aanganee
aakkaiyum aaviyum andharam ninRu uzhalumee.
933.meaning: Even hearing the cries He does not appear — our Kannan, the wondrous one. Do not raise an uproar against me, you and your cocks, O fowls! For us speech and mind and deed are all so directed to Him — and my body and life hang wandering in the void.

934.translit:
andharam ninRu uzhalkinRa yaan udaip poovaigaaL
nun thiRaththu edhum idai illai kuzhaReenminoo
indhira jaalangaL kaatti iv ezh ulagum koNda
nam thiru maarban nam aaviyuNNa nankeNNinaan.
934.meaning: O my mynah birds, while I hang wandering in the void! On your account there is no remedy at all — do not chatter so. Our Lord of the sacred chest, who showed conjuring magic and seized these seven worlds, has resolved well to devour my very life.

935.translit:
nankeNNi naan vaLarththa siRu kiLip paidhalee
in kural nee mizhaRReel en aar uyirkku aaguththan
nin seyya vaay okkum vaayan kaNNan kai kaavinan
nin pasunj saama niRRadhan koottuNdu neenginaan.
935.meaning: O little parrot child that I reared with fond care! Do not warble in your sweet voice — my precious life's refuge. His mouth is like your red mouth, that Kannan, the staff-bearer; partaking of the company of your fresh dark-hued sheen, He has departed.

936.translit:
koottuNdu neengiya koolath thaamaraik kat sevvaay
vaattam il en karu maaNikkam kaNNan maayan pol
koottiya villodu minnu meegak kuzhaangaLgaaL
kaatteenmin num uru en uyirkku adhu kaalanee.
936.meaning: Having shared my company He has departed — my flawless dark sapphire, beautiful lotus-eyed and red-mouthed, the wondrous Kannan. O cloud-masses gleaming with lightning, like a bent bow! Do not show me your form — for that is death to my life.

937.translit:
uyirkku adhu kaalan enRu ummai yaan irandheeRku nee
kuyil paidhalgaaL kaNNan naamamee kuzhaRik konReer
thayirp pazhanj soRRodu paaladisilum thandhu sol
payiRRiya nal vaLam oottineer paNbu udaiyeerree.
937.meaning: To me who begged you, saying that is death to my life, you, O young koels, have killed me by warbling the very name of Kannan! With rich curd-rice and milk-pudding I fed you and trained you to speak — such fine bounty I lavished, and you are well-bred indeed!

938.translit:
paNbu udai vaNdodu thumbigaaL paN mizhaRReelmin
puN purai veel kodu kuththaa okkum num in kural
thaN peru neerth thadan thaamarai malarndhal okkum
kaN peru kaNNan nam aaviyuNdu ezhu naNNinaan.
938.meaning: O well-mannered bees and dragonflies, do not warble your tunes! Your sweet voice is like a spear thrust into a wound. Large-eyed Kannan, whose eyes are like a great cool-watered tank's lotus in bloom, has come and risen up having devoured my very life.

939.translit:
ezha naNNi naamum nam vaana naadanodu onRineem
pazhana nan naaraik kuzhaangaLgaaL payinRu en ini
izhai nalla aakkaiyum paiyavee puyakkaRRadhu
thazhai nalla in bam thalaippeydhu engun thazhaikkalee.
939.meaning: Drawing near and rising up, we too have become one with our Lord of the celestial realm. O flocks of fine storks in the fertile fields, what use now to dwell on it? My finely-adorned good body has gently loosened its hold; let flourishing good bliss come upon us and flourish everywhere.

940.translit:
inpan thalaippeythu engum thazhaitha palluzhikku
than pukazh eththath thanakku aruL seytha maayanai
then kurukoorch chatakOpan sol aayiraththuL ivai
onpathOtu onRukkum, moovulakum urukumE.
940.meaning: This decad, one with nine, out of the thousand verses of Satakopan of beautiful southern Kurukur, sings of the wondrous Lord (Mayan) who graced him to praise His glory through endless ages of flourishing, ever-overflowing bliss. At these ten verses, all three worlds melt with love.

941.translit:
urukumaal nenjam uyir in param anRi
perukumaal vEtkaiyum en seykEn thoNtanEn
thiruvellaam kaal igazh thirukkaatkarai
maruviya maayan than maayam ninaithoRE.
941.meaning: My heart melts beyond what my soul can bear, and my longing only swells the more. What shall I do, I who am His servant, whenever I contemplate the wondrous deeds of the Mayan who abides in Tirukkatkarai, where all wealth abounds?

942.translit:
ninaithoRum sollun thoRum nenju itinthu ugum
vinai koL seer paatilum vEm enathu aaruyir
kanai koL poonjOlaith then kaatkarai ennappaa
ninaikilEn naan unakku aatcheyyum neermaiyE.
942.meaning: Each time I contemplate Him and each time I speak of Him, my heart breaks and dissolves; even when I sing His glorious traits that destroy karma, my precious soul burns. O my Father of southern Katkarai with its dense, sounding flower-groves, I cannot even conceive of the way to render You service.

943.translit:
neermaiyaal nenjam vanjiththup pukunthu ennai
eermai seythu en naayiraay en uyir uNtaan
seer malku chOlaith then kaatkarai ennappan
kaar mukil vaNNan than kaLvam aRikilEn.
943.meaning: With gentle guile He entered and beguiled my heart, melted me, became my master, and consumed my soul. I cannot fathom the stealthy ways of the dark-cloud-hued Lord, my Father of southern Katkarai with its glory-abounding groves.

944.translit:
aRikilEn than uL anaiththu ulakum niRka
neRimaiyaal thaanum avaRRuL niRkum piraan
veRi kamazh chOlaith then kaatkarai ennappan
chiriya en naayiruNta thiruvaruLE.
944.meaning: I cannot comprehend it: the Lord within whom all the worlds abide, who also in due order abides within them. The gracious favor by which my Father of southern Katkarai, of the fragrance-spreading groves, consumed my lowly soul, this I cannot grasp.

945.translit:
thiruvaruL seypavan pOla en uL pukunthu
uruvamum aaruyirum utanE uNtaan
thiru vaLar chOlaith then kaatkarai ennappan
karu vaLar mEni en kaNNan kaLvangaLE.
945.meaning: As though to bestow His grace, He entered within me and at once consumed both my form and my precious life. Such are the stealthy deeds of my Kannan, the Father of southern Katkarai with its prosperity-rich groves, whose body glows dark with deepening hue.

946.translit:
en kaNNan kaLvam enakkuch chemmaay niRkum
an kaNNan uNta en aaruyir kOthithu
pun kaNmaiyethip pulampi iraapakal
en kaNNan enRu avan kaatkarai EththumE.
946.meaning: The stealth of my Kannan stands plain and true before me. The precious soul that the lovely-eyed Lord consumed laments and wastes in wretchedness; yet, night and day, crying 'my Kannan,' it praises His Katkarai.

947.translit:
kaatkarai Eththum athanuL kaNNaa ennum
vEtkai nOy koora ninainthu karainthu ugum
aatkoLvaan oththu en uyir uNta maayanaal
kOtkuRai pattathu en aaruyir kOL uNtE.
947.meaning: It praises Katkarai and within it cries 'O Kannan!'; as the malady of longing grows intense it contemplates Him, dissolves and melts away. By the Mayan who, as though to take me into His service, consumed my soul, my precious life has been seized and brought to ruin.

948.translit:
kOL uNtaan anRi vanthu en uyir thaan uNtaan
naaLum naaL vanthu ennai muRRavum thaan uNtaan
kaaLa neer mEkath then kaatkarai ennappaRku
aaL anRE pattathu en aaruyir pattathE.
948.meaning: He did not merely seize it; He came and consumed my very soul, and day after day He came and devoured me wholly. Is not my precious life that has been so taken the rightful servant's lot for my Father of southern Katkarai, dark as a water-laden cloud?

949.translit:
aaruyir pattathu enathu uyir pattathu
pEr ithazhth thaamaraik kaN kani vaayathOr
kaar ezhil mEkath then kaatkarai kOyil koL
seer ezhil naal thatan thOL theyva vaarikkE.
949.meaning: My precious soul has surrendered, my very life has surrendered, to the divine engulfer who dwells in the temple of southern Katkarai, beautiful as a dark cloud, lotus-eyed with broad petals, with fruit-red lips and four splendid, broad, glorious shoulders.

950.translit:
vaarik koNtu unnai vizhunguvan kaaNil enRu
aarvaRRa ennai ozhiya ennil munnam
paariththu thaan ennai muRRap parukinaan
kaar okkum kaatkarai appan katiyanE.
950.meaning: 'If I see you, I shall scoop you up and swallow you,' I longed; but even before I, full of yearning, could act, He Himself prepared and drank me up wholly. The Father of Katkarai, dark like a cloud, is swift and overpowering.

951.translit:
katiyanaay kanjanaik konRa piraan thannai
koti mathiL then kurukoorch chatakOpan sol
vativu amai aayiraththu ip paththinaal sanmam
muti eythi naasam kaNteerkaL en kaanalE.
951.meaning: By this decad, of the thousand well-formed verses of Satakopan of southern Kurukur with its bannered ramparts, on the Lord who fiercely slew Kamsa, behold, O kindred, your births shall reach their end and be destroyed.

952.translit:
en kaanal aham kazhivaay irai thErnthu ingu inithu amarum
sen kaala mata naaraay thirumuzhikkaLaththu uRaiyum
konku aar poon thuzhaay muti em kutak kooththarkku en thoothaay
mun kaalkaL en thalai mEl kezhuminO numarOtE.
952.meaning: O gentle red-legged crane that seeks its prey in the backwaters of this seaside grove and rests here sweetly, go as my messenger to the dancer of the pot, my Lord crowned with honey-laden tulasi who abides at Tirumuzhikkalam. Place your forefeet upon my head and dwell here with your kin.

953.translit:
numarOtum piriyaathE neerum num chEvalumaay
amar kaathal kuruginangaaL aNi muzhikkaLaththu uRaiyum
emar aarum pazhippu uNtu ingu en? thammaal izhippu uNtu
thamarOtu angu uRaivaarkkuth thakkilamE kELeerE.
953.meaning: O flocks of herons that, never parting from your kin, abide here in loving union with your mates, why endure blame here from any of our people? Listen: are we not fit, having been scorned by Him, to go and dwell there with His own people who reside at beautiful Muzhikkalam?

954.translit:
thakkilamE kELeerkaL thatam punal vaay irai thErum
kokkinangaaL kuruginangaaL kuLir moozhikkaLaththu uRaiyum
chek kamalarth thavar pOlum kaN kai kaal sen kani vaay
ak kamalath ilai pOlum thirumEni atikaLukkE.
954.meaning: O flocks of cranes and herons that seek your prey in the wide waters, listen: are we not fit to serve the Lord who dwells in cool Muzhikkalam, whose eyes, hands and feet are like red lotuses, whose lips are like ripe red fruit, and whose sacred body is like the leaf of that lotus?

955.translit:
thirumEni atikaLukkuth theevinaiyEn vitu thoothaay
thirumoozhikkaLam ennum chemu nakar vaay aNi mukilkaaL
thirumEni avatku aruLeer enRakkaal ummaith tham
thirumEni oLi akaRRith theLi visumpu katiyumE.
955.meaning: O lovely clouds over the fair city called Tirumuzhikkalam, be the messengers I, of cruel deeds, send to my Lord of the sacred form. When you ask Him, 'Grace her your divine form,' He will impart to you the radiance of His own body and speed you across the clear bright sky.

956.translit:
theLi visumpu kati Otith thee vaLaiththu min ilakum
oLi mukilkaaL thirumuzhikkaLaththu uLaiyum oN sutarkku
theLi visumpu thirunaataath theevinaiyEn manaththu uRaiyum
thuLi vaar kar kuzhalaarkku en thoothu uraiththal cheppuminE.
956.meaning: O shining clouds that race swiftly across the clear sky, ringed with fire and flashing lightning, declare my message to the radiant brilliant One who dwells at Tirumuzhikkalam, who holds the clear heavens as His sacred realm yet abides in the heart of me of cruel deeds, the dark-tressed Lord from whom raindrops pour.

957.translit:
thoothu uraiththal cheppuminkaL thoo mozhi vaay vaNtinangaaL
pOthu iraiththu mathu nukarum pozhil muzhikkaLaththu uRaiyum
maatharaith tham maarvakaththE vaiththaarkku en vaay maaRRam
thoothu uraiththal chepputhirEl chutar vaLaiyum kalaiyumE.
957.meaning: O flocks of bees with pure-toned voices, who hum and sip nectar from the blossoms, declare my message to Him who dwells in the grove of Muzhikkalam, who holds the Lady upon His chest. If you carry my words as messengers, my gleaming bangles and my garments shall be restored.

958.translit:
chutar vaLaiyum kalaiyum koNta aru vinaiyEn thOL thuRanthu
patar pukazhaan thirumoozhikkaLaththu uRaiyum pankayak kaN
chutar pavaLa vaayanaik kaNtu oru naaL Or thooy maaRRam
patar pozhil vaay kuruginangaaL enakku onRu paNiyeerE.
958.meaning: O flocks of herons in the spreading groves, He of far-flung fame, who took away my gleaming bangles and garments and forsook my shoulders, leaving me with grievous karma, dwells at Tirumuzhikkalam. Behold the lotus-eyed Lord with radiant coral lips, and bring me back one day even a single pure word from Him.

959.translit:
enakku onRu paNiyeerkaL irum pozhi vaay irai thErnthu
manakku inpam pata mEvum vaNtinangaaL thumpikaaL
kanak koL thiN mathiL pataisoozh thirumoozhikkaLaththu uRaiyum
punak koL kaayaa mEnip poon thuzhaay mutiyaarkkE.
959.meaning: O flocks of bees and dragonflies that seek your prey in the great groves and gather there in heart's delight, bring me back a word from the Lord who dwells at Tirumuzhikkalam, girt by strong dense ramparts and weaponry, whose hue is like the wild kaya blossom and who is crowned with beautiful tulasi.

960.translit:
poonthuzhaay mudiyaarkkup ponnaazhik kaiyaarukku
enthu neer iLam kurukee thirumuzhikkaLaththaarukku
enthu pooN mulai payanthu en iNai malark kaNNeer thathumpa
thaan thammaik koNdu agalthal thagavu anREnRu uraiyeerE
960.meaning: O young heron standing in the brimming waters! Tell the Lord of Tirumuzhikkalam, who wears a crown of fresh tulasi and holds the golden discus in His hand, that it is not fitting (tagavu) for Him to take my heart and depart. My jewel-adorned breasts have grown pale and my flower-like eyes brim over with tears.

961.translit:
thagavu anREnRu uraiyeergaL thadam punal vaay irai theernthu
migal inbam pada meevum mennadaiya annangaaL
miga meeni melivu eythi meegalaiyum izhinthu en
agameeni ozhiyaamee thirumuzhikkaLaththaarkkee
961.meaning: O soft-stepping swans who seek your prey in the broad waters and dwell joining together in great bliss! Tell the Lord of Tirumuzhikkalam that it is not fitting, so that my body, grown greatly thin with my garments slipping, and my inner self may not be destroyed for His sake.

962.translit:
ozhivu inRith thirumoozhikkaLaththu uRaiyum oN sudarai
ozhivu illaa aNi mazhalaik kiLi mozhiyaal aivuRRiya sol
vazhu illaa vaN kurukoor satakOpan vaaynthu uraiththa
azhivu illaa aayiraththu ip paththum kOyilukkumee
962.meaning: Of the imperishable thousand verses graciously uttered by Satakopan of bountiful Kurukur, faultless and full of sweet parrot-like tender speech, addressed to the bright radiance who dwells without ceasing in Tirumuzhikkalam, these ten too are for the temple.

963.translit:
aRukkum vinai aayina aagam thavanai
niRuththum manaththu onRiya sinthaiyinaarkku
veRith thaN malarc cOlaigaL soozh thirunaavaay
kuRukkum vagai uNdukolO kodiyERkE
963.meaning: For those whose thoughts are fixed and united in a mind that keeps the Lord, all binding deeds are cut away. Is there any way for cruel me to draw near to Tirunavay, surrounded by cool fragrant flower-groves?

964.translit:
kodi Er idaik kOganaththavaL kELvan
vadi vEl thadangaN madappinnai maNaaLan
nediyaan uRai cOlaigaL soozh thirunaavaay
adiyEn aNugap peRu naaL evaikolO
964.meaning: The husband of her who dwells in the lotus, slender as a creeper, the bridegroom of the gentle Pinnai with wide eyes like sharp spears, the tall Lord who dwells in Tirunavay surrounded by groves -- when will be the days that I, His servant, may approach Him?

965.translit:
evaikol aNugap peRu naaL enRu appOthum
kalaiyil manam inRik kaNNeergaL kaluzhvan
navai il thirunaaraNan sEr thirunaavaay
avaiyuL pugalaavathOr naaL aRiyEnE
965.meaning: Even as I ask 'when will be the days I may approach,' my mind is without composure and I weep streams of tears. I know not the day when I may enter into that assembly at faultless Tirunavay where the sacred Narayana abides.

966.translit:
naaLEl aRiyEn enakku uLLa naanum
meeLaa adimaip paNi seyyap pugunthEn
neeLaar malarc cOlaigaL soozh thirunaavaay
vaaL Ey thadangaN madappinnai maNaaLaa
966.meaning: The day I know not; yet even the self that is within me has entered to do irreversible service as Your bondsman. O Bridegroom of the gentle Pinnai whose wide eyes shine like swords, Lord of Tirunavay surrounded by tall blossoming groves!

967.translit:
maNaaLan malar mangaikkum maN madanthaikkum
kaNNaaLan ulagaththu uyir dEvargatku ellaam
vaNNaaLan virumbi uRaiyum thirunaavaay
kaNNaarak kaLikkinRathu ingu enRukol kaNdE
967.meaning: He is the bridegroom of the lotus-Lady and of the Earth-maiden, the dear one of all the lives of the world and of the gods, the radiant-hued Lord who lovingly dwells in Tirunavay -- when will it be that I rejoice here, beholding Him to my eyes' content?

968.translit:
kaNdE kaLikkinRathu ingu enRukol kaNgaL
thoNdE unakkaay ozhinthEn thurisu inRi
vaNdaar malarc cOlaigaL soozh thirunaavaay
koNdE uRaikinRa em kOvalar kOvE
968.meaning: When will it be that my eyes rejoice here, beholding You? Without flaw I have become wholly Your servant. O King of cowherds, our Lord, who dwells claiming Tirunavay surrounded by flower-groves humming with bees!

969.translit:
kOvaagiya maavaliyai nilam koNdaay
dEvaasuram seRRavanE thirumaalE
naavaay uRaikinRa en naaraNa nambi
aavaal aL adiyaan in enRu aruLaayE
969.meaning: You who took the land from the king Mahabali, O Tirumal who destroyed the demons in the war of gods and asuras! O my Narayana, perfect Lord who dwells in Tirunavay! Saying 'alas,' graciously look upon this servant of Yours.

970.translit:
aruLaathu ozhivaay aruL seythu adiyEnaip
poruL aakki un ponnadik keezh puga vaippaay
maruL Ey inRi unnai en nenjaththu iruththum
tEruLE tharu then thirunaavaay en thEvE
970.meaning: Whether You withhold Your grace or bestow it, make this servant of worth and place me beneath Your golden feet. O my Lord of southern Tirunavay, who grants Yourself to be seated in my heart without any delusion!

971.translit:
dEvar munivarkku enRum kaaNdaRku ariyan
moovar muthalvan oru moo ulagu aaLi
dEvan virumbi uRaiyum thirunaavaay
yaavar aNugap peRuvaar ini anthO
971.meaning: He who is ever hard to behold even for the gods and sages, the source of the three (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva), the one ruler of the three worlds -- alas, who now may be able to approach Tirunavay where the divine Lord lovingly dwells?

972.translit:
anthO aNugap peRu naaL enRu eppOthum
sinthai kalangith thirumaa enRu azhaippan
konthaar malarc cOlaigaL soozh thirunaavaay
vanthE uRaikinRa em maa maNi vaNNaa
972.meaning: Alas, ever asking 'when will be the day I may approach,' my mind grows troubled and I call out 'O Tirumal!' O our great sapphire-hued Lord who has come and dwells in Tirunavay, surrounded by flower-groves thick with clusters of blossoms!

973.translit:
vaNNa maNi maada nal naavaay uLLaanai
thiNNam mathiL then kurukoorc catakOpan
paNNaar thamizh aayiraththu ip paththum vallaar
maN aaNdu maNam kamazhvar mallikaiyE
973.meaning: Those who master these ten of the thousand melodious Tamil verses by Satakopan of southern Kurukur with strong ramparts -- about the Lord who dwells in fair Tirunavay of jewelled mansions -- shall rule the earth and grow fragrant like jasmine.

974.translit:
mallikai kamazh thenRal eerumaalO
vaN kuRinji isaitha varumaalO
sel kathir maalaiyum mayakkumaalO
sekkar nal mEgangaL sithaikkumaalO
alli am thaamaraik kaNNan emmaan
aayargaL ERu ERu em maayOn
pulliya mulaigaLum thOLum koNdu
pugal idam aRikilam thamiyamaalO
974.meaning: Alas, the southern breeze fragrant with jasmine pierces me; the rich kurinji music comes upon me; the setting sun at evening bewilders me; the lovely red-streaked clouds shatter my heart. The lotus-eyed Kannan, our Lord, the bull among cowherds, our wondrous one -- taking the breasts and shoulders He once embraced, we lonely ones, alas, know no refuge.

975.translit:
pugal idam aRikilam thamiyamaalO
pulambu uRum aNi thenRal aambalaalO
pagal adu maalai vaN saanthamaalO
panjamam mullai thaN vaadaiyaalO
agal idam padaiththu idanthu uNdu umizhnthu
aLanthu engum aLikkinRa aayan maayOn
igal idaththu asurargaL kooRRam vaaraan
ini irunthu en uyir kaakkumaaRu en
975.meaning: We lonely ones, alas, know no refuge: the lovely southern breeze that makes me lament, the rich sandal-paste of evening that slays the day, the panchama melody, the jasmine, the cool north wind -- all torment me. The cowherd, the wondrous one who created, scooped up, swallowed, spat out, measured and ever protects the wide earth, who is death to the warring asuras, does not come; how then shall I remaining here save my life?

976.translit:
ini irunthu en uyir kaakkumaaRu en
iNai mulai namuga nuN idai nudanga
thuni irum kalavi seythu aagam thOynthu
thuRanthu emmai ittu agal kaNNan kaLvan
thani iLam singam em maayan vaaraan
thaamaraik kaNNum sevvaayum neelap
pani irum kuzhalgaLum naangu thOLum
paaviyEn manaththE ninRu eerumaalO
976.meaning: How shall I remaining here save my life? The thieving Kannan, who joined in deep love till my paired breasts grew faint and my slender waist swayed, who embraced my body and then forsook and abandoned us -- the lone young lion, our wondrous one, does not come. His lotus eyes, red mouth, cool dark tresses and four shoulders, alas, stand in this sinner's heart and pierce me.

977.translit:
paaviyEn manaththE ninRu eerumaalO
vaadai thaN vaadai vem vaadaiyaalO
mEvu thaN mathiyam vem mathiyamaalO
men malar paLLi vem paLLiyaalO
thooviyam puL udaith teyva vaNdu thuthaintha
em peNmai am poovithaalO
aaviyin paramallavaigaLaalO
yaam udai nenjamum thuNai anRaalO
977.meaning: Standing in this sinner's heart they pierce me, alas. The cool north wind has become a cruel wind; the gentle moon that arrives has become a cruel moon; the soft flower-bed has become a cruel bed; the swarming divine bees scatter our womanhood like a flower. These things are beyond my soul to bear, and even the heart that is mine, alas, is no companion to me.

978.translit:
yaam udai nenjamum thuNai anRaalO
aa pugu maalaiyum aaginRaalO
yaam udai aayan than manam kallaalO
avan udait teem kuzhal eerumaalO
yaam udait thuNai ennum thOzhimaarum
emmil munnu avanukku maayvaraalO
yaam udaiyaar uyir kaakkumaaRu en
avan udaiya aruL peRum pOthu arithE
978.meaning: Even the heart that is mine is no companion, alas; and the evening when the cows return is coming on. The mind of our cowherd is like stone, alas, while his sweet flute pierces me. Even the friends I called my support, alas, melt for him before I do; how then shall my life be saved, when it is hard to obtain his grace?

979.translit:
avan udaiya aruL peRum pOthu arithaal
av aruL allana aruLum alla
avan aruL peRum aLavu aavi nillaathu
adu pagal maalaiyum nenjum kaaNEn
sivanodu piraman vaN thiru madanthai
sEr thiru aagam em maa aavi irum
evam inip pugum idam evam seykEnO
aarukku en sollugEn annaimeergaaL
979.meaning: It is hard to obtain his grace; whatever is not that grace is no grace at all. My life will not hold out until I obtain his grace -- I see neither the slaying day nor the evening nor my own heart. His sacred form, where Shiva and Brahma and the bountiful Lakshmi abide, is my great life. Alas, where now shall I find refuge, what shall I do, to whom shall I speak, O mothers?

980.translit:
aarukken solluken annaimeerkaaL
 aaruyiraLavandrik koortaNvaadai
kaarokkum meeni nam kaNNan kaLvam
 kavarndavat tani nenjam avan kaN ahdee
seeruRRa akilpukai yaaznarambu
 panjamam taN pasum saandhaNaindu
poruRRa vaadai taN mallikaippoop
 pudumaNam mukandu koNderiyumaaloo
980.meaning: To whom shall I speak of my plight, O dear mothers? This cool, sharp north wind cuts beyond the measure of my precious life. My Kannan, whose body is like the dark rain-cloud, has stolen away my heart through his guile, and that lonely heart is now wholly his. The fragrant smoke of aghil incense, the strings of the yaazh tuned to the panchama note, the cool fresh sandal-paste, and the cool jasmine flowers borne on the wind rise up bearing their new fragrance and assail me. Alas!

981.translit:
pudumaNam mukandu koNderiyumaaloo
 pongiLa vaadai pun sekkaraaloo
adu maNandu akandra nam kaNNan kaLvam
 kaNNanil kodidu iniyadanilumbar
madumaNam mallikai mandakkoovai
 vaN pasum saandhinil panjamam vaittu
adu maNandu innaruLaaycciyarkkee
 oodum at teeng kuzaRkee uyyeen naan
981.meaning: The wind bears the new fragrance and assails me; the swelling young breeze and the pale red of dusk torment me too. The guile of my Kannan, who once embraced me with that fragrance and then went far away, is crueller than Kannan himself, and crueller still is what follows. With the honeyed fragrance of jasmine, the soft creeper-blossoms, and the rich fresh sandal, setting the panchama note to it, he blows that sweet flute graciously for the cowherd-women, breathing that fragrance into it. To that flute I cannot but yield; I shall not survive it.

982.translit:
oodum at teeng kuzaRkee uyyeen naan
 adu mozhindu idaiyidait tan sey koolat
toodu sey kaNkaL koNdondru peesit
 too mozhi isaikaL koNdondru nookki
peeduRu mukam seydu nondu nondu
 peedai nenjaRa vaRap paadum paaTTai
yaadum ondraRikilam ammavamma
 maalaiyum vandadu maayan vaaraan
982.meaning: To that sweet flute he blows I cannot but yield; I shall not survive it. As he speaks through it, in between he plays with his made-up beauty, his eyes that act as messengers saying one thing, and with his pure musical words gazing in another way. He puts on a face that bewilders, and again and again wounding, he sings a song that drains away my foolish heart utterly. We understand nothing of it at all, alas, alas! The evening has come, yet the wondrous one does not come.

983.translit:
maalaiyum vandadu maayan vaaraan
 maa maNi pulamba val eeRu aNainda
koola nal naakukaL ukaLumaaloo
 kodiyana kuzalkaLum kuzaRumaaloo
amal oLi vaLar mullai karumukaikaL
 mallikai alambi vaNdaalumaaloo
veelaiyum visumbil viNdalaRumaaloo
 en solli uyvan inga avanai viTTee
983.meaning: The evening has come, yet the wondrous one does not come. The great bells ring out, the beautiful good cows that the mighty bulls have mounted frolic about, and the cruel flutes too sound forth. The spotless bright-growing jasmine and the dark buds and the mallikai-blossoms stir, and the bees hum about them. The sea too roars and bursts forth into the sky. Bereft of him here, what can I say and live?

984.translit:
avanai viTTu akandru uyir aaRRakillaa
 aNi izhai aaycciyar maalaip poosal
avanai viTTakalvadaRkee irangi
 aNi kurukoor saTakoopan maaRan
avani uNdu umizhndavan meel uraitta
 aayirattuL ivai pattum koNdu
avani uL alaRri nindru uymin toNdeer
 acconna maalai naNNit tozhudee
984.meaning: These are the evening laments of the bejewelled cowherd-women who, parted from him, could not bear their lives. Out of compassion for their being separated from him, Maaran Satakopan of beautiful Kurukoor composed, upon him who swallowed and then spat forth the earth, these ten among the thousand. O devotees, take them and, crying aloud upon the earth and worshipping, attain that one of whom they speak, and be saved!

985.translit:
maalai naNNit tozhudezhuminoo vinai keda
kaalai malai kamala malar iTTu neer
veelai moodum madiL sooz tirukkaNNapurattu
aalin meel aal amarndaan adi iNaikaLee
985.meaning: Reach the evening and rise up worshipping, that your sins may perish! In the morning offer lotus blossoms and worship the twin feet of Him who reclined upon the banyan leaf at Tirukkannapuram, ringed by ramparts against which the sea beats.

986.translit:
kaLLavizhum malar iTTu neer iRainjumin
naLLi seerum vayal asooz kidanginpudai
veLLi eeynda madiL sooz tirukkaNNapuram
uLLi naaLum tozhudezhuminoo toNdaree
986.meaning: Offer flowers dripping with nectar and bow down. O devotees, daily meditate upon, worship, and rise up toward Tirukkannapuram, ringed by silver-bright ramparts beside its moat and its fields where crabs gather.

987.translit:
toNdar nundam tuyar poka neer eekamaay
viNdu vaadaa malar iTTu neer iRainjumin
vaNdu paadum pozhil sooz tirukkaNNapurat
taNda vaaNan amarar perumaanaiyee
987.meaning: O devotees, that your sorrow may depart, become one-pointed and offer fresh, unfaded, open flowers and bow down to the sceptred Lord of Tirukkannapuram, girt with groves where bees sing, the Lord of the immortals.

988.translit:
maanai nookki madappinnai tan keeLvanai
teenai vaadaa malar iTTu neer iRainjumin
vaanai undu madiL sooz tirukkaNNapuram
taan ayanda perumaan saraN aakumee
988.meaning: He is the husband of the gentle Pinnai of doe-like glances; He is honey itself. Offer unfaded flowers and bow down. The Lord who graciously chose Tirukkannapuram, ringed by ramparts that push against the sky, shall be your refuge.

989.translit:
saraN aakum tana taaL adaindaarkku ellaam
maraNam aanaal vaikuntam kodukkum piraan
araNam ainda madiL sooz tirukkaNNapurat
dharaNi aaLan tana toNdarkku anbaakumee
989.meaning: He is a refuge to all who reach His feet; the Lord who, when death comes, grants Vaikunta. The ruler of the earth at Tirukkannapuram, ringed by well-fortified ramparts, is loving to His own devotees.

990.translit:
anban aakum tana taaL adaindaarkku ellaam
sempon aakattu avaNan udal keeNdavan
nal pon eeynda madiL sooz tirukkaNNapurat
danban naaLum tana meyyarkku meyyanee
990.meaning: He is loving to all who reach His feet; He who tore open the body of that one (Hiranya) of the reddish-golden frame. The loving one of Tirukkannapuram, ringed by ramparts faced with fine gold, is ever true to those who are true to Him.

991.translit:
meyyan aakum virumbit tozhuvaarkku ellaam
poyyan aakum puRamee tozhuvaarkku ellaam
seyyil vaaLai ukaLum tirukkaNNapurat
taiyan aakattu aNaippaarkaTku aNiyanee
991.meaning: He is true to all who worship Him with longing; He is false (unattainable) to all who worship Him only outwardly. The lord of Tirukkannapuram, where vaaLai fish leap in the paddy fields, is near at hand to those who embrace Him in their hearts.

992.translit:
aNiyan aakum tana taaL adaindaarkku ellaam
piNiyum saaraa piRavi keduntaaLum
maNi pon eeynda madiL sooz tirukkaNNapuram
paNimin naaLum paramaeeTTi tan paadamee
