992.meaning: He is near to all who reach His feet; sickness will not approach them, and the bondage of birth too is destroyed. Daily bow before the feet of the Supreme One at Tirukkannapuram, ringed by ramparts faced with gems and gold.

993.translit:
paada naaLum paNiyat taNiyum piNi
eedaam enakkeel ini en kuRai
veeda naavar virumbum tirukkaNNapurat
taadiyaanai adaindaarkku allal illaiyee
993.meaning: As one daily bows to His feet, sickness is stilled; then what trouble remains for me at all? For those who have reached the Lord, the servant's-master of Tirukkannapuram whom the Veda-tongued ones cherish, there is no grief.

994.translit:
illai allal enakkeel ini en kuRai
alli maadar amarum tiru maarbinan
kallil eeynda madiL sooz tirukkaNNapuram
solla naaLum tuyar paadu saaraavee
994.meaning: There is no grief; then what trouble remains for me now? On His sacred breast dwells the lotus-born Lady (Lakshmi). For those who daily speak the name of Tirukkannapuram, ringed by stone-built ramparts, the burden of sorrow will not come near.

995.translit:
paadu saaraa vinai paRRaRa veeNduveer
maada needu kurukoor saTakoopan sol
paadalaana tamiz aayirattuL ip pattum
paadi aadi paNimin avan taaLkaLee
995.meaning: You who wish your karma to be wholly cut off, so that sorrow comes not near: take these ten among the thousand musical Tamil verses of Satakopan of Kurukoor, city of tall mansions, and singing and dancing, bow before His feet.

996.translit:
taaLa taamaraittada maNi vayal tirumookoor
naaLum meevi nan kamarndu nindra asurarait takarkkum
tooLum naankudai surikuzal kamalakkaN kanivaay
kaaLa meekattai andri maRRu ondrilam kadiyee
996.meaning: At Tirumokur of cool jewel-like fields where stalked lotuses bloom, abiding ever and dwelling well, He stands and crushes the asuras. With His four shoulders, curling locks, lotus eyes, and fruit-red lips, He is like a dark rain-cloud. Other than Him we have no refuge whatever.

997.translit:
ilang kadi maRRondru emmaikkum een taN tuzhaayin
alangalang kaNNi aayiram peerudai ammaan
nalangoL naanmaRai vaaNarkaL vaaz tirumookoor
nalang kazhal avan adinizhal tada andri yaamee
997.meaning: We have no other refuge for all time. The Lord who wears the sweet, cool tulasi garland and bears a thousand names dwells where the masters of the four auspicious Vedas live, at Tirumokur. There is no refuge for us other than the broad shade of the feet of Him whose ankleted feet are auspicious.

998.translit:
andri yaam oru pukalidam ilam endren dralaRri
nindru naanmukan aranodu deevarkaL naada
vendrim moovula kaLittu uzhalvaan tirumookoor
nandru naam ini naNukutum namadidar kedavee
998.meaning: "Other than You we have no place of refuge," so crying again and again, the four-faced Brahma with Shiva and the gods seek Him. He who, victorious, protects and pervades the three worlds dwells at Tirumokur. Good it is that we now draw near, that our affliction may perish.

999.translit:
idar keda emmaip poondaLi yaay endren dreetti
sudar koL sootiyait deevarum munivarum todara
padar koL paambaNaip paLLikoLvaan tirumookoor
idar keda adi paravutum toNdeer! vamminee
999.meaning: "Come and protect us, that our affliction may perish," so praising Him, the radiant Light whom gods and sages follow, who reclines upon the spreading serpent-couch at Tirumokur. O devotees, that our affliction may end, let us extol His feet; come!

0.translit:
alli naaL thaamarai meel aaraNanginn thuNaivi
malli naadu aaNda madamayil melliyalaaL
aayar kula veendhan aakaththaaL then puthuvai
veeyar payandha viLakku
0.meaning: She is the consort of the lord who dwells upon the lotus blooming on the day of its opening; she is the tender, peacock-like graceful lady who ruled the land of Malli (the fragrant country); she abides upon the chest of the king of the cowherd clan. She is the lamp born of the Veyar family of southern Puduvai (Srivilliputtur).

1.translit:
thai oru thingaLum tharai viLakkith thaN maNdalam ittu maasi munnaaL
aiya nuN maNal koNdu theru aNindhu azhaginukku alangarith thanangadhevaa
uyyavum aangolo enru solli unnaiyum umbiyaiyum thozhudheen
veyyadhor thazhal umizh chakkarak kai veengadhavarku ennai vidhikkirriyee
1.meaning: Throughout the month of Thai I sweep and cleanse the ground and lay out a fresh, cool sacred space; and on the early days of Masi, taking fine, delicate sand, I adorn the street and decorate it beautifully for your sake, O Kamadeva. Asking whether I too may be saved, I worship you and your younger brother. Ordain me, then, to him of Venkatam who holds in his hand the fierce, fire-spewing discus.

2.translit:
veLLai nuN maNal koNdu theru aNindhu veL varaip padhan munnam thurai padindhu
muLLum illaa chuLLi eri maduththu muyanru unnai norkinreen kaamadhevaa
kaLLavizh poon kaNai thoduththuk koNdu kadal vaNNan enbadhor peer ezhudhi
puLLinai vaai piLandhaan enbadhor ilakkinil puga ennai eykirriyee
2.meaning: Adorning the street with fine white sand, before dawn while the dew-white mountains still stand, I bathe at the bathing-ghat, kindle a thornless twig-fire, and striving I keep this vow to you, O Kamadeva. Take up your flowery arrows dripping with nectar, inscribe upon them the name 'the ocean-hued one,' and shoot me so that I enter into that target who is called the one who split the beak of the bird (the demon-crane).

3.translit:
maththa nan naru malar murukka malar koNdu mup poodhum un adi vaNangi
thaththuvam ili enru nenju eridhu vaasagaththu azhiththu unnai vaidhidaamee
koththu alar poon kaNai thoduththuk koNdu govindhan enbadhor peer ezhudhi
viththagan veengadha vaaNan ennum viLakkinil puga ennai vidhikkirriyee
3.meaning: With the good fragrant datura blossom and the murukku (coral-tree) flower, three times a day I bow at your feet. So that I may not, with a heart burning, call you powerless and revile you with my words, take up your flowery arrows of clustered blossoms, inscribe upon them the name 'Govinda,' and ordain me to enter into that lamp who is the wondrous lord dwelling at Venkatam.

4.translit:
suvaril puraaNa nin peer ezhudhich churava nar kodigaLum thurangangaLum
kavarip piNaakkaLum karuppu villum kaattith thandheen kaNdaai kaamadhevaa
avaraip piraayam thodangi enrum aadharith thezhundha en thada mulaigaL
thuvaraip piraanukkee sangarpiththuth thozhudhu vaiththeen ollai vidhikkirriyee
4.meaning: Upon the wall I have written your ancient name, and I have shown and offered you your fish-banners, your horses, your female yak-tail attendants, and your sugarcane bow, see, O Kamadeva. My full breasts, which have risen yearning ever since that age began for him, I have vowed and dedicated to none but the lord of Dvaraka, and worshipping I have set them apart; ordain it for me swiftly.

5.translit:
vaanidai vaazhum av vaanavarkku maraiyavar veeLviyil vaguththa avi
kaanidaith thirivadhor nari pugundhu kadappadhum moppadhum seyvadhu oppa
oonidai aazhi sangu uththamarkku enru unnith thezhundha en thada mulaigaL
maanidavarkku enru peechchup padil vaazhagilleen kaNdaai manmadhanee
5.meaning: Just as a jackal roaming in the forest might intrude upon the oblation that the brahmins have set apart in sacrifice for the celestials who dwell in the sky, and trample it and merely sniff it, so it would be if my full breasts of flesh, which rose up resolved to belong only to the Supreme One who bears the discus and conch, were to be spoken of as meant for mere mortals. See, I cannot live so, O Manmatha.

6.translit:
uruvudaiyaar iLaiyaarkaL nallaar oththu vallaarkaLaik koNdu vaigal
theruvidai edhir koNdu pangunii naaL thirundhavee norkinreen kaamadhevaa
karuvudai mugil vaNNan kaayaa vaNNan karuviLai pol vaNNan kamala vaNNath
thiruvudai mugaththinil thirukkaNgaLaal thirundhavee nokku enakku aruLu kaNdaai
6.meaning: Taking with me comely young maidens of good families and those skilled in chanting the Vedas, each day I go to meet them in the street, and on the days of Panguni I keep this vow faithfully, O Kamadeva. Grant me that the dark cloud-hued one, hued like the kaaya flower, hued like the karuvilai bloom, with his lotus-like, beauty-bearing face, may look upon me perfectly with his sacred eyes, see.

7.translit:
kaayudai nellodu karumbu amaiththuk katti arisi aval amaiththu
vaayudai maraiyavar mandhiraththaal manmadhanee unnai vaNangukinreen
theeyam mun aLandhavan thirivikkiraman thirukkaigaLaal ennaith theeNdum vaNNam
saayudai vayirum men thada mulaiyum tharaNiyil thalaip pugazh tharakkirriyee
7.meaning: Preparing paddy with its ears, sugarcane, jaggery-balls, rice and beaten rice, with the mantras of brahmins skilled in speech, O Manmatha, I worship you. Grant that he who once measured the worlds, Trivikrama, may touch me with his sacred hands, so that my slender waist and my soft full breasts may receive the foremost glory upon this earth.

8.translit:
maasudai udambodu thalai ularith thaai vaaip puram veLuththu oru poodhum uNdu
theesudai thiraludaik kaamadhevaa norkinra nonbinaik kurikkoL kaNdaai
peesuvadhu onru uNdu inge emperumaan peNmaiyaith thalai udaiththu aakkum vaNNam
keesava nambiyaik kaal pidippaaL ennum ip peeru enakku aruLu kaNdaai
8.meaning: With unwashed body, hair gone dry, and the corners of my mouth turned pale, eating but once a day, O radiant and mighty Kamadeva, take note of the vow I am keeping, see. There is one thing to ask here, my lord: grant me this boon, that it may be said, fulfilling the very purpose of my womanhood, 'She is the one who shall press the feet of Kesava the perfect lord,' see.

9.translit:
thozhudhu mup poodhum un adi vaNangith thoo malar thoovith thozhudhu eethukinreen
pazhudhinrip paaR kadal vaNNanukkee paNi seydhu vaazhap peraavidil naan
azhudhu azhudhu alamandhu mma vazhanga aarravum adhu unakku uraikkum kaNdaai
uzhuvadhor eruththinai nugang kodu paaindhu oottam inrith thurandhaal okkumee
9.meaning: Worshipping at your feet three times a day, scattering pure flowers and adoring you, I make my offering. If I cannot, without fail, serve and live for him who is hued like the milk-ocean, then if I weep and weep and grow distraught and wander loudly lamenting, that too will be a reproach to you, see. It would be like yoking a plough-ox, making it leap to work, yet driving it on without giving it any feed.

10.translit:
karuppu vil malark kaNaik kaama veeLaik kazhal iNai paNindhu angu or kari alara
maruppinai osiththup puL vaai piLandha maNi vaNNarku ennai vaguththidu enru
poruppanna maadam polindhu thonrum puthuvaiyar kon vittuchiththan kodhai
viruppudai in thamizh maalai vallaar viNNavar kon adi naNNuvaree
10.meaning: Bowing at the twin anklet-feet of Kamadeva of the sugarcane bow and flower-arrows, praying 'Ordain me to the sapphire-hued lord who, while an elephant cried aloud, snapped its tusk and split the beak of the bird (demon)' — this is the garland that Kodhai, daughter of Vishnuchittan, king of the people of Puduvai where mansions tall as mountains rise resplendent, has sung. Those who can master this loving, sweet Tamil garland shall attain the feet of the lord of the celestials.

11.translit:
naama aayiram eeththa ninra naaraayaNaa naranee unnai
maami than makan aakap perraal emakku vaadhai thavirumee
kaaman podharu kaalam enru pangunii naaL kadai paariththom
theemai seyyum sireedharaa engaL sirril vandhu sidhaiyelee
11.meaning: O Narayana, O Nara, you who stand to be praised by a thousand names — if our aunt could obtain you as her son(-in-law), our torment would cease. Declaring 'this is the season when Kama comes,' on the days of Panguni we have decked our doorways. O mischief-making Sridhara, do not come and destroy our little sand-house.

12.translit:
inru murrum mudhugu nova irundhu izhaiththa ich chirrilai
nanrum kaN ura nokki naang koLum aarvan thannaith thaNigidaai
anru paalakan aaki aal ilai meel thuyinra em maadhiyaai
enru un thanakku engaL meel irakkam ezhaadhadhu em paavamee
12.meaning: Look kindly upon this little sand-house that we built today, sitting until our backs ached, and so calm this longing that has seized us. O our primordial one, who once as an infant slept upon a banyan leaf — when will the day come? It is our own sin that compassion for us does not arise in you.

13.translit:
kuNdu neer urai koLari madha yaanai koL viduththaai unnaik
kaNdu maal uruvomgaLaik kadaik kaNgaLaal ittu vaadhiyeel
vaNdal nuN maNal theLLi yaam vaLaik kaigaLaal siramap pattom
theN thiraik kadaR paLLiyaai engaL sirril vandhu sidhaiyelee
13.meaning: O man-lion who dwells in the deep waters, who freed the rutting elephant from the crocodile's grip — do not torment us, who grow love-sick at the very sight of you, by casting your sidelong glances upon us. We have toiled hard with our bangled hands, sifting the fine, soft sand. O you who recline upon the ocean of clear waves, do not come and destroy our little sand-house.

14.translit:
peyyum maa mugil pol vaNNaa unran peechchum seygaiyum engaLai
maiyal eerri mayakka un mugam maaya mandhiran thaan kolo
noyyar piLLaigaL enbadharku unnai nova naangaL uraikkilom
seyya thaamaraik kaNNinaai engaL sirril vandhu sidhaiyelee
14.meaning: O you hued like the great rain-bearing cloud, your words and your deeds cast us into bewilderment and confound us — is your face itself some magical spell? Because we are tender little girls, we do not speak so as to wound you. O you of red lotus eyes, do not come and destroy our little sand-house.

15.translit:
veLLai nuN maNal koNdu sirril visiththirap pada veedhi vaaith
theLLi naangaL izhaiththa kolam azhiththiyaagilum unran meel
uLLam odi urugal allaal urodam onrum ilom kaNdaai
kaLLa maadhavaa keesavaa un mugaththana kaNgaL allavee
15.meaning: Even if you destroy the lovely sand-house and the intricate design that we, sifting fine white sand, fashioned carefully along the street, still — except that our hearts melt and break for you — we bear you no grudge at all, see. O deceitful Madhava, O Kesava, those upon your face — are they not eyes (that steal our hearts)?

16.translit:
murru ilaadha piLLaigaLom mulai pondhilaadhomai naaL thorum
sirril meel ittuk koNdu nee siridhu uNdu thiNNena naam adhu
surrilom, kadalai adaiththu arak- kar kulangaLai murravum
serru ilangaiyaip poosal aakkiya veegaa! emmai vaadhiyeel
16.meaning: We are unripe little girls whose breasts have not yet formed; day after day you set upon our sand-house and (mischievously) make as if to eat a little of it, and we have not firmly built it around. O swift one, who dammed the ocean and utterly crushed the clans of the demons, turning Lanka into a battlefield — do not torment us.

17.translit:
peedha nanku arivaarkaLodu ivai peesinaal peridhu in suvai
yaadhum onru ariyaadha piLLaigaLomai nee nalindhu en payan
oodha maa kadal vaNNaa un maNa vaattimaarodu suuzh arum
seedhu bandham thiruththinaai engaL sirril vandhu sidhaiyelee
17.meaning: If these things were spoken among those who well know the differences, there would be great sweetness in them. But we are little girls who know nothing at all — what do you gain by oppressing us? O you hued like the surging great ocean, who with your brides built the bridge of the great causeway (Setu) that none could otherwise span, do not come and destroy our little sand-house.

18.translit:
vatta vaaich chiru kaadhaiyodu siru suLagum maNalum koNdu
ittamaa viLaiyaaduvomgaLaich chirril eedu azhiththu en payan?
thottu udhaiththu naliyeel kaNdaai sudar chakkaram kaiyil eendhinaai
kattiyum kaith thaal innaamai aridhiyee kadal kaNNanee
18.meaning: With our round-mouthed little baskets and small winnowing-fans and sand, we who play to our hearts' content — what do you gain by spoiling and ruining our sand-house? Do not touch and kick and torment it, see. O you who hold the blazing discus in your hand — you know how it pains us, just as jaggery (sweet) turns bitter (when wronged), O ocean-hued Kannan.

19.translit:
murraththoodu pugundhu nin mugam kaattip pun muruval seydhu
sirrilodu engaL sindhaiyum sidhaik kak kadavaiyo govindhaa
murram aN idam thaavi viN ura neeNdu aLandhu koNdaai emmaip
parri mey piNakku ittak kaal indhap pakkam ninravar en sollaar
19.meaning: Entering into our courtyard, showing us your face and giving a faint smile, will you go on to destroy our little sand-house and our hearts as well, O Govinda? You who strode across all the wide earth and stretched out, reaching to the sky, and measured it all — if you seize us and pick a bodily quarrel, what will those standing here on this side say?

100.translit:
naDamaaDith thoogai virikkinDra maamayil kaaL ummai
naDamaaTTang kaaNap paaviyeen naan oor mudhalileen
kuDamaaDu kooththan koovindhan koomiDai seydhu emmai
uDaimaaDu koNDaan ungaLukku iniyonDru poodhumee
100.meaning: O great peacocks that dance spreading out your plumes! Sinful as I am, I have no resource left to even watch your dancing. Govinda, the dancer of the pot-dance, played his lordly trickery and seized me as his property, his cherished possession. So now even a single one of you is enough for me.

101.translit:
mazhaiyee mazhaiyee maNpuRam poosi uLLaay ninDra
mezhuk ooTRinaaR pool ooTRu nal veengkaDaththuL ninDra
azhagap piraanaar thammai en nenjaththu agappaDath
thazhuva ninDru ennaith thadharththik koNDu ooRavum vallaiyee
101.meaning: O rain, O rain! Like wax poured over and coating the earth's surface, can you not rain down so that the beautiful Lord who stands in good Venkatam may be caught within my heart? Embracing him, press me close and pour yourself down upon me — have you the power to do this?

102.translit:
kaDalee kaDalee unnaik kaDaindhu kalakkuRuththu
uDaluL pugundhu ninDru ooRal aruththavarkku ennaiyum
uDaluL pugundhu ninDru ooRal arukkinDra maayaRku en
naDalaigaL ellaam naagaNaikkee senDru uraiththiyee
102.meaning: O sea, O sea! He who churned you and stirred you up, who entered within your body and drained away your bounty — that wondrous Lord who has likewise entered within my body and is draining away my life — go and tell all my torments to him who reclines upon the serpent-couch.

103.translit:
nalla en thoozhi naagaNai misai namparar
selvar periyar siRu maanidavar naam seyvadhen
villi pudhuvai viTTusiththar thangaL deevarai
valli parisu varuvipparee adhu kaaNDumee
103.meaning: My good companion! The one we trust, who reclines upon the serpent-couch, is rich and great; we are but small mortals — what can we do? Yet if Vishnuchittan of Villiputtur brings his Lord here in a gracious way, then indeed we shall behold that.

104.translit:
thaam ugakkum tham kaiyil sangamee polaavoo
yaam ugakkum em kaiyil sangamum eendhizhaiyeer
theemugaththu naagaNai meel seerum thiruvarangar
aamugaththai nookkaaraal ammanee ammanee
104.meaning: O finely adorned women! Are not the bangles on my wrist that I cherish just like the conch in his own hand that he delights in? The Lord of Srirangam who reclines on the fierce-faced serpent does not even glance at my languishing face. O mother, O mother!

105.translit:
ezhil uDaiya ammanaimeer en arangaththu innamudhar
kuzhal azhagar vaay azhagar kaN azhagar koppoozhil
ezhu kamalap poo azhagar emmaanaar ennuDaiya
kazhal vaLaiyaith thaamum kazhal vaLaiyee aakkinaree
105.meaning: O lovely mothers! My ambrosia of Srirangam — beautiful of locks, beautiful of mouth, beautiful of eyes, beautiful with the lotus blossom that rises from his navel — my Lord has made my slipping bangles slip away entirely, and made them his own.

106.translit:
pongu oodham soozhndha buvaniyum viNNulagum
angu aadhum sooraamee aaLginDra em perumaan
sengool uDaiya thiruvaranga selvanaar
en kool vaLaiyaal iDar theervar aagaadhee
106.meaning: Our Lord rules the earth girt by surging waves and the world of the heavens too, so that nothing anywhere falls into ruin. The wealthy Lord of Srirangam who wields the just sceptre — will he not relieve the distress caused by my slipping bangles?

107.translit:
machchaNi maaDa madhiL arangar vaamananaar
pachchaip pasundheevar thaam paNDu neer eeTRa
pichchaik kuRaiyaagi ennuDaiya pey vaLaimeel
ichchai uDaiyaree iththeruvee poodhaaree
107.meaning: The Lord of Srirangam with its turreted mansions and ramparts, Vamana, the fresh emerald-hued god — if of old the alms he received as poured water fell short, and he now has desire for the bangles I wear, would he not come along this very street?

108.translit:
pollaak kuRaL uruvaayp poR kaiyil neer eeTRu
ellaa ulagum aLandhukoNDa em perumaan
nallaargaL vaazhum naLir aranga naagaNaiyaan
illaadhoom kaip poruLum eydhuvaan oththuLanee
108.meaning: Our Lord who took the form of an unseemly dwarf, received the poured water in his golden hand, and then measured out all the worlds! He who reclines on the serpent in cool Srirangam where the good dwell — he seems intent on taking even the meagre possessions in the hands of poor folk like me.

109.translit:
kaip poruLgaL munnamee kaikkoNDaar kaaviri neer
seyppuraLa ooDum thiruvaranga selvanaar
ep poruTkum ninDraarkkum eydhaadhu naanmaRaiyin
soR poruLaay ninDraar en meyp poruLum koNDaaree
109.meaning: He took the possessions in my hands long ago, that wealthy Lord of Srirangam where the Kaveri's waters roll tumbling through the fields. He who stands beyond reach of every object and every being, who stands as the meaning of the words of the four Vedas — he has taken even my very self, my true substance.

110.translit:
uNNaadhu uRangaadhu oli kaDalai ooDaRuththu
peN aakkai yaappuNDu thaam uTRa peedhellaam
thiNNaar madhiL soozh thiruvaranga selvanaar
eNNaadhee thammuDaiya nanmaigaLee eNNuvaree
110.meaning: Without eating, without sleeping, cutting across the roaring sea, bound for the sake of a woman, enduring all the hardships he underwent — the wealthy Lord of Srirangam ringed by strong ramparts thinks nothing of all that, and recounts only his own gracious deeds.

111.translit:
paasi thoorththuk kiDandha paar magaTku paNDu oru naaL
maasu uDambil neer vaara maanam ilaap panDriyaam
theesu uDaiya deevar thiruvaranga selvanaar
peesi iruppanagaL peerkkavum peeraavee
111.meaning: For the sake of the Earth-goddess who lay buried and covered with moss, of old he became a shameless boar with water dripping from his soiled body. The radiant god, the wealthy Lord of Srirangam — the boastful things he keeps speaking cannot be erased however one tries to erase them.

112.translit:
kaNNaalang kooDiththuk kannai thannaik kaippiDippaan
thiNNaarndhu irundha sisupaalan thees azhindhu
aNNaandhu irukkavee aangu avaLaik kaippiDiththa
peNNaaLan peeNum oor peerum arangamee
112.meaning: When Sisupala, who stood firm and confident, was readying the wedding to take the maiden's hand, his glory was shattered and he was left gaping up in vain, while right there the Lord took her hand. The town that this master of women cherishes is named Arangam (Srirangam).

113.translit:
semmai uDaiya thiruvarangar thaam paNiththa
meymmaip peru vaarththai viTTusiththar keeTTiruppar
thammai ugappaaraith thaam ugappar ennum sol
tham iDaiyee poyyaanaal saadhippaar aar iniyee
113.meaning: The great true word that the upright Lord of Srirangam himself declared — Vishnuchittan holds it firmly in his hearing. The saying that 'he delights in those who delight in him' — if even that proves false between them, then who henceforth can establish anything as true?

114.translit:
maTRu irundheergaTku aRiyalaagaa maadhavan enbadhu oor anbu thannai
uTRu irundheenukku uraippadhellaam oomaiyaroodu sevidar vaarththai
peTRu irundhaaLai ozhiyavee pooyp peerththu oru thaayil vaLarndha nambi
maR porundhaamaR kaLam aDaindha madhuraip puRaththu ennai uyththiDumin
114.meaning: To you others sitting here it cannot be understood — this love I have fixed upon Madhava. To tell it all to you is like words spoken between the dumb and the deaf. The noble one who, leaving the mother who bore him, grew up with another mother, who entered the arena where no wrestler could withstand him — take me and set me down on the outskirts of Mathura.

115.translit:
naaNi iniyoor karumam illai naal ayalaarum aRindhozhindhaar
paaNiyaadhu ennai marundhu seydhu paNDu paNDu aakka uRudiraagil
maaNi uruvaay ulagaLandha maayanaik kaaNil thalai maRiyum
aaNaiyaal neer ennaik kaakka veeNDil aayppaaDikkee ennai uyththiDumin
115.meaning: There is no use being shy any longer; the neighbours all around have already come to know. If you truly mean to cure me without delay and restore me to what I once was, know that should I but behold the wondrous Lord who took the form of a brahmin-boy and measured the worlds, my affliction will be overturned. By all that is sacred, if you wish to save me, take me away to Ayarpadi (the cowherds' village).

116.translit:
thandhaiyum thaayum uTRaarum niRkath thani vazhi pooyinaaL ennum sollu
vandha pinnaip pazhi kaappu aridhu maayavan vandhu uruk kaaTTuginDraan kondhaLamaakkip
parakkazhiththuk kuRumbu seyvaan oor maganaip peTRa nandhakoopaalan kaDaiththalaikkee
naL iruTkaN ennai uyththiDumin
116.meaning: Once the talk has come that 'she went off alone, leaving father and mother and kin behind,' the disgrace is hard to guard against. The wondrous one keeps coming and showing me his form. Take me, in the dead of dark night, to the doorstep of Nandagopa who begot that one son who throws me into turmoil, ruins my reputation, and works his mischief.

117.translit:
angaith thalaththiDai aazhi koNDaan avan mugaththu anDri vizhiyeen enDru
sengachchuk koNDu kaN aaDai aarththuch siRu maanidavaraik kaaNil naaNum
kongaith thalam ivai nookkik kaaNeer koovindhanukku allaal vaayil poogaa
inguththai vaazhvai ozhiyavee pooy yamunaik karaikku ennai uyththiDumin
117.meaning: Declaring 'I will not turn my gaze upon any face but his — he who holds the discus in his lovely palm,' these breasts of mine, bound up with a red band and a cloth over the eyes, grow ashamed if they so much as see ordinary little men. Look upon them and see! They will not open their door to anyone but Govinda. Leaving behind this life here, take me away to the banks of the Yamuna.

118.translit:
aarkkum en nooyi thu aRiyalaagaadh ammanaimeer thuzhadhippaDaadhee
kaark kaDal vaNNan enbaan oruvan kai kaNDa yoogam thaDavath theerum
neerk karai ninDra kaDambai eeRik kaaLiyan uchchiyil naTTam paayndhu
poork kaLamaaga nirudham seydha poygaik karaikku ennai uyththiDumin
118.meaning: This ailment of mine can be understood by no one; O mothers, do not trouble yourselves over it. It will be cured only by the practised art of the one called the dark-ocean-hued Lord. Take me to the bank of the pond where, climbing the kadamba tree that stood by the water, he leapt onto the head of Kaliya and danced his dance, making it a battlefield.

119.translit:
kaarth thaN mugilum karuviLaiyum kaayaa malarum kamalap poovum
eerththiDuginDrana ennai vandhiTTu iruDeekeesan pakkal pookee enDru
veerththup pasiththu vayiRu asaindhu veeNDu aDisil uNNum poodhu eedhu enDru
paarththirundhu neDu nookkuk koLLum baththa viloosanaththu uyththiDumin
119.meaning: The cool dark rain-clouds, the karuvilai blossoms, the kaaya flowers, and the lotus blooms keep dragging me away, urging 'come, go to the side of Hrishikesha!' Sweating, famished, my belly slackening, even when I should be eating the food I crave I instead sit gazing, casting long looks for him. Take me to Bhaktavilochana, the holy place that delights the devotees' eyes.

120.translit:
vaNNam thirivum manangkuzhaivum maanami laamaiyum vaaiveLuppum
uNNa luRaamaiyu muLmelivum oodhaneer vaNNanen paanoruvan
thaNNan thuzhaayennum maalaikoNdu sooTTath thaNiyum pilambanthannaip
paNNazhi yappala thaevanvenRa paaNdi vadaththennai yuiththidumin
120.meaning: My colour has changed, my mind has grown weak, my dignity is lost, my mouth turns pale, I cannot eat, and I am wasting away within. There is one called Him of the ocean-hued complexion; this affliction will be soothed only if I am adorned with the garland of the cool fragrant tulasi. Take me and place me at Paandi-vadam (the northern banyan place), the place of Balarama who, crushing his strength to pieces, conquered the demon Pilamban.

121.translit:
kaRRinam maeykkilum maeykkappeRRaan kaaduvaazh saadhiyu maakappeRRaan
paRRiyuralidai yaappumuNdaan paavikaaL ungaLuk kaechchukkolo
kaRRana paesi vasaiyuNaadhae kaalika Luyya mazhaithaduththu
koRRak kudaiyaaka vaendhininRa koovarth thanaththennai yuiththidumin
121.meaning: He tended the herds of young calves, and he was born into the forest-dwelling cowherd race; he was even bound at the mortar when caught. O sinners, is this a matter of mockery for you? Do not utter learned insults to revile him. To save the cattle he held back the rain and stood holding the mountain aloft as a victorious umbrella; take me and place me at that Govardhana.

122.translit:
kooTTilirundhu kiLiyeppoadhum koovindhaa koovindhaa venRazhaikkum
ooTTukkodaadhu seRuppanaakil ulakaLandhaanenRuyarakkoovum
naaTTiRRalaippazhiyeydhi ungaLnanmaiyazhindhuthalaiyidaadhae
sooTTuyarmaadangaL soozhndhuthoanRum thuvaraapadhikkennaiyuiththidumin
122.meaning: Sitting in its cage, the parrot ever calls out 'Govinda, Govinda!'; and if I become angry and refuse it food, it cries aloud 'He who measured the worlds!' Do not bring the foremost reproach upon yourselves in the land, ruining your own good name and bowing your heads in shame; take me and place me at Dwaraka where lofty mansions with crowning spires stand all around.

123.translit:
mannu madhurai thodakkamaaka vaNthuva raabadhi thannaLavum
thannaith thamaruiththup peyyavaeNdith thaazhkuzha laaLthuNindh adhuNivai
ponniyal maadampo lindhuthoanRum pudhuvaiyar koanviTTu siththankoadhai
innisai yaalsonna senjsolmaalai aeththaval laarkkidam vaikundhamae
123.meaning: From enduring Mathura as the beginning, up to bounteous Dwaraka, Kodhai with her long flowing tresses resolved upon and declared this resolve, longing that her kinsmen should take and convey her there. Kodhai, daughter of Vishnuchittan, lord of the people of Puduvai where golden mansions shine resplendent, sang this garland of fine words in sweet melody; for those able to praise it, the abode is Vaikuntha itself.

124.translit:
kaNNa nennum karundheyvam kaaTchi pazhakik kidappaenai
puNNil puLippei thaaRpoalap puRanin Razhaku paesaadhae
peNNin varuththa maRiyaadha perumaa naraiyil peedhaka
vaNNa aadai koNduennai vaaTTam thaNiya veeseerae
124.meaning: I lie habituated to the constant vision of the dark divinity called Kannan; do not, standing apart, speak of his beauty as if squeezing sour juice onto a raw wound. Take the yellow-hued silk garment from the waist of the Lord who knows nothing of a woman's anguish, and fan me with it to soothe my fading.

125.translit:
paalaa lilaiyil thuyilkoNda paraman valaippaT Tirundhaenai
vaelaal thunnam peithaaRpoal vaeNdiR Rellaam paesaadhae
koalaal niraimaeyth thaayanaaik kudandhaik kidandha kudamaadi
neelaar thaNNan thuzhaaikoNden neRimaen kuzhalmael sooTTeerae
125.meaning: I have fallen into the snare of the Supreme One who slept upon a fig leaf; do not speak whatever you please, as though stitching me through with a spear. He who grazed the cattle as a cowherd with his staff, who reclines at Kudandhai (Kumbakonam), who danced the pot-dance, take his cool dark-blue fragrant tulasi and place it upon my curled hair.

126.translit:
kanjsaik kaaindha karuvilli kadaikka NennumsiRaikkoalaal
nenjsoo duruva vaevuNdu nilaiyum thaLarndhu naivaenai
anjsae lennaa navanoruvan avanmaar vaNindha vanamaalai
vanjsi yaadhae tharumaakil maarvil koNarndhu puraTTeerae
126.meaning: That dark-bowed one who burned with anger against Kamsa shot me through the very heart with the arrow-shaft that is his side-glance, so that I am pierced and waste away, my whole being grown faint. That one who does not say to me 'Do not fear', the forest-garland adorning his chest — if he gives it without grudging, bring it and lay it upon my breast and turn it about there.

127.translit:
aarae yulakath thaaRRuvaar aayar paadi kavarndhuNNum
kaarae Ruzhakka vuzhakkuNdu thaLarndhum muRindhum kidappaenai
aaraa vamudha manaiyaanRan amudha vaayi looRiya
neerdhaan koNarndhu pulaaraamae parukki yiLaippai neekkeerae
127.meaning: Who in the world can endure it? Trodden and crushed by that dark bull who plunders and feeds upon Ayarpadi, I lie faint, broken and bruised. He is like nectar never cloying; bring the water that wells from his nectar-mouth and, before it can dry, make me drink it and so remove my exhaustion.

128.translit:
azhilum thozhilu murukkaaTTaan anjsae lennaa navanoruvan
thazhuvi muzhukip pukundhennaich suRRich suzhanRu poakaanaal
thazhaiyin pozhilvaai niraippinnae nedumaa loodhi varukinRa
kuzhalin thoLaivaai neerkoNdu kuLira mukaththuth thadaveerae
128.meaning: He shows no sign of either weeping or labour; that one will not even say to me 'Do not fear'. Having embraced and immersed himself, having entered and encircled and whirled about me, he will not depart. Take the water from the holes of the flute on which the tall Lord blows as he comes behind the herds in the leafy grove, and stroke it coolingly over my face.

129.translit:
nadaiyon Rillaa vulakaththu nandha koapan makanennum
kodiya kadiya thirumaalaal kuLappuk kooRu koLappaTTu
pudaiyum peyara killaennaan poaTkan midhiththa adippaaTTil
podiththaan koNarndhu pooseerkaL poakaa vuyiren nudambaiyae
129.meaning: In this world where no decorum holds, by that cruel, fierce Tirumal called the son of Nandagopa, I have been parcelled out and seized like the hollow of a hoof-print, and I cannot even stir to one side. Bring the dust from the footprint trodden by that reckless one and smear it upon my body, whose life will not depart.

130.translit:
veRRik karuLa kodiyaanRan meemee thaadaa vulakaththu
veRRa veRidhae peRRadhaai vaembae yaaka vaLarththaaLae
kuRRa maRRa mulaithannaik kumaran koalap paNaiththoaLoadu
aRRa kuRRa mavaitheera aNaiya vamukkik kaTTeerae
130.meaning: In a world over which the Lord who bears the victorious Garuda banner does not dance above all, my mother who bore me in vain reared me up as bitter as a neem-tree. Press my faultless breasts close against the comely rounded shoulders of that youth and bind them there, so that all my flaws and sorrows may end.

131.translit:
uLLae yuruki naivaenai uLaLo ilaLo vennaadha
koLLai koLLik kuRumbanaik koavarth thananaik kaNdakkaal
koLLum payanon Rillaadhakongkaithannaikkizhangkoadum
aLLip paRiththiT TavanmaarvileRindhen azhalaitheervaenae
131.meaning: I melt within and waste away, yet he does not even ask whether I exist or not — that plundering thief, that mischievous one, the Lord of Govardhana. If only I should see him, these breasts that yield no profit I would scoop up and pluck out by their very roots and fling upon his chest, and so end my burning torment.

132.translit:
kommai mulaika Lidartheerak koavin dhaRkoar kuRRaeval
immaip piRavi seyyaadhae inippoaichseyyum thavandhaanen
semmai yudaiya thirumaarvil saerththaa naelum orunjaanRu
meymmai solli mukamnoakki vidaidhaan tharumael mikananRae
132.meaning: That my full breasts may have their distress relieved, if I do not in this very birth render some intimate service to Govinda, what penance done hereafter can avail? Even if he should clasp me to his noble, beautiful chest but once, or speaking the truth and looking upon my face should grant me leave to depart — that too would be exceedingly good.

133.translit:
allal viLaiththa perumaanai aayar paadik kaNiviLakkai
villi pudhuvai nakarnambi viTTu siththan viyankoadhai
villaith tholaiththa puruvaththaaL vaeTkai yuRRu mikavirumbum
sollaith thudhikka vallaarkaL thunbak kadaLuL thuvaLaarae
133.meaning: The Lord who brought forth such torment, the beautiful lamp of Ayarpadi — Kodhai of great fame, daughter of Vishnuchittan, the noble one of the city of Villiputhuvai, she of brows that surpass the bow, filled with longing, sang these words she so deeply yearns for. Those able to praise this song will not be tossed and wearied in the ocean of sorrow.

134.translit:
paTTi maeyndhoar kaaraeRu palathae vaRkoar keezhkkanRaai
iTTee RiTTu viLaiyaadi ingae poadhak kaNdeerae
iTTa maana pasukkaLai inidhu maRiththu neerooTTi
viTTuk koNdu viLaiyaada virundhaa vanaththae kaNdoamae
134.meaning: A dark bull that grazed at large, as a young calf beneath Balarama, frisking and leaping and sporting — did you see him pass by here? Gently turning back the beloved cattle and giving them water to drink, letting them loose and playing among them — we saw him in Brindavan.

135.translit:
anunga vennaip pirivusey thaayar paadi kavarndhuNNum
kuNungu naaRik kuTTaeRRaik koavarth thananaik kaNdeerae
kaNanga Loadu minmaekam kalandhaaR poala vanamaalai
minunga ninRu viLaiyaada virundhaa vanaththae kaNdoamae
135.meaning: He who parted from me so that I pine away, who plunders and feeds upon Ayarpadi, that frolicsome young bull-calf fragrant with the cowherd scent, the Lord of Govardhana — did you see him? With his forest-garland glittering like lightning mingling with the dark cloud amid the throngs, standing there at play — we saw him in Brindavan.

136.translit:
maalaaip piRandha nambiyai maalae seyyum maNaaLanai
aelaap poyka Luraippaanai ingae poadhak kaNdeerae
maelaal parandha veyilkaappaan vinadhai siRuvan siRakennum
maelaap pinkeezh varuvaanai virundhaa vanaththae kaNdoamae
136.meaning: The perfect Lord who was born as Tirumal, the bridegroom who casts his bewildering spell, who utters unmatched playful untruths — did you see him pass by here? Sheltered from the sun spread above by the canopy that is the wing of Vinata's son (Garuda), coming beneath it — we saw him in Brindavan.

137.translit:
kaarththaN kamalak kaNNennum nedungkayi Rupaduththi ennai
eerththuk koNdu viLaiyaadum eesan RannaikkaNdeerae
poarththa muththin kuppaayap pukarmaal yaanaik kanRaepoal
vaerththu ninRu viLaiyaada virundhaa vanaththae kaNdoamae
137.meaning: Casting upon me the long ropes that are his cool, dark, lotus eyes, dragging me along and sporting with me — that Lord, did you see him? Like a mighty spotted elephant-calf in a pearl-embroidered jacket, standing there sweating and at play — we saw him in Brindavan.

138.translit:
maadha vanen maNiyinai valaiyil pizhaiththa panRipoal
aedhu monRum koLaththaaraa eesan RannaikkaNdeerae
peedhaka vaadai yudaithaazhap perungkaar maekak kanRaepoal
veedhi yaara varuvaanai virundhaa vanaththae kaNdoamae
138.meaning: Madhava, my jewel, who like a boar that has escaped the net will not let himself be caught by anything — that Lord, did you see him? With his yellow silk garment hanging low at the waist, like the calf of a great dark rain-cloud, coming filling the whole street — we saw him in Brindavan.

139.translit:
tharuma maRiyaak kuRumbanaith thangkaichsaarnga madhuvaepoal
puruva vaTTa mazhakiya poruththa miliyaik kaNdeerae uruvu
karidhaai mukamseydhaai udhayap paruppa thaththinmael
viriyum kadhirae poalvaanai virundhaa vanaththae kaNdoamae
139.meaning: The mischievous one who knows no righteousness, whose rounded brows are lovely like his own Saranga bow, that ill-suited one — did you see him? Dark in form yet radiant of face, like the sun spreading its rays upon the eastern rising-mountain — we saw him in Brindavan.

140.translit:
poruttam uDaiya nambiyaip puRampoo luLLum kariyaanai
karuttaip pizhaittu ninRa-ak karumaa mukilaik kaNDeeree
aruttit taaraa kaNangaLaal aarap peruku vaanampool
viruttam peritaay varuvaanai virundaa vanattee kaNDoomee
140.meaning: Have you seen Him, the perfect Lord who is fitting (for us), dark within as He is dark without, that great black cloud who stood having eluded our grasp? We have seen Him in Brindavan, coming in His vast greatness like the sky swelling and brimming with desirous clusters of stars.

141.translit:
veLiya sangon RuDaiyaanaip peetaka vaaDai yuDaiyaanai
aLinan kuDaiya tirumaalai aazhi yaanaik kaNDeeree
kaLivaN DengkuM kalandaaRpool kazhambpoong kuzhalkaL taDandoaLmEl
miLira ninRu viLaiyaaDa virundaa vanattee kaNDoomee
141.meaning: Have you seen Him who holds the white conch, who wears the golden silken garment, Tirumal full of grace, the Lord who bears the discus? We have seen Him in Brindavan, His fragrant flower-decked locks gleaming and swaying upon His broad shoulders, playing about as though joyful bees were swarming everywhere.

142.translit:
naaTTaip paDai enRu ayan mutalaat tanda naLir maa malar undi
veeTTaip paNNi viLaiyaaDum vimalantannaik kaNDeeree?
kaaTTai naaDit tEnukanum kaLiRum puLLum uDan maDiya
veeTTaiyaaDi varuvaanai virundaavanattee kaNDoomee
142.meaning: Have you seen the spotless Lord who, from His cool great lotus-navel gave birth to Brahma and the others, saying 'create the world,' and who sports and plays, creating all? We have seen Him in Brindavan, coming back from the hunt, having ranged the forest so that Dhenuka, the elephant, and the bird-demon all perished together.

143.translit:
paruntaaT kaLiRRuk karuLseyta paraman RannaipaarinmEl
virundaa vanattee kaNDamai viTTu sittan koataisol
maruntaa menRu tammanattee vaittuk koNDu vaazhvaarkaL
peruntaa LuDaiya piraanaDikkeezhp piriyaa tenRu miruppaaree
143.meaning: This is the song of Kothai (Andal), daughter of Vishnuchittan, telling how she beheld in Brindavan upon this earth the Supreme Lord who showed grace to the great-legged elephant (Gajendra). Those who keep these words in their heart as a healing medicine and live by them shall dwell forever, never parting, beneath the feet of the Lord who has mighty feet.

20.translit:
seethaivaay amudham uNDaay engaL chiRRil nee sidhaiyEl enRu
veedhivaay viLaiyaadum ayar chiRumiyar mazhalaich chollai
vEdhavaay thozhilaaLargaL vaazh villiputthoor man vishNuchittan than
kOdhaivaay thamizh vallavar kuRaivinRi vaikundham chErvarE.
20.meaning: You who ate the nectar-like words from Sita's lips, do not destroy our little sandcastle, said the young girls at their tender play in the streets. Whoever masters the Tamil verses born from the lips of Kodhai, daughter of Vishnuchittan, lord of Villiputtur where Vedic-mouthed ritual men dwell, shall reach Vaikuntha without any want.

21.translit:
kOzhi azhaippadhan munnam kudaindhu neeraaduvaan pOndhOm
aazhi anchelvan ezhundhaan aravaNai mEl paLLi koNDaay
EzhaimaiyaaRRavum paTTOm iniyenRum poygaikku vaarOm
thOzhiyum naanum thozhudhOm thugilaip paNiththaruLaayE
21.meaning: Before the cock crowed we came out to bathe by plunging into the water. O wealthy lord of the discus, you have risen, you who repose upon the serpent-couch! We have suffered greatly from our helplessness, and never again will we come to this pond. My friend and I both worship you, so please grant us back our garments.

22.translit:
idhu en pugundha ingu andhO ippoygaikku evvaaRu vandhaay
madhuvin thuzhaaymudi maalE maayanE engaL amudhE
vidhiyinmaiyaal adhu maaTTOm vitthagap piLLaay viraiyEl
kudhikoNDu aravil nadiththaay kurundhidaik kooRai paNiyaay
22.meaning: What is this that has happened here, alas! How did you come to this pond? O Tirumal with a honeyed tulasi crown, O wondrous one, O our nectar! Because of our ill fortune we cannot do it; O clever lad, do not hurry. You who sprang up and danced upon the serpent's hood, grant us our garments from amid the kurundha tree.

23.translit:
ellE eedhenna iLamai emmanaimaar kaaNil oTTaar
pollaangu eedhenRu karudhaay poonguruththu ERi irutthi
villaal ilangai azhitthaay nee vENDiyadhellaam tharuvOm
pallaarum kaaNaamE pOvOm paTTaip paNiththaruLaayE
23.meaning: Oh, what childishness is this! If our mothers see this they will not permit it. Do not think this any harm; you sit perched upon the blossoming kurundha tree. O you who destroyed Lanka with your bow, we will give you everything you desire. We will go away unseen by the crowd, so please grant us our silken robes.

24.translit:
parakka vizhitthu engum nOkkip palar kudaindhu aadum sunaiyil
arakka nillaa kaNNa neergaL alamaruginRavaa paaraay
irakkam El onRum ilaadhaay ilangai azhittha piraanE
kurakku arasaavadhu aRindhOm kurundhidaik kooRai paNiyaay
24.meaning: Look how, with wide-open eyes gazing everywhere in the spring where many plunge and play, the unrestrained tears whirl in confusion. O you who have not a single drop of mercy, O Lord who destroyed Lanka! We now know that you are the king of monkeys; grant us our garments from amid the kurundha tree.

25.translit:
kaalaik kadhuviduginRa kayalOdu vaaLai viravi
vElaip pidiththu ennaimaargaL OTTil enna viLaiyaaTTO
kOlach chiRRaadai palavum koNDu nee ERi iraadhE
kOlam kariya piraanE kurundhidaik kooRai paNiyaay
25.meaning: With the kayal and the vaaLai fish that nip at our feet mingling together, and our mothers chasing us off if we hold a spear-like reed - what kind of game is this? Do not sit perched up there having taken away all our pretty little garments. O Lord of dark and beautiful form, grant us our garments from amid the kurundha tree.

26.translit:
thadaththu avizh thaamaraip poygaith thaaLgaL en kaalaik kadhuva
vidaththEL eRindhaalE pOla vEdhanai aRRavum paTTOm
kudaththai eduthtu ERa viTTuk kootthaada valla em kOvE
padiRRai ellaam thavirndhu engaL paTTaip paNiththaruLaayE
26.meaning: As the stalks of the lotuses that bloom in the broad pond nip at my feet, we have suffered torment as if stung by a venomous scorpion. O our king who can take up the pots, toss them high and dance! Give up all this mischief and grant us our silken robes.

27.translit:
neerilE ninRu ayarkkinROm needhi allaadhana cheydhaay
ooragam chaalavum chEyttaal oozhi ellaam uNarvaanE
aarvam unakkE udaiyOm ammanaimaar kaaNil oTTaar
pOra vidaay engaL paTTai poonguruththu ERi iraadhE
27.meaning: Standing in the water we grow faint; you have done what is unjust. The village is very far away, O you who know all the ages! Our love is for you alone, but if our mothers see this they will not permit it. Send us off with our silken robes; do not sit perched upon the blossoming kurundha tree.

28.translit:
maamimaar makkaLE allOm maRRum ingu ellaarum pOndhaar
thoomalark kaNgaL vaLarath thollai iraaththuyil vaanE
chEmam El anRu idhu chaalach chikkena naam idhu chonnOm
kOmaLa aayar kozhundhE kurundhidaik kooRai paNiyaay
28.meaning: We are not merely the daughters of your aunts; everyone else here has come too. O you who in your beautiful flower-like eyes sleep the ancient night's slumber! This is not at all safe; we have told you this firmly. O tender shoot of the cowherds, grant us our garments from amid the kurundha tree.

29.translit:
kanjan valaivaittha anRu kaariruL ellil pizhaiththu
nenju thukkam cheyyap pOndhaay ninRa ik kanniyarOmai
anja urappaaL asOdhai aaNaada viTTiTTu irukkum
vanjagap pEychchi paal uNDa masumaiyilee kooRai thaaraay
29.meaning: On the day Kamsa set his snare, escaping in the dense darkness of night, you came forth to grieve our hearts. Yasoda would scold us maidens standing here until we were frightened, yet she lets you roam free as a boy. O you who drank the milk of the deceitful demoness, give back the garments, O shameless one.

30.translit:
kanniyarODu engaL nambi kariya piraan viLaiyaaTTaip
pon iyal maadangaL choozhndha pudhuvaiyarkOn paTTan kOdhai
innisaiyaal chonna maalai eeraindhum vallavar thaam pOy
manniya maadhavanODu vaikundham pukku iruppaarE
30.meaning: The sport of our Lord, the dark gracious one, with the maidens - Kodhai, daughter of Pattan, chief of Puduvai ringed with golden mansions, sang of it in this garland of sweet music. Those who master all ten of these verses shall go and dwell in Vaikuntha with the eternal Madhava.

31.translit:
theLLiyaar palar kaithozhum dhEvanaar
vaLLal maaliruncholai maNaaLanaar
paLLi koLLum idatthu adi voTTida
koLLum aagil nee koodidu koodalE
31.meaning: The Lord whom many clear-minded ones worship with folded hands, the bountiful bridegroom of Maaliruncholai - if he will let me place my foot at the spot where he reposes, then unite, O koodal, unite!

32.translit:
kaaTTil vEngadam kaNNapuranagar
vaaTTam inRi magizhndhuRai vaamanan
OTTar aavandhu en kaippaRRi thannODum
kooTTumaagil nee koodidu koodalE
32.meaning: Vamana who dwells joyfully without weariness in the forest, in Venkatam, and in the city of Kannapuram - if he will come running and take my hand and unite me with himself, then unite, O koodal, unite!

33.translit:
poomagan pugazh vaanavar pORRuthaR
kaamagan aNi vaaNudhal dhEvagi
maamagan migu cheer vasudhEvar tham
kOmagan varil koodidu koodalE
33.meaning: The son of the one on the lotus-flower, praised and extolled by the celestials, the precious son of bright-browed Devaki, the royal son of Vasudeva of abundant glory - if he comes, then unite, O koodal, unite!

34.translit:
aaychchimaargaLum aayarum anjida
pootttha neeL kadambu ERip pugappaayndhu
vaaththa kaaLiyan mEl nadamaadiya
kootthanaar varil koodidu koodalE
34.meaning: While the cowherd women and the cowherds trembled in fear, he climbed the tall blossoming kadamba tree, leapt in, and danced upon the hood of Kaliya who confronted him - if that dancer comes, then unite, O koodal, unite!

35.translit:
maada maaLigai choozh madhuraippadhi
naadi nam theruvin naduvE vandhiTTu
Odai maa madha yaanai udhaiththavan
koodumaagil nee koodidu koodalE
35.meaning: He who, in Mathura city ringed with mansions and palaces, seeking us came into the middle of our street, and who kicked down the great rutting elephant with its frontal ornament - if he will unite, then unite, O koodal, unite!

36.translit:
aRRavan marudham muRiya nadai
kaRRavan kanjanai vanchanaiyinaal
cheRRavan thigazhum madhuraippadhi
koRRavan varil koodidu koodalE
36.meaning: He who, when he had learned to walk, snapped the marudha trees; he who slew Kamsa by a counter-stratagem; the victorious lord of resplendent Mathura - if he comes, then unite, O koodal, unite!

37.translit:
anRu innaadhana chey sisupaalanum
ninRa neeL marudhum erudhum puLLum
venRi vEl viRal kanjanum veezha mun
konRavan varil koodidu koodalE
37.meaning: He who long ago felled Sisupala who did cruel deeds, the tall standing marudha trees, the bulls, the bird-demon, and the victorious-speared mighty Kamsa - if that slayer comes, then unite, O koodal, unite!

38.translit:
aaval anbudaiyaar tham manaththu anRi
mEvalan virai choozh thuvaraapadhik
kaavalan kanRu mEyththu viLaiyaadum
kOvalan varil koodidu koodalE
38.meaning: He who dwells nowhere but in the hearts of those filled with longing love, the guardian of fragrance-encircled Dvaraka, the cowherd who tends the calves and plays - if that Govinda comes, then unite, O koodal, unite!

39.translit:
koNDa kOlak kuRaLuruvaay chenRu
paNDu maavali than peru vELviyil
aNDamum nilanum adi onRinaal
koNDavan varil koodidu koodalE
39.meaning: He who long ago went in the assumed beautiful dwarf form to great Mahabali's mighty sacrifice, and seized both the cosmos and the earth with a single stride - if he comes, then unite, O koodal, unite!

40.translit:
pazhagu naanmaRai yinporu Laaymadam
ozhugu vaaraNa muyya vaLittha em
azhaga naaraNi yaaycchiyar sindhaiyuL
kuzhaga naarvaril kooDiDu kooDalE
40.meaning: He is the very meaning of the four ancient Vedas, intimate as long study; my beautiful Lord Narayana who saved the rutting elephant (Gajendra) so it might live. If that youthful Lord, who dwells in the hearts of the cowherd women, comes to me, may the kooDal (divining circle) come together and unite!

41.translit:
ooDal kooDal uNardhal puNardhalai
neeDu ninRa niRaipuga zhaaycchiyar
kooDa laikkuzhaR kOdaimun kooRiya
paaDal patthumval laarkkillai paavamE
41.meaning: Of sulking and uniting, of awakening and joining in love — Kothai of the dark tresses, of the cowherd women, spoke these ten verses about the kooDal divining game to enduring, full-flowering fame. For those who can master all ten songs there will be no sin.

42.translit:
mannu perumpugazh maadhavan maamaNi vaNNan maNimuDi maindhan
tannai ugandhadhu kaaraNa maagaen sangizhak kumvazhak kuNDE
punnai kurukkatthi njaazhal serundhip podhumbinil vaazhum kuyilE
panniyep pOdhu mirundhu viraindhen pavaLavaa yanvarak koovaay
42.meaning: Is it right that I should lose my bangles merely because I delighted in Madhava of enduring great fame, the gem-hued one, the youth with the jewelled crown? O cuckoo dwelling in the bower of punnai, kurukkatthi, njaazhal and serundhi trees, sitting there forever, sing out swiftly that my coral-mouthed Lord may come!

43.translit:
veLLai viLisang kiDangaiyiR koNDa vimala nenakkuruk kaaTTaan
uLLam pugundhennai naivitthu naaLum uyirpeydhu kootthaaTTuk kaaNum
kaLLavizh seNbagap poomalar kOdhik kaLitthisai paaDung kuyilE
meLLa virundhu mizhaTRi mizhaTRaadhen vEngaDa vanvarak koovaay
43.meaning: The pure one who holds the white sounding conch in his left hand does not show himself to me; having entered my heart he torments me daily, putting life into me only to make me dance as in a puppet-show. O cuckoo who nips the honeyed seNbaga blossoms and sings joyously in melody, sit gently and, without idle chattering, sing out that my Lord of Venkatam may come!

44.translit:
maadhali thErmunbu kOlkoLLa maayan iraavaNan mEl saramaari
thaaydhalai yaTRaTRu veezhath thoDuttha thalaivan varavengung kaaNEn
pOdhalar kaavil pudhumaNam naaRap poRivaNDin kaamarang kETTuun
kaadhali yODuDan vaazhkuyi lEen karumaaNik kamvarak koovaay
44.meaning: With Matali holding the reins before the chariot, the wondrous Lord shot a rain of arrows upon Ravana so that his many heads fell off one by one — yet nowhere do I see that chieftain coming. O cuckoo who lives with your beloved, hearing the love-music of the spotted bee while fresh fragrance spreads in the blossoming grove, sing out that my dark-sapphire Lord may come!

45.translit:
enburu giyina vElneDung kaNgaL imaiporun dhaapala naaLum
thunbak kaDalpukku vaikundha nenbadhOr thONi peRaadhuzhal kinREn
anbuDai yaaraip pirivuRu nOyadhu neeyu maRidhi kuyilE
ponburai mEnik karuLak koDiyuDaip puNNiya naivarak koovaay
45.meaning: My very bones melt, my long spear-like eyes do not close their lids for many days; I have plunged into the sea of sorrow and wander about, unable to find the boat called Vaikuntha's Lord. O cuckoo, you too know the disease of being parted from loved ones — sing out that the holy one of golden hue who bears the eagle-banner may come!

46.translit:
mennaDai yannam parandhuviLaiyaaDum villiput thooruRai vaanRan
ponnaDi kaaNbadhO raasayi naalen porukayaR kaNNiNai thunjaa
innaDi silODu paalamu dhooTTi eDutthaven kOlak kiLiyai
unnoDu thOzhamai koLvan kuyilE ulagaLan dhaanvarak koovaay
46.meaning: With the longing to behold the golden feet of the Lord who dwells in Villiputtur, where soft-gaited swans spread out and play, my pair of fish-like fighting eyes find no sleep. O cuckoo, I will take my lovely parrot that I raised, feeding it sweet milk with tender words, and make friends with you — only sing out that the Lord who measured the worlds may come!

47.translit:
etthisai yumama rarpaNin dhEtthum iruDeekE sanvali seyya
mutthanna veNmuRu vaRseyya vaayum mulaiyu mazhagazhin dhEnnaan
kotthalar kaavil maNitthaDam kaNpaDai koLLu miLanguyi lEen
thatthuva naivarak kookiTRi yaagil thalaiyallaal kaimmaaRi lEnE
47.meaning: Because Hrishikesha, whom the immortals of every direction bow down and praise, does me violence, I have lost the beauty of my red lips with their pearl-like white smile and of my breasts. O young cuckoo who sleeps in the jewel-grove where clustered blossoms bloom — if you can sing out so that my essential Lord comes, I have nothing to give in return but my very head.

48.translit:
pongiya paaRkaDal paLLikoL vaanaip puNarvadhO raasayi naalen
kongai kiLarndhu kumaitthuk kudhugalit thaaviyai yaagulanj seyyum
anguyi lE.unak kenna maRaindhuRaivu aazhiyum sangumoN thaNDum
thangiya kaiyava naivarak koovilnee saalath tharumam peRudhi
48.meaning: With the longing to be united with the Lord who reclines on the surging ocean of milk, my breasts swell and torment me and afflict my very soul with agitation. O cuckoo there — why do you stay hidden from me? If you call out so that the Lord whose hands bear the discus, the conch, and the fine cool mace may come, you will gain abundant merit.

49.translit:
saarngam vaLaiya valikkum thaDakkaic chadhuran poruttha muDaiyan
naangaLem millirun thoTTiya kacchangam naanu mavanu maRidhum
thEnganimaampozhil sendhaLir kOdhum siRukuyi lEthiru maalai
aangu viraindhollai kookiTRi yaagil avanainaan seyvana kaaNE
49.meaning: The clever one with broad hands that bend the Saarnga bow to draw it taut has a covenant with me. We two — he and I — both know the secret pact we made together in our home. O little cuckoo who nips the red tender shoots in the mango grove of honeyed fruit — if you swiftly call out at once to Tirumal, then behold what I shall do for him!

50.translit:
painkiLi vaNNan sireedhara nenbadhOr paasath thagappaTTi rundhEn
pongoLi vaNDiraik kumpozhil vaazhkuyi lEkuRik koNDidhu neekEL
sangoDu sakkarat thaanvarak koovudhal ponvaLai koNDu tharudhal
inguLLa kaavinil vaazhak karudhil iraNDatthon RElthiNNam vENDum
50.meaning: I have fallen captive in the bond called Sridhara, who is the color of a green parrot. O cuckoo who lives in the grove where bright bees swarm and hum, mark well and hear this from me. Either call out so that the Lord with conch and discus comes, or bring back my golden bangles — if you mean to go on living in this grove here, one of these two you must surely do.

51.translit:
anRula gammaLan dhaanaiyugan dhaDimaikkaN avanvali seyya
thenRalun thingaLu mooDarut thennai naliyum muRaimai yaRiyEn
enRumik kaavi lirundhirun dhennaith thagartthaadhE neeyum kuyilE
inRunaa raayaNa naivarak koovaayEl ingutthai ninRum durappan
51.meaning: Because I delighted in becoming the servant of the Lord who once measured the worlds, he does me violence; the southern breeze and the moon pierce through and torment me, and I know not this cruel way of theirs. O cuckoo, do not sit and sit forever in this grove crushing me — if you do not call out today so that Narayana comes, I will drive you out from this very place!

52.translit:
viNNuRa neeNDaDi thaaviya maindhanai vERkaN maDandhai virumbi
kaNNuRa venkaDal vaNNanaik koovu karunguyi lEenRa maaTRam
paNNuRa naanmaRaiyOr pudhu vaimannan paTTarpi raankOdhai sonna
naNNuRu vaasaga maalaivallaarnamO naaraaya Naayaven paarE
52.meaning: "O dark cuckoo, call out my ocean-hued Lord that the maiden with spear-like eyes who longs for him may behold him with her eyes" — thus spoke the words. Kothai, daughter of Pattarpiran, king of Puduvai of the four Vedas set to melody, uttered this garland of fitting words. Those who can master it will be those who say 'Namo Narayanaya.'

53.translit:
vaaraNa maayiram soozhava lamseydhu
naaraNa nambi naDakkinRaa nenRedhir
pooraNa poRkuDam vaitthup puRamengum
thOraNam naaTTak kanaakkaNDEn thOzheenaan
53.meaning: Surrounded by a thousand elephants, going in procession, perfect Narayana came walking forth; and before him they set full golden pots, and all around they raised festoons of welcome — this I saw in a dream, O friend!

54.translit:
naaLaiva dhuvaima Namen Runaaliddu
paaLaika muguba risuDaip pandhaRkeezh
kOLari maadhavan kOvindha nenbaanOr
kaaLaipu gudhakka naakkaNDEn thOzheenaan,
54.meaning: Having fixed the day, saying 'tomorrow is the wedding,' beneath a canopy adorned with areca palm sprouts, a youth — the man-lion, Madhava, called Govinda — came entering in. This I saw in a dream, O friend!

55.translit:
indhira nuLLiTTa dhEvarku zhaamellaam
vandhirun dhennaima gaTpEsi mandhirittu
mandhirak kOdiyu dutthima Namaalai
andhari sooTTakka naakkaNDEn thOzheenaan
55.meaning: The whole host of gods, Indra and the rest, came and sat and, conferring, asked for me as the bride; they clothed me in the sacred wedding silk, and Durga (the divine Lady) placed the marriage garland upon me. This I saw in a dream, O friend!

56.translit:
naaTRisaith theerththam koNarndhuna ninalgi
paarppanac chiTTarkaL pallaare DutthEtthi
poopunai kaNNippu nidhanO denRannai
kaappunaaN kaTTakka naakkaNDEn thOzheenaan
56.meaning: Bringing sacred waters from the four directions and pouring them generously, the foremost brahmins, many of them, lifted up their voices in praise; and they bound the protective wedding-thread upon me and upon the pure Lord adorned with a flower-garland. This I saw in a dream, O friend!

57.translit:
kadhiroLi theebamka lasamu DanEndhi
sadhiriLa mangaiyar thaamvandhe dhirkoLLa
madhuraiyaar manna naDinilai thoTTuengum
adhirap pugudhak kanaakkaNDEn thOzheenaan
57.meaning: Holding bright radiant lamps together with ceremonial pots, graceful young maidens came forth to receive him; and the king of the Mathura folk, his feet touching the threshold, entered so that all the place resounded. This I saw in a dream, O friend!

58.translit:
matthaLam koTTava risangam ninRootha
mutthuDaith thaamani raidhaazhndha pandhaRkeezh
maitthunan nambima dhusoodhan vandhuennaik
kaitthalam paTRakka naakkaNDEn thOzheenaan
58.meaning: With the drums beating and the striped conch sounding aloud, beneath a canopy hung with rows of pearl strands, my groom, the perfect Madhusudana, came and grasped my hand. This I saw in a dream, O friend!

59.translit:
vaaynallaar nallama RaiyOdhi mandhiratthaal
paasilai naaNalpa Dutthuppa ridhivaitthu
kaaysina maakaLi Rannaanen kaippaTRi
theevalam seyyakka naakkaNDEn thOzheenaan
59.meaning: The good-mouthed ones chanted the good Vedas, and by the sacred formulas spread the green-leaved reed-grass and set the sun (sacred fire); and he, like a fierce raging tusker-elephant, grasped my hand and went round the fire. This I saw in a dream, O friend!

60.translit:
immaikku mEzhEzh piRavikkum paRRaavaan
nammai uDaiyavan naaraayaNan nambi
semmai uDaiya tirukkaiyaal taaL paRRi
ammi mitikka kanaak kaNDEn tOzhee naan
60.meaning: He is our refuge for this life and for the seven-times-seven births to come; He is Narayana, the perfect Lord who owns us. With His beautiful sacred hand He took hold of my foot — I dreamt, my friend, that I trod upon the grinding-stone (in the wedding rite).

61.translit:
vari silai vaaL mukatt' ennai maar taam vandiTTu
eri mukam paaritt' ennai munnE niRutti
arimukan acchutan kaimmEl en kai vaittu
pori mukandu taTTa kanaak kaNDEn tOzhee naan
61.meaning: My brothers, with bows in hand and radiant faces, came and kindled the sacred fire and set me before it. The lion-faced Achyuta placed my hand upon His own, and as the parched grain was poured and offered — I dreamt of it, my friend.

62.translit:
kungkumam appik kuLir saandam maTTittu
mangala veedi valam seydu maNa neer
angavanODum uDan senR' angu aanai mEl
manjanam aaTTa kanaak kaNDEn tOzhee naan
62.meaning: Smeared with kumkum and anointed with cool sandal-paste, we circled the auspicious street, and with sanctified wedding-water I went along with Him there, and they bathed us upon the elephant — I dreamt of it, my friend.

63.translit:
aayanukk' aakat taan kaNDa kanaavinai
vEyar pukazh villiputtoor kOn kOdai sol
tooya tamizh maalai eer aindum vallavar
vaayu nan makkaLaip peRRu makizhvarE
63.meaning: These verses are Kodai's words — daughter of the chief of Villiputtur praised by the Vedic brahmins — telling the dream she dreamt for the sake of the Cowherd. Those who can master these ten pure Tamil garland-verses shall be blessed with good children and rejoice.

64.translit:
karuppooram naaRumO kamalap poo naaRumO
tiruppavaLac cevvaay taan tittittirukkummO
maruppositta maadavan tan vaaycc' uvaiyum naaRRamum
viruppuRRuk kETkinREn sollaazhi veN sangE
64.meaning: Does it smell of camphor, or does it have the fragrance of the lotus flower? Is that coral-red sacred mouth sweet to taste? O white conch, longingly I ask you — tell me of the taste and fragrance of the mouth of Madhava who broke the tusk (of the elephant).

65.translit:
kaDalil piRandu karudaadu panchasanan
uDalil vaLarndu pOy oozhiyaan kait talatt'
iDaril kuDiyERit teeya asurar naDalaippaDa
muzhangum tORRattaay naR sangE
65.meaning: Born in the sea, dwelling unregarded within the body of Panchajana, you then went and mounted firm upon the hand of the eternal Lord of the aeons. O good conch of glorious form, you roar so that the wicked asuras are made to suffer in distress.

66.translit:
taDavarai yin meedE saraR kaala sandiran
iDai uvaavil vand' ezhundaalE pOl neeyum
vaDa madurai yaar mannan vaasudEvan kaiyil
kuDiyERi veeRRirundaay kOlap perum sangE
66.meaning: Like the autumn moon rising over a great mountain at the full-moon tide, so you too, O beautiful great conch, have risen up and dwell enthroned in the hand of Vasudeva, the king of those of northern Mathura.

67.translit:
sandira maNDalam pOl daamOdaran kaiyil
andaram onR' inRi yERiya am seviyil
mandiram koLvaayE pOlum valampuriyE
indiranum unnODu selvattukk' ElaanE
67.meaning: Like the orb of the moon you have risen, with no gap, into the hand of Damodara; O right-spiralled conch, it seems you receive His secret mantra at His fair ear. Even Indra is no match for you in such fortune.

68.translit:
unnOD' uDanE oru kaDalil vaazhvaarai
innaar inaiyaar enR' eNNuvaar illai kaaN
mannaaki ninRa madusoodan vaay amudam pannaaLum
uNkinRaay paanchasanniyamE
68.meaning: Behold, none would reckon those who dwelt with you in the one same sea as being of any account beside you. O Panchajanya, day after day you drink the nectar of the mouth of Madhusudana, who stands as the abiding king.

69.translit:
pOyt teertt' am aaDaadE ninRa puNar marudam
saayt teerttaan kait talattE ERik kuDi koNDu
sEyt teertam aay ninRa seng kaN maal tannuDaya
vaayt teertam paayndu aaDa vallaay valampuriyE
69.meaning: O right-spiralled conch, you have mounted and taken up abode in the hand of Him who, without going to bathe in any sacred ford, felled the twin marudam trees. You are able to plunge and bathe in the mouth-nectar-ford of that red-eyed Tirumal, who Himself is the most distant and sacred of fords.

70.translit:
seng kamala naaL malar mEl tEn ukarum annam pOl
seng kaT karu mEni vaasudEvanuDaya
angait talam ERi anna vasam seyyum
sangu araiyaa un selvam saala azhakiyadE
70.meaning: Like the swan sipping honey upon the fresh-blown red lotus flower, you rest upon the fair palm of Vasudeva of the red-eyed dark form and softly murmur in bliss. O king of conches, your fortune is exceedingly beautiful.

71.translit:
uNpadu solill ulak' aLandaan vaay amudam
kaN paDai koLLil kaDal vaNNan kait talattE
peN paDaiyaar un mEl perum poosal saaRRukinRaar
paN pala seykinRaay paanchasanniyamE
71.meaning: If it is eating, you taste the nectar of the mouth of Him who measured the worlds; if it is sleep, you rest upon the hand of the ocean-hued Lord. The womenfolk raise a great clamour of complaint against you. O Panchajanya, you indeed enjoy many privileges.

72.translit:
padinaaR' aam aayiravar dEvimaar paarttiruppa
madu vaayil koNDaaR pOl maadavan tan
vaay amudam podu aaka uNbadanaip pukku nee uNDakkaal
sidaiyaarO unnODu selvap perum sangE
72.meaning: While the sixteen-thousand consorts look on, you enter and drink — as if drawing the honey at its source — the mouth-nectar of Madhava that ought to be shared in common. O great conch of fortune, will they not be vexed and quarrel with you when you drink it thus?

73.translit:
paanchasanniyattaip paRpa naabanODum
vaaynda perum suRRam aakkiya vaN puduvai
eeynda pukazhp paTTar piraan kOdai tamizh eer aindum
aayndu Etta vallaar avar aNukkarE
73.meaning: These ten Tamil verses are by Kodai, daughter of Periyalvar (Bhattar-piran) of bounteous Puduvai of abundant fame, who made the Panchajanya the close great kin of Padmanabha. Those able to study and praise them with these verses become His intimate ones.

74.translit:
viN neela mElaappu virittaaR pOl mEgangaaL
teN neer paay vEngkaDatt' en tirumaalum pOndaanE
kaNNeergaL mulaik kuvaTTil tuli sOrac cOrvEnai
peN neermai eeD azhikkum idu tamakk' Or perumaiyE
74.meaning: O clouds, spread out like a canopy across the blue sky — has my Tirumal of Venkatam, where clear waters flow, come forth? Tears trickle down upon my breasts and I waste away; is it any glory to Him to destroy thus my womanly modesty and worth?

75.translit:
maa mutta nidi soriyum maa mukilkaaL vEngkaDattuc
saamattin niRam koNDa taaDaaLan vaarttai ennE
kaamat tee uL pukundu kaduvappaTT' iDaik kangkul
Ematt' Or tenRalukk' ing ilakk' aay naan iruppEnE
75.meaning: O great clouds that pour forth a treasure of great pearls — what word is there of the generous Lord of Venkatam who bears the dark hue of the Sama? The fire of love has entered and seized me, and in the dead of night I lie here, a helpless target for a single cool breeze.

76.translit:
oLi vaNNam vaLai sindai uRakkattOD' ivai ellaam
eLimaiyaal iTT' ennai eeD azhiyap pOyinavaal
kuLir aruvi vEngkaDatt' en kOvindan kuNam paaDi
aLiyatta mEgangaaL aavi kaatt' iruppEnE
76.meaning: My radiance, my hue, my bangles, my sense, my sleep — all these have abandoned me, despising my helplessness, leaving me to waste away. O compassionate clouds, singing the virtues of my Govinda of Venkatam with its cool cascades, I would keep my very life intact.

77.translit:
min naakatt' ezhukinRa mEgangaaL vEngkaDattut
tan naakatt' tirumangai tangiya seer maarvaRku
en naakatt' iLam kongkai virumbit taam naaDORum
pon naakam pulkudaRk' en purivu uDaimai seppuminE
77.meaning: O clouds rising up with lightning in your bodies — to Him of Venkatam, on whose glorious chest Lakshmi the sacred lady abides, tell of my devotion: that my tender young breasts long, day after day, to embrace His golden chest.

78.translit:
vaan koNDu kiLarnd' ezhunda maa mukilkaaL vEngkaDattut
tEn koNDa malarc citaRat tiraND' ERip pozhiveergaaL
oon koNDa vaLL ukiraal iraNiyanai uDal iDandaan
taan koNDa sari vaLaigaL tarumaakil saaRRuminE
78.meaning: O great clouds that have surged up and filled the sky, gathering and rising to pour down so the honey-laden flowers scatter at Venkatam — if He who tore open the body of Hiranya with His sharp claws that took its flesh will return the curved bangles He has taken from me, then tell me so.

79.translit:
salam koNDu kiLarnd' ezhunda taN mukilkaaL maavaliyai
nilam koNDaan vEngkaDattE nirand' ERip pozhiveergaaL
ulang' uNDa viLangkani pOl uL meliyap pukundu ennai
nalam koNDa naaraNaRk' en naDalai nOy seppuminE
79.meaning: O cool clouds that have surged up bearing water, spreading and rising to pour down at Venkatam of Him who seized the land from Mahabali — tell Narayana, who entered and stole my well-being, leaving my inner self frail like a wood-apple eaten hollow by the beetle, of my anguished malady of love-sickness.

80.translit:
sangamaa kadalkadaindhaan thaNmukilkaaL veengadaththuch
sengaNmaal seevadikkeezh adiveezhchchi viNNappam
kongaimeel kungumaththin kuzhambazhiyap pukundhu orunaaL
thangumee lennaavi thangumen ruraiyeeree
80.meaning: O cool clouds! Make my prostration as a humble petition at the red feet of the lotus-eyed Tirumal of Venkatam, who churned the conch-filled ocean. Tell him that one day he should come and enter me, so that the kumkum paste upon my breasts is wiped away. Say to him: 'Will my soul remain (alive)? Stay with her!'

81.translit:
kaarkaalath thezhukinra kaarmukilkaaL veengadaththup
porkaalath thezhundharuLip poruthavanaar peersolli
neerkaalath therukkilam pazhavilaipool veezhveenai
vaarkaalath thorunaaLtham vaasakamthan dharuLaaree
81.meaning: O dark clouds that rise in the rainy season! Speaking the name of the Lord of Venkatam, who graciously rose up and fought in the time of war, will he not one day in this season grant me a single word of his message? For I am wilting like the old, falling blossom of the erukku plant in the rainy time.

82.translit:
madhayaanai poolezhundha maamukilkaaL veengadaththaip
padhiyaaka vaazhveerkaaL paambaNaiyaan vaarththaiyennee
kadhiyenrum thaanaavaan karudhaadhuoor peNkodiyai
vadhaiseydhaan ennumsol vaiyakaththaar madhiyaaree
82.meaning: O great clouds that rise like rutting elephants, who dwell making Venkatam your abode! What is the word of the Lord who reclines upon the serpent? Will not the people of the world hold him in low esteem, saying that he—who is meant to be the refuge of all—heedlessly destroyed a tender creeper of a girl?

83.translit:
naagaththi naNaiyaanai nannudhalaaL nayandhuraisey
meegaththai veengadakkoon viduthoodhil viNNappam
poogaththil vazhuvaadha pudhuvaiyarkoon koadhaithamizh
aagaththu vaiththuraippaar avaradiyaa raakuvaree
83.meaning: This is the petition sent as a message through the clouds, lovingly composed by the fair-browed maiden to the Lord of Venkatam who reclines upon the serpent. Those who hold in their hearts and recite this Tamil of Kodai, the unfailing daughter of the chief of Putuvai (Srivilliputtur), shall become the servants of his servants.

84.translit:
sindhurach chempodippool thirumaalirun choalaiyengum
indhira koapangaLee ezhundhumparan thittanavaal
mandharam naattiyanru madhurakkozhun chaarukoNda
sundharath thoaLudaiyaan suzhalaiyinin ruydhungkoloa
84.meaning: All over Tirumalirumsolai the velvet mites (indragopa insects) have arisen and spread everywhere, like scarlet vermilion powder. Will I ever be saved from the bewildering whirl caused by the one with beautiful shoulders, who long ago planted Mount Mandara (as a churning rod) and took the rich sweet nectar (from the ocean)?

85.translit:
poarkaLi rupporummaa lirunchoalaiyam poompuravil
thaarkkodi mullaikaLum thavaLanakai kaattukinra
kaarkkoL padaakkaLninru kazharichchirik kaththariyeen
aarkkidu koathoazhi avanthaarchcheydha poosalaiyee
85.meaning: In the beautiful flower-filled groves of Tirumalirumsolai where warlike elephants fight, the jasmine vines on their garlands and the dark clouds that have gathered stand mocking and laughing, showing their bright white smiles. I cannot bear it. O friend, to whom shall I tell the torment caused to me by his garland?

86.translit:
karuviLai yoNmalarkaaL kaayaamalar kaaLthirumaal
uruvoLi kaattukinreer enakkuyvazhak konruraiyeer
thiruviLai yaaduthiNdoaL thirumaalirun choalainambi
varivaLai yilpukundhu vandhipatrum vazhakkuLadhee
86.meaning: O bright karuvilai blossoms, O kaayaa flowers! You display before me the radiant hue of Tirumal's form—tell me of some way I might be saved. Is it right and just that Nambi of Tirumalirumsolai, of the strong shoulders where Lakshmi sports, should enter and seize my striped bangles as plunder?

87.translit:
paimpozhil vaazhkuyilkaaL mayilkaaLoaN karuviLaikaaL
vampak kaLanganikaaL vaNNappoovai narumalarkaaL
aimperum paadhakarkaaL aNimaalirun choalaininra
emperu maanudaiya niramungaLuk kenseyvadhee
87.meaning: O cuckoos who dwell in the lush groves, O peacocks, O bright karuvilai flowers, O fresh kalanga fruits, O lovely poovai blossoms of fragrant beauty—you five great sinners! What shall I do, for you have stolen and taken on the dark hue of my Lord who stands in beautiful Tirumalirumsolai?

88.translit:
thunga malarppozhilsoozh thirumaalirun choalaininra
sengaT karumukilin thiruvurup poolmalarmeel
thongiya vaNdinangaaL thokupoonjsunai kaaLsunaiyil
thangusen thaamaraikaaL enakkoarsaraN saarruminee
88.meaning: O swarms of bees hovering over the flowers, resembling the sacred form of the red-eyed dark cloud-hued Lord who stands in Tirumalirumsolai ringed with lofty blossoming groves! O clustered flowering springs! O red lotuses that rest within the springs! Declare to me some refuge.

89.translit:
naarunarum pozhilmaa lirunchoalai nambikkunaan
noorutadaavil veNNey vaayneerndhu paraavivaiththen
noorutadaa niraindha akkaara vadisilsonnaen
eerudhiruvudaiyaan inruvandhivai koLLungkoloa
89.meaning: To Nambi of fragrant, sweet-smelling Tirumalirumsolai, I have vowed and set aside, with my own promise, a hundred wide vessels of butter. I have pledged a hundred vessels filled with sweet rice-pudding (akkaravadisil). Will the Lord, possessed of surpassing fortune, come this very day and accept these?

90.translit:
inruvan diththanaiyum amudhusey didapperilnaan
onrunoo raayiramaak koduththuppinnum aaLumseyvan
thenral maNangamazhum thirumaalirun choalaithannuL
ninra piraanadiyeen manaththeevandhu neerpadilee
90.meaning: If today he comes and graciously partakes of all this, I shall offer it again a hundred-thousandfold and serve him forever besides—if only the Lord who stands within Tirumalirumsolai, where the southern breeze wafts its fragrance, would come and present himself in the heart of me, his servant.

91.translit:
kaalai yezhundhirundhu kariyakuru vikkaNangaL
maalin varavusolli maruLpaaduthal meymmaikoloa
soalai malaipperumaan thuvaraabadhi yempperumaan
aali nilaipperumaan avan vaarththai yuraikkinradhee
91.meaning: Rising early at dawn, flocks of dark sparrows announce the coming of Tirumal and sing the raga of bewilderment—is this truly so? They are speaking the word of him, the Lord of the grove-clad hill, our Lord of Dvaraka, the Lord who reposed upon the banyan leaf.

92.translit:
koongkala rumpozhilmaa lirunchoalayil konraikaLmeel
thoongupon maalaikaLoa dudanaayninru thoongukinreen
poongkoL thirumukaththu maduththoodhiya sangoliyum saarngavil
naaNoliyum thalaippeyvadhen njaanrukoloa
92.meaning: In Tirumalirumsolai, where the groves blossom with kongu flowers, I stand drooping along with the golden konrai garlands that hang upon the trees. When will the sound of the conch he raised to his lovely sacred mouth and blew, and the twang of the bowstring of his Sarnga bow, fall together upon my ears?

93.translit:
sandhodu kaarakilum sumandhuthadang kaLporudhu
vandhizhi yumsilampaa rudaimaalirun choalaininra
sundharanai surumpaar kuzhalkoadhai thokuththuraiththa
sendhamizh paththumvallaar thirumaaladi seervarkaLee
93.meaning: Those who master these ten verses of pure Tamil, which Kodai of the bee-swarming tresses composed and uttered upon the beautiful Lord who stands in Tirumalirumsolai—with its Silambaru stream that comes rushing down bearing sandalwood and dark agil, dashing against its broad slopes—they shall reach the feet of Tirumal.

94.translit:
kaarkkoadal pookkaaL kaarkkadal vaNNanen meelummaip
poarkkoalam seydhu poora viduththava nenkutraan
aarkkoa ininaam poosa liduvadhu aNithuzhaayth
thaarkkoadum nenjan thannaip padaikkaval lenandhoa
94.meaning: O dark koadal (glory-lily) flowers! Where is he, the ocean-dark-hued Lord, who arrayed you in battle-dress and sent you to war against me? To whom now shall I make my lament? Alas, I have a heart that yields itself to the garland of his lovely tulasi.

95.translit:
meertroonrip pookkaaL meelula kangaLin meedhupoay
meertroonrum soadhi veedha mudhalvar valangaiyil
meertroonru maazhiyin venjsudar poolach chudaadhuemmai
maatroalaip pattavar koottaththu vaiththukoL kitriree
95.meaning: O sentru (fire-flame) flowers that bloom facing upward! Go up above the higher worlds to the radiant Lord, the Source of the Vedas, who shines forth there. Like the fierce flame of the discus that gleams in his right hand—do not scorch me; instead, place me among the company of those who have entered into his keeping (as his own).

96.translit:
koavai maNaatti neeyun kozhungani koNduemmai
aavi tholaiviyeel vaayazha karthammai yanjsudhum
paavi yeenthoonrip paambaNai yaarkkumtham paambupool
naavu miraNduLa vaayththu naaNili yeenukkee
96.meaning: O lady kovai (scarlet ivy-gourd)! Do not destroy my life with your luscious red fruit—I tremble at the one whose lips are beautiful (like you). For me, a shameless sinner born to misery, even the serpent-couch Lord (and you) have come to have two tongues, like his own serpent.

97.translit:
mullaip piraatti neeyun muruvalkaL koNduemmai
allal viLaiviyee laazhinang kaayunna daikkalam
kollai yarakkiyai mookkarin thitta kumaranaar
sollum poyyaanaal naanum pirandhamai poyyanree
97.meaning: O lady jasmine! Do not bring me grief with your smiles (your white blossoms)—O conch-hued lord's own, I take refuge in you. If the word of the young prince (Rama) who cut off the nose of the wild demoness should prove false, then my very birth too would be no falsehood (i.e., his promise cannot fail).

98.translit:
paadum kuyilkaaL eedhenna paadalnal veengada
