naadar namakkoru vaazhvudhan thaalvandhu paadumin
aadum karuLak kodiyudai yaarvan dharuLseydhu
kooduva raayidil koovinum paattukaL keettumee
98.meaning: O singing cuckoos! What kind of song is this you sing? Only if the Lord of the fair Venkatam land grants us a life (by uniting with us) should you come and sing. If the Lord who bears the banner of the dancing Garuda graciously comes and joins with us, then call out and sing—and I too will listen to your songs.

99.translit:
kaNamaa mayilkaaL kaNNapi raanthiruk koalampoanru
aNimaa nadampayin raadukin reerkkadi veezhkinreen
paNamaa daravaNaip parpala kaalamum paLLikoL
maNavaaLar nammai vaiththa parisidhu kaaNminee
99.meaning: O flocks of great peacocks who practice and dance your lovely grand dance, resembling the sacred form of Kannan (Krishna)! I fall at your feet. Behold the manner in which the Bridegroom—who reclines for ages upon the couch of the hooded, dancing serpent—has left me (in this state).

0.translit:
maTRonRum veeNDaa manamee, madhiL arangar,
kaTRinam meeyttha kazhal iNaik keezh, uTRa
thirumaalai paaDum seer thoNDaraDippoDi em
perumaanai, eppozhudhum peesu.
0.meaning: O my mind, you need nothing else! Forever speak the praise of my lord Tondaradippodi, glorious in his service, who composed this Tirumalai at the feet of the Lord of walled Srirangam who once grazed the herds of calves.

1.translit:
kaavalil pulanai vaitthuk kali thanaik kaDakkap paayndhu
naaval iTTu uzhi tharuginROm naman thamar thalaigaL meedhee
moo ulagu uNDu umizhndha mudhalva nin naamam kaTRa
aavalip puDaimai kaNDaay arangamaa nagaruLaanee.
1.meaning: O Primal Lord dwelling in great Srirangam, who once swallowed and brought forth the three worlds! Having guarded our senses, leaped across the strife of the Kali age, we roam shouting in triumph over the very heads of Yama's servants. Behold the eager pride that is ours, who have learned to recite your name.

2.translit:
pachchai maa malai pool meeni pavaLa vaay kamalach chengkaN
achchudhaa amarar eeRee aayar tham kozhundhee ennum
ichchuvai thavira yaan pooy indhira loogam aaLum
achchuvai peRinum veeNDeen arangamaa nagaruLaanee.
2.meaning: O Lord of great Srirangam, whose body is like a great emerald mountain, with coral lips and red lotus eyes, called Achyuta, bull among the immortals, tender shoot of the cowherds! Even were I offered the joy of going to rule Indra's world, I would not want it, if it meant giving up this sweetness of yours.

3.translit:
veedha nool praayam nooRu manisar thaam puguvar eelum
paadhiyum uRangip poogum ninRadhil padhinaiyaaNDu
peedhai baalakan adhaagum piNi pasi moopputh thunbam
aadhalaal piRavi veeNDeen arangamaa nagaruLaanee.
3.meaning: O Lord of great Srirangam! Though men attain the hundred-year lifespan the Vedic scriptures allow, half of it is spent away in sleep; of what remains, fifteen years pass in foolish childhood; and the rest brings sickness, hunger, old age, and sorrow. Therefore I do not want rebirth.

4.translit:
moyttha val vinaiyuL ninRu moonRu ezhuthu uDaiya peeraal
katthira bandhum anRee paraangadhi kaNDu koNDaan
iththanai aDiyar aanaarkku irangum nam arangan aaya
pitthanaip peTRum andhoo piRaviyuL piNangumaaRee.
4.meaning: Standing amid swarming, cruel karma, even Kshatrabandhu found the supreme goal through the name of three syllables. Alas, though we have obtained our Lord of Srirangam, the One mad with love who shows mercy to even such lowly servants, how is it that we still struggle entangled in birth!

5.translit:
peNDiraal sukangaL uyppaan periyadhoor iDumbai pooNDu
uNDiraak kiDakkum poodhum uDalukkee karaindhu naindhu
thaN thuzhaay maalai maarban thamargaLaayp paaDi aaDi
thoNDu pooNDu amudham uNNaath thozhumbar sooRu ugakkumaaRee.
5.meaning: Taking on great misery to gain pleasures through women, even as they lie awake hungry at night, they pine and waste away only for the body's sake. How strange that these base ones relish mere rice, rather than becoming servants of the Lord whose chest bears the cool tulasi garland, singing and dancing in his service and drinking the nectar of devotion!

6.translit:
maRam suvar madhiL eDutthu marumaikkee veRumai pooNDu
puRam suvar OTTai maaDam puraLum poodhu aRiya maaTTeer
aRam suvar aagi ninRa arangaNaarkku aaTcheyyaadhee
puRam suvar koolam seydhu puL kavvak kiDakkinReerEE.
6.meaning: Raising walls and ramparts of cruelty, taking on emptiness for the next world, you do not realize the moment when this outer-walled crumbling mansion of a body will topple over. Instead of serving the Lord of Srirangam who stands as the very wall of righteousness, you adorn the outer wall of your body, only to lie down as prey for the birds to peck.

7.translit:
pulai aRam aagi ninRa putthoDu samaNam ellaam
kalai aRak kaTRa maandhar kaaNbaroo keeTparoo thaam
thalai aRuppuNDum saaveen satthiyam kaaNmin aiyaa
silaiyinaal ilangai seTRa deevanee deevan aavaan.
7.meaning: The Buddhist and Jain creeds, all of which stand as base, vile dharmas, will men who have thoroughly mastered the scriptures so much as look at them or listen to them? Sirs, behold this truth: even were my head cut off, I would not perish! The Lord who destroyed Lanka with his bow, He alone is the true God.

8.translit:
veRuppoDu samaNar muNDar vidhiyil saakkiyargaL nin paal
poRuppu ariyanagaL peesil poovadhee nooyadhu aagi
kuRippu enakku aDaiyumaagil kooDumeel thalaiyai aangee
aRuppadhee karumam kaNDaay arangamaa nagaruLaanee.
8.meaning: O Lord of great Srirangam! If the spiteful Jains, the shaven ascetics, and the luckless Buddhists speak unbearable things against you, it would be best that they perish as a disease. If I gain the resolve and it be possible, the right deed, behold, is to cut off their heads then and there.

9.translit:
maTRumoor deyvam uNDee madhi ilaa maaniDangaaL
uTRa poodhu anRi neengaL oruvan enRu uNara maaTTeer
aTRam meel onRu aReeyeer avan allaal deyvam illai
kaTRinam meeyttha endhai kazhal iNai paNiminEErEE.
9.meaning: O senseless mortals, is there any other God? Except in time of distress, you do not realize that He is the One. You know nothing of the ultimate truth: apart from Him there is no God. Bow down at the twin feet of my Father who grazed the herds of calves!

10.translit:
naaTTinaan deyvam engum nalladhoor aruL than naalee
kaaTTinaan thiruvarangam uybavarkku uyyum vaNNam
keeTTeerEE nambimeergaaL, geruDa vaahananum niRka
seeTTai than maDi agatthuch selvam paartthu irukkinReerEE.
10.meaning: By his own gracious mercy He established the Divine everywhere, and revealed Srirangam as the means of salvation for those who would be saved. Have you heard, O worthy ones? Though the Lord who rides Garuda stands ready, you sit gazing at wealth in the lap of the goddess of misfortune!

11.translit:
oru villaal Ongu mun neer anaitthu ulagangaL uyya
seruvilee arakkar koonaich cheTRa nam seevaganaar
maruviya periya kooyil madhiL thiruvarangam ennaa
karuvilee thiru ilaadheer kaalatthaik kazhikkinReerEE.
11.meaning: With a single bow, that our warrior-lord destroyed the king of the demons in battle, so that all the worlds upon the swelling ocean might be saved. You who lack the grace even from the womb, who will not say 'walled holy Srirangam,' the great temple where He abides, are merely wasting away your time.

12.translit:
namanum muR kalanum peesa naragil ninRaargaL keeTka
naragamee suvarggam aagum naamangaL uDaiya nambi
avanadhoor arangam ennaadhu ayartthu veezhndhu aliya maandhar
kavalaiyuL paDuginRaar enRu adhanukkee kavalginREnEE.
12.meaning: When Yama and Mrukalan's son spoke, and those standing in hell heard it, hell itself became heaven, such is our Lord rich in holy names. Yet I grieve over this: that pitiable men, not saying 'Arangam, His town,' fall down in forgetfulness and sink into sorrow.

13.translit:
eRiyu neer veRi koL veelai maa nilatthu uyirgaL ellaam
veRi koL poon thuLava maalai viNNavar koonai yeeththa
aRivu ilaa manidhar ellaam arangam enRu azhaippar aagil
poRiyil vaazh naragam ellaam pul ezhundhu ozhiyum anRee.
13.meaning: If all the senseless men, among all the creatures of the great earth bounded by the fragrant, wave-tossing sea, would but call out 'Arangam' to praise the King of the celestials who wears the fragrant tulasi garland, then surely every hell that flourishes by the senses would be overgrown with grass and laid waste.

14.translit:
vaNDinam muralum soolai mayilinam aalum soolai
koNDal meedhu aNavum soolai kuyilinam koovum soolai
aNDar koon amarum soolai aNi thiruvarangam ennaa
miNDar paayndhu uNNum sooTRai vilakki naaykku iDuminEErEE.
14.meaning: The grove where swarms of bees hum, the grove where flocks of peacocks dance, the grove the rain-clouds skim above, the grove where the koels call, the grove where the King of the celestials abides, the beautiful holy Srirangam: those who will not say its name and yet pounce to eat their rice, snatch away their food and throw it to the dogs!

15.translit:
meyyarkkee meyyan aagum vidhi ilaa ennaip poola
poyyarkkee poyyan aagum puL koDi uDaiya koomaan
uyyap poo uNarvinaarkaTku oruvan enRu uNarndha pinnai
aiyap paaDu aRutthuth thoonRum azhagan oor arangam anRee.
15.meaning: To the true He becomes the True One; to the false, like luckless me, He becomes false, that Lord whose banner bears the bird Garuda. To those of liberating wisdom, once they have known Him as the One, He appears and cuts away their every doubt: that is Srirangam, the town of the beautiful Lord.

16.translit:
soodhanaayk kaLvan aagith thoortthar OD isaindha kaalam
maadharaar kayaR kaN ennum valaiyuL paTTu azhundhuveenai
poodharee enRu sollip pundhiyil pugundhu than paal
aadharam peruga vaittha azhagan oor arangam anRee.
16.meaning: In the time when I was a gambler and a thief, consorting with debauched men, sunk and drowning in the net called the carp-like eyes of women, He said 'Come!' and entered my mind, and made my love for Him grow ever greater: that is Srirangam, the town of the beautiful Lord.

17.translit:
virumbi ninRu eeththa maaTTeen vidhi ileen madhi onRu illai
irumbu pool valiya nenjam iRai iRai urugum vaNNam
surumbu amar soolai soozhndha arangamaa kooyil koNDa
karumbinaik kaNDu koNDeen kaN iNai kaLikkumaaRee.
17.meaning: I cannot stand and praise Him with longing; I am without merit; I have not a particle of wisdom. Yet so that my iron-hard heart might melt little by little, I have beheld the sugarcane-sweet Lord who has taken his abode in the great temple of Srirangam, encircled by groves where bees abide, and lo, how my two eyes rejoice!

18.translit:
ini thirai thivalai moodha eRiyum thaN paravai meedhee
thani kiDandhu arasu seyyum thaamaraik kaNNan emmaan
kani irundhu anaiya sevvaayk kaNNanaik kaNDa kaNgaL
pani arumbu udhirum aaloo en seygeen paaviyeenee.
18.meaning: My Lord, the lotus-eyed One, who lies alone upon the cool, sweet sea whose spray dashes in droplets, and there holds sway. My eyes that have beheld Kannan, whose red lips are like ripe fruit, well up and shed tears of joy, alas, what shall I do, sinner that I am!

19.translit:
kuDa thisai muDiyai vaitthuk kuNa thisai paadham neeTTi
vaDa thisai pinbu kaaTTith then thisai ilangai nookki
kaDal niRak kaDavuL endhai aravu aNaith thuyilum aa kaNDu
uDal enakku urugum aaloo en seygeen ulagatthIIrEE.
19.meaning: Resting his crown toward the western quarter, stretching his feet toward the east, turning his back to the north, and gazing toward Lanka in the south, my Father, the ocean-hued God, reposes upon the serpent couch. Beholding this, my very body melts, alas, what shall I do, O people of the world!

20.translit:
paayu nee ararangkan thannuL paampaNaip paLLi konda
maayanaar thirunan maarpum marakatha uruvum thoLum
thooya thaamaraik kaNgaLum thuvaridhazh pavaLa vaayum
aaya seer mudiyum thesum adiyaror kakala laame.
20.meaning: In Srirangam, surrounded by flowing waters, the wondrous Lord reclines upon the serpent-couch. His sacred and beautiful chest, His emerald-hued form, His shoulders, His pure lotus eyes, His coral lips red as the bud, His glorious crown and His radiance—how can these ever depart from His devotees?

21.translit:
paNivinaal manama thondrip pavaLavaay arangka naarkku
thuNivinaal vaazha maattaath thollainenj cheenee sollaay
aNiyanaar semponaaya aruvarai anaiya koyil
maNiyanaar kidandha vaatrai manaththinaal ninaikka laame
21.meaning: O my old, weary heart, tell me—why can you not live by surrendering wholly and humbly to the coral-lipped Lord of Srirangam? He who is near, who is like a great mountain of red gold, who is the gem-Lord reclining in His temple—can we not at least contemplate His reclining within our minds?

22.translit:
pesitre pesal allaal perumaiyon uNara laakaadhu
aasatraar thangat kallaal ariyalaa vaanu mallan
maasatraar manaththu Laanai vaNangkinaa miruppa thallaal
pesaththaa naava thuNdo pedhainenj cheenee sollaay
22.meaning: Beyond what can be spoken, none can grasp even a fraction of His greatness; He cannot be truly known except by those who are flawless and free of desire. Other than worshipping Him who dwells in the hearts of the stainless ones, is there anything we can adequately say? O foolish heart, tell me!

23.translit:
kangkayil punitha maaya kaaviri naduvu paattu
pongkuneer parandhu paayum poompozhi larangkan thannuL
engkaLmaa liraiva neesan kidandhador kidakkai kaNdum
engngnganam marandhu vaazhken ezhaiye nezhai yene.
23.meaning: In flower-groves of Srirangam, where the surging waters of the Kaveri—holier than the Ganges—spread and flow through its midst, I have beheld the very reclining posture of my Lord Tirumal, my master, the Supreme. Having seen it, how can I, wretched and lowly as I am, live having forgotten Him?

24.translit:
veLLaneer parandhu paayum viripozhi larangkan thannuL
kaLLanaar kidandha vaarum kamalanan mukamum kaNdu
uLLame valiyaip polum oruvanen runara maattaay
kaLLame kaadhal seydhun kaLLaththe kazhikkin raaye.
24.meaning: In the spreading groves of Srirangam, where flood-waters spread and flow, having seen the reclining of the elusive Lord and His good lotus face—O heart, you seem hard indeed, for you will not recognize that He is the One. You love only deceit, and you waste yourself away in your own falseness.

25.translit:
kuLiththumoon ranalai yompum kurikoLan thaNamai thannai
oLiththitte nenka Nillai ninkaNum paththa nallen
kaLippadhen koNdu nampee kadalvaNNaa. kadharu kindren
aLiththenak karuLsey kaNdaay arangkamaa nakaru Laane.
25.meaning: I have abandoned the disciplined brahmin-hood of bathing and tending the three sacred fires—it is not in me; nor am I a devotee in Your regard. With what then can I be glad, O Lord? O sea-hued one, I cry aloud! Show me grace, O Lord who dwells in the great city of Srirangam!

26.translit:
podhellaam podhu koNdun ponnadi punaiya maatten
theedhilaa mozhigaL koNdun thirukkuNam seppa maatten
kaadhalaal nenja manpu kalandhile nadhuthan naale
edhile narangkarkku elle enjeyvaan thondri nene.
26.meaning: At every hour I do not gather flowers to adorn Your golden feet; with faultless words I do not utter Your divine virtues; out of love my heart has not mingled in devotion—and because of that, alas, I am a stranger to the Lord of Srirangam. Oh, for what purpose was I born!

27.translit:
kurangkugaL malaiyai thookkak kuLiththuththaam puraNdit todi
tharangkanee radaikka lutra salamilaa aNilam polen
marangkaLpol valiya nenjam vanjanen nenju thannaal
arangkanaark kaatsey yaadhe aLiyaththe nayarkkin rene.
27.meaning: I am not even like the guileless little squirrel that, when the monkeys lifted mountains, bathed itself and rolled and ran to help dam the wavy ocean. With a heart hard as trees, deceiver that I am, through this very heart I fail to serve the Lord of Srirangam, and so I, pitiable one, languish in weariness.

28.translit:
umparaa lariya laakaa oLiyuLaar aanaik kaaki
sempulaa luNdu vaazhum mudhalaimel seeri vandhaar
namparamaaya thuNde naaygaLom sirumai yoraa
empiraar kaatsey yaadhe enjeyvaan thondri nene.
28.meaning: For the sake of the elephant, the radiant One whom even the celestials cannot know came charging in fury upon the crocodile that lived feeding on red flesh. Is there anything beyond His power? Yet, not even reckoning the lowliness of us who are like dogs—failing to serve my Lord, for what purpose was I born!

29.translit:
oorilen kaaNi yillai uravumat roruva rillai
paaril nin paadha moolam patrilen parama moorthi
kaaroLi vaNNa neeen kaNNane kadharu kindren
aaruLark kaLaika Nammaa arangkamaa nakaru Laane.
29.meaning: I have no town, I have no land, I have no kin, none at all. On this earth I have not grasped the sole of Your feet, O Supreme Being. O dark-radiant-hued one, O my Kannan, I cry aloud! Who is there as my refuge, O mother-Lord who dwells in the great city of Srirangam?

30.translit:
manaththilor thooymai yillai vaayilo rinjo lillai
sinaththinaal setram nokkith theeviLi viLivan vaaLaa
punaththuzhaay maalai yaane ponnisoozh thiruva rangkaa
enakkinik kadhiyen sollaay ennaiyaa Ludaiya kove.
30.meaning: There is no purity in my mind, no sweet word on my lips; in anger I glare with malice and utter harsh, vain cries for nothing. O Lord wearing the garland of wild tulasi, O Srirangam girt by the Kaveri, tell me—what refuge is there now for me, O King who has made me His own?

31.translit:
thavaththuLaar thammi lallen thanampadath thaari lallen
uvarththaneer pola vendran utravark kondru mallen
thuvarththasev vaayi naarkke thuvakkarath thurisa naanen
avaththame piravi thandhaay arangkamaa nakaru Laane.
31.meaning: I am not among those rich in austerity; I am not among the generous who give wealth abundantly; like brackish water, I am of no use even to my own kin. Yet to the red, coral-lipped women alone I am wholly and unbreakably bound—a flawed wretch. In vain have You granted me this birth, O Lord who dwells in the great city of Srirangam!

32.translit:
aarththuvaN dalampum solai aNithiru varangkan thannuL
kaarththira lanaiya menik kaNNane unnaik kaaNum
maarkkamon dariya maattaa manisaril thurisa naaya
moorkkanen vandhu nindren moorkkanen moorkka nene.
32.meaning: In beautiful Srirangam, in the groves where bees hum and swarm with their din, O Kannan whose form is like a mass of dark rain-clouds—I, a brute who knows not even one path by which to behold You, the most flawed among men, have come and stood before You. A brute am I, a brute, a brute indeed!

33.translit:
meyyelaam poka vittu virikuzha laaril pattu
poyyelaam podhindhu koNda potkanen vandhu nindren
aiyane arangka neun aruLennu maasai thannaal
poyyanen vandhu nindren poyyanen poyya nene.
33.meaning: Having let all truth slip away, ensnared by women with flowing tresses, gathering up all falsehood within me, a fool I have come and stood. O Master, O Lord of Srirangam, drawn by the longing for Your grace, a liar I have come and stood—a liar am I, a liar, a liar indeed!

34.translit:
uLLaththe yuraiyum maalai uLLuvaa nuNarvon dhillaa
kaLLaththen naanum thoNdaayth thoNdukke kolam pooNden
uLLuvaa ruLLitrellaam udanirun darithi yendru
veLkippo yennuL Lenaan vilavarach chiriththit tene.
34.meaning: Having no insight even to contemplate the Lord who dwells within my own heart, deceiver that I am, I assumed the guise of a servant merely for show. Realizing that You, abiding within, know all that is in the minds of those who think—I, ashamed, laughed within myself till my sides ached.

35.translit:
thaaviyan dulaka mellaam thalaiviLaak koNda endhaay
seviye nunnai yallaalsik kenas sengkaN maale
aaviye.amudhe endran aaruyi ranaiya endhaay
paaviye nunnai yallaal paaviyen paavi yene.
35.meaning: O my Father who once strode and measured all the worlds, taking them as Your own! Firmly I will worship none but You, O lotus-red-eyed Tirumal. O my life-breath, O my nectar, O my Father dear as my very soul—save You, sinner that I am, I have none; a sinner am I, a sinner indeed!

36.translit:
mazhaikkandru varaimu nendhum maindhane madhura vaare
uzhaikkandre pola nokkam udaiyavar valaiyuL pattu
uzhaikkindrer kennai nokkaa thozhivadheunnai yandre
azhaikkindren aadhi moorthi arangkamaa nakaru Laane.
36.meaning: O youthful one who once lifted the mountain against the rain, O sweet flowing one! Caught in the snare of women whose glances are like a fawn's, I toil and suffer—is it right that You should not even look upon me as I labor? It is to You alone that I call, O primordial Lord who dwells in the great city of Srirangam!

37.translit:
theLivilaak kalangkal neersoozh thiruvarangkang kaththuL Longkum
oLiyuLaar thaame yandre thandhaiyum thaayu maavaar
eLiyadho raruLu mandre endhiraTH thempi raanaar
aLiyannam paiyal ennaar ammavo kodiya vaare.
37.meaning: The radiant One who rises within Srirangam, girt by turbid, muddied waters—is He not Himself both father and mother? Is not even a small grace within His power? Yet my Lord, in my regard, will not even say, 'this little one of ours is worthy of pity.' Alas, how cruel!

38.translit:
mempporuL poka vittu meymmaiyai mikavu Narndhu
aampari sarindhu koNdu aimpula nakaththa dakki
kaampharath thalaisi raiththun kadaiththalai yirundhuvaazhum
somparai ukaththi polum soozhpunal arangkaTH thaane.
38.meaning: Casting away worldly things prized as supreme, deeply knowing the truth, grasping the fitting way, restraining the five senses within, shaving the head clean—those who dwell content at Your threshold, even such 'idlers' You seem to cherish, O Lord of Srirangam encircled by waters.

39.translit:
adimaiyil kudimai yillaa ayalsadhup pedhi maaril
kudimaiyil kadaimai patta kukkaril pirappa relum
mudiyinil thuLapam vaiththaay moykazhar kanpu seyyum
adiyarai yukaththi polum arangkamaa nakaru Laane.
39.meaning: Even should they be born among the lowest outcastes, far beneath the householder dignity of the four-Veda-reciting brahmins who have no tradition of service—O Lord who wears the tulasi upon Your crown, those devotees who love Your honey-fragrant anklet-feet, even them You seem to cherish, O Lord who dwells in the great city of Srirangam!

40.translit:
thirumaru maarva ninnaich chindhaiyuL thigazha vaiththu
maruviya manaththa raagil maanilath thuyirga LeNNaam
veruvarak konRu suttitt eettiya vinaiya rElum
aruvinaip payana thuyyaar arangamaa nagaru LaanE.
40.meaning: O Lord of Srirangam, who bears the mark of Lakshmi (Tiru) upon Your chest! Even if those who, with their minds firmly fixed in love, hold You shining within their thought have committed grievous deeds — having terrifyingly slain and heaped up sins by killing all the lives of this great earth — they shall not suffer the fruit of their cruel karma. O You who dwell in the great city of Srirangam!

41.translit:
vaanuLaa raRiya laagaa vaanavaa enpa raagil
thEnulaan thuLapa maalaich chenniyaay enpa raagil
Unamaa yinagaL seyyum Unakaa ragarka LElum
pOnagam seydha sEdam tharuvarEl punitha manRE.
41.meaning: If men say "O celestial One whom even the dwellers of heaven cannot know!", if they say "O You who wear upon Your crown the tulasi garland over which the bees hum laden with honey!" — even should they be flesh-eating men who do base and degrading deeds, if they offer the remnant of the food they have eaten, is it not pure indeed? (Their offering and they themselves become holy.)

42.translit:
pazhudhilaa vozhuga laaRRup palasathup pEdhi maargaL
izhikulath thavarga LElum emmadi yaarga Laagil
thozhuminIr koduminkoLmin enRunin nOdu mokka
vazhipada aruLi naaypOnm madhiLthiru varangath thaanE.
42.meaning: O Lord of walled holy Srirangam! You graciously ordained that, even though men of faultless conduct who have performed many sacrifices and the four-fold knowledge be of high birth, if they are Your devotees one should worship them, give to them and receive from them — to be worshipped on equal footing with You. (Such is the grace You have shown toward Your devotees, whatever their birth.)

43.translit:
amaravO ranga maaRum vEdhamO naangu mOdhi
thamargaLil thalaiva raaya saadhiyan thaNarga LElum
numargaLaip pazhippa raagil nodippadhO raLavilaangE
avargaLthaam pulaiyar pOlum arangamaa nagaru LaanE.
43.meaning: O Lord who dwell in the great city of Srirangam! Even if they be brahmins of high caste, chiefs among their own people, who flawlessly recite the six Vedangas and the four Vedas — should they revile Your devotees, in that very instant, then and there, they themselves become as the lowliest outcastes.

44.translit:
peNNulaam sadaiyi naanum piramanu munnaik kaaNpaan
eNNilaa vUzhi yUzhi thavanjeydhaar veLgi niRpa
viNNuLaar viyappa vandhu aanaikkan RaruLai yIndha
kaNNaRaaunnai yennO kaLaikaNaak karudhu maaRE.
44.meaning: While the One with matted locks upon whom the maiden (Ganga) flows (Shiva) and Brahma, who performed penance through countless ages upon ages to behold You, stood ashamed (unable to see You), You came — to the wonder of the celestials — and bestowed Your grace upon the elephant (Gajendra). O ever-watchful Lord! How then could I regard You, whose eyes never close in care for Your devotees, as anything but my sole refuge?

45.translit:
vaLavezhum thavaLa maada madhuraimaa nagaran thannuL
kavaLamaal yaanai konRa kaNNanai aranga maalai
thuvaLaththoN daaya tholseerth thoNdara dippo disol
iLaiyapun kavidhai yElum empiRaar kiniya vaaRE.
45.meaning: This is the song of Thondaradippodi, of ancient and steadfast glory, who is a melting, devoted servant — sung upon Kannan (Krishna), the Lord of Srirangam, who slew the great rutting elephant within the great city of Madurai abounding in tall, gleaming white mansions. Though it be but a humble, immature poem, how sweet it is to those who are wholly His own!

0.translit:
vadathisai mathurai saaLakki raamam vaikuntham thuvarai ayoadhi
iDamuDai vadhari yiDavakai yuDaiya emburu Doaththaman irukkai
thaDavarai yadhirath tharaNiviN DiDiyath thalaippaRRik karaimaram saaDi
kaDalinaik kalangak kaDuththizhi gangaik kaNDamen num kaDi nakarae.
0.meaning: Northern Mathura, SaaLakkiraamam, Vaikuntha, Dvaraka, Ayodhya, and the spacious Badari are the dwelling-places of our Supreme Lord, Purushottama. The mighty Ganga, which makes the great mountains quake, splits the earth, thunders, seizes by the crest and topples the trees on its banks, and rushes down churning the sea into turmoil — on its bank stands the fragrant fortified city called Kandam (Badari).

1.translit:
muRRamooththuk koalthuNaiyaa munnaDinoakki vaLainthu
iRRakaalpoal thaLLi meLLa irunthangu iLaiyaamun,
peRRathaaypoal vantha paeychchi perumulaiyooDu uyirai
vaRRavaangki yuNDa vaayaan vadhari vaNangkuthumae.
1.meaning: Before we grow utterly old, leaning forward to look at our feet, supported by a staff, tottering on legs that seem broken, and sitting wearied — let us worship at Badari the Lord of the mouth that sucked dry the very life out of the great breast of the demoness Putana, who came disguised like the mother that bore Him.

2.translit:
muthukupaRRik kaiththa laththaal munnoru koaloonRi,
vidhirvidhirththuk kaN suzhanRu maeRkiLai koNDirumi,
idhuvennappar mooththavaa Renru iLaiyavar aesaamun,
madhuvuN vaNDu paNgaL paaDum vadhari vaNangkuthumae.
2.meaning: Before, gripping our backs with our hands and planting a staff before us, trembling, with eyes rolling, coughing and gasping for breath, the young ones mock saying 'See how old our father has become!' — let us worship at Badari, where the honey-drinking bees sing their melodies.

3.translit:
uRikaLpoal meynnaram bezhunthu oonthaLarnthu uLLam eLki,
neRiyainoakkik kaN suzhanRu ninRu naDungkaamun,
aRidhiyaakil nenjam anbaay aayira naamanjolli,
veRikoL vaNDu paNgaL paaDum vadhari vaNangkuthumae.
3.meaning: Before the veins rise up on our body like the cords of a hanging-rack, the flesh sags, the mind grows feeble, and we stand trembling with eyes rolling, peering at the path — O heart, if you have any wisdom, lovingly chant His thousand names and let us worship at Badari, where the fragrance-laden bees sing their melodies.

4.translit:
peeLai soarak kaNNiDungkip piththezha mooththirumi
thaaLkaL noavath thammil muTTith thaLLi naDavaamun,
kaaLaiyaakik kanRu maeyththuk kunReDuththu anRu ninRaan,
vaaLai paayum thaNDa Danjsoozh vadhari vaNangkuthumae.
4.meaning: Before, with rheum oozing and eyes shrinking, going mad and coughing in old age, our legs aching, stumbling against each other and walking with a totter — let us worship at Badari, ringed by cool dense groves where scabbard-fish leap, the abode of Him who, as a youth, grazed the calves and lifted up the hill on that day.

5.translit:
paNDu kaamaraana vaarum paavaiyar vaayamutham
uNDavaarum, vaazhntha vaarum okka uraiththirumi,
thaNDu kaalaa voonRi yoonRith thaLLi naDavaamun,
vaNDu paaDum thaNDu zhaayaan vadhari vaNangkuthumae.
5.meaning: Before we babble, coughing, of how we were once full of desire, how we drank the nectar of women's lips, and how we lived — and before we totter and walk leaning hard upon a staff for a leg — let us worship at Badari the Lord who wears the cool tulasi about which the bees sing.

6.translit:
eyththa solloa DeeLai aengki irumi yiLaiththu uDalam
piththarpoalach chiththam vaeRaayp paesi yayaraamun
aththan enthai aadhimoorththi aazhkaDalaik kaDaintha
maiththa soadhi yemperumaan vadhari vaNangkuthumae.
6.meaning: Before, with faltering speech and choking phlegm, coughing and wearied in body, our mind grown deranged like a madman's, we ramble and swoon — let us worship at Badari our Lord, the Father, the Primal Form, the dark-hued radiance who churned the deep ocean.

7.translit:
pappa appar mooththa aaRu paazhppadhu seeththiraLai
yoppa aikkaLpoadha vuntha unthamarkkaaNmin enru,
seppunaer menkongai nallaar thaamsiriyaadha munnam,
vaippum nangaL vaazhvu maanaan vadhari vaNangkuthumae.
7.meaning: Alas — before the lovely women with soft breasts like golden caskets laugh and say to their kin 'Look at your man — how ruined our father has grown, drooling spittle as the phlegm rises up!' — let us worship at Badari, the Lord who is our treasure-store and our very life.

8.translit:
eesi poamining ngiraenmin irumi yiLaiththeer uLLam
koosiyiTTeer enru paesum kuvaLai yangaNNiyarppaal,
naasamaana paasam viTTu nanneRi noakkaluRil,
vaasam malku thaNDu zhaayaan vadhari vaNangkuthumae.
8.meaning: If, casting off ruinous attachment toward the lily-eyed women who say 'Shoo, go away, keep off! you are coughing and worn out, you have made our hearts shrink' — we wish to look toward the good path, let us worship at Badari the Lord who wears the cool fragrance-filled tulasi.

9.translit:
pulankaL naiya meyyil mooththup poanthirunthu uLLam eLki,
kalangkavaik kaLpoadha vunthik kaNDa pidhaRRaamun,
alangkalaaya thaNDu zhaaykoNDu aayira naamamsolli,
valangkoL thoNDarppaaDi yaaDum vadhari vaNangkuthumae.
9.meaning: Before the senses fail, the body ages, the mind grows feeble and confused, the rising phlegm makes us babble at whatever we see — taking the cool garland-like tulasi and chanting His thousand names, let us worship at Badari, where the devotees in their fervour sing and dance in circumambulation.

10.translit:
vaNDu thaNthaenuNDu vaazhum vadhari neDumaalai,
kaNDalvaeli mangkai vaenthan kaliyan oli maalai,
koNDu thoNDarppaaDi yaaDak kooDiDil neeLvisumbil,
aNDamallaal maRRa varkku oaraaTsi aRiyoamae.
10.meaning: This garland of resounding verses by Kaliyan, the king of Mangai girded with screw-pine hedges, on the great Lord Tirumal of Badari where bees live drinking cool honey — if devotees take it up and sing and dance, then in the vast high heavens we know of no other dominion for them but the eternal realm itself.

11.translit:
aenamunaaki yirunilam iDanthu anRiNaiyaDi yimaiyavar vaNangka,
thaanavanaakam tharaNiyil puraLath thaDanjsilai kuniththa venthalaivan,
thaenamar soalaik kaRpakam payantha theyvananuru malarkkoNarnthu,
vaanavar vaNangkum gangkai yinkaraimael vadhariyaaschiraamaththuLLaanae.
11.meaning: Becoming the Boar of old, He raised up the wide earth, and the celestials worshipped His twin feet; He is the fierce Lord who bent the great bow so that the demon's body rolled upon the ground. On the bank of the Ganga — where the celestials bring the good divine fragrant blossoms borne by the kalpaka tree of the honey-rich groves and offer worship — He dwells in Badarikashrama.

12.translit:
kaaniDai yuruvaich chuDusaram thuranthu kaNDumum koDun thozhil uravoan,
oonuDai yakalaththu aDukaNai kuLippa uyirkkavarnthu ukantha vemmoruvan,
thaenuDaik kamalaththu ayanoDu thaevar senRu senRiRainjiDa peruku
vaaniDai mudhuneerk gangkai yingkaraimael vadhariyaaschiraamaththuLLaanae.
12.meaning: He shot the burning arrow at Maricha in the disguise that roamed the forest — the mighty One whose cruel deeds even his foes beheld; the dear Lord who delighted in seizing away the life of Ravana, plunging the deadly arrows into his fleshy chest. On the bank of the Ganga whose ancient waters swell in the sky, where Brahma seated on the honeyed lotus and the gods come again and again to bow down, He dwells in Badarikashrama.

13.translit:
ilangkaiyum kaDalum aDalarunthuppin iruniDhikkiRaivanum, arakkar
kulangkaLum keDamun koDun thozhilpurintha koRRavan kozhunjsuDarsuzhanRa,
vilangkalil urinji maelninRa visumbil veNthukiRkoDi yena virinthu,
valantharu maNineerk gangkaiyin karaimael vadhariyaaschiraamaththuLLaanae.
13.meaning: He is the victorious one who of old performed fierce deeds so that Lanka, the sea, the lord of the two treasures of mighty strength (Kubera/Ravana), and the whole race of demons were destroyed. On the bank of the Ganga of bright clear waters that grant blessing — its waters rubbing against the wheeling radiant mountains and spreading out like a white silken banner against the high sky above — He dwells in Badarikashrama.

14.translit:
thuNivini yunakkuch cholluvan manamae. thozhuthezhu thoNDarkaL thamakku,
piNiyozhith amararp peru visumbu aruLum paeraruLaaLan emperumaan,
aNimalark kuzhalaar arambaiyar thukilum aaramum vaarivanthu,
aNineer maNikozhith thizhintha gangkaiyin karaimael vadhariyaaschiraamaththuLLaanae.
14.meaning: O my heart, I will now tell you with certainty: He is our Lord of boundless grace who, removing all afflictions, grants the great heaven of the immortals to the devotees who rise up worshipping Him. On the bank of the beautiful-watered Ganga — which comes sweeping along the garments and pearl-strings of the celestial damsels with flowers in their lovely tresses, and tumbles down sifting out gems — He dwells in Badarikashrama.

15.translit:
paeyiDaik kirunthu vantha maRRavaLthan perumulai suvaiththiDa peRRa
thaayiDaik kiruththal anjsuvan enru thaLarnthiDa vaLarntha venthalaivan,
saeymukaTTuch chiyaNDamum sumantha semponsey vilangkalil ilangku,
vaaymukaTTizhintha gangkaiyin karaimael vadhariyaaschiraamaththuLLaanae.
15.meaning: He is the fierce Lord who grew up after suckling the great breast of that demoness Putana who came in disguise — saying as it were, 'I dread to remain in the lap of the mother who bore me,' so that she withered away. On the bank of the Ganga that descends from the mouth-summit, shining upon the red-golden mountain that bears up even the far-off vault of heaven, He dwells in Badarikashrama.

16.translit:
thaeraNangku alkul sezhungkayaRkaNNi thiRaththu oru maRaththozhilpurinthu,
paaraNangu imilaeRu aezhum munnaDarttha panimukil vaNNanemperumaan,
kaaraNanthannaal kaDumpunal kayaththa karuvaraipiLavezhak kuththi,
vaaraNangkoNarntha gangkaiyin karaimael vadhariyaaschiraamaththuLLaanae.
16.meaning: For the sake of Nappinnai of the chariot-like waist and lovely carp-eyes, He performed a heroic feat, subduing the seven humped bulls before Him — our Lord, dark as the cool rain-cloud. On the bank of the Ganga, which, by His purpose, He brought down with His conch, the fierce torrent piercing and splitting the deep black mountain in the chasm, He dwells in Badarikashrama.

17.translit:
venthiRal kaLiRum vaelaivaay amuthum viNNoDu viNNavarkkarasum,
indhiraRku aruLi yemakkum eentharuLum enthai emmaDikaL emperumaan,
antharaththu amarar aDi yiNaivaNangka aayiramukaththinaal aruLi,
mantharaththizhintha gangkaiyin karaimael vadhariyaaschiraamaththuLLaanae.
17.meaning: He graciously gave to Indra the fierce mighty elephant (Airavata), the nectar from the ocean's depths, and the lordship of heaven and its dwellers — our Father, our Master, our Lord, who bestows grace upon us too. On the bank of the Ganga that descended through a thousand mouths in His grace, from Mount Mandara, where the celestials of the firmament bow at His twin feet, He dwells in Badarikashrama.

18.translit:
maanmuninthu orukaal varisilai vaLaiththa mannavanpon niRaththuravoan,
oonmunintha vanadhu uDal irupiLavaa ukirnuthi maDuththu, ayan aranaith
thaanmuninthiTTa venthiRal saabam thavirththavan thavampurinthu uyarntha
maamuni koNarntha gangkaiyin karaimael vadhariyaaschiraamaththuLLaanae.
18.meaning: He is the king who once, in anger at the deer, bent His striped bow (as Rama); He who split in two the body of the golden-hued mighty one (Hiranyakashipu) who hated flesh, driving in the points of His claws; and He who removed the fierce curse that Brahma had laid in wrath upon Shiva. On the bank of the Ganga that the great sage (Bhagiratha), exalted by his penance, brought down, He dwells in Badarikashrama.

19.translit:
koNDalmaa ruthangkaL kulavarai thokuneerk kuraikaDal ulaku uDan anaiththum,
uNDamaa vayiRRoaN oNsuDar aeyntha umparum oozhiyumaanaan,
aNDamooDaRuth thanRu antharaththizhinthu angkavaniyaaL alamara, peruku
maNDumaa maNi neerk gangkaiyin karaimael vadhariyaaschiraamaththuLLaanae.
19.meaning: He of the great belly that swallowed the clouds, the winds, the cluster of mountains, the waters, the roaring sea, and the whole world together; He who became the radiant high heaven, its dwellers, and the aeons of time. On the bank of the Ganga of great clear gem-like waters that surge and rush — which once pierced through the cosmic shell and descended through the firmament so that the Earth-goddess was distressed — He dwells in Badarikashrama.

20.translit:
varundhirai maNi neerk kangaiyin karaimEl vadhariyaachchiraamaththuLLaanai,
karungkadal munneer vaNNanai eNNik kaliyan vaay oli seydha panuval,
varanjeydha aindhum aindhum vallaarkaL vaanavar ulagudan maruvi,
irungkadal ulagam aaNdu veN kudaikkeezh imaiyavar aaguvar thaamE.
20.meaning: On the bank of the clear gem-watered Ganga with its surging waves, He abides at Badari (Badarikashrama). Reflecting upon Him who is dark-hued like the deep black ocean, Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) composed this garland of resounding words. Those who master all ten of these grace-laden verses will dwell together in the world of the celestials; ruling this vast sea-girt earth beneath the white parasol, they themselves shall become immortal devas.

21.translit:
paarOr pukazhum vadhari vada madhurai
oor aaya ellaam ozhiyaamE naan avanai
oor aanai kombu osiththu Or aanai kOL viduththa
seeraanai sengaN nediyaanai thEn thuzhaayth
thaaraanai thaamarai pOl kaNNaanai eN arunj seer
21.meaning: Without leaving out any of the towns praised by the people of the earth—Badari, northern Mathura, and all the rest—I (seek) Him: the glorious one who broke the tusk of one elephant and released another elephant from its bondage, the red-eyed tall Lord, who wears the honey-laden tulasi garland, whose eyes are like the lotus, and whose glory is hard to measure.

0.translit:
paalaik karandhu adup peeRa vaiththup
 palvaLaiyaaL en magaL iruppa
meelai agaththee neruppu veeNDich chenRu
 iRaip pozhudhu angee peesi ninReen
saaLak giraamam uDaiya nambi
 saayththup parugiTTup poandhu ninRaan
aalaik karumbin mozhi anaiya
 asoadhai nangaay un maganaik kuuvaay.
0.meaning: "I milked the cow and set it on the stove to boil, while my daughter of many bangles sat by. I went off to the house up the street to ask for fire, and stood there talking just for a moment. (Meanwhile) the Lord who owns Salagramam tipped the pot, drank it all up, and now stands there (innocently)! O Yasoda, whose words are as sweet as the sugarcane of the press, call your son!"

1.translit:
vaDadhisai madhurai saaLakkiraamam vaigundham thuvarai ayoadhi
iDamuDai vadhari iDavagai uDaiya emburuDoaththaman irukkai
taDavarai adhirath tharaNi viNDiDiyath thalaippaRRik karaimaram saaDi
kaDalinaik kalangak kaDuththizhi gangaik kaNDam ennum kaDi nagaree.
1.meaning: Mathura in the north, Salagramam, Vaikuntha, Dvaraka, Ayodhya, the vast Badari, and many other places are the abodes of our Supreme Person (Purushottama). But this is the fortified city called Kandam, where the Ganga rushes down so fiercely that the great mountains tremble, the earth splits and resounds, it seizes and uproots the trees on its banks, and churns the very sea into turmoil.

2.translit:
kalaiyum kariyum parimaavum thiriyum kaanam kaDandhu poay,
silaiyum kaNaiyum thuNaiyaagach chenRaan venRich cheRukkaLaththu,
malaikoNDu alai neer aNaikaTTi madhiL neer ilangai vaaLarakkar talaivan,
talaipaththu aRuththu ugandhaan saaLakkiraamam aDai nenjee.
2.meaning: Crossing the forest where stags and elephants and great horses roam, He went with bow and arrow as His only companions. On the victorious battlefield He built a causeway of mountains across the surging sea, and rejoiced to cut off the ten heads of the sword-wielding demon-lord of moat-girt Lanka. O my heart, reach Salagramam!

3.translit:
kaDam suuzh kariyum parimaavum oli maandheerum kaalaaLum,
uDan suuzhndhu ezhundha kaDi ilangai poDiya vaDivaay charam thurandhaan,
iDam suuzhndhu engum iru visumbil imaiyoar vaNanga maNam kamazhum,
taDam suuzhndhu engum azhagaaya saaLakkiraamam aDai nenjee.
3.meaning: He loosed His arrows so that fortified Lanka, surrounded and risen up with rut-flowing elephants and horses, resounding chariots and foot-soldiers, was ground to powder. O my heart, reach Salagramam, fragrant and beautiful everywhere, ringed by broad ponds, where the celestials of the wide heavens gather on every side and bow down.

4.translit:
ulavu thiraiyum kulavaraiyum oozhi mudhalaa eN dhikkum,
nilavum suDarum iruLumaay ninRaan venRi viRal aazhi valavan
vaanoar tham perumaan maruvaa varakkarkku eññaanRum
salavan, salam suuzhndhu azhagaaya saaLakkiraamam aDai nenjee.
4.meaning: He stands as the rolling waves, the great mountains, the eight directions from the time of the deluge, as the moonlight, the sunlight and the darkness. He is the wielder of the victorious, valiant discus, the Lord of the celestials. To the demons who would not embrace Him He is forever an adversary. O my heart, reach Salagramam, beautiful and surrounded by waters.

5.translit:
uuraam kuDandhai uththaman orukaal irukaal silai vaLaiya,
theeraa varakkar theer veLLam seRRaan vaRRaa varupunal suuzh peeraan,
peeraayiram uDaiyaan piRangu siRai vaNDu aRaiginRa thaaraan
thaaraa vayal suuzhndha saaLakkiraamam aDai nenjee.
5.meaning: He is the Supreme One whose town is Kudandai (Kumbakonam); bending His bow once, then twice, He destroyed the flood of chariots of the relentless demons. He dwells at Peraan, surrounded by ever-flowing waters; He of the thousand names, wearing the garland on which bees with bright wings hum. O my heart, reach Salagramam, ringed by fields where teal-ducks (thaaraa) gather.

6.translit:
aDuththu aarththu ezhundhaaL pilavaay viTTu alaRa avaL mukku ayil vaaLaal
viDuththaan, viLangu suDar aazhi viNNoar perumaan naNNaar mun
kaDuththu aarththu ezhundha peru mazhaiyaik kal onRu eandhi ina nirai kaaththu
taDuththaan taDam suuzhndhu azhagaaya saaLakkiraamam aDai nenjee.
6.meaning: When she (Surpanakha) rushed up roaring, her cavernous mouth gaping in a shriek, He cut off her nose with His sharp sword. He is the Lord of the celestials who bears the shining radiant discus. Before His foes, when the great rain rose up roaring furiously, He lifted up a single hill and held back (the storm), guarding the herds of cattle. O my heart, reach Salagramam, beautiful and ringed by broad ponds.

7.translit:
taayaay vandha paey uyirum thayirum vizhudhum uDan uNDa vaayaan
thuuya ari uruvil kuRaLaay chenRu maavalaiyai eyaan irappa
muuvaDi maNNinRu eedhaa enRu ulagu eezhum taayaan
kaayaa malar vaNNan saaLakkiraamam aDai nenjee.
7.meaning: With the mouth that drank the life of the demoness who came in the guise of a mother (Putana), and that also ate the curds and butter, He of the pure form. As a dwarf of the lion-clan He went, and unfailingly begged of Mahabali, asking "Grant me three feet of earth," and then strode across all seven worlds. O my heart, reach Salagramam (the abode) of the Lord whose hue is the kaaya blossom (dark blue).

8.translit:
eenoar añja venjamaththuL ariyaayp pariya iraNiyanai,
uunaar agalam piLavu eDuththa oruvan thaanee iru suDaraay,
vaanaay theeyaay maaruthamaay malaiyaay alai neer ulagu anaiththum thaanaay
thaanum maanaan tan saaLakkiraamam aDai nenjee.
8.meaning: As the Man-Lion in the fierce battle, while all others trembled, He alone tore open the flesh-filled broad chest of the mighty Hiranya. He Himself became the two luminaries (sun and moon), the sky, fire, wind, mountains, the surging waters, and all the worlds; He who is the measureless One Himself. O my heart, reach His Salagramam!

9.translit:
vendhaar enbum suDu neeRum meyyil puusik kai agaththu oar
sandhaar talai koNDu ulagu eezhum thiriyum periyoan thaan chenRu, en
endhaay! saabam theer enna ilangu amudha neer thirumaarbil tandhaan,
sandhaar pozhil suuzhndha saaLakkiraamam aDai nenjee!
9.meaning: The great one (Siva) who smears burnt bones and hot ashes upon his body, and roams the seven worlds bearing in his hand a skull (the head of Brahma) - when he came and pleaded, "O my Father, remove my curse!", the Lord granted (relief) from the shining nectar-water of His sacred chest. O my heart, reach Salagramam, ringed by sandalwood-filled groves!

10.translit:
thoNDaam inamum imaiyoarum thuNai nuul maarbin andhaNarum,
aNDaa emakkee aruLaay enRu aNaiyum koayil arugu ellaam,
vaNDu aar pozhilin pazhanaththu vayalin ayalee kayal paaya,
taN taamaraigaL mugam malarththum saaLakkiraamam aDai nenjee.
10.meaning: The hosts of devotees, the celestials, and the brahmins with the sacred thread on their breasts draw near (His) temple, praying, "O Lord, grace us alone!" All around it, in the watered fields beside the bee-filled groves, carp leap, and the cool lotuses open their faces (blooms). O my heart, reach Salagramam!

11.translit:
thaaraa vaarum vayal suuzhndha saaLakkiraamath aDigaLai,
kaaraar puRavin mangai veendhan kaliyan oli sey tamizh maalai,
aaraar ulagaththu aRivuDaiyaar amarar nannaaTTu arasaaLa,
peeraayiramum oadhum ingaL anRi ivaiyee pidhaRRuminee.
11.meaning: This garland of melodious Tamil, composed by Kaliyan (Tirumangai), king of Mangai with its cloud-touching pasture-lands, on the Lord of Salagramam ringed by fields where teal-ducks throng - whoever among the discerning ones in this whole world would rule over the good land of the immortals (Paramapada), recite His thousand names, or else go on murmuring these very verses.

0.translit:
uRiyai muRRattu uruTTi ninRu aaduvaar
naRuney paal-tayir nanRaagat thoovuvaar
seRimen koondhal avizhat thiLaiththu engum
aRiv azhindhanar aayppaadi yaayare.
0.meaning: They roll the hanging milk-pots about the courtyard and dance there, scattering fragrant ghee, milk and curd in plenty. Their thick, soft tresses come loose as they revel everywhere, and the cowherd folk of Ayarpadi (Gokula) lost all their senses in joy.

1.translit:
aTTuk kuvi-sotRup paruppadhamum
 tayir-vaaviyum ney-yaLaRum adangap
poTTat thutRu maarip-pagai puNarththa
 porumaa kadal-vaNNan poRuththa malai
vaTTat thadangaN madamaan kanRinai
 valaivaayp patRik koNdu kuRa-magaLir
koTTaith thalaip-paal koduththu vaLarkkum
 kovarththanam ennum kotRak kudaiye.
1.meaning: The mountain-like-a-victory-umbrella that the ocean-hued Lord, who waged war on Indra the rain-foe, lifted up so that the heaped cooked rice resembling a mountain of dal, the lake of curd and the mire of ghee were all swallowed up by the people: this is Govardhana, the victorious parasol, where the hill-women catch in their nets the round wide-eyed fawn of a tender doe and rear it, feeding it milk from a coconut shell.

2.translit:
vazhuvonRum ilaac cheygai-vaanavar-kon
 valippaT-tu munindhu vidukkappaTTu
mazhai-vandhu ezhu-naaL peydhu maath-thaduppa
 madhusoodhan eduththu maRiththa malai
izhavu thariyaadhadhu oor eetRup-pidi
 iLanjeeyam thodarndhu mudukudhalum
kuzhavi idaik-kaal iTTu edhirndhu porum
 kovarththanam ennum kotRak kudaiye.
2.meaning: When the king of the flawless-deeded celestials (Indra), grown wrathful and pressed by force, was let loose so that storm-clouds came and rained for seven days to block the cattle, Madhusudana lifted up and held aloft the mountain. This is Govardhana, the victorious parasol, where a mother she-elephant unable to bear her loss, when a young lion follows and presses upon her, thrusts her calf between her legs and turns to fight it.

3.translit:
ammaith thadangaN madavaay-chchiyarum
 aanaayarum aa-niraiyum alaRi
emmaic charaN enRu koL enRu irappa
 ilangaazhik-kai endhai eduththa malai
thammaic charaN enRa tham-paavaiyaraip
 punam meyginRa maaninam kaaNmin enRu
kommaip puyak-kunRar silai kunikkum
 kovarththanam ennum kotRak kudaiye.
3.meaning: The mountain that my Father, of the hand bearing the gleaming discus, lifted up when the lovely wide-eyed gentle cowherd women, the cowherds, and the herds of cattle all cried out begging 'Take us as your refuge!': this is Govardhana, the victorious parasol, where the hill-men with shoulders like mounds bend their bows, saying to their women who took them as refuge, 'Behold the herd of deer grazing in the fields!'

4.translit:
kaduvaayc china-veng-kaN kaLitRinukkuk
 kavaLam eduththuk koduppaanavan pol
adivaay uRak-kai iTTu ezhap paRiththiTTu
 amarar perumaan koNdu ninRa malai
kadalvaayc chenRu megam kavizhndhu irangik
 kadhuvaayp pada-neer mugandhu eRi engum
kudavaayp pada ninRu mazhai pozhiyum
 kovarththanam ennum kotRak kudaiye.
4.meaning: The mountain that the Lord of the immortals plucked up and held, thrusting his hand deep to its base and raising it, like one who lifts a ball of rice to feed a furious red-eyed tusker elephant: this is Govardhana, the victorious parasol, where the clouds go to the sea, bend down and dip low, scooping up water at its mouth, then rise and stand pouring down rain everywhere like an overturned pot.

5.translit:
vaanaththil uLLeer valiyeer uLLeerel
 aRaiyo vandhu vaangumin enbavan pol
eenath-thuru aagiya eesan endhai
 idavan ezha vaangi eduththa malai
kaanak kaLi-yaanai-than-kombu izhandhu
 kadhuvaay madham sora than-kai eduththu
koonal piRai veNdi aNNaandhu niRkum
 kovarththanam ennum kotRak kudaiye.
5.meaning: The mountain that my Father, the Lord who took the form of the boar (Varaha), lifted up easily with his left side, as if challenging, 'You up in the heavens, if any of you are strong, come forth and snatch it away!': this is Govardhana, the victorious parasol, where the wild rutting elephant of the forest, having lost its tusk, with rut flowing from its cheek, raises its trunk and stands gazing upward longing for the crescent moon.

6.translit:
seppaadu udaiya thirumaal avan-than
 sendhaamaraik-kai viral aindhinaiyum
kappaagam aduththu maNi-nedun-thoL
 kaambaagak koduththuk kaviththa malai
eppaadum parandhu izhi-theL-aruvi
 ilangu maNi-muthth-uvadam piRazha
kuppaayam ena ninRu kaaTchi-tharum
 kovarththanam ennum kotRak kudaiye.
6.meaning: The mountain that gracious Tirumal, of measured speech, covered like an umbrella, fitting the five fingers of his red-lotus hand to its rim and giving his long jewel-like shoulders as its supporting pole: this is Govardhana, the victorious parasol, where clear cascades spread and fall on every side, the bright gem-and-pearl strands gleaming, so that it stands giving the sight of a jacket (worn over the hill).

7.translit:
padangaL palavum udaip-paamp-araiyan
 padar-boomiyaith thaangik kidappavan pol
thadangai viral-aindhum malar avaiththuth
 thaamodharan thaangu thada-varai-thaan
adangac chenRu ilangaiyai eed-azhiththa
 anuman pugazh paadit tham-kuTTangaLai
kudangaik koNdu mandhigaL kaN-vaLarththum
 kovarththanam ennum kotRak kudaiye.
7.meaning: The broad mountain that Damodara held, spreading out the five fingers of his large hand like blossoms, like the king of serpents with many hoods who lies bearing up the wide earth: this is Govardhana, the victorious parasol, where the female monkeys sing the praise of Hanuman who went and utterly destroyed Lanka, and cradling their little ones in their palms lull them to sleep.

8.translit:
salamaa-mugil pal-kaNap-poork-kaLaththuc
 charamaari pozhindhu engum poosaliTTu
nalivaan uRak-kedagam koppavan pol
 naaraayaNan mun mugam kaaththa malai
ilai-vey kurambaith thavamaa munivar
 irundhaar naduve senRu aNaar soriya
kolaivaayc china-vengaigaL ninRu uRangum
 kovarththanam ennum kotRak kudaiye.
8.meaning: The mountain that Narayana raised before himself to guard his face, like one holding a shield to ward off the assault, as the many ranks of dark battle-clouds poured down arrow-showers of rain everywhere with clamour, intent on destruction: this is Govardhana, the victorious parasol, where the murderous fierce tigers go and stand still among the great ascetic sages seated in their leaf-thatched huts, scratching their jaws (in tameness).

9.translit:
vanpey mulaiyuNdadhu or-vaay udaiyan
 van-thooN ena ninRadhu or-van-paraththai
than-per iTTuk-koNdu tharaNi-thannil
 thaamodharan thaangu thada-varai-thaan
munpe vazhi-kaaTTa musuk-kaNangaL
 mudhugil peydhu tham-mudaik-kuTTangaLai
kombu eRRi irundhu kudhi payitRum
 kovarththanam ennum kotRak kudaiye.
9.meaning: The broad mountain that Damodara bears up on this earth, calling it by his own name, he of the mouth that sucked the breast of the cruel demoness, the mighty strong one who once stood firm like a stout pillar: this is Govardhana, the victorious parasol, where, the leader monkeys going ahead to show the way, the troops of monkeys, carrying their own little ones on their backs, perch on the branches and train them to leap.

10.translit:
kodiyeRu sendhaamaraik-kai viralgaL
 kolamum azhindhila vaadiRRila
vadiveRu thiru-ugir nondhu mila
 maNivaNNan malaiyum or-sambiradham
mudiyeRiya maamugil-pal-kaNangaL
 mun-netRi naraiththana pola engum
kudiyeRi irundhu mazhai pozhiyum
 kovarththanam ennum kotRak kudaiye.
10.meaning: The fingers of his red-lotus hand, lovely like climbing creepers, did not lose their beauty nor wilt; his beautiful sacred nails did not ache or fade — to the gem-hued Lord even lifting the mountain was but a sport. This is Govardhana, the victorious parasol, where the many ranks of great clouds that mount its summit, as if its brow had turned grey, settle and dwell everywhere and pour down rain.

11.translit:
ara_vil paLLi-koNdu aravam thurandhiTTu
 aravap-pagai oordhiya-vanudaiya
kuravil kodi-mullaigaL ninRu uRangum
 kovarththanam ennum kotRak kudaimel
thiruvil poli-maa-maRai-vaaNar puththoorth
 thigazh paTTar-piraan sonna maalai paththum
paravu mana-nanku udaip-paththar uLLaar
 parama_aanai vaikundham naNNuvare.
11.meaning: Of him who reclines upon the serpent (Adisesha), who drove off the serpent (Kaliya), and who rides the serpent's foe (Garuda) — upon his Govardhana, the victorious parasol, where the bittergourd-creepers and jasmine vines stand and slumber: the ten garland-verses spoken by the glorious Bhattarpiran of Puduvai, abode of the great Vedic brahmins resplendent with prosperity. Those devotees who hold these well in their adoring minds will surely attain the supreme Vaikuntha.

12.translit:
vaan-iLa-varasu vaikundhak-kuTTan
 vaasudevan madhurai-mannan nandha
kon-iLa-varasu kovalar kuTTan
 kovindhan kuzhal-kodu oodhina podhu
vaan-iLam-padiyar vandhu-vandhu eeNdi
 manam urugi malark-kaNgaL panippa
then-aLavu seRi koondhal avizhac
 chenni-verppac chevi serththu ninRanare.
12.meaning: The young prince of heaven, the little child of Vaikuntha, Vasudeva (Krishna), king of Mathura, the young prince of king Nanda, the cowherds' little one, Govinda — when he played upon his flute, the young women of heaven came thronging again and again, their hearts melting and their flower-like eyes brimming with tears, their honey-laden thick tresses coming loose, their brows breaking into sweat, and they stood with ears attentive to the sound.

13.translit:
aanaayar koodi amaiththa vizhavai amarar-tham
konaark-kozhiyak kovarththanaththuc cheydhaan malai
vaanaaTTil ninRu maa-malark-kaRpagath thoththu izhi
thenaaRu paayum then-thiru-maaliruny cholaiye.
13.meaning: The mountain belonging to him who, withholding from the king of the immortals (Indra) the festival the cowherds had gathered and prepared, performed it instead at Govardhana: it is the southern Tirumalirunjolai, where rivers of honey pour down, dripping from the clusters of great blossoms of the Karpaka tree as if come down from the heavenly world.

14.translit:
vada-thisai madhurai saaLakkiraamam vaikundham thuvarai ayodhdhi
idam-udai vadhari idavagai udaiya em-purudoththaman irukkai
thadavarai adhirath tharaNi viNdu idiyath thalaip-patRik karai-maram saadi
kadalinaik kalangak kaduththizhi gangaik kaNdam ennum kadi-nagare.
14.meaning: Northern Mathura, Salagrama, Vaikuntha, Dvaraka, Ayodhya, the spacious Badari — these are the abodes of our Purushottama who possesses every place; and so too is the well-guarded city called Kandam (Tirukkandam), where the rushing Ganga descends, making the great hills tremble and the earth split and crash, seizing and felling the trees on its banks and churning the sea into turmoil.

15.translit:
naan edhum un-maayam onRu aRiyen naman-thamar patRi nalindhiTTu indha
oone pugey enRu modhum podhu angu edhum naan unnai ninaikka maaTTen
vaaney vaanavar-tangaL eesaa madhuraip piRandha maa-maayane en
aanaay nee ennaik kaakka veNdum arangath aravaNaip paLLiyaane.
15.meaning: I know nothing at all of your wondrous ways; when the servants of Yama seize me, torment me, and beating me cry 'Enter back into this body of flesh!' — at that moment I shall not be able to think of you at all. O Lord of the heavenly celestials, great wondrous one born in Mathura, my cowherd, you alone must protect me, O you who recline on the serpent-couch at Srirangam!

16.translit:
maayanai mannu vada-madhurai maindhanaith
thooya peru-neer yamunaith thuRaivanai
aayar kulaththinil thonRum aNi-viLakkaith
thaayaik kudal-viLakkam seydha thaamodharanaith
thooyomaay vandhu naam thoo-malar thoovith thozhudhu
vaayinaal paadi manaththinaal sindhikkap
poya pizhaiyum pugu-tharuvaan ninRanavum
theeyinil thoosaagum seppel or em-paavaay.
16.meaning: The wondrous one, the prince of eternal northern Mathura, the dweller on the banks of the holy abundant Yamuna, the radiant lamp arisen in the clan of cowherds, Damodara who brought glory to his mother's womb — if we come purified and worship him, scattering fresh flowers, singing his praise with our mouths and meditating on him with our minds, the wrongs we have done that are past and those yet to come will become like cotton-wisps cast into fire. Declare this, O our maiden!

17.translit:
maada maaLigai soozh-madhu raippadhi
naadi nan-theruvin naduve vandhiTTu
oodai maa-madha-yaanai udhaiththavan
koodumaagil nee koodidu koodale.
17.meaning: He who kicked down the great rutting elephant (Kuvalayapida), coming and standing in the very middle of our street in the city of Mathura girt with mansions and palaces — O koodal (love-divination circle), if it be that he will join with us, then join and show that he will come!

18.translit:
atRavan marudham muRiya nadai
katRavan kanjanai vanjanaiyinaal
setRavan thigazhum madhu raippadhi
kotRavan varil koodidu koodale.
18.meaning: He who, while learning to walk, snapped the marudha trees; he who destroyed Kamsa by stratagem; the victorious lord of the resplendent city of Mathura — O koodal, if he will come, then join and show it!

19.translit:
kadhir-oLi deepam kalasam udane endhi
sadhir-iLa-mangaiyar thaam vandhu edhir-koLLa
madhuraiyaar mannan adi-nilai thoTTu engum
adhirap pugudhak kanaak-kaNden thozhee naan.
19.meaning: O friend, I saw in a dream the king of the people of Mathura entering, while graceful young women came bearing radiant lamps and ceremonial pots to receive him — his footsteps falling so that everywhere resounded and trembled!

20.translit:
thaDavarai yinmeedhE saraRkaala chandiran
iDaiyuvaa vilvandhe zhundhaalE pOl neeyum
vaDamadhurai yaarmannan vaasudhE van kaiyil
kuDiyERi veeRRirundhaay kOlappe runjsangE
20.meaning: O beautiful great conch! Like the autumn moon that rises in the dark fortnight over the high mountain, you too have risen up and taken your seat, dwelling proudly in the hand of Vasudeva, the king of those in north Mathura. You have made your home there and reside in glory.

21.translit:
maRRirun dheerkaT kaRiyalaagaa maadhava nenbadhO ranbudhannai
uRRirun dhEnuk kuraippadhellaam oomaiya rODu seviDarvaarththai
peRRirun dhaaLai yozhiyavEpOyp pErththoru thaayil vaLarndhanambi
maRporun dhaamaR kaLamaDaindha madhuraip puRaththennai yuyththiDumin
21.meaning: To you others who remain detached, what I, who have come to bear a singular love for Madhava, say is unknowable. To speak of it to you is like talking to the deaf and dumb. Carry me out and set me down in the outskirts of Mathura, where the perfect Lord, leaving aside the mother who bore him to be raised by another mother, entered the wrestling arena before the wrestlers could close in.

22.translit:
kaRRinam mEykkilum mEykkappeRRaan kaaDuvaazh saadhiyu maagappeRRaan
paRRiyuraliDai yaappumuNDaan paavikaaL ungaLuk kEchchukkolO
kaRRana pEsi vasaiyuNaadhE kaalika Luyya mazhaithaDuththu
koRRak kuDaiyaaga vEndhinindra kOvarth thanaththennai yuyththiDumin
22.meaning: Even if he grazed the calves and cows, he gained that as his lot; he also gained birth in the forest-dwelling clan. He even let himself be caught and bound to the mortar. O sinful ones, is this a matter for your mockery? Do not speak the rehearsed slander and earn reproach; carry me and set me down at Govardhana, where he stood holding up the mountain as a victory-umbrella, stopping the rain so that the cattle might be saved.

23.translit:
uLLE yurugi naivEnai uLaLO ilaLO vennaadha
koLLai koLLik kuRumbanaik kOvarth thananaik kaNDakkaal
koLLum payanon Rillaadha kongai thannaik kizhangODum
aLLip paRiththiT Tavanmaarvil eRindhen azhalai theervEnE
23.meaning: He robs me wholesale and never asks whether I, melting and wasting away within, am alive or not — that mischievous one, the Lord of Govardhana. If I should ever behold him, I would tear out, roots and all, this bosom of mine that serves no purpose, and fling it upon his chest, and so be rid of my burning anguish.

24.translit:
paTTi mEyndhOr kaarERu paladhE vaRkOr keezhkkanRaay
iTTee RiTTu viLaiyaaDi ingE pOdhak kaNDeerE
iTTa maana pasukkaLai inidhu maRiththu neerooTTi
viTTuk koNDu viLaiyaaDa virundhaa vanaththE kaNDOmE
24.meaning: Have you seen the dark bull that grazed freely on the common pasture, who as a younger calf-brother to Baladeva came playing this way, raising the dust? — Yes, we saw him in Brindavan, gently turning back the cows he loves, giving them water to drink, and then letting them loose to roam while he sported with them.

25.translit:
anunga vennaip pirivusey thaayar paaDi kavarndhuNNum
kuNungu naaRik kuTTERRaik kOvarth thananaik kaNDeerE
kaNanga LODu minmEgam kalandhaaR pOla vanamaalai
minunga ninRu viLaiyaaDa virundhaa vanaththE kaNDOmE
25.meaning: Have you seen the young bull who left me to languish in separation, who plunders and devours from the cowherd village of Ayarpadi, smelling of the herd — the Lord of Govardhana? Yes, we saw him in Brindavan, standing and sporting while his wild-flower garland glittered, as though lightning had mingled together with massed clouds.

26.translit:
anunga vennaip pirivusey thaayar paaDi kavarndhuNNum
kuNungu naaRik kuTTERRaik kOvarth thananaik kaNDeerE
kaNanga LODu minmEgam kalandhaaR pOla vanamaalai
minunga ninRu viLaiyaaDa virundhaa vanaththE kaNDOmE
26.meaning: Have you seen the young bull who left me to languish in separation, who plunders and devours from the cowherd village of Ayarpadi, smelling of the herd — the Lord of Govardhana? Yes, we saw him in Brindavan, standing and sporting while his wild-flower garland glittered, as though lightning had mingled together with massed clouds.

27.translit:
maalaayp piRandha nambiyai maalE seyyum maNaaLanai
Elaap poyka Luraippaanai ingE pOdhak kaNDeerE
mElaal parandha veyilkaappaan vinadhai siRuvan siRagennum
mElaap pinkeezh varuvaanai virundhaa vanaththE kaNDOmE
27.meaning: Have you seen come this way the perfect Lord born as Tirumal, the bridegroom who casts a spell of love, who tells unconvincing untruths? — Yes, we saw him in Brindavan, coming beneath the canopy of the wings of Vinata's son (Garuda), spread out above to shield him from the sun blazing overhead.

28.translit:
kaarththaN kamalak kaNNennum neDungayi RupaDuththi ennai
eerththuk koNDu viLaiyaaDum eesan RannaikkaNDeerE
pOrththa muththin kuppaayap pugarmaal yaanaik kanREpOl
vErththu ninRu viLaiyaaDa virundhaa vanaththE kaNDOmE
28.meaning: Have you seen that Lord who, casting the long rope that is his cool dark lotus eyes, draws me to himself and sports with me? — Yes, we saw him in Brindavan, standing and playing, sweating, like a young spotted bull-elephant draped in a coat of pearls.

29.translit:
maadha vanen maNiyinai valaiyil pizhaiththa panRipOl
Edhu monRum koLaththaaraa eesan RannaikkaNDeerE
peedhaga vaaDai yuDaithaazhap perungaar mEgak kanREpOl
veedhi yaara varuvaanai virundhaa vanaththE kaNDOmE
29.meaning: Have you seen Madhava, my jewel, that Lord who, like a boar that has escaped the net, yields nothing to anyone's grasp? — Yes, we saw him in Brindavan, coming to fill the whole street, like a young calf of the great dark monsoon cloud, his yellow silk garment hanging low.

30.translit:
tharuma maRiyaak kuRumbanaith thangaich saarnga madhuvEpOl
puruva vaTTa mazhagiya poruththa miliyaik kaNDeerE uruvu
karidhaay mugamseydhaay udhayap paruppa thaththinmEl
viriyum kadhirE pOlvaanai virundhaa vanaththE kaNDOmE
30.meaning: Have you seen the mischievous one who knows no dharma, the unfitting one whose lovely arched brows curve like his own Saranga bow? — Though dark of form, with a radiant face, we saw him in Brindavan, like the sun spreading its rays over the mountain of dawn.

31.translit:
poruththa muDaiya nambiyaip puRambO luLLum kariyaanai
karuththaip pizhaiththu ninRaak karumaa mugilaik kaNDeerE
aruththith thaaraa kaNangaLaal aarap perugu vaanampOl
viruththam peridhaay varuvaanai virundhaa vanaththE kaNDOmE
31.meaning: Have you seen the well-fitted perfect Lord, dark within as he is dark without, that great black cloud who stands beyond the reach of my thoughts? — Yes, we saw him in Brindavan, coming grown vast and full, like the sky brimming and swelling with longed-for clusters of stars.

32.translit:
veLiya sangon RuDaiyaanaip peedhaga vaaDai yuDaiyaanai
aLinan kuDaiya thirumaalai aazhi yaanaik kaNDeerE
kaLivaN Dengum kalandhaaRpOl kazhampoong kuzhalkaL thaDandhOLmEl
miLira ninRu viLaiyaaDa virundhaa vanaththE kaNDOmE
32.meaning: Have you seen him who bears the white conch, who wears the yellow silk, the gracious Tirumal, the bearer of the discus? — Yes, we saw him in Brindavan, standing and sporting while his fragrant flower-decked locks shimmered over his broad shoulders, as though honey-bees had mingled everywhere.

33.translit:
parundhaaT kaLiRRuk karuLseydha paraman Rannai paarinmEl
virundhaa vanaththE kaNDamai viTTu sciththan kOdhaisol
marundhaa menRu thammanaththE vaiththuk koNDu vaazhvaarkaL
perundhaa LuDaiya piraanaDikkeezhp piriyaa dhenRu mirupaarE
33.meaning: These words of Kodhai, daughter of Vishnuchitta (Periyalvar), telling how she beheld in Brindavan upon this earth the Supreme Lord who showed grace to the elephant with sturdy feet — those who hold them in their hearts as a healing medicine and live by them shall remain forever, never parting, at the feet of the Lord whose great feet are their refuge.

34.translit:
naaTTaip paDai enRu ayan mudhalaath thandha naLir maa malar undhi
veeTTaip paNNi viLaiyaaDum vimalandhannaik kaNDeerE?
kaaTTai naaDith thEnuganum kaLiRum puLLum uDan maDiya
vETTaiyaaDi varuvaanai virundhaavanaththE kaNDOmE
34.meaning: Have you seen the immaculate Lord, who from his cool great-flowered navel gave forth Brahma and the rest, bidding them 'create the worlds,' and who plays at building dwellings? — Yes, we saw him in Brindavan, coming back from the hunt, having sought through the forest and laid low together Dhenuka, the elephant (Kuvalayapida), and the bird (Bakasura).

35.translit:
vaLavezhum thavaLa maaDa madhuraimaa nagaran dhannuL
kavaLamaal yaanai konRa kaNNanai aranga maalai
thuvaLaththoN Daaya tholseerth thoNDara DippoDisol
iLaiyapun kavidhai yElum embiRaar kiniya vaaRE
35.meaning: Within the great city of Mathura, rich and rising with white mansions, Kannan slew the rutting great elephant. These words of Thondaradippodi, of ancient excellence, devoted in pining service to that Lord of Srirangam — though they be young and humble verse, how sweet they are to our Lord!

36.translit:
villaar vizhavil vaDamadhurai virumbi virumbaa malladarththu
kallaar thiraDOL kanjsanaik kaayndhaan paayndhaan kaaLiyanmEl
sollaar suruthi muRaiyOdhich sOmuch seyyum thozhilinOr
nallaar maRaiyOr palarvaazhum naRaiyoor ninRa nambiyE
36.meaning: At the festival of the bow in north Mathura he, willing yet unwilling, crushed the wrestlers, and was wrathful with Kamsa whose rounded shoulders were hard as stone; he leapt upon Kaliya the serpent. He is the perfect Lord who stands at Naraiyur, where many good and noble Brahmins dwell, reciting the Vedas in due order with words and performing the Soma sacrifices.

37.translit:
mannu madhurai vasudhEvar vaazhmudhalai
nannaRaiyoor ninRa nambiyai vambavizhthaar
kannavilum thOLaan kaliya nolivallaar
ponnulagil vaanavarkkup puththELi raaguvarE
37.meaning: Those who can master the resounding verses of Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alvar) — whose shoulders are strong as stone and who wears a garland breathing fresh fragrance — about the perfect Lord who stands at good Naraiyur, the foundation of life for Vasudeva of enduring Mathura, shall become gods even above the celestials in the golden world (Vaikunta).

38.translit:
nEsami laadhavark kumninai yaadhavark kummariyaan,
vaasama larppozhil soozhvaDa maamadhu raippiRandhaan,
dhEsamel laamvaNang kumthiru maalirunj sOlaininRa,
kEsava nambidhan naikkeNDai yoNkaNNi kaaNungolO
38.meaning: He is hard to attain for those without love and for those who do not contemplate him; he was born in great north Mathura, ringed by groves of fragrant blossoms. Will the maiden with bright carp-shaped eyes ever behold Kesava, the perfect Lord who stands at Tirumalirunsolai, worshipped by all the lands?

39.translit:
paarOr pugazhum vadhari vaDamadhurai
oor aaya ellaam ozhiyaamE naan avanai
Or aanai kombu osiththu Or aanai kOLviDuththa
seeraanai sengaN neDiyaanai thEn thuzhaayth
thaaraanai thaamarai pOl kaNNaanai eN arunj seer
39.meaning: Without leaving out any of the towns — Badari praised by the people of earth, north Mathura, and all the rest — I think of him: the glorious one who broke off the tusk of one elephant (Kuvalayapida) and freed another elephant (Gajendra) from its peril; the tall red-eyed Lord, wearer of the garland of honeyed tulasi, whose eyes are like the lotus, of excellences beyond reckoning.

40.translit:
vaaykkungkol nichchalum eppozhu thummanaththu eengu ninaikkappeRa
vaaykkum karumbum perunjchen nelumvayal soozhthiru vaaRanviLai,
vaaykkum perumpugazh moovula geesan vadamadhu raippiRandha,
vaaykkum maNiniRak kaNNabi raanRan maladip pOdhugaLE.
40.meaning: Will it be granted to me daily, at every moment, to meditate here in my mind upon the lotus-flower feet of Kannan, the gem-hued Lord, who was born in northern Mathura, the Lord of the three worlds of abounding fame, who dwells in Tiruvaaranvilai surrounded by fields of sweet sugarcane and tall red paddy? May such a blessing come to me.

41.translit:
idhuvO poruththam minnaazhip padaiyaay ERum irunjchichiRaippuL,
adhuvE kodiyaa vuyarththaanE! enRen REngi yazhudhakkaal,
edhuvE yaagak karudhungkol immaa njaalam poRaitheerppaan,
madhuvaar sOlai yuththara madhuraip piRandha maayanE?
41.meaning: O Lord who wields the gleaming discus weapon, O Lord who raised high as His banner that bird (Garuda) with mighty wings! When I cry out and grieve, calling 'O You who bears that as Your emblem!'—is this fitting? What will the wondrous Lord, born in Uttara (northern) Mathura amid honey-dripping groves, who comes to relieve this great earth of its burden, deem proper to do for me?

42.translit:
koNda peNdir makkaL uRRaar suRRaththavar piRarum
kaNdadhOdu pattadhallaal kaadhal maRRiyadhum illai
eNdisaiyum keezhum mElum muRRava muNda piraan
thoNdarOmaay uyyalallaal illai kaNdeer thuNaiyE.
42.meaning: The wife one has taken, children, kinsmen, relatives, and others—their love extends no further than what is seen and gained in the moment, nothing more. See, there is no refuge for us other than to become servants of the Lord who swallowed up entirely the eight directions, the worlds below and above, and so be saved.

43.translit:
thuNaiyum saarvum aaguvaarpOl suRRaththavar piRarum
aNaiya vandha vaakkam uNdEl attaigaLpOl suvaippar
kaNaiyonRalE Ezhmaramum eydha engaarmugilE
puNaiyenRu uyyappOgilallaal illai kaNdeer poruLE.
43.meaning: Kinsmen and others who seem as though they would be one's support and refuge—if there is wealth that has come to draw near, they suck at it like leeches. See, there is no true treasure except to take as our raft and be saved by our dark rain-cloud-hued Lord, who pierced the seven trees with a single arrow.

44.translit:
poruLkai uNdaay sellakkaaNil pORRinaR REzhuvar
iruLkoL thunbaththu imaikaaNil ennE enbaarum illai
maruLkoL seykai asurar manga vadamadhuraip piRandhaaRku
aruLkoL aaLaay uyyavallaal illai kaNdeer araNE.
44.meaning: When they see one prospering with wealth in hand, seven such will rise up to praise; but in a moment's darkness of sorrow, there is not one to ask 'What ails you?' See, there is no fortress (no protection) except to become, by His grace, a servant of the Lord born in northern Mathura, who destroyed the deluded-deeded demons, and thereby be saved.

45.translit:
araNamaa vaRRakaalaikku enRenRu amaikkappattaar
iraNangkoNda dhepparaavar inRiyittalum naHdhE
varuNiththennE vadamadhuraip piRandha vanvaN pugazhE
saraNenRu uyyappOgilallaal illai kaNdeer sadhirE.
45.meaning: Those who, for some future time of want, are stored up to be a fortress—what will they ever become? Even when they are absent, it is just the same. Why describe it at length? See, there is no wise course except to take as refuge the great and bounteous glory of the Lord born in northern Mathura, and so be saved.

46.translit:
sadhiramenRu nammaiththaamE sammadhiththin mozhiyaar
madhurabOgam thuRRavarE vaigi maRRonRu uRuvar
adhikoL seykai asurar manga vadamadhuraip piRandhaaRku
edhirkoLLaay uyyalallaal illai kaNdeer inbamE.
46.meaning: Those who flatter us with sweet words, themselves agreeing that we are clever, and who feed upon sweet pleasures—they tarry awhile and then meet some other fate. See, there is no happiness except to go forth to receive the Lord born in northern Mathura, who destroyed the demons of overweening deeds, and so be saved.

47.translit:
illaikaNdeer inbam andhO uLLudhu ninaiyaadhE
thollaiyaargaL eththanaivar thOnRik kazhindhozhindhaar
mallai moodhoor vadamadhuraip piRandhavan vaNpugazhE
solli uyyappOgilallaal maRRonRillai surukkE.
47.meaning: Alas, see, there is no happiness—do not dwell on it without considering deeply. How many ancients have appeared and then passed away and perished! In brief: there is nothing else but to utter the bounteous glory of Him who was born in northern Mathura, the ancient city of abundance, and so be saved.

48.translit:
maRRonRillai surungachchonnOm maanilaththu evvuyirukkum
siRRavENdaa sindhippEyamaiyum kaNdeer kaLandhO
kuRRamanRu engaL peRRaththaayan vadamadhuraip piRandhaan
kuRRimil seerkaRRu vaidhal vaazhdhal kaNdeer guNaiyE.
48.meaning: We have said it in brief: there is nothing else for any living being on this great earth. There is no need to do little things—merely to meditate is enough, see. It is no fault: for us, the cowherd of our cattle, He who was born in northern Mathura—see, to learn and recite His flawless excellence is itself living (true life).

49.translit:
vaazhdhalkaNdeer guNamidhandhO maayavaN dibarali
pOzhdhupOga uLLaniRkum punmaiyilaadhavarkku
vaazhthuNaiyaa vadamadhuraip piRandhavan vaNpugazhE
veezhthuNaiyaapOm idhanil yaadhumillai mikkadhE.
49.meaning: This, see, is the virtuous life—alas! For those without baseness, who remain steadfast in mind as the time passes, the bounteous glory of Him born in northern Mathura becomes the support of their living and the support to which they cling and pass on. There is nothing greater than this.

50.translit:
yaadhumillai mikkadhanil enRenRadhu karudhi
kaadhuseyvaan koodhaiseydhu kadaimuRai vaazhkkaiyumpOm
maadhugilin kodikkoL maada vadamadhuraip piRandha
thaadhusErthOL kaNNanallaal illai kaNdeer saraNE.
50.meaning: Reflecting again and again that 'there is nothing greater than this,' even the ear-adorning, the body-decking lowliest manner of worldly life passes away. See, there is no refuge except Kannan of the pollen-smeared shoulders, who was born in northern Mathura with its mansions bearing banners of fine silk.

51.translit:
kaNNanallaal illai kaNdeer saraN adhuniRka vandhu
maNNinbaaram neekkudhaRkE vadamadhuraip piRandhaan
thiNNamaa nummudaimai uNdEl avanadi sErththu uyminO
eNNavENdaa nummadhaadhum avananRi maRRillaiyE.
51.meaning: See, there is no refuge but Kannan; and beyond that, He was born in northern Mathura precisely to remove the burden of the earth. If you truly have any belonging of your own, unite it with His feet and be saved. You need not reckon—nothing whatever is yours; there is nothing else but Him.

52.translit:
aadhumillai maRRavanil enRadhuvE thuNindhu
thaadhusErthOL kaNNanaik kurugoorch chadagOpan sonna
theedhilaadha voNdamizhgaL ivaiyaayiraththu Lippaththum
Odhavalla piraakkaL nammaiyaaLudaiyaargaL paNdE.
52.meaning: Resolving firmly that 'there is nothing whatever beyond Him,' Satakopan of Kurugur sang of Kannan of the pollen-smeared shoulders. Those great ones who can recite these ten among the thousand flawless, sweet Tamil verses have ever been, from of old, our masters who possess and rule us.

0.translit:
Oodu vaarvizhu vaar ugan thaalippaar
Naadu vaarnambi raanenguR Raanenpaar
Paadu vaarkaLum palpaRai koTTaninRu
Aadu vaarkaLum aayiRRuaayp paadiyae.
0.meaning: Some run about, some fall down, some rejoice and clap; others go searching, asking 'Where has our Lord gone?' Throughout Ayarpadi (the cowherds' village) there are those who sing and those who, beating many drums, stand and dance.

1.translit:
URiyai muRRaththu uruTTininRaaduvaar
NaRuney paalthayir nanRaagath thoovuvaar
SeRimen koonthal avizhath thiLaiththuengum
ARiva zhinthanar aaypaadi yaayarae.
1.meaning: They roll the hanging pots about the courtyard and dance; they happily fling all about the fragrant ghee, milk and curds. Everywhere the cowherds of Ayarpadi, their thick soft tresses coming loose, immerse themselves in delight and lose their senses.

2.translit:
Theeya punthik kanjan unmael sinamudaiyan soorvupaarththu
Maayanthannaal valaippaduk kilvaazhagillaen vaasudaevaa
Thaayarvaaych chol tharumamkaNdaay saaRRicchonnaen paogavaeNdaa
Aayarpaadik kaNiviLakkae amarnthuvanthuen mulaiyuNaayae.
