2.meaning: O Vasudeva, evil-minded Kamsa is full of wrath against you; if, watching for a lapse, he should ensnare you by treachery, I cannot bear to live. A mother's word is righteous duty - heed it; I tell you plainly, do not go. O bright lamp of the cowherds' Ayarpadi, come settle here and drink at my breast.

3.translit:
Mulaiyaethum vaeNdaenen Roodi
 nin kaadhil kadippaip paRiththeRin thiTTu
Malaiyai yeduththu magizhnthukal maari kaaththup
 pasunirai maeyththaay
SilaiyonRu iRuththaay thirivik kiramaa
 thiruvaayar paadip piraanae
Thalainilaap poadhaeun kaadhaip perukkaadhae
 viTTiTTaen kuRRamae yanRae.
3.meaning: Saying 'I want no breast at all,' you run off, snatch and fling away the ornament in your ear; you lifted up the mountain and, rejoicing, held off the hail of stones and grazed the herds of cows. You broke the great bow, O Trivikrama, O Lord of the holy cowherds' Ayarpadi. While you were still an infant I let your ear go un-pierced, and that was my fault, was it not?

4.translit:
Thaaymaar moarviRkap poavar thagappanmaar kaRRaa niRaippinpu poavar
Neeaayp paadiiLang kanni maarkaLai naerpada vaekoNdu poadhi
Kaaivaarkku enRum ugappana vaeseydhu kaNdaar kazhaRath thirivum
Aayaa unnai aRinthukoN daenunakku anjuvan ammam tharavae.
4.meaning: The mothers go off to sell buttermilk, the fathers go off to pen the cows, and meanwhile you go straight to the young maidens of Ayarpadi and carry them away. You always do the very things that vex those who resent you, and roam about so that all who see you reproach you. O cowherd, I have come to know you; I am afraid to give you my breast.

5.translit:
PaRRu manjaL poosip paavai maarodu paadiyil
SiRRil sidhaiththuengum theemai seydhu thiriyaamae
KaRRuth thooLi yudaivaedar kaanidaik kanRinpin
ERRukkuen piLLaiyaip poakkinaen ellae paavamae.
5.meaning: Rather than letting him roam smearing on fragrant turmeric, breaking the little play-houses of the doll-like girls and doing mischief everywhere - why did I send my child after a calf into the forest, the haunt of hunters covered in the dust of cattle? Alas, what a sin!

6.translit:
VaNNak karungkuzhal maadhar vanthualar thooRRida
PaNNip palaseyduip paadi yengum thiriyaamae
KaNNuk kiniyaanaikkaa nadharidaik kanRinpin
eNNaR kariyaanaip poakkinaen ellae paavamae.
6.meaning: Rather than letting him roam everywhere through this village, getting up to all his tricks while the dark-tressed lovely women come and spread their scandalous talk - why did I send him, the one dear to my eyes, the one beyond all reckoning, along the forest path after a calf? Alas, what a sin!

7.translit:
MidaRu mezhumezhuth thoada veNNey vizhungippoay
PadiRu palaseyduip paadi yengum thiriyaamae
KadiRu palathiri kaa nadharidaik kanRinpin
IdaRaen piLLaiyaip poakkinaen ellae paavamae.
7.meaning: Rather than letting him swallow butter so that his throat runs smooth and slippery, and roam everywhere through this village doing many sly deeds - why did I send my child, to stumble along the forest path where many wild elephants roam, after a calf? Alas, what a sin!

8.translit:
ViNNin meedhuama rarkaLvirum piththozha miRaiththuaayar paadiyil veedhiyoodae
KaNNang kaalippin naeezhun tharuLak kaNdu
 iLavaayk kannimaar kaamuRRa
VaNNam vaNdamar pozhilpudhu vaiyarkoan
 viTTu siththan sonna maalaipaththum
PaNNinpam varappaadum paththa ruLLaar
 paramaana vaikuntham naNNuvarae.
8.meaning: While the immortals above in heaven adore him with longing, Kannan came forth grandly through the streets of the cowherds' Ayarpadi behind the cattle; seeing this, the young maidens were filled with love. These are the ten garland-verses spoken by Vishnuchittan, lord of Puduvai of bee-filled groves, on that loveliness. Those devotees who sing them so that the joy of the melody arises will reach supreme Vaikuntha.

9.translit:
PuviyuLnaan kaNdadhoa raRputham kaeLeer
 pooNi maeykkumiLang koavalar kooTTaththu
AvaiyuL naagath thaNaiyaankuzha looththa
 amara loagath thaLavumsenR isaippa
AviyuNaa maRanthu vaanava rellaam
 aayar paadi niRaiyappugunthu eeNdi
Seviyu Naavin suvaikoNdu magizhnthu
 koavintha naiththodarnthu enRumvi daarae.
9.meaning: Hear of a wonder I saw on this earth: in the gathering of the young cowherds who graze the cattle, the Lord who reclines on the serpent played his flute, and its music carried all the way to the world of the gods. The celestials, forgetting their sacrificial offerings, all came crowding to fill the cowherds' Ayarpadi, and savoring the sweetness with ears and tongue, rejoiced and followed Govinda, never to leave him.

10.translit:
Maargazhith thingaL madhiniRaintha nannaaLaal
Neeraadap poadhuveer poadhuminoa naerizhaiyeer
Seermalkum aaypaadich chelvach chiRumeerkaaL
Koorvael kodunthozhilan nandhakoa pankumaran
Eeraarntha kaNNi yasoadhai iLamsingam
Kaarmaeni sengaN kadhirmadhiyam poalmugaththaan
Naaraa yaNanae namakkae paRai tharuvaan
Paaroar pugazhap padinthaeloar empaavaay.
10.meaning: In the month of Margazhi, on this good day of the full moon - you who would come to bathe, come along, O finely-adorned ones; O wealthy little girls of glorious Ayarpadi. Narayana himself - the son of Nandagopa whose cruel weapon is the sharp spear, the young lion of Yasoda of the beautiful eyes, the dark-bodied one with red eyes and a face like the radiant moon - will himself grant us the 'parai' (drum / boon). Come and immerse, that the people of the world may praise us, O our maiden.

11.translit:
NaaNi yiniyoar karumamillai naala yalaarum aRinthozhinthaar
PaaNiyaa thennai marunthuseydhu paNdupaN daakka vuRutheeraagil
MaaNi yuruvaa yulagaLantha maayanaik kaaNil thalaimaRiyum
AaNaiyaal neerennaik kaakkavaeNdil aaypaadik kaeyennai yuyththidumin.
11.meaning: There is no use now in shyness; the neighbors on all four sides have already found out and gone. If you would without delay make me well and restore me to how I once was, know this: my sickness will turn back the moment I behold the Mayan who took the form of the dwarf and measured the worlds. I charge you - if you truly wish to save me, then carry me to Ayarpadi.

12.translit:
Aarae yulagath thaaRRuvaar aayar paadi kavarnthuNNum
Kaarae Ruzhakka vuzhakkuNdu thaLarnthum muRinthum kidappaenai
Aaraa vamutha manaiyaanRan amutha vaayi looRiya
Neerthaan koNarnthu pularaamae parukki yiLaippai neekkeerae.
12.meaning: Who in this world is able to console me? Trampled and crushed by that dark bull who plunders and devours the cowherds' Ayarpadi, I lie languishing, broken. Bring the water that wells up from the nectar-mouth of him who is like insatiable ambrosia, and make me drink it before it dries, and so remove my fainting weakness.

13.translit:
Allal viLaiththa perumaanai aayar paadik kaNiviLakkai
Villi pudhuvai nagarnambi viTTu siththan viyankoadhai
Villaith tholaiththa puruvaththaaL vaeTkai yuRRu migavirumpum
Sollaith thudhikka vallaarkaL thunpak kadaLuL thuvaLaarae.
13.meaning: The Lord who wrought such love-torment, the bright lamp of the cowherds' Ayarpadi - of him the wondrous Kothai, daughter of Vishnuchittan, lord of Villiputuvai city, whose brows outdo the bow, spoke these words in great love and longing. Those who can recite these words will not languish in the ocean of sorrow.

14.translit:
Anunga vennaip pirivusey thaayar paadi kavarnthuNNum
KuNungku naaRik kuTTaeRRaik koavarth thananaik kaNdeerae
KaNangka Loadu minmaegam kalanthaaR poala vanamaalai
Minungka ninRu viLaiyaada virunthaa vanaththae kaNdoamae.
14.meaning: That one who plunders and devours the cowherds' Ayarpadi, who reeks of the young calf, who parted from me to leave me wasting away - O have you seen Govardhana? Like dark clouds mingled with flashing lightning in their masses, his forest-garland gleaming, we saw him standing there at play in Brindavan.

15.translit:
PaarththaR kaayanRupaaratham kai seydiTTu venRaparanjsudar,
Koaththangkaayar tham paadiyil kuravaipiNainthavengkoavalan,
Eaththuvaarththam manaththuLLaan idaventhaimaeviyavempiraan
Theerththaneerth thadanjsoalaisoozh thiruvaengkadamadainenjsamae.
15.meaning: The supreme radiance who that day waged the Bharata war with his own hand for Arjuna's sake and won; the dear cowherd who linked hands in the kuravai dance in the cowherds' village; he dwells in the hearts of those who praise him, our Lord who abides at Idaventai. O heart, reach holy Venkatam, ringed by groves and tanks of sacred waters.

16.translit:
PooNmulaimael saanthaNiyaaL porukayalkaN maiyezhuthaaL poovai paeNaaL,
EaNaRiyaa Leththanaiyum emperumaan thiruvaranga mengae ennum,
NaaNmalaraaL naayaganaay naamaRiya vaaypaadi vaLarntha nambi,
AaNmaganaa yenmagaLaich cheydhanagaL ammanaimeeraRiki laenae.
16.meaning: She no longer adorns her ornamented breast with sandal-paste, nor lines her fish-like fighting eyes with collyrium, nor cares for her myna-bird; she has lost all sense of bounds, ever crying 'Where is my Lord's holy Srirangam?' The Nambi who, as the consort of the lotus-dwelling Lakshmi, grew up in Ayarpadi for us all to know - O mothers, I cannot fathom what that virile one has done to my daughter.

17.translit:
Amponaa rulaga maezhum aRiyaaayp paadi thannuL,
Kombanaar pinnai koalam kooduthaR kaeRu konRaan,
Semponaar madhiLkaL soozhntha thenthirup paeruL maevum,
Empiraan naamam naaLum aeththinaa nuyntha vaaRae.
17.meaning: As all seven golden worlds came to know, within Ayarpadi he slew the bulls to win the hand and beauty of the vine-slender Pinnai. My Lord abides at southern Tiruppēr, girt with walls of ruddy gold. Day after day I praise his name, and that is how I have been saved.

18.translit:
Thanthai thaLaikazhalth thoanRippoay, aaypaadi
Nanthan kulamadhalai yaayvaLarnthaan kaaNaedee,
Nanthan kulamadhalai yaayvaLarnthaan naanmugaRkuth
ThanthaikaaN, enthai perumaankaaN saazhalae.
18.meaning: Behold, friend - as he was born his father's fetters fell away, and going off to Ayarpadi he grew up as the darling heir of Nanda's clan. He who grew up as the darling heir of Nanda's clan is the father of the four-faced Brahma; he is my Lord, my Father. Saazhale!

19.translit:
Aazhkadalsoozh vaiyagaththaa raesappoay, aaypaadith
Thaazhkuzhalaar vaiththa thayiruNdaan kaaNaedee,
Thaazhkuzhalaar vaiththa thayiruNda ponvayiRu,iv
Vaezhulagu muNdum idamudaiththaal saazhalae.
19.meaning: Behold, friend - though the people of this world girdled by the deep sea reproached him, he went off to Ayarpadi and ate the curds the long-tressed women had set aside. That golden belly which ate the curds the long-tressed women set aside - even after swallowing these seven worlds, still it has room to spare. Saazhale!

20.translit:
aRiyaadaark kaanaaya naagippooy, aayppaadi
uRiyaar naRuveNNe yuNdugandaan kaaNeedee
uRiyaar naRuveNNe yuNduganda ponvayiRukku,
eRineer ulaganaittum eydaadaal saazhalee.
20.meaning: Becoming a cowherd among those who did not recognize him, he went off to Ayarpadi (the cowherd village) and joyfully ate the fragrant white butter from the hanging pots — behold, dear girl! For that golden belly which delighted in eating the fragrant white butter stored in the hanging pots, all the worlds girt by the surging waters would not suffice — saazhalE (refrain).

21.translit:
aaraal kadaindhidappattadhu avan kaaNmin
oor aa nirai meyttu ulagu ellaam uNdu umizhndhum
aaraadha thanmaiyanaay aangu orunaaL aayppaadi
seer aar kalai algul seer adic chendhuvar vaay
vaar aar vanamulaiyaaL maththu aarap paRRikkoNdu
er aar idai noova eththanaiyoor poadhum aay
seer aar thayir kadaindhu veNNey thiraNdhadhanai
veer aar nudhal madavaaL veeRu oor kalaththu ittu
naar aar uRi eeRRi nanku amaiya vaiththadhanaip
poor aar veel kaN madavaaL poandhanaiyum poy uRakkam
ooraadhavan pool uRangi aRivu uRRu
thaar aar thadam thoaLgaL uL aLavum kain neetti
aaraadha veNNey vizhungi arugu irundha
21.meaning: By whom was he churned? Behold him! Even after grazing the village's herds of cattle and swallowing all the worlds and spitting them out again, he remained of an insatiable nature. There, one day in Ayarpadi, a fair-bodied woman with a graceful garment about her waist, lovely feet and red coral lips, her breast bound with a girdle, grasped the churning-staff and churned the fine curds for ever so long until her beautiful waist ached, and the butter gathered into a mass. That sweat-browed gentle woman placed it in another vessel, set it up high in a fibrous hanging pot and stored it well. Until that woman with spear-like eyes had gone away, he feigned sleep, lying like one who knew nothing of false slumber; then waking alert, he stretched his hand right up into his garlanded broad shoulders, gulped down the insatiable butter, and sat there beside it.

0.translit:
pollaa vaDivuDaip peycchi thunjap puNarmulai vaaymaDukka
vallaanai maamaNi vaNNanai maruvumiDam naaDuthireel
pallaayiram perunthevi maaroDu peLvam eeRithuvarai
ellaarumchoozhach singaasanaththe irunthaanaik kaNDaaruLar.
0.meaning: He who, when the demoness Putana of foul form came to suckle him to death, drew her into his mouth and slew her — the sapphire-hued lord precious as a great gem. If you seek the place where he dwells, there are those who have seen him: seated upon his throne in Dwaraka, having boarded the ocean, surrounded by his many thousands of great consorts and by all his people.

1.translit:
thiraiporu kaDalchoozh thiNmathiL thuvarai venthuthan maiththunan maarkkaay
arasinai yaviya arasinai yaruLum aripuru DOththaman amarvu
niraihirai yaaga neDiyana yoopam nirantharam ozhukkuviTTu iraNDu
karaipurai veLvip pugaikamazh gangai kaNDamen num kaDi nagare.
1.meaning: Lord of Dwaraka, girt by strong ramparts and the wave-tossing sea — for the sake of his brothers-in-law, he destroyed kingdoms and bestowed kingdoms; that Hari, the Supreme Purushottama, dwells there. The fragrant guarded city called Gangaikondam, where the tall sacrificial posts (yupa) stand set in unbroken rows and the smoke of the two-banked sacrifices spreads its scent.

2.translit:
vaDathisai mathurai saaLakki raamam vaikuntham thuvarai ayothi
iDamuDai vatari yiDavagai yuDaiya empuru DOththaman irukkai
thaDavarai yathirath tharaNiviN DiDiyath thalaippaRRik karaimaram saaDi
kaDalinaik kalangak kaDuththizhi gangaik kaNDamen num kaDi nagare.
2.meaning: Mathura of the north, Salagramam, Vaikuntham, Dwaraka, Ayodhya, the spacious Badari — the abodes that are the seats of my Lord, the Supreme Purushottama. The fragrant guarded city called Gangaikondam, where the Ganga rushes down fiercely so the great mountains shake, the earth and sky split and thunder, seizing and felling the trees on its banks and churning the sea into turmoil.

3.translit:
pathinaaRaam aayiravar thevimaar paNiseyyath thuvarai ennum
mathil naayakaraagi veeRYirundha maNavaaLar mannu koyil
puthu naaLmalark kamalam emperumaan pon vayiTRiR poove polvaan
pothu-naayakam paaviththu iRumaanthu pon saaykkum punal arangame.
3.meaning: Served by his sixteen thousand consorts, he became the lord of the citadel called Dwaraka, the bridegroom who reigned there — the enduring temple of that bridegroom. Watery Srirangam, where the fresh-blooming lotus of the new day, like the flower upon my Lord's golden belly, conceives itself sovereign over all and, swelling with pride, scatters its golden pollen.

4.translit:
thaDavaraivaay miLirndhuminnum thavaLaneDung koDipol
suDaroLiyaay nenjinuLLe thoonRumen sothinambee
vaDathaDamum vaikunthamum mathiLthuva raapathiyum
iDavagaigaL igazhndhiTTuenpaal iDavagai koNDanaiye.
4.meaning: Like a long white banner that gleams and flashes upon the broad mountain slopes, you appear within my heart as radiant light, O my brilliant perfect Lord. Spurning your abodes — the northern lake (Badari), Vaikuntham, and Dwaraka of the ramparts — you have taken your dwelling-place within me.

5.translit:
suvaril puraaNanin pereezhuthich chuRavanaR koDigaLum thurangangaLum
kavarip piNaakkaLum karuppuvillum kaaTTiththan theenkaNDaay kaamadhevaa
avaraip piraayan thoDangienRum aathariththezhunthaven thaDamulaigaL
thuvaraip piraanukke sangaRpiththuth thozhuthuvaith theenollai vidhikkiRRiye.
5.meaning: On the wall I have written your ancient name, and I have shown you the auspicious makara banners, the horses, the maiden chowrie-bearers, and the sugarcane bow — behold them, O Kamadeva (god of love). From the very dawn of my youth my full breasts have risen ever longing for him alone; I have vowed and consecrated them to the Lord of Dwaraka and worshipped — so ordain it for me swiftly.

6.translit:
aava lanpuDa yaartham manaththanRi
meeva lanvirai soozhthuva raapathik
kaava lankanRu meyththu viLaiyaaDum
kova lanvaril kooDiDu kooDale.
6.meaning: He does not unite with any heart save that of those who hold eager love for him — he, the protector of fragrance-girt Dwaraka, the cowherd Govinda who grazes the calves and plays. O kuudal (love-divination circle), if that cowherd will come, then join and unite!

7.translit:
kaalai yezhunthirundhu kariyakuru vikkaNangaL
maalin varavusolli maruLpaaDuthal meymmaikolO
solai malaipperumaan thuvaraapathi yemperumaan
aali nilaipperumaan avan vaarththai yuraikkinRathe.
7.meaning: Rising at dawn, the flocks of dark sparrows foretell the coming of Tirumal and sing the maruul (love-strain) — can this be true? The Lord of Sholaimalai, our Lord of Dwaraka, the Lord who rests upon the banyan leaf — it is his tidings that they are uttering!

8.translit:
kooTTilirundhu kiLiyeppothum kovindhaakovindhaavenRazhaikkum
ooTTukkoDaathuseRuppanaagil ulagaLandhaanenRuyarakkoovum
naaTTiRRalaippazhiyeythi ungaLnanmaiyazhinthuthalaiyiDaathe
sooTTuyarmaaDangaL soozhndhuthoonRum thuvaraapathikkennaiyuyththiDumin.
8.meaning: Sitting in its cage, the parrot ever calls out 'Govinda! Govinda!'; if you withhold its feed and chide it, it cries aloud 'He who measured the worlds!' Do not earn the foremost reproach of the land and ruin your own good name by hanging your heads — convey me to Dwaraka, which appears ringed with crest-topped lofty mansions.

9.translit:
mulaiththaDaththa nanjuNDu thunjap peycchi muthuthuvaraik kulapathiyaak kaalippinne
ilaiththaDaththa kuzhaloothi yaayar maathar inavaLaikoN DaanaDikkee zheythagiRpeer
malaiththaDaththa maNikoNarndhu vaiya muyya vaLangoDukkum varupunalam ponninaaDan
silaiththaDakkaik kulachchozhan seerntha koyil thiruhaRaiyoor maNimaaDam seerminkaLe.
9.meaning: He drank the poison of the demoness's breasts so that she perished; he became the clan-lord of ancient Dwaraka; behind the cattle he played his leaf-cool flute and seized the bangles of the cowherd women. If you would reach the feet of that one — the temple Tirunaraiyur with its jewelled mansions, the temple favored by the Chola of bow-bearing mighty hand, lord of the fertile Kaveri land whose flowing waters bring gem-laden bounty from the mountains to sustain the world — repair you all there!

10.translit:
kaTTeRu neeLsolaik kaaNDavaththaith theemooTTi
viTTaanai meyyam amarndha perumaanai
maTTeRu kaRpagaththai maatharkkaay vaNthuvarai
naTTaanai naaDi naRaiyooril kaNDene.
10.meaning: He who set fire to and consumed the long dense forest of Khandava clustered thick with growth; the Lord who abides at Meyyam; the honey-laden Kalpaka tree; he who, for the sake of women, founded bounteous Dwaraka — seeking him, I beheld him at Naraiyur.

11.translit:
seyan aNiyan siRiyan migapperiyan,
aayan thuvaraikko naayninRa- maayan,an
ROthiya vaakkathanaik kallaar, ulagaththil
eethilaraay meynnyaana mil.
11.meaning: He is far and he is near; he is small and exceedingly great; the cowherd who stood as the king of Dwaraka — the wondrous Maya. Those who do not learn the word he uttered of old (the Gita) remain strangers in this world, without true knowledge.

12.translit:
ennaiyen seyyilen oorenc sollilen thozhimeer,
ennai yiniyumak kaasai yillai yagappaTTen,
munnai yamarar muthalvan vaNthuva raapathi
mannan, maNivaN NanvaasudhevankalaiyuLe.
12.meaning: Whatever you do to me, whatever you say of my town or of me, O my friends — you have no more longing left for me (to reproach), for I am already caught. I am caught in the net of the ancient First-One of the celestials, the king of bounteous Dwaraka, the sapphire-hued Vasudeva.

0.translit:
angaN gnaalam anja angu oraaLariyaay avuNan
pongavaakam vaLLugiraal poozhnda punidan idam,
paingaN aanaikkombu koNDu pattimaiyaal, adikkeezhch
chengaNaaLi ittiRaignjum singavEL kunRamE.
0.meaning: The place of the holy Lord who, becoming a unique man-lion there so that the wide-eyed world trembled, tore open with His sharp claws the swelling chest of the demon (Hiranya): it is Singavel Kunram, where lions with red eyes, in devotion, bring tusks of green-eyed elephants and lay them at His feet and worship.

1.translit:
alaitta peezhvaay vaaL eyiRROr kOLariyaay, avuNan
kolaikkai aaLan nenjidanda koorugiraaLan idam,
malaitta sel saattu eRinda poosal van tudivaay kaduppa,
silaikkai vEDar tezhippu aRaada singavEL kunRamE.
1.meaning: The place of the Lord of sharp claws who, becoming a fierce man-lion with a gaping mouth, a tossing tongue and sword-like fangs, split open the heart of the murderous-handed demon: it is Singavel Kunram, where the uproar of attacking, surging caravans resounds like the loud beat of drums, and where the clamour of the bow-handed hunters never ceases.

2.translit:
Eynda peezhvaay vaaL eyiRROr kOLariyaay, avuNan
vaaynda aakam vaLLugiraal vagirnda ammaan adan idam,
Oynda maavum udainda kunRum anRiyum ninRu azhalaal,
tEynda vEyum alladu illaach chingavEL kunRamE.
2.meaning: The place of that great Lord who, becoming a fierce man-lion with a fitting gaping mouth and sword-like fangs, split with His sharp claws the firm chest of the demon: it is Singavel Kunram, where there is nothing but weary wild beasts, broken hill-slopes, and bamboo withered away by the standing flame.

3.translit:
evvam vevvEl pon peyarOn Edalin in uyirai
vavvi aakam vaLLugiraal vagirnda ammaan adu idam,
kavvu naayum kazhugum uchchi pOdodu kaal suzhanRu,
teyvam allaal sella oNNaach chingavEL kunRamE.
3.meaning: The place of that great Lord who, out of enmity, seized the sweet life of Hiranya of the fierce, cruel spear, and split his chest with His sharp claws: it is Singavel Kunram, where biting dogs and vultures roam, where at high noon the winds whirl, and which none but the divine can enter.

4.translit:
menRa peezhvaay vaaL eyiRROr kOLariyaay, avuNan
ponRa aakam vaLLugiraal poozhnda punidan idam,
ninRa sen tee moNDu sooRai neeL visumbu oodu iriya,
chenRu kaaNDaRku ariya kOyil singavEL kunRamE.
4.meaning: The place of the holy Lord who, becoming a fierce man-lion with a gnashing gaping mouth and sword-like fangs, tore open with His sharp claws the chest of the demon so that he perished: it is Singavel Kunram, a temple hard to reach and behold, where whirlwinds scoop up the standing red fire and rush scattering through the vast sky.

5.translit:
erinda paingaN ilangu peezhvaay eyiRRodu idu evvuru enRu,
irindu vaanOr kalangi ODa irunda ammaan adu idam,
nerinda vEyin muzhaiyuL ninRu neeL neRivaay uzhuvai,
tirinda aanaich chuvaDu paarkkum singavEL kunRamE.
5.meaning: The place of that great Lord who took such a form -- with blazing green eyes, a shining gaping mouth and fangs -- that the celestials, crying 'what manner of being is this?', fled in confusion and dispersed: it is Singavel Kunram, where the tiger, standing within the hollow of crushed bamboo, watches along the long path for the tracks of wandering elephants.

6.translit:
munaitta seeRRam viN sudappOy moovulagum piRavum,
anaittum anja vaaLariyaay irunda ammaan adu idam,
kanaitta teeyum kallum allaa vil udai vEDarumaay,
tinaittanaiyum sella oNNaach chingavEL kunRamE.
6.meaning: The place of that great Lord who abided as the fierce man-lion, His kindled wrath rising to scorch the heavens so that all the three worlds and all else trembled: it is Singavel Kunram, where, besides roaring fire and rock, there are bow-bearing hunters too, and which one cannot advance into even by a millet's measure.

7.translit:
naa tazhumba naanmuganum eesanumaay muRaiyaal
Etta angu OraaLariyaay irunda ammaan adu idam,
kaaytta vaagai neRRu olippak kall adar vEyngazhai pOy,
tEytta teeyaal viN sivakkum singavEL kunRamE.
7.meaning: The place of that great Lord who abided as the unique man-lion there, so that the four-faced Brahma and Siva, their tongues calloused, duly praised Him: it is Singavel Kunram, where the ripened vaakai pods crackle, and where the sky reddens with the fire kindled as the bamboo wears against itself along the rocky paths.

8.translit:
nallai nenjE. naam tozhudum nammudai nam perumaan,
alli maadar pulga ninRa aayiran tOLan idam,
nelli malgik kall udaippap pul ilai aarttu, adarvaaych
chilli sillenRu olla aRaadach chingavEL kunRamE.
8.meaning: O good heart, let us worship! The place of our own Lord, He of the thousand shoulders, who stands embraced by the lotus-dwelling Lady (Lakshmi): it is Singavel Kunram, where amla trees grow thick splitting the rocks, where grass and leaves rustle, and where along the paths the cicadas chirr 'silli silli' without cease.

9.translit:
sengaNaaLittu iRaignjum singavEL kunRudaiya,
engaL eesan em piraanai irun tamizh nool pulavan,
mangaiyaaLan mannu tol seer vaNDu aRai taar kaliyan,
sengaiyaaLan senjol maalai vallavar teedu ilarE.
9.meaning: Upon our Lord, our God of Singavel Kunram, where red-eyed lions worship Him, the poet of the great Tamil works, lord of Tirumangai, of enduring ancient glory, Kaliyan of the garland on which bees hum, the generous-handed one -- has composed this garland of fine words. Those who can master it shall be free from all evil.

0.translit:
suTTum oLivaTTam soozhndhu sothi parandhengum
ethanai seyyilum en-magan mugam neroVVaay
viththagan vengkata vaaNan unnai viLikkinTa
kaiththalam novaame ambuleekadi thodivaa.
0.meaning: Even though you spread a halo of radiant light circling all around you and shining everywhere, no matter how much you do, you can never equal the face of my son. The wise Lord, the Lord of Venkatam, is calling out to you; so that the palm of his hand does not ache from beckoning, O moon, run here at once.

1.translit:
ennidhu maayam ennappan aRindhilan
munnaiya vaNName koNdu aLavaayenna
mannu namuSiyai vaanil suzhaTiya
minnu mudiyane achcho vachcho
vengkata vaaNane achcho vachcho.
1.meaning: "What wonder is this? My father did not realize it" — saying "take on your former little form and measure the worlds," you who whirled the enduring demon Namuchi into the sky, O Lord with the gleaming crown, achcho vachcho! O Lord of Venkatam, achcho vachcho!

2.translit:
thennilangkai mannan-Siram-thoL thuNi seydhu
minnilang gupooN vibeedaNa nambikku
ennilang gunaamaththaLavum arasenTa
minnilang gaaraTkuor kolkoNduvaa
vengkata vaaNarkkuor kolkoNduvaa.
2.meaning: He who severed the head and shoulders of the king of southern Lanka, and granted to the noble Vibhishana, adorned with gleaming jewels, the rule "for as long as my radiant name endures" — for that lightning-bright Lord bring a sceptre, for the Lord of Venkatam bring a sceptre.

3.translit:
machchodu maaLigai yeRi maadhargaL thammidam pukku
kachchodu pattaik kizhiththuk kaambu thugilavai keeRi
nichchalum theemaigaL seyvaay neeL-thiru vengkadaththu endhaay
pachchaith thamanagath thodu paadhirip poochchootta vaaraay.
3.meaning: Climbing up the lofts and mansions, entering the place of the women, tearing their bodices and waistbands, ripping the cloth garments — daily you do mischievous deeds, O my Lord of vast and holy Venkatam. Come, that I may adorn you with fresh marjoram and trumpet-flower blossoms.

4.translit:
podharkaN daayingke podhar kaNdaay
podhare nennaadhe podhar kaNdaay
edhenum solli asala gaththaar
edhenum pesanaan ketka matten
kodhu kalamudaik kutta neyaa
kunTeduth thaaykuda maadukooththaa
vedhap poruLe envengkadavaa
viththaga neingke podha raaye.
4.meaning: Come here, please, come here; do not say "I will not come," please come. Should the neighbours say something, anything, I cannot bear to hear it. O playful little child, O you who lifted the hill, who danced the pot-dance, O essence of the Vedas, my Lord of Venkatam, O wise one, please come here.

5.translit:
kadiyaar pozhilaNi vengkada vaagarum pore reneeyukakkum
kudaiyum seruppum kuzhalum tharuvikkak koLLaadhe ponaay maale
kadiyaveng kaanidaik kanTinpin pona siRukkut tachcheng kamala
adiyum vedhumbiun kaNgaL sivandhaay asaindhit taaynee embiraan.
5.meaning: O dark Lord, fierce bull, you who delight in the fragrant groves that adorn Venkatam — though we had a parasol, sandals, and a flute brought for you, you went off without taking them. Following the calves through the harsh, scorching forest, your tender little lotus-red feet have grown scorched, your eyes have turned red, you have grown weary, O my Lord.

6.translit:
senTula gamkudain thaadum sunaiththiru maaliruny solaithannuL
ninTa piraanadi meladi maiththiTam nerpada viNNappanysey
pontigazh maadam polindhuthonTum pudhuvaikkon vittusiththan
onTino donbadhum paadavallaar ula gamaLan thaanthamare.
6.meaning: Vishnuchittan (Periyalwar), the chief of Puduvai where golden mansions shine resplendent, made this humble entreaty fittingly, in the manner of servitude, at the feet of the Lord who stands within Tirumaliruncholai with its springs where the world goes and bathes and sports. Those able to sing these ten verses become the devoted servants of Him who measured the worlds.

7.translit:
thaiyoru thingkaLum tharaiviLakkith thaNmaN dalamittu maasimunnaaL
aiyanuN maNaRkoNdu theruvaNindhu azhaginuk kalangkarith thanangkadhevaa
uyyavu maangkolo venTusolli unnaiyu mumbiyaiyum thozhudhen
veyyadhor thazhalumizh sakkarakkai vengkada vaTkennai vidhikkiTiye.
7.meaning: Through the whole month of Thai I swept and cleaned the ground, laid out a cool mandala-design, and in the early days of Maasi I took fine sand and adorned the street, decorating it for beauty's sake, O Ananga (god of love). Saying "may I be redeemed?" I worshipped you and your elder brother; ordain that I belong to the Lord of Venkatam, who holds in his hand the discus that spews fierce flame.

8.translit:
maththanan naRumalar murukkamalar koNdumup podhumun nadivaNangki
thaththuva miliyenTu nenycherindhu vaasagath thazhiththunnai vaidhidaame
koththalar poongkaNai thoduththukkoNdu kovindha nenbadhor pereezhudhi
viththagan vengkata vaaNanennum viLakkinil pugavennai vidhikkiTiye.
8.meaning: Taking fragrant datura blossoms and murukku (coral-tree) flowers, worshipping at your feet at all three times of day — so that I do not cry out "he is without substance," burning in my heart, and disgrace you with harsh words, O god of love, take up your flowered arrows, write that one name "Govinda," and ordain that I enter the lamp that is the wise Lord, the dweller of Venkatam.

9.translit:
kaattil vengkadam kaNNa puranagar
vaatta minTi magizhndhuRai vaamanan
ottaraavandhen kaippaTi thannodum
koottu maagilnee koodidu koodale.
9.meaning: Vamana, who dwells joyfully and without weariness in the forest at Venkatam and in the city of Kannapuram — if he will come running and clasp my hand and unite me with himself, then, O koodal (divination circle), come together and join.

10.translit:
veLLai viLisang gidangkaiyiR koNda vimala nenakkuruk kaattaan
uLLam pugundhennai naiviththu naaLum uyirpeydhu kooththaattuk kaaNum
kaLLavizh seNbagap poomalar kodhik kaLiththisai paadung kuyile
meLLa virundhu mizhaTi mizhaTaaden vengkata vanvarak koovaay.
10.meaning: The flawless Lord who holds the white sounding conch in his left hand does not show his form to me; he has entered my heart and made me waste away, daily putting life into me only to make me dance like a puppet. O cuckoo who pecks at the honey-dripping shenbaga blossoms and joyously sings melodies, do not sit there softly prattling on and on — call out so that my Lord of Venkatam may come.

11.translit:
viNneela melaappu viriththaaRpol megangkaaL
theNneerpaay vengkadaththen thirumaalum pondhaane
kaNNeergaL mulaikkuvattil thuLisorach sorvenai
peNNeermai yeedazhikkum idhuthamakkor perumaiye?
11.meaning: O clouds that spread out like a canopy over the blue sky — has my Tirumal of Venkatam, where clear waters flow, also departed? For me, who waste away with tears trickling down upon the peaks of my breasts, this destroying of a woman's delicacy — is it some great glory to them?

12.translit:
maamuththa nidhisoriyum maamugilkaaL vengkadaththuch
saamaththin niRangkoNda thaadaaLan vaarththaiyenne
kaamaththee yuLpugundhu kadhuvappattu idaikkangkul
emaththor thenTalukking gilakkaaynaa niruppene.
12.meaning: O great clouds that pour down treasure-like great pearls, what word is there of the mighty Lord of Venkatam who bears the dark hue of the rain-cloud? Pierced and seized by the fire of love that has entered within me, must I sit here in the depth of night, a target for a cool breeze in this hour of refuge?

13.translit:
oLivaNNam vaLaisindhai uRakkaththo divaiyellaam
eLimaiyaa littennai eedazhiyap poyinavaal
kuLiraruvi vengkadaththen kovindhan kuNampaadi
aLiyaththa megangkaaL aavigaath thiruppene.
13.meaning: My lustre of complexion, my bangles, my mind, my sleep — all these have slipped away as if cheaply given up, leaving me ruined and undone. O kindly clouds, must I keep on sustaining my life merely by singing the virtues of my Govinda of Venkatam with its cool cascading waterfalls?

14.translit:
minnaagath thezhukinTa megangkaaL vengkadaththuth
thannaagath thirumangkai thangkiyaseer maarvaTku
ennaagath thiLangkongkai virumbiththaam naadoRum
ponnaagam pulgudhaRken purivudaimai seppumine.
14.meaning: O clouds in whose midst lightning rises, to the Lord of Venkatam upon whose glorious chest the divine lady Lakshmi resides — tell him of my devotion: that day after day my young breasts long for his form, eager to embrace his golden chest.

15.translit:
vaankoNdu kiLarndhezhundha maamugilkaaL vengkadaththuth
thenkoNda malarchchidharath thiraNderip pozhiveerkaaL
oonkoNda vaLLugiraal iraNiyanai yudalidanthaan
thaankoNda sarivaLaigaL tharumaagil saaTumine.
15.meaning: O great clouds that have risen surging, filling the sky, who gather and pour your rain so that honey-laden blossoms scatter over Venkatam — if He who tore open Hiranya's body with his flesh-piercing claws will return the slipping bangles he has taken from me, then say so.

16.translit:
salangkoNdu kiLarndhezhundha thaNmugilkaaL maavaliyai
nilangkoNdaan vengkadaththe nirandheRip pozhiveerkaaL
ulangkuNda viLangganipol uLmeliyap pugundhuennai
nalangkoNda naaraNaTken nadalainoy seppumine.
16.meaning: O cool clouds that have risen surging, filled with water, who spread out and pour your rain over Venkatam of Him who took the earth from Mahabali — to Narayana, who entered me and stole my well-being, leaving me inwardly withered like a wood-apple eaten hollow by worms, tell of my grievous love-sickness.

17.translit:
sangkamaa kadalkadaindhaan thaNmugilkaaL vengkadaththuch
sengkaNmaal sevadikkeezh adiveezhchchi viNNappam
kongkaimel kungkumaththin kuzhambazhiyap pugundhuorunaaL
thangkume lennaavi thangkumen Turaiyeere.
17.meaning: O cool clouds, this is my humble entreaty, falling at the holy red feet of the red-eyed Tirumal of Venkatam who churned the conch-filled great ocean: tell him to say whether, if he would but enter one day so that the kumkum paste upon my breasts is smudged away, my life would then remain — yes, it would remain.

18.translit:
kaarkaalath thezhukinTa kaarmugilkaaL vengkadaththup
porkaalath thezhundharuLip porudhavanaar persolli
neerkaalath therukkilam pazhavilaipol veezhvenai
vaarkaalath thorunaaLtham vaasagamthan tharuLaare.
18.meaning: O dark rain-clouds that rise in the rainy season, speaking the name of the Lord of Venkatam who graciously came forth in time of war and fought — to me, who fall like the old withered leaves of the erukku plant in the season of rains, will he not, even on one day of this long season, grant the grace of a word of his?

19.translit:
madhayaanai polezhundha maamugilkaaL vengkadaththaip
padhiyaaga vaazhveerkaaL paambaNaiyaan vaarththaiyenne
kadhiyenTum thaanaavaan karudhaadhuor peNkodiyai
vadhaiseydhaan ennumsol vaiyagaththaar madhiyaare.
19.meaning: O great clouds risen up like rutting elephants, who live with Venkatam as your abode, what word is there of the Lord who reclines on the serpent? "He who is himself ever the refuge, heedlessly tormented a tender creeper of a woman" — will not the people of the world hold this saying against him?

100.translit:
paNNulaam menmozhip paavaimaar paNaimulai aNaidhum naam endru,
yeNNuvaar eNNamadhu ozhiththu nee pizhaiththuyyak karudhinaayel,
viNNuLaar viNNinmeedhu iyandra veengkadaththuLaar vaLangkoL munneer
vaNNanaar vallavaazh sollumaa vallaiyaay maruvu nenjee.
100.meaning: O my heart, if you think to abandon the thoughts of those who imagine 'we shall embrace the broad breasts of women whose words are soft as melody,' and instead resolve to escape your faults and be saved, then become skilled to utter the name of the Lord of Tiruvallavaazh, the ocean-hued one, who is the Lord of Venkatam that towers high above the heavens. Cling to that, O heart.

101.translit:
valampuri yaazhiyanai varaiyaar tiraL tooLandrannai,
pulampuri noolavanaip pozhil veengkada veedhiyanai,
silampiyal aarudaiya tiru maaliruñ soolainindra,
nalandhigazh naaraNanai naNugungkol en nannudhalee.
101.meaning: He who bears the right-spiralled conch and the discus, of mountain-like rounded shoulders, who wears the sacred thread upon his chest, the Vedic Lord dwelling in groved Venkatam, who stands at Tirumaliruñjolai endowed with its murmuring river full of sounding waters, the radiant and gracious Narayana. Will my fair-browed maiden draw near to him?

102.translit:
ponnai maamaNiyai aNiyaarndhadhoor
minnai veengkadath thucchiyiR kaNdupooy
ennai yaaLudai eesanai empiraan
Rannai yaam sendru kaaNdum thaN kaavilee.
102.meaning: Having beheld upon the peak of Venkatam that one who is like gold, like a great gem, like a perfectly adorned lightning-flash, the Lord who has taken me into His service, our Master. We shall go and see Him in cool Kaavi (Tirukkaavalampaadi).

103.translit:
sollaay paingkiLiyee, sudaraazhi valanuyarththa,
mallaar tooL vada veengkadavan vara, sollaay paingkiLiyee.
103.meaning: Speak, O green parrot! That the Lord of northern Venkatam, who raised aloft his radiant discus in his right hand, of strong wrestler-like shoulders, will come to me - speak it, O green parrot.

104.translit:
kaNNan manaththuLLee niRkavum, kaivaLaigaL
ennoo kazhandra? ivaiyenna maayangkaL?
peNNaanoom peNmaiyoom niRka, avanmeeya,
aNNal malaiyum arangkamum paadoomee.
104.meaning: Though Kannan stands within my mind, why have the bangles slipped from my hands? What kind of wonders are these? We have become women, our womanhood remains intact - yet shall we not sing of the hill of the Lord whom He chose, and of Srirangam?

105.translit:
kaLLaththaal maavaliyai moovadimaN koNdaLandhaan,
veLLaththaan veengkadaththaan enparaal kaaNeedee,
veLLaththaan veengkadaththaanelum, kalikandri
uLLaththin uLLee ulankaNdaay saazhalee.
105.meaning: He who by guile took the three measures of land from Mahabali and measured the worlds - they say, behold dear friend, that He is the One in the milk-ocean and the One in Venkatam. Yet though He be the One in the ocean and in Venkatam, He dwells, you see, deep within the heart of Kalikandri (Tirumangai Alwar). Saazhalee.

106.translit:
neeragaththaay neduvaraiyin ucchi meelaay
 nilaaththingkaL thuNdagaththaay! niraindha kacchi
ooragaththaay, oNthuraineer vehkaa uLLaay
 uLLuvaar uLLaththaay, ulagam eeththum
kaaragaththaay kaarvaanaththu uLLaay kaLvaa
 kaamarupoong kaaviriyin thenpaal mannu
peeragaththaay, peeraadhen nenjin uLLaay
 perumaan un thiruvadiyee peeNineenee.
106.meaning: O Lord who dwells in Tiruneeragam, who is atop the peak of the tall hill (Venkatam), who is in TiruththuNdagam of the moonlit crescent, who dwells in Tiru-Ooragam of full-flourishing Kanchi, who is within bright-watered Tiruvehkaa, who abides in the hearts of those who meditate on you, who dwells in Tirukkaaragam praised by the world, who is in the dark sky, O thief, who is firmly settled in Tiruppeeragam (Tirupper) on the southern bank of the lovely flowering Kaveri, who dwells without leaving within my heart - O Lord, I have cherished only your sacred feet.

107.translit:
vangkaththaal maamaNivandhu undhu munneer
 mallaiyaay madhiL kacchi ooraay! peeraay,
kongkaththaar vaLangkondrai alangkal maarvan
 kulavaraiyan madappaavai idappaal koNdaan,
pangkaththaay paaRkadalaay paarin meelaay
 panivaraiyin ucchiyaay pavaLa vaNNaa,
engkutraay emperumaan unnai naadi
 eezhaiyeen ingkanamee oozhi tharugeenee.
107.meaning: O Lord of Tirukkadalmallai where the ocean's ships bring and push ashore great gems, O Lord of walled Kanchi, O Lord of Tirupper! O you who hold on your left side the tender maiden, daughter of the mountain-king - you whose chest wears the cool kondrai garland of dripping nectar - O Lord who shared half your body, who reclines on the milk-ocean, who is above the earth, who is on the peak of the snowy mountain, O coral-hued one! Where are you, my Lord? Seeking you, I, a wretched one, must thus pass an age (each day) here.

108.translit:
kandrumeeyth thinidhuganda kaaLaay endrum,
 kadipozhilsoozh kaNapuraththen kaniyee endrum,
mandramarak kooththaadi magizhndhaay endrum,
 vada tiruveengkadam meeya maindhaa endrum,
vendrasurar kulangkaLaindha veendhee endrum,
 viripozhilsoozh tirunaRaiyoor nindraay endrum,
thundrukuzhal karuniRaththen thuNaiyee endrum
 thuNaimulaimeel thuLisoorach soorkkindraaLee.
108.meaning: Saying 'O youthful bull who herded the calves and rejoiced sweetly,' saying 'O my fruit (sweet one) of Tirukkannapuram girt by fragrant groves,' saying 'O you who danced in the public square and were glad,' saying 'O young one who abides in northern Tiruvenkatam,' saying 'O King who destroyed the clans of the conquered demons,' saying 'O you who stand at Tirunaraiyur encircled by spreading groves,' saying 'O dark-hued one of clustering locks, my companion' - she languishes, droplets trickling down upon her paired breasts.

109.translit:
aaraanum kaRpippaar naayagaree? naan avanaik
kaar aar tirumeeni kaaNum aLavum pooy
seer aar tiruveengkadamee tirukkoova
looree madhiL kacchi ooragamee peeragamee
peeraa marudhu iRuththaan vehLaRaiyee vehkaavee
peer aali thaNkaal naRaiyoor tiruppuliyoor
aaraamam soozhndha arangkam kaNamangkai.
109.meaning: Are those who would instruct me my masters (have they any authority over me)? Until I go and behold his dark cloud-hued sacred form, I will roam to glorious Tiruvenkatam, to Tirukkovalur, to walled Kanchi's Tiru-Ooragam, to Tiruppeeragam, to Tiruvellarai of Him who broke the marudu trees that did not yield, to Tiruvehkaa, to great Tiruvaali, to Tiruththankaal, to Naraiyur, to Tiruppuliyur, to Srirangam encircled by groves, and to Tirukkanamangai.

110.translit:
thennan uyar poruppum deyva vadamalaiyum
ennum ivaiyee mulaiyaa vadivu amaindha
anna nadaiya aNangkee adi iNaiyaith
110.meaning: O divine maiden of swan-like gait, whose form is shaped so that the lofty southern mountain (Podiyil/Malaya) and the sacred northern mountain (Venkatam) themselves are as your two breasts - your pair of feet...

111.translit:
minni mazhai thavazhum veengkadaththu em viththaganai
mannanai maaliruñjoolai maNaaLanai
kol navilum aazhip padaiyaanai koottiyoor
111.meaning: Our wondrous Lord of Venkatam where lightning flashes and rain-clouds creep, the King, the Bridegroom of Tirumaliruñjolai, the One who bears the slaying discus as his weapon, the Lord of Tirukkottiyur...

112.translit:
ezhuvaar vidaikoLvaar eenthuzhaayaanai,
vazhuvaa vakaininaindhu vaikal - thozhuvaar,
vinaicchudarai nandhuvikkum veengkadamee, vaanoor
manacchudaraith thooNdum malai.
112.meaning: Venkatam is the hill that extinguishes the burning flame of the sins of those who, rising up and taking leave (of worldly matters), worship daily without fail, meditating on the Lord adorned with sweet tulasi - and the very same hill kindles the lamp of the minds of the celestials.

113.translit:
vakaiyaRu nuNkeeLvi vaayvaarkaL, naaLum
pukaiviLakkum poompunalum eendhi, - thisaidhisaiyin
veedhiyarkaL sendriRaiñjum veengkadamee, veNsangkam
oodhiyavaay maalukandha voor.
113.meaning: Venkatam, where the Brahmins endowed with subtle, flawless, definitive learning come daily from every direction bearing incense, lamps, flowers and water to worship - that is the town beloved of Tirumal whose mouth blew the white conch.

114.translit:
oorum variyaravam oNkuRavar maalyaanai,
peera eRindha perumaNiyai, - kaarudaiya
minnendru putradaiyum veengkadamee, meelasurar
ennendra maaladhu idam.
114.meaning: Venkatam, where the moving striped serpent, mistaking for the dark cloud's lightning the great gem that the bright hill-dwelling hunters hurled to drive away a huge elephant, enters its anthill (in fear) - that is the abode of the great Lord (Tirumal) at whom the lofty demons cried out (in dread).

115.translit:
idandhadhu poomi eduththadhu kundram,
kadandhadhu kañjanaimun añja, - kidandhadhuvum
neeroodha maakadalee nindradhuvum veengkadamee,
peeroodha vaNNar peridhu.
115.meaning: He dug up the earth (as the Boar), He lifted up the hill (Govardhana), He overcame Kamsa formerly so that all feared, He reclined upon the great wave-tossed ocean, and He stood at Venkatam - great indeed are the deeds of the Lord whose hue is like the vast surging sea.

116.translit:
peruvil pakazhik kuRavarkaich sendhee
veruvip punamthuRandha veezham, - iruvisumbil
meenveezhak kaNdañjum veengkadamee, meelasurar
koonveezha kaNdugandhaan kundru.
116.meaning: Venkatam, where the elephant that fled, abandoning the millet-field in terror of the red fire from the arrows in the hands of the hunters with their great bows, then sees a meteor falling in the wide sky and trembles afresh (mistaking it for more fire) - that is the hill of Him who looked on with delight as the king of the lofty demons fell.

117.translit:
uNarvaaraar unperumai? yoozhidhoo Roozhi,
uNarvaaraar unnuruvan thannai?, uNarvaaraar
viNNagaththaay maNNagaththaay veengkadaththaay naalveedhap
paNNagaththaay nee kidandha paal?
117.meaning: Who can comprehend your greatness, through age after age? Who can comprehend your very form? O you who are in the heavens, who are upon the earth, who are at Venkatam, who are within the melody of the four Vedas - who can comprehend the milk-ocean upon which you recline?

118.translit:
vazhinindru ninnaith thozhuvaar, vazhuvaa
mozhinindra moorththiyaree yaavar, - pazhudhondrum
vaaraadha vaNNamee viNkodukkum, maNNaLandha
seeraan tiruveeng kadam.
118.meaning: Tiruvenkatam of the glorious One who measured the earth grants the heavenly realm, without any flaw at all, to those who stand firm in the path and worship you. Who are equal to those embodiments of unfailing, steadfast word?

119.translit:
veengkadamum viNNagarum vehkaavum, ahkaadha
poongkidangkil neeLkoovala ponnagarum, - naankidaththum
nindraan irundhaan kidandhaan nadandhaanee,
endraal kedumaam idar.
119.meaning: Venkatam, Tiruvinnagar, Tiruvehkaa, and the golden city of long Tirukkovalur with its never-diminishing flower-filled moats - in these four places He stood, He sat, He reclined, and He walked. If one but says so, all affliction will be destroyed.

120.translit:
paDaiyaarum vaaTkaNNaar paarasinaaL, paimpoon
toDaiyaloo Dheendhiya thoopam, - iDaiyiDaiyin
meenmaaya maasooNum veengkaDamee, meelorunaaL
maanmaaya eydhaan varai.
120.meaning: It is the hill of Him who once shot dead the deer-disguised demon. There, on great festival days, the women whose eyes are sharp as spears hold up fresh blossom-garlands and incense, whose rising smoke between times grows so thick that even the stars are hidden by its haze. That is Venkatam.

121.translit:
uLankaNDaay nannenjee. uththaman enrum
uLankaNDaay, uLLoovaa ruLLath - thuLankaNDaay,
veLLaththi nuLLaanum veengkaDaththu meeyaanum,
uLLaththi nuLLanen rOr.
121.meaning: O good heart, see, He exists! See, the Supreme One exists always! See, He exists within the hearts of those who hold Him within. He who is in the ocean of milk and He who dwells at Venkatam—know that He is the very one who abides within your heart.

122.translit:
senra thilangkaimeel sevveedhan seerraththaal,
konra thiraavaNanaik kooRungkaal, - ninradhuvum
veeyOngku thaNsaaral veengkaDamee, viNNavartham
vaayOngku tholpukazhaan vandhu.
122.meaning: When one tells how, in fierce wrath, the upright one marched upon Lanka and slew Ravana there—the place where He, of ancient fame ever praised on the lips of the celestials, came and stood is Venkatam, with its cool slopes where bamboo rises tall.

123.translit:
manaththuLLaan veengkaDaththaan maakaDalaan, marrum
ninaippariya neeLarangkath thuLLaan, - enaippalarum
theevaadhi theeva nenappaDuvaan, munnorunaaL
maavaay piLandha makan.
123.meaning: He dwells within the mind, He dwells at Venkatam, He is in the great ocean, and He is within the vast and unfathomable Srirangam. He whom countless ones call the God of gods is the very one who, in days of old, split open the mouth of the demon-horse.

124.translit:
thuNindhadhu sindhai thuzhaayalangkal, angkam
aNindhavanpee ruLLaththup pal kaal, - paNindhadhuvum
veeypirangku saaral viRalveeng kaDavanaiyee,
vaaythirangkaL sollum vakai.
124.meaning: My mind has resolved firmly. The way my mouth speaks forth utterances, again and again in my heart, upon the name of Him who is adorned with the tulasi garland—and the one to whom it bows—is none other than the valorous Lord of Venkatam, whose slopes shine with bamboo.

125.translit:
uLadhen riRumaavaa ruNDillai yenru,
taLardhal adhanarukum saaraar, - aLavariya
veedhaththaan veengkaDaththaan viNNOr muDithOyum,
paadhaththaan paadham payinru.
125.meaning: Those who constantly resort to the feet of Him of the measureless Vedas, who dwells at Venkatam, whose feet the crowns of the celestials touch—they will never come near such things as pride that says 'I have wealth' or despair that says 'I have nothing.'

126.translit:
payinra tharangkam thirukkOTTi, pannaaL
payinradhuvum veengkaDamee pannaaL, - payinra
taNidhikazhum sOlai yaNineer malaiyee
maNidhikazhum vaNthaDakkai maal.
126.meaning: Tirumal of the gem-bright, bountiful, broad hands frequented Srirangam and Tirukkottiyur; for many days He frequented Venkatam; and for many days He frequented Tirumalai (Tirukkadanmallai), bright with cool groves and beautiful waters.

127.translit:
neRiyaar kuzharkarrai munninru pindhaazhndhu,
ariyaa diLangkiriyen reNNi, - piriyaadhu
poongkoDikaL vaikum porupunal kunrenrum,
veengkaDamee yaamvirumpum veRpu.
127.meaning: The hill we love is Venkatam—the mount with battling waters, where flowering creepers cling and dwell unparting, mistaking it in their ignorance for a young hill, as it stands like a thick mass of curling tresses falling forward and trailing behind.

128.translit:
veRpen rirunjOlai veengkaDamen rivviraNDum
nirpenru neemadhikkum neermaipool, - nirpen
ruLangkOyi luLLamvaith thuLLineen, veLLath
tiLangkOyil kaiviDeel enru.
128.meaning: Just as you, O Lord, deem worthy the two hills Irunjolai (Tirumaliruncholai) and Venkatam as places where you will stand, so too I have set my heart upon the temple of my heart and meditated, praying: 'Do not abandon your young temple,'—the ocean (where you recline).

129.translit:
pOdharindhu vaanarangkaL poonjunaipukku, aangkalarndha
pOdharindhu koNDeththum pOdhu,uLLam - pOdhu
maNiveeng kaDavan malaraDikkee sella,
aNiveeng kaDavanpee raayndhu.
129.meaning: O heart, go!—reflecting upon the name of the beautiful Lord of Venkatam, set out toward the blossom-feet of the gem-hued Lord of Venkatam, at the time when the monkeys, knowing the hour, enter the flowery springs and, plucking the flowers that have bloomed there, offer them in worship.

130.translit:
peruku madhaveezham maappiDikki munninru,
iruka NiLamoongkil vaangki, - arukirundha
theenkalandhu neeTTum thiruveeng kaDamkaNDeer,
vaankalandha vaNNan varai.
130.meaning: Behold sacred Venkatam, the hill of the Lord whose hue blends with the sky—where the great rutting elephant stands before its lovely mate, bends down a young bamboo with its tusks, mixes it with the honey lying nearby, and holds it out to her.

131.translit:
maarpaal manamsuzhippa mangkaiyarthOL kaiviTTu,
noorpaal manamvaikka noyvidhaam,- naarpaala
veedhaththaan veengkaDaththaan viNNOr muDithOyum,
paadhaththaan paadham paNindhu.
131.meaning: When the mind whirls toward Tirumal, letting go of the shoulders of women and fixing itself instead upon the scriptures—this becomes easy by bowing to the feet of Him of the fourfold Vedas, who dwells at Venkatam, whose feet the crowns of the celestials touch.

132.translit:
sirandhaven sindhaiyum sengkaN aravum,
niRaindhaseer neeLkacchi yuLLum, - uRaindhadhuvum,
veengkaDamum vehkaavum veeLukkaip paaDiyumee,
taamkaDavaar taNDuzhaa yaar.
132.meaning: He of the cool tulasi, who is beholden to none, has come to abide in my exalted mind, upon the red-eyed serpent (Adisesha), within glorious and spacious Kanchi, and at Venkatam, at Tiruvehka, and at Velukkaippadi.

133.translit:
seerndha thirumaal kaDalkuDandhai veengkaDam
neerndhaven sindhai niRaivisumpu, - vaayndha
maRaiyaa Dakamanandhan vaNDuzhaayk kaNNi,
iRaipaaDi yaaya ivai.
133.meaning: Tirumal has joined and settled in these places: the ocean, Tirukkudandai (Kumbakonam), Venkatam, my own surrendered mind, the fulfilling sky (Paramapadam), the fitting Tiruvananthapuram of the Vedas, and—He of Anantha and of the tulasi-garland abuzz with bees—Tiruvithuvakkodu (the Lord's town).

134.translit:
paaRkaDalum veengkaDamum paampum panivisumpum,
noorkaDalum nuNNula thaamaraimeel, - paaRpaT
Dirundhaar manamum iDamaakak koNDaan,
kurundhosiththa kOpaa lakan.
134.meaning: The cowherd who broke the kurundu tree took as His abodes the ocean of milk, Venkatam, the serpent (Adisesha), the cool sky, the ocean of the scriptures, the subtle lotus (where Brahma sits), and the minds of those who are wholly devoted to Him.

135.translit:
iRaiyaay nilanaaki eNDisaiyum thaanaay,
maRaiyaay maRaipporuLaay vaanaay - piRaivaayndha
veLLath taruvi viLangkolineer veengkaDaththaan,
uLLaththi nuLLee uLan.
135.meaning: Being the Lord, becoming the earth, becoming the eight directions Himself, being the Vedas and the meaning of the Vedas and the heavens—the Lord of Venkatam, with its bright-sounding waters and cascades flowing in flood-streams that reach the crescent moon, dwells within the heart.

136.translit:
uLankaNDaay nannenjee. uththama nenrum
uLankaNDaay, uLLuvaa ruLLath thuLankaNDaay,
viNNeDungkak kODuyarum veeng karuvi veengkaDaththaan,
maNNeDungkath thaanaLandha man.
136.meaning: O good heart, see, He exists! See, the Supreme One exists always! See, He exists within the hearts of those who meditate on Him. The Lord of Venkatam, whose peaks rise to stir the sky and whose cascades swell, is the King who measured out the earth so that it trembled.

137.translit:
purindhu madhaveezham maappiDiyO Doodith,
tirindhu sinaththaal porudhu, virindhaseer veNkOTTu
muththudhirkkum veengkaDamee, meelorunaaL
maNkOTTuk koNDaan malai.
137.meaning: Venkatam—where the rutting elephant, sulking and quarrelling with its mate, wanders and fights in fury and scatters pearls from its splendid white tusks—is the hill of Him who once lifted up the earth upon His tusk (as the Boar).

138.translit:
teLindha silaadhalaththin meelirundha mandhi,
aLindha kaDuvanaiyee nOkki, - viLangkiya
veNmadhiyam thaavennum veengkaDamee, meelorunaaL
maNmadhiyil koNDukandhaan vaazhvu.
138.meaning: Venkatam—where a she-monkey, seated upon a clear flat rock, looks toward her loving mate and bids him 'Bring me that shining white moon!'—is the dwelling of Him who once gladly took the earth (measuring it cleverly in the days of Vamana).

139.translit:
paNDellaam veengkaDam paaRkaDal vaikundham,
koNDang kuRaivaarkkuk kOyilpool, - vaNDu
vaLangkiLarum neeLsOlai vaNpoong kaDikai,
iLangkumaran ranviN Nakar.
139.meaning: Just as Venkatam, the ocean of milk, and Vaikuntha have of old been the temples where He took up abode and dwelt, so too is Tirukkadigai—rich with long groves where bees swarm in plenty and with lovely blossoms—the heaven-town of the youthful Lord.

140.translit:
viNNakaram veqkaa viritirai neer veengkaTam,
maNNakaram maamaaTa veeLukkai, maNNakatta
ten kuTantai teenaar tiruvarangkam ten kooTTi,
tan kuTangkai neer eeRRaan taazhvu.
140.meaning: He who once received the offered water into the hollow of His palm (as the dwarf Vamana, bowing low) — His abodes of grace are Vinnagaram, Vehka, the place of spreading-waved waters at Venkatam, the great-mansioned Velukkai of Mannagaram, Tenkudantai (Kumbakonam) on this earth, honey-rich holy Srirangam, and southern Kotti.

141.translit:
taazh saTaiyum neeN muTiyum oN mazhuvum chakkaramum,
choozh aravum ponnaaNum tooNRumaal,- choozhum
tiraNT aruvi paayum tirumalai meel entaikku,
iraNT uruvum onRaay ichaintu.
141.meaning: Upon the holy hill where gathered cascades flow all around, my Lord appears with two forms harmonized into one: the hanging matted locks (of Shiva) and the long crown (of Vishnu), the bright battle-axe and the discus, the encircling serpent and the golden waist-cord — all manifest together.

142.translit:
paartta kaTuvan chunai neer nizhaR kaNTu, peert oor
kaTuvan enap peerntu, - kaartta
kaLangkanikkuk kai neeTTum veengkaTamee, meenaaL
viLangkanikkuk kanR eRintaan veRpu.
142.meaning: This is Venkatam, where a male monkey, seeing its own reflection in the spring water and mistaking it for another rival monkey, turns away — and then reaches out its hand toward the dark, ripe blackberry-like fruit. It is the hill of Him who long ago, to bring down the wood-apple fruit, hurled the calf (the demon Vatsasura) at it.

143.translit:
veRp enRu veengkaTam paaTum, viyan tuzhaayk
kaRp enRu chooTum karung kuzhal meel, maR ponRa
neeNTa tooL maal kiTanta neeL kaTal neer aaTuvaan,
pooNTa naaL ellaam pukum.
143.meaning: She sings 'the hill!' meaning Venkatam, and upon her dark tresses she wears wondrous tulasi as if it were her vow of chastity. To bathe in the vast ocean where reclines Tirumal, the long-shouldered one who destroyed wrestlers, she sets out — every appointed day she enters in to do so.

144.translit:
puku matattaal vaay poochik keezh taazhntu, aruvi
uku matattaal kaal kazhuvik kaiyaal, miku mata teen
viNTa malar koNTu viRal veeng kaTavaiyee,
kaNTu vaNangkum kaLiRu.
144.meaning: With the gushing rut-fluid (musth) it rinses its mouth and bows low; with the rut-fluid pouring like a cascade it washes its feet, and with its trunk it takes blossoms opened and brimming with abundant honey — thus the elephant beholds and worships none but the mighty Lord of Venkatam.

145.translit:
kaLiRu mukil kuttak kai eTutt ooTi,
oLiRu maruppu ochi kai yaaLi, - piLiRi
vizha, konRu ninR atirum veengkaTamee, meenaaL
kuzhak kanRu koNT eRintaan kunRu.
145.meaning: This is Venkatam, where an elephant, when a cloud strikes it, raises its trunk and runs; and the yaali (lion-beast), its gleaming tusk-fang snapping, trumpeting falls — slain, the place stands resounding. It is the hill of Him who long ago took the young calf and flung it (to kill the demon).

146.translit:
kunR onRin aaya kuRa makaLir kool vaLaik kai,
chenRu viLaiyaaTum teeng kazhai pooy, - venRu
viLangku mati kooL viTukkum veengkaTamee,
meelai iLang kumarar koomaan iTam.
146.meaning: This is Venkatam, where the sweet tall bamboo with which the bangle-wristed hill-women of one peak go and play, growing upward, frees the shining moon that has triumphed from its eclipse (Rahu's seizing). It is the abode of the Lord of the eternal young celestials.

147.translit:
iTam valam eezh pooNTa iravit teer ooTTi,
vaTa muka veengkaTattu mannum, - kuTam nayanta
koottanaay ninRaan kurai kazhalee kooRuvatee,
naat tannaal uLLa nalam.
147.meaning: He drives the sun's chariot yoked with seven (horses) on left and right, and abides on the northward-facing Venkatam; He who stood as the dancer who delighted in the pot-dance (kudak-koothu). To utter with the tongue the praise of His resounding anklet-feet — that alone is the true good the tongue can hold.

148.translit:
chaarnta kaTu teeyppat taTaaviya kooTT uchchi vaay
oornt iyangkum veN matiyin oN muyalai, - cheerntu
china veengkai paarkkum tirumalaiyee, aayan
puna veengkai naaRum poruppu.
148.meaning: This is the holy hill where, upon the high curved peak that grinds against the moving sky, the angry tiger gazes at the bright hare-mark of the white moon that travels rubbing past it (mistaking it for prey). It is the mountain fragrant with the wild venkai blossoms of the cowherd's land.

149.translit:
muTinta pozhutil kuRavaaNar, eenam
paTint uzhu chaal pain tinaikaL vitta, - taTint ezhunta
veeyng kazhai pooy viN tiRakkum veengkaTamee, meel oru naaL
teeng kuzhal vaay vaittaan chilampu.
149.meaning: This is Venkatam, where the bamboo — cut down yet shooting up again — rises and pierces the sky in the furrows the hill-dwellers ploughed (the way wild boars root the soil) when, the season ended, they had sown the fresh millet. It is the mountain of Him who once long ago set the sweet flute to His lips.

150.translit:
kaaNkinRanakaLum keeTkinRanakaLum kaaNil, in naaL
paaN kunRa naaTar payilkinRana, itu ellaam aRintoom
maaN kunRam eenti taN maa malai veengkaTatt umbar nampum
seeN kunRam chenRu poruL paTaippaan kaRRa tiNNanavee.
150.meaning: If we look at the things now being seen and being heard, the men of the song-filled hill-country are practising them these days — all this we have understood. It is surely the cunning they have learned, to go off to the far high hill, the cool great mountain Venkatam lifted up in glory which even the celestials trust, in order to amass wealth there.

151.translit:
maayoon vaTa tiruveengkaTa naaTa valli koTikaaL
nooyoo uraikkilum keeTkinRileer uraiyeer numatu
vaayoo atu anRi val vinaiyeenum kiLiyum eLkum
aayoo aTum toNTaiyoo aRaiyoo itu aRivu aritee.
151.meaning: O you slender creeper-like maidens of the northern holy Venkatam country of the dark wondrous Lord! Even when I speak of my malady you do not listen — do tell me: is it your mouth, or else (the lips of) this sinful one of mine, which even the parrot is shamed by? Is it the killing tondai-fruit-red lips? Alas! This is hard to know.

152.translit:
kayaloo numa kaNkaL? enRu kaLiRu vinavi niRReer,
ayaloor aRiyilum eet enna vaarttai, kaTal kavarnta
puyaloT ulaam koNTal vaNNan puna veengkaTatt emmoTum
payaloo vileer, kollaik kaakkinRa naaLum pala palavee.
152.meaning: O elephant-gaited men, you stand asking 'are your eyes carp-fish?' — what kind of speech is this, that even strangers may hear it? Were you not children together with us, of the dark-cloud-hued Lord (hued like the rain-cloud that drank up the sea), in His wild Venkatam — and many, many were the days we guarded the millet-field together?

153.translit:
ichaiminkaL toot enR ichaittaal ichaiyilam, en talai meel
achaiminkaL enRaal achaiyingkol aam, am pon maa maNikaL
tichaimin miLirum tiruveengkaTatt van taaL chimayam
michaimin miLiriya poovaan vazhik koNTa meekangkaLee.
153.meaning: O clouds that have taken the path to go gleaming upon the lofty-footed summit of holy Venkatam, where beautiful golden great gems flash like lightning in every direction! When I say 'consent to be my messengers,' you do not consent; yet if I say 'rest upon my head,' will you even rest here? (She pleads in vain.)

154.translit:
oN nutal maamai oLi paya vaamai, viraintu nan teer
naNNutal veeNTum valava! kaTaakinRu teen navinRa
vaN mutal naayakan neeL muTi veN mutta vaachikaittaay
maN mutal cheervuRRu aruvi cheyyaa niRkum maa malaikkee.
154.meaning: O skilled charioteer! Drive on now, so that the loveliness and radiance of her bright forehead may not drain away; our good chariot must swiftly reach the great mountain — where, like a garland of white pearls upon the long crown of the bounteous primal Lord whom the bees haunt, the cascades pour down and join with the earth at its base.

155.translit:
mulaiyoo muzhu muRRum poontila, moy poong kuzhal kuRiya
kalaiyoo arai illai naavoo kuzhaRum, kaTal maN ellaam
vilaiyoo ena miLirum kaN ivaL paramee perumaan
malaiyoo tiruveeng kaTam enRu kaRkinRaa vaachakamee.
155.meaning: Her breasts have not yet fully come forth, her thick lovely tresses are short, there is no garment about her waist, her tongue still lisps, her eyes shine as though to outprice all the sea-girt earth — is it within this little girl's power to learn and recite the words, 'the Lord's mountain is holy Venkatam'? (Wonders the mother.)

156.translit:
kaaviyum neelamum veelum kayalum pala pala enRu
aaviyin tanmai aLav alla paarippu asurar cheRRa
maa viyam puL valla maatavan koovintan veengkaTam cheer
too viyam peeTai annaaL kaNkaL aaya tuNai malaree.
156.meaning: Saying 'they are like the blue lily, the blue water-lily, the spear, the carp, and many such' — the spreading reach (of her glances) is beyond the measure of mere life. The twin blossoms that are the eyes of her, who is like the bright pure-winged pea-hen, belong to (long to reach) Venkatam of Madhava-Govinda, the slayer of demons who rides the great wondrous bird (Garuda).

157.translit:
urukinRa kanmangkaL meelaana oorppu ilaraay, ivaLaip
perukinRa taayar meyn nontu peRaarkol tuzhaay kuzhal vaayt
tuRukinRilar tollai veengkaTam aaTTavum choozhkinRilar
irukinRatu aal ivaL aakam, mel aavi eri koLLavee.
157.meaning: Having no thought of the soul-melting deeds (of devotion that ought to be done), did the mothers who reared this girl give birth to her without true care of body? They do not press tulasi into her tresses' mouth, nor do they contrive to take her to ancient Venkatam; meanwhile her body wastes away, and her tender life is seized by fire.

158.translit:
kaNNaa vaan enRum, maNNoor viNNoorkku,
taN aar veengkaTa, viNNoor veRpanee.
158.meaning: O You who are forever the eye (and Lord) for the people of earth and the dwellers of heaven; O Lord of cool-abounding Venkatam, You who are the hill-Lord of the celestials!

159.translit:
entaay taN tiruveengkaTattuL ninRaay! ilangkai cheRRaay, maraamaram
pain taaL eezhuruva oru vaali kootta villaa!
kontaar taN an tuzhaayinaay amutee! unnai en uLLee kuzhaitta em
maintaa! vaan eeRee, ini engkup pookinRatee?
159.meaning: O my Father who stand within cool holy Venkatam! O You who destroyed Lanka, O archer who fixed a single arrow to pierce through the seven green-trunked maramara trees! O Nectar wearing the cool, lovely, clustered tulasi! O my strong one who have melted Yourself into my very being, O Lord ascended in glory — now where could (my heart) possibly go (away from You)?

160.translit:
pookinRa kaalangaL pooya kaalan-
gaL poohu kaalangaL, taay tandai uyi-
raakinRaay unnai naan adaindeen viduveenoo,
paakinRa tol pukazh moovulahukkum naadanee. paramaa, taN veengkada-
meekinRaay taN tuzhaay virai naaRu kaNNiyanee.
160.meaning: The times that are passing, the times that have passed, and the times yet to come — through them all You are the very life-breath, my mother and my father. Having attained You, would I ever let You go? You are the Lord of the three worlds, of ancient widespread fame; O Supreme One, You who mount cool Venkatam, You who wear the fragrant tulasi garland.

161.translit:
taamoodaranai tani mudalvanai njaalam uNdavanai,
aam odara maRiya oruvarkku enRu tozhumavarhaL,
taam odaran uruvaakiya sivaRkum tisaimuhaRkum,
aam odara maRiya emmaanai en aazhi vaNNanaiyee.
161.meaning: Damodara, the one peerless First Cause, who swallowed the earth — those who worship Him, asking that the truth be made known to them. He is the Lord who makes the truth known even to Siva (who became part of His own form) and to the four-faced one (Brahma); He is my Lord of the hue of the dark ocean.

162.translit:
ozhivil kaalam ellaam udanaay manni,
vazhuvilaa adimai seyya veeNdum naam,
tezhi kural aruvi tiruveengkadattu,
ezhil koL soodi endai tandai tandaikkee.
162.meaning: For all unceasing time, abiding ever in His presence, we must render flawless service. To Him of beauteous radiance at Tiruvenkatam, where the cascades roar with sounding voice — He who is the father of my father's father.

163.translit:
endai tandai tandai tandaikkum
mundai, vaanavar vaanavar koonodum,
sindu poo mahizhum tiruveengkadattu,
anda mil pukazh kaar ezhil aNNalee.
163.meaning: He who is prior even to the father of my father's father's father; at Tiruvenkatam, where the celestials together with the king of celestials rejoice amid scattered flowers. He is the Lord of endless fame and dark cloud-like beauty.

164.translit:
aNNal maayan aNi koL sen-taamarai
kaNNan, sengkani vaay karu maaNikkam,
teN niRai sunai neer tiruveengkadattu,
eNNil tol pukazh vaanavar eesanee.
164.meaning: The Lord, the wondrous one, with eyes like the lovely red lotus, with lips red as ripe fruit, dark as a sapphire gem. At Tiruvenkatam of clear brimming mountain-pool waters, He is the Lord of the celestials, of countless ancient fame.

165.translit:
eesan vaanavarkku enban enRaal, adu
teesamoo tiruveengkadattaanukku?,
neesan en niRaivu onRum ileen, en kaN
paasam vaitta paranjsudar soodikkee.
165.meaning: If I should say 'He is the Lord of the celestials,' would that even be praise enough for Him of Tiruvenkatam? I am lowly, possessing no fullness of any kind — yet to that supreme effulgent radiance who has set His loving bond upon my eyes (I belong).

166.translit:
soodi yaahi ellaa ulahum tozhum,
aadi moortti enRaal aLavahumoo?,
veediyar muzhu veedattu amudattai,
teedil seer tiruveengkadattaanaiyee.
166.meaning: He is the radiance, the primal Lord whom all the worlds worship — and even to say so, can it measure Him? He is the nectar of the whole Veda of the brahmins, He of Tiruvenkatam of flawless glory.

167.translit:
veengkadangkaL meym meel vinai muRRavum,
taangkaL tangkadku nallanavee seyvaar,
veengkadattu uRaivaarkku nama venna-
laang kadamai, adu sumandaarkadkee.
167.meaning: For those upon whom accumulated karma presses down — they do only what is truly good for themselves who simply say 'namah' to Him who dwells at Venkatam. That is the duty fitting for those who have borne this (devotion) as their burden.

168.translit:
sumandu maa malar neer sudar deepam koNdu,
amarndu vaanavar vaanavar koonodum,
namanRu ezhum tiruveengkadam nangkadku,
saman koL veedu tarum tadang kunRamee.
168.meaning: Bearing great flowers, water, and bright lamps, abiding there, the celestials together with the king of celestials bow and rise — Tiruvenkatam. That vast hill grants us liberation of perfect equanimity.

169.translit:
kunRam eendi kuLir mazhai kaattavan,
anRu njaalam aLanda piraan, paran
senRu seer tiruveengkada maamalai,
onRumee tozha nam vinai ooyumee.
169.meaning: He who lifted up the hill and warded off the cold rain; the Lord who that day measured the earth, the Supreme One — He went and reached the great hill of Tiruvenkatam. If we but worship it alone, our karma will cease.

170.translit:
ooyum mooppu pirappu iRappu piNi,
veeyum aaRu seyvaan tiruveengkadat-
taayan, naaN malar aam adi taamarai,
vaayuLLum manattuLLum vaippaarkadkee.
170.meaning: Wasting age, birth, death, and disease — He makes them perish, the cowherd of Tiruvenkatam. For those who place His fresh-blossom lotus feet within their mouths and within their minds (these afflictions perish).

171.translit:
vaitta naaL varai ellai kuRuhi senRu,
eyttu iLaippadan munnam adaiminoo,
paitta paambu aNaiyaan tiruveengkadam,
moytta soolai moy poon-tadam taazhvaree.
171.meaning: Before the appointed span of your days draws near its limit and you grow weary and faint, go and reach Him — Tiruvenkatam of the Lord who reclines on the hooded serpent, with its dense groves and the lovely flowered ponds of its slopes.

172.translit:
taaL parappi maN vataaviya eesanai,
neeL pozhil kurukoor sadakoopan sol,
keezhil aayirattu ip pattum vallavar,
vaazhvar vaazhveydi njaalam pukazhavee.
172.meaning: Of the Lord who spread out His feet and measured the earth, these are the words of Satakopan of Kurukoor of vast groves. Those who master these ten of the peerless thousand will live in prosperity, attaining true life as the world praises them.

173.translit:
vaar punal an taN aruvi vada tiruveengkadattu endai,
peer pala solli pitaRRi pittar enRee piRar kooRa,
oor pala pukkum puhaadum ulohar sirikka ninRu aadi,
aarvam peruhi kunippaar amarar tozhappaduvaaree.
173.meaning: My Lord of northern Tiruvenkatam with its cool flowing cascades — those who, uttering His many names and babbling, are called madmen by others; who enter many a town and pass others by, dancing while the worldly laugh, swelling with love and leaping in joy — they shall become ones whom even the immortals worship.

174.translit:
sonnaal viroodam idu aahilum solluveen keeNminoo,
en naavil in kavi yaan oruvarkkum kodukkileen,
tennaa tenaa venRu vaNdu mural tiruveengkadattu,
en aanai en appan emperumaan uLanaahavee.
174.meaning: Though saying this may seem an offense, I will still say it — listen: I will not give the sweet poetry of my tongue to anyone (else). At Tiruvenkatam where the bees hum 'tennaa, tenaa,' my elephant, my father, my Lord exists (for me alone).

175.translit:
maari maaraada taN am malai veengkadattu aNNalai,
vaari vaaraada paim poom pozhil soozh kurukoor nahar,
kaari maaRan sadakoopan sol aayirattu ip pattaal,
veeri maaraada poo meel iruppaaL vinai teerkkumee.
175.meaning: Of the Lord of cool, beautiful Venkata hill where the rains never cease; (sung by) Satakopan, son of Kari, of Kurukoor city ringed by fresh flowered groves whose abundance never fails. Through these ten of his thousand verses, She who dwells upon the never-fading fragrant lotus (Lakshmi) will end our karma.

176.translit:
maalukku vaiyam aLanda maNaaLaRku,
neela karu niRa meeha niyaayaRku,
koola sen-taamarai kaNNaRku, en kongalar
eela kuzhali izhanddadu sangkee.
176.meaning: To Tirumal, to the bridegroom-Lord who measured the world, to Him of the form like a dark blue cloud, to Him of the lovely red-lotus eyes — my daughter, with fragrant blossom-decked, cardamom-scented tresses, has lost her bangles.

177.translit:
sangku vil vaaL taNdu sakkara kaiyaRku,
sengani vaay seyya taamarai kaNNaRku,
kongalar taN am tuzhaay mudiyaanukku, en
mangai izhanddadu maamai niRamee.
177.meaning: To Him who bears in His hands the conch, bow, sword, mace, and discus, to Him of lips red as ripe fruit and eyes like the red lotus, to Him crowned with the cool, lovely, blossom-laden tulasi — my maiden has lost the golden hue of her complexion.

178.translit:
niRam kariyaanukku needu ulahu uNda,
tiRam kiLar vaay siRu kaLLan avaRku,
kaRangiya sakkara kaiyavanukku, en
piRang iru koondal izhanddadu peedee.
178.meaning: To Him of dark complexion, to that little thief of surpassing prowess who swallowed the vast worlds, to Him in whose hand the discus whirls — my (daughter) of lustrous, dark, abundant hair has lost her dignity.

179.translit:
peed udai naanmuhanai padaittaanukku,
maad udai vaiyam aLanda maNaaLaRku,
naad udai mannarkku tuudu sel nambikku, en
paad udai alkul izhanddadu paNbee.
179.meaning: To Him who created the glorious four-faced Brahma, to the bridegroom-Lord who measured the wealth-laden earth, to the Lord who went as envoy to the kings who held the land — my (daughter) of the broad, lovely waist has lost her gentle nature.

180.translit:
paNbudai veedam payanda paranukku,
maNburai vaiyam idanda varaaharkku,
teNpunal paLLiyen deevap piraanukku, en
kaNpunai koodai izhandadu karpee.
180.meaning: To the Supreme One who brought forth the Veda full of noble qualities, to the Boar (Varaha) who lifted up the earth-clad world, to my Lord, the God who reclines on the clear waters — my daughter Kothai, of the eye-adorning garland, has lost her chastity (her self-possession in love).

181.translit:
karpagak kaavana narpala tooLarku,
porsudark kunranna poondaN mudiyarku,
narpala taamarai naaNmalark kaiyarku, en
virpuru vakkodi toorradu meyyee.
181.meaning: To Him whose many goodly shoulders are like a grove of celestial wish-granting (karpaka) trees, to Him whose cool flower-crown shines like a hill of golden radiance, to Him whose hands are like many fresh-blown lotus flowers — my girl whose eyebrows are like a bent bow has truly lost herself (her very being).

182.translit:
meyyamar palkalan nankaNin daanukku,
paiyara vinaNaip paLLiyi naanukku,
kaiyodu kaalseyya kaNNa piraanukku, en
taiyal izhandadu tannudaic chaayee.
182.meaning: To Him who has fittingly adorned His body with many fine ornaments, to Him who reclines on the bed of the hooded serpent, to Lord Kanna (Krishna) whose hands and feet are red — my tender maiden has lost her own lustre (her bodily glow).

183.translit:
saayak kurundam oshitta tamiyarku,
maayac chakadam udaitta maNaaLarku,
peeyaip piNampadap paaluN piraanukku, en
vaasak kuzhali izhandadu maaNbee.
183.meaning: To the peerless One who, alone, broke down the kurinda tree, to the Bridegroom who kicked apart the deceitful cart-demon, to the Lord who drank the milk and made the demoness (Putana) fall as a corpse — my girl of fragrant tresses has lost her dignity (her composure).

184.translit:
maaNbamai koolatte maayak kuraLarku,
seeNsudark kunranna senjudar moorttikku,
kaaNberun toorrattes kaakutta nambikku, en
pooNpunai menmulai toorradu porpee.
184.meaning: To our wondrous Dwarf (Vamana) of dignified, perfect beauty, to the form of red radiance like a far-shining hill of light, to our noble Lord Kakuttha (Rama) of grand, splendid appearance — my soft-breasted girl, adorned with jewels, has lost her loveliness.

185.translit:
porpamai neeNmudip poondaN duzhaayarku,
marporu tooLudai maayap piraanukku,
nirpana palluru vaaynirku maayarku, en
karpudai yaatti yizhandadu kattee.
185.meaning: To Him of the long crown of perfect beauty wreathed with cool fresh tulasi, to the wondrous Lord whose shoulders wrestle (in combat), to the marvel who stands taking the many forms of all that stands (all beings) — my chaste maiden has lost her firmness (her restraint).

186.translit:
katte zhil soolainal veengada vaaNanai,
katte zhil tenkuru koorcchada koopansol,
katte zhil aayirat tippattum vallavar,
katte zhil vaanavar poogamuN paaree.
186.meaning: He who dwells in Venkatam of abundant beauteous groves; the words of Satakopan of beautiful southern Kurukur; whoever masters these ten among the beautiful thousand — they shall partake of the abundant bliss of the radiant celestials (in the Eternal Realm).

187.translit:
ulagam uNda peruvaayaa! ulappil keerti yammaanee!
nilavum sudarsoo zhoLimoortti. nediyaay adiyee naaruyiree!
tiladam ulaguk kaayninra tiruveeng kadattem perumaanee!
kulatol ladiyeen unpaadam koodu maaru kooraayee.
187.meaning: O great-mouthed One who swallowed the worlds! O Lord of unending fame! O form of light girdled by abiding radiance! O lofty One, O very life of me Your servant! O our Lord of holy Venkatam who stand as the sacred mark (tilaka) of the world! Tell me the way by which I, Your servant of ancient lineage, may reach Your feet.

188.translit:
kooraay neeraay nilanaagik koduval lasurar kulamellaam
seeraa eriyum tirunemi valavaa. teyvak koomaanee!
seeraar sunaittaa maraisendee malarum tiruveeng kadattaanee!
aaraa anbil adi yeenun adiseer vaNNam aruLaayee.
188.meaning: O wielder of the holy discus in Your right hand, which rages and hurls down the whole race of cruel mighty demons, cleaving them to dust and ash! O divine King! O Lord of holy Venkatam, where lotuses bloom like red fire in the mud-rich pools! Grant the grace that I, Your servant of unceasing love, may reach Your feet.

189.translit:
vaNNa maruLkoL aNimeega vaNNaa! maaya ammaanee!
eNNam pugundu tittikkum amudee imaiyoor adipatiyee!
