teNNal aruvi maNiponmut talaikkum tiruveeng kadattaanee!
aNNa lee! un adiseera adiyeer kaavaa vennaayee.
189.meaning: O One hued like a lovely cloud of bewildering color! O wondrous Lord! O nectar that enters my thoughts and turns sweet, O sovereign of the deathless gods! O Lord of holy Venkatam, where the clear cool cascades dash down gems, gold and pearls! O noble Master! Say but 'Have no fear' so that I, Your servant, may reach Your feet.

190.translit:
aavaa vennaa dulagattai alaikkum asurar vaaNaaLmeel,
teevaay vaaLi mazhaipozhinda silaiyaa tirumaa magaLkeeLvaa,
teevaa! surargaL munik kaNangaL virumbum tiruveeng kadattaanee!
poovaar kazhalgaL aruvinaiyeen porundu maaru puNaraayee.
190.meaning: O master of the bow that rained down fiery arrows upon the lifespan of the demons who tormented the world without pity! O beloved consort of Tirumamagal (Lakshmi)! O God! O Lord of holy Venkatam, longed for by the gods and the throngs of sages! Grant that I, of grievous karma, may unite with Your flower-decked anklets (feet).

191.translit:
puNaraa ninra marameezhan reyda voruvil valavaavoo,
puNareey ninra maramiraNdin naduvee poona mudalvaavoo,
tiNaraar meegam enakkaLiru seerum tiruveeng kadattaanee,
tiNaraar saarngat tunpaadam seerva tadiyee nennaaLee?
191.meaning: O matchless master of the single bow who that day pierced the seven trees standing in a row! O primordial One who passed between the two joined (marudu) trees! O Lord of holy Venkatam, where elephants gather like dense thick clouds! When shall I, Your servant, reach Your feet, O wielder of the mighty Sharnga bow?

192.translit:
ennaa leenaam maNNaLanda iNaittaa maraigaL kaaNbadarken ru,
ennaa Lumnin rimaiyoorgaL eetti yiraignji yinaminamaay,
meynnaa manattaal vazhipaadu seyyum tiruveeng kadattaanee,
meynnaa neydi yennaaLun adikkaN adiyeen meevuvadee?
192.meaning: O Lord of holy Venkatam, where the deathless ones, group upon group, stand forever — saying 'when shall we behold the twin lotus feet that measured the earth?' — and worship with truthful word and mind, praising and bowing down! On what day shall I, Your servant, truly attain and abide at Your feet?

193.translit:
adiyeen meevi yama rkinra amudee. imaiyoor adipatiyee,
kodiyaa adupuL Ludaiyaanee. koolak kanivaayp perumaanee,
sediyaar vinaigaL teermarundee. tiruveeng kadattem perumaanee,
nodiyaar pozhudum unpaadam kaaNa noolaa daarreenee.
193.meaning: O nectar in whom I Your servant abide and rejoice! O sovereign of the deathless gods! O Lord who holds as banner the slaying bird (Garuda)! O Lord of the beautiful ripe-fruit (red) mouth! O medicine that cures the thicket-thick karmas! O our Lord of holy Venkatam! Without practicing penance, I cannot bear even for a split second not to behold Your feet.

194.translit:
noolaa daarreen nunpaadam kaaNa venru nuNNuNarvil,
neelaar kaNdat tammaanum niranaan muganu mindiranum,
seeleey kaNNaar palarsoozha virumbum tiruveeng kadattaanee,
maalaay mayakki yadiyeenpaal vandaay poola vaaraayee.
194.meaning: Saying 'without penance I cannot bear to behold Your feet' — O Lord of holy Venkatam, whom the blue-throated Lord (Shiva), the full four-faced Brahma, and Indra, and many maidens with fish-like eyes long for and surround, with subtle insight! As You once came to me, Your servant, casting Your beguiling spell — even so, come again!

195.translit:
vandaay poolee vaaraadaay. vaaraa daaypool varuvaanee,
sendaa maraikkaN sengaNivaay naaltoo Lamudee. enaduyiree,
sindaa maNigaL pagarallaip pagal sey tiruveeng kadattaanee,
andoo adiyeen unpaadam agala killeen iraiyumee.
195.meaning: O One who, having seemed to come, came not; and who, seeming not to come, yet comes! O nectar with red-lotus eyes, red ripe-fruit mouth and four shoulders! O my very life! O Lord of holy Venkatam, where wish-granting gems (chintamani) turn night into day! Alas — I, Your servant, cannot bear to part from Your feet even for an instant.

196.translit:
agala killeen iraiyum en ralarmeel mang kai yuraimaarbaa,
nigaril pugazhaay. ulagamoon rudaiyaay. ennai aaLvaanee,
nigaril amarar munikkaNangaL virumbum tiruveeng kadattaanee,
pugalon rillaa adiyeenun adikkee zhamarndu pugundeenee.
196.meaning: 'I cannot part even for an instant' — so saying, the Lady on the lotus (Lakshmi) dwells upon Your chest. O One of peerless glory! O owner of the three worlds! O One who rules me! O Lord of holy Venkatam, longed for by the matchless immortals and the throngs of sages! Having no other refuge, I Your servant have entered and settled beneath Your feet.

197.translit:
adikkee zhamarndu pugundadiyeer. vaazhmin enrenr aruLkodukkum
padikkee zhillaap perumaanaip pazhanak kurukoorc chadakoopan,
mudippaan sonna aayirattut tiruveeng kadattuk kivaipattum,
pidittaar pidittaar veerrirundu periya vaanuL nilaavuvaree.
197.meaning: Of the Lord of unmatched nature, who grants grace saying again and again 'O servants, having entered and settled beneath My feet, live and flourish!' — Satakopan of fertile Kurukur sang, to complete (his thousand): these ten on Venkatam, among the thousand. All who hold fast to them, all who grasp them, shall be enthroned and abide forever in the great heaven.

198.translit:
nangaL varivaLai yaayang kaaLoo! nammudai eedalar munbu naaNi,
nungat kiyaanonru raikkum maarram nookkugin reenengum kaaNa maatteen,
sangam sarindana saayi zhandeen tadamulai ponnira maayta Larndeen,
vengaN paravaiyin paaga nengoon veengada vaaNaNai veeNdic chenree.
198.meaning: O my companions of streaked bangles! Ashamed before our hostile kinsfolk, I look for some word to say to you — but I find none, anywhere. My bangles have slipped off, I have lost my lustre; my full breasts have grown pale-golden and faded — all because I went longing after the Lord of Venkatam, our King, the rider of the fierce-eyed bird (Garuda).

199.translit:
inrippooga iruvinaiyungeduttu
onriyaakkaipugaamai uyyakkoLvaan
ninraveengadam neeNilattuLLadu
senrudeevargaL kaitozhuvaargaLee.
199.meaning: Venkatam, where the Lord stands to redeem us — destroying both kinds of karma (good and bad) so that we never again enter and merge with the body (of birth) — exists here on this vast earth. The very gods go there and worship Him with folded hands.

20.translit:
naagatthi naNaiyaanai nannudhalaaL nayandhuraisey
meegatthai veengkadakkoon vidudhoodhil viNNappam
boogatthil vazhuvaadha pudhuvaiyarkoon koadhaidhamizh
aagatthu vaitthuraippaar avaradiyaa raaguvaree
20.meaning: This is the supplication contained in the message of envoy that fair-browed Goithai, with love, sent through the cloud to the Lord of Venkatam, He who reclines upon the serpent-couch. Those who keep upon their hearts and recite this Tamil of Goithai, chieftainess of Puduvai who never strays from devotion, they shall become His servants.

21.translit:
paadum kuyilkaaL eedhenna paadalnal veengkada
naadar namakkoru vaazhvudhan thaalvandhu paadumin
aadum karuLak kodiyudai yaarvan dharuLseydhu
kooduva raayidil koovinum paattukaL keettumee
21.meaning: O singing cuckoos, what song is this? If the Lord of the goodly Venkatam land grants us a life, come and sing. If He who bears the banner of the dancing eagle (Garuda) comes, shows grace, and unites with me, then I will call you and listen to your songs.

22.translit:
mazhaiyee mazhaiyee maNpuRam poosiyuL Laaynindra
mezhukoottri naaRpoal ootrunal veengkadath thuLnindra
azhagap piraanaar thammaiyen nenjath thagappadath
thazhuvanindru ennaith thadhartthikkoN dootravum vallaiyee
22.meaning: O rain, O rain! Like wax that is smeared outside with clay and melts and pours forth within, pour down! Are you able to make the gracious beautiful Lord who abides in goodly Venkatam come within the grasp of my heart, so that He embraces me, and to press me close and pour Himself upon me?

23.translit:
oonaeRu selvath thudaRpiRavi yaanveeNdeen
aaneeReezh vendraa nadimaith thiRamallaal
kooneeRu sangka midatthaanthan veengkadatthu
kooneeri vaazhum kurugaayp piRappeenee
23.meaning: I do not desire this birth in a flesh-grown, prosperous body. I want nothing but the state of servitude to Him who conquered the seven bulls. May I be born as a crane that lives at Koneri (the tank) in the Venkatam of Him who holds the curved conch in His left hand.

24.translit:
aanaadha selvath tharambaiyarkaL thaRsoozha
vaanaaLum selvamum maNNarasum yaanveeNdeen
theenaarpoonj soolaith thiruveengka dachchunaiyil
meenaayp piRakkum vidhiyudaiyee naaveenee
24.meaning: With ever-flowing wealth and celestial nymphs (rambha-maidens) surrounding me, even the wealth that rules heaven and the kingship over the earth I do not desire. May I have the destiny to be born as a fish in the spring (sunai) of holy Venkatam, set amid honey-laden flowering groves.

25.translit:
pinnitta sadaiyaanum biramanu mindhiranum
thunnittu pugalariya vaikundha neeLvaasal
minvattach chudaraazhi veengkadakkoon thaanumizhum
ponvattil piditthudanee pugappeRuvee naaveenee
25.meaning: At the long gate of Vaikuntha, hard to enter even by the matted-haired Lord (Shiva), by Brahma, and by Indra who crowd to come, may I be one who attains entry, holding the golden vessel that the Lord of Venkatam, who wields the lightning-discful radiant chakra, casts forth (offers).

26.translit:
oNpavaLa veelai yulavudhaN paaRkadaluL
kaNduyilum maayoon kazhaliNaikaL kaaNbadhaRku
paNpagarum vaNdinangkaL paNpaadum veengkadatthu
seNbagamaay niRkum thiruvudaiyee naaveenee
26.meaning: To behold the twin feet of the wondrous Lord (Mayon) who sleeps with open eyes upon the cool, surging milk-ocean bright with lustrous coral, may I have the blessing of standing as a shenbaka (champak) flower in Venkatam where swarms of bees that utter melody sing their tunes.

27.translit:
kambamadha yaanai kazhutthagatthin meelirundhu
inbamarum selvamu mivvarasum yaanveeNdeen
emberumaa neesa nezhilveeng kadamalaimeel
thambamaay niRkum thavamudaiyee naaveenee
27.meaning: Seated upon the neck of a trembling, rutting elephant, the wealth full of pleasure and this kingship I do not desire. May I have the austerity (the merit) of standing as a pillar (post) upon the beautiful Venkatam hill of my Lord, the supreme God.

28.translit:
minnanaiya nuNNidaiyaa ruruppasiyum meenakaiyum
annavardham paadalodu maadalavai yaadhariyeen
thennavena vaNdinangkaL paNpaadum veengkadatthuL
annanaiya poRkuvadaa marundhavattha naaveenee
28.meaning: The lightning-slender-waisted Urvashi and Menaka, and the singing and dancing of such ones - I do not care for these. May I become one of rare austerity, a golden peak in Venkatam where swarms of bees hum their tunes saying 'thenna-thenna' (their droning refrain).

29.translit:
vaanaaLum maamadhipoal veNkudaikkeezh mannavardham
koonaagi veetrirundhu koNdaadum selvaRiyeen
theenaarpoonj soolaith thiruveeng kadamalaimeel
kaanaaRaayp paayum karutthudaiyee naaveenee
29.meaning: Beneath a white parasol like the great moon that rules the sky, seated as the king of kings and celebrated in splendour - such wealth I do not know (care for). May I have the intent to flow as a forest stream upon the holy Venkatam hill set amid honey-laden flowering groves.

30.translit:
piRaiyeeRu sadaiyaanum biramanu mindhiranum
muRaiyaaya peruveeLvik kuRaimudippaan maRaiyaanaan
veRiyaardhaN soolaith thiruveeng kadamalaimeel
neRiyaayk kidakkum nilaiyudaiyee naaveenee
30.meaning: He who, for the crescent-bearing matted-haired one (Shiva), for Brahma, and for Indra, fulfils the deficiencies of their great rightful sacrifices, He who became the Vedas - upon His holy Venkatam hill amid cool fragrant groves, may I have the state of lying as the path (the very way to Him).

31.translit:
sediyaaya valvinaikaL theerkkum thirumaalee
nediyaanee veengkadavaa ninkooyi linvaasal
adiyaarum vaanavaru marambaiyarum kidandhiyangkum
padiyaayk kidandhun pavaLavaay kaaNbeenee
31.meaning: O Tirumal who removes the cruel, thicket-like karma! O lofty One, O Lord of Venkatam! At the doorway of Your temple, where devotees, celestials, and heavenly nymphs lie and move about, may I lie as the threshold-step and behold Your coral mouth.

32.translit:
umba rulagaaN doarukudaikkeezh uruppasidhan
ampoR kalaiyalgul petraalu maadhariyeen
sempavaLa vaayaan thiruveeng kadamennum
emberumaan ponmalaimee leedheenu maaveenee
32.meaning: Even were I to rule the world of the gods (the upper world) under a single parasol and win the beautiful golden-girdled hips of Urvashi, I would not care for it. May I become anything whatsoever upon the golden hill of my Lord of the red-coral mouth, the one called holy Venkatam.

33.translit:
manniyadhaN saaral vadaveeng kadatthaandran
ponniyalum seevadikaL kaaNbaan purindhiRainjsi
konnavilum koorveel kulasee karanjsonna
panniyanool thamizhvallaar paangkaaya patharkaLee
33.meaning: To behold the golden sacred feet of the Lord of the northern Venkatam with its abiding cool slopes, worshipping with devotion - those skilled in this elaborated Tamil work uttered by Kulasekhara of the sharp slaughtering spear, they are devotees true and fitting.

34.translit:
kundrilnindru vaanirundhu neeLkadalki dandhumaN
ondrusendra thondraiyuNda thondridandhu
pandriyaay nandrusendra naaLavatruL nalluyirpa daitthavarkku
andrudheeva maitthaLittha aadhidheeva nallayee
34.meaning: Standing on the hill, dwelling in the sky, reclining on the vast ocean; measuring the earth in one stride, swallowing the world, and lifting it up as a boar - on those good days of His goodly acts, to those who created good life, are You not the primal God who that day granted them the divine state?

35.translit:
sezhungkozhumbe rumbanipo zhindhida uyarndhaveey
vizhundhularndhe zhundhuviNpu daikkumveengka datthuLnindru
ezhundhirundhu theenporundhu poompozhildha zhaikkozhunj
sezhundhadangku dandhaiyuLki dandhamaalu mallaiyee
35.meaning: In Venkatam, where the tall bamboos, when rich abundant great mist pours down, fall and dry and rise again to beat against the sky - standing there, and rising up - are You not also the Mal who reclines within fertile, expansive Kudandai (Kumbakonam), with its lush sprouting honey-filled flowering groves?

36.translit:
kadaindhapaaRka dalkidandhu kaalaneemi yaikkadindhu
udaindhavaali dhandhanukku udhavavandhi raamanaay
midaindhaveezhma rangkaLuma dangkaveydhu veengkadam
adaindhamaala paadhamee yadaindhunaaLu muymminoo
36.meaning: He who churned the milk-ocean and reclines upon it, who destroyed Kalanemi; who came as Rama to help the heart-broken Sugriva (Vali's brother), shooting down the seven crowded mara-trees so they were felled - approach the feet of that Mal who reached Venkatam, and live (be saved) every day.

37.translit:
kuRippenakkuk koattiyoor meeyaanai yeettha,
kuRippenakku nanmai payakka, - veRuppanoo
veengkadatthu meeyaanai meyvinainoo yeydhaamal,
thaankadatthum thanmaiyaan thaaL.
37.meaning: My intent (resolve) is to praise the Lord who abides at Kottiyur; that intent yields me good. Would I then scorn the feet of Him who abides at Venkatam, He whose nature is to carry one across so that the disease of bodily karma does not befall? (No.)

38.translit:
azhaippan thiruveeng kadatthaanaik kaaNa,
izhaippan thirukkoodal kooda, - mazhaippee
raruvi maNivarandri vandhizhiya, yaanai
veruvi yaravodungkum veRpu.
38.meaning: I call out to behold the Lord of holy Venkatam; I trace (make) the sacred Koodal so that union may be attained - that hill where the great cascading rain-waterfalls come tumbling down scouring gems, and the elephant, terrified, makes the serpent shrink and hide.

39.translit:
veRpendru veengkadam paadineen, veedaakki
niRkindreen nindru ninaikkindreen, - kaRkindra
noolvalaiyil pattirundha noolaatti keeLvanaar,
kaalvalaiyil pattirundheen kaaN.
39.meaning: Saying 'a hill', I sang of Venkatam; making it my deliverance (liberation), I stand firm, and standing I meditate. Behold - I have fallen into the snare of the feet of the Lord, consort of Lakshmi (she of the lotus, the lady of learning), who is caught (held) within the net of the studied scriptures.

200.translit:
meeyaan veengkaTam kaayaa malarvaNNan
peeyaar mulaiyuNDa vaayaan maathavanee.
200.meaning: He who abides upon Venkatam, He whose hue is that of the dark kaaya blossom; He whose mouth drank the breast of the demoness (Putana) — He is Madhava, the Lord of Lakshmi.

201.translit:
thirumaalirunjcoolaimalaiyee thiruppaaR kaDalee enthalaiyee
thirumaalvaikunthamee thaN thiruveengkaTamee enathuDalee
arumaa maayath thenathuyiree manamee vaakkee karumamee
orumaa noDiyum piriyaan en oozhi muthalvan oruvanee.
201.meaning: He is the hill of Tirumalirunsolai, the sacred Ocean of Milk, and my own head; He is Tirumal’s Vaikuntha, the cool sacred Venkatam, and my own body; through His rare and great wonder He is my very life, my mind, my speech, and my deeds. The one Lord who is the source of all the aeons will not part from me for even a single great instant.

40.translit:
kaaNal uRukinReen kal-aruvi muttu-udhira,
oNa vizhavil oli-adhira - peeNi
varuveeng kadavaa en uLLam pugundhaay,
tiruveeng kadam-adhanaich chenRu.
40.meaning: I have come longing to behold Tiruvenkatam, where the rocky waterfalls scatter pearls and the festival of Onam resounds with clamour. O Lord who dwells at the Venkatam hill, cherishing me you came and entered my heart. Having gone to that sacred Venkatam, I yearn to see it.

41.translit:
senRu vaNangumineeo seeN-uyar veengadattai,
ninRu vinai-kedukkum neermaiyaal, enRum
kadik-kamala naanmuganum kaN-moonRattaanum,
adik-kamalam ittu-eettum angu.
41.meaning: Go and worship the lofty, towering Venkatam, for by its nature it stands ever destroying our karmic deeds. There the four-faced Brahma, born on the fragrant lotus, and the three-eyed Shiva offer lotus blossoms at His feet and praise Him.

42.translit:
mangul-thooy senni vada-veeng kadattaanai
kangul pugundhaargaL kaapp-aNivaan, - thingaL
sadai-eeRa vaittaanum thaamarai-meelaanum
kudai-eeRat thaam-kuvittuk koNdu.
42.meaning: To the Lord of northern Venkatam, whose peaks touch the clouds, even Shiva who set the crescent moon upon his matted locks and Brahma who is seated on the lotus come at nightfall to adorn Him with protection. They gather and heap up blossoms to form a canopy as their offering.

43.translit:
koNdu kudangkaal-meel vaitta kuzhaviyaay,
thaNda arakkan thalai-thaLaal - paNd-eNNi,
poom-kumaran niRkum pozhil-veeng kadamalaikkee,
poom-kumarar uLLeer purindhu.
43.meaning: As an infant taken and set upon the lap, He once counted and severed the heads of the punishable demon (Ravana). O youthful ones, go with devotion to the groves of Venkatam hill where that blossoming youthful Lord abides.

44.translit:
purindhu malar-ittup puNdarikap paadham,
parindhu padu-kaadu niRpa - therindh-engum
thaan-oongi niRkinRaan thaNN-aruvi veengadamee
vaanoorkkum maNNoorkkum vaippu.
44.meaning: Strewing flowers with devotion at His lotus feet, the worshippers stand in dense throngs. Discerned everywhere, He stands towering and supreme. Venkatam of cool cascades is the treasure-store for the celestials and for the people of the earth alike.

45.translit:
vaippan maNi-viLakkaa maa-madhiyai, maalukken
Ru-eppozhudhum kai-neettum yaanaiyai, - eppaadum
veedu vaLaikkak kuRavar vill-edukkum veengadamee,
naadu vaLaittu-aadumeel nanRu.
45.meaning: I shall set the great moon as a jewelled lamp; the elephant ever stretching out its trunk as if reaching for Tirumal; and Venkatam where, on every side, the hunters draw their bows as the tribal folk close in. To roam and dwell encircling that land would be a blessing.

46.translit:
nan-maNi vaNNan-oor aaLiyum koLariyum,
pon-maNiyum muttamum poo-maramum, - pan-maNi-nee
rodu porudh-uruLum kaanamum vaanaramum
veedum udai-veeng kadam.
46.meaning: The town of the Lord hued like a flawless gem is Venkatam, abounding in the yaali and the fierce lion, in golden gems and pearls and flowering trees, in forests where streams laden with many jewels surge and roll, and in monkeys and hunters' dwellings.

47.translit:
veengadamee viNNoor thozhuvadhuvum meymmaiyaal
veengadamee mey-vinai-noy theerppadhuvum, - veengadamee
thaanavarai veezhath-than aazhip padai-thottu
vaanavaraik kaappaan malai.
47.meaning: It is Venkatam, truly, that the celestials worship; it is Venkatam that cures the disease of our embodied karma; it is Venkatam, the hill of Him who, taking up His discus weapon and striking down the demons, protects the gods of heaven.

48.translit:
veeRRirundhu viN-naaLa veeNduvaar, veengadattaan
paal-thirundha vaittaaree pan-malargaL, - meel-thirundha
vaazhvaar varu-madhi-paartth-anbinaraay, maRRavarkkee
thaazhaa yiruppaar thamar.
48.meaning: Those who wish to be enthroned and rule the heavens have rightly offered their many flowers at the feet of the Lord of Venkatam. Those who would live exalted above, awaiting the coming wisdom in loving devotion, are His own people who remain humbly subject to Him alone.

49.translit:
amalan aadhi-piraan adiyaarkku ennai aatpaduttha
vimalan viNNavar koon virai-yaar pozhil veengadavan
nimalan ninmalan needhi-vaanavan neeL-madhiL arangath-ammaan tiruk-
kamala-paadham-vandh en-kaNNin uLLana vokkinRadhee.
49.meaning: The flawless one, the primordial Lord, the pure one who made me a servant to His devotees, the king of the celestials, the Lord of Venkatam with its fragrant groves; the spotless, stainless, righteous Lord of heaven, the Lord of Srirangam girt by lofty ramparts - His sacred lotus feet have come and now seem to dwell within my very eyes.

50.translit:
mandhi paay vada-veengada maa-malai vaanavargaL
sandhi seyya ninRaan arangath aravin aNaiyaan
andhi pool-niRatt-aadaiyum adhan-meel ayanaip padaittadh-or-ezhil
undhi meeladhu anRoo adiyeen uLLatt in-uyiree.
50.meaning: He who stands at the great northern Venkatam hill where monkeys leap, worshipped at the junctures of day by the celestials, He who reclines on the serpent-couch at Srirangam - is not His twilight-hued garment, and above it that one beautiful navel which brought forth Brahma, the very life within this servant's heart?

51.translit:
kong-alarndha malark-kurundham-mosittha koovalan em-piraan,
sangu-thangu thadang-kadal-thuyil-koNda thaamaraik-kaNNinan,
pongu puLLinai-vaay-piLandha puraaNar-tham idam, pongu-neerch
seng-kayal-thiLaikkum sunaith tiruveengadam adai nenjamee.
51.meaning: Our Lord the cowherd who toppled the kurundha tree dense with honey-laden blossoms, the lotus-eyed one who slumbers on the vast ocean where conches abound, the ancient one who split open the beak of the surging bird (Bakasura) - His abode where red carp sport in the brimming springs: O heart, reach that sacred Venkatam.

52.translit:
palli-yaavadhu paaR-kadal arangam irangu avan-peey-mulai,
piLLaiyaay uyir-uNda em-thai piraan-avan perugum idam,
veLLiyaan kariyaan maNi-niRa-vaNNan enR-eNNi, naadoRum
teLLiyaar vaNangum malai tiruveengadam adai nenjamee.
52.meaning: His couch is the milk-ocean and Srirangam; as an infant He drank the very life from the cruel demoness's breast - this is the abode where our gracious Father flourishes. Thinking of Him as silver-hued, dark-hued, and sapphire-hued, the clear-minded worship Him daily: O heart, reach that hill, sacred Venkatam.

53.translit:
ninRa maa-marudh-iRRu-veezha nadandha ninmalan-neemiyaan,
enRum vaanavar-kai-thozhum iNaith-thaamarai-adi em-piraan,
kanRi maari pozhindhidak kadidh-aa-niraikk-idar neekkuvaan,
senRu kunRam-eduttavan tiruveengadam adai nenjamee.
53.meaning: The stainless one bearing the discus, who crawled so that the great twin marudu trees broke and fell, our Lord whose paired lotus feet the celestials ever worship with joined hands, who, when the angry rain poured down, swiftly went and lifted the hill to remove the cattle-herds' distress: O heart, reach His sacred Venkatam.

54.translit:
paarthaRkaay anRu paaratham-kai-seydhittu venRa paranjudar,
kootthu-angaayar-tham-paadiyil kuravai-piNaindha veng-koovalan,
eetthuvaar-tham-manatthuLLaan idaveendhai meeviya em-piraan
theertha-neertth-thadanj-soolai-soozh tiruveengadam adai nenjamee.
54.meaning: The supreme radiance who, for Arjuna's sake, that day waged the Bharata war and won; our cowherd who danced the kuravai dance, joining hands in the cowherds' settlement; the Lord who dwells in the hearts of those who praise Him, abiding at Idavendai - His sacred Venkatam ringed by groves and tanks of holy water: O heart, reach it.

55.translit:
vaN-kaiyaana avuNarkku-naayagan veeLviyil senRu maaNiyaay,
maN-kaiyaal irandhaan maraa-maram-eezhum eydha-valatthinaan,
eN-kaiyaan imayatth-uLLaan irunj-soolai meeviya em-piraan,
thiN-kaim-maa thuyaradh-theertthavan tiruveengadam adai nenjamee.
55.meaning: He who, going as a dwarf to the sacrifice of Bali, lord of the open-handed demons, begged for land with his hand; the strong one who pierced the seven sala trees; who dwells with manifold powers at the Himalayas and abides at Irunjolai, our Lord who relieved the distress of the strong-trunked elephant (Gajendra): O heart, reach His sacred Venkatam.

56.translit:
eN-thisaigaLum eezh-ulagamum vaangip pon-vayiRRil-peydhu,
paNd-or-aala-ilaip-paLLi koNdavan paan-madhikk-idar-theertthavan,
oN-thiRal avuNan-uratthugir vaittavan oL-eyiRRodu
thiN-thiRal ariyaay-avan tiruveengadam adai nenjamee.
56.meaning: He who drew in the eight directions and the seven worlds and held them in His golden belly, who of old rested on a banyan leaf, who removed the affliction of the milky moon; who set His claws upon the chest of the mighty demon, who became the fierce, powerful lion (Narasimha) with gleaming fangs: O heart, reach His sacred Venkatam.

57.translit:
paarum-neer-eri-kaaRRinodu aagaasamum ivai-aayinaan,
peerum-aayiram peesa-ninRa piRappili perugum-idam,
kaarum vaarp-pani-neeL-visumbidaich soorum-aa-mugil-thoy-thara,
seerum-vaarp-pozhil-soozh ezhil tiruveengadam adai nenjamee.
57.meaning: He who became earth, water, fire, wind and sky; the birthless one who stands as a thousand names to be uttered - this is the abode where He flourishes, where dark, drifting clouds pour their long mist and trail down to touch the high heavens. O heart, reach that beautiful Venkatam ringed by spreading groves.

58.translit:
ambaram-anal-kaal-nilam salam-aagi-ninRa amararkkoon,
vambu-ulaa-malar-meel mali-mada-mangai-than-kozhunan-avan,
kombin-anna-idai madak-kuRa-maadhar neeL-idaNan-thoRum,
sem-punam-avai-kaaval-koL tiruveengadam adai nenjamee.
58.meaning: The king of the celestials who became sky, fire, wind, earth and water; the consort of the lovely modest lady (Lakshmi) who flourishes upon the fragrant flower; where the slender-waisted tribal maidens, waists like a vine, guard their red fields from every tall watch-platform: O heart, reach that sacred Venkatam.

59.translit:
peesum-in-tiru-naamam-ett-ezhutthum solli-ninRu, pinnarum,
peesuvaar-tham-mai-uyya-vaangip piRapp-aRukkum piraan-idam,
vaasa-maa-malar-naaRu-vaar pozhil-soozh-tharum ulagukk-ellaam,
theesamaay-thigazhum malai tiruveengadam adai nenjamee.
59.meaning: His abode, where the Lord who lifts up and ends the births of those who utter and keep uttering the sweet sacred name of eight letters; the hill ringed by spreading groves fragrant with great scented blossoms, shining as a radiant light for all the worlds: O heart, reach that sacred Venkatam.

60.translit:
sengkayal thiLaikkum sunaith thiruvEngkaTatthuRai selvanai,
mangaiyar thalaivan kalikanRi vaNthamizh senjsol maalaigaL,
sangaiyinRith tharitthuraikka vallaargaL thanjsam adhaagavE,
vangamaa kadal vaiyam kaavalaraagi vaanulagu aaLvarE.
60.meaning: On the Lord who dwells as the bountiful one at Tiruvenkatam, whose pools teem with leaping red carp, Kaliyan, the chief of the people of Mangai, composed these garlands of choice Tamil words. Those who can hold these in their hearts and recite them without any doubt will, with this as their refuge, become rulers and protectors of this earth girt by the great sea where ships sail, and shall reign over the heavenly world.

61.translit:
thaayE thandhai enRum thaaramE kiLai makkaL enRum,
nOyE pattu ozhindhEn unnaik kaaNbadhu Oraasaiyinaal,
vEy Ey poompozhil soozh viraiyaar thiruvEngkadavaa,
naayEn vandhadaindhEn nalgi aaL ennaik koNdaruLE.
61.meaning: Calling out 'mother,' 'father,' and 'wife,' 'kin,' 'children,' I have been afflicted and worn out by these as by a disease. Out of the one longing to behold You, O Lord of fragrant Tiruvenkatam ringed by lovely groves dense with bamboo, I, a wretched cur, have come and taken refuge. Show grace, accept me as Your own servant and rule over me.

62.translit:
maan Ey kaN madavaar mayakkil pattu maanilatthu,
naanE naanaavidha naragam pugum paavam seydhEn,
thEn Ey poompozhil soozh thiruvEngkada maamalai, en
aanaay vandhadaindhEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
62.meaning: Caught in the bewitchment of doe-eyed young women, on this wide earth I myself committed sins that lead into manifold hells. O great hill of Tiruvenkatam ringed by lovely groves dripping with honey, my own (Lord), I have come and taken refuge. Show grace and take me, Your servant, into Your service.

63.translit:
konREn palluyirai kuRikkOL onRu ilaamaiyinaal,
enREnum irandhaarkku inidhaaga uraitthu aRiyEn,
kunRu Ey mEgam adhir kuLir maamalai vEngkadavaa,
anRE vandhadaindhEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
63.meaning: Having no fixed principle, I have killed many living beings. Never once have I known how to speak kindly to those who came begging. O Lord of cool great Venkatam hill where the thundering clouds rest upon the peaks, on that very day I came and took refuge. Show grace and take me, Your servant, into Your service.

64.translit:
kulam thaan etthanaiyum piRandhE iRandhu eyththu ozhindhEn,
nalam thaan onRum ilEn nalladhu Or aRam seydhum ilEn,
nilam thOy neeL mugil sEr neRiyaar thiruvEngkadavaa,
alandhEn vandhadaindhEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
64.meaning: Having been born and having died in every kind of caste and family, I have grown weary and worn out. I possess not a single virtue, nor have I done even one good righteous deed. O Lord of beautiful-pathed Tiruvenkatam where long clouds touching the earth gather, in my distress I have come and taken refuge. Show grace and take me, Your servant, into Your service.

65.translit:
eppaavam palavum ivaiyE seydhu iLaitthu ozhindhEn,
thuppaa! ninnadiyE thodarndhu Etthavum kiRkinRilEn,
seppaar thiN varai soozh thiruvEngkada maamalai, en
appaa! vandhadaindhEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
65.meaning: Committing all these many sins, I have wasted away exhausted. O my strength and sustenance! I am unable even to follow and praise Your feet alone. O great hill of Tiruvenkatam ringed by firm peaks like upturned caskets, my Father, I have come and taken refuge. Show grace and take me, Your servant, into Your service.

66.translit:
maNNaay neer eri kaal manjsu ulaavu maakaasamum aam,
puN aar aakkai thannuL pulambith thaLarndhu eyththu ozhindhEn,
viNNaar neeL sigara viraiyaar thiruvEngkadavaa,
aNNaa! vandhadaindhEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
66.meaning: Within this wound-ridden body made of earth, water, fire, wind, and the sky where clouds drift, I have lamented, grown feeble and worn out. O fragrant Lord of Tiruvenkatam whose tall peaks reach the sky, my chief, I have come and taken refuge. Show grace and take me, Your servant, into Your service.

67.translit:
theri en baalaganaayp pala theemaigaL seydhumittEn,
periyEn aayina pin piRarkkE uzhaitthu Ezhai aanEn,
kari sEr poompozhil soozh kana maamalai vEngkadavaa!,
ariya! vandhadaindhEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
67.meaning: Consider: as a child I went about doing many evil deeds, and after I had grown to be an adult, I toiled only for others and became destitute. O rare Lord of dense great Venkatam hill ringed by lovely groves where dark clouds gather, I have come and taken refuge. Show grace and take me, Your servant, into Your service.

68.translit:
nORREn pal piRavi unnaik kaaNbadhu Oraasaiyinaal,
ERREn ip piRappE idar uRRanan em perumaan!,
kOl thEn paayndhu ozhugum kuLir sOlai soozh vEngkadavaa!,
aaRREn vandhadaindhEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
68.meaning: I underwent many births out of the one longing to behold You. In this very birth I accepted that longing and so came to suffer, O our great Lord! O Lord of Venkatam ringed by cool groves where honey flows streaming down the branches, unable to bear it any longer I have come and taken refuge. Show grace and take me, Your servant, into Your service.

69.translit:
paRREl onRum ilEn paavamE seydhu paaviyaanEn,
maRREl onRu aRiyEn maayanE! engaL maadhavanE!,
kal thEn paayndhu ozhugum kamalach sunai vEngkadavaa!,
aRREn vandhadaindhEn adiyEnai aatkoNdaruLE.
69.meaning: I have no support at all; doing only sin, I have become a sinner. I know nothing else to turn to, O wondrous one, our Madhava! O Lord of Venkatam with lotus-pools where honey flows streaming over the rocks, having lost all else I have come and taken refuge. Show grace and take me, Your servant, into Your service.

70.translit:
kaNNaay Ezhulagukku uyiraaya em kaarvaNNanai,
viNNOr thaam paravum pozhil vEngkada vEdhiyanai,
thiNNaar maadangaL soozh thirumangaiyar kOn kaliyan,
paNNaar paadal patthum payilvaarkku illai paavangaLE.
70.meaning: On our dark-hued Lord who is the eye and the very life of the seven worlds, the Brahmin-master of grove-filled Venkatam whom the celestials worship, Kaliyan, lord of Tirumangai ringed by strong solid mansions, composed these ten melodious songs. For those who practise them there shall be no sins.

71.translit:
kaNNaar kadal soozh ilangaikku iRaivan than,
thiN aagam piLakka saram sela uyththaay,
viNNOr thozhum vEngkada maamalai mEya,
aNNaa! adiyEn idaraik kaLaiyaayE.
71.meaning: You sent forth Your arrow to course through and split the strong chest of the lord of Lanka, which was girt by the wide sea. O chief who abides on the great hill of Venkatam that the celestials worship, deign to remove the distress of me, Your servant.

72.translit:
ilangaip padhikku anRu iRaiyaaya, arakkar
kulam kettu avar maaLa kodip puL thiritthaay!,
vilangal kudumith thiruvEngkadam mEya,
alangal thuLaba mudiyaay! aruLaayE!
72.meaning: In that day You wheeled about Your banner-bird so that the race of demons, whose lord ruled over the city of Lanka, was ruined and perished. O You who abide at Tiruvenkatam with its mountain peaks, You whose crown bears the garland of tulasi, grant Your grace!

73.translit:
neeraar kadalum nilanum muzhudhu uNdu,
Er aala iLandhaLir mEl thuyil endhaay!,
seeraar thiruvEngkada maamalai mEya,
aaraa amudhE! adiyERku aruLaayE!
73.meaning: O my Father who swallowed up the whole water-filled ocean and the entire earth, and then slept upon a tender shoot of the lovely banyan leaf! O nectar never cloying, who abides on the glorious great hill of Tiruvenkatam, grant Your grace to me, Your servant!

74.translit:
uNdaay uRimEl naRu ney amudhaaga,
koNdaay kuRaLaay nilam eer adiyaalE,
viN thOy sigarath thiruvEngkadam mEya,
aNdaa! adiyEnukku aruL puriyaayE!
74.meaning: You ate the fragrant butter on the hanging cradle as if it were nectar; as a dwarf You took the earth in two strides. O Lord of the cosmos who abides at Tiruvenkatam whose peaks touch the sky, bestow Your grace upon me, Your servant!

75.translit:
thooNaaya thanoodu ariyaay vandhu thOnRi,
pENaa avuNan udalam piLandhittaay,
sENaar thiruvEngkada maamalai mEya,
kONaaga aNaiyaaya! kuRikkoL enai neeyE!
75.meaning: Appearing as the man-lion from within the pillar, You tore apart the body of the unloving demon. O You who abide on the lofty great hill of Tiruvenkatam, who reposes upon the curved serpent (Adisesha), take heed of me Yourself!

76.translit:
mannaa im manisap piRaviyai neekki,
thannaagith thannin aruL seyyum thalaivan,
minnaar mugil sEr thiruvEngkadam mEya,
en aanai en appan en nenjsil uLaanE!
76.meaning: The Lord who removes this transient human birth and, making me His own, grants His own grace, who abides at Tiruvenkatam where lightning-laced clouds gather, my elephant, my Father, dwells within my heart!

77.translit:
maan Ey mada nOkki thiRatthu edhir vandha,
aan Ezh vidai setRa aNi varaith thOLaa!,
thEnE. thiruvEngkada maamalai mEya,
kOnE. en manam kudikoNdirundhaayE.
77.meaning: O You of beautiful mountain-like shoulders who slew the seven bullocks that came charging, for the sake of the doe-eyed, gentle-glancing maiden! O honey, O King who abides on the great hill of Tiruvenkatam, You have taken up residence within my mind.

78.translit:
sEyan aNiyan ena sindhaiyuL ninRa maayan,
maNi vaaL oLi veN tharaLangaL,
vEy viNdu udhir vEngkada maamalai mEya,
aayan adi alladhu maRRu aRaiyEnE.
78.meaning: The wondrous one who stands within my thought as both far and near, abiding on the great hill of Venkatam where the bamboos burst open and scatter bright gem-like radiant white pearls. Apart from the feet of that Cowherd, I will invoke no other.

79.translit:
vandhaay en manam pugundhaay manni ninRaay,
nandhaadha kozhunjsudarE engaL nambee.,
sindhaamaNiyE thiruvEngkadam mEya endhaay!
iniyaan unnai enRum vidEnE.
79.meaning: You came, You entered my heart, and You have stayed firmly within. O rich never-fading flame, our trusted Lord! O wish-granting gem, my Father who abides at Tiruvenkatam, from now on I will never let go of You, ever.

80.translit:
villaarmali veengkadamaamalaimeeya,
mallaarttiradooL maNivaNNanammaanai,
kallaarttiradooL kaliyansonnamaalai,
vallaaravar vaanavaraaguvarttaamee.
80.meaning: This garland of verses was composed by Kaliyan, of strong shoulders firm as rock, upon the Lord of jewel-hued complexion, broad-shouldered and mighty, who abides on the great Venkatam hill abounding in bows. Those who are able to master these will themselves become the celestial ones in heaven.

81.translit:
vaanavar tangkaL sintai pooleen nenjamee. inivantu, maadava
maanavar tangkaL sintaiyamarntuRai kinRaventai,
kaanavaridu kaaragiRpugai yoongku veengkadam meevi, maaNkuRaL
aana antaNaR kinRadi maittozil pooNdaayee.
81.meaning: O my heart, you do not resemble the celestials. Yet now coming hither, my Lord and Father dwells abidingly in the hearts of those who practise great penance. He who came as the noble dwarf Brahmin loves Venkatam, where the dark fragrant aloe smoke kindled by the foresters rises high; today you have taken up the servant's labour at his feet.

82.translit:
uRavu suRRamen RonRilaa voruvan ugantavar tammai,
maNmisaip piRavi yegeduppaanatu kaNden nenjamenpaay,
kuRavar maadarga Loodu vaNdu kuRinji maruLisai paadum
veengkadattu, aRava naayagaR kinRu adimait tozil pooNdaayee.
82.meaning: You say, O my heart, that you have seen how the One who has no kith or kin destroys utterly the earthly births of those whom he favours. He is the Lord of righteousness of Venkatam, where the bees sing the kurinji melody together with the hill-women; today you have taken up the servant's labour at his feet.

83.translit:
iNdai yaayina koNdu toNdargaL eettu vaaruRa voodum,
vaanidaik koNdu pooyidavu matukaNden nenjamenpaay,
vaNdu vaazvada veengkadamalai kooyil koNdata noodum,
meemisai aNda maaNdirup paaRku adimaittozil pooNdaayee.
83.meaning: You say, O my heart, that you have seen how he carries away into the heavens, together with their kinsfolk, the devotees who praise him bearing garlands of woven flowers. To him who took as his shrine the northern Venkatam hill where bees thrive, and who rules the cosmos high above, today you have taken up the servant's labour at his feet.

84.translit:
paavi yaatusey taayen nenjamee! paNdu toNdusey taarai,
maNmisai meevi yaadkoNdu pooyvi sumbeeRa vaikkumentai,
goovi naayagan koNda luntuyar veengka damalai yaaNdu,
vaanavar aavi yaayirup paaRku adimait tozil pooNdaayee.
84.meaning: O my heart, what sin have you done? My Father takes those who once rendered service, accepting them on this earth, and bears them up to dwell on high in the sky. He is the Lord of cowherds; he rules the Venkatam hill where clouds soar; today you have taken up the servant's labour at him who is the very life of the celestials.

85.translit:
pongku pootiyum piNdi yumudaip puttar noonbiyar paLLi yuLLuRai,
tangkaL teevarum taangkalu meeyaaga ennenjamenpaay!
engkum vaanavar taana varniRain teettum veengkadam meevi ninRaruL,
angka Naayagar kinRadi maittozil pooNdaayee.
85.meaning: You say, O my heart, leaving aside the Buddhists with their flourishing bodhi and asoka trees and the ascetics who dwell within their monasteries, with their own gods and themselves alone. He stands gracing Venkatam, which is everywhere filled and praised by celestials and demons alike; today you have taken up the servant's labour at the gracious-eyed Lord's feet.

86.translit:
tuvari yaadaiyar mattai yarsamaN toNdar kaLmaNdi yuNdu pinnarum,
tamarum taangkaLumee tadikkaen nenjamenpaay,
kavari maakkaNam seerum veengkadam kooyil koNdakaN Naarvi sumbidai,
amara naayagaRagu inRadimait tozil pooNdaayee.
86.meaning: You say, O my heart, leaving aside the ochre-robed ones and the dull Jain devotees who crowd together and gorge themselves and grow fat with their own folk. He took as his shrine Venkatam where herds of yak gather, in the vast eye-filling sky; today you have taken up the servant's labour at the Lord of the immortals.

87.translit:
tarukki naalsamaN seytu sooRutaN tayirinaaltiraLai,
midaRRidai nerukku vaaralak kaNatu kaNden nenjamenpaay,
marutkaL vaNdugaL paadum veengkadam kooyil koNdata noodum,
vaanidai arukkan meeviniR paaRkadi maittozil pooNdaayee.
87.meaning: You say, O my heart, that you have seen the misery of those who, in their arrogance practising Jain ways, cram their throats with balls of rice and cool curds. He took as his shrine Venkatam where bewildered bees sing, and he abides in the sky together with the sun; today you have taken up the servant's labour at his feet.

88.translit:
seeyananiyan siRiyan periya nenbatu silarppeesak keettirunte,
en nenjamen paay enak konRu sollaatee,
veeygaL ninRuveN mutta meesori veengka damalai kooyil meeviya,
aayar naayagaR kinRadi maittozil pooNdaayee.
88.meaning: Though you have heard a few people debating, saying he is far, he is near, he is small, he is great, O my heart, you do not say a single thing to me. He is the Lord of the cowherds who abides at his shrine on the Venkatam hill where the bamboos shed white pearls; today you have taken up the servant's labour at his feet.

89.translit:
koodi yaadi yuraitttee yuraittaay ennenjamen paay tuNintukeeL,
paadi yaadip palarum paNinteettik kaaNkilaa,
aadu taamarai yoonu meesanum amar koonumninR eettum,
veengkadattu aadu kuuttanuk kinRu adimaittozil pooNdaayee.
89.meaning: O my heart, you have only repeated what others repeated, gathering and dancing; now listen with resolve. He whom even the lotus-seated Brahma, Siva, and Indra king of the immortals cannot behold though they stand singing, dancing, and bowing in worship with many others. To the dancer who dances at Venkatam, today you have taken up the servant's labour at his feet.

90.translit:
minnu maamugil meevu taNtiru veengka damalai kooyilmeeviya,
annamaay nigaznta amarar perumaanai,
kanni maamatiL mangkai yarkkali kanRi yintami zaalu raitta,
immannu paadalval laarkkida maagum vaanulagee.
90.meaning: Kalikanri of Tirumangai, town with great rampart walls, has uttered in this Tamil verse upon the Lord of the immortals who took the form of a swan and who abides at the shrine on the cool sacred Venkatam hill where lightning-laced great clouds gather. For those who can master these enduring songs, the world of heaven shall be their abode.

91.translit:
anRiya vaaNa naayiram tooLum tuNiyavan Raazitot taanai,
mintigaz kudumi veengkada malaimeel meeviya veetanal viLakkai,
tentisait tilatam anaiyavar naangkaic semponsey kooyili nuLLee,
manRatu poliya magizntuninR aanai vaNangkinaan vaazntozinteenee.
91.meaning: Him who, when the hostile Banasura warred, hurled his discus so that his thousand shoulders were severed; the good lamp of the Vedas who abides upon the Venkatam hill of glittering peak; he who stands joyfully, brightening the assembly hall, within the golden temple of Nangai, town like an ornamental mark upon the southern quarter. Worshipping him, I have lived and been redeemed.

92.translit:
veedaar tiruveeng kadammeeya viLakkee,
naadaar pugazvee tiyarman niyanaangkuur,
seedaar pozilsuuz tiruveLLak kuLattaay,
paadaa varuveen viNaiyaa yinapaaRRee.
92.meaning: O lamp who abides at sacred Venkatam frequented by hunters; O Lord of Tiruvellakkulam girt with luxuriant groves, in Nangur where Vedic brahmins of land-wide fame dwell abidingly. Singing of you I come, that my accumulated sins may be driven away.

93.translit:
vaampa riyuga mannarta muyirsega aivarkkad karasaLitta,
kaampi naarttiru veengkadap poruppa. nin kaadalai yaruLenakku,
maampo ziltaLir kootiya madakkuyil vaayatu tuvarppeyta,
teempa langkanit teenatu nugartiru veLLaRai ninRaanee.
93.meaning: O Lord of the sacred Venkatam peak girt with bamboo, who in the war of the bounding horses gave the kingship to the five whilst the lives of the kings were destroyed: grant me your love. O Lord who stands at Tiruvellarai, where the tender koel that has pecked the mango-grove's shoots tastes its astringency and then sips the honey of the sweet jackfruit.

94.translit:
veruvaataaL vaayveruvi veengkadamee veengkadamee engkin RaaLaal,
maruvaaLaa lenkudangkaal vaaNedungkaN tuyilmaRantaaL, vaNdaar koNdal
uruvaaLan vaanavarta muyiraaLan olitiraineerp pauvang koNda
tiruvaaLan enmagaLaic seytanagaL engnganamnaan sintik keenee.
94.meaning: She who never used to be alarmed now babbles in alarm, crying 'Venkatam! Venkatam!' She no longer nestles in my lap; her long bright eyes have forgotten sleep. He of the form of a bee-swarmed dark cloud, the Lord of the celestials' very life, the Lord of Lakshmi who arose from the resounding-waved ocean - how shall I bear to think of what he has done to my daughter?

95.translit:
sintanaiyait tavaneRiyait tirumaalai, piriyaatu
vantenatu manattirunta vadamalaiyai, varivaNdaar
kontaNainta pozilkoova lulagaLappaa nadinimirtta
antaNanai, yaankaNda taNiineertten narangkattee.
95.meaning: He who is my very thought, the path of penance, Tirumal, the northern hill who came and dwelt unceasingly within my mind; the pure One who at Koval where groves are thick with striped-bee-laden flower clusters, stretched up his foot to measure the worlds - him I beheld at cool-watered southern Srirangam.

96.translit:
maankoNda toolmaarvil maaNiyaay, maavalimaN
taankoNdu taaLaal aLanta perumaanai
teenkoNda saaral tiruveeng kadattaanai
naan senRu naadi naRaiyuuril kaNdeenee.
96.meaning: The great Lord who came as a Brahmin lad with a deerskin upon his chest, and seizing the earth from Mahabali measured it with his foot; the Lord of sacred Venkatam with honey-laden slopes - him I went and sought and beheld at Naraiyur.

97.translit:
aangku vennara kattazun tumpootu
  anjee lenRadi yeenaiyang keevantu taangku,
taamarai yannapon naaradi empi raanai
  um parkkaNi yaayninRa,
veengkadattari yaippari keeRiyai
  veNNe yuNdura linidai yaappuNda teengka rumbinai,
teenainan paalinai anRi
  enmanam sintaisey yaatee.
97.meaning: When I sink drowning in cruel hell yonder, he comes there to me his servant saying 'Fear not!' and upholds me - my Lord of golden lotus-like feet, who stands as the ornament of the celestials, the lion of Venkatam, he who tore the horse-demon, the sweet sugarcane who ate the butter and was bound to the mortar. Apart from him, the honey and the good milk, my mind shall think of nothing else.

98.translit:
engka LukkaruL seykinRa eesanai
vaasa vaarkuza LaaLmalai mangkaitan
pangka nai, pangkil vaittugan taanRannaip
paanmai yaippani maamati yamtavaz
mangku laic, suda raivada maamalai
ucciyai, naccinaam vaNangka ppadum
kangkulai, pagalaic senRu naadik
kaNNa mangkaiyuL kaNdukoN deenee.
98.meaning: The Lord who shows grace to us; he who has on his half the mountain-maiden of fragrant flowing tresses, who rejoiced to keep her at his side; his very nature; the radiance amid the misty clouds over which the cool great moon glides; the summit of the great northern hill; the night and the day that we worship with longing - him I sought out and found within Kannamangai.

99.translit:
aruvisoor veengkadam niirmalai enRuvaay
veruvinaaL meyyam vinavi yirukkinRaaL,
perugusiirk kaNNapuram enRu peesinaaL
uruginaaL, uLmelin taaL itu venkoloo.
99.meaning: She babbled in alarm, saying 'Venkatam of cascading falls, Tirunirmalai!' She sits questioning about Meyyam; she spoke of glorious Kannapuram, she melted, she grew faint within - what can this be?

0.translit:
arivu thariyaan anaiththulagum udaiyaan ennai aaLudaiyaan
kuriya maaNi uruvaaya kooththan manni amarum idam,
nariya malar mEl surumbaarkka ezhilaar mañña nadamaada,
porikoL siraivaN disai paadum puLLam boodhang kudidhaanE!
0.meaning: He who bestows wisdom, who owns all the worlds, who has taken me as His servant, the dancer who took the form of a short bachelor (Vamana) — the place where He abides forever is Pullambhoodankudi, where bees hum upon fragrant flowers, beautiful peacocks dance, and spotted-winged bees sing melodiously.

1.translit:
kaLLak kuRaLaay maavaliyai vañchiththu ulagam kaippaduththu,
poLLaik karaththa bOdhagaththin thunbam thavirththa punidhan idam,
paLLach cheRuvil kayalugaLap pazhanak kazhani adhanuL pOy,
puLLup piLLaikku irai thEdum puLLam boodhang kudidhaanE!
1.meaning: As a wily dwarf He deceived Mahabali and seized the worlds, the pure one who removed the suffering of the elephant (Gajendra) with His hollowed hand — His place is Pullambhoodankudi, where in the marshy fields carp leap, and birds go into the watery paddy fields seeking food for their young.

2.translit:
mEvaa arakkar then ilangai vEndhan veeyach charam thurandhu,
maavaay piLandhu malladarththu marudham saayththa maaladhu idam,
kaavaar thengin pazham veezhak kayalgaL paayak kurugu iriyum,
poovaar kazhani ezhilaarum puLLam boodhang kudidhaanE!
2.meaning: He let fly His arrows so that the king of southern Lanka, lord of the hostile demons, perished; He split the mouth of the horse-demon (Kesi), crushed the wrestlers, and felled the marudu trees — Tirumal's place is Pullambhoodankudi, where ripe coconuts fall from groves of coconut palms, carp leap, cranes scatter, and flower-filled fields shine in beauty.

3.translit:
veRpaal maari pazhudhaakki viRal vaaL arakkar thalaivan than,
vaRpaar thiraL thOL ainnaangum thuNiththa valvil iraaman idam,
kaRpaar purisai sey kunRam kavinaar koodam maaLigaigaL,
porpaar maada mezhilaarum puLLam boodhang kudidhaanE!
3.meaning: He who with a mountain made the rain (of Indra) come to naught, the strong-bowed Rama who severed the twenty mighty rounded shoulders of the valiant sword-bearing demon king (Ravana) — His place is Pullambhoodankudi, with hill-like ramparts built of stone, lovely halls and mansions, and beautiful golden-bright storied buildings.

4.translit:
maiyaar thadangaN karunkoondhal aaychchi maRaiya vaiththa thayir,
neyyaar paalOdu amudhu seydha nEmi am kai maayan idam,
seyyaar aaral irai karudhich chengaal naarai senRaNaiyum,
poyyaa naavil maRaiyaaLar puLLam boodhang kudidhaanE!
4.meaning: The wondrous one with the discus in His lovely hand, who ate as nectar the curds along with ghee and milk that the dark-haired cowherd woman with wide collyrium-lined eyes had hidden — His place is Pullambhoodankudi, where red-legged cranes approach the fields seeking the eel for prey, and where dwell Vedic brahmins of unlying tongue.

5.translit:
minnin anna nuN marungul vEy Ey thadan thOL melliyaRkaa,
mannu sinaththa mazha vidaigaL Ezhu anRu adarththa maaladhu idam,
mannu mudhuneer aravindha malar mEl varivaN disai paada,
punnai ponnEy thaadhu udhirkkum puLLam boodhang kudidhaanE.
5.meaning: For the sake of the delicate maiden (Nappinnai) with slender lightning-like waist and rounded bamboo-like shoulders, Tirumal that day subdued the seven enduring, fiercely raging young bulls — His place is Pullambhoodankudi, where striped bees sing upon the lotus blossoms in the ancient abiding waters, and the punnai tree sheds its gold-like pollen.

6.translit:
kudaiyaa vilangal koNdEndhi maari pazhudhaa nirai kaaththu,
sadaiyaan Oda adal vaaNan thadan thOL thuNiththa thalaivan idam,
kudiyaa vaNdu kaLLuNNak kOla neelam mattu ugukkum,
pudaiyaar kazhani ezhilaarum puLLam boodhang kudidhaanE.
6.meaning: He lifted up a mountain as an umbrella, made the rain come to naught, and protected the cattle herd; the Lord who made the matted-haired one (Shiva) flee and severed the mighty rounded shoulders of the warrior Banasura — His place is Pullambhoodankudi, where bees settle and drink honey, beautiful blue lilies pour out their nectar, and surrounding fields shine in beauty.

7.translit:
kaRaiyaar neduvEl maRamannar veeya visayan thEr kadavi,
iRaiyaan kaiyil niRaiyaadha muNdam niRaiththa endhai idam,
maRaiyaal mooththee avai vaLarkkum mannu pugazhaal vaNmaiyaal,
poraiyaal mikka andhaNar vaazh puLLam boodhang kudidhaanE.
7.meaning: He drove Arjuna's chariot so that the warlike kings bearing long stained spears perished, my Father who filled the skull-bowl in the hand of the Lord (Shiva) that would not fill — His place is Pullambhoodankudi, where brahmins abounding in enduring fame, generosity and forbearance dwell, tending the ancient sacrificial fires by the Vedas.

8.translit:
thunni maNNum viNNaadum thOnRaadhu iruLaay moodiya naaL,
annamaagi aru maRaigaL aruLich cheydha amalan idam,
minnu sOdhi navamaNiyum vEyin muththum saamaraiyum,
ponnum ponni koNarndhu alaikkum puLLam boodhang kudidhaanE,
8.meaning: On the day when earth and the celestial realm did not appear, all densely covered in darkness, the spotless one who took the form of a swan (Hamsa) and graciously gave forth the rare Vedas — His place is Pullambhoodankudi, where the Kaveri (Ponni) river comes bearing and washing in shining bright nine gems, bamboo pearls, chowries and gold.

9.translit:
kaRRaa maRiththu kaaLiyan than senni nadunga nadam payinRa
porRaamaraiyaaL than kELvan puLLam boodhang kudi thanmEl
kaRRaar paravum mangaiyarkkOn kaaraar puyaR kai kalikanRi,
soldhaan eeraindhu ivai paadach chOra nillaa thuyardhaamE!
9.meaning: The consort of her who dwells on the golden lotus (Lakshmi), who herded the calves and danced upon Kaliya's hoods until his heads trembled — upon His shrine of Pullambhoodankudi, Kaliyan (Tirumangai), the lord of Mangai praised by the learned, generous with rain-cloud-like hands, has sung these ten verses; for those who sing them, sorrows will not stay but will utterly depart.

0.translit:
munnavanai moozhikkaLatthu viLakkinai
annavanai aadhanoor aaNDu aLakkum aiyanai
nennalai indrinai naaLaiyai neermalaimEl (70)
0.meaning: Him who is the Primordial One, the radiant lamp shining in Tirumoozhikkalam; that very Lord, the master who reigns over and rules Aadhanoor; Him who is the yesterday, the today, and the tomorrow, who abides upon Tiruneermalai.

0.translit:
kongung kuDandaiyum kooTTiyoo rumpaerum
engum tirindu viLaiyaaDum enmagan
sangam piDikkum taDakkaikkut takkanil
anga muDaiyadoor koolkoNDuvaa
arakku vazhittadoor koolkoNDuvaa
0.meaning: My son wanders everywhere and plays—in Kongu, in Kudandai, in Kottiyur and in Per. Bring a staff fit for his broad hand that holds the conch, a staff of suitable form. Bring a staff smoothed with lac.

1.translit:
aalat tilaiyaan aravi naNaimaelaan
neelak kaDaluL neDungaalam kaNvaLarndaan
paalap piraayattae paarttarkku aruLseyda
koolap piraanukkuoor koolkoNDuvaa
kuDandaik kiDandaarkkuoor koolkoNDuvaa
1.meaning: He who rests upon the banyan leaf, who reclines upon the serpent-couch, who slumbered for ages in the dark blue ocean. For the beautiful Lord who in his tender childhood years showed grace, bring a staff. For the One who reclines at Kudandai, bring a staff.

2.translit:
kuDanga leDuttaera viTTuk koottaaDa vallaem koovae
maDangoL madimugat taarai maalseyya vallaen maindaa
iDandiTTu iraNiyan nenjai irupiLa vaagamun keeNDaay
kuDandaik kiDandaem koovae kurukkattip poochchooTTa vaaraay
2.meaning: O my King who can toss up the pots and dance the kuda-koothu! O my son who can enchant the women with their gentle moon-bright faces! Of old you tore open and split in two the breast of Hiranya. O my King who reclines at Kudandai, come, that I may adorn you with kurukkatti flowers.

3.translit:
paalaa lilaiyil tuyilkoNDa paraman valaippaT Tirundaenai
vaelaal tunnam peydaaRRpoal vaeNDiRR ellaam paesaadae
koolaal niraimaeyt taayanaayk kuDandaik kiDanda kuDamaaDi
neelaar taNNan tuzhaaykoNDen neRimaen kuzhalmael chooTTeerae
3.meaning: O Supreme One who slept upon the leaf in infancy—I am caught in your snare. Do not say all that you wish, as though piercing me with a spear. O cowherd who herded the cattle with your crook, who reclines at Kudandai and dances the pot-dance, take the cool dark tulasi and set it upon my fragrant curled tresses.

4.translit:
ilangaimanna naindoDaindu paindalaini lattuga
kalangavanRu senRukonRu venRikoNDa veeranae
vilangunoolar vaedanaavar neediyaana kaeLviyaar
valangoLakku Dandaiy  ulki Dandamaalu mallaiyae
4.meaning: O hero who of old went forth and slew the king of Lanka, his ten heads tumbling to the earth in confusion, and won the victory! Are you not the Tirumal who reclines within Kudandai—where dwell those who wear the sacred thread, whose tongues utter the Vedas, who possess righteous learning—that he might be circumambulated in worship?

5.translit:
sangutangu munkainangai kongaitanga luRRavan
angamanga vanRusenRa Darttherinda vaazhiyaan
konkutangu vaarkuzhalma DandaimaarkuDaindaneer
pongutaNku Dandaiyu lkiDandapuNDa reekanae
5.meaning: He on whose chest dwells the Lady whose forearms bear bangles—he who of old went and crushed and hurled down the foe so their limbs were destroyed, the wielder of the discus. He is the lotus-eyed One who reclines within cool Kudandai, where the surging waters are stirred by maidens with long fragrant tresses.

6.translit:
marangeDa naDandaDarttu mattayaanai mattagattu
urangeDappu DaitthorkomboSitthuganda vuttamaa
turangamvaaypi LandumaNNa Landapaadavaediyar
varangoLakku Dandaiyu lkiDandamaalu mallaiyae
6.meaning: O noble One who walked and pressed down the trees, who struck the rutting elephant upon its forehead until its strength was destroyed, and rejoiced having broken off one of its tusks! O you who tore open the horse-demon's mouth, whose feet measured the earth—are you not the Tirumal who reclines within Kudandai, where the Vedic brahmins receive boons?

7.translit:
saalivaeli taNvayalta DangiDangu poompozhil
koolamaaDa neeDutaNku Dandaimaeya koovalaa
kaalanaemi vakkaranka ranmuranji ramavai
kaalanoDu kooDavilku nittaviRRkai veeranae
7.meaning: O cowherd-Lord who dwells in cool Kudandai with its tall ornate mansions, its cool fields bounded by paddy, its moats and flowering groves! O hero of the bow-bearing hand who bent the bow and sent the heads of Kalanemi, Vakra, Khara and Muran to keep company with Death!

8.translit:
sezhungozhumpe rumpanipo zhindiDau yarndavaey
vizhundularnde zhunduviNpu DaikkumvaengaDattuLninRu
ezhundirundu taenporundu poompozhilta zhaikkozhunj
sezhundaDangu DandaiyuLki DandamaalumallaiyaeI
8.meaning: From standing in Venkatam, where the rich thick heavy mist pours down and the tall bamboos fall, dry, then rise again to brush the sky—are you not the Tirumal who has come to recline in fertile, broad Kudandai with its flourishing flowering groves rich with honey-laden blossoms?

9.translit:
naDandakaalgaL nondavoo naDungunjaala maenamaay
iDandameygu lungavovi langumaalva raichchuram
kaDandakaalpa randakaavi rikkaraikku Dandaiyul
kiDandavaaRe zhundirundu paesuvaazhi kaesanae
9.meaning: Did the feet that strode (across the worlds) grow weary? When you tore up the earth as the boar, did your great body shake? With feet that crossed the trembling world and the towering mountain wilds—O Kesava, long may you live—rise up and speak of how you came to recline at Kudandai on the bank of the spreading Kaveri.

10.translit:
naagat taNaikkuDandai vehkaa tiruvevvuL,
naagat taNaiyarangam paeranbil, - naagat
taNaippaaR kaDalkiDakku maadi neDumaal,
aNaippaar karut tanaa vaan.
10.meaning: The primordial great Tirumal who reclines—at Kudandai upon the serpent-couch, at Tiruvehka, at Tiruvevvul, at Arangam (Srirangam) upon the serpent-couch, at Per, at Anbil, and upon the milk-ocean on the serpent-couch—he becomes the very thought of those who would embrace him.

11.translit:
aaviyae amudae enaninaindurugi avaravarppaNaimulaituNaiyaa,
paaviyaenuNaraa dettanaipagalum pazhudupooyozhindana naaLgaL,
toovisaerannam tuNaiyoDumpuNarum soozhpunaRkuDandaiyaetozhudu,
en naavinaaluyyanaan kaNDukoNDaen naaraayaNaavennum naamam.
11.meaning: Thinking 'O my life, O my nectar' of this woman and that, melting with desire, taking their full breasts as my companions—I the sinner, unknowing, let how many days pass, the days wasted away in vain. But worshipping Kudandai encircled by water, where the feathered swans unite with their mates, I with my tongue, that I might be saved, found and grasped the Name that is called 'Narayana.'

12.translit:
iRpiRappaRiyeer ivaravarenneer innadoorttanmaiyenRuNareer
kaRpagampulavarkaLaikaNenRoolagil kaNDavaadoNDaraippaaDum,
soRpuruLaaLeersollugaenvammin soozhpunaRkuDandaiyaetozhumin,
naRporuLkaaNmin paaDi neeruymmin naaraayaNaavennumnaamam.
12.meaning: You who do not know about birth in this world, who do not say 'this one' or 'that one,' who do not perceive any particular nature of things—you who master words yet only sing of the devotees you happen to see in the world, calling them 'wish-tree' and 'refuge of the learned': listen, I shall tell you, come. Worship Kudandai encircled by water. Behold the true wealth, and singing, save yourselves—the Name that is called 'Narayana.'

13.translit:
ooraanguDandaiyuttaman orukaalirukaalsilaivaLaiya,
taeraavarakkarttaerveLLamseRRaan vaRRaavarupunalsoozh paeraan,
paeraayiramuDaiyaan piRangusiRaivaNDaRaiginRa taaraan
taaraavayalsoozhnda saaLakkiraamamaDainenjae.
13.meaning: The Perfect One whose town is Kudandai, who once and twice bent his bow until the host of chariots of the unrelenting demons was destroyed; the Lord of Per encircled by ever-flowing waters; he of the thousand names, who wears the garland on which bright-winged bees hum—O my heart, take refuge in Salagramam encircled by fields where wild-ducks dwell.

14.translit:
anRaayarku lakkoDi yoDaNimaa malarmangaiyo Danbavi,
avuNarkkenRaanu mirakkami laadavanukkuk kuRaiyumiDa maavadu,
irumpozhilsoozh nanRaayapu nalnaRai yoorttiruvaa likuDandai taDandigazh koovalnagar,
ninRaaInirun taankiDan taannaDandaaR kiDammaamalai yaavadu neermalaiyae.
14.meaning: He who of old shared his love with the creeper of the cowherd clan and with the Lady of the lovely great lotus, yet was ever without mercy toward the demons—the places that serve as his abode are: Naraiyur of good waters girt with dark groves, Tiruvali, Kudandai, and Koval-nagar bright with its broad tank, where he stands, sits, reclines and strides; and the great hill that is his abode is Tirunirmalai.

15.translit:
vaaLaaya kaNpanippa menmulaigaL ponnarumba
naaNaaLum ninninaindu naivaeRku,oomaNNaLanda
taaLaaLaa taNkuDandai nagaraaLaa varaiyeDutta
toaLaaLaa, enRanakkoor tuNaiyaaLa naagaayae
15.meaning: My sword-like eyes brim with tears, my soft breasts break out like gold (in pallor); for me who pine, thinking of you every day and night—O Lord of the foot that measured the earth, O Lord of cool Kudandai-town, O Lord of the shoulders that lifted the mountain—be a companion-help to me.

16.translit:
kuyilaalum vaLarppozhilsoozh taNkuDandaik kuDamaaDi!
tuyilaada kaNNiNaiyaen ninninaindu tuyarvaenoo!
muyalaalu miLamadikkae vaLaiyizhandaeRkku, idunaDuvae
vayalaali maNavaaLaa. koLvaayoo maNiniRamae.
16.meaning: O pot-dancer of cool Kudandai encircled by groves where cuckoos call! Must I, with these eyes that find no sleep, grieve thinking of you? For me who lost my bangles to the young moon with its hare-mark—meanwhile, O bridegroom of Vayalali, will you carry off even my jewel-like complexion?

17.translit:
vaaraaLu miLangongai vaNNamvae Raayinavaa reNNaaL,eNNil
paeraaLan paerallaal paesaaLip peNpeRRae nenchey kaeInnaan,
taaraaLan taNkuDandai nagaraaLan aivarkkaa yamari luytta taeraaLan,
enmagaLaich cheydanagaL engnganamnaan seppu kaenae.
17.meaning: Her young breasts bound in the breast-band—how their color has turned (pale)! Reckoning the days, she will speak nothing but the name of the great-named One. Having borne this girl, what shall I do? The Lord of the garland, ruler of cool Kudandai-town, the charioteer who drove the chariot in the battle for the sake of the five (Pandavas)—the things he has done to my daughter, how shall I tell them?

18.translit:
pongaeRu neeLsoadip ponnaazhi tannoaDum
sangaeRu koolat taDakkaip perumaanai
kongaeRu soalaik kuDandaik kiDandaanai
nangoanai naaDi naRaiyooril kaNDaenae.
18.meaning: The Lord with the broad hand that bears the beautiful conch, together with the golden discus of surging, tall radiance; he who reclines at Kudandai with its honey-laden groves—our King—I sought him out and found him at Naraiyur.

19.translit:
kiDanda nambi kuDandai maevik kaezha laayulagai
iDanda nambi, engaL nambi eRinjar araNazhiya
kaDanda nambi kaDiyaa rilangai ulagai yeeraDiyaal
naDanda nambi naamam sollil namoanaa raayaNamae!
19.meaning: The Lord who reclines, dwelling at Kudandai; the Lord who as the boar dug up the earth; our Lord, who advanced and destroyed the fortifications of his enemies; the Lord who strode over fragrant Lanka and over the world with two steps—if one would speak his Name, it is 'Namo Narayanaya'!

20.translit:
vandha naaL vandhen nenjidang koNDaan
  maRROr nenjaRi yaan,
adi yEnudaich chindhai yaay vandhu
  thenpularkk ennaich chErko Daanidhu sikkenap peTREn,
kondhu laam pozhil sUzhkudan thaiththalaik kOvi naik
  kuda maaDiya kUththanai,
endhai yai endhai thandhai tham maanai
  empiraanai eth thaal maRak kEnE?
20.meaning: From the very day He came, He came and made my heart His abode, knowing no other heart. He came as the very thought of me His servant, and bound me to Himself in the southern land — this I have firmly attained. He is the Lord of Kudanthai, surrounded by groves rich with blossom-clusters, the dancer who danced the pot-dance. He is my father, the Lord of my father and forefathers, my Master — by what means could I ever forget Him?

21.translit:
pEraa naikkudandhaip perumaanai, ilangoLi sEr
vaaraar vanamulaiyaaL malarmangai naayakanai,
aaraa vinnamudhaith thennazhundhaiyil manniniNRa,
kaaraar karumugilaik kaNDukoNDu kaLiththEnE.
21.meaning: The Lord of great Kudanthai, the consort of the lotus-dwelling Lady whose lovely breasts are bound with a girdle of shining radiance, the never-cloying sweet nectar who stands firmly established in the southern Azhundhai — that dark rain-cloud-hued one I have beheld and rejoiced.

22.translit:
vandhaay en manaththE vandhunee pugundha pinnai,
endhaay pOyaRiyaay idhuvE amaiyaadhO
kondhaar paimpozhil sUzh kudandhaik kidandhugandha
maindhaa unnai enRum maRavaamaip peTREnE.
22.meaning: You came into my heart; and after You came and entered, my Father, You have not known to depart — is this not itself enough? O youthful Lord who joyfully reclined at Kudanthai, surrounded by fresh green groves laden with blossom-clusters, I have attained never to forget You at any time.

23.translit:
thOdavizh neelam maNango Dukkum sUzhpunal sUzhkudan thaikkidandha
sEDarko lenRu therikka maaTTEn senjuda raazhiyum sangumEndhi
paadaka mellaDi yaarva NaNGap panmaNi muththoDi langusOdhi
aaDagam pUNDoru naangu thOLum achchO oruvar azhagiyavaa.
23.meaning: He who reclines at Kudanthai, encircled by waters where the blooming blue lily spreads its fragrance — I cannot fathom what greatness is His. Holding the red-rayed discus and the conch, while ladies with tender feet adorned with anklets bow before Him, His four shoulders shine with gold ornaments set with many gems and pearls. Ah! how beautiful is this peerless One!

24.translit:
vaanai aaramu dham thandha vaLLalai
thEnai neeLvayal sERaiyil kaNDupOy
aanai vaaTTi yaruLum amararth tham
kOnai, yaam kudan thaichchenRu kaaNDumE.
24.meaning: The bountiful one who gave the ambrosia of heaven, the honey, beheld in Serai amid its long fields — the Lord of the immortals who graced by slaying the elephant — Him let us go to Kudanthai and see.

25.translit:
ingE pOdhungkolO, inavEl neDung kaNgaLippa,
kongaar sOlaik kudandhaik kidandhamaal, ingE pOdhungkolO.
25.meaning: Will He come here, so that my long spear-like eyes may rejoice? Tirumal who reclines at Kudanthai, surrounded by groves rich with nectar-laden blossoms — will He come here?

26.translit:
aRiyOmE yenRuraikka laamE emakku,
veRiyaar pozhilsUzh viyankudandhai mEvi,
chiRiyaanOr piLLaiyaay meLLa naDandhiTTu uRiyaar
naRuveNNe yuNDugandhaar thammaiyE?
26.meaning: Can we ever say 'We do not know Him' — the One who dwells in the great Kudanthai surrounded by fragrant groves, who as a little child toddled gently along and joyfully ate the sweet white butter from the hanging pots?

27.translit:
aNDaththin mugadazhundha alaimuna neerth thiraithathumba aava venRu,
thoNDarkkum amararkkum munivarkkum thaanaruLi, ulagamEzhum
uNDoththa thiruvayiTRin agampaDiyil vaiththummai yuyyakkoNDa,
koNDaRkai maNivaNNan thaNkudandhai nagarppaaDi yaaDeergaLE.
27.meaning: When the very pinnacle of the cosmos sank and the waves of the surging ocean welled up, He cried 'Alas!' and graced devotees, immortals and sages alike; swallowing all seven worlds and keeping them within His sacred belly, He saved you. That sapphire-hued Lord of the cloud-like hand — sing and dance of His town, cool Kudanthai!

28.translit:
mUvaril mudhalva naaya oruvanai yulagang koNDa,
kOvinaik kudandhai mEya gurumaNith thiraLai, inbap
paavinaip pachchaith thEnaip paimponnai yamarar chennip
pUvinai, pugazhum thoNDar enjollip pugazhvar thaamE?
28.meaning: The One who is foremost among the three, the Lord who measured and claimed the worlds, the precious heap of great gems who abides at Kudanthai, the verse of bliss, the fresh honey, the pure gold, the flower upon the heads of the immortals — by what words can the devotees who praise Him fully praise Him?

29.translit:
kaaviyai venRa kaNNaar kalaviyE karudhi, naaLum
paaviyE naaga veNNi adhanuLLE pazhuththo zhindhEn,
thUvisEr annam mannum sUzhpunal kudandhai yaanai,
paaviyen paavi yaadhu paaviyE naayi nEnE.
29.meaning: Thinking only of union with women whose eyes outdo the kaavi flower, day after day reckoning myself a sinner, I have rotted away within that very pursuit. Of the Lord of Kudanthai, encircled by waters where feathered swans abide, I — wretched sinner — meditated not at all, and so became a sinner indeed.

30.translit:
pongaarmel liLangongai ponnE pUppap
 porukayalkaN neerarumbap pOndhu ninRu
sengaala madappuRavam peDaikkup pEsum
 siRukuralukk udalurugich chindhiththu, aangE
thaNkaalum thaNkudandhai nagarum paaDith
 thaNkOva lUrppaaDi yaaDak kETTu,
nangaay nang kuDikkidhuvO nanmai? enna
 naRaiyUrum paaDuvaaL navilkin RaaLE.
30.meaning: Her swelling soft young breasts turning golden-pale, her battling carp-like eyes brimming with tears, she comes forth and stands; melting in body at the soft cooing with which the red-legged tender dove speaks to its mate, brooding there, she sings of Thankaal and the cool city of Kudanthai and dances singing of cool Kovalur. Hearing this, when asked 'Dear one, is this fitting for our family's honour?' she keeps on singing even of Naraiyur.

31.translit:
muTRaaraa vanamulaiyaaL paavai maayan
 moyyagalath thuLLiruppaaL aqdhum kaNDum
aTRaaL, than niRaiyazhindhaaL aavik kinRaaL
 aNiyaranga maaDudhumO thOzhee ennum,
peTREn vaaych chollaiRaiyum pEsak kELaaL
 pErppaaDith thaNkudandhai nagarum paaDi,
poTRaama raikkayamnee raaDap pOnaaL
 poruvaTRaa LenmagaLum ponnum aqdhE.
31.meaning: My doll-like daughter with full, lovely breasts — though she sees that the Lady dwells upon the broad chest of the wondrous Maayan, she has wasted away, her modesty undone, sighing for breath, saying 'Shall we dance at beautiful Arangam, my friend?' She will not heed even a word of counsel from me her mother; singing of PErppaadi and the cool city of Kudanthai, she has gone to bathe in the golden-lotus pond. Peerless is my daughter, and so too her preciousness.

32.translit:
anRaayar kulamagaLuk karaiyan Rannai
 alaikadalaik kadaindhadaiththa ammaan Rannai,
kunRaadha valiyarakkar kOnai maaLak
 koDunjilaivaaych charandhurandhu kulanga Laindhu
venRaanai, kunReDuththa thOLi naanai
 viridhiraineer viNNagaram maruvi naaLum
ninRaanai, thaNkudandhaik kidandha maalai
 nediyaanai adinaayEn ninaindhiT DEnE.
32.meaning: The Lord who long ago became the protector of the cowherd-clan's daughter, the Master who churned the surging ocean and dammed it; who, to destroy the king of the strong unfailing demons, loosed arrows from His cruel bow, wiped out their race and triumphed; the One with shoulders that lifted the hill; who dwells abidingly day after day at Vinnagaram of wide-billowing waters; the Tirumal who reclines at cool Kudanthai, the lofty One — I, a lowly cur at His feet, have meditated upon Him.

33.translit:
orupE rundhi yirumalarth thavisil,
oru muRai ayanai yeenRanai,
oru muRai irusudar meedhini liyangaa, mummadhiL
ilangai irukaal vaLaiya, orusilai
onRiya eereyiTR azhalvaay vaaLiyil aTTanai,
mUvaDi naanilam vENDi,
muppuri nUloDu maanuri yilangum.
maarvinil, irupiRapp orumaa Naagi,
oru muRai yeeraDi, mUvula kaLandhaanai,
naaTRisai naDunga anjiRaip paRavai
ERi, naalvaay mummadhath thirusevi
oru thani vEzhath tharandhaiyai, orunaaL
iruneer maduvuL theerththanai mutthee
naanmaRai aivagai vELvi, aRuthozhil
andhaNar vaNaNGum thanmaiyai, aimpulan
agaththinuL cheRuththu, naangudan adakki
mukkuNath thiraNDavai yagaTRi, onRinil
onRi ninRu, aang kirupiRapp aRuppOr
aRiyum thanmaiyai, mukkaN naaRROL
aivaay aravODu aaRupodhi chaDaiyOn
aRivarun thanmaip perumaiyuL ninRanai,
Ezhula keyiTRinil koNDanai, kURiya
aRusuvaip payanum aayinai, sudarviDum
aimpaDai angaiyuL amarndhanai, sundhara
naaTROL munneer vaNNa, nin eeraDi
onRiya manaththaal, orumadhi mugaththu
mangaiyar iruvarum malarana, angaiyil
muppozhudhum varuda aRidhuyil amarndhanai,
neRimuRai naalvagai varuNamum aayinai,
mEdhagum aimperum bUdhamum neeyE,
aRupadham muralum kUndhal kaaraNam
EzhviDai yadaNGach cheTRanai, aRuvagaich
samayamum aRivaru nilaiyinai, aimpaal
Odhiyai aagath thiruththinai, aRamudhal
naanga vaiyaay mUrththi mUnRaay
iruvagaip payanaay onRaay virindhu
ninRanai, kunRaa madhumalarch chOlai
vaNkodip paDappai, varupunal ponni
maamaNi yalaikkum, sennelon kazhanith
thigazhvana muDuththa, kaRpOr purisaik
kanaka maaLigai, nimirkodi visumbil
iLampiRai thuvakkum, selvam malgudhen
thirukkudandhai, andhaNar mandhira mozhiyudan
vaNaNGa, aaDara vamaLiyil aRidhuyil
amarndha parama, nin adiyiNai paNivan
varumidar agala maaTRO vinaiyE.
33.meaning: You brought forth Brahma upon the twin-blossom seat of a single great navel; once, so the sun and moon could not move above it, You bent the triple-walled Lanka's two flanks with a single bow and destroyed it with a fierce-mouthed twin-feathered arrow. Asking for three paces of the fourfold earth, You — wearing the sacred thread and gleaming deer-skin upon Your chest, the twice-born become one great Vamana — measured the three worlds in two strides at once. Mounting the fair-winged bird so the four directions trembled, You once delivered in the deep pool the suffering of the lone elephant of slack mouth, triple rut and two ears. You are the nature reverenced by Brahmins of the triple fire, four Vedas, fivefold sacrifice and six duties; You are known to those who, curbing the five senses within, subduing the four, casting out the two of the three strands, abiding fixed upon the One, sever the cycle of rebirth. You stood in the greatness of a nature hard for the three-eyed, four-shouldered, five-mouthed serpent-wearing one with matted locks bearing the six (Ganga) to comprehend. You took the seven worlds within Your teeth; You became the fruit of the six declared tastes; You rest the radiant five weapons in Your lovely palm. O beautiful four-shouldered One, hued like the ocean, with mind fixed upon Your two feet, while the two ladies of single-moon faces caress them like lotuses with their palms at all three watches, You repose in the mystic slumber. You became the fourfold castes in due order; You alone are the five great elements; for the sake of her whose hair, where bees hum, You subdued and slew the seven bulls; You are the state beyond the knowing of the six religions. You set upon Your body her of the five-braided tresses; You stand expanded as the four beginning with dharma, the threefold deity, the twofold fruit, becoming the One. At southern Tirukkudanthai of wealth abounding — with groves of unfading honeyed flowers, gardens of rich creepers, where the flowing Ponni dashes great gems, where fields of bright fine paddy gird the splendour, with golden mansions of stone-set ramparts whose rising banners brush the young crescent in the sky — where Brahmins bow with chanted mantras, O Supreme One reposing in mystic slumber upon the spreading serpent-couch, I bow at Your united feet: dispel my coming sorrow, undo my karma!

34.translit:
kaar aar maNi niRak kaNNanUr viNNagaram
seer aar gaNapuram sERai thiruvazhundhUr
kaar aar kudandhai kaDigai kadalmallai
Er aar pozhil sUzh idavendhai neermalai
seer aarum maalirunjOlai thirumOgUr
34.meaning: Vinnagaram, the town of Kannan whose hue is dark like the rain-laden sapphire cloud; glorious Ganapuram, Serai, Tiruvazhundhur; cloud-dark Kudanthai, Kadigai, Kadalmallai; Idavendhai encircled by beautiful groves, Neermalai; glorious Maaliruncholai, Tirumogur.

35.translit:
min idaiyaaL naayaganai viN nagaruL pon malaiyai
ponni maNi kozhikkum pUng kudandhaip pOr viDaiyai
thennan kuRunguDiyuL sem pavaLak kunRinai
manniya thaN sERai vaLLalai maamalarmEl
35.meaning: The consort of the Lady with a waist like lightning, the golden mountain in Vinnagaram, the warrior-bull of flowery Kudanthai where the Ponni washes down gems, the red-coral hill in the southern Kurunkudi, the bountiful one abidingly at cool Serai — He upon the great lotus-flower.

36.translit:
thamaruLLam thanjai thalaiyarangam thaNkaal,
thamaruLLum thaNporuppu vElai, - thamaruLLum
maamallai kOval madhiTkudandhai yenbarE,
Evalla endhaik kidam.
36.meaning: His devotees' hearts, Thanjai, foremost Arangam, Thankaal; the cool hill (Venkatam) cherished in His devotees' hearts, the ocean (Tiruparkadal); and Mamallai, Koval, walled Kudanthai cherished by His devotees — these they declare to be the abodes of my Father who is able to command all.

37.translit:
engaL perumaan imaiyOr thalaimagannee,
senga NeDumaal thirumaarbaa, - pongu
paDamUkki naayiravaayp paambaNaimEl sErndhaay,
kudamUkkil kOyilaak koNDu.
37.meaning: You are our Lord, the chief of the celestials; the red-eyed lofty Tirumal, the One of the sacred chest. You recline upon the serpent-couch of a thousand mouths with swelling hoods, having taken the temple at Kudamukku (Kudanthai) as Your abode.

38.translit:
sErndha thirumaal kadalkudandhai vEngadam
nErndhaven sindhai niRaivisumbu, - vaayndha
maRaiyaa Dagamanandhan vaNDuzhaayk kaNNi,
iRaipaaDi yaaya ivai.
38.meaning: Tirumal abides in the ocean (of milk), at Kudanthai, at Venkatam, and in my devoted heart, in the full sky (of Paramapadam), in the sacred Tiruvaadagam of the Vedas, upon Ananta, wearing the bee-humming tulasi garland — these are the praises sung of the Lord.

39.translit:
viNNagaram veqkaa viridhiraineer vEngadam,
maNNagaram maamaaDa vELukkai, maNNagaththa
thenkudandhai thEnaar thiruvarangam thenkOTTi,
thankuDangai neerERRaan thaazhvu.
