39.meaning: Vinnagaram, Vehka, Venkatam of wide-billowing waters; Mannagaram, Velukkai of great mansions; southern Kudanthai of this earth, honey-laden Tiruvarangam, southern Tirukkottiyur — these are the abodes He who once received water in His own cupped palm (as Vamana) humbled Himself to dwell in.

40.translit:
Aaraa amudhe! adiyen udalam ninpaal anbaaye,
Neeraay alaindhu karaiya urukkuginra nedumaale,
Seeraar sennel kavari veesum sezhuneerk tirukkudandhai,
Eraar kolam thigazhak kidandhaay kanden emmaane!
40.meaning: O insatiable nectar! My body, in its love for You, melts away dissolving like water, O great Tirumal who makes it so. In rich Tirukkudandhai of fertile waters, where flourishing red paddy waves like fans of honor, I have seen You reclining in resplendent beauty, O my Lord!

41.translit:
Emmaane! en vellai moorthi! ennai aalvaane,
Emmaa uruvum vendum aatraal aavaay ezhilere,
Semmaa kamalam sezhuneer misaikkanmalarum tirukkudandhai,
Ammaa malarkkan valarginraane, en naan seigene.
41.meaning: O my Lord! My white-hued form! You who rule over me! O majestic bull, You become whatever form is desired, in whatever way. In Tirukkudandhai, where great red lotuses blossom upon the abundant waters, You repose with great flower-like eyes. What shall I do?

42.translit:
En naan seigen? yaare kalaikan? ennai en seiginraay?
Unnaal allaal yaavaraalum onrum kuraivendhen,
Kannaar madhilsoozh kudandhaik kidandhaay! adiyen aruvaazhnaal,
Sennaal ennaal annaal un thaal pidithe selakkaane.
42.meaning: What shall I do? Who else is my refuge? What are You doing to me? Except from You, I seek nothing wanting from anyone else. O You who recline in Kudandhai girt by strong, lofty walls! Whatever day this servant's precious remaining days may run, on that very day let me see them spent clinging to Your feet.

43.translit:
Selakkaan kirpaar kaanum alavum sellum keerthiyaay!
Ulappilaane ellaa ulagum udaiya orumoorthi!
Nalaththaal mikkaar kudandhaik kidandhaay! unnaik kaanbaan naan
Alappaay, aagaasaththai nokki azhuvan thozhuvane.
43.meaning: O One of fame that extends as far as those who can perceive may perceive! O endless One, the sole Lord who owns all the worlds! O You who recline in Kudandhai of people abounding in goodness! To behold You, I grow distressed; gazing up at the sky I weep and worship.

44.translit:
Azhuvan thozhuvan aadik kaanban paadi alatruvan,
Thazhuval vinaiyaal pakkam nokki naanik kavizhndhiruppan,
Sezhuvon pazhanak kudandhaik kidandhaay! senthaamaraikkannaa!
Thozhuvanenai un thaal serum vagaiye soozhkandaay.
44.meaning: I weep, I worship, I dance and look about, I sing and cry aloud, and through clinging sins I glance to the side, grow ashamed and stand with head bowed. O You who recline in Kudandhai of rich, bright fields! O red-lotus-eyed One! I worship You; devise some way for me to reach Your feet.

45.translit:
Soozhkandaay en thollai vinaiyai aruththun adi serum
Oozhkandirundhe, thooraak kuzhithoorththu enainaal aganriruppan?
Vaazhthol pugazhaar kudandhaik kidandhaay! vaanor komaane!
Yaazhin isaiye! amudhe! arivin payane! ariyere!
45.meaning: Devise some way: cutting off my ancient sins, knowing the destined order of reaching Your feet, yet filling up the unfillable pit, how many days must I remain apart? O You who recline in Kudandhai of people of ancient, living glory! O King of the celestials! O music of the lute! O nectar! O fruit of wisdom! O lion-bull!

46.translit:
Ariyere! en ambor sudare! sengat karumugile,
Eriye pavalak kunre! naatrol endhaay! unadarule,
Piriyaa adimai ennaik kondaay kudandhait tirumaale,
Thariyen iniyun saranan thanthen sanmam kalaiyaaye.
46.meaning: O lion-bull! My beautiful golden radiance! O red-eyed dark cloud! O blazing fire, O coral hill! O my four-shouldered Father! By Your grace alone, O Tirumal of Kudandhai, You have taken me into unswerving servitude. I can bear no more; grant me Your refuge now and remove my births.

47.translit:
Kalaivaay thunbam kalaiyaa dhozhivaay kalaikan matrilen,
Valaivaay nemip padaiyaay! kudandhaik kidandha maamaayaa!
Thalaraa udalam enna thaavi sarindhu pompodhu,
Ilaiyaa dhunadhaal orungap pidiththu podha isaineeye.
47.meaning: Whether You remove my sorrow or leave it unremoved, I have no other refuge. O wielder of the curved-mouthed discus! O great wondrous One reclining in Kudandhai! When my body grows feeble and my soul slips and departs, grant that, without faltering, I may go holding fast together to Your feet.

48.translit:
Isaiviththu ennai yun thaal inaikeezh iruththum ammaane!
Asaivil amarar thalaivar! thalaivaa aadhi perumoorthi!
Thisaivil veesum sezhumaa manigal serum tirukkudandhai,
Asaivil ulagam paravak kidandhaay, kaana vaaraaye.
48.meaning: O my Lord who, having won my consent, seats me beneath Your pair of feet! O leader of the unwavering immortals! O Lord, O ancient great Being! In Tirukkudandhai, where splendid great gems gather scattering light in all directions, You recline as the steadfast world worships. Come, that I may behold You.

49.translit:
Vaaraa varuvaay varumen maayaa! maayaa moorthiyaay!
Aaraa amudhaay adiyen aavi agame thiththippaay,
Theeraa vinaigal theera ennai aandaay! tirukkudandhai
Uuraay! unakkaatpattum adiyen innam uzhalveno?
49.meaning: O my wondrous One who comes though never coming, who is yet undimmed! As a form that never fades, as insatiable nectar, You grow sweet within this servant's very soul. You ruled me so that my unending sins were ended! O You whose town is Tirukkudandhai! Having become Your servant, must this servant still wander on?

50.translit:
Uzhalai enbin peichchi mulaiyoodu avalai uyirundaan,
Kazhalgal avaiye saranaak konda kurugoorch chatakopan,
Kuzhalil maliyach chonna oraayiraththul ippaththum
Mazhalai theera vallaar kaamar maaney nokkiyarkke.
50.meaning: Of the thousand verses, sweeter than the flute, that Satakopan of Kurugur sang, who took as his refuge alone the feet of Him who drained the life of the bony-framed demoness through her breast, these ten, for those able to master them clearing away childish lisping, win the love of doe-eyed maidens.

0.translit:
vaNDuNu narumala riNDaikoNDu paNDainam vinaikeDa venDru, aDimeel
toNDaru mamarum paNiyaninDru angkaNDamo DakaliDa maLandavanee
aaNDaayunaik kaaNbatoo raruLenak karuLutiyeel,
veeNDeenmanai vaazhkkaiyai viNNakar meeyavanee.
0.meaning: O Lord who, while devotees gather to worship offering at Your feet two fragrant flower-garlands sipped by bees so that our age-old karma may be destroyed, stood and measured both this earth and the heavens! You who took me as Your own — if You grant me the grace of beholding You, then I want not the life of household and home, O Lord who abides in Tiruvinnagar!

1.translit:
aNNalsey talaikaDal kaDainttatu atanuL kaNNutal nanjuNak kaNDavanee
viNNava ramutuNa amutilvarum peNNamu
tuNDavem perumaanee aaNDaayunaik kaaNbatoo raruLenak karuLutiyeel,
veeNDeenmanai vaazhkkaiyai viNNakar meeyavanee.
1.meaning: O great One who, when the primal milk-ocean was churned, looked on while the brow-eyed Lord (Shiva) drank the poison that came from within it; my Lord who, when the celestials drank the nectar, partook of the woman-nectar (Lakshmi) that arose with that nectar! You who took me as Your own — if You grant me the grace of beholding You, then I want not the life of household and home, O Lord who abides in Tiruvinnagar!

2.translit:
kuzhalnira vaNNa nin kooRukoNDa tazhalnira vaNNannaN Naarnakaram
vizha nani malaisilai vaLaivuseytuang kazhalnira ambatu vaanavanee
aaNDaayunnaik kaaNbatooraruLenak karuLutiyeel,
veeNDeenmanai vaazhkkaiyai viNNakar meeyavanee.
2.meaning: O You whose hue is dark as a cloud, while the flame-hued one (Shiva) who shares half Your form stood by, You bent the mountain-bow and made the fire-hued arrow fly so that the cities of foes were utterly destroyed, O divine One! You who took me as Your own — if You grant me the grace of beholding You, then I want not the life of household and home, O Lord who abides in Tiruvinnagar!

3.translit:
nilavoDu veyilnila virucuDarum ulakamu muyirkaLu muNDorukaal,
kalaitaru kuzhaviyi nuru vinaiyaay alaikaDa laalilai vaLarndavanee
aaNDaayunaik kaaNbatooraruLenak karuLutiyeel
veeNDeenmanai vaazhkkaiyai viNNakar meeyavanee
3.meaning: O You who once swallowed the two luminaries that shed moonlight and sunlight, and the worlds and all living beings, and then in the form of a tender babe lay upon a banyan leaf afloat on the surging waters of deluge! You who took me as Your own — if You grant me the grace of beholding You, then I want not the life of household and home, O Lord who abides in Tiruvinnagar!

4.translit:
paarezhu kaDalezhu malaiyezhumaayc ceerkezhu mivvula keezhumellaam,
aarkezhu vayiRRini laDakki ninDruang koorezhut tooruru vaanavanee
aaNDaayunaik kaaNbatooraruLenak karuLutiyeel,
veeNDeenmanai vaazhkkaiyai viNNakar meeyavanee.
4.meaning: O You who held within Your sated belly all the seven worlds of earth, the seven seas, the seven mountains, and all these glorious seven worlds, and stood as the One who is the single sacred syllable (Om) and the one Form! You who took me as Your own — if You grant me the grace of beholding You, then I want not the life of household and home, O Lord who abides in Tiruvinnagar!

5.translit:
kaarkezhu kaDalkaLum malaikaLumaay eerkezhu mulakamu maaki,muta
laarkaLu maRivaru nilaiyinaiyaayc ceerkezhu naanmaRai yaanavanee
aaNDaayunaik kaaNbatoo raruLenak karuLutiyeel,
veeNDeenmanai vaazhkkaiyai viNNakar meeyavanee.
5.meaning: O You who became the cloud-laden oceans and the mountains, and the beautiful worlds, and who are of a nature that even the foremost sages cannot fully know; O You who are the glorious four Vedas! You who took me as Your own — if You grant me the grace of beholding You, then I want not the life of household and home, O Lord who abides in Tiruvinnagar!

6.translit:
urukkuRu naruneykoN Daarazhalil iRukkuRu mandaNar santiyinvaay,
perukkamo DamararkaL amaranalkum irukkini linnisai yaanavanee
aaNDaayunaik kaaNbatooraruLenak karuLutiyeel,
veeNDeenmanai vaazhkkaiyai viNNakar meeyavanee.
6.meaning: O You who are the sweet music of the Rig Veda, by which the brahmins who pour melted fragrant ghee into the abundant sacrificial fire, at the junctures of their worship, bestow plenty so that the celestials thrive! You who took me as Your own — if You grant me the grace of beholding You, then I want not the life of household and home, O Lord who abides in Tiruvinnagar!

7.translit:
kaatalsey tiLaiyavar kalavitarum veetanai vinaiyatu veruvutalaam,
aatali nunataDi yaNukuvan naan pootalaar neDumuDip puNNiyanee
aaNDaayunaik kaaNbatooraruLenak karuLutiyeel,
veeNDeenmanai vaazhkkaiyai viNNakar meeyavanee.
7.meaning: Since the torment that is the karma born of the dalliance which loving young women yield brings only dread, I therefore draw near to Your feet, O righteous One crowned with a tall crown bright as the sun! You who took me as Your own — if You grant me the grace of beholding You, then I want not the life of household and home, O Lord who abides in Tiruvinnagar!

8.translit:
saatalum piRattalu menDrivaRRaik kaatalsey yaatuna kazhalaDaindeen,
ootalsey naanmaRai yaakiyumpar aatalsey moovuru vaanavanee,
aaNDaay unaik kaaNbatooraruLenak karuLutiyeel
veeNDeenmanai vaazhkkaiyai viNNakar meeyavanee.
8.meaning: Without craving these things called dying and being born, I have reached Your feet, O You who are the four Vedas that are chanted, and who became the three forms (the Trinity) among the celestials! You who took me as Your own — if You grant me the grace of beholding You, then I want not the life of household and home, O Lord who abides in Tiruvinnagar!

9.translit:
poomaru pozhilaNi viNNakarmeel,
kaamaru seerkkali kanDrisonna,
paamaru tamizhivai paaDavallaar,
vaamanan aDiyiNai maruvuvaree.
9.meaning: Those who can sing these Tamil verses, rich in song, that Kaliyan of cherished fame composed upon Tiruvinnagar adorned with flower-filled groves — they shall draw close to the twin feet of Vamana.

10.translit:
poRutteen punsolnenj cilporu Linba menaviraNDum
iRutteen, aimpulan kaTkaDanaayina vaayiloTTi aRutteen,
aarvacceR Ramavai tannai manattakaRRi veRutteen,
ninnaDain teentiru viNNakar meeyavanee.
10.meaning: I have endured the petty words of my heart; I have cast off the two — wealth and pleasure; closing the door that was the debt owed to the five senses, I have severed them; driving from my mind desire and anger, I have come to loathe them; and I have reached You, O Lord who abides in sacred Tiruvinnagar!

11.translit:
maRandee nunnaimunnam maRan tamati yinmanattaal,
iRandee netta naiyumata naaliDum paikkuzhiyil
piRandee yeyttozhin teenpe rumaanee tirumaarpaa
siRandeen ninnaDik keetiru viNNakar meeyavanee.
11.meaning: Forgetting You of old, with a witless, undiscerning mind I have died countless times, and because of that, born again into the pit of misery, I had grown utterly weary, O my Lord, O You upon whose chest dwells Lakshmi! Now I have prospered, reaching Your very feet, O Lord who abides in sacred Tiruvinnagar!

12.translit:
maaney nokkiyar tamvayiR Rukkuzhi yiluzhaikkum,
ooneeraakkai tannai utavaamai yuNarnduNarndu,
vaanee maanila mee vandu vanden manattirunda teenee,
ninnaDain teentiru viNNakar meeyavanee.
12.meaning: Realizing again and again that this flesh-bound body, which toils in the pit of the womb of doe-eyed women, is of no avail, O You who are the heavens and the wide earth, O honey who came and came to dwell within my heart — I have reached You, O Lord who abides in sacred Tiruvinnagar!

13.translit:
piRindeen peRRamak kaLpeNDi renDrivar pinnutavaa
taRindeen nIpaNit tavaru LennumoL vaaLuruvi
eRindeen aimpulan kaLiDar tIra veRinduvandu
seRindeen ninnaDik keetiru viNNakar meeyavanee.
13.meaning: I have parted from these — the children I begot and my wife — knowing that they will not avail hereafter; drawing the bright sword that is the grace You decreed, I struck and routed the five senses so that suffering would end, and coming, I have clung fast to Your very feet, O Lord who abides in sacred Tiruvinnagar!

14.translit:
paaNDeen vaNDaRai yumkuzha laarkaLpal laaNDisaippa,
aaNDaar vaiyamel laam ara saaki, munnaaNDavaree
maaNDaa renDruvan taaranTHODE manai vaazhkkaitannai veeNDeen,
ninnaDain teentiru viNNakar meeyavanee.
14.meaning: Having ruled all the earth as kings, while women with tresses where bees hum and honey drips sang their praises 'Long may they live!' — those who once reigned, alas, they came to be spoken of as having perished. Therefore I want not the life of household and home; I have reached You, O Lord who abides in sacred Tiruvinnagar!

15.translit:
kallaa vaimpulan kaLavai kaNDavaa seyyakilleen,
mallaa, mallama ruLmal larmaaLa mallaDartta mallaa,
mallalam seermatiL nIrilang kaiyazhitta
villaa, ninnaDain teentiru viNNakar meeyavanee.
15.meaning: I am unable to do as the untaught, wayward five senses bid me, O wrestler who, in the wrestling-bout, crushed the wrestlers (Chanura and Mushtika) so that they died! O Archer who, with Your bow, destroyed prosperous, fair-walled Lanka set amid the waters! I have reached You, O Lord who abides in sacred Tiruvinnagar!

16.translit:
veeRaa yaaniran teenveku Laatu manakkoLandaay,
aaRaa vennara kattaDi yeenai yiDakkaruti,
kooRaa aivarvan tukumaik kakkuDi viTTavarai,
teeRaa tunnaDain teentiru viNNakar meeyavanee.
16.meaning: Apart from all else I have begged of You; receive me into Your mind without anger, O You who took it kindly. When the five (senses), who plot to cast this servant into the ceaseless burning hell, came and tormented me, I cast them out of my dwelling, and trusting them no more, I have reached You, O Lord who abides in sacred Tiruvinnagar!

17.translit:
tIvaay valvinai yaaruDa ninDru siRandavarpool,
meevaa vennara kattiDa uRRu virainduvandaar,
moovaa vaanavar tammutal vaa mati koLviDutta
teevaa, ninnaDain teentiru viNNakar meeyavanee.
17.meaning: The fierce, mighty karmas, dwelling with me as though they were my dearest, came hastening to cast me into the unwelcome burning hell. O foremost One of the ageless celestials, O God who freed the moon from the grip (of the eclipse)! I have reached You, O Lord who abides in sacred Tiruvinnagar!

18.translit:
pootaar taamarai yaaLpula vikkula vaanavartam
kootaa, kotilseng kolkuDai manna riDainaDanda
tootaa, tUmozhi yaay.suDar poolen manattirunda
veetaa, ninnaDain teentiru viNNakar meeyavanee.
18.meaning: O Consort of her who dwells on the blossoming lotus (Lakshmi); O Lord of the host of learned celestials; O Envoy who walked amid kings of flawless, righteous scepter and royal parasol (as Krishna's emissary); O You of pure, true speech; O Vedic One who, like a radiance, dwelt within my heart! I have reached You, O Lord who abides in sacred Tiruvinnagar!

19.translit:
teenaar poompuRa viltiru viNNakar meeyavanai,
vaanaa rummatil sUzhvayal mangaiyar koon, maruvaar
Unaar veelkali yanolisey tamizh maalaivallaar,
koonaay vaanavar tamkoDi maanakar kooDuvaree.
19.meaning: Those who master this melodious Tamil garland that Kaliyan — chief of the Mangai folk of fields girt by sky-high walls, he of the flesh-stained spear borne against foes — sang upon the Lord who abides at sacred Tiruvinnagar amid honey-laden flowering groves: they shall reach the great banner-decked city of the celestials, to reign there as lords.

20.translit:
thuRappEn allEn in pamthuRa vaadhu, ninnuruvam
maRappEn allEn enRum maRavaadhu, yaan ulagil
piRappEn aagaveNNEn piRavaamai peTRadhu, nin
thiRaththEn aadhanmaiyaal thiruviNNagaraanE.
20.meaning: I will not give you up, nor turn away from your joy; I will never forget your sacred form, and I shall never forget it. In this world I do not wish to be born again, for I have attained release from birth. This I gained because I belong wholly to you, O Lord of Tiruvinnagar.

21.translit:
thuRandhEn aarvac cheRRac cuRRam thuRandhamaiyaal,
ciRandhEn ninnadikkE adimai thirumaalE,
aRandhaanaayth thirivaay unnai en manaththagaththE,
thiRambaamal koNdEn thiruviNNagaraanE.
21.meaning: Having renounced desire, anger, and the bonds of kinship, I have been ennobled into servitude at your feet alone, O Tirumal. You who wander about as righteousness itself, I have held you firmly within my heart without wavering, O Lord of Tiruvinnagar.

22.translit:
maanEy nOkku nallaar madhi pOl mugaththu ulavum,
oonEy kaN vaaLikku udaindhu OttandhunnadaindhEn,
kOnE kuRunGudiyuL kuzhagaa thirunaRaiyoorth
thEnE, varupunal soozh thiruviNNagaraanE.
22.meaning: Broken by the flesh-piercing dart-like glances that play upon the moon-like faces of fair women with deer-like eyes, I came running and took refuge in you. O King, O graceful one of Kurungudi, O honey of Tirunaraiyur, O Lord of Tiruvinnagar girt by flowing waters!

23.translit:
saandhEndhu menmulaiyaar thadanthOL puNar inba veLLath
thaazhndhEn, arunagaraththu azhundhum payan padaiththEn,
pOndhEn, puNNiyanE. unai eydhi en theevinaigaL
theerndhEn, ninnadaindhEn thiruviNNagaraanE.
23.meaning: I sank into the flood of pleasure embracing the broad shoulders of women with soft sandal-anointed breasts, and so earned only the reward of drowning in cruel hell. But I have come away, O virtuous one; reaching you my evil deeds are ended, and I have taken refuge in you, O Lord of Tiruvinnagar.

24.translit:
maRROr deyvam eNNEn unnai en manaththu vaiththup
peTREn, peTRadhuvum piRavaamai emperumaan,
vaTRaa neeL kadal soozh ilangai iraavaNanaic
ceTRaay, koTRavanE. thiruviNNagaraanE.
24.meaning: I think of no other god; having placed you within my heart, I have attained you, and what I have attained is freedom from birth, O my Lord. You who destroyed Ravana of Lanka encircled by the never-drying vast ocean, O victorious one, O Lord of Tiruvinnagar!

25.translit:
mai oN karunGadalum nilanum maNivaraiyum,
ceyya cudar iraNdum ivaiyaaya ninnai, nenjil
uyyum vagai uNarndhE uNmaiyaal ini yaadhum maRROr
deyvam piRidhu aRiyEn thiruviNNagaraanE.
25.meaning: You who are the dark collyrium-bright ocean, the earth, the jewelled mountains, and the two red luminaries—having truly realized you in my heart in the way that brings deliverance, henceforth I know no other god whatsoever, O Lord of Tiruvinnagar.

26.translit:
vERE kooRuvadhu uNdu adiyEn viriththuraikkum
aaRE, nee paNiyaadhu adai nin thirumanaththu,
kooREn nenju thannaal guNamkoNdu maRROr deyvam
thERREn unnaiyallaal thiruviNNagaraanE.
26.meaning: There is something apart that I, your servant, would set forth at length—do not command otherwise but let it abide in your sacred mind. With my heart I will not extol the qualities of any other god, nor place trust in any but you, O Lord of Tiruvinnagar.

27.translit:
muLi theendha vEngadaththu moori perunG kaLiTRaal,
viLi theendha maamaram pOl veezhndhaarai ninaiyaadhE
aLindhu Orndha cindhai ninpaal, adiyERku, vaanulagam
theLindhE enRu eydhuvadhu? thiruviNNagaraanE.
27.meaning: Not thinking of those who fall ruined like a great tree destroyed by a mighty elephant in the scorched and burning hill, my mind has melted and fixed its thought upon you. For me your servant, when shall I clearly attain the celestial world, O Lord of Tiruvinnagar?

28.translit:
sollaay thirumaarvaa unakkaagith thoNdupatta
nallEnai vinaigaL naliyaamai nambu nambee,
mallaaguda aadi. madhusoodhanE ulagil sellaa
nallisaiyaay thiruviNNagaraanE.
28.meaning: Speak, O you who bear Lakshmi on your chest! Resolve that the deeds shall not torment me, your good servant who has become devoted to you, O trustworthy one. O you who danced the pot-dance like a wrestler, O Madhusudana, O you of pure fame that spreads through the world, O Lord of Tiruvinnagar!

29.translit:
thaaraar malark kamalath thadanG soozhndha thaN puRavil,
seeraar nedumaRugil thiruviNNagaraanai
kaaraar puyal thadakkaik kaliyan oli maalai,
aaraar ivai vallaar avarkku allal nillaavE.
29.meaning: This garland of resounding verses on the Lord of Tiruvinnagar of cool meadows ringed with ponds of garlanded lotus blooms and noble long streets was composed by Kaliyan whose broad hands are like the rain-cloud. Whoever can master these, no sorrow will abide for them.

30.translit:
baththar aaviyaip paal madhiyai, aNith
thoththai maaliruncOlaith thozhudhu pOy
muththinai maNiyai maNi maaNikka
viththinai, senRu viNNagark kaaNdumE.
30.meaning: Worshipping at Maliruncholai him who is the very life of devotees, the milk-white moon, the beautiful cluster (of flowers), going on—the pearl, the gem, the seed of sapphire and ruby—we shall go and behold him at Vinnagar.

31.translit:
anRaayar kula magaLukku araiyan thannai
 alaikadalaik kadaindhadaiththa ammaan thannai,
kunRaadha valiyarakkar kOnai maaLak
 kodunGcilaivaayc cazhandhurandhu kulangaLaindhu
venRaanai, kunRu eduththa thOLinaanai
 viridhirai neer viNNagaram maruvi naaLum
ninRaanai, thaN kudandhaik kidandha maalai
 nediyaanai adi naayEn ninaindhittEnE.
31.meaning: Him who long ago became the lord of the cowherd-clan's daughter, the Lord who churned and dammed the surging ocean; him who, to destroy the king of the mighty unyielding demons, shot arrows from his cruel bow and uprooted his lineage and conquered; him of the shoulders that lifted the hill, who dwells forever at Vinnagar of spreading wavy waters; Tirumal who reclines at cool Kudandai—that great one I, a lowly dog, have set my thought upon.

32.translit:
kaaraar maNi niRak kaNNanoor viNNagaram
seeraar gaNapuram sERai thiruvazhundhoor
kaaraar kudandhai kadigai kadalmallai
Eraar pozhil soozh idavendhai neermalai
seeraarum maaliruncOlai thirumOgoor
32.meaning: Vinnagar, the town of Kannan whose hue is like the dense dark cloud and the sapphire gem; glorious Ganapuram, Sherai, Tiruvazhundur; cloud-dark Kudandai, Kadigai, Kadalmallai; Idavendai surrounded by lovely groves, Neermalai; the glorious Maliruncholai, and Tirumogur.

33.translit:
min idaiyaaL naayaganai viN nagaruL pon malaiyai
ponni maNi kozhikkum poonG kudandhaip pOr vidaiyai
thennan kuRunGudiyuL sem pavaLak kunRinai
manniya thaN sERai vaLLalai maamalarmEl
33.meaning: The Lord of her of the lightning-slender waist, the golden mountain within Vinnagar; the battling bull of flowery Kudandai where the Ponni river washes down gems; the red-coral hill within Kurungudi of the Pandya land; the generous one of cool enduring Sherai—he who is upon the great flower (Lakshmi's lord).

34.translit:
paNdellaam vEngadam paaRkadal vaikundham,
koNdanGuRaivaarkkuk kOyil pOl, - vaNdu
vaLanGgiLarum neeL sOlai vaNpoonG kadigai,
iLanGgumaran than viNNagar.
34.meaning: For him who in former times dwelt and held court at Venkatam, the Milk-Ocean, and Vaikuntha as his temples—now his abode is the Vinnagar of the youthful prince, with its long groves where bees thrive and abundant blossoming Kadigai.

35.translit:
viNNagaram veqkaa viridhirai neer vEngadam,
maNNagaram maamaada vELukkai, maNNagaththa
thenkudandhai thEnaar thiruvarangam thenkOtti,
than kudanGgai neer ETRaan thaazhvu.
35.meaning: Vinnagar, Vehka, Venkatam of spreading wavy waters; the great town of mansions Velukkai; in this earthly realm southern Kudandai, honey-rich Srirangam, and southern Tirukkottiyur—these are the dwellings (the descent) of him who received the water of the gift in the hollow of his hand.

36.translit:
nalguravum selvum naragum suvarggamumaay,
velpagaiyum natpum vidamum amudhamumaay,
palvagaiyum parandha perumaan ennai aaLvaanai,
selvam malgu kudith thiruviNNagark kaNdEnE.
36.meaning: Being poverty and wealth, hell and heaven, conquering enmity and friendship, poison and nectar—the Lord who is spread out as all manifold things, who rules over me—I have seen him at Tiruvinnagar of prosperous, abounding settlements.

37.translit:
kaNda inbam thunbam kalakkanGgaLum thERRamumaay,
thaNdamum thaNmaiyum thazhalum nizhalumaay,
kaNdukOdaRkariya perumaan ennai aaLvaanoor,
theNdiraip punal soozh thiruviNNagar nannagarE.
37.meaning: Being the pleasure and pain that are seen, the confusions and the clarity, punishment and gentleness, flame and shade—the Lord hard to behold, who rules over me—his town is the goodly city of Tiruvinnagar, encircled by clear rippling waters.

38.translit:
nagaramum naadugaLumun GYaanamum moodamum aay,
nigar il soozh, sudar aay, iruL aay, nilan aay, visumbu aay,
sigara maadangaL soozh thiruvaNNagar sErndha piraan
pugar koL keerththi allaal, illai yaavarkkum puNNiyamE.
38.meaning: Being cities and countries, knowledge and ignorance, the peerless encompassing radiance, the darkness, the earth, the sky—the Lord who has come to dwell at Tiruvinnagar ringed by peaked mansions: apart from his lustrous fame there is no other merit for anyone.

39.translit:
puNNiyam paavam, puNarcci pirivu enRu ivai aay,
eNNa aay maRappu aay uNmai aay inmai aay, allan aay,
thiNNa maadangaL soozh thiruviNNagar sErndha piraan.
kaNNan in aruLE kaNdu koLminggaL – kaidhavamE?
39.meaning: Being merit and sin, union and separation, being thought and forgetting, existence and non-existence, and yet being none of these—the Lord who has come to dwell at Tiruvinnagar ringed by sturdy mansions: behold and grasp the sweet grace of Kannan—is there any deceit in this?

40.translit:
kaithavam semmai karumai veLumaiyumaay,
meypoy yiLamai mudumaipudumai pazhamaiyumaay,
seyyathiN madhiLsoozh thiruviNNagarc cheernthapiraan,
peythakaavu kaNDeer perunthevudai moovulagae.
40.meaning: He is deceit and uprightness, blackness and whiteness; He is truth and falsehood, youth and old age, newness and antiquity. He is the Lord who has come to dwell in Tiruvinnagar girt by red, strong ramparts. Behold, the three worlds with their great gods are but the grove He has planted.

41.translit:
moovulagang gaLumaay allanaayugap paaymunivaay,
poovilvaazh magaLaayth thavvaiyaayppu zhaaypazhiyaay,
devarme vitthezhum thiruviNNagarc cheernthapiraan,
paaviyaen manatthae yuraiginRa paranjsudarae.
41.meaning: He is the three worlds and also that which is not them; He is delight and He is wrath; He is the Lady who dwells in the lotus (Lakshmi), and her elder sister (Mudevi, misfortune); He is praise and He is blame. He is the Lord who has come to Tiruvinnagar where the celestials gather and rise in worship, the Supreme Effulgence who abides in the heart of sinful me.

42.translit:
paranjsudar udambaay azhukkupathitha vudambaay,
karanthumthon RiyumninRum kaithavangaL seyyum, viNNor
sirangaLaal vaNangum thiruviNNagarc cheernthapiraan,
varangoLpaatha mallaalillai yaavarkkum vansaraNae.
42.meaning: He has the body of the Supreme Effulgence, and also the body soiled with impurity; concealing Himself, appearing, and standing forth, He performs His wondrous plays. He is the Lord who has come to Tiruvinnagar, which the celestials worship bowing their heads. Save His boon-granting feet, there is no strong refuge for anyone.

43.translit:
vansaraN surarkkaay asurarkkuveng kooRRamumaay,
thansaraN nizhaRkee zhulagamvaitthum vaiyaathum,
thensaraN thisaikkuth thiruviNNagarc cheernthapiraan,
ensaraNen kaNNan ennaiyaaLudai ennappanae.
43.meaning: He is the strong refuge for the gods, and the fierce God of death for the demons; He places the worlds beneath the shade of His protecting feet, and sometimes does not. He is the Lord who has come to Tiruvinnagar in the southern quarter. He is my refuge, my Kannan, my Father who has taken me as His own.

44.translit:
ennappan enakkaayiguLaay ennaip peRRavaLaay,
ponnappan maNiyappan mutthappanen appanumaay,
minnappon madhiLsoozhthiru viNNagarc cheernthavappan,
thannoppaa rillappan thanthanantha thaaLnizhalae.
44.meaning: He is my Father, my dear comrade for my sake, and the mother who bore me; He is the golden Father, the gem Father, the pearl Father, my own Father. He is the Father who has come to Tiruvinnagar girt by ramparts of glittering gold. That Father who has no equal has granted me the shade of His feet.

45.translit:
nizhalveyil siRumaiperumai kuRumai nedumaiyumaay,
suzhalvananiR panamaRRu maayavai allanumaay,
mazhalaivaazh vaNduvaazh thiruviNNagar mannupiraan,
kazhalgaLanRi maRROr kaLaigaNilam kaaNmingaLae.
45.meaning: He is shade and sunlight, smallness and greatness, shortness and tallness; He is things that move and things that stand still, and also that which is none of these. He is the Lord who abides in Tiruvinnagar where prattling bees live. Behold, there is no other support for us save His anklet-adorned feet.

46.translit:
kaaNmingaL ulageer enRu kaNmugap paenimirntha,
thaaLiNaiyan thannaik kurugoorc chadagopan sonna,
aaNaiyaa yiratthutthiru viNNagarppath thumvallar,
koNaiyinRi viNNorkkonRumaavarkuravargaLae.
46.meaning: Of Him whose pair of feet stretched up right before all eyes, saying 'Behold, O people of the world!', Sadagopan of Kurugur sang. Those who can master these ten among the thousand authoritative verses on Tiruvinnagar will, without fault, become honored elders even to the celestials.

0.translit:
kaNsoora venguruti vandhizhiya venthazhalpool koondhalaaLai,
maNseera mulaiyuNda maamadhalaay vaanavartham koovee. enru,
viNseerum iLandhingaL agaduriñju maNimaada malgu, selvath thaNseeRai
yemperumaan thaaLthozhuvaar kaaNminen thalaimeelaaree.
0.meaning: O great child who, as an infant, drank the very life from the breast of the demoness Putana, whose hair blazed like cruel fire as her eyes faded and hot blood gushed forth as she fell to the ground! O King of the celestials! Thus extolling Him, behold those who worship the feet of our Lord at cool, prosperous Tirucherai, where rows of jeweled mansions rise so high they brush the young crescent moon in the sky -- such devotees I hold upon my head.

1.translit:
amburuva varineduNgaN, alarmagaLai varaiyagalath thamarndhu, mallal
komburuva viLangginamee liLangkanru koNderindha kooththar poolaam,
vambalarum thaNsoolai vaNseeRai vaanundhu kooyil meeya,
emperumaan thaaLthozhuvaa reppozhudhum enmanaththee yirukkin Raaree.
1.meaning: He who embraces upon His mountain-like chest Lakshmi, born of the lotus, with arrow-like brows and long streaked eyes, is the very dancer who hurled the calf-demon against the flourishing wood-apple tree to fell it. Those who worship the feet of our Lord, who dwells in His sky-touching temple at bounteous Tirucherai surrounded by cool fragrant groves, ever abide within my heart at all times.

2.translit:
meedhoodi vaaLeyiRu minnilaga munvilagu muruvi naaLai
kaadhodu kodimookkan rudanaRuththa kaiththalaththaa enru ninru,
thaathodu vaNdalambum thaNseeRai emperumaan thaaLai yeeththi,
poodhodu punalthoovum puNNiyaree viNNavaril poligin Raaree.
2.meaning: "O Lord with the mighty hand that cut off at once the ears and pointed nose of Surpanakha, whose sword-like teeth flashed like lightning and whose form leapt forward!" -- Thus standing and praising the feet of our Lord at cool Tirucherai, where bees hum over the pollen, the virtuous ones who scatter flowers and water in worship shine resplendent even among the celestials.

3.translit:
theeraaLum vaaLarakkan thennilangai veñjamaththup ponri veezha,
pooraaLum silaiyadhanaal porukaNaigaL pookkuviththaay enru, naaLum
thaaraaLum varaimaarban thaNseeRai emperumaa numba raaLum,
peeraaLan peeroodhum periyoorai orukaalum pirigi leenee.
3.meaning: "O Lord who, with Your battle-wielding bow, sent forth fighting arrows so that the sword-bearing demon king who commanded chariots perished and fell in the fierce battle at southern Lanka!" -- I shall never, even for a moment, part from the great ones who daily chant the names of that renowned Lord of cool Tirucherai, the garland-adorned, mountain-chested One who rules over the celestials.

4.translit:
vandhikkum maRRavarkkum maasudambin vallamaNar thamakku malleen,
mundhisen rariyuruvaa yiraNiyanai muraNazhiththa mudhalvark kallaal,
sandhappoo malarsoolaith thaNseeRai emperumaan thaaLai, naaLum
sindhippaark kennuLLam theenoori eppozhudhum thiththik kummee.
4.meaning: I belong neither to those others who bow to alien gods nor to the powerful Jains of defiled bodies; I belong only to the primordial Lord who came forth in the form of the man-lion and crushed the strength of Hiranya. For those who daily meditate on the feet of our Lord at cool Tirucherai, with its groves of fragrant sandal blossoms, my heart wells up with honey and grows sweet at every moment.

5.translit:
paNdeena maayulagai yanridandha paNbaaLaa enru ninru,
thoNdaaneen thiruvadiyee thuNaiyallaal thuNaiyilleen sollu kinreen,
vaNdeendhum malarppuRavil vaNseeRai emperumaa nadiyaar thammai,
kaNdeenuk kidhukaaNee renneñjam kaNNiNaiyum kaLikku maaRee.
5.meaning: "O gracious One who long ago, as the boar, dug up and rescued the world!" -- Thus standing, I have become Your servant; I declare I have no refuge save Your sacred feet alone. Behold how, having beheld the devotees of our Lord at bounteous Tirucherai, where bees throng the flowering meadows, my heart and both my eyes rejoice.

6.translit:
paiviriyum variyaravil padukadaluL thuyilamarndha paNbaa. enrum,
maiviriyum maNivaraipool maayavanee enrenrum, vaNdaar neelam
seyviriyum thaNseeRai yemperumaan thiruvadiyai sindhith theeRku,en
aiyaRivum koNdaanuk kaaLaanaark kaaLaamen anbu thaanee!
6.meaning: "O gracious One who reclines in slumber upon the spotted serpent with spreading hood in the deep ocean!" -- and ever, "O wondrous dark One like a sapphire mountain spreading darkness!" -- thus for me who meditate on the sacred feet of our Lord at cool Tirucherai, where bee-swarmed blue lilies bloom in the fields, my very love itself becomes a servant to those who are servants of Him who has taken hold of all my five senses.

7.translit:
uNNaadhu venguurram oovaadha paavangaL seeraa, meelai
viNNoorum maNNoorum vandhiRaiñjum mendhaLirpoo ladiyi naanai,
paNNaara vaNdiyambum paimpozhilsoozh thaNseeRai yammaan Rannai,
kaNNaarak kaNduruugik kaiyaarath thozhuvaaraik karudhuN kaalee!
7.meaning: To think upon those who behold to their eyes' content and melt, and worship with full hands the Lord of Tirucherai -- He whose feet are soft as tender shoots, before whom both the celestials above and the people of earth come and bow, so that cruel Death cannot consume them and ceaseless sins cannot cling -- the Lord of cool Tirucherai, girt by fresh groves where bees hum in full melody!

8.translit:
kaLLaththeen poyyagaththee naadhalaal poodhorukaal kavalai yennum,
veLLaththeer kenkoloo viLaivayaluL karuneelam kaLaiñjar thaaLaal
thaLLaththeen maNanaaRum thaNseeRai emperumaan thaaLai, naaLum
uLLaththee vaippaaruk kidhukaaNee rennuLLa muruugu maaRee!
8.meaning: Because I am deceitful and false within, what will become of me, caught in the flood called sorrow that surges up from time to time? Behold how my heart melts for those who daily keep in their hearts the feet of our Lord at cool Tirucherai, where honey, pushed loose by the feet of the weeders pulling dark blue lilies in the ripening fields, spreads its fragrance!

9.translit:
poomaaNseer karunguzhalaar poolnadandhu vayalninra pedaiyoodu, annam
theemaavin innizhalil kaNduyilum thaNseeRai yammaan Rannai,
vaamaantheerp parakaalan kaliganri olimaalai koNdu thoNdeer,
thoomaaNseer ponnadimeel soottumin num thuNaikkaiyaal thozhudhu ninree.
9.meaning: O devotees! Take this garland of melodious verses composed by Kalikanri, Parakala of the swift horse-drawn chariot, in praise of the Lord of cool Tirucherai -- where the swan, walking like women with dark beautiful tresses adorned with flowers, sleeps in the sweet shade of the mango tree beside its mate standing in the fields -- and place it upon His pure, glorious golden feet, worshipping with both your hands joined together.

10.translit:
vaanai aaramu thamthandha vaLLalai
theenai neeLvayal seeRaiyil kaNdupooy
aanai vaatti yaruLum amararththam
koonai, yaamgudan thaichchenru kaaNdumee.
10.meaning: The generous One who gave the celestials the nectar, the One sweet as honey, having beheld Him at Cherai of the long fields, we shall go on to Kudandai and behold Him -- the King of the immortals who graciously vanquished the elephant.

11.translit:
kaar aar maNi niRak kaNNanoor viNNagaram
seer aar kaNapuram seeRai thiruvazhundhoor
kaar aar kudandhai kadigai kadalmallai
eer aar pozhil soozh idaventhai neermalai
seer aarum maaliruñjoolai thirumoogoor
11.meaning: Tiruvinnagaram of Kannan dark as a cloud-hued sapphire, glorious Tirukkannapuram, Cherai, Tiruvazhundur, cloud-capped Kudandai, Kadigai, Kadalmallai (Mahabalipuram), Idaventhai girt by beautiful groves, Neermalai, glorious Maliruncholai, and Tirumohur.

12.translit:
min idaiyaaL naayaganai viN nagaruL pon malaiyai
ponni maNi kozhikkum poong kudandhaip poor vidaiyai
thennan kuRunguudiyuL sem pavaLak kunrinai
manniya thaN seeRai vaLLalai maamalarmeel
12.meaning: The Lord of her of the lightning-slender waist, the golden mountain in Tiruvinnagaram, the warrior-bull in lovely Kudandai where the Kaveri washes down gems, the coral hill in the southern land's Tirukkurungudi, the generous One enduring in cool Cherai, He upon the great lotus.

0.translit:
perumpu RakkaTal aiyaTal eeTRRinaip
peNNai yaaNai, eNNil munivarkkaruL
tarunda vaththaimuth thinthiraL koovaiyaip
paththa raaviyai niththilath thoththinai,
arumbi naiyala raiyaTi yeenmanath
taasai yai amu thampoti yiNsuvaik
karumbi naik, kani yaichchenRu naaTik
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNTukoN TEnE.
0.meaning: The great Lord who churned the vast wide ocean, the strong one mighty as a bull, who out of grace toward countless sages grants the fruit of austerity; He who is like a strand of gathered pearls, the very life-breath of His devotees, a cluster of pearls. He is the bud and the blossom, the desire within the heart of me His servant, the sugarcane sweet with nectar's essence, the ripened fruit -- Him I sought and found, and beheld within Kannamangai.

1.translit:
meynna laththavath thaiththivath thaiththarum
meyyaip poyyinaik kaiyilOr sangkuTai,
mainni RakkaTal aikkaTal vaNNanai
maalai aalilaip paLLikoL maayanai,
nenna laippaka laiyiTRRai naaLinai
naaLai yaayvarum thingkaLai yaaNTinai,
kanna laikkarum piniTaith thERalaik
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNTukoN TEnE.
1.meaning: He who is true goodness, austerity, and the heaven it grants; the truth and the falsehood; the one bearing a conch in His hand; the dark-hued ocean and the ocean-colored Lord; Tirumal, the wondrous one who reclines upon a banyan leaf. He is yesterday, today's day, and this very day, the tomorrow yet to come, the month and the year; He is the sweet cane and the distilled nectar within the sugarcane -- Him I sought and found, and beheld within Kannamangai.

2.translit:
engka LukkaruL seykinRa eesanai
vaasa vaarkuzha laaLmalai mangaithan
pangka nai, pangkil vaiththukan thaanRRannaip
paanmai yaippani maamathi yamthavazh
mangku laic, cuTa raivaTa maamalai
ucchiyai, nacchi naamvaNangk appaTum
kangkulai, pakalaic chenRu naaTik
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNTukoN TEnE.
2.meaning: The Lord who shows grace to us; He who shares half His form with the Mountain Maiden of fragrant flowing tresses, and who rejoiced to keep her at His side; He who is the very nature of things, the cloud over which the cool great moon glides, the radiance, the summit of the great northern mountain. He is the night and the day that we long to worship -- Him I sought and beheld within Kannamangai.

3.translit:
peymu laiththalai nanjuNTa piLLaiyath
teLLi yaarvaNangk appaTun thEvanai,
maaya naimathiL koovali Taikazhi
mainda naiyanRi yandhaNar sindhaiyuL eesanai,
ilangk umcuTarc cOthiyai endhai
yaiyenak keyppinil vaippinai
kaasi naimaNi yaicchenRu naaTik
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNTukoN TEnE.
3.meaning: The child who drank the poison upon the breast of the demoness; the God whom the clear-minded worship; the wondrous one, the youth of walled Kovaludaiyakazhi; besides this, the Lord dwelling in the hearts of the brahmins; the shining radiant light, my Father, the treasure laid up for me in my weakness, my gold coin, my gem -- Him I sought and beheld within Kannamangai.

4.translit:
EtRRi naiyima yaththuLem meesanai
immai yaimaru maikku marundhinai,
aaTRRalai aNTath thappuRath thuyththiTum
aiya naikkaiyi laazhiyon REndhiya
kooTRRi nai, kuru maamaNik kunRinai
ninRa voorninRa niththilath thoththinai,
kaaTRRi naippuna laicchenRu naaTik
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNTukoN TEnE.
4.meaning: The bull, our Lord upon the Himalaya, the remedy for both this life and the next; He is power itself, the Father who bears beyond the bounds of the cosmos; the death-dealing one holding the discus in His hand; the radiant hill of great gems, the cluster of pearls who stands at Ninravur. He is the wind and the water -- Him I sought and beheld within Kannamangai.

5.translit:
tuppa naiththurang kampaTac cheeRiya
thOnRa laicchuTar vaankalan peythathOr
ceppi nai, thiru mangaima Naalanaith
theeva naiththika zhumpava Laththu oLi
oppinai, ula kEzhinai yoozhiyai
aazhi yEndhiya kaiyanai andhaNar
kaRpinai, kazhu neermala rumvayal
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNTukoN TEnE.
5.meaning: The Lord of strength who raged and slew the horse-demon; the manifest one; the casket filled with shining heavenly ornaments; the beloved Lord of Lakshmi; the God whose hue is like the radiance of glowing coral; the seven worlds and the aeon itself; He of the hand that holds the discus; the very learning of the brahmins -- Him I beheld within Kannamangai, whose fields blossom with red water-lilies.

6.translit:
thiruththa naiththisai naanmukan thandhaiyaith
theeva theevanai moovaril munniya
viruththanai, viLangk umcuTarc cOthiyai
viNNai maNNinaik kaNNuthal kooTiya
aruththa nai, ari yaippari keeRiya
appa nai appi laararazha laayninRa
karuththa nai, kaLi vaNTaRai yumpozhil
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNTukoN TEnE.
6.meaning: The flawless one, father of the four-faced Brahma of the directions; God of gods, the ancient one foremost among the three; the shining radiant light; the sky and the earth; the meaning that the forehead-eyed Shiva attained; Hari; the Father who tore apart the horse-demon; the will that stood as fire amid the waters -- Him I beheld within Kannamangai, in groves where joyous bees hum.

7.translit:
venji nakkaLiR Raiv iLangk aayvizhak
kanRu veesiya eesanai, peymakaL
tunja nanjucu vaiththuNTa thOnRalaith
thOnRal vaaLarak kankeTath thOnRiya
nanji nai, amu thaththinai naathanai
nacchu vaarucchi mElniRkum nambiyai,
kanja naiththunja vanjiththa vanjanaik
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNTukoN TEnE.
7.meaning: The Lord who hurled a calf to make wood-apples fall, who slew the fierce rut-maddened elephant; the manifest one who tasted and drank the poison so that the demoness perished; the poison that arose to destroy the brave sword-bearing demon, and the nectar; the Master; the perfect one who stands upon the heads of those who long for Him; the deceiver who deceived Kamsa to his death -- Him I beheld within Kannamangai.

8.translit:
paNNi naippaNNil ninRathOr paanmaiyaip
paaluL neyyinai maaluru vaayninRa
viNNi nai, viLangk umcuTarc cOthiyai
vELvi yaiviLak kinoLi thannai,
maNNi naimalai yaiyalai neerinai
maalai maamathi yaimaRai yOrthangkaL
kaNNi nai, kaNka LaaraLa vumninRu
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNTukoN TEnE.
8.meaning: He who is the melody and the very nature abiding within the melody; the ghee within the milk; the sky that stood forth as Tirumal's form; the shining radiant light; the sacrifice and the light of its lamp; the earth, the mountain, the surging waters; the dusk and the great moon; the eye of the Vedic seers -- Him I stood and beheld to my eyes' fullest content within Kannamangai.

9.translit:
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNTukoN TEnenRu
kaatha laalkali kanRiyu raiseytha,
vaNNa voNTami zhonpathO TonRivai
valla raayuraip paarmathi yamthavazh
viNNil viNNava raaymakizh veythuvar
meymmai chollilveN sangkamon REndhiya
kaNNa, ninRanak kumkuRip paakil
kaRka laamkavi yinporuL thaanE.
9.meaning: These nine-and-one beautiful, bright Tamil verses, which Kalikanri (Tirumangai Alwar) composed with love, saying 'I have beheld Him within Kannamangai' -- those able to recite them will become celestials in the moon-gliding heaven and attain joy. To speak truly, O Kanna who bears the white conch, even You, if it pleases You, may learn the meaning of these poems.

10.translit:
orunaR cuTRRam enakkuyir oNporuL
varunal tholkathi yaakiya maindhanai
nerunal kaNTathu neermalai yinRupOy
karunel cuzhkaNNa mangaiyuL kaaNTumE.
10.meaning: He who is my one good kinsman, my life, my radiant treasure, the youth who is the good ancient goal that comes -- yesterday I beheld Him at Tirunirmalai; today let us go and behold Him within Kannamangai, encircled by dark paddy fields.

11.translit:
maNNaaTum viNNaaTum vaanavarum thaanavarum maTRumellaam
uNNaatha peruveLLam uNNaamal thaanvizhungki yuyyakkoNTa,
kaNNaaLan kaNNamangai nakaraaLan kazhalsooTi, avanaiyuLLath
teNNaatha maaniTathai yeNNaatha pOthellaa miniyavaaRE.
11.meaning: The Lord who, when the great deluge that none could consume would have engulfed the world of earth, the world of heaven, the celestials, the demons and all else, Himself swallowed it and saved all; the gracious-eyed Lord, ruler of Kannamangai town. Adorning oneself with His feet, and not heeding the mortals who do not contemplate Him in their hearts -- every such moment is sweet.

12.translit:
aaraanum kaRpippaar naayakarE? naan avanaik
kaar aar thirumEni kaaNum aLavum pOy
seer aar thiruvEngkaTamE thirukkOva
loorE mathiL kacchi ooragamE pEragamE
pEraa maruthu iRuththaan veLLaRaiyE veqkaavE
pEr aali thaNkaal naRaiyoor thiruppuliyoor
aaraamam soozhndha arangkam kaNamangai
12.meaning: Will any who instruct me be my master? I -- until I go and behold Him of the cloud-dark sacred form -- shall journey: to glorious Tiruvengadam, to Tirukkovalur, to walled Kacchi (Kanchi) Uragam, to Peragam, to Vellarai where He who snapped the great marudu trees stands, to Vehka, to great Ali, to Thankal, to Naraiyur, to Tiruppuliyur, to Arangam (Srirangam) girt with gardens, to Kanamangai.

13.translit:
annam thuyilum aNi neer vayal aali
ennuTaiya in amuthai evvuL peru malaiyai
kanni mathiL soozh kaNamangaik kaRpakathai
minnai iru cuTarai veLLaRaiyuL kal aRaimEl
ponnai marakathathai puTkuzhi em pOr ERRai
mannum arangkaththu em maa maNiyai vallavaazhp
13.meaning: Him who is my sweet nectar in beautiful watery fields of Ali where swans sleep; the great hill at Evvul; the wish-granting tree (Karpaka) of Kanamangai girt by maiden-fresh walls; the lightning, the twin luminaries; the gold upon the rock-shrine within Vellarai; the emerald; my fighting bull at Putkuzhi; my great gem at abiding Arangam (Srirangam); at Vallavazh.

0.translit:
unnaiyum okkalaiyil koNDu tamizh maruvi
unnoDu tangaL karuttaayina seydhuvarum
kanniyarum magizhak kaNDavar kaN kuLirak
kaRRavar theRRivarap peRRa enakku aruLi
mannu kuRunkuDiyaay veLLaRaiyaay madhiL soozh
soolai malaikku arasee kaNNapurattu amudhee
en avalam kaLaivaay aaDuga sengeerai
eezhulagum uDaiyaay aaDuga aaDugavee
0.meaning: Carrying you upon their hips, the maidens spoke sweet Tamil and did whatever pleased your heart, and rejoiced; those who beheld you found their eyes refreshed; the learned ones came pressing close in delight. Grant your grace to me who attained you, O Lord who abides in Kurungudi and Tiruvellarai, O King of Solaimalai girt with ramparts, O nectar of Kannapuram! Dispel my sorrow; dance the sengeerai, O Lord of the seven worlds, dance, dance!

1.translit:
kaaTTil veengaDam kaNNapura nagar
vaaTTam inRi magizhndhuRai vaamanan
ooTTaraa vandhu en kaippaRRi thannoDum
kooTTumaagil nee kooDiDu kooDalee
1.meaning: In the forest of Venkatam and in the city of Kannapuram, Vamana abides joyfully without any decline. If he will come running, take my hand, and unite me with himself, then unite, O kuudal!

2.translit:
mannu pugazh kausalai than maNi vayiRu vaaytthavanee
then ilangai kOn muDigaL sindhuvitthaay sem pon sEr
kanni nan maa madhiL puDai soozh kaNapurattu en karumaNiyE
ennuDaiya in amudhE iraaghavanE thaalElO
2.meaning: O you who graced the jewel-like womb of Kausalya of enduring fame! You who shattered the crowns of the king of southern Lanka, O my dark gem of Kanapuram girt round with fine fresh ramparts set with ruddy gold! O my own sweet nectar, O Raghava, thaalelo!

3.translit:
puNDarika malar adhan mEl buvani ellaam paDaitthavanE
thiN thiRalaaL thaaDagai than uram uruvas silai vaLaitthaay
kaNDavar tham manam vazhangum kaNapurattu en karumaNiyE
eN thisaiyum aaL uDaiyaay iraaghavanE thaalElO
3.meaning: O you who created all the worlds upon the lotus flower! You who bent your bow so that it pierced through the chest of the mightily strong Thaadagai, O my dark gem of Kanapuram who captivates the heart of all who behold you! O Lord who rules the eight directions, O Raghava, thaalelo!

4.translit:
kongu mali karunkuzhalaaL kausalai than kula madhalaay
thangu perum pugazh sanagan thiru marugaa! thaasarathee
gangaiyilum theerttham mali kaNapurattu en karumaNiyE
engaL kulattu in amudhE iraaghavanE! thaalElO
4.meaning: O dear child of the line of Kausalya whose dark tresses are fragrant with pollen! O cherished son-in-law of Janaka of abiding great fame, O son of Dasaratha! O my dark gem of Kanapuram whose sacred waters surpass even the Ganges! O sweet nectar of our clan, O Raghava, thaalelo!

5.translit:
thaamarai mEl ayanavanaip paDaitthavanE dasarathan than
maa madhalaay maithili than maNavaaLaa! vaNDinangaL
kaamarangaL isai paaDum kaNapurattu en karumaNiyE
E maruvum silai valavaa! iraaghavanE! thaalElO
5.meaning: O you who created Brahma upon the lotus! O great son of Dasaratha, O bridegroom of Maithili (Sita)! O my dark gem of Kanapuram where swarms of bees sing the kaamaram melody! O skilled wielder of the bow joined with arrows, O Raghava, thaalelo!

6.translit:
paaraaLum paDar selvam paratha nambikkE aruLi
aaraa anbu iLaiyavanODu arunkaanam aDaindhavanE
seeraaLum varai maarbaa thiruk kaNNapurattu arasE
thaaraaLum neeN muDi en thaasarathee thaalElO
6.meaning: O you who graciously granted the vast wealth that rules the earth to noble Bharata, and went to the harsh forest with your younger brother of unceasing love! O you with mountain-like chest abounding in splendour, O King of sacred Kannapuram! O my son of Dasaratha with the long crown adorned with garlands, thaalelo!

7.translit:
suRRam ellaam pin thoDarath thol kaanam aDaindhavanE!
aRRavarkaTku aru marundhE! ayOtthi nagarkku adhipathiyE!
kaRRavargaL thaam vaazhum kaNapurattu en karumaNiyE!
siRRavai than sol koNDa seeraamaa! thaalElO!
7.meaning: O you who entered the ancient forest with all your kin following behind! O rare medicine for those bereft, O Lord of the city of Ayodhya! O my dark gem of Kanapuram where the learned ones dwell! O glorious Rama who heeded the word of your young step-mother (Kaikeyi), thaalelo!

8.translit:
aalin ilaip baalaganaay anRu ulagam uNDavanE
vaaliyaik konRu arasu iLaiya vaanaratthukku aLitthavanE
kaalin maNi karai alaikkum kaNapurattu en karumaNiyE
aali nagarkku adhipathiyE ayOtthi manE thaalElO
8.meaning: O you who, as an infant on the banyan leaf, swallowed the worlds of old! O you who slew Vali and gave the kingship to the younger monkey (Sugriva)! O my dark gem of Kanapuram where the breeze drives the waves of gems against the shore! O Lord of the city of Aali, O King of Ayodhya, thaalelo!

9.translit:
malai adhanaal aNai kaTTi madhiL ilangai azhitthavanE
alai kaDalaik kaDaindhu amararkku amudhu aruLis seydhavanE
kalai valavar thaam vaazhum kaNapurattu en karumaNiyE
silai valavaa sEvaganE seeraama thaalElO
9.meaning: O you who built a bridge with mountains and destroyed walled Lanka! O you who churned the surging ocean and graciously gave the gods their nectar! O my dark gem of Kanapuram where masters of the arts and sciences dwell! O wielder of the bow, O valiant warrior, O glorious Rama, thaalelo!

10.translit:
thaLai avizhum naRunkunjith thayarathan than kula madhalaay
vaLaiya oru silai adhanaal madhiL ilangai azhitthavanE
kaLai kazhuneer marungu alarum kaNapurattu en karumaNiyE
iLaiyavarkaTku aruL uDaiyaay iraaghavanE thaalElO
10.meaning: O dear child of the line of Dasaratha with fragrant curls whose bindings loosen! O you who destroyed walled Lanka by bending a single bow! O my dark gem of Kanapuram where the kaluneer (red lily) blooms all around amid the weeds! O you who hold grace for the young ones, O Raghava, thaalelo!

11.translit:
dEvaraiyum asuraraiyum thisaigaLaiyum paDaitthavanE
yaavarum vandhu aDi vaNanga aranga nagarth thuyinRavanE
kaaviri nal nadhi paayum kaNapurattu en karumaNiyE
E ari venj silai valavaa iraaghavanE thaalElO
11.meaning: O you who created the gods, the asuras, and the directions! O you who reclined in the city of Srirangam so that all may come and bow at your feet! O my dark gem of Kanapuram where the good river Kaveri flows! O wielder of the fierce bow with deadly arrows, O Raghava, thaalelo!

12.translit:
kanni nan maa madhiL puDai soozh kaNapurattu en kaakutthan
than aDi mEl thaalElO enRu uraittha thamizh maalai
kol navilum vEl valavan kuDaik kulasEkaran sonna
panniya nool patthum vallaar paangaaya pattargaLE
12.meaning: This Tamil garland uttered as "thaalelo" upon the feet of my Kakutstha (Rama) of Kanapuram girt round with fine fresh ramparts, composed by Kulasekara of the Kudai (umbrella) lineage who wields the killing spear — those who master these ten well-wrought verses become true devotees who are dear to the Lord.

13.translit:
silai ilangu pon aazhi thiN paDai thaNDu oN sangam enginRaaLaal
malai ilangu thOL naangE maRRavanukku eRRE kaaN enginRaaLaal
mulai ilangu poom payalai mun pODa anbODu irukkinRaaLaal
kalai ilangu mozhiyaaLar kaNNapurattu ammaanaik kaNDaaL kollO
13.meaning: She says he holds the shining bow, the golden discus, the stout club, and the lovely conch; she says, behold, that Lord has four mountain-like shining shoulders! As the flower-pale love-sickness spreads first upon her bosom, she sits there in love. Has this maiden of speech bright like the arts beheld the Lord of Kannapuram?

14.translit:
seru varai mun aasaRuttha silai anRO kaitthalattha thenginRaaLaal
poru varai mun pOr tholaittha pon aazhi maRRoru kai enginRaaLaal
oruvaraiyum nin oppaar oppu ilar en appaa enginRaaLaal
karu varai pOl ninRaanaik kaNNapurattu ammaanaik kaNDaaL kollO
14.meaning: She says, is it not the bow that long ago cut down the battling mountains that rests in his hand; she says the golden discus that of old ended the war against the warring mountains is in his other hand; she says, O my father, there is none, no one at all, equal to you. Has she beheld him who stands like a dark mountain, the Lord of Kannapuram?

15.translit:
thunnum maa maNi muDi mEl thuzhaay alangal thOnRumaal enginRaaLaal
minnum maa maNi magara kuNDalangaL vil veesum enginRaaLaal
ponnin maa maNi aaram aNi aagatthu ilangumaal enginRaaLaal
kanni maa madhiL puDai soozh kaNNapurattu ammaanaik kaNDaaL kollO
15.meaning: She says the tulasi garland appears upon his great close-set jewelled crown; she says the great gleaming jewelled makara earrings cast forth light like a bow (rainbow); she says the great golden jewel-necklace shines upon his beautiful chest. Has she beheld the Lord of Kannapuram girt round with great fresh ramparts?

16.translit:
thaaraaya thaN thuLaba vaNDu uzhutha varai maarban enginRaaLaal
pOr aanaik kombu osittha puTpaaganennammaanenginRaaLaal
aaraanum kaaNmingaL am pavaLam vaay avanukku enginRaaLaal
kaar vaanam ninRa thiruk kaNNapurattu ammaanaik kaNDaaL kollO
16.meaning: She says he has a mountain-like chest where bees burrow into the cool tulasi garland; she says my Lord who rides the bird (Garuda) broke the tusk of the battle-elephant; she says, all of you behold, that Lord has a mouth like beautiful coral! Has she beheld the Lord of sacred Kannapuram where the dark clouds stand?

17.translit:
aDitthalamum thaamaraiyE am kaiyum pangayamE enginRaaLaal
muDitthalamum poR pooNum en nenjatthuL agalaa enginRaaLaal
vaDi thaDam kaN malaravaLO varai aagatthuL iruppaaL enginRaaLaal
kaDik kamalam kaL ugukkum kaNNapurattu ammaanaik kaNDaaL kollO
17.meaning: She says the soles of his feet are lotuses and his fair hands too are lotuses; she says his crowned head and golden ornaments never leave my heart; she says the lotus-born lady (Lakshmi) of wide cool eyes dwells upon his mountain-like chest. Has she beheld the Lord of Kannapuram where the fragrant lotuses pour forth honey?

18.translit:
pEr aayiram uDaiya pEraaLan pEraaLan enginRaaLaal
Er aar kana magara kuNDalatthan eN thOLan enginRaaLaal
neer aar mazhai mugilE neeL varaiyE okkumaal enginRaaLaal
kaar aar vayal amarum kaNNapurattu ammaanaik kaNDaaL kollO
18.meaning: She says he is the mighty one, the mighty one bearing a thousand names; she says he wears beautiful heavy makara earrings and has eight shoulders; she says he resembles the water-laden rain cloud and the tall mountain. Has she beheld the Lord of Kannapuram who abides amid the cloud-covered fields?

19.translit:
sevvarattha uDai aaDai adhan mEl Or sivaligaik kachchu enginRaaLaal
avvarattha aDi iNaiyum angaigaLum pangayamE enginRaaLaal
mai vaLarkkum maNi uruvam maragadhamO mazhai mugilO enginRaaLaal
kai vaLarkkum mazhalaiyaaLar kaNNapurattu ammaanaik kaNDaaL kollO
19.meaning: She says he wears a bright red garment and over it a sivalikai waistband; she says his pair of feet of that hue and his fair hands too are lotuses; she says his dark-hued jewel-like form is like an emerald or a rain cloud. Has she, the maiden of tender lisping speech who grows up cherished in arms, beheld the Lord of Kannapuram?

100.translit:
katraar patraRukkum piRavip perungkaDalee
patraa vandaDiyeen piRandeen piRandapinnai
vatraa neervayalsoozh vayalaali yammaanaip
petreen petradum piRavaamai petreenee.
100.meaning: This is the great ocean of birth from which the learned cut away all attachment. Clinging to nothing else, I, your servant, came and was born; and after being born, I obtained the Lord of Vayalaali, surrounded by paddy fields fed by waters that never dry. I obtained Him, and in obtaining Him I obtained release from being born ever again.

101.translit:
kaNNaar kaNNapuram kaDigai kaDikamazhum
taNNaar taamaraisoozh talaichchanga meeltisaiyuL
viNNoor naaNmadiyai virikinRa venjuDarai
kaNNaarak kaNDukoNDu kaLikkinRading kenRukoloo.
101.meaning: In Kannapuram that fills the eyes, in Kadigai, and to the western side of Talaichchangam surrounded by cool fragrant lotuses, dwells He who is the bright daytime moon of the celestials and the fierce spreading radiance. When, O when, shall I behold Him to my eyes' full content and rejoice?

102.translit:
seruneera veelvalavan kalikanRi mangaiyarkoon
karuneer mugilvaNNan kaNNa purattaanai
irunee rindamizh innisai maalaigaL koNDutoNDeer,
varuneer vaiyamuyya ivaipaaDi yaaDuminee.
102.meaning: Kalikanri, lord of the Mangai land, skilled with the war-spear that thirsts in battle, sang this garland of sweet-musical Tamil rich as the deep waters upon the Lord of Kannapuram, who is dark like a rain-laden cloud. O devotees, that this water-girt world may be redeemed, sing and dance to these verses.

103.translit:
vaNDaarpoo maamalar mangai maNanookkam
uNDaanee unnai yugandugan dunRanakkee
toNDaaneeRku enseykin Raaysollu naalveedam
kaNDaanee kaNNa puRattuRai yammaanee.
103.meaning: O You who received the bridal glance of the Lady seated on the great flower swarming with bees, what will You do for me, who delight and delight again in You and am a servant to You alone? Tell me, O You who revealed the four Vedas, O Lord who dwells in Kannapuram.

104.translit:
peruneerum viNNum malaiyu mulageezhum
orutaaraa ninnu LoDukkiya ninnaiyallaal
varudeevar matruLaren Renmanat tiRaiyum
karudeennaan kaNNa purattuRai yammaanee.
104.meaning: O Lord who dwells in Kannapuram, who in a single instant drew the vast waters, the sky, the mountains and the seven worlds into Yourself, apart from You I will not, even for a moment in my mind, reckon that there are other gods who come forth to be reckoned.

105.translit:
matrumoor teyva muLaden Riruppaaroo
Dutrileen utradu munnaDi yaarkkaDimai
matrellam peesilum nintiru veTTezhuttum
katru naan kaNNa purattuRai yammaanee.
105.meaning: O Lord who dwells in Kannapuram, I have not joined with those who hold that there is some other deity. What I have attained is to be a servant to the servants who came before. Though all else may be spoken of, I have learned Your sacred eight syllables.

106.translit:
peNNaanaaL peeriLang kongaiyi naarazhalpool
uNNaananj suNDugan daayai yugandeennaan
maNNaaLaa vaaLneDung kaNNi madumalaraaL
kaNNaaLaa kaNNa purattuRai yammaanee.
106.meaning: You gladly drank, as if it were sustenance, the deadly poison from the great young breasts of her who took the form of a woman, blazing like fire; in You I have taken delight. O Ruler of the earth, O beloved of the long sword-bright-eyed Lady born on the honeyed lotus, O Lord who dwells in Kannapuram.

107.translit:
petraarum sutramu menRivai peeNeennaan
matraarum patrile naadalaal ninnaDaindeen
utraanen RuLLattu vaittaruL seykaNDaay
katraarchcheer kaNNa purattuRai yammaanee.
107.meaning: I do not cherish these things called parents and kinsmen, and since I have no hold upon anyone else, I have come to You. Hold me in Your heart as one who is Your own and grant me grace, behold! O Lord who dwells in Kannapuram, the resort of the learned.

108.translit:
eettiyun seevaDi yeNNi yiruppaarai,
paarttirun dangu namanRamar patraadu
soottamnaam anjudu menRu toDaamai nee
kaattipool kaNNa purattuRai yammaanee.
108.meaning: Those who praise You and remain meditating on Your red lotus-feet, the servants of Yama, watching them, will not lay hold of them, but saying "We salute you, we are afraid," they will not touch them. You guard such ones thus, O Lord who dwells in Kannapuram.

109.translit:
veLLaineer veLLat taNainda aravaNaimeel
tuLLuneer meLLat tuyinRa perumaanee
vaLLalee unRamark kenRum namanRamar
kaLLarpool kaNNa purattuRai yammaanee.
109.meaning: O great Lord who gently slumbers upon the serpent-couch set within the flood of clear white waters where the waves leap, O bounteous one! To Your devotees the servants of Yama are ever like thieves who slink away, O Lord who dwells in Kannapuram.

110.translit:
maaNaagi vaiya maLandaduvum vaaLavuNan
pooNaagam keeNDaduvum eeNDu ninaindirundeen
peeNaada valvinai yeeniDa rettanaiyum
kaaNeennaan kaNNa purattuRai yammaanee.
110.meaning: Becoming a dwarf and measuring the world, and tearing open the jewelled chest of the sword-bearing demon -- on these I have sat here meditating. Though I am one of cruel deeds and unloving, I see for myself not the slightest affliction, O Lord who dwells in Kannapuram.

111.translit:
naaTTinaa yennai yunakkumun toNDaaga
maaTTinee nattanaiyee koNDen valvinaiyai
paaTTinaa lunnaiyen nenjat tirundamai
kaaTTinaay kaNNa purattuRai yammaanee.
111.meaning: You established me long ago as a servant unto You; that was all I could do, and with that You took away my cruel deeds. Through song You revealed that You dwell within my heart, O Lord who dwells in Kannapuram.

112.translit:
kaNDaseerk kaNNa purattuRai yammaanai
koNDaseert toNDan kaliya nolimaalai
paNDamaayp paaDu maDiyavark kenjnaanRum
aNDampoo yaaTchi yavarkka taRindoomee.
112.meaning: This resounding garland by Kaliyan, the devotee of acclaimed glory, sung upon the Lord of manifest glory who dwells in Kannapuram -- for the devotees who sing it as their treasure, we know that ever the rule of the celestial realm beyond is theirs to attain.

113.translit:
kanRumeeyt tinituganda kaaLaay enRum,
 kaDipozhilsoozh kaNapuratten kaniyee enRum,
manRamarak koottaaDi magizhndaay enRum,
 vaDatiruveeng kaDammeeya maindaa enRum,
venRasurar kulangaLainda veendee enRum,
 viripozhilsoozh tirunaRaiyoor ninRaay enRum,
tunRukuzhal karuniRatten tuNaiyee enRum
 tuNaimulaimeel tuLisoorach soorkkin RaaLee.
113.meaning: "O youthful one who grazed the calves and rejoiced sweetly," she says; "O my sweet fruit of Kanapuram girt with fragrant groves," she says; "O You who danced in the public square and were glad," she says; "O youth who dwells upon northern Tiruvenkatam," she says; "O King who destroyed the clans of the conquering demons," she says; "O You who stand at Tirunaraiyur ringed by spreading groves," she says; "O my dark-hued companion with thick clustering locks," she says -- and she swoons, while drops trickle down upon her paired breasts.

114.translit:
sengaala maDanaaraa yinRe senRu 
 tirukkaNNapuram pukken sengaNmaalukku 
enkaadalen tuNaivarkkuraitti yaagil 
 ituvoppa demakkinba millai naaLum
paingaana meedellaa munadeeyaagap 
 pazhanameen kavarnduNNat taruvan tandaal
ingee vandini dirundun peDaiyum neeyum 
 iruniladilinitenbameydalaamee.
114.meaning: O red-legged tender stork, if you go this very day to Tirukkannapuram and tell my red-eyed Tirumal, my beloved, my mate, of my love -- there is no joy for us to equal that. If you do, I will give over to you all these wide green fields to seize and feed on the fish of the meadows; and if I give them, then come here, and you and your mate together may dwell sweetly and attain delight upon this broad earth.

115.translit:
kaar aar maNi niRak kaNNanoor viNNagaram 
seer aar kaNapuram seeRai tiruvazhundoor
kaar aar kuDandai kaDigai kaDalmallai
eer aar pozhil soozh iDavendai neermalai
seer aarum maaliirunjoolai tirumoogoor (37)
115.meaning: Vinnagaram, the town of Kannan whose hue is dark as a rain-cloud and gem; glory-filled Kanapuram, Sherai, Tiruvazhundur; cloud-laden Kudandai, Kadigai, Kadalmallai; Idavendai ringed by lovely groves, Neermalai; glorious Malirunjolai, and Tirumogur.

116.translit:
kal navilum kaaTTagattu oor vallik kaDi malarin 
nal naRu vaasam matru aaraanum eydaamee 
mannum vaRu nilattu vaaLaangu uguttadu pool 
ennuDaiya peNmaiyum en nalanum en mulaiyum 
mannum malar mangai maindan kaNapurattu (48)
116.meaning: Like the good fragrant scent of a creeper's fresh blossom in a stony wilderness, spilled in vain upon barren ground where no one else can reach it -- so too my womanhood, my goodness, and my breasts are spent in vain; (for the sake of) the youth of the Lady seated on the lasting flower, who is in Kanapuram.

117.translit:
kaNNanai kaNNapurattaanai ten naRaiyoor 
mannum maNimaaDakkoyil maNaaLanai 
kal navil tooL kaaLaiyaik kaNDu aanguk kaitozhudu 
en nilaimai ellaam aRivittaal em perumaan 
tan aruLum aagamum taaraaneel tannai naan (72)
117.meaning: If, having seen Kannan, the Lord of Kannapuram, the Bridegroom of the abiding gem-towered temple of southern Naraiyur, the youth whose shoulders are firm as stone, and folding my hands there in worship, I make known all my plight -- should my Lord then not grant me His grace and His embrace, then I myself... (the verse continues).

118.translit:
maalainaNNit tozhudezhuminoovinaikeDa
kaalaimalai kamalamalariTTuneer
veelaimoodummadiLsoozh tirukkaNNapurattu
aalinmeelaalamarndaan aDiyiNaigaLee.
118.meaning: Drawing near at evening, rise up and worship, that your deeds may be destroyed; at morning, O you, offer fresh lotus blossoms at the twin feet of Him who rested upon the banyan leaf, in Tirukkannapuram girt with walls that the sea beats against.

119.translit:
kaLLavizhummalariTTu neeriRainjumin
naLLiseerumvayalasoozh kiDanginpuDai
veLLiyeeyndamadiLsoozh tirukkaNNapura
muLLi naaLundozhudeezhuminootoNDaree.
119.meaning: Offer honey-dripping blossoms and worship; on the side of the moat girt with paddy-fields where conch shells gather, lies Tirukkannapuram ringed by walls bright as silver. O devotees, holding it within you, worship it day after day and rise up.

120.translit:
toNDar nun'tam tuyar pooka neer eekamaay
viNDu vaaDaa malar iTTu neer iRainjumin
vaNDu paaDum pozhil soozh tirukkaNNapurat
taNDavaaNan amarar perumaanaiyee.
120.meaning: O devotees, that your sorrows may depart, become one in heart and worship Him, casting fresh unfading flowers upon Him. Worship that Lord who dwells in cool Tirukkannapuram, girt with groves where bees sing, the Lord who is master of the celestials.

121.translit:
maanai nookki maDappinnai tan keeLvanai
teenai vaaDaa malar iTTu neer iRainjumin
vaanai un'tu matiL soozh tirukkaNNapuram
taan ayan'ta perumaan saraN aakumee.
121.meaning: Worship the beloved consort of gentle Nappinnai, whose eyes are like a doe's, casting fresh unfading flowers upon Him who is sweet as honey. The Lord who loved and made His abode in Tirukkannapuram, encircled by walls that thrust up to the sky, shall be your refuge.

122.translit:
saaN aam aakum tana taaL aDain'taark ku ellaam
maraNam aanaal vaikun'tam koDukkum piraan
araN amain'ta matiL soozh tirukkaNNapurat
taraNi aaLan tana toNDarkku anpu aakumee.
122.meaning: To all who have reached His feet He becomes near at hand; the Lord who, when death comes, bestows the realm of Vaikunta. He who rules the earth and dwells in Tirukkannapuram, girt with strong fortress walls, becomes love itself to His devotees.

123.translit:
anpan aakum tana taaL aDain'taarkku ellaam
sem pon aakattu avaNan uDal keeNDavan
nan pon eey'n'ta matiL soozh tirukkaNNapurat
tu anpan naaLum tana meyyarkku meyyanee.
123.meaning: He becomes a loving one to all who have reached His feet; He who tore open the body of the demon Hiranya of reddish-golden frame. The loving Lord of Tirukkannapuram, girt with walls adorned with fine gold, is ever truth itself to those who are true to Him.

124.translit:
meyyan aakum virumpit tozhuvaarkku ellaam
poyyan aakum puRamee tozhuvaarkku ellaam
seyyil vaaLai ukaLum tirukkaNNapurat
taiyan aakattu aNaippaarkaTku aNiyanee.
124.meaning: He becomes truth to all who worship Him with longing love; He becomes false to all who worship Him only outwardly. The Lord of Tirukkannapuram, where scabbard-fish leap in the paddy fields, is near at hand to those who embrace Him within their hearts.

125.translit:
aNiyan aakum tana taaL aDain'taarkku ellaam
piNiyum saaraa piRavi keDum naaLum
maNi pon eey'n'ta matiL soozh tirukkaNNapuram
paNimin naaLum parameeTTi tan paatamee.
125.meaning: He becomes near at hand to all who have reached His feet; sickness shall not approach them, and their births shall come to an end. Worship daily the feet of the Supreme One who dwells in Tirukkannapuram, girt with walls inlaid with gems and gold.

126.translit:
paatam naaLum paNiyat taNiyum piNi
eetaam enakkeel ini en kuRai
veeta naavar virumpum tirukkaNNapurat
aatiyaanai aDain'taarkku allal illaiyee.
126.meaning: As one worships His feet day by day, affliction is stilled; what then can ail me, what want is left to me? For those who have reached the Primordial One of Tirukkannapuram, whom those with the Vedas upon their tongues hold dear, there is no sorrow at all.

127.translit:
illai allal enakkeel ini en kuRai
alli maatar amarum tiru maarpinan
kallil eey'n'ta matiL soozh tirukkaNNapuram
solla naaLum tuyar paaDu saaraavee.
127.meaning: There is no affliction for me; what want is left to me now? He on whose sacred breast the lotus-born Lakshmi abides dwells there. If one but speaks of Tirukkannapuram, girt with walls wrought of stone, the burden of sorrow shall never draw near, day after day.

128.translit:
paaDu saaraa vinai paRRu aRa veeNDuveer
maaDa neeDu kurukoorc caTakoopan sol
paaDal aana tamizh aayirattuL ip pattum
paaDi aaDi paNimin avan taaLkaLee.
128.meaning: You who wish that the bondage of binding deeds be utterly cut away: of the thousand sweet Tamil verses sung by Satakopan of Kurugur with its towering mansions, sing and dance to these ten, and worship His holy feet.

20.translit:
koRRap puL onRu eeRi vaan oodee varuginRaan enginRaaLaal
veRRip poor indiraRkum indiranee okkumaal enginRaaLaal
peRRakkaal avan aagam peN piRandhoom uyyoomoo enginRaaLaal
kaRRa nool maRaiyaaLar kaNNapuratt ammaanaik kaNdaaL kollo.
20.meaning: She keeps saying that He comes riding through the sky upon His one victorious bird (Garuda). She keeps saying that even to victorious Indra He himself is like another Indra. She keeps saying: if only we could embrace His form! Having been born women, shall we ever be saved? Has she perhaps beheld the Lord of Kannapuram, served by the learned brahmins of the Vedas?

21.translit:
vaNdu amarum vanamaalai maNi mudimeel maNam naaRum enginRaaLaal
uNdu ivar paal anbu enakku enRu oru kaalum pirigilEn enginRaaLaal
paNdu ivaraik kaNdaRivadhu evvooril yaam enRE payilginRaaLaal
kaNdavar tham manam vazhangum kaNNapuratt ammaanaik kaNdaaL kollo.
21.meaning: She keeps saying that upon His jeweled crown the forest-garland where bees swarm spreads its fragrance. She keeps saying: I have love toward Him, and I shall never at any time part from Him. She keeps repeating: in what town was it that I knew Him before? Has she perhaps beheld the Lord of Kannapuram, who steals away the minds of all who behold Him?

22.translit:
maa vaLarum mel nokki maadharaaL maayavanaik kaNdaaL enRu
kaa vaLarum kadi pozhil soozh kaNNapuratt ammaanaik kaliyan sonna
paa vaLarum tamizh maalai panniya nool ivai aindhum aindhum vallaar
poo vaLarum kaRpagam seer pon ulagil mannavaraay pugazh takkOrE.
22.meaning: Saying that this maiden of soft, deer-like glance and luxuriant beauty has beheld the wondrous Lord, Kaliyan composed this garland of Tamil verses, rich in song, upon the Lord of Kannapuram girt by fragrant groves where gardens flourish. Those who master these ten verses of the well-wrought work shall become kings worthy of fame in the golden world where the flowering Karpaka tree thrives.

23.translit:
teLLiyeer, deevarkkum deevar tirut takkeer
veLLiyeer veyya vizhu nidhi vaNNar, O
tuLLu neerk kaNNa puram tozhudhaaL ivaL kaLviyO,
kai vaLai koLvadhu takkadhE?
23.meaning: O lucid One, O God even of the gods, You who are worthy of Lakshmi, O One fair as silver, O One of the hue of precious treasure! This girl worshipped Kannapuram of leaping waters - is she a thief? Is it fitting that You should seize away the bangles from her wrists?

24.translit:
neeL nilaa muRRattu ninRu ivaL nokkinaaL,
kaaNumO kaNNa puram enRu kaattinaaL,
paaNanaar tiNNam irukka ini ivaL
naaNumO, nanRu nanRu naRaiyoorarkkE.
24.meaning: Standing in the courtyard bathed in broad moonlight she gazed away. She pointed and asked, 'Can Kannapuram be seen?' With the bard surely present here, will this girl now feel any shame any longer? Fine, fine indeed - this is the doing of the Lord of Naraiyur!

25.translit:
aruvi soor veengadam neer malai enRu vaay
veruvinaaL meyyam vinavi irukkinRaaL,
perugu seerk kaNNapuram enRu pEsinaaL
uruginaaL, uL melindhaaL idhu en kolO.
25.meaning: With her mouth she babbled in agitation, 'Venkatam where streams pour down, Tirunirmalai!' She sits questioning about Tirumeyyam. She spoke the name 'Kannapuram of ever-growing glory,' and at that she melted and grew faint within. What can this be?

26.translit:
uNNum naaL illai uRakkamum taan illai,
peNmaiyum saala niRaindhilaL pEdhai taan,
kaNNan oor kaNNa puram tozhum kaark kadal
vaNNarmeel, eNNam ivatku idhu en kolO.
26.meaning: There is no day she eats, and sleep too is gone from her; this innocent girl is not fully filled with womanly modesty either. Her thought is fixed upon the One dark as the rain-cloud ocean, whose town is Kannan's Kannapuram that she worships. What is this longing of hers?

27.translit:
kaNNan oor kaNNa puram tozhum kaarigai,
peNmaiyum tannudai uNmai uraikkinRaaL,
veNNey uNdu aappuNda vaNNam viLambinaaL,
vaNNamum pon niRamaavadhu ozhiyumE.
27.meaning: This lovely girl who worships Kannan's town, Kannapuram, lays bare both her womanly modesty and her own true state. She told of the manner in which He ate the butter and was bound for it. And her complexion - it is turning to a golden pallor and wasting away.

28.translit:
vada varai ninRum vandhu inRu kaNapuram,
idavagai koLvadhu yaam enRu pEsinaaL,
madavaral maadhar en pEdhai ivarkku ivaL
kadavadhu en, kaN tuyil inRu ivar koLLavE.
28.meaning: She spoke saying, 'Having come this day from the northern mountain (Venkatam), we shall make Kanapuram our dwelling-place.' My innocent girl is a gentle, tender maiden - what makes her so bound to Him, so that He has now taken away her very sleep?

29.translit:
taranga neer pEsinum taN madhi kaayinum,
irangumO ettanai naaL irundhu eLginaaL
turangam vaay keeNdu ugandhaanadhu tonmai oor
arangamE enbadhu ivaL tanakku aasaiyE.
29.meaning: Whether the wave-tossed waters speak to her or the cool moon scorches her, will she find relief? For how many days she has sat pining and wasting away! The ancient town of Him who joyfully split open the horse-demon's jaws - that very Srirangam - is her sole desire.

30.translit:
toNdu ellaam nin adiyE tozhudhu uyyumaa
kaNdu, taan kaNapuram kai tozhap pOyinaaL
vaNdu ulaam koodhai en pEdhai maNi niRam
koNdu taan, kOyinmai seyvadhu takkadhE.
30.meaning: Seeing how all devotees are saved by worshipping Your feet alone, she herself set out to worship Kanapuram with folded hands. My innocent girl whose tresses bees roam about - is it fitting that You should commit this wrong of seizing away her jewel-like radiance?

31.translit:
muL eyiRu eeyndhila, koozhai mudi kodaa,
teLLiyaL enbadhu Or tEsu ilaL en seygEn,
kaLLavizh sOlaik kaNapuram kai tozhum
piLLaiyai, piLLai enRu eNNap peRuvarE.
31.meaning: Her small teeth have not yet worn smooth, her hair has not yet grown into a knot to be tied up, she has not even the radiance that marks a discerning girl - what shall I do? This mere child who worships with folded hands Kanapuram of groves where honey drips - can anyone still regard her as just a child?

32.translit:
kaar mali kaNNa purattu em adigaLai,
paar mali mangaiyar kOn paragaalan sol,
seer mali paadal ivai pattum vallavar,
neer mali vaiyattu needu niRpaarkalE.
32.meaning: Upon our Lord of Kannapuram thick with rain-clouds, these are the words of Parakalan, king of Mangai renowned over the earth. Those who master these ten songs rich in excellence shall endure long upon this water-girt world.

33.translit:
karai eduttha suri sangum kana pavalattu ezhu kodiyum,
tirai eduttu varu punal soozh tirukkaNNa purattu uRaiyum,
