virai eduttha tuzhaay alangal viRal varait tOL pudai peyara
varai eduttha perumaanukku izhandhEn en vari vaLaiyE.
33.meaning: He dwells in Tirukkannapuram, encircled by waters that surge up in waves, casting onto the banks spiralled conches and rising branches of rich coral. To the Lord who lifted up the mountain (Govardhana), whose mighty mountain-like shoulders sway adorned with fragrant tulasi garlands - to Him I have lost my striped bangles.

34.translit:
ari viravu mugil kaNattaan agil pugaiyaal varaiyOdum
teri ariya maNi maadat tirukkaNNa purattu uRaiyum,
vari aravin aNait tuyinRu mazhai madatta siRu taRukaN,
kari veruva maruppu osittaaRku izhandhEn en kana vaLaiyE.
34.meaning: He dwells in Tirukkannapuram of jeweled mansions, hardly to be told apart from the mountains amid the masses of dark cloud and the smoke of burning agil-incense. To Him who rests upon the coiled serpent's couch, and who - while the fierce small-eyed rut-flowing elephant (Kuvalayapida) trembled - snapped off its tusk, to Him I have lost my heavy bangles.

35.translit:
tunga maa maNi maada nedu mugattin soolikai pOm
tingaL maa mugil tuNikkum tirukkaNNa purattu uRaiyum
paing kaN maal vidai adarttu pani madhi kOL viduttu ugandha
seng kaN maal ammaanukku izhandhEn en seRi vaLaiyE.
35.meaning: He dwells in Tirukkannapuram, where the finials atop the lofty crests of tall jeweled mansions cleave the moon and the great clouds passing by. To the red-eyed exalted Lord who subdued the green-eyed great bulls and joyfully released the cool moon from its affliction (the eclipse), to Him I have lost my close-set bangles.

36.translit:
gaNa maruvu mayil agavu kadi pozhil soozh nedu maRugil,
tiNam maruvu kana madhiL soozh tirukkaNNa purattu uRaiyum,
maNam maruvu tOL aaycchi aarkkap pOy uralOdum
puNar marudham iRa nadandhaaRku izhandhEn en pon vaLaiyE.
36.meaning: He dwells in Tirukkannapuram, girt by strong massive ramparts, with long streets ringed by fragrant groves where flocks of peacocks dance and cry. To Him who - while the fragrant-shouldered cowherd-mother (Yashoda) cried out - crawled along tied to the mortar and walked between the twin marudham trees so that they snapped and fell, to Him I have lost my golden bangles.

37.translit:
vaay eduttha mandhirattaal andhaNar tham sey tozhilgaL
tee eduttu maRai vaLarkkum tirukkaNNa purattu uRaiyum
taay eduttha siRu kOlukku uLaindhu Odit tayir uNda,
vaay tudaittha maindhanukku izhandhEn en vari vaLaiyE.
37.meaning: He dwells in Tirukkannapuram, where the brahmins, with mantras raised in their mouths, kindle the fire and nurture the Vedas through their appointed rites. To that young Lord who, frightened at the little stick His mother raised, ran off after eating the curds and wiped His mouth, to Him I have lost my striped bangles.

38.translit:
madal eduttha nedun taazhai marungu ellaam vaLar pavaLam,
tidal eduttuc cudar imaikkum tirukkaNNa purattu uRaiyum,
adal adarttu anRu iraNiyanai muraN azhiya aNi ugiraal,
udal eduttha perumaanukku izhandhEn en oLi vaLaiyE.
38.meaning: He dwells in Tirukkannapuram, where all around the tall fragrant screwpine spreads its blossoms, and the coral grows in mounds that gleam and sparkle. To the Lord who, in battle long ago, with His beautiful claws tore open the body of Hiranya so that his might was destroyed, to Him I have lost my shining bangles.

39.translit:
vaNdu amarum malar punnai vari neezhal aNi muttam,
deN tiraigaL varat tirattum tirukkaNNa purattu uRaiyum,
eN disaiyum ezhu sudarum iru nilanum peru visumbum,
uNdu umizhndha perumaanukku izhandhEn en oLi vaLaiyE.
39.meaning: He dwells in Tirukkannapuram, where the clear waves come and heap up lovely pearls in the dappled shade of the bee-haunted punnai blossoms. To the Lord who swallowed and then brought forth again the eight directions, the seven luminaries, the broad earth and the vast sky, to Him I have lost my shining bangles.

40.translit:
kongumali karunguvaLai kaNNaaga teNkayangaL
sengamala mugamalarttum tirukkaNNa purattuRaiyum,
vangamali tadangadaluL variyaravi naNaittuyinRaa,
sengamala naabanuk kizhandheenen seRivaLaiyee.
40.meaning: The clear ponds, with dark kuvalai flowers laden with fragrance for their eyes, make their red lotus faces blossom in the Lord's abode of Tirukkannapuram. To Him who reclines upon the striped serpent in the ship-filled vast ocean, the lotus-naveled Lord, I have lost my close-fitting bangles.

41.translit:
vaaraaLu miLangongai nedumpaNaittooL madappaavai,
seeraaLum varaimaarvan tirukkaNNa purattuRaiyum,
peeraaLa naayirambee raayiravaa yaravaNaimeel
peeraaLar perumaanuk kizhandheenen peyvaLaiyee.
41.meaning: He who bears upon His mountain-like chest the gentle maiden of bodiced youthful breasts and long bamboo-like shoulders, dwelling in glory, abides in Tirukkannapuram. To the great one of a thousand names, the Lord of the great ones who reclines upon the thousand-mouthed serpent-bed, I have lost the bangles I wear.

42.translit:
teemaruvu pozhilpudaisoozh tirukkaNNa purattuRaiyum
vaamananai, maRikadalsoozh vayalaali vaLanaadan,
kaamaruseerk kalikanRi kaNduraitta tamizhmaalai,
naamaruvi yivaipaada vinaiyaaya naNNaavee.
42.meaning: Of the Vamana who dwells in Tirukkannapuram girdled by groves filled with honey, Kalikanri (Tirumangai Alwar), lord of the fertile land of Vayalali surrounded by the surging sea, of beloved excellence, beheld and uttered this Tamil garland. If one sings these with the tongue dwelling on them, accumulated karmic deeds will not come near.

43.translit:
viNNavar tangaL perumaan tirumaarvan,
maNNava rellaam vaNangum malipugazhseer,
kaNNa puratttem perumaan kadirmudimeel,
vaNNa naRunduzhaay vandhoodaay kooltumbee.
43.meaning: He is the Lord of the celestials, who bears Lakshmi on His chest, whom all the people of the earth worship, abounding in fame and glory. O graceful bee, come and hum upon the radiant crown of our Lord of Kannapuram, upon His beautiful fragrant tulasi.

44.translit:
veeda mudalvan viLangu purinoolan,
paadham paravip palarum paNindheetti,
kaadhanmai seyyum kaNNaburat temperumaan,
taadhu naRunduzhaay taazhndhoodaay kooltumbee.
44.meaning: He is the first cause of the Vedas, wearing the shining sacred thread, whose feet many praise, bow before, extol, and serve with love -- our Lord of Kannapuram. O graceful bee, bend low and hum upon His pollen-laden fragrant tulasi.

45.translit:
viNdamala rellaa moodhinee yenpeRudi,
aNda mudalva namararga lellaarum,
kaNdu vaNangum kaNNaburat temperumaan
vaNdu naRunduzhaay vandhoodaay kooltumbee.
45.meaning: Even if you hum upon all the opened flowers, what would you gain? He is the first cause of the cosmos, whom all the immortals behold and worship -- our Lord of Kannapuram. O graceful bee, come and hum upon His bee-swarmed fragrant tulasi.

46.translit:
neermali kinRadhoor meenaayoo raamaiyumaay,
seermali kinRadhoor singa vuruvaagi,
kaarmali vaNNan kaNNaburat temperumaan,
taarmali taNduzhaay taazhndhoodaay kooltumbee.
46.meaning: He became a fish abiding in the abundant waters, and a tortoise too, and took the form of an excellence-laden lion (Narasimha) -- He of the dark cloud's hue, our Lord of Kannapuram. O graceful bee, bend low and hum upon His garland-laden cool tulasi.

47.translit:
eeraar malarellaa moodhinee yenpeRudi,
paaraa rulagam paravap perungadaluL,
kaaraamai yaana kaNNaburat temperumaan,
taaraar naRunduzhaay taazhndhoodaay kooltumbee.
47.meaning: Even if you hum upon all the lovely flowers, what would you gain? While all the wide world praised Him, He became the great tortoise in the vast ocean -- our Lord of Kannapuram. O graceful bee, bend low and hum upon His garland-rich fragrant tulasi.

48.translit:
maarvil tiruvan valaneendhu sakkarattan,
paaraip piLandha paraman paranjoothi,
kaaril tigazhkaayaa vaNNan kadirmudimeel,
taaril naRunduzhaay taazhndhoodaay kooltumbee.
48.meaning: He bears Lakshmi on His chest and holds the discus in His right hand, the Supreme One who clove the earth (as Varaha), the supreme effulgence, of the hue of the kaaya flower glowing like a rain-cloud. O graceful bee, bend low and hum upon the fragrant tulasi in the garland on His radiant crown.

49.translit:
vaamanan kaRki madhusoodhan maadhavan
taarmannu daasa radhiyaaya tadamaarvan,
kaamanRan taadhai kaNNaburat temperumaan,
taama naRunduzhaay taazhndhoodaay kooltumbee.
49.meaning: He is Vamana, Kalki, Madhusudana, Madhava, and the broad-chested one who became Dasaratha's son (Rama) adorned with an enduring garland, the father of Manmatha -- our Lord of Kannapuram. O graceful bee, bend low and hum upon His fragrant tulasi garland.

50.translit:
neela malargaL neduneer vayalmarungil,
saala malarellaa moodhaadhee, vaaLarakkar
kaalan kaNNaburat temperumaan kadirmudimeel,
koola naRunduzhaay koNdoodaay kooltumbee.
50.meaning: Do not hum upon all the many flowers, the blue blossoms beside the deep-watered fields. He is the death of the sword-wielding demons -- our Lord of Kannapuram. O graceful bee, take up and hum upon the beautiful fragrant tulasi on His radiant crown.

51.translit:
nandhan madhalai nilamangai nalthuNaivan,
andha mudalvan amararga LtamperumaaN,
kandham kamazhkaayaa vaNNan kadirmudimeel,
kondhu naRunduzhaay koNdoodaay kooltumbee.
51.meaning: He is Nanda's child (Krishna), the good consort of the Earth-maiden, the first cause and the end, the Lord of the immortals, of the hue of the fragrance-spreading kaaya flower. O graceful bee, take up and hum upon the cluster of fragrant tulasi on His radiant crown.

52.translit:
vaNdamarun soolai vayalaali nannaadan,
kaNdaseer venRik kaliya nolimaalai,
koNdal niRavaNNan kaNNa purattaanai,
toNdaroom paada ninaindhoodaay kooltumbee.
52.meaning: Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar), lord of the good land of Vayalali with its bee-thronged groves and fields, of beheld excellence and victory, composed this resonant garland. So that we devotees may sing of the Lord of Kannapuram, dark-cloud-hued, O graceful bee, hum with that in mind.

53.translit:
tandhai kaalil vilangaRavandhu toonRiya toonRalpin, tamiyeenRan
sindhai pooyiRRut tiruvaruL avanidaip peRumaLa virundheenai
andhi kaavalanamudhuRu pasungadhi ravaisuda adhanoodum
mandha maarudham vanamulai tadavandhu valiseyva dozhiyaadhee.
53.meaning: My mind has gone after that supreme one who appeared to free His father's feet from fetters (Krishna born to release Vasudeva). As I waited, lonely, to receive His divine grace, the evening guardian's nectar-bearing cool moonbeams scorch me, and along with them the gentle breeze comes caressing my fair breasts and torments me without ceasing.

54.translit:
maari maakkadal vaLaivaNaR kiLaiyavan varaipurai tirumaarbil
taari naasaiyil pooyina nenjamum taazhndhadhoor tuNaikaaNeen
ooorum tunjiRRulagamum tuyinRadhu oLiyavan visumbiyangum
teerum pooyiRRut tisaigaLum maRaindhana seyvadhon RaRiyeenee.
54.meaning: My heart has gone, longing for the garland on the mountain-like sacred chest of the younger brother of the conch-bearer of the dark rain-filled sea (Krishna, brother of Balarama), and I find no companion in my lowliness. The town has fallen asleep, the world slumbers, the sun's chariot that moved through the bright sky has departed, and the directions have vanished -- I know not what to do.

55.translit:
aayan maayamee yanRimaR Renkaiyil vaLaigaLum iRainillaa
peeyin aaruyi ruNdidum piLLainam peNNuyirk kirangumoo
tooya maamadhik kadhirssudat tuNaiyillai iNaimulai veeginRadhaal
aayan veeyinuk kazhiginRa duLLamum anjeelen paarilaiyee.
55.meaning: It is nothing but the cowherd's (Krishna's) magic -- even the bangles on my hands will not stay a moment. Will the child who drank the very life of the demoness (Putana) take pity on our woman's life? As the pure great moon's rays scorch me, I have no companion, and my two breasts are burning. My heart melts away at the cowherd's flute, yet there is none to say 'do not fear.'

56.translit:
kayangoL puNtalaik kaLiRundhu vendhiRal kazhalmannar perumboril
mayangaveNsangam vaayvaitta maindhanum vandhilan, maRikadalneer
tayangu veNtiraitt tivalainuN paniyennum tazhalmugan diLamulaimeel
iyangu maarudham vilangilen aaviyai enakkenap peRalaamee.
56.meaning: The mighty one who set the white conch to His mouth in the great battle of the anklet-wearing kings, driving forth the wounded-headed elephants -- He has not come. The breeze that moves over my young breasts, bearing what they call fine mist from the spray of the white waves of the surging sea, scoops up fire; if only it would turn aside, could I keep my own life for myself?

57.translit:
eezhu maamaram tuLaipadas silaivaLaitt tilangaiyai malanguvitta
aazhi yaan,namak karuLiya aruLodum pagalellai kazhiginRadhaal
toozhi naamidhaR kenseydhum tuNaiyillai sudarpadu mudhuneeril
aazha aazhginRa aaviyai aduvadhoor andhivan dadaiginRadhee.
57.meaning: He of the discus, who bent His bow to pierce the seven great trees and threw Lanka into confusion -- with the grace He showed us, the span of day is passing away. O friend, what shall we do for this? There is no companion. And the evening comes to slay my life, which is sinking deep into the ancient waters where the sun sets.

58.translit:
muriyum veNtirai mudhukayam teeppada muzhangazha leriyambin
varigoL venjilai vaLaivitta maindhanum vandhilan enseygeen
eriyum vengadhir tuyinRadhu paaviyeen iNainedung kaNtuyilaa
kariya naazhigai oozhiyil periyana kazhiyumaa RaRiyeenee.
58.meaning: The mighty one who bent His fierce striped bow with fire-roaring arrows so that the ancient waters of the breaking white waves caught fire (Rama at the ocean) -- He has not come. What shall I do? The blazing hot sun has gone to sleep, but my two long eyes find no sleep, sinner that I am, and I know not how to pass these dark hours that loom larger than aeons.

59.translit:
kalanga maakkadal kadaindhadait tilangaiyar koonadhu varaiyaagam
malanga venjamatt tadusaram turandhavem madigaLum vaaraanaal
ilangu vengadhi riLamadhi yadhanodum vidaimaNi yadum,aayan
vilangal veeyina toosaiyu maayini viLaivadhon RaRiyeenee.
59.meaning: My Lord who churned the great ocean to confusion and dammed it (for Lanka), who loosed cruel arrows in fierce battle to shatter the mountain-like body of the king of the Lankans (Ravana) -- He does not come. The bright burning sun, together with the young moon, and the bells of the bullocks, and the cowherd's flute that sounds like a mountain -- all torment me now, and I know not what will come of this.

60.translit:
muzhuthiv vaiyagam muRaikeDa maRaithalum munivanum munivu eythi
mazhuvinaal mannar aaruyir vavviya maindhanum vaaraanaal
ozhugu nuNpanik koDungiya pEDaiyai aDanga vanj chiRaikOli
thazhuvu naLLiruL thanimaiyil kaDiyathOr koDuvinai aRiyEnE.
60.meaning: When this whole earth sank into ruin with all order lost, the sage (Parasurama) grew wrathful—the mighty one who with his axe seized the precious lives of the kings—yet he has not come to me. Like a female bird shrunken with the flowing fine dew, drawing in its trembling wings, I am wrapped in the lonely deep darkness of night. I know no cruel deed more bitter than this.

61.translit:
kananjchey maamadhiL kaNapurath thavanoDum kanavinil avan thandha
mananjchey inbam vandhu uLpuga veLgi en vaLai neka irundhEnai
sinanjchey maal viDaich chiRumaNi Osai en sindhaiyaich chindhuvikkum
ananthal anRilin arikural paaviyEn aaviyai aDuginRathE.
61.meaning: Together with the Lord of Kanapuram girt by great solid ramparts, in a dream the joy of mind he granted entered into me, and I sat ashamed as my bangles loosened. The sound of the little bell of the angry great bull scatters my thoughts; and the harsh cry of the sleepless anril bird slays the very life of me, a sinner.

62.translit:
vaarkoL menmulai maDandhaiyar thaDangaDal vaNNanaith thaaL nayandhu
aarvath thaal avar pulambiya pulambalai aRindhu mun uraiseydha,
kaarkoL paimpozhil mangaiyar kaavalan kalikanRi olivallaar
ErkoL vaikuntha maanagar pukku imaiyavaroDum kooDuvarE.
62.meaning: The tender-breasted maidens, bound in their bodices, longed for the feet of the Lord whose hue is the vast ocean; knowing their lamenting lament uttered in love, the chief of Tirumangai, the protector of the people, sang these verses set in the cloud-dark fresh groves. Those who can recite this song will enter the beautiful great city of Vaikuntha and join with the celestials.

63.translit:
thoNDeer. uyyum vakai kaNDEn thuLangaa arakkar thuLanga mun
thiNDOL nimirach chilai vaLaiyach chiRidhE munindha thirumaarban
vaNDaar koondhal malarmangai vaDikkaN maDandhai maanOkkam
kaNDaaL kaNDu koNDu kandha kaNNapuram naam thozhudhumE.
63.meaning: O devotees! I have found the way to be saved. The Lord on whose breast Lakshmi dwells—who long ago, when the unshaken demons were made to tremble, stretched his firm shoulders and bent his bow and grew but a little angry—at fragrant Kannapuram, the flower-maiden of bee-swarming tresses, the lady with sharp eyes, has gazed with her great glance: let us, beholding it, worship there.

64.translit:
porundhaa arakkar venjchamaththup ponRa anRu puLLoorndhu
perundhOL maali thalai puraLap pErndha arakkar tennilangai
irundhaar thammai uDankoNDu ang ezhilaar pilaththup pukku oLippa
karundhaaL silai kaik koNDaanoor kaNNapuram naam thozhudhumE.
64.meaning: That day, riding upon his bird (Garuda), so that the hostile demons perished in fierce battle and the head of broad-shouldered Mali rolled, the fleeing demons of southern Lanka who remained were taken along, and when they hid entering a deep cavern there—Kannapuram is the town of Him who took up the black-stemmed bow in his hand: let us worship there.

65.translit:
valli iDaiyaaL poruTTaaka madhiL neer ilangaiyaar kOvai,
allal seydhu venjchamaththuL aaTRal mikundha aaTRalaan
vallaaL arakkar kulappaavai vaaDa muni than vELviyai
kalvich chilaiyaal kaaththaanoor kaNNapuram naam thozhudhumE.
65.meaning: For the sake of her of the creeper-slender waist (Sita), he afflicted the king of moated, sea-girt Lanka and showed surpassing might in the fierce battle; the mighty one who, making the demon-clan's doll (the demoness) wither, guarded the sage's sacrifice with his learned bow—Kannapuram is His town: let us worship there.

66.translit:
mallai munneer adharpaDa varivenjchilai kaal vaLaiviththu,
kollai vilangu paNiseyyak koDiyOn ilangai pukaluTRu
thollai marangaL pukappeydhu thuvalai nimirndhu vaaNaNava
kallaal kaDalai aDaiththaanoor kaNNapuram naam thozhudhumE.
66.meaning: So that the way opened across the surging sea of the great waters, he bent and drew his striped fierce bow; making the wild forest creatures do his service, he set out to enter the Lanka of the wicked one. Casting in ancient trees, the spray rising up to reach the sky, He dammed the ocean with stones—Kannapuram is His town: let us worship there.

67.translit:
aamaiyaaki ariyaaki annamaaki andhaNar tham
Omamaaki oozhiyaay ulaku soozhndha neDumpuNari
sEma madhiL soozh ilangaikkOn siramum karamum thuNiththu mun
kaamaR payandhaan karudhumoor kaNNapuram naam thozhudhumE.
67.meaning: Becoming the tortoise, becoming the lion (man-lion), becoming the swan, becoming the fire-oblation of the brahmins, becoming the aeon, the vast ocean-flood that encircled the worlds; severing the heads and arms of the lord of Lanka girt by protecting ramparts—Kannapuram is the town cherished by Him who long ago begot Kama (Manmatha): let us worship there.

68.translit:
varundhaadhiru nee maDanenjchE nammEl vinaikaL vaaraa mun
thirundhaa arakkar tennilangai sendhee uNNach chivandhu orunaaL,
perundhOL vaaNaR karuLpurindhu pinnai maNaaLanaaki mun
karundhaaL kaLiRonRu osiththaanoor kaNNapuram naam thozhudhumE.
68.meaning: Do not grieve, O foolish heart of mine; before the deeds (karma) fall upon us, one day, reddening so that southern Lanka of the corrupt demons was consumed by the red fire, granting grace to broad-shouldered Bana, becoming the bridegroom of Nappinnai, and long ago breaking one black-legged elephant—Kannapuram is His town: let us worship there.

69.translit:
ilaiyaar malarppoom poykaivaay mudhalai thannaal aDarppuNDu
kolaiyaar vEzham naDukkuTRuk kulaiya adhanuk karuLpurindhaan
alainee rilangaith thasakkireevaRku iLaiyOR karasai aruLi, mun
kalaimaach chilaiyaal eydhaanoor kaNNapuram naam thozhudhumE.
69.meaning: When the murderous elephant, seized and pressed down by a crocodile at the edge of a leafy, blossom-flowered pond, trembled and quailed, He granted it grace; granting the kingdom to the younger brother of the ten-headed one (Vibhishana over Ravana) of wave-washed Lanka, and long ago shooting (the golden deer) with his great bow—Kannapuram is His town: let us worship there.

70.translit:
maalaay manamE arundhuyaril varundhaadhiru nee valimikka
kaalaar marudhum kaaysinaththa kazhudhum kadhamaak kazhudhaiyum,
maalaar viDaiyum madhakariyum mallar uyirum maDiviththu,
kaalaal sakaDam paayndhaanoor kaNNapuram naam thozhudhumE.
70.meaning: O heart, do not be deluded and grieve in unbearable sorrow. He destroyed the strong-rooted marudu trees, the hot-wrathed demoness (Putana), the furious donkey-demon, the deluding bull, the rut-maddened elephant, and the lives of the wrestlers; and with His foot He kicked through the cart (Sakatasura)—Kannapuram is His town: let us worship there.

71.translit:
kunRaal maari pazhudhaakkik koDiyEr iDaiyaaL poruTTaaka,
vanRaaL viDaiyEzh anRaDarththa vaanOr perumaan maamaayan,
senRaan thoodhu panjchavarkkaayth thirikaal sakaDam sinamazhiththu
kanRaal viLangaay eRindhaanoor kaNNapuram naam thozhudhumE.
71.meaning: With the hill (Govardhana) he made the rain (of Indra) come to naught; for the sake of her of the creeper-beautiful waist (Nappinnai), that day he subdued the seven strong-legged bulls; the Lord of the celestials, the great wondrous one, went as envoy for the five (Pandavas), destroyed the fury of the three-wheeled cart, and with a calf flung down the wood-apple fruits—Kannapuram is His town: let us worship there.

72.translit:
karumaa mukilthOy neDumaaDak kaNNapuraththem aDikaLai
thirumaa makaLaal aruLmaari sezhuneeraali vaLanaaDan
maruvaar puyalkaik kalikanRi mangai vEndhan olivallaar
irumaa nilaththuk karasaaki imaiyOr iRainja vaazhvaarE.
72.meaning: Our Lord of Kannapuram of tall mansions that touch the great dark clouds—Kalikanri, lord of Tirumangai, of the fertile land of rich-watered Tirunaraiyur, a rain of grace through Lakshmi, with hands generous as the rain-cloud—those able to recite his song will become kings of the great wide earth and live so that even the celestials bow before them.

73.translit:
viyamuDai viDaiyinam uDaidhara maDamakaL,
kuyamiDai thaDavarai akalam adhuuDaiyavar,
nayamuDai naDaiyanam iLaiyavar naDaipayil,
kayamiDai kaNapuram aDikaLtham iDamE.
73.meaning: He whose broad chest, vast as a great mountain, the tender maiden (Lakshmi) with full breasts presses close, when he broke the herd of fierce bulls—Kanapuram, where young women of graceful swan-like gait stroll, where ponds abound, is the abode of the Lord.

74.translit:
iNaimali marudhinoD erudhiRa ikalseydhu
thuNaimali mulaiyavaL maNamiku kalaviyuL,
maNamali vizhavinoD aDiyavar aLaviya,
kaNamali kaNapuram aDikaLtham iDamE.
74.meaning: He who fought to break the paired marudu trees and the bulls, and in the fragrant union with her of matching breasts (Nappinnai)—Kanapuram, thronged with the company of devotees mingling amid festivals rich with fragrance, is the abode of the Lord.

75.translit:
puyaluRu varaimazhai pozhidhara maNinirai,
mayaluRa varaikuDai eDuviya neDiyavar,
muyalthuLar miLaimuyal thuLavaLa viLaivayal,
kayalthuLu kaNapuram aDikaLtham iDamE.
75.meaning: When the storm-cloud poured rain upon the hills and the rows of cattle were bewildered, the tall Lord lifted the mountain as an umbrella—Kanapuram, with its fields where hares burrow in the thickets and carp leap in the rich growing crops, is the abode of the Lord.

76.translit:
Edhalar nakaisey iLaiyavar aLaiveNNey
pOdhuseydh amariya punithar nalviraimalar
kOdhiya madhukaram kulaviya malarmakaL
kaadhalsey kaNapuram aDikaLtham iDamE.
76.meaning: The pure one, who though enemies laughed ate the curd-butter as a child and abided there—Kanapuram, beloved of the flower-maiden (Lakshmi), where bees that have sipped the fragrant fine flowers hum, is the abode of the Lord.

77.translit:
thoNDarum amararum munivarum thozhudhezha
aNDamoD akaliDam aLandhavar amarchcheydhu
viNDavar paDamadhiL ilangaimun neriyezha
kaNDavar kaNapuram aDikaLtham iDamE.
77.meaning: He whom devotees, celestials and sages rose worshipping; who measured the cosmos and the wide earth; who made war and long ago looked upon walled Lanka so that the foes fell and fire blazed up—Kanapuram is the abode of that Lord.

78.translit:
mazhuviyal paDaiyuDai yavaniDam mazhaimukil,
thazhuviya uruvinar thirumakaL maruviya
kozhuviya sezhumalar muzhusiya paRavaipaN
ezhuviya kaNapuram aDikaLtham iDamE.
78.meaning: The abode of Him whose form embraces (the hue of) the rain-cloud, in whom Lakshmi dwells, who bears the axe-shaped weapon—Kanapuram, where birds that have dived into the lush rich flowers raise their song, is the abode of the Lord.

79.translit:
paridhiyoD aNimadhi panivarai thisainilam
erithiyoD enavina iyalvinar selavinar
suruthiyoD arumaRai muRaisolum aDiyavar
karudhiya kaNapuram aDikaLtham iDamE.
79.meaning: He whose nature and motion are spoken of as the sun, the lovely moon, the snowy mountains, the directions, the earth and the fire—Kanapuram, cherished by devotees who recite in due order the sruti and the rare Vedas, is the abode of the Lord.

80.translit:
padipulhu madiyiNai palartozha malarvaihu
kodipulhu tadavarai ahalamadu udaiyavar
mudipulhu neduvayal padaisela adimalar
kadipulhu kaNapuram adihaLtam idamE.
80.meaning: He whose broad chest, embraced by the creeper-like Lakshmi seated upon a blossom, is vast as a great mountain, and at whose feet flower-soft many bow with folded hands clinging to his lap and feet. Where the long fertile fields stretch and armies move, in fragrant Kannapuram of flowering feet — that is the abode of the Lord.

81.translit:
pulamanu malarmisai malarmahaL puNariya
nilamaha LenavinamahaLirhaL ivarodum
valamanu padaiyudai maNivaNar nidikuvai
kalamanu kaNapuram adihaLtam idamE.
81.meaning: He, the sapphire-hued Lord bearing the mighty discus, is joined with the flower-born Lakshmi who dwells upon the lotus, and with these maidens, the Earth-goddess and her companion handmaids. In Kannapuram, heaped with treasures and laden vessels, is the abode of the Lord.

82.translit:
malipuhazh kaNapuram udaiyavem adihaLai
valihezhu madiLayal vayalaNi mangaiyar
kaliyana tamizhivai vizhumiya isaiyinodu
olisolum adiyavar uRutuya rilarE.
82.meaning: These choice Tamil verses of Kaliyan — lord of Mangai with its strong-walled town adorned by fields — sing of our Lord who possesses Kannapuram of abundant fame. The devotees who recite them aloud with sweet melody shall have no enduring sorrow.

83.translit:
vaanO raLavum mudumunneer vaLarndha kaalam, valiyuruvil
meenaay vandhu viyandhuyyak koNda taNdaa maraikkaNNan
aanaa vuruvi laanaayan avanai yammaa viLaivayaluL
kaanaar puRavil kaNNapurat tadiyEn kaNdu koNdEnE.
83.meaning: When the ancient deep ocean swelled up to the realm of the gods, the cool-lotus-eyed One came in a mighty form as a fish and wondrously saved the world. That Lord of unending forms, the cowherd — I, his servant, have beheld and gained Him in Kannapuram of wondrous fertile fields and forest-skirted groves.

84.translit:
malanghu vilanghu neduveLLam maRuha angOr varainattu
ilanghu sOdhi yaaramudham eydhu maLavOr aamaiyaay
vilanghal tiriyat tadangaduluL sumandhu kidandha vittahanai,
kalangal munneerk kaNNapuratadiyEn kaNdu koNdEnE.
84.meaning: When the vast surging waters churned, He planted a mountain there, and to obtain the radiant precious nectar He became a tortoise, bearing on his back the wheeling mountain in the broad ocean — that clever One. In Kannapuram by the turbid ocean, I, his servant, have beheld and gained Him.

85.translit:
paaraar aLavum mudumunneer parandha kaalam, vaLaimaruppil
eeraar uruvat tEnamaay edutta aaRRa lammaanai,
kooraar aaral iraikarudik kuruhu paayak kayaliriyum,
kaaraar puRavil kaNNapurat tadiyEn kaNdu koNdEnE.
85.meaning: When the ancient ocean spread up to cover the earth, He arose as a boar of splendid form with curved tusk, lifting up the earth by his might — the Lord. In Kannapuram of cloud-dark groves, where the heron darts at the sharp eel for its prey and the kayal fish flee, I, his servant, have beheld and gained Him.

86.translit:
uLaindha ariyum maanidamum udanaayt tOnRa onRuvittu
viLaindha seeRRam viNveddumba vERROn ahalam venjamattu
piLandhu vaLaindha vuhiraanaip perundaN sennaR kulaitadindhu
kaLanjey puRavil kaNNapuratadiyEn kaNdu koNdEnE.
86.meaning: Joining the fierce lion and the human together into one form, his arisen wrath made the heavens tremble; with curved claws He clove open the chest of the foe in fierce battle. In Kannapuram of forest-fields, where they cut the tall cool red-paddy clusters and make threshing-floors, I, his servant, have beheld and gained Him.

87.translit:
tozhuneer vadivil kuRaLuruvaay vandhu tOnRi maavalipaal
muzhuneer vaiyam munkoNda moovaa vuruvi nammaanai
uzhuneer vayaluL ponkiLaippa orupaal mullai muhaiyOdum
kazhuneer malarum kaNNapuratadiyEn kaNdu koNdEnE.
87.meaning: In the form of a worshipful dwarf He came and appeared, and of old took the whole ocean-girt earth from Mahabali — the Lord of ageless form. In Kannapuram, where in the plowed wet fields gold gleams forth, and on one side with jasmine buds the lily blossoms, I, his servant, have beheld and gained Him.

88.translit:
vadivaay mazhuvE padaiyaaha vandhu tOnRi moovezhuhaal
padiyaar arasu kaLaihatta paazhi yaanai yammaanai
kudiyaa vaNdu koNduNNak kOla neelam mattuhukkum
kadiyaar puRavil kaNNapurat tadiyEn kaNdu koNdEnE.
88.meaning: Coming and appearing with the shapely axe as his weapon, the powerful Lord destroyed thrice-seven times over the kings who filled the earth. In fragrant Kannapuram of groves, where the swarming bees settle and drink and the beautiful blue lilies pour out their nectar, I, his servant, have beheld and gained Him.

89.translit:
vaiya mellaa mudanvaNanga vaNangaa manna naayttOnRi
veyya seeRRak kadiyilangai kudikoN dOda venjamattu,
seydha vempOr namparanaic chezhundaN kaanal maNanaaRum
kaidai vElik kaNNapurat tadiyEn kaNdu koNdEnE.
89.meaning: Appearing as a king to whom all the world bowed yet who himself bowed to none, in fierce battle He waged terrible war so that the fortified Lanka of cruel wrath was uprooted and fled. That supreme Lord — in Kannapuram of lush cool seaside groves fragrant with scent, hedged by screw-pine, I, his servant, have beheld and gained Him.

90.translit:
oRRaik kuzhaiyum naanjilum orupaal tOnRat taantOnRi
veRRit tozhilaar vElvEndar viNpaaR sella venjamattu
seRRa koRRat tozhilaanaic chendee moonRum milliruppa
kaRRa maRaiyOr kaNNapuratadiyEn kaNdu koNdEnE.
90.meaning: Appearing himself with a single earring and a plough on one side, in fierce battle He of victorious deeds and conquering might sent the spear-wielding kings to the heavens. That Lord of mighty deeds — in Kannapuram where the learned vedic ones keep the three sacred fires in their homes, I, his servant, have beheld and gained Him.

91.translit:
tuvarik kanivaay nilamangai tuyartheern duyyap paaradattuL
ivarit tarasar tadumaaRa iruLnaaL piRandha ammaanai
uvari yOdham muttundha orupaa lorupaa loNsennel
kavari veesum kaNNapurat tadiyEn kaNdu koNdEnE.
91.meaning: So that the coral-lipped Earth-maiden's sorrow might end and she be saved, He was born on a dark night within the Mahabharata, throwing the assembled kings into confusion — the Lord. In Kannapuram, where on one side the ocean waves toss up pearls and on the other the bright red-paddy waves like a fly-whisk, I, his servant, have beheld and gained Him.

92.translit:
meenO daamai kEzhalari kuRaLaay munnu miraamanaayt taanaay
pinnu miraamanaayt taamO daranaayk kaRkiyum aanaan Rannai
kaNNapurat tadiyEn kaliya nolisaiyda
tEnaa rinsol tamizhmaalai seppap paavam nillaavE.
92.meaning: He who became the fish, tortoise, boar, lion, dwarf, then Rama of old, then himself, then again Rama, then Damodara, and Kalki too — of Him the servant of Kannapuram, Kaliyan, has composed this honeyed garland of sweet-worded Tamil. To those who recite it, sin shall not abide.

93.translit:
kaimmaana madayaanai yidartheertta karumuhilai
maimmaana maNiyai aNikoL marahadattai,
emmaanai yempiraanai yeesanai yenmanattuL
ammaanai, adiyE nadaindhuyndhu pOnEnE.
93.meaning: The dark cloud who removed the distress of the trunked rutting elephant, the dark-lustred gem, the splendid emerald, my Lord, my master, the supreme God, the Father within my heart — I, his servant, have reached Him, been saved, and gone forth.

94.translit:
tarumaana mazhaimuhilaip piriyaadhu tannadaindhaar,
varumaanam tavirkkum maNiyaiyaNiyuruvil,
tirumaalai yammaanai amudattaik kadaRkidandha
perumaanai adiyE nadaindhuyndhu pizhaittEnE.
94.meaning: The bountiful rain-cloud, the gem who removes the affliction of those who, never parting, take refuge in Him, Tirumal of beautiful form, the Lord, the nectar, the great One who reclines upon the ocean — I, his servant, have reached Him, been saved, and survived.

95.translit:
vidaiyEzhan RadarttuveeguNdu vilangaluRa
padaiyaalaazhi tatta paraman paranjOdhi,
madaiyaar neelammalhum vayalsoozh kaNNapuramon
RudaiyaanukkudiyEn oruvarkkuriyEnO?
95.meaning: The supreme One who, in wrath, subdued the seven bulls, and as a weapon hurled the discus when the mountain blocked him — the supreme light, who possesses the one Kannapuram girded by fields where blue lilies flourish at the sluices. I am his servant — shall I be subject to any other?

96.translit:
mikkaanai maRaiyaay virindha viLakkai ennuL
pukkaanaip puhazhsEr poliginRa ponmalaiyai
takkaanaik kadihait tadangunRin misaiyirundha
akkaarak kaniyai adaindhuyndhu pOnEnE.
96.meaning: The supreme One, the lamp that spread forth as the Vedas, who entered within me, the glorious gleaming golden mountain, the worthy One, the sweet sugary fruit who abides upon the broad hill of Kadigai — Him I have reached, been saved, and gone forth.

97.translit:
vandhaayen manattE vandhunee puhundhapinnai,
endaay pOyaRiyaay iduvE yamaiyaadhO
kondaar paimpozhilsoozh kudandhaik kidandhuhandha
maindhaa unnaiyenRum maRavaamaip peRREnE.
97.meaning: You came into my mind, and since You entered, my Father, You no longer depart — is this not enough? O youthful Lord who delights to recline at Kudandhai surrounded by clustering green groves, I have gained the grace of never forgetting You.

98.translit:
enjaa vennarahat tazhundhi nadunguhinRERku,
anjElen Radiyenai aatkoLLa vallaanai,
nenjE neeninaiyaadhu iRaippozhudumiruttikaNdaay,
manjaar maaLihaisoozh vayalaali maindhanaiyE.
98.meaning: He who is able to take me, his servant, into his service, saying 'fear not' to me who was sinking and trembling in the unceasing cruel hell — O heart, do not be without thinking of Him even for a moment, the youthful Lord of Vayalali girded by cloud-touching mansions.

99.translit:
peRRaar peRRozhindhaar pinnumninRadiyEnukku,
uRRaanaay vaLarttu ennuyiraahi ninRaanai,
muRRaa maamadikOL viduttaanai yemmaanai
ettaal yaanmaRakkEn idusollenanEzhainenjE.
99.meaning: Those who bore me bore me and were done; yet still He became my own kin, raised me, and stood as my very life. He who released the great young moon from its eclipse, my Lord — by what means could I ever forget Him? Tell me this, O foolish heart.

0.translit:
vangamaa munneer variniRap periya vaaLaravinai meevi
sangamaar angait tadamalar undic chaamamaa meeniyen talaivan
angamaaRaindu veeLvinaalveedam arungalai payinRu eri moonRum
sengaiyaal vaLarkkum tuLakkamil manattoor tirukkaNNankudiyuL ninRaanee.
0.meaning: My Lord of the dark complexion, who reclines upon the great striped-bodied serpent Adisesha in the vast ocean where ships sail, and who, with conch in His beautiful hand, drinks the nectar of the broad lotus blossom -- He dwells at Tirukkannankudi among those of unshakable mind who, learned in the six angas, the four Vedas and the rare arts, nurture the three sacred fires with their pure red hands.

1.translit:
kavaLamaa kadatta kariyuyyap poygaik karaamkoLak kalangiyuL ninaindu
tuvaLamel vandu toonRivan mudalai tuNipadac chudupadai turandoon
kuvaLaineeL muLari kumudamoN kazhuneer koymmalar neydaloN kazhani
tivaLummaaLigai soozh sezhumaNip purisait tirukkaNNankudiyuL ninRaanee.
1.meaning: He who, to save the rutting elephant that fed on cud when a fierce crocodile seized it and it grew distressed and called Him to mind, came swiftly and appeared, hurling His burning discus so that the mighty crocodile was cut asunder -- He dwells at Tirukkannankudi, ringed by gleaming jeweled ramparts and shining mansions, amid fields rich with blue lilies, tall lotuses, fine red lotuses, fresh-plucked blossoms and water-lily flowers.

2.translit:
vaadaivan dadara vaanamum nilanum malaigaLum alaikadal kuLippa
meedukoN dugaLum meenuru vaagi viripunal variyakat toLittoon
poodalar punnai malligai mauval putuvirai madumalar aNaindu
seedavoN tenRal tisaitoRum kamazhum tirukkaNNankudiyuL ninRaanee.
2.meaning: When destruction drew near and the heavens, the earth, the mountains and the surging seas were submerged, He took the form of a great fish leaping above the waters, and hid the whole world within His striped belly amid the spreading flood. He dwells at Tirukkannankudi, where the cool fragrant southern breeze, having touched the budding punnai, jasmine, wild jasmine and fresh honeyed blossoms, wafts its sweetness in every direction.

3.translit:
venRiseer tiNmai vilangalmaa meeni veLLeyiR RoLLerit taRukaN
panRiyaay anRu paarmagaL payalai teerttavan panjavar paagan
onRalaa vuruvat tulappilpal kaalattu uyarkodi yoLivaLar madiyam,
senRuseer senniic chikarana maadat tirukkaNNankudiyuL ninRaanee.
3.meaning: He who once, as the boar of victorious strength with a mountain-like body, white tusks, blazing fiery eyes and fearless gaze, ended the pallor of grief of the Earth-maiden; He who was the charioteer of the five Pandavas. He of forms beyond a single shape, who endures through endless ages -- He dwells at Tirukkannankudi, whose lofty banners and the radiant growing moon reach and rest upon the peaks of its fine soaring mansions.

4.translit:
mannavan periya veeLviyil kuRaLaay moovadi neerodum koNdu
pinnumeezh ulagum eeradi yaagap peruntisai yadangida nimirndoon
annamen kamalat taNimalarp peedat talaipuna lilaikkudai neezhal
sennaeloN kavari yasaiyaveeR Rirukkum tirukkaNNankudiyuL ninRaanee.
4.meaning: He who, as a dwarf at the great sacrifice of the king Mahabali, received three feet of ground with a pouring of water, then grew so that the seven worlds and all the broad directions became but two of His strides. He dwells at Tirukkannankudi, where He sits enthroned upon the lovely-petalled seat of the soft swan-like lotus, in the shade of a leaf-canopy over the water, while bright fans of fine red paddy sway beside Him.

5.translit:
mazhuvinaal avani arasaimoo vezhukaal maNimudi podipaduttu udirak
kuzhuvuvaar punaluL kuLittuveng koopam tavirndavan, kulaimali kadalik
kuzhuvumvaar kamugum kuravumnaR palavum kuLirtaru soodammaa taviyum
sezhumaiyaar pozhilgaL tazhuvunaa maadat tirukkaNNankudiyuL ninRaanee.
5.meaning: He who, with His axe, thrice-seven times ground to dust the jeweled crowns of the kings of the earth till blood flowed, then bathed in the gathered streams of that blood and stilled His fierce wrath. He dwells at Tirukkannankudi, whose fine mansions are embraced by lush groves of clustering banana, tall areca, kuravu, fine jackfruit, cool mango and maatavi creepers.

6.translit:
vaanuLaa ravarai valimaiyaal naliyum malikadal ilangaiyaar koonai
paanuseer sarattaal panangani poolap parumudi yudiravil vaLaittoon
kaanulaa mayilin kaNangaLnin Raadak kaNamugil murasanin Radhira
teenulaa varivaN dinnisai muralum tirukkaNNankudiyuL ninRaanee.
6.meaning: He who, with sun-bright arrows, bent His bow so that the heavy heads of the Lanka king -- who by his might tormented the dwellers of heaven -- fell like ripe palmyra fruits. He dwells at Tirukkannankudi, where flocks of forest-roaming peacocks stand and dance, the gathered clouds thunder like drums, and the striped honey-bees hum their sweet music.

7.translit:
aravuneeL kodiyoon avaiyuLaa sanattai anjidaa teeyida, adaRkup
periyamaa meeni yaNdamoo duruvap peruntisai yadangida nimirndoon
varaiyinmaa maNiyum maragatat tiraLum vayiramum vetirutir muttum
tiraikoNarn dundi vayaltoRum kuvikkum tirukkaNNankudiyuL nin Raanee.
7.meaning: When the one with the long serpent-banner (Duryodhana) fearlessly offered a seat in his assembly, He, for that seat's sake, grew so His great vast body pierced the cosmic egg and spanned the broad directions. He dwells at Tirukkannankudi, where the waves bring and heap up in every field the great gems of the mountains, masses of emerald, diamonds, and the pearls that fall from the bamboo.

8.translit:
panniya paaram paarmagat kozhiyap paarata maaperum poaril
mannargaL madiya maNiNedun tiNteer maittunark kuyttamaa maayan
tunnumaa taviyum surapunaip pozhilum soozhndezhu seNbaga malarvaay
tennaven RaLigaL muranRisai paadum tirukkaNNankudiyuL ninRaanee.
8.meaning: The great Mayan who, to relieve the Earth-maiden of her abundant burden, drove the jeweled, tall, sturdy chariot for His brother-in-law (Arjuna) in the great Bharata war so that the kings fell. He dwells at Tirukkannankudi, where the bees, humming 'thennaa,' sing their music upon the blossoming shenbaga flowers amid encircling clustered maatavi creepers and fragrant groves of surapunnai.

9.translit:
kalaiyulaaval kul kaarigai tiRattuk kadalperum padaiyodum senRu
silaiyinaal ilangai teeyezhac cheRRa tirukkaNNankudiyuL ninRaanai
malaikulaa maada mangaiyar talaivan maanaveel kaliyanvaa yoligaL
ulavusol maalai yonbadoa donRum vallavarkkillainal kuravee.
9.meaning: For the sake of the lady whose waist is robed with garments, He went with the great army across the sea and with His bow burned and destroyed Lanka -- this Lord who stands at Tirukkannankudi. Those who can master these ten flowing-worded verses, the garland of song from the mouth of Kaliyan, the spear-bearing chief of Tirumangai of the hill-like mansions, will know no poverty.

0.translit:
ponnivar meeni maragathaththin pongiLanj soothi yagalaththaaram min
ivar vaayil nal veetha moothum veethiyar vaanavar aavar thoozhii,
ennaiyum nookki en nalgulum nookki eenthiLam kongaiyum nookkukinraar
annai en nookkum enru anjukinreen achchoo oruvar azhagiyavaa.
0.meaning: His body gleams like gold, and on His chest shines the surging youthful radiance of an emerald necklace. O friend, the brahmins who chant the good Vedas from His mouth are themselves celestials. He gazes at me, gazes at my waist, and gazes at my budding young bosom, and I tremble fearing what my mother will think when she sees His look. Achcho! How beautiful is this peerless one!

1.translit:
thoodavizh neelam maNangodukkum soozhpunal soozhkudan thaikkidantha
seedarkol enru therikka maatteen senjudar aazhiyum sangum eenthi
paadaga melladi yaarvaNangap panmaNi muththodi langusoothi
aadagam pooNdoru naangu thooLum achchoo oruvar azhagiyavaa.
1.meaning: I cannot tell whether He is the great Lord reclining at Kudandai (Kumbakonam), girded by waters where blooming blue lilies spread their fragrance. Bearing the red-rayed discus and the conch, with four shoulders that shine with radiance from many gems and pearl-bracelets and golden ornaments, as women with soft anklet-adorned feet worship Him. Achcho! How beautiful is this peerless one!

2.translit:
veeyirunj soolai vilangal soozhntha meyya maNaaLar iv vaiyamellaam
thaayina naayagar aavar thoozhii thaamaraik kaNgaL irunthavaaru,
seeyirung kunram thigazhntha thoppach seviya vaagi malarnthasoothi
aayiram thooLodu ilangu pooNum achchoo oruvar azhagiyavaa.
2.meaning: O friend, He is the bridegroom of Meyyam, surrounded by hills and groves of dense bamboo, and He is the Lord who measured out all this world. Behold the beauty of His lotus eyes! His radiance, red and blossomed, glows like a great red hill, and His thousand shoulders shine with ornaments. Achcho! How beautiful is this peerless one!

3.translit:
vambavizh umthuzhaay maalai thooLmeel kaiyana aazhiyum sangum eenthi,
nambar nam millam pugunthu ninraar naagarigar peri thumiLaiyar
sempava lamivar vaayin vaNNam theevar ivar uruvam sollil
ambava laththira leeyum oppar achchoo oruvar azhagiyavaa.
3.meaning: With a garland of fresh fragrant tulasi on His shoulders, bearing the discus and conch in His hands, that trusted one entered and stood within our home; most refined is He, and very youthful. The color of His mouth is like red coral, and if one were to describe the form of this god, He resembles a glowing mass of pure coral. Achcho! How beautiful is this peerless one!

4.translit:
koozhiyum koodalum kooyil koNda koovala reeyoppar kunramanna
paazhiyum thooLumoor naangu daiyar paNdivar thammaiyum kaNdariyoom
vaazhiyaroo ivar vaNNam eNNil maakadal poonruLar kaiyilveyya,
aazhiyonru eenthiyoor sangu parri achchoo oruvar azhagiyavaa.
4.meaning: He resembles the cowherd Lord who has taken Uraiyur (Kozhi) and Madurai (Koodal) as His shrines, possessed of four hill-like, mighty shoulders. We have never seen Him before this. May He live long! If one reflects on His hue, He is like the great dark ocean. In His hand He holds one fierce discus and grasps a conch. Achcho! How beautiful is this peerless one!

5.translit:
venjina veezham maruppo siththa veentharkol eenthizhai yaarmanaththai
thanjudai yaaLarkol yaan ariyeen thaamaraik kaNgaL irunthavaaru
kanjanai yanjamun kaalvi saiththa kaaLaiyaar avar kaNdaarvaNangum
anjana maamalai yeeyum oppar achchoo oruvar azhagiyavaa.
5.meaning: Is He the king who broke the tusk of the furious elephant? Is He the one who holds refuge in the hearts of bejeweled women? I do not know. Behold the way His lotus eyes are! He is the youthful one who once kicked out His leg to make Kamsa quake; whoever sees Him bows before Him, and He resembles a great mountain of dark collyrium. Achcho! How beautiful is this peerless one!

6.translit:
piNiyavizh thaamarai mottalarththum peeraru LaaLarkol? yaanariyeen,
paNiyum en nenjam ithenkol thoozhii paNdivar thammaiyum kaNdariyoom
aNigezhu thaamarai yanna kaNNum angaiyum pangaya meenivaanaththu,
aNigezhu maamugi leeyum oppar achchoo oruvar azhagiyavaa.
6.meaning: Is He the one of supreme grace who unfolds the bound lotus into bloom? I do not know. Why does my heart bow before Him so, O friend? We have never seen Him before. His eyes are like the lovely lotus, His palms too are like lotus, and His body resembles a beautiful great cloud in the sky. Achcho! How beautiful is this peerless one!

7.translit:
manjuyar maamathi theeNda neeNda maalirunj soolaima Naalarvanthu en
nenjuLLum kaNNuLLum ninru neengaar neermalai yaarkol? ninaikkamaatteen?
manjuyar ponmalai meele zhuntha maamugil poonruLar vanthukaaNiir
anjiraip puLLumonru eeri vanthaar achchoo oruvar azhagiyavaa.
7.meaning: The bridegroom of Tirumaalirunjoolai, whose hills rise so high that the cloud-touched great moon brushes them, has come and stands within my heart and within my eyes and will not depart. Is He the Lord of Tirunirmalai? I cannot fathom it. He resembles a great cloud risen over a cloud-high golden mountain - come and see! He has come riding upon the bird of beautiful wings. Achcho! How beautiful is this peerless one!

8.translit:
eNdisai yumeri neerkka dalum eezhula gumuda neevizhungi
maNdiyoor aalilaip paLLi koLLum maayarkol? maayam ariyamaatteen
koNdalnan maalvarai yeeyum oppar kongalar thaamaraik kaNNumvaayum
aNdath thamarar paNiya ninraar achchoo oruvar azhagiyavaa.
8.meaning: Is He the wondrous one who swallowed up all the eight directions, the surging ocean of waters, and the seven worlds together, and then rested on a single banyan leaf? I cannot comprehend His wondrous ways. He resembles a great rain-cloud-laden mountain; His eyes and mouth are like fragrant blossoming lotuses, and He stands while the immortals of the celestial realm bow before Him. Achcho! How beautiful is this peerless one!

9.translit:
annamum keezhalum miinu maaya aathiyai naagai yazhagiyaarai
kanninan maamathiL mangai veenthan kaamaru seerk kalikanri kunraa
innisai yaal sonna senjol maalai eezhu miraNdumo ronrum vallaar,
mannava raayulagu aaNdu miiNdum vaanava raaymagizh veythuvaree.
9.meaning: To the primordial Lord who became the swan, the boar, and the fish - the beautiful one of Tirunagai (Nagapattinam) - Kalikanri (Tirumangai Alwar), king of Mangai with strong fine great ramparts, of desirable glory, has spoken this garland of true words in unfailing sweet music. Those who can master these ten verses (seven and two and one) shall rule the earth as kings, and then again become celestials and attain bliss.

0.translit:
karuvuDai meekangaL kaNDaal unnaik kaNDaalokkum kaNgaL
uruvuDaiyaay ulageezhum uNDaaga vandu pirandaay
tiruvuDaiyaaL maNavaaLaa tiruvarangatte kiDandaay
maruvi maNamkamazh kinra mallikaip poochchooTTa vaaraay.
0.meaning: O Lord whose dark eyes, when one beholds rain-laden clouds, seem like the very sight of You! O You of beautiful form, You came and were born so that all seven worlds might come to be. O Bridegroom of her who is Lakshmi, You who recline at Srirangam — come, that I may adorn You with fragrant, sweet-smelling jasmine blossoms.

1.translit:
seemaalikanavanoDu toozhamai koLLavum vallaay
saamaaru avanai nee eNNich chakkarattaal talai koNDaay
aamaaru aRiyum piraanee aNiyarangatte kiDandaay
eemaaRRam ennait tavirttaay iruvaaTchip poochchooTTa vaaraay.
1.meaning: O You who were able to feign friendship with that demon Simhalika (Cesimalikan), yet, resolving how he must die, took off his head with Your discus. O Lord who knows the right course of all things, You who recline at beautiful Srirangam — You who freed me from despair and deception, come, that I may adorn You with iruvaatchi (red jasmine) blossoms.

2.translit:
vaNDugaLit tiraikkum pozhilsoozh
 varupunal kaaviritten arangan
paNDavan seyda kiriDai yellaam
 paTTar piraan viTTusittan paaDal
koNDivai paaDik kunikka vallaar
 koovindan tanaDi yaargaL aagi
eNDisaik kumviLak kaagi niRpaar
 iNaiyaDi en talai meel anave.
2.meaning: Of the Lord of southern Srirangam, surrounded by groves where bees swarm in delight, girdled by the flowing waters of the Kaveri — of all the sportive play He did of old, Vishnuchittan (Periyalwar), beloved of the learned, has sung. Those able to sing these songs and dance shall become the servants of Govinda and stand as a lamp shining to all eight directions; their twin feet rest upon my head.

3.translit:
kanninan maamadiL soozhdaru poompozhil kaaviritten arangam
manniya seermadu soodanaa keesavaa paaviyeen vaazhvu gandu
unnai iLangaNru meeykkach chiRukaalee yooTTi orup paDutteen
ennin manamvali yaaLoru peNillai en kuTTanee muttamtaa.
3.meaning: At Srirangam in the south, by the Kaveri, ringed with great new ramparts and flowering groves — O Madhusudana of enduring glory, O Kesava! Sinful as I am, delighting in my own life I fed You early in the morning and made You ready to go and graze the young calves. There is no woman with a heart as hard as mine — O my little one, give me a kiss!

4.translit:
maadavattoon puttiranpooy maRikaDalvaay maaNDaanai
ooduvitta takkaNaiyaa uruvuruvee koDuttaanoor
toodavattit tooymaRaiyoor tuRaipaDiyat tuLumbiengum
poodilvaitta teensoriyum punalaranga menbaduvee.
4.meaning: As the fee for the teaching he received, He gave back, form by very form, the son of the great ascetic (Sandipani) who had perished in the surging sea — that is His town. There the pure brahmins of perfect penance bathe at the ghats, and everywhere the waters brim over, raining down the honey stored within the blossoms — that place is called water-girt Srirangam.

5.translit:
piRappagatte maaNDozhinda piLLaigaLai naalvaraiyum
iRaippozhudil koNarndukoDuttu orup paDitta uRaippanoor
maRaipperundee vaLarttiruppaar varuvirundai yaLittiruppaar
siRappuDaiya maRaiyavarvaazh tiruvaranga menbaduvee.
5.meaning: He brought back in an instant and restored the four children who had died at the moment of their birth — that is the town of the resolute Lord. There dwell those who tend the great fire of the Vedas and those who give welcome to guests who come; the place where these glorious vedic brahmins live is called holy Srirangam.

6.translit:
marumagantan santatiyai uyirmeeTTu maittunanmaar
urumagatte veezhaamee gurumugamaayk kaattaanoor
tirumugamaaych chengamalam tiruniRamaayk karunguvaLai
porumugaamaay ninRalarum punalaranga menbaduvee.
6.meaning: He brought back to life the lineage of His sister's son, and, taking the form of a teacher, guarded His brothers-in-law lest they fall into the fierce fire of war — that is His town. There red lotuses bloom like His sacred face, and the dark blue lilies, like His sacred hue, stand opening as if rival faces — that place is called water-girt Srirangam.

7.translit:
koondozhuttai sidakuraippak koDiyavaLvaayk kaDiyasol keeTTu
eenReDutta taayaraiyum iraachchiyamum aangozhiya
kaandoDutta neRipoogik kaNDagaraik kaLaindaanoor
teendoDutta malarchchoolait tiruvaranga menbaduvee.
7.meaning: When the hunchbacked maid (Manthara) spoke evil, He heard the harsh words from that wicked woman's mouth, left behind there the mother who bore Him and the kingdom too, went along the forest path, and destroyed the demon-foes (the rakshasas) — that is His town. The place of honey-laden blossoming groves is called holy Srirangam.

8.translit:
peruvarangaL avaipaRRip pizhaguDaiya iraavaNanai
uruvarangap porudazhittu iv vulaginaikkaN
peruttaanoor guruvarumbak koongalarak kuyilkoovum kuLirpozhilsoozh
tiruvaranga menbaduvee en tirumaal seerviDamee.
8.meaning: Relying on the great boons he held, the wicked Ravana — Him He fought, His form ravaged in battle, destroyed, and so let this world be regained — that is His town. Ringed by cool groves where buds swell, kongu flowers bloom and the cuckoo calls, the place called holy Srirangam is the abode where my Tirumal dwells.

9.translit:
keezhulagil asurargaLaik kizhangirundu kiLaraamee
aazhiviDuttu avaruDaiya karuvazhitta vazhippanoor
taazhaimaDal ooDurinjit tavaLavaNNap poDiyaNindu
yaazhinisai vaNDinangaL aaLamvaikkum arangamee.
9.meaning: So that the asuras in the nether world should not rise up from their very roots, He loosed His discus and uprooted their brood — that is the town of the destroyer. There the swarms of bees, rubbing against the petals of the fragrant screwpine and dusted with its white-hued pollen, raise their lute-like music — that is Srirangam.

10.translit:
kozhuppuDaiya sezhunguruti kozhittizhindu kumizhtteRiya
pizhakkuDaiya asurargaLaip piNampaDutta perumaanoor
tazhuppariya sandanangaL taDavaraivaay eerttukkoNDu
tezhippuDaiya kaavirivandu aDitozhum seerarangamee.
10.meaning: So that thick rich blood gushed down and spurted up bubbling, He laid low the offending asuras as corpses — that is the town of the Lord. There the roaring Kaveri, dragging down sandalwood trees hard to embrace from the broad mountainsides, comes and worships at His feet — that is glorious Srirangam.

11.translit:
valleyiRRuk keezhalumaay vaaLeyiRRuch cheeyamumaay
ellaiyillaat taraNiyaiyum auNanaiyum iDandaanoor
elliyamboodu irunjchiRaivaNDu emperumaan guNampaaDi
mallikaiveN sangoodum madiLaranga menbaduvee.
11.meaning: As the boar with mighty tusks He lifted up the boundless earth, and as the lion with gleaming fangs He tore open the demon (Hiranya) — that is His town. There at evening the broad-winged bees sing the qualities of our Lord, and the white jasmine blows like a sounding conch — that place is called rampart-girt Srirangam.

12.translit:
kunRaaDu kozhumugilpool kuvaLaigaLpool
kuraikaDalpool ninRaaDu kaNamayilpool niRamuDaiya neDumaaloor
kunRaaDu pozhilnuzhaindu koDiyiDaiyaar mulaiyaNavi
manRooDu tenRalumaam madiLaranga menbaduvee.
12.meaning: The town of tall Tirumal, dark of hue like the rich clouds that play upon the hills, like the blue lilies, like the roaring ocean, like the flock of dancing peacocks standing in display — there the southern breeze enters the groves upon the hills, embraces the breasts of slender-waisted women, and wanders through the public square — that place is called rampart-girt Srirangam.

13.translit:
paruvarangaL avaipaRRip paDaiyaalit tezhundaanai
seruvarangap porudazhitta tiruvaaLan tiruppadimeel
tiruvarangat tamizhmaalai viTTusittan virittanakoNDu
iruvarangam erittaanai eettavallaa raDiyoomee.
13.meaning: Upon the holy city of the gracious Lord, who fought and destroyed in battle the one (Ravana) who, relying on his great boons, rose up surging with his army, Vishnuchittan has composed a garland of Tamil verses about Srirangam. We are the servants of those who, taking up these verses, are able to praise Him who burnt up both kinds of foes (or burnt the demons utterly).

14.translit:
maravaDiyait tambikku vaanpaNaiyam vaittuppooy vaanoor vaazhach
seru uDaiya tisaikkarumam tiruttivandu ulagaaNDa tirumaal kooyil
tiruvaDitan tiruuruvum tirumangai malarkkaNNum kaaTTi ninRu
uru uDaiya malarneelam kaaRRu aaTTa olisalikkum oLi arangamee.
14.meaning: Setting the wooden sandals as the precious pledge of kingship for His younger brother (Bharata), He went forth, set right the war-torn business of the directions so that the celestials might thrive, and returned to rule the world — that is the temple of Tirumal. There the beautiful blue lotuses of lovely form, swayed by the wind, sway and murmur, showing His sacred feet's sacred form and the flower-like eyes of Tirumangai (Lakshmi) — that is radiant Srirangam.

15.translit:
tan aDiyaar tiRattagattut taamaraiyaaL aagilum sidagu uraikkumeel
en aDiyaar adu seyyaar seydaareel nanRu seydaar enbar poolum
man uDaiya vibeeDaNaRkaa madil ilangait tisaineokki malarkkaN vaitta
ennuDaiya tiruvarangaRku anRiyum maRRu oruvarkku aaL aavaree?
15.meaning: Even if it be Lakshmi of the lotus herself, should she speak ill concerning His devotees, He seems to say: 'My devotees would not do such a thing; and if they did, they did well.' For the sake of Vibhishana, who would be king, He turned His flower-like eyes toward the direction of walled Lanka — to this my Lord of Srirangam, and to none other, can one ever be a servant?

16.translit:
karuL uDaiya pozhil marudum kadak kaLiRum pilambanaiyum kaDiya maavum
uruL uDaiya sagaDaraiyum mallaraiyum uDaiya viTTu oosai keeTTaan
iruL agaRRum eRi kadiroon maNDalattooDu eeRRi vaittu eeNi vaangi
aruL koDuttiTTu aDiyavarai aaTkoLvaan amarum oor aNi arangamee.
16.meaning: He broke the twin marudu trees of the dark grove, the rutting elephant, Pralamba, the fierce horse (Kesi), the rolling cartwheel-demon (Sakatasura) and the wrestlers, and heard the sound of their breaking — that is His town. He raises His devotees up into the orb of the light-radiating sun that dispels darkness, then draws away the ladder, bestows His grace and takes them as His own — the town where He abides is beautiful Srirangam.

17.translit:
padinaaRaam aayiravar deeviimaar paNiseyyat tuvarai ennum
madil naayagaraagi veeRRirunda maNavaaLar mannu kooyil
pudu naaLmalarrk kamalam emperumaan pon vayiRRiR poovee poolvaan
podu-naayagam paavittu iRumaandu pon saaykkum punal arangamee.
17.meaning: While the sixteen thousand and more divine consorts render Him service, He sat enthroned as the lord of the walled city called Dwaraka — the Bridegroom — and this is His abiding temple. There the fresh-blooming lotus of the new day, as if longing to be like the flower upon our Lord's golden belly, assuming a common lordship and growing proud, sheds its gold — that is water-girt Srirangam.

18.translit:
aamaiyaayk kangaiyaay aazh kaDalaay avaniyaay aru varaigaLaay
naanmuganaay naanmaRaiyaay veeLviyaayt takkaNaiyaayt taanum aanaan
seemam uDai naaradanaar senRu senRu tudittu iRainjak kiDandaan kooyil
poo maruvip puL inangaL puL araiyan pugazh kuzhaRum punal arangamee.
18.meaning: He became the tortoise, the Ganga, the deep ocean, the earth, the rare mountains, the four-faced Brahma, the four Vedas, the sacrifice and its offering-fee, and He Himself became all — and there He reclines while the protecting sage Narada comes again and again to praise and bow — that is His temple. There the flocks of birds, nestling among the flowers, warble the glory of the king of birds (Garuda) — that is water-girt Srirangam.

19.translit:
maittunanmaar kaadaliyai mayir muDippittu avargaLaiyee mannar aakki
uttaraitan siRuvanaiyum uyakkoNDa uyiraaLan uRaiyum kooyil
battargaLum bagavargaLum pazhamozhivaay munivargaLum paranda naaDum
sittargaLum tozhudu iRainjat tisai-viLakkaay niRkinRa tiruvarangamee.
19.meaning: He had the beloved (Draupadi) of His cousins (the Pandavas) bind up her hair again, made those very ones into kings, and saved the little son of Uttara (Parikshit) — the temple where that Lord of all life dwells. There the devotees, the holy ones, the sages of ancient utterance, the wide-spread lands and the siddhas all worship and bow — that is holy Srirangam, standing as a lamp to all directions.

100.translit:
oruvillaal oongu munneer anaittulagangaL uyya
seruvilee arakkar koonai serrana sEvaganaar
maruviya periya kOyil madiL-tiru-arangam ennaa
karuvilE tiru ilaadeer kaalattaik kazhikkinreerE.
100.meaning: With a single bow He raised up and saved all the worlds surrounded by the swelling ocean; in battle our Champion destroyed the king of the demons (Ravana). Without saying 'the walled, sacred great temple of Tiruvarangam where He abides,' O you who lacked grace even in the womb, you are merely wasting away your time.

101.translit:
namanum murkalanum pEsa naragil ninraargaL kEtka
naragamE suvarkkam aagum naamangaL udaiya nambi
avanadu oor arangam ennaadu ayarttu veezhndu aLiya maandar
kavalaiyuL paduginraar enru adanukkE kavalginrEnE.
101.meaning: When Yama and Mrkandu's son debate, and those standing in hell listen, hell itself becomes heaven — such is our perfect Lord who possesses these holy names. Yet pitiable men, forgetting and falling without saying 'Arangam, His town,' sink into sorrow; and it is for this very reason that I grieve.

102.translit:
eriyuneer verikoL vElai maanilattu uyirgaL ellaam
verikoL poon-tuLava maalai viNNavar koonai Etta
arivilaa manidar ellaam arangam enru azhaippar aagil
poriyil vaazh naragam ellaam pul ezhundu ozhiyum anrE.
102.meaning: If all the witless men of this great earth — surrounded by the splashing, fragrant sea — would praise the King of the celestials who wears the fragrant flowering tulasi garland, calling out 'Arangam,' then all the hells where sinners dwell would, that very day, be overgrown with grass and undone (emptied).

103.translit:
vaNdinam muralum sOlai mayilinam aalum sOlai
koNdal meedu aNavum sOlai kuyilinam koovum sOlai
aNdar koon amarum sOlai ani-tiru-arangam ennaa
miNdar paayndu uNNum sOrrai vilakki naaykku idumineerE.
103.meaning: The grove where swarms of bees hum, the grove where flocks of peacocks dance, the grove that the rainclouds graze above, the grove where the koels call — the grove where the King of the celestials abides, the beautiful sacred Arangam. Without saying its name, you should rather snatch away and throw to the dogs the rice that arrogant men leap upon and devour.

104.translit:
meyyarkkE meyyan aagum vidhi-ilaa ennaip pOla
poyyarkkE poyyan aagum putkodi udaiya kOmaan
uyyap-pOm uNarvinaarkatku oruvan enru uNarnda pinnai
aiyappaadu aruttut tOnrum azhaganoor arangam anrE.
104.meaning: He is true to the truthful alone, and to the false — like graceless me — He is false; the Lord whose banner bears the bird (Garuda). Once one realizes 'He is the One' for those with the understanding to be saved, He appears, cutting away all doubt: that is the town of the Beautiful One, Arangam.

105.translit:
soodanaayk kaLvan aagit toorttarOdu isainda kaalam
maadaraar kayarkaN ennum valaiyuL pattu azhunduvEnai
pOdharE enru sollip pundiyil pugundu tanbaal
aadharam peruga vaitta azhaganoor arangam anrE.
105.meaning: When I was a gambler and a thief, consorting with debauchees, and was sinking caught in the net called the carp-like eyes of women — saying 'Come away!', He entered into my mind and made love for Him grow within me: that is the town of the Beautiful One, Arangam.

106.translit:
virumbi ninru Etta maattEn vidhiyilEn madhi onru illai
irumbu pOl valiya nenjam irai irai urugum vaNNam
surumbu amar sOlai soozhnda arangamaa kOyil koNda
karumbinaik kaNdu koNdEn kaNniNai kaLikkumaarE.
106.meaning: I am unable to stand and praise Him with longing; I am luckless, I have not a particle of wisdom. So that my heart, hard as iron, might melt little by little, I have beheld the Sugarcane (the sweet Lord) who has taken as His temple the great Arangam encircled by groves where the bees gather — beheld Him in a way that delights both my eyes.

107.translit:
ini-irait tivalai mOda eriyum-taN paravai meedE
tani kidandu arasu seyyum taamaraik kaNNan emmaan
kani irundu anaiya sevvaay kaNNanaik kaNda kaNgaL
pani arumbu udirumaalO en seygEn paaviyEnE.
107.meaning: Upon the cool, surging sea whose sweet waves dash and break their spray, our Lord, the lotus-eyed One, reclines alone and holds sway. My eyes that have beheld Kannan, whose red lips are like a ripe fruit, well up and shed cool tears — alas, what shall I do, sinner that I am?

108.translit:
kudadisai mudiyai vaittuk kuNadisai paadam neetti
vadadisai pinbu kaattit tendisai ilangai nOkki
kadal-niram kadavuL endai aravu-aNait tuyiluma kaNdu
udal enakku urugumaalO en seygEn ulagatteerE.
108.meaning: Setting His crown toward the west, stretching His feet toward the east, showing His back toward the north, and gazing toward Lanka in the south — my Father, the God of ocean hue, reclines upon the serpent-couch. Having seen this, my very body melts away — alas, what shall I do, O people of the world?

109.translit:
paayuneer arangan-tannuL paambaNaip paLLi koNda
maayanaar tiru-nan-maarbum maragada uruvum tOLum
tooya taamaraik kaNgaLum tuvar-idazh pavaLa vaayum
aaya seer mudiyum tEsum adiyarorkku agalalaamE.
109.meaning: In Arangam of flowing waters the wondrous Lord reclines upon His serpent-couch: His sacred good chest, His emerald-hued form and His shoulders, His pure lotus eyes, His coral mouth with red lips, His glorious crown and His radiance — can these ever be parted from His devotees?

110.translit:
paNivinaal manam adu onrip pavaLa-vaay arangan-aarkku
tuNivinaal vaazha maattaat tollai-nenjE nee sollaay
aniyanaar sembon aaya aru-varai anaiya kOyil
maNiyanaar kidanda aarrai manattinaal ninakkalaamE.
110.meaning: O ancient heart of mine, unable through resolve to live with your mind fixed in humility upon the coral-lipped Lord of Arangam — speak to me! The way in which the Lord, beautiful and like fine gold, the Sapphire-hued One, reclines in His temple that is like a precious mountain — can it ever be conceived by the mind?

111.translit:
pEsirrE pEsal allaal perumai onru uNara laagaadu
aasarraar tangatku allaal ariyalaa vaanum allan
maasarraar manattuLaanai vaNangi naam iruppadu allaal
pEsattaan aavadu uNdO pEdai-nenjE nee sollaay.
111.meaning: Apart from speaking what others have already spoken, His greatness cannot be fully grasped; nor can He be known except by those who are wholly free of flaw. Save for our standing and worshipping Him who dwells in the hearts of the stainless, is there anything one can really say of Him? O foolish heart, you tell me!

112.translit:
kangaiyil punitam aaya kaaviri naduvu paattu
pongu neer parandu paayum poom-pozhil arangan-tannuL
engaL maal iraivan eesan kidandadOr kidakkai kaNdum
enganam marandu vaazhgEn EzhaiyEn Ezhai-yEnE.
112.meaning: In Arangam set amid flowering groves, in the midst of the Kaveri that is more holy than the Ganga, where the swelling waters spread and flow — though I have seen the very posture in which our Tirumal, our Lord and Sovereign, reclines, how can I go on living forgetting Him? Wretched am I, wretched indeed!

113.translit:
veLLa-neer parandu paayum viri-pozhil arangan-tannuL
kaLLanaar kidanda aarum kamala nan-mugamum kaNdu
uLLamE valiyaip pOlum oruvan enru uNara maattaay
kaLLamE kaadal seydu un kaLLattE kazhikkinraayE.
113.meaning: In Arangam of spreading groves, where the flood-waters spread and flow, beholding the way the elusive Lord reclines and His fair lotus face — O my heart, you seem to be strong (stubborn), for you will not realize 'He is the One.' Loving only deceit, you are wasting your life away in your own falseness.

114.translit:
kuLittu moonru analai Ombum kuri-koL andaNamai tannai
oLittittEn en-kaN illai nin-kaNum pattan allEn
kaLippadu en koNdu nambee kadal-vaNNaa kadaruginrEn
aLittu enakku aruL sey kaNdaay arangamaa naguruLaanE.
114.meaning: The marked Brahminhood of bathing and tending the three sacred fires — that I have hidden away; it is not in me, and toward You too I am no true devotee. By what virtue could I rejoice, O perfect One, O ocean-hued Lord? I cry aloud. Take pity and grant me Your grace, O Lord who dwells in the great city of Arangam!

115.translit:
pOdellaam pOdu koNdu un ponnadi punaiya maattEn
teedilaa mozhigaL koNdu un tiruk-kuNam seppa maattEn
kaadalaal nenjam anbu kalandilEn adu-tannaalE
EdilEn arangarkku ellE en seyvaan tOnrinEnE.
115.meaning: At every hour I am unable to gather flowers and adorn Your golden feet; with faultless words I am unable to recite Your sacred virtues. Through love I have not mingled my heart in devotion — and because of that I am a stranger to the Lord of Arangam. Alas! For what purpose was I ever born?

116.translit:
kurangugaL malaiyait tookkak kuLittut taam puraNdittu Odi
taranga-neer adaikkal urra salam-ilaa aNilam pOlEn
marangaL pOl valiya nenjam vanjanEn nenju tannaal
arangan-aarkku aatsey yaadE aLiyattEn ayarkkinrEnE.
116.meaning: When the monkeys lifted up mountains, the guileless squirrels bathed and rolled themselves and ran to help dam the wave-tossed sea — but I am not even like them. With a heart hard as trees, deceiver that I am, through that very heart, doing no service to the Lord of Arangam, pitiable I am wasting away in stupor.

117.translit:
umbaraal ariyalaagaa oLiyuLaar aanaikkaagi
sem-pulaal uNdu vaazhum mudalai mEl seeri vandaar
nam-param aayadu uNdE naaygaLOm sirumai Oraa
em-piraarkku aatsey yaadE en seyvaan tOnrinEnE.
117.meaning: The Radiant One whom even the celestials cannot fully know came in fury against the crocodile — which lives feeding on red flesh — for the sake of the elephant (Gajendra). Is there anything that is our own (any merit of ours)? Dogs that we are, He regards not our lowliness. Doing no service to such our Lord, for what purpose was I ever born?

118.translit:
oorilEn kaaNi illai uravu marru oruvar illai
paaril nin paada moolam parrilEn parama moorti
kaaroLi vaNNanE en kaNNanE kadaruginrEn
aaruLar kaLai-kaN ammaa arangamaa naguruLaanE.
118.meaning: I have no town, I have no land, and no kinsman or anyone else at all; on this earth I have not even grasped the soles of Your feet, O Supreme Lord. O dark-radiant-hued One, O my Kannan, I cry aloud — who else is there as my refuge, O Lord, O You who dwell in the great city of Arangam?

119.translit:
manattil Or tooymai illai vaayil Or in-sol illai
sinattinaal serram nOkkit tee-viLi viLivan vaaLaa
punat-tuzhaay maalaiyaanE ponni soozh tiru-arangaa
enakku ini kadhi en sollaay ennai aaLudaiya kOvE.
119.meaning: There is not a trace of purity in my mind, nor a single sweet word on my lips; in anger I glare with hatred and shout cruel cries to no purpose. O You who wear the garland of wild tulasi, O Lord of sacred Arangam girded by the Kaveri, tell me — what refuge is there now for me, O my King who has taken me as Your own?

120.translit:
thavaththuLaar thammil allEn thanam pada thaaril allEn
uvarththa neer pOla venRan uRRavarkku onRum allEn
thuvarththa sevvaayinaarkkE thuvakkaRa thurisan aanEn
avaththamE piRavi thandhaay arangamaa nagaruLaanE
120.meaning: I am not among the austere ascetics, nor among those who give wealth in charity. Like brackish water, I have been of no use even to my own kin. I became one wholly devoted, without restraint, only to red-lipped women. O Lord who dwells in great Srirangam, you gave me this birth in vain.

121.translit:
aarththu vandu alambum sOlai ani thiru arangam thannuL
kaarth thiraL anaiya mEnik kaNNanE unnaik kaaNum
maarkkam onRu aRiya maattaa manisaril thurisan aaya
moorkkanEn vandhu ninREn moorkkanEn moorkkanEnE
121.meaning: O Krishna of dark hue like a mass of rain-clouds, dwelling in the beautiful holy Srirangam set amid groves where bees swarm and hum! Knowing no path at all to behold you, a wretch most depraved among men, I have come and stood here — a wretch, a wretch, a wretch am I.

122.translit:
meyyelaam pOga vittu viri kuzhalaaril pattu
poyyelaam podhindhu konda pOtkanEn vandhu ninREn
aiyanE arang+anE un aruL ennum aasai thannaal
poyyanEn vandhu ninREn poyyanEn poyyanEnE
122.meaning: Letting go of all that is true, ensnared by women with flowing tresses, packed full with every falsehood, this villain has come and stood here. O my master, O Lord of Srirangam, drawn by the longing for your grace, I the false one have come and stood — a false one, a false one am I.

123.translit:
uLLaththE uRaiyum maalai uLLuvaan uNarvu onRu illaa
kaLLaththEn naanum thondaay thondukkE kOlam poondEn
uLLuvaar uLLiRRu ellaam udan irundhu aRidhi enRu
veLgip pOy ennuLLE naan vilavaRa siriththittEnE
123.meaning: Having not even the faculty to contemplate Tirumal who dwells within the heart, I, a deceitful one, took on the guise of a devotee only for the sake of seeming devout. Knowing that you abide within and know everything in the minds of those who think, I grew ashamed and laughed within myself till my sides ached.

124.translit:
thaavi anRu ulagam ellaam thalai viLaak konda endhaay
sEviyEn unnai allaal sikkena sengaN maalE
aaviyE amudhE enRan aaruyir anaiya endhaay
paaviyEn unnai allaal paaviyEn paaviyEnE
124.meaning: O my Father who that day strode and took possession of all the worlds, holding them beneath your feet! Firmly I will worship none but you, O red-eyed Tirumal. O my life-breath, O nectar, O my Father dear as my own precious soul — sinner that I am, I will turn to none but you; a sinner, a sinner am I.

125.translit:
mazhaikku anRu varai mun Endhum maindhanE madhura aaRE
uzhaik kanRE pOla nOkkam udaiyavar valaiyuL pattu
uzhaikkinRERku ennai nOkkaadhu ozhivadhE unnai anRE
azhaikkinREn aadhi moorththi arangamaa nagaruLaanE
125.meaning: O youthful one who long ago lifted up the mountain against the rain, O sweet river of grace! Fallen into the snare of women whose glances are like those of a young fawn, as I toil and suffer, is it right that you should not look upon me? It is upon you alone that I call, O primordial Lord who dwells in great Srirangam.

126.translit:
theLivilaa kalangal neer soozh thiru arangam aththuL Ongum
oLiyuLaar thaamE anRE thandhaiyum thaayum aavaar
eLiyadhu Or aruLum anRE en thiRaththu em piraanaar
aLiyan nam paiyal ennaar ammavO kodiya vaaRE
126.meaning: Is it not the radiant One who rises within holy Srirangam, girt by turbid, clouded waters, who is himself my father and mother? Yet in my case my Lord shows no easy grace at all; he does not say 'this poor lad of ours deserves compassion.' Alas, how cruel a thing this is!

127.translit:
mEm poruL pOga vittu meymmaiyai miga uNarndhu
aam parisu aRindhu kondu aimpulan agaththu adakki
kaambu aRa thalai siraiththu un kadaiththalai irundhu vaazhum
sOmbarai ugaththi pOlum soozh punal arangaththaanE
127.meaning: Casting away possessions deemed great, deeply realizing the truth, comprehending the fitting way, restraining the five senses within, shaving the head clean to be rid of bondage — those quietists who dwell at your threshold and live so, it seems you delight in them, O Lord of Srirangam encircled by waters.

128.translit:
adimaiyil kudimai illaa ayal sadhuppEdhimaaril
kudimaiyil kadaimai patta kukkaril piRapparElum
mudiyinil thuLabam vaiththaay moy kazhaRku anbu seyyum
adiyarai ugaththi pOlum arangamaa nagaruLaanE
128.meaning: O you who wear the tulasi upon your crown! Even if they be born among the lowliest of low castes, lower than the four-Veda brahmins who lack the heritage of true service — yet if they are devotees who love your thronging anklet-adorned feet, it seems you delight in such servants, O Lord who dwells in great Srirangam.

129.translit:
thiru maRu maarva ninnai sindhaiyuL thigazha vaiththu
maruviya manaththar aagil maa nilaththu uyirgaL ellaam
veruvara konRu suttittu eettiya vinaiyarElum
aru vinaip payanadhu uyyaar arangamaa nagaruLaanE
129.meaning: O you who bear the auspicious mark (Srivatsa) upon your chest! Even those who have committed grievous deeds — slaying all the creatures of the wide earth in terror, burning and amassing such sins — if their minds become fixed in cleaving to you, set radiant within their thought, they will not suffer the fruit of those terrible deeds, O Lord who dwells in great Srirangam.

130.translit:
vaan uLaar aRiyalaagaa vaanavaa enpar aagil
thEn ulaam thuLaba maalai senniyaay enpar aagil
oonam aayinagaL seyyum oona kaaragargaLElum
pOnagam seydha sEdam tharuvarEl punidham anRE
130.meaning: If they declare 'O celestial One unknowable even to those dwelling in heaven!', if they declare 'O you who wear upon your head a garland of tulasi over which honey flows!' — even if they be flesh-eaters who do base and defiling deeds — if they offer the remnant of food they have eaten, is it not sacred?

131.translit:
pazhudhilaa ozhugalaaRRu pala sadhuppEdhimaargaL
izhi kulaththavargaLElum em adiyargaL aagil
thozhumin neer kodumin koLmin enRu ninnOdum okka
vazhipada aruLinaay pOnRum madhiL thiru arangaththaanE
131.meaning: You have granted that even the four-Veda brahmins of faultless conduct, and even those of low-born castes — if they are your devotees — should be worshipped on a par with you yourself, bidding 'worship them, give to them, receive from them.' So it seems, O Lord of holy Srirangam girt with ramparts.

132.translit:
amara Or angam aaRum vEdam Or naangum Odhi
thamargaLil thalaivar aaya saadhi andhaNargaLElum
numargaLai pazhippar aagil nodippadhu Or aLavil aangE
avargaL thaam pulaiyar pOlum arangamaa nagaruLaanE
132.meaning: Even if they be brahmins of high caste, chiefs among their own kindred, who chant the six auxiliary sciences and the four Vedas so as to abide steadfast — if they revile your devotees, then in that very instant, in the snap of a finger, they become as outcastes, O Lord who dwells in great Srirangam.

133.translit:
peN ulaam sadaiyinaanum biramanum unnaik kaaNbaan
eNNilaa oozhi oozhi thavam seydhaar veLgi niRpa
viN uLaar viyappa vandhu aanaikku anRu aruLai eendha
kaNNaRaa unnai ennO kaLai kaNaa karudhumaaRE
133.meaning: Even Shiva, on whose matted locks the woman (Ganga) wanders, and Brahma, performed austerities for countless aeons upon aeons to behold you, yet stood put to shame. Wonder of the heaven-dwellers, you came that day and granted your grace to the elephant (Gajendra). O ever-merciful one, how then shall I reckon you as my one refuge?

134.translit:
vaLavu ezhum thavaLa maada madhurai maa nagaram thannuL
kavaLa maal yaanai konRa kaNNanai aranga maalai
thuvaLa thondaaya thol seerth thondaradippodi sol
iLaiya pun kavidhaiyElum em piRaarkku iniya vaaRE
