134.meaning: This song of Thondaradippodi — who became a devotee melting in love through ancient glory — about Krishna, the Lord of Srirangam, who slew the great rutting elephant in the city of Madurai with its rising white mansions: though it be a youthful, humble verse, how sweet it is to my Lord!

135.translit:
kadhiravan kuNa disai sigaram vandhu aNaindhaan
 kana iruL aganRadhu kaalai am pozhudhaay
madhu virindhu ozhugina maa malar ellaam
 vaanavar arasargaL vandhu vandhu eendi
edhir disai niRaindhanar ivarodum pugundha
 irum kaLiRRu eettamum pidiyodu murasum
adhirdhalil alai kadal pOnRuLadhu engum
 arangaththu am maa paLLi ezhundhu aruLaayE
135.meaning: The sun has come and rested upon the peak in the eastern quarter; the dense darkness has fled and the lovely hour of dawn has come. All the great flowers brim with honey and overflow. The celestials and kings come thronging on and on, filling the opposite quarter; and with them have entered herds of mighty elephants with their cows, and drums whose thunder makes everywhere resound like the surging sea. O our Lord of Srirangam, be pleased to rise from your sacred couch!

136.translit:
kozhum kodi mullaiyin kozhu malar aNavi
 koorndhadhu kuNa disai maarudham idhuvO
ezhundhana malar aNaip paLLi koL annam
 een pani nanaindhana irum siRagu udhaRi
vizhungiya mudhalaiyin pilam purai pEzh vaay
 veL eyiRu uRa adhan vidaththanukku anungi
azhungiya aanaiyin arum thuyar keduththa
 arangaththu ammaa paLLi ezhundhu aruLaayE
136.meaning: Touching the lush blossoms of the rich jasmine creeper, the east wind has grown keen — is this not it? The swans that slept upon their flower-beds, drenched with sweet dew, have risen, shaking their great wings. O Lord who ended the grievous agony of the elephant (Gajendra) that suffered, anguished by the venom, when the gaping cavern-like maw and white tusks of the crocodile that swallowed it pressed upon it — O our Lord of Srirangam, be pleased to rise from your couch!

137.translit:
sudar oLi parandhana soozh disai ellaam
 thunniya thaaragai min oLi surungi
padar oLi pasuththanan pani madhi ivanO
 paay iruL aganRadhu paim pozhil kamugin
madal idai keeRi vaN paaLaigaL naaRa
 vaigaRai koorndhadhu maarudham idhuvO
adal oLi thigazh tharu thigiri am thadak kai
 arangaththu ammaa paLLi ezhundhu aruLaayE
137.meaning: Rays of light have spread through all the surrounding quarters; the sparkling brightness of the clustered stars has dwindled. The cool moon yonder has paled, its spreading light grown wan; the rushing darkness has fled. As the dawn breaks open and the rich spathes of the areca palms in the verdant grove split and shed their fragrance, the morning wind has grown keen — is this not it? O Lord whose broad, beautiful hand bears the discus blazing with conquering splendour — O our Lord of Srirangam, be pleased to rise from your couch!

138.translit:
mEttu iLa mEdhigaL thaLai vidu maayargaL
 vEyngkuzhal Osaiyum vidai maNik kuralum
eettiya isai disai parandhana vayaluL
 irundhina surumbinam ilangaiyar kulaththai
vaattiya vari silai vaanavar ERE
 maa muni vELviyai kaaththu ava birudham
aattiya adu thiRal ayOdhdhi em arasE
 arangaththu ammaa paLLi ezhundhu aruLaayE
138.meaning: The cowherds release their young buffaloes upon the heights; the sound of their bamboo flutes and the jingling bell-notes of the bulls — this gathered music spreads through all the quarters. In the fields the swarms of bees have settled. O bull among the celestials whose striped bow withered the race of the men of Lanka, O our king of Ayodhya, mighty in slaying, who guarded the great sage's sacrifice and performed the purifying ablution — O our Lord of Srirangam, be pleased to rise from your couch!

139.translit:
pulambina putkaLum poom pozhilgaLin vaay
 pOyiRRu kangul pugundhadhu pulari
kalandhadhu kuNa disai kanai kadal aravam
 kaLi vandu mizhaRRiya kalambagam punaindha
alangal am thodaiyal kondu adi iNai paNivaan
 amarargaL pugundhanar aadhalil ammaa
ilangaiyar kOn vazhipaadu sey kOyil
 em perumaan paLLi ezhundhu aruLaayE
139.meaning: The birds have begun to cry out in the flowering groves; the night has departed and dawn has entered. In the eastern quarter the roar of the resounding sea mingles. Bearing swaying, lovely garlands woven of varied blossoms over which joyful bees hum, the immortals have entered to bow at your twin feet. Therefore, O our Lord, in this temple where the king of Lanka (Vibhishana) offered worship, be pleased to rise from your couch!

140.translit:
iraviyar maNineduny theerodu mivaroo
 iRaiyavar pathinoru vidaiyaru mivaroo
maruviya mayilina naRumuka nivanoo
 marutharum vasukkaLum vandhuvan dheeNdi
puraviyoo daadalum paadalum theerum
 kumarathaN dampukun dheeNdiya veLLam
aruvarai yanaiyanin koyilmun nivaroo
 arangaththammaa paLLi yezhundharu Laayee.
140.meaning: Behold, here come the sun-gods with their great jewelled chariots; behold the eleven Lords (the Rudras) with their bulls; behold here the six-faced one (Murugan) seated upon his peacock; the Maruts and the Vasus throng and crowd together. With horses, with dancing and singing and chariots, the army of celestials presses in like a flood, gathering before your temple that rises like a great mountain. O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred rest!

141.translit:
antharath thamararkaL kooTTang kaLivaiyoo
 arundhava munivarum marutharu mivaroo
inthira naanaiyum thaanumvan thivanoo
 emperu maanun koyilin vaasal
sundharar nerukkavich chaatharar nookka
 iyakkarum mayanginar thiruvadi thozhuvaan
antharam paarida millaimaR Rithuvoo
 arangaththam maapaLLi yezhundharu Laayee.
141.meaning: Behold the assembled hosts of the immortals in the sky; behold the munis of rare penance and the Maruts; behold Indra coming with his elephant himself. At the gateway of your temple, O our Lord, the Sundharas press in and the Vidyadharas push, while the Yakshas grow bewildered, all longing to worship your sacred feet. There is no room left, neither in heaven nor on earth, for them all! O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred rest!

142.translit:
vampavizh vaanavar vaayuRai vazhanga
 maanidhi kapilaiyoN kaNNaadi mudhalaa
emperu maanpadi makkalam kaaNdaRku
 eeRpana vaayina koNdunan munivar
thumpuru naaradar pukundhana rivaroo
 thoonRina niraviyum thulangoLi parappi
ampara thalaththilnin RakalkinRa thiruLpoy
 arangaththam maapaLLi yezhundharu Laayee.
142.meaning: The fragrant celestials offer their salutations; with the great treasure-cow Kapila, with the bright mirror and all such things fit for your worship, the good munis come bearing the proper offerings to behold the adornment of you, our Lord. Behold here Tumburu and Narada have entered; the sun too has risen, spreading its radiant light, and the darkness departs and dissolves from the vault of the sky. O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred rest!

143.translit:
eedhamil thaNNumai yekkammath thaLiyee
 yaazhkuzhal muzhavamoo disaithisai kezhumi
geethangaL paadinar kinnarar kerudargaL
 kandharu varavar kangulu Lellaam
maadhavar vaanavar saaraNa riyakkar
 siththarum mayanginar thiruvadi thozhuvaan
aadhali lavarkkunaa Lolakka maruLa
 arangaththam maapaLLi yezhundharu Laayee.
143.meaning: The faultless drum, the ekkam and the mathaLi drums, the yazh-lute and the flute, the muzhavu drum, all blend their music in every direction. The Kinnaras, the Garudas and the Gandharvas have sung their songs all through the night. The great ascetics, the celestials, the Charanas, the Yakshas and the Siddhas grow bewildered, longing to worship your sacred feet. Therefore, to grant them your daily audience, O Lord of Srirangam, graciously rise from your sacred rest!

144.translit:
kadimalark kamalangaL malarndhana ivaiyoo
 kadhiravan kanaikadal muLaiththanan ivanoo
thudiyidai yaarsuri kuzhalpizhin dhuthaRith
 thukiludhuth theeRinar soozhpuna langaa
thodaiyoththa thuLavamum koodaiyum polindhu
 thoonRiya thoLthoNda radippodi yennum
adiyanai aLiyanen RaruLiyun nadiyaark
 kaaTpaduth thaaypaLLi ezhundharu Laayee
144.meaning: Behold the fragrant lotuses have blossomed open; behold the sun has risen from the roaring sea. The slender-waisted women, having wrung out and shaken dry their curling tresses and donned their garments, have climbed up from bathing, O Rangan girt by waters. With shoulders adorned by a garland of fitting tulasi and a basket, your servant called Thondaradippodi appears. Showing grace upon this servant, saying 'he is one to be pitied,' you made me serve your devotees. O Lord, graciously rise from your sacred rest!

145.translit:
amala naadhipiraa nadiyaark kennai yaaTpaduththa
vimalan viNNavar koonvirai yaarpozhil veengadavan
nimalan ninmalan needhivaanavan neeLmadhi Larangaththammaanthiruk
kamalapaadhamvan thenkaNNi nuLLana vokkinRadhee.
145.meaning: The spotless one, the first Lord, who made me a servant to his devotees; the pure one, king of the celestials, Lord of Venkatam with its fragrant groves; the stainless, the immaculate, the righteous celestial, the Lord of Srirangam with its lofty walls — his sacred lotus feet have come and now seem to dwell within my eyes.

146.translit:
uvandha vuLLaththanaa yulakamaLan thaNdamuRa
nivandha neeLmudiyan anRu neerndha nisaasararai
kavarndha vengaNaikkaakuththan kadiyaarpozhil arangaththammaanaraich
sivandha aadaiyin meelsenRa thaamen sindhanaiyee,
146.meaning: With a gladdened heart he measured out the worlds, his long crown rising up to the very limit of the cosmos; that Kakutstha (Rama) who with his fierce arrows swept away the night-wandering demons who once opposed him; the Lord of Srirangam with its fragrant groves — upon the reddened garment about his waist my thoughts have gone and rest.

147.translit:
manthi paayvada veengada maamalai vaanavarkaL
sandhi seyya ninRaa narangath tharavi naNaiyaan
andhi poolniRath thaadaiyu madhanmeel ayanaip padaiththa dhoorezhil
undhi meeladhan Rooadi yeenuLLath thinnuyiree.
147.meaning: He who stands on the great northern hill of Venkatam where monkeys leap, where the celestials come to offer worship; he who reclines upon the serpent at Srirangam — upon his garment, hued like the twilight, and upon the one matchless beautiful navel above it from which Brahma was created — there, is it not, rests the sweet life within the heart of me, his servant?

148.translit:
sadhuramaa madhiLsoozh zhilangaik kiRaivan thalaipaththu
udhira vooTTioor vengaNai yuyththava noodha vaNNan
madhuramaa vaNdu paada maamayi laadarangath thammaan thiruvayiR
Rudharaban dhanamen nuLLaththuLnin RulaaginRadhee
148.meaning: He who drove forth a single fierce arrow that made the ten heads of the king of Lanka, ringed by its fine four-square walls, fall and bleed; he the ocean-hued one; the Lord of Srirangam where sweet bees hum their songs and great peacocks dance — the sacred binding-band about his holy belly stands and moves about within my heart.

149.translit:
paaramaaya pazhavinai paRRaRuththu ennaiththan
vaaramaakki vaiththaan vaiththadhanRi yennuL pukundhaan
koora maadhavam seydhanankko laRiyee narangath thammaanthiru
vaara maarpadhan Rooadi yeenai yaaTkoNdadhee.
149.meaning: Cutting away root and all the heavy load of my old karma, he made me his own and kept me; and not stopping at that, he entered within me. I know not what severe great penance I must have performed — was it not the loving breast of the Lord of Srirangam that took me, his servant, into his service?

150.translit:
thuNda veNpiRai yaanthuyar theerththavan anjiRaiya
vaNduvaazh pozhilsoo zharanganagar meeyavappan
aNda raNdapaki raNdaththoru maanilam ezhumaalvarai muRRum
uNda kaNdangaN deeradi yeenai yuyyakkoNdadhee!
150.meaning: He who removed the sorrow of the one bearing the white crescent shard (Siva); the Father who dwells in the city of Srirangam ringed by groves where lovely-winged bees live. Behold that throat which swallowed the egg-shaped universes, the great single earth, the seven worlds and all the great mountains entire — see, it is that which took me, his servant, and saved me!

151.translit:
kaiyinaar surisanga nalaazhiyar neeLvaraipool
meyyanaar thuLapa viraiyaar kamazhneeL mudiyem
aiyanaar aNiyaranganaa raravina Naimisai meeyamaayanaar
seyyavaa yaiyoo ennaich chindhai kavarndhadhuvee.
151.meaning: He who holds in his hand the curved conch and the blazing discus; he whose body is like a great mountain; he our Lord with the long crown fragrant with tulasi; the Lord of beautiful Srirangam, the wondrous one who reclines upon the serpent-bed — alas, his red mouth has stolen away my mind!

152.translit:
pariya naaki vandha avuNa nudalkeeNda amararkku
ariya aadhipiraa narangath thamalan mukaththu
kariya vaakip pudaiparandhu miLirndhu sevvari yoodi neeNdavap
periya vaaya kaNka Lennaip peedhaimai seydhanavee.
152.meaning: He who tore apart the body of the demon that came in a vast form; the first Lord, hard for even the immortals to attain; the spotless one of Srirangam — upon his face those great long eyes, dark-hued, spreading wide at their corners, glistening, with red lines running through them — those eyes have made me lose my wits.

153.translit:
aalamaamaraththi nilaimee lorupaalakanaay
nyaalameezhu muNdaa narangath tharavi naNaiyaan
koolamaamaNi yaaramum muththuth thaamamum mudivilla dhoorezhil
neelameeni yaiyoo niRai koNdadhen nenjinaiyee.
153.meaning: He who, as a single infant upon the leaf of the great banyan tree, swallowed all the seven worlds; he who reclines upon the serpent at Srirangam — his lovely necklace of great gems and his garland of pearls, and that one endless, matchless beauty of his dark blue-hued body — alas, it has stolen away the composure of my heart!

154.translit:
koNdal vaNNanaik koovala naayveNNey
uNda vaayanen nuLLam kavarndhaanai
aNdar koonaNi yarangaNen namudhinaik
kaNda kaNgaLmaR RonRinaik kaaNaavee.
154.meaning: The cloud-hued one, who as a cowherd ate butter, the herdsman who stole away my heart; the king of the celestials, the Lord of beautiful Srirangam, who is my nectar — the eyes that have once beheld him will look upon nothing else!

155.translit:
palliyaavadhupaaRkadalarangam irangavanpeeymulai,
piLLaiyaayuyiruNdavendhai piraanavanperukumidam,
veLLiyaan kariyaan maNiniRavaNNanenReNNi, naadoRum
theLLiyaarvaNangummalai thiruveengadamadainenjamee.
155.meaning: His resting place is the ocean of milk and Srirangam; the Father, the Lord, who as a child took the life from the breast of the cruel demoness Putana — the place where he abounds, thinking of him as the white-hued, the dark-hued, the sapphire-hued one, the hill that the clear-minded worship day after day — reach that Tiruvenkatam, O my heart!

156.translit:
endhuNai yenReduththeeR kiRaiyeenu mirangiRRilaL,
thandhuNai yaayavenRan thanimaikku mirangiRRilaL,
vandhuNai vaanavarkkaay varanjeeRRarang kaththuRaiyum,
inthuNai vannodumpoo yezhilaali puguvarkkoloo.
156.meaning: When I cried out 'You are my support,' she showed not the least pity for me; she showed no pity even for my loneliness, who had been her own companion. Will she go off with that supporting Lord who, becoming the mighty refuge of the celestials, crushed the boons (of the demons) and dwells at Srirangam, and enter beautiful Tiruvaali?

157.translit:
undhi meelnaan mukanaip padaiththaan ula kuNdavan
endhai pemmaan, imaiyoorkaL thaadhaikkida menparaal,
sandhi noodu maNiyum kozhikkumpunal kaaviri,
andhi poolum niRaththaar vayalsoozhthen narangamee!
157.meaning: He who created the four-faced Brahma upon his navel, who swallowed the worlds, my Father, my Lord — they say the abode of him who is father to the celestials is southern Srirangam, encircled by fields watered by the Kaveri whose floods wash up sandalwood and gems, where the people are hued like the twilight!

158.translit:
vaiyamuN daalilai meevu maayanmaNi neeLmudi,
paikoL naagath thaNaiyaan payilumida menparaal,
thaiyal nallaar kuzhalmaa laiyummaRRavar thadamulai,
seyya saandhum kalandhizhi punalsoozhthen narangamee!
158.meaning: The wondrous one with the great long crown of gems, who swallowed the world and rested on the banyan leaf; he who reclines upon the hooded serpent — they say the place he frequents is southern Srirangam, encircled by waters in which flow down, all mingled, the garlands from the tresses of fair women and the red sandal-paste from their full breasts!

159.translit:
paNdiv vaiya maLappaan senRumaavali kaiyilneer
koNda aazhith thadakkaik kuRaLanida menparaal,
vaNdu paadum madhuvaar punalvandhizhi kaaviri
aNda naaRum pozhilsoozhndhu azhakaarthen narangamee.
159.meaning: He who, going of old to measure this world, took the water in the hand of Mahabali — they say the abode of that dwarf with the broad discus-bearing hands is beautiful southern Srirangam, encircled by groves whose fragrance reaches the heavens, by the Kaveri whose honeyed waters come flowing down where the bees sing!

160.translit:
viLaitta vempoor viRalvaaL arakkan nakar paazhpaTa,
vaLaitta valvil thaDakkai avanukku iDam enparaal,
thuLaikkai yaanai maruppum akilum koNarndu undi, mun
thiLaikkum selvap punal kaaviri soozh then arangamee,
160.meaning: They say the abode of Him whose great strong-handed bow was bent to lay waste the city of the valiant, sword-wielding demon (Ravana) who waged fierce, dreadful war, is beautiful southern Srirangam, encircled by the bountiful waters of the Kaveri, which carries down tusks of the hollow-trunked elephants and fragrant aghil wood, and surges joyfully in front.

161.translit:
vampulaam koondal maNDoodari kaadalan vaan puka,
ampu thannaal muninda azhakan iDam enparaal,
umpar koonum ulakeezhum vandeeNDi vaNangum, nal
sempon aarum madiL soozhndu azhakaar then arangamee.
161.meaning: They say the abode of the beautiful Lord who, in anger, with His arrow sent to the heavens the husband of fragrant-haired Mandodari (Ravana), is beautiful southern Srirangam, encircled by fine ramparts rich with red gold, where the king of the gods and all the seven worlds gather and bow down in worship.

162.translit:
kalai uDutta akalalkul vanpeey makaL thaay ena,
mulai koDuttaaL uyir uNDavan vaazhum iDam enparaal,
kulai eDutta kadalip pozhil ooDum vandu undi, mun
alai eDukkum punal kaaviri soozh then arangamee.
162.meaning: They say the abode where He dwells who drank the very life of the cruel demoness (Putana) who, dressed in fine garments over her broad hips, offered her breast pretending to be His mother, is beautiful southern Srirangam, encircled by the waters of the Kaveri, which flow down through groves of clustered, bunch-bearing plantains and raise up waves in front.

163.translit:
kanjan nenjum kaDumallarum sakaDamum kaalinaal,
thunja venRa suDar aazhiyaan vaazhum iDam enparaal,
manju seer maaLikai neeD akil pukaiyum, maRaiyoor
senjol veeLvip pukaiyum kamazhum then arangamee.
163.meaning: They say the abode where He dwells who bears the radiant discus, who triumphed in destroying the heart of Kamsa, the fierce wrestlers, and the cart (asura), is southern Srirangam, fragrant with the smoke of aghil incense from the cloud-kissing mansions and with the smoke of the sacrifices performed by the Vedic brahmins with their righteous words.

164.translit:
eena meen aamaiyoDu ariyum siRu kuRaLum aay,
thaanum aaya tharaNith thalaivan iDam enparaal,
vaanum maNNum niRaiyap pukundu eeNDi vaNangum, nal
theenum paalum kalanthu annavar seer then arangamee.
164.meaning: They say the abode of the Lord of the earth, who Himself became the boar, the fish, the tortoise, the man-lion, and the little dwarf, is beautiful southern Srirangam, where the people of heaven and earth crowd in and bow down in worship, and which is as sweet and excellent as honey mingled with milk.

165.translit:
seeyan enRum mikapperiyan nuN neermaiyinaay, im
maayaiyai aarum aRiyaa vakaiyaan iDam enparaal,
veeyin muttum maNiyum koNarndu aarppu punal kaaviri,
aaya ponmaa madiL soozhndu azhakaar then arangamee.
165.meaning: They say the abode of Him who is ever far beyond reach, exceedingly great, yet of the subtlest fineness, whose wondrous nature (maya) none can comprehend, is beautiful southern Srirangam, encircled by great golden ramparts, where the resounding waters of the Kaveri carry down pearls from the bamboo and gems.

166.translit:
alli maadar amarum thirumaarvan arangattai,
kallin mannu madiL mangaiyar koon kalikanRi sol,
nallisai maalaikaL naal iraNDum iraNDum uDan,
vallavar thaam ulaku aaNDu pin vaan ulaku aaLvaree.
166.meaning: Those who master these ten sweet-melodied garlands of verse (eight and two together), composed by Kalikanri, the king of the Mangai people with their stone-set ramparts, about Srirangam, the abode of the Lord on whose sacred chest dwells the Lady of the lotus (Lakshmi) - they shall rule this world, and thereafter rule the heavenly world.

167.translit:
veruvaadaaL vaay veruvi veengaDamee veengaDamee enginRaaLaal,
maruvaaLaal en kuDangaal vaaL neDungaN thuyil maRandaaL, vaNDaar koNDal
uruvaaLan vaanavar tham uyiraaLan oli thirai neerp pauvam koNDa
thiruvaaLan en makaLaic seydanakaL engnganam naan sindik keenee.
167.meaning: She who once knew no fear now babbles in fear, crying 'Venkatam! Venkatam!'; she no longer clings to my lap, and her long bright eyes have forgotten sleep. How shall I contemplate what He has done to my daughter - He of the form of the dark rain-cloud swarming with bees, the Lord who is the very life of the celestials, the Lord of Lakshmi who took the ocean of resounding waves as His abode?

168.translit:
kalaiyaaLaa akalalkul kana vaLaiyum kaiyaaLaa en sey keen naan,
vilaiyaaLaa aDiyeenai veeNDudiyoo veeNDaayoo? ennum, meyya
malaiyaaLan vaanavar tham talaiyaaLan maraamaram eezh eyda venRic
silaiyaaLan, en makaLaic seydanakaL engnganam naan sindik keenee.
168.meaning: She no longer keeps her garment upon her hips, nor the rich bangles upon her wrists - what shall I do? She keeps saying, 'Do You want this slave of Yours, who has no worth - do You want me, or do You not?' How shall I contemplate what He has done to my daughter - the Lord of the true mountain (Tirumala), the chief of the celestials, the wielder of the bow whose victorious arrow pierced through the seven maramara trees?

169.translit:
maan aaya men nookki vaa neDungaN neer malkum vaLaiyum soorum,
theen aaya naRum thuzhaay alangalin thiRam peesi uRangaaL kaaNmin,
kaan aayan kaDi manaiyil thayir uNDu ney paruka nandan peRRa
aan aayan, en makaLaic seydanakaL ammanaimeer aRiki leenee.
169.meaning: Behold, the gentle-glancing one with eyes like a doe - her long beautiful eyes brim with tears, her bangles slip off, and she sleeps not, speaking endlessly of the nature of His garland of fragrant, honey-laden tulasi. O mothers, I cannot fathom what He has done to my daughter - the cowherd of the forest, the cattle-herd born to Nanda, who ate curd and drank ghee in the well-guarded house.

170.translit:
thaay vaayil sol keeLaaL than naayattooDu aNaiyaaL thaDa men kongaiyee,
aarac saandu aNiyaaL, emperumaan thiruvarangam engee? ennum,
peey maaya mulai uNDu iv vulaku uNDa peru vayiRRan peesil nangaay,
maa maayan en makaLaic seydanakaL mangaimeer madikki leenee.
170.meaning: She heeds not her mother's words, joins not her band of companions, nor adorns her broad soft breasts with cooling sandal paste; she keeps asking, 'Where is the sacred Srirangam of my Lord?' O dear ones, if I am to speak of Him - the great-bellied One who drank the deceitful demoness's breast and swallowed this world - O women, I cannot reckon what the great wondrous Lord has done to my daughter.

171.translit:
pooN mulaimeel saandu aNiyaaL poru kayalkaN mai ezhudaaL poovai peeNaaL,
eeN aRiyaaL ettanaiyum emperumaan thiruvarangam engee ennum,
naaL malaraaL naayakanaay naam aRiya vaayppaaDi vaLarnda nambi,
aaN makanaay en makaLaic seydanakaL ammanaimeer aRiki leenee.
171.meaning: She adorns not her jewelled breasts with sandal paste, paints not her struggling carp-like eyes with collyrium, cares not for her myna bird, and knows no sense of shame at all; she keeps asking, 'Where is the sacred Srirangam of my Lord?' O mothers, I cannot fathom what He has done to my daughter, as a man - the Lord of the lady born of the fresh lotus (Lakshmi), the noble One who grew up in Ayarpadi (Gokula) as we all know.

172.translit:
thaadu aaDu vana maalai thaaraanoo enRu enRee thaLarndaaL kaaNmin,
yaadaanum onRu uraikkil emperumaan thiruvarangam ennum, poomeel
maadaaLan kuDam aaDi madusoodan mannarkkaay munnam senRa
thoodaaLan, en makaLaic seydanakaL engnganam naan solluk eenee.
172.meaning: Behold, she grows faint, crying again and again, 'Will He not give me His forest garland with pollen-dancing flowers?'; if anyone speaks of anything, she says only 'My Lord's sacred Srirangam.' How shall I tell what He has done to my daughter - the Lord of the lady on the lotus (Lakshmi), the dancer of the pot-dance, Madhusudana, who once went as a messenger for the kings (the Pandavas)?

173.translit:
vaaraaLum iLam kongai vaNNam veeRu aayinavaaRu eNNaaL, eNNil
peeraaLan peer allaal peesaaL ip peN peRReen en sey keen naan,
thaaraaLan thaN kuDandai nakar aaLan aivarkkaay amaril uytta theeraaLan,
en makaLaic seydanakaL engnganam naan seppuk eenee.
173.meaning: She heeds not how her young, bodice-bound breasts have changed their hue; if she speaks, she speaks nothing but the names of the Lord of names - having borne this girl, what shall I do? How shall I declare what He has done to my daughter - the Lord of the garland, the Lord who rules the city of cool Kudandai (Tirukkudandai), the charioteer who drove the chariot in battle for the five (Pandavas)?

174.translit:
uRavu aadum ilaL enRu enRu ozhiyaadu palar eesum alar aayiRRaal,
maRavaadee eppozhudum maayavanee maadavanee enginRaLaal,
piRavaada peeraaLan peNNaaLan maNNaaLan viNNoor thangaL
aRavaaLan, en makaLaic seydanakaL ammanaimeer aRiki leenee.
174.meaning: Saying again and again 'She has no regard for kin,' many people ceaselessly slander her, and it has become public scandal; for she, never forgetting, cries always 'O wondrous One! O Madhava!' O mothers, I cannot fathom what He has done to my daughter - the unborn Lord of names, the Lord of the Lady (Lakshmi), the Lord of the earth, the righteous Lord of the celestials.

175.translit:
pandooDu kazhal maruvaaL paingkiLiyum paal ooTTaaL paavai peeNaaL,
vandaanoo thiruvarangan vaaraanoo enRu enRee vaLaiyum soorum,
sandookan paulizhiyan ain thazhal oompu thaittiriyan saama veedi,
andoo van en makaLaic seydanakaL ammanaimeer aRiki leenee.
175.meaning: She busies not herself with ball and anklet-play, feeds not milk to her green parrot, cares not for her doll; saying again and again, 'Has the Lord of Srirangam come? Will He not come?', her bangles slip off. Alas, O mothers, I cannot fathom what He has done to my daughter - He who is the Chandoga, the Paushya, the Taittiriya who tends the five sacred fires, the chanter of the Sama Veda.

176.translit:
seel ukaLum vayal puDai soozh thiruvarangat ammaanaic sindai seyda,
neela malark kaN maDavaaL niRai azhivai thaay mozhinda adanai, neeraar
kaala veel parakaalan kalikanRi oli maalai kaRRu vallaar,
maalai seer veN kuDaikkeezh mannavaraay pon ulakil vaazhvar thaamee.
176.meaning: Those who learn and master this resounding garland of verses by Kalikanri - Parakala of the war-spear like the deadly Kala (Death) - which the mother spoke of the loss of modesty of the gentle maiden with eyes like blue lotuses, who set her heart on the Lord of Srirangam encircled by fields where carp leap - they shall live as kings beneath the garlanded white parasol and dwell in the golden world.

177.translit:
kaimmaana azhakaLiRRaik kaDal kiDanda karumaNiyai,
maimmaana marakatattai maRai uraitta thirumaalai,
emmaanai enakku enRum iniyaanaip pani kaatta
vammaanai, yaan kaNDadu thaNi neerth then arangattee.
177.meaning: The beautiful young elephant with its lovely trunk, the dark gem reclining on the ocean, the dark-hued emerald, Tirumal whom the Vedas proclaim, my Lord who is ever sweet to me, the great One who guarded the cattle from the cold (lifting Govardhana) - Him I have seen in southern Srirangam of the cool waters.

178.translit:
peeraanaik kuRungkuDi em perumaanai, thiru thaNkaal
ooraanaik karampanoor uttamanai, mut ilangu
kaar aar thiN kaDal eezhum malai eezh iv vulaku eezh uNDum,
araadu enRu irundaanaik kaNDadu then arangattee.
178.meaning: The Lord of Tirupper, our Lord of Tirukkurungudi, the Lord of the town of Tirutthankal, the Supreme One of Karambanur (Uttamarkoyil) - He who, having swallowed the seven dark mighty pearl-gleaming seas, the seven mountains, and these seven worlds, yet remained as though unsated - Him I have seen in southern Srirangam.

179.translit:
een aaki ulaku iDandanRu iru nilanum peru visumbum,
thaan aaya perumaanaith than aDiyaar manattu enRum
theen aaki amudu aakit thikazhndaanai, makizhndu oru kaal
aan aayan aananaik kaNDadu then arangattee.
179.meaning: Becoming the boar, He once raised up the earth; He who Himself became both the vast earth and the great sky; He who ever shines as honey and as nectar in the hearts of His devotees; He who once joyfully became Himself a cowherd tending the cattle - Him I have seen in southern Srirangam.

180.translit:
vaLarndhavanaith thadangkadaluL valiyuruvil thirisagadam,
taLarndhudhira udhaiththavanaith thariyaadhan'r iraNiyanaip
piLandhavanai, perunilamee radineettip paNdorunaaL
aLandhavanai, yaankaNda thaNineerth then narangaththE,
180.meaning: I have seen the Lord who grew vast within the wide ocean; who kicked the strong-bodied demon Sakata so that it fell apart and was destroyed; who could not bear and so split open Hiranyan; and who long ago stretched out His two feet and measured the great earth. I have seen Him here in cool-watered southern Srirangam.

181.translit:
neerazhalaay nedunilanaay nin'raanai, an'rarakkan
ooorazhalaal uNdaanaik kaNdaarpin kaaNaamE,
peerazhalaayp peruvisumbaayp pinma'raiyOr mandhiraththin,
aarazhalaal uNdaanaik kaNdadhu then narangaththE,
181.meaning: I have seen the Lord who stands as water and fire and as the long-stretched earth; who long ago consumed the city of the demon (Lanka) with fire so that those who saw it would see it no more; who is the great fire and the vast sky, and who is consumed (grasped) through the precious fire of the mantra of the seers of the Veda. I have seen Him here in southern Srirangam.

182.translit:
tanjinaththaith thavirththadaindhaar thavane'riyai, thariyaadhu
kanjanaikkon 'ran'rulagam uNdumizhndha kar'pagaththai,
venjinaththa kodundhozhilOn visaiyuruvai asaiviththa,
anji'raippud paaganaiyaan kaNdadhu then narangaththE,
182.meaning: He is the austere path for those who, casting off their fierce anger, take refuge in Him; the wish-granting tree who, unable to bear it, slew Kamsa, and who once swallowed and brought forth the worlds. He shook and overcame the powerful form of the cruel-deeded one of fierce wrath, and He rides the beautiful-winged bird (Garuda). I have seen Him here in southern Srirangam.

183.translit:
sindhanaiyaith thavane'riyaith thirumaalai, piriyaadhu
vandhenadhu manaththirundha vadamalaiyai, varivaNdaar
kondhaNaindha pozhilkOva lulagaLappaa nadinimirththa
andhaNanai, yaankaNda thaNineerth then narangaththE,
183.meaning: He is my thought, He is the austere path, He is Tirumal; the northern hill (Venkatam) who came and dwelt unceasingly within my heart; the pure one who at Tirukkovalur, with groves where striped bees cluster on blossoms, raised up His feet to measure the worlds. I have seen Him here in cool-watered southern Srirangam.

184.translit:
thuvariththa udaiyaarkkum thooymaiyillaach chamaNarkkum,
avarkadkang aruLillaa aruLaanai, thannadaindha
emarkadkum adiyErkum emmaa'rkkum emmanaikkum,
amararkkum piraanaaraik kaNdadhu then narangaththE.
184.meaning: He is the one who shows no grace to the ochre-robed ones (Buddhists) and to the impure Jains; yet He is the gracious Lord to my kinsmen who have taken refuge in Him, to me His servant, to my master, to my household, and to the immortals. I have seen this Lord here in southern Srirangam.

185.translit:
poyvaNNam manaththagar'rip pulanaindhum selavaiththu,
meyvaNNam ninaindhavarkku meynnin'ra viththaganai,
maivaNNam karumugilpOl thigazhvaNNa maradhagaththin,
avvaNNa vaNNanaiyaan kaNdadhu then narangaththE.
185.meaning: For those who drive away falsehood from the mind, restrain the five senses, and meditate on His true nature, He is the wise one who stands as Truth. I have seen Him, dark-hued like a black rain-cloud, shining with the color of emerald, the Lord of that very hue, here in southern Srirangam.

186.translit:
aamaruvi niraimeyththa aNiyarangath thammaanai,
kaamarusee rkkalikan'ri yolisaiydha malipugazhsEr
naamaruvu thamizhmaalai naaliraNdO diraNdinaiyum,
naamaruvi vallaarmEl saaraadhee vinaidhaamE.
186.meaning: Kaliyan of fine and lovely fame composed this garland of Tamil verses, eight and two (ten) in number, rich in renown and full of words, upon the Lord of beautiful Srirangam who tended the herds of cattle. For those who can master and recite them well, evil deeds will not draw near.

187.translit:
paNdainaal ma'raiyum vELviyum kELvip padhangaLum padhangaLin poruLum,
piNdamaay viriththa pi'rangoLi yanalum perugiya punalodu nilanum,
koNdalmaa rudhamum kuraikad lEzhum Ezhumaa malaigaLum visumbum,
aNdamum thaanaay nin'raveem perumaan arangamaa nagaramarn dhaanE.
187.meaning: The four ancient Vedas, the sacrifices, the sacred words heard from them and the meaning of those words; the bright-shining fire that He spread out as a mass, the abundant waters together with the earth; the clouds and the wind, the seven roaring oceans, the seven great mountains and the sky, and the cosmic egg itself - our Lord who stands as all these has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

188.translit:
indhiran piraman eesanen 'rivargaL eNNilpal guNangaLE yiyar'ra,
thandhaiyum thaaiyum makkaLum mikka su'r'ramum su'r'rinin 'ragalaap
pandhamum, pandha ma'ruppadhOr marundhum paanmaiyum palluyirk kellaam,
andhamum vaazhvu maayaveem perumaan arangamaa nagaramarn dhaanE.
188.meaning: He through whom Indra, Brahma, Shiva and these others act out their countless many qualities; who is the father and mother and children, and the great kindred and the bonds of relationship that surround and never leave one; who is also the medicine that severs such bondage; and who is the nature, the end and the life of all the many souls - our Lord has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

189.translit:
mannumaa nilanum malaigaLum kadalum vaanamum thaanava rulagum,
thunnumaa yiruLaayth thulangoLi surungith thollainaan ma'raigaLum ma'raiya,
pinnumvaanavarkkum munivarkkum nalgip pi'rangiruL ni'rangeda, orunaaL
annamaay an'rang karuma'rai payandhaan arangamaa nagaramarn dhaanE,
189.meaning: When the enduring great earth, the mountains, the ocean, the sky and the world of the asuras were enveloped in dense darkness, the shining light shrank, and the ancient four Vedas were hidden away - then, granting grace to the celestials and the sages so that the spreading darkness lost its hue, He one day took the form of a swan (Hamsa) and brought forth the precious Veda. He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

190.translit:
maayirung kunra mon'rumath thaaga maasuNa madhanodum aLavi,
paayirum pauvam pagaduviN dala'rap padudhirai visumbidaip padara,
sEyiru visumbum thingaLum sudarum dhEvarum thaamudan thisaippa,
aayiran thOLaa lalaikadal kadaindhaan arangamaa nagaramarn dhaanE.
190.meaning: Making one great dark mountain (Mandara) the churning rod, joining it with the serpent (Vasuki), so that the spreading vast ocean roared and burst, and the rising waves scattered into the sky; while the high vast heaven and the moon and the sun and the gods themselves were all bewildered - with His thousand arms He churned the surging ocean. He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

191.translit:
engngane yuyvar thaanavar ninaindhaal iraNiyan ilangubooN agalam,
pongkuveng kurudhi ponmalai piLandhu pozhidharu maruviyoth thizhiya,
vengaNvaa Leyi'r'rOr veLLimaa vilangal viNNu'rak kanalvizhith thezhundhadhu,
angngane yokka ariyuru vaanaan arangamaa nagaramarn dhaanE,
191.meaning: How could the asuras be saved, if one thinks of it? He split the chest of Hiranyan that bore shining ornaments, so that the surging hot blood poured down like a waterfall streaming from a golden mountain. Like a great silver mountain with fierce eyes and a sword-like fang, rising up with blazing fire-darting eyes reaching to the sky - just so He took the form of the man-lion (Narasimha). He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

192.translit:
aayiram kunram sen'rudhok kanaiya adalburai yezhilthigazh thiradOL,
aayirand thuNiya adalmazhup pa'r'ri ma'r'ravan agalvisum baNaiya,
aayiram peyaraal amarsen 'ri'rainja a'ridhuyi lalaikadal naduvE,
aayiram sudarvaa yaravaNaith thuyin'raan arangamaa nagaramarn dhaanE.
192.meaning: To sever the thousand rounded shoulders, gleaming with beauty and like a thousand hills gathered together in martial valor (of Kartavirya), He seized the powerful axe so that that one (Parasurama's foe) reached the wide sky. He is the one who, while a thousand gods came and worshipped Him by His thousand names, slept His mystic sleep in the midst of the surging ocean upon the thousand-bright-mouthed serpent (Adisesha). He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

193.translit:
surikuzhal kanivaayth thiruvinaip piriththa kodumaiyi'r kaduvisai yarakkan,
erivizhith thilangu maNimudi podisey thilangaipaazh paduppadha' rkeNNi,
varisilai vaLaiya adisaram thurandhu ma'rikadal ne'ripada, malaiyaal
arikulam paNikoN dalaikad ladaiththaan arangamaa nagaramarn dhaanE.
193.meaning: Resolving to lay waste to Lanka and to crush the jeweled crowns of the swift, violent demon (Ravana) who in his cruelty had separated Him from the curly-haired, sweet-lipped Lakshmi (Sita) - blazing with fiery eyes - He bent His striped bow and loosed sharp arrows, made the rolling sea give way, and with the help of mountains and taking the service of the host of monkeys, He dammed the surging ocean. He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

194.translit:
oozhiyaay Omath thuchchiyaay orukaal udaiyadhE roruvanaay ulagil
soozhimaal yaanaith thuyargeduth thilangai malangavan 'radusaran thurandhu
paazhiyaal mikka paarththanuk karuLip pagalava noLigeda, pagalE
aazhiyaa lan'rang kaazhiyai ma'raiththaan arangamaa nagaramarn dhaanE.
194.meaning: He who is the eon, who is the summit of the sacrifice, who became the one with the single (peerless) chariot - in the world He removed the suffering of the great rutting elephant (Gajendra); long ago He loosed destroying arrows so that Lanka was thrown into confusion; and showing grace to the mighty Arjuna of great strength, He hid the sun with His discus in broad daylight so that the sun's light was quenched. He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

195.translit:
pEyinaar mulaiyooN piLLaiyaay orukaal perunilam vizhungiyadhumizhndha
vaayanaay maalaay aalilai vaLarndhu maNimudi vaanavar thamakkuch
sEyanaay, adiyEr kaNiyanaay vandhen sindhaiyuL vendhuya ra'rukkum,
aayanaay an'ru kun'ramon 'reduththaan arangamaa nagaramarn dhaanE.
195.meaning: As a child He drank from the breast of the demoness (Putana); once with His mouth He swallowed the great earth and brought it forth again; as the great One He grew (lay) upon the banyan leaf; He is far beyond the jeweled-crowned celestials, yet near to me His servant, coming to dwell in my heart and cutting away my burning sorrow; as the cowherd (Krishna) He once lifted up a mountain (Govardhana). He has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam.

196.translit:
ponnumaa maNiyum muththamum sumandhu porudhirai maanadhi pudaisoozhndhu,
annamaa dulavum alaipunal soozhndha arangamaa nagaramarn dhaanai
mannumaa maada mangaiyar thalaivan maanavE rkaliyanvaa yoligaL
panniya panuval paaduvaar naaLum pazhavinai pa'r'ra'rup paarE.
196.meaning: Upon the Lord who has taken His abode in the great city of Srirangam, encircled by the great river bearing gold, great gems and pearls with its battling waves, surrounded by rippling waters where swans roam - Kaliyan, lord of Tirumangai with its enduring great mansions, bearing a great spear, has uttered these verses. Those who sing this elaborated poem will daily sever the hold of their old karma.

197.translit:
Ezhai Edhalan keezhmagan ennaa thirangi ma'r'rava' rkinnaruL surandhu
maazhai maanmada nOkkiyun thOzhi, umbi yembi yen 'rozhindhilai, ukandhu
thOzha neeyenak kingozhi en'ra so'rkaL vandhadi yEnmanath thirundhida,
aazhi vaNNanin nadiyiNai yadaindhEn aNipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanE.
197.meaning: Without thinking 'he is lowly, an outsider, a base man', You took pity and poured out Your sweet grace upon him (Guha); not stopping at calling him 'companion of my doe-eyed, tender-glancing one (Sita)', and 'younger brother to my brother', You gladly said 'You shall be my friend, stay here with me.' Since those words have come and dwelt in this servant's heart, I have reached Your twin feet, O Lord of Srirangam with its beautiful groves, O discus-hued one.

198.translit:
vaadha maamagan markkadam vilangu ma'r'rOr saadhiyen 'rozhindhilai, ukandhu
kaadhal aadharam kadalinum perugach cheydha thagavinuk killaikaim maa'renru
kOdhil vaaymaiyi naayodu mudanE uNban naan en'ra ONporuL enakkum
aadhal vENdumen 'radiyiNai yadaindhEn aNipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanE.
198.meaning: Not stopping to say 'he is the son of the Wind, a monkey, a beast of another kind', You gladly received him (Sugriva/Hanuman/the monkey host); and saying 'for the grace they showed in making their love and devotion swell greater than the ocean there is no recompense', and 'I will eat together even with the faultlessly truthful hunter-folk (the monkeys/Guha's people)' - desiring that this noble truth might be mine too, I have reached Your twin feet, O Lord of Srirangam with its beautiful groves.

199.translit:
kadikoL poompozhil kaamaru poygai vaigu thaamarai vaangiya vEzham,
mudiyum vaNNamOr muzhuvali mudhalai pa'r'ra ma'r'radhu ninsaraN ninaippa
kodiya vaayvilang kinnuyirmalangak koNdaseer'r'ramon 'ruNduLa dha'rindhu, un
adiya nEnumvan dhadiyiNai yadaindhEn aNipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanE,
199.meaning: When the elephant (Gajendra), reaching for a lotus in a lovely pond within fragrant flower-groves, was seized by a mighty full-strong crocodile so that it was about to perish, and it called upon You as its refuge - knowing that You have a certain wrath that makes the very life of cruel-mouthed beasts writhe in agony, I too, Your servant, have come and reached Your twin feet, O Lord of Srirangam with its beautiful groves.

20.translit:
kuRaL piramasaariyaay maavaliyaik kuRumbu adakki arasu vaangki
iRaippozhudil paadaaLam kalavirukkai koduttu ugandha emmaan kooyil
eRippu udaiya maNivaraimeel iLanjaayiRu ezhundhaaRpool aravu aNaiyin
siRappu udaiya paNangkaLmisaic chezhumaNigaL vittu eRikkum tiruvarangkamee
20.meaning: The temple of our Lord who, as a dwarf-Brahmacharin (Vamana), humbled the pride of Mahabali, seized his kingdom, and in a single instant bestowed the netherworld upon him as his abode, rejoicing in it. That is Tiruvarangam (Srirangam), where upon the gem-bright serpent-couch the radiant jewels flash forth from the splendid hoods, like the young sun rising over a glittering mountain of gems.

21.translit:
uram paRRi iraNiyanai ugir-nudhiyaal oLLiya maarvu uRaikka uunRic
chiram paRRi mudi idiyak kaN pidhungka vaay alaRat tezhittaan kooyil
uram peRRa malarkkamalam ulagu aLandha seevadi pool uyarndhu kaatta
varambu uRRa kadhirchchennel taaLsaaytthut talaivaNakkum taN arangkamee
21.meaning: The temple of Him who seized Hiranyan by the chest, pierced his bright bosom with the points of His claws, grasped his head so the crown shattered, his eyes burst forth, and his mouth shrieked, as He roared in fury (as Narasimha). That is the cool Arangam (Srirangam), where the strong-stalked lotus blossoms rise up resembling the sacred feet that measured the worlds, and where the ripe red paddy at the field's edge bends its stalks and bows its head.

22.translit:
deevu udaiya meenamaay aamaiyaay eenamari kuRaLum aagi
muu-uruvil iraamanaayk kaNNanaayk kaRkiyaay mudippaan kooyil
seevalodu pedai annam sengkamala malar eeRi uusal aadip
puu-aNaimeel tudhaindhu ezhu sempodi aadi viLaiyaadum punal arangkamee
22.meaning: The temple of the divine One who became the Fish, the Tortoise, the Boar, the Man-Lion, and the Dwarf, who took the three forms of Rama, who became Kannan (Krishna), and who as Kalki shall bring all to an end. That is the watery Arangam (Srirangam), where the cock-swan with its mate climbs the red lotus flower and swings upon it, then nestling on the flower-bed sport about, bathing in the rising red pollen.

23.translit:
seru aaLum puLLaaLan maNNaaLan seruchcheyum naandham ennum
oru vaaLan maRaiyaaLan oodaadha padaiyaaLan vizhukkai yaaLan
iravu aaLan pagalaaLan enaiyaaLan eezhu ulagap perum peeraaLan
tiruvaaLan inidhaagat tirukkaNgaL vaLarginRa tiruvarangkamee
23.meaning: He is the master of the war-waging bird (Garuda), the lord of the earth, the wielder of the unique sword called Nandaka that wages battle, the lord of the Vedas, the master of the army that never retreats, the giver of bounteous gifts. He is the lord of the night and of the day, my own lord, the great great lord of the seven worlds, the consort of Lakshmi (Tiru). That is Tiruvarangam (Srirangam), where He sweetly rests with His sacred eyes in repose.

24.translit:
kainnaagattu idar kadindha kanal aazhip padai udaiyaan karudhum kooyil
tennaadum vadanaadum tozhaninRa tiruvarangkat tiruppadhiyin meel
meynnaavan mey adiyaan vittusittan viritta tamizh uraikka vallaar
enjnjaanRum emperumaan iNaiyadikkeezh iNai piriyaadhu iruppar taamee
24.meaning: The temple that is dear to Him who wields the flaming discus, who removed the distress of the elephant (Gajendra) of the trunk-snake. Those who can recite the Tamil verses composed by Vishnuchittan (Periyalwar) — the truthful-tongued, the true devotee — upon this sacred shrine of Tiruvarangam (Srirangam), worshipped by the southern lands and the northern lands alike, shall forever dwell beneath the paired feet of our Lord, never parting from that union.

25.translit:
tuppudai yaarai adaiva dellaam soorvidatt tuttuNai yaava rendRee
oppilee naagilum ninnadaindheen aanaikku nee aruL seydha maiyaal
eyppu ennai vandhu naliyum poodhu angu eedhum naan unnai ninaikka maatteen
appoodhaikku ippoodhee solli vaitheen arangkat taravaNaip paLLi yaanee
25.meaning: One approaches the strong and capable only because, in times of weakness, they will be a help and support. Though I am without any worth, I have taken refuge in You, because of the grace You showed to the elephant (Gajendra). When exhaustion comes and afflicts me, at that time I shall not be able to think of You at all; therefore, for that hour I have told You now, O You who recline upon the serpent-couch at Arangam (Srirangam).

26.translit:
saamidattu ennaik kuRikkoL kaNdaay sangkodu chakkara meendhi naanee
naamadittu ennai aneega taNdam seyvadhaa niRpar naman tamargaL
poomidattu un tiRattu et tanaiyum pugaavaNNam niRpadhoor maayai vallai
aamidat tee unnaic cholli vaitheen arangkat taravaNaip paLLi yaanee
26.meaning: At the time of death, take heed of me, O You who hold the conch and the discus. The messengers of Yama stand ready to torment me with their tongues and inflict many punishments upon me. You are master of a wondrous power (maya) that can stand by and let none of them come near, in any way, in their place of departure. Therefore, while it is still possible, I have told You now, O You who recline upon the serpent-couch at Arangam (Srirangam).

27.translit:
ellaiyil vaasal kuRugac chendRaal eRRi naman tamar paRRum poodhu
nillumi nennum ubaaya millai neemiyum sangkamum eendhi naanee
sollalaam poodhee un naama mellaam sollineen ennaik kuRikkoNdu endRum
allal padaavaNNam kaakka veeNdum arangkat taravaNaip paLLi yaanee
27.meaning: When I draw near the gate at the boundary's end, when the messengers of Yama strike and seize me, there will be no means to make them say 'Halt!' — O You who hold the discus and the conch. So while I still can speak, I have spoken all Your names; take heed of me, and ever guard me so that I suffer no distress, O You who recline upon the serpent-couch at Arangam (Srirangam).

28.translit:
oRRai vidaiya num naan muganum unnai yaRiyaap perumai yoonee
muRRa ulagellaam neeyee yaagi muunRezhut taaya mudalva neeyaa
aRRadhu vaaNaaL ivaRkendRu eNNi anjcha naman tamar paRRa luRRa
aRRaikku nee ennaik kaakka veeNdum arangkat taravaNaip paLLi yaanee
28.meaning: O You of such greatness that even the one of the single bull (Shiva) and the four-faced one (Brahma) cannot know You — You who became all the worlds entirely, the primal One who is the three-lettered syllable (Om / AUM). On that day when the messengers of Yama, thinking 'this man's lifespan is ended,' come to seize me as I tremble — on that day You must guard me, O You who recline upon the serpent-couch at Arangam (Srirangam).

29.translit:
paiyara vinaNaip paaR kadaluL paLLi koLginRa parama muurtti
uyya ulagu padaikka veeNdi undhiyil tooRRi naay naan muganai
vaiya manisaraip poyyendRu eNNik kaala naiyum udanee padaitthaay
aiya ini ennaik kaakka veeNdum arangkat taravaNaip paLLi yaanee
29.meaning: O supreme Form who reposes upon the hooded serpent-couch within the Milk-Ocean! Desiring to create the worlds that they might flourish, You brought forth the four-faced one (Brahma) from Your navel. Deeming the people of the earth to be transient (false), You created Yama (Death) along with them. O Lord, now You must guard me, O You who recline upon the serpent-couch at Arangam (Srirangam).

30.translit:
taNNena villai naman tamargaL saalak kodumaigaL seyyaa niRpar
maNNodu neerum eriyum kaalum maRRum aagaasamu maagi ninRaay
eNNalaam poodhee un naama mellaam eNNineen ennaik kuRikkoNdu
endRum aNNalee nee ennaik kaakka veeNdum arangkat taravaNaip paLLi yaanee
30.meaning: The messengers of Yama know no mercy; they stand ready to inflict many cruelties. O You who stand as the earth, the water, the fire, the wind, and all else, even the sky (ether)! While I still could reckon, I reckoned all Your names; take heed of me. O Lord supreme, ever You must guard me, O You who recline upon the serpent-couch at Arangam (Srirangam).

31.translit:
senjsol maRaipporu Laagi ninRa deevargaL naayaga nee em maanee
enjca lilennu daiyin namudhee eezhula gumudai yaay en nappaa
vanjca vuruvin naman tamargaL valindhu nalindhu ennaip paRRum poodhu
anjcalai mendRu ennaik kaakka veeNdum arangkat taravaNaip paLLi yaanee
31.meaning: O You who stand as the meaning of the Vedas of beautiful words, the lord of the celestials, my Master! O my undiminishing sweet nectar, You who possess the seven worlds, my Father! When the deceitful-formed messengers of Yama forcibly oppress and seize me, You must say 'Fear not!' and guard me, O You who recline upon the serpent-couch at Arangam (Srirangam).

32.translit:
naan eedhum un maaya mondRa Riyeen naman tamar paRRi nalindhittu indha
uunee pugeeyendRu moodhum poodhu angu eedhum naan unnai ninaikka maatteen
vaaneey vaanavar tangka Liisaa madhuraip piRandha maamaaya nee en
aanaay nee ennaik kaakka veeNdum arangkat taravaNaip paLLi yaanee
32.meaning: I know nothing at all of Your maya; when the messengers of Yama seize me, afflict me, and strike me saying 'Enter this body!', then I shall not be able to think of You at all. O Lord of the celestials of the high heavens, O great wondrous One born in Madhura (Mathura), You who became my all — You must guard me, O You who recline upon the serpent-couch at Arangam (Srirangam).

33.translit:
kundRedutthu aaniRai kaattha aayaa koonirai meytthava neeyem maanee
andRu mudalindRu aRudhi yaaga aadhiyanj soodhi maRandha Riyeen
nandRum kodiya naman tamargaL nalindhu valindhu ennaip paRRum poodhu
andRangku nee ennaik kaakka veeNdum arangkat taravaNaip paLLi yaanee
33.meaning: O cowherd who lifted the mountain and guarded the herds of cows, O You who grazed the lordly cattle, my Master! From that day until this day, to the very end, I have never forgotten the primal radiant Light. When the exceedingly cruel messengers of Yama afflict me, oppress me and seize me, on that day there You must guard me, O You who recline upon the serpent-couch at Arangam (Srirangam).

34.translit:
maaya vanaimadhu soodhanan tannai maadhava naimaRai yoorga Leetthum
aayarga LeeRRinai achchudhanan tannai arangkat taravaNaip paLLi yaanai
veeyar pugazh villiput tuurman vittusit tan sonna maalai patthum
tuuya manatthana raagi vallaar tuumaNi vaNNanuk kaaLar taamee
34.meaning: The wondrous One, Madhusudana, Madhava, whom the men of the Vedas praise, the Bull of the cowherds, Achyuta, He who reclines upon the serpent-couch at Arangam (Srirangam) — those who, with pure minds, can master all ten of these garland-verses spoken by Vishnuchittan (Periyalwar), lord of Villiputtur praised by the Veyar clan, shall themselves become servants of the pure-gem-hued Lord.

35.translit:
taamugakkum tamkaiyil sangkamee poolaavoo
yaamugakku mengkaiyil sangkamu meendhizhaiyiir
teemugatthu naagaNaimeel seerum tiruvarangkar
aamugatthai nookkaaraal ammanee ammanee
35.meaning: O you maidens adorned with fine jewels! Is the conch in His own hand, which delights Him, anything like the bangles on our hands which we cherish? The Lord of Tiruvarangam (Srirangam) who rests upon the fierce-faced serpent-couch — He will not so much as look upon my face. O mother, O mother!

36.translit:
ezhiludaiya vammanaimiir ennarangkat tinnamudhar
kuzhalazhagar vaayazhagar kaNNazhagar koppoozhil
ezhukamalap puuvazhaga remmaanaar ennudaiya
kazhalvaLaiyait taamum kazhalvaLaiyee yaakkinaree
36.meaning: O beautiful mothers! The sweet nectar of my Arangam (Srirangam), beautiful of locks, beautiful of mouth, beautiful of eyes, beautiful with the lotus that rises from His navel — my Lord — He has made my slipping bangles to slip away, even as they are; my bangles He has caused to loosen and fall.

37.translit:
pongkoodham soozhndha buvaniyum viNNulagum
angkaadhunj sooraamee yaaLginRa vemperumaan
sengkoo ludaiya tiruvarangkac chelvanaar
engkool vaLaiyaa lidardheervo raagaadhee
37.meaning: Our Lord who rules over both the earth surrounded by the surging ocean and the world of the heavens, letting nothing there fail in the least — the wealthy Lord of Tiruvarangam (Srirangam) who wields the righteous scepter — will He not relieve the distress of my bangles? Surely He must.

38.translit:
machchaNi maada madhiLarangkar vaamananaar
pachchaip pasundheevar taampaNdu neereeRRa
pichchaik kuRaiyaagi yennudaiya peyvaLaimeel
ichchai yudaiyaree littheruvee poodhaaree
38.meaning: The Lord of Arangam (Srirangam) of the storeyed mansions and ramparts, Vamana, the fresh emerald-hued God — if the alms He once received as poured water (from Mahabali) fell short, and He has desire for my adorning bangles, will He not come along this very street?

39.translit:
pollaak kuRaLuruvaayp poRkaiyil neereeRRu
ellaa vulagu maLandhukoNda vemperumaan
nallaargaL vaazhum naLirarangka naagaNaiyaan
illaadhoom kaipporuLu meydhuvaa noTRuLanee
39.meaning: Our Lord who, taking the form of a small ungainly dwarf, received water poured into His golden hand, and measured out all the worlds — He of the serpent-couch in cool Arangam (Srirangam) where the good people dwell — He seems intent on obtaining even the few possessions in the hands of us who have nothing.

200.translit:
nañju soorvadhoor veñjina aravam veruvi vandhunin saraNenac charaNaa
neñjiR koNdunin nañchiRaip paRavaik kadaikka lamkoduth tharuLseydha thaRindhu
veñjo laaLargaL namanRamar kadiyar kodiya seyvana vuLa adhaR kadiyEn
añji vandhunin nadiyiNai yadaindhEn aNipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanE,
200.meaning: A fierce, venom-dripping serpent, terrified, came and took refuge in You crying 'You are my refuge,' and holding that in its heart, You gave protection to it through Your green-winged bird (Garuda) — knowing this. The fierce-tongued servants of Yama are cruel and do cruel deeds; fearing them, I, Your servant, have come and reached Your two feet, O Lord of beautiful, grove-girt Srirangam.

201.translit:
maaga maanilam muzhuvadhumvan diraiñjum malara digaNda maamaRai yaaLan,
thOgai maamayi lannava rinbam thuRRi laamai yilaththaving kozhindhu
bOgam neeyeydhip pinnumnam midaikkE pOdu vaay, enRa ponnaruL, enakkum
aaga vENdumen RadiyiNai yadaindhEn aNipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanE,
201.meaning: The great Vedic sage (Vasishtha?), whose lotus feet the vast sky and great earth come and worship — to him, whose wife is like a great peacock with a fine train, when he had not fully tasted the joys of life, You said the golden grace: 'Leave this place, enjoy pleasures, and afterwards come again to us.' Praying that such golden grace be granted to me too, I have come and reached Your two feet, O Lord of beautiful, grove-girt Srirangam.

202.translit:
mannu naanmaRai maamuni peRRa maindha naimadhi yaadhaveng kooRRan
thannai yañjinin saraNenac charaNaayth thagavil kaalanai yugamunin thozhiyaa
pinnai yenRumnin thiruvadi piriyaa vaNNa meNNiya pEraruL, enakkum
anna thaagumen RadiyiNai yadaindhEn aNipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanE,
202.meaning: When the fierce, heedless Death (for Markandeya) came against the son obtained by the great sage steadfast in the four Vedas, You — as that boy cried 'You are my refuge' — grew angry and destroyed the merciless Kala (god of death), then granted the great grace that he would never thereafter part from Your sacred feet. Praying that the same be mine too, I have come and reached Your two feet, O Lord of beautiful, grove-girt Srirangam.

203.translit:
Odhu vaaymaiyum uvaniyap piRappum unakku munthandha andhaNa noruvan,
kaadha lenmagan pugalidang kaaNEn, kaNdu needharu vaayenak kenRu,
kOdhil vaaymaiyi naanunai vENdiya kuRaimu diththavan siRuvanaik koduththaay,
aadha laalvandhun adiyiNai yadaindhEn aNipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanE,
203.meaning: A certain Brahmin, who had earlier given You both the recited Vedic truth and the existence of the world, came saying: 'I do not find a place of refuge for my beloved son; find him and give him to me.' You, fulfilling the request of him who besought You with flawless truthfulness, gave back his little son. Therefore I have come and reached Your two feet, O Lord of beautiful, grove-girt Srirangam.

204.translit:
vEdha vaaymozhi yandhaNa noruvan endhai ninsara Nennudai manaivi,
kaadhal makkaLaip payaththalum kaaNaaL kadiyadhOr theyvangkoN doLikkum, enRazhaippa
Edha laarmunnE yinnaru Lavarkkuc cheydhun makkaLmaR Rivarenru koduththaay,
aadha laalvandhun adiyiNai yadaindhEn aNipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanE,
204.meaning: A certain Brahmin of Vedic utterance cried out: 'My Father, You are my refuge! My wife, as soon as she bears our beloved children, does not see them — some cruel deity seizes and hides them away.' You, before his enemies (the scoffers), showed him sweet grace, and giving back the children said 'These are indeed your offspring.' Therefore I have come and reached Your two feet, O Lord of beautiful, grove-girt Srirangam.

205.translit:
thuLanggu neeNmudi arasarthang kurisil thoNdai mannavan thiNdiR aloruvaR ku
uLangko Lanbinoo dinnaruL surandhang kOdu naazhigai yEzhuda niruppa,
vaLangkoL mandhiram maRRavaR karuLic cheydha vaaRadi yEnaRindhu, ulagam
aLandha ponnadi yEyadain duyndhEn aNipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanE.
205.meaning: To the chief of crowned kings, the ruler of the Thondai land, the one of firm valour, You poured out sweet grace with heartfelt love, and abiding there for seven naazhigai of time, You graciously imparted to him the auspicious mantra — knowing this manner, I, Your servant, have reached and been saved by the very golden feet that measured the worlds, O Lord of beautiful, grove-girt Srirangam.

206.translit:
maadamaaLigai soozhthiru mangai mannan onnalar thangaLai vellum,
aadal maaval vankali kanRi aNipo zhilthiru varangaththam maanai,
needu tholpuga zhaazhival laanai endhai yainedu maalaini naindha,
paadal paththivai paadumin thoNdeer paada nummidaip paavamnil laavE.
206.meaning: Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar), king of Tirumangai girt with mansions and palaces, the mighty hero of battle-steeds who conquers his foes, sang these ten songs of devotion contemplating my Father Nedumal — the Lord of beautiful, grove-girt Srirangam, the wielder of the discus of enduring ancient fame. O devotees, sing these; as you sing, sin will not stay near you.

207.translit:
thaaraaLan thaNNaranga vaaLan poomEl thaniyaaLan muniyaaLa rEththaninRa
pEraaLan aayirambE rudaiya vaaLan pinnaikku maNavaaLan perumaikEtpeer,
paaraaLa raviraivaren RazhundhaiyERRa padaimanna rudalthuNiyap parimaa vuyththa
thEraaLan kOcchOzhan sErndha kOyil thirunaRaiyoor maNimaadam sErminkaLE.
207.meaning: Hear the greatness of Him: the one wearing the tulasi garland, the Lord of cool Srirangam, the matchless Lord on the lotus, the great One whom the sages stand praising, the Lord of a thousand names, the bridegroom of Nappinnai. To cut down the bodies of the warring kings who fought at Azhundai claiming 'It is they, it is we who shall rule the earth,' He drove the horse-chariot — the charioteer-king Kochchozhan. Resort, then, to the jewelled mansion of Tirunaraiyur, the temple He attained.

208.translit:
uranga laaliyan Ramannar maaLap
  paara thaththoru thEraivar kkaaycchenRu,
irangi yoorndhavar kkinnaruL seyyum empi raanai
  vambaar punal kaaviri,
aranga maaLiyen naaLiviN NaaLi
  aazhi soozhilang kaimalang kacchenRu,
saranga LaaNdathaN daamaraik kaNNanuk kanRi
  enmanam thaazhndhunil laadhE!
208.meaning: When the strong-armed kings perished, He went as the one chariot(eer) for the five (Pandavas) in the Bharata war, and out of compassion drove for them, showing sweet grace — my Lord, who rules Srirangam where the fresh waters of the fragrant Kaveri flow, who rules me, who rules the heavens, who went so that Lanka encircled by the ocean was thrown into turmoil, and reigned with His arrows — to that cool lotus-eyed One, and to none other, let not my heart bow down and abide!

209.translit:
tharanganeer pEsinum thaNmadhi kaayinum,
irangumO eththanai naaLirun theLginaaL
thurangamvaay keeNdugan thaanadhu thonmai oor
arangamE enpa dhivaLthanak kaasaiyE.
209.meaning: Though the wave-tossed sea may roar, though the cool moon may scorch, will she relent? For so many days she has sat pining. She says that Srirangam — the ancient town of Him who joyfully tore open the horse(-demon's) mouth — is the very object of this girl's longing.

210.translit:
punaivaLar poompozhi laar ponni soozharang kanagaruL
munaivanai, moovula kumpadaith tha mudhal moorththithannai,
sinaivaLar poompozhil soozh thiru maaliruñ soolaininRaan
kanaikazhal kaaNungo lOkayal kaNNiyem kaarigaiyE.
210.meaning: Will my fair beloved one, with carp-like eyes, behold the resounding anklets of Him — the Foremost One who dwells in the city of Srirangam encircled by the Ponni (Kaveri) and its adorned flowering groves, the Primal Form who created all the three worlds, who stands at Tirumalirunsolai surrounded by blossoming, branch-laden flower groves?

211.translit:
kaNNan manaththuLLE niRkavum, kaivaLaigaL
ennO kazhanRa? ivaiyenna maayangaL?
peNNaanOm peNmaiyOm niRka, avanmEya,
aNNal malaiyum arangamum paadOmE.
211.meaning: Even while Kannan stands within my heart, why have the bangles slipped from my hands? What illusions are these? We have become women — let our womanhood remain as it is — and we shall surely sing of the Lord's hill (Tirumalai) and of Srirangam, where He abides.

212.translit:
aNiyaar pozhilsoozh aranga nagarappaa,
thuNiyEn ininin aruLalla dhenakku,
maNiyE maNimaa NikkamE madhusoodhaa,
paNiyaa yenakkuy yumvagai, parañjOdhi.
212.meaning: O Father of Srirangam city girt with beautiful groves! I am resolved that for me there is nothing but Your grace. O Gem, O precious sapphire, O slayer of Madhu, O Supreme Light — tell me the way by which I may be saved.

213.translit:
immaiyai maRumai thannai emakkuvee daagi ninRa,
meymmaiyai virindha soolai viyandhiru varangam mEya,
semmaiyaik karumai thannaith thirumalai orumai yaanai,
thanmaiyai ninaivaa renRan thalaimisai mannu vaarE.
213.meaning: He who is this life and the life to come, who stands as our liberation, the Truth, who abides in wondrous Srirangam surrounded by spreading groves, the Crimson One, the Dark One, the Singular One of Tirumalai — those who contemplate His nature shall ever abide upon my head.

214.translit:
aaviyai yaranga maalai azhukkuram pecchil vaayaal,
thooymaiyil thoNda nEnnaan sollinEn thollai naamam,
paaviyEn pizhaththa vaaRen RañjinER kañja lenRu
kaavipOl vaNNar vandhen kaNNuLE thOnRinaarE.
214.meaning: With this filthy body and a saliva-stained mouth, I — an impure servant — uttered the ancient holy names of the Lord of Srirangam who is the very soul (of all). When I, a sinner, feared 'In what way have I transgressed?', He, of the hue of the kaavi-blossom, came saying 'Fear not!' and appeared before my very eyes.

215.translit:
irumbanan RuNda neerum pOdharum koLka, enRan
arumbi Ni paava mellaam aganRana ennai vittu,
surumbamar soolai soozhndha arangamaa kOyil koNda,
karumbinaik kaNdu koNden kaNNiNai kaLikku maaRE.
215.meaning: Even as the water once drunk and absorbed by red-hot iron comes forth again, take it as so: all my grievous afflictions and sins have departed and left me — when my two eyes beheld and held the Sugarcane (sweet Lord) who has taken His abode in the great temple of Srirangam, surrounded by groves where bees gather, and how they (my eyes) rejoice!

216.translit:
piNdiyaar maNdai Endhip piRarmanai thiridhan duNNum,
uNdiyaan saabam theerththa oruvanoor, ulaga mEththum
kaNdiyoor arangam meyyam kacchipEr mallai enRu
maNdinaar, uyyal allaal maRRaiyaark kuyya laamE?
216.meaning: He is the One who removed the curse of the begging eater (Shiva, who roamed others' houses eating, holding the asoka-leaf skull-bowl) — His towns, praised by the world, are Kandiyur, Arangam (Srirangam), Meyyam, Kachi (Kanchi), Per (Tiruppernagar), Mallai (Tirukkadalmallai). Except that those who eagerly throng to these are saved, can salvation come to any others?

217.translit:
pattudukkum ayarndhirangum paavai pENaaL
 paninedungkaN Neertthathumbap paLLi koLLaaL,
ettanaippO dhengudangkaal irukka killaaL
 emperumaan thiruvaranga mengE? ennum
mattuvikki maNivaNdu muralum koondhal
 madamaanai idhuseydhaar thammai, meyyE
kattuvicchi sol, ennac chonnaaL nangaay
 kadalvaNNa ridhuseydhaar kaappaa raarE?
217.meaning: She wraps herself in silk, then grows faint and pines; she does not heed her doll; cool tears brim in her long eyes and she takes no rest; not even for a moment can she sit still upon my lap; she keeps asking 'Where is my Lord's Srirangam?' — this fawn-like girl whose tresses are so honey-laden that the jewel-bees, choked with nectar, hum about them. O lady, when I said 'Soothsayer, tell me truly who has done this to her,' she said: 'He of the ocean hue has done this — and who then is there to protect her?'

218.translit:
neñjurugik kaNpanippa niRkum sOrum
 nedidhuyirkkum uNdaRiyaaL uRakkam pENaaL,
nañjaravil thuyilamarndha nambee ennum
 vambaarpoom vayalaali maindhaa ennum,
añjiRaiya putkodiyE yaadum paadum
 aNiyaranga maaduthumO thOzhee ennum,
eñjiRa kin keezhadangkaap peNNaip peRREn
 irunilaththu Orpazhipadaith thEnE paavamE.
218.meaning: Her heart melts, her eyes brim, she stands and grows limp, she sighs long, she neither eats nor knows it, she shuns sleep. She says 'O Nambi who rests upon the venom-serpent,' she says 'O youth of fresh-blossomed Vayalali,' she dances and sings 'O bird-bannered One of the beautiful wings,' she says 'Shall we go and bathe in lovely Srirangam, O friend?' I have borne a daughter who will not be contained beneath my wing — alas, I have earned a singular reproach in this wide world. Oh, what a sin!

219.translit:
muLaikkadhiraik kuRunggudiyuL mugilai moovaa
 moovulagum kadandhappaal mudhalaay ninRa,
aLappariya aaramu dhai arangam mEya
 andhaNanai andhaNardham sindhai yaanai,
viLakkoLiyai maragadhaththaith thiruththaN kaavil
 vehkaavil thirumaalaip paadak kEttu
vaLarththadhanaal payanpeRREn varuga enRu
 madakkiLiyaik kaikooppi vangi naaLE.
219.meaning: When I heard (the parrot) sing of Tirumal — the young ray of light at Kurunkudi, the rain-cloud, the never-aging One who stands as the Primal Cause beyond the three worlds, the immeasurable ambrosia, the Brahmin who abides at Srirangam, the One in the hearts of the Brahmins, the lamp's radiance, the emerald, the One at Tiruttankaa and at Vehka — (the mother) said 'I have gained the fruit of having raised you; come!' and, folding her hands to the tender parrot, she beckoned it.

220.translit:
kaarvaNNam thirumeeni kaNNum vaayum
 kaiththalamum adiyiNaiyum kamala vaNNam,
paarvaNNa madamangai piththar piththar
 panimalarmeel paavaikkup paavam seydheen,
eervaNNa enbeedhai enjsol keeLaaL
 emberumaan thiruvaranga mengee? ennum,
neervaNNan neermalaikkee pooveen ennum
 iduvandroo niRaiyazhindhaar niRku maaree?
220.meaning: His sacred body is the colour of the dark rain-cloud, while His eyes, mouth, palms and twin feet are the colour of the lotus. My innocent young daughter, lovely as the earth, has become utterly maddened with love for Him who is mad for the maiden seated on the cool flower (Lakshmi) — what sin have I committed! She heeds none of my words; 'Where is the Tiruvarangam of my Lord?' she asks, and 'I will go to Neermalai of the water-hued one,' she says. Is this not the way of those whose modesty has been undone?

221.translit:
mutraaraa vanamulaiyaaL paavai maayan
 moyyagalath thuLLiruppaaL ahdhum kaNdum
atraaL,than niRaiyazhindhaaL aavik kindraaL
 aNiyaranga maaduthumoo thoozhee ennum,
petreenvaaych sollaRaiyum peesak keeLaaL
 peerppaadith thaNkudandhai nagarum paadi,
porRaama raikkayamneer aadap poonaaL
 poruvatraa Lenmagal um ponnum ahdhee.
221.meaning: Though she sees that the maiden Lakshmi, of full youthful breasts, ever dwells in the broad fragrant chest of the wondrous Lord, my daughter has cut herself loose, her modesty undone, and she pines, sighing for breath. 'Shall we go and bathe in beautiful Arangam, my friend?' she says. Though I, her mother, plead with her, she will not heed a single word; singing the glory of His name and of cool Kudandhai city, she has set off to bathe in the golden-lotus pond. My peerless daughter — her wealth too is just that (the Lord).

222.translit:
naivaLamon draaraayaa nammai nookkaa
 naaNinaar pooliRaiyee nayangaL pinnum,
seyvaLavi lenmanamum kaNNu moodi
 emberumaan thiruvadikkeezh aNaiya, ippaal
kaivaLaiyum meegalaiyum kaaNeen kaNdeen
 kanamagarak kuzhaiyiraNdum naangu thooLum,
evvaLavuN demberumaan kooyil? endreeRku
 iduvandroo ezhilaali? endraar thaamee.
222.meaning: He played upon the sweet music of the naivaLam tune without seeking it out, glanced at me, and like one feeling shy, showed still more of his graces. As my mind and eyes ran and clung beneath the sacred feet of my Lord, on this side I could no longer find my bangles or my girdle (lost in love); but I beheld His two heavy crocodile-shaped earrings and His four shoulders. When I asked Him, 'How far off is my Lord's temple?', He Himself replied, 'Is this not the beautiful Aali?'

223.translit:
uLLoorum sindhainooy enakkee thandhen
 oLivaLaiyum maaniRamum koNdaa ringee,
theLLooru miLandhengin theeRal maandhich
 seelugaLum thiruvarangam nammoo rennak
kaLLoorum paindhuzhaay maalai yaanaik
 kanavidaththil yaankaaNban kaNda poodhu,
puLLoorum kaLvaanee pookeel, enban
 endraalu midhunamakkoor pulavi thaanee?
223.meaning: Giving me only this heart-pervading sickness of love, He took away here my bright bangles and my fine complexion. Saying that Tiruvarangam — where the carp leap, drinking the clear toddy of the young coconut — is our town, that Lord who wears the fresh basil garland dripping with nectar, I behold Him in my dreams; and when I see Him, I say, 'O thief who rides the bird (Garuda), do not go!' Yet even so, is this a quarrel worthy of us?

224.translit:
irukaiyilsang kivainillaa ellee paavam
 ilangkolineer perumbeLavam maNdi yuNda,
peruvayitra karumugilee yoppar vaNNam
 perundhavaththar arundhavaththu munivar soozha
orukaiyilsang korukaimar raazhi yeendhi
 ulaguNda peruvaaya ringee vandhu,en
porukayalkaN neerarumbap pulavi thandhu
 punalaranga moorendru pooyinaaree.
224.meaning: These bangles on my two hands will not stay — alas, what a misfortune! His hue resembles the great-bellied dark cloud that has gulped down the vast deep ocean of shining roaring waters. Surrounded by sages of great penance and rare austerity, bearing the conch in one hand and the discus in the other, that wide-mouthed One who swallowed the worlds came here; and having made tears well up in my fighting carp-like eyes and given me this sulking grief, He went off, saying 'Punal-Arangam (Srirangam) is my town.'

225.translit:
minnilangu thiruvuruvum periya thooLum
 karimunindha kaiththalamum kaNNum vaayum,
thannalarndha naRundhuzhaay malarin keezhee
 thaazhndhilangum magaramseer kuzhaiyum kaatti
ennalanum enniRaiyum enjsin thaiyum
 envaLaiyum koNdennai yaaLung koNdu,
ponnalarndha naRunjserundhip pozhili noodee
 punalaranga moorendru pooyinaaree.
225.meaning: Showing His sacred form shining like lightning, His great shoulders, His palm that destroyed the elephant, His eyes and mouth, and the crocodile-shaped earrings gleaming low beneath His fresh fragrant tulasi blossoms, He took away my well-being, my modesty, my mind and my bangles, and made me His servant. Then, through the grove of fragrant golden-blossomed serundhi trees, He went off, saying 'Punal-Arangam (Srirangam) is my town.'

226.translit:
aaraanum kaRpippaar naayagaree? naan avanaik
kaar aar thirumeeni kaaNum aLavum pooy
seer aar thiruveengadamee thirukkoova
looree madhiL kachchi ooragamee peeragamee
peeraa marudhu iRuththaan veLLaRaiyee vehkaavee
peer aali thaNkaal naRaiyoor thiruppuliyoor
aaraamam soozhndha arangam kaNamangai
226.meaning: Are there any masters who can instruct me? I myself, until I see Him of the cloud-dark sacred body, will keep going on — to glorious Tiruvengadam, to Tirukkovalur, to walled Kacchi, to Uragam, to Peragam, to Vellarai of Him who broke the marudu trees that would not move, to Vehka, to great Aali, to Thankal, to Naraiyur, to Tiruppuliyur, to Arangam (Srirangam) girt with groves, to Kanamangai.

227.translit:
annam thuyilum aNi neer vayal aali
ennudaiya in amudhai evvuL peru malaiyai
kanni madhiL soozh kaNamangaik kaRpagaththai
minnai iru sudarai veLLaRaiyuL kal aRaimeel
ponnai marakadhaththai putkuzhi em poor eetrai
mannum arangaththu em maa maNiyai vallavaazhp
227.meaning: My sweet nectar at Aali of lovely watered fields where swans sleep, the great mountain at Evvul, the wish-granting Karpaka-tree at Kanamangai girt with maiden walls, the lightning, the twin luminaries (sun and moon) upon the stone shrine within Vellarai, the gold and the emerald, my battling bull at Putkuzhi, my great gem in eternal Arangam (Srirangam), at Vallavaazh —

228.translit:
ondrum maRandhaRiyeen oodhaneer vaNNanainaan,
indru maRappanoo eezhaigaaL - andru
karuvarangath thutkidandhu kaithozhudheen kaNdeen
thiruvaranga meeyaan thisai.
228.meaning: I have never once forgotten Him of the colour of the surging ocean. O ignorant ones, would I forget Him today? Long ago, lying within the dark womb, I worshipped Him with folded hands; I have beheld the direction (the sacred presence) of the Lord who dwells in Tiruvarangam (Srirangam).

229.translit:
manaththuLLaan veengadaththaan maagadalaan, matrum
ninaippariya neeLarangath thuLLaan, - enaippalarum
deevaadhi deeva nenappaduvaan, munnorunaaL
maavaay piLandha magan.
229.meaning: He dwells within my mind, He dwells at Vengadam, He dwells in the great ocean, and He dwells too within the vast Arangam (Srirangam) that is hard to conceive. He, whom so very many proclaim as the God of gods, is the child who long ago split open the mouth of the horse-demon.

230.translit:
payindra tharangam thirukkootti, pannaaL
payindradhuvum veengadamee pannaaL, - payindra
thaNidhigazhum soolai yaNineer malaiyee
maNidhigazhum vaNthadakkai maal.
230.meaning: Tirumal of the generous broad hands, gleaming like a gem, has long frequented Arangam (Srirangam) and Tirukkotti for many days; for many days too He has frequented Vengadam; and He has frequented Neermalai of lovely waters where cool radiant groves shine.

231.translit:
thamaruLLam thanjsai thalaiyarangam thaNkaal,
thamaruLLum thaNboruppu veelai, - thamaruLLum
maamallai koaval madhitkudandhai yenbaree,
eevalla endhaik kidam.
231.meaning: The hearts of His devotees, Thanjai, Thalai-Arangam, Thankal, the cool hill (Vengadam) cherished by His devotees, the ocean, and also — as His devotees declare — Mamallai, Koval and walled Kudandhai: these they say are the abodes of my Father who is able to do all things.

232.translit:
pinnindru thaayirappak keeLaan, perumbaNaiththooL
munnindru thaanirappaaL moymmalaraaL - sol nindra
thooLnalandhaan neerillaath thoondral, avanaLandha
neeNilandhaan aththanaikkum neer.
232.meaning: Even when the Mother (Lakshmi) stands behind and pleads, He does not heed; yet she of the fragrant flower, of large bamboo-like shoulders, stands before Him and pleads herself. The praiseworthy shoulders of that peerless First One — and the vast earth which He measured out — are equal in worth to all of that (His glory).

233.translit:
paNdellaam veengadam paaRkadal vaikundham,
koNdang kuRaivaarkkuk kooyilpool, - vaNdu
vaLangkiLarum neeLsoolai vaNboong kadigai,
iLangkumaran Ran viN Nagar.
233.meaning: In olden times Vengadam, the Ocean of Milk, and Vaikuntha were like temples for those who took them up and dwelt there for refuge; but now the heaven (Vinnagar) of the youthful prince at fertile, flower-rich Kadigai — with its long groves where bees swell forth their abundance — is His abode.

234.translit:
viNNagaram vehkaa viridhiraineer veengadam,
maNNagaram maamaada veeLukkai, maNNagaththa
thenkudandhai theenaar thiruvarangam thenkootti,
thankudangai neereetraan thaazhvu.
234.meaning: Vinnagaram, Vehka, Vengadam of waters with spreading waves, the great-mansioned earthly city Veelukkai, southern Kudandhai of this earth, honey-rich Tiruvarangam (Srirangam), and southern Kotti: these are the resting-places (the condescension) of Him who received water into the hollow of His own hand (when He took the worlds from Mahabali).

235.translit:
thaNNan thuzhaayvaLai koLvadhu yaamizhap poom- naduvee
vaNNam thuzhaaviyoor vaada yulaavum- vaLvaayalagaal
puLnan thuzhaamee poruneerth thiruvarang kaa! aruLaay
eNNan thuzhaavu midaththuuLa voopaNdum innannavee?
235.meaning: His cool fragrant tulasi He keeps; my bangles are what I lose. In between, a lonely north wind blows about, stirring up my complexion (paleness). O Lord of Tiruvarangam (Srirangam) of battling waters — grant your grace, so that the birds with sharp-pointed beaks do not torment me! In the places where thought wanders and gropes, was there ever such a thing as this even in the past?

236.translit:
kangulum pagalum kaN thuyi laRiyaaL kaNNaneer kaigaLaal iRaikkum,
sangusak karanga Lendrukai kooppum thaamaraik kaN endru thaLarum,
enganee tharikkee nunnaividdu ennum irunilam kaidhuzhaa virukkum,
sengayal paayneerth thiruvarang kaththaay. ivaLthiRath thenjseykin Raayee?
236.meaning: Night and day she knows no sleep of the eyes; she bails out the tears with her hands. She folds her palms saying 'the conch and the discus!'; she grows faint saying 'the lotus eyes!'
