She says 'How shall I bear to live, leaving You?'; she lies groping the wide earth with her hand. O Lord of Tiruvarangam (Srirangam) where red carp leap in the waters — what are You doing to this girl?

237.translit:
enjseykin Raayen thaamaraik kaNNaa ennumkaN Neermalka irukkum,
enjseykee neRineerth thiruvarang kaththaay? ennumvev vuyirththuyirth thurugum
munseydha vinaiyee. mugappadaay ennum mugilvaNNaa. thaguvadhoo? ennum,
munjseydhivv ulagam uNdumizhandh thaLandhaay engoloo mudigindra thivatkee?
237.meaning: 'What are You doing, O my lotus-eyed One?' she says, and sits with her eyes brimming with tears. 'What shall I do, O Lord of Tiruvarangam (Srirangam) of streaming waters?' she says, and melts away, sighing hot breaths again and again. 'O karma I committed before, will you not show yourself plainly?' she says; 'O cloud-hued One, is this fitting?' she says. O You who once swallowed, spat out and measured this world — what end is going to come upon this girl?

238.translit:
vatkilaL iRaiyum maNivaNNaa! ennum vaanamee nookkummai yaakkum,
utkudai yasurar uyirellaa muNda oruvanee ennumuL Lurugum,
katkilee unnaik kaaNumaa raruLaay kaaguththaa! kaNNanee! ennum,
thitkodi madhiLsoozh thiruvarangaththaay! ivaLthiRath thenseydhid daayee?
238.meaning: She is not ashamed in the least; 'O sapphire-hued One!' she says, and gazes at the sky and grows bewildered. 'O peerless One who consumed the lives of all the terror-bearing demons!' she says, and melts within. 'O One invisible to the eye, grant that I may behold You! O Kakutstha (Rama)! O Kannan (Krishna)!' she says. O Lord of Tiruvarangam (Srirangam) ringed by walls with firm banners — what have You done to this girl?

239.translit:
iddakaal idda kaiyaLaay irukkum ezhundhulaay mayangumkai kooppum,
kattamee kaadhal endrumoorch sikkum kadalvaNNaa. kadiyaikaaN ennum,
vattavaay neemi valangaiyaa ennum vandhidaay endrendree mayangum,
sittanee sezhuneerth thiruvarang kaththaay ivaLthiRath thenjsindhith thaayee
239.meaning: She stays just as she is, her foot set down, her hand set down (motionless); then she rises and wanders and grows confused; she folds her palms. 'Love is sheer torment,' she says, and swoons; 'O ocean-hued One, You are cruel, behold!' she says. 'O One with the round-mouthed discus in Your right hand!' she says; 'Come!' she says again and again, and grows bewildered. O perfect, righteous Lord of Tiruvarangam (Srirangam) of abundant waters — what have You resolved concerning this girl?

240.translit:
sindhikkum thisaikkum theerumkai kooppum thiruvarang kaththuLLaay ennum
vandhikkum, aangee mazhaikkaNNeer malka vandhidaay enRenRee mayangum,
andhippoo thavuNan udalidan thaanee alaikadal kadaindhavaa ramudhee,
sandhiththun saraNam saarvadhee valiththa thaiyalai maiyalsey thaanee.
240.meaning: She broods, she grows bewildered, she recovers and folds her hands, crying 'O You who dwell in Srirangam!' She bows, and with rain-cloud tears welling in her eyes she pleads again and again, 'Come to me!', and so swoons. O Ambrosia who churned the surging ocean, who at the dusk hour tore open the body of the demon (Hiranya), You have driven this young woman mad — she who has resolved that to attain Your feet, having met You, is her only goal.

241.translit:
maiyalsey thennai manamkavarndhaanee ennum maa maayanee! ennum,
seyyavaay maNiyee ennum thaN punalsoozh thiruvarang kaththuLLaay! ennum,
veyyavaaL thaNdu sangusak karamvil eendhumviN Noormudhal ennum,
paikoLpaam paNaiyaay! ivaLthiRath tharuLaay paaviyeen seyyaRpaa ladhuvee.
241.meaning: She says, 'O You who maddened me and stole my heart!' She says, 'O great wondrous one!' She says, 'O gem with the red mouth!' She says, 'O You who dwell in Srirangam girt by cool waters!' She says, 'O foremost of the celestials, who bear the fierce sword, mace, conch, discus and bow!' O Lord who reclines on the hooded serpent's couch, show Your grace toward her — for what else is left for me, a sinner, to do?

242.translit:
paaladhun pangaL inbangaL padaiththaay! paRRilaar paRRanin Raanee,
kaalasak karaththaay! kadalidang koNda kadalvaNNaa kaNNaNee! ennum,
seelkoLthaN punalsoozh thiruvarang kaththaay. ennum en theerthhanee! ennum,
koolamaa mazhaikkaN panimalka irukkum ennudaik koomaLak kozhundhee!
242.meaning: She says, 'O You who created sorrows and joys in their measure! O You who stand as the refuge of those who have no other refuge! O wielder of the wheel of Time! O ocean-hued one who made the sea Your abode! O Kannan! O You of Srirangam girt by cool waters teeming with fish! O my purifying one!' So my tender, lovely sprout sits with chill tears welling in her beautiful rain-cloud eyes.

243.translit:
kozhundhuvaa navarkatku ennum kunReendhik koonirai kaaththavan! ennum,
azhundhozhum aavi analavev vuyirkkum anjana vaNNanee! ennum,
ezhundhumeel nookki yimaippilaL irukkum engngaanee nookkugeen? ennum,
sezhundhadam punalsoozh thiruvarang kaththaay. enseykeen enthiru makatkee?
243.meaning: She says, 'O crest-jewel of the celestials!' She says, 'O You who lifted the hill and guarded the herd of cows!' Her sinking, melting spirit breathes out hot sighs as she says, 'O collyrium-hued one!' She rises and gazes upward, unblinking, and asks, 'How shall I behold Him?' O Lord of Srirangam girt by abundant flooding waters — what shall I do for this dear daughter of mine?

244.translit:
enthiru makaLseer maarvanee! ennum ennudai yaaviyee. ennum,
ninthiru eyiRRaal idandhunee koNda nilamakaL keeLva nee! ennum,
anRuru veezhum thazhuvinee koNda aaymakaL anba nee! ennum,
thenthiru varangam kooilkoN daanee! theLikileen mudivivaL thanakkee!
244.meaning: She says, 'O You on whose chest my Lakshmi resides!' She says, 'O my very life!' She says, 'O consort of Earth-goddess, whom You raised up and bore upon Your sacred tusk!' She says, 'O beloved of the cowherd-girl (Nappinnai), whom You won that day by embracing the seven bulls!' O You who have taken the temple at lovely southern Srirangam as Your abode — I cannot make out what the end will be for her!

245.translit:
mudivivaL thanakkon RaRikileen ennum moovula kaaLiyee. ennum,
kadikamazh konRaich sadaiyanee. ennum naanmukak kadavuLee! ennum,
vadivudai vaanoor thalaiva nee!ennum vaNthiru varanganee! ennum,
adiyadai yaadhaaL poolivaL aNugi adaindhanaL mukilvaNNan adiyee!
245.meaning: She says, 'I cannot know any end for her (myself)!' She says, 'O Ruler of the three worlds!' She says, 'O You who are the one with matted locks bearing fragrant konrai blossoms (Shiva)!' She says, 'O four-faced god (Brahma)!' She says, 'O comely chief of the celestials!' She says, 'O bountiful Lord of Srirangam!' Like one who would never reach His feet, she has drawn near and attained the feet of the cloud-hued Lord!

246.translit:
mukilvaNNan adiyai adaindharuL soodi uyndhavan moypunal porunal,
thukilvaNNath thooneerch seerppanvaNpozhilsoozh vaNkuru koorchada koopan,
mukilvaNNan adimeel sonnasonmaalai aayirath thippaththum vallaar,
mukilvaNNa vaanath thimaiyavar soozha irupparpee rinbaveL Laththee.
246.meaning: Satakopan (Nammazhwar) of bountiful Kurukur — set amid rich groves on the banks of the abundant-watered Porunal river whose pure waters are bright as fine cloth — was saved by attaining the cloud-hued Lord's feet and wearing His grace as his crown. Those who can master these ten verses, of the thousand, of the word-garland sung upon the cloud-hued Lord's feet, shall abide in the flood of supreme bliss, surrounded by the unblinking celestials of the cloud-hued sky.

40.translit:
kaipporuLkaL munnamee kaikkoNDaar kaaviri neer
seypuraLa voodum tiruvarangach chelvanaar
epporuTkum ninRaarkkum eytaadu naanmaRaiyin
soRporuLaay ninRaaren meypporuLum koNDaaree
40.meaning: He took my hand-held possessions long ago; the lord of wealth of holy Srirangam, where the Kaveri's waters flow flooding the fields. He who stands for all things, who cannot be attained even by them, who stands as the very meaning of the words of the four Vedas — He has seized even my true self, my very being.

41.translit:
uNNaa duRangaa doli kaDalai ooDaRuttu
peNNaakkai yaappuNDu taamuRRa peedellaam
tiNNaar matiL soozh tiruvarangach chelvanaar
eNNaadee tammuDaiya nanmaigaLee eNNuvaree
41.meaning: Without eating, without sleeping, he cut across the roaring ocean (to build the bridge), and bound himself to recover the woman (Sita) — yet all the troubles he underwent for that, the lord of wealth of Srirangam, girt by strong firm ramparts, He does not count. He counts only the good deeds done by His devotees.

42.translit:
paasi toortuk kiDanda paarmagaTkup paNDorunaaL
maasu uDambil neervaara maanam ilaap panRiyaam
teesu uDaiya teevar tiruvarangach chelvanaar
peesi yiruppanagaL peerkkavum peeraavee
42.meaning: Long ago, for the Earth-goddess who lay covered over with moss, He became a boar with no sense of shame, water streaming down its dirt-covered body. The radiant Lord, the lord of wealth of holy Srirangam — the wondrous things people tell of Him can never be removed however much one tries to remove them.

43.translit:
kaNNaalang koodittuk kannitannaik kaippiDippaan
tiNNaarndu irunda sisupaalan teesazhindu
aNNaandu irukkavee yaangavaLaik kaippiDitta
peNNaaLan peeNumoor peerum arangamee
43.meaning: Sisupala, who sat firm and confident, having arranged the wedding to take the maiden's hand, was disgraced and left gazing up helplessly, while right there the Lord took her hand instead. The town that this master of the maiden (Rukmini) cherishes is named Arangam (Srirangam).

44.translit:
semmai uDaiya tiruvarangar taampaNitta
meymmaip peruvaarttai viTTuchittar keeTTiruppar
tammai ugappaaraith taamugappar ennum sol
tammiDaiyee poyyaanaal saadippaar aariniyee
44.meaning: The true and great word that the upright Lord of Srirangam Himself declared, Vishnuchittar (Periyazhwar) holds in his heart: that He loves those who love Him. If this word should prove false in His own case, who henceforth could ever establish any truth?

45.translit:
iruLiriyach chuDarmaNigaL imaikkum neRRi
inattutti aNi paNamaa yirangaL aarnda
aravarasap perunjoodi anantan ennum
aNiviLangu muyarveLLai aNaiyai meevi
tiruvarangap perunaguruL teNNeerp ponni
tiraikkaiyaal aDivaruDap paLLi koLLum
karumaNiyaik koomaLattaik kaNDu koNDuen
kaNNiNaigaL enRukoloo kaLikkum naaLee
45.meaning: Reclining upon the lovely lofty white serpent-couch — Anantan, the king of serpents, great radiance, his forehead set with glowing gems that scatter the darkness, his beautiful clustered hoods adorned with rows of patterned markings — within the great city of holy Srirangam, where the clear-watered Ponni (Kaveri) strokes His feet with its wave-hands, He takes His rest. Beholding that dark-gem-hued, tender beautiful Lord, when, oh when, will my pair of eyes rejoice?

46.translit:
vaayoor iiraingnyooRu tudangaL aarnda
vaLai uDambin azhalnaagam umizhnda sentee
viiyaada malarchchenni vidaanamee pool
meenmeelum mihavengum paranda tankeezh
kaayaampoo malarppiRangal anna maalaik
kaDi arangat taravaNaiyil paLLi koLLum
maayoonai maNattooNee paRRi ninRen
vaayaara enRukoloo vaazhttum naaLee
46.meaning: The fiery serpent, with a thousand mouths bearing ten-times-fifty hoods, with a coiled body, spews red flame; its undying flower-like hoods spread out high above on every side like a canopy. Beneath it, wearing a garland resembling a heap of kaya blossoms, the wondrous Lord rests upon the serpent-couch in fragrant Arangam (Srirangam). Clasping the marriage-pillar (of the sanctum), when, oh when, will I stand and praise Him to my heart's fill?

47.translit:
emmaaNbin ayan naangu naavinaalum
eDuttetti iirireNDu mukamung koNDu
emmaaDum ezhiRkaNgaL eTTinooDum
tozhudetti yinitiRainja ninRa semppon
ammaanRan malarkkamalak koppoozh toonRa
aNi arangat taravaNaiyil paLLi koLLum
ammaanRan aDiyiNaikkeezh alargaL iTTang
kaDiyavaroDu enRukoloo aNugum naaLee
47.meaning: Brahma of wondrous nature, with all his four tongues lifting up his praise, with his four faces, and with his eight beautiful eyes on every side, worships and bows down sweetly — he springs forth from the lotus navel of that pure-golden Lord, who rests upon the serpent-couch in beautiful Arangam (Srirangam). When, oh when, will I draw near, together with the close devotees, to scatter flowers beneath that Lord's two feet?

48.translit:
maavinaivaay piLanduganda maalai veelai
vaNNanai en kaNNanaivan kunRam eendi
aavinaiyanRu uyakkoNDa aayar eeRRai
amarargaLtan talaivanai antamizhin inbap
paavinai avvaDamozhiyaip paRRaRRaarkaL
payil arangat taravaNaiyil paLLi koLLum
koovinainaa vuRavazhutti enRan kaigaL
koymmalar tooy enRukoloo kooppum naaLee
48.meaning: He who joyfully split open the mouth of the horse-demon (Kesi), the Lord ocean-hued, my Kannan, who lifted the strong mountain (Govardhana), the bull among cowherds who that day saved the cattle, the chief of the immortals, who is the sweet poetry of refined Tamil and the northern tongue (Sanskrit) — He rests upon the serpent-couch in Arangam, frequented by those free of attachment. Pressing His name firm with my tongue, scattering fresh-plucked flowers with my hands, when, oh when, will I fold my palms to Him?

49.translit:
iNaiyillaa vinnisaiyaazh kezhumi yinbat
tumburuvum naaradanum iRainji yeetta
tuNiyillaat tonmaRainool toottirattaal
tonmalarkkaN ayan vaNangi yoovaadu eetta
maNimaaDa maaLigaigaL malgu selva
madiL arangat taravaNaiyil paLLi koLLum
maNivaNNan ammaanaik kaNDu koNDen
malarsenni enRukoloo vaNangum naaLee
49.meaning: Tumburu of peerless sweet melodious lute and Narada bow and praise Him; with hymns from the flawless ancient Vedic scriptures, Brahma seated on the ancient lotus worships and praises Him unceasingly — within walled Arangam (Srirangam), rich and abounding in jeweled mansions and palaces, the sapphire-hued Lord rests upon the serpent-couch. Beholding that Lord, when, oh when, will my flower-decked head bow before Him?

50.translit:
aLimalarmeel ayan aran indiranooDu eenai
amarargaLtam kuzhuvum rambaiyarum maRRum
teLimadiseer munivargaLtam kuzhuvum mundit
tisaitisaiyil malartoovich chenRu seerum
kaLimalarseer pozhil arangat turaga meeRik
kaNvaLarum kaDalvaNNar kamalak kaNNum
oLimadiseer tirumukamum kaNDu koNDen
uLLamiga enRukoloo urugum naaLee
50.meaning: Brahma on the bee-laden lotus, Shiva, Indra and the rest of the immortals with their hosts, the celestial maidens (Rambha and others), and the throngs of sages bright with clear wisdom — all hasten, strewing flowers from every direction, and gather there. In Srirangam, ringed by groves rich with honeyed blossoms, the ocean-hued Lord sleeps upon the serpent. Beholding His lotus eyes and His sacred face bright as the radiant moon, when, oh when, will my heart utterly melt?

51.translit:
maRandigazhu manamozhittu vanjam maaRRi
aimpulangaL aDakki iDarp paarat tunbam
tuRandu irumuppozhudeetti yellai yillaat
tonneRikkaN nilaininRa toNDar aana
aRamdigazhum manattavartam gatiyaip ponni
aNi arangat taravaNaiyil paLLi koLLum
niRamdigazhum maayoonaik kaNDen kaNgaL
neermalga enRukoloo niRkum naaLee
51.meaning: Casting off the wavering mind, ridding themselves of deceit, subduing the five senses, renouncing the heavy burden of sorrow, praising Him at the six divisions of the day, standing firm in the boundless ancient path — He is the refuge of such devotees of righteous heart. Surrounded by the Ponni (Kaveri), in beautiful Arangam He rests upon the serpent-couch. Beholding that radiant wondrous Lord, with my eyes brimming with tears, when, oh when, will I stand before Him?

52.translit:
koolaarnda neDunjaarngam koonaR sangam
kolaiyaazhi koDuntaNDu koRRa voLvaaL
kaalaarnda gatikkaruDan ennum venRik
kaDumpaRavai ivaiyanaittum puRanjoozh kaappa
seelaarnda neDungazhani soolai soozhnda
tiruvaranga taravaNaiyil paLLi koLLum
maaloonaik kaNDinbak kalavi yeydi
valvinaiyeen enRukoloo vaazhum naaLee
52.meaning: The long Sharnga bow fitted with arrows, the curved conch, the slaying discus, the fierce mace, the victorious bright sword, and the swift-moving fierce bird called Garuda of speedy flight — all these guard Him round about. In holy Srirangam, surrounded by long fields full of fish and by groves, He rests upon the serpent-couch. Beholding that great Lord and attaining the joyful union with Him, when, oh when, will I, of cruel deeds, truly live?

53.translit:
tooraada manakkaadal toNDar tangaL
kuzhaamkuzhumit tiruppugazhgaL palavum paaDi
aaraada manakkaLippooDu azhuda kaNNeer
mazhaisoora ninaindurugi yeetti naaLum
seeraarnda muzhavoosai paravai kaaTTum
tiruvaranga taravaNaiyil paLLi koLLum
pooraazhi yammaanaik kaNDu tuLLip
poodalattil enRukoloo puraLum naaLee
53.meaning: The devotees of insatiable heart-love, gathering in throngs, singing many sacred praises, with hearts of unappeasable joy weeping tears that pour like rain, melting in thought and praising Him daily — where the rich sound of the drums resembles the roar of the sea, in holy Srirangam He rests upon the serpent-couch. Beholding that Lord of the warring discus, leaping up, when, oh when, will I roll upon the ground (in ecstasy)?

54.translit:
vanperuvaa nakamuyya amarar uyya
maNNuyya maNNulagil manisar uyya
tunbamigu tuyaragala ayarvon RillaachchukamvaLara akamagizhun toNDar vaazha
anpoDuten tisainookkip paLLi koLLum
aNiyarangan tirumuRRattu aDiyaar tangaL
inbamigu perungkuzhuvu kaNDu yaanum
isainduDanee enRukoloo virukku naaLee
54.meaning: That the strong vast heaven may be saved, that the immortals may be saved, that the earth may be saved, that humans in the earthly world may be saved, that grievous sorrow may depart, that bliss without any languor may grow, and that the joyful devotees may flourish — with love He faces the southern direction and takes His rest, the beautiful Lord of Arangam. Beholding the great, blissful throng of devotees in His sacred temple-court, when, oh when, will I too join in harmony and abide with them?

55.translit:
tiDarviLangu karaipponni naDuvu paaTTut
tiruvaranga taravaNaiyil paLLi koLLum
kaDalviLangu karumeeni yammaan RannaikkaNNaarak kaNDugakkum kaadal tannaal
kuDaiviLangu viRaltaanaik koRRa voLvaaL
kooDalarkoon koDaikulasee karansoRcheyda
naDaiviLangu tamizhmaalai pattum vallaar
nalandigazhnaaraNan aDikkeezh naNNu vaaree
55.meaning: In the midst of the Ponni (Kaveri) with its bright sandbanked shores, in holy Srirangam He rests upon the serpent-couch — the ocean-bright, dark-bodied Lord. Out of the love that delights in beholding Him to the eyes' content, Kulasekara — king of Kudal (Madurai), of victorious sword and umbrella-shaded mighty army, the generous one — composed this. Those able in these ten verses of graceful Tamil garland will reach the feet of Narayana, who shines with goodness.

56.translit:
teeTTarum tiRal teeninait ten naranganait tiru maaduvaazh
vaaTTamilvana maalaimaarvanai vaazhttimaalkoLsin taiyaraay
aaTTameevi yalandazhaittayar veydummeyyaDi yaarkaLtam
iiTTam kaNDiDak kooDumeel adu kaaNumkaNpaya naavadee
56.meaning: The hard-to-attain honey of mighty power, the Lord of beautiful southern Srirangam, on whose chest the goddess Lakshmi dwells, adorned with a fresh unfading forest-garland — praising Him, with hearts seized by love, the true devotees who dance and call out and grow weary in loving languor: if it should be granted to behold their gathering, then to behold that is the true purpose of having eyes.

57.translit:
tooDulaamalar mangaitooLiNai tooyndadum suDar vaaLiyaal
neeDumaamaram seRRadum nirai meeyttum ivaiyee ninaindu
aaDippaaDi arangavoo enRazhaikkumtoNDar aDippoDi
aaDanaampeRil gangaineerkuDain daaDumveeTkaiyen naavadee
57.meaning: He who embraced the twin shoulders of the lady (Sita) wearing earring-like blossoms, who with His blazing arrow struck down the tall great trees (the seven sal trees), and who grazed the herds — meditating only on these deeds, dancing and singing, the devotees cry out 'O Aranga!'. If we could but bathe in the dust of their feet, of what account then is the longing to plunge and bathe in the waters of the Ganga?

58.translit:
eeRaDarttadum eenamaaynilam keeNDadummunni raamanaay
maaRaDarttadum maNNaLandadum sollippaaDivaN ponnippee
raaRupoolvarum kaNNaneerkoNDa rangankooyil tirumuRRam
seeRuseydoNDar seevaDicchezhunj seeRenjennik kaNivanee
58.meaning: Singing of how He subdued the bulls (for Nappinnai), how as the boar He raised up the earth, how as Rama of old He crushed His foes, and how He measured the world — with tears that come flowing like the broad Ponni river, the devotees make muddy the sacred temple-court of the Lord of Arangam. That rich mud from their reddened sacred feet I shall set as an ornament upon my own head.

59.translit:
tooyttadaN tayir veNNeypaaluDan uNDalum uDan
RaaychchikaNDu aarttatooLuDai yempiraan enna ranganukkaDi
yaarkaLaay naattazhumbezha naaraNaa venRa zhaittumeytazhum pattozhu
deettiinburum toNDarseevaDi eettivaazhttumen nenjamee
59.meaning: He who dipped and ate the cool curds, butter and milk together, and whom Yashoda, having seen, bound by His strong shoulders — my Lord, who is Aranga: O my heart, praise and bless the sacred feet of the devotees who, as His servants, calling out 'Narayana!' until their tongues grow calloused and worshipping until their bodies grow calloused, praise Him and attain bliss.

60.translit:
poychilaik kura lEtreruttami ruttupOrara veerttakOn
seychilaichchudar soozholiththiNNa maamadiL thenna ranganaam
meychilaik karu mEkamondrutham nenjilnindru thigazhappOy
meychilirppavar thammaiyEninain thenmanammeychi lirkkumE
60.meaning: He who broke the neck of the bull that bellowed as a false bow, the King who tore apart the warring Ravana, dwells as the Lord of southern Srirangam girt by bright firm great walls of glowing brilliance. When that dark cloud-hued one with the true bow rises shining within their hearts and they go forth with their bodies thrilling in ecstasy, my heart, thinking of those very devotees, thrills with rapture.

61.translit:
aadhiyandhama nandhamatpudham aanavaanavar thambiraan
paadhamaamalar soodumpatthiyi laadhapaaviga Luyndhida
theedhilnanneri kaattiyengum thirindhanganem maanukkE
kaadhalseydhoNdark keppirappilum kaadhalseyyumen nenjamE
61.meaning: He is the beginning, the end, the limitless, the wondrous one, the Lord of the celestials. So that sinners who lack the devotion to wear his great lotus-feet may be saved, he wanders everywhere showing the faultless good path. My heart will, in every birth, love the devotees who do loving service to our Lord of Srirangam.

62.translit:
kaarinampurai mEninalkadhir muthtaveNNagaich cheyyavaay
aaramaarva nanganennum arumperunjuda rondrinai
sErumnenjina raagichchErndhuka sindhizhindhakaN Neergalaal
vaaranirpavar thaaLiNaikkoru vaaramaagumen nenjamE
62.meaning: He whose body resembles a flock of dark rain-clouds, whose red mouth holds a bright white pearl-like smile, whose chest is adorned with garlands — he, the one rare great radiance called Ranganatha. My heart will become a devoted servant to the twin feet of those who, with hearts that reach and cling to him, stand pouring forth tears that flow down in melting love.

63.translit:
maalaiyutraka dalkidandhavan vaNdukiNduna rundhuzhaay
maalaiyutrava raipperundhiru maarvanaimalark kaNNanai
maalaiyutrezhun thadippaadiththi rindhanganem maanukkE
maalaiyutridum thoNdarvaazhvukku maalaiyutradhen nenjamE
63.meaning: He who lies upon the encircling ocean, who wears the fragrant tulasi garland bees burrow into, who bears great Lakshmi on his chest, the lotus-eyed one. For our Lord of Srirangam, the devotees rise up infatuated with love, singing and dancing at his feet and wandering about; and my heart has become enraptured for the life of those devotees who are utterly devoted to him.

64.translit:
moyththukkaNpani sOrameykaLsi lirppaEngi yiLaiththunindru
eyththukkumbidu nattamittezhun thaadippaadiyi rainjiyen
aththanachcha nanganukkadi yaargaLaagi avanukkE
piththaraamavar piththarallargaL matraiyaarmutrum piththarE
64.meaning: With cool tears welling and pouring from their eyes, their bodies thrilling, yearning, growing faint and weary, prostrating and dancing, rising up to dance and sing and worship — those who become servants of Ranganatha, my Father and dear one, and who are mad only for him: they are not the mad ones. All the others are wholly the mad ones.

65.translit:
allimaamalar mangainaadhan aranganmeyyadi yaargaLtham
ellaiyiladimai thiratthinil endrumEvu manaththanaam
kollikaavalan koodalnaayagan kOzhikkOnkula sEkaran
sollininthamizh maalaivallavar thoNdarthoNdarga LaavarE
65.meaning: The Lord of the Lady seated on the great lotus of fine petals, Ranganatha — Kulasekhara, guardian of Kolli, lord of Kudal, king of Kozhi, whose heart is ever set upon boundless servitude to that Lord's true devotees, sang this. Those who master this sweet Tamil garland of words shall become servants of his servants.

66.translit:
meyyil vaazhkkaiyai meyyenak koLLum
iv vaiyan thannodum kooduva dhillaiyaan
aiya nEarang kaaendra zhaikkindrEn
maiyal koNdozhin dhEnendran maalukkE
66.meaning: I will not join with this world that takes as real the life lodged in this body. "O my Master! O Lord of Srirangam!" — thus I call out. I have become utterly lovesick for my Lord.

67.translit:
noolin nEridai yaarthirath thEnirkum
nyaalan thannodum kooduva dhillaiyaan
aaliyaa azhaiyaa arangaa vendru
maale zhundhozhin dhEnendran maalukkE
67.meaning: I will not join with this world that stands devoted to women slender of waist as a thread. Crying out and calling "Ranganatha!" with surging fervor, I have been overcome with love for my Lord.

68.translit:
maara naarvari venjilaik kaatcheyyum
paari naarodum kooduva dhillaiyaan
aara maarva narangan ananthannal
naara Nannara kaandhagan piththanE
68.meaning: I will not join with the people of this earth who do service to the cruel striped bow of Kama. I am mad for the garland-chested Ranganatha, the infinite one, the good Narayana, the destroyer of hell.

69.translit:
uNdi yEyudai yEyugan thOdumim
maNda laththodum kooduva dhillaiyaan
aNda vaaNa naranganvan pEymulai
uNda vaayandra nunmaththan kaaNminE
69.meaning: I will not join with this earthly realm that runs about delighting only in food and clothing. Behold — I am one driven mad for the Lord of the celestial worlds, Ranganatha, the one whose mouth drank from the breast of the cruel demoness Putana.

70.translit:
theedhil nanneri nirkaal laadhusey
needhi yaarodum kooduva dhillaiyaan
aadhi aaya naranganan thaamaraip
pEdhai maamaNa vaaLandran piththanE
70.meaning: I will not join with those people of worldly justice who, instead of standing in the faultless good path, do otherwise. I am mad for the primal Krishna, Ranganatha, the great bridegroom of the maiden seated on the beautiful lotus.

71.translit:
emba ratharal laarodum koodalan
umbar vaazhvaiyon raaga karudhilan
thambi raanama rarkkuarang anagar
empi raanukke zhumaiyum piththanE
71.meaning: I will not join with those who are not devoted to my Lord; I do not reckon even the life of the heavens as worth anything. For my Lord of the city of Srirangam, the Master of the immortals, I am mad through all seven births.

72.translit:
eththi raththilum yaarodum koodumach
siththan thannaith thavirththanan sengaNmaal
aththa nEarang kaaendra zhaikkindrEn
piththa naayozhin dhEnempi raanukkE
72.meaning: I have cast off that heart of mine that would join with anyone in any manner whatsoever. "O red-eyed Tirumal! O my Father! O Lord of Srirangam!" — thus I call out. I have become mad for my Lord.

73.translit:
pEya rEyenak kiyaavarum yaanumOr
pEya nEyevark kumidhu pEsiyen
aaya nEarang kaaendra zhaikkindrEn
pEya naayozhin dhEnempi raanukkE
73.meaning: To me everyone is a madman, and I too am a madman to everyone — what use is there in speaking of this? "O Krishna! O Lord of Srirangam!" — thus I call out. I have become a madman for my Lord.

74.translit:
angai yaazhi yarang nadiyiNai thangu
sindhaith thanipperum piththanaay
kongar kOnkula sEkaran sonnasol
ingu vallavark kEdhamon rillaiyE
74.meaning: Kulasekhara, king of the Kongu land, having become the unique great madman whose mind rests upon the twin feet of Ranganatha who holds the discus in his beautiful hand, sang these words. For those here who can master them, there shall be no harm whatsoever.

75.translit:
dhEvaraiyu masuraraiyum thisaigaLaiyum padaiththavanE
yaavarumvan dhadivaNanga aranganagarth thuyindravanE
kaavirinal nadhipaayum kaNapuratthen karumaNiyE
Evariven silaivalavaa iraagavanE thaalElO
75.meaning: O you who created the gods, the asuras, and the directions! O you who recline in the city of Srirangam where all come and bow at your feet! O my dark gem of Kannapuram where the good river Kaveri flows! O master of the cruel arrow-laden bow, O Raghava — sleep, lullaby, taalelo!

76.translit:
aranganE! tharanganeerka langavandru kundrusoozh
marangadEya maanilamku lungamaasu Namsulaay
nerunganee kadaindhapOdhu nindrasoora renjeydhaar
kurangaiyaa Lugandhavendhai kooru thERa vERidhE
76.meaning: O Ranganatha! On that day when, with the waters and waves churning, with the surrounding hill and the trees wearing away, with the great earth shaking, and the serpent coiled around, you drew near and churned — what did the asuras who stood there do? O my Father who delighted in ruling over the monkeys, tell it so I may understand clearly.

77.translit:
koNdai koNda kOdhai meedhu thEn ulaavu nuni
uNdai koNdu araka Otti uL magizhndha naadhan oor,
naNdai uNdu naarai yO, vaaLai paaya, neelamE
aNdai koNdu keNdai mEyumu andhaN neerarangamE
77.meaning: The town of the Lord who, with great inner delight, drove away (the demon) and where bees hover over the honey-laden tresses gathered in a knot — that town is cool, watered Srirangam, where herons feed on crabs, scabbard-fish leap, and carp graze nestled close among the blue lilies.

78.translit:
veNdiraik karungadal sivandhuvEva, mun or naaL
thiNdiratch silaikkaivaaLi vittaveerar sErum oor,
eNa thisaik kaNangaLum irainjiaadu theerththa neer,
vaNduiraiththa sOlai vEli, mannuseer arangamE
78.meaning: On a former day the warrior who, with his firm-strong bow in hand, loosed an arrow so that the white-waved dark ocean turned red and burned — the town he abides in is the ever-glorious Srirangam, with sacred waters where the hosts of the eight directions come bowing to bathe, and fenced round by groves humming with bees.

79.translit:
sarangaLaith thurandhu vil vaLaiththu, ilangai mannavan
sirangaL paththu aruththu udhirththa selvar mannu pon-idam,
parandhupon nirandhu nundhi vandhu alaikkum vaar punal
arangam enbar naanmugaththu ayanpaNindha kOyilE
79.meaning: Bending the bow and driving forth arrows, the wealthy Lord cut off and felled the ten heads of the king of Lanka. His enduring golden abode, where the long stream of water spreads out, scatters gold, surges and comes washing the banks — they call it Srirangam, the temple that the four-faced Brahma worshipped.

80.translit:
poRRai uRRa muRRal yaanai poar edhirndhu vandhadhaip
paRRi uRRu maRRu adhan maruppu osiththa paagan uur,
chiRReyiRRu muRRal moongil moonRu thaNdar onRinar,
aRRa paRRar, suRRi vaazhum andhaN niir arangamE.
80.meaning: The town of the charioteer (Krishna) who, when the mighty mountain-like full-grown elephant came charging at Him in battle, seized it and broke off its tusk — this is the cool, beautiful, water-girt Srirangam, where dwell the ascetics of pure life, bearing the triple staff of mature small-jointed bamboo, who have renounced all other attachments and live gathered around the Lord.

81.translit:
moadiyoadi lachchaiyaaya saabameydhi mukkaNaan
kooduseenai makkaLoadu koNdumaNdi venjamath
thOdavaaNa naayiram karangazhiththa vaadhimaal
peedukoayil kooduniira rangamenRa pEradhE.
81.meaning: When the three-eyed Shiva, attended by Durga, came rushing in with his hosts and host of beings into the fierce battle and was overcome, the primordial Tirumal who severed the thousand arms of Banasura — His glorious temple is the place renowned as Srirangam, encircled by gathering waters.

82.translit:
ilaiththalaicha randhurandhi langaikatta zhiththavan
malaiththalaippi RandhizhindhuRu vandhunun, du sandhanam
kulaiththalaiththi RuththeRindha kungumakku zhambinOdu
alaiththozhugu kaaviriya rangameeya vaNNalE.
82.meaning: He who let fly the leaf-bladed arrows and destroyed the fortress of Lanka — that gracious Lord abides in Srirangam, where the Kaveri, born and descending from the mountain-tops, comes rushing down, uprooting and dashing the sandalwood trees and bearing along their fragrant paste mingled with churned-up saffron, surging in waves.

83.translit:
mannumaama larkkizhaththi vaiyamangai maindhanaay
pinnumaayar pinnaithoaLma NampuNarndha thanRiyum
unnapaadha mennachindhai mannavaiththu nalkinaay
ponnisoo zharangameeya puNdarika nallaiyE.
83.meaning: You became the beloved consort of Lakshmi who dwells ever on the great lotus, and of the Lady Earth; and besides wedding the shoulders of Nappinnai, the cowherds' maiden, You have graciously set Your feet firmly within my heart and blessed me. Are You not the lotus-eyed Lord who abides in Srirangam encircled by the Kaveri (Ponni)?

84.translit:
surumbarangu thaNduzhaaydhu thaindhalarndha paadhamE
virumbininRi RainjuvERki rangaranga vaaNanE
karumbirundha kattiyEka dalkidandha kaNNanE
irumbaranga venjaramdhu randhavilli raamanE.
84.meaning: O Lord who reign in Srirangam, show grace to me who stand worshipping and longing for Your feet, where the cool tulasi garland, humming with bees, lies scattered and blooming! O Kannan who reclined upon the ocean, sweet as a solid block of sugarcane! O Rama, the archer who let fly fierce arrows that pierced even iron!

85.translit:
ponnisoozha rangameeya poovaivaNNamaayakEL
ennathaavi yennum valvinaiyinuLko zhundhezhundhu
unnapaadha mennaninRa voNsudarkko zhumalar
mannavandhu pooNduvaatta uminRuyengum ninRadhE.
85.meaning: Listen, O Lord of the hue of the kaaya-flower, who abide in Srirangam girt by the Ponni (Kaveri)! From within the cruel karma called 'my soul,' a tender shoot has sprung up — saying 'these are Your feet,' it came and clung as a luxuriant blossom to that bright effulgence which stands as Your feet, and now, never wilting, it has spread itself everywhere.

86.translit:
paaliR kidandhadhuvum paNdaragam meeyadhuvum,
aaliR Ruyin RadhuvuRum aaraRivaar, - njaalath	horuporuLai vaanavartham meypporuLai, appil
aruporuLai yaanaRindha vaaRu?
86.meaning: His reclining upon the Milk-ocean, His abiding in Naimisharanya of old, His sleeping upon the banyan leaf — who can fully know these? How is it that I have come to know Him — the one Reality of this world, the true Substance of the celestials, the subtle Essence resting upon the waters?

87.translit:
avanennai yaaLi arangaththu, arangil
avanennai eydhaamal kaappaan, avanenna
thuLLaththu ninRaa nirundhaan kidakkumE,
veLLath thar avaNaiyin mEl.
87.meaning: He is the one who rules me, dwelling in Srirangam; there in Srirangam He guards me from harm reaching me. He who stood, sat, and reclines within my heart — does He not lie reclining upon the serpent-couch in the midst of the ocean of waters?

88.translit:
naagath thaNaikkudandhai vehkaa thiruvevvuL,
naagath thaNaiyarangam pEranbil, - naagath
thaNaippaaR kadalkidakku maadhi nedumaal,
aNaippaar karuth thanaa vaan.
88.meaning: He who reclines on the serpent-couch at Kumbakonam, at Tiruvehka, at Tiruvevvul, at serpent-couched Srirangam, at Tirupper and Tiruvanbil — the ancient, exalted Tirumal who lies upon the serpent-couch of the Milk-ocean — He becomes the very thought (and refuge) of those who embrace and cling to Him.

89.translit:
aatpaarth thuzhidharuvaay kaNdukoL enRu, nin
thaatpaarth thuzhidharuvEn thanmaiya - kEtpaark
karumporuLaay ninRa aranganE, unnai
virumbuvadhE viLLEn manam.
89.meaning: You go about seeking those who would serve You, saying 'Behold and take refuge!' — and I, of such a nature, go about seeking Your feet. O Lord of Srirangam who stands as the rare Truth for those who would hear of You — I shall never let go from my mind the longing for You alone.

90.translit:
kaavaliR pulanai vaiththuk kalithanaik kadakkap paayndhu
naavalit tuzhidharu kinROm naman thamar thalaigaL meedhE
moovula guNdu mizhndha mudhalvanin naamamkaRRa
aavalip pudaimai kaNdaay arangamaa nagaru LaanE.
90.meaning: Holding our senses under guard, leaping across the ocean of Kali, we now roam about proclaiming our triumph over the heads of Yama's messengers. O primordial Lord who swallowed the three worlds and gave them forth again! Behold the boldness that has come to us from having learned Your Name, O Lord who dwell in the great city of Srirangam!

91.translit:
pachchaimaa malaipoalmEni pavaLavaay kamalach chengaN
achchudhaa amara rERE aayartham kozhundhE ennum
ichchuvai thavira yaanpoay indhira loaga maaLum
achchuvai peRinum vENdEn arangamaa nagaru LaanE.
91.meaning: 'O You whose form is like a great emerald mountain, with coral lips and lotus-red eyes! O Achyuta, lion among the celestials, tender sprout of the cowherds!' — abandoning this sweetness of such praise, I would not desire, even if it were offered, that other sweetness of going and ruling over Indra's heaven, O Lord who dwell in the great city of Srirangam!

92.translit:
vEdhanool piraayam nooRu manisardhaam puguva rElum
paadhiyu muRangip poagum ninRadhil padhinai yaaNdu
pEdhaipaa lakana thaagum piNipasi moopputh thunbam
aadhalaal piRavi vENdEn arangamaa nagaru LaanE.
92.meaning: Even if men attain the full hundred years of life set by the Vedic scriptures, half of it passes away in sleep; and of what remains, fifteen years are spent in foolish childhood. The rest is full of disease, hunger, old age, and sorrow. Therefore I desire no birth at all, O Lord who dwell in the great city of Srirangam!

93.translit:
moyththaval vinaiyuL ninRu moonRezhuth thudaiya pEraal
kaththira pandhu manRE paraangadhi kaNdu koNdaan
iththanai yadiya raanaark kirangumnam maranga naaya
piththanaip peRRu mandhO piRaviyuL piNangu maaRE.
93.meaning: Standing amid thick and cruel karma, Kshatrabandhu by the Name of three letters attained the supreme goal, did he not? And alas — having obtained our Lord of Srirangam who became enamored (mad with love) for us, He who shows grace to such lowly servants as these — how is it that men still struggle on entangled in births!

94.translit:
peNdiraal sukanga LuyppaaN periyathoa ridumbai pooNdu
uNdiraak kidakkumpoadhum udalukkE karaindhu naindhu
thaNduzhaay maalai maarban thamargaLaayp paadiyaadi
thoNdupooN damudha muNNaath thozhumbarsoa Rugakkumel.
94.meaning: Taking on great misery for the sake of enjoying pleasures through women, even as they lie sleepless through the night they waste and pine away only for the body's sake. Alas, how the base ones relish mere food — those who will not become the devotees of the Lord who wears the cool tulasi garland on His chest, will not sing and dance, take up His service, and partake of the nectar of devotion!

95.translit:
maRamsuvar madhiLe duththu maRumaikkE veRumai pooNdu
puRamsuva roattai maadam puraLumpoa dhaRiya maattiir
aRamsuva raaki ninRa aranganaark kaatsey yaadhE
puRamsuvar koalanj seydhu puLkavvak kidakkin Riire.
95.meaning: Raising up walls of cruelty as fortifications, taking on utter poverty for the life to come, you do not realize the moment when this outer-walled, hollow mansion of the body will topple and fall. Instead of rendering service to the Lord of Srirangam who stands as the very wall of righteousness (dharma), you go on adorning the outer wall of the body — and so you will lie there for the vultures to peck at!

96.translit:
pulaiyaRa maaki ninRa puththodu samaNa mellaam
kalaiyaRak kaRRa maandhar kaaNparoa kEtpa roadhaam
thalaiyaRup puNdum saavEn saththiyang kaaNmin aiyaa
silaiyinaa lilangai seRRa dhEvanE dhEvanaavaan.
96.meaning: Buddhism and Jainism, which stand as base and lowly creeds — will men who have learned the scriptures to their fullness even look at them or listen to them? Even were my head to be cut off, I would not die in spirit — this is the truth, behold it, sirs! The God who destroyed Lanka with His bow — He alone is the true God.

97.translit:
veRuppodu samaNar muNdar vidhiyilsaak kiyargaL ninpaal
poRuppari yanagaL pEsil poavadhE noaya thaagi
kuRippenak kadaiyu maagil koodumEl thalaiyai aangE
aRuppadhE karumang kaNdaay arangamaa nagaru LaanE.
97.meaning: If the Jains, the shaven-headed ones, and the unfortunate hapless Sakyas (Buddhists) speak with hatred things unbearable against You, my disease (of attachment to such talk) would only depart. If but the inclination came to me, and it were possible, then to cut off their heads then and there is the very duty to be done — behold, O Lord who dwell in the great city of Srirangam!

98.translit:
maRRumoar theyva muNdE madhiyilaa maani dangaaL
uRRapoa dhanRi niingaL oruvanen RuNara maattiir
aRRamE lonRa Riyiir avanallaal theyva millai
kaRRinam mEyththa vendhai kazhaliNai paNimi niirE.
98.meaning: Is there any other God, O witless men? Except in the hour of distress, you will not realize that He is the One. You do not know even one truth utterly — there is no God other than Him. Bow before the twin ankleted feet of our Father who once grazed the herds of calves!

99.translit:
naattinaan theyva mengum nallathoa raruLthan naalE
kaattinaan thiruvarangam uybavark kuyyum vaNNam
kEttirE nambi miirgaaL. kerudavaa kananum niRka
sEttaidhan madiya gaththuch chelvampaarth thirukkin Riire.
99.meaning: He established the worship of God everywhere, and by His own gracious mercy He revealed Srirangam, so that those who would be saved might find their salvation. Have you heard this, O worthy ones? Even while the Lord who rides the Garuda stands ready, you keep waiting upon wealth in the lap of Mudevi (the goddess of misfortune)!

0.translit:
empiraan, endai, ennudaich chuRRam enakku arasu, ennudai vaazhnaaL
ambinaal arakkar verukkoLa nerukki avar uyir seguththa em aNNal,
vambu ulaam soolai maa madiL thanjai maamaNikkooyilee vaNangki,
nambikaaL! uNNa naan kaNdukoNdeen naaraayaNaa ennum naamam.
0.meaning: He is my Lord, my father, my kinsmen, my king, the very span of my life; my noble Lord who, in days of old, pressed upon the demons with his arrows till they trembled in terror and destroyed their lives. Worshipping at the great-jewelled temple of Thanjai, girt with high ramparts and fragrant groves, O devotees, I have found and obtained the Name that is 'Narayana' for you to feed upon.

1.translit:
udamburuvil moonRoRaay moorththiveeRaay ulaguyya ninRaanai,
anRupeeychchi vidamparugu viththaganaik kanRumeeyththu viLaiyaada vallaanai varaimeekaanil,
thadamparugu karumugilaith thanjaikkooyil thavaneRikkoor peruneRiyai vaiyangkaakkum,
kadumparimeel kaRkiyainaankaNdukoNdeen kadipozhilsoozh kadanmallaith thalasayanaththee.
1.meaning: He who became one body in three forms, distinct in his manifestations, and stood for the world's deliverance; the clever one who in days of old drank the poison-milk of the demoness Putana, and who could herd the calves and play upon the high hills; the dark cloud that drinks up the broad waters, the temple at Thanjai, the great path for those on the way of penance. I have found him as Kalki riding the swift horse who guards the world, at Thala-sayanam of seaside Mallai (Mahabalipuram) surrounded by fragrant groves.

2.translit:
enchey keenadi neenurai yeerithaR
  kenRu menmanath theeyiruk kumpugazh,
thanjai yaaLiyaip ponpeya roonRan
  nenja manRidan thavanaiththazha leepurai
minjey vaaLarak kannagar paazhpadach
  soozhka dalsiRai vaiththu imai yoortozhum,
ponsey maalvarai yaimaNik kunRinai
  anRi yenmanam pooRRiyen naathee.
2.meaning: What shall I, your humble servant, do? Tell me, O kinsmen, about this. The famed Lord of Thanjai dwells ever in my tender heart - he who tore open the chest of the golden-named Hiranya, who laid waste the city of the sword-wielding demon (Ravana) fiercer than blazing fire, who bound the surrounding sea into a bridge, and whom the celestials worship. My heart will utter praise to none but that great golden mountain, that mountain of sapphire.

3.translit:
thamaruLLam thanjai thalaiyarangkam thaNkaal,
thamaruLLum thaNporuppu veelai, - thamaruLLum
maamallai kooval madiTkudandai yenparee,
eeval la endaik kidam.
3.meaning: The hearts of his devotees, Thanjai, lofty Arangam (Srirangam), Thankaal, the cool hill cherished by his devotees, and the sea - and they say it is also Mamallai (Mahabalipuram), Koval (Tirukkovalur), and walled Kudandai (Kumbakonam): these are the abodes of my Father who is capable of all things.

4.translit:
maasaRu soothiyen seyya vaaymaNik kunRaththai
aasaRu seelanai yaadi moorththiyai naadiyee,
paasaRa veydhi yaRivizhan thenainaa Laiyam?,
eesaRu mooravar kavvai thoozhee. enseyyumee?
4.meaning: Seeking that flawless radiance, my red-mouthed jewelled hill, the one of faultless virtue, the primordial Lord, I have gained bewilderment and lost my senses - for how many days must this doubt remain? O friend, what will the slander of the people of this blameless town do to me?

0.translit:
theedharu nilaththodu eri kaalinodu neerkkezhu visumbum avaiyaay,
maasaru manaththinodu uRakkamodu iRakkai avai aaya perumaan,
thaaiyseRa uLaindhu thayir uNdu kudam aadu thada maarvar thakaisEr,
naadhanuRai kinRanagar nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamE.
0.meaning: He who became the faultless earth, the fire, the wind, and the water-laden sky; the Lord who became the spotless mind, sleep and wakefulness, and death itself. This is the city where dwells the broad-chested Lord, full of grace, who, distressed when scolded by His mother, ate the curd and danced upon the pot. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram.

1.translit:
uyyumvakai uNdu sona seyyil ulagu Ezhum ozhiyaamai munanaaL,
meyyin aLavE amudhu seyyavalla aiyan avan mEvu nagar thaan,
maiya vari vaNdu madhu uNdu kiLaiyOdu malar kiNdi adhanmEl,
naivaLam naviRRu pozhil nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamE.
1.meaning: There is a way to be saved if one but acts upon His word: in days of old He swallowed all the seven worlds into His own body so they would not perish, the Lord skilled to consume them. This is the city He loves, where dark striped bees with their swarms drink honey, scattering the flowers and humming the Naivalam melody in the groves. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram.

2.translit:
umpar ulagu Ezhu kadal Ezhu malai Ezhum ozhiyaamai munanaaL,
tham pon vayiRu aar aLavum uNdu avaiyum izhndha thada maarvar thakaisEr,
vambu malar kinRa pozhil paimpon varu thumbi maNi kangul vayal soozh,
nambanuRai kinRanagar nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamE.
2.meaning: In days of old He swallowed the seven upper worlds, the seven seas and the seven mountains into His golden belly until it was full, so they would not perish, then gave them back forth, the broad-chested gracious Lord. This is the city where the trustworthy Lord dwells, ringed by fields and gem-dark nights, with golden bees coming to the fragrant blossoming groves. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram.

3.translit:
piRaiyin oLi eyiRu ilagu muRugi edhir porudhum ena vandha asurar,
iRaikaL avai neRu neRu ena veRiya avar vayiRu azhala ninRa perumaan,
siRaikoL mayil kuyil payila malargaL uka aLi mural adi koL nedumaa,
naRaisey pozhil mazhai thavazhum nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamE.
3.meaning: The Lord who stood firm when the demons, their teeth gleaming like crescent moonlight, hardened in fury and came to do battle, so that their chieftains were crushed crackling and their bellies burned. This is the tall and great Lord's place, where winged peacocks and koels throng, flowers fall and bees hum, and clouds creep over the honey-laden groves. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram.

4.translit:
mooLa eri sindhi muni eydhi amar seydhum ena vandha asurar,
thOLum avar thaaLum mudiyOdu podi aaga nodi aam aLavu eydhaan,
vaaLum vari villum vaLai aazhi gadhai sangam ivai angai udaiyaan,
naaLum uRai kinRanagar nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamE.
4.meaning: When the demons came in blazing wrath, scattering fire and seeking to wage war, He in but the snap of a finger reduced their shoulders, their feet and their crowns to dust. He who holds in His fair hands the sword, the striped bow, the curved discus, the mace and the conch. This is the city where He dwells day after day. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram.

5.translit:
thambiyodu thaam oruvar than thuNaivi kaadhal thuNaiyaaga munanaaL,
vembi eri kaanagam ulaavum avar thaam inidhu mEvu nagar thaan,
kombu kudhi koNdu kuyil koova mayil aalum ezhil aar puRavu sEr,
nambiyuRai kinRanagar nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamE.
5.meaning: In days of old He, with His brother and His own beloved consort as His loving companions, wandered through the scorching burning forest; this is the city He gladly loves. This is the city where the perfect Lord dwells, set among lovely groves and meadows where koels leap upon the branches and call, and peacocks dance. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram.

6.translit:
thandhai manam undhu thuyar nandha iruL vandha viRal nandhan madhalai,
endhai ivan enRu amarar kandha malar koNdu thozha ninRa nagar thaan,
mandha muzhavu Osai mazhai aaga ezhu kaar mayilgaL aadu pozhil soozh,
nandhi paNi seydhanagar nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamE.
6.meaning: He who, to end the sorrow welling in His father's heart, was born as the son of mighty Nanda when darkness came; this is the city where the celestials worship Him with fragrant flowers, crying 'this one is our Father!' Ringed by groves where dark peacocks rise and dance, mistaking the soft drum-like thunder for the sound of rain. This is the city Nandi served. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram.

7.translit:
eNNil ninaivu eydhi ini illai iRai enRu muni aaLar thiru aar,
paNNil mali geedhamodu paadi avar aadalodu kooda ezhil aar,
maNNil idhu pola nagar illai ena vaanavargaL thaam malargaL thooy,
naNNi uRai kinRanagar nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamE.
7.meaning: Sages, attaining boundless contemplation and declaring 'there is no Lord but Him,' sing songs rich in melody and join in joyful dance. This is the lovely city where the celestials, scattering flowers and saying 'there is no city on earth like this,' draw near and dwell. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram.

8.translit:
vanga mali pauvam adhu maa mugadin ucchi puga mikka peru neer,
anga azhiyaar avanadhu aaNai thalai soodum adiyaar aRidhiyEl,
pongu punal undhu maNi kangul iruL seeRum oLi engum uLadhaal,
nangaL perumaan uRaiyum nandhipura viNNagaram naNNu manamE.
8.meaning: When the great waters of the ship-laden ocean swelled to rise over the peak of the mighty mountain, those there did not perish; if you would be devotees who bear His command upon your heads, know this: the radiance of the gems carried by the surging waters, which scorns the darkness of night, is everywhere. This is where our Lord dwells. O my mind, draw near to Nandipura Vinnagaram.

9.translit:
naRaisey pozhil mazhai thavazhum nandhipura viNNagaram naNNi uRaiyum,
uRaikoL pugar aazhi suri sangam avai angai udaiyaanai, oLisEr
kaRai vaLarum vEl valla kaliyan oli maalai ivai aindhum aindhum,
muRai ilavai payila valla adiyavargaL kodu vinaigaL muzhudhu agalumE.
9.meaning: Of the Lord who dwells in Nandipura Vinnagaram where clouds creep over the honey-laden groves, who holds in His fair hands the gleaming sheathed discus and the curved conch, these ten resounding garland-verses were sung by Kaliyan, master of the bright blood-stained spear. The devotees who can recite these in proper order will have all their cruel karma wholly depart.

0.translit:
aaychchiyar azhaippa veNNey uNDu oru kaal
 aalilai vaLarnda em perumaan
peychchi azhalai uNDu iNai marudu iruththu
 perunilam aLanda van koayil
kaayththa neeL kazhakum kadaliyum thengum
 engum aam pozhilkaLin naduvee
vaayththa neer paayum maNNiyin then paal
 thiruveLLiyanguDi athuvee.
0.meaning: He who, when the cowherd women called out, ate the butter, and once in time lay as a babe upon the banyan leaf, our Lord; He who sucked the life from the demoness, broke the twin marudu trees, and measured the vast earth — this is His temple. Amid groves where tall ripened areca palms, plantains and coconuts grow everywhere, on the southern side of the river Manni whose abundant waters flow, lies that Tiruvelliyangudi.

1.translit:
aanirai meyththu anRu alaikadal adaiththittu
 arakkar tham sirangaLai uruTTi,
kaarnirai meegam kalandu oar uruvak
 kaNNanaar karudiya koayil,
poo neerai serundi punnai muththu arumbi
 podumbidai vari vaNDu miNDi,
thean iraiththu uNDu angu in isai muralum
 thiruveLLiyanguDi athuvee.
1.meaning: He who grazed the herds of cattle, and once dammed the surging ocean, and rolled away the heads of the demons; Kannan whose form is blended like a dark, full rain-cloud — this is the temple He cherished. Where, amid blossoming serunti and punnai trees in the flowering waters, pearl-like buds open and striped bees crowd, drinking honey in swarms and humming sweet music — this is that Tiruvelliyangudi.

2.translit:
kaduvidam udaiya kaaLiyan thadaththaik
 kalakki mun alakkazhiththu, avan Ran
padam iRap paayndu pal maNi sindap
 pal nadam payinRavan koayil,
pada aravu algul paavai nallaarkaL
 payiRRiya naadagaththu oli poay,
adai pudai thazhuvi aNDam ninRu adhirum
 thiruveLLiyanguDi athuvee.
2.meaning: He who long ago churned and devastated the pool of fierce-venomed Kaliya, leaping upon him so his hoods were crushed and his many gems scattered, and danced many a dance there — this is His temple. Where the sound of the drama enacted by lovely maidens, whose hips are like the hood of a snake, rises up, spreading on every side and resounding to the very firmament — this is that Tiruvelliyangudi.

3.translit:
karavai mun kaaththuk kanjanaik kaayththa
 kaaLa meegath thiru uruvan,
paRavai mun uyarththup paaRkadal thuyinRa
 paramanaar paLLikoL koayil,
thuRai thuRai thoarum pon maNi sidaRum
 thoku thirai maNNiyin then paal,
seRi maNi maaDak kodi kadhir aNavum
 thiruveLLiyanguDi athuvee.
3.meaning: He of the dark cloud-hued sacred form, who once protected the cattle and grew angry with Kamsan; the Supreme One who raised aloft the bird (Garuda) and reposed upon the milk-ocean — this is the temple where He lies. On the southern side of the Manni river, whose gathered waves scatter gold and gems at every ford, where the banners atop jewel-studded mansions reach up to touch the sun's rays — this is that Tiruvelliyangudi.

4.translit:
paarinai uNDu paarinai umizhndu
 paaratham kai eRindu, oru kaal
theerinai oorndu therinaith thuranda
 sengaN maal senRu uRai koayil,
eer nirai vayaluL vaaLaigaL maRugi
 emakku idam anRu idhu enRu eNNi,
seer mali poygai senRu aNaiginRa
 thiruveLLiyanguDi athuvee.
4.meaning: He who swallowed the earth and spewed it forth, who urged on the Bharata war with His hand, and once drove the chariot and pressed it forward — the red-eyed Tirumal who came and dwells here — this is His temple. Where in the rows of fertile fields the vaaLai fish leap about and, thinking 'this is no place for us,' go and gather in the glorious ponds — this is that Tiruvelliyangudi.

5.translit:
kaaRRidaip pooLai karandana arandai uRak
 kadal arakkar tham seenai,
kooRRidaich chellak kodum kaNai thuranda
 koala vil iraaman than koayil,
ooRRidai ninRa vaazhaiyin kanigaL
 oozhththu veezhndana uNDu maNDi,
seaRRidaik kayalgaL uL thigazh vayal soozh
 thiruveLLiyanguDi athuvee.
5.meaning: He who, so that the army of the ocean-dwelling demons scattered into anguish like pooLai cotton in the wind and passed into the jaws of Death, loosed His cruel arrows — Rama of the beautiful bow — this is His temple. Where the fruits of the plantain standing by the springs ripen and fall, and the carp feed greedily and crowd into the mire, shining within the surrounding fields — this is that Tiruvelliyangudi.

6.translit:
oLLiya karumam seyvan enRu uNarnda
 maavali veaLviyil pukku,
teLLiya kuRaLaay moovadi koNDu
 thikku uRa vaLarndavan koayil
aLLiyam pozhil vaay irundu vaazh kuyilgaL
 ari ari enRavai azhaippa
veLLiyaar vaNanga viraindu aruL seyvaan
 thiruveLLiyanguDi athuvee!
6.meaning: He who, entering the sacrifice of Mahabali — who thought 'I shall do a brilliant deed' — as a clear-formed dwarf, took three paces and grew until He filled all the quarters — this is His temple. Where the cuckoos that dwell amid the lush groves call out 'Hari, Hari,' He who swiftly grants His grace when the devotees bow down — this is that Tiruvelliyangudi!

7.translit:
mudi udai amararkku idar seyum asurar
 tham perumaanai, anRu ariyaay
madi idai vaiththu maarvai mun keeNDa
 maayanaar manniya koayil,
padi idai maaDath thadi idaith thooNil
 padiththa pal maNigaLin oLiyaal,
vidi pagal iravu enRu aRivu aridhaaya
 thiruveLLiyanguDi athuvee.
7.meaning: He who, becoming the man-lion of old, placed upon His lap the lord of the asuras — those demons who brought distress to the crowned celestials — and tore open his chest; that wondrous One whose abiding temple this is. Where, by the radiance of the many gems set into the pillars at the bases of the storeyed mansions, it becomes hard to tell whether it is the dawning day or the night — this is that Tiruvelliyangudi.

8.translit:
kudi kudiyaagak koodi ninRu amarar
 guNangaLee pidhaRRi ninRu eaththa
adiyavarkku aruLi aravu aNaith thuyinRa
 aazhiyaan amarndu uRai koayil,
kadi udaik kamalam adi idai malarak
 karumbodu perum sennel asaiya,
vadivu udai annam pedaiyodum seerum
 vayal veLLiyanguDi athuvee!
8.meaning: The temple where the discus-bearing Lord, who graced His devotees and reposed upon the serpent-couch, abides — while the celestials, gathered clan upon clan, stand babbling forth and praising only His qualities. Where the fragrant lotuses bloom at the base, the sugarcane and tall red paddy sway, and the graceful swans gather with their mates — this is that field-girt Velliyangudi!

9.translit:
paNDu mun eanam aagi anRu oru kaal,
 paar idandu eyiRRinil koNDu,
teN thirai varudap paaRkadal thuyinRa
 thiruveLLiyanguDi aanai,
vaNDu aRai soalai mangaiyar thalaivan
 maana veal kaliyan vaay oligaL,
koNDu ivai paadum thavam udaiyaarkaL
 aaLvar ik kurai kadal ulagee.
9.meaning: Of the Lord of Tiruvelliyangudi, who long ago became the boar and once raised up the earth, holding it on His tusk, and who reposed upon the milk-ocean lapped by clear waves — these are the songs from the lips of Kaliyan, the proud-speared chief of Mangai with its bee-humming groves. Those blessed with the merit to take up and sing these verses shall rule over this world girt by the resounding sea.

0.translit:
tandai kaalil peruvi langu thaaLavizha, naLLirutkaN vandha
endhai perumaanaar maruvi ninRa voorpoolum,
mundi vaanam mazhaipozhiyum moovaa vuruvil maRaiyaaLar
andhi moonRum analoombum aNiyaar veedhi azhundhoore.
0.meaning: This seems to be the town where my Lord, my Father, came in the dead of night to free the great fetters from His father's feet. It is Azhundhur, where Brahmins of undying form, before whom the sky pours forth rain, tend the sacred fire at all three twilights, with its beautiful streets.

1.translit:
paarit thezhundha padaimannar thammai yaaLa,
paarathathth theril paaka naayoorndha theva theva noorpoolum,
neeril paNaitha neduvaaLaik kanjip poona kuruginangaL,
aaral kavuLo daruganaiyum aNiyaar vayalsoozh azhundhoore!
1.meaning: This seems to be the town of the God of gods, who, to rule the kings with their swelling armies, drove the chariot as a charioteer in the Mahabharata war. It is Azhundhur, surrounded by beautiful fields, where flocks of cranes that have eaten the long scabbard-fish grown in the waters draw near with eel in their cheeks.

2.translit:
sempon madhiLsoozh tennilangaik kiRaivan sirangaL aiyiraNdum,
umbar vaaLik kilakkaaga udhirththa vuravo noorpoolum,
kombi laarndha maadhavimel kodhi meyndha vaNdinangaL,
ambaraavum kaNmadavaar aimbaalaNaiyum azhundhoore.
2.meaning: This seems to be the town of the mighty one who shattered and made fall the ten heads of the lord of southern Lanka, encircled by walls of pure gold, with His heavenly arrow. It is Azhundhur, where swarms of bees that grazed and fed upon the maadhavi creeper flourishing on its branches settle upon the five-fold braided tresses of women with sky-reaching eyes.

3.translit:
veLLath thuLLo raalilaimel mevi yadiyen manampugundhu, en
uLLath thuLLum kaNNuLLum ninRaar ninRa voorpoolum,
puLLup piLLaik kiraidhedip poona kaadhal pedaiyodum,
aLLal seRuvil kayalnaadum aNiyaar vayalsoozh azhundhoore.
3.meaning: This seems to be the town where He stood—He who reclined upon a banyan leaf amid the flood waters, who entered the mind of me His servant, and abides within my heart and within my eyes. It is Azhundhur, surrounded by beautiful fields, where birds, together with their loving mates, seek out carp fish in the muddy paddy fields, having gone to find food for their chicks.

4.translit:
pagalu miravum thaaneyaaip paarum viNNum thaaneyaai,
nigaril sudaraa yiruLaagi ninRaar ninRa voorpoolum,
thugilin kodiyum theerthugaLum thunni maadhar koondhalvaai,
agilin pugaiyaal mugileykkum aNiyaar veedhi azhundhoore.
4.meaning: This seems to be the town where He stood—He who is Himself both day and night, Himself both earth and sky, who is the matchless radiance and also the darkness. It is Azhundhur, with beautiful streets where the banners of fine cloth and the dust of chariots crowd together, and the fragrant smoke of agarwood from the hair of women resembles the clouds.

5.translit:
edi langu thaamaraipool sevvaai muRuval seydharuLi,
maadu vandhen manampugundhu ninRaar ninRaa voorpoolum,
needu maadath thanichchoolam pozhak koNdal thuLidhoova,
aada laravath thaarppoovaa aNiyaar veedhi azhundhoore.
5.meaning: This seems to be the town where He stood—He who graciously smiled with red lips like a petalled lotus, came near, and entered my mind. It is Azhundhur with beautiful streets, where the lone trident atop the tall mansions pierces the cloud so that it drops its rain, and the sound of dancing serpents never ceases.

6.translit:
maalaip pugundhu malaraNaimel vaigi yadiyen manampugundhu, en
neelak kaNgaL panimalga ninRaar ninRa voorpoolum
velaik kadalpool neduveedhi viNthoy sudhaiveN maNimaadathtu,
aalaip pugaiyaal azhalkadhirai maRaikkum veedhi azhundhoore.
6.meaning: This seems to be the town where He stood—He who came at evening, reclined upon the flower-bed, entered the mind of me His servant, so that my dark eyes brimmed with tears of joy. It is Azhundhur, with long streets like the surging sea and sky-touching white plastered jewel-mansions, whose streets hide the burning sun with the smoke of the sugarcane mills.

7.translit:
vanji marungu lidainova maNandhu ninRa kanavagathtu, en
nenju niRaiyak kaikooppi ninRaar ninRa voorpoolum,
panji yanna melladinaR paavai maargaL, aadagatthin
anji lambi naarppoovaa aNiyaar veedhi azhundhoore.
7.meaning: This seems to be the town where He stood—He whom, in a dream, I embraced till my willow-slender waist ached, and before whom, with my heart full, I stood with hands joined in worship. It is Azhundhur with beautiful streets, where the sound of the lovely golden anklets on the cotton-soft tender feet of doll-like maidens never ceases.

8.translit:
ennaim pulanu mezhilungoN dinge nerundhal ezhundharuLi
ponnang kalaigaL meliveydhap poona punidha roorpoolum,
mannu mudhunee ravindha malarmel varivaN disaipaada
annam pedaiyo dudanaadum aNiyaar vayalsoozh azhundhoore!
8.meaning: This seems to be the town of the pure One who came here yesterday, took away my five senses and my beauty, and departed so that my golden ornaments grew loose upon my wasting form. It is Azhundhur, surrounded by beautiful fields, where streaked bees sing upon the lotus blossoms in the abiding ancient waters, and swans dance together with their mates.

9.translit:
nellil kuvaLai kaNkaatta ril kumudham vaaikaatta,
allik kamalam mugangaattum kazhani yazhundhoor ninRaanai,
vallip podhumbil kuyilkoovum mangai vendhan paragaalan,
sollil polindha thamizhmaalai sollap paavam nillaave.
9.meaning: Upon the One who stands at Azhundhur—whose fields show eyes through the blue waterlilies amid the paddy, show mouths through the red lilies, and show faces through the petalled lotuses—Parakaalan, king of Mangai where the cuckoo coos in the creeper-bowers, has composed this garland of Tamil resplendent in words. For those who recite it, sins will not stand.

10.translit:
singama thaayavuNan thiRalaagamun keeNdugandha,
sangami dathtaanaith thazhalaazhi valathtaanai,
sengamalath thayananaiyaar tennazhundhaiyil mannininRa,
angama lakkaNNanai adiyenkaNdu koNdene.
10.meaning: The One who, becoming a man-lion, joyfully tore open the mighty chest of the demon long ago, who holds the conch in His left hand and the blazing discus in His right, the lotus-eyed Lord who abides firmly in southern Azhundhai where dwell Brahmins like Brahma seated on the red lotus—Him I, His servant, have beheld and attained.

11.translit:
kovaa naarmadiyak kolaiyaarmazhuk koNdaruLum,
moovaa vaanavanai muzhuneervaN Nanai, adiyaarkku,
aavaa enRirangith tennazhundhaiyil mannininRa,
thevaadhi thevanaiyaan kaNdukoNdu thiLaiththene.
11.meaning: The undying celestial One who graciously wielded the deadly axe so that kings perished, who is the color of the deep waters, the God of all gods who, taking pity on His devotees crying 'Alas, alas!', abides firmly in southern Azhundhai—Him I have beheld and rejoiced in.

12.translit:
udaiyaa naiyolinee rulagangaL padaiththaanai,
vidaiyaa noodavan_Ru viRalaazhi visaiththaanai,
adaiyaar tennilangai yazhiththaanai aNiyazhundhoor
udaiyaanai, adiye nadaindhuyndhu poonene.
12.meaning: The Lord who created the worlds amid the resounding waters, who that day hurled the mighty discus so that the foe fled, who destroyed hostile southern Lanka, the Lord who possesses beautiful Azhundhur—Him I, His servant, have approached and been saved.

13.translit:
kunRaal maaridhaduth thavanaikkula vezhaman_Ru
ponRaa mai, adhanuk karuLseydha poreRRai,
an_Raa vinnaRunei yamarndhuNNa aNiyazhundhoor
ninRaanai, adiyen kaNdukoNdu niRaindhene.
13.meaning: The One who held up the mountain to ward off the rain, the bull-like warrior who showed grace to the noble elephant so that it would not perish that day, who stands at beautiful Azhundhur delighting to eat the fragrant ghee of the cows—Him I, His servant, have beheld and been fulfilled.

14.translit:
kanjanaik kaayndhaanaik kaNNamangaiyuL ninRaanai,
vanjanap peymulaiyoo duyirvaaimaduth thuNdaanai,
senjol naanmaRaiyor tennazhundhaiyil mannininRa
anjanak kunRandhannai yadiyenkaNdu koNdene.
14.meaning: The One who burned with anger against Kamsa, who stands within Kannamangai, who took the life of the deceitful demoness through her breast by setting His mouth to it and drinking, the mountain dark as collyrium who abides firmly in southern Azhundhai of the Brahmins of the sweet-worded four Vedas—Him I, His servant, have beheld and attained.

15.translit:
periyaanai yamarar thalaivaRkum piramanukkum,
uriyaanai yugandhaa navanukku muNarvadhanuk
kariyaanai, azhundhoor maRaiyorga LadipaNiyum
kariyaanai, adiyen kaNdukoNdu kaLiththene!
15.meaning: The great One, who belongs to the chief of the immortals and to Brahma and to one who loves Him, who is hard to comprehend by the understanding, the dark One whom the Brahmins of Azhundhur bow at His feet—Him I, His servant, have beheld and rejoiced in.

16.translit:
thiruvaazh maarvanRannaith thisaimaNNee rerimudhalaa,
uruvaai ninRavanai yoliserum maaruthatthai,
aruvaai ninRavanaith tennazhundhaiyil mannininRa
karuvaar kaRpagatthaik kaNdukoNdu kaLiththene!
16.meaning: The One on whose chest Lakshmi dwells, who stands as form—as the directions, the earth, water, fire and the rest—who is the resounding wind, who stands also as the formless, the deep-rooted wish-fulfilling Karpaka tree who abides firmly in southern Azhundhai—Him I have beheld and rejoiced in.

17.translit:
nilaiyaa Laagavennai yugandhaanai, nilamagaLthan
mulaiyaaL vithtaganai mudhunaanmaRai veedhidhoRum,
alaiyaa rumkadalpool muzhangazhundhaiyil mannininRa
kalaiyaar soRporuLaik kaNdukoNdu kaLiththene.
17.meaning: The One who delighted in me to be His steadfast servant, the skilled lord who embraces the Earth-goddess, who abides firmly in Azhundhai that resounds like the wave-tossed sea along every street of the ancient four Vedas—the very meaning of the words of the sacred arts—Him I have beheld and rejoiced in.

18.translit:
peraa naikkudandhaip perumaanai, ilangoLiser
vaaraar vanamulaiyaaL malarmangai naayaganai,
aaraa vinnamudhaith tennazhundhaiyil mannininRa,
kaaraar karumugilaik kaNdukoNdu kaLiththene.
18.meaning: The great One named Perar, the Lord of Kudandhai, the consort of the flower-dwelling Lakshmi with her shining bright breasts bound in a bodice, the insatiable sweet nectar, the dark rain-laden cloud who abides firmly in southern Azhundhai—Him I have beheld and rejoiced in.

19.translit:
thiRalmuru kananaiyaar tennazhundhaiyil mannininRa
aRamudhal vanavanai aNiyaaliyar kon, maruvaar
kaRainedu velvalavan kalikanRisol aiyiraNdum,
muRaivazhu vaamaivallaar muzhudhaaLvar vaanulage.
19.meaning: Upon the One who is the foremost source of dharma, who abides firmly in southern Azhundhai where dwell those like the valiant Murugan—Kalikanri, lord of beautiful Aali, wielder of the long stained spear against his foes, has spoken these ten verses. Those who can recite them in proper order without fault shall rule wholly over the heavenly world.

20.translit:
tiruvuk kum tiru vaagiya selvaa
   deyvath thuk kara sE seyya kaNNaa,
uruvac cen-cuda raazhi val laanE
   ulagu NDa oru vaa, tiru maarbaa,
oruvaR kaaTRi uyyum vagai enRaal
  udanin Raivaren n-uL pugundu, ozhiyaa
daruvith thinRida anjinin n-adaindEn
  azhundoor mEl-tisai n-inRa am maanE.
20.meaning: O wealthy one who became the very wealth of Lakshmi, O king among the gods, O lovely red-eyed Kannan, O mighty wielder of the discus blazing with ruddy fire and form, O peerless one who swallowed the worlds, O one whose chest bears Lakshmi! When I ask how I might be saved by serving you, the five senses stand together, enter within me, and never cease to draw and devour me. Afraid of them, I have come and taken refuge in you, O Lord who stands at Tirumel-tisai of Azhundur.

21.translit:
pandaar mel viral n-al vaLaith thOLi
  paavai poo-magaL tannodu mudanE
vandaay, en manath thE manni n-inRaay
  maal vaNNaa mazhai pOl oLi vaNNaa,
sandOgaa pauzhiyaa thaiththiriyaa
  saama vEdiyanE n-edu maalE,
andO n-in adi anRi maTR aRiyEn
  azhundoor mEl-tisai n-inRa am maanE.
21.meaning: O dark-hued Lord, lustrous as the rain-cloud, you came together with the doll-like flower-born Lakshmi, who has graceful soft fingers fit for the ball and shoulders adorned with fine bangles, and you have abidingly settled in my mind. O Lord of the Chandoga, Paushya, Taittiriya and Sama Vedas, O great Tirumal! Alas, I know nothing other than your feet, O Lord who stands at Tirumel-tisai of Azhundur.

22.translit:
n-eyyaa raazhiyum sangamu mEndum
  n-eeNda thOLudai yaay, adi yEnaic
ceyyaa thavula gath thidaic ceydaay
  ciRumaik kum peru maikkum uL pugundu,
poyyaal aivar en mey kudi yERip
  pORRi vaazhvadaR kanjinin n-adaindEn
aiyaa n-in adi anRi maTR aRiyEn
  azhundoor mEl-tisai n-inRa am maanE.
22.meaning: O long-shouldered one who bears the discus dripping with ghee and the conch, you placed me, your servant, into this world where good is not done. Entering both in smallness and in greatness, the five senses through falsehood take up dwelling in my body, and afraid of being made merely to extol and live by them, I have come and taken refuge in you. O master, I know nothing other than your feet, O Lord who stands at Tirumel-tisai of Azhundur.

23.translit:
paranE panjavan pauzhiyan sOzhan
 paar man narman nar thaam paNin dEththum
varanE, maadhava nE madhu soodhaa
 maTROr n-al thuNai n-inn-alaal ilEn kaaN
n-aranE n-aaraNa nE tiru n-aRaiyoor n-ambee
 em peru maan umba raaLum
aranE aadhi va raagam un naanaay
 azhundoor mEl-tisai n-inRa am maanE.
23.meaning: O Supreme One, O exalted one whom the Pandya, the Pauzhiya, the Chozha and the king of all earthly kings bow down and praise, O Madhava, O slayer of Madhu! I have no other good companion than you, behold. O Nara, O Narayana, O Lord of Tirunaraiyur, our master, O Hara who rules the celestials, O one who of old became the Primordial Boar, O Lord who stands at Tirumel-tisai of Azhundur.

24.translit:
viNDaan viN puga venj-samath thariyaayp
 pariyOn maarvagam paTRip piLandu,
paNDaan uyya Or maal varai yEndum
 paNbaaLaa paranE pavith thiranE,
kaNDEn n-aan kali yugath than tanmai
 karuma maavadhum en thanak kaRindEn,
aNDaa, n-in adi anRi maTR aRiyEn
 azhundoor mEl-tisai n-inRa am maanE.
24.meaning: Becoming a lion in fierce battle so that the foe might enter heaven, you seized and tore open the chest of the powerful one (Hiranya); O gracious one who long ago lifted up a great mountain so that the cowherds might be saved, O Supreme One, O Pure One! I have seen the nature of this Kali age, and I have understood for myself what my proper duty is. O Lord of the cosmos, I know nothing other than your feet, O Lord who stands at Tirumel-tisai of Azhundur.

25.translit:
thOyaa vin thayir n-ey amudh uNNac
 connaar colli n-agum pari sE, peTRa
dhaayaal aappuN dirun dazhudh EngkkuM
 thaadaaLaa tarai yOrkkum viN NOrkkum
sEyaay, kirEdha thirEdha thuvaapara
 kali yuga mivai n-aangu mun naanaay,
aayaa n-in adi anRi maTR aRiyEn
 azhundoor mEl-tisai n-inRa am maanE.
25.meaning: Those who spoke laughed at the way you ate the fresh unchurned curd, ghee and ambrosial butter; bound by your own mother you sat weeping and sobbing, O lord of mighty step! O one far beyond reach for the people of earth and the dwellers of heaven, O one who of old became these four ages, Krita, Treta, Dvapara and Kali, O herdsman, I know nothing other than your feet, O Lord who stands at Tirumel-tisai of Azhundur.

26.translit:
kaRuththuk kanjanai yanja munindaay
 kaar vaN Naa kadal pOl oLi vaNNaa
iRuth thiTTaan vidai yEzhum mun venRaay
 endaay andara mEzhum un naanaay,
poRuth thuk koNdirun thaal poRuk koNaap
 pOga mE n-ugar vaan pugundu, aivar
aRuth thuth thinRida anjinin n-adaindEn
 azhundoor mEl-tisai n-inRa am maanE.
26.meaning: In anger you raged so that Kamsa trembled, O cloud-hued one, O one lustrous as the ocean; long ago you conquered and broke the seven bulls, O my father, O one who became the seven heavens! Entering to enjoy pleasures unbearable were one to endure them, the five senses cut and devour me. Afraid of this, I have come and taken refuge in you, O Lord who stands at Tirumel-tisai of Azhundur.

27.translit:
n-ediyaa nE kadi aar kali n-ambee
    n-innai yE n-inain thingiruppEnai
kadiyaar kaaLaiya raivar pugundu
    kaaval ceydhavak kaavalaip pizhaiththu
kudipOn thun nadik keezh vandu pugundEn
    kooRai sORivai tandhe nakk aruLi
adiyE naip paNi yaaNDu koL endaay
    azhundoor mEl-tisai n-inRa am maanE.
27.meaning: O tall one, O Lord of the resounding sea! As I dwell here thinking only of you, the five fierce youths (the senses) entered and set a guard over me; escaping that guard, I have come forth and entered beneath your feet. Granting me this clothing and food, take me, your servant, into your service and accept me, O my father, O Lord who stands at Tirumel-tisai of Azhundur.

28.translit:
kOvaay aivar en mey kudi yERik
 kooRai sORivai thaa enRu kumaiththup
pOgaar, n-aan ava raip poRuk kakilEn
 punidhaa puT kodiyaay n-edu maalE,
theevaay n-aagaNai yil thuyil vaanE
 tirumaalE inic ceyvadhon RaRiyEn,
aavaa venRadi yERk iRai yirangaay
 azhundoor mEl-tisai n-inRa am maanE.
28.meaning: As masters, the five senses take up dwelling in my body and torment me, demanding 'Give this clothing and food,' and they will not depart; I cannot endure them. O Pure One, O one with the bird-banner, O great Tirumal, O one who sleeps upon the flame-mouthed serpent-couch, O Tirumal, I know nothing now that I can do. Saying 'Alas, alas,' show a little compassion to me, your servant, O Lord who stands at Tirumel-tisai of Azhundur.

29.translit:
anna mannu paim poom pozhil soozhnda
 azhundoor mEl-tisai n-inRa am maanai,
kanni mannu thiN dOL kali kanRi
 aali n-aadan mangaik kula vEndan,
conna in tamizh n-an maNik kOvai
 thooya maalai yivai paththum vallaar,
manni mannava raay ula gaaNDu
 maana veN kudaik keezh magizh vaarE.
29.meaning: These ten verses, a fine garland of gem-like sweet Tamil, a pure garland, sung by Kalikanri the king of the Mangai clan, lord of the Ali land with strong shoulders firm and youthful, upon the Lord who stands at Tirumel-tisai of Azhundur, surrounded by fresh fragrant groves where swans abide. Those who can master them shall abidingly become kings, rule the world, and rejoice beneath the proud white parasol.

30.translit:
sengamalath tirumagaLum puviyum sempon
  tiruvadiyin iNai varuda munivar Eththa
vangamali thadangkadaluL ananthan ennum
  variyaravin aNaith thuyinRa maayOn kaaNmin
engum mali n-iRaipugazh n-aal vEdham aindu
  vELvigaLum kELvigaLum iyanRa tanmai
angamalath thayananaiyaar payilum selvath
  thaNiyazhundoor n-inRu uganda amarar kOvE.
30.meaning: Lakshmi seated on the red lotus and the Earth-goddess stroke the pair of his lovely pure-golden feet while sages praise him; behold the wondrous Lord who reposes upon the couch of the striped serpent named Ananta in the broad ship-filled ocean. Behold the king of the celestials who delights to stand at cool Azhundur, rich in wealth, where dwell those resembling Brahma of the lotus, in whom abide the everywhere-abounding full glory of the four Vedas, the five sacrifices and the sacred hearings.

31.translit:
munniv vula gEzhum iruL maNdi yuNNa
  munivarodu thaanavargaL thigaippa, vandu
pannukalai n-aal vEdhap poruLai yellaam
  parimuga maay aruLiyavem paraman kaaNmin
cen-n-el mali kadhirk kavari veesac cangam
  avaimuralac cengamala malarai yERi
annamali pedaiyOdum amarum selvath
  thaNiyazhundoor n-inRu uganda amarar kOvE.
31.meaning: Of old, when darkness thickened and swallowed all the seven worlds, and the sages and the Danavas were dismayed, he came as the Horse-faced one (Hayagriva) and graciously gave forth the essence of all the studied arts and the four Vedas; behold this our Supreme Lord. Behold the king of the celestials who delights to stand at cool Azhundur, rich in wealth, where the ears of ripe red paddy wave like fans, the conches resound, and swans mounted upon red lotus blossoms abide with their hens.

32.translit:
kulath thalaiya madha vEzham poygai pukkuk
  kOL mudalai pidikka adhaR kanungki n-inRu
n-ilath thigazhum malarc cudarEy sOdhee enna
  n-enjidar theerth tharuLiyaven n-imalan kaaNmin
malaith thigazh sandh agil kanaga maNiyum koNDu
  vanduntdi vayalgaL thoRum madaigaL paaya
alaiththu varum ponni vaLam perugum
  selvath thaNiyazhundoor n-inRu uganda amarar kOvE.
32.meaning: When the lordly rutting elephant, chief of its herd, entered the pond and the cruel crocodile seized it, the elephant grew faint and cried 'O radiance like the lotus-light gleaming over the earth!'; behold my flawless Lord who removed the anguish of its heart and graced it. Behold the king of the celestials who delights to stand at Azhundur, rich in wealth where prosperity swells, where the Ponni (Kaveri) comes surging, bearing sandalwood, eaglewood, gold and gems from the mountains, thrusting them forward and flooding through the sluices into every field.

33.translit:
silambu mudhal kalan aNindhOr cengaN kunRam
  thigazhndhadhen tiruvuruvam panRi yaagi
ilangu puvi madandhai thanai yidandhu pulgi
  eyiRRidai vaiththaruLiyavem meesan kaaNmin
pulambu ciRai vaNDolippap poogam thokka
  pozhilkadoRum kuyil koova mayilgaL aala
alambu thiraip punal pudaisoozhndh azhagaar selvath
