  thaNiyazhundoor n-inRu uganda amarar kOvE.
33.meaning: His sacred form, adorned with anklets and other ornaments, shone like a red-eyed hill; becoming a Boar, he dug up and embraced the shining Earth-maiden and bore her gracefully upon his tusk; behold this our Lord. Behold the king of the celestials who delights to stand at beautiful, wealthy Azhundur, surrounded by water with surging waves, where buzzing winged bees hum, cuckoos call and peacocks dance in every grove thick with areca palms.

34.translit:
sinamEvum adalariyin uruva maagith
  thiRalmEvum iraNiyan thaagam keeNdu
manamEvu vanjanaiyaal vandha pEycci
  maaLav uyir vavviyavem maayOn kaaNmin
inamEvu varivaLak kai yEndum kOvai
  Eyvaaya maragadham pOl kiliyin in col
anamEvu n-adai madavaar payilum selvath
  thaNiyazhundoor n-inRu uganda amarar kOvE.
34.meaning: Taking the form of a furious, vanquishing lion, he tore open the body of the mighty Hiranya; and he seized the life of the demoness (Putana) who came through the deceit set in her heart, so that she perished; behold this our wondrous Lord. Behold the king of the celestials who delights to stand at cool, wealthy Azhundur, where dwell graceful women of swan-like gait, who hold up hands adorned with rows of striped bangles, with lips red as the kovai-fruit and sweet speech like emerald-hued parrots.

35.translit:
vaanavar tham thuyar theera vandu thOnRi
  maaNuruvaay moovadi maavaliyai vENDi
thaanamara vEzhulagum aLanda venRith
  thanimudhal cakkarap padaiyen thalaivan kaaNmin
thEnamarum pozhil thazhuvum ezhilkoL veedhic
  cezhumaada maaLigaigaL koodan thORum
aanathol seer maRaiyaaLar payilum selvath
  thaNiyazhundoor n-inRu uganda amarar kOvE.
35.meaning: To end the sorrow of the celestials he came and appeared, took the form of a dwarf-brahmacharin, asked the great Bali for three paces, and victoriously measured all the seven worlds for himself; behold my lord, the peerless Primal One whose weapon is the discus. Behold the king of the celestials who delights to stand at cool, wealthy Azhundur, where in every hall of the rich storeyed mansions along the lovely streets embraced by honey-laden groves dwell brahmins of ancient enduring excellence.

36.translit:
pandhaNaindha mel viralaaL seethaik kaagip
  pagalavanmee thiyangaadha ilangai vEndhan
andhamil thiN karam cirangaL puraNdu veezha
  adukaNaiyaal eydhugandha am maan kaaNmin
sen tamizhum vada kalaiyum thigazhndha n-aavar
  thisaimuganE yanaiyavargaL semmai mikka
andhaNarth maaguthiyin pugaiyaar selvath
  thaNiyazhundoor n-inRu uganda amarar kOvE.
36.meaning: For the sake of Sita of the soft fingers fit for the ball, he rejoiced to strike with deadly arrows the king of Lanka, over whom even the sun could not pass, so that his countless mighty arms and heads rolled and fell; behold the Lord. Behold the king of the celestials who delights to stand at wealthy Azhundur, filled with the smoke of the sacrifices of those most upright brahmins, resembling the four-faced Brahma, on whose tongues shine both refined Tamil and the northern arts.

37.translit:
kumbamigu madha vEzham kulaiyak kombu
  paRiththu mazha vidaiyadarth thuk kuravai kOththu
vamb avizhum malark kuzhalaa Laaycci vaiththa
  thayir veNNe yuNDugandha maayOn kaaNmin
cempavaLa maragadha n-an muththam kaaTTath
  thigazh poogam kadhali pala vaLam mikk engum
ampon madhiL pozhil pudaisoozhndh azhagaar selvath
  thaNiyazhundoor n-inRu uganda amarar kOvE.
37.meaning: He broke off the tusk of the great rutting elephant so that it trembled, subdued the young bulls, danced the kuravai round-dance, and joyfully ate the curd and butter set aside by the cowherd-woman of fragrance-shedding flower-tresses; behold the wondrous Lord. Behold the king of the celestials who delights to stand at beautiful, wealthy Azhundur, surrounded by groves and lovely golden ramparts, abounding everywhere in plenty, where shining areca and plantain display the hues of red coral, emerald and fine pearl.

38.translit:
oodERu kanjanodu mallum villum
  oNkariyu muruL sakadu mudaiyac ceTRa
n-eedERu peruvalith thO Ludaiya venRi
  n-ilavupugazh n-Emiyangai n-ediyOn kaaNmin
sEdERu pozhil thazhuvu mezhilkoL veedhith
  tiruvizhavil maNiyaNindha thiNNai thORum
aadERu malark kuzhalaar payilum selvath
  thaNiyazhundoor n-inRu uganda amarar kOvE.
38.meaning: He destroyed the haughty Kamsa, the wrestlers, the bow, the splendid elephant, and the rolling cart so that they broke; behold the tall one of victory and abiding glory, whose hand bears the discus and whose shoulders are of surpassing great strength. Behold the king of the celestials who delights to stand at wealthy Azhundur, where along the lovely streets embraced by lofty groves, on every gem-adorned pyol during the temple festival, dwell women with dancing flowered tresses.

39.translit:
panRiyaay meenaagi yariyaayp paaraip
  padaiththuk kaath thuNDu umizhndha paraman Rannai
anRamararkk adhipathiyum ayanum sEyum
  adipaNiya aNiyazhundoor n-inRa kOvai
kanRi n-edu vEl valava naali n-aadan
  kalikanRi yolisaidha vinbap paadal,
onRinodu n-aangumO raindum vallaar
  olikadalsoo zhulagaaLum umbar thaamE.
39.meaning: Upon the Supreme One who, becoming Boar, Fish and Lion, created the earth, protected it, swallowed it and spat it forth, the Lord who stands at lovely Azhundur where the lord of the celestials, Brahma and Murugan bow at his feet, Kalikanri, lord of the Ali land, skilled with the long spear, composed this resounding, joyful song. Those who can master its ten verses, the one with the four and the five, shall themselves rule the world girt by the roaring sea and become the dwellers of the heavens above.

40.translit:
koontha laarmagizh koova lanaay veNNey
maantha zhunthaiyil kaNdu magizhnthupooy
paanthaL paazhiyil paLLi virumbiya
veentha naicchenru kaaNdumveq kaavuLe.
40.meaning: He became the cowherd who delights the women of beautiful tresses, and was caught eating butter and was seen as a child; rejoicing, he went and lovingly reclined upon his serpent-bed. Let us go and behold that King who chose to rest there—at Tiruvehka.

41.translit:
kalluyarntha nedumathiLsoozh kacchi meeya
 kaLirenrum kadalkidantha kaniye enrum,
alliyampoo malarppoygaip pazhana veeli
 aNiyazhunthoor ninrukantha ammaan enrum,
solluyarntha neduveeNai mulaimeel thaangith
 thoomuruval nagaiyiraiye thoonra nakku,
melviralgaL sivappeythath thadavi yaange
 menkilipool migamizhatrum enpethaiye.
41.meaning: "He is the elephant who dwells in Kanchi girded by tall stone-built lofty ramparts," she says; "He is the sweet fruit reclining on the ocean," she says; "He is the Lord who stood rejoicing in lovely Azhunthur, ringed by paddy fields and lotus ponds bright with blossoms," she says. Holding upon her breast the long, sweet-toned veena, with a faint pure smile dawning on her face she laughs, and stroking it till her soft fingers turn red, my innocent girl prattles on like a tender parrot.

42.translit:
theemaruvu pozhilidaththu malarntha pothaith
 thenathanai vaaymaduththun pedaiyum neeyum,
poomaruvi yini thamarnthu poriyi laarntha
 arukaala siruvaNde thozhuthen unnai,
aamaruvi niraimeyttha amarar koomaan
 aNiyazhunthoor ninraanuk kinre senru,
neemaruvi yanjaathe ninroor maathu
 ninnayanthaaL enriraiye iyambik kaaNe.
42.meaning: O little six-legged bee, speckled with spots, who together with your mate sip the honey from the flowers that have bloomed in the honey-laden grove, and dwell sweetly nestled among the blossoms—I bow to you. Go this very day to Him who stands in lovely Azhunthur, the Lord of the celestials who once herded the cattle, and drawing near without fear, just say to Him, "There is a maiden who has stood here longing for you"—say it and see.

43.translit:
kaar aar maNi nirak kaNNanoor viNNagaram
seer aar gaNapuram seerai thiruvazhunthoor
kaar aar kudanthai kadigai kadalmallai
er aar pozhil soozh idaventhai neermalai
seer aarum maaliruñjoolai thirumohoor (37)
43.meaning: Kannanur Vinnagaram, dark-hued like the gem-colored rain cloud; glory-filled Kanapuram, Cherai, Tiruvazhunthur; cloud-laden Kudanthai, Kadigai, Kadalmallai; Idaventhai girded by beautiful groves, Neermalai; and the glory-rich Maliruncholai, Tirumohur.

44.translit:
annam irai ther azhunthoor ezhum sudarai
then thillaic chiththirakoodaththu en selvanai (66)
44.meaning: The radiant light that rises in Azhunthur, where swans seek their food; my Lord of wealth at Chitrakuta in southern Thillai.

0.translit:
kaLLam manam viLLum vakai karutik kazhal thozhuveer
veLLam mudhu paravai thirai viriya, karai engum
theLLum maNi thigazhum siRu puliyoor chala sayanath
thuLLum, enadhu uLLaththuLum uRaivaaraiyuL LeerE.
0.meaning: You who worship His feet, intending to drive deceit out of your minds, behold: where the flood of the ancient sea spreads its waves and clear gems shine all along the shore at Siruppuliyur, He who reclines in Chala-sayanam dwells also within my heart. Hold Him within you.

1.translit:
theruvil thiri siRu noanbiyar senchoaRRodu kanji maruvi,
pirindhavar vaaimozhi madhiyaadhu vandhu adaiveer,
thiruvil poli maRaiyoar siRu puliyoor chala sayanaththu,
uruvak kuRaL adigaL adi uNarmin uNar veerE.
1.meaning: O you who would draw near, pay no heed to the words of those petty ascetics who roam the streets craving red rice and gruel, those who have strayed apart. Know the feet of the Lord who took the dwarf form, enshrined at Siruppuliyur Chala-sayanam where Vedic brahmins flourish in glory; understand this, you who are able to understand.

2.translit:
paRaiyum vinai thozhudhu uymin neer paNiyum siRu thoNdeer.
aRaiyum punal orupaal vayal orupaal pozhil orupaal
siRai vaNdinam aRaiyum siRu puliyoor chala sayanath thuRaiyum,
iRai adi alladhu onRu iRaiyum aRi yEnE.
2.meaning: O humble servants, worship Him whose grace dissolves your karma and be saved. At Siruppuliyur Chala-sayanam — with rushing waters on one side, fields on one side, groves on one side, where flocks of caged bees hum — the Lord abides. I know nothing else, not even in the least, apart from His feet.

3.translit:
vaanaar madhi podhiyum sadai mazhuvaaLiyodu orupaal,
thaanaagiya thalaivan avan amararkku adhi padhiyaam
thEnaar pozhil thazhuvum siRu puliyoor chala sayanath
thaan aayanadhu, adi alladhu onRu aRiyEn adi yEnE.
3.meaning: He who, on one side, became Himself together with the axe-wielding one (Shiva) whose matted locks enclose the sky-touching moon, He is the supreme Lord, chief of the celestials. He dwells at Siruppuliyur Chala-sayanam embraced by honey-laden groves; I, His servant, know nothing apart from His feet.

4.translit:
nandhaa nedu narakaththidai naNugaa vakai, naaLum
endhaai ena imaiyoar thozhudhu Eththum idam,
eRineerch chendhaamarai malarum siRu puliyoor chala sayanaththu
an-thaamarai adiyaai. unadhu adiyERku aruL puriyE.
4.meaning: This is the place the celestials worship and praise daily, crying 'My Father!', so that they may never approach the unending, vast hell. O Lord whose feet are the lovely lotus, enshrined at Siruppuliyur Chala-sayanam where red lotuses blossom in the splashing waters — grant Your grace to me, Your servant.

5.translit:
muzhu neelamum alaraambalum aravindhamum viravi,
kazhuneerodu madavaar avar kaN vaai mukam malarum,
sezhu neer vayal thazhuvum siRu puliyoor chala sayanam,
thozhu neermaiyadhu udaiyaar adi thozhuvaar thuyar ilarE.
5.meaning: Where deep blue lilies, white night-lilies and red lotuses mingle, and with the lily blooms the eyes, mouths and faces of fair women blossom too — at Siruppuliyur Chala-sayanam, embraced by fertile, watered fields — those who have the nature to worship and who bow at His feet are free of all sorrow.

6.translit:
sEyu Ongu thaN thirumaaliruny choalai malai uRaiyum
maayaa enakku uraiyaai idhu maRai naankinuL aayoa,
theeyu Ombu kai maRaiyoar siRu puliyoor chala sayanath
aayoa unadhu adiyaar manaththaayoa aRi yEnE.
6.meaning: O wondrous One who dwells on the lofty, cool Tirumalirumsolai hill, tell me this: are You within the four Vedas? Are You at Siruppuliyur Chala-sayanam of the brahmins whose hands tend the sacred fire? Are You in the minds of Your devotees? I do not know.

7.translit:
maiyaar vari neelam malark kaNNaar manam vittittu,
uyvaan una kazhalE thozhudhu ezhuvEn, kiLi madavaar
sevvaai mozhi payilum siRu puliyoor chala sayanaththu,
aivaai aravaNai mEl uRai amalaa. aruLaayE.
7.meaning: Letting go my heart from women whose eyes are dark, lined, blossom-like blue lilies, I rise worshipping Your feet alone that I may be saved. O stainless One who reclines upon the five-mouthed serpent-couch at Siruppuliyur Chala-sayanam, where fair women with parrots practice red-lipped speech — grant me Your grace.

8.translit:
karu maa mukil uruvaa! kanal uruvaa! punal uruvaa,
peru maal varai uruvaa! piRa uruvaa! ninadhu uruvaa,
thiru maa makaL maruvum siRu puliyoor chala sayanaththu,
aru maa kadal amudhE! unadhu adiyE saraN aamE.
8.meaning: O You whose form is the great dark cloud! Whose form is fire! Whose form is water! Whose form is the great towering mountain! Whose form is every other thing, all are Your form! O rare nectar of the great ocean, dwelling at Siruppuliyur Chala-sayanam where the divine great Lakshmi abides — Your feet alone are my refuge.

9.translit:
seeraar nedu maRukil siRu puliyoor chala sayanaththu,
Eraar mukil vaNNan thanai imaiyoar peru maanai,
kaaraar vayal mangaikku iRai kaliyan oli maalai,
paaraar ivai paravith thozhap paavam payi laavE.
9.meaning: This garland of resounding verse by Kaliyan, lord of Tirumangai with its cloud-dark fields, sings of the beautiful cloud-hued One, the Lord of the celestials, who dwells at Siruppuliyur Chala-sayanam with its long, glorious streets. For the people of the world who recite and worship with these verses, sin will not cling.

0.translit:
kaNNaar kaNNapuram kadigai kadikamazhum
taNNaar taamaraisoozh talaichchanga meelthisaiyuL
viNNoor naaNmadhiyai virikinRa venjsudarai
kaNNaarak kaNdukoNdu kaLikkinRathing kenRukoloo.
0.meaning: When, oh when, will I gaze my eyes full upon the Lord and rejoice -- He who dwells in lovely Kannapuram, in fragrant Kadigai, and to the west of Talaichangam that is ringed with cool, sweetly perfumed lotuses, He who is the day-moon of the celestials and the spreading, fierce-rayed sun?

1.translit:
kaNNanai kaNNapurattaanai then naRaiyoor
mannum maNimaadakkooyil maNaaLanai
kal navil thooL kaaLaiyaik kaNdu aanguk kaithozhuthu
en nilaimai ellaam aRiviththaal em perumaan
than aruLum aagamum thaaraaneel thannai naan (72)
1.meaning: Kannan, the Lord of Kannapuram, the bridegroom of the jewelled-mansion temple that abides in southern Naraiyur, the youthful one whose shoulders are firm as rock -- if, having beheld Him there and worshipped Him with folded hands and made known to Him all my plight, my Lord still does not grant me His grace and His embracing form, then I, of Him...

0.translit:
nummait tozhuthoom nuntham paNisey thirukkum nummadiyoom,
immaik kinbam pettroo menthaay indhaLooreere,
emmaik kadithaak karuma maruLi aavaa venthirangi,
nammai yorukaal kaatti nadanthaal naanga Luyyoomee?
0.meaning: We worship You; we are Your servants who abide doing Your service. O Lord of Indaloor, we have gained bliss even in this life, our Father! If You graciously grant us our task swiftly, sighing 'alas' in compassion, and showing Yourself to us but once go on Your way, shall we who are Yours not be saved?

1.translit:
sinthai thannuL neengaa thirun thiruvee maruviniya
maindhaa, antha NaaLi maalee soolai mazhakaLiree
nandhaa viLakkin sudaree naraiyoor nintra nambee, en
enthaay, indha Looraay, adiyeer kiraiyu mirangaayee.
1.meaning: O treasure who abide unceasingly within my thought, O sweet youth in whom Lakshmi has united! O Tirumal of beautiful Tirunaangoor of the sages, O young elephant of the groves, O flame of the unfading lamp, O Nambi who stands at Naraiyoor, my Father! O Lord of Indaloor, will You not show a little compassion to this servant of Yours?

2.translit:
peesu kintra thithuvee vaiya meeradi yaaLantha,
moosi vaNdu muralum kaNNi mudiyeer, ummaikkaaNum
aasai yennum kadalil veezhnthing kayarnthoom, ayalaarum
eesu kintra thithuvee kaaNum indha Looreere.
2.meaning: O You who measured the whole earth with two strides, wearing a garland-crown over which bees swarm and hum! We have fallen into the sea called the longing to behold You, and here grow weary. This is the very thing strangers all around mock, O Lord of Indaloor.

3.translit:
aasai vazhuvaa theththu emaking kizhukkaayththu, adiyoorkkuth
theesa mariya vumakkee yaaLaayth thirigin troomukku,
kaasi noLiyil thigazhum vaNNam kaatteer, emperumaan
vaasi valleer indha Looreer vaazhnthee poomneeree.
3.meaning: Praising You with unfailing love has here become a fault for us; for us servants who wander as Yours alone, known to the whole world, You do not show the form that gleams like the brilliance of gold. O my Lord, You who are skilled in discrimination, O Lord of Indaloor, may You alone go on prospering!

4.translit:
theeyem perumaan neerem perumaan thisaiyu mirunilanu
maay, em perumaa naagi nintraa ladiyoom kaaNoomaal,
thaayem perumaan thanthai thanthai yaaveer, adiyeemuk
keeyem perumaa nalleer roneer indha Looreere.
4.meaning: My Lord is the fire, my Lord is the water; though You stand as the directions and the broad earth, having become my Lord, we Your servants do not behold You. You are my Lord who is mother, You are father and father's father; are You then not the very Lord of us servants, O Lord of Indaloor?

5.translit:
sollaa thozhiya killeen arintha sollil, nummadiyaar,
ellaa roodu mokka veNNi yirunthee radiyeenai,
nalla rariveer theeyaa rariveer namakkiv vulagaththil,
ellaa marriveer eethee yariyeer indha Looreere.
5.meaning: I cannot keep from speaking the words I know: You have reckoned this servant as equal with all Your devotees. You know the good, You know the wicked; in this world You know all that is ours, yet this one thing alone You do not know, O Lord of Indaloor.

6.translit:
maatteer aaneer paNineer koLLa emmaip paNiyariyaa
vitteer, ithanai veeree sonnoom indha Looreere,
kaatteer aaneer nuntha madikkaL kaattil umakkintha,
naattee vanthu thoNdaraana naanga Luyyoomee.
6.meaning: You have become unwilling to accept service from us; You have left us not knowing service. We have told You this apart, O Lord of Indaloor. You have become one who will not show; if You will not show us Your feet, shall we who have come into this land and become Your servants be saved?

7.translit:
munnivaNNam paalin vaNNam muzhuthum nilainintra
pinnaivaNNam koNdalvaNNam vaNNameNNungkaal
ponninvaNNam maNiyin vaNNam puraiyunthirumeeni
innavaNNamentru kaatteer indhaLooreere!
7.meaning: Your former hue was the hue of milk, and the hue that abode fully thereafter is the hue of the rain-cloud; when one reckons Your hues, Your sacred form resembles the hue of gold and the hue of the sapphire-gem. You do not show, saying 'such is the hue,' O Lord of Indaloor!

8.translit:
enthai thanthai thammaa nentren tremeree remare zheeLavum,
vanthu nintra thoNdaroorkkee vaasi valleeraal,
sinthai thannuL munthi nittreer sirithum thirumeeni,
intha vaNNa mentru kaatteer indha Looreere!
8.meaning: Crying again and again 'my Father, father's father,' our forefathers and our forefathers' forefathers through the generations — to the devotees who came and stood thus You are skilled in discrimination. You stand foremost within my thought, yet not even a little do You show Your sacred form, saying 'this is its hue,' O Lord of Indaloor!

9.translit:
eeraar pozhilsoozh indha Loori lenthai perumaanai,
kaaraar puravin mangai veenthan kaliya noliseytha,
seeraarin sol maalai katruth thirivaa rulagaththu,
aaraa ravaree yamararkennu mamara raavaaree.
9.meaning: This sweet-worded garland of praise was composed by Kaliyan, the king of Mangai of the cloud-covered woodlands, upon my Father the Lord who stands in Indaloor girt by beauty-filled groves. Whoever in this world learns it and goes about reciting it — they, whoever they be, shall forever become as the immortals to the gods.

10.translit:
anna uruvin ariyai thirumeyyaththu
in amutha veLLaththai indhaloor anthaNanai
mannu mathiL kacchi veeLukkai aaL ariyai
Manniya paadagaththu em maindhanai vehkaavil
10.meaning: He is the Hari of such form, the flood of sweet nectar at Tirumeyyam, the holy one of Indaloor; He is the man-lion (Narasimha) at Veelukkai in Kachchi of enduring ramparts, my Lord who is the youth at the abiding Paadagam, and He who reclines at Tiruvehka.

0.translit:
taavaLan thulaka muRRum thadamalarp poygai pukku,
naavaLam navinRing kettha naagaththin nadukkan theerthaay,
maavaLam perugi mannum maRaiyavar vaazhum naangaik,
kaavaLam paadi meeya kaNNanee kaLaikaNeeye.
0.meaning: You who measured the whole world with Your stride entered the broad lotus-pond, and as the elephant praised You with eloquent tongue You removed the trembling fear of the great serpent. O Kannan who abide at Naangai's Kavaḷampaadi, where wealth of cattle abounds and Vedic brahmins dwell in plenty, You are my sole refuge.

1.translit:
maNNidan theena maagi maavali valitho laippaan,
viNNavar veNdach chenRu veeLviyil kuRaiyi randhaay,
thuNNena maaRRaar thammaith tholaiththavar naangai meeya,
kaNNanee kaava LandhaN paadiyaay kaLaika Neeye.
1.meaning: To shatter the might of Mahabali You became a dwarf and, at the gods' request, went and begged a boon at his sacrifice. O You who in an instant destroyed Your foes and abide at Naangai, O Kannan of cool Kavaḷampaadi, You are my sole refuge.

2.translit:
uruththezhu vaali maarvil oorugaNai yuruva vootti,
karuththudaith thambik kinpak kathimudi yarasa Liththaay,
paruththezhu palavum maavum pazhamvizhun thozhugum naangaik
karuththanee kaava LandhaN paadiyaay kaLaika Neeye.
2.meaning: You drove a single arrow through the chest of the surging, wrathful Vali and granted the joyous, swift-won crown and kingdom to his wise younger brother Sugriva. O Lord cherished by hearts at Naangai, where ripe jackfruit and mangoes drop and flow from their thick laden trees, O You of cool Kavaḷampaadi, You are my sole refuge.

3.translit:
munaimagath tharakkan maaLa mudigaLpath thaRuththu veezhththu,aang
kanaiyavaR kiLaiya vaRkee arasaLith tharuLi naanee,
sunaigaLil kayalgaL paayach churumbutheen nugarum naangaik,
kanaikazhal kaava LandhaN paadiyaay kaLaika Neeye.
3.meaning: That the battle-front demon Ravana might perish, You cut off and felled his ten heads, and then graciously bestowed the kingdom upon his younger brother Vibhishana. O You of resounding anklets at Naangai, where carp leap in the springs and bees sip honey, O Lord of cool Kavaḷampaadi, You are my sole refuge.

4.translit:
padavara vucchi thanmel paayndhu pannadangaL seydhu,
madavaral mangai thannai maarvagath thiruththi naanee,
thadavarai thangu maadath thagupugazh naangai meeya,
kadavuLee kaava LandhaN paadiyaay kaLaika Neeye.
4.meaning: You leapt upon the head of the hooded serpent Kalinga and danced many dances, and You who seated the tender young lady Lakshmi upon Your chest. O God abiding at renowned Naangai with mansions tall as great mountains, O Lord of cool Kavaḷampaadi, You are my sole refuge.

5.translit:
mallarai yattu maaLak kanjanai malaindhu konRu,
pallara savindhu veezhap paaradhap poormu diththaay,
nallaraN kaavin neezhal naRaikamazh naangai meeya,
kallaraN kaava LandhaN paadiyaay kaLaika Neeye.
5.meaning: You struck down the wrestlers so they perished, fought and slew Kamsa, and brought to its close the Bharata war in which many kings withered and fell. O You who abide at fragrant Naangai, shaded by groves of good defence, O Lord of stone-walled, cool Kavaḷampaadi, You are my sole refuge.

6.translit:
mooththavaR karasu veeNdi munbu thoodhezhun tharuLi,
maaththamar paagan veezha madhakari maruppo siththaay,
pooththamar soolai yoongip punalparan thozhugum, naangaik
kaaththavanee kaava LandhaN paadiyaay kaLaika Neeye.
6.meaning: Seeking the kingdom for the elder brothers, You graciously went of old as their envoy, and when its great mahout fell You snapped off the tusk of the rutting elephant. O Protector of Naangai, where blossoming groves rise high and waters spread and flow, O Lord of cool Kavaḷampaadi, You are my sole refuge.

7.translit:
eeviLang kannik kaagi imaiyavar koonaich cheRRu,
kaavaLam kadithi Ruththuk kaRpagam koNdu pondhaay,
poovaLam pozhilgaL soozhndha purandharan seydha naangaik,
kaavaLam paadi meeya kaNNanee kaLaika Neeye.
7.meaning: For the sake of the lovely young maiden Satyabhama You overcame Indra, the king of the immortals, broke open the guarded grove and carried off the Parijata tree. O Kannan who abide at Kavaḷampaadi in Naangai, founded by Indra and ringed by groves rich in flowers, You are my sole refuge.

8.translit:
sandhamaay samaya maagich chamayavaim poodha maagi,
andhamaa yaadhi yaagi arumaRai yavaiyu maanaay,
mandhamaar pozhilka doaRum madamayi laalum naangai,
kandhamaar kaava LandhaN paadiyaay kaLaika Neeye.
8.meaning: You became the cadenced order, the systems of belief, and the five elements of which those systems consist; You became the end and the beginning, and the precious Vedas themselves. O Lord at Naangai, where in every gentle-breeze-filled grove the lovely peacocks dance, O You of fragrant, cool Kavaḷampaadi, You are my sole refuge.

9.translit:
maavaLam perugi mannum maRaiyavar vaazhum, naangaik
kaavaLam paadi meeya kaNNaNaik kaliyan sonna,
paavaLam paththum vallaar paarmisai yarasa raagi,
koaviLa mannar thaazhak kudainizhal polivar thaame.
9.meaning: Those who master these ten rich verses that Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) sang upon Kannan abiding at Kavaḷampaadi in Naangai, where the wealth of cattle abounds and Vedic brahmins dwell, shall themselves become kings upon the earth, and, with lesser royal lords bowing before them, shall shine in the shade of the royal parasol.

0.translit:
oru kuRaLaay iru nilam moo adi maN veeNDi
 ulaganaiththum eer adiyaal oDukki, onRum
taruga enaa maavaliyaich chiRaiyil vaiththa
 taaDaaLan taaL aNaiveer,
takka keerththi aru maRaiyin tiraL naankum veeLvi aindum
 angangaL avaiyaaRum isaigaL eezhum,
teruvil mali vizhaa vaLamum siRakkum
 kaazhich cheeraama viNNagaree seermin neeree.
0.meaning: He who became a single small dwarf, begged for three feet of land upon the wide earth, then with two strides measured all the worlds and pressed them down, and when Mahabali would grant nothing more, imprisoned him in bondage — you who would reach the feet of that mighty one of fitting renown! Where the four bundles of the precious Vedas, the five sacrifices, those six limbs, and the seven musical notes abound, and where the wealth of overflowing festivals on the streets flourishes — go and join Kaazhi Sirama Vinnagar.

1.translit:
naanmuganaaL migaith tarukkai irukku vaaymai
 nalam migu seer uromasanaal naviRRi,
nakkan oon mugamaar talaiyooD ooN ozhiththa endai
 oLi malarch chee vaDi aNaiveer, uzhu seey ooDach
choon mugamaar vaLai aLai vaay uguththa muththai
 tolkurugu sinai enach choozhndi yanga,
teen mugamaar kamala vayal seel paay kaazhich
 cheeraama viNNagaree seermin neeree.
1.meaning: Through the truthful Rig Veda words of the greatly virtuous and excellent sage Romasa, He humbled the swollen pride of the four-faced Brahma, and removed the curse of feeding from the flesh-filled skull that clung to the naked Siva's hand — you who would reach His radiant lotus-like red feet! Where, as the ploughing oxen move, the pregnant conches shed pearls at the mouths of their burrows, and the old herons mistake them for their young and gather round them, and where carp leap in the honey-laden lotus fields — go and join Kaazhi Sirama Vinnagar.

2.translit:
vai aNainda nuthik kooTTu varaagam onRaay
 maNN ellaam iDandeDuththu madangaL seydu,
neyy aNainda tigiriyinaal vaaNan tiNDooL
 neernda andaaL aNaigiRpeer, neydaloDu
mai aNainda kuvaLaigaL tang kaNgaL enRum
 malark kumudam vaay enRum kaDaisimaargaL,
seyy aNaindu kaLai kaLaiyaadu eeRum
 kaazhich cheeraama viNNagaree seermin neeree.
2.meaning: As a single boar with a sharp tusk like a blade, He scooped up the entire earth and lifted it, performing wondrous feats, and with His oil-smeared discus He hewed down the strong shoulders of Bana — you who would reach His feet that came forth thus! Where the women working the fields, saying the dark blue-lily and water-lily flowers are their own kohl-darkened eyes and the lotus blooms are their mouths, stand there forgetting to weed — go and join Kaazhi Sirama Vinnagar.

3.translit:
panji al mel aDip pinnai tiRaththu
 munnaaL paay viDaigaL eezh aDarththum, ponnan paimpooN
nenj iDandu kurudi uga ugir veel aaNDa
 ninmalan taaL aNaigiRpeer, neela maalaith
tanj uDaiya iruL tazhaippath taraLam aange
 taN madiyin nilaak kaaTTap pavaLan tannaal,
senj suDara veyil virikkum azhag aar
 kaazhich cheeraama viNNagaree seermin neeree.
3.meaning: In days of old, for the sake of Nappinnai of soft cotton-tender feet, He subdued the seven charging bulls, and with His nail like a spear He tore open the chest of the golden, fresh-jewelled Hiranya so the blood gushed forth — you who would reach the feet of that spotless one! Where the blue water-lily garlands spread out their sheltering darkness like night, and the pearls there cast a glow like the cool moon's light, and the corals spread red rays like the brilliant sunshine — go and join beautiful Kaazhi Sirama Vinnagar.

4.translit:
tevvaaya maRa mannar kurudi koNDu
 tirukkulaththil iRandoorkkuth tiruththi seydu,
vevvaaya maa keeNDu veezham aTTa
 viNNavarkkoon taaL aNaiveer, vigirda maadar
avvaaya vaaL neDung kaN kuvaLai kaaTTa
 aravindam mugang kaaTTa arugee aambal,
sevvaayin tiraL kaaTTum vayal soozh
 kaazhich cheeraama viNNagaree seermin neeree.
4.meaning: With the blood of the cruel-mouthed warlike kings He performed funeral rites for the ancestors who had died in the sacred lineage, He split open the fierce-mouthed horse-demon and slew the elephant — you who would reach the feet of that Lord of the celestials! Where, surrounded by fields, the blue water-lilies display the bright long eyes of incomparable women, the lotuses display their faces, and beside them the white lilies display the cluster of their red mouths — go and join Kaazhi Sirama Vinnagar.

5.translit:
paing kaN viRal semmugaththu vaali maaLap
 paDar vanaththuk kavandanoDum paDai aarth tiNkai,
veng kaN viRal viraadan uga viRkuniththa
 viNNavarkkoon taaL aNaiveer, veRpup poolum
tungamuga maaLigaimee laayang kooRum
 tuDiyiDaiyaar mugak kamalch chooti tannaal,
tingaL mugam pani paDaikkum azhag aar
 kaazhich cheeraama viNNagaree seermin neeree.
5.meaning: So that the green-eyed, valiant, red-faced Vali perished, and with Kabandha in the spreading forest, and so that the fierce-eyed mighty Viradha of armed strong arms perished, He bent His bow — you who would reach the feet of that Lord of the celestials! Where, atop the lofty mansions tall as mountains, the slender-waisted women gather and converse, and by the radiance of their lotus faces the moon's face grows pale with dew — go and join beautiful Kaazhi Sirama Vinnagar.

6.translit:
poruvil valam puri arakkan muDigaL paththum
 puRRu maRindana poolap puvimeel sinda,
seruvil valam puri silaik kai malaith tooL
 veendan tiruvaDi seerndu uygiRpeer, tirai neerth
teLgi maruvi valam puri kaidaik kazhi ooD aaDi
 vayal naNNi mazhai taru neer tavazh kaal manni
teruvil valam puri taraLam eenum
 kaazhich cheeraama viNNagaree seermin neeree.
6.meaning: So that the matchless ten crowns of the demon (Ravana) scattered on the earth as if anthills had been overturned, the king with mountain-like shoulders and a curved bow in His valiant right hand — you who would take refuge and be saved by reaching the sacred feet of that king! Where the clear wave-water of the right-spiralling conches mingles and plays through the screw-pine backwaters, reaching the fields, where the rain-bearing water creeps along the channels and abides, and the right-spiralling conches in the streets give forth pearls — go and join Kaazhi Sirama Vinnagar.

7.translit:
paTT aravee r agal algul pavaLach chevvaay
 paNai neDun dooL piNai neDung kaN paalaa min sol,
maTT avizhung kuzhalikkaa vaanoor kaavil
 marang koNarndaan aDi aNaiveer, aNilgaL taava
neTTilaiya karung kamugin seng kaay veezha
 neeL palavin taazh sinaiyil nerungu, peenath
theTTa pazham sidaindu maduch choriyum
 kaazhich cheeraama viNNagaree seermin neeree.
7.meaning: For the sake of His lady (Satyabhama) of broad loins like a hooded serpent, of coral-red mouth, of full long shoulders, of long deer-like eyes, of sweet milk-like speech and of fragrance-shedding tresses, He brought the (Parijata) tree from the celestials' grove — you who would reach the feet of that one! Where, as the squirrels leap, the red unripe fruit of the long-leaved dark areca falls, and on the low boughs of the tall jackfruit the dense, plump ripe fruit bursts open and pours out its honey — go and join Kaazhi Sirama Vinnagar.

8.translit:
piRai tangu saDaiyaanai valaththee vaiththup
 piramanaith tann undiyilee tooRRu viththu,
kaRai tangu veel taDang kaN tiruvai maarbil
 kalanda andaaL aNaigiRpeer, kazhuneer kooDith
turai tangu kamalaththuth tuyinRu kaidaith
 tooD aarum pothi sooRRuch chuNNam naNNi,
siRai vaNDu kaLi paaDum vayal soozh
 kaazhich cheeraama viNNagaree seermin neeree.
8.meaning: He who placed on His right side Siva of the crescent-bearing matted locks and brought forth Brahma from His own navel — you who would reach the feet of that one who holds upon His chest Lakshmi of large eyes like the stained (kohl-lined) lance! Where the winged bees, joining the red water-lilies, sleep upon the lotuses of the ghats, then reach the powdery pollen of the screw-pine's leaf-wrapped clusters and sing in joy, amid the surrounding fields — go and join Kaazhi Sirama Vinnagar.

9.translit:
sengamalath ayan anaiya maRaiyoor
 kaazhich cheeraama viNNagar en sengaN maalai
ang kamalath taDa vayal soozh aali naaDan
 aruL maari araTTa mukki aDaiyaar seeyam
kongu malark kuzhaliyar veeL mangai veendan
 koRRa veel parakaalan kaliyan sonna
sanga mugath tamizh maalai paththum vallaar
 taDang kaDal soozh ulagukkuth talaivar taamee.
9.meaning: This garland on my red-eyed Lord (Tirumal) of Kaazhi Sirama Vinnagar, where dwell the Vedic Brahmins resembling Brahma seated on the red lotus — sung by Kaliyan (Tirumangai), lord of the land of Aali surrounded by broad lotus-bearing fields, the merciful raincloud who crushes the arrogant, a lion to his foes, the beloved of women with fragrant flower-decked tresses, the king of Mangai, the wielder of the victorious spear, the Parakalan: those who can master all ten of this Tamil garland fashioned of conch-like (eloquent) words shall themselves become lords of the world girdled by the vast ocean.

0.translit:
thirumadanthai maNmadanthai irupaalum thigazhath
 theevinaigaL pooyagala adiyavarkaT kenRum
aruLnadanthu, iv vEzhulagath thavarppaNiya
 vaanOr amarnthEttha irundha-idam
perumpugazhvE thiyarvaazh tharumidangaL malargaLmigu kaithaigaL sengazhuneer
 thaamaraigaL thadangadoRu midangadoRum thigazha,
aruvidangaL pozhilthazhuvi yezhilthigazhu naangoor
 arimEya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjE.
0.meaning: With Lakshmi the lady of fortune and Bhumi the earth-goddess shining on either side of Him, ever extending His grace upon His devotees so their cruel deeds depart, He resides in the place where the dwellers of these seven worlds bow before Him and the celestials gladly abide and praise Him. In the abodes where Brahmins of great renown flourish, where in every tank and every spot abundant flowers, fragrant screw-pine, red water-lilies and lotuses gleam, and beautiful groves embrace the watery places — there in Naangoor, O my humble heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram.

1.translit:
venRimigu naraganura mathuvazhiya visiRum
 viRalaazhith thadakkaiyan viNNavarkatku, anRu
kunRukodu kuraikadalaik kadainthamutha maLikkum
 gurumaNiyen naaramutham kulaviyuRai kOyil,
enRumigu perunjelvath thezhilviLangu maRaiyOr
 Ezhisaiyum kELvigaLu miyanRaperung guNaththOr,
anRulagam padaiththavanE yanaiyavargaL naangoor
 arimEya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjE.
1.meaning: He of the broad hand wielding the mighty discus, which He hurled to cleave open the chest of the victorious Naraka — long ago He took up a mountain and churned the roaring ocean to grant the celestials nectar. That precious-gem, my sweet ambrosia, joyfully dwells in this temple. Where Brahmins of ever-abounding great wealth shine in beauty, masters of the seven musical notes and sacred lore and of great virtue, men like Him who fashioned the worlds of old — there in their Naangoor, O my humble heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram.

2.translit:
umbarumiv vEzhulagu mEzhkadalu mellaam
 uNdapiraan aNdargaLmun kaNdumagizha veytha,
kumbamigu mathayaanai marupposiththuk
 kanjan kunjipidith thadiththapiraan kOyil,
marung kengum paimponodu veNmuththam palapunnai kaattap
 palanganigaL thEnkaattap padavaravE ralgul,
ambanaiya kaNmadavaar magizhveythu naangoor
 arimEya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjE.
2.meaning: The Lord who swallowed the heavens, these seven worlds and the seven seas, who leapt forth so the celestials beheld and rejoiced; who snapped the tusks of the rutting, frontal-globed elephant, and who seized Kamsa by the hair and struck him down — this is His temple. Where on every side fresh gold and white pearls are displayed in plenty, the punnai trees show their blossoms and the ripe fruits offer their honey — there in Naangoor, where maidens with arrow-like eyes and hips like the cobra's hood rejoice, O my humble heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram.

3.translit:
OOdaatha vaaLariyi nuruvamathu koNdu
 anRulappilmigu peruvaraththa hiraNiyanaip paRRi,
vaadaatha vaLLugiraal piLanthavanRan maganuk
 karuLseythaan vaazhumidam malligaiseng kazhuneer,
sEdERu malarccherunthi sezhungamugam paaLai
 seNbagangaL maNanaaRum vaNpozhili noodE,
aadERu vayalaalaip pugaikamazhu naangoor
 arimEya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjE.
3.meaning: Taking the form of the man-lion that never retreats, He of old seized Hiranya who held boundless, vast boons, tore him open with His sharp, unwithering claws, and granted His grace to that one's son. This is the place where He dwells: amid lush groves fragrant with jasmine, red water-lilies, the long-stalked blossoming serunthi, rich areca-palm spathes and shenbaga flowers, and where the smoke from the bustling sugarcane mills set among the fields wafts forth — there in Naangoor, O my humble heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram.

4.translit:
kaNdavartham manammagizha maavalithan vELvik
 kaLavilmigu siRukuRaLaay moovadiyen Riranthittu,
aNdamumiv valaikadalu mavanigaLumellaam
 aLanthapiraa namarumidam vaLangoLpozhi layalE,
aNdamuRu muzhavoliyum vaNdinanga Loliyum
 arumaRaiyi noliyummada vaarsilambi noliyum,
aNdamuRu malaikadali nolithigazhu naangoor
 arimEya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjE.
4.meaning: As a tiny dwarf of measureless beauty, gladdening the hearts of all who saw Him, He begged at Mahabali's sacrifice for three paces of land, then measured out the cosmos, this surging sea and all the worlds. This is the abode where that Lord abides. Beside its fertile groves, the sky-filling sound of drums, the hum of swarms of bees, the chanting of the rare Vedas and the jingling of women's anklets — the very din that fills the heavens like that of mountain and sea — there in Naangoor, O my humble heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram.

5.translit:
vaaNedungaN malarkkoonthal maithilikkaa
 ilangai mannanmudi yorupathumthO LirupathumpO yuthira,
thaaNedunthin silaivaLaiththa thayarathansEy
 enthan thaniccharaNvaa navarkkarasu karuthumidam, thadamaar
sENidangoL malarkkamalam sElkayalgaL vaaLai
 sennelodu maduththariya vuthirndhasezhu muththam,
vaaNedungaN kadaisiyargaL vaarumaNi naangoor
 arimEya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjE.
5.meaning: For the sake of Sita of Mithila, with long sword-bright eyes and flower-decked hair, He bent His mighty long bow so the ten crowns and twenty shoulders of Lanka's king fell and scattered. The son of Dasaratha, my own sole refuge, the king the celestials hold dear — this is the place He cherishes. Where, in the broad far-stretching expanses, lotus blossoms, sael and kayal fish and vaalai fish together with the red paddy press in, and the rich pearls shaken loose are gathered up by the long sword-eyed gleaning women — there in fair Naangoor, O my humble heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram.

6.translit:
theemanaththaan kanjanathu vanjanaiyil thiriyum
 thEnuganum poothanaitha naaruyirum seguththaan,
kaamanaiththaan payanthakaru mEniyudai yammaan
 karuthumidam poruthupunal thuRaithuRaimuth thunthi,
naamanaththaal manthirangaL naalvEdham ainthu
 vELviyO daarangam navinRukalai payinRu, ang
kaamanaththu maRaiyavargaL payilumaNi naangoor
 arimEya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjE.
6.meaning: He destroyed the very life of Dhenuka, who roamed in the deceit of evil-minded Kamsa, and of Putana too; the dark-hued Lord who fathered Kama — this is the place He cherishes. Where the surging waters wash pearls ashore at every ghat, and where the Brahmins, with desire-filled hearts, recite from the heart the mantras, the four Vedas, the five sacrifices and the six auxiliary sciences, practising all the arts — there in fair Naangoor, O my humble heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram.

7.translit:
kanRathanaal viLaveRinthu kaniyuthirththa kaaLai
 kaamaruseer mugilvaNNan kaaligaLmun kaappaan,
kunRathanaal mazhaithaduththuk kudamaadu kooththan
 kulavumidam kodimathiLgaL maaLigaikO purangaL,
thunRumaNi maNdapangaL saalaigaLthoo maRaiyOr
 thokkeeNdith thozhuthiyodu migappayilum sOlai,
anRalarvaay mathuvuNdang kalimuralu naangoor
 arimEya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjE.
7.meaning: The youth who flung the calf to strike down the wood-apple tree and bring its fruit raining down, the cloud-hued Lord of lovely glory; to guard the cattle He held up a mountain to ward off the rain, the dancer who danced with the pots — this is His dear place. Where banner-topped ramparts, mansions and gate-towers, jewel-thronged pavilions and halls abound, and groves where the pure Brahmins gather thronging in great assembly to worship — there in Naangoor, where bees that drink the honey of new-blown flowers hum, O my humble heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram.

8.translit:
vanjanaiyaal vanthavaLtha nuyiruNdu vaayttha
 thayiruNdu veNNeyamu thuNdu, valimikka
kanjanuyi rathuvuNdiv vulaguNda kaaLai
 karuthumidam kaavirisan thagilkanaga munthi,
manjulavu pozhiloodum vayaloodum vanthu
 vaLangoduppa maamaRaiyOr maamalargaL thoovi,
anjaliththang karisaraNen RiraignjumaNi naangoor
 arimEya viNNagaram vaNangumada nenjE.
8.meaning: He drank up the life of her who came by deceit (Putana), ate the well-set curd and the white butter, took the life of mighty Kamsa, and swallowed this world — that youth holds this place dear. Where the Kaveri brings down sandalwood, eagle-wood and gold, flowing through the cloud-touched groves and the fields to bestow abundance, and where the great Brahmins scatter choice flowers, fold their hands in worship and bow saying 'Hari is our refuge' — there in fair Naangoor, O my humble heart, worship Arimeya Vinnagaram.

9.translit:
senRusina vidaiyEzhum padavadarththup pinnai
 sevviththOL puNarnthugantha thirumaalthan kOyil,
anRayanu maransEyu manaiyavargaL naangoor
 arimEya viNNagara mamarnthasezhung kunRai,
kanRinedu vElvalavan mangaiyartham kOmaan
 kaliganRi yolimaalai yainthinodu moonRum,
onRinodu monRumivai kaRRuvallaar ulagath
 thuththamarkat kuththamaraa yumbarumaa vargaLE.
9.meaning: Tirumal who joyfully embraced the lovely shoulders of Nappinnai after charging at and quelling the seven furious bulls — this is His temple. The rich hill who gladly abides in Arimeya Vinnagaram in Naangoor of those who are like Brahma and the son-of-Siva of old — of Him, Kaliyan, lord of Mangai, master of the long wrathful spear, sang this garland of melody: those able to learn these ten verses, five with three and one with one, shall become the noblest among the noble of this world, and indeed celestials in the world above.

0.translit:
koazhiyum kooDalum koayil koNDa koavalar-eayoppar kunRamanna
paazhiyum thoaLumoar naanku uDaiyar paNDivar thammaiyum kaNDaRiyoam
vaazhiyaroa ivar vaNNam eNNil maakaDal poanRuLar kaiyil veyya,
aazhiyonRu eanthiyoar shangku paRRi achchoa oruvar azhagiyavaa.
0.meaning: They who have taken as Their temples Uraiyoor (the place of the cock) and Madurai (Kooshal) resemble the cowherd (Krishna); like a mountain They are, possessed of four mighty strong shoulders. Never before have we beheld such a One as this. Long may They live! If one reckons Their hue, They are like the great dark ocean. Bearing in Their hand a fierce discus and grasping a conch — ah, how beautiful is this One!

0.translit:
kamba maakatal aDaitt-ilangaikku man kadir-muDi avai pattum
ampin-aal aRuttu, arasu avan tambikku aLittavan uRai-kooyil
sempalaa-nirai seNpakam maadhavi soothakam vaazhaigaL soozh
vambu-ulaam-kamuku oongiya naangkoor vaN-puruDoottamamee.
0.meaning: He dammed the trembling great ocean and cut off with His arrows all ten of the radiant crowns of the king of Lanka, then granted the kingdom to that one's younger brother (Vibhishana) — the temple where He dwells is Vanpurudottamam of fertile Nangur, surrounded by rows of sembalu trees, shenbaga, madhavi, mango and plantain groves, where fragrant areca palms rise tall.

1.translit:
pallavam tikazh poong-kadambu eeRi-ak-kaaLiyan paNa-varangil,
ollai vandu-uRap paaynd-aru naDam-seyda umbarkkoon uRai-kooyil,
nalla vendazhal moonRu naal-vedam aivelviyooDu aaRangam,
valla vandaNar malkiya naangkoor vaN-puruDoottamamee.
1.meaning: Climbing the kadamba tree bright with sprouting shoots, He leapt swiftly upon the hood-stage of the serpent Kaliya and danced His rare dance — the temple where that Lord of the celestials dwells is Vanpurudottamam of bountiful Nangur, thronged with skilled Brahmins versed in the three good fierce fire-sacrifices, the four Vedas, the five yajnas and the six Vedangas.

2.translit:
aNDar-aanavar vaanavar koonukku enRu amaitta sooRu aduvellaam
uNDu koo-nirai meyttu avai kaattavan ukandu inidu uRai-kooyil,
koNDalaar muzhavil kuLir-vaar-pozhil kula-mayil naDam-aaDa,
vaNDu taan-isai paaDiDu naangkoor vaN-puruDoottamamee.
2.meaning: All the food prepared and offered for Indra, lord of the gods of the heavens, He ate up; then He grazed the herds of cattle and protected them — the temple where He gladly and sweetly dwells is Vanpurudottamam of bountiful Nangur, where in cool wide groves the noble peacocks dance to the thunder-like drumming of the rain clouds, and the bees sing their music.

3.translit:
parum-kai yaanaiyin kombinaip paRittu adan paakanaic chaaDip pukku,
oRunga mallaraik konRu pin kanjanai udaittavan uRai-kooyil,
karumbin-ooDu uyar saaligaL viLaitaru kazhaniyil mali-vaavi
marungu-elaam-pozhil oongiya naangkoor vaN-puruDoottamamee.
3.meaning: He wrenched off the tusk of the broad-trunked elephant and struck down its mahout, then entering, He destroyed the wrestlers utterly and afterward kicked Kamsan to death — the temple where He dwells is Vanpurudottamam of bountiful Nangur, where amid the sugarcane the tall paddy ripens in the fields, with abundant ponds all around and groves rising high on every side.

4.translit:
saaDu pooy-vizhat taaL-nimirtt-eesan tan paDaiyoDum kiLaiyooDum
ooDa vaaNanai aayiram tooLgaLum tuNittavan uRai-kooyil,
aaDu-vaan-koDi akal-visumbu aNavip-pooyp pakalavan-oLi maRaikkum
maaDa maaLigai soozhdaru naangkoor vaN-puruDoottamamee.
4.meaning: He stretched out His foot so that the cart (the demon Sakatasura) fell shattered; and so that Siva fled with his army and his kin, He severed the thousand shoulders of Banasura — the temple where He dwells is Vanpurudottamam of bountiful Nangur, encircled by mansions and palaces whose waving flags reach into the wide sky and hide the light of the sun.

5.translit:
angaiyaal aDi moonRu neer-eeRRu ayan alar-koDu tozhud-eetta,
gangai pootara kaal-nimirtt-aruLiya kaNNan vandu uRai-kooyil,
kongai koongu-avai kaaTTa vaay kumudangaL kaaTTa maa-padumangaL,
mangaimaar mukam kaaTTiDu naangkoor vaN-puruDoottamamee.
5.meaning: Receiving the gift of three paces with water in His cupped palm, He stretched out His foot — so that the Ganga came forth — while Brahma worshipped and praised Him with flowers: the temple where that Kannan came and dwells is Vanpurudottamam of bountiful Nangur, where the kongu buds show forth as maidens' breasts, the water-lilies show their lips, and the great lotuses show forth their faces.

6.translit:
uLaiya oN-tiRal pon-peyaroon tanadu uram piLandu udirattai
aLaiyum, venjinat-tari-pari keeRiya appan vandu uRai-kooyil,
iLaiya mangaiyar iNai-aDic chilambinooDu ezhil-koL pandu aDippoor-kai
vaLaiyil ninRu-oli malkiya naangkoor vaN-puruDoottamamee.
6.meaning: So that he writhed, He of the bright valor (Hiranya, the golden-named one) — his chest He split open and stirred his blood: as the man-lion of fierce wrath He tore him apart — the temple where that Father came and dwells is Vanpurudottamam of bountiful Nangur, where the sound swells from the paired anklets on the feet of young maidens and from the bangles on the hands of those who strike the lovely play-ball.

7.translit:
vaaLaiyaar-taDam-kaNN umai-pangan van-saabam aRRadu-neenga
mooLaiyaar-sirattai aiyam mun aLitta em mukil-vaNNan uRai-kooyil
paaLai vaan-kamuku-ooDu uyar tengin van-pazham vizha veruvip-pooy
vaaLai paaytaDam soozhdaru naangkoor vaN-puruDoottamamee.
7.meaning: So that the harsh curse of Him whose half is Uma of wide vaalai-fish eyes was removed and ceased, our cloud-hued Lord long ago gave alms into the marrow-filled skull — the temple where He dwells is Vanpurudottamam of bountiful Nangur, where, startled as the firm fruit of the tall coconut palm falls among the spathed great areca palms, the vaalai fish leap, surrounded by ponds.

8.translit:
indu-vaar-saDai eesanaip payanda naan-mukanait tan ezhil-aarum
undi maa-malar mee-misaip paDaittavan ukandu-inidu uRai-kooyil,
kundi vaazhaiyin kozhung-kani karndu tan kuruLaiyait tazhuvippooy,
mandi maam-paNai meel-vaiku naangkoor vaN-puruDoottamamee.
8.meaning: Upon the great beautiful lotus on His navel He created the four-faced one (Brahma), who in turn brought forth Siva of the long matted locks bearing the crescent moon — the temple where He gladly and sweetly dwells is Vanpurudottamam of bountiful Nangur, where the female monkey, stooping to bite the rich ripe fruit of the plantain, embraces its young one and goes to rest upon the great tree-branches.

9.translit:
maNNuLaar pukazh vediyar naangkoor vaN-puruDoottamattuL,
aNNal sevaDik-keezh aDaindu uyndavan aalimaan aruL-maari,
paNNuLaar-tara paaDiya paaDalip pattum-vallaar, ulagil
eNNil-aada peer-inbam-uRRu imaiyavarooDum kooDuvaree.
9.meaning: Lakshmana, lord of Ali, the rain-cloud of grace, who took refuge beneath the red feet of the Lord and was saved, within Vanpurudottamam of bountiful Nangur of the Brahmins praised by all on earth — those who can master these ten flowing songs that he sang with melody shall attain in this world boundless great bliss and shall join the deathless celestials.

0.translit:
peeraNin thulagath thavar thozhu theettum peeraru LaaLan em piraanai,
vaaraNi mulaiyaaL malar makaLoodu maNmaka Lum udan niRpa,
seeraNi maada naangai nan naduvuL sempon sey kooyili nuLLee,
kaaraNi meegam ninRathop paanaik kaNdu koN duyndhozhin theenee.
0.meaning: I beheld and was saved by my Lord, the One of great grace, whom the people of the world adorned with glory worship and praise, who stands with Lakshmi of the flower (Mahalakshmi) clad in a bodice and with Bhudevi the Earth-goddess by His side, within the temple wrought of pure red gold in the fair midst of Nangai whose mansions are decked with splendor, and who resembles a dark rain-cloud standing still.

1.translit:
piRappodu mooppon Rillavan Rannaip peethiyaa vinba veL Laththai,
iRappethir kaalak kazhivumaa naanai eezhisai yinsuvai thannai,
siRappudai maRaiyoor naangai nan naduvuL sempon sey kooyili nuLLee,
maRaipperum poruLai vaanavar koonaik kaNdu naan vaazhndhozhin theenee.
1.meaning: He who has neither birth nor old age, who is an unchanging flood of bliss, who became the past, the future and the present, who is the sweet savor of the seven musical notes, the great inner meaning of the Vedas and the King of the celestials, within the temple wrought of pure red gold in the fair midst of Nangai of the glorious Vedic scholars, I beheld and lived in fulfillment.

2.translit:
thida visum perineer thingaLum sudarum sezhunilath thuyirkaLum maRRum,
padarporuL kaLumaay ninRavan Rannai, pangayath thayanava nanaiya, thidamozhi
maRaiyoor naangai nan naduvuL sempon sey kooyili nuLLee,
kadalniRa vaNNan Rannainaa nadiyeen kaNdu koN duyndhozhin theenee.
2.meaning: He who stands as the firm sky, the great waters, the moon and the sun, the lives of the rich earth and all other spreading things, within the temple wrought of pure red gold in the fair midst of Nangai of the Vedic scholars of firm speech, who are like Brahma seated on the lotus, that One of ocean-hued complexion did I, His servant, behold and was saved.

3.translit:
vasaiyaRu kuRaLaay maavali veeLvi maNNaLa vittavan Rannai,
asaivaRu mamara radiyiNai vaNanga alaikadal thuyinRavam maanai,
thisaimuka nanaiyoor naangai nan naduvuL sempon sey kooyili nuLLee,
uyarmaNi makudam soodinin Raanaik kaNdu koN duyndhozhin theenee.
3.meaning: He who as the flawless dwarf (Vamana) measured the earth at Mahabali's sacrifice, the Lord who reclines upon the surging ocean while the unwavering celestials bow at His twin feet, who stands wearing a lofty jeweled crown within the temple wrought of pure red gold in the fair midst of Nangai whose people are like the four-faced Brahma, I beheld and was saved.

4.translit:
theemanath tharakkar thiRalazhith thavanee enRusen RadainthavarthamakkU,
thaaymanath thirangi yaruLinaik kodukkum thayarathan mathalaiyaich chayamee,
theemalarp pozhilsoozh naangai nan naduvuL sempon sey kooyili nuLLee,
kaamanaip payanthaan RannainaanadiyeenkaNdu koN duyndhozhin theenee.
4.meaning: To those who came and took refuge crying 'You are the one who destroyed the might of the evil-minded demons,' He relents with a mother's tender heart and bestows His grace; that son of Dasaratha, victorious, who begot Kama (Manmatha), within the temple wrought of pure red gold in the fair midst of Nangai girt by groves of honeyed flowers, did I, His servant, behold and was saved.

5.translit:
mallaimaa munneer atharpada malaiyaal aNaisey thumakizhndhavan Rannai,
kallinmee thiyanRa kadimathi Lilangai kalangavoor vaaLithot Raanai,
selvanaan maRaiyoor naangai nan naduvuL sempon sey kooyili nuLLee,
allimaa malaraaL thannodu madiyeen kaNdu koN dallaltheern theenee.
5.meaning: He who joyfully built a bridge of mountains across the mighty surging ocean so that a path opened, who loosed a single arrow to shatter Lanka with its strong walls raised upon rock, within the temple wrought of pure red gold in the fair midst of Nangai of the wealthy scholars of the four Vedas, I His servant beheld together with the great lotus-seated Lady (Lakshmi) and was rid of all sorrow.

6.translit:
venjinak kaLiRum villodu mallum vekuNdiRuth thadarththavan Rannai,
kanjanaik kaaynta kaaLaiyam maanaik karumukil thiruniRath thavanai,
senjolnaan maRaiyoor naangai nan naduvuL sempon sey kooyili nuLLee,
anjanak kunRam ninRathop paanaik kaNdu koN dallaltheern theenee.
6.meaning: He who in wrath broke and crushed the fierce raging elephant, the bow, and the wrestlers, the youthful Lord who scorched Kamsa, of the sacred hue of a dark cloud, within the temple wrought of pure red gold in the fair midst of Nangai of the scholars of the four Vedas of true speech, who resembles a standing hill of black collyrium, I beheld and was rid of all sorrow.

7.translit:
anRiya vaaNa naayiram thoaLum thuNiyavan Raazhithot Raanai,
minthikazh kudumi veengada malaimeel meeviya veetha nal viLakkai,
thenthisaith thiladham anaiyavar naangaich chempon sey kooyili nuLLee,
manRathu poliya makizhndhunin Raanai vaNangi naan vaazhndhozhintheenee.
7.meaning: He who that day loosed His discus to sever the thousand shoulders of the hostile Banasura, the good lamp of the Vedas who abides upon the Venkatam hill with its lightning-bright peak, within the temple wrought of pure red gold in Nangai whose people are like the auspicious mark (tilaka) of the southern land, who stands rejoicing to grace the common hall, I bowed before and lived in fulfillment.

8.translit:
kaLangani vaNNaa. kaNNanee enRan kaarmuki lEena ninaindhittu,
uLanganin thirukku madiyavar thangaL uLLaththu LooRiya theenai,
theLinthanaan maRaiyoor naangai nan naduvuL sempon sey kooyili nuLLee,
vaLangoLpee rinbam manninin Raanai vaNangi naan vaazhndhozhin theenee.
8.meaning: 'O You of the hue of the kalanga fruit! O Kannan! O my dark rain-cloud!' thus meditating, He is the honey that wells up within the hearts of His devotees who abide with melting, ripened hearts; who stands abiding as abundant great bliss within the temple wrought of pure red gold in the fair midst of Nangai of the clear-minded scholars of the four Vedas, I bowed before and lived in fulfillment.

9.translit:
theenamar soolai naangai nan naduvuL sempon sey kooyili nuLLee,
vaanavar koonaik kaNdamai sollum mangaiyaar vaaTkali kanRi,
uoonamil paada lonbathoa donRum ozhivinRik kaRRuval laarkaL,
maana veNkudaik keezh vaiyaka maaNdu vaanava raakuvar makizhndhee.
9.meaning: This flawless decad of ten verses, in which Kaliyan of Mangai wielding the sword sings of beholding the King of the celestials within the temple wrought of pure red gold in the fair midst of Nangai of honey-laden groves -- those who learn it well without omission shall rule the world beneath a great white parasol and, rejoicing, shall become as the celestials.

0.translit:
nandhaa viLakkE aLaththaR kariyaay naranaa raNanE! karumaa mugilpOl endhaay,
emakkE yaruLaay, enaninRu imaiyOr paravu midam, eth thisaiyum
kandhaa ramandhE nisaipaadamaadE kaLivaN dumizhaR Ranizhal thudhaindhu,
mandhaa raninRu maNamal kunaangkoor maNimaadak kOyil vaNangken mananE.
0.meaning: O ever-burning lamp, O One immeasurable, O Nara-Narayana, O my Father dark as a great rain-cloud! 'Grace us alone,' so the celestials stand and praise Him at the place where, in every direction, bees hum sweet honeyed music to the high notes, where joyful bees buzz in dense shade. Bow, O my heart, to the jeweled-mansion temple of Naangoor, where the coral trees stand fragrant and abundant.

1.translit:
mudhalaith thanimaa muraNdheera vanRu mudhuneerth thadaththuch chengkaNvEzham muyya,
vidhalaith thalaichchenRu adhaRkE yudhavi vinaitheerththa vammaanidam viNNaNavum
padhalaik kapOdhath thoLimaada neRRip pavaLak kozhungkaal paingkaal puRavam,
madhalaith thalaimen pedaikoodu naangkoor maNimaadak kOyil vaNangken mananE.
1.meaning: To save the red-eyed elephant in the ancient deep pool when the lone fierce crocodile's strength was to be broken, the Lord rushed in distress to its aid and ended its suffering. His place is Naangoor, reaching the sky, where on the bright mansions' brows beneath gabled eaves the green-legged doves with coral-bright feet, their tender mates nestle together. Bow, O my heart, to the jeweled-mansion temple of Naangoor.

2.translit:
kolaippuN thalaikkunRa monRuyya vanRu kodumaa mudhalaik kidarseydhu,
kongkaar ilaippuNda reegath thavaLinba manbOdaNaindhitta vammaanidam,
aaLariyaal alaippuNda yaanai maruppu magilum aNimuththum veNchaa maraiyOdu,
ponni malaippaNda maNdath thiraiyundhu naangkoor maNimaadak kOyil vaNangken mananE.
2.meaning: To save the mountain-like elephant struck with the wound of slaughter, He once caused distress to the cruel great crocodile and lovingly drew near it like the bliss of the fragrant-petaled lotus. His place is Naangoor, where the Kaveri's waves push up to crowd it with hill-treasures: tusks of elephants harried by the man-lion's might, fragrant agil-wood, fine pearls and white chowries. Bow, O my heart, to the jeweled-mansion temple of Naangoor.

3.translit:
siRaiyaar uvaNappuL LonRERi yanRu thisainaangkum naangku miriya,
seruvil kaRaiyaar neduvE larakkar madiyak kadalsoo zhilangkai kadandhaa nidandhaan,
muRaiyaal vaLarkkinRa muththeeyar naalvEdhar aivELvi yaaRangkar Ezhi nisaiyOr,
maRaiyOr vaNangkap pugazheydhu naangkoor maNimaadak kOyil vaNangken mananE!
3.meaning: Once mounting the winged eagle-bird, He made the eight directions tremble and, in battle, conquered Lanka girt by the sea, slaying the demons of long blood-stained spears. His place is Naangoor, where the keepers of the three sacred fires, the followers of the four Vedas, those of the five sacrifices and the six limbs of scripture, the masters of the seven musical notes, the brahmins of the Vedas worship and bring it fame. Bow, O my heart, to the jeweled-mansion temple of Naangoor.

4.translit:
izhaiyaadu kongkaith thalainanja muNditu iLangkanRu koNdu viLangkaa yeRindhu,
thazhaivaada vandhaaL kurundha mosiththuth thadandhaama raippoykai pukkaani dandhaan,
kuzhaiyaada vallik kulamaada maadE kuyilkoova needu kodimaada malku,
mazhaiyaadu sOlai mayilaalu naangkoor maNimaadak kOyil vaNangken mananE.
4.meaning: He drank the poison from the jeweled breast, hurled the calf to fell the wood-apple fruits, snapped the kurundham tree that came to crush him with withering foliage, and entered the great lotus-pond. His place is Naangoor, where slender creepers sway, clusters of mansions abound, koels call, tall flag-bannered mansions thrive, and peacocks dance in groves where rain-clouds play. Bow, O my heart, to the jeweled-mansion temple of Naangoor.

5.translit:
paNNEr mozhiyaay chiyaranja vanjap paguvaayk kazhudhuk kirangkaadhu,
avaLthan uNNaa mulaimaR RavaLaavi yOdum udanE suvaiththaa nidam,
Ongku paindhaaL kaNNaar karumbin kazhaithinRu vaikik kazhuneeril moozhgich chezhuneerth thadaththu,
maNNEndh iLamEdhi gaLvaiku naangkoor maNimaadak kOyil vaNangken mananE.
5.meaning: While the maidens of melodious speech trembled, without pity for the deceitful gaping-mouthed demoness, He sucked away her unfed breast together with her very life. His place is Naangoor, where young buffaloes graze on the joints of tall lush dark sugarcane, then dwell and plunge into the red-lily ponds of clear water, bearing mud. Bow, O my heart, to the jeweled-mansion temple of Naangoor.

6.translit:
thaLaikkat tavizhthaa maraivaiku poykaith thadampukku adangkaa vidangkaa laravam,
iLaikkath thiLaiththit tadhanuchchi thanmEl adivaiththa ammaa nidam,
maamadhiyam thiLaikkum kodimaaLigaisoozh theruvil sezhumuththu veNNeR kenachchenRu,
moonRil vaLaikkai nuLaippaavai yarmaaRu naangkoor maNimaadak kOyil vaNangken mananE.
6.meaning: He entered the pond where bonds-loosening lotuses bloom, and wearying the unsubdued venom-spewing serpent, He set His foot upon its very head. His place is Naangoor, in whose streets ringed by flag-bannered mansions that the great moon brushes against, the bangle-handed fisher-maidens go bartering fine pearls in exchange for white paddy. Bow, O my heart, to the jeweled-mansion temple of Naangoor.

7.translit:
thuLaiyaar karumen kuzhalaay chiya rdham thugilvaari yumsiRRil sidhaiththum,
muRRaa viLaiyaar viLaiyaat todukaadhal veLLam viLaiviththa vammaanidam,
vEl nedungkaN muLaivaaLeyiRRu madavaar payiRRu mozhikEt tirundhu mudhiraadhavinsol,
vaLaivaaya kiLLai maRaipaadu naangkoor maNimaadak kOyil vaNangken mananE.
7.meaning: He took away the garments of the cowherd-maidens with soft dark perforated tresses, broke down their little sand-houses, and through unripe childish games stirred up floods of love. His place is Naangoor, where curved-beaked parrots, having listened to the words taught by maidens with spear-like long eyes and gleaming sprout-white teeth, recite the Vedas in tender unripe sweet speech. Bow, O my heart, to the jeweled-mansion temple of Naangoor.

8.translit:
vidaiyOda venRaay chimendhOLnayandha vigirthaa viLangku sudaraazhi yennum,
padaiyOdu sangkon Rudaiyaay enaninRu imaiyOr paravu midam,
paindhadaththup pedaiyOdu sengkaala vannam thugaippath thogaippuNda reegaththi daichchengkazhuneer,
madaiyOda ninRu madhuvimmu naangkoor maNimaadak kOyil vaNangken mananE.
8.meaning: O wondrous One who conquered the bulls and desired the soft-shouldered cowherd-maiden, 'O You who hold the discus of blazing radiance and the conch as weapons,' so the celestials stand and praise Him at His place. There in the lush pond, as red-legged swans tread with their mates, among clusters of lotuses the red-lilies brim with honey that flows along the water-channels. Bow, O my heart, to the jeweled-mansion temple of Naangoor.

9.translit:
vaNdaar pozhilsoozhn dhazhagaaya naangkoor maNimaadak kOyil nedumaalukku,
enRum thoNdaaya tholseer vayalmang kaiyarkkOn kaliya nolisey thamizhmaalai vallaar,
kaNdaar vaNangkak kaLiyaanai meedhE kadalsoo zhulagukku orukaa valaraay,
viNdOy neduveN kudaineezha linkeezh virinee rulagaaN duvirum buvarE.
9.meaning: Those who master this garland of Tamil sung by Kaliyan, the chief of the men of Mangai with fields, of ancient enduring fame and ever in service to the great Lord Nedumal of Naangoor's beautiful jeweled-mansion temple ringed by bee-filled groves, shall, while beholders bow to them, ride upon a rutting elephant and become sole protectors of the sea-girt world, and beneath the shade of a tall white sky-touching parasol they will gladly rule the world girt by spreading waters.

10.translit:
vElai yaalilaip paLLi virumbiya
paalai aaramu thaththinaip paindhuzhaay maalai
aaliyil kaNdu magizhndhu pOy
njaala munniyaik kaaNdumnaang koorilE.
10.meaning: Having joyfully beheld at Aali Him who rests upon the banyan-leaf on the ocean, milk-sweet ambrosia adorned with the garland of fresh tulasi, let us go and behold at Naangoor the One who is foremost in the world.

11.translit:
mannum maRai naangkum aanaanai pullaaNith
thennan thamizhai vadamozhiyai naangkooril
mannum maNimaadakkOyil maNaaLanai
nal neer thalaichchangka naaL madhiyai naan vaNangkum
11.meaning: I bow to Him who became the four enduring Vedas, the sweet Tamil of the southern land and the northern Sanskrit at Pullaani, the bridegroom of the enduring jeweled-mansion temple in Naangoor, the moon rising over the good waters of Thalaichangam.

0.translit:
salangkoNDa iraNiyanadha agalmaarvam keeNDu
 thaDangkaDalaik kaDaindhamudham koNDugandha kaaLai,
nalangkoNDa karumugilpool thirumeeni yammaan
naaDooRum magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai kooyil,
salangkoNDu malarsoriyum mallikaiyoN serundhi
saNbagangkaL maNanaaRum vaNpozhili nooDee,
valangkoNDu kayalooDi viLaiyaaDu naangkoor
vaigundha viNNagaram vaNangumaDa nenjee.
0.meaning: He who tore open the broad chest of the deceitful Hiranya, and who, as the youthful bull, rejoiced after churning the vast ocean and taking the nectar — the Lord whose sacred form is like a fine dark rain-cloud — this is the temple where day after day He gladly and sweetly abides: Naangoor Vaikunta Vinnagaram, where amid lush groves fragrant with bright mallikai jasmine, serundhi and shenbaga blossoms that scatter pollen and bloom upon the water, the carp-fish swim, circling and sporting. Bow to it, O simple heart.

1.translit:
thiNNiyadhoo rariyuruvaayth thisaiyanaiththum naDungath
 theevaroDu thaanavargaL thisaippa,
iraNiyanai naNNiyavan maarvagalath thugirmaDuththa naadhan
 naaDooRum magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai kooyil,
eNNilmigu perunjselvath thezhilviLangu maRaiyum
 eezhisaiyum keeLvigaLu miyanRaperung guNaththoor,
maNNilmigu maRaiyavargaL maliveydhu naangkoor
 vaigundha viNNagaram vaNangumaDa nenjee.
1.meaning: Taking the form of a firm, mighty man-lion so that all the directions trembled and gods and demons alike were stunned, the Lord who drew near Hiranya and sank His claws into the breadth of his chest — this is the temple where day after day He gladly and sweetly abides: Naangoor Vaikunta Vinnagaram, where Brahmins of great virtue, masters of the countless rich and beautiful Vedas, the seven musical notes and the sacred lore, abound upon the earth. Bow to it, O simple heart.

2.translit:
aNDamumiv valaikaDalu mavanigaLu mellaam
 amudhuseydha thiruvayiRRan arankoNDu thiriyum,
muNDamadhu niRaiththavankaN saabamadhu neekkum
 mudhalvanavan magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai kooyil,
eNDisaiyum perunjsennha LiLandhengu kadhali
 ilaikkoDiyoN kulaikkamugo Digalivalam soriya
vaNDupala visaipaaDamayilaalu naangkoor
 vaigundha viNNagaram vaNangumaDa nenjee.
2.meaning: He of the sacred belly that swallowed as His food the cosmic egg, this wave-tossed ocean and all the worlds, the First One who removed the curse upon Siva — who wandered carrying the skull — and made it whole again: this is the temple where He gladly and sweetly abides. At Naangoor Vaikunta Vinnagaram, where in every one of the eight directions tall red paddy, young coconut palms, plantains, bright leafy creepers and clustered areca trees vie with one another to pour out abundance, and where many bees sing their tunes and the peacocks dance. Bow to it, O simple heart.

3.translit:
kalaiyilangu magalalgul arakkarkkulak koDiyaik
 kaadhoDumook kuDanariyak kadhaRiyava LooDi,
thalaiyilangai vaiththumalai yilangaipugach seydha
 thaDandhooLan magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai kooyil,
silaiyilangu maNimaaDath thuchsimisaich soolam
 sezhungkoNDa lagaDiriyach sorindhasezhu muththam,
malaiyilangu maaLigaimeel maliveydhu naangkoor
 vaigundha viNNagaram vaNangumaDa nenjee.
3.meaning: He of the broad shoulders who sliced off the ears and nose of the creeper of the demon clan, the one with the garment-draped wide waist, making her flee shrieking, and who set Lanka aflame upon the head of the demon king — this is the temple where He gladly and sweetly abides: Naangoor Vaikunta Vinnagaram, where upon the crests of jewelled mansions the tridents pierce the rich rain-clouds so that they tear and shed rich pearls, and where, upon the mountain-like palaces gleaming with rainbows, prosperity abounds. Bow to it, O simple heart.

4.translit:
minnanaiya Nmarungkul melliyaRkaa
 yilangai veendhanmuDi yorubadhumthoo Lirubadhumpoo yudhira
thannigaril silaivaLaiththan Rilangaipodi seydha
 thaDandhooLan magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai kooyil,
sennheloDu sengkamalam seelkayalgaL vaaLai
 sengkazhunee roDumiDaindhu kazhanidhigazhndh thengum,
mannupugazh veediyargaL maliveydhu naangkoor
 vaigundha viNNagaram vaNangumaDa nenjee.
4.meaning: For the sake of the tender-natured one of lightning-like slender waist, He of the broad shoulders bent His unrivalled bow that day, so that the ten crowns and twenty shoulders of the king of Lanka fell scattered, and reduced Lanka to dust — this is the temple where He gladly and sweetly abides: Naangoor Vaikunta Vinnagaram, where red paddy mingles with red lotuses, sel and kayal fish and vaalai fish with red water-lilies, and the fields shine everywhere, and Brahmins of enduring fame abound. Bow to it, O simple heart.

5.translit:
peNmaimigu vaDivukoDu vandhavaLaip
 periyapeeyinadhu uruvukoDu maaLavuyi ruNDu
thiNmaimigu marudhoDunaR sagaDamiRuth tharuLum
 theevanavan magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai kooyil,
uNmaimigu maRaiyoDunaR kalaigaLniRai poRaigaL
 udhavukoDai yenRivaRRi nozhivillaa, periya
vaNmaimigu maRaiyavargaL maliveydhu naangkoor
 vaigundha viNNagaram vaNangumaDa nenjee.
5.meaning: He who took the life of her who came in a richly feminine form (Putana), making her perish in her huge demonic shape, and who graciously broke the sturdy marudha trees and the good cart (Sakatasura) — the Lord-God: this is the temple where He gladly and sweetly abides. At Naangoor Vaikunta Vinnagaram, where great and bountiful Brahmins abound — who never fall short in the true Vedas, the noble arts, in restraint, forbearance and open-handed giving. Bow to it, O simple heart.

6.translit:
viLangkaniyai yiLangkanRu koNDudhira veRindhu
 veelneDungka Naaychiyargal vaiththadhayir veNNey
uLangkuLira amudhuseydhiv vulaguNDa kaaLai
 ugandhinidhu naaDooRum maruviyuRai kooyil,
iLampaDinaR kamugukulaith thengukoDich sennhel
 eenkarumbu kaNvaLarak kaaltaDavum punalaal,
vaLangkoNDa perunjselvam vaLarumaNi naangkoor
 vaigundha viNNagaram vaNangumaDa nenjee.
6.meaning: The youthful bull who hurled a young calf to knock down the wood-apple fruit (Kapittha), who ate to His heart's cooling content the curds and butter stored by the cowherd women of long spear-like eyes, and who swallowed this whole world — He who gladly, sweetly abides here day after day: this is the temple. At Naangoor Vaikunta Vinnagaram, where the water that washes their roots makes the young areca palms in clusters, the coconut trees, the creepers, the red paddy and the yielding sugarcane flourish and bud, and rich, great wealth grows. Bow to it, O simple heart.

7.translit:
aaRaadha sinaththinmigu naraganura mazhiththa
 aDalaazhith thaDakkaiyan alarmagaTkum araRkum,
kooRaagak koDuththaruLum thiruvuDamban imaiyoor
 kulamudhalvan magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai kooyil,
maaRaadha malarkkamalam sengkazhuneer thadhumbi
 madhuveLLa mozhugavaya luzhavarmaDai yaDaippa,
maaRaadha perunjselvam vaLarumaNi naangkoor
 vaigundha viNNagaram vaNangumaDa nenjee.
7.meaning: He of the broad hand bearing the mighty discus, who destroyed the strength of Naraka of unquenchable fury, the Lord with the sacred body that He graciously gave as a half-share to the lotus-born goddess (Lakshmi) and to Siva, the source of the clan of the gods — this is the temple where He gladly and sweetly abides: Naangoor Vaikunta Vinnagaram, where the ever-blooming lotuses and red water-lilies brim over so that floods of honey flow, and the ploughmen close off the field-sluices, and unfailing great wealth grows. Bow to it, O simple heart.

8.translit:
vangkamali thaDangkaDaluL vaanavarga LooDu
 maamunivar palarkooDi maamalargaL thoovi,
engaLthani naayaganee emakkaruLaay ennum
 eesanavan magizhndhinidhu maruviyuRai kooyil,
sengkayalum vaaLaigaLum sennheliDaik kudhippach
 seelugaLum sezhumpaNaisoozh veedhidhoRum miDaindhu,
mangulmadhi yagaDurinjsu maNimaaDa naangkoor
 vaigundha viNNagaram vaNangumaDa nenjee.
8.meaning: He, the Lord-God, to whom the celestials together with many great sages, gathered in the ship-laden vast ocean and strewing great flowers, cry out, 'You are our peerless Lord, grant us Your grace!' — this is the temple where He gladly and sweetly abides: Naangoor Vaikunta Vinnagaram, where red carp and vaalai fish leap amid the red paddy and sel fish frolic, crowding every street ringed by lush fields, and jewelled mansions rise so high they graze the moon among the clouds. Bow to it, O simple heart.

9.translit:
sangkumali thaNDumudhal sakkaramuneendhum
 thaamaraikkaN neDiyabiraan thaanamarum kooyil,
vangkamali kaDalulagil maliveydhu naangkoor
 vaigundha viNNagarmeel vaNDaRaiyum pozhilsoozh,
mangaiyarthm thalaivanmaru valardhamuDal thuNiya
 vaaLveesum parakaalan kaligan_Ri sonna,
sangkamali thamizhmaalai paththivaivallaargaL
 tharaNiyoDu visumbaaLum thanmaiperu vaaree.
9.meaning: This is the temple where the lotus-eyed, exalted Lord who bears the conch, the mace and foremost the discus, Himself abides: Naangoor Vaikunta Vinnagaram, abounding in the ship-laden ocean-world and surrounded by groves where bees hum. Those who can master these ten verses of this Tamil garland rich with sound, sung by Kaliyan — Parakaalan, the lord of the women (of Tirumangai), who wields the sword to cut asunder the bodies of his foes — shall attain the state of ruling both the earth and the heavens.

0.translit:
aalinilaip paalakanaay anRu ulagam uNDavanee
vaaliyaikkon RarasiLaiya vaanaratthuk kaLitthavanee
kaalin maNi karaiyalaikkum kaNapuratthen karumaNiyee
aalinagark kathipathiyee ayoddhimanee taaleelo
0.meaning: O you who as an infant lay upon the banyan leaf and swallowed all the worlds on that day! O you who slew Vali and bestowed the kingdom upon the younger monkey (Sugriva)! O my dark gem of Kanapuram, where the wind drives gems to beat upon the shore! O lord of Aali city, O king of Ayodhya, sleep softly, taaleelo!

1.translit:
anRu aayarkula kkoDiyoDu aNimaa malarmangaiyoDu anbaLavi,
avuNarkku enRaanum irakkam ilaadhavanukku kuRaiyum iDam aavadhu,
irumpozhilsoozh nanRaaya punalnaRai yoorth thiruvaali kuDandhai taDandhigazh koovalnagar,
ninRaan irundhaan kiDandhaan naDandhaaRku iDam maamalai yaavadhu neermalaiye.
1.meaning: He who of old joined in love with the creeper of the cowherd clan and with the lady of the splendid lotus, yet never showed mercy to the demons - the place that becomes a refuge for him is the cool Neermalai, the great hill that is the abode of the one who stood, sat, reclined and walked, in well-watered Naraiyur surrounded by dark groves, in Tiruvali, Kudandai, and Koval city bright with great tanks.

2.translit:
vandhu unadhu aDiyeen manam pugundhaay pugundhadhaRpin vaNangum en
sindhanaikku iniyaay. thiruvee en aaruyiree,
andhaLir aNiyaar asoki iLandhaLirgaL kalandhu, avai engum
sendhazhal puraiyum thiruvaali yammaane.
2.meaning: You came and entered the heart of me, your servant; and after entering you became sweet to my thought that worships you. O Treasure (Sri), O my precious life! O Lord of Tiruvali, where the lovely fresh red shoots of the beautiful asoka trees mingle everywhere and seem like red flame!

3.translit:
neelath thaDavarai maamaNi nigazhak kiDandhadhupool,
aravaNai veelaith thalaikkiDandhaay aDiyeen manatthirundhaay,
soolaith thalaikkaNa maamayil naDamaaDa mazhaimugil poonRezhundhu,
engum aalaip pugaikamazhum aNiyaali yammaane.
3.meaning: Like a great gem gleaming as it lies upon the blue expanse of a mountain, you reclined upon the serpent-couch on the surface of the sea, and you dwelt within the heart of me, your servant. O Lord of beautiful Aali, where in the groves flocks of great peacocks dance, and where, rising like rain-clouds, the smoke of the sugar-mills is fragrant everywhere!

4.translit:
nennalpooy varumenRenRu eNNi iraamai en manatthee pugundhadhu,
immaikku enRirundheen neRineer vaLam seRuvil,
sennel koozhai varamporeei arivaar mugatthezhu vaaLaipooy,
karumbu annaR kaaDaNaiyum aNiyaali yammaane.
4.meaning: Thinking 'yesterday is gone, it will come again,' I waited; and unable to wait, He entered my heart, that I might be His in this very life. In the well-watered fertile fields of beautiful Aali, the scabbard-fish (vaaLai), leaping up to the faces of those who cut and bank the red paddy, goes and reaches the fine sugarcane groves - O Lord of that Aali!

5.translit:
minnil mannu nuDangiDai maDavaartham sindhai maRandhuvandhu,
ninmannu seevaDikkee maRavaamai vaitthaayaal,
punnai mannu serundhi vaNpozhilvaay agan paNaigaL kalandhengum,
annam mannum vayalaNi aali yammaane.
5.meaning: Forgetting the thought of slender-waisted women whose waists waver like lightning, I came; and you set me in unforgetting devotion to your enduring sacred feet. O Lord of Aali, adorned with fields where swans dwell everywhere amid the wide expanses, in the rich groves of punnai and serundi trees!

6.translit:
neeDu panmalar maalaiyiTTu nin iNaiyaDi thozhudheettum,
en manam vaaDa nee ninaiyeel maram eydha maamunivaa,
paaDal innoli sangin oosai parandhu palpaNaiyaal malindhu,
engum aaDal oosaiyaRaa aNiyaali yammaane.
6.meaning: O great sage who shot the (seven) trees! Do not think to make my heart wither, for I offer long garlands of many flowers and worship and praise your twin feet. O Lord of beautiful Aali, where the sweet sound of song and the noise of conches spread abroad, abounding with many drums, and the sound of dancing never ceases anywhere!

7.translit:
kandha maamalar eTTumiTTu nin kaamar seevaDi kaithozhudhezhum,
pundhiyeen manatthee pugundhaayaip poogaloTTeen,
sandhi veeLvi saDangu naanmaRai oodhi oodhuvith thaadhi yaayvarum,
andhaNaaLar aRaavaNiyaali yammaane.
