7.meaning: Offering eight kinds of fragrant great flowers, I rise worshipping your lovely red feet with my hands; you have entered my mind, O wise one - I will not let you go! O Lord of beautiful Aali, never lacking in brahmins who, as the foremost, recite and teach the four Vedas and perform the twilight rites and sacrifices and ceremonies!

8.translit:
ulavuthiraik kaDaRpaLLi koNDu vandhu un aDiyeen manam pugundha,
appulava! puNNiyanee! pugundhaayaip poogaloTTeen,
nilavu malarppunnai naazhal neezhal thaNDaamarai malarin misai,
mali alavan kaNpaDukkum aNiyaali yammaane.
8.meaning: O learned one who, taking your rest upon the sea of rolling waves, came and entered the heart of me, your servant! O righteous one! Now that you have entered, I will not let you go! O Lord of beautiful Aali, where in the shade of the blossoming punnai and naazhal trees the abundant crabs sleep upon the cool lotus flowers!

9.translit:
sangu thangu thaDangaDal kaDanmallai uLkiDandhaay
aruLpurindhu ing ennuL pugundhaay inippooyi naalaRaiyoo,
kongu seNbaga malligai malarppulgi inniLa vaNDu pooy,
iLam thengin thaadhaLaiyum thiruvaali yammaane.
9.meaning: O you who reclined within Kadalmallai (Mahabalipuram), the wide sea where conches abide! Granting grace, you entered into me here; if you now go away, is that fitting? O Lord of Tiruvali, where the sweet young bees, embracing the fragrant senbaga and jasmine blossoms, fly off and stir the pollen of the young coconut palms!

10.translit:
oodhi yaayira naamamum paNindheetthi nin aDaindheeRku,
oru poruL veedhiyar! araiyaa uraiyaay orumaaRRam endhaay!
needhi yaagiya veedhamaa muniyaaLar thooRRam uraitthu,
maRRavarkku aadhiyaay irundhaay! aNiyaali yammaane.
10.meaning: To me who has reciting the thousand names, bowed and praised, and reached you - O master of the Vedic brahmins, speak one word of truth, O my Father! You who declared the origin of the great Vedic sages who are righteousness itself, and remained as the first cause for them - O Lord of beautiful Aali!

11.translit:
pulli vaNDaRaiyum pozhil puDaisoozh thennaali orundha maayanai,
kallin mannu thiNDooL kaliyan olisaiydha,
nalla innisai maalai naalumoor aindhumonRum navinRu, thaamuDan
vallaraay uraippaarkku iDamaagum vaanulagee.
11.meaning: On the wondrous Lord enshrined in southern Aali, surrounded on all sides by groves where embracing bees hum, Kaliyan (Tirumangai) of strong shoulders firm as rock composed this melody. For those who, becoming masters, recite all ten and one (these eleven) sweet good garland-verses together, the abode shall be the celestial world.

12.translit:
thooviriya maluzhakkith thuNaiyoDum piriyaadhee,
pooviriya madhunugarum poRivariya siRuvaNDee,
theeviriya maRaivaLarkkum pugazhaaLar thiruvaali,
eevarivenj silaiyaanukku ennilaimai uraiyaaye.
12.meaning: O little spotted, striped bee who, never parting from your mate, trample the spreading petals and sip the honey as the flowers open! Go to Tiruvali of the renowned ones who tend the sacrificial fire and the spreading Vedas, and tell the Lord with the cruel arrow-bow of my state.

13.translit:
piNiyavizhu naRuneela malarkkizhiyap peDaiyoDum,
aNimalarmeel madhunugarum aRukaala siRuvaNDee,
maNigezhuneer marungalarum vayalaali maNavaaLan,
paNiyaRiyeen nee senRen payalainoy uraiyaaye.
13.meaning: O little six-legged bee who, with your mate, tearing open the fragrant blue flower whose bud unfolds, sip the honey upon the lovely blossom! I do not know how to serve the Bridegroom of fertile Aali, where gem-bright waters blossom on the borders of the fields - go and tell him of my malady, my pallor of love-sickness.

14.translit:
neervaanam maNNerikaa laayninRa neDumaal, than
thaaraaya naRundhuLavam perundhagaiyeRku aruLaane,
seeraarum vaLarppozhilsoozh thiruvaali vayalvaazhum,
koorvaaya siRukurugee kuRippaRindhu kooRaaye.
14.meaning: The great Tirumal who stands as water, sky, earth, fire and wind has not granted to me, his devotee, the fragrant tulasi garland that he wears. O sharp-beaked little heron who live in the fields of Tiruvali surrounded by rich flourishing groves - perceive his intent and tell it to me.

15.translit:
thaanaaga ninaiyaaneel thanninaindhu naiveeRku, oor
meenaaya koDineDuveeL valiseyya meliveenoo?
theenvaaya varivaNDee. thiruvaali nagaraaLum,
aanaayaRku ennuRunoy aRiyachchen RuraiyaaYe.
15.meaning: If he himself will not think of me, must I, who waste away thinking of him, grow weak when the tall god (Kama) with the fish-banner does his violence? O honey-mouthed striped bee! Go to the Cowherd who rules the city of Tiruvali and tell him, that he may know, the affliction that has befallen me.

16.translit:
vaaLaaya kaNpanippa menmulaigaL ponnarumba
naaNaaLum ninninaindhu naiveeRku, oo maNNaLandha
thaaLaaLaa thaNkuDandhai nagaraaLaa varaiyeDutdha
thooLaaLaa, enRanakkoor thuNaiyaaLan aagaaye.
16.meaning: To me who waste away thinking of you night and day, while my sword-like eyes brim with tears and my soft breasts grow pale as gold - O you of the feet that measured the earth, O ruler of cool Kudandai city, O you of the shoulders that lifted the mountain (Govardhana) - become a companion-helper to me!

17.translit:
thaaraaya thaNDuLava vaNDuzhudha varaimaarban,
pooraanaik kombosiitha puTpaaga ennammaan,
theeraarum neDuveedhith thiruvaali nagaraaLum,
kaaraayan ennuDaiya kanavaLaiyum kavarvaanoo.
17.meaning: He of the mountain-like chest where bees plough the cool tulasi garland, the rider of Garuda who broke the tusk of the battle-elephant, my Lord - the dark cowherd who rules the city of Tiruvali with its long streets filled with chariots - will he carry off even my fine bangles?

18.translit:
koNDaravath thiraiyulavu kuraikaDalmeel kulavaraipool,
paNDaravin aNaikkiDandhu paaraLandha paNbaaLaa!
vaNDamarum vaLarppozhilsoozh vayalaali maindhaa! en
kaNDuyilnee koNDaaykken kanavaLaiyum kaDaveenoo!
18.meaning: O gracious one who, like a great mountain, reclined of old upon the serpent-couch on the roaring sea where rolling roaring waves move, and who measured the earth! O youth of fertile Aali, surrounded by flourishing groves where bees gather! You have taken away the sleep of my eyes - must I lose my fine bangles too?

19.translit:
kuyilaalum vaLarppozhilsoozh thaNkuDandhaik kuDamaaDi!
thuyilaadha kaNNiNaiyeen ninninaindhu thuyarveenoo!
muyalaalum iLamadhikkee vaLaiyizhandheeRku, idhunaDuvee
vayalaali maNavaaLaa. koLvaayoo maNiniRamee.
19.meaning: O you who danced with the pots, in cool Kudandai surrounded by flourishing groves where koels sing! Must I, whose two eyes find no sleep, suffer thinking of you? To me who have already lost my bangles to the young moon bearing the hare - O Bridegroom of fertile Aali, in the midst of this will you carry off even my gem-like complexion too?

20.translit:
nilaiyaaLaa nin vaNanga vENDaayE yaakinum, en
mulaiyaaLa orunaaL un nakalattaal aaLaayE,
silaiyaaLaa maram eyda thiRalaaLaa thirumeyya
malaiyaaLaa, nii aaLa vaLaiyaaLa maaTTOmE.
20.meaning: O eternal Lord, even if You do not wish that I should worship You and stand before You, yet for one day rule me with Your broad chest where my breasts may rest. O wielder of the bow, O mighty one who shot the trees, O Lord of the sacred hill of Tirumeyyam, unless You take me as Yours, I will not be able to keep my bangles upon my wrists.

21.translit:
mai ilangu karungkuvaLai marungku alarum vayalaali,
ney ilangu sudar aazhip paDaiyaanai neDumaalai,
kai ilangu vEl kaliyan kaNDuraittha thamizh maalai,
ai iraNDum ivai vallaarkku aru vinaikaL aDaiyaavE.
21.meaning: The great Tirumal of Vayalaali, where dark kuvalai blossoms gleam like collyrium open all around the fields, holds in His hand the bright flaming discus dripping as if with ghee. These ten Tamil verses of Kaliyan, whose hand shines with the spear, beheld and sang of Him; for those who can master these ten, cruel karmas shall never draw near.

22.translit:
kaLvankol yaan aRiyEn kariyaan oru kaaLai vandhu,
vaLLi marungku ennRan maDa maaninaip pOdha enRu,
veLLi vaLaik kaippaRRap peRRa thaayarai viTTakanRu,
aLLalam poongkazhani aNiyaali pukuvarkkolO.
22.meaning: Is He a thief? I do not know. A dark youth came and said to my tender doe-like daughter of the slender creeper-like waist, 'Come away,' and seizing her hand bright with silver bangles, made her leave the mother who bore her. Will they now enter beautiful Tiruvaali, with its lovely flower-filled fields in the soft mire?

23.translit:
paNDu ivan aayan nangaay. paDiRan pukundhu, en makaL than
thoNDaiyam sengkani vaay nukarndhaanai ukandhu, avan pin
keNDai oN kaN miLira kiLi pOl mizhaRRi naDandhu,
vaNDamar kaanal malkum vayalaali pukuvarkkolO.
23.meaning: O lady, this one of old was a cowherd; the deceiver entered and tasted the red ripe tondai-fruit lips of my daughter. Delighting in him, she went behind him, her bright carp-like eyes flashing, prattling like a parrot. Will they now enter Vayalaali, abounding in groves where bees swarm?

24.translit:
anjuvan vensol nangaay arakkarkku ulap paavai thannai,
vensina mookkarindha viRalOn thiRam kETkil, meyyE
panji yal mel aDi em paNaiththOLi parakkazhindhu,
vanji am thaN paNai soozh vayalaali pukuvarkkolO.
24.meaning: O lady, I am afraid of harsh words. If she has heard of the prowess of the valorous one who in fierce wrath cut off the nose of the demon-race's woman, then truly my bamboo-shouldered girl with soft feet tender as cotton, casting off all reserve, will she enter Vayalaali girt by cool fields lovely as the vanji creeper?

25.translit:
Edhu avan thol piRappu iLaiyavan vaLai oodhi,
mannar thoodhuvan aaya avan oor soluveerkaL! soliir aRiyEn,
maadhavan than thuNaiyaa naDandhaaL thaDam soozh puRavil,
pOdhu vaNDu aaDu semmal punalaali pukuvarkkolO.
25.meaning: What is His ancient birth? He, the younger one, who blew the conch and became the messenger of kings - O you who know His town, tell me, tell me, for I do not know! With Madhava as her companion she walked away; through the groves girt by broad ponds where bees dance upon the blossoms, will that noble girl enter Punalaali?

26.translit:
thaay enai enRu irangaaL thaDanthOLi thanakku amaindha,
maayanai maadhavanai madhiththu ennai akanRavaL,
vEyana thOL visiRip peDai annam ena naDandhu,
pOyina poongkoDiyaaL punalaali pukuvarkkolO.
26.meaning: My broad-shouldered daughter showed no pity, never thinking 'this is my mother'; esteeming the wondrous Mayan, Madhava, as the one fit for her, she left me. Swinging her bamboo-like arms, walking like a female swan, that flower-creeper of a girl has gone - will she enter Punalaali?

27.translit:
en thuNai enRu eDuththERku iRaiyEnum irangiRRilaL,
than thuNai aaya enRan thanimaikkum irangiRRilaL,
van thuNai vaanavarkkaay varam seRRu arangaththu uRaiyum,
im thuNaivanoDum pOy ezhilaali pukuvarkkolO.
27.meaning: To me who raised her saying 'she is my support,' she showed not the least pity; nor did she pity my loneliness, she who was my own companion. With this companion of hers - He who is the strong refuge of the celestials, who destroyed the boon and dwells in Arangam (Srirangam) - she has gone; will she enter beautiful Aali?

28.translit:
annaiyum aththanum enRu aDiyOmukku irangiRRilaL,
pinnai than kaadhalan than perunthOL nalam pENinaLaal,
minnaiyum vanjiyaiyum venRu ilangum iDaiyaaL naDandhu,
punnaiyum annamum soozh punalaali pukuvarkkolO.
28.meaning: She showed no pity for us, her servants, who are mother and father to her; rather she cherished the goodness of the broad shoulders of Nappinnai's beloved. With her waist that gleams surpassing the lightning and the vanji creeper, that girl has walked away - will she enter Punalaali, ringed by punnai trees and swans?

29.translit:
muRRilum paingkiLiyum pandhum oosalum pEsukinRa,
siRRil men poovaiyum viTTakanRa sezhungkOdhai thannai,
peRRilEn muRRizhaiyaip piRappili pinnE naDandhu,
maRRellaam kaithozhap pOy vayalaali pukuvarkkolO.
29.meaning: My richly-garlanded daughter has left behind her little basket, her green parrot, her ball, her swing, the prattling mynah of her toy-house - I have lost her, that girl decked in full ornaments. Walking behind the birthless One while all others fold their hands in worship, will she enter Vayalaali?

30.translit:
kaaviyam kaNNi eNNil kaDi maa malarp paavai oppaaL,
paaviyEn peRRamaiyaal paNaiththOLi parakkazhindhu,
thoovi sEr annam anna naDaiyaaL neDumaaloDum pOy,
vaaviyam thaN paNai soozh vayalaali pukuvarkkolO.
30.meaning: If you reckon, my girl of dark-lily eyes is like Lakshmi, the lady of the fragrant great lotus. Because I, a sinner, bore her, my bamboo-shouldered girl has cast off all reserve; she of the gait like a feathered swan has gone with the great Tirumal - will she enter Vayalaali, girt by cool fields and lovely ponds?

31.translit:
thaay manam ninRu irangath thaniyE neDumaal thuNaiyaa,
pOyina poongkoDiyaaL punalaali pukuvar enRu,
kaaysina vEl kaliyan oli sey thamizh maalai paththum,
mEviya nenju uDaiyaar thanjamaavadhu viNNulakE.
31.meaning: While the mother's heart stood grieving, the flower-creeper girl went off alone with the great Tirumal as her companion, to enter Punalaali. For those whose hearts cleave to these ten resounding Tamil verses composed by Kaliyan of the wrath-hot spear, the refuge they gain is the world of heaven (Vaikuntha).

32.translit:
sindhai thannuL niingaadhu irundha thiruvE maruvu iniya
maindhaa, andhaNaali maalE sOlai mazha kaLiRE
nandhaa viLakkin sudarE naRaiyoor ninRa nambii, en
endhaay. indhaLooraay. aDiyERku iRaiyum irangaayE.
32.meaning: O Wealth that abides never leaving my mind, O sweetly-dwelling young one, O Tirumal of beautiful Tiruvaali, O young elephant of the groves, O flame of the never-dimming lamp, O perfect Lord who stand at Naraiyur, my Father, O Lord of Indaluur - show even a little grace to me, Your servant.

33.translit:
munniirai munnaaL kaDaindhaanai moozhththa naaL
anniirai miinaay amaiththa perumaanai
thennaali mEya thirumaalai emmaanai
nanniir vayal soozh naRaiyooril kaNDEnE.
33.meaning: He who in days of old churned the ocean of three waters; the great Lord who, on the day of the deluge, took the form of a fish in those waters; the Tirumal who dwells in southern Tiruvaali, my Lord - Him I beheld in Naraiyur, girt by fields of good water.

34.translit:
enjaa ven narakaththu azhundhi naDungukinRERku,
anjEl enRu aDiyEnai aaTkoLLa vallaanai,
nenjE nii ninaiyaadhu iRaippozhudhum iruththikaNDaay,
manjaar maaLikai soozh vayalaali maindhanaiyE.
34.meaning: To me who was sinking and trembling in the unending, cruel hell, He said 'Fear not' and was able to take me, His servant, into His service. O heart, see that you do not remain even for a moment without thinking of Him - the youthful Lord of Vayalaali, ringed by mansions that touch the clouds.

35.translit:
kaRRaar paRRaRukkum piRavip perungkaDalE
paRRaa vandhu aDiyEn piRandhEn piRandha pinnai
vaRRaa niir vayal soozh vayalaali ammaanaip
peRREn peRRadhum piRavaamai peRREnE.
35.meaning: In this great ocean of birth, which the learned cut their bonds from and escape, I, His servant, came clinging and was born; and after being born, I obtained the Lord of Vayalaali, girt by fields of never-failing water. Having obtained Him, what I gained was the gift of being born no more.

36.translit:
vElai aal ilaip paLLi virumbiya
paalai aar amudhaththinaip paindhuzhaay maalai
aaliyil kaNDu makizhndhu pOy
nyaalam munniyaik kaaNDum naangoorilE.
36.meaning: He who delighted to rest upon the banyan leaf on the sea, the One sweet as nectar mingled with milk, garlanded with fresh tulasi - having beheld Him with joy at Aali, let us go and see Him who measured the world, at Naangur.

37.translit:
thooyaanaith thooya maRaiyaanai, thennaali
mEyaanai mEvaaL uyir uNDu amudhu uNDa
vaayaanai, maalai vaNangi avan perumai,
pEsaadhaar pEcchu enRum pEcchu alla kETTaamE.
37.meaning: The pure One, He of the pure Vedas, who dwells in southern Tiruvaali; He of the mouth that consumed the life of the unwilling (demoness Putana) and drank the nectar - having worshipped Tirumal, the speech of those who do not speak His greatness is, as we have heard, never speech at all.

38.translit:
paDai ninRa paindhaamaraiyODu aNi niilam
maDai ninRu alarum vayalaali maNaaLaa,
iDaiyan eRindha maramE oththu iraamE,
aDaiya aruLaay enakku unRan aruLE.
38.meaning: O Bridegroom of Vayalaali, where fresh lotuses set in array and lovely blue lilies bloom standing at the sluices - let me not be like the trees the cowherd (Krishna) struck and felled; rather grant me Your grace, that I may attain it fully.

39.translit:
nenjuruki kaN panippa niRkum sOrum
 nedidhu uyirkkum uNDu aRiyaaL uRakkam pENaaL,
nanju aravil thuyil amarndha nambii ennum
 vambaar poom vayalaali maindhaa ennum,
anjiRaiya puT koDiyE aaDum paaDum
 aNi arangam aaDudhumO thOzhii ennum,
enjiRakin kiizh aDangaap peNNaip peRREn
 iru nilaththu Or pazhi paDaiththEn E paavamE.
39.meaning: Her heart melts, her eyes brim with tears, she stands transfixed, she swoons, she sighs long; she knows no food, she cares not for sleep. 'O perfect Lord who reposes upon the venomous serpent,' she cries; 'O youthful Lord of fragrant blossoming Vayalaali,' she cries. To the bird-bannered Garuda of beautiful wings she dances and sings, 'Shall we go and bathe in lovely Arangam (Srirangam), O friend?' she cries. I have borne a daughter who will not stay under the shelter of my wing; I have brought upon myself a reproach upon this wide earth - alas, what sin is this!

40.translit:
aaraanum karpippaar naayakarE? naan avanaik
kaar aar tirumEni kaaNum aLavum pOy
seer aar tiruvEngkadamE tirukkOva
loorE matiL kacchi ooragamE pEragamE
pEraa marudu iRuttaan veLLaRaiyE vehkaavE
pEr aali taNkaal naRaiyoor tiruppuliyoor
aaraamam soozhnda arangkam kaNamangkai
40.meaning: Who indeed is fit to be the teacher to instruct me of any path? Until I go and behold Him of the cloud-dark sacred form, I shall journey on — to glorious Tiruvengkadam, to Tirukkovalur, to Urakam within walled Kachchi, to Peragam, to Vellarai where He who snapped the unbending marudu trees abides, to Vehka, to great Ali, to Tankal, to Naraiyur, to Tiruppuliyur, to Arangkam (Srirangam) girt by groves, and to Kanamangkai.

41.translit:
annam tuyilum aNi neer vayal aali
ennudaiya in amudai evvuL peru malaiyai
kanni matiL soozh kaNamangkaik kaRpagattai
minnai iru sudarai veLLaRaiyuL kal aRaimEl
ponnai maragadattai putkuzhi em pOr ERRai
mannum arangkattu em maa maNiyai vallavaazhp
41.meaning: He who dwells in Ali of lovely watered fields where swans sleep, my sweet nectar; the great mountain at Evvul; the wish-granting kalpaka tree at Kanamangkai girt by maiden-strong walls; the lightning, the twin radiant lights; the gold upon the stone-rock within Vellarai; the emerald; my fighting bull at Putkuzhi; my great gem in everlasting Arangkam (Srirangam); and at Vallavazh —

0.translit:
poodhalarndha pozhilsoolaip puRamengum porudhiraigaL
taadhudhira vandhalaikkum tadamaNNit tenkaraimEl
maadhavandRaa nuRaiyumidam vayalnaangai varivaNdu
tEdhenaven Risaipaadum tiruttEva naar-thogaiyE.
0.meaning: On the southern bank of the broad Manni river, where the surging waves come dashing so that the pollen scatters from the blossoming groves and gardens all around, is the place where Madhavan dwells. It is Tiruttevanartogai in the fertile fields of Nangai, where the striped bees hum and sing their music 'te-tena'.

1.translit:
yaavarumaa yaavaiyumaa yezhilvEdhap poruLgaLumaay
moovarumaay mudhalaaya moorthiyamarn dhuRaiyumidam,
maavarundhiN padaimannai vendRikoLvaar mannunaangai
dEvarumsen dRiRainjupozhil tiruttEva naar-thogaiyE.
1.meaning: Being all beings and all things, being the meanings of the beautiful Vedas, being the three (Brahma, Vishnu, Siva), the primal Lord-form abides here in peace. It is Tiruttevanartogai, where the gods too come and worship in the groves, in enduring Nangai of those who win victory over the strong armies with horses.

2.translit:
vaanaadum maNNaadum maRRuLLa palluyirum
taanaaya vemperumaan talaivanamarn dhuRaiyumidam,
aanaadha perunjelvath tarumaRaiyOr naangaidhannuL
tEnaaru malarpozhilsoozh tiruttEva naar-thogaiyE.
2.meaning: The place where my great Lord, who is Himself the world of the heavens, the world of the earth, and all the many other living beings, abides as the Sovereign, is here. It is Tiruttevanartogai, surrounded by flowering groves full of honey, within Nangai of the rare Vedic brahmins of unfailing great wealth.

3.translit:
indhiranu mimaiyavarum munivargaLum ezhilamaindha
sandhamalarch sadhumuganum kadhiravanum sandhiranum,
endhaiyemak karuL, enanindR aruLumidam ezhilnaangai
sundharanal pozhilpudaisoozh tiruttEva naar-thogaiyE.
3.meaning: The place where the Lord stands and grants His grace when Indra and the unblinking gods, the sages, the four-faced Brahma seated on the beautiful fragrant flower, the sun and the moon all pray 'Our Father, grace us!', is here. It is Tiruttevanartogai in lovely Nangai, surrounded on all sides by fine beautiful groves.

4.translit:
aNdamumiv valaikadalu mavanigaLum kulavaraiyum
uNdapiraa nuRaiyumidam OLimaNisan dhagilkanagam,
deNdiraigaL varaththiraddum tigazhmaNNith tenkaraimEl,
tiNdiRalaar payilnaangaith tiruttEva naar-thogaiyE.
4.meaning: The place where the Lord who swallowed the cosmic egg, this wave-filled ocean, the earths, and the great mountains dwells, is here. It is Tiruttevanartogai of Nangai inhabited by men of firm valor, on the southern bank of the shining Manni river whose clear waves come heaping up bright gems, sandalwood, aloe-wood and gold.

5.translit:
njaalamellaa mamudhuseydhu naanmaRaiyum todaraadha
paalaganaa yaalilaiyil paLLikoLLum paramanidam,
saalivaLam perugivarum tadamaNNith tenkaraimEl
sElugaLum vayalnaangaith tiruttEva naar-thogaiyE.
5.meaning: The place of the Supreme One who, having consumed all the world (at deluge), reposes as an infant upon a banyan leaf, whom even the four Vedas cannot reach, is here. It is Tiruttevanartogai of Nangai whose fields are leaping with sEl-fish, on the southern bank of the broad Manni river that comes swelling with abundant paddy-wealth.

6.translit:
Odaadha vaaLariyi nuruvaagi yiraNiyanai
vaadaadha vaLLugiraal piLandhaLaindha maaladhidam,
EdERu perunjelvath tezhilmaRaiyOr naangaidhannuL,
sEdERu pozhilthazhuvu tiruttEva naar-thogaiyE.
6.meaning: The place of Tirumal, who took the form of the never-retreating fierce-maned lion and tore apart Hiranya with His never-withering strong claws, is here. It is Tiruttevanartogai embraced by lofty groves, within Nangai of the beautiful Vedic brahmins of flourishing great wealth that grows like a swelling lotus.

7.translit:
vaaraaru miLangongai maidhiliyai maNampuNarvaan,
kaaraarthiN silaiyiRuththa tanikkaaLai karudhumidam
Eraarum perunjelvath tezhilmaRaiyOr naangaidhannuL,
seeraarum malarpozhilsoozh tiruttEva naar-thogaiyE.
7.meaning: The place cherished by the matchless young bull (Rama) who, to wed Sita of Mithila with her tender breasts bound in a corset, broke the dark mighty bow (of Siva), is here. It is Tiruttevanartogai surrounded by splendid flowering groves, within Nangai of the beautiful Vedic brahmins of beautiful great wealth.

8.translit:
kumbamigu madhayaanai paaganodum kulaindhuveezha
kombadhanaip paRiththeRindha kooththanamarn dhuRaiyumidam,
vambavizhum seNbagaththin maNangamazhum naangaidhannuL,
semponmadhiL pozhilpudaisoozh tiruttEva naar-thogaiyE.
8.meaning: The place where the Dancer (Krishna), who plucked out and hurled the tusk of the rutting elephant of great forehead so that it fell crumbling along with its mahout, abides in peace, is here. It is Tiruttevanartogai surrounded on all sides by reddish-gold ramparts and groves, within Nangai fragrant with the scent of blossoming shenbaga flowers.

9.translit:
kaaraarndha tirumEnik kaNNanamarn dhuRaiyumidam,
seeraarndha pozhilnaangaith tiruttEva naar-thogaimEl
kooraarndha vERkaliyan kooRudhamizh paththumvallaar
Eraarndha vaikundhath timaiyavarO dhiruppaarE.
9.meaning: Those who master these ten Tamil verses uttered by Kaliyan of the sharp spear, upon Tiruttevanartogai in glorious grove-filled Nangai—the place where Kannan of the cloud-dark sacred form abides in peace—will dwell together with the unblinking immortals in resplendent Vaikuntha.

0.translit:
maatrarasar maNimudiyum thiRalum thEsum matravartham kaadhalimaarkuzhaiyum, thandhai
kaalthaLaiyum udankazhala vandhu thOnRik kadhanaagam kaaththaLiththa kaNNar kaNdeer,
nooRRidhazhko Laravindham nuzhaindha paLLath thiLangamugin mudhupaaLai paguvaay naNdin,
sEtraLaiyil veNmuththam sindhu naangoorth thiruththetri yambalaththen sengaN maalE.
0.meaning: Behold the Lord with eyes who, when He appeared (as Krishna), made the jeweled crowns, the strength and splendor of opposing kings, and the earrings of their beloved women — and even His own father's foot-fetters — fall away all at once, and who protected (the cattle) by guarding off the furious serpent. He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tetriyambalam temple in Nangur, where in the muddy burrows white pearls are scattered by the gaping-mouthed crabs amid the old spathes of young areca palms, in pools where hundred-petalled lotuses have entered.

1.translit:
potrodiththOL madamagaLthan vadivukoNda pollaadha vanpEychchi kongai vaangi,
petreduththa thaaypOla maduppa aarum pENaanan jamuNdugandha piLLai kaNdeer,
nelthoduththa malarneelam niRaindha soozhal irunjiRaiya vaNdoliyum nedunga Naartham,
sitradimEl silamboliyum mizhatru naangoorth thiruththetri yambalaththen sengaN maalE.
1.meaning: Behold the Child who, when the wicked, cruel demoness Putana took the form of a tender maiden with golden-bangled arms and offered her breast as if she were the very mother who bore Him, gladly drank the poison that none would relish (and so destroyed her). He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tetriyambalam temple in Nangur, where in the groves filled with blue-lily blossoms set among the paddy, the hum of broad-winged bees mingles with the sound of the anklets upon the small feet of the long-eyed (women).

2.translit:
padaladaindha siRukurambai nuzhaindhu pukkup pasuveNNey padhamaarap paNNai mutrum,
adaladarththa vERkaNNaar thOkkai patri alandhalaimai seydhuzhalu maiyan kaNdeer,
madaleduththa nedunthengin pazhangal veezha maanganigaL thiratturuttaa varuneerp ponni,
thidaleduththu malarsumandhang izhiyu naangoorth thiruththetri yambalaththen sengaN maalE
2.meaning: Behold the Lord who would slip into the small thatched huts roofed with leaves, eat His fill of fresh white butter, and roam the whole settlement creating mischief — seizing the garments of the women whose eyes were like battle-piercing spears. He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tetriyambalam temple in Nangur, where the waters of the Ponni (Kaveri) come bearing flowers — heaping up the banks, rolling along ripe mangoes and toppling old coconut clusters from the tall-leaved palms.

3.translit:
vaaraarum mulaimadavaaL pinnaik kaagi vaLaimaruppiR kadunjinaththu vanthaa Laarndha,
kaaraarthiN vidaiyadarththu vadhuvai yaaNda karumugilpOl thiruniRaththen kaNNar kaNdeer,
Eraarum malarppozhilgaL thazhuvi yengum ezhilmadhiyaik kaalthodaa viLangu sOdhi,
seeraaru maNimaadam thigazhum naangoorth thiruththetri yambalaththen sengaN maalE.
3.meaning: Behold my Lord, dark-hued like a rain-cloud in His sacred form, who subdued the seven fierce, strong-legged bulls with curved horns and furious rage, and thus won the hand of the simple maiden Nappinnai of the well-veiled bosom in marriage. He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tetriyambalam temple in Nangur, embraced everywhere by lovely flower-groves, where the radiant towers shine so high that their light touches the beautiful moon, gleaming with fine jeweled mansions.

4.translit:
kalaiyilangu magalalgul kamalap paavai kadhirmuththa veNNagaiyaaL karunga Naaychchi,
mulaiyilangu moLimaNippooN vadamum thEyppa moovaadha varainedunthOL moorththi kaNdeer,
malaiyilangu niraichchandhi maada veedhi aadavarai madamozhiyaar mugaththu iraNdu
silaivilangi mananjiRaikoN dirukkum naangoorth thiruththetri yambalaththen sengaN maalE.
4.meaning: Behold the Lord, the Embodiment with ageless long mountain-like shoulders against which press the strands of bright jeweled ornaments shining on the breast of the lotus-like dark-eyed cowherd-maiden — she of the broad waist adorned with a glowing girdle and the pearl-bright smile. He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tetriyambalam temple in Nangur, in whose streets of jeweled mansions, ranged like rows on hills, the two bow-like (brows) on the faces of the soft-spoken women bend and hold captive the hearts of men who pass.

5.translit:
thaanpOlu menRezhundhaan tharaNi yaaLan adhukaNdu thariththiruppaa narakkar thangaL,
kOnpOlu menRezhundhaan kunRa manna irubadhuthO LudanthuNiththa voruvan kaNdeer,
maanpOlu mennOkkin seyya vaayaar maragadham pOl madakkiLiyaik kaimEl koNdu,
thEnpOlu menmazhalai payitrum naangoorth thiruththetri yambalaththen sengaN maalE.
5.meaning: Behold the peerless One who severed at once the twenty mountain-like shoulders of (Ravana) — the king of the demons who rose up thinking himself supreme; though the ruler of the earth (Indra) had risen up declaring himself supreme, when he saw this he could no longer hold his ground. He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tetriyambalam temple in Nangur, where the red-lipped, fawn-eyed women hold emerald-green tender parrots upon their hands and teach them to lisp soft words sweet as honey.

6.translit:
pongilangu purinoolum thOlum thaazhap pollaadha kuRaLuruvaayp porundhaa vaaNan
mangalamsEr maRaivELvi yadhanuL pukku maNNagalam kuRaiyirandha maindhan kaNdeer,
kongalarndha malarkkuzhalaar kongai thOyndha kungumaththin kuzhambaLaindha kOlandh thannaal,
sengalangal veNmaNalmEl thavazhum naangoorth thiruthetri yambalaththen sengaN maalE
6.meaning: Behold the young Lord who, taking the form of a small, unsightly dwarf with the bright sacred thread and the deerskin hanging upon Him, entered the auspicious Vedic sacrifice of the hostile Bali and begged a small measure of the earth's expanse. He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tetriyambalam temple in Nangur, where (the Lord as a child) crawls upon the red-mingled white sand, His form smeared with the paste of saffron that had touched the breasts of the women with fragrant-flowered tresses.

7.translit:
silambinidaich chiRuparalpOl periya mEru thirukkuLambil kaNakaNappath thiruvaa kaaram
kulunga, nilamadandhai thanaiyidandhu pulgik kOttidaivaith tharuLiyaveng kOmaan kaNdeer,
ilangiyanaan maRaiyanaiththu manga maaRum Ezhisaiyum kELvigaLu meNdik kengum,
silambiyanaR perunjelvam thigazhum naangoorth thiruthetri yambalaththen sengaN maalE.
7.meaning: Behold our great King who, (as the Boar) lifting up the Earth-maiden, embraced her and bore her safely upon His tusk — while the mighty Meru rattled like a tiny pebble within an anklet against His sacred hoof, and His holy form swayed. He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tetriyambalam temple in Nangur, where the four shining Vedas with their six Angas, the seven-fold music, and the sacred learning have all spread abroad — great good fortune flourishing throughout the eight directions everywhere.

8.translit:
Ezhulagum thaazhvaraiyu mengu moodi eNdisaiyu maNdalamum maNdi, aNdam
mOzhaiyezhun thaazhimigum oozhi veLLam munnagatti lodukkiyavem moorththi kaNdeer,
oozhithoRu moozhithoRu muyarndha selvath thOngiyanaan maRaiyanaiththum thaangu naavar,
sEzhuyarndha maNimaadam thigazhum naangoorth thiruthetri yambalaththen sengaN maalE.
8.meaning: Behold our Lord, the Embodiment, who at the great deluge — when the welling floodwaters surged up and covered all the seven worlds and the low hills, pressed upon the eight directions and the spheres, and rose bubbling over the cosmic egg and the oceans — drew it all in and contained it within His own belly. He is my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tetriyambalam temple in Nangur, where dwell those whose tongues uphold all the four lofty Vedas, ever-flourishing in exalted wealth age after age, and where tall jeweled mansions shine.

9.translit:
seeraNindha maNimaadam thigazhum naangoorth thiruthetri yambalaththen sengaN maalai,
kooraNindha vElvalavan aali naadan kodimaada mangaiyarkOn kuRaiya laaLi
paaraNindha tholpugazhaan kaliyan sonna paamaalai yivaiyaindhu maindhum vallaar,
seeraNindha vulagaththu manna raagich chENvisumbil vaanavaraayth thigazhvar thaamE.
9.meaning: These are the ten verses of the garland of song sung by Kaliyan — Tirumangai, lord of the realm of Aali, master of the sharp spear, king of Tirumangai of bannered mansions, lord of Kuraiyalur, possessed of ancient fame that adorns the earth — upon my red-eyed Tirumal of the Tetriyambalam temple in adorned Nangur. Those who can master all these ten will become kings in this fair world and then shine as celestials in the lofty heavens.

0.translit:
thoombudaip panaikkai vezham thuyarkeduth tharuLi, mannu
kaambudaik kunRa menthik kadumazhai kaaththa enthai,
poompunal ponni muRRum pukunthupon varaNda, engum
thempozhil kamazhum naangkoorth thirumaNik koodath thaane.
0.meaning: My Lord who graciously removed the suffering of the elephant (Gajendra) with its hollow trunk-like hands, and who lifted up the lasting mountain with its bamboo groves to shield the cows from the fierce rain — He dwells at Tirumanikoodam in Nangur, where the flower-laden waters of the Ponni (Kaveri) flood everywhere depositing gold, and where the honeyed groves spread their fragrance all around.

1.translit:
kavvaivaaL eyiRRu vanpeyk kathirmulai suvaiththu, ilangai
vavviya idumbai theerak kadungkaNai thurantha enthai,
kovvaivaay makaLir kongaik kungkumam kazhuvip pontha,
theyvaneer kamazhum naangkoorth thirumaNik koodath thaane.
1.meaning: My Lord who sucked away the life from the radiant breast of the cruel demoness Putana with her clamorous sword-like fangs, and who shot fierce arrows to end the calamity by seizing Lanka — He dwells at Tirumanikoodam in Nangur, fragrant with sacred waters that come bearing the kumkumam washed from the breasts of women with kovvai-red lips.

2.translit:
maaththozhil madangkach cheRRu maRuthiRa nadanthu vanthaaL
seeththozhil sithaiththup pinnai sevviththoL puNarntha enthai,
naaththozhil maRaival laarkaL nayantharam payantha vaNkaith
theeththozhil payilum naangkoorth thirumaNik koodath thaane.
2.meaning: My Lord who destroyed the mighty deeds (of the demonic horse Kesi), who strode across in another form, who subdued the fierce strong-legged bulls and then embraced the lovely shoulders of Nappinnai — He dwells at Tirumanikoodam in Nangur, where the masters skilled in the Vedas through their tongues lovingly practice the rites of fire with bounteous, charitable hands.

3.translit:
thaangkarunj chinaththu vanthaaL thadakkaimaa maruppu vaangki,
poongkurun thosiththup puLvaay piLanntheru thadarttha enthai,
maangkani nukarntha manthi vanthuvaN diriya
vaazhaiththeengkani nukarum naangkoorth thirumaNik koodaththaane.
3.meaning: My Lord who broke off the tusks of the great elephant (Kuvalayapida) with its strong legs, broad trunk and unbearable fury, who snapped the flowering kurunda tree, who split open the beak of the bird (Bakasura), and who subdued the bulls — He dwells at Tirumanikoodam in Nangur, where the monkey that had eaten the mango fruit comes and, driving away the bees, eats the sweet plantain fruit.

4.translit:
karumaka Lilangkai yaatti pilangkoLvaay thiRanthu thanmel
varumavaL seviyum mookkum vaaLinaal thadintha enthai,
perumakaL pethai mangkai thannodum pirivi laatha,
thirumakaL maruvum naangkoorth thirumaNik koodath thaane.
4.meaning: My Lord who, when the dark demoness Surpanakha, ruler of Lanka, came at Him opening her cavern-like mouth, cut off her ears and nose with His sword — He dwells at Tirumanikoodam in Nangur, where the great goddess Lakshmi, never parted from Him, abides together with the tender young lady (Bhudevi).

5.translit:
keNdaiyum kuRaLum puLLum kezhalu mariyum maavum,
aNdamum sudarum alla aaTRalu maaya enthai,
oNdiRal thenna noeda vadavara soettang kaNda,
thiNdiRa laaLar naangkoorth thirumaNik koodath thane.
5.meaning: My Lord who became the fish, the dwarf (Vamana), the bird (Garuda incarnate / swan), the boar, the man-lion, and the horse (avataras), and who is the cosmic egg, the luminaries, and all other powers — He dwells at Tirumanikoodam in Nangur, the home of men of firm valor who once made the mighty southern king flee and saw the northern kings routed.

6.translit:
kunRamum vaanum maNNum kuLirpunal thingka Loedu,
ninRavenj chudarum allaa nilaikaLu maaya enthai,
manRamum vayalum kaavum maadamum maNangkoNdu, engum
thenRalvanth ulavum naangkoorth thirumaNik koodath thaane.
6.meaning: My Lord who became the mountains, the sky, the earth, the cool waters along with the moon, the blazing fierce sun, and all the other states of being — He dwells at Tirumanikoodam in Nangur, where the southern breeze, gathering fragrance from the assembly-halls, the fields, the groves and the mansions, wanders everywhere.

7.translit:
sangkaiyum thuNivum poyyum meyyum ith tharaNi yoembum,
pongkiya mukilum allaap poruLkaLu maaya venthai,
pangkaya mukuththa theRal parukiya vaaLai paaya,
sengkayal ukaLum naangkoorth thirumaNik koodath thaane.
7.meaning: My Lord who became doubt and certainty, falsehood and truth, the swelling cloud that nourishes this earth, and all other things — He dwells at Tirumanikoodam in Nangur, where the scabbard-fish (vaalai) that has drunk the nectar dripping from the lotus leaps about, and the red carp frolic.

8.translit:
paavamum aRamum veedum inbamun thunban thaanum
koavamum aruLum allaak kuNangkaLu maaya enthai,
moovari lengkaL moorththi ivan, ena munivaroedu,
thevarvanth iRainjum naangkoorth thirumaNik koodath thane.
8.meaning: My Lord who became sin and virtue, liberation, pleasure and pain itself, anger and grace, and all other qualities — He dwells at Tirumanikoodam in Nangur, where, saying 'Among the three (deities) this one is our Lord,' the sages together with the gods come and worship.

9.translit:
thingkaLthoy maada naangkoorth thirumaNik koodath thaanai
mangkaiyar thalaivan vaNthaark kaliyanvaa yolikaL vallaar,
pongkunee rulaka maaNdu ponnula kaaNdu, pinnum
vengkathirp parithi vattath thoodupoy viLangku vaare.
9.meaning: Those who are able to recite these verses uttered by Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar), the lord of Tirumangai adorned with bounteous garlands, on the Lord of Tirumanikoodam in Nangur where the mansions touch the moon — they shall rule this world surrounded by surging seas, then rule the golden world (of the heavens), and beyond that shall pass through the orb of the hot-rayed sun and shine in radiance.

0.translit:
kaNNaar kadalpool tirumeeni kariyaay,
naNNaarmunai veNRikoL vaarmannu naangoor,
tiNNaar madiLsoozh tiruveLLak kuLattuL
aNNaa, adiyeen idaraik kaLaiyaayee.
0.meaning: O dark one whose sacred body is hued like the wide eye-filling ocean, you dwell forever in Naangoor where warriors win victory over their foes on the battlefield. O Lord enshrined in Tiruvellakkulam ringed by strong, lofty ramparts, deign to remove the sorrows of me, your servant.

1.translit:
kondaar tuLavamalar konda Nivaanee,
nandaada perumpugazh veediyar naangoor,
sendaa maraineert tiruveLLak kuLattuL
endaay, adiyeen idaraik kaLaiyaayee.
1.meaning: O Lord who wears the clustered, blossoming tulasi garland, you reside in Naangoor of the Vedic brahmins of unfading great fame. O my Father in Tiruvellakkulam of waters where red lotuses bloom, deign to remove the sorrows of me, your servant.

2.translit:
kunRaal kuLirmaa ritaduttu ugandaanee,
nanRaa yaperum pugazhvee diyarnaangoor,
senRaar vaNangum tiruveLLak kuLattuL
ninRaay, nediyaay adiyeen idarneekkee.
2.meaning: O Lord who joyfully held up a hill to ward off the cold, drenching rain, you abide in Naangoor of the Vedic brahmins of truly great fame. O tall one who stands in Tiruvellakkulam which those who come there worship, remove the sorrows of me, your servant.

3.translit:
kaanaar karikom batosittha kaLiRee,
naanaa vagainal lavarman niyanaangoor,
teenaar pozhilsoozh tiruveLLak kuLattuL
aanaay, adiyeenuk karuLpuri yaayee.
3.meaning: O elephant-strong Lord who snapped off the tusk of the forest elephant, you abide in Naangoor where good people of every kind dwell firmly. O Lord enshrined in Tiruvellakkulam girt by honey-laden groves, grant your grace to me, your servant.

4.translit:
veedaar tiruveeng kadammeeya viLakkee,
naadaar pugazhvee diyarman niyanaangoor,
seedaar pozhilsoozh tiruveLLak kuLattaay,
paadaa varuveen vinaiyaa yinapaaRRee.
4.meaning: O light who dwells at sacred Venkatam abounding in hunters, you abide in Naangoor of the Vedic brahmins whose fame fills the land. O Lord of Tiruvellakkulam encircled by lush groves, drive away the karmic sins of me who comes singing your praise.

5.translit:
kallaal kadalai yaNaikatti yugandaay,
nallaar palarvee diyarman niyanaangoor
selvaa, tiruveLLak kuLatturai vaanee,
ellaa idarum kedumaa RaruLaayee.
5.meaning: O Lord who joyfully built the bridge across the sea with stones, you abide in Naangoor where many good Vedic brahmins dwell firmly. O wealthy one who resides in Tiruvellakkulam, grant your grace so that all my sorrows may perish.

6.translit:
koolaal niraimeettha engoo valarkoovee,
naalaa giyavee diyarman niyanaangoor,
seelaar vayalsoozh tiruveLLak kuLattuL
maalee, enaval vinaiteert taruLaayee.
6.meaning: O King of our cowherds who tended the cattle with your staff, you abide in Naangoor of the brahmins of the four Vedas. O Tirumal in Tiruvellakkulam surrounded by fields teeming with carp fish, graciously cut away my cruel karma.

7.translit:
vaaraa gamataagi yimmaNNai yidandaay,
naaraa yaNanee nallavee diyarnaangoor,
seeraar pozhilsoozh tiruveLLak kuLattuL
aaraa vamudee, adiyeeR karuLaayee.
7.meaning: O Lord who became the Boar and lifted up this earth, O Narayana, you dwell in Naangoor of the good Vedic brahmins. O insatiable nectar in Tiruvellakkulam ringed by splendid groves, grant your grace to me, your servant.

8.translit:
poovaar tirumaa magaLpul liyamaarbaa,
naavaar pugazhvee diyarman niyanaangoort
teevaa tiruveLLak kuLatturai vaanee,
aavaa adiyaa nivan, enRaruLaayee.
8.meaning: O Lord on whose chest the lotus-dwelling Lakshmi is embraced, you abide in Naangoor of the Vedic brahmins whose praise fills every tongue. O God who resides in Tiruvellakkulam, taking pity, grant your grace saying 'Ah, this one is my servant.'

9.translit:
nallan pudaivee diyarman niyanaangoorch
selvan tiruveL Lakkulat turaivaanai,
kallin malitooL kaliyan sonnamaalai,
valla renaval lavarvaa navartaamee.
9.meaning: This garland of verses was sung by Kaliyan of shoulders mighty as stone, upon the wealthy Lord who resides in Tiruvellakkulam in Naangoor where the loving good Vedic brahmins dwell firmly. Those who are able to master these verses shall themselves become the celestials of heaven.

0.translit:
kavaLa yaanaik kombosittha kaNNan enRum, kaamaru seerk
kuvaLai meega mannameeni koNDa koon ennaanai enRum,
tavaLa maaDa neeDu naangaith taamaraiyaaL keeLvan enRum,
pavaLa vaayaaL en maDandai paarthan paLLi paaDuvaaLe.
0.meaning: "He is Kannan who broke the tusk of the rutting elephant," she says; "He is my Lord, my elephant, whose beautiful, lovely body took on the hue of the blue water-lily and the dark cloud," she says; "He is the Consort of Lakshmi who dwells in lofty Nangur of white mansions," she says — my coral-lipped maiden sings of Parthanpalli.

1.translit:
kanjan viTTa venjinaththa kaLiRu aDartha kaaLai enRum,
vanjam meevi vandapeeyin uyirai uNDa maayan enRum,
senjol aaLar neeDu naangaith teeva teevan enRu enRu oodi,
panji anna mel aDiyaaL paarthan paLLi paaDuvaaLe.
1.meaning: "He is the youthful one who crushed the fiercely enraged elephant that Kamsa set upon him," she says; "He is the wondrous one who drank the life of the demoness Putana who came in deceit," she says; reciting again and again "He is the God of gods of lofty Nangur where the masters of perfect speech dwell" — she of feet soft as cotton sings of Parthanpalli.

2.translit:
aNDar koon en aanai enRum, aayar maadar kongai pulgu
seNDan enRum, naanmaRaigaL teeDi ooDum selvan enRum,
vaNDu ulavu pozhil koL naangai mannu maayan enRu enRu oodi,
paNDu pool anRu en maDandai paarthan paLLi paaDuvaaLe.
2.meaning: "He is the Lord of the celestials, my elephant," she says; "He is the gallant one who embraces the breasts of the cowherd women," she says; "He is the wealthy one whom the four Vedas seek and run after," she says; reciting again and again "He is the abiding wondrous one of Nangur with groves where bees wander" — my maiden, no longer as she was before, sings of Parthanpalli.

3.translit:
kollai aanaaL parisazhindaaL kool vaLaiyaar tam mugappee,
mallai mun neer taTTilangai kaTTu azhitta maayan enRum,
selvam malgu maRaiyoor naangai teeva teevan enRu enRu oodi,
pal vaLaiyaaL en maDandai paarthan paLLi paaDuvaaLe.
3.meaning: She has become wild, she has lost her grace before the very faces of the bangle-bedecked women; "He is the wondrous one who destroyed the fortifications of Lanka set amid the great surging waters," she says; reciting again and again "He is the God of gods of Nangur of the Vedic scholars abounding in wealth" — my maiden of many bangles sings of Parthanpalli.

4.translit:
arakkar aavi maaLavanRu aazh kaDal soozh ilangai seRRa,
kurakku arasan enRum kola villi enRum, maa madiyai
nerukku maaDa neeDu naangai ninmalan taan enRu enRu oodi,
parakkazhindaaL en maDandai paarthan paLLi paaDuvaaLe.
4.meaning: "He is the king of the monkeys who, that day, destroyed Lanka surrounded by the deep ocean so that the demons' lives perished," she says; "He is the wielder of the beautiful bow," she says; reciting again and again "He is the flawless pure one of lofty Nangur whose mansions press against the great moon" — my maiden, grown bewildered and disordered, sings of Parthanpalli.

5.translit:
njaalam muRRum uNDu umizhinda naadan enRum, naanilam soozh
veelai anna kolameeni vaNNan enRum, meel ezhundu
seelu ugaLum vayal koL naangaith teeva teevan enRu enRu oodi,
paalin nalla men mozhiyaaL paarthan paLLi paaDuvaaLe.
5.meaning: "He is the Lord who swallowed the whole earth and brought it forth again," she says; "He is the one of beautiful form colored like the ocean that surrounds the four-fold land," she says; reciting again and again "He is the God of gods of Nangur with fields where sael-fish leap up and frolic" — she of soft words sweet as milk sings of Parthanpalli.

6.translit:
naaDi enRanuL Long koNDa naadan enRum, naanmaRaigaL
teeDi enRum kaaNa maaTTaach selvan enRum, siRai koL vaNDu
seeDu ulavu pozhil koL naangaith teeva teevan enRu enRu oodi,
paaDagam seer mel aDiyaaL paarthan paLLi paaDuvaaLe.
6.meaning: "He is the Lord who, seeking, took his place within my heart," she says; "He is the wealthy one whom even the four Vedas search for yet cannot see," she says; reciting again and again "He is the God of gods of Nangur with groves where winged bees roam in their abundance" — she of soft feet adorned with the paadagam anklet sings of Parthanpalli.

7.translit:
ulagam eettum oruvan enRum oN suDarooDum paraeydaa,
nilavu maazhip paDaiyan enRum neesan enRum, ten disaikkuth
tilatam anna maRaiyoor naangaith teeva teevan enRu enRu oodi,
palarum eesa en maDandai paarthan paLLi paaDuvaaLe.
7.meaning: "He is the unique one whom all the worlds praise," she says; "He is the bearer of the gleaming discus that shines beyond even the bright luminaries," she says; "He is my beloved," she says; reciting again and again "He is the God of gods of Nangur of the Vedic scholars, the very tilaka-mark of the southern land" — though many mock her, my maiden sings of Parthanpalli.

8.translit:
kaNNan enRum vaanavargaL kaadaliththu malargaL toovum,
eNNan enRum inban enRum eezhulugukku aadi enRum,
tiNNa maaDa neeDu naangaith teeva teevan enRu enRu oodi,
paNNin anna men mozhiyaaL paarthan paLLi paaDuvaaLe.
8.meaning: "He is Kannan," she says; "He is the one upon whom the celestials, in love, scatter flowers," she says; "He is the joyful one, the origin of the seven worlds," she says; reciting again and again "He is the God of gods of lofty Nangur of sturdy mansions" — she of soft words sweet as melody sings of Parthanpalli.

9.translit:
paaruL nalla maRaiyoor naangaip paarthan paLLi sengaN maalai,
vaar koL nalla mulai maDavaaL paaDal aindaay mozhinda maaRRam,
koor koL nalla veel kaliyan kooRu tamizh paththum vallaar,
eer koL nalla vaigundaththuL inbam naaLum eyduvaare.
9.meaning: These are the words, fashioned into five-fold song, spoken by the lovely lady of fair breasts about the dark-hued, red-eyed Lord of Parthanpalli in Nangur of the good Vedic scholars of this earth. Those who can master all ten of these Tamil verses uttered by Kaliyan of the fine sharp spear shall forever attain bliss in the beautiful, glorious Vaikuntha.

0.translit:
peeraanaik kuRunguDiyem perumaanai, tirudaNkaal
ooraanaik karambanoor uttamanai, muttilangu
kaaraart tiN kaDaleezhum malaiyeezhiv vulageezhuNDum,
araaden Riruntaanaik kaNDadu ten naranggatte,
0.meaning: I beheld Him who is the great Lord of Tiruppeer, our Lord at Tirukkurunguudi, the One of Tiruttankaal, the Supreme One at Karambanuur where pearls gleam — Him who swallowed the seven dark mighty oceans, the seven mountains, and the seven worlds, and yet reclined as though still unsatisfied. This One I saw at the southern Srirangam.

0.translit:
angkaNedu madhiLpudaisoo zhayoththi yennum
aNinagaraththu ulaganaiththum viLakkum soadhi
vengkadhiroan kulaththukkoar viLakkaayth thoandri
viNmuzhudhu muyakkoNda veeran Ranai
sengkaNedung karumugilai yiraaman Ranaith
thillainagarth thiruchchithra koodan thannuL
engkaLthani mudhalvanaiyem perumaan Ranai
endrukoloa kaNkuLirak kaaNu naaLea.
0.meaning: In the beautiful city called Ayodhya, ringed by great walls and tall ramparts, He appeared as a lamp that lights up all the worlds, born as a unique lamp to the dynasty of the fierce-rayed Sun, the hero who took possession of all the heavens. He is Rama, the red-eyed one, dark as a great cloud. When, oh when, shall my eyes be cooled by beholding Him who dwells in Tiruchitrakutam in the city of Tillai, our sole Primal Lord, our great Master?

1.translit:
vandhedhirndha thaadagaidhan uraththaikkeeri
varugurudhi pozhidharavan kaNaiyon Rea-vi
mandhirangkoL maraimunivan veaLvi kaaththu
vallarakka ruyiruNda maindhan kaaNmin
sendhaLirvaay malarnagaiseazh sezhundhaN soalaith
thillainagarth thiruchchithra koodan thannuL
andhaNarga LorumooVaa yirava reaththa
aNimaNiyaa sanaththirundha vammaan Raanea.
1.meaning: Tearing open the chest of Tadaka who came charging at Him so that gushing blood poured forth, He loosed a single mighty arrow; He guarded the sacrifice of the sage who held the sacred Vedic mantras, and the young one drank the lives of the strong demons. Behold Him! In Tiruchitrakutam in the city of Tillai, set in cool luxuriant groves with red tender shoots and blossoming smiles, He sits enthroned on a beautiful gem-studded seat, praised by three thousand Brahmins.

2.translit:
sevvariNaR karunedungkaN seedhaik kaagich
sinaVidaiyoan silaiyiruththu mazhuVaa Leandhi
vevvariNaR silaiVaangki vendrikoNdu
vealveandhar pagaidhadindha veeran Ranai
thevvaranja nedumpurisai yuyarndha paangkarth
thillainagarth thiruchchithra koodan thannuL
evvarivenj silaiththadakkai yiraaman Ranai
iraignjuvaa riNaiyadiyea yiraignji neanea.
2.meaning: For the sake of Sita of the long dark eyes with red lines, He broke the bow of Him who rides the raging bull (Siva); then He took up the axe-wielding one's fierce striped bow and, winning victory, the hero cut down the enmity of spear-bearing kings. He is Rama of the broad hands holding the cruel striped bow, who dwells in Tiruchitrakutam in the city of Tillai, beside its high long ramparts that make foes tremble. I have bowed at the joined feet of those who worship Him.

3.translit:
thoththalarpoonj surikuzhalkai keasi sollaal
thonnagaran thurandhuthuraik kangkai thannai
paththiyudaik kugankadaththa vanampoayp pukkup
paradhanukku paadhukamu marasu meendhu
siththiraku daththirundhaan Ranai yindru
thillainagarth thiruchchithra koodan thannuL
eththanaiyum kaNkuLirak kaaNap petra
iruNilaththaark kimaiyavarnea rovvaar thaamea.
3.meaning: At the word of Kaikeyi of the curling tresses adorned with flower-clusters, He left the ancient city; Guhan the devoted ferried Him across the Ganga at its landing-ford, and entering the forest He went and gave to Bharata the sandals and the kingship, and dwelt in Chitrakuta. The dwellers of this wide earth who have today been blessed to behold to their hearts' fullest content Him in Tiruchitrakutam in the city of Tillai - even the celestials cannot equal them.

4.translit:
valivaNakku varainedundhoaL viraadhaik kondru
vaNdamizhmaa munikoduththa variVil vaangki
kalaivaNakku noakkarakki mookkai neekkik
karanoadu thoodaNan Ra nuyirai vaangki
silaivaNakki maanmariya veydhaan Ranaith
thillainagarth thiruchchithra koodan thannuL
thalaivaNakkik kaikooppi yeaththa vallaar
thiridhalaal thavamudaiththith tharaNi thaanea.
4.meaning: Slaying Viradha of mountain-like long arms that bowed all strength, He took up the striped bow given by the great sage of bounteous Tamil; He cut off the nose of the demoness whose glance shamed the gentle doe, and seized the lives of Khara and Dushana, and bending His bow He aimed and shot down the deer. Because those able to bow their heads and join their palms in praise of Him who dwells in Tiruchitrakutam in the city of Tillai walk upon it, this earth itself is rich in merit.

5.translit:
manamaruvu vaidheaki piriyaluttruth
thaLarveydhich sadaayuvaivai kundhath theatri
vanamaruvu kaviyarasan kaadhal koNdu
vaaliyaikon Rilangkainaga rarakkar koamaan
sinamadangka maaruthiyaal suduviththaanaith
thillainagarth thiruchchithra koodan thannuL
inidhamarndha ammaanai iraaman Ranai
eaththuvaa riNaiyadiyea yeaththi neanea.
5.meaning: Parting from heart-cherished Vaidehi (Sita) and growing faint, He sent Jatayu to Vaikunta; winning the love of the forest-dwelling monkey-king, He slew Vali, and so that the wrath of the demon-lord of Lanka might subside, He had the city burned through Hanuman (Maruti). I have praised at the joined feet of those who praise that Lord Rama, who dwells contentedly in Tiruchitrakutam in the city of Tillai.

6.translit:
kuraikadalai yadalampaal maruga veydhu
kulaikatti marukaraiyai yadhanaa leari
erineduvea larakkarodu milangkai veandhan
innuyirkoN davandhampik karasu meendhu
thirumagaLoa dinidhamarndha selvan Ranaith
thillainagarth thiruchchithra koodan thannuL
arasamarndhaa nadisoodu marasai yallaal
arasaaga veNNeanmaR Rarasu thaanea.
6.meaning: He shot His mighty arrows so the roaring ocean churned and recoiled, built a bridge and reached the far shore by it; taking the dear life of the king of Lanka along with the demons of fiery long spears, He gave the kingship to that one's younger brother (Vibhishana), and abides happily with Lakshmi. He is the wealthy Lord who dwells in Tiruchitrakutam in the city of Tillai. Except for the sovereignty of wearing on my head the feet of that enthroned King, I count no other sovereignty as sovereignty at all.

7.translit:
amponedu maNimaada ayoththi yeydhi
araseydhi agaththiyanvaayth thaanmun kondraan
Ranperundhol kadhaikkeaTTu midhilaich selvi
ulaguyyath thiruvayiru vaaththa makkaL
sempavaLath thiraLvaaththan saridhai keaTTaan
thillainagarth thiruchchithra koodan thannuL
emperumaan Ranjsaridhai seviyaal kaNNaal
paruguvoa minnamudhai madhiyoa mindrea.
7.meaning: Reaching Ayodhya of tall jewelled mansions of fine gold and gaining the kingship, He heard from Agastya's lips His own great ancient story of those He had formerly slain; the children whom Mithila's princess (Sita) bore from her sacred womb for the world's salvation recounted His coral-lipped story, and He listened to it. We shall drink in through ear and eye the never-ceasing nectar of the story of our Lord who dwells in Tiruchitrakutam in the city of Tillai; we heed nothing else.

8.translit:
serithavachcham pukandrannaich sendru kondru
sezhumaraiyoa nuyirmeeTTuth thavaththoa neendha
niraimaNippoo NaNiyungkoN dilavaNan Ranaith
thambiyaal vaaneatri munivan veaNda
thiralViLangku milakkumanaip pirindhaan Ranaith
thillainagarth thiruchchithra koodan thannuL
uraivaanai maravaadha vuLLan thannai
udaiyoammaR Rurudhuyara madaiyoa mindrea.
8.meaning: Going forth He slew Sambuka of fierce austerities and restored the life of the Brahmin's son, and took the perfect gem-set ornaments the ascetic gave; through His brother He sent Lavana up to heaven, and at the sage's bidding He parted from might-shining Lakshmana. We possess a heart that never forgets Him who dwells in Tiruchitrakutam in the city of Tillai; therefore we shall meet with no grave sorrow at all.

9.translit:
andrusaraa sarangkaLaivai kundhath theatri
adalaravap pagaiyeari yasurar thammai
vendruilangku maNinedundhoaL naangkum thoandra
viNmuzhudhu medhirvaraththan thaamam mea-vi
sendrinidhu veetrirundha vammaan Ranaith
thillainagarth thiruchchithra koodan thannuL
endrumnindraa navanivanen Reaththi naaLum
indraignjuminoa veppozhudhum thoNdeer neerea.
9.meaning: On that day He bore the moving and unmoving beings up to Vaikunta; mounting Garuda, the foe of the mighty serpents, He conquered the asuras, and with His four shining jewelled long arms revealed, while all the heavens came forth to meet Him, He went and reached His own abode and sat there happily. O devotees! Praising day after day, 'He who stood ever-eternal is this very one,' the One who dwells in Tiruchitrakutam in the city of Tillai - do you bow to Him at every moment.

10.translit:
thillainagarth thiruchchithra koodan thannuL
thiralViLangku maaruthiyoa damarndhaan Ranai
ellaiyilseerth thayaradhandran maganaayth thoandriRtru
adhumudhalaath thannulagam pukka theeraa
kolliyalum padaiththaanaik kotra voLvaaL
koazhiyarkoan kudaikkulasea karanjsoR seydha
nalliyalin thamizhmaalai paththum vallaar
nalandhigazhnaa raNanadikkeezh naNNu vaarea.
10.meaning: This faultless-natured garland of ten Tamil verses, composed by Kulasekhara - king of the Cholas of Uraiyur, lord of the victorious sharp sword and royal parasol - tells of Him who dwells with might-shining Hanuman (Maruti) in Tiruchitrakutam in the city of Tillai: from His appearing as the son of boundlessly-glorious Dasaratha down to His entering His own world, and of His wielder of cruel weapons. Those who master these ten will reach the feet of grace-radiant Narayana.

11.translit:
oonvaada vuNNaa thuyirkkaava liTTu udaliR piriyaap pulanaindhum nondhu,
thaamvaada vaadath thavamseyya veaNdaa thamadhaa imaiyoa rulagaaLa kiRpeer
kaanaada manjnjaik kaNamaada maadea kayalaadu kaaneerp pazhanam pudaipoay,
theanaada maadak kodiyaadu thillaith thiruchchithra koodam sendrusear minkaLea.
11.meaning: If you would rule as your own the world of the immortals, you need not perform austerities that make the body waste with eating nothing, that set guard over the life-breath, and that torment the five senses inseparable from the body until you wither away. Just go and reach Tiruchitrakutam in Tillai - where forests sway, flocks of peacocks dance, carp-fish frolic in the watery fields on every side, bees hum, and banners flutter on the mansions.

12.translit:
kaayoadu needu kaniyuNdu veesu kadungkaal nugarndhu nedungkaalam,
aindhu theeyodu nindru thavanjseyya veaNdaa thirumaarbanaich sindhai yuLvaiththu menbeer,
vaayoadhu veadham malgindra tholseer maraiyaaLar naaLum muraiyaal vaLarththa,
theeyoangka voangkap pukazhoangku thillaith thiruchchithra koodam sendrusear minkaLea.
12.meaning: You who say you will set the Lord with Lakshmi on His chest within your minds - you need not stand for ages amid the five fires performing austerities, eating sprouts and aged fruit and feeding on the harsh wind. Go and reach Tiruchitrakutam in Tillai, whose glory rises high as the sacrificial fires rise high, kindled in due order day after day by the Brahmins of ancient excellence in whom the Veda chanted by mouth abounds.

13.translit:
vembum sinaththup punakkeazha londraayvirineer mudhuveLLa muLpuk kazhundha,
vambuN pozhilsoo zhulagan Reduththaan adippoa thaNaivaan viruppoa diruppeer,
paimponnu muththum maNiyum koNarndhu padaimannavanpal lavarkkoan paNindha,
sempon maNimaadang kaLsoozhndha thillaith thiruchchithra koodam sendrusear minkaLea.
13.meaning: You who wait with longing to attain the lotus-feet of Him who, becoming a single boar of seething wrath, plunged into the deep flood of the spreading waters and raised up the world girt by fragrant groves - go and reach Tiruchitrakutam in Tillai, ringed by mansions of red gold and gems, where the warlike king, the lord of the Pallavas, bowed and brought fresh gold, pearls, and jewels in offering.

14.translit:
arumaa nilaman RaLappaan kuRaLaay avuNan peruveaL viyilsen Rirandha,
perumaan thirunaa mampidhaR Rinundham piravith thuyarneengku dhumenna kiRpeer!
karumaa kadaluL kidandhaa nuvandhu kavainaa varavi naNaippaLLi yinmeal,
thirumaal thirumang kaiyodaadu thillaith thiruchchithra koodam sendrusear minkaLea.
14.meaning: If you would say that, by ceaselessly uttering the holy names of the Lord who became a dwarf to measure the rare great earth that day and went and begged at the asura's great sacrifice, you will be freed of the sorrow of birth - then go and reach Tiruchitrakutam in Tillai, where Tirumal, who reclines gladly within the dark great ocean upon His serpent-couch with forked tongues, sports with Lakshmi (Tirumangai).

15.translit:
koamangka vangkak kadalvaiya muyyak kulamanna rangkam mazhuvil thuNiya,
thaamang kamaruL padaithoTTa vendrith thavamaa muniyaith thamakkaakka kiRpeer,
poomangkai thangkip pulamangkai mannip pukazhmangkai yengkum thigazhappukazhsear
seamangkoL paimpoom pozhilsoozhndha thillaith thiruchchithra koodam sendrusear minkaLea.
15.meaning: If you would make your own that victorious great ascetic (Parasurama) who - so that kings might perish and the world of ships and seas be saved - took up his weapon in the garland-strewn battle and cut the bodies of royal lines with his axe, then go and reach the glory-laden Tiruchitrakutam in Tillai, girt by guarded fresh-flowering groves, where the Lady of the Lotus (Lakshmi) abides, the Lady of the Earth dwells, and the Lady of Fame shines forth everywhere.

16.translit:
neyvaa yazhalam pudhurandhu munneer thuNiyap paNikoN daNiyaarndhu,ilangku
maiyaar vaNivaN NanaiyennNi nundham manaththea yiruththum padivaazha valleer,
avvaayiLamang kaiyarppeasa vundhaan arumaa maraiyan thaNarsindh aipuga,
sevvaayk kiLinaan maraipaadu thillaith thiruchchithra koodam sendrusear minkaLea.
16.meaning: You who are able to live so as to set within your minds, meditating upon Him - the beautifully-adorned shining one dark as collyrium who launched fire-mouthed arrows so that the ocean parted and took service of it - go and reach Tiruchitrakutam in Tillai, where, while the young women there speak of Him, red-mouthed parrots sing the four Vedas so that even He enters the thoughts of the great Vedic Brahmins.

17.translit:
mauval kuzhalaaychchi mendhoaL nayandhu makaram suzhalach suzhalneer payandha,
theyvath thirumaa malarmangkai thangku thirumaar banaichchindhai yuLvaiththu menbeer
kauvaik kaLittrin maruppum poruppil kamazhsandhu mundhi nivaava langkoL,
theyvap punalsoozhndh azhagaaya thillaith thiruchchithra koodam sendrusear minkaLea.
17.meaning: You who say you will set within your minds the Lord on whose chest dwells the divine Lady born of the great holy lotus (Lakshmi), He who delighted in the soft shoulders of the jasmine-tressed cowherd-girl - go and reach the beautiful Tiruchitrakutam in Tillai, encircled by the divine waters of the Kaveri (Niva) that surge bearing along the tusks of fierce elephants and fragrant sandalwood from the hills, and bring pearls.

18.translit:
maavaayi nangkam madhiyaadhu keeri mazhaimaa mudhukun Reduththu,aayar thangkaL
koavaay niraimeayth thuluguNda maayan kuraimaa kazhalkoo dumkurip pudaiyeer,
moovaa yiranaan maraiyaaLar naaLum muraiyaal vaNangka aNangkaaya soadhi,
theavaadhi deavan thigazhgindra thillaith thiruchchithra koodam sendrusear minkaLea.
18.meaning: You who hold the intent to reach the resounding great anklets of the wondrous one (Maya) who, heedless, tore open the mouth-frame of the horse-demon, lifted the great old mountain against the rains, became the cowherds' king and grazed their herds, and swallowed the worlds - go and reach Tiruchitrakutam in Tillai, where the Lord of lords, the God of gods, the divine effulgence, shines forth, worshipped duly day after day by three thousand Brahmins of the four Vedas.

19.translit:
seruneela veaRkaN madavaar thiRaththuch sinaththoadu nindru manaththaal vaLarkkum,
aruneela paava magalap pukazhsear amararkku meydhaadha aNdaththi ruppeer,
peruneer nivaavundhi muththang koNarndhu engkum viththum vayaluL kayalpaayn dhugaLa,
thiruneela nindru thigazhgindra thillaith thiruchchithra koodam sendrusear minkaLea.
19.meaning: You who would dwell in that highest realm of glory unattainable even to the immortals, so that the grievous dark sin nourished in the mind through standing in wrathful desire toward women of battle-blue spear-like eyes may depart - go and reach Tiruchitrakutam in Tillai, where the divine sapphire-hued one stands resplendent, where the great waters of the Kaveri (Niva) surge bearing pearls and scattering them everywhere, and carp-fish leap and frisk amid the fields.

20.translit:
seeraar pozhilsoozhn dazhakaaya thillait thiruchitra koodath thuraichengaN maalukku,
aaraadha vuLLath thavarkkEt tuvappa alainee rulakuk karuLE puriyum,
kaaraar puyarkkaik kalikanRi kunRaa olimaalai yoRonpa thOdonRum vallaar,
paaraa rulaka maLandhaa nadikkeezhp palakaalam niRkum padivaazhvar thaamE.
20.meaning: To the red-eyed Tirumal who dwells in Tiruchitrakootam at Tillai, surrounded by beautiful and fragrant groves, the Lord who out of grace gives Himself to those whose hearts are never sated of Him, and who bestows His grace upon the world girdled by the surging seas. Those who can master these ten faultless, melodious garland-verses of Kalikanri whose hand is bounteous like the dark rain-cloud, shall live for a long age standing beneath the feet of Him who measured the wide worlds.

21.translit:
vaada marudhidai pOgi mallaraik konRokka littittu,
aadalnal maavudaith thaayar aaniraik kanRidar theerppaan,
koodiya maamazhai kaaththa kooththa nenavaru kinRaan,
sEduyar poompozhil thillaich chiththira koodaththuL Laane.
21.meaning: He went between the marudha trees so that they withered, slew the wrestlers and tumbled them down, and as the dancer who removed the distress of the cowherds and their good cattle and calves, He warded off the gathering great rains. He who comes thus dwells within Chitrakootam at Tillai with its lofty flowering groves.

22.translit:
pEymakaL kongainanj cuNda piLLai parisidhu venRaal,
maanila maamakaL maadhar kELva nivanenRum, vaNduN
poomakaL naayaka nenRum pulangezhu kOviyar paadi,
thEmalar thoova varuvaan chiththira koodaththuL Laane.
22.meaning: If this be the nature of the child who sucked the poisoned breast of the demoness, then He is the consort of the lady Earth, the great Goddess of the wide land, and the lord of the lotus-born Lakshmi where bees hum. So sing the radiant cowherd women, and scattering honeyed flowers He comes, He who dwells within Chitrakootam at Tillai.

23.translit:
paNdivan veNNeyuN daanen Raaicchiyar koodi yizhippa
eNdisai yOrumva Nanga iNaimaru thoodu nadandhittu,
aNdarum vaanath thavaru maayira naamanga LOdu,
thiNdiRal paada varuvaan chiththira koodaththuL Laane.
23.meaning: While the cowherd women gathered and chided Him, saying "long ago this one ate the butter," and while those of the eight directions bowed down, He walked between the twin marudha trees. As the celestials and the dwellers of the sky sang of His firm valour with His thousand names, He comes, He who dwells within Chitrakootam at Tillai.

24.translit:
vaLaikkai nedungaN madavaa raaicchiya ranji yazhaippa,
thaLaiththavizh thaamaraip poikaith thaNthadam pukkaNdar kaaNa,
muLaiththa eyiRRazhal naagath thucchiyil ninRadhu vaada,
thilaiththamar seydhu varuvaan chiththira koodaththuL Laane.
24.meaning: As the bangled, long-eyed gentle cowherd women called out to Him in fear, He entered the cool, broad lotus-pond where blossoms unfurl, and while the celestials watched, He stood upon the crest of the fiery serpent with sprouted fangs until it wilted. Having fought that frenzied battle He comes, He who dwells within Chitrakootam at Tillai.

25.translit:
paruvak karumuki loththu muttudai maakada loththu,
aruvith thiraLthikazh kinRa vaayiram ponmalai yoththu,
uruvak karunguzha laaicchi thiRaththina maalvidai cheRRu,
theruvil thiLaiththu varuvaan chiththira koodaththuL Laane.
25.meaning: Like the dark cloud of the rainy season, like the great churning sea, and like a thousand golden mountains shining with cascading waterfalls, He destroyed the great rutting bulls for the sake of the cowherd girl with beautiful dark tresses. Sporting along the street He comes, He who dwells within Chitrakootam at Tillai.

26.translit:
eyyach chidhaindha dhilangai malanga varumazhai kaappaan,
uyyap paruvarai thaangi aaniRai kaaththaanen REththi,
vaiyath thevarum vaNanga aNangezhu maamalai pOlE,
theyvappuL LERi varuvaan chiththira koodaththuL Laane.
26.meaning: He shot His arrows so that Lanka was ruined and thrown into turmoil; to save the cattle from the coming rains He bore up the great mountain and protected the herds, and all on earth bow and praise Him. Like a great mountain charged with divine power, riding His sacred bird He comes, He who dwells within Chitrakootam at Tillai.

27.translit:
aava rivaisey dhaRivaar anjana maamalai pOlE,
mEvu sinaththadal vEzham veezha munindhu, azha kaaya
kaavi malarnedung kaNNaar kaithozha veedhi varuvaan,
thEvar vaNanguthaN thillaich chiththira koodaththuL Laane.
27.meaning: Who else could do these deeds and know how? He grew wrathful so that the fierce, raging elephant, like a great mountain of dark collyrium, fell dead. As the women with long, beautiful eyes like the blue water-lily fold their hands in worship, He comes along the street, He who dwells within cool Tillai's Chitrakootam, which the gods adore.

28.translit:
pongi yamari lorukaal ponpeya rOnai veruva,
anga vanaaka maLaindhit taayiran thOLezhun thaada,
painga NiraNderi kaanRa neeNda eyiRRodu pEzhvaai,
singa vuruvil varuvaan chiththira koodaththuL Laane.
28.meaning: Surging up in battle one time, to terrify the one named Hiranya (golden-named), He clawed apart that demon's body so that His thousand shoulders rose and danced. With two green eyes spewing fire, with long fangs and a gaping mouth, in the form of the man-lion He comes, He who dwells within Chitrakootam at Tillai.

29.translit:
karumukil pOlvadhOr mEni kaiyana vaazhiyum sangum,
peruviRal vaanavar soozha Ezhula kumthozhu dhEththa,
orumaka Laayar madandhai yoruththi nilamakaL, maRRait
thirumaka LOdum varuvaan chiththira koodaththuL Laane.
29.meaning: With a form like the dark cloud, bearing in His hands the discus and the conch, surrounded by the celestials of great might while the seven worlds bow and praise Him, He comes together with the one cowherd maiden, with the Earth-Goddess, and with the other, Lakshmi the Goddess of fortune. He dwells within Chitrakootam at Tillai.

30.translit:
thEnamar poompozhil thillaich chiththira kooda mamarndha,
vaanavar thangaL piraanai mangaiyar kOnmaru vaar,
Unamar vElkali kanRi yoNdami zhonpadhO donRum,
thaanivai kaRRuval laarmEl saaraa dheevinai thaane.
30.meaning: To the Lord of the celestials who has settled within Chitrakootam at Tillai with its honey-laden flowering groves, Kalikanri, the lord of Mangai who wields the flesh-piercing spear, sang these ten beautiful Tamil verses. Evil deeds shall never draw near those who are able to learn these well.

31.translit:
annam irai thEr azhundhoor ezhum sudarai
then thillaich chiththirakoodaththu en selvanai (66)
31.meaning: The radiant light that rises at Azhundhur, where swans seek their food; my treasured Lord within the Chitrakootam of southern Tillai. (66)

0.translit:
irundtaN maanila meenamadaay vaLai maruppinil agattodukki,
karundtaN maakadal kaNduyindravanidam kamalanan malarttERal arundti,
innisai murandrezhum aLikulam podhuLiyam pozhilUdE,
serundti naaNmalar sendraNaindt uzhitharu thiruvayindtirapuramE.
0.meaning: He who became the great Boar to lift the vast cool earth and held it tucked within his curved tusk, then lay in yoga-sleep upon the great dark cool ocean — his abode is Tiruvayindirapuram, where swarms of bees, drinking the honeyed nectar of fine lotus blossoms, hum sweet music as they cluster thick within the groves, and where they fly to settle upon the fresh-blooming flowers of the serunti tree.

1.translit:
minnu maazhiyang kaiyavan seyyavaL uRaitharu thirumaarban,
pannu naanmaRaip palaporu Laagiya paranidam varaichchaaral,
pinnu maadhavip pandalil pedaivarap piNiyavizh kamalattu,
tenna vendruvaN dinnisai muraltharu thiruvayindtirapuramE.
1.meaning: He who holds the flashing beautiful discus in his hand, on whose sacred chest the lovely Lakshmi dwells, the Supreme One who is the many meanings of the four oft-recited Vedas — his abode is Tiruvayindirapuram, on the mountain slopes where, in the bowers of intertwining maadhavi creepers, the female bees come, and the bees upon the unfolding lotuses hum their sweet music crying 'tenna, tenna.'

2.translit:
vaiya mEzhumuN daalilai vaigiya maayavan,
adiyavarkku meyya naagiya deyvanaa yaganidam meydhagu varaichchaaral,
moykoL maadhavi saNbagam muyangiya mullaiyang kodiyaada,
seyyadhaamaraich chezhumpaNai thigazhtharu thiruvayindtirapuramE.
2.meaning: The wondrous Maayan who swallowed all seven worlds and reclined upon a banyan leaf, the divine Lord who is true to his devotees — his abode is Tiruvayindirapuram, on the fitting mountain slopes where the swarming maadhavi and champak creepers entwine and the beautiful jasmine vine sways, and where rich fields gleam with red lotuses.

3.translit:
maaRu koNdudandr edhirndtaval lavuNandran maarbaga mirupiLavaa,
kooRu koNdavan kulamagaR kinnaruL koduttava nidam,
midaindtu saaRu koNdamen karumbiLang kazhaithagai visumbuRa maNineezhal,
sERu koNdadaN pazhanama dezhilthigazh thiruvayindtirapuramE.
3.meaning: He who tore in two the chest of the mighty demon Hiranya who came to oppose him in enmity, and granted his sweet grace to that demon's noble son Prahlada — his abode is Tiruvayindirapuram, where the soft young sugarcane stalks, dense and full of juice, rise sky-high casting jewel-like shade, and where the cool muddy fertile fields shine with beauty.

4.translit:
aangu maavali vELviyil irandtusen ragalida maLandtu
aayar poongo dikkina vidaiporu thavanidam ponmalar thigazh
vEngai kongu seNbagak kombinil kudhikodu kurakkinam iraittOdi
thEnka landaN palanganni nugarttharu thiruvayindtirapuramE.
4.meaning: He who went and begged at Mahabali's sacrifice and measured the wide earth, and who fought the bulls for the sake of the flower-like cowherd maiden — his abode is Tiruvayindirapuram, where troops of monkeys leap upon the branches of the vEngai, kongu and champak trees laden with golden blossoms and, chattering, run to feast on the cool honey-mixed sweet jackfruit.

5.translit:
koonu laaviya madandtaitan kodunjsolin thiRattiLang kodiyOdum,
kaanu laaviya karumugil thiruniRat thavanidam kavinaarum,
vaanu laaviya madhithavazh maalvarai maamadhiL pudaisoozha,
thEnu laaviya chezhumpozhil thazhuviya thiruvayindtirapuramE.
5.meaning: He of the sacred hue of a dark rain-cloud who, by reason of the cruel words of the hunchbacked woman Manthara, wandered the forest together with the tender vine-like lady Sita — his abode is Tiruvayindirapuram, encircled all around by great walls tall as mountains over which the wandering moon glides through the sky, and embraced by rich groves where honey overflows in beauty.

6.translit:
minnin nuNNidai madakkodi kaaraNam vilangalin misaiyilangai mannan,
neeNmudi podiseydha maindtana tidammaNi varaineezhal,
anna maamala raravindtat thamaLiyil pedaiyodu minidhamara,
senne laarkkava rikkulai veesudaN thiruvayindtirapuramE.
6.meaning: The young hero who, for the sake of the modest vine-waisted lady with a waist slender as lightning, ground to dust the long crowns of Lanka's king upon the island beyond the mountain — his abode is Tiruvayindirapuram, in the shade of jewelled hills, where swans dwell sweetly with their mates upon couches of great lotus blossoms, and where the cool red paddy spreads, its clusters waving like fly-whisks.

7.translit:
viraika mazhndtamen karunguzhal kaaraNam villiRuttu adalmazhaikku,
niraika langida varaikudai yeduttavan nilaviya idamdadamaar,
varaiva Landtigazh madhagari maruppodu malaivaLa ragilundti,
thiraiko Narndtanai chezhunadhi vayalpugu thiruvayindtirapuramE.
7.meaning: He who, for the sake of the lady with soft dark fragrant tresses, broke the bow, and who lifted up the mountain as an umbrella when the herds of cattle were thrown into distress by the fierce rain — the place where he dwells is Tiruvayindirapuram, where the rich brimming river, its waves bearing along the tusks of mighty rutting mountain-elephants and the mountain-grown agil-wood, carries them down into the broad fields.

8.translit:
vElkoL kaitthalat tharasarvem pOrinil visayanuk kaay,
maNitthErk kOlkoL kaitthalat tendtaipem maanidam kuluvudaN varaichchaaral,
kaalkoL kaNkodik kaiyezhak kamugiLam paaLaigaL kamazhsaaral,
sElkaL paaytharu chezhunadhi vayalpugu thiruvayindtirapuramE.
8.meaning: My Father, my Lord, who took the reins in his hand upon the jewelled chariot for Arjuna's sake in the fierce war against the spear-wielding kings — his abode is Tiruvayindirapuram, on the lovely cool mountain slopes, where the slopes are fragrant with the young spathes of the areca palms rising on their tall trunks, and where the rich river, its sEl fish leaping, flows into the fields.

9.translit:
moova raagiya voruvanai moovula guNdumizhndt aLandtaanai,
dhEvar thaanavar sendrusendr iRainjsattaN thiruvayindtirapurattu,
mEvu sOdhiyai vElvala vankali kandri viritthuraittha,
paavu thaNdamizh pattivai paadidap paavangaL payilaavE.
9.meaning: The One who is the Three (Brahma, Vishnu, Siva), who swallowed, spat forth and measured the three worlds, the radiant Light who abides in cool Tiruvayindirapuram where gods and demons come again and again to worship — upon this Light, Kaliyan, mighty with the spear, expounded these ten flowing cool Tamil verses; for those who sing them, sins will not draw near.

0.translit:
anRaayark kulakkodi yodaNi maa malarmangaiyo danpaLavi,
avuNarkkenRaanu mirakkami laadavanukkuk kuRaiyumida maavadu,
irumpozhilsoozh nanRaayapu nalnaRai yoorttiruvaa likudandai tadandigazh koovalnagar,
ninRaanirun daankidan daannadandaaR kidammaamalai yaavadu neermalaiye.
0.meaning: He who once joined in love with the cowherd clan's creeper-like maiden and with the great Lakshmi of the beautiful flower, yet who showed no mercy whatsoever to the demons — is there any place that could be a refuge for Him? In Nanmai-rich Naraiyur surrounded by dark groves, in Tiruvali, in Kudandai (Kumbakonam), in the broad and shining Kovalnagar, He stands, sits, reclines, and walks. The fitting place for Him, the great hill, is Neermalai.

1.translit:
manjaadu varaiyezhum kadalga Lezhum vaanagamum maNNagamum maRRu mellaam,
enjaamal vayiRRadakki yaalin meelor iLandaLiril kaNvaLarnda eesan Rannai,
tunjaaneer vaLanjurakkum peNNait tenpaal tooyanaan maRaiyaaLar somucceyya,
senjaali viLaivayaluL tigazhndu toonRum tirukkova loorataNuL kaNdeeN naane.
