1.meaning: The Lord who, without remainder, drew into His stomach the seven cloud-wreathed mountains, the seven seas, the heavens, the earth and all else, and then lay sleeping with His eyes resting upon a tender shoot of a banyan leaf — Him I myself saw in Tirukkovalur, which shines forth amid fields where red paddy ripens, on the southern bank of the Pennai whose unceasing waters yield abundance, where the pure Brahmins of the four Vedas perform the Soma sacrifice.

2.translit:
kondalarnda naRunduzhaay saandam toobam deepamkoN damarart tozhap paNangoLpaambil,
sandaNimen mulaimalaraaL daraNi mangai taamiruva radivarudum tanmai yaanai,
vandanaiseydu isaiyezhaa Rangam aindu vaLarveeLvi naanmaRaigaL moonRu teeyum,
sindanaisey dirupozhudu monRum selvat tirukkova loorataNuL kaNdeeNnaane.
2.meaning: He who reclines upon the hooded serpent, whom the immortals worship bringing fragrant blossoming tulasi clusters, sandal paste, incense and lamps — He whose two feet are gently pressed by the flower-dwelling Lakshmi of soft fragrant breasts and by the Earth-goddess. Worshipping Him with the seven musical notes, the six Vedangas, the five great sacrifices, the four Vedas and the three sacred fires, contemplating Him at both twilights and uniting with Him — Him I myself saw in wealthy Tirukkovalur.

3.translit:
kozhundaluru malarccholaik kuzhaangoL poigaik koolmudalai vaaLeyiRRuk koNdaRkeLgi,
azhundiyamaa kaLiRRinukkan Raazhi yeendi andarame varattoonRi yaruLsey daanai,
ezhundamalark karuneela mirundil kaadda irumpunnai muttarumbic cempoNkaadda,
sezhundadaneerk kamalamtee vigaipool kaaddum tirukkova loorataNuL kaNdeeN naane.
3.meaning: Long ago, when the great elephant Gajendra sank in the lotus-pool of the flowering grove, trapped by a powerful crocodile with sword-sharp teeth, the Lord, distressed for him, appeared in the sky bearing His discus and granted grace. Him I myself saw in Tirukkovalur, where blooming dark blue lilies make darkness, where great punnai trees bud forth pearls and show reddish gold, and where lotuses in the rich deep waters shine like flaming torches.

4.translit:
taangarumpoor maalipadap paRavai yoorndu taraadalattor kuRaimuditta tanmaiyaanai,
aangarumbik kaNNeersoorn danbu koorum adiyavarkad kaaramuda maanaaN Rannai,
koongarumbu surapunnai kuravaar solaik kuzhaavarivaN diSaipaadum paadal keeddu,
teengarumbu kaNvaLarum kazhani soozhnda tirukkova loorataNuL kaNdeeN naane.
4.meaning: He who, riding the Garuda bird, slew the demon Mali in unbearable battle and fulfilled the wants of those on earth — He who became the rare nectar to His devotees whose love swells, whose hearts overflow and whose tears stream forth in devotion. Him I myself saw in Tirukkovalur, surrounded by fields where sweet sugarcane sleeps, listening to the songs that swarms of striped bees sing in groves of kongu blossoms, fragrant punnai, and kura flowers.

5.translit:
kaRaivaLarveel karanmudalaak kavandan vaali kaNaiyonRi naalmadiya ilangaidannuL,
piRaiyeyiRRu vaaLarakkar seenai yellaam perundagaiyo dudanduNitta pemmaanRannai,
maRaivaLarap pugazhvaLara maadan dooRum maNdapamoN doLiyanaittum vaaramoda,
siRaiyaNainda pozhilaNainda tenRal veesum tirukkova loorataNuL kaNdeeN naane.
5.meaning: The Lord who with a single arrow felled Khara of the blood-dripping spear, Kabandha, and Vali, and who in Lanka destroyed all the army of the crescent-tusked sword-bearing demons together with their great chief (Ravana) — Him I myself saw in Tirukkovalur, where the Vedas flourish and His glory grows, where in every mansion and bright pavilion the Vedas are chanted melodiously, and where the southern breeze wafts in, embracing the groves bounded all around.

6.translit:
uRiyaarnda naRuveNNe yoLiyaal senRang guNdaanaik kaNdaaycci yuralo daarkka,
taRiyaarnda karungaLiRe pola ninRu tadangaNgaL panimalgum tanmai yaanai,
veRiyaarnda malarmagaLnaa mangai yodu, viyankalaiyeN tooLinaaL viLangu,
selvac ceRiyaarnda maNimaadam tigazhndu toonRum tirukkova loorataNuL kaNdeeN naane.
6.meaning: He who slipped in and ate the fragrant white butter hanging in the slings — He whom the cowherd-woman caught and bound to the mortar, so that, standing like a dark elephant tethered to a post, His wide eyes brimmed with tears. Him I myself saw in Tirukkovalur — abiding with the fragrant flower-maiden Lakshmi and with Saraswati of the vast learning and eight shoulders — that town which shines forth with wealthy, close-set jeweled mansions.

7.translit:
irungaimmaa karimunindu pariyaik keeRi inavidaiga Lezhadarttu marudam saayttu,
varumsagada miRavudaittu mallai yaddu vanjaganjey kanjanukku nanjaa naanai,
karungamugu pasumpaaLai veNmut teenRu kaayellaam maragadamaayp pavaLam kaadda,
serundimiga moddalarttum teenkoL solait tirukkova loorataNuL kaNdeeN naane.
7.meaning: He who in anger slew the great long-trunked elephant (Kuvalayapida), tore apart the horse-demon (Kesi), subdued the seven bulls, felled the marudam trees, kicked apart the oncoming cart (Sakatasura), destroyed the wrestlers, and became poison to the treacherous Kamsa — Him I myself saw in Tirukkovalur, where the dark areca palms yield white pearls from their green spathes, the unripe fruits all show as emeralds and corals, and the serundi trees abundantly open their buds in honey-laden groves.

8.translit:
paareeRu perumpaaran teerap paNdu paaradattut toodiyangi,
paarttan selvat teereeRu saaradiyaa yedirndaar seenai serukkaLattut tiRalazhicc cetRaaNRannai,
pooreeRon Rudaiyaanu maLagaik koonum purandaranum naanmuganum porundu moorppool,
seereeRu maRaiyaaLar niRainda selvat tirukkova loorataNuL kaNdeeN naane.
8.meaning: He who long ago, to relieve the great burden weighing upon the earth, went as messenger in the Bharata war, and mounting Arjuna's rich chariot as charioteer, destroyed in the field of battle the strength of the opposing armies — Him I myself saw in wealthy Tirukkovalur, filled with glorious Vedic Brahmins, a town like the dwelling-place where the bull-bannered Siva, the lord of Alaka (Kubera), Indra (Purandara), and the four-faced Brahma abide together.

9.translit:
toovadivin paarmagaLpoo mangai yodu sudaraazhi sangirupaal polindu toonRa,
kaavadivin kaRpagame pola ninRu kalandavarkad karuLpuriyung karutti naanai,
seevadigai tiruvaaykaN sivanda vaadai sempoNsey tiruvuruva maanaaN Rannai,
teevadivin sivanayaNe poolvaar mannu tirukkova loorataNuL kaNdeeN naane.
9.meaning: He who appears with the pure-formed Earth-goddess and the flower-dwelling Lakshmi, the radiant discus and conch gleaming on either side — He who stands like the wish-granting Kalpaka tree of the grove, His mind ever bent on granting grace to those who unite with Him. He whose holy feet, hands, sacred mouth and eyes are reddened, whose form is fashioned of pure red gold — Him I myself saw in Tirukkovalur, where dwell men resembling fire-formed Siva and Brahma.

10.translit:
vaaraNango Lidarkkadinda maalai neela maradagattai mazhaimugile poolvaaNRannai,
seeraNangu maRaiyaaLar niRainda selvat tirukkova loorataNuL kaNdeeN, enRu
vaaraNangu mulaimadavaar mangai veendan vaadkaliya noliyaindu maindum vallaar,
kaaraNanga laalugang kalandang keettak karandengum parandaanaik kaaNbar taame.
10.meaning: "Tirumal who removed the elephant's (Gajendra's) distress, dark as blue sapphire, resembling the rain-cloud — Him I saw in wealthy Tirukkovalur filled with glorious Vedic Brahmins" — thus sang Kaliyan of the victorious sword, lord of the maidens with bodice-clad breasts and king of the land. Those who can master these ten verses of his will themselves behold the Lord who, pervading and yet hidden everywhere, mingles with the world so that all may praise Him without cause.

11.translit:
peeymagaL kongainanj cuNda piLLai parisidu venRaal,
maanila maamagaL maadar keeLva nivanenRum, vaNduN
poomagaL naayaga nenRum pulangezhu kooviyar paadi,
teemalar toova varuvaan sittira kooddattuL Laane.
11.meaning: "If this is the nature of the child who sucked the poison from the breast of the demoness — He is the lord of the great Earth-goddess, and the lord of the flower-dwelling Lakshmi where bees feed" — thus singing, the discerning cowherd-women scatter honeyed blossoms as He comes. He is the One who dwells in Chitrakuta (Tiruchitrakutam).

12.translit:
peeymu laittalai nanjuNda piLLaiyat
teLLi yaarvaNang gappadun deevanai,
maaya namadiL koovali daikazhi
mainda nayanRi yandaNar sindaiyuL eesanai,
ilang gumsudarc sotiyai endai
yaiyenak keyppinil vaippinai
kaasi naimaNi yaiccenRu naadik
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNdukoN deene.
12.meaning: The child who drank the poison from the breast of the demoness, the God worshipped by the clear-minded, the wondrous Lord, the youth of walled Kovalur and Idaikazhi, the Lord within the hearts of the Brahmins, the shining radiant flame, my Father, the treasure laid up for me in my time of helplessness, the precious gold and gem — Him I sought out and found in Kannamangai.

13.translit:
alampurinda nedundadakkai amarar veendan
anjiRaippuL tanippaagan avuNark kenRum,
salampurindang garuLillaat tanmai yaaLan
taanuganda voorellaam tandaaL paadi,
nilamparandu varumkaluzhip peNNai yeertta
neduveeygaL padumutta munda vundi,
pulamparanda ponviLaikkum poigai veelip
poongova loorttozhudum poodu nenje.
13.meaning: The king of the immortals with the long broad arms that churn (the ocean), the matchless rider of the beautiful-winged bird (Garuda), who is ever without grace toward the demons in His enmity — let us sing all the towns He cherished, His feet, and go. O heart, let us worship and depart to lovely Kovalur, hedged by ponds that yield gold in the spreading fields, where the wide-flowing Pennai river, swelling across the land, drags down tall bamboos and pushes forth the pearls they bear.

14.translit:
vaRpudaiya varainedundooL mannar maaLa
vadivaaya mazhuvendi yulaga maaNdu,
veRpudaiya nedungadaluL tanive luytta
veeLmudalaa venRaanoor vindam meeya,
kaRpudaiya madakkanni kaaval pooNda
kadipozhilsoozh nedumaRugil kamala veeli,
poRpudaiya malaiyaraiyan paNiya ninRa
poongova loorttozhudum poodu nenje.
14.meaning: He who, bearing the well-shaped axe, slew the mighty kings of strong mountain-like long shoulders, ruled the world, and conquered foes such as the demon who hurled his lone spear into the great mountain-girded sea (Parashurama / the destroyer) — His town. O heart, let us worship and depart to lovely Kovalur, set upon Vindam, guarded by the chaste tender maiden, ringed by fragrant groves and long streets and hedged with lotuses, where the gracious mountain-king bows in homage.

15.translit:
pongaarmel liLangongai ponne pooppap
porukayalkaN neerarumbap poondu ninRu
sengaala madappuRavam pedaikkup peesum
siRukuraluk kudaluvugic cindittu, aange
taNkaalum taNkudandai nagarum paadit
taNkova loorppaadi yaadak keettu,
nangaay nang gudikkiduvo nanmai? enna
naRaiyoorum paaduvaaL navilgin Raale.
15.meaning: With her swelling soft young breasts turning pale-golden, her battling carp-like eyes brimming with tears, the lovesick maiden came and stood; her heart melting at the soft cooing of the red-legged tender dove speaking to its mate, she brooded and there sang of Tankal, of cool Kudandai town, and danced singing of cool Kovalur. When (her mother) asked, hearing this, "O daughter, is this fitting for our family's honor?" — she only goes on, singing now of Naraiyur as well.

16.translit:
aaraanum kaRpippaar naayagare? naan avanaik
kaar aar tirumeeni kaaNum aLavum poy
seer aar tiruveengadame tirukkova
loore madiL kacci ooragame peeragame
peeraa marudu iRuttaan veLLaRaiye vehkaave
peer aali taNkaal naRaiyoor tiruppuliyoor
aaraamam soozhnda arangam kaNamangai
16.meaning: Are there any masters who could teach me (otherwise)? I will go until I behold His dark cloud-like sacred form — to glorious Tiruvenkatam, to Tirukkovalur, to walled Kacchi (Kanchi), to Uragam, to Peragam, to Peeraa (where He broke the marudam trees), to Vellarai, to Vehka, to great Ali, to Tankal, to Naraiyur, to Tiruppuliyur, to Arangam (Srirangam) surrounded by pleasure-groves, and to Kanamangai.

17.translit:
senni maNic cudarai taNkaal tiRal valiyai
tannaip piRar aRiyaat tattuvattai muttinai
annattai meenai ariyai aru maRaiyai
mun iv ulagu uNda moorttiyai koovaloor
17.meaning: The jewel-radiance of my head, the strong might of Tankal, the Reality whom no others can know, the pearl, the swan, the fish, the lion (Narasimha), the rare Vedas, the Lord who once swallowed this whole world — He of Kovalur (I will reach).

18.translit:
eezhai eedalan keezhmagan ennaa dirangi maRRavaR kinnaruL surandu
maazhai maanmada nokkiyun toozhi, umbi yembi yenR ozhindilai, ugandu
toozha neeyenak kingozhi enRa soRkaL vandadi yeenmanat tirundida,
aazhi vaNNanin nadiyiNai yadaindeen aNipo zhiltiru varangattam maane.
18.meaning: Without saying "he is poor, an outsider, a low-born man," You took pity and poured out Your sweet grace upon him (Guha); You did not hold back, but gladly called him "your friend, my younger brother, my brother," saying "You are my companion — stay here with me." Those words came and abode in this servant's heart, and so, O ocean-hued Lord of beautiful-groved Srirangam, I have reached Your twin feet.

19.translit:
vaata maamagan markkadam vilangu maRRor saadiyen Rozhindilai, ugandu
kaadal aadaram kadalinum perugac ceyda tagavinuk killaikaim maaRenRu
kodil vaaymaiyi naayodu mudane uNban naan enRa oNporuL enakkum
aadal veeNdumen Radiyinai yadaindeen aNipo zhiltiru varangattam maane.
19.meaning: Without saying "they are monkeys, sons of the wind-god, beasts, of another race," You gladly made their love and devotion swell greater than the sea; for that grace there is no recompense, You declared, and in flawless truth You said "I will eat together with you" (to Sugriva and the monkeys). That precious blessing — I too long for it; and so, O ocean-hued Lord of beautiful-groved Srirangam, I have reached Your twin feet.

20.translit:
kadikoL poompozhil kaamaru poygai vaiku taamarai vaangiya vEzham,
mudiyum vaNNamOr muzhuvali mudalai paRRa maRRadu nin charaN ninaippa
kodiya vaayvilang kinnuyirmalangak koNda seeRRamon RuNduLa daRindu, un
adiyanEnum van dadiyiNai yadaindEn aNipo zhiltiru varangattam maanE,
20.meaning: When the elephant that came to pluck a lotus in the lovely fragrant flower-filled pond was seized by a mighty crocodile and was about to perish, it thought of Your feet as its refuge. Knowing that You have a fury that makes the life of that cruel-mouthed beast writhe and that destroys it, I too, even though I am only Your servant, have come and reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful groves at Srirangam.

21.translit:
nanju sOrvadOr venjina aravam veruvi vandunin charaNenach charaNaa
nenjiR koNdunin nanjiRaip paRavaik kadaikka lamkoduttaruL seydadaRindu
venjo laaLargaL namanRamar kadiyar kodiya seyvana vuLa adaRkadiyEn
anji vandunin nadiyiNai yadaindEn aNipo zhiltiru varangattam maanE,
21.meaning: When a venom-dripping, fiercely-angry serpent, terrified, came and took refuge saying You are its shelter, holding You in its heart, You graciously granted protection to it through Your beautiful-winged bird (Garuda). Knowing this, and because Yama's harsh-spoken servants are cruel and do dreadful deeds, I, Your servant, have come in fear and reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful groves at Srirangam.

22.translit:
maaka maanilam muzhuvadumvan diraignjum malara diga Nda maamaRai yaaLan,
tOgai maamayi lannava rinbam tuRRi laamai yilattaving kozhindu
pOgam neeyeydip pinnumnam midaikkE pOdu vaay, enRa ponnaruL, enakkum
aaga vENdumen RadiyiNai yadaindEn aNipo zhiltiru varangattam maanE,
22.meaning: To the great seer of the sacred Vedas, whose blossom-like feet are worshipped by all who come from the great wide sky and earth, You showed grace, telling him to forsake worldly pleasures here, attain enjoyment hereafter, and then come once more to our side. Desiring that such golden grace be granted to me too, I have come and reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful groves at Srirangam.

23.translit:
mannu naanmaRai maamuni peRRa maintha naimati yaadaveng kooRRan
tannai yanjinin charaNenach charaNaayt tagavil kaalanai yugamunin dozhiyaa
pinnai yenRumnin tiruvadi piriyaa vaNNa meNNiya pEraruL, enakkum
anna daagumen RadiyiNai yadaindEn aNipo zhiltiru varangattam maanE,
23.meaning: When the son begotten by the great sage of the eternal four Vedas, fearing fierce Death who disregarded him, took You as his refuge, You grew wrathful at the merciless Yama and did not desist, and then granted the great grace whereby he would never thereafter be parted from Your sacred feet. Trusting that such grace will be mine too, I have come and reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful groves at Srirangam.

24.translit:
Odu vaaymaiyum uvaniyap piRappum unakku muntanda andaNa noruvan,
kaada lenmagan pugalidang kaaNEn, kaNdu neetaru vaayenak kenRu,
kOdil vaaymaiyi naanunai vENdiya kuRaimu dittavan siRuvanaik koduttaay,
aada laalvandun adiyiNai yadaindEn aNipo zhiltiru varangattam maanE,
24.meaning: When a certain Brahmin, master of the chanted truth and of the science of birth, who had earlier offered these to You, said "I cannot find a refuge for my beloved son; find him and give him to me," You fulfilled the want of that flawless, truthful one who sought You, and gave him back his child. Therefore I have come and reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful groves at Srirangam.

25.translit:
vEda vaaymozhi yandaNa noruvan endai ninchara Nennudai manaivi,
kaadal makkaLaip payattalum kaaNaaL kadiyadOr deyvangoN doLikkum, enRazhaippa
Eda laarmunnE yinnaru Lavarkkuch seydun makkaLmaR Rivarendu koduttaay,
aada laalvandun adiyiNai yadaindEn aNipo zhiltiru varangattam maanE,
25.meaning: When a certain Brahmin of the Vedic word cried out, "My Father, You are my refuge; as soon as my wife bears our beloved children, she does not see them, for some terrible deity carries them off and hides them," You showed this sweet grace before the strangers, saying "Here, these are your children," and gave them back. Therefore I have come and reached Your twin feet, O Lord of beautiful groves at Srirangam.

26.translit:
thuLangu neeNmudi arasartang kurisil toNdai mannavan tiNdiRa loruvaRku
uLango LanbinO dinnaruL surandang kOdu naazhigai yEzhuda niruppa,
vaLangoL mantiram maRRavaR karuLich seyda vaaRadi yEnaRindu, ulagam
aLanda ponnadi yEyadain duyndEn aNipo zhiltiru varangattam maanE.
26.meaning: To the chief of the kings of glittering tall crowns, the firm-valoured Tondaiman king, You poured forth sweet grace with heartfelt love, abiding there with him together for seven naazhikai, and graciously imparted to him the fruitful sacred mantra. Knowing the manner of this, I have been redeemed by reaching the golden feet that measured the worlds, O Lord of beautiful groves at Srirangam.

27.translit:
maadamaaLigai soozhtiru mangai mannan onnalar tangaLai vellum,
aadal maaval vankali kanRi aNipo zhiltiru varangattam maanai,
needu tolpuga zhaazhival laanai endai yainedu maalani naintha,
paadal pattivai paadumin toNdeer paada nummidaip paavamnil laavE.
27.meaning: The king of sacred Tirumangai girt by mansions and palaces, the one of battle-steeds who conquers his foes, the strong Kalikanri — these ten verses he composed contemplating the Lord of beautiful groves at Srirangam, my Father Nedumal of enduring ancient fame and mighty discus. O devotees, sing these ten songs of praise; as you sing, no sin will remain upon you.

28.translit:
vEngadamum viNNagarum vehkaavum, ahkaada
poongidangil neeLkOval ponnagarum, - naankidattum
ninRaa nirundaan kidandaan nadandaanE,
enRaal kedumaam idar.
28.meaning: At Venkatam, at Vinnagar, at Vehka, and at the golden city of long Tirukkoval with its unfading flower-filled moats — in these four places He stood, He sat, He reclined, and He walked. If one says this, all afflictions are destroyed.

29.translit:
neeyum tirumagaLum ninRaayaal, kunReduttup
paayum panimaRaitta paNbaaLaa, - vaayil
kadaikazhiyaa vuLpugaak kaamarpoong kOval
idaikazhiyE paRRi yini .
29.meaning: You and Lakshmi stand together, O gracious One who lifted the mountain (Govardhana) and held it up to ward off the pouring cold rain. Henceforth I take hold of the courtyard threshold of lovely flowering Tirukkoval, neither passing out by the doorway gate nor entering within.

30.translit:
tamaruLLam tanjai talaiyarangam taNkaal,
tamaruLLum taNporuppu vElai, - tamaruLLum
maamallai kOval madiTkudandai yenbarE,
Evalla endaik kidam.
30.meaning: His devotees' hearts, Tanjai, the foremost Srirangam, Tankal, the cool hill (Tirumalai) and the sea cherished by His devotees, and also Mamallai, Koval, and walled Kudandai that His devotees hold dear — these, they say, are the abodes of my Father who is able to command all.

0.translit:
kallaar madiL soozh kacchi nagaruL nacchip paadagatthuL,
ellaa vulagum vaNanga virundha ammaan, ilangaikkOn
vallaaL aagam villaal munindha endhai, vibheeDaNaRku
nallaan udaiya naamam sollil namO naaraayaNamE!
0.meaning: The Lord who, longed for, took His seat in Pādagam within the city of Kanchi girt with walls of stone, that all the worlds might bow to Him. He is my Father who in anger pierced with His bow the strong body of Lanka's king, and who is good to Vibhishana. If one would utter His name, it is 'namō Nārāyaṇa'!

1.translit:
piNdiyaar maNdai Endhip piRar manai tiridhandh uNNum,
uNdiyaan saabam teerttha oruvanoor, ulaga mEttum
kaNdiyoor arangam meyyam kacchipEr mallai enRu
maNdinaar, uyyal allaal maRRaiyaark kuyya laamE?
1.meaning: He is the One who removed the curse of Shiva, who bearing a skull begged at others' houses to eat. His towns, praised by all the world, are Kandiyur, Arangam (Srirangam), Meyyam, Kachi (Kanchi), Per, and Mallai. Only those who throng eagerly to these places find salvation; can it be possible for others to be saved?

2.translit:
vangatthaal maamaNi vandh undhu munneer
 mallaiyaay madiL kacchi yooraay! pEraay,
kongatthaar vaLam konRai yalangal maarvan
 kulavaraiyan madappaavai idappaal koNdaan,
pangatthaay paaRkadalaay paarin mElaay
 panivaraiyin ucchiyaay pavaLa vaNNaa,
enguRRaay emperumaan unnai naadi
 EzhaiyEn inganamE oozhi tarugEnE.
2.meaning: O Lord of Mallai whose triple-streamed sea brings forth great gems borne by ships! O Lord of walled Kanchi, O Lord of Per! He on whose left side rests the tender maiden, daughter of the great mountain (Parvati)—being part of the chest of Shiva who wears a fragrant garland of konrai blossoms thick with pollen. O Lord of the Milk-Ocean, O Lord above the earth, O One atop the snowy peak, O coral-hued One! Where are You, my Lord? Seeking You, wretched as I am, I am spending an age like this.

3.translit:
kalluyarndha nedumadiL soozh kacchi mEya
 kaLiRu enRum kadal kidandha kaniyE enRum,
alliyam poo malarp poygaip pazhana vEli
 aNi azhundhoor ninRu ugandha ammaan enRum,
solluyarndha neduveeNai mulaimEl thaangith
 thoo muRuval nagai iRaiyE thOnRa nakku,
melviralgaL sivapp eydhath thadavi yaangE
 menkiLi pOl miga mizhaRRum enbEdhaiyE.
3.meaning: My innocent girl, holding upon her bosom the great famed veena, smiling so that a flash of her pure-white teeth appears, stroking it till her soft fingers turn red—chatters on like a tender parrot, saying: 'the elephant-Lord who dwells in Kanchi girt with tall walls of stone,' and 'the ripe fruit reclining on the ocean,' and 'the Lord who stood rejoicing at beautiful Azhundur, hedged with fields and ponds full of fine lily-blossoms.'

4.translit:
en nenja mEyaan en senniyaan, taanavanai
van nenjang keeNda maNivaNNan, - munnam sEy
oozhiyaan oozhi peyarttaan, ulagEttum
aazhiyaan attiyoo raan.
4.meaning: He who dwells in my heart, who is upon my head, the sapphire-hued One who tore open the hard breast of the demon (Hiranya). He is the One of the long-ago aeons, who turned the cycle of aeons; the Lord of the discus whom the world praises, the Lord of Attiyur (Tiruvattiyur, Kanchi).

5.translit:
attiyoo raan puLLai yoorvaan, aNimaNiyin
tutthi sEr naagatthin mEl tuyilvaan, - mootthee
maRaiyaavaan maakadal nanj uNdaan tanakkum
iRaiyaavaan engaL piraan.
5.meaning: The Lord of Attiyur, who rides upon the bird (Garuda), who slumbers upon the serpent (Adishesha) adorned with beautiful jeweled spots. He is the ancient fire and the Vedas; He is the Master even of Shiva who drank the poison of the great ocean—He, our Benefactor.

6.translit:
siRandha ven sindhaiyum sengaN aravum,
niRaindha seer neeL kacchi yuLLum, - uRaindhadhuvum,
vEngadamum veqkaavum vELukkaip paadiyumE,
taam kadavaar taN tuzhaayaar.
6.meaning: The Lord adorned with cool tulasi never departs from my excellent heart, nor from the red-eyed serpent (Adishesha), nor from vast Kanchi full of glory; and the places where He abides are Venkatam, Vehka, and Velukkai-padi.

0.translit:
tiripura muunReri ttaanummaTRai malarmisai meelaya numviyappa,
muritirai maakadal poolmuzhangki muuvula kummuRai yaalvaNangka,
eriyana keesara vaaLeyiTROo diraNiya naaka miraNdukuuRaa,
ariyuru vaamiva raarkkolenna atta buyakarat teenenRaaree.
0.meaning: Even He who burnt the three cities (Shiva) and Brahma seated upon the lotus stood amazed, as the great rolling-waved ocean roared and the three worlds bowed in due order; with fiery mane and sword-like fangs He split Hiranya's body in two, taking the form of the Man-Lion. 'Who can these be?' they wondered — and He answered, 'I am He of Ashtabhuja-karam.'

1.translit:
ventiRal viiraril viiraroppaar veeda muraittimai yoorvaNangkum,
sentamizh paaduvaar taamvaNangkum teeva rivarkol terikkamaatteen,
vandu kuRaLaru vaaynimirndu maavali veeLviyil maNNaLanda,
andaNar poonRiva raarkkolenna atta buyakarat teenenRaaree.
1.meaning: He resembles the very champion among fierce mighty heroes; He whom the immortals worship reciting the Vedas, and whom those who sing pure Tamil also bow to — are these that very God? I cannot tell. He who came as a dwarf, then grew tall and measured the earth at Mahabali's sacrifice — 'Who can this be, resembling that brahmin?' they wondered, and He answered, 'I am He of Ashtabhuja-karam.'

2.translit:
sempo nilangku valangkaivaaLi tiNsilai taNdodu sangkamoLvaaL,
umpa rirusuda raazhiyoodu keedaka moNmalar paTRiyeTRee,
vembu sinattadal veezhamviizha veNmarup ponRu paRittu,
iruNda ambudam poonRiva raarkkolenna atta buyakarat teenenRaaree.
2.meaning: Holding in his eight arms a red-gold-gleaming arrow in the right hand, a strong bow, a club, a conch, a bright sword, the disc bright as the twin luminaries above, a shield, and a fair lotus — how wondrous! He who, when the burning-wrathed mighty elephant fell, plucked off one of its white tusks, resembles a dark rain-cloud. 'Who can this be?' they wondered, and He answered, 'I am He of Ashtabhuja-karam.'

3.translit:
manjuyar maamaNik kunRameendi maamazhai kaattoru maayavaanai yanja,
adanmarup ponRuvaangkum aayarkol maaya maRiyamaatteen,
venjuda raazhiyum sangkumeendi veedamu nooduvar niitivaanattu,
anjudar poonRiva raarkkolenna atta buyakarat teenenRaaree.
3.meaning: Lifting the great gem-mountain that towers into the clouds, He sheltered the people from the great rains; is He that cowherd who, frightening a great wondrous elephant, plucked away one of its tusks? I cannot fathom the mystery. Bearing the fierce-flaming disc and the conch, He of old who chanted the Vedas resembles the bright sun in the heaven of righteousness. 'Who can this be?' they wondered, and He answered, 'I am He of Ashtabhuja-karam.'

4.translit:
kalaikaLum veedamum niitinuulum kaRpamum soRporuL taanum,
maTRai nilaikaLum vaanavarkkum piRarkkum niirmaiyi naalaruL seydu,
niiNda malaikaLum maamaNi yummalarmeel mangkaiyum sangkamum tangkukinRa,
alaikadal poonRiva raarkkolenna atta buyakarat teenenRaaree.
4.meaning: By his grace he bestowed, out of his nature, the arts, the Vedas, the books of righteousness, the kalpa (ritual lore), the meaning of words, and all the other states of being upon the celestials and upon others. He resembles the wave-tossed ocean wherein dwell the long mountains, great gems, the Lady seated on the lotus (Lakshmi), and the conch. 'Who can this be?' they wondered, and He answered, 'I am He of Ashtabhuja-karam.'

5.translit:
engnganum naamivar vaNNameNNil eedu maRikilam, eendizhaiyaar
sangkum manamum niRaivumellaam tammana vaakap pukundu,
taamum pongku karungkadal puuvaikaayaap pootavizh niilam punaindameekam,
angnganam poonRiva raarkkolenna atta buyakarat teenenRaaree.
5.meaning: However much we ponder his hue, we can understand nothing at all; he entered and took into himself, as his own, the bangles, the hearts and the fullness of the well-ornamented women. He himself resembles the surging dark ocean, the puvai flower, the kaaya blossom, the opened blue lily, and the cloud adorned with such hues. 'Who can this be?' they wondered, and He answered, 'I am He of Ashtabhuja-karam.'

6.translit:
muzhusivaN daadiya taNduzhaayin moymmalark kaNNiyum,
meeniyanjaan tizhisiya koola mirundavaaRum engngananj sollukeen. oovinallaar,
ezhutiya taamarai yannakaNNum eendezhi laakamum tooLumvaayum,
azhakiya taamiva raarkkolenna atta buyakarat teenenRaaree.
6.meaning: The garland of dense tulasi blossoms where bees plunge and hum, and the beauty of his form anointed with fragrant sandal-paste — how shall I describe how it was arranged? His eyes like lotuses drawn by skilled artists, his lifted beautiful chest, his shoulders and his mouth are lovely. 'Who can this be?' they wondered, and He answered, 'I am He of Ashtabhuja-karam.'

7.translit:
meeviyep paalumviN Noorvanangka veeda muraipparmun niirmadandai teevi,
ap paalatir sangkamippaal sakkaram maTRivar vaNNameNNil,
kaaviyop paarkada leeyumoppaar kaNNum vadivum nediyaraay,
en aaviyop paariva raarkkolenna atta buyakarat teenenRaaree.
7.meaning: Pervading everywhere, while the celestials on every side bow down, they recite the Vedas; the Lady of the ocean (Lakshmi) is his consort, the roaring conch on that side, the disc on this side — and if one reckons his hue, he resembles the kaava flower and the ocean too. With his eyes and form, being lofty, he is like my very life-breath. 'Who can this be?' they wondered, and He answered, 'I am He of Ashtabhuja-karam.'

8.translit:
tanja mivarkken vaLaiyumnillaa nenjamum tammatee sinditteeRku,
vanji marungkul nerungkanookki vaaytiran tonRu paNittatuNdu,
nanja mudaittivar nookkumnookkam naaniva rtammai yaRiyamaatteen
anjuvan maTRiva raarkkolenna atta buyakarat teenenRaaree.
8.meaning: For me who think only of him, he is my refuge — my bangles stay not on me and my heart too has become his. Gazing closely at my slender vine-like waist, he opened his mouth and commanded me something. The glance with which he looks holds poison; I cannot truly know him. I am afraid of him. 'Who can this be?' they wondered, and He answered, 'I am He of Ashtabhuja-karam.'

9.translit:
mannavan toNdaiyar koonvaNangkum niiLmudi maalai vayirameekan,
tanvali tanpukazh suuzhndakacchi atta buyakarat taatitannai,
kanninan maamatiL mangkaiveendan kaamaru siirkkali kanRi,
kunRaa innisai yaalsonna senjolmaalai yeettaval laarkkidam vaikuntamee.
9.meaning: The Lord of Ashtabhuja-karam, the primal one, dwells in Kanchi which is encircled by the might and fame of Vayiramegan, the king crowned with the tall garland-wreath whom the lord of the Tondaiyar bows to. Upon him Kalikanri (Tirumangai Alwar), king of Mangai with its ever-fresh great ramparts, of lovely renown, composed this garland of true words with unfailing sweet melody — for those able to praise it with this, their abode is Vaikuntha.

10.translit:
unniya yoogattu uRakkattai uuragattuL
annavanai atta buyakarattu emmaan eeRRai
ennai manam kavarnda iisanai vaanavartam (69)
10.meaning: He who is in the contemplative yogic sleep at Uragam, that one; the bull-like Lord of mine at Ashtabhuja-karam; the Lord who stole away my heart, of the celestials' — (69)

11.translit:
totta padaiyettum toolaada venRiyaan,
atta buyakarattaan agngnaanRu, - kuttattuk
kooLmudalai tunjak kuRitterinda sakkarattaan
taaLmudalee nangkatkuc saarvu.
11.meaning: He of unfailing victory who wields the eight weapons He has taken up, He of Ashtabhuja-karam — in that bygone day, He who, to slay the fierce crocodile in the pond, took aim and hurled His disc; His feet alone are our refuge and support.

0.translit:
ponnai maamaNi yaiyaNi yaarnthathoor
minnai veengkadath thucchiyiR kaNdupooy
ennai yaaLudai yeesanai yempiraan
Rannai yaamsenRu kaaNdumthaN kaavilee.
0.meaning: Like gold, like a great gem set and adorned, like a flash of lightning I beheld Him upon the summit of Venkatam; then going on, the Lord who has taken me as His own, our Master Himself — Him we shall go and behold at cool Tirukkaavalur (thaN kaa).

1.translit:
muLaikkathiraik kuRungkudiyuL mukilai moovaa
moovulakum kadanthappaal muthalaay ninRa,
aLappariya aaramu thai arangkam meeya
anthaNanai anthaNartham sinthai yaanai,
viLakkoLiyai marakathaththaith thiruththaN kaavil
veḥkaavil thirumaalaip paadak keettu
vaLarththathanaal payanpeRReen varuka enRu
madakkiLiyaik kaikooppi vangki naaLee.
1.meaning: The sprouting ray of light dwelling in Tirukkurungkudi, the rain-cloud, the One who stands as the primordial cause beyond the three never-aging worlds; the immeasurable nectar, the pure Brahmin who reposes at Srirangam, He who dwells in the minds of the Brahmins; the light of the lamp, the emerald — when she heard her parrot sing of Tirumal at Tirutthankaa and at Tiruveḥkaa, she said, 'I have gained the fruit of having raised you; come here,' and folding her hands she beckoned her tender parrot.

2.translit:
naanilam vaaykkoNdu nannee raRamenRu koothukoNda,
veenilanj selvan suvaiththumizh paalai kadanthaponnee!
kaalnilan thoaynthuviN Noortozhum kaNNanveḥ kaavuthuampoonth
theeniLanj soolaiyap palathuep paalaikkum seemaththathee.
2.meaning: O golden one who crossed the desert wasteland (paalai) — that the summer's lord (the sun) sucked dry with his mouth, scorning even the good water as bitter dross! That Tiruveḥkaa of Kannan, whom the celestials worship with their feet touching the earth, with its beautiful flowering groves of tender honey beyond — it is a refuge and safe haven for every land.

0.translit:
siRandhaven sindhaiyum sengkaN aravum,
niRaindhaseer neeLkachchi yuLLum, - uRaindhadhuvum,
veengkadamum vehkaavum veeLukkaip paadiyumee,
thaamkadavaar thaNthuzhaa yaar.
0.meaning: He who wears the cool tulasi garland abides in my distinguished heart, and upon the red-eyed serpent (Adisesha), and within great glorious Kanchi; He has also taken His abode at Venkatam, at Vehka, and at Velukkai Padi. These are the places where the Lord adorned with the cool tulasi dwells.

1.translit:
anRiv vulagam aLandha asaiveekol,
ninRirundhu veeLukkai neeNagarvaay, - anRu
kidandhaanaik keedilsee raanai,mun kanjaik
kadandhaanai nenjamee kaaN.
1.meaning: O my heart, behold Him! Is it the weariness from measuring this world long ago that makes Him stand and sit and recline in the great city of Velukkai? Behold that Lord of faultless glory who reclined (on the ocean) of old, and who long before overcame and slew Kamsa.

2.translit:
viNNagaram vehkaa viridhiraineer veengkadam,
maNNagaram maamaada veeLukkai, maNNagaththa
thenkudandhai theenaar thiruvarangam thenkoatti,
thankudangkai neereeRRaan thaazhvu.
2.meaning: Vinnagaram, Vehka, Venkatam of spreading waves of water, the city on earth Velukkai with its great mansions, southern Kudandhai upon the earth, honey-filled Tiruvarangam (Srirangam), and southern Kotti (Tirukkottiyur) - these are the humble abodes (taken on earth) by Him who received the water poured into the hollow of His own palm (as Vamana receiving the gift from Bali).

3.translit:
anna uruvin ariyai thirumeyyaththu
in amudha veLLaththai indhaLoor andhaNanai
mannu madhiL kachchi veeLukkai aaL ariyai
Manniya paadagaththu em maindhanai vehkaavil
3.meaning: He is the swan-formed Hari at Tirumeyyam, the flood of sweet nectar; at Indaloor He is the pure holy one; within enduring walled Kanchi at Velukkai He is the man-lion (Narasimha) who rules; at established Padagam He is our youthful Lord; and He dwells at Vehka.

0.translit:
neeragaththaay neduvaraiyin uchchi meelaay
 nilaaththingaL thuNdagaththaay! niRaintha kachchi
ooragaththaay, oNthuraineer vehkaa uLLaay
 uLLuvaar uLLaththaay, ulagam eeththum
kaaragaththaay kaarvaanaththu uLLaay kaLvaa
 kaamarupoong kaaviriyin thenpaal mannu
peeragaththaay, peeraathen nenjin uLLaay
 perumaan un thiruvadiyee peeNineenee.
0.meaning: O Lord who dwells in Thiru-neeragam, who is upon the summit of the lofty mountain (Venkatam), who is in Thiru-nilaa-thingaL-thuNdam adorned with the crescent moon! O Lord of Thiru-ooragam in flourishing Kanchi, who is within Thiru-vehkaa of bright clear waters, who abides in the hearts of those who meditate on you. O Lord of Thiru-kaaragam whom the world praises, who is in Thiru-kaar-vaanam, O cunning one (thief who steals hearts)! O Lord who is enshrined in Thiru-peeragam south of the lovely beautiful Kaveri, who dwells within my heart never departing -- O great Lord, your sacred feet alone have I sought and cherished.

0.translit:
naNRirundhu yoga needhi naNNuvaarkaL sindhaiyuL
senRirundhu theevinaigaL theerttha dheva dhevane
kunRirundha maada needu paadagattu uuragattum
ninRirundhu veqkaNaik kidandhadhu enna neermaiye.
0.meaning: O God of gods, who enter and dwell within the minds of those who steadfastly attain the discipline of yoga, and there destroy their evil deeds! At hill-like, tall-mansioned Paadagam and at Uragam you stand and you sit, and at Vehka you recline — what a wondrous nature is this!

1.translit:
ninRadhu endhai uuragattu irundhadhu endhai paadagattu
anRu veqka Naik kidandhadhu en ilaadha munnelaam
anRu naan piRandhilen piRandhapin maRandhilen
ninRadhum irundhadhum kidandhadhum en nenjuLe.
1.meaning: My Father stood at Uragam, my Father sat at Paadagam, and that day He reclined at Vehka. In all the long time before I existed, I had not yet been born then; but since I was born I have never forgotten. His standing, His sitting, and His reclining are all within my heart.

2.translit:
kallaar madhiL soozh kachchi nagaruL nachchip paadagattuL,
ellaa ulagum vaNanga irundha ammaan, ilangaikkon
vallaaL aagam villaal munindha endhai, vibeedaNaRku
nallaan udaiya naamam sollil namo naaraayaName!
2.meaning: The Lord who, longing for it, sat at Paadagam within the city of Kanchi girded by stone walls so that all the worlds might worship Him; my Father who in wrath shattered with His bow the strong body of the king of Lanka; He who was good to Vibhishana — if one would utter His name, it is ‘Namo Narayana’!

3.translit:
anna uruvin ariyai thirumeyyattu
in amudha veLLattai indhaLuur andhaNanai
mannu madhiL kachchi veLukkai aaL ariyai
manniya paadagattu em maindhanai veqkaavil
3.meaning: The Lord (Hari) of such a form, at Tirumeyyam; the flood of sweet nectar, the Brahmin-like one at Indaluur; the lion who rules at Velukkai in Kanchi of enduring walls; my young strong Lord at the abiding Paadagam, and at Vehka.

4.translit:
uRRu vaNangith thozhumin, ulagezhum
muRRum vizhungum mugilvaNNam, - paRRip
porundhaadhaan maarbidandhu poom paadagattuL
irundhaanai, eththum en nenju.
4.meaning: Draw near, bow, and worship Him — the cloud-hued One who swallowed all the seven worlds entire! My heart praises Him who tore open the chest of the foe who would not be reconciled, and who then sat within flowery Paadagam.

5.translit:
serndha thirumaal kadal kudandhai vengadam
nerndha en sindhai niRai visumbu, - vaayndha
maRai aadagam ananthan vaNduzhaayk kaNNi,
iRai paadi aaya ivai.
5.meaning: Tirumal who is united with the milk-ocean, Kudandhai, and Venkatam; He whom my devoted mind has met; the full heaven; the one of the fitting Vedas; the Lord of Anantha (Adisesha) wearing a garland of cool tulasi humming with bees — these are sung in praise of that Lord.

0.translit:
neeragatthaay neduvaraiyin uchchi meelaay
 nilaatthingaL thuNDagatthaay! niRaindha kachchi
ooragatthaay, oNthuRai neer vehkaa uLLaay
 uLLuvaar uLLatthaay, ulagam eetthum
kaaragatthaay kaarvaanatthu uLLaay kaLvaa
 kaamaru poong kaaviriyin thenpaal mannu
peeragatthaay, peeraadhu en nenjin uLLaay
 perumaan un thiruvadiyee peeNineenee.
0.meaning: O Lord who dwells in Tiruneeragam, who stands upon the summit of the lofty mountain (Tiruvenkatam), who abides in TirunilaatthingalthuNDam, and in Tiruvooragam within the abundant city of Kanchi! You who reside in Tiruvehkaa with its bright fair waters, who dwell in the hearts of those who meditate upon you, who abide in Tirukkaaragam praised by the world, and in the dark sky (Tirukkaarvaanam), O thief (who steals hearts)! You who abide steadfast in Tirupperagam on the southern bank of the lovely flower-filled Kaveri, and who dwell within my heart without ever departing, O great Lord, your sacred feet alone have I sought and cherished.

0.translit:
unnaiyum okkalaiyil koNDu tamizh-maruvi
 unnoDu tangaL karuttaayina seydu varum
kanniyarum magizhak kaNDavar kaN kuLirak
 katravar tetri varap petra enakku aruLi
mannu kuRunguDiyaay veLLaRaiyaay madhiL soozh
 solai malaik karase kaNNa purattamude
en avalam kaLaivaay aaDuga sengeerai
 ezhulagum uDaiyaay aaDuga aaDugave.
0.meaning: They carry you upon their hips, delighting in Tamil and doing whatever pleases them along with you, so that the young maidens rejoice, the eyes of all who behold you grow cool, and the learned gather flocking near. Having graced me thus, O Lord who abides at Tirukkurungudi, who stands at Tiruvellarai, O King of the orchard-clad hill girt with ramparts, O nectar of Kannapuram! You who remove my sorrow, dance the chenkirai dance; O Lord of all the seven worlds, dance, dance!

1.translit:
indiranoDu biraman eesan imaiyavar ellaam
mandira maa malar koNDu maRaindu uvaraay vandu ninraar
chandiran maaLigai serum sadhirargaL veLLaRai ninraay
andhi am podu idhuvaagum azhagane kaappiDa vaaraay.
1.meaning: Indra, Brahma, Siva and all the celestials have come bearing sacred mantra-blossoms and stand hidden, longing for you. O graceful one who stands at Tiruvellarai of the noble ones, where mansions rise to touch the moon! Now is the dusk hour come; O beautiful one, come that I may put the protective charm upon you.

2.translit:
kanrugaL illam pugundu kadhaRuginra pasu ellaam
ninru ozhinden unnaik koovi nesam el onrum ilaadhaay
manril nal el andhip podhu madhiL tiru veLLaRai ninraay
ninru kaNDaay en-tan sollu naan unnaik kaappiDa vaaraay.
2.meaning: The calves have entered the houses, and all the cows are lowing aloud; I stand here worn out, calling you, O you who show me not a trace of affection. It is the fine dusk hour in the common; O Lord who stands at walled holy Tiruvellarai! Stay and heed my word, I pray; come, that I may put the protective charm upon you.

3.translit:
sep podhu men mulaiyaargaL siru sorum illum sidhaitthiTTu
ap podhu naan urappap poy aDisilum uNDilai aaLvaay
mup podhum vaanavar ettum munivargaL veLLaRai ninraay
ip podhu naan onrum seyyen embiraan kaappiDa vaaraay.
3.meaning: You wrecked the little play-meals and play-houses of the soft-breasted girls, and when I scolded you then you went off and would not even eat your rice, O my master. O you who stand at Tiruvellarai of the sages, whom the celestials praise at all three times of day! Now I shall do nothing to you, my Lord; come, that I may put the protective charm upon you.

4.translit:
kaNNil maNal koDu toovik kaalinaal paayndanai enrenru
eNNarum piLLaigaL vanditTu ivaraal muRaippaDuginraar
kaNNane veLLaRai ninraay kaNDaaroDe theemai seyvaay
vaNName velaiyadhu oppaay vaLLale kaappiDa vaaraay.
4.meaning: "He threw sand into our eyes, he kicked at us with his foot!" — so, again and again, countless children come and complain of you. O Kannan who stands at Tiruvellarai, who works mischief upon all you meet! O you whose hue is like the ocean, O generous one, come, that I may put the protective charm upon you.

5.translit:
pallaayiravar iv vooril piLLaigaL theemaigaL seyvaar
ellaam un mel anrip pogaadhu embiraan nee inge vaaraay
nallaargaL veLLaRai ninraay gnaanac chuDare un meni
sollaara vaazhthi ninru etthic choppaDak kaappiDa vaaraay.
5.meaning: Many thousands of children in this town do mischief, yet all the blame goes upon none but you, my Lord; come here, you! O you who stand at Tiruvellarai of the good, O lamp of wisdom! Praising your form with full-throated words and extolling you as I stand, that all may be well, come, that I may put the protective charm upon you.

6.translit:
kanjan kaRukkoNDu nin mel karu nirac chem mayirp peyai
vanjippadharku viduttaan enbadhu or vaarthaiyum uNDu
manju tavazh maNi maaDa madhiL tiru veLLaRai ninraay
anjuvan nee angu nirka azhagane kaappiDa vaaraay.
6.meaning: There is even a rumor abroad that Kamsa, in his enmity, has sent against you that dark-hued, red-haired demoness to beguile and destroy you. O Lord who stands at walled holy Tiruvellarai, with jeweled mansions over which the clouds creep! I am afraid when you stand out there; O beautiful one, come, that I may put the protective charm upon you.

7.translit:
kaLLac chagaDum marudum kalakkazhiya udhai seydha
piLLai yarase nee peyaip piDitthu mulaiyuNDa pinnai
uLLavaaRu onrum aRiyen oLiyuDai veLLaRai ninraay
paLLi koL podhu idhuvaagum paramane kaappiDa vaaraay.
7.meaning: O prince among children who kicked apart the deceitful cart and the marudu trees till they were shattered — after you seized the demoness and drank her breast, I know not in the least how things truly are. O radiant Lord who stands at Tiruvellarai! Now is the hour to lie down to sleep; O Supreme One, come, that I may put the protective charm upon you.

8.translit:
inba madhanai uyarthaay imaiyavarkku enrum ariyaay
kumbak kaLiRaTTa kove koDum kanjan nenjinil kootre
sem pon madhiL veLLaRaiyaay selvattinaal vaLar piLLaay
kambak kabaali kaaN angu kaditu oDik kaappiDa vaaraay.
8.meaning: You who have exalted bliss itself, ever unattainable to the celestials, O King who slew the rutting tusker, O death lodged in the heart of cruel Kamsa! O Lord of Tiruvellarai with its ramparts of red gold, O child reared in abundance! Look — there is the terrifying Kapali (ghoul) yonder; run swiftly here and come, that I may put the protective charm upon you.

9.translit:
irukkoDu neer sangil koNDiTTu ezhil maRaiyor vandu ninraar
tarukkel nambi sandhi ninru taay sollukku oLLaay sila naaL
tirukkaappu naan unnaic chaattat thesuDai veLLaRai ninraay
urukkaaTTum andhi viLakku inru oLi koLLa etrugen vaaraay.
9.meaning: With Rik hymns and with water in the conch, the lovely Vedic brahmins have come and stand here; do not be willful, my dear one — for a few days at least, stand at the twilight juncture and heed your mother's word. O resplendent Lord who stands at Tiruvellarai, that I may fasten the sacred protection upon you! I am lighting the dusk-lamp that reveals your form to shine; come.

10.translit:
podhamar selvak kozhundhu puNar tiru veLLaRaiyaanai
maadharkku uyarndha asodhai magan-thannaik kaappiTTa maatram
vedhap payan koLLa valla viTTucitthan sonna maalai
paadhap payan koLLa valla pattaruLLaar vinai pome.
10.meaning: This is the garland of verses sung by Vishnuchitta (Periyalvar), who is able to grasp the fruit of the Vedas, about how Yashoda — exalted above all women — put the protective charm upon her son, the flourishing precious sprout abiding at holy Tiruvellarai. For the devotees who are able to gain the fruit of his feet, all their evil karma shall depart.

11.translit:
venri maa mazhu vendhi mun maN misai mannarai moo ezhu kaal
konra deva, nin kurai kazhal tozhuvadhu or vagai enakku aruL puriye,
manril maam pozhil nuzhai tandhu malligai mauvalin podhu alarthi,
tenral maa maNam kamazhtara varu tiru veLLaRai ninraane!
11.meaning: O God who, bearing the great victorious axe, slew the kings of the earth thrice seven times of old — grant me some way to worship your resounding anklet-clad feet. O Lord who stands at holy Tiruvellarai, where the southern breeze comes wafting in through the great grove, opening the jasmine and mauval blossoms and spreading their rich fragrance!

12.translit:
vasai il naal maRai keDutha am malar ayaRku aruLi, mun pari mugamaay,
isai koL vedha noolenru ivai payandhavane enak karuL puriye,
uyar koL maadhavip podhoDu laaviya maarudham veedhiyin vaay,
disai ellaam kamazhum pozhil soozh tiru veLLaRai ninraane.
12.meaning: O you who, taking the horse-faced (Hayagriva) form of old, graced the lotus-born Brahma whose flawless four Vedas had been lost, and brought forth these melodious Vedic scriptures again — grant me your grace. O Lord who stands at holy Tiruvellarai, girt with groves whose fragrance, borne on the breeze that plays among the lofty madhavi blossoms, perfumes the streets and all the directions!

13.translit:
veyyanaay ulagu ezhuDan nalindhavan uDalagam iru piLavaa,
kaiyil neeL ugirp paDaiyadhu vaaytthavane enak karuL puriye,
maiyinaar taru vaaralinam paaya vaN taDattiDaik kamalangaL,
deyva naaRum oN poygaigaL soozh tiru veLLaRai ninraane.
13.meaning: O you to whom the weapon of the long claws upon your hand came fitting, that you might cleave in two the body of him who, fierce, oppressed all the seven worlds (Hiranya) — grant me your grace. O Lord who stands at holy Tiruvellarai, girt with bright ponds where the dark vaaral fish leap in the rich pools and the lotuses breathe a divine fragrance!

14.translit:
vaam pari yuga mannar tam uyir sega aivarkkaT karasaLitta,
kaambin aar tiru vengaDap poruppa, nin kaadhalai aruL enakku,
maam pozhil thaLir kodhiya maDak kuyil vaayadhu tuvarp peydha,
theem palang kanit thenadhu nugar tiru veLLaRai ninraane.
14.meaning: O Lord of the holy Venkata hill thick with bamboo, who bestowed the kingdom upon the five (Pandavas) when the lives of the kings perished in the age of leaping steeds — grant me love for you. O Lord who stands at holy Tiruvellarai, where the gentle koel, having pecked the tender shoots of the great grove until its mouth turned astringent, then sips the sweet honey of the ripe pala (jackfruit)!

15.translit:
maana vel oN kaN maDavaral maN magaL azhunga mun neerp parappil,
ena maagi anru iru nila miDandhavane enak karuL puriye,
kaana maa mullai kazhaik karumbu eRi veN muRuval seydhu alarginra,
thenin vaay malar murugu kukkum tiru veLLaRai ninraane.
15.meaning: O you who of old, becoming the Boar, raised up the broad earth when the gentle Earth-goddess, with bright deer-startling eyes, languished sunk in the spread of the waters — grant me your grace. O Lord who stands at holy Tiruvellarai, where the wild jasmine, climbing the tall sugarcane, blossoms forth like a white smile and spills its nectar from honey-mouthed flowers!

16.translit:
pongu neeN muDi amarargaL tozhudhu ezha amudhinaik koDuttaLippaan,
angu or aamai adhaagiya aadhi nin aDimaiyai aruL enakku,
tangu peDaiyoDu ooDiya madhukaram taiyalaar kuzhal aNaivaan,
tingaL toy senni maaDam senru aNai tiru veLLaRai ninraane.
16.meaning: O Primordial One who there became the Tortoise to give and share out the nectar, while the celestials with surging tall crowns worshipped and rose up — grant me servitude to you. O Lord who stands at holy Tiruvellarai, where the bee that has sulked with its mate goes off to nestle in the tresses of the women, and the mansions, their summits touching the moon, reach up to it!

17.translit:
aaRinoDu oru naangu uDai neDu muDi arakkan-tan siram ellaam,
veRu veRuga villadhu vaLaitthavane enak karuL puriye,
maaRil sodhiya maradhagap paasaDait taamarai malar vaarndha,
teRal maandhi vaNDu in isai murala tiru veLLaRai ninraane.
17.meaning: O you who bent your bow so that all the heads upon the tall ten crowns (six plus four) of the demon (Ravana) were severed apart, each from each — grant me your grace. O Lord who stands at holy Tiruvellarai, where the bee, drinking the nectar that brims from the lotus blossom on its emerald-green leaf of matchless radiance, hums its sweet music!

18.translit:
mun iv vezhulagu uNarvu inri iruL miga umbargaL tozhudhu ettha,
anna maagi anru aru maRai payandhavane enak karuL puriye,
mannu kedhagai soodhagam enru ivai vanattiDaic churumbinangaL,
tenna venna vaNDu in isai mural tiru veLLaRai ninraane.
18.meaning: O you who of old, when these seven worlds were without awareness and darkness abounded, became the Swan and brought forth the rare Vedas while the celestials worshipped and praised — grant me your grace. O Lord who stands at holy Tiruvellarai, where amid the woods of the abiding ketaki and mango the swarms of bees, humming "tenna, venna," murmur their sweet music!

19.translit:
aangu maavali velviyil irandhu senru agal iDam muzhudhinaiyum,
paanginaal koNDa parama nir paNindhu ezhuven enak karuL puriye,
oongu piNDiyin sem malar eRi vaNDu uzhitara, maa veRit
theen kuyil mizhatrum paDappait tiru veLLaRai ninraane.
19.meaning: O Supreme One who there went begging at Mahabali's sacrifice and skilfully took the whole of the wide earth — I rise bowing to you; grant me your grace. O Lord who stands at holy Tiruvellarai, with its groves where the bees rove over the red blossoms of the lofty asoka, and the honey-sweet koel, mounting the mango, warbles its song!

20.translit:
manju laamaNi maaDangaL soozh tiru veLLaRai adan meeya,
anjanam puraiyum tiru uruvanai aadiyai amudattai,
nanju ulaaviya veel valavan kalikanRi sol ai iraNDum,
enjal inRi ninRu eetta vallaar imaiyoorkku arasaavaarkaLE.
20.meaning: He who dwells in sacred Tiruvellarai, encircled by jewelled mansions that reach into the clouds, whose holy form is dark like collyrium, who is the primordial First Cause and the ambrotsia of immortality. Those who can stand and praise Him without fail through these ten verses of Kalikanri (Tirumangai Alwar), the mighty wielder of the poison-smeared spear, shall become rulers over the celestial ones.

21.translit:
thuLakkam il suDarai, avuNan uDal
piLakkum maindanai, peeril vaNangi poy
aLappu ilaa amudai amararkku aruL
viLakkinai senRu veLLaRaik kaaNDumE.
21.meaning: The flawless radiance that never wavers, the youthful Lord who split open the body of the demon (Hiranya); having worshipped Him at Tiruppernagar and gone onward, the boundless ambrosia, the lamp of grace shining for the immortals. Let us go and behold Him at Tiruvellarai.

22.translit:
aaraanum kaRpippaar naayagarE? naan avanai
kaar aar tirumEni kaaNum aLavum poy
seer aar tiruvEngaDamE tirukkOvaloorE
madiL kacchi ooragamE pEragamE
pEraa marudu iRuttaan veLLaRaiyE veqkaavE
pEr aali thaNkaal naRaiyoor tiruppuliyoor
aaraamam soozhnda arangam kaNamangai
22.meaning: Will anyone at all instruct me about my Lord? Until I go and behold His cloud-dark sacred form, I shall journey on -- to glorious Tiruvenkatam, to Tirukkovalur, to Tiru-Uragam within walled Kanchi, and to Tiru-Peragam; to Tiruvellarai of Him who, never moving, broke the marudu trees, to Tiruvehka, to great Tiruvali, to Thankal, to Naraiyur, to Tiruppuliyur, and to Srirangam encircled by gardens, and to Kanamangai.

23.translit:
annam thuyilum aNi neer vayal aali
ennuDaiya in amudai evvuL peru malaiyai
kanni madiL soozh kaNamangai kaRpagattai
minnai iru suDarai veLLaRaiyuL kal aRai mEl
ponnai maragadattai puTkuzhi em pOr ERRai
mannum arangattu em maa maNiyai vallavaazhp
23.meaning: At Tiruvali of beautiful waters and fields where swans sleep, my sweet ambrosia; the great mountain at Tiruvevvul; the wish-granting Kalpaka tree at Kanamangai encircled by ever-new ramparts; the lightning and the two luminaries (sun and moon); upon the stone shrine within Tiruvellarai the gold and the emerald; my fighting bull at Tirupputkuzhi; my great gem in eternal Srirangam; and at Tiruvallavazh.

0.translit:
nandRirundhu yoga needhi naNNuvaarkaL sindhaiyuL
sendRirundhu theevinaikaL theerththa deva devane
kundRirundha maada needu paadaganthu mooraganthum
nindRirundhu vehkaNaik kidandhadhenna neermaiye.
0.meaning: O God of gods who, entering and dwelling well within the minds of those who attain the discipline of yoga and right knowledge, dissolved their evil deeds! What manner of grace is it that You stand in Paadagam with its tall mansions rising like hills, sit in Ooragam, and recline in Vehka (Tiruvehka)?

1.translit:
nindRadhendhai yooraganthi rundhadhendhai paadaganthu
andRuvehka Naikkidandha thennilaadha munnelaam
andRunaanpi Randhilenpi RandhapinmaR andhilen
nindRadhum mirundhadhumki dandhadhummen nenjuLe.
1.meaning: My Father stood (in Ooragam), my Father sat in Paadagam, and in days of old He reclined in Vehka. In the times before all this, I was not yet born; but since I was born, I have not forgotten. His standing, His sitting, and His reclining are all within my heart.

2.translit:
neeraganthaay neduvaraiyi nucchi melaay
 nilaaththingaL thuNdaganthaay! niRaindha kacchi
ooraganthaay, oNduRaineer vehkaa vuLLaay
 uLLuvaa ruLLaththaay, ulaga meththum
kaaraganthaay kaarvaanath thuLLaay kaLvaa
 kaamarupoong kaaviriyin thenpaal mannu
peraganthaay, peraadhen nenji nuLLaay
 perumaanun thiruvadiye peNinene.
2.meaning: O You who are in Tiruneeragam, who are upon the peak of the lofty hill, who are in TiruththaNkaa (where the crescent moon shines)! You who dwell in Ooragam in flourishing Kacchi (Kanchi), who abide within Vehka of clear flowing waters, who reside in the hearts of those who meditate on You; You who are in Tirukkaaragam praised by the world, who dwell in the dark sky, O thief (who steals hearts)! You who dwell forever in Tiruppergam on the southern bank of the lovely flowering Kaveri, who are within my heart never to leave it; O Lord, I have cherished and sought only Your sacred feet.

3.translit:
kalleduththuk kalmaari kaaththaay endRum
 kaamarupoong kacchiyoo raganthaay! endRum,
villiRuththu melliyal thoaL thoaynhdaay endRum
 vehkaavil thuyila marndha vendhe. endRum,
alladarththu mallaraiyandR attaay endRum,
 maakeeNda kaththalaththen maindhaa endRum,
solleduththuth thangiLiyaich solle endRu
 thuNaimulaimel thuLisoarach soarkkindRaaLe.
3.meaning: "You who lifted the hill and warded off the rain of stones (Govardhana)!" she cries; "You who are in lovely flowering Kacchi-Ooragam!" she cries; "You who broke the bow and embraced the shoulders of the tender-natured one (Sita)!" she cries; "O King who reclines in slumber at Vehka!" she cries; "You who, dispelling the darkness, slew the wrestlers that day!" she cries; "O my strong one who split the demon-horse (Kesi) with Your bare hand!" she cries; bidding her parrot to repeat these words, she swoons, while drops fall upon her paired breasts.

4.translit:
aaraanum kaRpippaar naayagare? naan avanaik
kaar aar thirumeni kaaNum aLavum poay
seer aar thiruvengadame thirukkova
loore madhiL kacchi ooragame perakame
peraa marudhu iRuththaan vehrLaiye vehkaave
per aali thaNkaal naRaiyoor thiruppuliyoor
aaraamam soozhndha arangam kaNamangai
4.meaning: Will anyone instruct me as to who my Lord is? I will go until I behold him of the cloud-dark sacred form — to glorious Tiruvenkatam, to Tirukkovalur, to walled Kacchi-Ooragam, to Pergam, to Vehrlarai, to Vehka (where dwells He who broke the marudu trees), to great Aali, to ThaNkaal, to NaRaiyur, to Tiruppuliyur, to Arangam (Srirangam) girt with groves, and to KaNamangai.

5.translit:
unniya yogaththu uRakkaththai ooraganthuL
annavanai atta puyagaraththu emmaan eRRai
ennai manam kavarndha eesanai vaanavartham
5.meaning: Him whose sleep is the yoga of contemplation, who dwells in Ooragam; my Lord, the bull-like one of Ashtabhujakaram (Atta-buyagaram); the Lord who has stolen away my heart; the Lord of the celestials —

0.translit:
nandrirundhu yoga-needhi naNNuvaarkaL sindhaiyuL
sendrirundhu theevinaigaL theerttha-deva devane
kundrirundha maada-needu paadakatthu moorakatthum
nindrirundhu vehkaNaik kidandhadhenna neermaiye.
0.meaning: O God of gods, who entering the hearts of those who, sitting well in steadfast yogic discipline, draw near to You, dwell there and dissolve their cruel sins! What a gracious nature is Yours, that in tall-mansioned Paadakam where hills seem to stand, and in Ooragam You stand and sit, and in Vehka (Vehkanai) You recline!

1.translit:
nindradhendhai yooragatthu irundhadhendhai paadakatthu
andru vehka Naik kidandhadhu ennilaadha munnelaam
andru naan pirandhilen pirandha pin marandhilen
nindradhum irundhadhum kidandhadhum en nenjuLe.
1.meaning: My Father stood in Ooragam, my Father sat in Paadakam, and that day He reclined in Vehkanai. In all those ancient times when I was not, in that day I was not yet born; but since I was born I have never forgotten. His standing, His sitting and His reclining are all there within my heart.

2.translit:
naagatthaNaik kudandhai vehkaa thiru-evvuL,
naagatthaNai arangam peranbil, - naagat
taNaip paarkadal kidakkum aadhi nedumaal,
aNaippaar karutthanaavaan.
2.meaning: He who reclines on the serpent-couch at Kudandhai (Kumbakonam), at Vehkaa, at sacred Evvul (Thiruvallur), He who reclines on the serpent at Srirangam and at Peranbil, the primordial Nedumal who lies upon the serpent-couch on the Milk-Ocean - He becomes the very thought (the cherished aim) of those who embrace Him in their hearts.

3.translit:
koondhalaar magizh kovalanaay veNNey
maandhu azhundhaiyil kaNdu magizhndhu poy
paandhaL paazhiyil paLLi virumbiya
vendhanaic chendru kaaNdum vehkaavuLe.
3.meaning: Becoming the cowherd in whom the long-tressed women delight, eating butter, He was seen and rejoiced over at Azhundhai; then going on, that King who loved His resting-bed upon the serpent's spread coils - Him let us go and behold there within Vehkaa.

4.translit:
neeragatthaay nedu-varaiyin ucchi melaay
nilaa-tthingaL thuNdagatthaay! niraindha kacchi
ooragatthaay, oN-thurai-neer vehkaa uLLaay
uLLuvaar uLLatthaay, ulagam ettham
kaaragatthaay kaar-vaanatthu uLLaay kaLvaa
kaamaru poong kaaviriyin thenbaal mannu
peragatthaay, peraadhu en nenjin uLLaay
perumaan un thiruvadiye peNinene.
4.meaning: O You who are in Neeragam, who are upon the summit of the tall mountain, who wear the crescent moon as an ornament! O You of full Kacchi's Ooragam, O You within Vehkaa of bright pure waters, O You who dwell in the hearts of those who meditate, O You whom the world praises in Kaaragam, O You within the dark sky, O Thief! O You who abide in Peragam fixed on the southern bank of the lovely flowering Kaveri, O You who, never departing, are within my heart - my Lord, Your sacred feet alone have I longed for.

5.translit:
kalleduttuk kal-maari kaatthaay endrum
kaamaru poong kacchi ooragatthaay! endrum,
villiruttu melliyal thoaL thoyndhaay endrum
vehkaavil thuyil amarndha vendhe endrum,
alladarttu mallarai andru attaay endrum,
maa keeNda kattalatthu en maindhaa endrum,
solleduttut tang kiLiyaic chollee endru
thuNai-mulai mel thuLi soarac chorkkindraaLe.
5.meaning: Saying 'O You who lifted up the hill and warded off the hail-storm!', saying 'O You of lovely flowering Kacchi's Ooragam!', saying 'O You who broke the bow and embraced the shoulders of the tender-natured one (Sita)!', saying 'O King who rested in slumber at Vehkaa!', saying 'O You who in that day, crushing the strength, slew the wrestlers!', saying 'O my young Lord whose hand split open (the elephant / the horse-demon)!', she takes up words and bids her parrot 'Say these!' - and, with drops trickling upon her paired breasts, she swoons away.

6.translit:
muLaik-kadhiraik kuRunguDiyuL mugilai moovaa
moovulagum kadandhu appaal mudhalaay nindra,
alappariya aaramudhai arangam meya
andhaNanai andhaNar tham sindhaiyaanai,
viLak-koLiyai maragadhatthaith thiruttaN kaavil
vehkaavil thirumaalaip paadak kettu
vaLarttadhanaal payan petren varuga endru
madak kiLiyaik kai-koopi vangkinaaLe.
6.meaning: The young ray of light who is in Kurunguḍi, the cloud-hued one, the unaging First Cause who stands beyond the three worlds transcending them, the immeasurable nectar who dwells in Srirangam, the holy Brahmin (Lord) who is in the minds of the holy ones, the radiant lamp, the emerald - hearing her parrot sing of Thirumal of Thiruttankaa and of Vehkaa, she said, 'By raising you I have gained my reward; come!' and with folded hands she called the tender parrot to her.

7.translit:
aaraanum kaRpippaar naayagare? naan avanaik
kaar aar thirumeni kaaNum aLavum poy
seer aar thiruvengadame thirukkova
loore madhiL kacchi ooragame perakame
peraa marudhu iRutthaan veLLaRaiye vehkaave
per aali thaNkaal naRaiyoor thiruppuliyoor
aaraamam soozhndha arangam kaNamangai
7.meaning: Will any teach me? Is He my Lord? Until I go and behold Him of the dark cloud-like sacred form, I shall wander - to glorious Thiruvenkatam, to Thirukkovalur, to walled Kacchi's Ooragam and Peragam, to Vellarai of Him who shattered the marudha trees without straining (uprooting them while crawling), to Vehkaa, to great Aali, to Thankaal, to Naraiyur, to Thiruppuliyur, to Srirangam encircled by groves, to Kanamangai.

8.translit:
anna uruvin ariyai thirumeyyatthu
in amudha veLLatthai indhaLoor andhaNanai
mannu madhiL kacchi veLukkai aaL ariyai
manniya paadakatthu em maindhanai vehkaavil
8.meaning: The Lord (Hari) of swan-like form at Thirumeyyam, the flood of sweet nectar, the holy Brahmin (Lord) of Indhalur, the ruling man-lion (Narasimha) of Velukkai in enduring walled Kacchi, our young Lord of abiding Paadakam, and (Him) at Vehkaa -

9.translit:
vengadamum viNNagarum vehkaavum, ahkaadha
poong-kidangil neeL-koval ponnagarum, - naangidatthum
nindraan irundhaan kidandhaan nadandhaane,
endraal kedumaam idar.
9.meaning: At Venkatam, at Vinnagaram, at Vehkaa, and at the golden city of long Koval with its unfailing flower-filled moats - in these four places He stood, He sat, He reclined, and He walked. If one but says so, all affliction will perish.

10.translit:
sirandhaven sindhaiyum sengaN aravum,
niraindha-seer neeL-kacchi uLLum, - uRaindhadhuvum,
vengadamum vehkaavum velukkaip paadiyume,
thaam-kadavaar thaN-thuzhaiyaar.
10.meaning: He of the cool tulasi never quits my excellent mind, nor the red-eyed serpent (Adisesha), nor full-glorious long Kacchi where He abides, nor Venkatam, nor Vehkaa, nor the town of Velukkai - in all these the wearer of the cool tulasi dwells and departs not.

11.translit:
viNNagaram vehkaa viri-thirai-neer vengadam,
maNNagaram maa-maada velukkai, maNNagatta
then-kudandhai thenaar thiruvarangam then-kotti,
than-kudangai neereRRaan thaazhvu.
11.meaning: Vinnagaram, Vehkaa, Venkatam of spreading wavy waters, the earthly city of great-mansioned Velukkai, southern Kudandhai of this earth, honey-rich sacred Srirangam, southern Kotti - these are the humble abodes (condescensions) of Him who received the water (of Mahabali's gift) into the hollow of His hand.

12.translit:
isaindha aravamum veRpum kadalum,
pasaindhangu amudhu paduppa, - asaindhu
kadaindha varutthamo kacchi-vehkaavil,
kidandhirundhu nindradhuvum angu.
12.meaning: With the fitting serpent (as churning-rope) and the mountain and the ocean joined together so that the nectar might rise up - is it from the weariness of swaying and churning that in Kacchi's Vehkaa He lies down, and there too He sits and stands?

13.translit:
poruppidaiye nindrum punal kuLitthum, aindhu
neruppidaiye niRkavum neer veNDaa - viruppudaiya
vehkaave serndhaanai meym-malar thooyk kai-thozhudhaal,
ahkaave theevinaigaL aayndhu.
13.meaning: You need not stand upon mountain-tops, nor bathe in waters, nor stand amid the five fires (in austerities). If, with loving devotion, you scatter true flowers and worship with folded hands Him who has settled at Vehkaa, your cruel sins, all sifted out, will surely waste away and vanish.

14.translit:
naanilam vaayk-koNDu nan-neer aRam endru kodhu koNDa,
venilanj selvan suvaitthu umizh paalai kadandha ponne!
kaal-nilam thoyndhu viNNoar thozhum kaNNan vehkaa-vudhu am-poong
theniLanj soalaiyap paaladhu eppaalaikkum semattadhe.
14.meaning: O Gold who has crossed beyond the desert (paalai) which the rich lord of summer has tasted and spat out, seizing the four-fold land and rejecting even its good water and virtue as worthless chaff! The Vehkaa of Kannan, whom the celestials worship with feet touching the ground - beyond its lovely flowering grove of fresh honey lies that which is a refuge and safety for every desolate waste.

0.translit:
neeragatthaay neduvaraiyin ucchi meelaay
nilaatthingaL thuNdagatthaay! niRaindha kacchi
ooragatthaay, oNthuRaineer vehkaa uLLaay
uLLuvaar uLLatthaay, ulagam eetthum
kaaragatthaay kaarvaanatthu uLLaay kaLvaa
kaamarupoong kaaviriyin thenpaal mannu
peeragatthaay, peeraadhen nenjin uLLaay
perumaan un thiruvadiyee peeNineenee.
0.meaning: O Lord who dwells in Tiruneeragam, who abides upon the summit of the lofty mountain, who dwells in Tiruninravur of the slender moonlit crescent! You who reside in Tiru-ooragam within abundant Kanchi, who is in Tiruvehka of the bright clear waters, who dwells in the hearts of those who meditate upon You, whom all the world praises. You who abide in Tirukkaragam, who dwells in Tirukkarvanam, O cunning thief, who is enthroned forever on the southern bank of the lovely beautiful Kaveri at Tiruppeeragam, and who dwells without ever departing within my heart! O great Lord, I have sought and cherished only Your sacred feet.

0.translit:
neeragattaay neduvaraiyin ucchi meelaay
 nilaaththingaL thuNdagattaay! niRaindha kacchi
ooragattaay, oNthuraineer vehkaa uLLaay
 uLLuvaar uLLaththaay, ulagam eeththum
kaaragattaay kaarvaanaththu uLLaay kaLvaa
 kaamarupoong kaaviriyin thenpaal mannu
peeragattaay, peeraadhen nenjin uLLaay
 perumaan un thiruvadiyee peeNineenee.
0.meaning: O Lord who dwells in Tiruneeragam, who stands upon the summit of the tall mountain (Tiruvengadam), who abides in Tiruninravur where the cool crescent moon shines! You dwell in Tiruvooragam within prosperous Kanchi, and in Tiruvehka of the bright clear waters; You reside in the hearts of those who meditate upon You. O Lord praised by the whole world, who dwells in Tirukaaragam and in Tirukkaarvaanam, O thief (who steals hearts)! You who abide forever in Tiruppeeragam (Tiruppernagar) on the southern bank of the beautiful, lovely Kaveri, and who, never leaving, dwell within my heart -- O my Lord, I have sought and cherished only Your sacred feet.

0.translit:
neeragatthaay neduvaraiyin ucchi meelaay
 nilaatthingaL thuNDagatthaay! niRaindha kacchi
ooragatthaay, oNthuRaineer vehkaa uLLaay
 uLLuvaar uLLatthaay, ulaga eetthum
kaaragatthaay kaarvaanatthu uLLaay kaLvaa
 kaamarupoong kaaviriyin thenpaal mannu
peeragatthaay, peeraadhen nenjin uLLaay
 perumaan un thiruvadiyee peeNineenee.
0.meaning: You who dwell in Thiruneeragam, You who are atop the lofty mountain peak, You who abide in Thiru-Nilaatthingal-Thundam! You who reside in Thiru-Ooragam within prosperous Kanchi, You who dwell in Thiru-Vehka of the clear bright fords, and You who dwell in the hearts of those who meditate upon You. You who abide in Thirukkaaragam praised by the world, You who dwell in Thirukkaarvaanam, O divine thief! You who are firmly established in Thiruppeeragam on the southern bank of the lovely, beautiful Kaveri, and You who never depart from within my heart. O Lord, it is Your sacred feet alone that I have cherished and sought.

0.translit:
vaNgatthaal maamaNivan thunthu munneer
 mallaiyaay madhiLkacchi yooraay! peeraay,
koNgatthaar vaLaNgonRai yalaNgal maarvan
 kulavaraiyan madappaavai yidappaal koNdaan,
paNgatthaay paaRkadalaay paarin meelaay
 panivaraiyi nucchiyaay pavaLa vaNNaa,
eNguRRaay emperumaan unnai naadi
 eezhaiyeen iNganamee oozhitharukeenee.
0.meaning: O Lord of Mallai (Tirukkadalmallai), where ships upon the three-fold ocean push ashore great gems! O Lord of walled Kacchi (Kanchipuram)! O Lord of Pera! You who took to Your left side the tender, lovely maiden, daughter of the great mountain (king), her lord wearing on his chest a garland of fragrant konrai blossoms. O You who hold her as part of Yourself, O Lord of the milk-ocean, O You who are above the whole earth, O You who are on the peak of the cool snowy mountain, O coral-hued one! Where have You gone, my Lord? Seeking You, wretched as I am, must I thus pass age upon age?

0.translit:
solluvan sorporuL thaanavaiyaay-ch chuvai yooroli naatramum thootramumaay,
nallaran naanmukan naaraNa nukkidan thaanthadanj choozhndhazha kaayakacchi,
pallavan villava nendrulakil pala raayppala vEndhar vaNangukazhar pallavan,
mallaiyar kOnpaNindha para mEcchura viNNaka ramadhuvE.
0.meaning: I shall declare it: He is the words and their meaning themselves, and He is taste, touch, sound, smell, and form. He is the Lord of beautiful Kanchi, surrounded by broad ponds, the abode of the gracious Hara (Siva), the four-faced Brahma, and Narayana. This is Parameswara Vinnagaram, worshipped by the Pallava king, lord of Mallai, the Pallava before whose feet many kings of the world bow, called Pallava and Villava.

1.translit:
kaarmannu neeLvisum pumkada lumsudarum nila nummalai yum,
thannundhith thaarmannu thaamarai-k kaNNanidam thada maamadhiL soozhndhazha kaayakacchi,
thErmannu thennava naimunaiyil seru vilthiral vaattiya thiNsilaiyOn,
paarmannu pallavar kOnpaNindha paramEcchura viNNaka ramadhuvE.
1.meaning: He from whose navel sprang the lasting lotus, the dark-eyed Lord on whom rest the abiding broad sky, the sea, the luminaries, the earth and the mountains. His place is beautiful Kanchi, girt with great wide ramparts. This is Parameswara Vinnagaram, worshipped by the Pallava king, lord of the earth, the strong-bowed one who shattered in battle the might of the chariot-borne Pandya king at the battle-front.

2.translit:
urandharu mellaNaip paLLi koNdaan orukaal munnam maavuru vaaykkadaluL,
varandharum maamaNi vaNNanidam maNimaa dangaL soozhndhazha kaayakacchi,
nirandhavar maNNaiyil puNNukar vElnedu vaayi lukaccheru vilmunanaaL,
parandhavan pallavar kOnpaNin dhapara mEcchura viNNaka ramadhuvE.
2.meaning: He who reclines in slumber upon the soft, strength-giving serpent-couch, who once long ago took the form of a great horse in the sea and grants boons, the Lord of the gem-hued sapphire form. His place is beautiful Kanchi, ringed with jewelled mansions. This is Parameswara Vinnagaram, worshipped by the Pallava king who in battle long ago spread his forces wide at Mannai, where the long blood-drinking spears made the broad gates fall.

3.translit:
aNdamu meNdisai yumnilanum alaineerodu vaaneri kaalmudhalaa uNdavan,
endhaipi raanadhi damoLi maadangaL soondhazha kaayakacchi,
viNdava riNdaikku zhaamudanE virain thaariri yaccheru vilmunaindhu,
paNdoru kaalvaLaith thaanpaNin dhapara mEcchura viNNaka ramadhuvE.
3.meaning: He who swallowed the cosmic egg, the eight directions, the earth, the surging waters, the sky, fire and wind and all, my Father and Lord. His place is beautiful Kanchi, ringed with bright mansions. This is Parameswara Vinnagaram, worshipped by the Pallava king who long ago bent his bow in battle, charging at the foe so that the throng of enemies fled scattering in haste.

4.translit:
thoompudaith thiNkaivan thaaLkaLitrin thuyar theerthara vamveruva,
munanaaL poompunal poykaipuk kaanava nukkidan thaanthadanj choozhndhazha kaayakacchi,
thEmpozhil kundreyil thennavanaith thisaip paccheru mElviyan thandruchendra,
paampudaip pallavar kOnpaNindha paramEcchura viNNaka ramadhuvE.
4.meaning: He who long ago entered the flower-filled pond and removed the anguish of the strong-legged, hollow-trunked elephant, as the serpent fled in terror. His abode is beautiful Kanchi, surrounded by broad ponds. This is Parameswara Vinnagaram, worshipped by the serpent-bannered Pallava king who once marched against the Pandya king of the honey-groved hill-fort and bewildered him, charging upon him in battle with wonder.

5.translit:
thiNpadaik kOLari yinuru vaayth thiralOnaka lamseru vilmunanaaL,
puNpadap pOzhndha piraanadhidam poru maadangaL soozhndhazha kaayakacchi,
veNkudai neezhalsen kOlnadap pavidai velkodi vElpadai munnuyarththa,
paNpudaip pallavar kOnpaNindha paramEcchura viNNaka ramadhuvE.
5.meaning: He who in the form of the fierce man-lion long ago in battle clove open the broad chest of the mighty one (Hiranya) and wounded him, the Lord. His place is beautiful Kanchi, ringed with battling (tall) mansions. This is Parameswara Vinnagaram, worshipped by the gracious Pallava king, under whose white parasol's shade the just sceptre prevails, who raises high before him the victorious banner and the spear-army.

6.translit:
ilakiya neeNmudi maavali thanperu vELviyil maaNuru vaaymunanaaL,
salamodu maanilang koNdava nukkidan thaanthadanj choozhndhazha kaayakacchi,
ulakudai mannavan thennavanaik kanni maamadhiL soozhkaru voorveruva,
palapadai saayaven raanpaNin dhapara mEcchura viNNaka ramadhuvE.
6.meaning: He who long ago took the form of a dwarf at the great sacrifice of Mahabali of the gleaming long crown, and with guile (and water poured) seized the whole great earth. His abode is beautiful Kanchi, surrounded by broad ponds. This is Parameswara Vinnagaram, worshipped by him who, ruling the world, conquered the Pandya king so that his many armies fell, making Karuvur, ringed by virgin-strong great ramparts, tremble in terror.

7.translit:
kudaiththiral mannava naayorukaal kuran kaippadai yaa,
malai yaalkadalai adaiththava nendhaipi raanadhi dammaNi maadangaL soozhndhazha kaayakacchi,
vidaiththiral villavan nenmeliyil veruvaccheru vElvalan kaippidiththa,
padaiththiral pallavar kOnpaNindha paramEcchura viNNaka ramadhuvE.
7.meaning: He who once became the mighty parasol-bearing king (Rama), and making the monkeys his army, dammed the sea with mountains, my Father and Lord. His place is beautiful Kanchi, ringed with jewelled mansions. This is Parameswara Vinnagaram, worshipped by the war-strong Pallava king who, gripping the battle-spear in his right hand at Nenmeli, made the strong-bowed Chera (rebellious) one tremble in fear.

8.translit:
piraiyudai vaaNudhal pinnai thiraththu munnoru kaalseru villurumin,
maraiyudai maalvidai yEzhadarththaar kidan thaanthadanj choozhndhazha kaayakacchi,
karaiyudai vaaLmara mannarkkedak kadal pOla muzhangum kuralkaduvaay,
paRaiyudaip pallavar kOnpaNindha paramEcchura viNNaka ramadhuvE.
8.meaning: He who once long ago, for the sake of Nappinnai of the crescent-bright brow, in battle subdued the seven great Vedic bulls like a thunderbolt. His abode is beautiful Kanchi, surrounded by broad ponds. This is Parameswara Vinnagaram, worshipped by the drum-bearing Pallava king, whose fierce war-drums roar like the sea to destroy the bloodstained, sword-bearing warrior kings.

9.translit:
paarmannu tholpukazhp pallavarkOn paNin dhapara mEcchura viNNakarmEl,
kaarmannu neeLvayal mangaiyar thandhalai vankali kandrikun dhaadhuraiththa,
seermannu sendhamizh maalaivallaarth thiru maamakaL thannaru Laal,
ulakil thErmanna raayoli maakadalsoozh sezhu neerula kaaNdu thikazhvarkaLE.
9.meaning: Upon Parameswara Vinnagaram, worshipped by the Pallava king of ancient, earth-abiding fame, Kaliyan Tirumangai, lord of Tirumangai of long fields where rain-clouds gather, sang without fault this garland of fine, abiding pure Tamil. Those who master it, by the grace of Lakshmi the great divine Lady, shall become charioted kings in the world and rule the rich-watered earth girt by the loud-roaring great sea, and shine in glory.

0.translit:
alang kezhu thaDakkai aayan vaay aambaR kazhiyumaal ennuLLam ennum,
pulang kezhu poru neerp puTkuzhi paaDum poadumoa neermalaikku ennum,
kulang kezhu kolli koamaLa valli koDiyiDai neDumazhaik kaNNi,
ilang kezhil thoaLikku en ninainthirunthaay iDaventhai enthai piraanae.
0.meaning: My heart says it melts at the flute-music from the mouth of the cowherd (Krishna) of the beautiful, large hands; she says, 'Shall we go to TirunNeermalai, singing of Tiruppuṭkuzhi where on the fertile flowing waters the birds gather?'
