O Lord, my Father of Idaventhai, what have you been thinking concerning this girl of distinguished lineage, tender as a kolli vine, slender-waisted, with long rain-cloud eyes and bright, beautiful shoulders?

1.translit:
annam thuyilum aNi neer vayal aali
ennuDaiya in amuthai evvuL peru malaiyai
kanni mathiL soozh kaNamangaik kaRpagaththai
minnai iru suDarai veLLaRaiyuL kal aRaimeal
ponnai marakathaththai puTkuzhi em poar eaRRai
mannum arangaththu em maa maNiyai vallavaazhp
1.meaning: He who reclines like a swan in beautiful Tiruvaali of watery fields, my own sweet nectar, the great mountain of Evvul, the wish-granting Kalpaka tree of Kanamangai girded by maiden-fresh ramparts, the lightning and the two luminaries, the gold upon the stone-shrine of Tiruveḷḷaṟai, the emerald, my fighting bull of Tiruppuṭkuzhi, my great gem of everlasting Srirangam, He of Vallavazh.

0.translit:
pooNDavattam piRarkkaDaindu toNDupaTTup poynnoolai meynnoolen
DenDrumOdi maaNDu,
avattam pOgaadE vamminendai en vaNangap paDuvaanai,
kaNangaLEttum neeNDavattaik karumugilai emmaandannai ninDravoor nittilattait tottaarcOlai,
kaaNDavattaik kanalerivaayp peyvittaanaik kaNdadunaan kaDalmallait talasayanattE.
0.meaning: Do not waste yourselves by clinging to others, falling into servitude, and forever reciting false scriptures as if they were true scriptures, perishing in vain. Come, O my people, and worship Him who is my Father, the One worshipped by me, whom the hosts of celestials praise, the tall and lofty One, the dark rain-cloud, my Lord, the pearl that stands at Ninravur amid groves clustered with blossoms, who once made the Khandava forest blaze with consuming fire. Him I beheld at Talasayanam in Kadalmallai.

1.translit:
EtRi naiyima yattuLem meesanai
immai yaimaru maikku marundinai,
aaTRalai aNDat tappuRat tuyttiDum
aiya naikkaiyi laazhiyon DREndiya
kooTRi nai, kuru maamaNik kunDrinai
ninDra voorninDra nittilat tottinai,
kaaTRi naippuna laicchenDru naaDik
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNDukoN DEnE.
1.meaning: He who rides the bull, our Lord upon the Himalaya, the remedy for this life and the next, the mighty One who casts beyond the bounds of the cosmos, the Father, Death itself bearing the discus in His hand, the great hill of radiant precious gems, the cluster of pearls that stands at Ninravur, the very wind and the very water. Searching for Him I went and found Him within Kannamangai.

0.translit:
kaasai yaadai moodiyoodik kaadhalsey thaanavanoor,
naasa maaga nambavalla nambi namperumaan,
veyi nanna thoaLmadavaar veNNeyuN daanivanendru
esa nindra vemperumaa nevvuL kidandhaane.
0.meaning: He who, covering Himself with a coarse garment, ran and lovingly sported—our perfect Lord, the trusted One who is able to bring destruction to His foes; the great Lord at whom the slender-shouldered, bamboo-armed maidens jeered, saying "This is the one who ate the butter!"—that Lord is reclining at Evvul.

1.translit:
thaiya laaLmel kaadhalseydha thaanavan vaaLarakkan,
poyyi laadha ponmudiga LondrodhO Ndrum, andru
seydha vemporr thanni langOr senjarath thaaluruLa,
eydha vendhai yemperumaa nevvuL kidandhaane.
1.meaning: The sword-wielding demon (Ravana) who lusted after the woman (Sita)—on that day, in the fierce war he waged, my Father, my Lord, shot a single red arrow so that his ten true golden crowns rolled to the ground; that Lord is reclining at Evvul.

2.translit:
munnOr thoodhu vaanaraththin vaayil mozhindhu, arakkan
mannoor thannai vaaLiyinaal maaLa munindhu avane
pinnOr thoodha naadhimannark kaagip perunilaththaar,
innaar thoodha nenanindraa nevvuL kidandhaane.
2.meaning: First He sent His message through the mouth of a monkey (Hanuman), and in wrath He destroyed the demon's royal city with His arrows; then He Himself became a messenger for the ancient kings (the Pandavas), so that the people of this great earth declared, "What kind of messenger is this!"—that Lord is reclining at Evvul.

3.translit:
pandha Naindha melviralaaL paavaidhan kaaraNaththaal,
vendhi raLE rEzhumvendra vendhan viripugazhser,
nandhan maindha naagavaagum nambi namperumaan,
endhai thandhai thamperumaan evvuL kidandhaane.
3.meaning: The king who, for the sake of the doll-like maiden with soft fingers that held a ball, conquered the seven fierce bulls—our perfect Lord of widespread fame who came to be the son of Nanda; the Lord of my father and forefathers, that Lord is reclining at Evvul.

4.translit:
paala Naagi njaalamEzhu muNdupaN daalilaimel,
saala naaLum paLLikoLLum thaamaraik kaNNan eNNil,
neela maarvaN duNduvaazhum neydhalan thaNkazhani,
Ela naarum paimpuravi levvuL kidandhaane.
4.meaning: The lotus-eyed One who, becoming a babe, swallowed the seven worlds and of old reclined for many days upon a banyan leaf—He, beyond count, is reclining at Evvul, the fresh wilderness fragrant with spices, set amid cool watery fields of waterlilies where dark blue bees live and feed.

5.translit:
sOththa nambi yendruthoNdar miNdith thodarndhazhaikkum,
aaththanambi sengaNambi yaagilum thevarkkellaam,
mooththa nambi mukkaNambi yendru munivarthozhu-
dhEththum, nambi yemperumaa nevvuL kidandhaane.
5.meaning: He whom the devotees throng and follow, calling out "Refuge, O Lord!"—the trustworthy Lord, the red-eyed Lord; the One whom the sages worship and praise as the elder Lord, the three-eyed Lord above all the gods—that perfect Lord, my Lord, is reclining at Evvul.

6.translit:
thinga Lappu vaanerikaalaagi, thisaimuganaar
thanga Lappan saamiyappan paagath thirundha, vaNduN
thonga lappu neeNmudiyaan soozhkazhal sooda nindra,
enga Lappa nemperumaan evvuL kidandhaane.
6.meaning: Becoming the moon, water, sky, fire and wind, He is the father of the four-faced Brahma, the Lord's own father; He on one side of whose body resides Shiva, whose long crown wears garlands sipped by bees—our Father, our Lord, who stands so that His encircling anklets may be adorned, is reclining at Evvul.

7.translit:
munivan moorththi moovaraagi vedham viriththuraiththa
punidhan, poovai vaNNanaNNal puNNiyan viNNavarkOn,
thaniyan seyan thaanoruvan aagilum thannadiyaarkku
iniyan, endhai yemperumaan evvuL kidandhaane.
7.meaning: The sage, the One who became the three forms, the pure One who expounded the Vedas, the Lord dark as the kaya flower, the righteous One, the king of the celestials; the solitary One, the distant One, who though He is the unique One, is sweet to His own devotees—my Father, my Lord, is reclining at Evvul.

8.translit:
pandhi rukkum melviralaaL paavai panimalaraaL,
vandhi rukkum maarvanneela meni maNivaNNan,
andha raththil vaazhum vaanOr naayaga naayamaindha,
indhi rarkkum thamperumaa nevvuL kidandhaane.
8.meaning: On whose chest abides the doll-like one with soft fingers holding a ball, she of the cool lotus (Lakshmi)—the dark-bodied, sapphire-hued Lord; He who became the chief of the celestials dwelling in the heavens, the very Lord even of Indra, is reclining at Evvul.

9.translit:
iNdai koNdu thoNdarEththa evvuL kidandhaanai,
vaNdu paadum paipuravil mangaiyar kOnkaliyan,
koNda seeraal thaNdamizhsey maalaiyee raindhumvallaar,
aNda maaLva thaaNaiyandrE laaLva rarulagE.
9.meaning: Of Him who reclines at Evvul, whom the devotees worship bearing flower-garlands, Kaliyan, the king of Mangai where bees sing in the fresh wilderness, made this garland of twice-five (ten) verses in cool Tamil with the glory he received. Those who can master them shall surely rule the celestial world; if not that, they shall rule this fair world.

10.translit:
annam thuyilum aNi neer vayal aali
ennudaiya in amudhai evvuL peru malaiyai
kanni madhiL soozh kaNamangaik kaRpagaththai
minnai iru sudarai veLLaRaiyuL kal aRaimel
ponnai maragadhaththai puTkuzhi em pOr Eatrai
mannum arangaththu em maa maNiyai vallavaazhp
10.meaning: He who is at Thiruvali of beautiful watery fields where swans sleep, my sweet nectar, the great mountain at Evvul, the wish-granting tree at Kanamangai girt by maiden-strong ramparts, the lightning and the two luminaries, the gold upon the stone-chamber at Vellarai, the emerald, my battle-bull at Putkuzhi, my great gem at everlasting Srirangam, He of Vallavaazh—

11.translit:
naagath thaNaikkudandhai veqkaa thiruvevvuL,
naagath thaNaiyarangam peranbil, - naagath
thaNaippaaR kadalkidakku maadhi nedumaal,
aNaippaar karuth thanaavaan.
11.meaning: The primordial, vast Lord who reclines—upon the serpent-couch at Kudandhai, at Thiruvehka, at sacred Evvul, upon the serpent-couch at Srirangam, at Peranbil, and upon the serpent-couch on the Ocean of Milk—becomes the very thought of those who embrace and hold Him close.

0.translit:
naagath thaNaikkudandai veqkaa tiruvevvuL,
naagath thaNaiyarangam peeranbil, - naagath
thaNaippaaR kadalkidakku maadi nedumaal,
aNaippaar karuth thanaa vaan.
0.meaning: The great Lord, the ancient Nedumaal, reclines upon the serpent couch at Kudandai (Tirukkudandai), at Veqkaa, at TiruvevvuL, at the serpent-couched Arangam (Srirangam), at Peeranbil, and upon the serpent-bed of the milky ocean. He becomes the very thought and resolve of those who embrace and take refuge in Him.

0.translit:
viRperu vizhavum kanjanum mallum vEzhamum paaganum veezha,
seTRavan RaNNai, puram-eri seyda sivanuRu tuyar-kaLai dEvai,
paTRalar veeyak kOl-kaiyil koNDu paarttan-Ran tEr-mun nin RaaNai,
siTRavai paNiyaal muDi-tuRan thaaNait tiruvallik kENik kaN DEnE.
0.meaning: He who destroyed the great bow-festival, and made Kamsa, the wrestlers, the elephant and its mahout fall slain; the Lord who removed the suffering that came upon Shiva when he had burnt the three cities; who, taking up his discus in hand, slew his foes and stood before Arjuna's chariot; and who, at the bidding of his stepmother, renounced the crown — Him I saw at Tiruvallikkeni.

1.translit:
vEdattai vEdat tin-suvaip payanai vizhumiya munivargaL vizhungum,
kOdil in-kaniyai nandanaar kaLiTRaik kuvalayat tOr-tozhu dEttum,
aadiyai amudai ennai aaLuDai appanai oppavar illaa maadargaL vaazhum,
maaDamaa mayilait tiruvallik kENik kaN DEnE.
1.meaning: He who is the Vedas, and the very fruit and savour of the Vedas, the flawless sweet fruit that the noble sages relish; the elephant-strong son of Nanda, whom the people of the earth worship and praise; the primordial One, the nectar, my Father who has taken me into His service — Him, the peerless one, I saw at Tiruvallikkeni in Mayilai (Mylapore) of grand mansions where lovely women dwell.

2.translit:
vanjanai seyyat taay-uru vaagi vandapEy-alaRi maN sEra,
nanjamar mulaiyooD uyir-seka vuNDa naadanait taanavar kooTRai,
vinjai-vaa navar-saa raNar-sittar viyandu tudi-seyyap peN-uru vaagi,
anjuvai amuda manRu aLit thaaNait tiruvallik kENik kaN DEnE.
2.meaning: The Lord who, when the demoness came in a mother's form to deceive him, sucked away her life along with the poison-laden breast so that she fell wailing to the earth; the Death of the demons; He who, while the celestials of wisdom, the chaaranas and the siddhas wondered and praised, once took on a woman's form and served the nectar of five flavours — Him I saw at Tiruvallikkeni.

3.translit:
indiranuk kenRu aayargaL eDutta ezhil-vizha vil pazha naDai-sey,
mandira vidiyil poosanai peRaadu mazhai-pozhin Did-aLarndu,
aayar andamOD ina-vaa nirai-taLa raamal emperu maan-aruL enna,
andamil varaiyaal mazhai-taDut thaaNait tiruvallik kENik kaN DEnE.
3.meaning: When the cowherds raised a splendid festival of old custom for Indra, and Indra, getting no worship by the ordained rites of mantras, poured down rain so that they grew faint; when the cowherds, lest their herds of cattle perish utterly, cried 'Grace us, our Lord!' — He held off the rain with the endless mountain. Him I saw at Tiruvallikkeni.

4.translit:
in-tuNaip padumat talar-magaL tanakkum inban naR-puvi-tanak kiRaivan,
tan-tuNai aayar-paavai nappinnai tanakkiRai maTRaiyOrk kellaam van-tuNai,
panja paaNDavark kaagi vaay-urai toodu-senR iyangum en-tuNai,
endai tandai-tam maaNait tiruvallik kENik kaN DEnE.
4.meaning: He who is the delight even of the lotus-born Lakshmi his fitting consort, and the Lord of the good earth; the lord of his own beloved Nappinnai, the cowherd maiden, and the strong refuge of all others; my companion who, for the sake of the five Pandavas, went forth uttering words as their envoy — He, the Lord of my father and forefathers, I saw at Tiruvallikkeni.

5.translit:
andagan siRuvan arasar-tam arasaR kiLaiyavan aNi-izhai yais-senRu,
endamak kurimai sey enat tariyaadu emperu maan-aruL enna,
sandam-al kuzhalaaL alakkaN nooTRu-var-tam peNDirum eydi nool-izhappa,
indiran siRuvan tEr-mun nin RaaNait tiruvallik kENik kaN DEnE.
5.meaning: When the son of the blind king (Duryodhana), to the younger brother (Yudhishthira) of the emperor of kings, went and said of the bejewelled lady (Draupadi), 'Make her ours by right' — and she, unable to bear it, cried 'Grace me, my Lord!' — the woman of fragrant flowing tresses was saved from distress, while the wives of the hundred Kauravas were widowed and lost their marriage-threads. He who stood before the chariot of Indra's son (Arjuna) — Him I saw at Tiruvallikkeni.

6.translit:
baradanum tambi satturuk kanannum ilakkuma nODum maidiliyum
iravu-nan pagalum tudi-seyya ninRa iraavaNaan dakanai-em maaNai,
kuravamE kamazhum kuLir-pozhi looDu kuyilodu mayilgaL ninR-aala,
iraviyin kadirgaL nuzhaidal-sey daRiyaat tiruvallik kENik kaN DEnE.
6.meaning: He, our Lord the slayer of Ravana, before whom Bharata and his brother Shatrughna, together with Lakshmana and Maithili (Sita), stood praising him night and good day — Him I saw at Tiruvallikkeni, whose cool groves are fragrant with kuravam blossoms, where koels and peacocks stand and dance, and into which the rays of the sun have never known how to pierce.

7.translit:
paLLiyil Odi vandatan siRuvan vaayil-Or aayira naamam,
oLLiya vaagip pOda-vaang adanuk konRum-Or poRuppil naagi,
piLLaiyais seeRi veguNDu-tooN puDaippap piRai-eyiTR anal-vizhip pEzhvaay,
teLLiya singa maagiya dEvait tiruvallik kENik kaN DEnE.
7.meaning: When his little son (Prahlada), come home from school, brought forth from his mouth a thousand shining names of the Lord, and he (Hiranya) could not bear even one of them — and raging in fury at the child, struck the pillar — He, the God who became the radiant lion with crescent fangs, fiery eyes and a gaping mouth — Him I saw at Tiruvallikkeni.

8.translit:
meenamar poygai naaL-malar koyvaan vETkaiyi nODu-senR izhinda,
kaanamar vEzham kai-eDut talaRak karaa-vadan kaalinaik kaduva,
aanaiyin tuyaram teerap puL-loorndu senRu-ninR aazhi-toT DaaNai,
tEnamar sOlai maaDamaa mayilait tiruvallik kENik kaN DEnE.
8.meaning: When the forest elephant, descending in eagerness into the fish-filled pool to pluck the day's fresh blossoms, was seized at its foot by the crocodile and raised its trunk and trumpeted in agony — He who, to end the elephant's suffering, rode upon his bird (Garuda), came, stood, and hurled his discus — Him I saw at Tiruvallikkeni in Mayilai of honey-laden groves and great mansions.

9.translit:
mannu-taN pozhilum vaaviyum madhiLum maaDamaa Ligaiyum maN Dapamum,
tennan-toN Daiyark-kOn seydanan-mayilait tiruvallik kENi-ninR aaNai,
kanni-nan maaDa mangaiyar talaivan kaamaru seerk kalikanRi,
sonna-sol maalai pattudan vallaar suka-minidu aaLvar-vaa nulagE.
9.meaning: Him who stands at Tiruvallikkeni in the good Mayilai — made by the lord of the southern Tondai land, with its enduring cool groves, ponds, ramparts, mansions, palaces and halls — those who can master these ten verses, the garland of words sung by Kalikanri (Tirumangai), chief of Tirumangai of fine fresh mansions, rich in beauty, shall happily and sweetly rule over the heavenly world.

10.translit:
vandu-udaitta veN-tiraigaL sem-pavaLa veN-muttam
andi viLakkum aNi-viLak kaam, - endai
oru-vallit taamaraiyaaL onRiya-seer maarvan,
tiruvallik kENiyaan senRu.
10.meaning: The white waves that come surging and break upon the shore cast up red coral and white pearls, which become the lovely lamps lighting the evening twilight. My Father, on whose chest the lotus-seated lady (Lakshmi), unique like a single creeper, abides in perfect union, is the Lord of Tiruvallikkeni — go there.

11.translit:
taaLaal ulagam aLanda asaivE-kol,
vaaLaa kiDand-aruLum vaay-tiRavaan, - neeL-Odam
vand-alaikkum maa-mayilai maa-vallik kENiyaan,
aindalai-vaay naagat taNai.
11.meaning: Is it perhaps the weariness of having measured the worlds with his foot that makes him lie there in silence, gracing us yet not opening his mouth? The Lord of great Vallikkeni in grand Mayilai, where the long sea-waves come surging and beat upon the shore, reclines upon the couch of the five-headed serpent.

0.translit:
anRRaayark kulakkoDi yoaDaNimaa malarmangaiyo Danpalavi,
avuNarkken Raanu mirakkami laadavanukkuk kuRaiyumiDa maavadu,
irumpozhilsoozh nanRRaayapu nalnaRai yoorttiruvaa likuDandai taDandigazh koavalnagar,
ninRRaanirun daankiDan daannaDandaaR kiDammaamalai yaavadu neermalaiyae.
0.meaning: He who once joined in love with the creeper-like daughter of the cowherd clan and with the lovely Lakshmi of choice flowers, yet who never showed mercy to the demons — what could ever be a place of want for Him? He stood, sat, reclined and strode about in Naraiyur of good waters ringed by dense groves, in Tiruvali, in Kudantai, and in Koval city of broad splendor; and the great hill that is His abode is Neermalai.

1.translit:
kaaNDaava nameNbador kaaDamarark karaiyanadu kaNDavan niRka,
munae mooNDaarazha luNNamu nindaduvum aduvanRiyum munnula kamporaitheert taaNDaan,
avuNanavan maarvagalam ugiraalvagi raagamu nindu,
ariyaayneeN Daankural aaginimirndavanuk kiDammaamalai yaavadu neermalaiyae.
1.meaning: While Indra, king of the gods, looked on, He angrily made the forest called Kandava blaze up to be devoured by fire; and beyond that, of old He relieved the earth of its burden and ruled it. He grew tall as Man-Lion (Narasimha) and angrily clove the broad chest of the demon (Hiranyakashipu) with His nails; and He who became the dwarf and then stretched up tall — the great hill that is His abode is Neermalai.

2.translit:
alamannu maDalsuri sangameDut taDalaazhiyi naalaNi yaaruruvil,
pulamannu vaDampunai kongaiyinaaL poRaitheeramu naaLaDu vaaLamaril,
palamannar paDacchuDa raazhiyinaip pagaloanmaRai yappaNi koNDu aNisaer
nilamannanu maayula gaaNDavanuk kiDammaamalai yaavadu neermalaiyae.
2.meaning: Taking up the curving conch with its mighty whorl and the destroying discus, in His richly adorned form, to relieve the burden of the broad-breasted earth-goddess decked in jewels, in fierce sword-battle He made many kings fall by His blazing discus, causing the sun to set as His instrument; and He who became the splendid king of the earth and ruled the world — the great hill that is His abode is Neermalai.

3.translit:
taangaadadoa raaLari yaavauNan RanaiveeDa munindava naalamarum,
poongoadaiyar pongeri moozhgavilait taduvanRiyum venRikoL vaaLamaril,
paangaagamun aivaro Danpalavip patiRRaindiraT TippaDai vaendarpaDa,
neengaaccheru vilniRai kaattavanuk kiDammaamalai yaavadu neermalaiyae.
3.meaning: He angrily destroyed the unbearable demon (Hiranyakashipu) as the Man-Lion, and caused the women with blossom-garlands who clung to him to plunge into surging fire; and beyond that, in victorious sword-battle, joining lovingly beside the five (Pandavas), He brought down the kings with their fivefold-doubled (a hundred) armies and guarded honor in the unwavering combat — the great hill that is His abode is Neermalai.

4.translit:
maalungaDa laaramalaik kuvaDiT aNaikaTTi varampuruva
madisaerkoalamadiL aayavilangaikeDap paDaitoTToru kaalama riladira,
kaalamidu venRayan vaaLiyinaal kadirneeNmuDipattu maRuttamarum,
neelamugil vaNNane makkiRaivaR kiDammaamalai yaavadu neermalaiyae.
4.meaning: He cast peaks of hills into the great roaring sea and built a causeway across it as a boundary; and to destroy Lanka with its beautiful, sky-high, moon-touching ramparts, He took up His weapons and made the battle resound for one whole age. Saying 'this is the time,' with arrows He severed the ten radiant tall crowns (of Ravana) and fought on — He of the dark-cloud hue, our Lord, whose abode is the great hill, Neermalai.

5.translit:
paaraarula gumpani maalvaraiyum kaDalumsuda rumivai yuNDum,
enakkaaraa dena ninRava nemperumaan alaineerula gukkarasaagiya,
appaeraanai munindamunik karaiyan piRarillai nunakkenu mellaiyinaan,
neeraarppae raanneDu maalavanuk kiDammaamalai yaavadu neermalaiyae.
5.meaning: Having swallowed this earth and all its worlds, the cool great mountains, the seas, and the luminaries, He stood saying 'this is not yet enough for me' — He, our Lord. He is the sage-king who grew angry at that great king of the wave-tossed watery world (the demon), one of whom it was said 'there is none other equal to you.' Tall Tirumal of the abundant waters — the great hill that is His abode is Neermalai.

6.translit:
pugaraaruru vaagimunindavanaip pugazhveeDa muninduyi ruNDu,
asuranna garaayina paazhpaDa naamameRindaduvanRiyum venRikoL vaaLavuNan,
pagaaadava naayira naamamaDip paNiyaadava naippaNi yaamalaril,
nigaaaayavan nenjiDan daanavanuk kiDammaamalai yaavadu neermalaiyae.
6.meaning: Taking a fierce fearsome form, He angrily destroyed the demon's fame, devoured his life, and laid waste the demon's cities so their very names were cast down; and beyond that, the victorious sword-bearing demon (Hiranyakashipu) who would not utter even one of His thousand names nor bow at His feet — He who tore open the heart of that matchless one without his bowing in battle — the great hill that is His abode is Neermalai.

7.translit:
picchacchiRu peelipi Ditthulagil piNandinmaDavaaravar poal,
angNganae acchamilar naaNila raadanmaiyaal avarseygai veRutthaNi maamalartthooy,
nacchinama naaraDai yaamainamak karuLsey enavuLkuzhain daarvamoDu,
niccham ninai vaarkkaruL seyyumavaR kiDammaamalai yaavadu neermalaiyae.
7.meaning: Unlike those mad corpse-eating ones who roam the world clutching small peacock-feathers in their begging frenzy — being thus without fear and without shame — those who, loathing such conduct, scatter choice great flowers and, desiring Him, melt within and pray with love saying 'grant us that we may not reach the realm of Yama' — He who grants grace to those who daily meditate on Him — the great hill that is His abode is Neermalai.

8.translit:
paesumaLa vanRiduvammin namar! piRarkkaeTpadan munpaNi vaarvinaigaL,
naasamadu seyyiDum aadanmaiyaal aduvaenama duyviDam naaNmalarmael
vaasamaNi vaNDaRai paimpuRavil manamaindoDu nainduzhal vaar,
madiyil neesaravar senRaDai yaadavanuk kiDammaamalai yaavadu neermalaiyae!
8.meaning: Come, our people! This is no matter for mere talking — for those who bow before others even hear of it, their deeds (karma) are utterly destroyed; therefore that very place is our refuge. In the green meadows where fragrant bees hum upon fresh blossoms, He whom the witless base ones — those who waste away pining with deluded minds — cannot reach: the great hill that is His abode is Neermalai!

9.translit:
neDumaalavan maeviya neermalaimael nilavumpugazh mangaiyar koan,
amaril kaDamaagaLi yaanaivallaan kaliyan oliseydamizh maalaival laarkku,
uDanae viDumaalvinai vaeNDiDil maelulagum eLidaayiDu manRiyi langolisaer,
koDumaagaDal vaiyaga maaNDumadik kuDaimannava raayaDi kooDuvarae.
9.meaning: For those skilled in this resounding Tamil garland composed by Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar), king of the Mangai land of abiding fame, master of the rutting war-elephant in battle — sung upon Neermalai where tall Tirumal dwells — their great evil karma is at once released. Should they desire it, even the higher world becomes easy for them; and they shall rule the world girt by the resounding fierce sea, become kings beneath the moon-white parasol, and reach His feet.

10.translit:
alangezhu taDakkai yaayanvaa yaampaR kazhiyumaa lenpuLLam ennum,
pulangezhu poruneerp puTkuzhi paaDum poadumoa neermalaik kennum,
kulangezhu kolli koamaLa vallik koDiyiDai neDumazhaik kaNNi,
ilangezhil thoaLik kenninain dirundaay iDavendai yendai piraanae.
10.meaning: She says 'my heart melts at the flute-sound from the mouth of the cowherd of the swaying broad hands'; she sings of Putkuzhi by the contending waters of the rich land and asks 'shall we go to Neermalai?' O Lord of Idavendai, my Father! For this maiden — slender as a tender vine of noble Kolli mountain, with eyes wide as long rain-clouds, with radiantly lovely shoulders — what have you been thinking, that you remain thus?

11.translit:
oaDaa ariyaay iraNiyanai yooniDanda
saeDaar pozhilsoozh tiruneer malaiyaanai
vaaDaa malartthuzhaay maalai muDiyaanai
naaDoaRum naaDi naRaiyooril kaNDaenae.
11.meaning: Him who became the never-fleeing Man-Lion and split open the flesh of Hiranyan; Him of Tiruneermalai ringed by groves abounding in splendor; Him who wears on His crown a garland of unfading tulsi flowers — Him I sought day after day and beheld in Naraiyur.

12.translit:
gatiyae lillainin narulal ladenakku,
nidiyae tiruneer malainit tilattotthae,
patiyae paravit tozhumtoN Dartamakkuk
gatiyae unaikkaNDu koNDuyn dozhindaenae.
12.meaning: There is no refuge for me other than Your grace, O Treasure! O cluster of pearls of Tiruneermalai! O Lord, the very goal of the devotees who praise and worship You! Having seen and attained You, I have been saved and delivered.

13.translit:
aruvisoar vaengaDam neermalai enRuvaay
veruvinaaL meyyam vinavi yirukkinRaaL,
peruguseerk kaNNapuram enRu paesinaaL
uruginaaL, uLmelin daaLidu venkoloa.
13.meaning: With her mouth she names in awe 'Vengadam where waterfalls pour down,' 'Neermalai'; she sits asking after Meyyam; she speaks the name 'Kannapuram of swelling glory'; she has melted away and grown faint within — what can this be?

14.translit:
manjuyar maamadi theeNDa neeNDa maaliruny soalaima Naalarvandu en
nenjuLLum kaNNuLLum ninRu neengaar neermalai yaarkol? ninaikkamaaTTaen?
manjuyar ponmalai maele zhunda maamugil poanRuLar vandukaaNeer
anjiRaip puLLumon RaeRi vandaar acchoavoru varazhagiyavaa.
14.meaning: The Bridegroom of Maliruncholai, which rises tall to touch the great cloud-high moon, has come and stands within my heart and within my eyes and will not depart. Is He the Lord of Neermalai? I cannot fathom it. Come and see — He is like a great cloud risen over a golden mountain wreathed in mist; He came mounted upon a beautiful-winged bird (Garuda). Oh, how surpassingly lovely is He!

15.translit:
orunaR suRRam enakkuyir oNporuL
varunal tolgati yaagiya maindanai
nerunal kaNDadu neermalai inRupoay
karunel soozhkaNNa mangaiyuL kaaNDumae.
15.meaning: Him who is my one good kinsman, my very life, my radiant wealth, the strong One who is my good ancient refuge to come — yesterday I beheld at Neermalai; today, going on, we shall behold Him within Kannamangai encircled by dark paddy fields.

16.translit:
kaarvaNNam tirumaeni kaNNum vaayum
 kaitthalamum aDiyiNaiyum kamala vaNNam,
paarvaNNa maDamangai pittar pittar
 panimalarmael paavaikkup paavam seydaen,
aervaNNa enbaedai enjol kaeLaaL
 emperumaan tiruvaranga mengae? ennum,
neervaNNan neermalaikkae poavaen ennum
 iduvanRoa niRaiyazhindaar niRku maaRae?
16.meaning: His sacred form is of cloud-dark hue, His eyes, mouth, palms, and pair of feet are of lotus hue; He is mad for the gentle earth-goddess, mad for the Lady on the cool flower (Lakshmi) — and I have sinned against this child of mine. My beautiful-hued innocent girl heeds not my words; she asks 'where is the Srirangam of my Lord?'; she says 'I will go to the Neermalai of the water-hued One' — is this not the way of those whose modesty is undone?

17.translit:
kaar aar maNi niRak kaNNanoor viNNagaram
seer aar gaNapuram saeRai tiruvazhundoor
kaar aar kuDandai kaDigai kaDalmallai
ear aar pozhil soozh iDavendai neermalai
seer aarum maaliruncholai tirumoagoor (37)
17.meaning: Tiruvinnagaram, the town of Kannan dark as a cloud-laden gem; Ganapuram full of excellence; Sarai; Tiruvazhundur; cloud-dark Kudandai; Kadigai; Kadalmallai; Idavendai ringed by lovely groves; Neermalai; the glory-filled Maliruncholai; Tirumogur (37).

18.translit:
munnavanai moozhikkaLatthu viLakkinai
annavanai aadanoor aaNDu aLakkum aiyanai
nennalai inRinai naaLaiyai neermalaimael (70)
18.meaning: Him the Primordial One; the Lamp at Mozhikkalam; that very One; the Lord who reigns over and measures out Adanur; Him who is yesterday, today, and tomorrow; Him upon Neermalai (70).

19.translit:
payinRa tarangam tirukkoaTTi, pannaaL
payinRaduvum vaengaDamae pannaaL, - payinRa
taNidigazhum soalai yaNineer malaiyae
maNidigazhum vaNDaDakkai maal.
19.meaning: Where He long abode is Srirangam, and Tirukkotti; and where He abode for many days is Vengadam too, for many days; and the place He long dwelt is cool-gleaming Neermalai ringed with groves of beauty — Tirumal of the generous broad hands gleaming like a gem.

0.translit:
paarttaR kaayanRu paaratam kai seydhittu venRa paranjsudar,
koottu angu aayar tam paadiyil kuravai piNaindha vem kovalan,
eettuvaar tam manattu uLLaan ida-vendhai meviya empiraan,
tiirttha niirt taDanj soolai suuzh tiruvenkaTam adai nenjsamee.
0.meaning: The supreme effulgence who long ago, for Arjuna's sake, undertook the Bharata war with His own hands and won it; the gracious cowherd who linked arms with the cowherds in the kuravai dance there in their settlement; He who dwells in the hearts of those who praise Him, my Lord who has chosen Ida-vendhai as His abode. O my heart, reach Tiruvenkatam, ringed by sacred-watered tanks and groves.

1.translit:
tivaLum veN madhi pool tirumukatt arivai sezhum kaDal amudhinil piRandha avaLum,
ninn aagatt iruppadhum aRindhum aagilum aasai viDaaLaal,
kuvaLai am kaNNi kolli am paavai sollu nin taaL nayandh irundha ivaLai,
un manattaal en ninaindh irundhaay ida-vendhai endhai piraanee.
1.meaning: She of the radiant face like the shimmering white moon, the maiden, even Lakshmi who was born from the rich ocean of nectar—though this girl knows that Lakshmi resides upon Your chest, still she will not abandon her love for You. This girl, lovely as a doll of Kolli, with eyes like the kuvalai bloom, longs for Your feet—tell me, what have You been thinking of her in Your heart, O my Lord, my Father at Ida-vendhai?

2.translit:
tuLam paDu muRuval toozhiyarkku aruLaaL tuNai mulai saandhu koNDu aNiyaaL,
kuLam paDu kuvaLaik kaN iNai ezhudhaaL kola nan malark kuzhaRku aNiyaaL,
vaLam paDu munniir vaiyam mun aLandha maal ennum maalina mozhiyaaL,
iLam paDi ivaLukku en ninaindh irundhaay ida-vendhai endhai piraanee.
2.meaning: She gives not even a flickering smile to her companions; she does not adorn her twin breasts with sandal-paste; she does not paint her pair of eyes like kuvalai flowers of the pond; she does not deck her beautiful flowering tresses with ornaments; she speaks only love-maddened words, calling on Tirumal who once measured this world girdled by the fertile oceans. For this tender young girl, what have You been thinking, O my Lord, my Father at Ida-vendhai?

3.translit:
saandhamum pooNum sandhanak kuzhambum taDa mulaik aNiyilum tazhalaam,
poondha veN tingaL kadhir suDa meliyum poru kaDal pulambilum pulambum,
maan taLir meeni vaNNamum ponnaam vaLaigaLum iRai nillaa,
en tan eendhizhai ivaLukku en ninaindh irundhaay ida-vendhai endhai piraanee.
3.meaning: Even when she adorns her ample breasts with sandal-paste, jewels, and fragrant sandal-balm, they turn to fire; when the rays of the risen white moon scorch her she wastes away, and when the roaring sea laments she too laments; the mango-tender hue of her body has turned to gold, and her bangles will not stay a moment on her wrists. For this my well-adorned girl, what have You been thinking, O my Lord, my Father at Ida-vendhai?

4.translit:
uuzhiyil peridhaal naazhigai ennum oN suDar tuyinRadhaal ennum,
aazhiyum pulambum anRilum uRangaadh enRalum tiiyinil koDidhaam,
toozhiyoo ennum tuNai mulai arakkum sollumin en seykeen ennum,
ezhai en ponnukku en ninaindh irundhaay ida-vendhai endhai piraanee.
4.meaning: She cries that a single naazhigai (moment) is longer than an aeon; she cries that the bright sun has gone to sleep; the sea laments, the anril bird does not sleep, and the breeze is more cruel than fire, she says; 'O companion!' she cries, and she presses her twin breasts; 'Tell me, what shall I do?' she cries. For this my helpless golden girl, what have You been thinking, O my Lord, my Father at Ida-vendhai?

5.translit:
oodhilum un peer anRi maRROodhaaL urugum nin tiru uru ninaindhu,
kaadhanmai peridhu kaiyaRavuDaiyaL kayal neDum kaN tuyil maRandhaaL,
peedhaiyeen peedhai piLLaimai peridhu teLLiyaL vaLLi nuN marungul,
eedhalar munnaa en ninaindh irundhaay ida-vendhai endhai piraanee.
5.meaning: Even when she utters, she utters nothing but Your name; she melts as she contemplates Your sacred form; her love is vast and she is utterly helpless; she has forgotten the sleep of her long carp-like eyes. The innocent daughter of foolish me is full of childlike artlessness yet so clear-minded, with a slender creeper-like waist. Even before hostile gossips, what have You been thinking of her, O my Lord, my Father at Ida-vendhai?

6.translit:
tan kuDikkeedhum takkavaa ninaiyaaL taDam kaDal nuDangu eyil ilangai,
van kuDi maDanga vaaL amar tolaittha vaarttai keettu inbuRum mayangum,
min koDi marungul surunga mel nerungi men mulai pon payandh irundha,
en koDi ivaLukku en ninaindh irundhaay ida-vendhai endhai piraanee.
6.meaning: She gives no thought to what is fitting for her own family's honour; hearing the tale of how You destroyed in fierce sword-battle the mighty households of Lanka with its swaying ramparts by the vast sea, she rejoices and swoons. Her waist slender as a lightning-creeper grows thinner, while above, her soft breasts crowd close and have turned pale-gold. For this my creeper-like girl, what have You been thinking, O my Lord, my Father at Ida-vendhai?

7.translit:
uLam kanindh irukkum unnaiyee pidhaRRum unakku anRi enakku anbu onRu ilaLaal,
vaLam kani pozhil suuzh maaliruum soolai maayanee enRu vaay veruvum,
kaLam kani muRuval kaarigai peridhu kavalaiyoDu avalam seerndh irundha,
iLam kani ivaLukku en ninaindh irundhaay ida-vendhai endhai piraanee.
7.meaning: Her heart melts and she babbles of none but You; she has not a particle of love for me, only for You; she raves aloud, 'O Mayan of Maaliruncholai ringed with groves rich in ripe fruit!' This beautiful girl with a smile like a ripe gem, deep in worry and grief, this tender unripe fruit—what have You been thinking of her, O my Lord, my Father at Ida-vendhai?

8.translit:
alangu ezhu taDak kai aayan vaay aambaRku azhiyumaal en uLLam ennum,
pulam kezhu poru niirp puTkuzhi paaDum poodhumoo niirmalaikku ennum,
kulam kezhu kolli komaLa vallik koDi iDai neDu mazhaik kaNNi,
ilam kezhil tooLikku en ninaindh irundhaay ida-vendhai endhai piraanee.
8.meaning: 'My heart breaks for the flute at the mouth of that cowherd of broad swaying hands,' she says; she sings of Putkuzhi by its fields and rippling waters, and asks, 'Shall we go to Nirmalai?' This girl of the noble Kolli line, a delicate flowering creeper with a vine-slender waist and long rain-cloud eyes—for this radiantly beautiful-shouldered one, what have You been thinking, O my Lord, my Father at Ida-vendhai?

9.translit:
pon kulaam payalai puuttana en tooL poru kayal kaN tuyil maRandhaaL,
anbinaal un meel aadharam peridhu iv aNanginukku uRRa nooy aRiyeen,
min kulaa marungul surunga mel nerungi viingiya vana mulaiyaaLukku,
en kolaam kuRippil en ninaindh irundhaay ida-vendhai endhai piraanee.
9.meaning: Golden pallor has blossomed upon my shoulders, she says; she has forgotten the sleep of her fighting-carp eyes; out of love her longing for You is immense, yet I cannot fathom the affliction that has seized this lovely girl. Her lightning-like waist grows ever more slender, while above her full beautiful breasts press close. What may be Your intent—what have You been thinking of her, O my Lord, my Father at Ida-vendhai?

10.translit:
annamum miinum aamaiyum ariyum aaya em maayanee aruLaay,
ennum in toNDarkku in aruL puriyum ida-vendhai endhai piraanai,
mannu maa maaDa mangaiyar talaivan maana vel kaliyan vaay oligaL,
panniya panuval paaDuvaar naaLum pazhavinai paRRaRuppaaree.
10.meaning: 'O our Mayan who became the swan, the fish, the tortoise, and the man-lion, grant Your grace!'—to the devotees who thus call out, my Lord, my Father at Ida-vendhai, bestows His sweet grace. Those who sing the well-wrought poems that flowed from the mouth of Kaliyan of the proud spear, chief of the Mangai people of enduring great mansions, shall day by day sever the grip of their old karma.

11.translit:
kaar aar maNi niRak kaNNan uur viNNagaram
siir aar kaNapuram seeRai tiruvazhundhuur
kaar aar kuDandhai kaDigai kaDalmallai
eer aar pozhil suuzh ida-vendhai niirmalai
siir aarum maaliruunjsoolai tirumoogur (37)
11.meaning: The towns of Kannan, dark-hued like a sapphire gem laden with rain-clouds—Vinnagaram, glory-filled Kanapuram, Cherai, Tiruvazhundur, cloud-capped Kudandai, Kadigai, Kadalmallai (Mahabalipuram), Ida-vendhai ringed by beautiful groves, Nirmalai, the glory-rich Maaliruncholai, and Tirumogur. (37)

12.translit:
pinnai maNaaLanai peeril piRappiliyai
tol niirk kaDal kiDandha tooLaa maNic suDarai
en manattu maalai ida-vendhai iisanai
mannum kaDalmallai maayavanai vaanavar tam (63)
12.meaning: The bridegroom of Nappinnai; the birthless One of Perumal-koyil (Peril); the radiant uncut-gem effulgence who reclines upon the ancient-watered ocean; Tirumal who dwells in my heart; the Lord of Ida-vendhai; the Mayavan of enduring Kadalmallai (Mahabalipuram); He who is the Lord of the celestials. (63)

0.translit:
paaraaya thuNdumizhntha pavaLath thooNaip paadukadali lamuthaththaip parivaaykeeNda seeraanai,
emmaanaith thoNdar thangaL sinthaiyuLLE muLaiththezhuntha theengarumpinai,
pooraanaik komposiththa poorERRinaip puNarmarutha miRanadantha poRkunRinai,
kaaraanai yidark kadintha kaRpagaththaik kaNdathunaan kadalmallaith thalasayanaththE.
0.meaning: I beheld Him who is the coral pillar that swallowed and then spewed forth the wide earth, the nectar churned from the roaring milk-ocean, the glorious One who lovingly split open Hiranya; my Lord, the sweet sugarcane that sprouted up within the hearts of His devotees; the warring bull that broke the tusk of the elephant, the golden hill that walked between and passed through the twin marutha trees; the wish-granting Kalpaka tree that drove away the distress of the dark elephant Gajendra — I beheld Him at Kadalmallai Talasayanam.

1.translit:
pooNdavaththam piRarkkadainthu thoNdupattup poynnoolai meynnoolen RenRumOthi maaNdu,
avaththam pOgaathE vamminenthai envaNangap paduvaanai,
kaNangaLEththum neeNdavaththaik karumugilai emmaanthannai ninRavoor niththilaththaith thoththaarsOlai,
kaaNdavaththaik kanaleri vaayp peyviththaanaik kaNdathunaan kadalmallaith thalasayanaththE.
1.meaning: Take refuge in others to no avail, become enslaved, forever chanting false scriptures as though they were true, and perish in waste — do not go thus to ruin, but come, O kinsmen, to Him who is worshipped when I bow! He is the dark cloud whom the celestial hosts praise, the lofty truth, my Lord, the pearl of Ninravur of the blossom-clustered groves, who cast the Khandava forest into blazing fire — I beheld Him at Kadalmallai Talasayanam.

2.translit:
udampuruvil moonRoRaay moorththivERaay ulaguyya ninRaanai,
anRupEychchi vidamparugu viththaganaik kanRumEyththu viLaiyaada vallaanai varaimeegaanil,
thadamparugu karumugilaith thanjaikkOyil thavaneRikkOr peruneRiyai vaiyangaakkum,
kadumparimEl kaRkiyainaankaNdukoNdEn kadipozhilsoozh kadanmallaith thalasayanaththE.
2.meaning: He stood for the salvation of the world as the three forms united in one body yet distinct in deity; the clever One who that day drank the poison of the demoness Putana, who could herd the calves and play His games; the dark cloud that drinks from the lakes upon the high hills and forest, the Lord of Thanjai temple, the great path for the way of penance, who will protect the world mounted on a swift horse as Kalki — I found and beheld Him at Kadalmallai Talasayanam, surrounded by fragrant groves.

3.translit:
pEyththaayai mulaiyuNda piLLaithannaip piNaimaruppil karungaLiRRaip piNaimaannOkkin,
aayththaayar thayirveNNe yamarnthakOvai anthaNarthamamuthaththaik kuravaimunnE kOththaanai,
kudamaadu kooththanRannaik kOgulangaL thaLaraamal kunRamEnthik kaaththaanai,
emmaanaik kaNdukoNdEn kadipozhilsoozh kadalmallaith thalasayanaththE.
3.meaning: The Child who drank from the breast of the false-mother demoness; the dark elephant with curved tusks; the cowherd-king who delighted in the curd and butter of the doe-eyed cowherd-women; the nectar of the Brahmins, who long ago joined the maidens in the kuravai dance; the dancer who danced with the pots, who lifted up the hill so the cattle-herds would not falter and sheltered them — my Lord, I found and beheld at Kadalmallai Talasayanam, surrounded by fragrant groves.

4.translit:
paaynthaanaith thirisakadam paaRiveezhap paalaganaa yaalilaiyil paLLiyinpam Eynthaanai,
ilangoLisEr maNikkunRanna eeriraNdu maalvaraiththO LemmaanRannai,
thOynthaanai nilamagaLthOL thoothiRchenRap poyyaRaivaayp pugappeytha mallarmangak kaayththaanai,
emmaanaik kaNdukoNdEn kadipozhilsoozh kadalmallaith thalasayanaththE.
4.meaning: He who leapt so that the rolling cart-demon Sakatasura shattered and fell; who as an infant enjoyed the bliss of sleeping upon the banyan leaf; my Lord with four great mountain-like shoulders, gleaming like a radiant gem-hill; who embraced the shoulders of the Earth-maiden; who went as a messenger and, casting the wrestlers into the deceitful hall, destroyed them so they perished — my Lord, I found and beheld at Kadalmallai Talasayanam, surrounded by fragrant groves.

5.translit:
kidanthaanaith thadangadaluL paNangaLmEvik kiLarppoRiya maRithiriya vathaninpinnE padarnthaanaip,
padumathaththa kaLiRRinkompu paRiththaanaip paaridaththai eyiRukeeRa idanthaanai,
vaLaimaruppi nEnamaagi irunilanum peruvisumpu meythaavaNNam kadanthaanai,
emmaanaik kaNdukoNdEn kadipozhilsoozh kadalmallaith thalasayanaththE.
5.meaning: He who reclines upon the wide ocean amid the hoods of the bright-speckled serpent, and then rose up and spread forth; who tore off the tusk of the rut-flowing elephant; who as the boar with curved tusks dug up the Earth, cleaving it with His tusk; who strode across so that both the vast earth and the great sky could not contain Him — my Lord, I found and beheld at Kadalmallai Talasayanam, surrounded by fragrant groves.

6.translit:
pENaatha valiyarakkar meliyavanRu peruvaraiththO LiRanerithan RavuNarkkOnai,
pooNaagam piLaveduththa pOrvallOnaip porukadaluL thuyilamarntha puLLoorthiyai
ooNaagap pEymulainanj suNdaan thannai uLLuvaa ruLLaththE yuRaiginRaanai,
kaaNaathu thiritharuvEn kaNdukoNdEn kadipozhilsoozh kadanmallaith thalasayanaththE.
6.meaning: He who that day crushed the mighty arrogant demons so they grew weak, breaking the great mountain-shoulders of the demon-king Hiranya; the warrior who split open the jeweled chest of the asura; who rides the bird Garuda and sleeps upon the surging ocean; who drank the poison of the demoness's breast as His food; who dwells within the hearts of those who meditate on Him — wandering about unable to see Him, at last I found and beheld Him at Kadalmallai Talasayanam, surrounded by fragrant groves.

7.translit:
peNNaagi yinnamutham vanjiththaanaip piRaiyeyiRRan Radalariyaayp peruginaanai,
thaNNaarntha vaarppunalsoozh meyyamennum thadavaraimEl kidanthaanaip paNangaLmEvi,
ennaanai yeNNiRantha pugazhinaanai ilangoLisE raravintham pOnRuneeNda
kaNNaanai, kaNNaarak kaNdukoNdEn kadipozhilsoozh kadalmallaith thalasayanaththE.
7.meaning: He who became a woman (Mohini) and bewitchingly carried off the sweet nectar; who that day grew vast as the fierce man-lion Narasimha with crescent-like fangs; who reclines upon the great hill called Meyyam, surrounded by cool flowing streams, upon the serpent's hoods; my elephant-Lord of measureless glory, whose long eyes resemble the radiant gleaming lotus — Him I beheld to my eyes' content at Kadalmallai Talasayanam, surrounded by fragrant groves.

8.translit:
thoNdaayar thaamparavu madiyinaanaip padikadantha thaaLaaLaR kaaLaayuythal viNdaanai,
thennilangai yarakkarvEnthai vilanguNNa valangaivaaych charangaLaaNdu,
paNdaaya vEthangaL naankumainthu vELvigaLum kELviyO dangamaaRum kaNdaanai,
thoNdanEn kaNdukoNdEn kadipozhilsoozh kadalmallaith thalasayanaththE.
8.meaning: He whose feet the devotees praise, who released salvation to those who become servants of the mighty-footed One who strode across the worlds; who wielded the arrows of His right hand so that the king of the demons of southern Lanka was devoured by beasts; who revealed the four ancient Vedas, the five sacrifices, and the six auxiliary disciplines together with sacred learning — I, the devotee, found and beheld Him at Kadalmallai Talasayanam, surrounded by fragrant groves.

9.translit:
padanaagath thaNaikkidanthan RavuNarkOnaip padaveguNdu maruthidaippOyp pazhanavEli,
thadamaarntha kadalmallaith thalasayanaththuth thaamaraikkaN thuyilamarntha thalaivanthannai,
kadamaarum karungaLiRu vallaanvelpOrk kalikanRi yoliseytha inpappaadal,
thidamaaga vivaiyainthu mainthumvallaar theevinaiyai muthalariya vallaarthaamE.
9.meaning: The Lord who reclines upon the bed of the hooded serpent, who that day raged so the demon-king fell, who passed between the marutha trees; who rests in lotus-eyed sleep at Kadalmallai Talasayanam, set amid fields and lakes — of Him, Kalikanri (Tirumangai Alwar), master of the dark rutting elephant and victor in war, has composed this sweet song. Those who can firmly master these ten verses, all together, shall be able to destroy their evil deeds at the root.

10.translit:
naNNaatha vaaLavuNa ridaippukku, vaanavaraip
peNNaagi yamuthoottum perumaanaar, maruviniya
thaNNaarntha kadanmallaith thalasayanath thuRaivaarai,
eNNaathE yiruppaarai yiRaippozhuthu meNNOmE.
10.meaning: The Lord who entered into the midst of the unapproachable sword-bearing demons and, becoming a woman, fed the celestials the nectar — He who dwells at the sweet, cool Kadalmallai Talasayanam: those who remain without thinking of Him, we shall not think of even for a single moment.

11.translit:
paarvaNNa madamangai paninanmaa malarkkizhaththi,
neervaNNan maarvagaththi lirukkaiyaimun ninainthavanoor,
kaarvaNNa muthumunneerk kadalmallaith thalasayanam,
aareNNum nenjudaiyaa ravaremmai yaaLvaarE.
11.meaning: The Earth-hued gentle maiden and the lady of the cool fine lotus (Lakshmi) — He recalled first their dwelling upon the chest of the ocean-hued Lord. That town of His is the cloud-hued ancient Kadalmallai Talasayanam by the deep sea. Whoever has a heart that fully contemplates it — they shall rule over us.

12.translit:
Enaththi nuruvaagi nilamangai yezhilkoNdaan,
vaanaththi lavarmuRaiyaal magizhnthEththi valangoLLa,
kaanaththin kadalmallaith thalasayanath thuRaiginRa,
nyaanaththi noLiyuruvai ninaivaaren naayagarE.
12.meaning: He who took the form of the boar and recovered the beauty of the Earth-maiden, whom those in the heavens joyfully praise in due order and circumambulate — He dwells at the forest-girt Kadalmallai Talasayanam. Those who meditate upon that radiant form of wisdom are my masters.

13.translit:
viNdaarai venRaavi vilanguNNa, melliyalaar
koNdaadum mallagalam azhalERa venjamaththuk
kaNdaarai, kadalmallaith thalasayanath thuRaivaarai,
koNdaadum nenjudaiyaa ravarengaL kulatheyvamE.
13.meaning: He who conquered His foes so their lives were devoured by beasts, who in fierce battle made the strong chest praised by the tender-natured women burn with fire as He gazed upon His enemies — He who dwells at Kadalmallai Talasayanam: those who have a heart that celebrates Him are our family deity.

14.translit:
pichchach chiRupeelich chamaNkuNdar muthalaayOr,
vichchaik kiRaiyennu mavviRaiyaip paNiyaathE,
kachchik kidanthavanoor kadanmallaith thalasayanam,
nachchith thozhuvaarai nachchenRan nannenjE.
14.meaning: Without bowing to that lord whom the lowly Jains with their little peacock-feather fans and others call the master of their learning — instead, the town of Him who reclines at Kanchi is Kadalmallai Talasayanam. O my good heart, desire those who lovingly worship there.

15.translit:
pulankoLnithik kuvaiyOdu puzhaikkaimaa kaLiRRinamum
nalangoLnava maNikkuvaiyum sumanthekkum naanRosinthu,
kalangaLiyang kummallaik kadalmallaith thalasayanam,
valangoLmanath thaaravarai valangoLLen madanenjE.
15.meaning: Where ships move about laden and bending low everywhere, bearing heaps of captivating treasures, herds of trunk-handed great elephants, and piles of fine nine gems — that Kadalmallai Talasayanam of bustling Mallai. O my simple heart, circumambulate those whose minds devotedly circle it.

16.translit:
panjich chirukoozhai yuruvaagi, maruvaatha
vanjappeN nanjuNda aNNalmun naNNaatha,
kanjaik kadanthavanoor kadanmallaith thalasayanam,
nenjil thozhuvaaraith thozhuvaayen thooynenjE.
16.meaning: The Lord who drank the poison of the deceitful, hostile woman (Putana) with her cotton-soft little tresses — He who overcame the unapproaching Kamsa; His town is Kadalmallai Talasayanam. O my pure heart, worship those who worship Him in their hearts.

17.translit:
sezhuneer malarkkamalam thiraiyuntha vanpagattaal,
uzhuneer vayaluzhava ruzhappinmun pizhaiththezhuntha,
kazhuneer kadikamazhum kadanmallaith thalasayanam,
thozhuneer manaththavaraith thozhuvaayen thooyanenjE.
17.meaning: Where the lush flowering lotuses are pushed by the waves, and the cluster-lilies that escaped and rose up before the plowing of the farmers who till the watered fields with strong oxen spread their fragrance — that Kadalmallai Talasayanam. O my pure heart, worship those whose minds are devoted to worshipping it.

18.translit:
piNangaLidu kaadathanuL nadamaadu pinjnyaganOdu,
iNanguthiruch chakkaraththem perumaanaark kidam,visumpil
kaNangaLiyang kummallaik kadanmallaith thalasayanam,
vaNangumanath thaaravarai vaNangenRan madanenjE.
18.meaning: The abode of our Lord, who bears the sacred discus and is in concord with Pinnagan (Shiva), who dances in the cremation-ground strewn with corpses, is Kadalmallai Talasayanam of Mallai, where hosts of celestials move about in the sky. O my simple heart, bow to those whose minds bow there.

19.translit:
kadikamazhu nedumaRugil kadalmallaith thalasayanaththu,
adigaLadi yEninaiyu madiyavargaL thammadiyaan,
vadikoLnedu vElvalavan kalikanRi yolivallaar,
mudikoLnedu mannavartham muthalvarmutha laavaarE.
19.meaning: At Kadalmallai Talasayanam with its long fragrant streets, Kalikanri (Tirumangai Alwar), wielder of the sharp long spear, the servant of the servants of those devotees who think only of the Lord's feet, has composed this song. Those who can master it shall become the foremost among the foremost of crowned great kings.

20.translit:
sangu thangu thadang-kadal kadan-mallai yuL kidanthaay
aruL purinthu ingu ennuL pugunthaay ini poyin aalaraiyo,
kongu seNbaka mallikai malar pulgi in iLa vaNdu poy,
iLam thengin thaadhu aLaiyum thiruvaali ammaane.
20.meaning: O Lord who reclined within the deep sea of Kadalmallai where conches abide, having shown your grace you have entered into me here — now where could you go and depart? O Lord of Tiruvali, where the young bees, after embracing the fragrant champaka and jasmine blossoms, go and stir the pollen of the tender coconut palm.

21.translit:
puL vaay piLantha punithaa endru azhaikka,
uLLe nindru en uLLam kuLirum oruvaa,
kaLvaa kadan-mallaik kidantha karumbe,
vaLLaal unnai engnganam naan marakkene.
21.meaning: When I call out, 'O pure one who split open the beak of the demon-bird,' you, the peerless one, stand within and cool my heart. O cunning one, O sugarcane that reclines at Kadalmallai, O generous benefactor — how could I ever forget you?

22.translit:
piNdiyaar maNdai enthip piRar manai thiritharthu uNNum,
uNdiyaan saabam theerttha oruvanoor, ulagam eettum
kaNdiyoor arangam meyyam kacchi per mallai endru
maNdinaar, uyyal allaal matraiyaark ku uyyal aame?
22.meaning: It is the town of the One who removed the curse of him (Shiva) who, holding a skull as a begging-bowl, wandered into others' houses to beg and eat. Those who throng eagerly saying 'Kandiyur, Arangam (Srirangam), Meyyam, Kacchi (Kanchi), Per, Mallai' — praised by the world — they alone shall be saved; how can salvation be possible for others?

23.translit:
vangatthaal maamaNi vanthu unthu munneer
 mallaiyaay madhiL kacchi yooraay! peraay,
kongatthaar vaLam kondrai alangal maarvan
 kulavaraiyan madappaavai idappaal kondaan,
pangatthaay paarkadalaay paarin melaay
 panivaraiyin ucchiyaay paLava vaNNaa,
engutraay emperumaan unnai naadi
 ezhaiyen inganame oozhi tharugene.
23.meaning: O Lord of Mallai, where the ocean's ships push forth great gems; O Lord of walled Kanchi, O Lord of Per! O coral-hued one — you who shared half your body with the gentle maiden, daughter of the king of mountains, he (Shiva) who wears upon his chest the lush garland of golden konrai blossoms; O Lord of the milk-ocean, O Lord above the earth, O Lord upon the peak of the snowy mountain! Where are you, my Lord? Seeking you, wretched as I am, must I pass an age here in this way?

24.translit:
kaar aar maNi niRak kaNNanoor viNNagaram
seer aar kaNapuram seRai thiruvazhunthoor
kaar aar kudanthai kadigai kadal-mallai
er aar pozhil soozh idaventhai neermalai
seer aarum maaliruncholai thirumogoor
24.meaning: Vinnagaram, the town of Kannan whose hue is like the dark rain-cloud-gemstone; glorious Kanapuram, Sherai, Tiruvazhundur; cloud-touching Kudandai, Kadigai, Kadalmallai; Idavendai surrounded by lovely groves, Neermalai; the glory-filled Maliruncholai, and Tirumogur.

25.translit:
pinnai maNaaLanai peril piRappiliyai
thol neer kadal kidantha thoLaa maNic chudarai
en manatthu maalai idaventhai eesanai
mannum kadal-mallai maayavanai vaanavar tham
25.meaning: The bridegroom of Nappinnai, the birthless one of Per, the radiant unpierced gem who reclines upon the ancient watery sea; Tirumal who dwells in my mind, the Lord of Idavendai, the wondrous one of enduring Kadalmallai, and of the celestials.

26.translit:
thamar uLLam thanjai thalaiyarangam thaNkaal,
thamar uLLum thaNporuppu velai, - thamar uLLum
maamallai koval madhiL kudanthai enbare,
e valla enthaikku idam.
26.meaning: The devotees' hearts, Tanjai, Talaiyarangam, Tankal; the cool hill (Tirumalirumcholai) cherished by the devotees, the sea (Tiruppparkadal); and cherished by the devotees — Mamallai, Koval, walled Kudandai — these, they say, are the abodes of my Father who is capable of all deeds.

0.translit:
mikkaanai maraiyaai virintha viLakkai ennuL
pukkaanaip pugazhseer poliginRa ponmalaiyai
takkaanaik kadigaith thadangunRin misaiyiruntha
akkaarak kaniyai adainthuuyndhu pOnEnE.
0.meaning: I reached and was saved by Him who is supreme, who shines forth expanded as the Vedas, the lamp that has entered into me; the golden mountain that gleams crowned with glory; the worthy Lord who dwells upon the great hill of Kadigai (Tirukkadigai/Sholingur); the fruit sweet as rock-sugar. Having attained Him, I am redeemed and saved.

1.translit:
kaNNaar kaNNapuram kadigai kadikamazhum
thaNNaar thaamaraisoozh thalaichchanga mEldhisaiyuL
viNNOr naaNmadhiyai viriginRa venjudarai
kaNNaarak kaNdukoNdu kaLikkinRadhing kenRukolO.
1.meaning: He is at Kannapuram that fills the eyes, at Kadigai, at fragrant Thalaichchangam in the northwest girded by cool lotuses; He is the rising moon of the celestials, the blazing radiant sun that spreads forth. When, oh when, shall it be that I behold Him to my eyes' content and rejoice here?

2.translit:
kaar aar maNi niRak kaNNanoor viNNagaram
seer aar kaNapuram sERai thiruvazhundhoor
kaar aar kudandhai kadigai kadalmallai
Er aar pozhil soozh idavendhai neermalai
seer aarum maalirunjsOlai thirumOgoor
2.meaning: Kannanur clothed in the dark cloud-hued gem, Vinnagaram, glorious Kanapuram, Serai, Tiruvazhundur; cloud-wreathed Kudandai (Kumbakonam), Kadigai, Kadalmallai (Mahabalipuram); Idavendai surrounded by beautiful groves, Neermalai; glory-filled Maliruncholai (Azhagar Koyil), and Tirumogur.

3.translit:
paNdellaam vEngadam paaRkadal vaikuntham,
koNdang kuRaivaarkkuk kOyilpOl, - vaNdu
vaLangkiLarum neeLsOlai vaNpoong kadigai,
iLangkumaran RanviN Nagar.
3.meaning: In times of old Venkatam, the Ocean of Milk, and Vaikunta were as temples where He took up His abode for those who sought refuge there; now the young youthful Lord's Vinnagar is bountiful, fragrant, blossoming Kadigai, ringed by long groves where bees swarm in abundance.

0.translit:
thoovaaya puLLoorndhu, vandhu thuRaivEzham
moovaamai nalgi mudhalai thuNiththaanai
dhEvaadhi dhEvanaich chengamalak kaNNaanai
naavaayuLaanai naRaiyooril kaNdEnE.
0.meaning: Mounting the bird with mighty wings, He came and saved the elephant at the ghat from old age and death, severing the crocodile. Him who is the God of gods, who has lovely red-lotus eyes, who dwells in Tirunaavaay—Him I beheld at Naraiyoor.

1.translit:
kamba maagaLi Ranjik kalanga, Or
kombu koNda kuraikazhal kooththanai
kombu laampozhil kOttiyoork kaNdupOy
namba naichchenRu kaNdumnaa vaayuLE.
1.meaning: The Dancer with sounding anklets who broke off one tusk so that the trembling great elephant cried out in terror—Him I saw at Kottiyoor of groves where branches sway, and then, going on, I beheld my trustworthy Lord at Naavaay too.

2.translit:
aRukkumvinaiyaayina aagandhavanai
niRuththummanath thondRiya sindhaiyinaarkku
veRiththaNmalarchchOlaigaLsoozh thirunaavaay
kuRukkumvagaiyuNdukOlO kodiyERkE.
2.meaning: He cuts away the karmas that have become one's very body. For those whose minds are settled and fixed upon Him in steady thought—is there a way that I, cruel-hearted one, may draw near to Tirunaavaay, girt with cool fragrant flower-groves?

3.translit:
kodiyEridaik kOganaththavaLkELvan
vadivElthadangaN madappinnaimaNaaLan
nediyaanuRaichOlaigaL soozh thirunaavaay
adiyEnaNugapperunaaL evaikovO.
3.meaning: He is the consort of her of the lotus (Lakshmi), slender as a creeper; He is the bridegroom of the gentle Pinnai of wide eyes like sharp spears. When shall be the day that I, His servant, may draw near to Tirunaavaay—girt with groves where the tall One abides?

4.translit:
evaikOlaNugapperunaa LendRappOdhum
kalaibilmanaminRik kaNNeergaLkaluzhvan
navaiyil thinaaraNansEr thirunaavaay
avaiyuLpugalaavadhOr naaLaRiyEnE.
4.meaning: 'When will the day come that I may draw near?'—even as I say this, my mind without learning grows restless and my eyes brim and stream with tears. I do not know the day on which I may enter into that assembly of faultless Tirunaavaay where Narayana abides.

5.translit:
naaLElaRiyEn enakkuLLanaanum
meeLaavadimaip paNiseyyappugundhEn
neeLaarmalarchchOlaigaLsoozh thirunaavaay
vaaLEythadangaN madappinnaimaNaaLaa.
5.meaning: I know no fixed day; yet I, with all that is within me, have entered upon doing the service of an irredeemable bondsman. O bridegroom of the gentle Pinnai whose wide eyes shine like swords, You of Tirunaavaay girt with tall, abundant flower-groves!

6.translit:
maNaaLanmalarmangaikkum maNmadandhaikkum
kaNNaaLanulagaththuyil dhEvarkatkellaam
vaNNaaLanvirumbiRaiyum thirunaavaay
kaNNaarakkaLikkindRadhu ingendRukolkaNdE.
6.meaning: He is the bridegroom of the Lady of the flower (Lakshmi) and of the Lady Earth; He is the dear one (the very eye) of all the high celestials of the world; the beautiful Lord who lovingly abides in Tirunaavaay. When, oh when, shall I rejoice here, beholding Him to my eyes' full content?

7.translit:
kaNdEkaLikkindRadhu ingendRukolkaNgaL
thoNdEpunakkaayozhindhEn thurisindRi
vaNdaarmalarchchOlaigaLsoozh thirunaavaay
koNdE yuRaikindRa engOvalarkOvE.
7.meaning: When, oh when, shall my eyes rejoice here, beholding Him? Without flaw I have become wholly given over to service. O King of our cowherds, who dwell taking as Your own the Tirunaavaay girt with flower-groves humming with bees!

8.translit:
kOvaagiya maavaliyainilangoNdaay
dhEvaasuramsetRavanE thirumaalE
naavaayuRaikindRa ennaaraNanambi
aavaalavadiyaani nendRaruLaayE.
8.meaning: You who took the earth from Mahaabali who was a king! O You who destroyed (the demons in) the war of gods and asuras, O Tirumal! My Narayana, the perfect One who dwells in Naavaay—'Alas, alas, he is My servant,' so saying, grant me Your grace.

9.translit:
aruLaadhozhivaayaruLseydhu adiyEnaip
poruLaakki unponnadikkeezhpugavaippaay
maruLEyindRi unnaiyennenjaththiruththum
dhEruLEtharu thenthirunaavaayendhEvE.
9.meaning: Whether You withhold Your grace, or showing grace make me, Your servant, of some worth and set me beneath Your golden feet—either way, grant that, free of all delusion, I may enshrine You within my heart. O my God who dwells in beautiful southern Tirunaavaay!

10.translit:
dhEvar munivarkkendRum kaaNdaRkariyan
moovarmudhalvan orumoovulagaaLi
dhEvanvirumbi yuRaiyum thirunaavaay
yaavaraNugapperuvaar iniyandhO.
10.meaning: He is ever hard to behold even for the celestials and the sages; He is the first cause of the three (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva), the sole ruler of the three worlds. Alas, who henceforth shall be able to draw near to Tirunaavaay where that God lovingly abides?

11.translit:
andhOvaNugapperunaaL endReppOdhum
sindhaikalangith thirumaavendRazhaippan
kondhaarmalarchchOlaigaLsoozh thirunaavaay
vandhEyuRaikindRa emmaamaNivaNNaa.
11.meaning: 'Alas, when will the day come that I may draw near?'—at every moment my mind grows distraught, and I cry out 'O Tirumal!' O our great sapphire-hued Lord, who have come and abide in Tirunaavaay, girt with clustering flower-groves!

12.translit:
vaNNammaNimaada nannaavaayuLLaanai
thiNNammadhiL thenkurugoorchchadagOpan
paNNaarthamizh aayiraththippaththumvallaar
maNNaaNdu maNangamazhvarmallikaiyE.
12.meaning: Of Him who dwells in good Naavaay of beautiful jewelled mansions, Satakopan of southern Kurugoor with its strong ramparts (sang). Those who master these ten of the tuneful thousand Tamil verses shall rule the earth and spread fragrance like the jasmine flower.

0.translit:
tharu thuyaram thadaayel un sharanallaal sharanillai
virai kuzhuvu malarp pozhil soozh viRRuvakkOTTu ammaanE
ari sinaththaal eenRa thaay agaRRiDinum maRRavaL than
aruL ninaindhE azhum kuzhavi adhuvE ponRu irundhEnE
0.meaning: O Lord of Vittuvakkodu, surrounded by fragrant blossoming groves where flowers cluster thick! If you do not ward off the sorrow that comes upon me, there is no refuge for me but your feet. Even when the mother who bore me, in her fierce anger, casts me away, I have become like that infant who, remembering only her grace, weeps for her still.

1.translit:
kandaar igazhvanavE kaadhalan thaan seydhiDinum
kondaanai allaal aRiyaak kulamagaL pOl
viN thOy madhiL pudai soozh viRRuvakkOTTu ammaa nee
kondaaLaay aagilum un kurai kazhalE kooRuvanE
1.meaning: Like a high-born woman who knows none but her wedded husband, even though her beloved does what onlookers would despise, O Lord of Vittuvakkodu girdled by walls that touch the sky, even if you take me as yours or do not, I shall speak only of your resounding anklet-adorned feet.

2.translit:
meen nOkkum neeL vayal soozh viRRuvakkOTTu ammaa en
paal nOkkaay aagilum un paRRallaal paRRu illEn
thaan nOkkaadhu eth thuyaram seydhiDinum thaar vEndhan
kOl nOkki vaazhum kudi ponRu irundhEnE
2.meaning: O Lord of Vittuvakkodu, surrounded by vast fields where fish dart about! Even if you do not turn your gaze toward me, I have no support other than your support. Though the garlanded king, not looking upon them, inflicts whatever suffering he will, I have become like the subjects who go on living looking only to his sceptre.

3.translit:
vaaLaal aRuththuch chuDinum maruththuvan paal
maaLaadha kaadhal nOyaaLan pOl maayaththaal
meeLaath thuyar tharinum viRRuvakkOTTu ammaa nee
aaLaa unadhu aruLE paarppan adiyEnE
3.meaning: Like the patient whose unwavering love for his physician does not die even when he is cut with the knife and cauterized, O Lord of Vittuvakkodu, even if through your maya you give me unending sorrow, I, your servant, shall look only to your grace, longing to be taken into your service.

4.translit:
vem kaN thiN kaLiRu adarththaay viRRuvakkOTTu ammaanE
engup pOy uygEn un iNaiyadiyE adaiyal allaal
engum pOyk karai kaaNaadhu eRi kadal vaay meeNDEyum
vangaththin koombu ERum maap paRavai ponREnE
4.meaning: O Lord of Vittuvakkodu, who subdued the fierce-eyed, mighty elephant! Where else can I go and be saved, except by reaching your twin feet? I have become like the great bird that flies everywhere over the surging sea, and finding no shore anywhere, returns again to perch upon the mast of the ship.

5.translit:
sen thazhalE vandhu azhalaich cheydhiDinum sem kamalam
andharam sEr vem kadhirORku allaal alaraavaal
ven thuyar veeTTaaviDinum viRRuvakkOTTu ammaa un
andham il seerkku allaal agam kuzhaiya maaTTEnE
5.meaning: Just as the red lotus blooms for none but the fierce-rayed sun that rides the sky, even though scorching flames come and sear it, so too, O Lord of Vittuvakkodu, even if you do not release me from my burning sorrow, my heart will melt for nothing but your endless glory.

6.translit:
eththanaiyum vaan maRandha kaalaththum paingoozhgaL
maiththu ezhundha maa mugilE paarththirukkum maRRavai pOl
meyth thuyar veeTTaaviDinum viRRuvak
kOTTu ammaa en siththam miga un pOlE vaippan adiyEnE
6.meaning: Just as the green crops, even in a season when the sky has wholly forgotten them, keep gazing only at the great dark-risen cloud, so too, O Lord of Vittuvakkodu, even if you do not release me from my real sorrow, I, your servant, shall fix my heart entirely upon you alone.

7.translit:
thokku ilangi aaRellaam parandhu Odi thodu kadalE
pukku anRip puRam niRka maaTTaadha maRRavai pOl
mikku ilangu mugil niRaththaay viRRuvakkOTTu ammaa un
pukku ilangu seerallaal pukkilan kaaN puNNiyanE
7.meaning: Just as all the rivers, gathering and gleaming, spread and run only to enter the deep sea and can find no rest outside of it, so too, O abundantly resplendent one of cloud-dark hue, Lord of Vittuvakkodu, O righteous one, behold: I have no refuge to enter save your radiant glory.

8.translit:
ninnaiyE thaan vENdi neeL selvam vENdaadhaan
thannaiyE thaan vENdum selvam pOl maayaththaal
minnaiyE sEr thigiri viRRuvakkOTTu ammaanE
ninnaiyE thaan vENdi niRpan adiyEnE
8.meaning: Like the wealth that comes seeking of its own accord to the one who, desiring you alone, desires not vast riches, O Lord of Vittuvakkodu who bears the discus that holds the lightning's flash, I, your servant, shall stand desiring you, and you alone.

9.translit:
viRRuvakkOTTu ammaa nee vENdaayE aayiDinum
maRRaarum paRRu illEn enRu avanaith thaaL nayandha
koRRa vEl thaanaik kulasEkaran sonna
naR Tamizh paththum vallaar naNNaar naragamE
9.meaning: "O Lord of Vittuvakkodu, even if you do not want me, I have no other refuge" - thus longing for his feet, Kulasekara of the victorious spear and great army has sung these ten verses of good Tamil. Those who can master them shall never come near hell.

0.translit:
urukumaal nenjam uyir aan paramanRi
perukumaal veeTkaiyum en seykeen toNDaneen
teruvellaam kaaligazh tirukkaaTkarai
maruviya maayanRan maayam ninaitoRee.
0.meaning: My heart melts away beyond bearing, and beyond the loss of my very life my longing keeps swelling — what shall I do, I who am His servant? This happens each time I think upon the wondrous deeds of the Maayan who has settled in Tirukkatkarai, whose streets are all crowded with cattle.

1.translit:
ninaitoRum solluntoRum nenju iDintukum
vinaikoL seerpaaDilum veem enadu aaruyir
kanaikoL pooncoolait ten kaaTkarai ennappaa
ninakileen naan unakku aaTceyyum neermaiyee.
1.meaning: Each time I think of You and each time I speak of You, my heart crumbles and dissolves; even when I sing Your auspicious deeds my dear life burns. O my Father of southern Katkarai with its dense flowering groves, I cannot even conceive of the manner in which I might render service to You.

2.translit:
neermaiyaal nenjam vanjittup pugundu ennai
eermai seydu en naay iraay en uyir uNDaan
seer malgu coolait ten kaaTkarai ennappan
kaarmugil vaNNanRan kaLvam aRigileen.
2.meaning: With seeming graciousness He deceived my heart and entered in, melted me, and devouring my very life held me as His own. I cannot fathom the cunning of my Father of southern Katkarai with its groves abounding in splendor, He who is hued like a dark rain-cloud.

3.translit:
aRigileen tannuL anaittulagum niRka
neRimaiyaal taanum avaTRuL niRkumpiraan
veRi kamazh coolait ten kaaTkarai ennappan
siRiya en naay iruNDa tiruvaruLee.
3.meaning: I cannot comprehend it — within Himself all the worlds abide, and by His own ordering He too abides within them, the Lord. I cannot grasp the sacred grace of my Father of southern Katkarai with its fragrance-spreading groves, who melted into lowly me.

4.translit:
tiruvaruL seybavan poola en uL pugundu
uruvamum aaruyirum uDanee uNDaan
tiruvaLar coolait ten kaaTkarai ennappan
karu vaLar meeni en kaNNan kaLvangaLee.
4.meaning: As though one bestowing sacred grace He entered within me and at once consumed both my body and my dear life together. Such are the wiles of my Kannan of dark-growing form, my Father of southern Katkarai with its prosperity-swelling groves.

5.translit:
en kaNNan kaLvam enakkuc cemmaay niRkum
an kaNNan uNDa en aaruyirkku oodu idu
punkaNmai edi pulambi iraappagal
en kaNNan enRu avan kaaTkarai eettumee.
5.meaning: My Kannan's stealth stands fair and right before me; for my dear life which that lovely-eyed One consumed there is this longing. Stricken with sorrow, lamenting night and day, crying 'My Kannan!', it praises His Katkarai.

6.translit:
kaaTkarai eettum adanuL kaNNaa ennum
veeTkai nooy koora ninaindu karaindukum
aaTkoLvaan ottu en uyir uNDa maayanaal
kooT kuRaipaTТadu en aaruyir kooL uNDee.
6.meaning: It praises Katkarai, and within it cries 'O Kannan!'; as the malady of longing grows intense, thinking of Him it dissolves and melts away. By the Maayan who, as though to take me into His service, consumed my life, my dear life has been seized and made deficient, captured.

7.translit:
kooL uNDaan anRi vandu en uyir taan uNDaan
naaLu naaL vandu ennai muRRavum taan uNDaan
kaaLa neer meegat ten kaaTkarai ennappaRku
aaL anRee paTТadu en aaruyir paTТadee.
7.meaning: Not content with capturing it, He came and consumed my very life; day after day He came and consumed me wholly and entirely. To my Father of southern Katkarai, dark as a water-laden cloud, has my dear life not become a servant? — this is what has befallen it.

8.translit:
aaruyir paTТadu enadu uyir paTТadu
peer idazht taamaraikkaN kanivaay adoor
kaar ezhil meegat ten kaaTkarai kooyilkoL
seer ezhil naal tadan tooL deyva vaarikkee.
8.meaning: My dear life has become subject, my life has become subject — to that divine, all-engulfing One with eyes like the great-petalled lotus and ripe-red lips, dark and beautiful as a cloud, who has taken His temple at southern Katkarai, of splendid, lovely four broad shoulders.

9.translit:
vaarik koNDu unnai vizhunguvan kaaNil enRu
aarvaTТa ennai ozhiya ennil munnam
paarittu taan ennai muRRap paruginaan
kaar okkum kaaTkarai appan kaDiyanee.
9.meaning: Thinking 'If I see you I shall scoop you up and swallow you whole', even before I, full of yearning, could do so, He made ready and drank me up entirely first. The Father of Katkarai, like unto a dark cloud, is swift and fierce indeed.

10.translit:
kaDiyanaay kanjanaic conRa piraanRannai
koDi madiL ten kurugoorc caDagoopan sol
vaDivu amai aayirattu ip pattinaal sanmam
muDivu eydi naasam kaNDeergaL em kaanalee.
10.meaning: Of that Lord who, fierce and swift, slew Kamsa, these are the words of Satakopan of southern Kurugur with its banner-decked ramparts. By these ten verses of the well-formed thousand, behold — your births shall come to an end and meet destruction, O people of our seaside groves.

0.translit:
paNiyeey parangkundrin pavaLath thiraLee,
muniyee thirumoozhik kaLaththu viLakkee,
iniyaay thoNdaroom paruginn amudhaaya
kaniyee unnaikkaNdu koNduyndh thozhindheenee!
0.meaning: O coral cluster of Parankunram that shines like a serpent's hood! O sage, O lamp of Tirumoozhikkalam! O sweet One who became the nectar that we devotees drink! O fruit, having beheld You I have been redeemed and saved.

1.translit:
ponnaanaay. pozhilezhum kaaval pooNda
  pugazhaanaay igazhvaaya thoNda neennaan,
ennaanaay? ennaanaay? ennal allaal
 ennariva nezhaiyeen, ulaga meththum
thennaanaay vadavaanaay kudapaa laanaay
 guNapaala thaayinaay imaiyoork kendrum
munnaanaay pinnaanaar vaNangkum soodhi
thirumoozhik kaLaththaanaay mudhalaanaayee.
1.meaning: You became golden in hue; You took up the guardianship of all the seven worlds and won fame. I, a contemptible servant, can only cry out 'What have You become to me? What have You become to me?' for I am ignorant and helpless. You who are praised by the world, You became the southern one, the northern one, the western one, and the eastern one; for the celestials You are ever the foremost. O Light whom those who come after worship, You who dwell at Tirumoozhikkalam, You are the very first cause.

2.translit:
munnavanai moozhikkaLaththu viLakkinai
annavanai aadhanoor aaNdu aLakkum aiyanai
nennalai indrinai naaLaiyai neermalaimeel
2.meaning: He who is the primordial One, the lamp of Moozhikkalam, He who is such a One, the Lord who rules and measures Adanur, He who is yesterday, today, and tomorrow, and who is upon Tiruneermalai.

3.translit:
engkaanal agangkazhivaay iraidheernidhinidhamarum
sengkaala madanaaraay thirumuzhikkaLaththuRaiyum
kongkaarpoondhuzhar mudi yengkudak kooththarken thoodhaay
mungkaalgaL en thalaimeel kezhumiroo numaroodee.
3.meaning: O tender red-legged crane that searches for prey within the backwaters of our seashore grove and dwells here happily! Be my messenger to my Lord, the dancer of the western land, who wears a crown of fragrant honey-laden blossoms and dwells at Tirumoozhikkalam. Set your front feet upon my head and unite there together with your kin.

4.translit:
numaroodum piriyaadhee neerum num seevalumaay
amar kaadhal kuruginangkaaL aNimuzhikkaLaththuRaiyum
emalaarum pazhippuNdu ingen? thammaalizhippuNdu
thamaroodangkuRaivaarkkuth thakkilamee keeLeeree.
4.meaning: O flocks of herons who, never parting from your kin, abide in deep love together with your mates! Why should we, who are reproached by all our people and demeaned by Him, remain blamed here? Hear me: we are not fit to dwell yonder with His own people at beautiful Tirumoozhikkalam.

5.translit:
thakkilamee keeLeergaL thadampunalvaay iraidheerum
kokkinangkaaL kuruginangkaaL kuLirmoozhikkaLaththuRaiyum
sekkamalarth thavarpoolum kaN kai kaal sengkanivaay
akkamalath ilaipoolum thirumeeni adigaLukkee.
5.meaning: Hear me: we are not fit! O flocks of cranes and herons that search for prey in the broad waters! For the Lord who dwells at cool Tirumoozhikkalam, whose eyes, hands and feet are like red lotus blossoms, whose lips are like ripe red fruit, and whose sacred body is like the lotus leaf.

6.translit:
thirumeeni adigaLukkuth theevinaiyeen vidudhoodhaay
thirumoozhikkaLamennum semunagarvaay aNimugilgaaL
thirumeeniyavadkaruLeer endrakkaal ummaith tham
thirumeeniyoLi yagatrith theLivisumbu kadiyumee.
6.meaning: O lovely clouds gathered over the fair city called Tirumoozhikkalam, be the messenger sent by me of cruel deeds to the Lord of the sacred form! If you plead, 'Grant Your grace to her, for her sake, O sacred body,' then He will bestow upon you the radiance of His own sacred form and clear the bright sky for you.

7.translit:
theLivisumbu kadidhoodith theevaLaiththu minnilagum
oLimugilgaaL thirumuzhikkaLaththuLaiyum oNsudarkku
theLi visumbu thirunaadaath theevinaiyeen manaththuRaiyum
thuLivaarkat kuzhalaarkku en thoodhuraiththal seppuminee.
7.meaning: O bright clouds that race swiftly across the clear sky, ringed with fire and flashing with lightning! To the radiant Lord who dwells at Tirumoozhikkalam, who holds the clear sky as His sacred realm yet abides in the heart of me of cruel deeds, the Lord with dripping cool dark locks, tell my message, I pray, and convey it.

8.translit:
thoodhuraiththal seppuminkaL thoomozhivaay vaNdinangkaaL
poo dhiraiththu madhunugarum pozhilmuzhikkaLaththuRaiyum
maadharaith tham maarvagaththee vaiththaarkken vaaymaatram
thoodhuraiththal seppudhireel sudarvaLaiyum kalaiyumee.
8.meaning: O flocks of bees of pure sweet humming! Speak and bear my message to Him who dwells in the grove-filled Tirumoozhikkalam where you scatter through the blossoms and sip honey, He who has placed His Lady within His own chest. If you will speak and bear the words of my mouth as a message, then my bright bangles and my garment shall be saved.

9.translit:
sudarvaLaiyum kalaiyumkoNda aruvinaiyeen thoorhuRandha
padarpugaazhaan thirumoozhikkaLaththuRaiyum pangkayakkaN
sudarpavaLavaayanaikkaNdu orunaaLoor thooymaatram
padarpozhilvaay kuruginangkaaL enakkondru paNiyeeree.
9.meaning: O flocks of herons in the spreading groves! He who has taken away my bright bangles and my garment, the Lord of far-spreading fame, who dwells at Tirumoozhikkalam, with lotus eyes and a radiant coral mouth, having beheld Him, do me this one favor: speak for me one pure message some day.

10.translit:
enakkondru paNiyeergaL irumpozhivaay iraidheernidhu
manakkinbam padameevum vaNdinangkaaL thumbigaaL
kanakkoL thiNmadhiirpadaisoozh thirumoozhikkaLaththuRaiyum
punakkoL kaayaameenip poondhazhaaymudiyaarkkee.
10.meaning: Do me this one favor: O flocks of bees and dragonflies who, searching for prey in the dark groves, gather there to your heart's delight! Bear it to the Lord who dwells at Tirumoozhikkalam encircled by strong dense ramparts, He whose body is dark like the wild kaaya blossom and who wears a crown of fragrant tulasi.

11.translit:
poondhuzhaaymudiyaarkkup ponnaazhikkaiyaarukku
eendhuneer iLangkurugee thirumuzhikkaLaththaarukku
eendhupooNmulaipayandhu en iNaimalark kaNNeerthadhumba
thaandhammaikkoNdagaldhal thagavandreendru uraiyeeree.
11.meaning: O tender heron of the gathered waters! To the Lord who wears a crown of fragrant tulasi, who holds the golden discus in His hand, the Lord of Tirumoozhikkalam, go and tell Him: 'My adorned breasts have grown pale and tears brim from my paired flower-like eyes; for You to take my heart and then depart is not worthy of You.'

12.translit:
thagavandreendru uraiyeergaL thadampunalvaay iraidheernidhu
migalinbam padameevum mennadaiya vannangkaaL
migameeni meliveydhi meegalaiyum idazhindhu en
agameeni ozhiyaamee thirumuzhikkaLaththaarkkee.
12.meaning: Say to Him, 'It is not worthy of You!' O soft-gaited swans who, searching for prey in the broad waters, gather there to your great delight! Tell the Lord of Tirumoozhikkalam, lest my inner being perish, that my body has grown exceedingly thin and my girdle has slipped away.

13.translit:
ozhilindrith thirumoozhikkaLaththuraiyum oNsudarai
ozhivillaa aNimazhalaik kiLimozhiyaalai vatriyasol
vazhuvillaa vaNkugoorch chadagoopan vaayndhuraiththa
azhivillaa aayiraththu ippandhum koyanukkumee.
13.meaning: Upon the radiant Lord who dwells unfailingly at Tirumoozhikkalam, Satakopan of bounteous Kurukur, free of all flaw, fittingly uttered these words in the sweet incessant parrot-like tender speech. Of his thousand imperishable verses, these ten are also for the Lord.

0.translit:
tantai-taay makkaLee suRRam enRu uRRuvar paRRi ninRa
pantam aar vaazhkkaiyai nontu nee pazhi enak karuti naayeel
antam aay aati aay aatikkum aati aay aayan aaya
maintanaar vallavaazh sollumaa vallai aay maruvu neñjee!
0.meaning: O heart!
