If, growing weary, you regard as blameworthy this bondage-bound life clung to by those who claim 'father, mother, children, kin' as your own, then strive to be able to say the name of Vallavazh, the abode of the Lord who is the end, the beginning, and the very beginning of all beginnings, who became the cowherd (Krishna). Cling to that name, O heart!

1.translit:
minnum aa valliyum vañjiyum venRa nuN idai nudangum,
annam men nadaiyinaar kalaviyai aruvaruttu añji naayeel,
tunnum aa maNi mudip pañjavarkku aaki mun tootu senRa
mannanaar vallavaazh sollumaa vallai aay maruvu neñjee.
1.meaning: O heart! If, in revulsion and fear, you shun the union with women of swan-soft gait whose slender waists sway, surpassing the gleaming lightning, the creeper, and the cane, then strive to be able to say the name of Vallavazh, abode of the King (Krishna) who once went as envoy for the five Pandavas wearing close-set gem-crowns. Cling to that name, O heart!

2.translit:
pooN ulaa men mulaip paavaimaar poyyinai meyyitu enRu,
peeNuvaar peesum ap peeccai nee pizhai enak karuti naayeel
neeL nilaa veN kudai vaaNanaar veeLviyil maN iranta
maaNiyaar vallavaazh sollumaa vallai aay maruvu neñjee.
2.meaning: O heart! If you regard as error the talk spoken by those who cherish doll-like women with soft, ornament-laden breasts, taking their falsehood for truth, then strive to be able to say the name of Vallavazh, abode of the celibate youth (Vamana) who begged for land at the sacrifice of Bana of the long, moonlit white parasol. Cling to that name, O heart!

3.translit:
paN ulaam men mozhip paavaimaar paNai mulai aNaitum naam enRu,
eNNuvaar eNNam atu ozhittu nee pizhaittu uyyak karuti naayeel,
viNNuLaar viNNin meet iyanRa veengkadattuLaar vaLam koL munneer
vaNNanaar vallavaazh sollumaa vallai aay maruvu neñjee.
3.meaning: O heart! If, casting off the thought of those who scheme 'we shall embrace the full breasts of doll-like women of soft, melodious speech,' you resolve to escape error and be saved, then strive to be able to say the name of Vallavazh, abode of the Lord who dwells on Venkatam set above the heavens of the celestials, whose hue is the rich ocean. Cling to that name, O heart!

4.translit:
mañju tooy veN kudai mannar aay vaaraNam soozha vaazhntaar
tuñjinaar enpatu oor sollai nee tuyar enak karuti naayeel
nañju tooy kongkai meel am kai vaay vaitta avaL naaLai uNda
mañjanaar vallavaazh sollumaa vallai aay maruvu neñjee.
4.meaning: O heart! If you take as sorrow the saying that those who lived as kings with cloud-touching white parasols, surrounded by elephants, have died, then strive to be able to say the name of Vallavazh, abode of the cloud-dark Lord (Krishna) who, setting His lovely mouth and hand upon the poison-smeared breast of Putana, drank away her very lifespan. Cling to that name, O heart!

5.translit:
uruvinaar piRavi seer oon poti narampu tool kurampai uL pukku
aruvi nooy seytu ninRu aivar taam vaazhvataRku añji naayeel
iruvinaar veetam naangku aintu tee veeLviyodu angkam aaRum
maruvinaar vallavaazh sollumaa vallai aay maruvu neñjee.
5.meaning: O heart! If you fear that the five senses, having entered this hut of flesh, sinew and skin that belongs to embodied birth, cause flowing diseases and live on within you, then strive to be able to say the name of Vallavazh, abode of the Lord cherished by those endowed with the four Vedas, the five sacred fires, and the six auxiliary sciences (angas). Cling to that name, O heart!

6.translit:
nooy elaam peytatu oor aakkaiyai mey enak koNdu vaaLaa
peeyar taam peesum ap peeccai nee pizhai enak karuti naayeel,
tee alaa veng katirt tingkaL aay mangkul vaan aaki ninRa
maayanaar vallavaazh sollumaa vallai aay maruvu neñjee.
6.meaning: O heart! If you regard as error the empty talk spoken by deluded folk who take as real this body into which every disease has been poured, then strive to be able to say the name of Vallavazh, abode of the wondrous Lord (Maya) who stands as fire, as the fierce-rayed sun and moon, and as the cloudy sky. Cling to that name, O heart!

7.translit:
mañju seer vaan eri neer nilam kaal ivai mayangki ninRa
añju seer aakkaiyai araNam anRu enRu uyyak karuti naayeel,
santu seer men mulaip pon malarp paavaiyum taamum naaLum
vantu seer vallavaazh sollumaa vallai aay maruvu neñjee.
7.meaning: O heart! If, knowing this body formed of the confused mingling of the five elements—cloud-bearing sky, fire, water, earth, and wind—to be no true refuge, you resolve to be saved, then strive to be able to say the name of Vallavazh, where the Lord and the golden, flower-like Lady (Lakshmi) of sandal-anointed soft breasts come and abide each day. Cling to that name, O heart!

8.translit:
veLLiyaar piNdiyaar pootiyaar enRu ivar ootukinRa
kaLLa nool tannaiyum karumam anRu enRu uyyak karuti naayeel,
teLLiyaar kai tozhum teevanaar maa muneer amutu tanta,
vaLLalaar vallavaazh sollumaa vallai aay maruvu neñjee.
8.meaning: O heart! If you resolve to be saved, deeming that the deceitful doctrines recited by these—the Saivas (of the silver), the Jains (of the asoka tree), and the Buddhists (of the bodhi tree)—are not worthy deeds, then strive to be able to say the name of Vallavazh, abode of the generous Lord, the God worshipped by the clear-minded, who gave forth the nectar from the great ocean. Cling to that name, O heart!

9.translit:
maRai valaar kuRaivu ilaar uRaiyum oor vallavaazh adikaL tammai,
siRai kulaa vaNdu aRai soolai soozh koola neeL aali naadan
kaRai ulaa veel valla kaliyan vaay oli ivai kaRRu vallaar
iRaivar aay iru nilam kaaval pooNdu inpa nanku eytuvaaree.
9.meaning: Those who learn and master these melodies from the mouth of Kaliyan—lord of the beautiful, vast Ali country girt with groves where winged bees hum, skilled with the bloodied spear—sung upon the Lord of Vallavazh, the abode where masters of the Vedas, the flawless ones, dwell, shall become lords, take up the guardianship of this wide earth, and attain good and excellent bliss.

10.translit:
annam tuyilum aNi neer vayal aali
ennudaiya in amutai evvuL peru malaiyai
kanni matiL soozh kaNamangkaik kaRpakattai
minnai iru sudarai veLLaRaiyuL kal aRai meel
ponnai marakatattai putkuzhi em poor eeRRai
mannum arangkattu em maa maNiyai vallavaazhp
10.meaning: He is my sweet nectar at Ali of lovely watered fields where swans sleep; the great hill at Evvul; the wish-granting tree at Kanamangai girt with maiden-fresh ramparts; the lightning and the twin luminaries (sun and moon); the gold upon the rock-shrine at VeLLaRai; the emerald; my battling bull at Putkuzhi; my great gem at ever-enduring Srirangam; and at Vallavazh—

11.translit:
maan eey nokku nalleer! vaikalum vinaiyeen meliya,
vaan aar vaN kamukum matu mallikaiyum kamazhum,
teen aar soolaikaL soozh tiruvallavaazh uRaiyum
koonaarai adiyeen adi kooduvatu enRu koloo?
11.meaning: O fair ones with eyes like a doe's! As I, the sinful one, waste away day by day—when will I, His servant, attain the feet of the Lord who dwells in Tiruvallavazh, encircled by honey-rich groves where sky-touching lush areca palms and the sweet jasmine spread their fragrance? When shall it be?

12.translit:
enRu kol toozhimeerkaaL emmai neer nalintu en seyteeroo?
pon tikazh punnai makizh putu maatavi meet aNavi,
tenRal maNam kamazhum tiruvallavaazh nakaruL
ninRa piraan, adi neeRu adiyoom koNdu sooduvatee?
12.meaning: When, O friends? Why do you torment me—what would you do with me? When shall we, His servants, take and wear upon ourselves the dust of the feet of the Lord who stands in the town of Tiruvallavazh, where the southern breeze, brushing over the golden punnai, the makizh, and the fresh madhavi blooms, spreads its fragrance?

13.translit:
soodum malark kuzhaleer! tuyar aaTTiyeenai meliya,
paadu nal veeta oli paravait tirai pool muzhangka,
maadu uyarntu ooma pukai kamazhum taN tiruvallavaazh
needu uRaikinRa piraan kazhal kaNdum kol niccalumee?
13.meaning: O women who wear flowers in your tresses! As I, the sorrow-stricken one, waste away—will I ever, day after day, behold the anklet-adorned feet of the Lord who long abides in cool Tiruvallavazh, where the chant of the good Veda resounds like the surging waves of the sea, and where the homa-sacrifice smoke wafts fragrant up over the lofty surroundings?

14.translit:
niccalum toozhimeerkaaL! emmai neer nalintu en seyteeroo?
paccilai neeL kamukum palavum tengkum vaazhaikaLum,
macc aNi maadangkaL meet aNavum taN tiruvallavaazh
nacc aravu in aNai meel nam piraanatu nan nalamee.
14.meaning: Day after day, O friends, why do you torment me—what would you do with me? It is the goodness and grace of our Lord who reclines upon the bed of the venomous serpent (Adisesha) in cool Tiruvallavazh, where green-leaved tall areca palms, jackfruit, coconut, and plantain trees rise up over the upper-storied mansions adorned with terraces.

15.translit:
nan nalat toozhimeerkaaL. nalla antaNar veeLvip pukai,
mai nalam koNdu uyar viN maRaikkum taN tiruvallavaazh,
kannal am kaTTi tannaik kaniyai in amutam tannai,
en nalam koL sudarai enRu kol kaNkaL kaaNpatuvee?
15.meaning: O good and gracious friends! When will my eyes behold that radiance which has taken my well-being—who is like a lump of sweet sugar-candy, a ripe fruit, sweet nectar—dwelling in cool Tiruvallavazh, where the sacrificial smoke of the good brahmins, dark in its richness, rises to hide the high heavens?

16.translit:
kaaNpatu eñ ñaanRu kolo vinaiyeen kani vaay madaveer,
paaN kural vaNdinodu pasun tenRalum aaki engkum,
seeN sinai oongku marac sezhung kaanal tiruvallavaazh,
maaN kuRaL koolap piraan malart taamaraip paatangkaLee?
16.meaning: O tender women of fruit-red lips! When, O when, will I, the sinful one, behold the lotus-flower feet of the comely Lord—the noble dwarf (Vamana)—at Tiruvallavazh of rich seaside groves where tall trees with far-reaching branches spread everywhere amid humming bees of song-like voice and the cool fresh southern breeze?

17.translit:
paatangkaL meel aNi poo tozhak koodum kol paavai nalleer,
oota nedun tadattuL uyar taamarai sengkazhuneer,
maatarkaL vaaN mukamum kaNNum eentum tiruvallavaazh,
naatan iñ ñaalam uNda nam piraan tannai naadooRumee?
17.meaning: O lovely women like dolls! Will it ever be granted to worship, offering beautiful flowers upon His feet, the Lord of Tiruvallavazh—where in the deep wave-filled tanks the lofty lotuses and red lilies present, as it were, the radiant faces and eyes of fair women—our Lord who swallowed this world, day after day?

18.translit:
naaL tooRum veedu inRiyee tozhak koodum kol nan nutaleer,
aadu uRu teeng karumpum viLai sennelum aaki engkum,
maadu uRu poon tadañ seer vayal soozh taN tiruvallavaazh,
needu uRaikinRa piraan nilam taaviya neeL kazhalee?
18.meaning: O fair-browed women! Will it ever be granted to worship without cease, day after day, the long feet that strode across the earth—of the Lord who long abides in cool Tiruvallavazh, encircled by fields where everywhere the swaying sweet sugarcane and ripening red paddy flourish, set with lovely flower-filled tanks all around?

19.translit:
kazhal vaLai poorippa yaam kaNdu kai tozhak koodum kolo,
kuzhal enna yaazhum enna kuLir soolaiyuL teen arunti,
mazhalai vari vaNdukaL isai paadum tiruvallavaazh,
suzhalin mali sakkarap perumaanatu taaL aruLee?
19.meaning: Will it be granted that we, with bangles loosening (in joy of love), behold and worship with folded hands the grace of the feet of the great Lord bearing the whirling, abundant discus, at Tiruvallavazh, where in the cool groves the lisping striped bees, drinking honey, sing music like the flute and like the lute?

20.translit:
tholaruL nalvinaiyaal sollak kooDungkol thoozhimeerkaaL,
thollaruL maNNum viNNum thozhaninRa thirunagaram,
nallaru Laayiravar nalanEndhum thiruvallavaazh,
nallaruL namperumaan naaraayaNan naamangaLE?
20.meaning: O my dear companions! By the ancient grace and good deeds, will it ever be possible for us to utter them — the names of our gracious Lord Narayana of Tiruvallavazh, that sacred city which both earth and heaven, by His ancient grace, stand worshipping, where thousands of devotees full of goodly grace hold up His glory?

21.translit:
naamanga Laayira muDaiyanamperu maanaDimEl,
sEmangoL thenkurukoorch chaDakOpan therindhuraiththa,
naamanga Laayiraththu Livaipaththum thiruvallavaazh,
sEmangoL thennagarmEl seppuvaarsiRan thaarpiRandhE.
21.meaning: Upon the feet of our Lord who possesses a thousand names, Satakopan of beautiful southern Kurukur, who attained security, discerned and uttered these. Among the thousand sacred verses, those who recite these ten upon Tiruvallavazh, the secure southern city, will be born exalted and supreme.

0.translit:
konguN kuDandaiyum kOTTiyoo rumpEErum
eNGgum tirindu viLaiyaaDum enmaganQ
saNGgam piDikkum taDakkaikkut takkanil
aNGga muDaiyadoor kOlkoNDuvaa
 arakku vazhittadoor kOlkoNDuvaa
0.meaning: My son roams everywhere and plays — in Kongu, in Kudandai, in Kottiyur and in Per. Bring a staff fit for his large hand that holds the conch; bring a staff of suitable size and shape, a staff smoothed and lacquered. (Yashoda speaks of the child Krishna.)

1.translit:
koNDalvaN NaaiNGgE pOda raayEE
 kOyiRpiL Laai-iNGgE pOda raayEE
teNdirai soozhtirup pEErkkiDanda
 tirunaara NaaiNGgE pOda raayEE
uNDuvan dEEn-amma nenQru solli
 OoDi akampuka aaicci taanum
kaNDedi rEEsenQ ReDuttuk koLLak
 kaNNabi raankaRRa kalvi taanEE.
1.meaning: O cloud-hued one, come here! O child of the temple, come here! O Tirunarayana who reclines at Tiruppēr girt by clear waves, come here! Saying ‘Mother, I have eaten and come,’ he ran and entered the house, and when Yashoda came facing him and caught him up — such was the learning that Kannan, our Lord, had mastered.

2.translit:
kaiyilaNG kaazhi saNGgan karumukil tiruni Rattan,
poyyilan meyyan tantaaL aDaivarEE laDimai yaakkum,
seyyalar kamala mONGgu seRipozhil tendi ruppEEr
paiyara vaNaiyaan naamam paravinaa nuyndavaaRE,
2.meaning: In His hand shine the conch and the discus; His sacred form is the hue of a dark cloud; He is free of falsehood, the true one. If men reach His feet He makes them His servants. Praising the name of the Lord who reclines on the hooded serpent at Tendiruppēr — where dense groves rise full of blossoming red lotuses — I have been saved.

3.translit:
vaNGgamaar kaDalka LEzhum malaiyumvaa nakamum maRRum,
aNGgaNmaa NYaala mellaam amudusey duvizhnda endai,
tiNGgaLmaa mukila Navu seRipozhil tendi ruppEEr,
eNGgaLmaa liRaivan naamam EEttinaa nuyndavaaRE,
3.meaning: My Father who swallowed up and then spat forth the seven ship-filled seas, the mountains, the heavens and all the rest — all this wide beautiful earth. Extolling the name of our great Lord Mal who dwells at Tendiruppēr, where dense groves rise so high that moon and great clouds graze them, I have been saved.

4.translit:
oruvanai yundip poomEEl OoNGguvit taakan tannaal,
oruvanaic caapam neekki umparaaL, enQru viTTaan,
peruvarai madiLkaL soozhnda perunakar arava NaimEEl
karuvarai vaNNan tenpEEr karudinaa nuyndavaaRE,
4.meaning: By the power of His body He raised one (Brahma) high upon the lotus of His navel; and freeing another (Shiva) from his curse He sent him forth saying ‘rule over the celestials.’ Meditating on Tenpēr, the great city ringed by walls upon vast hills, the abode on the serpent-couch of the Lord dark as a mountain, I have been saved.

5.translit:
oonamar talaiyon REndi ulakelaam tiriyu meesan
eenamar saapam neekkaai, ennavoN punalai yeendaan,
tEEnamar pozhilkaL soozhnda seRivayal tendi ruppEEr,
vaanavar talaivan naamam vaazhttinaa nuyndavaaRE,
5.meaning: When the lord (Shiva), who wandered through all the worlds bearing a fleshless skull, prayed ‘remove this base curse,’ He granted the pure water (that cleansed it). Hailing the name of the chief of the celestials at Tendiruppēr — ringed by honey-laden groves and dense paddy fields — I have been saved.

6.translit:
vakkaran vaaymun keeNDa maayavanEE enQru vaanEEr
pukku, araN tanda ruLvaai, ennappon naakat taanai,
nakkari yuruva maaki nakaNGgiLarn diDandu kanda,
sakkarac celvan tenpEErt talaivantaa LaDainduyn dEEnEE,
6.meaning: When the celestials entered and prayed ‘O wondrous one who once split open the mouth of Vakkaran (Hiranya), grant us refuge and grace,’ He, becoming the form of the fierce man-lion (Narasimha), rose up and with bristling claws gladly tore open the golden-bodied (Hiranyakashipu). Reaching the feet of that lord of Tenpēr, the wealthy holder of the discus, I have been saved.

7.translit:
vilaNGgalaal kaDala Daittu viLaNGgizhai poruTTu, villaal,
ilaNGgaimaa nakarkki Raivan irubadu puyamtu Nittaan,
nalaNGgoLnaan maRaival laarkaL ottoli yEEttak kEETTu
malaNGgupaay vayalti ruppEEr maruvinaan vaazhndavaaRE,
7.meaning: For the sake of her of shining jewels (Sita), He dammed the sea with mountains, and with His bow severed the twenty shoulders of the king of great Lanka. Hearing the masters of the four noble Vedas chant praise in unison, I dwelt at Tiruppēr where eels leap in the fields, and so I have lived (been blessed).

8.translit:
veNNeytaa namudu seyya vekuNDumat taaicci yOcci,
kaNNiyar kuRuNGga yiRRaal kaTTaveT TenQRi rundaan,
tiNNamaa madiLkaL soozhnda tentirup pEEruL, vEElai
vaNNanaar naamam naaLum vaaymozhin duyndavaaRE,
8.meaning: When He had eaten the butter, the cowherd-woman in anger raised the churning-rod and bound Him with a short rope, and He sat there crestfallen. Daily uttering with my mouth the name of the ocean-hued Lord at Tentiruppēr, ringed by firm great walls, I have been saved.

9.translit:
amponaa rulaka mEzhum aRiya-aaip paaDi tannuL,
kombanaar pinnai kOlam kooDudaR RERu konQraan,
sempponaar madiLkaL soozhnda tentirup pEEruL mEEvum,
empiraan naamam naaLum EEttinaa nuyndavaaRE,
9.meaning: As all seven worlds of fine gold looked on, within Ayppadi He slew the bulls to win the beauty of Pinnai, slender as a creeper. Daily extolling the name of our Lord who abides at Tentiruppēr, ringed by walls of pure red gold, I have been saved.

10.translit:
naalvakai vEda maindu vEELviyaa RaNGgam vallaar,
mEElaivaa navarin mikka vEEdiya raadi kaalam,
sEElukaL vayalti ruppEErc ceNGgaNmaa lODum vaazhvaar,
seelamaa tavattar sindai yaaLiyen sindai yaanEE,
10.meaning: Those masters of the four kinds of Veda, the five sacrifices and the six auxiliary sciences — brahmins surpassing even the celestials above, who from the beginning of time live with the red-eyed Mal at Tiruppēr where carp leap in the fields — He who dwells in the hearts of those of great virtue and austerity dwells also in my heart.

11.translit:
vaNDaRai pozhilti ruppEEr variyara vaNaiyil paLLi
koNDuRai kinQra maalaik koDimadiL maaDa maNGgai,
tiNDiRal tOLka liyan senYJsolaal mozhinda maalai,
koNDivai paaDi yaaDak kooDuvaar neeLvi sumbE,
11.meaning: To the Lord Mal who abides reclining upon the striped serpent-couch at Tiruppēr of bee-humming groves, Kaliyan of mighty shoulders, of Mangai of bannered walls and mansions, has spoken this garland of sweet words. Those who take up these and sing and dance them shall attain the vast high heaven.

12.translit:
tuLakka milcuDa rai,avu Nanu Dal
piLakkum maindanaip pEEril vaNaNGgippOy
aLappi laaramu taiyama rarkkaruL
viLakkinai senQru veLLaRaik kaaNDumEE.
12.meaning: Having worshipped at Pēr the unwavering Light, the mighty one who split open the body of the demon, the boundless nectar, the lamp who graces the celestials — let us go on and behold Him at Vellarai.

13.translit:
peRRam aaLiyai pEEril maNaaLanai
kaRRa noolkali kanQRi yuraiseyda
soRRi Ramivai solliya toNDarkaTku
aRRa millaiyaN Dam-avark kaaTciyE.
13.meaning: To the Lord who tends the cattle, the bridegroom of Pēr, Kaliyan learned in the scriptures has uttered these. For the devotees who recite this skilled garland of words there will be no lack — the celestial realm shall be theirs to behold.

14.translit:
pEEsinaar piRavi neettaar pEEruLaan perumai pEEsi,
eesinaar uyndu pOnaar enpadiv vulakin vaNNam,
pEEsinEEn EEsa maaTTEEn pEEdaiyEEn piRavi neettaRku,
aasaiyO peridu koLka alaikaDal vaNNar paalEE.
14.meaning: Those who spoke of the greatness of the Lord at Pēr cast off rebirth; even those who reviled Him were saved — such is the way of this world. I have spoken; I cannot revile. Though I am foolish, my longing to cast off rebirth is great — take it so, toward the Lord hued like the surging sea.

15.translit:
piNDiyaar maNDai Endip piRarmanai tiritan duNNum,
uNDiyaan saapam teertta oruvanoor, ulaka mEEttum
kaNDiyoor araNGgam meyyam kaccipEEr mallai enQru
maNDinaar, uyyal allaal maRRaiyaark kuyya laamEE?
15.meaning: The city of the One who removed the curse of him (Shiva) who, bearing a skull, wandered begging through others’ houses to eat — those who throng saying ‘Kandiyur, Arangam, Meyyam, Kacchi, Pēr, Mallai,’ the places the world extols, find deliverance. Save by that, can deliverance be had for others?

16.translit:
neeragattaay neDuvarayi nucci mEElaay
 nilaattiNGgaL tuNDakattaay! niRainda kacci
Uuragattaay, oNDuraineer veHkaa vuLLaay
 uLLuvaa ruLLattaay, ulaka mEEttum
kaaragattaay kaarvaanat tuLLaay kaLvaa
 kaamarupooNG kaaviriyin tenpaal mannu
pEEragattaay, pEEraaden nenYJi nuLLaay
 perumaan-un tiruvaDiyE pEENinEEnEE.
16.meaning: O You who are in Tirunīragam, who are upon the peak of the lofty mountain (TiruveNkaTam), who are at Tiruttūngaṇai with the moon-fragment! O You who are at Tiruvuragam in full Kacchi, who are within Tiruvekkā of bright clear waters, who are in the hearts of those who meditate; O You whom the world extols at Tirukkāragam, who are in the dark sky, O hidden thief! O You who abide at Tiruppēragam south of the lovely flowering Kaviri, who, never leaving, are within my heart — Lord, it is Your sacred feet alone that I have cherished.

17.translit:
vaNGgattaal maamaNivan dundu munneer
 mallaiyaaymadiLkacci yooraay! pEEraay,
koNGgattaar vaLaNGgonQRai yalaNGgal maarvan
 kulavarayan maDappaavai yiDappaal koNDaan,
paNGgattaay paaRkaDalaay paarin mEElaay
 panivarayi nucciyaay pavaLa vaNNaa,
eNGguRRaay emperumaan unnai naaDi
 EzhaiyEEn iNGganamE OozhitarukEEnEE.
17.meaning: O You of Mallai where ships push in great gems on the sea, O You of walled Kacchi, O You of Pēr! O You who hold on Your one side the tender maiden, daughter of the mountain-king — He of the chest garlanded with honey-laden konrai flowers (Shiva); O You who are the Milk-Ocean, who are above the earth, who are on the snowy mountain-peak, O coral-hued one! Where have You gone, my Lord? Seeking You, wretched I pass age upon age like this.

18.translit:
muRRaaraa vanamulaiyaaL paavai maayan
 moyyakalat tuLLiruppaaL aHdum kaNDum
aRRaaL,tan niRaiyazhindaaL aavik kinQRaaL
 aNiyaraNGga maaDutumO tOzhee ennum,
peRREEnvaaic colliRaiyum pEEsak kEELaaL
 pEErppaaDit taNkuDandai nakarum paaDi,
poRRaama raikkayamnee raaDap pOnaaL
 poruvaRRaa LenmakaLum ponnum aHdE.
18.meaning: Though she sees that the doll-like maiden (Lakshmi) of full, fresh breasts dwells within the broad chest of the Wondrous One, my daughter has given herself wholly to Him, has lost her reserve, and grieves. ‘Shall we go play at lovely Arangam, friend?’ she says. Though I gave her birth, she will not heed a word I speak; singing of Pērppādi and cool Kudandai’s city she has gone to bathe in the golden-lotus pool. Peerless is my daughter, and her golden hue — it too is His.

19.translit:
aaraanum kaRpippaar naayakarEE? naan avanaik
kaar aar tirumEEni kaaNum aLavum pOy
seer aar tiruvEENgaDamEE tirukkOva
loorEE madiL kacci Ooragam-EE pEEragamEE
pEEraa marudu iRuttaan veLLaRaiyE veHkaavEE
pEErAali taNkaal naRaiyoor tiruppuliyoor
aaraamam soozhnda araNGgam kaNamaNGgai
19.meaning: Is there any teacher who is my master (that I would heed)? Until I go and behold His cloud-dark sacred form, I will go to glorious Tiruvēnkaṭam, to Tirukkōvalūr, to walled Kacchi’s Ūragam and Pēragam, to Vellarai of Him who broke the great marudu trees, to Vehkā, to great Āli, to Tankāl, Naraiyūr, Tiruppuliyūr, and to Arangam ringed by gardens, and Kaṇamaṅgai.

20.translit:
annam thuyilum aNi neer vayal aali
ennudaiya in amuthai evvuL peru malaiyai
kanni mathiL soozh kaNamangaik karpagaththai
minnai iru sudarai veLLaRaiyuL kal aRaimEl
ponnai marakathaththai putkuzhi em pOr ERRai
mannum arangaththu em maa maNiyai vallavaazhp
20.meaning: He who dwells in Tiruvāli of lovely watered fields where swans sleep, my sweet ambrosia, the great hill at EvvuL; the wish-granting tree of Kanamangai girt with maiden-fresh ramparts; the lightning and the two luminaries (sun and moon); upon the rock-ledge at Veḷḷaṟai the gold and the emerald; my battling bull at Putkuzhi; my great gem in everlasting Srirangam — and He of Vallavāzh.

21.translit:
naagath thaNaikkudanthai vehkaa thiruvevvuL,
naagath thaNaiyarangam pEranbil, - naagath
thaNaippaaR kadalkidakku maathi nedumaal,
aNaippaar karuth thanaa vaan.
21.meaning: He who reclines on the serpent-couch at Kudanthai, at Tiruvehkā, at sacred Evvuḷ, on the serpent-bed at Arangam (Srirangam) and at Pēranbil — the primordial Nedumāl who lies upon the serpent-couch on the milk-ocean — becomes the very thought of those who would embrace Him.

22.translit:
thirumaalirunjOlai malaimenREn enna
thirumaalvanthu en nenju niRaiyap pugunthaan
kurumaa maNiyunthu punalponnith thenbaal
thirumaalsenRu sErvidam then thiruppErE
22.meaning: The moment I uttered the words 'the hill Tirumālirunjōlai,' Tirumāl came and entered, filling my heart full. To the south of the Poṉṉi (Kaveri) river whose waters push along precious great gems, the place that Tirumāl goes to and reaches is southern Tiruppēr.

23.translit:
pErE uRaiginRa piraan inRu vanthu
pErEn enRu en nenju niRaiyap pugunthaan
kaarEzh kadalEzh malaiyE zhulaguNdum
aaraava yiRRaanai yadangap pidiththEnE
23.meaning: The Lord who dwells in Tiruppēr came this day and, declaring 'I will not depart,' entered and filled my heart full. Him of the never-satisfied belly, who swallowed the seven clouds, the seven seas, the seven mountains and the worlds, I have wholly grasped and held.

24.translit:
pidiththEn piRavi keduththEn piNisaarEn
madiththEn manaivaazhkkai yuLniRpathOr maayaiyai
kodikkO puramaadangaL soozhthirup pEraan
adichchErvathenenakkeLi thaayina vaaRE
24.meaning: I have grasped Him; I have destroyed rebirth; I shall not be touched by disease. I have folded away the illusion that abides within household life. How easy it has become for me to reach the feet of the Lord of Tiruppēr, ringed with bannered towers and mansions!

25.translit:
eLithaa yinavaaRen Reng kaNgaL kaLippa
kaLithaa giyasin thaiyanaayk kaLikkinREn
kiLithaa viyasOzhaigaL soozhthirup pEraan
thteLithaa giyasEN visumbutharu vaanE
25.meaning: Crying 'how easy it has become!' so that my eyes brim with joy, I rejoice with a heart steeped in delight. The Lord of Tiruppēr, surrounded by groves where parrots leap about, is the one who grants the clear, far-stretching high heaven.

26.translit:
vaanE tharuvaa nenakkaayen nOdotti
UnEy kurambai yithanuL pugunthu inRu
thaanE thadumaaRRa vinaigaL thavirththaan
thEnE pozhilthen thiruppEr nagaraanE
26.meaning: Pledging to me that He Himself would grant me heaven, He entered this very day into this hut of flesh and, of His own accord, removed the deeds that cause my wavering — He, the Lord of southern Tiruppēr Nagar whose groves drip honey.

27.translit:
thiruppEr nagaraan thirumaalirunjOlaip
poruppE uRaiginR apiraaninRuvanthu
iruppEn enRu en nenju niRaiyap pugunthaan
viruppE peRRamu tha muNdu kaLiththEnE
27.meaning: The Lord of Tiruppēr Nagar, who dwells on the mountain of Tirumālirunjōlai, came this day and, declaring 'I will abide here,' entered and filled my heart full. Gaining my heart's desire, I drank the ambrosia and rejoiced.

28.translit:
uNdu kaLiththaRkku umbaren kuRai mElaith
thoNdu kaLiththanthi thozhumsollup peRREn
vaNdu kaLikkum pozhilsoozh thiruppEraan
kaNdu kaLippak kaNNuLnin Ragalaane
28.meaning: Having drunk and rejoiced, what lack have I among the celestials above? I have received the speech to render loving service and to worship Him at eventide. The Lord of Tiruppēr, ringed with groves where bees revel, stands within my eyes for me to behold and delight in, never departing.

29.translit:
kaNNuLnin Ragalaan karuththinkaN periyan
eNNilnuN poruLE zhisaiyin suvaithaanE
vaNNanan maNimaadangaL soozhthirup pEraan
thiNNamen manaththup pugunthaan seRinthinRe
29.meaning: He stands within my eyes and does not depart; in the mind He is vast beyond reckoning. He is the subtle essence beyond counting, the very savour of the seven notes of music. The Lord of Tiruppēr, surrounded by fair gem-set mansions, has firmly entered my mind this day and pressed close within it.

30.translit:
inRennaip poruLaakkith thannaiyen nuLvaith thaan
anRennaip puRambogap puNarththathen seyvaan?
kunRennath thigazhmaadangaL soozhthirup pEraan
onRenak karuLseyya vuNarththaluR REnE
30.meaning: This day He made me into something of worth and set Himself within me. Then why, in former time, did He contrive to cast me outside? The Lord of Tiruppēr, surrounded by mansions gleaming tall as hills — that He might grace me with the one thing, I have come to make Him aware of my plea.

31.translit:
uRREn uganthu paNisey thunapaatham
peRREn eethEyin namvEN duvathenthaay
kaRRaar maRaivaaNar kaLvaazh thiruppEraaRku
aRRaar adiyaar thamakkallal nillaavE
31.meaning: I have drawn near, and serving You with gladness I have gained Your feet — this alone is yet what I desire, O my Father. For the devotees who belong wholly to the Lord of Tiruppēr, where learned masters of the Vedas dwell, no affliction can stand.

32.translit:
nillaa allal neeLvayalsoozh thiruppErmEl
nallaar palarvaazh kurugoorch chadagOpan
sollaar thamizhaa yiraththuL ivaipaththum
vallaar thoNdaraaL vathusoozhpon visumbE
32.meaning: Of these ten among the thousand eloquent Tamil verses of Caṭakōpaṉ (Nammālvār) of Kurugūr, where many good people live, sung upon Tiruppēr girt with broad fields — affliction shall not stand. Those who master them shall have the encircling golden high heaven serve them as devotees.

0.translit:
elliyum kaalaiyum thannai ninainthezha,
nalla aruLkaL namakkae thantharuLseyvaan,
alliyan thaNNan thuzhaaymudi appanoor,
selvarkaL vaazhum thirukkadithaanamae.
0.meaning: That the Lord may rouse us to meditate upon Him at night and at dawn, and graciously grant us His good and gracious blessings—this is Tirukkadithanam, the town of that Father who wears upon His crown the cool, beautiful, petalled tulasi garland, where the prosperous devotees dwell.

1.translit:
thirukkadithaanamum ennudaich chinthaiyum,
orukkaduthuLLae uraiyum piraankaNdeer,
serukkaduthanru thigaiththa arakkarai,
urukkeda vaaLi pozhintha oruvanae.
1.meaning: Behold the Lord who, drawing both Tirukkadithanam and my own mind together, dwells within them as one! He is the peerless One who, when the arrogant demons rose up bewildered in pride, poured forth His arrows until their very forms were destroyed.

2.translit:
oruvar iruvaror moovar enaninru,
uruvu karanthuL Lunthorum thiththippaan,
thiruvamar maarvan thirukkadithaanaththai,
maruvi uraikinra maayap piraanae.
2.meaning: He stands as One, as Two, as Three, hiding His form, growing ever sweeter each time He is contemplated within. He, the Lord upon whose chest Lakshmi abides, is the wondrous Lord who has lovingly entered and abides in Tirukkadithanam.

3.translit:
maayap piraanena valvinai maaynthara,
naeyaththinaal nenjam naadu kudikoNdaan,
thaesath thamarar thirukkadithaanaththai,
vaasap pozhilmannu koayilkoNdaanae.
3.meaning: As I called Him 'the wondrous Lord,' my fierce karma withered and perished, and through love He took my heart as His abiding realm and settled there. He has taken as His temple the Tirukkadithanam of the celestials of the shining world, the temple set amid fragrant groves.

4.translit:
koayilkoNdaan than thirukkadithaanaththai,
koayilkoNdaan athanaedumen nenjagam,
koayilkoL theyvamel laam thozha, vaikuntham
koayilkoNda kudak kooththa ammaanae.
4.meaning: He has taken His Tirukkadithanam as His temple; together with it He has taken my heart too as His temple. He is the Lord of the pot-dance, who—while all the temple-dwelling gods worship Him—has taken Vaikuntham as His temple.

5.translit:
kooththa ammaan kodiyaen idar mutravum,
maaytha ammaan mathusootha ammaanurai,
pooththa pozhilthaN thirukkadithaanaththai,
aethanil laakuRik koNdamin idarae.
5.meaning: The Lord of the dance, the Lord who utterly destroyed all the sufferings of cruel me, the Lord who slew Madhu—He dwells in the cool Tirukkadithanam of blossoming groves. Take care to praise it without ceasing, and your sorrows will vanish.

6.translit:
koNdamin idarkeda vuLLaththuk koavinthan,
maNviN muzhuthum aLanthavoN thaamarai,
maNNavar thaam thozha vaanavar thaam vanthu,
naNNu thirukkadithaana nagarae.
6.meaning: Take Govindan into your heart that your sorrows may perish—He whose bright lotus feet measured the whole of earth and sky. This is the city of Tirukkadithanam, worshipped by the people of earth and approached by the celestials who come down to it.

7.translit:
thaana nagarkaL thalaisiranth engengum,
vaanin nilamkadal mutrumem maayaRkae,
aana vidaththumen nenjum thirukkadith
thaana nagarum, thanathaayap pathiyae.
7.meaning: Though the foremost of all towns everywhere, and the sky, the earth, the sea and all that is, belong to our wondrous Lord, yet my heart and the city of Tirukkadithanam are His own ancestral, inherited domain.

8.translit:
thaayap pathigaL thalaisiranth engengum,
maayaththinaal manni veetrirun thaanurai,
thaeyath thamarar thirukkadithaanaththuL,
aayark kathipathi aRputhan thaanae.
8.meaning: Though He abides, established in glory, by His wondrous power in the foremost of His inherited domains everywhere, He dwells within Tirukkadithanam of the celestials of the realm. He Himself is the wondrous One, the Lord of the cowherds.

9.translit:
aRputhan naaraayaNan ari vaamanan,
niRpathu maevi yiruppathen nenjagam,
naRpugazh vaethiyar naanmaRai ninRathir,
kaRpagach choalaith thirukkadithaanamae.
9.meaning: The wondrous One, Narayana, Hari, Vamana—the place He chooses to stand and abide is my heart, and Tirukkadithanam, with its kalpaka-tree groves where the well-renowned brahmins make the four Vedas resound.

10.translit:
soalai thirukkadithaanath thuRaithiru maalai,
mathiLkuru koorch chadagoapan sol,
paaloda muthanna aayiraththip paththum,
maelaivai kunthath thiruththum viyanthae.
10.meaning: This decad of ten, out of the thousand verses—sweet as milk and nectar—of Satakopan of walled Kurukur, sung upon Tirumal who dwells in groved Tirukkadithanam, will gladly establish those who master it in the supreme Vaikuntham above.

0.translit:
vaarkaDaa aruvi yaanaimaa malaiyin maruppiNaik kuvaDiRut turuTTi,
uurkoLthiN paagan uyirsegut tarangin mallaraik konRusoozh paraNmEl,
poorkaDaa varasar puRakkiDa maaDa miimasaik kanjanait tagartta,
seerkoLsiR Raayan tirucchengun Ruuril tirucchiRRaa Rengkaḷsel saarvE.
0.meaning: He who, on the great mountain with streaming flowing waterfalls, broke off and rolled away the twin tusks of the elephant like two crags; the strong driver who seized the town -- He took his life; on the encircling platform He slew the wrestlers; and as the warring kings turned their backs and fled, He smashed Kamsa from atop the high palace. That glorious little cowherd's Thiruchengunrur and its Thiruchitraru are our refuge.

1.translit:
engaḷsel saarvu yaamuDai amudam imaiyavar appanen appan,
pongkumoo vulagum paDaittaḷit tazhikkum porundumoo vuruvanem aruvan,
sengkayal ugaḷum tEmpaNai puDaisoozh tirucchengun Ruurttiruch chiRRaaRu
angamar kinRa, aadiyaan allaal yaavarmaR Renamar tuNaiyE?
1.meaning: He is our refuge, our own ambrose, the father of the celestials and my father; the One united in three forms who creates, protects and destroys the three swelling worlds, my formless One. Other than the Primordial One who dwells there in Thiruchengunrur's Thiruchitraru, encircled on every side by honey-rich fields where red carp leap -- who else is my fitting companion?

2.translit:
ennamar perumaan imaiyavar perumaan irunilam iDanthavem perumaan,
munnaival vinaigaḷ muzhuthuDan maaḷa ennaiyaaḷ kinREm perumaan,
tenthisaik kaNikoḷ tirucchengun Ruuril tirucchiRRaa Rangkarai miipaal
ninRavem perumaan, aDiyallaal saraNam ninaippilum piRithillai enakkE.
2.meaning: He is my abiding Lord, the Lord of the celestials, my Lord who scooped up the wide earth. So that all my ancient and fierce karmas perish utterly, He rules me, my Lord. The Lord who stands on the upper bank of the Thiruchitraru in Thiruchengunrur, the jewel of the southern quarter -- other than His feet there is no refuge, nor even any other thought, for me.

3.translit:
piRithillai yenakkup periyamoo vulagum niRaippaer uruvamaay nimirntha,
kuRiya maaN emmaan kuraikaDal kaDaintha kOlamaa Nikkamen emmaan,
seRikulai vaazhai kamuguthengk aNisoozh tirucchengun Ruurttiruch chiRRaaRu
aRiya,meym maiyE ninRavem perumaan aDiyiNai yallathOr araNE.
3.meaning: There is nothing else for me. My Lord, the noble dwarf who then rose up with a great form filling the three vast worlds; my Lord, the beautiful, noble gem who churned the roaring ocean. The Lord who stands truly and manifestly, so that all may know Him, in Thiruchengunrur's Thiruchitraru encircled by beauty -- clustered plantains, areca palms and coconut trees -- His twin feet are my only fortress, none other.

4.translit:
allathOr araNum avanilvE Rillai athuporu ḷaagilum, avanai
allathen aavi amarnthaNai killaa thaathalaal avanu Rai kinRa,
nallanaan maRaiyOr vELviyuḷ maDuttha naRumpugai visumboḷi maRaikkum,
nallaneeḷ maaDat tirucchengun Ruuril tirucchiRRaa Renakkunal laraNE.
4.meaning: Even if such a fortress as one apart from Him is no reality, my soul cannot rest content and find peace in anything other than Him. Therefore Thiruchitraru in Thiruchengunrur of fine tall mansions -- where the fragrant smoke offered in the sacrifices of the good masters of the four Vedas veils the light of the sky -- is my good fortress.

5.translit:
enakkunal laraNai enathaa ruyirai imaiyavar tantaithaay thannai,
tanakkuntan tanmai aRivari yaanait taDangkaDal paḷḷiyam maanai,
manakkoḷseer moovaa yiravarvaN sivanum ayanum thaanumop paarvaazh,
kanakkoḷthiN maaDat tirucchengun Ruuril tirucchiRRaa RathanuḷkaN DEnE.
5.meaning: My good fortress, my very life, the father and mother of the celestials; the Lord whose own nature is hard for even Himself to fully know, the Lord who reclines on the vast ocean -- I beheld Him in the Thiruchitraru of Thiruchengunrur with its dense firm mansions, where the three thousand of cherished glory dwell, where the bounteous Siva, Brahma and He Himself appear as equals.

6.translit:
tirucchengun Ruuril tirucchiRRaa Rathanuḷ kaNDavath tiruvaDi yenRum,
tiruchcheyya kamalak kaNNoomsev vaayum sevvaDi yumseyya kaiyum,
tiruchcheyya kamala vunthiyum seyya kamalamaar pumseyya vuDaiyum,
tiruchcheyya muDiyum aaramum paDaiyum tigazhaven sinthaiyu ḷaanE.
6.meaning: That holy Lord whom I beheld in the Thiruchitraru of Thiruchengunrur -- forever with His sacred red lotus eyes and red mouth, red feet and red hands, His sacred red lotus navel and red lotus chest and red garment, His sacred red crown and garland and weapon all shining -- He dwells within my thought.

7.translit:
tigazhaven sinthai yuḷḷirun thaanaich chezhunilat tEvarnaan maRaiyOr,
tisaikaikoopi yEttum tirucchengun Ruuril tirucchiRRaa Rangkarai yaanai,
pugarkoḷvaa navargaḷ pugaliDan thannai asurarvan kaiyarveng kuuRRai,
pugazhumaa RaRiyEn porunthumoo vulagum paDaippoDu keDuppukkaap pavanE.
7.meaning: He who shiningly dwells within my thought; He on the bank of the Thiruchitraru in Thiruchengunrur, whom the gods of the rich earth and the masters of the four Vedas praise with hands joined toward Him from every quarter; the refuge of the radiant celestials, and the fierce death to the strong-handed asuras -- I know not how to praise Him, He who creates, destroys and protects the three coherent worlds.

8.translit:
paDaippoDu keDuppuk kaappavan pirama paramparan sivaperumaan avanE,
iDaippukkO ruruvum ozhivillai yavanE pugazhvillai yaavaiyum thaanE,
koDaipperum pugazhaar inaiyarthan naanaar kuuRiya vicchaiyO Dozhukkam,
naDaippali yiyaRkait tirucchengun Ruuril tirucchiRRaa Ramarntha naathanE.
8.meaning: He who creates, destroys and protects is Himself Brahma the supreme, and the great Lord Siva. There is no form set in between that is apart from Him, He alone is the praise, He Himself is all things. Of bounteous great glory, none can say He is of such-and-such a measure -- and the very knowledge and conduct people speak of, He is that. He is the Lord who abides in the Thiruchitraru of Thiruchengunrur, where the offering-rites are observed in due course.

9.translit:
amarntha naathanai yavarava raagi avarkkaruḷ aruḷumam maanai
amarnthathaN pazhanat tirucchengun Ruuril tirucchiRRaaR Rangkarai yaanai,
amarnthaseer moovaa yiravarvE thiyarkaḷ thampathi yavanithE varvaazhvu,
amarnthamaa yOnai mukkaNam maanai naanmuga naiyamarn thEnE.
9.meaning: The Lord who abides there, who becomes each and every being and bestows His grace upon them; the Lord on the bank of the Thiruchitraru in Thiruchengunrur of cool abiding fields; the home of the three thousand of cherished glory, the masters of the Vedas, the very life of the gods of earth -- that abiding mysterious One, who is also the three-eyed Lord and the four-faced One -- to Him I have come and rested.

10.translit:
thEnainan paalaik kannalai yamuthait tirunthula guNDavam maanai,
vaananaan mukanai malarnthaN koppuuzh malarmisaip paDaitthamaa yOnai,
kOnaivaN kurukuurc vaNsaDa kOpan sonnavaa yiratthuḷip patthum,
vaaninmee thERRi yaruḷseythu muDikkum piRavimaa maayakkuut tinaiyE.
10.meaning: He who is honey, fine milk, sweet cane-sugar and ambrose, the Lord who swallowed the well-set worlds; the mysterious One who created the four-faced Brahma upon the lotus blossoming on His cool navel; the King -- these ten among the thousand verses sung by Satakopan of bountiful Kurukur, when uttered, raise one up above the heavens, and by His grace they end the dance of great illusion that is birth.

0.translit:
aaraanum karpippaar naayagarE? naan avanaik
kaar aar tirumEni kaaNum aLavum pOy
seer aar tiruvEngkadamE tirukkOva
lUrE matiL kacchi UragamE pEragamE
pEraa marudu iRuttaan veLLaRaiyE vehkaavE
pEr aali taNkaal naRaiyUr tiruppuliyUr
aaraamam soozhnda arangkam kaNamangkai
0.meaning: Is there anyone who can instruct me, my lord? Until I go and behold Him of the cloud-dark sacred form, I will journey to glorious Tiruvenkatam, Tirukkovalur, walled Kacchi (Kanchi), Uragam, Peragam, to Him who snapped the marudu trees, to Vellarai, Vehka, great Ali, Tankal, Naraiyur, Tiruppuliyur, Srirangam encircled by groves, and Kanamangai.

1.translit:
karumaaNikka malaimEl maNit tadandaamaraik kaadugaL pOl,
tirumaarvu vaay kaN kai undi kaal udai aadaigaL seyya piraan
tirumaal emmaan sezhuneer vayal kuttanaattut tiruppuliyUr,
arumaayan pEr anRip pEcchilaL annaimeer itaR ken seygEnaa.
1.meaning: Like a forest of jewel-like great lotuses upon a mountain of dark sapphire, the gracious Lord's sacred chest, mouth, eyes, hands, navel and feet, and His garments, all glow red. He is Tirumal, my master, who dwells in Tiruppuliyur of Kuttanadu with its lush watered fields. O mothers, she speaks of nothing but the names of that rare wondrous one, what shall I do about this?

2.translit:
annaimeer itaR ken seygEn? aNimEruvin meedu ulavum,
tunnu soozh sudar njaayiRum anRiyum pal sudargaLum pOl,
minnu neeN mudi aaram pal kalan taan udai emperumaan,
punnai am pozhil soozh tiruppuliyUr pugazhum ivaLE.
2.meaning: O mothers, what shall I do about this? Like the bright crowding sun that moves above lovely Mount Meru, and like the many other lights besides, my Lord wears a flashing long crown, a chest-garland and many ornaments. She praises Tiruppuliyur ringed by beautiful punnai groves.

3.translit:
pugazhum ivaL ninRu iraappagal poru neerk kadal teeppattu, engkum
tigazhum eriyOdu selvadu oppac sezhung kadir aazhi mudal
pugazhum poru padai Endip pOr pukka asurarai ponRuvittaan
tigazhum maNi nedu maadam needu tiruppuliyUr vaLamE.
3.meaning: She stands praising, night and day, the glory of Tiruppuliyur with its tall jewelled mansions, the abode of Him who, bearing the bright-rayed discus and other dread weapons - as though the surging-watered ocean had caught fire and spread everywhere with blazing flame - destroyed the asuras who came to war.

4.translit:
Ur vaLam kiLar sOlaiyum karumbum peruny sennelum soozhndu
Er vaLam kiLar taN paNaik kuttanaattut tiruppuliyUr,
seer vaLam kiLar moovulagu uNdu umizh dEvapiraan,
pEr vaLam kiLarndu anRip pEcchilaL inRip punaiyizhaiyE.
4.meaning: In Tiruppuliyur of Kuttanadu, with its cool fields whose beauty and abundance flourish, encircled by groves rich in the town's bounty, by sugarcane and tall red paddy - this maiden adorned with fine jewels speaks of nothing but the abundant glory of the divine Lord who swallowed and spat forth the three worlds, whose excellence and richness ever increase.

5.translit:
punaiyizhaigaL aNivum aadai udaiyum putuk kaNippum,
ninaiyum neermaiyadu anRu ivatku itu ninRu ninaikkap pukkaal,
sunaiyin uL tadandaamarai malarum taN tiruppuliyUr,
munaivan moovulagu aaLi appan tiruvaruL moozhginaLE.
5.meaning: The adorning with fine jewels, the wearing of garments, the fresh dressing - this is no longer in her nature to think of, if one comes to ponder it. She has plunged into the sacred grace of the foremost Lord, the father who rules the three worlds, who dwells in cool Tiruppuliyur where great lotuses bloom within the springs.

6.translit:
tiruvaruL moozhgi naaLgaLum sezhuneer niRak kaNNapiraan,
tiruvaruLgaLum sErndu amaik kadaiyaaLam tirunda uLa,
aruLaal tiruvaruL avan senRu sEr taN tiruppuliyUr,
tiruvaruL kamuga oN pazhuttadu melliyal sevvidazhE.
6.meaning: Plunged day after day into His sacred grace, the soft-natured maiden's red lips have ripened beautifully like areca - this is the perfect mark that His graces too have joined and settled in her, by the grace of Kannapiran of lush-water hue, the sacred grace of Him who went and dwells in cool Tiruppuliyur.

7.translit:
mel ilaic sel vaN kodi pulga veeng kiLan taaL kamugin,
mal ilai madal vaazhai eeng kani soozhndu maNam kamazhndu,
pul ilait tengkin oodu kaal ulavum taN tiruppuliyUr,
mallalam selvak kaNNan taaL adaindaaL im madavaralE.
7.meaning: In cool Tiruppuliyur, where the breeze wanders among the broad-leaved coconut palms - where slender young areca trees are embraced by tender-leaved spreading creepers, and lush-leaved plantains laden with fragrant fruit cast their scent all around - this gentle maiden has reached the feet of bountiful, gracious Kannan.

8.translit:
madavaral annaimeergatku en solli sollugEn? mallaic selva
vadamozhi maRaivaaNar vELviyuL ney azhal vaan pugai pOy
tida visumbil amarar naattai maRaikkum taN tiruppuliyUr,
pada arava aNaiyaan tan naamam allaal paravaaL ivaLE.
8.meaning: What shall I tell you, O gentle mothers? In cool Tiruppuliyur, where the great rising smoke of the ghee-fire in the sacrifices of the prosperous Sanskrit-Veda-masters mounts up and veils the land of the immortals in the firm sky - this maiden chants nothing but the name of Him who reclines on the hooded serpent.

9.translit:
paravaaL ivaL ninRu iraappagal pani neer niRak kaNNapiraan,
viravaar isai maRai vEdiyar oli vElaiyin ninRu olippa,
karavaar tadandoRum taamaraik kayam teevigai ninRu alarum,
puravaar kazhanigaL soozh tiruppuliyUrp pugazh anRi maRRE.
9.meaning: She stands praising, night and day, nothing but the glory of Kannapiran of cool-water hue, and of Tiruppuliyur encircled by guarded fields - where, like the sound of the sea, the chanting of the brahmins blending Vedic hymn and music resounds, and where in every broad unfailing pond the lotuses bloom like lit lamps.

10.translit:
anRi maRRu Or upaayam en ivaL andaN tuzhaay kamazhdal,
kunRa maa maNi maada maaLigaik kOlak kuzhaangaL malgi,
ten disait tiladam puraik kuttanaattut tiruppuliyUr,
ninRa maayappiraan tiruvaruLaam ivaL nErppattadE.
10.meaning: What other remedy is there? That she is fragrant with the cool beautiful tulasi - this is nothing but the sacred grace of the wondrous Lord who stands in Tiruppuliyur of Kuttanadu, which, like an ornamental mark adorning the southern quarter, abounds in lovely clusters of mountain-like great jewelled mansions and palaces. To this she has been united.

11.translit:
nErppatta niRai moovulagukkum naayagan tan adimai,
nErppatta toNdar toNdar toNdar toNdan sadagOpan, sol
nErppatta tamizhmaalai aayirattuL ivaiyum Or pattum
nErppatt aaravar, nErppattaar nedumaaRkadimai seyyavE.
11.meaning: Of the perfect garland of a thousand fitting Tamil verses by Satakopan - the servant of the servants of the servants of the servants who are fitted for the service of the Lord, master of all the three full and perfect worlds - these ten too: those who become fit with them have become fit indeed to perform service to the great Lord (Nedumal).

0.translit:
inbam payakka ezhilmalar maatharum thaanumiv vEzhulakai,
inbam payakka inithudan veetrirun thaaLkinra engaLpiraan,
anbut ramarnthurai kinraaN ipozhil soozhthiru vaaranviLai,
anbut ramarnthu valanjseythu kaithozhum naaLkaLum aakungkolO.
0.meaning: Our Lord, who, to bestow bliss, dwells gracefully and sweetly seated together with the lovely lotus-born Lady (Lakshmi), bestowing bliss upon these seven worlds — He abides lovingly and contentedly at Tiruvaaranvilai, surrounded by beautiful groves. Will the days ever come when I too, filled with love, contentedly circumambulate Him and worship Him with folded hands?

1.translit:
aakungkol aiyamon rinri akalidam mutravum, EyradiyE
aakum parisu nimirntha thirukkuRaL appan amarnthuRaiyum,
maakam thikazhkodi maadangaL needum mathiLthiru vaaranviLai,
maakantha neerkondu thoovi valanjseythu kaithozhung koodungkolO.
1.meaning: He is my Father, the sacred Vamana (the dwarf-Brahmin), who grew and measured the whole wide earth with but two strides — He abides at Tiruvaaranvilai with its tall mansions flying banners that gleam against the sky, encircled by ramparts. Will it come to pass, without any doubt, that I take fragrant water, sprinkle it in offering, circumambulate Him and join my hands in worship?

2.translit:
koodungkol vaikalum kOvintha naimathu soothanaik kOLariyai,
aadum paravai misaikkandu kaithozhu thanri yavanuRaiyum,
paadum perumpukazh naanmaRai vELviyain thaarangam panninarvaazh,
needu pozhilthiru vaaran viLaithozha vaaykkungkol nichchalumE.
2.meaning: Will it ever come to pass that, every day, I behold Govinda, Madhusudana, the fierce man-lion (Narasimha), mounted upon the soaring bird (Garuda), and worship Him with folded hands? Will it daily be granted to me to worship at Tiruvaaranvilai of long groves, where dwell those who sing His vast glory, who expound the four Vedas, the five sacrifices and the six auxiliary sciences?

3.translit:
vaaykkungkol nichchalum eppozhu thummanaththu eengu ninaikkappeRa
vaaykkum karumbum perunjsen nelumvayal soozhthiru vaaranviLai,
vaaykkum perumpukazh moovula keesan vadamathu raippiRantha,
vaaykkum maNiniRak kaNNapi raanran maraladip pOthukaLE.
3.meaning: Will it daily be granted to me, here, to think upon Him at every moment in my heart? Will I obtain the blossoming lotus-feet of Lord Kannan of glowing sapphire hue, the renowned Lord of the three worlds, who was born in northern Mathura — He who dwells at Tiruvaaranvilai surrounded by fields where sugarcane and great red paddy flourish?

4.translit:
maraladip pOthukaL ennenjsath theppozhu thumiruth thivaNanga,
paladi yaarmun paruLiya paampaNai yappan amarnthuRaiyum,
malaril maNinedu maadangaL needu mathilthiru vaaranviLai,
ulakam malipukazh paadanam mElvinai onrumnil laakedumE.
4.meaning: That I may forever enshrine His blossom-like lotus-feet within my heart and bow to them — He is my Father reclining on the serpent-couch, who showed grace before many devotees, and abides at Tiruvaaranvilai with its tall jeweled mansions amid blossoms and lofty ramparts. When we sing the praise of Him that fills the world, all the karma fastened upon us will not stand — it will utterly perish.

5.translit:
onrumnil laakedum mutravum theevinai yuLLith thozhuminthondeer,
anrang amarven ruruppi Ninangai yaNinedun thOLpuNarnthaan,
enrumep pOthumen nenjsam thuthippa vuLLEyiruk kinrapiraan,
ninra aNithiru vaaran viLai yennum neeNaka ramathuvE.
5.meaning: O devotees, contemplate and worship Him, and all your evil karma will not stand — it will wholly perish! That day He won the battle and embraced the beautiful long shoulders of the lady Rukmini — that Lord who abides within my heart, praised by my mind at all times and forever. He stands ever-present in that great and beautiful city called Tiruvaaranvilai.

6.translit:
neeNaka ramathu vEmalarch chOlaikaL soozhthiru vaaranviLai,
neeNaka raththuRai kinrapi raannedu maalkaNNan viNNavarkOn
vaaNa purampukku muk katpi raanaith tholaiyavem pOrkaLseythu,
vaaNanai yaayiran thOLthuNith thaanjsaraN anrimat ronrilamE.
6.meaning: Tiruvaaranvilai, encircled by blossoming groves, is that great city; the Lord who dwells in that great city is Nedumal, Kannan, the King of the celestials. He entered Bana's city, waged fierce wars until the three-eyed Lord (Shiva, who fought for Bana) was undone, and severed the thousand arms of Bana — His feet are our refuge; we have no other refuge but Him.

7.translit:
anrimat ronrilam ninsara NE!en rakalirum poykai yinvaay,
ninruthan neeLkazha lEththiya aanaiyin nenjsidar theerththa piraan,
senrang inithuRai kinra sezhumpozhil soozhthiru vaaranviLai,
onri valanjseyya onrumO? theevinai yuLLaththin saarvallavE.
7.meaning: "We have no other; Your feet alone are our refuge!" — so cried the elephant (Gajendra), standing in the wide deep pond, praising His long anklets; and the Lord who removed the anguish in that elephant's heart has come and sweetly abides at Tiruvaaranvilai, ringed by lush groves. If one becomes united with it and circumambulates it, is it possible? Evil karma can no longer cling to the heart.

8.translit:
theevinai yuLLaththin saarvalla vaakith theLivisum pERaluttraal,
naavinuL LumuLLath thuLLum amaintha thozhilinuL Lumnavinru,
yaavarum vanthu vaNangum pozhilthiru vaaran viLaiyathanai,
mEvi valanjseythu kaithozhak koodungkol ennumen sinthanaiyE.
8.meaning: So that evil karma can no longer cling to my heart, and I may attain the bright sky (of liberation) — uttering Him constantly upon my tongue, within my heart, and in the well-set acts of worship — will it ever come to pass that I draw near to Tiruvaaranvilai, surrounded by groves, where all people come and bow, and circumambulate it and worship with folded hands? So muses my mind.

9.translit:
sinthaimat ronrin thiraththathal laaththanmai thEvapi raanaRiyum,
sinthaiyi naalseyva thaanaRi yaathana maayangaL onrumillai,
sinthaiyi naalsolli naalseykai yaalnilath thEvar kuzhuvaNangum,
sinthai makizhthiru vaaran viLaiyuRai theerththanuk katrapinnE.
9.meaning: That my heart is not turned toward any other thing — this nature the Lord of the celestials knows. There is no illusion that He, who knows my deeds, does not know within His own mind — there is nothing at all. After I have become wholly devoted, in thought and word and deed, to the Holy One who dwells at heart-delighting Tiruvaaranvilai, where the throngs of earthly gods (the Brahmins) bow — there remains nothing else.

10.translit:
theerththanuk katrapin mattrOr saraNillai yenreNNi,
theerththanuk kE theerththa manaththan naakich chezhungkuru koorchchada kOpansonna,
theerththanga LaayiraththuLLivai paththumval laarkaLai, thEvarvaikal
theerththanga LEyenru poosiththu nalki yuraippaartham thEviyarkkE.
10.meaning: Having resolved that after becoming devoted to the Holy One there is no other refuge, and with a mind made pure and dedicated to that Holy One alone, Satakopan of fertile Kurukur composed these holy verses. Of these thousand holy verses, those who can master these ten — the celestials will daily honor them as if they were sacred waters themselves, and will graciously commend them, indeed to their own consorts.

0.translit:
vaikalpoon kazhivaay vandhu meyum kuruginangaaL,
seykoL sennel uyartthiruvaNvaN dooruraiyum,
kaikoL sakkaratth enkani vaayperu maanaikkaNdu,
kaikaL kooppich chollIr vinaiyaattiyEn kaadhanmaiyE.
0.meaning: O flocks of cranes that come daily to feed at the flower-filled backwaters! At Tiruvanvandur where the rich fields of red paddy grow tall, behold my Lord of the sweet red lips who holds the discus in His hand, and folding your hands, tell Him of the love-longing of me, this woman bound by her sinful deeds.

1.translit:
kaadhal menpedai yOdudan meyum karunaaraay!
vEdha vELvi yolimuzhangum thaN thiruvaNvaNdoor,
naadhan njaalamel laamuNda namperu maanaikkaNdu,
paadham kaithozhudhu paNiyI radiyEn thiramE.
1.meaning: O dark stork that feeds together with your beloved soft mate! At cool Tiruvanvandur where the sound of Vedic sacrifices resounds, behold our Lord the Master who swallowed all the worlds, and worshipping His feet with your hands, plead with Him about the plight of me, His servant.

2.translit:
thirangaL aagiyeng kumseykaLooduzhal puLLinangaaL,
siranndha selvam malku thiruvaNvaN dooruraiyum,
kaRangu sakkarak kaikkani vaaypperu maanaikkaNdu,
iRangi neerthozhudhu paNiyIradiyE nidarE.
2.meaning: O flocks of birds that wander in groups everywhere among the fields! At Tiruvanvandur where surpassing wealth abounds, behold the great Lord of the sweet red lips who bears the whirling discus in His hand, and bowing low and worshipping, plead with Him about the suffering of me, His servant.

3.translit:
idaril bOgam moozhgi yiNainndhaadum madavannangaaL,
vidalil vEdha volimuzhangumthaN thiruvaNvaNdoor,
kadalil mEnip piraankaN NaNainedu maalaikkaNdu,
udalam nainndhorutth iyuru kum endruNartthuminE.
3.meaning: O graceful swans that, sunk in unfailing pleasure, sport together in pairs! At cool Tiruvanvandur where the ceaseless Vedic sound resounds, behold the Lord with the ocean-hued body, Kannan the great Tirumal, and make Him understand that a certain woman wastes away in body and melts in love.

4.translit:
uNartth lood luNarnndhudan meyum madavannangaaL,
thiNartth vaNdalkaL mElsangu sErum thiruvaNvaNdoor,
puNartth poondhaN thuzhaaymudi namperu maanaikkaNdu,
puNartth kaiyinaraa yadiyEnukkum pOtruminE.
4.meaning: O graceful swans that, knowing love-quarrel and reconciliation, feed together! At Tiruvanvandur where conches gather upon the heaped sandbanks, behold our Lord whose crown is woven with cool fresh tulasi, and with hands joined together, intercede on behalf of me too, His servant.

5.translit:
pOtriyaan iran dhEnpunnai mEluRai poonguyil kaaL,
sEtril vaaLai thuLLum thiruvaNvaN dooruraiyum,
aatra laazhiyang kaiyama rarperu maanaikkaNdu,
maatrang koNdaru LIrmaiyal theerva dhoruvaNNamE.
5.meaning: O lovely cuckoos that dwell upon the punnai tree, I beg you, praising you! At Tiruvanvandur where the scabbard-fish leap in the mire, behold the Lord of the gods who holds the mighty discus in His beautiful hand, and bring back from Him a word of grace, that in some way my love-madness may be cured.

6.translit:
oruvaNNamsendru pukkenakku ondrurai yoNkiLiyE,
seruvoN poompozhil soozhsekkar vElai thiruvaNvaNdoor,
karuvaNNam seyyavaay seyyakaN seyyakai seyyakaal,
seruvoN sakkaram sangadai yaaLam thirunndhak kaNdE.
6.meaning: O bright parrot, go somehow and enter in, and bring me back a word! At Tiruvanvandur set amid lovely battle-worthy flower-groves by the red-glowing sea, having clearly beheld His dark-hued form, His red mouth, red eyes, red hands and red feet, and the marks of the bright battle-discus and conch.

7.translit:
thirunndhak kaNdenak kondrurai yaayoN siRupoovaay
serunndhi njaazhal magizhpunnai soozhthaN thiruvaNvaNdoor,
perunndhaN thaamaraik kaNperu neeNmudi naaLthadanndhOL,
karunndhiN maamugil pOlthiru mEni yadigaLaiyE.
7.meaning: O bright little mynah, behold Him clearly and bring me back a word! At cool Tiruvanvandur ringed by serunti, njaazhal, magizh and punnai trees, behold the Lord of the sacred form like a dark dense great cloud, with large cool lotus eyes, a great tall crown, and broad ample shoulders.

8.translit:
adigaL kaithozhu dhalarmEl asaiyum annangaaL,
vidivai sangolik kumthiru vaNvaN dooruraiyum,
kadiya maayandhan naikkaNNanai nedu maalaikkaNdu,
kodiya valvinai yEnthiram kooRumin vERukoNdE.
8.meaning: O swans that sway upon the blossoms, worship His feet with your hands! At Tiruvanvandur where conches sound at the break of dawn, behold the swift wondrous one, Kannan the great Tirumal, and taking Him aside, tell Him of the plight of me, of cruel and grievous deeds.

9.translit:
vERu koNdummai yaaniran dhEnveRi vaNdinangaaL,
thERu neerppam paivada paalaith thiruvaNvaNdoor,
maaril pOrarak kanmadhiL neeRezhach chetrugandha,
ERusEvaga naarkkennai yumuLaL enminkaLE.
9.meaning: O swarms of fragrance-seeking bees, taking you aside I beg you! At Tiruvanvandur on the northern bank of the clear-watered Pampai, to the bull-like heroic Lord who joyously destroyed, reducing to ashes, the ramparts of the matchless warring demon, say that I too exist for Him.

10.translit:
minkoL sErpuri nooLkuRa LaayagaL njaalamkoNda,
vankaL vanadi mElkuru koorchchada gOpansonna,
paNkoL aayiratth uLLivai patthum thiruvaNvaNdoorkku,
inkoL paadal vallaar madhanarmin nidaiyavarkkE.
10.meaning: Of the thousand melodious verses sung by Satakopan of Kurukur upon the feet of the cunning thief who, as the dwarf wearing the bright sacred thread, seized the whole wide earth—these ten on Tiruvanvandur: those who can master this sweet song shall become as charming as Manmatha, the god of love, to slender-waisted women.

0.translit:
keDumiDa raayavellaam kEsavaa veLLa naaLum
koDuvinai seyyum kooTRin tamarkaLum kuRukakillaar
viDamuDai yaravil paLLi virumbinaan surumbalaTRum
taDamuDai vayal anantapura nagarip puguduminRE.
0.meaning: Hardships and ruin will be utterly destroyed; call out 'Kesava!' always, and the agents of Yama who inflict cruel deeds will not come near. The Lord who delighted in reclining upon the venom-bearing serpent dwells here — enter this very day the city of Anantapuram, ringed with broad fields where bees hum.

1.translit:
inRupOyp puguthiraagil ezhumaiyum Edam saaraa
kunRunEr maaDamaaDE kurundu sEr serundi punnai
manRalar pOzhil anantapura nagar maayan naamam
onRumOraayiramaam uLLuvaarkku umbaroorE.
1.meaning: If you go and enter today, evils will not cling to you for seven births. In Anantapuram, with hill-like mansions and groves fragrant with blossoming kurundu, serunti and punnai trees, the names of the wondrous Lord — each one becomes a thousand — and for those who meditate upon them, the world of the gods above is theirs.

2.translit:
oorum puT koDiyum aHdE ulagollaam uNDumizhndaan
sErum taN ananthapuram sikkenap puguthiraagil
theerum nOy vinaigaLellaam thiNNam naam aRiyacchonnOm
pErum OraayiratthuL onRu neer pEsuminE.
2.meaning: His mount and the emblem on His banner are one and the same (Garuda); He who swallowed all the worlds and spat them forth dwells in cool Anantapuram. If you firmly enter there, all your diseases and karmic deeds will surely end — this we declare so you may know. Speak even one of His thousand names.

3.translit:
pEsumin koosaminRip periya neer vElai soozhndhu
vaasamE kamazhum sOlai vayalaNi ananthapuram
nEsam seydhu uRaiginRaanai neRimaiyaal malargaL thoovi
poosanai seyginRaargaL puNNiyam seydhavaaRE.
3.meaning: Speak His names without shame. Anantapuram, encircled by the great waters of the sea, adorned with fragrance-spreading groves and fields — those who, with proper rite, scatter flowers and perform worship to the Lord who lovingly abides there, have indeed performed great merit.

4.translit:
puNNiyam seydhu nalla punaloDu malargaL thoovi
eNNumin endhai naamam ip piRap paRukkum appaal
thiNNam naam aRiyacchonnOm seRipozhil ananthapurattu
aNNalaar kamala paadham aNuguvaar amararaavaar.
4.meaning: Doing good deeds, with pure water and scattered flowers, meditate upon my Father's names — they will sever this birth, and beyond that too. Firmly we declare so you may know: those who reach the lotus feet of the Lord of Anantapuram, with its dense groves, shall become immortals.

5.translit:
amararaayth thiriginRaargaTku aadhi sEra ananthapurattu
amararkOn arcchikkinRu angu agappaNi seyvar viNNOr
namargaLO! sollak kENmin naamum pOy naNugavENDum
kumaranaar thaadhai thunbam thuDaittha gOvindhanaarE.
5.meaning: To be the source for those who wander as immortals, in Anantapuram Indra, king of the gods, worships, and the celestials perform intimate service there. O our kinsmen! Listen to what I say — we too must go and draw near to Govinda, who wiped away the sorrow of the father of Kumara (the one who fathered Manmatha / removed grief).

6.translit:
thuDaittha gOvindhanaarE ulaguyir dhEvum maTRum
paDaittha em paramamoorthi paambaNaip paLLikoNDaan
maDaitthalai vaaLai paayum vayalaNi yananthapuram
kaDaitthalai seeykkap peTRaal kaDuvinai kaLaiyalaamE.
6.meaning: Govinda who wiped away (our grief), our supreme Lord who created the worlds, living beings, the gods and all else, reclines upon His serpent-couch. If we are granted to sweep the threshold of Anantapuram — adorned with fields where scabbard-fish leap at the sluice-gates — our fierce karma can be cast away.

7.translit:
kaDuvinai kaLaiyalaagum kaamanaip payandha kaaLai
iDavagai koNDadhenbar ezhilaNi yananthapuram
paDamuDai yaravil paLLi payinRavan paadham kaaNa
naDaminO namargaLuLLeer! naam umakku aRiyacchonnOm.
7.meaning: Your fierce karma can be removed. They say the youthful Lord who begot Manmatha took Anantapuram, adorned with beauty, as His place. To behold the feet of Him who reclines upon the hooded serpent, walk there, O our kinsmen! We have declared this for you to know.

8.translit:
naam umakku aRiyacchonna naaLgaLum naNiyavaana
sEmam nanguDaitthuk kaNDeer seRipozhil ananthapuram
thooma nal viraimalargaL thuvaLaRa aaynthukoNDu
vaamanan aDikkenRu eththa maayndhaRum vinaigaL thaamE.
8.meaning: The days we have told you about are drawing near, and see — it holds rich safety for us: Anantapuram with dense groves. Selecting unblemished fragrant flowers along with incense, when offered praising the feet of Vamana, your deeds themselves perish and end.

9.translit:
maayndhaRum vinaigaL thaamE maadhavaa enna naaLum
eeyndha pon madhiL ananthapura nagar endhaikku enRu
saandhoDu viLakkam dhoopam thaamarai malargaL nalla
aayndhukoNDu Ethavallaar andhamil pugazhinaarE.
9.meaning: Your deeds will perish and end of themselves — call out 'Madhava!' always. Saying 'this is for my Father of Anantapuram, the city of fitted golden ramparts,' with sandal-paste, lamps, incense and fine lotus flowers carefully selected — those able to praise Him gain endless glory.

10.translit:
andhamil pugazh ananthapura nagar aadhi thannai
konthalar pozhil kurugoor maaRan sol laayiratthuL
ainthinODainthum vallaar aNaivar pOy amarulagil
paindhoDi maDandhaiyar tham vEymaru thOLiNaiyE.
10.meaning: Of the boundless-glory Anantapuram, the Primal Lord — of the thousand verses of Maran (Nammalwar) of Kurugur with its blossom-filled groves — those who master these ten (five and five) will go and attain, in the immortal world, the bamboo-like twin shoulders of the maidens wearing fresh golden bangles.

0.translit:
aruLperuvaar adiyaartham adiyaneerkku aazhiyaan
aruLtaruvaan amaikinraan adhunamadu vidhivagaiye
iruLtarumaa NYaalatthuL inippiravi yaanveeNdeen
maruLozhinee madaneNYce! vaattaatraan adivaNange.
0.meaning: The Lord who bears the discus is making ready to grant grace to me, the servant of His servants who receive His grace; this has become our destiny. I no longer desire birth in this vast world that yields only darkness. O foolish heart, cast off your delusion and worship the feet of the Lord of Vaattaaru.

1.translit:
vaattaatraa nadivaNangi maaNYaalap pirappaRuppaan
keettaayee madaneNYce! keesavanem perumaanai
paattaaya palapaadip pazhavinaigaL patrarutthu
naattaaroo diyalvozhindu naaraNanai naNNinamee.
1.meaning: O foolish heart, have you heard? Worshipping the feet of the Lord of Vaattaaru, He cuts off birth in this great world. Singing many songs in praise of Kesava, our Lord, and severing the grip of old karma, we have given up the ways of worldly folk and have drawn near to Narayana.

2.translit:
naNNinam naaraNanai naamangaL palasolli
maNNulagil vaLammik ka vaattaatraan vandinru
viNNulagam taruvaanaay viraikinraan vidhivagaiye
eNNinavaa raagaavik karumanga LenneNYcee
2.meaning: We have drawn near to Narayana, uttering His many names. Today the Lord of Vaattaaru, abounding in prosperity in this earthly world, has come and hastens to grant us the world of heaven; this is by destiny. O my heart, these deeds will surely come to pass as we have thought.

3.translit:
enneNYcatth uLLirunding irundamizhnoolivaimozhindu
vanneNYcath tiraNiyanai maarvidanda vaattaatraan
mannaNYcap paaratatthup paaNdavarkkaap padaitottaan
nanneNYcee! namperumaan namakkaruLtaan ceyvaanee.
3.meaning: Dwelling here within my heart and inspiring me to utter these sweet Tamil verses is the Lord of Vaattaaru, who tore open the chest of the hard-hearted Hiranya. He who took up arms for the sake of the Pandavas in the Bharata war, so that the kings trembled. O good heart! Our Lord will surely grant His grace to us.

4.translit:
vaaneeRa vazhidanda vaattaatraan paNivagaiye
naaneeRap peRu kinren naragatthai nakuneNYcee
teeneeRu malartthuLavam tigazhpaadan cezhumparavai
taaneeRith tirivaana taaLiNaiyen talaimeelee.
4.meaning: O heart that mocks at hell, by the command-service of the Lord of Vaattaaru who has given the path to ascend to heaven, I am being raised up. The pair of feet of Him whose feet shine with honey-laden tulasi blossoms, who roams mounted upon the splendid bird Garuda, are upon my head.

5.translit:
talaimeela taaLiNaigaL taamaraikkaN ennammaan
nilaipeeraan enneNYcath teppozhudum emperumaan
malaimaadath taravaNaimeel vaattaatraan madamikka
kolaiyaanai maruppocith taan kuraikazhaltaL kuRuginamee.
5.meaning: His pair of feet are upon my head; my lotus-eyed Lord never departs from my heart at any time, my Lord. The Lord of Vaattaaru who reclines upon the serpent-couch amid mountain-like mansions, who broke the tusk of the murderous, rut-maddened elephant, we have reached His resounding anklet-clad feet.

6.translit:
kuraikazhalgaL kuRuginam nam koovindan kudikoNdaan
tiraikuzhuvu kadalpudaisoozh tennaattut tiladhamanna
varaikuzhuvum maNimaada vaattaatraan malaradimeel
viraikuzhuvum naRuntuLavam meynninRu kamazhumee.
6.meaning: We have reached His resounding anklet-clad feet; our Govinda has taken up His abode there. Upon the lotus feet of the Lord of Vaattaaru, with its mountain-like clusters of jeweled mansions, like a sacred mark upon the southern land girdled by the wave-tossing sea, the fragrant tulasi laden with sweet scent abides truly and spreads its perfume.

7.translit:
meynninRu kamazhtuLava viraiyeeRu tirumudiyan
kainninRa cakkaratthan karudumidam porudupunal
mainninRa varaipoolum tiruvuruva vaattaatraaRku
ennanRi ceydeenaa enneNYcil tigazhavaduvee?
7.meaning: He bears upon His sacred crown the fragrance of the tulasi that abides and spreads its scent; He holds the discus firm in His hand. Dwelling in the place of His choosing, the Lord of Vaattaaru of sacred form like a dark mountain charged with rain-clouds. What good deed have I done that He should shine forth within my heart?

8.translit:
tigazhkinRa tirumaarbil tirumangai tannoodum
tigazhkinRa tirumaalaar seervidamtaN vaattaaRu
pugazhkinRa puLLoo rdi poorarakkar kulamkeduthaan
igazhvinRi enneNYcath teppozhudum piriyaanee.
8.meaning: The place where the resplendent Tirumal abides together with Tirumangai (Lakshmi) on His shining sacred chest is cool Vaattaaru. He who rides the celebrated bird (Garuda) and destroyed the race of the warring demons, never departs from my heart at any time, without scorn.

9.translit:
piriyaadaat ceyyenRu pirappaRutthaaL aRakkoNdaan
ariyaagi iraNiyanai aagangeeN daananRu
periyaarkkaat pattakkaal peRaadabayan peRumaaRu
varivaaLvaay aravaNaimeel vaattaatraan kaattinanee.
9.meaning: Saying 'Serve Me without parting,' He cut off my birth and fully took me as His own. As the Man-Lion, on that day He tore open the body of Hiranya. The Lord of Vaattaaru, who reclines upon the striped, sharp-fanged serpent-couch, has shown the way that, by becoming the servant of the great ones, one attains the benefit otherwise unattainable.

10.translit:
kaattitthan kanaikazhalgaL kadunaragam pugalozhitha
vaattaatrem perumaanai vaLangurugoorc cadagoopan
paattaaya tamizhmaalai yaayiratthuL ippatthum
keettu aaraar vaanavargaL cevikkiniya ceNYcollee.
10.meaning: These ten verses, among the thousand sweet Tamil garland-songs of Satakopan of fertile Kurugur, about our Lord of Vaattaaru who, showing His resounding anklet-clad feet, removed entry into fierce hell - the celestials never tire of hearing them, these beautiful words sweet to the ear.

0.translit:
varuvaar selvaar vaNpari saarath thirundha, en
thiruvaazh maarvaR ken thiRam sollaar seyvadhen,
uruvaar chakkaram sangu sumandh ing ummOdu,
oru paadu uzhalvaan Oradiyaanum uLan enRE.
0.meaning: Many come and many go amid the lovely retinue of attendants here, yet none of them speak a word to my Lord on whose breast Lakshmi (Tiru) dwells, telling Him of my plight. What am I to do? Oh that someone would go and say to Him, carrying the shapely discus and conch, moving about there among you all, that there is one lowly servant pining away on her own.

0.translit:
unnaiyum okkalaiyil koNDu tamilmaruvi
 unnoDu tangaL karuttaayina seydhuvarum
kanniyarum magizhak kaNDavar kaNkuLirak
 kaRRavar theRRivarap peRRa enakku aruLi
mannu kuRunguDiyaay veLLaRaiyaay madhiL soozh
 soolai malaikkarasee kaNNapuratthamudhee
ennavalam kaLaivaay aaDuga sengeerai
 eezhulagum uDaiyaay aaDuga aaDugavee.
0.meaning: Carrying you too upon their hips, lisping sweet Tamil, doing whatever they wished along with you, the young maidens rejoiced, the eyes of those who beheld you grew cool, and the learned drew near — having graced me thus, O Lord who dwells eternally in Kurungudi, who abides in Vellarai, who is King of the orchard-hill girt with ramparts, O nectar of Kannapuram, drive away my sorrows! Dance the sengeerai dance, O Owner of the seven worlds — dance, dance!

1.translit:
karaNDam aaDu poygaiyuL karumbanaip perumbazham
puraNDuveezha vaaLai paayu kuRunguDi neDunthagaay
tiraNDathoL iraNiyan sinangoL aagam onRaiyum
iraNDukooRu seydhuganda singamenba.
1.meaning: O great One of Kurungudi, where in ponds amid which water-fowl sport, the huge ripe fruits of the black palmyra tumble down and the scabbard-fish leap! He is called the Lion (Narasimha) who rejoiced to cleave in two the wrath-swollen body of Hiranya, of the firm rounded shoulders.

2.translit:
eevinaark kaliyaar naligavenRu enmeel engngaNee vaazhumaaRu?
aivar koovinaar seyyuk koDumaiyai maDitthEn kuRunguDi neDungkaDal vaNNaa
paavinaar in sol panmalark koNDu un paadhamee paravinaan paNindhu,
en naavinaal vandhun thiruvaDi aDaindhEn naimisaaraNiyatthuL endhaay.
2.meaning: How am I to live, while those who command me afflict and torment me? I have rolled up and cast away the cruelties wrought by the five senses, O Lord of Kurungudi, hued like the vast ocean! With many sweet flower-like words of verse I extolled and bowed to your feet alone; with my tongue I came and reached your sacred feet, O my Father of Naimisaranyam.

3.translit:
peeraanaik kuRunguDi em perumaanai, tiruthaNkaal
ooraanaik karambanoor utthamanai, mutthilangu
kaaraar thiN kaDal eezhum malai eezh ivvulageezh uNDum,
aaraadhenRu irundhaanaik kaNDadhu then arangatthee,
3.meaning: The great Lord, our Master of Kurungudi, the Dweller of Tiruthankal, the Supreme One of Karambanur where pearls gleam, who having swallowed the seven dark mighty seas, the seven mountains and these seven worlds yet remained as if unsated — Him I beheld at the southern Srirangam.

4.translit:
maaneey nOkku nallaar madhi pol mugatthulavum,
ooneey kaN vaaLikku uDaindhu ODi vandhun aDaindhEn,
kOnee kuRunguDiyuL kuzhagaa tirunaRaiyoorth
thEnee, varupunal soozh tiruviNNagaraanee.
4.meaning: Broken by the flesh-piercing arrow-glances that play upon the moon-like faces of fair women whose gaze is gentle as a doe's, I came running and took refuge in you. O my King, O graceful youth of Kurungudi, O honey of Tirunaraiyur, O Lord of Tiruvinnagar girt with flowing waters!

5.translit:
thavaLa iLampiRai thuLLu munneerth thaNmalarth thenRalODu anRil onRith
thuvaLa en nenjagam sOravErum soozhpani naaL thuyilaadhiruppEn
ivaLumOr peNkoDi enRu irangaar en nalam aindhum mun koNDupOna
kuvaLai malarniRa vaNNarmannu kuRunguDikkee ennai uytthiDumin.
5.meaning: The white young crescent moon, the tossing sea, the cool flowery southern breeze together with the anril bird's cry — they make my heart droop and waste away; on these mist-encircled nights I lie sleepless. He who took away all five of my graces never relents to think 'she too is but a tender maiden.' To Kurungudi, abode of the One hued like the blue-lily blossom, convey me!

6.translit:
thaadhavizh malligai pulli vandha thaNmadhiyin iLavaaDai inneE
oodhai tiritandhu uzhaRiyuNNa Or iravum uRangEn uRangum
pEdhaiyar pEdhaimaiyaal irundhu pEsilum pEsuga peyvaLaiyaar
kOdhai naRumalar mangaimaarvan kuRunguDikkee ennai uytthiDumin.
6.meaning: The cool gentle northerly that comes embracing the pollen-shedding jasmine, the whirling wind roaming and gnawing at me — for these I sleep not even a single night. Let the foolish girls in their folly sit and gossip if they will, let those bangled ones speak on! To Kurungudi of the Lord who bears upon his chest the Lady of fragrant flower-garlands, convey me!

7.translit:
kaalaiyum maalai otthu uNDu kanguL naazhigai oozhiyin neeNDulaavum,
pOlvadhOr thanmai pugundhuniRkum pongazhalE okkum vaaDai solli
maalavan maamaNi vaNNan maayam maRRum uLavavai vandhiDaamun kOla
mayilpayilum puRavil kuRunguDikkee ennai uytthiDumin.
7.meaning: Morning too has become like evening and is consumed away; the watches of the night stretch out long as an aeon and drag on — such is the state that has seized me. The north wind speaks, scorching like blazing fire. Before the wiles of the great dark Lord hued like a fine sapphire, and all the other things, come upon me — convey me to Kurungudi of the groves where lovely peacocks throng!

8.translit:
karumaNi pooNDu veN naagu aNaindhu kaarimil ERRanar thaazhndhulaavum
orumaNi yOsai en uLLandhaLLa Or iravum uRangaadhiruppEn
perumaNi vaanavar ucchivaittha pEraruLaaLan perumaipEsi
kurumaNi neerkozhikkum puRavil kuRunguDikkee ennai uytthiDumin.
8.meaning: As the dark-maned bulls embrace the white cows and roam with lowered necks, the single bell's ringing pushes against my heart, and I lie sleepless not a single night. Telling of the greatness of the great-jeweled, infinitely gracious One whom the celestials hold upon their heads — convey me to Kurungudi of the groves where waters wash up gleaming gems!

9.translit:
thiNDimil ERRin maNiyum aayan theenguzhal yOsaiyum thenRalODu
koNDadhOr maalaiyum andhi eenRa kOla iLampiRaiyODu kooDi
paNDaiya allavivai namakkup paaviyEn aaviyai vaaTTanjeyyum
koNDal maNiniRa vaNNarmannu kuRunguDikkee ennai uytthiDumin.
9.meaning: The bell of the strong-humped bull, the sound of the cowherd's sweet flute, the evening that came with the southern breeze, joined with the lovely young crescent that dusk brought forth — these are not as they once were to me; they torment and wither the life of sinful me.
