To Kurungudi, abode of the One hued like the rain-cloud, like a dark gem, convey me!

10.translit:
elliyum nanpagalum irundhE Esilum Esuga Endhizhaiyaar
nallar avar tiRam naam aRiyOm naaNmaDam accham namakku ingillai
vallana solli magizhvarEnum maamaNi vaNNarai naam maRavOm
kollai vaLariLa mullaipulgu kuRunguDikkee ennai uytthiDumin.
10.meaning: Let the well-adorned women sit and revile me by night and by broad day if they will — those good ones, we know not their way. Modesty, reserve and fear are no longer ours here. Though they speak cutting words and take delight, we will not forget the Lord hued like a fine sapphire. To Kurungudi where the tender wild-jasmine of the meadows grows and clings, convey me!

11.translit:
sengaN neDiya kariyamEnith thEvar oruvar ingE pugundhu en
angam meliya vaLaikazhala aadhukolO enRu sonnapinnai
aingaNai villidhan aaNmai ennODu aaDum adhanai aRiyamaaTTEn
kongalar thaNpaNai soozh puRavil kuRunguDikkee ennai uytthiDumin.
11.meaning: A certain Lord of the gods, red-eyed, tall, with a dark body, entered here, and after my frame wasted away and my bangles slipped loose, He asked 'what may this be?' — and then I cannot understand the manliness with which the five-arrowed bowman (Kama) sports against me. To Kurungudi amid groves girt with cool, nectar-blossoming fields, convey me!

12.translit:
kEvalam anRu kaDalinOsai kENminkaL aayankai aambalvandhu en
aavi aLavum aNaindhuniRkum anRiyum aindhu kaNaitherindhiTTu
EvalangaaTTi ivanoruvan ippaDiyE pugundhu eydhiDaamun
kOvalar kootthan kuRippaRindhu kuRunguDikkee ennai uytthiDumin.
12.meaning: It is no small thing — listen, the roar of the sea! And the cowherd's flute-pipe comes and presses upon me to the very limit of my life. Besides, this one (Kama), choosing out his five arrows and displaying his archer's prowess, comes thus and reaches me — before that, knowing the intent of the dancing cowherd-Lord, convey me to Kurungudi!

13.translit:
sOtthena ninRu thozhavirangaan thonnalangoNDu enakku inRukaaRum
pOrppadhOr poRpaDam thandhupOnaan pOyina oor aRiyEn en kongai
mootthiDuginRana maRRavanRan moyyagalam aNaiyaadhu vaaLaa
kootthan imaiyavar kOnvirumbum kuRunguDikkee ennai uytthiDumin.
13.meaning: Though I stood worshipping, crying 'I bow to you,' He did not relent; having taken away my long-cherished grace, He gave me to wear, until this day, but a golden garment, and departed — and I know not the town to which He has gone. My breasts grow aged and waste, never resting against His broad firm chest, all in vain. To Kurungudi, beloved of that Dancer, the King of the celestials, convey me!

14.translit:
seRRavan thennilangai malangath thEvarpiraan tirumaamagaLaip peRRum
en nenjagam kOyilkoNDa pEraruLaaLan perumai pEsak
kaRRavan kaamaru seerk kaliyan kaNNagatthum manatthum agalaak
koRRavan muRRulagu aaLininRa kuRunguDikkee ennai uytthiDumin.
14.meaning: The Lord of the gods who destroyed and shattered southern Lanka — though He has won the great Lady Lakshmi, He yet took my heart for His temple, that infinitely gracious One whose greatness Kaliyan (Tirumangai), learned and of lovely fame, has sung. The Victor who departs not from my eyes nor my mind, who stands ruling all the worlds — to His Kurungudi convey me!

15.translit:
akkum puliyin adhaLum uDaiyaar avaroruvar,
pakkam niRka ninRa paNbar oor pOlum
thakka maratthin thaazhsinaiyERi, thaayvaayil
kokkin piLLai veLLiRavu uNNum kuRunguDiyee.
15.meaning: This is, it seems, the town of that gracious One beside whom stands the One (Shiva) who wears bone-ornaments and a tiger's skin. It is Kurungudi, where the young heron climbs the low bough of a fitting tree and eats the white wood-apple at its mother's mouth.

16.translit:
thungaaraaravu thiraivandhu ulavath thoDukaDaluL,
pongaaraaravil thuyilum punidhar oor pOlum,
sengaal annam tigazhthaN paNaiyil peDaiyODum,
kongaar kamalath thalaril sErum kuRunguDiyee.
16.meaning: This seems to be the town of the Pure One who sleeps amid the encircling deep sea, where the lofty roaring waves come surging and rolling. It is Kurungudi, where the red-legged swan, together with its mate, settles upon the honey-laden lotus blossoms in the gleaming cool fields.

17.translit:
vaazhak kaNDOm vandhu kaaNmin thoNDeerkaaL,
kEzhal sengaN maamugil vaNNar maruvumoor,
Ezhaic sengaal in thuNai naaraikku iraitheeDi,
koozhaip paarvaik kaarvayal mEyum kuRunguDiyee.
17.meaning: We have seen that which gives life — come and behold it, O devotees! This is the town where the red-eyed One, the Boar, hued like a great dark cloud, abides. It is Kurungudi, where for its tender red-legged sweet mate the stork seeks food, feeding with watchful sidelong gaze in the dark fields.

18.translit:
siramunnaindhum aindhum sindhach chenRu, arakkan
uramum karamum thuNittha uravOn oor pOlum,
iravum pagalum een thEn mural, manRellaam
kuravin poovee thaan maNam naaRung kuRunguDiyee.
18.meaning: This seems to be the town of the mighty One who went and made the ten heads of the demon (Ravana) scatter, and severed his strength and his arms. It is Kurungudi, where night and day the bees hum over the sweet honey, and through all the common-grounds the kuravu flowers alone spread their fragrance.

19.translit:
kavvaik kaLiRRu mannar maaLak kalimaanthEr
aivarkkaay, anRu amaril uytthaan oor pOlum,
maivaitthilangu kaNNaar thangaL mozhiyoppaan,
kovvaik kanivaayk kiLLai pEsum kuRunguDiyee.
19.meaning: This seems to be the town of Him who, on that day, drove the swift-horsed chariot in battle for the five (Pandavas), so that the kings with their tumultuous war-elephants perished. It is Kurungudi, where the parrots of kovvai-fruit-red beaks speak words like the speech of women whose eyes gleam darkened with collyrium.

20.translit:
theeneer vaNNa maamalar koNDu viraiyeenthi,
thooneer paravith thozhumin ezhumin thoNDeerkaaL,
maaneer vaNNan maruvi yuRaiyum iDam vaanil
kooneer mathiyai maaDam theeNDum kuRunkuDiyee.
20.meaning: O devotees, taking up fragrant great blossoms cool as water and holding their perfume, with pure water offer worship, praise Him, rise up and bow down. The place where the ocean-hued Lord dwells abidingly is Kurungudi, whose mansions touch the curved crescent moon up in the sky.

21.translit:
vallich chiRunuN NiDaiyaa riDaineer vaikkinRa,
allal sinthai thavira aDaiminaDiyeerkaaL
sollil thiruvee yanaiyaar kanivaay eyiRoppaan,
kollai mullai mel arumbu eenum kuRunkuDiyee.
21.meaning: O servants, to be rid of the sorrow that places your hearts amid women of slender, tiny vine-like waists, take refuge there. It is Kurungudi, where the wild jasmine of the open tracts puts forth its soft buds, resembling the teeth set in the sweet-fruit mouth of women who in word are like Lakshmi herself.

22.translit:
naaraar iNDai naaNmalar koNDu namthamarkaaL,
aaraa anboo Demperumaanoor aDaiminkaL,
thaaraavaarum vaarpunal meeynthu vayalvaazhum koorvaay
naarai peeDaiyo DaaDum kuRunkuDiyee.
22.meaning: O our kinsmen, taking fresh-blossomed flowers strung into garlands, with insatiable love reach the town of our Lord. It is Kurungudi, where sharp-beaked herons sport with their mates, and ducks grazing in the flowing waters thrive in the fields.

23.translit:
ninRa vinaiyum thuyarum keDamaa malareenthi,
senRu paNimin ezhumin thozhumin thoNDeerkaaL,
enRum miravum pakalum vaNDisaipaaDa,
kunRin mullai manRiDai naaRum kuRunkuDiyee.
23.meaning: O devotees, that your enduring karma and grief may perish, holding up great blossoms, go and pay homage, rise up and worship. It is Kurungudi, where ever, night and day, the bees sing their music, and the hill-jasmine spreads its fragrance through the village commons.

24.translit:
silaiyaal ilangai seRRaan maRRoor sinaveezham,
kolaiyaar kombu koNDaan meeya kuRunkuDimeel,
kalaiyaar panuval vallaan kaliya nolimaalai
nilaiyaar paaDal paaDap paavam nillaavee.
24.meaning: He who with His bow destroyed Lanka, and who seized the deadly tusk of a furious raging elephant, abides at Kurungudi. If one sings the steadfast songs of this resonant garland of verses by Kaliyan, master of learned compositions, sin will not endure.

25.translit:
muLaikkathiraik kuRunkuDiyuL mukilai moovaa
 moovulakum kaDanthappaal muthalaay ninRa,
aLappariya aaramu thai arangam meeya
 anthaNanai anthaNartham sinthai yaanai,
viLakkoLiyai marakathaththaith thiruththaN kaavil
 veqkaavil thirumaalaip paaDak keeTTu
vaLarththathanaal payanpeRReen varuka enRu
 maDakkiLiyaik kaikooppi vangi naaLee.
25.meaning: The sprouting ray of light, the cloud at Kurungudi, the unaging First Cause that stands beyond the three worlds, the immeasurable nectar, the Brahmin Lord who abides at Srirangam, He who dwells in the minds of the holy ones, the lamp's radiance, the emerald, Tirumal at Tiruttanka and Tiruvehka -- having heard her parrot sing of Him, she said, 'By rearing you I have gained my reward; come,' and folding her hands toward the gentle parrot, she beckoned it.

26.translit:
min iDaiyaaL naayakanai viN nakaruL pon malaiyai
ponni maNi kozhikkum poong kuDanthaip poor viDaiyai
thennan kuRunkuDiyuL sem pavaLak kunRinai
manniya thaN seeRai vaLLalai maamalarmeel
26.meaning: He is the Lord of her of the lightning-slender waist, the golden mountain in the celestial city, the warrior-bull at lovely Kudandai where the Kaveri river casts up gems, the red-coral hill within southern Kurungudi, the bountiful one of cool abiding Sirai, and He who is upon the great lotus flower.

27.translit:
nambiyaith then kuRunkuDininRa, ach
semponeethika zhumthirumoorththiyai,
umbarvaanava raathiyanjoothiyai,
empiraanaiyen sollimaRappanoo.
27.meaning: The perfect Lord who stands in southern Kurungudi, the sacred Form that gleams like pure red gold, the radiance who is the source of the celestial gods above, my Master -- by what words could I ever forget Him?

28.translit:
uLanaaka veeyeNNith thannaiyonRaakaththan
selvaththai vaLanaa mathikkumim maaniDaththaikkavi paaDiyen,
kuLanaar kazhanisoozh kaNNan kuRunkuDi meymmaiyee,
uLanaaya enthaiyai enthaipemmaanai ozhiyavee?
28.meaning: What use is it to sing songs in praise of this mortal humankind, who fancy themselves to be something, esteeming their own wealth as worthy of regard -- forsaking my Father, the truly existent One, my Father's Lord who is verily present at Kannan's Kurungudi, girt by tank-fed fields?

29.translit:
engnganeeyoo annai meerkaaL! ennai munivathu neer?,
nangaLkoolath thiruk kuRunkuDi nambiyai naankaNDapin,
sanginooDum neemi yooDum thaamaraik kaNkaLooDum,
senkanivaa yonRi nooDum selkinRa thennenjamee.
29.meaning: O mothers, in what way is it that you reproach me? After I beheld the Lord of our beautiful Tirukkurungudi, my heart goes coursing toward Him -- with His conch and His discus, with His lotus eyes, and with His one red ripe-fruit mouth.

30.translit:
ennenji naalnookkik kaaNeer ennai muniyaathee,
thennan soolaith thirukkuRunkuDi nambiyai naankaNDapin
minnum noolum kuNDalamum maarvil thirumaRuvum,
mannum pooNum naankuthooLum vanthengum ninRiDumee.
30.meaning: Without reproaching me, look and see with my heart's eye: after I beheld the Lord of Tirukkurungudi of the southern groves, the lightning-bright sacred thread, the earrings, the holy mark (Srivatsa) on His chest, the abiding ornaments, and the four shoulders come and stand everywhere before me.

31.translit:
ninRiDum thisaikkum naiyumenRu annaiya rummunithir,
kunRa maaDath thirukkuRunkuDi nambiyai naankaNDapin,
venRi villum thaNDum vaaLum sakkaramumsangamum,
ninRu thoonRik kaNNuLneengaa nenjuLLum neengaavee.
31.meaning: 'She stands stock-still, she grows bewildered, she pines away' -- thus the mothers reproach me. After I beheld the Lord of Tirukkurungudi of hill-like mansions, His victorious bow, mace, sword, discus and conch stand manifest, never leaving my eyes nor leaving my heart.

32.translit:
neengkanillaak kaNNa neerkaLenRu annaiyarum munithir,
theenkoL soolaith thirukkuRunkuDi nambiyai naankaNDapin,
poonthaN maalaith thaNDuzhaayum ponmuDi yumvaDivum,
paangu thoonRum paTTumnaaNum paaviyeen pakkaththavee.
32.meaning: 'Her tears will not cease to flow' -- thus the mothers reproach me. After I beheld the Lord of Tirukkurungudi of honey-laden groves, the cool fragrant tulasi garland, the golden crown, His form, the gracefully appearing silk garment and waist-cord -- all these are beside sinful me.

33.translit:
pakkamnookki niRkum naiyumenRu annaiya rummunithir,
thakkakeerththik thirukku RunkuDi nambiyai naankaNDapin
thokkasoothith thoNDai vaayum neeNDa puruvangaLum,
thakkathaamaraik kaNNum paaviyee naaviyin meelanavee.
33.meaning: 'She stares to her side, she pines away' -- thus the mothers reproach me. After I beheld the Lord of Tirukkurungudi of fitting fame, His radiant coral-red lips, His long eyebrows, and His worthy lotus eyes hover over the very life of sinful me.

34.translit:
meelum vanpazhi nankuDikkivaL enRannai kaaNakkoDaaL
soolaisoozh thaNthiruk kuRunkuDi nambiyai naankaNDapin,
koolaneeL koDimookkum thaamaraik kaNNum kanivaayum,
neelameeniyum naanku thooLumen nenjam niRainthanavee.
34.meaning: 'She is yet another disgrace to our good house' -- so my mother will not let me see Him. After I beheld the Lord of cool Tirukkurungudi girt by groves, His shapely long curved nose, His lotus eyes, His ripe-fruit mouth, His dark-blue body and His four shoulders have filled my heart.

35.translit:
niRaintha vanpazhi nankuDikkivaL enRannai kaaNakkoDaaL
siRanthakeerththith thirukku RunkuDi nambiyai naankaNDapin,
niRainthasoothi veLLanj soozhntha neeNDapon meeniyoDum
niRainthen nuLLee ninRozhinthaan neemiyang kaiyuLathee.
35.meaning: 'She has become a full disgrace to our good house' -- so my mother will not let me see Him. After I beheld the Lord of Tirukkurungudi of surpassing fame, with His tall golden form surrounded by a flood of full radiance He came and stood abidingly filling my inmost self, the lovely discus in His hand.

36.translit:
kaiyuLnan mukamvaikkum naiyumenRu annaiya rummunithir,
maikoL maaDath thirukkuRunkuDi nambiyai naankaNDapin,
seyyathaamaraik kaNNu malkulum siRRiDai yumvaDivum,
moyyaneeLkuzhal thaazhntha thooLkaLum paaviyeen munniRkumee.
36.meaning: 'She rests her fair face on her hand, she pines away' -- thus the mothers reproach me. After I beheld the Lord of Tirukkurungudi of cloud-kissing mansions, His red lotus eyes, His waist and slender middle and form, and His shoulders over which the thick long tresses hang down stand before sinful me.

37.translit:
munnin Raayenru thoozhimaarkaLum annaiya rummunithir,
mannu maaDath thirukkuRunkuDi nambiyai naankaNDapin,
senni neeNmuDi yaathiyaaya ulappi laNikalaththan,
kannal paalamu thaakivanthen nenjam kazhiyaanee.
37.meaning: 'You stand fixed before Him' -- thus both my companions and the mothers reproach me. After I beheld the Lord of Tirukkurungudi of abiding mansions, He of endless adornments beginning with the tall crown upon His head, becoming sugarcane, milk and nectar, has come and will not leave my heart.

38.translit:
kazheeyamikkathoor kaathala LivaLen Rannai kaaNakkoDaaL,
vazhuvil keerththith thirukkuRunkuDi nambiyai naankaNDapin,
kuzhumith theevar kuzhaangaLthozhach soothiveL LaththinummuLLee,
ezhuvathoo ruruven nenjuLLezhum aarkku maRivarithee.
38.meaning: 'This girl is one of excessive, transgressing love' -- so my mother will not let me see Him. After I beheld the Lord of Tirukkurungudi of flawless fame, while the gathered hosts of the gods throng to worship, a single Form rises up within the flood of radiance and rises within my heart -- hard for anyone to know.

39.translit:
aRivariya piraanai yaazhiyangaiyanai yeeyalaRRi,
naRiyanan malarnaaDi nankurukoorch chaDakoopan sonna,
kuRikoLaa yiraththuL Livaipaththum thirukkuRun kuDiya thanmeel
aRiyak kaRRuvallaar vaiTTaNavaraazhkaDal njaalaththuLLee.
39.meaning: Crying out to the Lord hard to know, He of the discus in His beautiful hand, and seeking fragrant fine blossoms, Satakopan of good Kurukur spoke these ten among the thousand significant verses upon Tirukkurungudi. Those who learn them well so as to understand become true Vaishnavas in this world girt by the deep sea.

0.translit:
nootRa noNbileen nuNNaRi vileenaakilum ini unnaivittu, onRum
aatRa kinRileen aravinaNai ammaanee,
seetRut taamarai sennel oodumalar siriivara mangalanagar,
veetRirunda endaay, unakku migaiyalleen angee.
0.meaning: I have observed no austerities, I possess no subtle wisdom; yet now, having left You, I can no longer endure anything at all, O my Lord who reclines upon the serpent-couch! O my Father who graciously dwells enthroned in Sirivaramangalanagar, where red paddy blossoms amid the lotuses of the muddy fields - I am no excess burden to You there.

1.translit:
angutReen alleen ingutReen alleen unnaik kaaNum avaavil
veezhndu, naan engutReenum alleen ilangaiseRRa ammaanee,
tingaL seermaNi maadam needu siriivara mangala nagaruRai,
sangu sakkarat taay! tamiyeenukku aruLaayee.
1.meaning: I am not there, nor am I here; fallen into the longing to behold You, I am nowhere at all, O my Lord who destroyed Lanka! O You who bear the conch and discus, dwelling in Sirivaramangalanagar with its tall, jewelled mansions that reach up to touch the moon - grant Your grace to me, forlorn and alone.

2.translit:
karuLa putkodi sakka rappadai vaana naada! eng kaarmugil vaNNaa,
poruLallaada ennaip poruLaakki adimai koNdaay,
teruLkoL naanmaRai vallavar palarvaazh siriivara mangalanagarkku,
aruLseydangirun daayaRi yeenoru kaimmaaRee.
2.meaning: O Lord of the celestial realm, who bears Garuda the eagle as Your banner and the discus as Your weapon, O You of my dark-cloud hue! Though I was a thing of no worth, You made me into something of worth and took me into Your service. Having graciously dwelt there in Sirivaramangalanagar, where many masters of the lucid four Vedas live - I know not any single recompense to render You.

3.translit:
maaRu seerpadai nootRuvar manga voraivarkku aayanRu maayappoor paNNi,
neeRu seyda endaay, nilangeeNda ammaanee,
teeRu nyaanattar veeda veeLviyaRaac siriivara mangalanagar,
eRiveetRu irundaay, unnai engeydak koovuvanee?
3.meaning: O my Father, who, for the sake of the five, waged a wondrous war that day and reduced to ashes the hundred hostile foes arrayed for battle! O my Lord who once split open the earth! You dwell exalted and enthroned in Sirivaramangalanagar, where the Vedic sacrifices of those of clear wisdom never cease - how shall I call out so as to reach You?

4.translit:
eydak koovudal aavadee enakku? evvadey vattu Laayumaay ninRu,
kaida vangaLsey yumkaru meeniyam maanee,
seyda veeLviyar vaiyat teevaraRaac siriivara mangalanagar,
kaittozha irundaay adunaanum kaNdeenee.
4.meaning: Is it even possible for me to call out and reach You? O my Lord of dark hue, who abides within every deity and works Your wondrous deeds! You remain in Sirivaramangalanagar, where the gods of earth who perform sacrifices never cease, so that hands may fold in worship before You - and that too I have now beheld.

5.translit:
eena maaynilang keeNdaven appanee! kaNNaa! enRum ennai yaaLudai,
vaana naayakanee! maNimaa NikkaccudaRee,
teena maampozhil taNsiriivaramangalat tavarkkai tozhavuRai
vaana maamalai yee! adiyeen tozha vandaruLee.
5.meaning: O my Father who became the boar and split open the earth! O Kanna (Krishna)! O Lord of the heavens who forever holds me as Your own! O radiant gem and ruby! O great heavenly mountain who dwells, so that the people may fold their hands in worship, in cool Sirivaramangalam with its honey-rich mango groves - come and grant Your grace that I, Your servant, may worship You.

6.translit:
vandaruLi yennenj idangoNda vaanavar kozhundee! ulagukkoor
mundait taaytandayee! muzhueezhulagu muNdaay,
sendo zhilavar veeda veeLviyaRaac siriivara mangalanagar,
andamil pugazhaay! adiyeenai agatReelee.
6.meaning: O tender shoot among the celestials, who came graciously and made my heart Your dwelling-place! O primordial Mother and Father to all the world! O You who swallowed all the seven worlds entire! O One of endless glory, who dwells in Sirivaramangalanagar where the Vedic sacrifices of the righteous-doing never cease - do not cast me, Your servant, away.

7.translit:
agatRa neevaitta maayaval laimpulangaLaam avai nankaRindanan,
agatRi ennaiyum neearuny seetRil veezhtti kaNdaay,
pagaRkadir maNimaadam needu siriivara mangai vaaNanee! enRum
pugaRkariya endaay! puLLinvaay piLandaanee!
7.meaning: I have well understood those five powerful, deluding senses that You set in place to drive me away. Having driven me off, You have, behold, cast even me into the cruel mire (of worldly existence). O Lord who dwells in Sirivaramangai, with its jewelled mansions tall as the daytime sun-rays! O my Father, hard ever to describe! O You who tore apart the beak of the (demon) bird!

8.translit:
puLLinvaay piLandaay, marudidai pooyinaay, erudeezh adartta, en
kaLLa maayavanee! karumaaNikkaccudaree!
teLLiyaar tirunaan maRaigaL vallaar malitaN siriivara mangai,
uLLirunda endaay! aruLaay uyyumaa Renakkee.
8.meaning: O You who tore apart the beak of the (demon) bird, who passed between the twin marudu trees, who subdued the seven bulls, my deceptive, wondrous One! O radiant dark ruby! O my Father who dwells within cool, abundant Sirivaramangai, where the clear-minded masters of the sacred four Vedas thrive - grant me Your grace, that I may be saved.

9.translit:
aaRe nakkunin paadamee saraNaagat tandozhindaay, unakkoorkaim
maaRu naanonRu ileenenadaaviyum unadee,
seeru koLkarum pumperuny sennellum malitaN siriivara mangai
naaRu poondaN tuzhaaymudi yaay! teyva naayakanee!
9.meaning: You have granted me Your very feet as my sole refuge and way; I have not one recompense to render You, and even my soul itself is Yours. O You crowned with the cool, fragrant, blossoming tulasi, who dwells in cool, abundant Sirivaramangai, rich with thick sugarcane and tall red paddy! O Lord of the gods!

10.translit:
teyva naayagan naaraNan tirivikkiraman adiyiNaimisai,
koykoL poompozhil soozhkuru koorccada koopan
seyda aayirat tuLLivai taNsiriivaramangai meeya pattudan,
vaigal paada vallaar vaanoorkku aaraa amudee.
10.meaning: Upon the twin feet of the divine Lord, Narayana, Trivikrama, Satakopan of Kurugur - girt with flowering groves whose blossoms are plucked - composed a thousand verses. Those who can daily sing these ten among them, set upon cool Sirivaramangai, shall be insatiable nectar even to the celestials.

0.translit:
piNDiyaar maNDai eenthip piRar-manai thiritan thuNNum,
uNDiyaan saabam theerttha oruvanoor, ulaka meettum
kaNDiyoor arangam meyyam kacchipeer mallai enRu
maNDinaar, uyyal allaal maRRaiyaark kuyya laamee?
0.meaning: He who freed Shiva—the one who, holding a skull-bowl made from the flower-laden tree, wandered begging at others’ houses and ate from it—of his curse: that incomparable Lord’s towns are those the whole world praises. Only those who eagerly throng to Kandiyur, Arangam (Srirangam), Meyyam, Kachi (Kanchi), Per, and Mallai (Mallapuram) can be saved—for whom else is there any salvation?

0.translit:
puLingkuDikkiDandhu vaaguNamangkaiyirundhu vaikundhaththuLninRu
dheLindha en sindhai agangazhiyaadhE ennai aaLvaay enak karuLi
naLirndha seer ulagam moonRuDan viyappa naangkaL kooththaaDi ninRu aarppa
puLingku neer mugilin pavaLam pOl kanivaay sivappa ri kaaNa vaaraayE.
0.meaning: O Lord, You who recline at Tiruppulingudi, who are seated at Varagunamangai, and who stand within Vaikuntha! Having so ruled me that You would never depart from my clear mind, please grant me Your grace. While the three cool and glorious worlds marvel together, and while we dance and shout in joy, come forth so that I may behold You, Your fruit-like mouth reddening like coral against the rain-cloud bright as clear water.

1.translit:
engkaL kaN mugappE ulargaL ellaam iNaiyaDi thozhudhu Ezhu niRainjki
thangkaL anbu aaRa thamadhu sol valaththaal thalaiththalai siRandha poosippa
thingkaL sEr maaDath thiruppuLingkuDiyaay thiruvaikundhaththuLLaay dhEvaa
ingkaN maa njaalaththu idhanuLum oru naaL irundhiDaay veeRRiDangkoNDE.
1.meaning: O Lord of Tiruppulingudi whose mansions reach up to the moon, You who dwell within the sacred Vaikuntha, O God! Before our very eyes, may all the people of the world worship Your twin feet, bow low, and -- so that their love may overflow -- by the power of their praising words, vie with one another in offering ever more excellent worship. For even one day, take Your seat here too, in this great wide earth, occupying it as Your exalted abode.

0.translit:
puLingkudik kidandhu vaakuNamangkai irundhu vaikundhaththuL ninRu
theLindha en sindhai agam kazhiyaadhE ennai aaLvaay enakku aruLi
aLirndha seer ulagam moonRudan viyappa naangaL kooththaadi ninRaarppa
puLingku neer mugilin pavaLam pol kanivaay sivappa ari kaaNa vaaraayE.
0.meaning: O Lord, You who recline at Puḷiṅkuḍi, who are seated at Vaḻuṇaṅkai, and who stand in Vaikuntha (Tiruvaikuntham) — having graced me so that You will rule over me and never depart from my clear-thinking heart. While the three cool, glorious worlds wonder together, and while we dance and shout in joy, come forth so that I may behold You like a lion, Your fruit-red lips reddening like coral against the dark rain-cloud hue.

0.translit:
kodiyaar maadak koazh urakatthum puLingkudiyum,
madiyaadhu inne nee thuyil mEvi makizhndhadhu thaan,
adiyaar allal thavirttha asaivO? anREl,
ippadi thaan neeNdu thaaviya asaivO? paNiyaayE.
0.meaning: In banner-decked TirukkOzhi with its mansions, and in TiruppuLingkudi, You repose unwearied in joyful slumber. Is this restful repose because You have removed the suffering of Your devotees? Or is it the weariness from once stretching out and striding across this whole world? Pray tell me.

1.translit:
paNdai naaLaale nin thiruvaruLum pangkayatthaaL thiruvaruLum
koNdu nin kOyil seeyththup palpadikaal kudi kudi vazhi vandha naatcheyyum
thoNdarorkku aruLi sOdhi vaay thiRandhu un thaamaraik kaNkaLaal nOkkaay
thoNdu iraippu orunal thaNpaNai soozhndha thiruppuLingkudik kidaadhaane.
1.meaning: To the devotees who from days of old, blessed by Your grace and by the grace of the lotus-dwelling Goddess, have swept Your temple and served generation upon generation, open Your radiant mouth and look upon them with Your lotus eyes. O Lord who reclines at TiruppuLingkudi, surrounded by cool watery fields beside the murmuring Porunal river full of devotees!

2.translit:
kudik kidandhu aakkam seydhu nin theerttha padimaikku uRREval seydhu un pon
adik kudavaadhe vazhi varuginRa adiyarorkku aruLi nee oru naaL
padikkaLavaaka nimirttha nin paadha pangkayame thalaikku aNiyaay
kodik koL pon madhiL soozh kuLir vayal sOlai thiruppuLingkudik kidandhaane.
2.meaning: Bless the devotees who, dwelling here as Your servants and prospering, perform humble service at Your sacred waters and follow the path without ever ceasing to honor Your golden feet. One day place upon our heads those very lotus feet of Yours which once stretched to measure out the whole earth. O Lord who reclines at TiruppuLingkudi, encircled by golden ramparts bearing banners, by cool fields and groves!

3.translit:
kidandha naaL kidandhaay ettanai kaalam kidatthi un thiruvudambu asaiya
thodarndhu kuRReval seydhu thol adimai vazhi varum thoNdarorku aruLi
thadangkoL thaamaraik kaN vizhitthu nee ezhundhu un thaamarai mangkaiyum neeyum
idangkoL moovulakum thozha irundhu aruLaay thiruppuLingkudik kidandhaane.
3.meaning: For all the days You have reclined, how much longer will You lie, wearying Your sacred body? Bless the devotees who, following the ancient path of bondage, continuously perform their humble service. Open Your wide lotus eyes and rise, O Lord; together with Your lotus-like Lady, grace us by being seated so that the three spacious worlds may worship You. O Lord who reclines at TiruppuLingkudi!

4.translit:
puLingkudik kidandhu vaa kuNamangkai irundhu vaikundhatthuL ninRu
theLindha en sindhai akam kazhiyaadhe ennai aaLvaay enakku aruLi
naLirndha seer ulaku moonRudan viyappa naangkaL kootthaadi ninRu aarppa
puLingu neer mukilin pavaLam pOR kanivaay sivappa ari kaaNa vaaraaye.
4.meaning: You who recline at PuLingkudi, who are seated at TirukkuNamangkai, and who stand in Vaikuntha, You rule me without ever leaving my clarified mind. Bless me, so that while the three cool glorious worlds marvel and we dance and shout in joy, You may come, Your coral-red fruit-like lips reddening, O lion-like Lord, that I may behold You like a rain-cloud.

5.translit:
pavaLam pOl kanivaay sivappa nee kaaNa vandhu nin pal nilaa mutham
thavazh kadhir muRuval seydhu nin thirukkaN thaamarai thayangka ninRu aruLaay
pavaLa nal padar keezh sangku uRai porunal thaN thiruppuLingkudik kidandhaay
kavaLa maa kaLiRRin idar keda thadatthuk kaaysinap paRai oorndhaane.
5.meaning: Come so that I may behold Your coral-like fruit-red lips, smiling a moonlit smile with the radiance of Your teeth, and graciously stand with Your sacred lotus eyes shining. O Lord who reclines at cool TiruppuLingkudi by the Porunal where conches dwell beneath fine coral spreads, who rode the fierce-natured Garuda to the pool to destroy the affliction of the great mouthful-eating elephant!

6.translit:
kaaysinap paRavai oorndhu pon malaiyin meemisaik kaar mukil pOl
maa sinam maali maali maan enRu angku avar pada kananRu mun ninRa
kaaysina vEndhe kadhir mudiyaane kali vayal thiruppuLingkudiyaay
kaaysina vaazhi sangku vaaL vil thaNdu Endhi em idar kadivaane.
6.meaning: Mounting the fierce-natured bird and standing before them like a dark cloud atop the golden mountain, You blazed in wrath so that the furious Mali, MalimAn and their kind perished. O King of fierce anger, O Lord with the radiant crown, O Lord of TiruppuLingkudi rich with paddy fields! Bearing the fierce discus, conch, sword, bow and mace, You destroy our afflictions.

7.translit:
em idar kadindhu ingu ennai aaLvEne imaiyavar thamakkum aangu aLaiyaay
sem madal maru thaamaraip pazhanath thaN thiruppuLingkudik kidandhaay
nammudaiya adiyar kavvai kaNdu uhandhu naangaL ithu uLam koora
im madavulakar kaaNa nee oru naaL irundhidaay engkaL kaN mukappE.
7.meaning: O Lord who removes our distress and rules me here, who are unattainable even to the celestials, who reclines at cool TiruppuLingkudi with its fields of fragrant red-petaled lotuses! Seeing the clamor of joy of us, Your servants, and rejoicing, so that our hearts overflow, do one day be seated before our very eyes, that these simple worldly folk may behold You.

8.translit:
engkaL kaN mukappE ulakar ellaam iNaiyadi thozhudhu ezhu niRainjki
thangkaL anbu aara thamadhu sol valatthaal thalaitthalai siRandhu poosippa
thingkaL sEr maadath thiruppuLingkudiyaay thiruvaikundhatthuL Laay dhEvaa
ingaN maa njaalath ithanuLum oru naaL irundhidaay veeRRu idangkoNde.
8.meaning: O Lord of TiruppuLingkudi with mansions touching the moon, who dwells within sacred Vaikuntha, O God! So that before our very eyes all the people, worshipping Your twin feet, rise and bow down and, with their love brimming over, vie with one another in praising You with the eloquence of their words, do one day take Your seat in glory here too, within this great wide world.

9.translit:
veeRRu idangkoNdu viyan koL maa njaalath ithanuLum irundhidaay adiyOm
pORRi OvaadhE kaN iNai kuLira poodhu malar aakatthaip paruka
sERR iLa vaaLai sennel oodu kaLum sezhum paNai thiruppuLingkudiyaay
kooRRam aaya asurar kula mudhal arindha kodu vinaip padaikaL vallane.
9.meaning: Take Your seat in glory within this vast great world too, so that we Your servants may praise You ceaselessly, our eyes cooled, and drink in the flower-like beauty of Your form. O Lord of TiruppuLingkudi with rich fields where tender vaaLai fish move through the red paddy in the mud! O wielder of the cruel deadly weapons that, like Death itself, cut down the very root of the race of demons!

10.translit:
kodu vinaip padaikaL vallaiyaay amararkku idar keda asurarkatku idar sey
kadu vinai nanjE ennudai amudhe kali vayal thiruppuLingkudiyaay
vadivu iNai illaa malar makaL maRRai nila makaL pidikkum mel adiyai
kodu vinaiyEnum pidikka nee oru naaL koovudhal varudhal seyyaaye.
10.meaning: O wielder of cruel deadly weapons, who removes the affliction of the celestials and inflicts affliction on the demons, O fierce destroying poison to the wicked, yet my own nectar! O Lord of TiruppuLingkudi with its abundant fields! Those tender feet which the peerlessly beautiful flower-Goddess Lakshmi and the Earth-Goddess press in service — that even sinful I may press them, do one day call me to You or come to me.

11.translit:
koovudhal varudhal seydhidaay enRu kurai kadal kadaindhavan thannai
mEvi nangu amarndha viyan punal porunal vazhudhi naadan satakOpan
naa iyal paadal aayiratthuLLum ivaiyum Or patthum vallaarkaL
Ovudhal inRi ulakam moonRu aLandhaan adi iNai uLLatthu OrvaarE.
11.meaning: "Do call me or come to me" — thus did SatakOpan of the Vazhudhi land, by the vast-watered Porunal, sing of the Lord who churned the roaring ocean. Among his thousand tongue-born songs, those who can master these ten too will, without ceasing, ever contemplate within their hearts the twin feet of the Lord who measured the three worlds.

0.translit:
thuvaLil maamaNi maada moongu tolaivil limanga lamthozhum
ivaLai, neerini yannai meer! umak kaasai yillai vidumino,
tavaLa voNsangu sakka ramendrum thaama raiththadang kaNendrum,
kuvaLai yoNmalark kaNgaL neermalka nindru nindru kumurume.
0.meaning: O mothers, you have no further hope in this girl who worships Tolaivillimangalam, where flawless great-gemmed mansions rise high — so let her be! Saying 'the bright white conch,' saying 'the discus,' saying 'eyes wide as the lotus pool,' her eyes, bright as the lovely kuvaLai blossom, brim with tears, and standing again and again she sobs and laments.

1.translit:
kumiru moosai vizhavo liththolai villi mangalam koNdupukku,
amuda menmozhi yaaLai neerumak kaasai yindri agatrineer,
timirkkoN daaloththu nirkkum matrivaL deeva deevapi raanendre,
nimiyum vaayodu kaNgaL neermalka nekko sindu karaiyume.
1.meaning: Having let her enter Tolaivillimangalam that resounds with the swelling clamor of festivals, you, O mothers, have driven away from yourselves this girl of nectar-soft words, no longer of any use to you. Now she stands as if seized by stupor; uttering 'O Lord, God of gods,' with quivering lips and eyes brimming with tears, she melts and dissolves and wears away.

2.translit:
karaikoL paimpozhil thaNpa Naiththolai villi mangalam koNdupukku,
uraiko linmozhi yaaLai neerumak kaasai yindri agatrineer,
tiraikoL peLvaththu seerntha tumthisai gnaalam thaavi yaLandadum,
niraigaL meeyththadu mepi tatri nedungaN Neermalka nirkkume.
2.meaning: Having let her enter cool-watered Tolaivillimangalam, ringed with fresh groves along its banks, you, O mothers, have driven away from yourselves this girl of sweet established speech, no longer of any use to you. Babbling of His reclining on the wave-tossed ocean, of His striding and measuring the worlds of all directions, and of His herding the cattle, she stands with her long eyes brimming with tears.

3.translit:
nirkkum naanmarai vaaNar vaazhtolai villi mangalang kaNdapin,
arka mondrum arivu raaLmalin daaLkaN deerivaL annaimeer,
karkkum kalviyel laamka rungadal vaNNan kaNNapi raanendre,
orka mondrumi laLugandugandu uLmagizhndu kuzhaiyume.
3.meaning: O mothers, behold — since she beheld Tolaivillimangalam, where the masters of the four Vedas dwell in steadfast prosperity, she has not a particle of sense left, but is overflowing. All the learning she would learn becomes only 'Kannan, the gracious Lord, dark-ocean-hued'; without the least weariness, rejoicing and rejoicing, glad within, she softens and melts.

4.translit:
kuzhaiyum vaaLmugath thezhai yaiththolai villi mangalam koNdupukku,
izhaikoL soothichchen thaama raikkat piraani rundamai kaattineer,
mazhaipey thaalokkum kaNNa neerinodu andru thottummai yaandu,ivaL
nuzhaiyum sindaiyaL annai meer.thozhum aththi saiyutru nokkiye.
4.meaning: Having led this tender girl of radiant bright face into Tolaivillimangalam, you showed her the Lord seated there with red-lotus eyes glowing like set jewels. From that day on, with tears that fall like pouring rain, O mothers, she has had her mind ever entering and dwelling there, worshipping and gazing toward that direction.

5.translit:
nokkum pakkamel laamka rumpodu sennel oongusen thaamarai,
vaaykkum thaNporu nalva dakarai vando laivilli mangalam,
nokku meelath thisaiyal laalmaru noki laLvaigal naaLdorum,
vaaykkoL vaasaka mumma NivaNNan naama meyivaL annaimeer.
5.meaning: Wherever she looks, on every side, sugarcane and red paddy and tall red lotuses flourish — such is Tolaivillimangalam on the northern bank of the cool Porunal river. If she looks at all, she looks toward no other than that direction, every single day; and the words her mouth holds, O mothers, are only the names of the sapphire-hued Lord.

6.translit:
annaimeer!aNimaamayil sirumaani vaLnammaik kaivalindu,
enna vaarththaiyum keetkuraaL tolaivilli mangalam endrallaal,
munnam nootra vidhiko lomugil vaNNan maayango lo,avan
sinnamumtirunaama mumivaL vaayanagaL tirundave.
6.meaning: O mothers, this little doe-like girl, lovely as a beautiful peacock, has wrested herself from us and will heed no word of ours but only 'Tolaivillimangalam.' Is it the fruit of some penance she performed long ago, or is it the magic of the cloud-hued Lord? — for His emblems and His holy names alone come forth, perfected, from her mouth.

7.translit:
tirundu vedamum veelvi yumtiru maama gaLirum thaam,malin,
tirundu vaazhporu nalva dakarai vaNdo laivilli mangalam,
karunda dangaNNi kaitho zhuthaannaaLto dangiyin naaLdorum,
irundi rundutaravinda losana! endren drenaindi rangume.
7.meaning: On the northern bank of the Porunal, the prosperous and flourishing Tolaivillimangalam abounds with perfected Vedas, sacrifices, and glorious noble ladies. From the day this girl of large dark eyes folded her hands and worshipped it, every day since, sitting and sitting she cries 'O lotus-eyed One!' again and again, and softens and grieves.

8.translit:
iranginaaLdorum vaayve reeei yivaL kaNNaneergaL alamara,
maranga Lumirang uvagai maNivaNNavo. endru koovumaal,
turangam vaaypiLan daanu raitolai villi mangala mendru ,tan
karangaL kooppith thozhumav voorththiru naamang katradar pinnaiye.
8.meaning: Day after day she grieves, her mouth raving, her tears whirling, and in a way that would make even the trees take pity she cries out 'O sapphire-hued One!' Saying it is Tolaivillimangalam, the abode of Him who tore apart the horse-demon's mouth, she folds her hands and worships — ever since she learned the holy name of that town.

9.translit:
pinnaikol nilamaamagaLkol? tirumagaLkol? pirandittaaL,
enna maayango lo?i vaLnedu maalen drenindru koovumaal,
munni vandavan nindri runduraiyum tolaivilli mangalam
senni yaalvaNang kumavvoorththiru naamam keetpadu sindaiye.
9.meaning: Is it Nappinnai, or the great Earth-goddess, or Lakshmi, that has been born as her? What magic is this? — for she stands ever crying 'O Nedumal!' Bowing with her head to Tolaivillimangalam, where He came forth and stands and abides, her one thought is to hear the holy name of that town.

10.translit:
sindai yaalumsol laalum seygaiyinaalum deeva piraanaiye,
tandai thaayen radainda vaNkuru koora varsada kopansol,
mundai yaayirath thuLLi vaitolai villi mangalath thaichchonna,
sendamizhpaththum vallaaradimai sey vaartiru maalukke.
10.meaning: In thought, in word, and in deed, taking the Lord, God of gods, alone as father and mother and refuge — these are the words of Satakopan of bountiful Kurukur. Those who master these ten pure-Tamil verses on Tolaivillimangalam, set within his foremost thousand, shall render loving service to Tirumal alone.

0.translit:
kooDac chen reenini enko DukkeeN? koalvaLai nenjath thoDakka mellaam,
paaDaR Roziya izhanthu vaikal palvaLai yaarmun parisa zhintheen,
maaDak koDimathiL thenku Lanthai vaNkuDa paalninRa maayak kootthan,
aaDal paRavai uyartha velpoar aazhi valavi nai yaatharitthee.
0.meaning: Having gone seeking to be united with him, what can I now offer? Through love of the victorious wielder of the discus, that wondrous dancer who stands in the rich western quarter of southern Kulanthai, the city of mansions, banners and moon-touching ramparts, who raised the bird (Garuda) as his flag in battle, I have lost all the bangles on my rounded wrists, my beauty waning day by day, and I have lost my dignity before the women adorned with many bangles.

0.translit:
uNNuny sooru parukuneer tinnumveR Rilaiyumellaam
kaNNan, emperumaan nenRenRee kaNkaL neermalki,
maNNinuL avanseer vaLammik kavanoor vinavi,
tiNNa menniLa maanpuku moortiruk kooLooree.
0.meaning: The rice she eats, the water she drinks, the betel-leaf she chews — all of it she calls 'Kannan, my Lord!' over and over, her eyes brimming with tears. Inquiring after the town where his glory and abundance abound upon this earth, the place into which my tender doe-like girl has surely entered is the sacred Tirukkolur.

1.translit:
oorum naadum ulakamum tannaippool avanudaiya
peerum taarkaLumee pitaRRa, kaRpu vaan idaRi,
seerum nal vaLam seer pazhanat tirukkooloorkkee
poorumkol, uraiyeer, kodiyeenkodi-poovaikaLee?
1.meaning: Her town, her land, the whole world — like her — babble only of his name and his garlands, and her chastity has stumbled and fallen away. Tell me, O myna-birds of this sinful woman: will she reach the sacred Tirukkolur set amid good and abundant fields?

2.translit:
poovaipaingiLikaL pantutootaipoompattilkaL
yaavaiyumtirumaal tirunaamangaLee kooviyezhum en
paavaipooyinit taNpazhanattirukkooloorkkee
koovaivaaytudippa mazhaikkaNNoden seyyungkoloo?
2.meaning: The myna, the green parrots, the ball, the playthings, the silken garments — all of these she names only with Tirumal's sacred names as she rises calling out. Now that my girl has gone to cool, field-girt Tirukkolur, what will she do, with her bimba-red lips quivering and her rain-cloud eyes brimming?

3.translit:
kollai yenparko lookuNam mikkanaL enparkoloo,
sillai vaayppeN dukaLayaR seeriyuL Laarumellee,
selvam malki yavan kidanta tirukkoo loorkkee,
mellidai nudanga iLamaan sella meevinaLee.
3.meaning: Will they say it is unruly conduct? Or will they say she was a woman of surpassing virtue? Gently now, you idle-tongued women of the neighboring quarter — my young doe has set her heart on going, her slender waist swaying, toward Tirukkolur where he reclines amid swelling prosperity.

4.translit:
meevi nainthu nainthuviLai yaadaluRaa LensiRut
teevipooy, inittan tirumaal tirukkoo loooril,
pooviyal pozhilum tadamum avankoo yilungkaNdu,
aaviyuL kuLira engngaanee yukakkungkol inRee?
4.meaning: Drawn to him, wasting and wasting away, my little goddess no longer joins in any play. Going now to her own Tirumal's Tirukkolur, beholding the flower-filled groves, the ponds, and his temple — how today will she rejoice so that her very soul grows cool?

5.translit:
inRenak kutavaa takanRa iLamaan inippooy,
tentisait tilata manaiya tirukkoo loorkkee senRu,
tan tirumaal tirukkaNNum sevvaayum kaNdu,
ninRu ninRu naiyum nedungkaNkaL panimalkavee.
5.meaning: The young doe who departed without staying to comfort me today — going now and reaching Tirukkolur, which is like a tilaka-mark of the southern land, beholding her own Tirumal's sacred eyes and red lips, she will stand and stand and melt, her long eyes overflowing with tears.

6.translit:
malkuneer kaNNoodu maiyaluRRa manattanaLaay,
allunan pakalum nedumaalenRazhait tinippooy,
selvam malki avankidanta tirukkoo loorkkee,
olki yolki nadantheengngaanee pukungko loosinthee?
6.meaning: With eyes brimming with tears and a heart seized by bewildered love, by night and good day she calls out 'Nedumal!' Now, going to Tirukkolur where he reclines amid swelling prosperity — walking faintly, faintly, how, O my heart, will she enter in?

7.translit:
osinta nuNNidai meelkaiyai vaittu nonthunonthu,
kasinta nenjina LaaykkaNNa neertthuLumbac chellungkol,
osinta voNmala raaLkozhunan tirukkoo loorkkee,
kasinta nenjina Laayemmai neetthae m kaarikaiyee?
7.meaning: Resting her hand upon her drooping slender waist, aching and aching, her heart melting and tears welling in her eyes — will she go? My beautiful one, who with a melting heart forsook us, goes to Tirukkolur, the town of the lord of the bright lotus-seated lady of the swaying form.

8.translit:
kaariyam nallana kaLavai kaaNilen kaNNanukkenRu,
eeriyaa yiruppaaLi tellaam kidakka inippooy,
seeri palpazhi tooyiraippat tirukkoo loorkkee,
neerizhai nadanthaa Lemmai yonRum ninaittilaLee.
8.meaning: Saying 'whatever good things I see, they are for my Kannan,' she who remained ever tender — leaving all this behind, going now while the neighborhood loudly raises many slanders, the fine-jeweled one has walked off to Tirukkolur, not thinking of us at all.

9.translit:
ninaikkileen teyvangkaaL nedungkaN iLamaan inippooy,
anaittu lakumu daiya aravinta loosananai,
tinaittanai yumvidaa Lavansheer tirukkoo loorkkee,
manaikku vaanpazhiyum ninaiyaaL sella vaittanaLee.
9.meaning: I cannot comprehend it, O gods! The long-eyed young doe, going now and not leaving for even a grain's measure the lotus-eyed one who possesses all the worlds, has set herself to go to Tirukkolur where he dwells, not even minding the great disgrace it brings upon our house.

10.translit:
vaitta maanidi yaammathu soodanai yeyalaRRi,
kottha larpozhil soozhkuru koorcchada koopansonna,
pattu nooru Lippat tavansheer tirukkooloorkkee,
sittham vaittu raippaar tikazhpon nulakaaLvaaree.
10.meaning: Crying out to Madhusudana alone, who is her treasured great wealth — of the thousand verses spoken by Satakopan of Kurukur girt with clustering-blossomed groves, those who fix their minds and recite this ten about Tirukkolur where he dwells will rule the shining golden world.

11.translit:
kodiyaar maadak kooLu rakatthum puLingkudiyum,
madiyaa thinnee neetuyil meevi makizhntathutaan,
adiyaar allal tavirtta asaivoo? anReel,
ippaditaan neeNdu taaviya asaivoo? paNiyaayee.
11.meaning: In Tirukkolur of banner-bedecked mansions, and in Tiruppulingudi, that you should recline here in sweet sleep without weariness, delighting — is it the weariness of having removed your devotees' sorrows? Or else, is it the weariness of having stretched out and strode across these worlds thus? Pray tell me.

0.translit:
veLLaich churisangko daazhi yeendhith thaamaraik kaNNanen nenjci nooDe,
puLLaik kaDaakinRa vaaTRaik kaaNeer enjcollich cholluken annai meerkaaL,
veLLach chukamavan veeTRi rundha veda voliyum vizhaa voliyum,
piLLaik kuzhaaviLai yaaTToli yum aRaath thiruppe raiyil servan naane.
0.meaning: Behold how the lotus-eyed Lord, holding the white spiralled conch and the discus, drives His bird (Garuda) straight through my heart! What can I say of it, O mothers? I will go and join Him at Tirupperai, where in great bliss He is enthroned, and where the sound of the Vedic chanting, the sound of festivals, and the sound of children's play at games never cease.

1.translit:
naanak karunguzhal thozhi meerkaaL! annai yarkaaL! ayal seriyeerkaaL,
naanith thaninenjcam kaakka maaTTen envasam anRithi raapppa kalpoy,
thenmoyththa poompozhil thaNpa Naisoozh thendhiruppe raiyil veeTRi rundha,
vaanappi raanmaNi vaNNan kaNNan sengani vaayin thiRaththa thuve.
1.meaning: O friends with dark musk-fragrant tresses, O mothers, O neighbours of the nearby quarter! I can no longer guard this lonely heart of mine; it is no longer in my control, night and day. It has fled to the red-fruit lips of Kannan, the sapphire-hued Lord of the celestials, who is enthroned in southern Tirupperai surrounded by honey-laden flower groves and cool watery fields.

2.translit:
sengani vaayin thiRaththa thaayum senjcuDar neeNmuDith thaazhndha thaayum,
sangoDu chakkaram kaNDu kandhum thaamaraik kaNkaLuk kaTRuth theerndhum,
thingaLum naaLum vizhaava Raatha thendhiruppe raiyil veeTRi rundha,
nangaL piraanuken nenjcam thozhee! naaNum niraiyu mizhandha thuve!
2.meaning: Drawn to His red-fruit lips, bowing low at His tall crown of bright radiance, rejoicing at the sight of His conch and discus, wholly lost and given over to His lotus eyes -- O friend, my heart, devoted to our Lord who is enthroned in southern Tirupperai where festivals never cease, month after month and day after day, has lost both its modesty and its decorum!

3.translit:
izhandhavem maamai thiRaththup pona ennenjci naarumang ke ozhindhaar,
uzhandhini yaaraikkoN denu saako? ?odhak kaDaloli pola engum,
ezhundhanal vedath tholinin Roongu thendhiruppe raiyil veeTRi rundha,
muzhangusang kakkaiyan maayath thaazhndhen annaiyar kaaL!ennai yenmu nindhe?
3.meaning: My heart, which went off after Him, losing my fair complexion, has stayed behind there as well. With whom now shall I strive and toil? I have sunk into the spell of the Lord with the conch-bearing hand, who is enthroned in southern Tirupperai where the sound of the good Vedas rises and resounds everywhere like the roar of the surging ocean. O mothers, why do you reproach me?

4.translit:
munindhu chakaDam udhaiththu maayap peymulai yuNDu maruthi Dappoy,
kanindha viLavukkuk kanRe Rindha kaNNa piraanuken peNmai thoTRen,
munindhini yenseydheer annai meerkaaL. munni yavanvandhu veeTRi rundha,
kanindha pozhilthiruppe rai yiRke kaalam peRavennaik kaaTTu mine.
4.meaning: To Kannan, my dear Lord -- who in wrath kicked and shattered the cart, who sucked the life from the demoness's poisoned breast, who passed between the marudu trees, and who flung a calf to bring down the ripe wood-apples -- I have lost my womanhood. What use is your scolding now, O mothers? Take me in good time to ripe-groved Tirupperai, where, coming of His own accord, He is enthroned.

5.translit:
kaalam peRavennaik kaaTTu minkaL kaadhal kaDalin mikap peridhaal,
neela mukilvaN Naththem perumaan niRkumun nevandhen kaikkum eydhaan,
njaalath thavanvandhu veeTRi rundha naanmaRai yaaLarum velvi yovaa,
kolach chenneRkaL kavari veesum kooDu punalthiruppe rai yiRke.
5.meaning: Take me in good time, for my love is greater even than the sea! He, our Lord of the dark-cloud hue, does not come and stand before me, nor reach into my hand. Take me to watery Tirupperai, where He came down to the earth and is enthroned, where the masters of the four Vedas never cease their sacrifices and the lovely red paddy stalks sway like fly-whisk fans.

6.translit:
pereyil soozhkaDal thenni langai seTRa piraanvandhu veeTRi rundha,
peRaiyiR kepukken nenjcam naaDip perththu varavengum kaaNa maaTTen,
aarai yiningu uDaiyam thozhi. ennenjcam koova vallaaru millai,
aarai yinikkoNDen saathik kinRadhu? ennenjcam kaNDadhu vekaN Dene.
6.meaning: My heart entered Tirupperai, where the Lord who destroyed Lanka of the south, girt by walled ramparts and the sea, is enthroned; though I seek for it to come back, I cannot find it anywhere. Whom do we have here now, my friend? There is none who can call my heart back. With whom now can I accomplish anything? I have seen only what my heart has seen.

7.translit:
kaNDadhu vekoNDel laarung kooDik kaarkkaDal vaNNano dendhi Raththuk
koNDu,alar thooTRiR Radhumudha laak koNDaven kaadha luraikkil thozhee,
maNDiNi njaala mumezh kaDalum neeLvi sumbum kazhiyap peridhaal,
theNthirai soozhndhavan veeTRi rundha thendhiruppe raiyil servan senRe.
7.meaning: Seeing only that, all of them gathered together and spread scandal about me concerning my affair with the ocean-hued Lord; but from that very beginning, O friend, if I were to speak of the love I bear Him, it exceeds the dense-packed earth, the seven seas and the vast sky. I will go and join Him at southern Tirupperai, where, surrounded by clear waves, He is enthroned.

8.translit:
servanjcen Rennudaith thozhi meerkaaL! annaiyar kaaL! ennaith theTRa veNDaa,
neerkaL uraikkinRa thenni thaRku? nenjcum niRaivum enakking killai,
kaarvaNNan kaarkkaDal njaala muNDa kaNNa piraanvandhu veeTRi rundha,
ervaLa voNkazha nippazha naththuth thendhiruppe raiyinmaa nakare.
8.meaning: I will go and join Him! O my friends, O mothers, do not try to console me. What is the use of what you keep saying? I have neither heart nor composure left here with me. I will go to the great city of southern Tirupperai with its rich, bright watery fields, where Kannan, my dark-hued Lord who swallowed the cloud-girt sea-bound world, came and is enthroned.

9.translit:
nakaramum naaDum piRavum therven naaNenak killaiyen thozhi meerkaaL!
sikaram aNineDu maaDam neeDu thendhiruppe raiyil veeTRi rundha,
makara neDunguzhaik kaadhan maayan nooTRuva raiyanRu manga nooTRa,
nikaril mukilvaNNan nemi yaanen nenjcam kavarndhenai yoozhi yaane?
9.meaning: I will seek through city and country and all else; I have no modesty left, O my friends! The wondrous Lord wearing the long crocodile-shaped earrings, who is enthroned in southern Tirupperai with its tall mansions adorned with soaring spires, the peerless cloud-hued discus-bearer who that day slew the hundred (Kauravas) -- having stolen away my heart, how shall I endure the ages?

10.translit:
oozhitho Roozhi yuruvam perum seykaiyum veRavan vaiyang kaakkum,
aazhineer vaNNanai yachchu thannai aNikuru koorchchaDa kopan sonna,
kezhilan thaadhiyo raayi raththuL ivaithiruppe raiyil meya paththum,
aazhiyang kaiyanai yeththa vallaar avaraDi maiththiRath thaazhi yaare.
10.meaning: Of the matchless thousand antadi-verses sung by Satakopan of beautiful Kurukur about the ocean-hued, water-tinted Lord Achyuta -- who through age after age guards the world, His form, His names and His deeds ever differing -- these ten set in Tirupperai are these. Those who can praise the Lord with the lovely discus in His hand will sink deep into the bliss of servitude to His devotees.

0.translit:
onRun thevu mulagum uyirum maRRum yaadhumillaa
anRu, naanmugan thannodu theva rulago duyirpadaiththaan,
kunRam poolmaNi maadam needu thirukkuru kooradhanuL,
ninRa aadhippi raanniRka maRRaith theyvam naadudhire.
0.meaning: In that beginning when nothing existed at all — no deities, no worlds, no living beings, nor anything else — He created Brahma of the four faces, and the worlds of the gods, and all living creatures. While that Primal Lord stands present in Tirukkurukur, whose jewelled mansions rise tall like mountains, why do you go seeking other gods?

1.translit:
naadi neerva Nangum theyvamum ummaiyu munpadaiththaan,
veedil seerppuga zaadhippi raanavan mevi yuRaikoyil,
maada maaLigai soozhndhazha gaaya thirukkuru kooradhanaip,
paadi yaadip paravich chelmingaL pallula geerparandhe.
1.meaning: He created, of old, both you and the very deities you seek out and worship. The temple where that Primal Lord — whose glory and unbroken fame have no end — abides with delight is beautiful Tirukkurukur, encircled by mansions and palaces. O people spread across the many worlds, go forth singing, dancing, and praising it.

2.translit:
parandha theyvamum pallula gum padaith thanRuda nevizhungik,
karandhu mizhndhu kadandhi dandhadhu kaNdum theLiyagilleer,
siranga laala mararva Nangum thirukkuru kooradhanuL,
parandhiRa manRip pallulageer theyvam maRRillai pesumine.
2.meaning: He created the widespread gods and the many worlds, and in that same beginning swallowed them, hid them within, spat them forth, strode across them and lifted up the earth — yet though you have seen this you remain unenlightened. In Tirukkurukur, where the immortals bow with their heads, there is no god apart from the Supreme One; O people of the many worlds, say so.

3.translit:
pesa ninRa sivanukkum piraman thanakkum piRarkkum
naayagan avane kabaala nan mokkaththuk kaNdukoNmin
tesa maamadhiL soorndhazhagaaya thirukkuru kooradhanuL
eesan paalor avampaRaidhal ennaavadhilingiyarkke?
3.meaning: He alone is the Lord of Shiva who once stood begging, of Brahma, and of all others — recognize this in the matter of the skull and of his fine deliverance. He is the Lord present in radiant, beautiful Tirukkurukur girt with great ramparts; O you who wear the linga, what use is it to utter empty words against this Lord?

4.translit:
ilingaththitta puraaNaththeerum samaNarum saakkiyarum
valindhu vaadhu seyveergaLum maRRunun theyvamum aagininRaan
malindhu sennel kavari veesum thirukkuru kooradhanuL
polindhu ninRa piraan kaNdeer onRum poyyillai poRRumine.
4.meaning: You followers of the Puranas devoted to the linga, you Jains and Buddhists, you who argue forcefully — He has become your gods as well, and all else. He is the Lord who stands resplendent in Tirukkurukur, where abundant red paddy waves like fly-whisks. Behold him; there is nothing false in this at all — so praise him.

5.translit:
poRRi maRRor theyvam peNap puRaththittu ummaiyinne
theRRi vaiththadhel leerum veedu peRRaalula gillaiyenRe,
seRRil sennel kamalam oongu thirukkuru kooradhanuL,
aaRRa vallavan maayam kaNdeeradhu aRin dharindh oodumine.
5.meaning: Casting aside the worship of other gods, He has thus made clear and established you all — for if you attain release there will be no worlds left. In Tirukkurukur, where red paddy and lotuses rise tall from the mire, behold the wondrous power of Him who is all-capable; knowing this fully, run to him.

6.translit:
oodi yoodip palpiRappum piRandhumaR Roortheyvam,
paadi yaadip paNindhupalpadigaal vazhiye Rikkandeer,
koodi vaanava reththaninRa thirukkuru kooradhanuL,
aadu putkodi yaadhi moorththik kadimai puguvadhuve.
6.meaning: Running on and on, born into countless births, singing and dancing and bowing to other gods, climbing many a path again and again — you have seen where it leads. The true way is to enter into service of the Primal Lord whose banner bears the dancing bird (Garuda), who stands in Tirukkurukur where the celestials gather and praise him.

7.translit:
pukku adi maiyinaal thannaik kaNda maarkkaNdeyan avanai
nakkapiraanuman Ruyyak koNdadhu naaraayaNan aruLe
kokkalar thadanthaazhai velith thirukkuru kooradhanuL
mikka aadhippiraan niRka maRRaith theyvam viLambudhire.
7.meaning: When Markandeya, who entered into service and beheld Him, was saved — that salvation through the naked Lord (Shiva) was itself the grace of Narayana. While the supreme Primal Lord stands in Tirukkurukur, hedged round with broad screwpine bushes where cranes alight, why do you speak of other gods?

8.translit:
viLambum aaRu samaya mumavai yaagiyum maRRum thanpaal,
aLandhu kaaNdaR kariya naagiya aadhippi raanamarum,
vaLangoL thaNpaNai soozhndhazha gaaya thirukkuru kooradhanai,
uLangoL gnaanaththu vaimmin ummai uyyak koNdu poguRile.
8.meaning: He has become the six religions that are spoken of, and all else besides, while remaining within Himself beyond the measure of any who would fathom Him — that Primal Lord abides here. If you would be saved and carried across, set in the heart-held knowledge of your minds this beautiful Tirukkurukur, encircled by cool, fertile fields.

9.translit:
uRuva thaava deththevum evvulaga gangaLum maRRumthanpaal,
maRuvil moorththiyo doththith thanaiyum ninRavaN Namniṟkave,
seRuvil sennel karumbo doongu thirukkuru kooradhanuL
kuRiya maaNuru vaagiya neeLkudak kooththanuk kaadcheyvadhe.
9.meaning: What is worth attaining is this: while every deity and every world and all else stand within Him, He yet remains the same flawless Form abiding as Himself. It is to render service to the Lord who took the small dwarf-Brahmin form, the tall one of the pot-dance (Krishna), who stands in Tirukkurukur where red paddy and sugarcane rise tall in the fields.

10.translit:
aadcheydha thaazhippi raanaich cherndhavan vaNkuru koornagaraan
naatka mazhmagizh maalai maarbinan maaRan sadagopan,
vetkai yaalsonna paadal aayiraththuLippath thumvallaar,
meetchi yinRi vaigundha maanagar maRRadhu kaiyadhuve.
10.meaning: He who reached and served the Lord bearing the discus is Maran Satakopan of bountiful Kurukur city, on whose chest hangs a garland of fragrant magizh blossoms. Those who can master these ten among the thousand songs he sang out of longing will, without return, hold the great city of Vaikuntha as their own.

0.translit:
minnanaiya nuNNiDaiyaar virikuzhalmEl nuzhaindavaNDu
innisaik kumvilliputtoor inidamarndaay unnaikkaNDaar
ennanOnbu nORRaaLkolO ivanaippeRRa vayiruDaiyaaL
ennumvaarttai yeyduvitta iruDeekEsaa mulaiyuNaayE.
0.meaning: O Hrishikesha, you who reside lovingly in Villiputtur, where the bees that slip into the spread tresses of women with lightning-slender waists hum sweet music — please take my breast and feed. You have brought about the saying that those who behold you exclaim, "What austerity must she have performed, the woman whose womb bore this child!"

1.translit:
mennaDai yannam parandu viLaiyaaDum villiput tooruRai vaanRan
ponnaDi kaaNbadO raasayi naalen porukayaR kaNNiNai tunjaa
innaDi silODu paalamu tooTTi eDuttaven kOlak kiLiyai
unnoDu thOzhamai koLvan kuyilE ulakaLan daanvarak koovaay
1.meaning: Out of longing to behold the golden feet of Him who dwells in Villiputtur, where soft-gaited swans spread out and play, my pair of fighting carp-like eyes do not sleep. O koel, I shall make you my friend — I, who raised my lovely parrot, feeding it sweet milk-rice with my own hands. Call out so that He who measured the worlds may come!

0.translit:
Peraanaik kuRunGkudi yem perumaanai, tiru-thaNgaal
ooraanaik karambanoor uttamanai, mut-tilanGgu
kaaraar-thiN kadal-eezhum malai-eezh iv-vulagu-eezh uNdum,
araaden RiruntaanaikkaNdadu ten naranGgatte,
0.meaning: The Lord of Thirupper, our Master of Kurungkudi, the Lord of Thirutthankaal, the Supreme One of Karambanoor; He who, having swallowed up the seven dark mighty seas, the seven mountains and the seven worlds, where pearls shine, still remained as though not satisfied -- Him I beheld in southern Srirangam.

1.translit:
ponGgaar mel iLanG-konGgai ponne pooppap
 porukayal kaN neer-arumbap pohtu ninRu
senGkaala madap-puRavam pedaikkup pesum
 siRukuraluk kudal-urugic cintittu, aange
taNgaalum taNgudantai nagarum paadit
 taNgoval oorp paadi yaadak kettu,
nanGgaay nanG kudikkidhuvo nanmai? enna
 naRaiyoorum paaduvaaL navilginRaaLe.
1.meaning: Her swelling soft young breasts turn pale as gold, and her fighting carp-like eyes brim with tears; she comes forth and stands. Hearing the soft cooing call of the gentle red-legged dove to its mate, her inward being melts and she broods; right there she sings of ThaNkaal and the city of cool Kudantai, and dances singing of cool Thirukkovaloor. When asked, 'Dear girl, is this a fitting thing for our family?' she goes on singing even of Naraiyoor.

2.translit:
aaraanum kaRpippaar naayakare? naan avanaik
kaar aar tirumeni kaaNum aLavum poy
seer aar tiruvenGgadame tirukkova
loore madiL kacchi ooragame perakame
peRaa marudu iRuttaan veLLaRaiye vehkaave
per aali taNgaal naRaiyoor tiruppuliyoor
aaraamam soozhnda aranGgam kaNamanGgai
2.meaning: Are there any who can instruct me, O lords? Until I go and behold His sacred form, dark as a rain-cloud, I shall journey on -- to glorious Thiruvenkatam, to Thirukkovaloor, to walled Kacchi, to Ooragam, to Peragam, to ThiruveLLaRai where He broke the marudu trees, to Vehkaa, to great Thiruvaali, to ThaNkaal, to Naraiyoor, to Thiruppuliyoor, to Srirangam girt with groves, to KaNamangai.

3.translit:
senni maNic cudarai taNgaal tiRal valiyai
tannaip piRar aRiyaat tattuvattai muttinai
annattai meenai ariyai aru maRaiyai
mun iv ulagu uNda moorttiyai kovaloor
3.meaning: The jewel-radiance upon the crown, the strong might of ThaNkaal, the Truth that none other can know, the pearl, the swan, the fish, the lion (Hari), the rare Vedas, the divine Form that of old swallowed up this world -- (Him at) Thirukkovaloor.

4.translit:
tamar-uLLam tanjai talai-aranGgam taNgaal,
tamar-uLLum taNporuppu velai, - tamar-uLLum
maamallai koval madiL-kudantai yenbare,
e-valla entaikkidam.
4.meaning: The hearts of His devotees, Thanjai, the foremost Srirangam, ThaNkaal; the cool hill (Venkatam) that His devotees cherish, and the ocean; and the places His devotees hold dear -- they say these are Mamallai, Kovaloor, walled Kudantai. Such are the abodes of my Father who is able to do all things.

0.translit:
taamtam perumai yaRiyaar, thuudhu
veendhark kaaya veendha ruurpool,
kaandhaL viralmen kalainan madavaar,
kuundhal kamazhum kuuda luuree.
0.meaning: Like the city of the King who became the King of kings by going as an envoy on behalf of those who did not know their own greatness, is Kudalur, where dwell good gentle women adorned in fine raiment, their fingers soft like the kaandhal flower, and where the fragrance of their tresses pervades.

1.translit:
seRumthiN thimile ruDaiya, pinnai
peRumthaN koolam peTRaa ruurppool,
naRundhaN thiimthe nuNda vaNDu,
kuRinji paaDum kuuda luuree!
1.meaning: Like the city of Him who won the cool comely Nappinnai by overcoming the fierce mighty bulls, is Kudalur, where the bees that have drunk the cool fragrant sweet honey sing the kurinji melody.

2.translit:
piLLai yuruvaayth thayiruNDu, aDiyeen
uLLam pugundha voruva ruurpool,
kaLLa naarai vayaluL, kayalmiin
koLLai koLLum kuuda luuree!
2.meaning: Like the city of the peerless One who, as a child, ate the curds and entered the heart of me, His servant, is Kudalur, where the cunning heron in the fields plunders the kayal fish in plenty.

3.translit:
kuuTRee ruruvin kuRaLaay, nilaniir
eeTRaa nendhai perumaa nuurpool,
seeTRe ruzhuvar koodhaip poodhuuN,
kooltheen muralum kuuda luuree!
3.meaning: Like the city of my Father, the great Lord, who in the form of a dwarf received the gift of land and water (from Mahabali), is Kudalur, where, as the ploughmen plough the mire, the rich honey hums while feeding upon the flowers of their garlands.

4.translit:
thoNDar paravac chuDarsen RaNava,
aNDath thamarum aDiga LuurpooL,
vaNDa lalaiyuL keNDai miLira,
koNDa ladhirum kuuda luuree.
4.meaning: Like the city of the Lord who, as His devotees praise Him, abides in the cosmos where the radiant luminaries draw near, is Kudalur, where the keNDai fish gleam amid the rippling silt-waters while the rain-clouds thunder.

5.translit:
thakkan veeLvi thagarttha thalaivan,
thukkam thuDaiththa thuNaiva ruurpool,
ekka liDunuN maNalmeel, engum
kokkin pazhamviizh kuuda luuree!
5.meaning: Like the city of the Lord, the Companion who wiped away sorrow and who is the chief that shattered Daksha's sacrifice, is Kudalur, where upon the fine sand washed up everywhere the kokku (mango-like) fruits fall.

6.translit:
karundhaN kaDalum malaiyu mulagum,
arundhum aDigaL amaru muurpool,
perundhaN mullaip piLLai yooDi,
kurundham thazhuvum kuuda luuree!
6.meaning: Like the city where dwells the Lord who swallowed the dark cool ocean, the mountains and the worlds, is Kudalur, where the young shoots of the great cool jasmine creeper run and embrace the kurundham tree.

7.translit:
kalaivaazh piNaiyo DaNaiyum, thiruniir
malaivaa zhendhai maruvu muurpool,
ilaithaazh thengin meelninRu,
iLaniirk kulaithaazh kiDangin kuuda luuree!
7.meaning: Like the city which my Father, who dwells on the sacred watery hill where the stag rests beside its doe, frequents, is Kudalur, where upon the leaf-drooping coconut palms the clusters of tender coconuts hang low over the moats.

8.translit:
perugu kaadha laDiyeen uLLam,
urugap pugundha voruva ruurpool,
arugu kaidhai malara, keNDai
kurugen RanjUm kuuda luuree.
8.meaning: Like the city of the peerless One who entered and melted the heart of me, His servant, with surging love, is Kudalur, where, as the screw-pine (kaidhai) blossoms nearby, the keNDai fish take fright, mistaking it for a crane.

9.translit:
kaavip peruniir vaNNan, kaNNan
meevith thigazhum kuuda luurmeel,
koovaith thamizhaal kaliyan sonna,
paavaip paaDap paavam poomee.
9.meaning: Of Kudalur where Kannan, hued like the vast waters and the kaavi flower, abides resplendent, Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) has sung this string of garland-like Tamil verses; whoever sings this song, their sins shall depart.

0.translit:
koozhiyum kooDalum koayil koNDa koavalarey oppar kunRamanna
paazhiyum thoaLum oar naanku uDaiyar paNDu ivar thammaiyum kaNDaRiyoam
vaazhiyaroa ivar vaNNam eNNil maakaDal poanRuLar kaiyil veyya,
aazhi onRu eenthi oar changu paRRi achchoa oruvar azhakiyavaa.
0.meaning: They resemble the cowherd (Krishna) who has taken the temple at Kozhi (Uraiyur) and Kudal (Madurai) as His abodes; they bear four mountain-like mighty shoulders. We have never before seen these two. May they live long! When one reflects on their colour, they are like the great dark ocean; bearing in their hands a fierce discus and grasping a conch — ah, how beautiful is each one of them!

1.translit:
azhaippan thiruvENG kaTaththaanaik kaaNa,
izhaippan thirukkooDal kooDa, - mazhaippeyr
aruvi maNivaranthi vanthizhiya, yaanai
veruvi aravoDungkum veRpu.
1.meaning: I call out to behold the Lord of holy Venkatam; I yearn to join in the sacred Kudal worship. There, on that hill where the rain-fed cascading streams come tumbling down rolling gemstones, the elephant, startled, shrinks back together with the serpent.

0.translit:
kaar aar maNi niRak kaNNanoor viNNakaram
seer aar kaNapuram seeRai tiruvazhundoor
kaar aar kuDandai kaDikai kaDalmallai
er aar pozhil soozh iDavendai neermalai
seer aarum maaliruNjoolai tirumookoor
0.meaning: Kannan's Tiruvinnagaram, dark like the rain-cloud-hued sapphire; glorious Kanapuram, Seerai, Tiruvazhundoor; the cloud-touched Kudandai, Kadikai, Kadalmallai (Mahabalipuram); Idavendai surrounded by beautiful groves, Neermalai; the glorious Maaliruncholai (Azhagar Koyil), and Tirumokoor.

1.translit:
taaLa taamarait taDamaNi vayal tirumookoor
naaLum meevi nan kamarndu ninRu asurarait takarkkum
tooLum naankuDaic curikuzhal kamalakkaN kanivaay
kaaLa meegattaiyanRi maRRu onRilam katiye
1.meaning: At Tirumokoor with its broad jeweled fields of stalked lotuses, He abides forever, well-pleased, and standing firm He crushes the asuras. He of four shoulders, curly locks, lotus eyes and ripe-fruit lips, the One dark as a rain-cloud, is our only refuge; we have no other.

2.translit:
ilaNgati maRRu onRu emmaikkum een taN tuzhaayin
alaNgal aNg kaNNi aayiram peeruDai ammaan
nalaNgoL naanmaRai vaaNarkaL vaazh tirumookoor
nalaNg kazhalavan aDinizhal taDam anRi yaame
2.meaning: There is no other refuge for us at any time: the Lord who wears the sweet cool tulasi garland, the beautiful coronet, and bears a thousand names. At Tirumokoor where the masters of the goodly four Vedas dwell, His gracious anklet-adorned feet's shade alone is our shelter, nothing else.

3.translit:
anRi yaam oru pukal iDam ilam enRu enRu alaRRi
ninRu naanmukan aranoDu teevarkaL naaDa
venRu im moovulagu aLittu uzhalvaan tirumookoor
nanRu naam ini naNugudum namadu iDar keDave
3.meaning: Crying out again and again 'We have no other place of refuge,' Brahma, Siva and the gods come seeking Him. He who triumphantly protects and roams over these three worlds dwells at Tirumokoor; let us now draw near it well, so that our affliction may be destroyed.

4.translit:
iDar keDa emmaip pondu aLiyaay enRu enRu eetti
suDar koL sootiyait teevarum munivarum toDara
paDar koL paambu aNaip paLLi koLvaan tirumookoor
iDar keDa aDi paravudum toNDeer! vammine
4.meaning: Praising Him again and again, 'Come and grant us grace so our distress may end,' the gods and sages follow the radiant Effulgence. He who reposes upon the bed of the spreading serpent dwells at Tirumokoor; let us extol His feet so our affliction is destroyed. O devotees, come!

5.translit:
toNDeer! vammin nam suDaroLi oru tani mudalvan
aNDam moovulagu aLandavan aNi tirumookoor
eN disaiyum een karumboDu poru cennel viLaiya
koNDa kooyilai valaNjeydu iNg aaDudum koottee
5.meaning: O devotees, come! Our radiant Light, the one matchless primordial Lord, who measured the cosmos and the three worlds, dwells at beautiful Tirumokoor. Where in all eight directions sweet sugarcane and fine paddy ripen abundantly, let us circumambulate the temple He has taken as His abode and dance here in joy.

6.translit:
koottan koovalan kutaRRu val asurarkaL kooRRam
eettum naNgaTkum amararkkum munivarkkum inban
vaaytta taN paNai vaLa vayal soozh tirumookoor
aattan taamarai aDi anRi maRRilam araNe
6.meaning: The Dancer, the Cowherd, the death of the mighty cruel asuras, He is the delight of us who praise Him, of the celestials and of the sages. He is the trusted refuge dwelling at Tirumokoor encircled by cool fertile fields and rich farmland; we have no other fortress than His lotus feet.

7.translit:
maRRilam araN vaan perum paazh tani mudalaa
suRRu neer paDaittu adan vazhit ton muni mudalaa
muRRum teevaroDu ulagu seyvaan tirumookoor
suRRi naam valaNj seyya nam tuyar keDum kaDite
7.meaning: We have no other fortress. He who from the vast primal void as sole origin created the encircling waters, and through them the ancient sage (Brahma) and all the gods and the worlds, dwells at Tirumokoor. If we circumambulate it, our sorrow will be swiftly destroyed.

8.translit:
tuyar keDum kaDidu aDaindu van toNDar aDiyavar tozhumin
uyar koL soolai oN taDamaNi oLi tirumookoor
peyarkaL aayiram uDaiya val arakkar pukku azhunda
tayaratan peRRa maradaka maNit taDattinaiye
8.meaning: Your sorrow will be swiftly destroyed; O staunch devotees, draw near and worship. At radiant Tirumokoor with its lofty groves and bright jeweled tanks abides the emerald-gemmed pool, He whom Dasaratha begot, into whom the mighty thousand-named demon (Ravana) plunged and sank.

9.translit:
maNit taDattu aDi malarkkaNgaL pavaLac cevvaay
aNigaL koL naal taDan tooL deyvam asuraraiyenrum
tuNikkum val araTTan uRai pozhil tirumookoor
naNittu nammuDai nal araN naam aDaindaname
9.meaning: The emerald-gem-pooled Lord with feet, lotus eyes, coral-red mouth, and four broad ornamented shoulders, the Divine One who ever cuts down the asuras, the mighty Valiant One, dwells in the groves of Tirumokoor. Near at hand is our good fortress; we have attained it.

10.translit:
naam aDainda nal araN tamakku enRu nal amarar
teemai seyyum val asuraiyaynjic cenRu aDaindaal
kaama roopam koNDu ezhundu aLippaan tirumookoor
naama me navinRu eNNumin eettumin namarkaaL
10.meaning: When the good immortals, fearing the mighty asuras who do evil, come and take refuge saying 'this is the good fortress we have attained,' He rises taking the form He wills and protects them, dwelling at Tirumokoor. O my kinsmen, recite, meditate upon and praise that Name.

11.translit:
eettumin namarkaaL enRu taan kuDam aaDu
koottanai kurukoorc caDakoopan kuRReeval
vaaytta aayirattuL ivai vaN tirumookoorkku
eetta pattu ivai eetta vallaarkku iDar keDume
11.meaning: Of the thousand fitting verses of devoted service by Sadagopan of Kurukur to the Dancer who danced the pot-dance, saying 'Praise Him, O my kinsmen,' these ten were offered to bountiful Tirumokoor. For those able to recite these ten, all affliction will be destroyed.

0.translit:
vaNNa maaDangaL soozh-tiruk koTTiyoor
kaNNan kesavan nambi piRandinil
eNNey suNNam etir-etir tooviDak
kaNNan muRRam kalandu-aLa raayiRRe.
0.meaning: In Tirukkottiyur, ringed by colorful mansions, when the Lord Kannan—Kesava, the perfect one—was born, oil and fragrant powder were scattered upon one another from every side, and so Kannan's courtyard became all mingled into mud.

1.translit:
ODu vaar-vizhu vaar-ugan daalippaar
naaDu vaar-nambi raan-engkuR Raanenbaar
paaDu vaarkaLum pal-paRai koTTanindru
aaDu vaarkaLum aayiRRu-aayp paaDiye.
1.meaning: Some run, some fall, some rejoice and make the auspicious sound; some search, asking 'Where is our Lord, the perfect one?' Some sing, and some, while many drums beat, stand and dance—such became Ayarpadi, the cowherds' village.

2.translit:
peNich cheeruDaip piLLai piRandinil
kaaNat taam-pugu vaar-pukkup pootuvaar
aaNop paar-ivan neril-lai kaaN-tiru
voNat taanula LaaLumen paarkaLe.
2.meaning: When the cherished, glorious child was born, those who came in to behold Him would come and go; they would say, 'There is none equal to this male child, no one to match Him—behold, He of the Sravana star shall rule the world!'

3.translit:
uRiyai muRRattu uruTTinin RaaDuvaar
naRuney paal-tayir nandRaagat toovuvaar
seRimen koondal avizhat tiLaittu-engkum
aRiva zhindanar aayppaaDi yaayare.
3.meaning: They roll the hanging milk-pots in the courtyard and dance; they joyfully scatter fragrant ghee, milk, and curds in plenty; their thick soft tresses come loose as they revel, and everywhere the cowherds of Ayarpadi lost their senses in delight.

4.translit:
koNDa taaLuRi kolak koDumazhu
taNDi narpaRi yolaich chayanattar
viNDa mullai yarumban na pallinar
aNDar miNDip pugundu-ney yaaDinaar.
4.meaning: With hanging pots they hold, with handsome curved axe-staffs, with rolled-up palm-leaf scrolls and resting-mats, with teeth like the buds of the blooming jasmine, the cowherds thronged and crowding in bathed themselves in the ghee.

5.translit:
kaiyum kaalum nimirttuk kaDaaraneer
paiya vaaTTip pasunjiRu manjaLaal
aiya naavazhit taaLukku-ang kaantiDa
vaiya mezhumkaN DaaL-piLLai vaayuLe.
5.meaning: Stretching out His hands and feet, gently warming water in a cauldron, with tender little turmeric she washed the dear child's tongue; and when He opened His mouth wide for her, she beheld the seven worlds within the child's mouth.

6.translit:
vaayuL vaiyagam kaNDa maDanallaar
aayar puttira nallan arundeyvam
paaya seeruDaip paNbuDaip paalagan
maaya nendRu magizhndanar maadare.
6.meaning: The good gentle women who saw the world within His mouth said, 'He is not a cowherd's son but a rare divinity—this child of abounding glory and noble nature is the wondrous Lord, Mayan,' and so the women rejoiced.

7.translit:
pattu naaLum kaDanda iraNDaa-naaL
etti saiyum sayamaram koDittu
matta maamalai taangiya maindanai
uttaa nam-seydu ugandanar aayare.
7.meaning: On the day past the tenth and second, raising victory-poles in every direction and adorning them, the cowherds, with delight, performed the rite of lifting up the strong one who had upheld the great churning-mountain (Mandara), and rejoiced.

8.translit:
kiDakkil toTTil kizhiya udaittiDum
eDuttuk koLLil marungai yiRuttiDum
oDukkip pulkil udaratte paayndiDum
miDukki laamaiyaal naan-melin den-nangaay.
8.meaning: If He lies down, He kicks the cradle till it tears; if I take Him up, He wrenches my waist; if I draw Him close and embrace Him, He springs against my belly. For want of strength, O dear friend, I have grown weak.

9.translit:
sennne laar-vayal soozh-tiruk koTTiyoor
mannu naaraNan nambi piRandamai
minnu nool-viTTu sittan viritta-ip
pannu paaDal-val laark-killai paavame.
9.meaning: Tirukkottiyur is ringed by fields rich with red paddy, where the enduring Narayana, the perfect Lord, was born. For those skilled in these well-wrought songs that Vishnuchittan—bright with the sacred thread—has set forth, there shall be no sin.

10.translit:
konguNG kuDandaiyum koTTiyoo rumperum
engkum tirindu viLaiyaaDum en-magan
sangam piDikkum taDakkaikkut takka-nil
anga muDaiyador kol-koNDuvaa
arakku vazhittador kol-koNDuvaa.
10.meaning: My son who wanders everywhere playing—through Kongu, Kudandai, Kottiyur and Per—bring a staff fitting and shapely for His broad hand that grasps the conch; bring a staff that has been polished smooth with lac.

11.translit:
naava-kaariyam sollilaadavar naaL-toRum-virun dombuvaar
deva-kaariyam seydu-vedam payindru-vaazh-tiruk koTTiyoor
moovar-kaariya mum-tiruttum mudalvanaich chinti yaada-ap
paava-kaarika Laip-paDaittavan engngnam-paDait taankolo.
11.meaning: Tirukkottiyur, where dwell those who speak no idle word with their tongues, who daily host guests, who perform the rites of the gods and study the Vedas. How did the Creator come to create those sinful folk who do not contemplate the Primal Lord who sets right the affairs of the three (Brahma, Vishnu, Siva)?

12.translit:
kuRRamindRik kuNam-perukkik kurukkaLukku-anu koolaraay
