seRRamondRumi laadavaN-kaiyi naarkaL-vaazh-tiruk koTTiyoor
tuRRiyezhula guNDa-toomaNi vaNNan-tannait tozhaadavar
peRRa-taayar vayiRRinaip-peru noy-seyvaan-piRan daarkaLe.
12.meaning: Tirukkottiyur, where dwell the generous-handed who are without fault, who increase their virtues, who are favorable to their teachers, and who bear no enmity. Those who do not worship the flawless-gem-hued Lord, who swallowed and held the seven worlds, were born only to bring great grief to the wombs of the mothers who bore them.

13.translit:
vaNNanal-maNi yum-maragadamum azhutti nizhalezhum
tiNNai-soozh-tiruk koTTiyoort-tiru maalavan-tiru naamangkaL
eNNak-kaNDa viralkaLaal iRaip pozhudum eNNagi laadupoy
uNNak-kaNDatam oottaivaaykkuk kavaLam undugin RaarkaLe.
13.meaning: Tirukkottiyur, ringed by porches set with lustrous fine gems and emeralds from which a radiance rises. With the very fingers fit to count out the holy names of Tirumal the Lord, men do not for even a moment count them, but instead go and shove morsels of food into their foul mouths to eat.

14.translit:
uragamel-laNai yaan-kaiyil uRai sangkam-pol-maDa vannangkaL
niraikaNam-paran deRum-sengkama lavayal-tiruk koTTiyoor
naraga-naasanai naaviR-koNDazhai yaadamaaniDa saadiyar
parugu-neerum uDukkungkooRaiyum paavam-seydana taangkolo.
14.meaning: Tirukkottiyur with its red-lotus fields where flocks of gentle swans spread in ranks and rise up, like the conch that abides in the hand of Him who reclines on the serpent-couch. For the race of men who do not take upon their tongues and call upon the Destroyer of hell—was even the water they drink and the cloth they wear made through some sin?

15.translit:
aamaiyin mudugattiDaik kudikoNDu toomalar saaDippoy
teemai-seydu-iLa vaaLaikaL-viLai yaaDuneert-tiruk koTTiyoor
nemi-ser-taDang kaiyinaanai ninaippilaavali nenjuDai
poomi-paarangka LuNNum-soRRinai vaangkip-pullait tiNimine.
15.meaning: Tirukkottiyur, where in the waters young carp leap upon the backs of tortoises, dash against the pure flowers, do mischief and play. As for the hard-hearted who have no thought of Him whose broad hand holds the discus—they are but a burden on the earth; snatch away the rice they would eat and stuff them with grass instead.

16.translit:
pootamaindoDu velviyaindu pulangkaLaindu poRikaLaal
edamondRumi laadavaN-kaiyi naarkaL-vaazh-tiruk koTTiyoor
naadanai-nara singkanainavin Rettuvaarka Luzhakkiya
paada-tooli paDudalaal-iv vulagam-paakkiyam seydade.
16.meaning: Tirukkottiyur, where dwell the generous-handed who, with the five elements, the five sacrifices, the five senses and the organs, are free of any flaw. Because the dust of the feet of those who recite and praise the Lord, the Man-Lion (Narasimha), falls and settles upon it, this world has gained great merit.

17.translit:
kurundamondRosit taanoDum-sendRu kooDiyaaDi vizhaachcheydu
tirundunaan-maRai yor-iraappagal ettivaazh-tiruk koTTiyoor
karundaDamugil vaNNanaik-kaDaik koNDukaitozhum pattarkaL
irundavoori lirukkummaaniDar et-tavangkaL-sey daarkolo.
17.meaning: Tirukkottiyur, where the masters of the four perfect Vedas, going and joining together, dancing and holding festival, praise night and day Him who broke the single kurunda tree, and so dwell. What austerities did those men perform who dwell in the same town where the devotees abide who, holding the dark-vast-cloud-hued Lord as their refuge, worship with folded hands?

18.translit:
naLirndaseelan nayaasalanabimaanatungkanai naaDoRum
teLindaselvanaich chevagangkoNDa sengkaNmaal tirukkoTTiyoor
kuLirnduRaikindRa govindan guNam paaDuvaaruLLa naaTTinuL
viLaindaadaaniyamu miraakkar meedukoLLagi laarkaLe.
18.meaning: Tirukkottiyur of the red-eyed Tirumal, the cool gracious one, the gentle-natured, the unfailing refuge, the lofty in dignity, the lucid treasure, who has taken devotees into His service. In the land where there are those who sing the virtues of Govinda who dwells there in cool delight, the demons (rakshasas) cannot lay hold upon even the grain that has ripened.

19.translit:
kombinaarpozhil vaaykuyilinam kovindanguNam paaDuseer
sempon-aarmadiL soozh-sezhungkazha niyuDait-tiruk koTTiyoor
nambanainara singkanainavin Rettuvaarkalaik kaNDakkaal
empiraan-tana sinnangkaL-iva rivarendRu-aasaikaL teervane.
19.meaning: Tirukkottiyur, ringed by ramparts rich with pure gold and possessed of fertile fields, where flocks of cuckoos in the branch-filled groves sing the glory of the virtues of Govinda. Whenever I behold those who recite and praise the trusted Lord, the Man-Lion (Narasimha), I think, 'These, these are the very signs of my Lord!'—and so my longings are fulfilled.

20.translit:
kaasin vaaykaram virkilum karavaadu maatrili sootittu
dhesa vaarthai padaikkum van kaiyinaarkaL vaazh tirukkoottiyoor
kesavaa purudoottamaa kiLar soodhiyaay kuRaLaa enRu pesuvaar adi
yaarkaL endhammai virkavum peRuvaarkaLe.
20.meaning: In Tirukkottiyur dwell the bountiful-handed ones who, even when selling something for a coin's worth, give without holding back, feeding food to all and earning a good name throughout the land. The devotees there call upon Him "O Kesava, O Supreme Person (Purushottama), O radiant Effulgence, O Dwarf (Vamana)"; such devotees are worthy even to sell us (the Lord gladly belongs to them).

21.translit:
seedha neer pudai soozh sezhung kazhani udai tirukkoottiyoor
aadhiyaan adiyaaraiyum adimai inRi tirivaaraiyum
kodhil pattar piraan kuLir puduvai man vittuchittan sol
edham inRi uraippavar irudeekesanukku aaLare.
21.meaning: This is the song of Vishnuchittan (Periyalvar), the faultless lord of the Bhattas, king of cool Srivilliputtur, concerning the Primordial Lord of Tirukkottiyur, which is girt with cool waters and rich paddy fields, and concerning both His devotees and those who wander without rendering service. Those who recite these words without flaw shall become servants of Hrishikesha.

22.translit:
taaren piRarkku un aruL en idai vaitthaay,
aaren adhuve parugik kaLikkinRen,
aarey kadale malaye tirukkootti
yoore, ugandhaay ai ugandhu adiyene.
22.meaning: You have placed Your grace within me, and I will not give it to others; I drink that very grace and grow ever fulfilled, never sated. O You who are like the dark cloud, the ocean, and the mountain, You who delight in Tirukkottiyur, I, Your servant, rejoice in You who rejoices.

23.translit:
engaL em iRai em piraan imaiyoorkku naayagan, eth adiyavar
tangaL tam manatthu piriyaadhu aruL purivaan,
pongu taN aruvi pudham seyya pon kaLe sidhaRum ilangu oLi,
sem kamalam malarum tirukkoottiyoorane.
23.meaning: He is our Lord, our Master, the chief of the celestials, who, never departing from the minds of His praising devotees, bestows His grace upon them. He is the Lord of Tirukkottiyur, where the surging cool cascades, falling, scatter glittering gold of radiant brilliance, and where red lotuses bloom.

24.translit:
evva noy tavirppaan emakku iRai innagai tuvar vaay, nila magaL
sevvi thoya vallaan tirumaa magatku iniyaan,
mauval maalai vandaadum malligai maalaiyodu maNandhu maarudham
dheyvam naaRa varum tirukkoottiyoorane.
24.meaning: He removes every grievous affliction, He is our Lord with the sweet smile and coral-red lips, capable of uniting in the beauty of Bhudevi (the Earth-goddess), and dear to the great Tirumagal (Lakshmi). He is the Lord of Tirukkottiyur, where the breeze comes mingled with the fragrance of jasmine garlands over which bees swarm and with mauval-flower garlands, breathing a divine scent.

25.translit:
veLLiyaan kariyaan maNi niRa vaNNan viNNavar tamakku iRai, emakku
oLLiyaan uyarndhaan ulagu ezhum uNdu umizhndaan,
tuLLu neer moNdu koNdu saamaraik katraich sandhanam undhi vandhasai,
teLLu neerp puRavil tirukkoottiyoorane.
25.meaning: He is the white-hued one, the dark one, the one of sapphire-gem complexion, the Lord of the celestials, the radiant and exalted one to us, who swallowed and brought forth all the seven worlds. He is the Lord of Tirukkottiyur with its clear-watered groves, where the leaping waters, scooping up and bearing along clusters like chamara-fans, surge forward pushing sandalwood trees.

26.translit:
eeRu eRi ilangu oN mazhup paRRum eesaR kisaindhu, udambil or
kooRu thaan koduttaan kula maa magat kiniyaan,
naaRu saNbaga malligai malar pulgi in niLa vaNdu, nal naRum
dheRal vaay madukkum tirukkoottiyoorane.
26.meaning: Befriending Shiva, who rides the bull and grasps the gleaming bright axe, He gave him one half of His own body; He is dear to the great Lady of noble lineage (Lakshmi). He is the Lord of Tirukkottiyur, where the young sweet bees, embracing the fragrant champak and jasmine blossoms, drink in the fine sweet nectar.

27.translit:
vanga maa kadal vaNNan maa maNi vaNNan viNNavar kon madhu malar
dhongal neeN mudiyaan nediyaan padi kadandhaan,
mangul thoy maNi maada veN kodi maaga meedhu uyarndhu eRi, vaan uyar
tingaL thaan aNavum tirukkoottiyoorane.
27.meaning: He is hued like the great ship-bearing ocean, of the complexion of a great gem, the king of the celestials, wearing a long crown with garlands of honeyed flowers; the boundless One who strode across the worlds. He is the Lord of Tirukkottiyur, where the white banners atop the jeweled, cloud-touching mansions rise high into the sky and reach up to the lofty moon.

28.translit:
kaavalan ilangaikku iRai kalanga charam sela uyttu, matravan
evalam tavirthaan ennai aaLudai em piraan,
naavalam puvi mannar vandhu vaNanga maal uRaiginRadhu inge ena,
dhevar vandhu iRainjum tirukkoottiyoorane.
28.meaning: He drove forth His arrow so that the guardian king and lord of Lanka (Ravana) was thrown into turmoil, and put an end to that one's might; He is my Master who has taken me as His own. He is the Lord of Tirukkottiyur, where the kings of the rose-apple-girt earth come and bow, and where the gods come and worship, saying "Here is where Tirumal dwells."

29.translit:
kanRu koNdu viLanganni eRindhu aa niraik kazhivu enRu, maa mazhai
ninRu kaatthu ugandhaan nila maa magat kiniyaan,
kunRin mullaiyin vaasamum kuLir malligai maNamum aLaindhu, iLan
dhenRal vandhu ulavum tirukkoottiyoorane.
29.meaning: Taking the calf and hurling it to strike down the wood-apples, and holding up the mountain to guard the herds of cattle from destruction by the great rain-storm, He delighted in their protection; He is dear to the great Lady of the Earth. He is the Lord of Tirukkottiyur, where the gentle young southern breeze comes wandering, mingled with the fragrance of hillside jasmine and the scent of cool mallika flowers.

30.translit:
poong kurundhu osittaanai kaayndhu ari maa segutthu, adiyenai aaL ugandhu
eeng en uL pugundhaan imaiyoorkaL tam perumaan,
toongu taN palavin kani toku vaazhaiyin kaniyodu maang kani
dhengu taN punal soozh tirukkoottiyoorane.
30.meaning: Wrathfully He broke the flowering kurundha tree and slew the lion-demon (Hiranya), and gladly accepting me as His servant, He entered within me here; He is the Lord of the celestials. He is the Lord of Tirukkottiyur, encircled by cool flowing waters where the hanging cool jackfruit, clustered plantain fruit, and mango fruit abound.

31.translit:
kovaiyin tamizh paaduvaar kudam aaduvaar tada maa malar misai,
mevu naan muganil viLangu puri noolar,
mevu naan maRai vaaNar aivagai velvi aaRangam vallavar tozhum,
dheva deva piraan tirukkoottiyoorane.
31.meaning: He is worshipped by those who sing sweet Tamil in garland-like verse, by those who dance the pot-dance, by the wearers of the sacred thread who shine like the four-faced Brahma seated on the great lotus, and by the masters of the four Vedas, the five sacrifices, and the six Vedic auxiliaries. He is the God of gods, the Lord of Tirukkottiyur.

32.translit:
aalu maa valavan kalikanRi mangaiyar talaivan aNi pozhil
selgaL paay kazhanith tirukkoottiyoorai,
neela maa mugil vaNNanai nedumaalai in tamizhaal ninaindha, in
naalum aaRum vallaarkku idam aagum vaan ulage.
32.meaning: Kalikanri (Tirumangai Alvar), lord of the men of Tirumangai and master of the rampaging steed, contemplated in sweet Tamil the Lord of Tirukkottiyur with its lovely groves and fields where carp-fish leap, the Lord of the hue of the great dark cloud, the great Tirumal. For those who can master these ten and the further (verses), the world of heaven shall be their abode.

33.translit:
kamba maa kaLiRu anjik kalanga, or
kombu koNda kurai kazhal kootthanai
kombu laam pozhil koottiyoork kaNdu poy
namban aich chenRu kaNdum naavaayuLe.
33.meaning: He is the dancer of the resounding anklets who seized the tusk so that the great rutting elephant (Kuvalayapida) trembled in terror and was thrown into confusion. Having seen Him at Kottiyur with its groves where flowering branches sway, go and behold the trustworthy Lord Naavaay (at Tirunavaay) as well.

34.translit:
minni mazhai tavazhum vengadattu em vitthaganai
mannanai maalirunjsolai maNaaLanai
kol navilum aazhip padaiyaanai koottiyoor
34.meaning: He is our wondrous One of Venkatam, where the lightning flashes and the rain-clouds creep along; the King; the Bridegroom of Tirumalirunsolai; the One who bears the discus-weapon skilled in slaying; (and the Lord of) Kottiyur.

35.translit:
payinRa tarangam tirukkootti, pannaaL
payinRadhuvum vengadame pannaaL, - payinRa
taNi tigazhum solai aNi neer malaiye
maNi tigazhum vaN tadakkai maal.
35.meaning: Tirumal of the gem-gleaming generous broad hands has long dwelt at Tirukkottiyur amid the murmuring (groves), long dwelt too at Venkatam, and dwelt in the cool-shining grove of the lovely-watered Tirumalirunsolai (the beautiful hill).

36.translit:
inRaa aRiginRen allen iru nilatthaich
senRaang aLandha tiruvadiyai, - anRu
karukkootti yuL kidandhu kai tozhudhen kaNden,
tirukkootti endhai tiRam.
36.meaning: It is not only today that I have come to know the sacred feet that went and measured this vast earth. Long ago, lying within the cage of the womb, I worshipped with folded hands; I have beheld the glory of my Father of Tirukkottiyur.

37.translit:
viNNagaram veqkaa viri tirai neer vengadam,
maNNagaram maa maada veLukkai, maNNagattha
ten kudandhai tenaar tiruvarangam ten kootti,
tan kudangai neer eatraan taazhvu.
37.meaning: The one who received the water in the hollow of His hand (at Mahabali's gift) has His humble abodes at: Vishnukaram (Tiruvinnagar), Veqka, Venkatam of the spreading-billowed waters, the great-mansioned Mannagaram of Velukkai, southern Kudandai upon the earth, the honey-laden holy Srirangam, and southern Kottiyur.

38.translit:
kuRippu enakku koottiyoor meyaanai etttha,
kuRippu enakku nanmai payakka, - veRuppano
vengadattu meyaanai mey vinai noy eydhaamal,
taan kadatthum tanmaiyaan taaL.
38.meaning: My intent is to praise the Lord who abides at Kottiyur, and my intent is that good may come to me. Would I then ever spurn the feet of Him of the very nature that carries us across, so that the affliction of bodily karma may not befall us, the Lord who abides at Venkatam?

0.translit:
tannai naivik kilEn val vinaiyEn thozhuthum ezhu
ponnai naivikkum ap pooñ cherunthi maNi neezhalvaay
ennai naivith thezhal koNda kanRa perumaan idam,
punnai mutham pozhil soozhntha zhakaaya pullaaNiyE.
0.meaning: I, of cruel deeds, cannot bring myself to torment my own self; come, let us rise and worship Him. The place of the Lord who, in the jewel-like shade of that lovely cherunthi tree whose blossoms outshine gold, made me waste away and then departed, is beautiful Tiruppullani, surrounded by groves of punnai trees that scatter pearl-like blossoms.

1.translit:
uruki neñchE ninainthing kirunthen thozhuthum ezhu
muruku vaNd uN malark kaithaiyin neezhalil mun oru naaL,
peruku kaathanmai ennuLLam eythap pirinthaan idam
poruthu munneerk karaikkE maNi yunthu pullaaNiyE.
1.meaning: O heart, what use is it to stay here melting and grieving? Come, let us rise and worship. The place of Him who, one day long ago in the shade of the fragrant screw-pine where honey-bees feed on the blossoms, kindled in my heart a swelling love and then parted from me, is Tiruppullani where the surging ocean waves dash upon the shore and cast up gems.

2.translit:
Edhu seythaal maRakkEn manamE thozhuthum ezhu
thaadhu malku thadañ choozh pozhil thaazhvar thodarnthu pin
pEdhai ninaip piriyEn ini enRu akanRaan idam pOdhu
naaLum kamazhum pozhil soozhntha pullaaNiyE.
2.meaning: O my heart, whatever I do, how can I forget? Come, let us rise and worship. The place of Him who, following me through the vast pollen-laden groves, said 'O innocent one, I will never part from you henceforth' and then departed, is Tiruppullani encircled by groves whose blossoms are fragrant day after day.

3.translit:
kongu uN vaNdE kariyaaka vanthaan kodiyERku mun
nangaL eesan namakkE paNiththa mozhi seythilan
mangai nallaay thozhuthum ezhu pOy avan mannum oor
pongu munneerk karaikkE maNi yunthu pullaaNiyE.
3.meaning: With only the honey-sipping bee as witness, He came before cruel me; our Lord did not keep the word He had vowed to me alone. O good lady, come, let us rise and worship; the eternal town of Him is Tiruppullani, where the swelling ocean casts up gems upon its shore.

4.translit:
uNaril uLLam sudumaal vinaiyEn thozhuthum ezhu
thuNari naazhal naRum pOthu nam soozh kuzhal peythu pin
thaNaril aavi thaLarum ena anbu thanthaan idam,
puNari yOdham paNilam maNi yunthu pullaaNiyE.
4.meaning: If I but recall it, my heart burns; I of evil deeds, come, let us rise and worship. The place of Him who placed the fragrant cluster of naazhal blossoms upon my encircling tresses and gave me his love, saying 'if you grow distant my life will wither,' is Tiruppullani where the surging tides cast up conches and gems.

5.translit:
eLki neñchE ninainthing kirunthen thozhuthum ezhu
vaLLal maayan maNivaNNan emmaan maruvum idam
kaLLa vizhum malark kaaviyum thoo madal kaithaiyum,
puLLum aLLal pazhanangaLum soozhntha pullaaNiyE.
5.meaning: O heart, what gain in staying here ashamed and grieving? Come, let us rise and worship. The dwelling place of the generous, wondrous, sapphire-hued Lord, my master, is Tiruppullani, surrounded by lily-blossoms dripping honey, pure-petalled screw-pines, birds, and marshy paddy fields.

6.translit:
paravi neñchE thozhuthum ezhu pOy avan paalamaay
iravum naaLum inik kaN thuyilaa thirunthen payan?
viravi mutham nedu veN maNal mEl koNdu veN thirai
puravi enna putham seythu vanthunthu pullaaNiyE.
6.meaning: O heart, let us praise and worship and set forth toward Him; what use is it to remain sleepless night and day for His sake? It is Tiruppullani, where the white waves, like prancing horses, rear up and surge in, casting scattered pearls upon the long white sands.

7.translit:
alamum aazhip padaiyum udaiyaar namakku anbaraay,
salam adhaakith thakavu onRu ilar naam thozhuthum ezhu,
ulavu kaal nal kazhi Ongu thaN paim pozhil oodu isai
pulavu kaanal kaLi vaNdu inam paadu pullaaNiyE.
7.meaning: The Lord who bears the plough-like weapon and the discus, though loving toward us, has become deceitful and shows no grace; yet come, let us rise and worship. It is Tiruppullani, where through the cool green groves rising by the breeze-swept backwaters, swarms of joyful bees hum their music in the fragrant seaside woods.

8.translit:
Odhi naamam kuLiththu uchchi thannaal oLi maa malarp
paadham naaLum paNivOm namakkE nalam aathalin
aadhu thaaraan enilum tharum anRiyum anbaraayp
pOdhum maathE thozhuthum avan mannu pullaaNiyE.
8.meaning: Reciting His names, bathing, and bowing our heads daily at His radiant lotus feet brings us only good. Even if He gives nothing, still He will give; and moreover with love, come, O lady, let us go and worship at Tiruppullani where He abides.

9.translit:
ilangu muththum pavaLak kozhunthum ezhil thaamarai
pulangaL muRRum pozhil soozhnth azhakaaya pullaaNimEl
kalangal illaap pukazhaan kaliyan oli maalai
valangoL thoNdarkku idamaa vadhu paadil vaikunthamE.
9.meaning: Upon Tiruppullani, made beautiful by surrounding groves and fields full of gleaming pearls, coral sprigs, and lovely lotuses, Kaliyan of untarnished fame composed this garland of melodious verse. For the devotees who recite it with reverence, the very place becomes Vaikuntha.

10.translit:
kaavaar madal peNNai anRil arikuralum
Evaayin oodu iyangum ehkil kodithaalO
poovaar maNam kamazhum pullaaNi kai thozhuthEn
paavaay idhu namakkOr paanmaiyE aakaadhE.
10.meaning: The plaintive cry of the anRil bird on the leafy palmyra grove is crueller to me than a spear-tip piercing through. I have worshipped with folded hands Tiruppullani fragrant with the scent of blossoms. O lady, is this not become a settled way of fate for me?

11.translit:
munnam kuRaL uruvaay moovadi maN koNdu aLantha,
mannan sarithaikkE maalaakip pon payanthEn
ponnam kazhik kaanal puLLinangaaL pullaaNi
annamaay nool payanthaaRku aangu idhanaich cheppuminE.
11.meaning: Enthralled by the story of the King who once, as a dwarf, begged three feet of earth and measured the worlds, I have grown pale as gold. O flocks of birds in the golden seaside backwater groves, go and tell this my plight there to Him who, as a swan, brought forth the Vedas, at Tiruppullani.

12.translit:
vavvith thuzhaai adhanmEl senRa thani neñcham
sevvi aRiyaadhu niRkungol niththilangaL
pavvath thirai ulavu pullaaNi kai thozhuthEn
theyvach chilaiyaaRken sinthai nOy seppuminE.
12.meaning: My lonely heart that ran captivated to His sacred tulasi garland - does it still stand there, not knowing the right moment? I have worshipped Tiruppullani where ocean waves roll pearls along the shore. O birds, tell my heart's affliction to Him of the divine bow.

13.translit:
pariya iraNiyan thaakam aNi ukiraal
ari uruvaayk keeNdaan aruL thanthavaa namakku
poru thiraikaL pOnthu ulavu pullaaNi kai thozhuthEn
ari malark kaN neer thathumba an thukilum nillaavE.
13.meaning: He who, taking the form of the man-lion, tore open with His lovely claws the breast of the massive Hiranya - behold the way He has shown grace to me! I have worshipped Tiruppullani where the battling waves roll in; yet my flower-like eyes brim with tears and my beautiful garment will not stay in place.

14.translit:
villaal ilangai malangach charam thurantha
vallaaLan pin pOna neñcham varum aLavum,
ellaarum enThannai Esilum pEsidinum,
pullaaNi emperumaan poy kEttu irunthEnE.
14.meaning: Until my heart, which followed the mighty hero who with his bow shot arrows to confound and destroy Lanka, returns to me - though everyone reviles and slanders me - I have remained, having listened to the false promises of my Lord of Tiruppullani.

15.translit:
suzhanRu ilangu vem kathirOn thErOdum pOy maRainthaan
azhanRu kodithaaki am sudaril thaan adumaal
sezhun thadam pooñ chOlai soozh pullaaNi kai thozhuthEn
izhanthirunthEn enThan ezhil niRamum sangumE.
15.meaning: The fierce, wheeling, blazing Sun has set and vanished with his chariot; and now the gentle moonlight burns me, crueller and more scorching than that very sun. I have worshipped Tiruppullani encircled by lush, broad, flowering groves; yet I have lost my lovely complexion and my bangles.

16.translit:
kanaiyaar idi kuralin kaar maNiyin naavaadal
thinaiyEnum nillaadhu theeyiR kodithaalO punaiyaar
maNi maadap pullaaNi kai thozhuthEn
vinaiyEnmEl vElaiyum venthazhalE veesumE.
16.meaning: The clanging tongue of the dark bell, like the roar of crashing thunder, will not cease even for a moment, and it is crueller than fire. I have worshipped Tiruppullani of jewelled, adorned mansions; yet even the ocean flings burning flames upon wretched me.

17.translit:
thoombu udaik kai vEzham veruva maruppu osiththa
paambin aNaiyaan aruL thanthavaa namakku
pooñ cherunthi pon soriyum pullaaNi kai thozhuthEn
thEmbal iLam piRaiyum enRanakku Or venthazhalE.
17.meaning: He who reclines on the serpent and who, as the elephant with its hollow trunk trembled, broke off its tusk - behold the way He has shown grace to me! I have worshipped Tiruppullani where the flowering cherunthi trees shower gold; yet even the slender waning crescent moon is to me but a scorching flame.

18.translit:
vEdhamum vELviyum viNNum iru sudarum,
aadhiyum aanaan aruL thanthavaa namakku,
pOdhalarum punnai soozh pullaaNi kai thozhuthEn,
Odhamum naanum uRangaadhu irunthEnE.
18.meaning: He who became the Vedas, the sacrifices, the heavens, the two luminaries, and the primal source of all - behold the way He has shown grace to me! I have worshipped Tiruppullani surrounded by blossoming punnai trees; and both the roaring sea and I have stayed awake, unable to sleep.

19.translit:
pon alarum punnai soozh pullaaNi ammaanai
min idaiyaar vEtkai nOy koora irunthadhanai
kan navilum thiN thOL kaliyan oli vallaar
mannavaraay maN aaNdu vaan naadu munnuvarE.
19.meaning: Of the Lord of Tiruppullani, surrounded by punnai trees that bloom golden, and of the lovesickness that grew intense in the slender-waisted lady - these verses Kaliyan of stone-hard, firm shoulders composed melodiously. Those able to recite them will become kings, rule the earth, and then attain the heavenly realm.

20.translit:
mannum maRai naangum aanaanai pullaaNith 
tennan tamizhai vadamozhiyai naangoodil 
mannum maNimaadakkoyil maNaaLanai 
nal neer talaicchanga naaL matiyai naan vaNangum (71)
20.meaning: He who became the four eternal Vedas, who is at Tiruppullani, who is the southern Tamil and the northern Sanskrit, the bridegroom of the abiding jewel-turreted temple in Naangoor, the fresh-watered Talaicchangam — the youthful crescent moon: Him do I worship.

0.translit:
uDamburuvil moondroraay moorttiveeraay ulaguyya ninraanai,
anrupeeycchi viDamparuhu vittahanaik kanrumeettu viLaiyaaDa vallaanai varaimeekaanil,
taDamparuhu karumuhilait tanjaikkooyil tavanerikkoor peruneriyai vaiyangaakkum,
kaDumparimeel karkiyainaankaNDukoNDeen kaDipozilsoozh kaDanmallait talasayanatte.
0.meaning: He who, taking on bodily form, stood as the three (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva) yet as the distinct Lord, that the world might be saved; the wondrous one who that day drank the poison from the demoness Putana, and who could play at grazing the calves; the dark cloud that drinks up the broad ponds upon the high mountain; the Lord of the Tanjai temple, the great path that is the very way of penance; the Kalki riding the swift horse who will guard the world -- Him I have seen and gained at Talasayanam in Kadanmallai (Mahabalipuram) surrounded by fragrant groves.

1.translit:
nilaiyaaLaa ninvaNanga veNDaaye yaaginum, en
mulaiyaaLa vorunaaLun nahalattaal aaLaaye,
silaiyaaLaa marameyta tiralaaLaa tirumeyya
malaiyaaLaa, neeyaaLa vaLaiyaaLa maaTToome.
1.meaning: Even though You will not let me worship You enduringly, at least one day let my breasts be ruled (embraced) by Your chest -- so make me Yours. O wielder of the bow, mighty one who shot through the trees (the seven sal trees), O Lord of the sacred Meyyam hill! While You rule me, I shall not be able to keep my bangles (they slip away in my pining for You).

2.translit:
kaTTeeru neeLccoolaik kaaNDavattait teemooTTi
viTTaanai meyyam amarnda perumaanai
maTTeeru karpahattai maatarkkaay vaNTuvarai
naTTaanai naaDi naraiyooril kaNDeene.
2.meaning: He who set fire to the Khandava forest of long groves dense with clusters; the great Lord who abides at Meyyam; the honey-laden wish-granting Kalpaka tree; He who, for the sake of women, founded fertile Dwaraka -- seeking Him, I have found Him at Naraiyur.

3.translit:
aruvisoor veengaDam neermalai enruvaay
veruvinaaL meyyam vinavi yirukkinraaL,
peruhuseerk kaNNapuram enru peesinaaL
uruhinaaL, uLmelin daaLitu venkoloo.
3.meaning: With trembling lips she utters 'Venkatam where waterfalls cascade, Neermalai!' She sits questioning about Meyyam. She speaks of 'Kannapuram of abounding glory.' She melts within, she grows faint within -- what indeed is this (that has come over my daughter)?

4.translit:
veeyirunj coolai vilangal soozhnda meyya maNaaLar iv vaiyamellaam
taayina naayaka raavar toozhee taamaraik kaNgaL irundavaaru,
seeyirung kunram tihazhnda toppac cevviya vaahi malarndacooti
aayiram tooLo Dilangu pooNum acco oruvar azhahiyavaa.
4.meaning: The Bridegroom of Meyyam, encircled by mountains and groves rich with bamboo -- He is the Lord who has measured and pervaded all this world, O friend! Behold how His lotus eyes are! Like a tall reddish hill shining forth, His radiance blossoms fair and crimson; with His thousand shoulders and the ornaments that gleam upon them -- accho! how beautiful is that peerless one!

5.translit:
suDalai yilsuDu neeran amarndatuoor
naDalai teerttava nainarai yoorkaNDu, en
uDalai yuLpuhun duLLa murukkiyuN
viDalai yaiccenru kaaNDummey yattuLe.
5.meaning: He who removed the affliction of the one who smears himself with the burning ash of the cremation ground (Shiva) -- seeing Him at Naraiyur; the youthful one who enters into my body and melts and consumes my very soul -- Him we shall go and behold within Meyyam.

6.translit:
maiyaar kaDalum maNivaraiyum maamuhilum,
koyyaar kuvaLaiyum kaayaavum poonriruNDa
meyyaanai, meyya malaiyaanaic cankeendum
kaiyaanai, kaitozhaa kaiyalla kaNDaame.
6.meaning: He whose body is dark like the collyrium-dark sea, the sapphire mountain, the great cloud, the freshly plucked blue lily, and the kaaya flower; the Lord of the Meyyam hill, He who bears the conch in His hand -- the hands that do not worship Him, we see, are not (worthy to be called) hands at all.

7.translit:
piNDiyaar maNDai eentip pirarmanai tiritan duNNum,
uNDiyaan caapam teertta oruvanoor, ulaha meettum
kaNDiyoor arangam meyyam kaccipeer mallai enru
maNDinaar, uyyal allaal marraiyaark kuyya laamee?
7.meaning: He who removed the curse of the one (Shiva) who, holding a skull as a begging bowl, wandered to others' houses to eat his food -- this is the town of that peerless one. Those who throng eagerly saying 'Kandiyur, Arangam (Srirangam), Meyyam, Kacchi (Kanchi), Per, Mallai' which all the world praises -- they alone are saved; can salvation come to any others?

8.translit:
anna uruvin ariyai tirumeyyattu
in amuta veLLattai indaLoor andaNanai
mannu matiL kacci veeLukkai aaL ariyai
manniya paaDahattu em maindanai veqkaavil
8.meaning: Hari of the swan-like form at Tirumeyyam; the flood of sweet nectar at Indaloor, the pure Brahmin (Lord) there; the man-lion (Narasimha) who is master at Velukkai in walled Kacchi (Kanchi); our strong youth abiding at Paadagam; and at Vehka (I beheld Him).

0.translit:
mudiyonRi moovula gangaLum aaNDu un
adiyERku aruLenRu avanpin toDarnda
paDiyil guNattu paratha nambikku anRu
adinilai yeendaanai paaDip paRa
 ayoddhiyar koomaanai paaDip paRa.
0.meaning: Sing and leap for joy of Him who, when Bharata of flawless virtue followed Him saying, 'You should wear the crown and rule the three worlds; grant grace to me, your servant,' gave on that day His sandals as a token of His rule. Sing and leap for joy of the King of the people of Ayodhya.

1.translit:
taarkku iLan tambikku arasee eendu taNDakam
nooRRavaL solkoNDu poogi nuDangiDai
soorppa Nagaavai seviyoDu mookku ava
aarkka arindaanai paaDip paRa
 ayoddhikku arasanai paaDip paRa.
1.meaning: Sing and leap for joy of Him who gave the kingdom to His young garlanded brother, went forth to the Dandaka forest on the word of her (Kaikeyi) who had her hundreds, and cut off the ears and nose of slender-waisted Surpanakha so that she shrieked. Sing and leap for joy of the King of Ayodhya.

2.translit:
kaaraar kaDalai yaDaittiTTu ilangai pukku
oraadaan ponmuDi onbathoo DonRaiyum
neeraa avantambikkE nee Larasu eenda
aaraa amudanai paaDip paRa
 ayoddhiyar vEndanai paaDip paRa.
2.meaning: Sing and leap for joy of the never-cloying nectar who dammed the dark vast ocean, entered Lanka, and granted the long kingdom to the brother of that unrighteous one (Ravana) whose ten golden crowns He struck down. Sing and leap for joy of the Sovereign of the people of Ayodhya.

3.translit:
vaaraninda mulai maDavaay vaidEvee viNNappam
tEraninda ayoddhiyar koon perundEvee kETTaruLaay
kooraninda vEl valavan guganoDum gangai tannil
seeraninda toozhamaiyai koNDadum oo radaiyaaLam.
3.meaning: O Vaidehi (Sita) of breasts bound with a bodice, hear this entreaty: O great Queen of the chariot-adorned King of Ayodhya, listen graciously. That He formed glorious friendship with Guha, the wielder of the sharp spear, by the Ganga is also a token of recognition.

4.translit:
maittagu maa malarkkuzhalaay vaidEvee viNNappam
ottapugazh vaanara koon uDanirundu ninaittEDa
attagu seer ayoddhiyar koon adaiyaaLam ivai mozhindaan
ittagaiyaal adaiyaaLam eedu avankai moodhiramE.
4.meaning: O Vaidehi (Sita) with dark, flower-decked tresses, hear this entreaty: while the matchlessly famed king of the monkeys (Sugriva) sat with Him recalling and searching, the King of Ayodhya of fitting glory spoke these marks of recognition. By such tokens, here is the recognition - this is the ring from His hand.

5.translit:
vaDadisai madurai saaLakki raamam vaikuntam tuvarai ayoddhi
idamuDai vadari iDavagai yuDaiya emburu Doottaman irukkai
taDavarai yadira taraNi viNDiDiya talaippaRRi karaimaram saaDi
kaDalinai kalanga kaDuttizhi gangai kaNDam ennum kaDi nagarE.
5.meaning: Northern Mathura, Salagrama, Vaikunta, Dvaraka, Ayodhya, spacious Badari - these are the abodes of our Supreme Lord Purushottama. And His seat is the well-guarded city called Gangaikondam, where the Ganga, making the great mountains tremble and the earth split and thunder, rushes down seizing and felling the trees on its banks and churning the sea into turmoil.

6.translit:
suRRam ellaam pin toDara tol kaanam adaindavanE!
aRRavarkaTku arumarundE! ayoddhi nagarkku adipatiyE!
kaRRavargaL taam vaazhum kaNapuratthu en karumaNiyE!
siRRavai tan sol koNDa seeraamaa! taalElO!
6.meaning: O You who reached the ancient forest with all Your kinsfolk following behind! O rare medicine for those who have nothing! O Sovereign of the city of Ayodhya! O my dark gem of Kannapuram where the learned dwell! O glorious Rama who heeded the word of Your little (step)mother! Sleep, lullaby!

7.translit:
aalinilai paalaganaay anRu ulagam uNDavanE
vaaliyai konRu arasu iLaiya vaanaratthukku aLittavanE
kaalin maNi karai alaikkum kaNapuratthu en karumaNiyE
aali nagarkku adipatiyE ayoddhimanE taalElO
7.meaning: O You who, as an infant on a banyan leaf, swallowed the worlds on that day! O You who slew Vali and gave the kingdom to the younger monkey (Sugriva)! O my dark gem of Kannapuram, where the streams' waters wash gems upon the banks! O Sovereign of the city of Tirunagari (Aali), O King of Ayodhya, sleep, lullaby!

8.translit:
anganeDu madiL puDaisoozh ayoddhi yennum
aNinagaratthu ulaganaittum viLakkum joodhi
vengadiroon kulattukkoor viLakkaay toonRi
viNmuzhudum uyakkoNDa veeran Rannai
senganeDum karumugilai iraaman Rannai
tillainagar tiruchchitra kooDan tannuL
engaL tani mudalvanai em perumaan Rannai
enRukolO kaNkuLira kaaNu naaLE.
8.meaning: He is the light that illumines all worlds in the beautiful city called Ayodhya, girt about by tall fair ramparts; appearing as a lamp for the dynasty of the fierce-rayed Sun, the hero who redeemed all the heavens. The red-eyed, tall, dark cloud-hued Rama, who dwells in the holy Chitrakuta of Tillai city - our sole Primal Lord, our Lord - when will be the day that I behold Him to my eyes' cool delight?

9.translit:
amponeDu maNimaaDa ayoddhi yeydi
araseydi agattiyanvaay taanmun konRaan
Ranperundol kadai kETTu mitilai selvi
ulaguyya tiruvayiRu vaaytta makkaL
sempavaLa tiraLvaay tan saridai kETTaan
tillainagar tiruchchitra kooDan tannuL
emperumaan Ran saridai seviyaal kaNNaal
paruguvoo minnamudai madiyoo minRE.
9.meaning: Reaching Ayodhya of tall golden gem-studded mansions and attaining the kingship, He heard from Agastya's mouth the great ancient tale of the one (Ravana) He had earlier slain; and the children born of the sacred womb of Mithila's princess (Sita) for the redemption of the world recited His story with their coral-red lips, which He listened to - that Lord of ours who dwells in the holy Chitrakuta of Tillai city. His story we shall drink through our ears and eyes, like ambrosia; we shall not weigh or count it now.

10.translit:
mETTiLa mEdigaL taLaiviDu maayargaL
 vEyngkuzhal oosaiyum viDaimaNi kuralum
eeTTiya isai disai parandana vayaluL
 irundina surumbinam ilangaiyar kulattai
vaaTTiya varisilai vaanavar ERE
 maamuni vELviyai kaattu ava bratham
aaTTiya aDutiRal ayoddhi em arasE
 arangattammaa paLLi yezhundaru LaayE.
10.meaning: As the cowherds loose the young buffaloes onto the uplands, the sound of their bamboo flutes and the bells at the bulls' necks gather into music spreading across the directions; the swarms of bees settle in the fields. O Bull among the celestials with the striped bow that ravaged the race of the Lanka-dwellers! O King of Ayodhya, of slaying might, who guarded the sacrifice of the great sage (Vishvamitra) and performed the avabhrita ablution! O Lord of Srirangam, deign to rise from Your sacred sleep.

11.translit:
kavaLa yaanai paaypura vittE roDa rakkar ellaam
tuvaLa, venRa venRi yaaLan Ran tamar kollaamE
tavaLa maaDam neeDa yoottikaavalan Ran siRuvan
kuvaLai vaNNan kaaNa aaDeer kuzhamaNi tooramE.
11.meaning: He is the victor who triumphed so that the elephants taking their fodder, the leaping horses, the chariots, and all the demons drooped in defeat. So that His kinsmen may not be harmed, and that the lily-hued little son of the guardian of Ayodhya with its tall white mansions may behold it, play the game - play kuzhamani-tooram far afield!

12.translit:
kaRpaar iraama piraanaiyallaal maRRum kaRparoo?,
puRpaa mudalaa pullerum baadhi onRu inRiyE,
naRpaal ayoddhiyil vaazhum saraasaram muRRavum,
naRpaalukku uyttanan naanmuga naar peRRa naaTTuLE.
12.meaning: Those who learn - would they learn of any other than Lord Rama? For in the land that the four-faced Brahma had received, He led to the good state every moving and unmoving being that lived in fair Ayodhya - all of them, without leaving out even a single one, from the grass and the plants to the smallest ant.

0.translit:
vaaNilaa muruval siRu nudhal perun thOL maadharaar vana mulaip payanE pENinEn
adhanaip pizhai enak karudhip pEdhaiyEn piRavi nOy aRuppaan,
EN ilEn irundhEn eNNinEn eNNi iLaiyavark kalavi in thiRaththai naaNinEn,
vandhun thiruvadi adaindhEn naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay
0.meaning: O my Lord dwelling in Naimisaranyam! Bright was the smile, small the forehead, broad the shoulders of those women, and I desired only the fruit of their lovely bosoms. Realizing at last that this was my error, fool that I am, to cut off the disease of birth I, who had been without measure and helpless, reflected; and reflecting, I grew ashamed of the sweet pleasure of union with such young women. So I have come and reached Your sacred feet.

1.translit:
silambadi uruvil karu neDun kaNNaar thiRaththanaay aRaththaiyE maRandhu,
pulam padindhu uNNum bOgamE perukkip pOkkinEn pozhudhinai vaaLaa,
alam puri thaDak kaiyaa! aayanE! maayaa! vaanavarkku arasanE!
vaanOr nalam purindhu iRainjun thiruvadi adaindhEn naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay
1.meaning: O my Lord of Naimisaranyam! Devoted to women whose dark, long eyes are like the form of anklets, I forgot all righteousness, and immersed in the senses I only increased the pleasures of enjoyment, wasting my time in vain. O You of the broad hand bearing the discus, O cowherd, O wondrous Lord, O King of the celestials! I have reached Your sacred feet, which the heavenly ones lovingly worship.

2.translit:
soodhinaip perukkik kaLavinai thuNindhu suri kuzhal madandhaiyar thiRaththu,
kaadhalE miguththuk kaNDavaa thirindha thoNDanEn naman thamar seyyum,
vEdhanaikku oDungi naDunginEn vElai veN thirai alamarak kaDaindha, naadhanE
vandhun thiruvadi adaindhEn naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay
2.meaning: O my Lord of Naimisaranyam! Multiplying gambling, daring to steal, increasing my lust for women with curling tresses, I, your wretched servant, wandered as I pleased; but cowering and trembling at the torment that Yama's servants inflict, O Lord who churned the sea so that its white waves whirled, I have come and reached Your sacred feet.

3.translit:
vambu ulaan koondhal manaiviyaith thuRandhu piRar poruL thaaram endrivaRRai,
nambinaar iRandhaal naman thamar paRRa eRRi vaiththu,
eri ezhuginRa sembinaal iyanRa paavaiyaip paavee! thazhuvu ena mozhivadaRku anji,
nambanE! vandhun thiruvadi adaindhEn naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay
3.meaning: O my Lord of Naimisaranyam! Forsaking my own wife with her fragrant hair, those who trust in others' wealth and others' women, when they die, Yama's servants seize, fling them down, and saying 'O sinner, embrace it!' make them clasp a glowing red statue cast of copper. Fearing such a word, O trustworthy One, I have come and reached Your sacred feet.

4.translit:
iDumbaiyaal aDarppuNDu iDuminO thuRRu ennum irandhavarkku illaiyE enRu,
neDunj solaal manuththa neesanEn andhO! ninaikkilEn vinaip payan thannai,
kaDunj solaar kaDiyaarkku aalanaar thamaraal paDuvadhOr koDu miRaikku anji,
naDungi naan vandhun thiruvadi adaindhEn naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay
4.meaning: O my Lord of Naimisaranyam! When those oppressed by misery and crushed begged, 'Give us a morsel,' I, a base wretch, repelled them with harsh, cutting words. Alas, I never considered the fruit of my deeds. Now trembling in fear of the cruel torment to be suffered at the hands of Yama's servants, the agents of death meted out for the harsh-tongued and pitiless, I have come and reached Your sacred feet.

5.translit:
kODiya manaththaal sinath thozhil purindhu thirindhu naay inaththoDun thiLaiththiTTu,
Odiyum uzhanRum uyirgaLEk konREn uNarvilEn aadhalaal, namanaar
paaDiyaip peridhum parisu azhiththiTTEn paramanE! paaR kaDal kiDandhaay!,
naaDi naan vandhun thiruvadi adaindhEn naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay
5.meaning: O my Lord of Naimisaranyam! With a crooked mind I performed deeds of anger, and wandering and consorting with the pack of dogs, running and toiling, I killed living beings. Because I was without true understanding, I greatly spoiled and crowded Yama's city with my evil. O Supreme One, O You who recline on the ocean of milk! Seeking You, I have come and reached Your sacred feet.

6.translit:
nenjinaal ninaindhum vaayinaal mozhindhum needhi allaadhana seydhum,
thunjinaar sellun thol neRi kEttE thuLanginEn viLan kani munindhaay!
vanjanED iyEn nenjinil piriyaa vaanavaa! thaanavarkku enRum nanjanE!,
vandhun thiruvadi adaindhEn naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay
6.meaning: O my Lord of Naimisaranyam! Plotting with my heart, speaking with my mouth, and doing things contrary to justice, I trembled when I heard of the ancient path that those who die must travel. O You who in anger felled the wood-apple demon! O celestial One who never departs from this deceiver's heart! O You who are forever poison to the demons! I have come and reached Your sacred feet.

7.translit:
EvinaarkkaliyaarnaligavenRen mEl enganE vaazhumaaRu? aivar
kOvinaar seyyuk koDumaiyai maDiththEn kuRunkuDi neDunkaDal vaNNaa
paavinaar in sol pan malark koNDu un paadhamE paravi naan paNindhu,
en naavinaal vandhun thiruvadi adaindhEn naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay
7.meaning: O my Lord of Naimisaranyam! How can I survive when the five senses, my masters, set affliction upon me at their command? I have crushed the cruelty wrought by them. O Lord of Kurunkudi, O One hued like the vast dark sea! Taking sweet poetic words like many flowers, praising and bowing to Your feet alone, with my tongue I have come and reached Your sacred feet.

8.translit:
oonidaic chuvar vaiththu enbu thooN naaTTi urOmam vEyndhu onbadhu vaasal,
thaan uDaik kurambaip piriyum bOdhu unRan saraNamE saraNam enRirundhEn,
thEn uDaik kamalath thiruvinukku arasE! thirai koL maandun kaDal kiDandhaay!,
naan uDaith thavaththaal thiruvadi adaindhEn naimisaaraNiyaththuL endhaay
8.meaning: O my Lord of Naimisaranyam! Built with walls of flesh, pillared with bones, thatched with hair, having nine gates - when the time comes to depart from this frail hut of a body, I resolved that Your feet alone are my refuge. O King of Lakshmi who dwells in the honey-filled lotus! O You who recline on the wave-tossed great ocean! By such merit as I possess, I have reached Your sacred feet.

9.translit:
Edham vandhu aNugaa vaNNam naam eNNi ezhuminO thozhudhum enRu,
imaiyOr naadhan vandhu iRainjum naimisaaraNiyath thendhaiyaic chindhaiyuL vaiththu,
kaadhalE miguththa kaliyan vaay oli sey maalai thaam katRu vallaargaL,
Odha neer vaiyagam aaNDu veN kuDaik keezh umbarum aaguvar thaamE
9.meaning: Saying, 'So that no evil may approach us, let us reflect and rise up and worship,' keeping in their thought our Father of Naimisaranyam, where even Indra, lord of the unblinking celestials, comes and worships - those who can master this garland of words sung with abundant love by the mouth of Kaliyan (Tirumangai Alwar) shall rule this sea-girt earth beneath the white parasol, and shall themselves become dwellers of the heavenly world above.

0.translit:
vaali maavalat toruvanat udalkeda varisilai vaLaiviththu anRu
Ela naaRu thaN thadam pozhil idam peRa irundha nalimaiyaththuL,
aali maa mukil adhirtara aru varai akaduRa mukadu ERi,
peeli maa mayil nadanjeyum thadanjunaip piridhi senRadai nenjE.
0.meaning: Long ago He bent His striped bow to destroy the body of the one (Vaali) endowed with mighty strength, and He dwells in the good Himalayas where fragrant, cool, broad groves give Him a place. There the great dark clouds thunder, climbing high over the lofty mountain peaks. O heart, go and reach Piridhi, where peacocks with grand plumes dance by the wide springs.

1.translit:
kalanga maak kadal arikulam paNiseyya aru varai aNai kaddi,
ilangai maa nakarp podiseydha vadikaL thaam irundha nallimaiyaththu,
vilangal pOl vana viRal irunjinaththana vEzhangaL thuyark kUra,
pilangoL vaaL eyiRRariya vai thiritaru piridhi senRadai nenjE.
1.meaning: He had the monkey hosts build a dam of huge mountains so that the vast sea churned and grew troubled, and the Lord who ground the great city of Lanka to dust dwells in the good Himalayas. There the elephants, mighty and fierce as mountains, suffer grief, while sharp-fanged lions whose teeth gleam like bright swords roam about. O heart, go and reach Piridhi.

2.translit:
thudikoL nuNNidaich churikuzhal thuLangu eyiRRu iLangodi thiRaththu aayar
idikoL venguralina vidaiyadarththavan irundha nallimaiyaththu,
kadikoL vEngaiyin naRu malar amaLiyin maNiyaRai misai vEzham,
pidiyinOdu vaNdisaisolath thuyilkoLum piridhi senRadai nenjE.
2.meaning: For the sake of the cowherd girl, a tender creeper with a slender waist like a small drum, curling locks, and gleaming teeth, He subdued the bulls whose bellowing was like thunder; that Lord dwells in the good Himalayas. There upon a fragrant bed of vengai blossoms, on jewelled rocks, the bull elephant sleeps with his mate while bees hum their song. O heart, go and reach Piridhi.

3.translit:
maRangoL aaL ariyuru ena veruvara oruvanadhu akal maarvam
thiRandhu vaanavar maNi mudi paNitara irundha nallimaiyaththuL,
iRangi EnangaL vaLai maruppidandhidak kidandhu aruke eri veesum,
piRangu maa maNi aruviyodu izhitaru piridhi senRadai nenjE.
3.meaning: Taking the dread form of a fierce man-lion so that all trembled, He tore open the broad chest of that one (Hiranya), and as the celestials bowed their jewelled crowns to Him He dwells in the good Himalayas. There wild boars descend and root up the earth with their curved tusks, and beside them gleaming great gems flow down with the cascading streams, casting forth fiery light. O heart, go and reach Piridhi.

4.translit:
karaisey maak kadal kidandhavan kanaikazhal amararkaL thozhudhEththa,
arai sey mEkalai alar makaL avaLodum amarndha nallimaiyaththu,
varaisey maak kaLiRu eeLa vedhir vaLar muLai aLai miku thEn thOyththu,
pirasa vaari than iLam pidikku aruLseyum piridhi senRadai nenjE.
4.meaning: He who reclines on the great wave-bordered ocean, whom the immortals worship and praise His resounding anklets, abides happily in the good Himalayas together with the flower-born Lakshmi who wears a girdle at her waist. There the great mountain-like bull elephant pulls down the tender bamboo shoots, dips them in honey-laden flowing nectar, and graciously offers it to his young mate. O heart, go and reach Piridhi.

5.translit:
paNangaL aayiram udaiya nalla aravaNaip paLLikoL paramaa enRu,
iNangi vaanavar maNi mudi paNitara irundha nallimaiyaththu,
maNangoL maadhavi nedung kodi visumbuRa nimirndhavai mukil paRRi,
piNangu pUm pozhil nuzhaindhu vaNdisaisolum piridhi senRadai nenjE.
5.meaning: "O Supreme One who reposes upon the good serpent-bed of a thousand hoods!" - thus the celestials draw near and bow their jewelled crowns to Him, who dwells in the good Himalayas. There the long fragrant madhavi creepers stretch up to the sky and cling to the clouds, and entering the tangled flowering groves the bees hum their song. O heart, go and reach Piridhi!

6.translit:
kaarkoL vEngaikaL kanavarai thazhuviya kaRivaLark kodi thunni,
pOrkoL vEngaikaL punavarai thazhuviya pUmpozhil imaiyaththuL,
ErkoL pUnjunaith thadam padindhu ina malar eddum iddu imaiyOrkaL,
pErkaL aayiram paravi ninRadi thozhum piridhi senRadai nenjE.
6.meaning: Dark vengai trees embrace the high mountains, thick with pepper-bearing creepers, and battling tigers cling to the wooded hills - in such flowering groves of the Himalayas. There the celestials bathe in the beautiful flowering springs and pools, offer the eight kinds of choice flowers, and praising His thousand names stand and worship His feet. O heart, go and reach Piridhi.

7.translit:
iravu kUrndhu iruL perukiya varai muzhai irum pasiyadhu kUra,
aravam aavikkum akan pozhil thazhuviya aru varai imaiyaththu,
paraman aadhi em panimukil vaNNan enRu eNNi ninRu imaiyOrkaL,
piramanOdu senRadi thozhum perundhakaip piridhi senRadai nenjE.
7.meaning: In the mountain caverns where night deepens and darkness grows, where the great hunger mounts and serpents hiss within the broad surrounding groves - such are the steep hills of the Himalayas. There the celestials, contemplating Him as the Supreme, the Primal One, our cool-cloud-hued Lord, go along with Brahma to worship His feet - the great noble one of Piridhi. O heart, go and reach it.

8.translit:
Odhi aayira naamangaL uNarndhavarkku uRu thuyar adaiyaamal,
Edham inRi ninRu aruLum nam perundhakai irundha nallimaiyaththu,
thaadhumalkiya piNdi viNdu alarkinRa thazhal puraiyezhil nOkki,
pEdhai vaNdukaL eri ena veruvaru piridhi senRadai nenjE.
8.meaning: Our great noble Lord, who stands and graciously protects those who chant and realize His thousand names so that no grief befalls them and no harm comes near, dwells in the good Himalayas. There the asoka flowers, rich with pollen, burst open and bloom, and gazing on their fire-like beauty the foolish bees take fright as if at flame. O heart, go and reach Piridhi.

9.translit:
kariya maamukiR padalangaL kidandhu avai muzhangida, kaLiRu enRu
periya maasuNam varai enap peyartaru piridhi em perumaanai,
varikoL vaNdaRai paimpozhil mangaiyar kaliyanadhu oli maalai,
ariya innisai paadu nalladiyavarkku aru vinai adaiyaavE.
9.meaning: Where banks of great dark clouds lie and thunder, the huge serpents move about taking them for elephants, on the mountains - of such Piridhi is our Lord. Of this Lord, Kaliyan of Mangai, where striped bees hum in the verdant groves, has sung this sounding garland of verses; to the good devotees who sing its rare sweet melody, cruel karma shall never come near.

10.translit:
vaNkaiyaana avuNarkku naayakan vELviyil senRu maaNiyaay,
maNkaiyaal irandhaan maraamaram Ezhum eydha valaththinaan,
eNkaiyaan imayaththuLLaan irunjOlai mEviya em piraan,
thiNkaimmaa thuyar adhu theerththavan thiruvEngadam adai nenjamE.
10.meaning: He who went as a dwarf-student to the sacrifice of the chief of the generous-handed demons (Bali) and begged for land with His hand; He of the strength who pierced the seven maa trees; He of mighty hands who is in the Himalayas; our Lord who abides at Irunjolai; He who removed the suffering of the strong-trunked elephant - O heart, reach His Tiruvenkatam!

11.translit:
pEy mulaiththalai nanjuNda piLLai ath
teLLiyaar vaNangap padum dhEvanai,
maayanai madhiL kOvalidaikazhi
maindhanai anRi andhaNar sindhaiyuL eesanai,
ilangum sudarch chOdhiyai endhai
aai enakku eyppinil vaippinai
kaasinai maNiyaich chenRu naadik
kaNNa mangaiyuL kaNdukoNdEnE.
11.meaning: The child who drank the poison from the breast-tip of the demoness (Putana), the God whom the clear-minded worship, the wondrous one, the young Lord of walled Tirukkovalur and Idaikazhi, the Master within the hearts of the brahmins, the brilliant radiant light, my father and refuge in times of exhaustion, the precious gem - Him I sought out and found in Kannamangai!

0.translit:
thangaiyai mookkum thamaiyanaith thalaiyum thadinthaem thaa sarathipoy
eNGgum thanpuka zaavirunthu arasaaNDa emburu Doaththaman irukkai
kaNGgai kaNGgaiyenra vaasakath thaalae kaduvinai kaLainthida kirkum
kaNGgaiyin karaimael kaithozha ninra kaNDamen num kadi nakarae.
0.meaning: Our Dasarathi (Rama) who cut off the nose of the sister (Surpanakha) and the heads of the elder brother (Ravana), then went forth, dwelt with his fame spread everywhere and ruled his kingdom — that is the abode of our Purushottama. By merely uttering the word 'Ganga, Ganga' it can wash away even cruel sins; on the bank of such a Ganga stands the holy city called Kandam, where men worship Him with folded hands.

1.translit:
salampothi yudampin thazalumiz paezhvaayc chandiran veNGgathir anja
malarnthezun thaNavi maNivaNNa vuruvin maalpuru Doaththaman vaazhvu
nalamthikaz sadaiyaan mudikkonrai malarum naaraNan paathaththu zaayum
kalanthizhi punalaal pukarpadu kaNGgaik kaNDamen num kadi nakarae.
1.meaning: The Ganga, blossoming forth and rising so that the moon with its body full of cool water and its wide fire-spewing mouth, and the hot rays of the sun grow afraid, is the dwelling of the sapphire-hued Tirumal, the Purushottama. There flows water made fragrant and bright, mingled with the konrai blossoms from the crown of the good-natured matted-haired one (Siva) and with the tulasi from the feet of Narayana — on its bank is the holy city called Kandam.

2.translit:
athirmuka mudaiya valampuri kumizththi azhalumiz aazhikoN derinthuaNGgu
ethirmuka vasurar thalaikaLai yidarum emburu Doaththaman irukkai
sathumukan kaiyil sathuppuyan thaaLil saNGkaran sadaiyinil thaNGgi
kathirmuka maNikoN dizhipunal kaNGgaik kaNDamen num kadi nakarae.
2.meaning: Blowing the loud-roaring conch (valampuri) and hurling the fire-spewing discus to strike down the heads of the asuras who came against Him face to face — that is the abode of our Purushottama. It is the holy city called Kandam, on the Ganga whose radiant waters, set with jewels, descend after lingering in the four-faced Brahma's hand, at the feet of the four-armed Lord, and in the matted hair of Sankara (Siva).

3.translit:
imaiyavar irumaan thirunthara saaLa aerruvan thethirporu saenai
namapuram naNuka naanthakam visirum nampuru Doaththaman nakarthaan
imavantham thodaNGgi iruNGkadalaLavum irukarai ulakiraith thaada
kamaiyudaip perumaik kaNGgaiyin karaimael kaNDamen num kadi nakarae.
3.meaning: So that the celestials may rule their kingdom in proud contentment, He brandishes his sword Nandaka to send the opposing army that comes to fight him near to the city of Yama (death) — that is the city of our Purushottama. Beginning from the Himalayas down to the vast sea, with the world thronging on both its banks, on the bank of the patient and great Ganga is the holy city called Kandam.

4.translit:
uzhuvathoar padaiyum ulakkaiyum villum oNsuda raazhiyum saNGgum
mazhuvodu vaaLum padaikkala mudaiya maalpuru Doaththaman vaazhvu
ezhumaiyum koodi eeNDiya paavam iraippozhu thaLavinil ellaam
kazhuvidum perumaik kaNGgaiyin karaimael kaNDamen num kadi nakarae.
4.meaning: It is the dwelling of Tirumal, the Purushottama, who wields the weapons — the plough, the pestle, the bow, the bright radiant discus, the conch, the axe and the sword. On the bank of the great Ganga that can in the space of a single instant wash away all the sins heaped up across all seven births, is the holy city called Kandam.

5.translit:
thalaippeythu kumuricc chalampothi maekam salasala pozhinthidak kaNDu
malaipperuNG kudaiyaal maraiththavan mathurai maalpuru Doaththaman vaazhvu
alaippudaith thiraivaay arunthava munivar avapira thamkudain thaada
kalappaikaL kozhikkum kaNGgaiyin karaimael kaNDamen num kadi nakarae.
5.meaning: Seeing the water-laden clouds gather, thunder and pour down 'salasala', He of Mathura shielded the cattle and people with the great mountain as an umbrella — that is the dwelling of Tirumal, the Purushottama. On the bank of the Ganga, whose surging waves the great-penanced sages plunge into to bathe at the rite's conclusion (avabhrtha), and which casts up ploughs (washed down from the fields), is the holy city called Kandam.

6.translit:
virpidith thiruththu vaezhaththai murukki maelirun thavanthalai saadi
marporu thezhappaayndhu araiyana yuthaiththa maalpuru Doaththaman vaazhvu
arputha mudaiyaayi raavatha mathamum avariLam padiyaroN saanthum
karpaka malarum kalanthizhi kaNGgaik kaNDamen num kadi nakarae.
6.meaning: Bending and breaking the bow, crushing the elephant (Kuvalayapida), striking down the head of the one seated upon it, leaping up to wrestle and kicking the king (Kamsa) to death — that is the dwelling of Tirumal, the Purushottama. On the Ganga where flow, mingled together, the rut-fluid of the wondrous celestial elephant Airavata, the fine sandal-paste of the young celestial women, and the blossoms of the kalpaka tree, is the holy city called Kandam.

7.translit:
thiraiporu kadalsoozh thiNmathiL thuvarai vaenthuthan maiththunan maarkkaay
arasinai yaviya arasinai yaruLum aripuru Doaththaman amarvu
nirainirai yaaka nediyana yoopam nirantharam ozhukkuvittu iraNDu
karaipurai vaeLvip pukaikamaz kaNGgai kaNDamen num kadi nakarae.
7.meaning: The king of Dwaraka, ringed by strong ramparts and the wave-beating sea, who for the sake of his brothers-in-law (the Pandavas) destroyed one kingdom and bestowed another — that is the seat of Hari, the Purushottama. On the Ganga, fragrant with the smoke of sacrifices, where tall sacrificial posts (yupa) are set up row upon row in continuous succession filling both banks, is the holy city called Kandam.

8.translit:
vadathisai mathurai saaLakki raamam vaikuntham thuvarai ayoaththi
idamudai vathari yidavakai yudaiya emburu Doaththaman irukkai
thadavarai yathirath tharaNiviN didiyath thalaipparrik karaimaram saadi
kadalinaik kalaNGgak kaduththizhi kaNGgaik kaNDamen num kadi nakarae.
8.meaning: Northern Mathura, Salagrama, Vaikunta, Dwaraka, Ayodhya and spacious Badari — He who holds all these places as His own is our Purushottama, and this is His abode. On the Ganga that descends in fury, making the great mountains tremble, the earth split and crack, seizing and uprooting the trees on its banks, and stirring the sea into turmoil, is the holy city called Kandam.

9.translit:
moonrezhuth thathanai moonrezhuth thathanaal moonrezhuth thaakkimoon rezhuththai
aenrukoN diruppaarkku irakkamnan kudaiya emburu Doaththaman irukkai
moonradi nimirththu moonrinil thoanri moonrinil moonruru vaanaan
kaanthadam pozhilsoozh kaNGgaiyin karaimael kaNDamen num kadi nakarae.
9.meaning: It is the abode of our Purushottama, who bears tender compassion for those who, by means of the three letters, make the three letters into the three letters, and hold fast to the three letters (the sacred syllable Om / the threefold mantra). He stretched out three strides (as Trivikrama), appeared among the three, and became the three forms within the three. On the bank of the Ganga, encircled by forest groves and wide gardens, is the holy city called Kandam.

10.translit:
poNGgoli kaNGgaik karaimali kaNDaththu uraipuru Doaththaman adimael
veNGgali naliyaa villiput thoorkkoan vittusith thanvirup purru
thaNGgiya anpaal seythamiz maalai thaNGgiya naavudai yaarkku
kaNGgaiyil thirumaal kazhaliNaik keezhae kuLiththirun thakaNak kaamae.
10.meaning: Upon the feet of the Purushottama who dwells in Kandam, abounding on the bank of the loud-roaring Ganga, the lord of Villiputtur — Vishnuchittan (Periyalvar), whom the cruel Kali age does not afflict — out of his desire and abiding love composed this garland of Tamil verses. For those who hold these on their tongue, it shall be as if they had bathed and remained beneath the twin ankleted feet of Tirumal in the Ganga.

0.translit:
kooramum saaraa koduvinaiyum saaraa, tee
maatramum saaraa vakaiyaRindheen, - aatrang
karaikkidakkum kaNNan kadalkidakkum, maayan
uraikkidakku muLLath thenakku.
0.meaning: No want shall come near me, no cruel sin shall come near me, and no evil speech shall come near me — for I have learned the way. Kannan who reclines upon the river bank, the wondrous one who reclines upon the ocean, his glory abides within my heart. Therefore none of these afflictions can ever touch me.
